text: Ordered Caramel frappe at Drive thru, BIG MISTAKE! Took 30 min and there were only 2 cars in front of me.

This SBX has varied wildly in quality of service. Evening staff (6PM to close) is less friendly than I generally expect in Starbucks. Morning staff is more upbeat and less wrapped up in discussing their personal life and plans for after work.   The layout does not encourage one to stay and order multiple items over time. All of the tables except for the handicapped table are small (two-chair) and are crammed around the front door. The cushion-chair area in the back is nice, but the store is clearly focused on its very heavy drive-through business because the area behind the bar is VERY spacious at the expense of the customer-seating area.  The parking lot is always jammed during the day, but SBX is the only business in the building open at night, so parking eases after 6PM.  No real issues with the joint and the quality of the items is the same as any other decent SBX, but it doesn't stand out as iniviting, a shame given how often I am in Franklin.
this is my favorite local starbucks! always get my drinks right, handle long lines efficiently, and great customer service! shoutout to alyssa, she's always been very nice and helpful. :)

Megan at this location is absolutely amazing she always knows my order before I even tell her packs it exactly how I like in a bag without me asking and always is so friendly early in the mornings you don't find that often I come to the Starbucks just for her and her service. keep up the great work Megan you're making peoples days
Yes your typical Starbucks line moves fast coffee strong.  Lemon pound cake is banging as usual. I love the fact that I can prepare my own coffee to my liking. I'm a Starbucks girl.

Most of the time I go through the drive thru here.  While sometimes the line is a bit long I have to say these guys move in record time.  The line moves fast and my coffee is waiting for me when I get to the window.  How much more can you ask for?   Inside there is a decent amount of seating and there is quite a bit of outdoor seating too.  Overall this is a great Starbucks.

i dont know what has happened to the in store service in this place!  We have been down here for a few weeks now and have been here 6 times.Only once was the order completed without a screw up!  Today 2 tall decaf coffees took close to 20 minutes.By then our bagels were cold.First i was told the decaf was "brewing' and then i was told there was something 'wrong" with the brewer? After 15 minutes i was offered a decaf americano. Before we left i politely told a fellow behind the counter the service was abominable and he said 'well we can only store 2 vente size decafs at a time? HUH? I asked if the management knew about this and he just shrugged. Clearly this staff has never been trained to make it right for the customer.No apology.No coupon.Just a convoluted story.   Amazing how you can have numerous people running around a store but you cant get the simplest order straight My next visit to a Starbucks will certainly not be this one.

Nothing makes my busy day easy like my iced coffee at Starbucks and this location botched it up for me:( see my photo yup those are coffee grinds at the bottom a whole bunch of coffee grinds:( yuck yuck!

This morning I placed my order on the mobile app which is normally very convenient but today I somehow accidentally sent my order to the keystone store which is way out of my way. When I got to the Robb store and realized the mistake, I was already late for work and stressed but the staff there was very nice and helpful, my made my order quickly and even honored the fact that I already paid to the keystone store. Thanks Robb Starbucks!

We were in need of coffee numerous times while in Indianapolis and Starbucks is a fairly reliable place to get strong coffee.  The employees were friendly and helped guide us with ordering something that we would love since neither of us get to Starbucks very often. I'd say in about a year, we've only been to various locations about 3 times. I usually go for a chai tea latte but sometimes something with more caffeine. Our first full day, I didnt sleep the night before because of anxiety, so Starbucks saved me from being a zombie.  Overall, this Starbucks is pretty standard but I liked the employees and the store had a very zen atmosphere. This specific location will definitely help you get caffeinated.

Its a good location.I don't like the coffee at the place next door, but combine a Starbucks coffee with a tart from the other place and its a win-win. Well maybe not for my waist line or pocket book.   I like they have large common tables with plenty of outlets under them for computers. I really appreciate the multiple outlet on the walls. Too many people bogart every one of the power plugs, but thats not going to happen here.

I have reviewed this Starbucks before, and for this exact same reason. Why is this location just completely unable to remain open for a fixed schedule? For the past 2 weeks they have either been closed or running on a reduced schedule. There is NO EXCUSE for this. Someone on your team got COVID? Not an excuse, you are a fucking business. Your team is doing training? Not an excuse, do it outside of business hours. There is nothing more important to a BUSINESS than making MONEY and this reduced schedule/not being open is bullshit. This location just needs to be closed down. There is a Starbucks the next exit down with better service anyways. Please Starbucks, get a hold on this location. Get it under control. Poor leadership and business decisions being made here.

I think this place is the best of all location there is. the staff is so super freindly and seems to know everyone by name. Jane is the boss and  Ms ASHLEY is the most super bristia in the world. Very clean, My coffee is the same for 3 years no matter whom makes it is is always fresh and perfect.
Changed location. Now across from Suburban Community Hospital; next to American Heritage Bank.  Although it is drive up they also have walk in and seating inside & a patio.

Last time I am ever going here.  I called at about noon, and inquired about picking up some spent coffee grounds.  The fella was very helpful, took down my name, said he would tell his fellow workers, and to come by after 6pm so there would be a lot.   I stopped  by at 6:45pm, and was told a girl comes every day at 4pm to pick up grounds, and that's who got them.  Thanks a lot.

Wallet was stolen today at this starbucks.  The workers there were not helpful at all.  They wanted me to "file a report" to corporate and would call if it showed up.  Meanwhile someone was having fun with my credit card in cherry hill, new jersey.  And they made it seem like it was my fault that it was stolen, but it was stolen when i was sitting with my items. Not helpful or sympathetic.

Rude employees and they always run out of items. If it wasn't for the convenience and time factor, I wouldn't go there at all. If you have time, definitely head over to the Saxby's near polett walk instead.

Spacious, clean Starbucks located next to St. Joe's. Baristas were friendly but a little inefficient. 2-3 people in line and 10 minutes to get my drink. Hate to see what would happen if the line really started to back up. Not sure how long this store has been around, but it seems pretty new. Strange hours as it closed at 5 on the day that I visited. Let's not stay open too late now; we might pull in too many college kids in need of a study break.
Great little Starbucks hubs and I came across when we were visiting NOLA! Great neighborhood, clean and great service!

I was so excited to learn that there was a Starbucks being built near my home. However after multiple visits to this location I am very disappointed with the quality of the drinks. The staff is friendly, but not so helpful. I regularly order a "hot vanilla bean coconut milk latte" at Starbucks. Not one time have they made my drink correctly. I even attempt to show them the description of how the beverage is prepared located on the Starbucks app BUT STILL they make it as though it's a vanilla latte. For future reference in case any of the baristas see this review...please steam the coconut-milk with the vanilla bean POWDER inside (there is no vanilla syrup in the recipe what so ever). The coffee is overpriced as it is, I'm just asking for what I ordered.
My favorite coffeeshop in Rittenhouse. Aged Sumatra on the clover! The crew here is top notch, and a pleasant place to study or work on the laptop.

Being a long time Starbucks customer, this Starbucks is my ALL TIME favorite. All the ladies and gentlemen here are always are welcoming and accommodating. Not once have I been here and had a hard time. Even when I'm the one being difficult. I appreciate all of you there!!

First and foremost, I love starbucks but this particular branch is terrible!  Surprising considering it's location and constant influx of customers... It's always a long wait while the barista's chat it up...  Special drinks are never consistent even when the same barista makes it.  Subpar starbucks....needs a revamp of mature and versed management or should  lose it's franchise

Parking is horrible. The service is way below standards unless the store's manager Cara is there. There is a brand new drive thru Starbucks just 1/3 of a mile south at Robb & Sharlands right next to the I-80 freeway.  The employees freely share the only reason Starbucks didn't close this outdated store when the new one right down the street opened is the company can't get out of their lease.   You can avoid a store full of loud McQueen High School students by passing this location up.

Possibly the friendliest Starbucks I've ever been in. I sat inside for a few hours quietly working on a project. The baristas made the initiative to make every "guest" feel welcome. Very upbeat and positive!

I am not a fan of Starbucks except for a few favorites so I only go when there is nothing else around. That is the case with this location inside the Westin hotel. The staff is always friendly and makes a really nice breakfast sandwich. The coffee here seems less burnt than other Starbuck locations, so I give them some high marks for that. I also like the early hours that they open (6 am).   When you have no real choice, this place is worth going to.   As for the small location, there is nothing wrong with going outside underneath the umbrellas and taking in the fresh air at one of the many hotel provided tables, you just need to head left towards the beach and you will see them.   They also accept American Express which many places in Tama do not.

The cashier working the register at the Starbucks by gate E-26 is the rudest and most ignorant woman I have come across!! She is rude and just downright nast! My suggestion, if you don't like your JOB, and have 0 people skills, don't work at a takeout place; especially at an airport. Starbucks, you can do a better job training your employees. She was working 3/1/18 @ 10am

This place is supposedly open until 1 am but this was the first time i actually saw workers. I came around 10pm saturday night and they did not have ice coffee. They did not offer for me to wait while they brewed a fresh batch, or offer me something else. I ended up getting two shots over ice. $3.10   I do like the connivence of this inside of the casino.

I used to stop in this Starbucks in the Montgomery Mall every now and then when my friend worked here back in 2016/2017 or when I was hanging out with a group of friends and we stopped here. Since my friend left in August 2017, I haven't really been back since. This can be attributed to a few reasons. One is that I'm not a regular Starbucks goer, the second is I mainly visited this location to visit my friend and third is I'm not usually in the Montgomery Mall as much as I used to be. I don't mind Starbuck's products but since my friend left it seems the few times I did stop in this location has gone a bit downhill with service and employees. There's a Starbucks much closer to where I live (and even a bigger one across the street from the mall) and I'll stop in this one only if it is convenient.
Slow "baristas" and for the price you pay for coffee, really disappointing to have burnt coffee taste , getting our morning coffee elsewhere from now on
This Starbucks always makes drinks correctly and the drive thru is fast... Even on busy mornings.

Strawberry smoothie, you are too good to me. If you're still okay with drinking cold drinks before work when it's 20 degrees outside, head to Starbucks.   The staff is friendly, the drinks are delicious (although they are overpriced) and all of the Christmas goodies inside are awesome.   Great place to relax or prep for a work day.   See you again soon Southport Starbucks!

To me, this is one of the best Starbucks I've ever been to. This isn't the closest Starbucks to my house, but it is the one I frequent the most because of the great service, personable staff, and overall environment.   Each time I've been there I was always kindly greeted, offered many different suggestions, and just treated like a human being instead of somebody they are just taking an order from. The staff will actually hold a nice conversation with you, which is something I cherish.   As for the products that they offer, I've never been disappointed. Anything I've ordered has been top notch, even the suggestions from the staff when I was just getting into drinking coffee.   This location is excellent because they have a decent sized indoor area which is perfect for having coffee with a friend, studying, or even just reading the morning paper. If you wanted some fresh air you could even step outside and have a seat at one of their outdoor tables and enjoy your coffee.  If you love coffee you should check out this Starbucks location!
Good customer service. Decent coffee, messed up order twice but fixed it both times. Espresso sometimes tastes burnt but I think that's just the bean they use. Overall good
Had to have my daily Starbucks! It's not for luxury it's a necessity!! Love this store since it's so close to my house. Clean store, yummy drinks & a nice crews! Until next time,...probably tomorrow!

Mobile order team dropped the ball today.  Placed order when I arrived.  Kid apologized saying he "didn't see it" (the order) This location has a nice team but some lack enthusiasm.    No "real Starbucks vibe" here this eve..  Corporation is losing its touch on all fronts. Service. Orders.  Product line.    Having a mobile order feature for customers involves more than an app and collecting g money.  You need to have the product prepared.
Great coffee and in a good location.  Come here regularly and the staff are always nice

I'm a partner so I know the standards and I'm usually pretty understanding since I know what it's like. But the minute I walked into the store the girl on bar had an attitude with another customer. The store wasn't busy and I was the only one waiting, the Barista at the register didn't acknowledge me. I probably waited a good couple minutes before being greeted. The guy didn't give me my discount after I told him I was a partner.... luckily I mobile ordered my other drinks. Even then I didn't even drink my caramel macchiato because it was gross.

This is one of my favorite Starbucks locations - it's large, has ample indoor & outdoor seating, generally good customer service and delicious coffee.   I like that they always have nice displays of their coffee cups and other merchandise you can buy - I have purchased a few gifts for fellow coffee lovers here! Good place to study - with headphones on - and always love the free wifi!

I ordered the grande carmel machiatto. My second time here...the barista forgot to remove the foam and had to put more Carmel for taste. It also want extra hot...but once all that was done, my warm drink was almost perfect and the barista was so nice, i didn't care it was not extra hot and there was some foam. It was still a good drink.

The staff here is friendly and efficient. After a few visits they knew my order and made me feel like a regular. Now I am. And as always, the perfect latte. Love using the app, these guys are ALWAYS on time. Very much appreciated. Yes, parking can be tight. Hint: there are more spaces in the back.

They've yet to get even my most basic of orders right (iced black coffee), and when I do order specialty coffees, they suck. I ask for extra espresso every time and even though they'll charge me for it, they rarely add it. Specialty Lattes? They'll taste like mostly milk. Service is also mediocre. They will give me the correct drink if I drive back, but who wants to have to drive back every time? Not to mention it comes with a side of attitude.   Whoever is running this place, please get it together.

This Starbucks is BUSY!  Have some patience when you come here.  You see the long line, you know your order is not going to be ready in 2 seconds.   Even with as packed as it is, the hard working employees have a smile on their face and are doing their best job.   Seeing the negative reviews kinda surprises me.   Look up from your phone and see the LOOOONG line, and understand that you are not the only customer!  Take a seat and wait for your coffee to come.  If you dont want to wait, then dont order.

I though I had already written a review of this location. the drive through is quite slow and the line can extend into broadway, if that happens go to the next street, make 2 rights and you are in the parking lot. if you go inside it is just as fast, if not faster then the drive through.   they also messed up my order once, wanted an iced americano, got a hot... not too pleasant on a hot day but I had a test to get to so I couldn't even get a new one. To be fair it was busy, and I dont think the barista was fully listening.  although I like within walking distance and have been here many times so im still commin' back

Decent Starbucks coffee and cappuccino from here.   I ordered ahead through the app and my better half went in to pick out drinks up.  Two drawbacks here:  firstly, parking here is horrendous so the drive through is probably the only option here during busy times unless there are two of you, and secondly, the lid was not placed on my drink properly so it ended up on my car.  Not fun.

Love this location for its convenience, outdoor patio (dog friendly and doesn't face busy Broadway traffic), and fun and friendly staff. A consistently good experience every time I go. I have never used the drive-thru...always go in.
I always get some iced coffee that is very complicated, and they always get my drink right! They're fast and kind. Megan is my favorite barista, always taking care of me!

I like to study in starbucks, so my review reflects what I find conducive / disadvantageous -   Pros - bright           - good location (in terms of sitting outside / walking around)          - friendly staff          - closes at 10/11 pm as opposed to 9:30/10 pm (cherry hill location)          - good drink selection + clover brew  Con - Bathrooms almost always dirty, and usually 1 out of 2 are "out of order".          - Floors around seats often have crumbs/garbage around them.          - Parking is challenging  I understand this is a very busy starbucks and like the atmosphere, but the fact that the bathrooms / floors are always less than stellar bother me.

I would give no stars if that was possible. This is the absolute worst Starbucks ever in the name of Customer Service/hospitality employees everywhere. We are in the age of customer service, get with the program. Worst wait times in the world. And I have traveled the world and gone to Starbucks in many many countries.

Yes. I am at it again. A day does not go by that I don't have my coffee from Starbucks. I come here so much be it through drive through or going in at the batista  knows exactly what I want and how I want it.  I couldn't ask for a better way to start my morning.   Although they are extremely busy in the morning the drive thru moves very fast and my coffee is always piping hot. To top it off the customer service is always good.  Thank you for the invention of coffee. Thank you for the invention of Starbucks!
Just a regular Starbucks. Nothing special. I came here for a Starbucks mug. Paid and then left.   Ok service. Starbucks is Starbucks

Finally! A Starbucks with some COMFY chairs. There is a nice patio outside with misters and some mountain views. The drinks are the usual fare. But it's still a chain in my book. I just wish we had more local biz' to support. But I will take this location over the others.

I come to this Starbucks every single day. I have nothing but good things to say about the service as well as the staff. LJ, Mike, Kyle, Zac, Bethel and Sara never fail to serve me the perfect drink while also making genuine conversation with me. The store is always clean and usually pretty busy which I believe is due to the excellent service. I go out of my way to make time to go to this store everyday because of the staff.   Not only does the staff know my usual drink but when I feel like switching it up they give me spot on honest recommendations. I will continue to come to Starbucks and this one in particular everyday for as long as I live in the area.
Very long wait and the worst frapuccino I ever tasted. Big chunk of ice with a little coffee. Never going back.

My brother and I went here and our coffee was just okay. I had better a different locations. It was almost empty inside but the guy making our coffees was taking a very long time. Then a lady he was friends with came in and he took even longer to make them. He forgot to put the vanilla syrup in one coffee but corrected this. He also almost spilt the second one. We probably won't be returning to this location.
Really great coffee. My favorite is the classic grande iced coffee with almond milk and two sugars! Also really love the cake pops and little snack boxes.

I get this Starbucks can be very busy and it is stressful for the people that work there, but when I went in it was not busy at all. Somehow the baristas still managed to take forever to make the drink, and they made the drink wrong. If you're going to take forever when it is slow at least make the drink right.

A great place to unwind, relax, and enjoy a good cup of coffee, tea, or even a bite to eat. The staff is very friendly and helpful. It is clean and welcoming. Never had an issue. I never felt rushed to leave either. Sometimes I enjoy reading or book or studying for school, just for a change of scenery. It is a quiet, peaceful place to be for a break. Good beverages and snacks as well. I have always enjoyed my time here. Good location as well if one had time or the desire to check out other local places to eat or explore.
Very disappointed. Order was incorrect & no one seemed interested in helping to correct the problem by refunding the overcharge.  Will avoid this location in the future.

Amazing strawberry banana soy smoothie.  Since I actually watch what they put into the smoothie, I know that they put in real fruit and the ingredients are fresh.  It's really filling and is just delicious.  The wait staff is by far the most efficient I have ever seen at any Starbucks.  They are friendly, too.  I enjoy the visits I have here for that very reason.  There are way too many people in there sitting and taking up space, however.  Whether people are taking up sitting space with bags and laptops/ipads for hours on end without leaving.  If the Starbucks were bigger, I could understand more, but the cafe is a) fairly small and b) has a steady flow of traffic.  30 minutes to an hour?  Have at it.  After that, get off your butt and go to a library or somewhere appropriate for your attention getting tactics.  Sorry, but there.  I said it.  Ever heard of a library?  There is one a hop, skip, and jump away.  Usually people are at least talking audibly, the hot coffee drinks do get cold just as the cold drinks get lukewarm, and the place does close eventually.    That is no fault of the Starbucks, so I will not lower the stars.  But ugh, people.  Ugh, I tell you.  Let everyone have a seat to enjoy their frothy beverage.

Another starbucks review but seriously, this place is awesome! I am an addict of the Doppio Espresso, and for some reason this place makes it that much stronger and appropriate. Especially over ice, they simply know the way I like it which is not filled to the brim with ICE.  Their services are impeccable, and speedy. Their layout is better than most starbucks in the sense that there is actual parking, and you don't have to fight through hordes of people waiting for coffee to get to a seat. A great relaxing place for a good cup of joe.
What happened? There were only three cars ahead of me. I think I waited 10 minutes to order and 10 minutes to get one drink. It was never this bad before.
The very best caramel Frappuccino I have ever had and I've had LOTS of them! Friendly, fast, and delicious!
Slow, very slow, extremely slow!  This place is way understaffed.  Unless you have a lot of time, I wouldn't go here.

My go to Starbucks!!! Staff are friendly and know my cinnamon dolce latte obsession. Lol. I was annoyed when this place was closed for construction because there aren't any really near me other than this one besides the BHP one... and there's just too much traffic. Drive thru here can be slow but it's not usually a big deal since I stop after work. Can't wait for pumpkin spice to come back because I'm basic. Not sorry.

Never seen a covered drive-thru quite like this at a Starbucks before- way cool! And that Boise sky tonight - Wow!!!  At first we went through the drive-thru. Later I visited inside to use their wifi. Shhh-  I'm a paying customer, no judging! Ha! Everything looks typical of most Starbucks stores. Clean and comfortable with easy listening music overhead and friendly baristas behind the counter.   There was almost no line either inside or in the drive thru. But the little there was moved quickly. They made my drink correctly and fast - yay!   Was a little disappointed to find this Starbucks apparently does not have Idaho designed products. No "I'm here" mugs or localized artsy gift cards. Darn.   Kudos to the barista at the window for his extra patience when my cell phone decided to randomly reboot just as we were pulling up the Starbucks app to pay. Gotta collect those magic drink stars! Wheee!
Why did corporate think it was ok to attach a Starbucks to a bank??? Small cramped crowded drive thru is always packed! No parking!  Friendly and spot on staff!!!

This Starbucks location is usually crowded and the crowds usually mean you're in for a long wait, unfortunately. The drinks come out a little bit more slowly here, and it's tough to get a table. The layout works well, but the place is small, so there's not a lot of seating.  What I can say, though, is that I am usually waited on with a smile and a nice "how are you today" which makes the waiting suck a little less. Nice staff, long waits adds up to an A-OK in my book.

This is a new drive thru starbucks that opened up in the perfect location.  The coffee is now as I would expect a starbucks cup of Joe to be.  It was not as good when it first opened up but all is trained well now and no more burnt coffee.   Yessssss.   My life is now complete with this new location and with the drive thru!!!   Also ordering thru the app now..... Dangerously good..

Parking is not great in the small cramped parking lot in front of the store, but I suppose you always have the option of using the bigger parking lot around the back and walking a bit. This is the only Starbucks in the area that isn't part of another store and apparently, the only one that carries the fox tail mug. Anyway, I thought service was quite good at the counter and the ambiance was comfortable. It's only the $4 coffee that scares me.

My favorite Starbucks in NE Philly area. Great selection comparing to those inside Target. This Starbucks have indoor and outdoor seating which I found nice on a comfortable sunny day. Be aware that their parking lot is very limited, I believe is less than 15 cars can be parked. That's when the drive thrus come in handy. There is plenty of times when my hubby can't find parking but settle for the drive thrus.            The only complaint I have is drive thrus are supposed to be quick, but not this one. Drinks are usually ready by the time you drive to the window. I should wait no more than 5 minutes for one venti drink. Instead every time I've waited nearly 15 minutes for 1 drink. Other than that, everything else is good.

I visited this Starbucks during the Summer and sat outside; plenty of seating even on a Sunday. The outdoor music brings the typical Starbucks ambience outdoors making it the ultimate dog watching place in town.   The barista remembered my name when I got a second drink & all was great.
A very crowded Starbucks!  The staff was extremely nice, but unfortunately did not have my go to chai tea :(

At 5:45pm, this Starbucks is dirty. The trash bins are overflowing and staff is checked out. One Male bearded barista was counting money as I came to the counter and after a minute never looked up, never acknowledged, so i went to the bathroom. After coming out there was a customer before me. I was next and I was still not acknowledged. I had to present myself to the barista, who only claimed he was waiting for me. It's the worst service I've ever received. I've never seen staff throwing away breakfast stuff already as customers are ordering. I will not be returning to this location.

Everyone there is always so nice to me. They take your order with a happy tone, they smile and ask you about your day. Every time I go there, I leave smiling. The guy I just talked to asked me about my plans for the day and even talked about my future plans in life. I feel really appreciated and happy every time I go to this starbucks.

Great service from Blake who helped me with a defunct starbucks' card that I got for my sister .  He gave us a free lemon bread slice since he couldn't help us with the card.  Outstanding customer relations from this gentleman .

My husband and I are frequent customers of this Starbucks location because we live nearby to it. We typically drink Starbucks anywhere we go due to the fact that they offer the specialty coffee drinks and we run into little to no problems when ordering. Unfortunately at this location, more often than not, we have to send the drink back because it's missing something we asked for. This is alarming because what if someone who was ordering a specialty drink made a certain way due to health issues such as diabetes and their request for sugar free sweetener was ignored? I've never ran into this issue so much at any other location that we have ordered from and hopefully the next time a barista repeats the order back, they actually make it the way they are saying it.

I come in today with high hopes that a second chance could redeem them, and it did. Music was set to a reasonable level and my order was quick and accurate. However, the young girl who appeared to be running the show seemed to be short with her staff and was very condescending and rude to them. I obviously don't have enough context to know how the day had been or any personal issues this girl may have been going through, BUT if you're in a customer service business you know going in that means there will be times where business is VERY busy, and vice versa. Also, if you choose to enter into any kind of management role that means balancing that responsibility with the training and development of your staff. It was unsightly and very unprofessional to see her being condescending to the staff.

On Fat Tuesday you have a staff of 5. Are you serious?  You are on one of the most popular corner Poydras and St. Charles day of Mardi Gras and you don't properly staff your location. Come on Starbucks. They have one person on the register and on guy who looks like he is trying to look busy so he doesn't have to do work. This is ridiculous.   The staff is moving slow as turtles no smiles no hi how are you. Heads down they have one door open and the line is out if the door. The only good thing was I got bathroom tickets. DO BETTER

I find it difficult to rate a chain, but this location is one of the closest to me, although not much a coffee drinker, I do visit more than the others.  I have never had a poor beverage from this location, and recently they started serving limited gluten free treats.  I spotted a wrapped 2pk of chocolate cookies in the case yesterday. The USA menu is coming up here, just slower than molasses.  They upgraded a while back and added power outlets. Handy in a pinch!  Yes, I definitely come back. And you should get a Starbucks card and the app for the perks. So easy to pay from my phone if I forget my wallet - heck even if I have my card or wallet. Gold stars! Earn beverage upgrades, and freebies. Nothing wrong with that.

My office comes in here every single day. Multiple times a day. We happen to come in on a Sunday, when Pricilla who is normally our 10/10 Starbucks barista was off shift. Ordered what we always do, Nitro Cold Brew, light sweet cream. Got our order, and noticed the cold brew had way too much cream. Politely mentioned it to the barista and instead of making a fresh one..... dumped half my coffee out into the sink and added more cold brew. Said "that should be okay". Not your best work Starbucks. I would expect a fresh cup with the right amount of cream, especially with all the business we consistently give.
Asked for a granda latte with 2 inch of room. Drink came with maybe a inch. The girl said here is the drink. Not sure what he did? Refused to correct the issue.

We stopped in for a quick recharge on our way back to Sacramento. We were rudely helped by the cashier but Rachel, the one running around and making the orders, was flustered but still maintained a smile and friendly tone with us. It's customer service like Rachel's that will definitely have me stop back by this location when I'm in the area again.
The barista was the rudest Starbucks employee I have ever encountered. Take your money elsewhere. Costumer service here is truly lacking.
I stopped by while I was waiting for a flight arrival at tpa .My coffee was perfect.  The attendant fulfilled my request as asked . I would visit again

gross. early in the afternoon today, I went in here for a drink. watching the barista make my drink... he stuck his fingers in his mouth then went to finish making my drink. I stopped him and told him I wanted him to wash his hands and for someone else to make my drink over again. I regret that I didn't obtain his name or demand my money back. I'm feeling disgusted now thinking about how many other times he's dug around in his mouth with his fingers and touched equipment, food, and anything else for that matter. truly horrified by this and will not be returning to this location
Have waited 25 minutes for my order on numerous occasions. Store is busy, but less so than other locations. Staff is unfriendly and unapologetic.
They always run out of items! Line is super long! Wish they open up another star bucks around there so the amount of traffic at drive through can be split in two!

I don't usually review chains..but I think something needs to be said. I went here today to pick up some coffee and general treats on my way to see a friend. The staff were so rude. I mean they couldn't seem to understand my order in the fist place and then were pretty slack about making my order. And the drinks still turned out wrong. Was pretty disappointed and probably won't be back to this location, especially considering there are sooo many Starbucks locations in the downtown.
I've tried to give this location a chance but every time I come here the baristas are so rude. I come here because it's closest to my school but I will find another location.

I saw one of the girls at the dental office with a refreshing pink drink.. asked her what it was and she said it was "The pink drink"...  So I am easily influenced.. and since the hubby was in getting his check up, off I went.  We haven't been to this Starbucks, so I was impressed not having to wait on a long line. I love looking at all the Starbucks cups and mugs...  I walked back with 2 pink drinks and also a bottle of their vanilla syrup.. I could never get any stores to sell them to me, but this time I lucked out! I know I can make my own syrup, but this was a nice treat.  The drink was refreshing, but a tad too sweet , I'll get my iced Carmel macchiato next time.

It's tough for me to give any Starbucks higher than a 3 star review (my hometown being the exception), so consider this a positive review overall.  The service is good, though I found it very slow to get my drink. Sometimes the place is packed beyond belief, which stinks, but others it's empty.  The space is good - lots of seating - and they're friendly and pass out samples often.  Worth a visit if you're nearby looking for some Starbucks. But if it were up to me, I'd go to a local shop instead.

I have been going to this particular Starbucks in the morning for almost a year now and they are amazing. I use the mobile order app when I'm about 5 minutes out and 9/10 times it's ready for me to walk in and grab, I have never had to wait longer than the estimated wait time at this locatio. I may be jinxing myself but I have also NEVER had a bad coffee from this location. So to summarize, best coffee, best service and the fastest starbucks I have received since moving to Tennessee. Keep up the great work.

Thks Starbucks location is keeping great safety measurements and protocol. The staff, generally, wear masks along with the customers who are suggested to wearing it. There is hand sanitizer around for use. I mainly order using their mobile app, which I've seen it to be pretty efficient.   During peak times, orders might delayed a bit but I haven't waited for more then say 5-7 times for my order. I think it's fairly quick. Thank you to Starbucks and the staff at this location.

I am no fun when I go to Starbucks. My order usually includes at least five modifications (non-dairy milk, less syrup, extra foam, etc.) so I usually try to order through the app.  But I had to order in person yesterday and, surprisingly, it was a great experience. Amalia, who took my order, was so helpful and developed a real rapport with me in the time it took to order.   I'm going to cut this review short because I've written "order" (as a noun and verb!) so many times it's starting to sound weird, but long story short, I found this Starbucks to be one of the friendliest I've ever gone to.
Great location! They are always open on time (6am) and the staff is super friendly. Never had a mistake on my order. Top notch!
This is a really good starbucks. One thing I like is that it had really quick service. All the drinks I have had have been on point

The Starbucks locations in Tampa Bay are below average. Usually they are mobbed and have below average staff in terms of drink-making skills. This location is no exception. If you want a large Starbucks to hang out in, check out the one on MLK Jr. Blvd. in Tampa. It's never mobbed and staff are nice.

Well, as this is a Starbuck on campus for Drexel's student, doesn't matter how terrible it is, this is our only choice. Nobody is even bother to add this on Yelp.  Try to order something simple, like dark roasted coffee or just tea, so there will be less chance the staff here mess up your drinks.

Terrible Starbucks. Go to the one across the street in the grocery store. The barista didn't put the top on my tea, I picked it up, and burnt myself as the top fell off. I believe procedure is to put the top on, not just lay it on there. After burning myself, they barely acknowledge that they messed up. Never going there again.

Not one of my favorite Starbucks. When I walked in to pick up a mobile order, a worker said "Good Morning", but I had headphones in. So I took them out to respond and say "Good Morning, but they were already talking about how rude I was for not saying "Good Morning" Back. They continued to do so as I stood there waiting for my drink (maybe they didn't realize I took my headphones out).  When I grabbed my drinks and said "Thank you so much" to show them I wasn't intending to be rude, but they wouldn't even look at me or say anything back. Kind of petty in my opinion.   Overall, wouldn't return to this place. Just makes me feel awkward now. Plus there's another one like a couple blocks away, so no reason to.

This place is a hot mess ..... I just moved to the area and this is close to me on my way to work. Well #1 they messed up my mocha sooooo bad #2 time they gave my coffee away #3 time waited 13 minutes for a online order and they gave me a card to make up for it, I come back the NEXT DAY and the service was a joke and the person making the coffee and the one putting them out looked like they had no idea what they were doing so I was given my coffee by a customer. Won't be back to this store again. Gave them more then enough chances
Terrible drive thru 20 minutes waiting. Locked in the line no option to get out.  No apologies for the wait.

Great friendly starbucks. I never have any problems at this location. Drinks and service is consistent. Fun fact, this location does coffee tastings similar to a wine tasting in select nights. You get to try 3 coffees and three pastries that would pair with it well. And it's free!!!

What is GREAT about this Starbucks is its location, as Gerald S noted.   Given the dearth of decent "to go" coffee purveyors in this area, Starbucks is a nice addition to this neighborhood.   As other Yelpers have noted, this is a relatively small store.  It has comfy chairs and is a nice place to meet a friend for coffee and convo. The staff is pleasant and helpful even if they are not always burdened with a sense of urgency.
I didn't get the right order, but the staff was still exceptionally friendly despite being slammed and I appreciate that!

I mean, who doesn't like Starbucks?  I am a fan, but of this location, not so much.  This Starbucks is a very cramped and I often feel claustrophobic when I walk in.  There is hardly any seating anywhere due to the masses of college students who camp out there all day with their computers, books and oversized headphones drowning out the chatter and even the kind fellow patron simply asking "Is this seat taken?"    I do not want to discourage kids from studying, they just need a larger facility to do it in.  The staff here is great and have always made my drinks to my liking.  I'm just a "space case" and I like to have options and room to maneuver in.  Thats all.

The couple times I've visited this Starbucks location, I have received phenomenal service. All the baristas I have encountered here are very friendly. Drinks are made quickly and to standard.  Store is clean, welcoming, and nicely decorated. Lots of seating.   As a former barista myself, I know what is expected, and appreciate when they deliver that.  No complaints!
Worst Starbucks experience. The baristas here need to find a job in something other than customer service.

The employees in this Starbucks are some of the best and sweetest baristas I've encountered in a while. Their products are consistent, and if not they are always there for easy fixes. This store is always clean and the wait is never too long!!!

This is the location in Canal Place. Line moved quickly and I ordered the peppermint hot cocoa. The cashier rang me up then a barista said, "we only have skinny mocha for hot chocolate." I'm not a regular visitor but how do you not have enough syrup to make the hot cocoa especially on a cold day?

Well what else would one expect from a MALL Starbucks except for mall employees - not Starbucks quality employees.   Aside from watching four other people get their drink before my own, they made my first drink correctly; however, they literally threw my straw at me and my second drink was made wrong. Vegan and dairy free mean hmmm I don't know maybe NO MILK. Is it that hard of a concept?   The size of the smoothie that I received after they finally remade the drink was smaller than the first - therefore I over paid for my drink which really pisses me off seeing as I paid $10 for two smoothies.   Never again! I don't recommend anyone getting a drink in the place. Horrible.

Allow me to preface this review with: I prefer local coffee shops, however I do like this place for the occasional meeting location with friends since it's pretty easy to access.   Parking can sometimes be an issue so be prepared. There's plenty of adequate seating and for a chain store it's not too bad :)

The staff here clearly enjoys working here. Always very lively here, which is appreciated. Although the lounge area is "average" when you consider tables to relax & converse at, there is outdoor seating that makes up for it.  As far as seasonal themes to market products, this location offers such as "Frappuchino Friday."The ladies dress up Hawaiian style, and the men wear a Don Ho Hawaii 5-0 shirt. It makes for good fun. The products are still very good, as well as certain new promotional ones such as Oprah's Chai Latte.   Parking in this Starbuck's front lot can be challenging, particularly with the last two parking spaces on the front right (very tight). I would recommend parking in the adjacent shopping center lot and just walking over during the busy times of the day (early morning, late afternoon). Overall, a pretty good venue.

Starbuck's definitely generates more crowd in G-Hall especially in the morning when students are half awake. Friendly baristas. Sometimes they get the orders wrong but everyone makes mistakes.  Most students order drinks to go. Getting rid of that unnecessarily large dark wood rectangular table could really alleviate the traffic. Not many people would sit there anyways

This Starbucks is spacious and filled with comfortable chairs, tables and a large area to work on your laptop, read, or have a cup of coffee.  There are also plenty of tables and chairs outside.   The problem is not with the Starbucks store, but the employees. They are subpar for Starbucks employees.  I have never had such bad customer service at a Starbucks. The Baristawass unfriendly and had zero people skills. Hewas not familiar with the the drinks and did not seem to care.  The other Barista who assisted me, was rude and quick with my order and she gave me cold coffee  .In addition, the area where you put milk and sugar etc in your coffee was not stocked.  The music was very loud and I could not concentrate on what I was working on at the time. I ended up leaving early.  It is a shame, as the store itself is very nice.  I usually receive excellent and friendly, fast service from Starbucks employees, so I was surprised by this service.

This Starbucks location is very convenient!  My favorite hot drink here is the Peppermint white chocolate mocha and cold drink is Green tea frappuccino. I highly recommend signing up for their rewards program as the prices of these drinks a little high. Free drinks on your birthday and other free rewards thru out the year. This location always have a friendly staff. I made a mobile order and had accidentally put the wrong location and they gave me my drink anyways!

How hard is it to pour liquid into a cup to the proper height of the cup. I took the lid off and the coffee instantly starting pouring out. Not little drops, but POURING out obviously overfilled and spilling in my truck, on my clothes. You don't want to leave room for cream and sugar, that's fine, DONT FILL IT TO THE RIM THINKING Nothing can happen. Last I check there's still gravity and pressure on earth so movement and grasping the cup will change the dynamics of the damn contents inside the cup people!

Bad experience with the cashiers and all here.  Very rude, careless, loud, just lack of service and no knowledge of service, and overheard making racist jokes and impressions.  The drinks they have no knowledge of and carelessly makes it.  Hire new staff.

You know how when you go to Dunkin' Donuts and you ask for a little bit of cream or a little bit of sugar, they put WAY too much in there? Yeah, well that's this place. I got drinks there two days in a row, first day I ordered iced black coffee, they put about 5 pumps of vanilla and sugar into it - totally sucked. Then the next day I got cold brew coffee with LIGHT sweet cream. It looked like a cup or cream with a splash of coffee. It's a shame too, because their cold brew is good. Idk why they feel the need to dump a gallon of full fat cream into it. This place also runs out of almost every breakfast item daily. Do yourself a favor and skip this place! BOO!!

This Starbucks is very clean, in a small and nice location, their service is great, the people here always serve with a smile. I came here to get coffee with my girlfriend and they make the coffee perfect every time that I have been here, I'll have to go back and put them to the test again, I know Starbucks isn't for a lot of people because they are a little bit higher than other coffee places, but I think the customer service makes up for a lot of the negativity that is given to Starbucks, give them a try and if their not for you more power to you enjoy!

Employees are always friendly. Lines can tend to be long at times but it's worth the wait. I'm a creature of habit and typically order a caramel macchiato most days. Absolutely love their scones as well!

I'm here a few times a week. I would like to give it a higher star rating, but due to the service issues, I am giving it a two.   The drinks here are always on point.   The lines are long and they rarely are running both registers.   On a regular basis there is something missing at the Customer Prep Bar: missing cream, sugar, stir sticks, stoppers.
Always slow inside ... so frustrating ... don't bother going in even if the line in the drive through is long.

Why do I have to keep leaving updates? I spoke with the district manager. She was super nice and promised to mail me a gift card to compensate for the horror I experienced. That was in january. It's almost march.
Best Starbucks in NOLA I have been a regular there for years now. Everyone is so nice at it.

This location is filthy. I received my drink with coffee stains all over it. Not from my drink but from other drinks that splashed on it. The restroom is also filthy! The only cool thing about this location is all the gift card options they have. Over 50 to choose from!!! What?!?!

I go through this drive thru every morning on my way to the hospital before my 12 hour shifts. I order an ice coffee extra light on the cream (because other wise it just tastes like milk and I can't even taste the coffee). Starbucks is expensive. I could go across the street to Dunkin' and get a $2 coffee that's larger but I don't because I like Starbucks. Anyway, when they hand it to me and it's too light colored I know it's going to taste like milk so I just ask if they could please pour some out and add more coffee to it, that's all. Again it's expensive to not be able to enjoy it. This morning I asked her to do this and she had an attitude, turned around to her coworker and started laughing while mocking me loudly. I could hear her from the drive thru window. She brought it back to me, mocked me to my face while handing it back to me. And I left just thinking you know what... all I wanted was an ice coffee that doesn't taste like milk. I have to go to a hospital now where I have to keep patients alive for 12 hours which can be exceedingly difficult and exhausting. The most peaceful and easy part of my day from 5:45am until getting home exhausted at 8:30pm tonight is this coffee. My intentions are never to be difficult.

This is my "neighborhood" Starbucks as its the closest to my house. As i'm not a huge fan of Starbucks in general, i do seek them out sometimes because of the convenience. This location has a drive thru which can be nice.. avoid this place on saturday morning. SO BUSY!

Starbucks isn't usually at the top of my coffee shop list, but this location is pretty nice. It's easy to find and has a lot of parking, so it makes a good meeting point.  The staff are always very friendly, and it's usually busy, but I've never seen it busy enough that there are no free seats.

Too crowded, loud (some students don't understand volume control yet) and double check your cup contents before you walk out because it can be filled only partially.  Had better service and experience in crowded busy airports or freeway rest areas.

I know, it's late... and everyone has a bad day every once in a while. Today was a roughy day for me, which is probably why the cashier's bad attitude stuck out even more. But given the fact that my latte was decent and showing a little mercy by assuming that the cashier isn't extremely disconnected and dry everyday, I'll spare this Starbucks 3 stars.....

I love reading down my tips here because it's a good log of the different drinks I have had here. I often come here and try something different, and I seem to like most of the drinks. I also come whenever there is a special, or also more often at Christmas time for holiday drinks or gift cards.  They are fast, efficient and friendly. I find the outside area more comfy than the inside. The inside is smaller than many other Starbucks, but it does serve it's purpose if getting refreshed and caffeinated. Wish I had one closer to me, but at least this is conveniently located next to other stores I like to shop at.

Always great. If it's ever not great they will go above and beyond to fix it. I understand that it's a trend to go to local coffee shops but Starbucks is quick efficient consistent and less expensive.
Great customer service! Sometimes I have to wait a lengthy time but they serve with a smile!

Cashier and staff is very rude at this location. They don't greet you nor do they seem happy at all. Normally when I order an iced green tea latte, I'll ask them to add an extra scoop of matcha powder. NORMALLY, there are no issues and they do not charge for it. At this location, the cashier charged me 95 cents additional for one more scoop of matcha. This hasn't happened before so I'm wondering if it's because this Starbucks is  operated by the airport restaurant group. The cashier actually seemed like she was trying to find something to charge for that additional scoop of matcha so she put it as "add syrup" ...  If I ask for one additional pump of vanilla in my vanilla latte I wonder if they'll charge a dollar for that too. I'll avoid this Starbucks next time and try the green bean coffee shop in this terminal instead.  Oh, and my $6 grande iced green tea latte tasted like water. Avoid.
I love Starbucks but do not have a coffee on your menu if you do not have all the ingredients for. I don't think its right to pay full price for something that your not getting the full product.

If I can give this starbucks negative stars I would. I was staying at gsr for a night and Sunday morning my family and I decided to get some breakfast sandwiches and some teas. My daughter and I both love their black tea lemonades so we ordered 2 of those and my brother ordered the peach green tea. Cashier was very nice and helpful. The girl making my drink I believe her name was Maria or Mariah but she had like blueish hair was extremely rude. I always try my drink before leaving and while I was waiting for my other drinks I tried mines and it I already knew it was wrong by looking at the color. It tasted disgusting. Not only that the way she made it was wrong. What I ordered is a shaken black tea lemonade and I had asked her to remake my drink because it was not sweetened at all but she made a remark and said well you need to specify it when ordering well fyi the cashier never even bothered to ask and usually at Starbucks they do. So she then takes back my drink and asks me to hold my lid. I myself work in a same field as hers and I tell her that I didn't want to cross contaminate and asked her to toss it out and give me new one. She takes my drink back and just pumps sweetener in one cup and poured the exact same one over! And not only that she didn't even use the shaker like she was supposed to. I had to ask the other employee there and she said yes she's doing it wrong. Out of my whole order I was missing my daughters tea as well and had to inform her. My brothers peach tea was made the same way. She didn't shake it. She just poured it all in a cup and put ice in so his drink was overly sweet. How was I supposed to shake my tea and make sure the sweetener tea and lemonade gets all mixed up? It's not possible! I don't know how the employees are trained here or if the girl was having a bad morning because it really looked like she was out of it. I don't blame her since it's Reno but for paying almost 40$ for Starbucks I expected it to be good. Worst Starbucks visit ever. My daughter is only 6 years old and she even noticed that her tea was not what it's supposed to taste like. If a 6 year old says something then you guys really have a problem and need to fix it asap since you guys are a well known name. I don't think I will be visiting this Starbucks location when I come back to gsr.

It was about 20 min to closing and I stopped by to grab a coffee  and cookie  as I asked for the cookie the guy on shift told me that they where all out so I asked what else they had. He said all the food was sold for the day and there wasn't anymore left. Unfortunately I looked over and saw plenty of stuff. Horrible service

WORST DRINK I HAVE EVER RECEIVED FROM A STARBUCKS!!! All I ordered was a hot chocolate (simple) it tasted horrible so I asked for another one and AGAIN... HORRIBLE!! Love wasting my money, thanks bad barista!

This locations seems pretty slow.  If you are in a hurry I would suggest you move along.  I have been here a handful of time in December 2015 and each time I am finding myself checking my watch out of boredom.
Can't stand their coffee.  It tastes like it was strained through an old sock.  I'll stick to Tim Hortons.

Quick service. Very clean. I ordered a blonde Cappuccino, and after the foam, I had less then half a drink, that's was really disappointing. Flavor was good, just wish I could have had a full cup for the price of Starbucks drinks

I never met a Starbuck's that I didn't like.  Until this one.  They were shorted staffed the morning that we visiting.  Apparently, an employee had wandered off, leaving the other to fend for herself.  Service was decent considering.  Our beverages were good, but seriously, I was almost no longer in the mood.  That's how long it took to get them.
Great service always and long lines. Great for them. Another location on 54 is badly needed.

They weren't the fastest but weren't the slowest either! . the one on terry parkway is really slow... much slower than this one.  My boyfriend says they weren't that slow and I am impatient in the mornings without caffeine lol. He's probably right.

Pretty fast service (even during a very busy day) and decently clean.  Unfortunately due to Starbucks regulations, when I asked to try one of the teas, they couldn't give me a sample but hey I guess with a chain we can't be too picky. They could have been a little bit more informed on the coffee and tea choices. When my boyfriend asked one of the baristas which of their coffees has the most bold flavor, she answered "sorry I don't know that answer, I don't drink coffee" hmmm what?! How are you a barista with no opinion in coffee because you don't drink it. Ouch that was not a good thing. My boyfriend eventually got a coffee that was not nearly as strong as hoped.
Wish they would stop giving me a Java Chip Frapp every time I ask for a Double Chocolate Chip Frapp! I don't drink coffee but I like their teas and non coffee drink options!

I frequently visit this Starbucks when I am down town, which in the past 8 months has been a lot! This Starbucks is very central as well as it is very close to my boyfriends (and soon to be my) place! I love Starbucks with all of my heart, you could say I'm a bit of a coffee snob! I like that there is parking near by, although I usually just walk over. I've always been pleased with the service as well as the beverages here!

This Starbucks is very rude, always seem to have smart remarks. Aren't very updated on new drinks and almost try to make you feel stupid for saying something wrong. This Starbucks location is a bad review in itself.
When I informed a partner that there was no soap in one of the bathrooms her immediate response was, "well just use the other bathroom".
Fast and efficient. Happy Hour is also a plus. Got 2 venti green tea frappucino, 1 venti vanilla bean frappucino and 1 venti caramel coffee frap. All were delicious.

I've been to this Starbucks countless times and have not been disappointed with my beverage. The drive thru is convenient, but can get mobbed especially at peak times, due to the location. I have only experienced one tremendous wait time to pick up an order place in advance on the app, and that was at the very beginning of COVID restrictions when they were still figuring out the kinks. The staff are friendly enough, though the volume of customers certainly prevents them from trying to make a connection with people like a smaller Starbucks are able to do.

I have been to this Starbucks twice, specifically to get my Starbucks whole coffee beans ground up and the staff is just amazing!  They are always friendly, courteous and happy to help.  Last night, however they really went above and beyond.  They were grinding up a 1 pound bag of Starbucks coffee that I received as a Christmas gift and the bag split down the side.  They alerted me of the issue right away, salvaged what they could of the spilled Christmas blend, and gave me another free pound of coffee which they ground up for me as well.  I realize that it's part of the Starbucks culture, but it is so nice to be treated as an important customer when you are spending your time and money at a business.  This location, just gets it!  Thank you for another great night.  I've been telling everyone how great the staff at the Dale Mabry and Linebaugh drive-thru Starbucks is.  :)

Staff is helpful and perky on weekday mornings. The lines can be very long and at times slow moving. Atmosphere is usually nice, with a cozy space and decent amount of tables. Retail selection is excellent with a great variety of mugs, thermoses, etc. Breakfast sandwiches always stocked and available, even if they're not in the case. Usually better to go early as the line can get long later in the morning. Clean restrooms. K-cups and other take-out coffee readily available. Simply a lovely place to spend an hour getting set for the day.

I work at a local business that is not far from this Starbucks location. I often come to the Starbucks to buy a large number of gift cards for our client base. Typically I will drive through the drive-through tell them that I need upwards of 20 gift cards, pull forward, and pay in a matter of five minutes. I have done this probably 10 times over the past few months and there has never been an issue. I really think that on most days this Starbucks deserves four out of five stars. They are typically very fast and accommodating.   However I just visited this Starbucks hoping to go through my typical routine of ordering gift cards. I was confronted with a flustered employee who told me to just pull forward and she would deal with me at the window. Not a problem.   However when I got there, it was as though she had completely forgotten.  She tried to hand me the coffee for the car behind me. No biggie, I just reminded her that I was the one who wanted the gift cards. She said it was going to take a minute because she had to go get a few packets of gift cards from the other side of the store. Not a big deal, I can wait.   It took her maybe 30 seconds to grab the cards before she proceeded to count them out in front of me to ensure it was the number I had asked for. I hand her my card through the window, and mind you this is a company card so it says the name of the company on it. She looks at the card, looks at the line of cars behind me, looks back at me and says She needs identification. I explained to her that I could give her my ID but that it wouldn't match the name on the card because the name on the card was the business I work for. She rolled her eyes at me and proceeded to run the card.   I signed off on the receipt and was about to pull away when she said, "Next time you come to do this, come inside. It's very inconvenient for me to have to do this through the drive-through." and gestured to the line of cars behind me.  I explained to her that I had done this several times before to which she retorted, "Not through the drive-through." I drove off.   Now, normally this Starbucks is actually a really nice establishment. That being said you should not argue with your customer when you have a growing line of cars in the drive-through. Don't treat your clients like an inconvenience when the only thing making the line grow longer is your stalling. And don't give your customers attitude when they're coming to you for continued business. I mean, we're referring our clients to YOU and you're going to give attitude like that? Whatever.   2 stars for the lady working the drive-through today. Your costumer service needs some work!

Since there was a district convention event going on in the hotel, a lot of people were in line to buy drinks.  The service is really fast and the staff are still consistently friendly.  I got my drink within 5 min despite the long wait in line

My go to Starbucks that is temporarily closed due to renovations where they bought the bank next door and are making a drivethru. They always got my order right and would often put any of the drizzled for the Frapps on the bottom and sides of the cup as well as the top, where many others only do the top.

Nice Starbucks with outside seating. Too bad they don't have a drive thru like the one at Linebaugh and Dale Mabry, but this is in a strip mall and not a free standing building. They currently have a deal until May 10th where from 3-6pm, Frappucinos are half off! There is a new frappuccino called the "S'mores". I sadly was underwhelmed. I LOVE s'more flavored things. This had a burnt marshmallow taste and it was way too sweet and not frozen enough. I'd be willing to give it another try with another barista though. It was busy and the girl seemed overwelmed and "in the weeds".
waited an hour for a medium 8 dollar hot chocolate and they didn't even put whipped cream on it. heartless.

The team that works at this location is nothing short of amazing. You made my day with your great service when I had a problem, no question asked. I couldn't ask for more. Try the pineapple infused back tea, zero calories and it tastes like you're on a tropical island. See you tomorrow guys.
Typical Starbucks quality food OK prices but this particular time service was super slow. We even had to remind them in the drive-through of the cake. Forgive them though

Best Starbucks I have been to in the Country. My wife and I frequented this Starbucks quite often while we were waiting to move into our new home and have internet activated. We sat in the lobby for hours at a time. The lobby is very comfortable, the staff is very friendly, our orders are always correct and done quickly. Can't say enough about this Starbucks. I have frequented several and this is the best one hands down.
This has become my fave Starbucks... the service and quality in the AM is spot on.  No more issues... just rockin' coffee and espresso!

This Starbucks is very efficient -- almost mechanical.  But truthfully, do I need a song and dance as you make my drink?  No.  It's a little brighter than other Starbucks.  Or maybe I've always there when the sun shines in particular manner.  But that some how adds to the clinicaliness of the place.  If that's a real word.  And if not, I don't care.  The drive thru is a plus, though I've never used it.  Just never been in that much of a hurry.
my once-a-month place while waiting for my wife's meeting to end. Always busy, a bit noisy but if I xan sit in the comfy chair. Im happy. Friendly baristas.

I've pretty much given up on this place. No matter what time of day it is, the team doesn't seem to be working together cohesively, and the line inside seems to get neglected while most of the employees scramble to take care of the drive-thru. I hate going through the drive-thru anywhere, but I didn't realize I would start getting penalized for my preferences.

I will NEVER go to this location again. I love my coffee and used to stop here all the time because of how pleasant the service was even though the space was small and not conducive to leisurely chatting or homework. Today was the 3rd time that I've gone in and the baristas were rude and disrespectful. They act like taking your order is such an inconvenience and one of them rolled their eyes and said "unfortunately" at the fact we ordered bacon SV bites. My iced coffee tasted horrible too but they were so flippant I didn't want to waste their time having them fix it. Very disappointed.

This Starbucks forces you to buy something in order to use their bathroom.  That would be okay if they were a small business, but since they are a large corporation I find this policy aggravating.  Even in New York City the staff will give you a code irregardless.  I won't be buying Starbucks ever again--hopefully.  I think the bathroom code was 5832 of you need it.

So I'm not a huge fan of reviewing super big chains like Starbucks, because let's face it: regardless of if you have a negative experience you are probably going to continue going back for your over priced latte. At least I do. That being said, this Starbucks is special and I feel that it needs some love on Yelp. So this is me giving the Cool Springs Starbucks some love!   I am working on my MBA right now, why is this important? Well, I have an endless amount of work that never is finished. This Starbucks is the perfect location for a halfway spot for all the members in my cohort. We are at this place 4+ times a week. This location is PERFECT for group work. Towards the back of the shop there is a huge long table that can fit like 14 people. It is an ideal spot for any study group or group of people wanting to work on something.  The staff is extremely friendly. I mean so great. I have had such cool conversations with so many of them, and they remember us. It's like a small town coffee shop feel as a huge chain. I have heard that this is the busiest location, but the line backs up outside primarily. Not inside.  Overall, if you live in the area and need a place to work, I recommend this location 110%.
Stopped here when we passed by to grab some coffees. Went thru the drive thru. Service was quick and our drinks were made correctly.
Very bad planning. Both the Canal Pl.ace Starbucks and the Canal Street @ St. Charles Avenue Starbucks are out of the eggnog latte! Boo. Hiss! :-(

I was greeted with a warm welcome. It has been at least 10 years since I last visited a Starbucks. Excellent customer service, friendly, and super helpful with helping me to signup for the rewards and selecting a beverage. I see why people come here for these delightful designer beverages hot and cold. The strawberry Açaí with lemonade venti is so delicious. It was sweet but not too sweet. There were slices of strawberry that were suspended between ice cubes. So cute! I will be back.

I'm a regular at this location and the staff is always friendly and welcoming. My drinks are always made the way I like them and I'm never waiting long - even if there's a few cars ahead of me in the drive-thru. When I come in with my daughter (who's 4) they acknowledge her and treat her with the same respect they treat me, and as a parent that means the world to me.

Today I got my usual drink, it's the venti Caffe vanilla Frappuccino. Man oh man I have never had my coffee taste so good. I like my drinks sweet but I also like to taste of coffee and I could taste both this time. Service was wasn't really as friendly as other experiences but it was definitely efficient I didn't have to wait very long. I've been to this location a couple of times before and never had any complaints. I don't really live in south Reno so its not a Starbucks that I would frequent but if I'm in the area I will return.

I frequent Starbucks often. I have Crohns Disease and their Chocolate Banana protein Smoothie makes a great breakfast replacement meal without upsetting my stomach, however this Starbucks location can never get it right. The few times Ive asked them to make it, it always comes out very bland... Equivalent to the taste of milk, bananas and ice. It's very disappointing and seems to be the only location that serves it this way.  This morning my husband wanted iced coffee and this particular Starbucks happened to be closest to where we were. He ordered a Mocha Frappuccino with a shot of espresso. He too was very disappointed with the flavor. He said it tasted  as if they were skimping on using all the ingredients, much like my smoothie. If I'm going to pay the same price regardless, I'd much rather go to a different Starbucks location with better quality.

This Starbucks has one of the better airport layouts that I have seen with both an entrance and an exit area for customers.  Everyone was very helpful and the staff did not mind taking the time to provide me a pour-over decaf.

UPDATE: Today they were out of the sleeves for the cups.  I order my drink extra hot and definitely needed a sleeve but of course they were out... I also had to ask TWICE for a stopper for my drink since they refuse to set them out in the lobby like other Starbucks.  They have them in a cup at the drive thru window only. UPDATE: Nothing has changed... It's a disaster... Mobile Order placed 30mins ago... Been here waiting 20mins.... Finally got my drink and had to basically beg for a stopper... Yesterday they didn't have stoppers at all... -------------/ UPDATE: Still a tragedy... Waiting for my mobile order for over 20minutes  UPDATE: Still terrible and my drink ALWAYS has too much cream smh...  The manager that is there in the mornings (light skin African American) is to blame for how terrible things are in the mornings... She looks miserable and pissed at all times and treats the staff miserably... Those girls are never smiling and it's cus she talks to them like they are 12... No constructive criticism or actual managing... Just barking out orders with soooo much attitude... I've literally traveled the world and have never been to a more depressing Starbucks in my life...  Once you bring in a MANAGER that isn't miserable and retrain the staff they'll have a shot... I sure hope it's soon smh ----------------/ I have made 5 trips here (all mobile orders) and 4 of my orders were wrong and the 1 that was right was only 2/3 full... I'm assuming the baristas don't know how to make the drinks smh It su I was so excited we were getting a Starbucks in Algiers but this is a huge disappointment...

In the age of franchise coffee, the atmosphere of this location make it worth a revisit. This crew is just amazing- super friendly service, hilarious banter behind the counter- and makes you feel like you're part of the community somehow. I don't care about any other Starbucks, but this one is super special.

I've been to a ton of Starbucks in Philadelphia, in New Jersey, in Delaware. But this 18th and Chestnut Starbucks was amazingly impressive. The Baristas were friendly, but not overly fake.  They were prompt with my order, and asked me out of courtesy if I wanted it a bit hotter since it was cold out.  I loved the upstairs seating which has an awesome view of Chestnut Street.   You may think that all Starbucks are the same, but this is totally not the case whatsoever.  Also worth mentioning is that the bathroom was very clean.

Was there yesterday around noon, several people inside and outside seated, mostly on their laptops. Was immediately catered to by a kind attendant. Four plush padded seats facing each other, separated by a table. Several outside seats arranged in twins around small tables, some with umbrellas. Pleasant music playing as always, in a pleasant place to relax and unwind from stress.

I gave this place two stars because I still drank most of it, but this is a green tea latte... or at least that was what it was supposed to be. The green tea powder was not dissolved into the drink, so it just tasted like milk with chunks of powder. I always go for the green tea latte at Starbucks, but this franchise seems like they don't know how to make it.  Also, they don't accept Starbucks gift cards, but that doesn't bother me. Service was whatever. They weren't mean or anything.

I ban you. This Starbucks will never get my business. There is a reason for handicap spots and what they are not for is your hoity toity customers that think THEY and only they are too good to walk that extra ten feet to buy your overpriced coffee.

The employees at this location are a disgrace to the Starbucks brand. I've been to countless Starbucks locations and the employees are ANGELS. Nope not at this one. I walked up to place my order and the cashier said, "Yes??" Uhhhh okay sorry am I bothering you, miss? Made me feel so uncomfortable to order. The moment I started to order she walked away to do something else and then came back with a miserable smirk on her face. In addition to all of this wonderful stuff, the prices are significantly higher than other locations? Not sure if the cashier charged me wrong but this was an awful experience. Won't be coming back to this specific location.
Parking and driver thru here sucks especially on a rainy day. If you're looking for a place to plug in and web surf probably not the place.
Your typical Starbucks.   It's always busy. I've never had a problem with service and the interior is really clean.

I went in there about 2 weeks ago for sandwich and a drink I use to always get.  I went through the DT which I always do because I have no time for lines inside. Lady on speaker super nice and upbeat. Never said anything about being out of a ingredient that for me makes the drink and maybe silly to others. But if I am going to spend 8+ bucks its not petty for me. I waited 6 or 7 minutes just to make it to the window which was more than fine I know what I am getting myself into when I go here. Got to the window and she took my payment than told me my drink could not be made with strawberries and said but that is okay does not matter. I looked at her and said to me it does because it makes my drink and I use the strawberries in water after I finish my refresher. She said well we do not have it so you can just drink it without. I asked for a refund and she did so. But she was not willing to come up with another option. She was just rude and snappy because she assumed someone paying almost an hours of work for coffee would be fine with missing what is her favorite part of the drink is. Not sure what her name is and honestly does not matter she wants to be sour she has to live with her self.
