Just make sure you ask if they have what is needed for the refreshers if you are like me and its the best part of your drink!
Closed during normal business hours. Sign said "drive Thur only" with the hours posted and they had the drive thru closed/blocked off during those hours of operation.

Starbucks needs to help this location big time. Wait times are ridiculous any time of the day! The drive thru has to share a freakin card reader and pin is required which slows down the line even more! 20 minutes is way too long to serve 6 cars.
This is my favorite starbucks. The staff is always friendly with me. They're knowledgable and the drinks are consistent. I work nearby and frequent this location. I've never had a bad experience.

Slowest Starbucks drive thru in history. I could have driven back home, brewed a pot of coffee, and packed a travel mug in the time it takes to get through their drive thru. Parking your car and heading inside is not any better either. It's unfortunate, but it's the only Starbucks with a drive thru in the Riverview area.
I love this Starbucks. Everyone is so friendly. They are fast. Easy to get in and out of too. Inside is super cozy.

This is my favorite Starbucks in the area. I've got a lot of choices and this one isn't the closest, but I really love this one and will go out of my way to choose this Starbucks over the others. The people are very nice and make delicious beverages (everyone makes them a little different, but this is the most consistent and delicious!). It doesn't ever seem too crowded and the service is very quick!
Nice location. Friendly and knowledgeable staff. I ordered a Venti Mocha Frapp with two shots!

Standard Starbuck - mid-size location (Not huge and not tiny). Decent service - youngish staff of disinterested baristas just "doing their job".  Efficient but not warm nor friendly.  Drinks were fine - I got my kid a decaf frappucino so she was in 11 year old heaven. Ice tea was fine.  No big whoop. Glad it's here to get our four-bucks fix.
Beautiful, spacious, and convenient. Doesn't get much better than this. Every barista I've encountered as been courteous and fast. Great wifi too ever since they switched from at&t to google wifi.

Love this Starbucks; I go at least a couple of times per week. My drinks always come out on point and the staff is very friendly! It's a little bit small, so finding a table is difficult at certain hours, but that is not the end of the world. The sandwiches and bakery items that I get are always tasty.
This my favorite Starbucks on campus! The only Starbucks that closes at midnight which is great for coffee addicts like me.
Computer issues, slowed drive thru to a stop.  This was at the Sheldon store...

This SBUX can get long lines during the busy shopping times of the year but they consistently deliver a good cup of coffee.  Seating is cramped due to the location, but overall this Starbucks is a shining star in what is a fading location.  (Note to Meadowood management - dump the Kiosks - they cheapen the whole experience)

Not only is this location at a weird intersection the drive through also sucks tremendously. If you don't mind waiting 15 minutes or more for your order then you found the spot. So you must be thinking I was there at the wrong time of day, like during morning rush? Uh No! This is their MO, morning noon and night.

This Starbucks is in the Safeway shopping center south of the Golden Dragon and Einstein's bagels.  There is outside and inside seating.  I went to get the pumpkin spice skinny frappuccino which I have always liked and was glad it is back.  The baristas were friendly and very helpful.  It took a little bit to get the drink but a big order ahead of me.  I enjoyed the frappuccino.  It was extra spicy and I like it that way.  I appreciate that I can get a "skinny" version and it is just as tasty.

One of the most dirty bathrooms anywhere. I will spare the picture of the toilet  as was way to dirty to use and the covers were of course empty   Service was blah and the back area were the drinks were being served were also not very tidy.
Best Starbucks on 54! Thank you so much to all the baristas! You guys are so fast and friendly! Christine and Alex make my day every morning in the drive thru! You girls rock!!! My daily fav!

Really happy to have discovered this Starbucks so close to me after I moved away from my old fave - its quick, efficient, and large with lots of seating! The baristas know what they're doing and don't hold you up, so I will absolutely be making this my new spot

Now that UPenn is back in session the lines are to the door.  I am impressed with how quickly the baristas are able to complete orders.  I have always been greeted with a smile & my order has always been made correctly.  The second floor makes finding seating convenient; however, some people will hog the big tables with homework.

I absolutely LOVE this location!! I stop here everyday before work. The morning staff during the week are always upbeat and super friendly. They write little smilies on my cup which makes me smile! They are always so quick, even when there's a mad rush. The weekend crew is a little bit slower and less friendly, but they still get the job done. I switched my Starbucks location to these guys about three months ago and I don't miss my old location one bit!

This Starbucks is always busy. Same people out front with their pets ... A lot of people on laptops ... Ultra thin Starbucks very little standing room.  Not much of a place to go for the ambiance, the employees and location makes up for the lack of interior flare.    I do like when I complained about some food they gave me another sandwich on the house.  Amazing customer service.

Have been several times as my office is nearby.  Service is generally friendly but slow.  I would love to get coffee before my early trains to New York but they are almost never open on time.  Great location, they just need execution help in a big way.

The drug addicts need to be kicked out! They are disgusting. I'm glad they have high top tables in front which seems to help eliminate the people who only come to sleep. Starbucks and drug addicts are a huge problem!

Absolutely the worst Starbucks ever! No matter what they cannot get an order right. Every time I order a sandwich. They either don't have it and don't tell me until I've waited for 10 min or they give me egg bites instead.

Hi! I don't normally write reviews but I just had to give a shoutout/credit to these two wonderful and sweet Baristas, Aron and Carmen! Today I came in and asked if they knew how to make a drink I had once heard about.. a Freddocino lol . It was a complicated drink that required watching a Youtube video to learn how to make. After learning this I disregarded it but It wasn't busy so they insisted that they'd try and make it for me! It's the little things and this is why I love Starbucks. Most friendly staff and incredible quality coffee. :)

I briefly eat/drink here every now and then at this Starbucks location if I happen to be in the South Tampa bay to bay area. This location is like your average size Starbucks with decent amount of seating indoors and outdoors. Always cozy and comfortable for your coffee/tea drinking pleasure. The tea/coffee/gourmet food selections are no different from any other Starbucks. There is plenty of window for daylighting. I like the way how this location is not too busy as it's location is surrounded by a residential neighborhood in South Tampa.
This store has a great staff and a good location near I-580.  It is in the same shopping area as Home Depot.  The staff works hard for quality in their product and it shows.

I normally go to Starbucks every morning before work, there is always a nice gentleman and an older lady with beautiful snowy hair there to make my beverage.. So normally I make an online order in advance simply because I'm normally in a rush.. This one day I decided I'd go in earlier simply because I was already out.. Well the two people who normally served me weren't there so there was this other heavier set girl with brunette hair there. She questioned me when I was just waiting 2 minutes earlier.. I told her that I had made an online order but there was No rush and she goes "well it's not even that time yet so why are you even here" with a nasty attitude which I didn't appreciate. I told her I go there every morning with the same order, she shouldn't have had an attitude for me Being two minutes early especially if I didn't rush anyone.

Power tip:  HUGE line out the door at the C terminal location at 5am. Toughed it out because I knew we had time. Took 30 minutes to get to the counter. Another 3 minutes to get the drinks. Another agonizing 3 minutes to get the food.  The turkey bacon sandwich was ok. The chocolate cookie crumble Frappuccino (without the crumble) was delicious but probably wasn't too healthy.  :)  After dropping off the food to the family, still saw the line out the door about 20 minutes later but definitely better than I was there.

Always great service and always busy! If you can, order ahead on the app so you won't have to wait 15-20min+! My only complaint with this location is that they close early on some days compared to others around.

If certain people are working, this is a pleasant and quick experience. When other, certain people are working, the line gets lengthy and you will repeat your order more than once. Not the end of the world, but you may want to give yourself some room on time at this locale.

Extremely rude. Ordered on the app and when we got there tasked us "what took you so long, everything is bad" still proceeded to give it to us with an attitude. All our food was cold and ice was melted. Will never go back.

This is my favorite starbucks around. I work nearby and I go almost everyday. All of the staff is extremely friendly and my order is always perfect. The strawberry Açaí refresher is my go to and it's so delicious! Stacy is awesome, and great to talk to. I'll be back soon (probably tomorrow).

This is now one of two Starbucks in the shopping district (another inside Fry's). Being my closest Starbucks, I have come here numerous times for a hot cup of Joe. There's some seating outside and it's small inside, but it's a Starbucks, so you know what you are getting. Never had a complaint or any issues, just hot coffee and tea!

It is 7pm on a Saturday night.  If you want a snack no pastry for u.   They are open two more hours and food case is completely empty and set for tomorrow.   Just see two ladies working.  Pleasant service but nothing above and beyond.    They proudly post their 100 on TN food visit. But store is not clean today. Pastry glass filthy and the front door windows look like cleaned with a dirty cloth.   Oh well the one up the street across the highway was a better experience

I'm new to the area and I've been craving my starbucks! Little did I know it's literally 2 mini from me!!!  I stopped by and got my usual caramel macchiato with an extra shot and it's like heaven in my mouth!!!!! Super fast service and awesome employees!!! I definitely will be back!

This coffee shop and the staff is by far the best around! The staff is awesome and super friendly. Thank you Annie, Ronnie, Brett, Abbie and Krissy, Aron and Noah.  I know dealing with rude people can be challenging but, you guys do a superb job!!! Thank you for your hard work and thanks for keeping me happy and keeping it real! Thanks... I appreciate you!!!

This is probably my favorite Starbucks in the Tucson area. The staff is awesome, the service is fast and the seating is the best. Lots of space for studying and relaxing (I never feel cramped unlike other Starbucks in the area). I would totally recommend this Starbucks!

Always enjoy a treat from Starbucks although the prices are getting out of hand- I actually had a reward on my card and it was only good for 3 days- so people watch your free rewards because I honestly think they put them out for so little time they hope you don't see it and it would be lost!

Great staff and ultra lively atmosphere.  Always crowded with nice people on the weekends.  Plus, it's in the perfect spot so I  always have a reason to stop in for my favorite beverage.   My favorite coffee place in Tucson by far.

Wife and I go to Starbuck a couple time a week, more when we travel. Probably been to over a hundred locations throughout the world. The location is great in the middle of a major shopping district, though we do miss the one in the mall they closed when this one opened. Now that Cinnabon is open again in the mall, they really need to bring that one back. A Cinnabon and a Starbucks coffee..... yummmm.  As note by other reviewers, the customer service training is lacking here. This location is the only one where I feel a little ripped off sometimes. About 30% of the time we stop in they seem to short the drinks; order a Grande I expect a full cup. I'd order a tall if I wanted less. Most of the time, I complain and they top it off, but you have to wonder if the drive thur customers deal with the same thing and how many just never come back.

I travel for work and spend a lot of time and money at Starbucks all over the Philadelphia area.  But I will never go back to this location, the employees here especially one who was a male in his 20's are extremely rude and were too busy to talk to me or even get my order right.  When I questioned him about my drink he got very arrogant and told me that's the way it is.  Even more surprising in the shadows of what happened in Philly a few weeks ago.  I've been going to Starbucks for over 20 years and will continue to, but recommend you stay clear of this one in Collegeville

This Starbucks is in my neighborhood.  A go-to place, in case of emergency, when I needed coffee pronto.  I came here somewhat early in the morning, on our way to a day trip to Sacramento, and there was a line.  But, I'm happy to say, the line moved fast, and my coffee was prepared quickly.  Now I can stay awake during the 2-hr drive.

I frequent this Starbucks for business and, out of the 3 in Doylestown, this one is the most 'work-friendly.' It's not too crowded and there's plenty of seats and outlets. Word of advise though: avoid like the plague on weekend nights due to teenie boppers getting dropped off for movie night.
Great Starbucks! Super fast baristas and my drink is always right! The only drawback is no drivethru and sometimes parking is a little crowded.
I went on Friday. Was stuck in line for over 30 minutes with no care in the world. Service is bad, manager is bad. Please get yourself together.

The staff at this location are super friendly and I've never had a bad experience with my drinks or mobile order pickups inside! Although ever since this location opened up a few years ago they have probably one of the slowest drive thru lines I've ever seen despite what time of the day I go. I just wish they could have some more emphasis on this!

Starbucks is pretty consistent with menu, recipes,  and service and this store does not disappoint.  The parking lot is small and cramped, almost to a point of unsafe as the drive thru customers zoom right thru to get to the lane too. The drive thru gets busy at all times of the day without much reason other than it's proximity to the freeway and feeds into several neighborhoods.  Inside is clean and friendly.

The better of the two uptown starbucks locations.  This long, narrow starbucks has more seating than the maple street location.  In all other aspects, it is a completely typical starbucks.  How can there be any difference?  All the drinks are made by machines anyway.

Still running slow at drive thru...people are friendly and seem to be trying to give good service, but it just takes forever to get thru the drive up. I also wish there were some big improvement in the breakfast sandwich offerings...it sounds great to offer a bacon, cheese, egg sandwich, but, when the roll is funky, semi stale tasting, the egg is a pre cooked, maybe frozen disc...yuk!  Bacon was ok on taste, terribly undercooked and not crisp at all.  Combination just totally bad and at the price, really, really bad.

This is the slowest Starbucks known to man. You can fly to Guatemala, pick the beans, roast them, grind them, and brew your own coffee faster than sitting in the drive thru. Seriously, 9 times out of 10, it's better to park and walk in. You'll be finished faster than the last car that was in line when you got there. All that said, I've not yet had an order messed up, which is a pro.

Coffees are great and perfectly made but low stars are for service.   If I place an order on line and it says ready 3-7 mins and 18 mins later it's not and walk in people are getting drinks before me than there is a breakdown in who should be serviced first   This has happened everyday this week between hours of 6:50am -7:10 am.

Please make the "no smoking signs" bigger, and more visible during the night. Right now the signs are too small and not visible at all. At the streets, in the parking, please make those arrows more visible to indicate right traffic way.

One thing Starbucks does well is brand recognition. This was my first time to this location and it looks like all the others so I felt like I had been there a million times before. This can be seen as a positive and a negative - it is great because you are familiar with the layout and the menus etc but not so great because there is no individuality, uniqueness, or more personal feel to the place.  The drinks and treats are exactly what is expected from a Starbucks location (any Starbucks location). What separates the good from the bad is location and service. Unfortunately this particular Starbucks fails on both accounts.  I went at 4:30pm on a Saturday afternoon and the place was not busy however finding a parking spot and negotiating the parking lot was a feat all on its own. Unfortunately there is a T&T Supermarket directly across the parking lot and all of these customers were consuming the parking and walking haphazardly in the paved lot. This made it a unique challenge even to get in the door.  Once I had made it in I was able to walk right up to the counter and attempt to order. The baristas were all busy cleaning and so it took a while to get their attention but eventually I placed my order. The friend I was meeting had arrived before me so instead of waiting at the counter for my drink I sat with her and chatted for a while. Next thing I realize that ten minutes had gone by and my drink was still not prepared so back up to the counter I went. Again all of the baristas were cleaning machines and organizing items behind the counter - they all had forgotten about my drink. Luckily I was not in a rush because from the time I walked in until I received my beverage was over 15 minutes.   There are two other Starbucks' at the intersection of 97th Street and 137th Ave and (without being to either) I would recommend trying both of them over this location.

Worst customer service EVER in a Starbucks. Dark haired female employee working today around 4pm should not be dealing with customers, she was rude and disrespectful over us picking up the wrong drink. It's a freaking drink lady...

This is a rather large Starbucks compared to some in the area but only had two people working in the morning so the line was awful! I don't normally come to this location since there's no drive through and had I known how busy it got I would have ordered online ahead of time which I def recommend! My drink was good tho :)

The longest lines and slowest service.  There have been times where it took me a full 30 minutes from the time I got in line to the time I leave with a drink.  I've found it to be faster to walk to the Starbucks on 34th street than it is to go here.  Also, this location doesn't accept Starbucks Rewards

This is a pretty nice Starbucks. The staff are friendly, the drinks are made well, and there is a seating area upstairs that's away from the noise of the espresso machine and endless small talk, so it's a great place to study.

Rude slow service. Had sat at the window at 530 am for what seemed like forever while the staff with the window open didn't even pass my drink cup to the person making the coffee until I drove up. Just slow. Really slow.

Came to this location for the first time today. Manger was awesome and super helpful getting my app and gift card balance to transfer. The staff were also very friendly and the coffee was perfectly made. The inside was awesome as well with a cozy fireplace to warm up next to. I will definitely make this a weekly spot.

Must be nice having this right on the UNR campus! I was only a visitor but still needed my coffee fix. Naturally there was a pretty long line but they went fast. I only grabbed a plain coffee and while Starbucks isn't my fav I still knew what to expect, and as long as it's hot & fresh I can't complain.  I didn't stay inside Starbucks to enjoy my coffee but it looks like a nice place to do so. Spacious and clean and overall felt like a friendly place.

This is the most inefficient and just plain awful Starbucks I have ever been to. They've never gotten my order correct and all it is only an iced vanilla coffee with heavy cream. I went there once and I had to go back in the store THREE different times and I still left with a drink that didn't taste how it normally does from all other locations. I only go here when it is convenient as far as location. Don't even consider the drive thru...unless you're prepared to wait thirty minutes for your incorrect order.
A good place to hop to for a coffee fix when I'm at work. I also love their couch, perfect to do a little or a lot of work on the laptop.

Stopped in because Starbucks is the only place in this area that has hot teas. My girlfriend has a cold and didn't know what to order. The Barista, Dustin, explained a bunch of different hot teas and their benefits, then made my girlfriend an amazing beverage. She was extremely happy and Dustin saved the day! This Starbucks definitely earned repeat customers based off this visit and Dustin's excellent level of customer service.

Their Starbucks is the friendliest! The manager is so personable! I had to take a phone call and go outside. He brought out my drinks. He went above and beyond! And, he remembered my name with my mask on!!
I hate how much I love you Starbucks.  This Starbucks has excellent customer service.  I did not want to leave my dogs unattended so they brought my drink to the door when I placed a mobile order.
Typical Starbucks, and the people who work here are nice. But it is consisistenty the slowest Starbucks I've ever been to. In the country.

It's a Starbucks.  There isn't a whole lot of difference between them.    The coffees, especially the bold ones, are always good, hot, and somewhat overpriced.  The baristas are always very friendly but unhurried, and they no longer try to correct me when I ask for a large coffee.

This isnt my usual Starbucks, but stopped here because of convenience. I ordered an Iced Caramel Macchiato with Soy milk, since i am lactose intolerant. When i received my drink I automatically new something was off due to the color of my drink. It looked very light, like almost just pure milk. And it tasted pretty much only milk, so i think they didn't add the right amount of expresso shots. However i needed to be on mu way so I didn't ask about it. Also later i started feeling off... kind of how i feel when i drink cows milk. Sure enough when i looked on the cup, it was not made with soy milk like i asked. So now im at work, sick, and tired. I know mistakes happen, but pretty much everything about the drink was incorrect.

I can always count on Starbucks when the little shops let me down!   I changed my mind several times, even gave back a drink, and the staff worked with a smile the entire time!  Not only were they nice, but they were FAST!  The last thing I want is a SLOW coffee shop.

I am guilty of coming here a lot.. only because its literally 1.5 blocks away from my house. This is my favorite starbucks location because you can do a lot of people watching, and or enjoy the Geronimo Plaza with your dog. Also I just love old historic buildings/houses, especially ones in the West University neighborhood, so this starbucks is just a no brainer for me.

I have recently moved and this is my new Starbucks location. I have gone repeatedly and it has been the slowest and worst service I've ever gotten at Starbucks. First, no one ever acknowledges you when you come in and go to the counter for mobile order. You have to say "excuse me, I'm here to pick up my mobile order." Today, I mobile ordered at 8:13am... but 8:30am it still was not ready. At other locations, my order can be ready in as quick as 4-5 minutes. There was one girl doing all of the drinks for in store orders and mobile orders. Her tickets for mobile orders were excessively long and when a guy asked to see where his drink was in line, I asked as well, she told him and did not acknowledge me. I left because I would be late for work. There was at one point, 12 people there waiting for mobile orders. My total was  $8.24 and I did not receive my order this morning. Quality of drinks and food are usually fine but it just takes WAY too long and they aren't very friendly or accommodating and there's definitely not enough workers for the demand of this location.
Just like any other starbucks and the service is nice :)

Somehow it always seems as though no one who works at this Starbucks knows what day it is or what is happening. I always find that there are either not enough people taking orders or not enough people making drinks even though there are somehow like 6 people behind the counter. This typically causes super slow service which sucks considering this location is small and in a high traffic area.

This drive thru is a nuisance to the entire shopping center. Sometimes they close the lobby for unknown reasons and the line will wind down two aisles and block people who are parked. One time the lobby was closed but they were still accepting mobile orders and made people wait in the drive thru for their order. Drinks are inconsistent and the availability of the items on the mobile app are incorrect so I can't order ahead.

Not a very good Starbucks.  The service is okay but they are alway out of the Dark Roast.  I went there one day and they were had just started brewing.  They started pouring the coffee while it was still brewing.  I'm not a barista but I don't think you should do that.  The next person in line will get a subpar brew.  If you have time, go a few miles up Eagle Road and you'll find a much better store.

Wanting Starbucks in this area is an inconvenience. Needing some coffee before or after your long day at work should be an easy task but here it's not. It's stressful. You have to decided if you have the time or energy to get starbucks. Because it takes 15 minutes in the drive thru to get some coffee. And it's no better in the store. Now, this is not the fault of the employees. They are doing great! It's the company's fault for neglecting the needs of the community. And we need another drive thru starbucks. I personally have written emails to all the right people and they simply thank me for my email. I'm originally from California so I am used to a Starbucks on every corner. But here in this area the closest Starbucks from this one is the one at the mall. (Which is also packed) It would benefit Stabucks to put in more Starbucks in our area. This is simple supply and demand people. I'm about to venture out and try other coffee places because I don't have the time and patience for this one anymore.

The people here work hard, I praise the hard work that goes into this location. but the organization of this place is lacking. I was standing in the restaurant for 10 minutes while it was packed with people he ignored me and asked everyone else their name. And finally when everyone cleared and he asked my name it has been sitting there the whole time.   I get that it was busy, but I feel like the people who work should be slightly more concerned about safety and when there's more then 10 people in the store the first priority should be making sure the people who's drinks are prepared are called to them so they don't have to risk their safety any longer.   Otherwise, love this location.
If I could give this place 10, I would. I walk past 2 Starbucks just to go to this one. Great staff, great service, fast, reliable. Wish all Starbucks (especially NY) were like this one.

Best Starbucks I live in fishers and will always stop at this one on my way to work. Fast service, drinks are always right and everyone is always so friendly!!!! Probably my favorite Starbucks on the north side!!!

I'm just going to have to join everyone here. This place doesn't hold up to the rest. I like my drinks a certain way but every time I order here they always like to make their "suggestions" for my drink. And when I say no, I end up getting a drink with their suggestions. Does anyone listen here?
20 minutes later we received half of our order. I had to ask for the rest because they were missed. The store wasn't even busy. Ridiculous.

I ordered a Bold Coffee, then I noticed they used whatever coffee is available. Didn't offer to brew fresh coffee or even make me aware that they were currently out of the bold, just take it upon yourself Starbucks to serve me whatever you like.  Starbucks, you've lost your way as a service based company and seem to have an air of entitlement as your new company culture.  Not exactly what I'm looking for when I need a cup of coffee and a few minutes to collect my thoughts.   If you can't deliver, I'm sure the free market will take up your slack.  Very Disappointing!

The staff here is great! They are in a busy location so it's always crazy busy every time I come, I'm talking a line outside the door. There is no drive thru at this location and parking is a nightmare. That's the only downfall. It's downtown so you have to find street parking and walk here.

Typical Wednesday morning, windy, cloudy, and trying to get over that hump. Figured I would stop by Starbucks to grab me a "pick me up." Got a grande white chocolate mocha, and a sausage biscuit. I must say the barista (insert name here) because I didn't catch it, was super friendly and made me feel like he actually cared how my morning was going. He served me with the best white mocha I had ever had - thanks friend...and my biscuit was piping hot. I always go to this Starbucks because they have the most amazing service in town! This mini breakfast just jumpstarted my morning.

An essential pit stop after a night of drinking. The staff was incredibly kind and patient with my rather lengthy order.   Their cafe was quaint and very clean, I was even able to grab a few reusable cups for back home.

Potentially the the worst Starbucks in the entire chain. If you plan on mobile ordering, do it approximately 30 to 45 minutes before you need to pick up the order. They are chronically slow, even when they're not that busy. You're better off going to nearby Mamas Kitchen or Cook's deli to satisfy your morning cravings.
Standard Starbucks experience! Plenty of seating area both indoors and outdoors. Service was quick and ample parking available.
We order two cups of coffee every day.  Nothing fancy just coffee, milk and sugar. Every single time- our order is wrong.
Friendly and great service. Love that I can get my mobile order at the outdoor window if I have my dog.

On my way out of Target and wanted to try a new fall flavor brew. Selected a salted caramel mocha,hot. It was quiet so I was waited on promptly by two very personable and friendly barristas. The drink was great, mocha with a hint of salt and caramel. I think non chocolate lovers would even enjoy this.

Every time I go to this Starbucks it is absolutely packed! I've worked in the service industry before and it is not always easy to keep a positive attitude when you are busy and working under pressure. The staff here is amazing. The drive-thru is so long, so I often park and go in. Everyone I encounter is so happy and smiling and I really have never had to wait an obnoxious amount of time for my drink and breakfast sandwich, they are pretty quick considering the volume of people they are serving.
Been here twice, and both times, the wait was extremely long.  The employees are very friendly, but there is definitely not enough of them at this location.
Incredibly friendly staff on this brilliant Easter morning. Numerous outdoor tables that don't feel directly adjacent to road. Rambunctous guys playing backgammon. All around splendid experience.

The only reason it's getting 3 stars is due to the staff. Who are very sweet.  However they are slower than a sloth on a hot day! Place is large but always looks grimy.  I have never seen such slow service before.
Friendly staff. Great coffee. Recently renovated which gave this location a bit more seating. Still find  it hard to find a seat when it's busy.

This is where we always go on early Sunday morning work caffeine runs. There's usually not much of a line when I'm there, but I've seen it pretty bad on occasion while walking by. I'll be back many many times for sure!

I used to feel Starbucks kept up with the times. Everyone's feeling the pinch of the economy right now, so why raise prices. However, with the new year came new prices. I'm not happy that a house coffee is a 1.99 for a venti drip.  I was content with a  1.75, but come on that's still pricey compared to McDonald's for a hot cup of joe. I used to give that extra quarter as a tip, but now I'm keeping my penny. The personality of the barista's only go so far.

I visited this Starbucks three times in two days and it was good. They have quick service and most of the people working there are nice. However, I asked for water with no ice and they gave me ice twice. When I brought it up, the barista was a little snotty with me.  I tweeted Starbucks about the ice issue and they replied. Perhaps they'll change their policy because giving customers ice without asking is weird. The body assimilates water best at room temperature and they could at least ask first.
Love the outdoor seating and very large interior compared to some other Starbucks.

Recently waited over 10 minutes for a drink while walking in. Rather disappointing.  I drove through the next day for an order and the person working the register looked at my twice but didn't open the window until finally the third time. She asked what i ordered and said it wasn't on the screen. She then yelled to one of her coworkers "you didn't input his order!"  Honestly better off driving to a different Starbucks location because disappointing service seems to be the constant.

Really bad customer service i asked for a cappuccino with 2 sugars on the bottom and was told to put it on my own. I told her it doesn't mix the same and was told again put it yourself. This is the worst experience i ever had in a Starbucks location. Good luck travelers ordering coffee from this place

Y'all need to step y'all's coffee game up cuz y'all coffee wack asf  I literally went up 3 times because it wasn't what I wanted and somehow y'all still managed to fuck up smh I won't be coming back to this one that's for sure

This is the worst Starbucks. Staying at the hotel for a conference, it's closed Mondays & Tuesdays and closes at noon the rest of the week. I get that there's a personnel problem right now but come on. You break from meetings & want coffee and you can't get it.
Looks like a great place to study or meet people. The staff was courteous and was a great place to have a little breakfast before getting to know St. Louis.

Not my favorite location, but not the worst that I have been in. Stopped in on a Friday afternoon before heading up to the Northside for an afternoon of shopping and doing pretty much whatever we wanted. They were busy when we arrived. Normally I would have stuck with the drive-thru, but for some reason decided to go in. The inside as busy just like the drive thru. When we got into line, we had about 4 people a head of us.  Surprisingly for as busy as they were, they got us through pretty quickly. They all seemed to work well together to get people through and on their way.  Some of the furniture looked a little beat up like perhaps it may need to be replaced soon. Other than that the overall location seemed to be clean with several places to sit. Perhaps this was because everyone was sitting outside, but still...lots of places to sit.
Starbucks Hot chocolate puts dunkin donuts and all of those just add water packets to shame. i also enjoyed the iced lemon pound cake, the prices are kinda steep but worth it for the quality.
My compliments to Starbucks at 96 th street they know how to make it. Cafe vanilla frap with a shot of expresso.
Even though they're closed for walk-in orders, they saw me outside and offered me a coffee. This was the nicest thing I experienced all year. Thank you for your kindness.

The coffee is good here and the car line typically moves efficiently. If you're in the mood for a good, convenient coffee, this Starbucks works well. I haven't been inside so I can't vouch for the interior service or setting.
This is a small place, have a few tables and some outdoor seating.  however, the staff is very friendly and also environment is quiet.

This Starbucks is bigger in area than most and has good wifi and access to power plugs on the "bench" seating close to where you pick up your drinks. There is a nice patio area with umbrella tables and a great view of the Sierras. The staff is on the ball, polite and efficient. In this Starbucks you can actually put some tables together and have meeting. The drinks are consistent with Starbucks standards so if you like Starbucks coffee you will like it, if not you won't.

Seriously the worst Starbucks I've ever been to. Super slow and always messes something up. I don't know if everyone is just new but the only reason I go there is because it's convenient but the only good thing is everyone is friendly.
I've mobile ordered 3 different occasions at this Starbucks location. Each time I've waited more than 30 mins for my coffee. Pretty ridiculous! Seems like staff is not able to keep up.

They work hard and are very nice here.  The ladies are lovely.  There are some "character" customers, but they aren't troublesome. I tried the "new" pastry...raspberry donut, and it was fine.    It's my "home" Starbuck's and I like it.

Having not had breakfast before my midday flight, I was happy to find a nice shiny Starbucks in the Southwest terminal. The line was short and the guy at the register was friendly. My breakfast sandwich was hot, though I really wish it was possible to get a crispy English muffin out of those ovens they use to heat them up. My blueberry muffin was very moist, which was a nice change from the scones that I usually get.   However, I requested a hazelnut latte, and the drink I received didn't seem to be quite that. If I had to guess, I'd say it was a mocha latte -- definitely heavier and sweeter than the delicate hazelnut flavor I was expecting. My receipt just said flavor shot, with no specification, as did the cup. I thought about going back to ask, but honestly I had just gotten settled at my gate and didn't feel like it. So I'm sure I'd return to this Starbucks, but I might stick with drip coffee next time.

Was just in here the other day. One of the baristas spit in my double chocolate chip, then called me a nigger and threatened to call the police on me because I took to long to get from the entrance to the cashier. I'm not even black I'm just a dark Puerto Rican with curly hair. If it wasn't for her calling me a nigger and spitting in my drink it would have been a fine experience. 3 stars

Pleasant and cozy shop with friendly baristas and a killer "Red Eye". This shop is my first choice on State and I've never been disappointed yet.  I would definitely recommend this shop and remember;  "Coffee should be served black as the night, hotter than Hell and sweeter than a womans' heart.."

I'm a gold member and love everything about this location!! They always make my cool lime refresher just how I like it. They are the nicest people I greet in the morning. My dog always gets the puppuchino from Starbucks as well.

Literally the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. Great team but zero sense of urgency. I am in the area monthly for business and this is the closest store for me. Unfortunately it takes a minimum of 15-20 minutes to get my order. Once okay but every visit is not acceptable.
Just got a coffee made so poorly I had to throw it out. That's $5 completely wasted. Save your money and don't go to this one.

Okay.... so Starbucks is a Starbucks, is a Starbucks. This place isn't much different than any other location. Baristas are very friendly and are very helpful with any questions you might have. I love the standardization of Starbucks. Everything is the same no matter where you go. Starbucks has done a great job at that I'll say!   They offer two bathrooms which is awesome, and are always clean whenever I've used them!   The only downfall to this location is the lack of seating. For its central location I am surprised they didn't choose a larger store. Its often hard just to find a seat if you come alone, let alone if you come with a friend or two. Only time its not as difficult is if you visit in the warm seasons when you can take advantage of their outside seating.   I do love the location of this store. Its close to home and is central, which makes meeting friends for coffee very easy.

I went in with my friend Gabby. She was disgusted by her drink as she can taste a molecule of wrongness in her coffee. I however was delighted by the staff. She wanted to cry out against all that is unholy about this starbucks.  I calmed her down and bestowed upon her the wonders of this location's craftsmanship, friendliness, and sense of duty to their coffee. I apologize for any inconvenience this caused. Coffee good.

I am very picky on my macchiato. For some reason this location is the only one where it always tastes amazing!  The staff is always so friendly. My office is a couple doors down from here so I stop in regularly to get out of the office and work from there.  I'm sad to hear that they will be closing down and moving to a different location.
Expect to wait a painfully long amount of time unless you have a mobile order. Annoying.

Wow had great customer service here !! They helped me reload my card so I could get my points an reminded me to never feel rushed always take your time an get what you want an if someone behind you is pushy not to worry !!!  Very good advice everyone should take it easy, say hello instead of being pushy!!

I am a frequent visitor here.  I have never had a problem with the service at this starbucks.  Only one out of my gazillion times have i ever had a complaint about my drink, which they remade promptly.    If you see the drive-thru line one car further past the menu just go inside, it would be quicker (as long as there isn't a huge line inside).    As far as coffeehouses in the metro-east illinois area, they are lacking.  There have been a few that have tried but starbucks just puts them out of business.  I hate that for the local coffee shops, however, i love the hours of starbucks open from 5-11 most days so its a great place to get my homework done.  Great access to the highway as well.
This Starbucks looks/feels like you are at an airport.  But, they have the nicest people working here.

Love Starbucks and this location. Everyone is really nice and I love sitting here for hours studying. My only gripe is the drive thru. It's completely hidden so you can see the line before you pull in. Once you're in, even before you order, you're stuck, which is annoying when you're running late. It needs to be redesigned to allow people to get in and out in the event of an emergency or if you charge your mind, especially since there's usually a long wait.

They just finished renovating the entire place and it looks great! The coffee is mediocre just like any Starbucks, but the ambiance is great, and the people that work there are great. The only issue with the location it's self rather than the Starbucks builders, is that there is not a great flow for people to pay for coffee, then pick it up. It's MUCH better than it was before renovation, but people tend to bunch up after the cash register while waiting for their coffee.
Good Venue.  One of few with a Cover Brewer and Reserve roasts by the mug. A tad cramped and on the chilly side indoors.  Quaint patio if you tolerate highway noise.

I stop at this location frequently before my commute to work. The servers are really nice and always have a smile on there face. On a particular morning I asked for a warm beverage that was typically served on ice. When I got to the window that they informed me they messed up the order and made it cold rather than hot. They asked me if I wanted to keep both drinks and I graciously accepted them. The inside of this location is a lot smaller than other Starbuck locations I have been to. They are always neat and organized and fully stocked on all the goods. Highly recommend this location :)

This is located inside a service plaza travelling on the turnpike I76W.     Have stopped here multiple times but always found it to be one of the worst ones. Service is very slow and they take their own time in attending to the customers. I had only two people in front of me and I had to wait 15 minutes for my order of two cups of coffee. The coffee was also not good and was not even hot on top of it. They are even slower when they're busy. Have a bunch of snacks also including nuts, snack bars, Madeline cakes etc. to choose from. Avoid it if you can.

Early morning regular customer here. Baristas are friendly and knowledgeable. Once you're a regular, they have your coffee ready for you almost before you step up to the counter. Lines back up later in the day, and it can get noisy and a bit chaotic, but early morning is nice and quiet.

I'm not sure if it's poor leadership or what but I go to two Starbucks on a regular basis and this one is consistently subpar. I'm a former Starbucks employee so I know how stressful this job can be at times... so I want to have some grace... But the staff at this particular store has failed me numerous times and I'm often greeted with bad attitudes. Granted, there are some employees that have treated me very kindly and with respect, so maybe there are just a few bad apples spoiling the bunch? But they also seem to halfass their drinks on a regular basis and have given me a burned bagel numerous times. I'm glad I have a Starbucks near my house because i love their drinks and breakfast... but I wish this one was a bit better and treated the drinks, customers and their jobs with more respect. I'll continue to return here for the convenience, but I hope they undergo some improvements soon.
Slow service.  Staff needs to move quickly to serve long line of customers.  Milk station may not have milk varieties.

This is the closest Starbucks to me and it is on my way to work so I have been here multiple times.  I have never gone in as this is a drive-thru location and I am always on my way somewhere.  They are fast, very nice and efficient every time.  I have never had any issues with my service or what I have gotten.  The location can be tricky to get out of just due to the volume of cars at the intersection.  Parking is also a negative, but that has never been an issue for me.  I will continue to get my coffee here!

Unacceptably slow, shoddy service.  Seems like they don't like to fill mobile orders, for some reason -- the order-in-person line turned over multiple times this morning while i waited for a simple latte.  For 20 minutes.  The girl at the in-person line milled about sullenly when that line was empty and a cluster of people eating or waiting for orders bumped into each other like chickens bumbling around a stockyard.  I go to this Starbucks more than any other, and regret doing so every time.

I come here a lot. A LOT! Usually multiple time per week, I have amassed a collection of 10 personal cups and counting, and have earned more "stars" than I really need. Which means I spend quite a bit of money here. I also work across the street and have for over five years, which means I have been coming here for five years. I also live the area so I frequently choose this location to do personal work as well.  Honestly, it's a 50/50.  Today's incident aside, you may or may not get friendly service. There is one guy who will hardly acknowledge my existence. However, the lady at the register knows me and my order very well, as do a couple others, and always greet me warmly. Some of the baristas make my order correctly every time, some do not. One girl in particular, who usually  works drive through can N.E.V.E.R. get it correct. If she is at the helm, my friend won't even bother, he just gets tea! I will absolutely sing the praises of some of the staff.  Communication isn't the best between the employees or with customers at times. The seating has been bunk since the remodel. Gone are the comfy chairs, replaced with small tables and a bench, obviously for the study crowd. Plugs are a hit and miss on whether they work and some are protruding from the wall or sitting on the floor. Wi-Fi works well.  The ladies bathroom is a frequent mess with TP on the floor and almost always dangerously low. There is a bit of a stuffy "I'm always being used for a #2" odor and no paper towels to dry hands, just blowers. Seriously, look up the health info of hand dryers. The air they are blowing onto your hands is the very particle laden, odoriferous air you are breathing and flushing into. :\  If you like it cold this is the place to be! I frequently find my hands and feet going numb, winter or summer and have even resorted to purchasing a HEATED MOUSE to try and stave off the cold of my mousing hand. Great investment, btw. I also frequently wear warm hats, sweaters, hoodies, etc to keep my body temperature up. Winter or summer. All. The. Time.  tl;dr: long time customer, big spender, overall experience is a resounding MEH.
I like this Starbucks because it has a drive-through which is usually run prety efficiently and quickly!

The staff in this location are great always friendly welcoming. It is a very small location and sometimes very cramped so unless it's not a grande pike I am ordering I pre order through the app!! I love this!!! In the summer they have a patio that is nice to sit in no way would I chill inside and have a coffee way to cramped!

This location has shorted me twice now when I ordered multiple espresso shots. On 2 separate occasions within the same month I ordered 4 espresso shots as a solo order. When I got home and measured it out, I was only given 2 shots.

I am sorry to leave such a poor review, because I have been a regular here for years (6-7 visits a week). There are a number of great people who work here, but I just can't bring myself to go to this location anymore--I'd rather drive a little farther.   First: drinks are rarely as ordered. I have frequently had to throw away my americanos as there is so much Splenda dumped in them, they are undrinkable. (I travel frequently and have not had this trouble at any other Starbucks location, and it is a consistent occurrence here.) The last time, I gave it back--an unusual step because I would always rather avoid a scene--and of course was met with attitude.  Second: don't try to order breakfast sandwiches here. Or anything on the menu, really. They are always out. Occasional outages I could understand, but...They. Are. Out. Every. Single. Time. Afternoon cake pop? Good luck. Bacon Gouda at 10a? Sorry, out. Ok, turkey bacon that no one probably orders because it is gross, so surely there must be one left? HAHAHAHAHAHAH  I would love to ask their manager what they are doing, because I actually think this would be an excellent case study in how not to manage your inventory par levels. Or maybe they are just trying to get rid of a surplus chicken sausage, because that is the only food item they ever have.  At any rate, I give up. Do yourself a favor and go to the one at Hwy 70 and Old Hickory. Or heck, even Nashville West. Pro tip: if you stubbornly go here anyway and intend to order food, check online ordering first to see that handy "Sold out at this store" notification and save yourself the aggravation.
Who would think that we would want a coffee at 5:00 AM before our flight??  Starbucks still hasn't opened.  This is a crap airport with crap concessions.
Are there any adults that work here and can help these kids out? 20 minutes for a ice tea and panini... The drink wasn't even correct.   Parking lot is a nightmare.

So so SO happy this location has reopened for drive thru during the pandemic.   The staff here are fabulous and David is a social media whiz keeping everyone up to date with their hours and what was going on when they were closed.   2 thumbs way up!  Having to work during the pandemic is stressful but being able to enjoy a treat like a matcha green tea latte makes the day a little easier.    Mar props to David for keeping the community in the loop and for constantly doing updates like how we can do ride up on our bikes and do mobile orders for those of us trying to find ways to treat our kidlets who miss normal.  For now, Starbucks bicycle Frappuccino dates are a great way to celebrate special days like birthdays!!!   Thank you Starbucks

While the window server is nice, the service is so incredibly slow that it ruins the convenience of a drive thru. I only use it when I have sleeping kids in the car because no matter what time you come you end up waiting 15 min in the drive thru line.

I really enjoy going here, because it is less than a mile away from my house. The service is very good, they are pretty quick about getting orders out, and they have never made a mistake about my order.

This is my favorite Starbucks in the foothills. It's a great place to come and people watch. The employees here are always cheerful and nice. The coffee is consistent, and I've never had a bad cup here.   The Swan/Sunrise Starbucks is just an all around friendly spot. Here you'll find everyone from retirees to students. It has the flavor and ambiance of a true coffee house. In the afternoon, it's a popular study spot for the high school crowd- which I think is great! At least they are studying...   The location at Skyline and Campbell is also nice, but in my opinion- the Swan and Sunrise Starbucks is the best Starbucks in the foothills.

So after finishing lunch at White Castle - before I leave - I go up to the counter and ask one of the ladies - "Is there a place around here when I get some espresso?" The lady thinks about it for a few seconds - then tells me "there is one of them fancy coffee shops right before the interstate."  Wondering what I would find - I head back the way I came - lo and behold - it's a friggin' Starbucks! I laughed to myself - wondering what response I would have gotten back at White Castle had I asked for Starbucks by name.  This one is a little hard to get into - there is only one entrance to the parking lot as far as I could see - and there is a lot of traffic going to the Interstate.  This Sbux also has a Drive Thru - which I refuse to use. So I parked and went in. There were three young ladies working behind the counter. I ordered a Dopio Espresso Machiatto - the girl working the register - bless her heart - it was her first day and she had no idea what I had ordered. But the other two girls did not know what my drink was either - the girl who made my drink essentially made me a mini Latte. The cup was filled to the top with milk.  Then the other girl came over - we had a discussion about what the drink is supposed to be - and the manager came out to assist. Meanwhile - the register locked up and they had to reboot in order to give me a receipt.  The pace of life out here in Brownsburg, IN is much slower than the rat race of Washington, DC - and I was in no hurry. The folks behind the counter were so very friendly and wanted to make the drink right - which they did. They asked me where I was from and where I was going - and offered directions and advice. They laughed at me when I asked if I was going to encounter rush hour traffic heading to Champaign - because there is no rush hour! :-)  Lots of interior seating at this Starbucks. If you are ever headed out across the Midwest on I-74 westbound - stop at this Starbucks!

I get my morning coffee (more times than I would like to admit) every week from this location. The drinks are always well made (compared to the Campbell location-yuck).  I have no experience with the bathrooms because I am trying to get my coffee and go but the service is always friendly and extremely fast for the volume they have. Parking may be an issue but they do have a few 15 minute street spots out front for free that I never see people having an issue getting.

I've never had such poor service in my entire life. I dread coming to this location, and the only reason I ever do is because the location closer to me never has multigrain bagels. This location REPORTS that they do on the mobile app. Yet every time I place an order for this location, they "run out" of what ever I ordered.  On top of the lack of accurate reporting on the mobile app, I have yet to receive a drink order that I haven't had to reorder. The baristas here must never read the label on the cup because my order is incorrect 99% of the time. Every time I complain to the baristas about my incorrect order, I never even receive so much as an apology. They usually just shrug their shoulders and offer me something else that's not compatible. The only time I've ever received any kind of sympathy or compensation from a barista was today. After I've been coming to this location for over a year, today was the first time anybody ever did anything to reconcile the mishaps that occur here. Again I placed mobile order because their location reported to have what I wanted, but yet again once I arrived at the location, they were out of whatever I ordered. Shocking. After I made it aware that I was not happy, a barista, I believe his name is Chris, approached a manager on his own will and asked that my order be refunded. Chris, I will give you a 5 star customer service rating. But the other employees at this location, need to go back to barista training. Actually, they might even benefit from learning what customer service actually is. Thank you, Chris. You're the only salvation this location has.
My girlfriend and I ordered cups of coffee only a quarter of the cup was coffee the rest was foam and Air worst 15$ I've ever spend.
Not your best model of starbucks... tiny... teenwage driven and no drive thru... parking is a nightmare lol

After a long day of traveling (that isn't over yet) i needed a cup of coffee.  While the coffee is good the friendly staff was what really made my day.  The nice girl behind the counter greeted me with a smile and made me feel appreciated!  Thank you so much for a little ray of sunshine today!
Starbucks with a friendly staff and not that busy compared to the ones on state st

Love the coffee but the employees at this location are horrible. Every time I have the misfortune of needing to stop at this location they act like they're doing you a favor by waiting on you. Terrible customer service

I come to this Starbucks often. I've been a gold card member for years and was so excited about this location opening (it's on my way to work). This is the second time I've had an issue with Gwen. Simply put she is rude. Speaks to you like you're 3 years old. Coming here is the highlight and start to my day but I'll be driving right on through if she's working...felt like somebody should at least read my frustration.

Every Starbucks is the same bland, boring, sterile yuppie infested, "I'm in a rush, get my latte quick" type place.  Awful Coffee, lousy rude service, terrible sandwiches, overpriced everything.  I avoid it always unless I get a free gift card.
They made my drink right! 4/5 times Starbucks locations make it wrong. Ordered on app, was ready within minutes of ordering. No complaints here!

Okay so I know it's not the end of the world, and mistakes happen, but I wanted to make the store aware- if it doesn't say iced on the Uber eats item order (like this sticker does not say iced anywhere) it's because someone ordered a hot coffee. I know it was probably a genuine mistake, but would appreciate awareness for future orders. Thanks!

I kind of hate this Starbucks but also not really?   Pros:  - it is close to my home and also on the way to the highway (65).   Cons: - The interior layout is dreadful. In regards environment, it is just not a cozy space. The Windows face west and north, and let in harsh light that (if you are bringing a laptop) makes it impossible to sit it (but pull the blinds and it's too dark). When sitting with folks you're liable to have sunlight stab you in the eye if you don't face in a weird direction. There's one large long table where there should be multiple small tables pushed together - rarely do large parties sit there so instead a couple of folks on laptops or talking will take up a huge space. There's one good table by the coffee shelves that's ok. They have another corner table that is accessible to folks in a wheelchair.  - omg the parking and drive-thru is terrible. NEVER PARK IN THE DRIVE THRU AREAS. Often the drive-thru gets backed up and then angry jerks in the drive-thru line are too rude to give space to folks backing out so they can leave. Multiple times I have gotten stuck there for a period of time. It's just the dumbest set up for a drive thru. They should have taken the lane that's between them and LA Fitness.  - the people working here are ok. Sometimes I am just not impressed with service and other times they are fine.   Overall, it is a Starbucks. It has stuff you probably want. I go here for convenience because the south side doesn't have a lot of coffee options. Sometimes it is actually nice. But usually I come here and am fairly unhappy, but glad to have coffee on my way to work.

I'm surprised that during a busy time there is only one cashier. The person before me had an item return and it took such a long time. No one said anything to me while I waited and waited. I come here a lot and just expected to be welcomed and not ignored.   Today Austin made my day! He gave me lots of ideas on how to enhance my drink. Nice! I feel like an important customer and will be back soon. My cascara latte with an extra shot is perfect! Thanks Austin!   David took my order and he was just wonderful! He was helpful, friendly and made my customer experience amazing. I'm now giving this location a top rating because of David's efforts! Keep it up David and I hope others follow suit!

Line at this Starbucks in Marriott Downtown lobby is always insane. There weren't any spices (I always have cinnamon in my coffee) and the milk containers were empty. Not what you'd expect on a busy Saturday morning. The staff was nice enough, but they could have used another hand it looked like.

Amazing Staff! So very busy and packed BUT quick, efficient service with smiles! Nice sitting areas and attached to the Hilton. NOT just serving Starbucks, but a REAL Starbucks! Probably the best Starbucks I've ever visited since they are so fast and correct with orders! Go here for your morning Latte! I go every morning when I'm at the Hilton, which is a lot.

This used to be my go-to Starbucks, but since this day I think I'll just have to drive an extra block or two to get my daily cup, or try the new local place around the corner. I was annoyed about the milk ring (so gross), but even more annoyed that my Americano was so poorly made. I would have forgotten all about it but I complained to the supervisor, gave my information, and was told the manager would contact me the following day. Well, that was weeks ago so apparently they didn't care to make things right with one of their regulars. Which sucks because my original barista there, Bailey, is legit one of the best in town. Miss her already   Check your coffee before you pull off people! I know that's easier said than done.

I am not really a fan of Starbucks.  Unfortunately at this mall all they have for coffee is Starbucks.  I ordered a venti Cold Brew which I can tolerate.  Most of the coffee at Starbucks just tastes burnt to me but as I said the cold brew is ok.  My wife ordered a Carmel frapucino light.  However they did not have the sugar free Carmel.  It is very sad to me that Starbucks would ever be missing any of the needed ingredients for their signature drinks.  The first drink they made did not have any coffee at all.  I did not even know they would make a Frapucino with no coffee.  The guy behind the counter said that kids get it that way.  My wife said it was "gross."  In any case she settled on a mocha frapucino light which ended up tasting ok.  The people behind the counter were nice which is why I am giving 2 stars instead of 1.    It is also overly busy here which explains the slow service.  The bottom line is that I will continue to get coffee here when I am at the Mall because they seem to have a monopoly which explains the high prices.
Great conversion and reuse keeping the feel of the old house while making it familiar. Friendly team here.
Turn up the heat! It is winter. Doesn't have to be 80 degrees in here but cutting off the cool air coming from the vent would be a nice touch for patrons.

Making my usual morning pit stop! Grabbed a delicious pumpkin spice latte and a breakfast sandwich. I ordered the double smoked bacon, egg, and cheese sandwich. It was really good. Bacon cooked perfectly and the bread was unbelievable soft. Wes the barista was super friendly and helpful! Thanks Wes!!

I like Starbucks and drink their drip coffee often. And my review really isn't just about this store it's about all of them, it just so happens that this is most recent store where I have experienced this reoccurring problem that seems to happen nationwide.  I can drink coffee whenever and don't have problems sleeping so I often will get coffee in the evenings, and sometimes it's close to closing time. More times than not the coffee is either cold or lukewarm at best. I know Starbucks cares about it's service and I don't understand why they let this go on. It can't cost them hardly any money to brew a new pot when the last one is empty or a half pot but no you get coffee that has been sitting for hours and it's horrible.   There have been times when I have been told that they can make some quickly and that is fine but to just give out cold coffee shouldn't be an option. In this particular instance it was just before 8 pm and they don't close until 9 pm so really no viable excuse for this happening.

Lines can be a hit or miss but the Starbucks galliera is a very busy location! There's very limited seating inside but there's some outside and additional seating down stairs by panera and Nordstrom.  I usually do a mobile order if I decide to get a drink here. I have never had a problem with this location and the drinks I've order here are super delicious :)

Great area upstairs to socialize with friends or relax with a drink.  Also, keep an eye out for events they have going on.  I've caught a couple musical acts and a touring author while I've been there.

Lots of seats outside, and it is in a shaded area.  This Starbucks reminds me of the one at El Con except with a drive-thru so it is a bit more chaotic.  It is probably not a place you want to sit and read quietly.  There are a few couch chairs.    The staff is excellent.  If you are thinking about getting a modification to your normal drink, they will make you a regular drink if you absolutely dislike the new drink.  I love this about the employees.

This Starbucks is inside Target, I had gotten a wrong order from another store and was in Target, I mentioned it and Andrew working was incredibly polite and corrected it for me. Would absolutely return to this location. Not to mention it was delicious when I did get what I wanted!

Baristas are friendly here, but they really need to reconfigure the layout of this place.  In the mornings it was packed and they moved the line quickly, but the number of people waiting for their drinks at the bar made it unclear where the line ended.  I watched multiple people stand back by the door behind people who had already ordered, thinking they were in line.  It's just a mess.    Also, they may want to consider a second person working the bar in the morning considering how busy they were.

I love Java Frappuccino from Starbucks, but this one from the Wynnewood Starbucks was horrible! I went there during the week around 5pm, I don't know if it's always the same lady who works by that time, but anyway, the Frappuccino tasted like water. There was too much ice, I couldn't even taste the coffee or the chocolate. I couldn't drink with the straw, I only ate the whipped cream with a spoon. Really disappointed!

This is one of the worst Starbucks I have ever been to. They are constantly out of multiple food and drink items at a time, and service is very slow and often poorly executed. I have received many specialty drinks from here that simply weren't made properly. The inside atmosphere is cramped and not good for sitting around, so definitely get your drinks to go.

I never thought I would say this but I really like Isla Vista. This Starbucks is alright, but nothing special. It seems like they run out of things a lot. This most recent time: Venti sized cups. So they gave me a trenta instead. Whatever, I'm good with getting more.  I made a joke at the point where I was offered a trenta cup instead, I said "oh only if you fill the cup". Bad joke, but super serious face from Starbucks girl. Have some fun people.  Self-serve water, I actually think that's a good thing.

This is downtown Wilmington where there is about 4 reserved spaces for customers of this Starbucks.  Employees of this Starbucks park in these spaces all day long.  Especially a Red Mazda 3 with Delaware Plates.

I've gone to this Starbucks solely for convenience because its nearby and I don't drive. Baristas don't do a good job of making sure the trash is taken out, twice I came and it was overflowing. I walked in and one table was covered in trash. And I know they weren't that busy because one of the baristas was on her phone. I had to say excuse me loudly before she even knew I was standing in front of her. This location is also rampant with loud teenagers every night. I know its not a library, but people come to establishments like this to enjoy coffee and  get a little work done. Never have I been to a Starbucks where patrons are so loud. Not to mention the seating area is way too small.  You are better off getting your just okay drink to go. I probably won't be back anytime soon.

Terrible. The only good thing about this place is its location. Even if there is no line it will take you at least 10 minutes to get a drink. My beverage of choice is a cafe misto, which is not a hard order, and they look at me like I am talking gibberish when I order it. Not a problem at ANY other Starbucks I have ever been to. They are too busy talking to each other to even acknowledge customers when they walk up to the counter. The last time I was there the cashier took my order with a wet mop in her hand. Lovely and very sanitary.

Busy place in the mornings better download the app to order ahead of time to get your coffee . Or you will be waiting and you will be waiting in rush hour traffic . But don't order it till your close to location bc when you order ahead  they make it faster

Great Starbucks. Plenty of seating, and DIVERSE seating (armchairs facing a real fireplace, handicap-accessible/designated larger tables, group tables, mini tables, couches with tables alongside the wall, outdoor, etc.) The employees are quick, efficient and professional. It can get pretty crowded depending on the time of day and on weekends, but if you pick your time wisely, it can be a pretty great study spot. Of course, there is the standard constant music playing, so if that's not within your study habit, bear that in mind. Also, there are two different single bathrooms, which I think is more than your average starbucks (= less waiting).

Always love coming to this location. Everyone always is so friendly and they always make my drinks right. Not being rude but other locations don't compare to the quality of customer service I receive at this location. Thanks for always being so friendly and for always letting me rant about the long hot summers.

Hey Starbucks! A big wag of the finger!! I bring my own personal cup to get my iced coffee and yet, your staff thinks that it is okay to use a plastic cup to simply write what my drink was and then that cup gets discarded. Chuck You #Starbucks. #wasteful

I'm did not appreciate this Starbucks. I go to a Starbucks pretty much everyday and order the same exact drink. The girl working the counter was rude and charged me for a refresher drink not a plain tea. I asked her if she had charged me correctly because my drink was over $2 dollars more than usual. I have been at basically a Starbucks in every city. Even in downtown LA, San Francisco, and NYC a plain tea does not cost that much. She told me "You got a trenta it costs more!" Meanwhile, I order a trenta every single day. Then I heard her tell another costumer that it was only her third day. Super rude and full of herself for only working there a couple days. Definitely will just skip this Starbucks in the future.
Love this place. Our Barista's are so friendly and remember us every time we go in? They even remember my usual drink! Great new sandwiches. 5more days till La Boulange.

Haven't been to this one for awhile and this was a  refreshing visit! They have a board up with  post-it notes to share what you are looking forward to this summer. Fun to read and write your own.  And then outside,  ( where there are lovely tables and spots to sit),   there is a little art  rock garden.  Great service, as usual. Delicious fresh and fast  coffee.
Not for the squeamish. A rat just ran the length of this busy Starbucks outpost. If you're part of the Muridae family, this is your place.

Not enough seating for normal folks wanting to sit and enjoy a coffee with a date. Is taken over by students using Starbucks for a study hall. Funny cause some of them don't have a purchase that I have noticed?  Sorry but I pay good money for Starbucks coffee .

Prime location on the way to/from work or accessing highway 40 at Jefferson.  I appreciate the alternative to the busy DelTaco location at Grand and 40.   Interior is a bright, welcoming, open floor plan with plenty of seating once we get back to dining, working, meeting, chillaxing at cafes again. The restrooms are upscale chic as a public restroom can be with the soap/water flow/dryer in a single sink location.  This really cuts down on paper towel waste  it I like to have a little tissue to open the door after washing my hands personally.   Service was prompt and pleasant (I was the only one brave enough to walk inside I guess) the drive thru was popping.  My coffee was made just as I'd requested and I was on my merry way.   Oh, plenty of outdoor patio seating and parking available.   ~Christy

Had my 8-shot espresso, some vanilla bean frappacinno (plus a hair caught on the lid halfway inside the drink). I love Starbucks and this one had great service. However, the hair killed all the other upsides of this place. C'mon kids. You can have all the friendly and prompt service and a great product but putting a hair in the product you serve is poor service.

Reading some of the prior reviews and patronizing this Starbucks on an almost daily basis, I almost didn't recognize the place.  The reviews for the prior years were accurate, reflecting some of what was going on at the time they were written, but thankfully, none of that's the case now.  Corinne is the store's manager, and she has an awesome staff; I've seen them really working hard and getting food & drinks assembled at a breakneck pace, even when the line of customers is literally out the door.  They do all of this with a smile, and also remember the regular customers' customized drinks and names accurately.  They are so nice.  I only have one complaint/suggestion, and it's not under the control of the store's staff:  the store is too small!  The next-nearest Starbucks is located at the corner of Bloomingdale & Providence, and the square footage and furniture is much more conducive to hanging out, working on a laptop, sitting outside, or reading.  Hey Starbucks Corporate:  put some money into the Lithia-Pinecrest store and give them an upgrade!  Bigger, please.
Bad service , needs help understanding that at lunch customers come first then restock candy later
5 stars for their barista John Main. Best barista in the country. 10/10 doctors recommend you buy your coffee from him. 4 real.

Yesterday for National Croissant Day there was a very nice option to donate a Croissant to the Landsdale food bank, myself and many of the customers in line, obliged the offer and donated a Croissant. After I left it dawned on me that this is not a good thing.   We paid FULL price for the Croissant, which is around $2.60. I am sure Starbucks out-of-pocket cost per Croissant is likely much less than $2.60. Essentially customers donated more to Starbucks than the food bank. While I am glad that someone will enjoy the croissant that I donated, I truly do. I think Starbuck just increased their sales of croissants that day by ten-fold. This was a nice gesture but I think it would be better to give $3 directly to the food bank with out there being a profit for Starbucks. Or maybe Starbucks could donate the difference in price. It is a good thing just work the details out if you do this in the future. Thanks!

The barista who worked on July 4 was slightly rule. I was Starbuck customer for years. I was very shocked about  Starbuck staff who was bad behavior on that day. I ordered the double espresso and cup of ice. After paid and picked my order, I went to condiment table to pur some skim milk on my espresso. I was told  to pay extra if I used condiment milk. I told him that it was ok to charge extra. I willing to pay extra. I was so shock about the way how he talked to the customers. He was loud in the front of every others and he seemed  to let everyone could hear his. The principle was not paying extra for using condiment milk, but it showed how the Starbuck staffs to talk to the customers. I was very embrassing in the front of others. I was humiliated by the barista on that day at Startbucks which located at Civic Blvd. Philadelphia.   I called to the store and asked for speak to the manager. it was too bad. I was told he was a manager. As a manager, I think he should learn how communicate  and respect the customers. Communication is best way to keep the business to grow.  Especially, this Starbuck located in best area. He should realize he deals with differences levels of different professional  around Penn and Chop.
