Horribly slow service every time I come here no matter what time it is! Drinks are inconsistent but still better than any other coffee around big bend, and they have a drive thru, unlike most other sb in Riverview. Been waiting over 5 min in drive thru with one car in front of me for a grande coffee.

I normally love this place, however... the obviously new girl who doesn't seem to know what she's doing at all and is the slowest moving person I've ever seen should not be cashiering during the busy morning when the line is out the door.    I've had her twice - the first time they were out of Pike's Place and she had to brew it, but she didn't know how.  She got someone else to brew it and then she went to help make drinks.  I waited.  And waited.  Meanwhile, she never checked back with me or bothered to pour the coffee I had already paid for.  Eventually I found another person to help me while the first girl was wandering around behind the counter, totally oblivious to the fact that she left me standing there for 15 minutes while the line grew longer.  Then today I get her again and it's like it is her first day all over again. She looked so lost and confused - it was a struggle for her to pull a cup out and pour the coffee. They were out of Pike's Place again (go figure) and she had to ask the other cashier what to do!  Didn't we just do this last week? How have you regressed in your knowledge of your job during that time?  For god sake, the line is out the door.  Learn your job or get out of the way.    Besides that, the coffee is good and I love getting a free same-day refill when I bring back my cup and receipt. And the other employees are great - it's just this one girl who needs to go back to training.
Loved Jeremy!! So nice loved him never fire him pls or else I will be in tears also this app is telling me need to write more which is kinda MAKUNG me mad Rn
It is nice to have Starbuck's signature coffee in the hotel, but they are definitely  missing Starbuck's brand of food.  The food is not that great.

Love Starbucks.. this location however is a complete mess.. I've been waiting for 20 minutes for my tea meanwhile I've witnessed them mess up four drinks in front of me. I haven't seen a single smile from any of the employees.. or customers. Clearly a management issue

I like to get coffee from this starbucks. The baristas  are very friendly and polite. Typically I go there when I want to take some break from my school work. They have nice outside area that you can sit and wait for your drink. It is a crowded place to work inside

I'm not going to argue that this is the best Starbucks ever - because it's not. At the same time, however, I don't think it deserves the low rating it currently holds. First of all, whether because of the strange address entry or because of a Yelp database error, this particular Bucks location is hard to find. In fact the only way I could find it was by choosing "nearby" on my Yelp app and zoom in on 8th and Walnut.   The management of this location is superb and for the most part, they handle the morning rush very well. That said, there are a few downsides to this location. I've had one or two Pike Places in which the coffee's clearly been burned. In addition, there is one employee, an older gentleman, who not only makes frequent mistakes with orders, but also has a knack for taking an absurd amount of time to process orders. When he is working register, long lines are guaranteed, as well as his typical curt attitude with customers.  When it comes to the quality of the drinks and the friendliness of its staff, otherwise, I think this is a strong Starbucks. On the average morning, you can be in and out of this location in 5 minutes, or less if you are only ordering a drip coffee like me. Given its proximity to Independence Mall, Washington Park, and the bustle of Jewelers Row, it is primed for a little line and busy tables. If you're looking for a place to sit and read, I'd recommend looking to the Starbucks at 10th and Chestnut. Here, there are very few tables, even less available, and because of the 2 sets of double doors, it can be a bit drafty.
I love Starbucks Coffee it brighten up my day and it help me stay up to read more books like always. :D and watch more anime.

Customer service is great! Usually when I get my drink and ask for light ice, I still get a bunch of ice even when i say very very light ice please. But on my drink, the worker wrote light ice and then light light ice underneath it and I really appreciate it. After the person making my drink asked if the ice amount was alright. Staff is friendly and makes the experience much better

Quality is consistent with this Starbucks. Their parking situation is rather ruff and I believe that is why only Starbucks has been successful in this plaza. The staff inside was very pleasant and immediately greeted me with a smile. I'd recommend doing a mobile order in most cases but if you can order inside as the layout is nice.   Ps make sure you have Starbucks rewards it's so worth it!
Friendly service and nice seating area. Staff was nice even after I accidentally broke a mug! Sometimes lines were long in the morning.

Fastest starbucks I've ever been to and I have definitely been to more Starbucks than I care to admit.  Seven of us rolling through the drive thru, everyone ordered and they not only got everyone's order correct, it was all ready when we got to the window!!  We were super impressed!!  Enjoy!!

How does this location have no reviews? It ya da huge indoor seating area and the friendliest people! I wasn't even halfway through the door before someone poked their head up behind the counter to say hi with the biggest smile! I wish I would've taken her name . Short name starts with a maybe?  She was so bubbly and friendly! Service was super fast too! Location has tons of parking, a drive through, and was very clean when I saw it :)

Date night, Apparently there are 2 locations in Oldsmar and I was at one while the beau was at another. While I waited for him to join me at this location, I ordered my hot chai Tea. Jonah was absolutely wonderful. He was personable and friendly and exceptionally warm. We chatted about how our names are different (my alternate Id) and how we don't often hear them, though mine is growing popular due to Anime.   Once my order was placed, it took only a few minutes to have it in my hands so I could wait for Bruce to join me. I'd come back.

Worst customer service ever experience at a Starbucks, with the exception of one barista who is very sweet. I comment on the rudeness of the barista who had an attitude and instead of apologizing, she says "sorry you feel that way". Very unprofessional to say the least. There are far better Starbucks in the area you can go to than wasting your time at this one.

Ok, it is a Starbucks. If you drink coffee, you've been to a Starbucks. I typically don't write reviews for chains, especially the really big ones. However, what makes this Starbucks great is the service.  I have never had bad service at this Starbucks. There have been one or two time that something has taken longer than it should have. (Usually it is my son's Java chip frappuchino) However, they go out of their way to make it right and correct the issue.  The staff is always very friendly and typically speedy, even when they are really busy. This Starbucks is a well-oiled machine.

I came here almost every day for 6 years, and my experience has been hit or miss. There's constantly staff changes. There was a very nice barista who used to work there at least a few years ago but left to the King of Prussia Starbucks; he even remembered me and my order when I went there. Mel, a current barista, is the best! Ever since I first met her, she's always been smiling and making my drinks perfectly. If you want light ice, less drizzle, more drizzle, no whip, whatever, she's the girl to ask. Whenever I ask another barista (don't know her name) for light ice, she fills the cup with so much ice that I had to shimmy my straw through a couple times. Sometimes if Mel isn't working, I don't even bother to ask. Because this is the University Starbucks: The inside is pretty spacious but always full. The line can get really long, especially between xx:50 and xx:10. There's never available parking spaces after 7:30 AM. Rewards don't work, but you can still get stars.  One nice addition is that they do have a barcode scanner now so the baristas no longer have to punch in the card number.
Thank you! Even in terrible weather, the service was great & efficient. The man clearing the snow off the lot was also courteous!

Probable one of my favorite Bucks in the area. There is plenty of parking both in the back parking lot and street parking in the front. I would recommend ordering ahead if you are in a rush because this one gets fairly busy. There is a ton of tables and seating throughout the store so you have a pretty good chance of finding a spot to enjoy your beverage or get some work done. I personally love the fact that as a gold member you get free refills or hot coffee, hot tea, and iced tea or iced coffee.

I love Starbucks inside of grocery stores - it's so convenient :)  The staff at this Starbucks have always been friendly and the coffee yummy.  This Genuardi super market also has wireless so you can get your coffee and sit down to use the free wireless.

I have come to this location multiple times hoping my experience will be different than the last, but nope. I seem to have to bad experience each time I visit this location. The staff are not very friendly (I understand it can be a tough job, but a little kindness can go a long way). My order has either been messed up or the staff forgot to make it and this has happened nearly each time. This happened even when I've used the mobile app to place an order. The times I have ordered inside the store have been no different. The customer service is terrible and I have often been ignored by the cashier and other staff. I have finally learned my lesson and will avoid this location at all costs. The staff deserves to be treated with kindness, but so do customers.

Nothing like your local Starbucks , well my Starbucks is definitely better than yours lol, this place is in a good location for those of you that live in Zionsville like I do, this Starbucks is right next to the interstate and have some of the friendliest barista in the business

Finally, it opens!  The shell of this Starbucks had mocked the better half and I for at least a year. Driving past it twice going to visit her folks "Opening soon" it said for ever and ever.  Then, suddenly it was open and operating!  The staff seems more than excited about the new location. My barista, while making my Grande Double Blended Caramel Frappucino, was filled with so much zeal that he must have put almost the entire can of whipped cream on the top.  When he went to put the lid on, an almost obscene display of it ejected from the hole where the straw goes!  I'm looking forward to many future visits to this location, and I hope they keep putting on just the same amount of whipped cream!

I recently came to this Starbucks because it's close to my place. I wasn't that impressed with it. The store is understaffed. The people who work there were clearly overworked and they weren't really friendly. It's a pretty small Starbucks and became overcrowded really quickly. Just wasn't a fan of this one and probably won't be coming back to it.
Great service, especially by a friendly barista named Zah who was working fast to help a long with a smile on his face. Thank you!!

I lived in Bensalem for a year and came here multiple times a week. Never had any issues whether I used the drive-through or walked in. They have a smaller inside area but they have a decent outside seating area. Even though I've since moved, I still come here occasionally and have no complaints.
Coffe is good but the black-haired girl behind the counter is a total troll. Seems friendly enough with her co-workers but rude to the customers. 01/01/2015 @ 1745
3.5 stars My neighborhood Starbucks.. Fast and always friendly service. The only thing that sucks is the lack of parking.

This Starbucks has really gone down hill in the last year.  This recent incident was the tipping point for me as I am pretty tolerate of stressful situations and understand that food service is not the most happy environment in which to work.  I was in earlier this week and ordered 4 drinks for people from work.  The staff person who took my order quickly wrote my name on all four drinks.  Just catching it as this was 7:10 am I politely asked if we could go back on three of the drinks and write in the names of the people I was ordering for.  The staff person looked me straight in the face and sighed and did not break eye contact.  I was completely shocked.  I said to him, "Oh I'm sorry, I did not mean to be annoying."  There was no response, no thank you when I paid, nothing.  This was rather hostile behavior especially when you look someone right in the eye, sigh, and maintain eye contact without speaking.  The staff person also forgot to provide me the danish for which I paid for.  I was so taken back and embarrassed that I did not even go back in when I discovered the missing danish.  When I  shared this story, my colleagues said verbatim "We don't even go to that one anymore and will drive out of our way to go to the Starbucks on Main Street.  Interesting to note that of all the Starbucks in Indy this one has the lowest stars with the most complaints.  Will never be back.  Management needs to read this and do something about their staff.

If you love Starbucks, well here ya go.  Conveniently close to your gate so easy to grab something for your flight.   Yup, you pay more because you're at an airport, but you need to expect that.  And if you are already a Starbucks fan, then you're already used to spending way to much for coffee.  But, I like knowing there is one close to my gate and that I will have a yummy drink and scone for my flight!!

Ordered a sandwich and they forgot to make it. Not a huge deal at first. Mistakes happen. But I stood there and the barista (Alexis maybe, pink hair) wouldn't make eye contact with me at all. I could have been standing there bleeding out and she wouldn't have looked at me. I waited it out trying to get her attention, as I had plenty of time while waiting for my flight. But finally another employee noticed me, apologized that they forgot, and took care of my order. Maybe a bad day for Alexis, but with the way she was interacting with her fellow employees, I believe it was just a bad attitude and bad customer service.

Located on the corner.  It's a huge storefront.  You'd never know this was a Starbucks except for the name.   Lots of dark wood, clusters of recycled brass horns used for lighting above throughout the store.  High ceilings.  Plenty of seating.  It's very Nawlins.  I'm not sure who the previous store occupant was, but this Starbucks is the most amazing one I've been to.   And the baristas are very friendly.   Even if you don't drink Starbucks coffee it's worth taking a peek inside.
One of the first Starbucks in St. Louis. Nice staff fast service. The only complaint is no paper towels in bathroom. I hate hand dryers- they are too noisy and spread germs
Quick swing through the drive through on the way to the St. Louis Turkey Trot in Tower Grove Park.  Super convenient location.  Friendly drive through employees.

There's finally a new Starbucks drive thru open further down Hillsborough that's probably worth the drive. This location is frustrating. On the corner of extremely congested Memorial and Hillsborough, and paired with GameStop, in the smallest plaza ever. If you can squeeze past the drive thru line to enter the parking lot, you'll be lucky to find a spot. Going inside is typically faster but who wants to risk getting trapped? Exiting is tricky because if you park you basically have to exit right onto Memorial. If you drive thru, you can loop around the building again to turn right onto Memorial -or- turn right onto Hillsborough. Very rarely does either option go smoothly. On more than one occasion they were unexpectedly closed for unknown reasons. It's too bad because the staff is super friendly, and do a fine job prepping drinks and breakfast sammiches. I'd only recommend this Starbucks if you travel during times of little to no traffic.

This Starbucks has been great to me over the years. I came here the other day ordered some drinks and sat up in their loft seating area, very cool place to sit.  If you have to go pee, don't hold it, the bathrooms are key code entry... so a barista needs to shout the code to you, this could lead to problems. This is probably because of the insane amount of peddlers and homeless that crowd the streets of Santa Barbara.  The service here was great. The parking in downtown can be tough if you think you are going to park right out in front of wherever you are going.  Great spot!

This is by far my favorite starbucks in town. The location is perfect and the atmosphere is awesome. I went in a few weeks back and I ordered a pretty complicated drink and all I could hear was "have bar star do it" in the back. I was a little confused until a kid came around and started working on it. I couldn't catch his real name but he lives up to his nick name for sure! Bar star is truly a star....

I guess the problem with this Starbucks is its location.  It's great if you're cruising around the mall, but for a morning java it doesn't work. Why you might ask? Well, because it doesn't open until 9:30am. Unless you work at the mall or a bank, the business hours are limited. I personally like to have a fresh cup in the morning to get my day going, and I don't work at the mall.  Aside from the operating hours, it's a Starbucks we are all familiar with, clean and an escape to the java world.  The interior is cozy but only offers six small tables. Cool for relaxing during crazy shopping sprees. Staff was friendly, as on most Starbucks.  It's closest to Sears and Macy's entry.

This is my first time in this location and I can't wait to come back again. It has kind of a rustic atmosphere and I love it. The space is really well illuminated, enough tables for everyone. You can come in from the parking lot using two different doors.   I ordered a soy green tea latte - smooth and creamy ground green tea, lightly sweetened and served with steamed soy milk and my friend a caramel macciato - Freshly steamed milk with vanilla-flavored syrup, marked with espresso and topped with caramel drizzle.  This Starbucks perfectly located, in the middle of two important streets but parking is not a problem.
Great store. Super friendly employees. But... No outside seats. A Starbucks without al fresco seating is like a forest without trees. A farm without produce. A pool with no water.

So, I wanted to change my review from last time. I came back here to study but I realized that some of the tables were very wobbly and not in great conditions. Furthermore, the bathrooms (both of them) were very disgusting. As well, when I got my Pumpkin Spice Latte, there was already a lot of splatter on my cup, and the rim looked like someone drank out of it (though it is more reasonable to believe that the latte had overflowed and left its mark). However, I highly recommend the Pumpkin Spice Latte - very sweet and warm. Service was okay.

Woefully understaffed and can't handle the crush of students in line. Hot coffee can be had at the register, but inexplicably ICED coffee has to wait in the same queue as all the espresso drinks. Seems inefficient, when it is just pouring already made coffee on ice, but worse yet it contributes to the crazy wait times and massive crowd of people waiting to get their drinks.
The best Starbucks I have ever been in Philadelphia. An amazing staff, especially, Jawuan who made the amazing attached recipe, which is gonna be my favorite from now on :) Thanks Jawuan
This is the worst Starbucks out there! Second day in a row no vanilla in a vanilla latte! I waited five minutes as three employees watched coffee pour into a cup. Worst tasting coffee.

generic Starbucks.  In Tampa across from hotel & office location.  Coffee was excellent.  Very good selection of non-coffee goods and foods.  Wifi - noted a large number of persons having coffee & plugged in to their notebooks/laptops.  Comfortable seating although i, as is my want, ordered take out.   The coffee here was very good.  I'm not usually a huge fan of Starbucks coffee but the coffee i got here was excellent.    Service was professional, prompt, kind, good humored and most courteous.  Recommended.
Gave attitude and was short and snappy! Poor customer service. Better off going to other coffee shops in the airport!
convenient location, but slowest service i've received at a starbucks.

We stop at this Starbucks usually on the way to my husband's parents' home. Service is fine, although they don't put sprinkles on my skinny cinnamon dolce latte unless I ask (unless the stores on Binford and 96th St). The odd thing is that there is always something weird going on in the parking lot. This last time, a guy was driving in reverse across the lot; another time, a car was trying to exit while another car was trying to enter and neither car would move. I ended up having to get up out of the car to order while my husband waited to get into the lot!

Nothing different or disassociative about this location other than it's in the airport.  Dropped in right before my flight to get a perk me up and was served quickly and politely.  This airport is pretty laid back so I doubt they get a crowd ever.  No seating in this location, it's just a walk up style setup.  Smaller in size overall than most any other I've seen but that's relative to the size of the airport I would think.

I love this Starbucks!! I travel for work, so I go to a lot of Starbucks all over the US & I have to say that this location is one of my favorites! I usually order the same drink but occasionally I want something different. They are always helpful. When I redeem my rewards they are just as helpful & always polite with a smile!  My Latte is always perfect too! My drink is a grande triple shot Latte so I know how it should taste. Some locations don't get it right but this location always gets it right! However, there is one employee who is not as nice and helpful as the others. She wasn't helpful when my Starbucks app was not working. Not that it's her job but she acted as if it was an inconvenience when I asked her about it, even though I had my cash in hand because the app wasn't showing any money. I've had the same experience with her on several occasions.

Consistently the SLOWEST SB location in Reno, with rude, flighty workers. They have this random order of making drinks...it's clearly not which one was ordered first. I think they all hold off on doing blended drinks or drinks that require some real work (like stirring!) because they don't want to, and it sits there until someone is forced to make it after all other drinks and customers are gone. People should not have to wait close to 30 minutes for one drink when everyone behind them places the order, gets the drink, drinks it, and leaves. That's inexcusable. As is your crappy response when questioned about it. Here are a few tips for you: have an adult in the store once in a while. It's great that you hire young people, but some of them just aren't professional or concerned enough to work with customers. Don't stop what you are doing to flirt with visiting friends for ten minutes. Make the drinks. (I know that one is asking for a lot!) Send your workers to a real SB location to see how other employees do it. Or better yet, let them go across the street and see how McDonalds workers treat their customers. That's even better service.
The music is so stinkin loud in this place most days.  Really annoying for people who come here to work or have a nice quiet lunch break to get away from the office.

Wazzzzup fam? ill cut to the chase. So starbucks gets alot of the hate cause the white girls are like "OMG CUPCAKE YAY" but in all realness its pretty swag. Some of thier drinks kinda ehhhhhh and I dont really like the actual coffee (find it acidic and bitter like) but they frapachinos/tea be killin. SHoutout @chaitealatte aka my LYFE. The cake pops are cake... on a pop. HWAT MMORE DO YOU POEPLE WANT????? its so good like i could probs live off them. TBH i probas alreDY DO. k so starbucks = starswag  PEACE HOMMIES COnan outtttttttttttttttttttttttttttttttttttttt
Love Starbucks & the wonderful coffees available. The staff is courteous & professional :)
No outlets at all so find another place to do schoolwork. Bathrooms could've been cleaner.  Coffee is alright.

Starbucks is my favorite coffee hang out anyway. But I was especially impressed with the great customer service at this location. Speedy service, the flavoring was done perfectly and all of the employees were professional polite and friendly.

This Starbucks inside of Safeway is quick, friendly, and has quite a bit of seating even though it's inside a grocery store. The seating is one long half booth/half chair seating, but a huge group of women seemed to be having a great time when we were there.   It was very convenient for the day we were having. We only had to make one stop on our way to a picnic. I got my frappuccino as well as a fruit tray and veggie tray all in one location.  My favorite drink was done just right and the baristas there were really pleasant.

I am a sucker for good coffee, and there are very few local options that I have found, so I end up defaulting to Starbuck, but that does not mean settling.  This particular location could benefit from some more seating, or maybe some different arrangement of seating, but all in all, it's adequate.  I didn't like that I ordered the Narino 70 cold brew on their menu, because it's on their menu, but "they don't sell that here" was the response I got.  That was frustrating as I don't like false or dated advertising, hence the three stars.

This Starbucks isn't the usual Starbucks. They have very few selection. The smoothies was extremely great! My BF ordered an ice coffee something and it was too watered down. I wouldn't recommend this place unless you need something badly.

Thank you Starbucks! You saved the day!  I went to this location twice this morning, in the odd snowy conditions on this 11th of March. I ordered the wrong drink my wife asked me to go get her. She was not happy at all. So I went back to order another drink (the right one this time) and I told them I screwed up the order and needed another drink. (Grande White Mocha Frapp no whip) When I got to the window the Head Barista ,(Mgr.) ,asked me if they made the mistake or did I. I told them ot was totally my mistake and he told me he appreciated my brutally honest admission and rewarded me with the drink for no charge. I am forever greatful and will continue being a loyal customer. Not because I was given a free drink but because that's damn good customer service showing appreciation for my business, and that is in short supply in todays world. Than you again!                   Sincerely Greatful Patron!,                               Justin G.
By far the best Starbucks in Doylestown and the area. Staff is great and quick,  line doesn't stay long quick. Great for in and out.
I love the Vanilla bean Frappe which my friend Jeremiah introduced me to. Friendly staff and clean store. I love the smell of coffee but I don't drink it

Such a great store! The store is brand new and beautiful, the ceiling has this recessed thing that helps keep the sound down, and the giant windows let in a ton of natural light. There's not a lot of seating room, but I think most of the crowd are business people grabbing a cup and moving on. I never have trouble finding space. The best part is the staff, who really make you feel welcome. After like my third time, the barista remembered my name! I've never been a regular before, it's so nice when the barista starts remembering you and suggesting new things you'll like.
Great hidden place to relax and enjoy a cup of coffee with friends. Great staff and a great little patio.
I love this Starbucks. It's comfortable, roomy, and the service is awesome. Definitely recommend stopping in there if you're in Turnersville
Located inside the Safeway, it's your standard Starbucks, as it should be. You can grab a coffee while you shop or grab one one the go. Convenient for Westsiders.
I love this Starbucks and location.  The staff are always super nice and the coffee's are on point.  This is a great chill place too. I am always ready for my latte with extra foam yummm

I am a big fan of the Starbucks chain in general.  When I worked on political campaigns these became my unofficial offices. They have comfy furniture, free internet,  and nice music playing in the background.  I have never felt pressured to buy anything or leave a Starbucks - no matter how long I have spent there.  It is a great place to have a one on one meeting, prepare yourself for a presentation, or just catch up on work between meetings.   My fun Starbucks tip- join the rewards program.  It is free- cost you absolutely nothing- but it does provide you with at least one free drink a year, discounts through out the year, and every 13th food or beverage item is free. If you buy regular coffee, iced tea, or iced coffee- there are free refills with the card for your whole visit- which just contributes to my willingness to stay there for hours on end. Extra tip- they count the whole purchase as one purchase towards your free item- so split each purchase into individual items.  I think Starbucks is moving away from this and will start counting the items on one receipt individually, but they haven't yet.   This particular Starbucks is very small, which is why I am only giving it 4 stars.  The parking lot is hard to maneuver  around in- and it only has a handful of tables. It only has one gender neutral bathroom.   But the staff are fantastic, friendly people. They always get my drink order right. I have seen them handle some really difficult people and always keep their cool. if you like Starbucks this is one to check out- or if you are looking for a coffee shop in the area where you can get some work done, this is a good place for that too.

Horrible Starbucks. I was with my family looking for a place to have a quick bite. They didn't have any of the displayed sandwiches when we asked for them. So, instead we asked for five caramel Frappuccino's. First of all, they tasted like water. The drinks had more ice than anything else. The employees did a horrible job making them. They charged me 30 dollars for drinks that we ended up throwing away. Very disappointing experience.

They had my favorite breakfast sandwich here! The slow roasted ham and Swiss on a croissant! So many Starbucks are sold out of this delicious sandwich but this one always seems to have it! Thanks guys. They also have a drive thru

With a solid view of the parking lot, you too can enjoy a proper espresso or the frappuccino of your choice. For reasons I can't fathom, they are relatively friendly here which sets this location apart from many others. Finally, there is a decent amount of seating inside and a few nice tables outside too!

Stopped by this Starbucks before a trip to the DMV. My order was made perfectly and the girls that worked there were nice. The only problem was the cleanliness. I went later in the day so I understand it being a bit messy but it wouldn't hurt to wipe down your tables and check your bathroom every hour or so.  I did like that this location had ample outside seating, as well as a counter top seating section. Overall the look was very modern. Definitely better than other Starbucks I frequent.

I frequent this Starbucks location often on my way to work. Can I just say, that besides the obviously good coffee, the food is pretty scrumptious as well! I recently tried a piping hot tomato & mozzarella panini that was incredible, along with a satisfying piece of banana bread. My absolute favorite drink is a chai tea latte -milky ,creamy, sweet, with some spice. Yum. Pumpkin spice lattes are up there too :)

This Starbucks location is the gold standard.  I have been to many others in Nashville and in small towns and far bigger cities, and this West End location provides the friendliest and speediest service and most consistent quality. Have I gotten a wrong order once or twice in my history of going there at least 2x/week for years and years... yes, maybe so, but really... what do we expect, fellow Nashvillians, if our vehicles are lined up for a block and half on West End and we get thru the line in ten minutes.

Of all the Starbucks in town, this is my go-to location. It helps that it's the closest location, but really it's about the staff. For the most part everyone that works here is very friendly. I've gotten to know a few of them over the years, and it's nice to go somewhere where you are recognized.   I've gone to various coffee shops around town, and Starbucks is the most consistent in the way it makes its drinks. I just wish the atmosphere was more unique and had more character.

If the drive through didn't take 10 minutes per customer, I wouldn't have the time to get super annoyed enough to write this review. Not sure why there's plenty employees in the building to have the drive through move this slow?? Other then that, friendly staff for the most part. As of now, I've been in the drive through with only 3 cars in front of me for almost 20 minutes. This is ridicules.

If you are using the drive through be prepared to wait in line for long time since this seems to be a very popular drive-through location. And unfortunately this location has really bad and sloooooow service.  I have been inside many times and 90% of the time had to just wait for someone to actually take my order. There are always employees walking around but dis-interested in actually taking an order. They only know what they do walking around. Since I have to wait to get my drink for a long time as well. This location really needs to improve on the service.

First time I have EVER walked into a StarBucks location WITHOUT being greeted or asked if I desired to purchase what woulda coulda been a daily norm. Sadly, to all ALL my social media followers, listeners & viewers ... REROUT to another location where your greeted/acknowledged and where they will gladly take your order. TOTALLY ACCEPTABLE. NOT STARBUCK'S PROTOCOL.
Over 23 min for two drinks. Scorching hot coffee and iced cold hot chocolate. Crew inside is oblivious to what they are doing.

Very poor service. Had to cancel my order and get a refund as first barista did not alert second barista of my order. Waited 15 minutes! Second barista was very rude and had a disrespectful attitude. First barista Alec could not give me management info. I will find on my own and follow up. Avoid this Starbucks at all costs.

This is my favorite Starbucks in Tucson. I am always greeted with a smile and if I see a new item on the menu board the baristas will explain to me what it is. They are very busy here and these baristas will do anything to make your drink perfect. Please be kind when at this Starbucks. I am an older adult and have witnessed customers being nasty. There is a good group here and I appreciate each and every one.

Standard Starbucks. There are not many planes if any at the Summit to get a beverage on a hot or cold day. The Starbucks location is in the middle building of the Summit parking lot. I have gone there many times and have never really had bad service. Honestly, unless you want your drink ready before you walk in the doors, I'm not too sure how you can have bad device there.  Check it out!

Just keeps going downhill.   After this morning and the long wait for just a simple triple shot and rude barista,I will be taking my business to the Lemay location from now on.  I have been a loyal customer since the place opened.  But that does not seem to matter.  Mobile orders seem to take precedence over actual people standing in front of them. Other than amber who is wonderful, they need a better crew.  Nothing but sub par experiences.
Clean, modern, friendly service. This is the best Starbucks location in Philadelphia.

I usually use the drive thru here and the line moves quickly, which I appreciate! The main problem I have is there's not enough space for the drive thru line when it's busy in the mornings. Many times, cars are having to wait out in the road and block traffic before they can turn into the parking lot/drive thru lane. This Starbucks is very spacious on the inside though, so would be great for studying, working, etc. throughout the day.

This is the new Starbucks that just opened on Vista on the end of the vista village shopping center. I almost drove by it but saw that it was open today. I stopped in and got the white chocolate mocha for me and a caramel ribbon crunch frappacinno for the hubby. Great service and same great coffee that I expect from Starbucks! Great location! My mornings just got easier!
Friendly staff and they almost always get the order right.  My only complaint is occasionally the coffee isn't as hot as it should be.
Fast service during non-peak hours. No complaints (or extraordinary compliments either), just what you'd expect from a Starbucks!

This Starbucks has a good location for hitting up the drive thru on the way into town from the East. As others have said, it shares indoor space with an adjoining bank. This doesn't affect anything really, as the people in the bank are quiet. The interior is comfortable and has a good variety of seating, even though it is not overly spacious inside. The drive thru is one of the more efficient and accurate drive thrus at any Starbucks I have been to. The staff is experienced and high quality. Service is quick. Reasons I give 4 instead of 5 stars is the inside being on the smaller side, and the fact that there are too many "regulars" who will hang out here all day every day, consuming space. Also, homeless people have the tendency to wander in and ask for money. Sometimes a cop is there who will tell them to leave.
Slow service. Inconvenient. Nowhere near the quality of a stand alone store.

I'm surprised that during a busy time there is only one cashier. The person before me had an item return and it took such a long time. No one said anything to me while I waited and waited. I come here a lot and just expected to be welcomed and not ignored.   Today Austin made my day! He gave me lots of ideas on how to enhance my drink. Nice! I feel like an important customer and will be back soon. My cascara latte with an extra shot is perfect! Thanks Austin!

excellent amount of sitting room, right in the fancy neighborhood of Montecito, where you never know what celeb you might/will see. They have very friendly staff, and the line moves fairly quickly. I enjoyed their free wifi and ample seating. Perfect for a student/group of students studying for their next big test or for a group who just finished dinner to decompress on their comfy couches and enjoy a nice latte or chai tea. love it!

The clover machine is awesome and the reserve blends are very good.  I am not a coffee drinker but after some of the tastings of the Cameroon I now like some coffee.  Baristas are very nice and patient to help me find the type of coffee I might like.
They are the best they didn't have what I ordered , they replaced it but not only that they send me a cookie with my order. Thanks keep it up.
Very nice baristas extremely friendly and considerate .clean ,always has my special cake
Relatively roomy Starbucks with a fairly decent parking. Nice to see in the neighborhood!

Asked my butter croissant to not be heated..and it wasn't but then I get home and it's still in the plastic packaging... which is good,I guess, because on the packaging it said it was produced on Nov 2...today's Dec. 4.

Why can't they have the inside open to pick up mobile orders at least.  I get the employee shortage reason for closing but this is INSANE to wait in a jammed packed drive thru to simply grab a mobile order.  Many times it's cold by the time the line moves too.  What the heck is going on!!

My favorite one of all but Only 4 stars reason, lately they are taking for EVER to make drinks. May because more business are opening around. A call center for a insurance company was just open. Hope they expand there personal.

This is a convenient location on your way in or out of town from the North. Although the parking lot is a little bit of a nightmare but that's not Starbucks fault. I almost always go inside rather than use the drive thru but on the rare occasion I've used the drive thru it's gone pretty quick.  The inside of the store is nice, the inside seating area is on the small side but they have outdoor seating as well.  The staff are always friendly, chatty and attentive. They seem to enjoy working here and make it pleasant for customers.  My drinks have always been made correctly and quickly.
Wanted a reusable cup but they said out of them On the upside my drink was good and I liked the sprinkles.

Came to this Starbucks and I will definitely be coming back. I frequent Starbucks in different cities vet often. I love that they have a larger patio to sit outside, a drive thru, and a very convenient location to easily get in and out. Ample parking as well. The service could of been quicker on a not too busy Saturday mid-afternoon. But I didn't mind waiting a little bit longer. But my dog thoroughly enjoyed the Puppychino from the barista and meeting new customers that walked by. I will be coming back soon! Great experience here at his location
This Starbucks has it together! Staff is always super friendly, food/drinks always consistent! Drive thru is usually busy but it's worth the wait for a friendly barista and well prepared food/drink.

Well as Comcast did not yet connect Internet to my new Apt. I became Internet-Less person, it is harsh, unbelievable sad reality ! I never thought I can live 2 days without Internet, after long angry calls with Comcast, I started to analysis my situation, looking around me for wifi places I saw this Starbucks, I know other branches in the city but this one open late on Saturday. I went there with my laptop looking for Internet shelter after Comcast turn us to Internet homeless! With my laptop in my arms I entered the shop and I liked the design, yes it is Starbucks should all look the same but this one look more special to me, more detailed with good finishing touches. the Register was friendly and smiley  asked me what I want, I said caramel macchiato for me  and Internet for my "dog" oops my laptop, to my surprise she told me they have sitting upstairs devoted for Internet hungry laptops, yay! here i go, here i come, finally getting my Internet dose and enjoying my caramel macchiato. The place is clean, very quiet and to my surprise empty! Good privacy and no snoopy eyes into your laptop. I do not usually review brand names but this place definitely deserve every word i wrote it!

I come here 2-3x a week. These girls are so damn rude!!!! One of the absolute slowest Starbucks I've ever been to. If I had another option, I'd be allllll over it.  For about $15 every other day, you'd definitely expect way more out of this establishment & brand.

This Starbucks is always super busy but they always do a great job. Today we ordered two Frappuccino's they accidentally made one of the drinks hot but gave us the hot one and made us a cold one as well so we ended up getting three drinks and the one they remade actually was given to us as a venti. All around great job with kind people

I went here on a whim in between classes today. I had a three hour break and went here just because.  The parking lot was the worst part of the whole experience. This was something I wasn't expecting at all. It was such a let down and I'm thankful I had a few hours to spare.   The store itself was cozy and the barista seemed friendly enough. The seating inside seemed cramped yet a little roomier than the parking lot.  I'd go back again if I needed a mid-day pick me up but I'll know what to expect.
By far the best Starbucks in Tampa. They're 24/7 and the staff is very helpful!

The service here fails more often than not. I've submitted a couple of online orders, 3/4 of the time, the pastry is missing, the coffee isn't ready within the estimated time or they haven't even started working on the order until I arrive at the counter.  I waited a total of 12 minutes after my 4-9 minute estimate for a coffee...because 'oops, I guess  I missed the online order'.    The service at the window isn't any better.  "hey, what can I get you?"    Brush up on some customer service skills here, Starbucks.

Hit Reverse & go to the Starbucks in Target!  This is practically impossible for a wheelchair to get through, due to the close high top tables I usually go the Wrong direction & turn into the line there. I think I've seen one chair by the fireplace empty, why are people camping out there??  If you order anything slightly off the menu, they don't handle it well?
The most miserable staff I ever encountered at a Starbucks. If you can't take the pressure. Work somewhere else.

To be fair, I'm not really a Starbucks fan. This is conveniently located near my house, but otherwise, it's really not convenient at all. The line in the mornings for drive-thru service and counter service are both absurd. It's probably not as bad in the afternoon/evenings, but I only "need" a $5 coffee first thing in the morning.  I would much rather drive down the street to J'aime Le Cafe. The options as far as flavor syrups may not be as extensive, but the wait is often much shorter and, IMO, the drinks are better. Plus, it's local.  So, why do I even go to Starbucks? For the most part, I don't. They really got me with this whole 1/2 price Frappuccino happy hour promotion this past week, though. I'll pay $2.50 for a grande frappuccino in the afternoon, and enjoy every sip. Sorry, though, once the promotion is over, so are my days of visiting Starbucks on a regular basis. I can't justify the wait time in the mornings to pay full price for a coffee.
Speedy and friendly service. Excellent customer service and great food! Highly recommend.

A Starbucks is a Starbucks is a Starbucks, right? Wrong!  Sure, you can get your beloved Venti non-fat latte (my drink of choice) and whatever seasonal flavored beverage your heart desires (peppermint mocha, anyone?), but you'll also get service from a group of darn nice folks who care about your coffee experience. Yeah, I could make coffee at home, but then I wouldn't have my favorite barristas smiling faces to get my morning off to a good start.  Parking can be tricky but I can deal with it - 5 Star(bucks) to the Flourtown location!

Cute, cozy Starbucks. I'm only giving it three stars because of the music.  No one comes to a cafe to listen to every song Taylor Swift ever wrote... People come to cafes to write, read, study, think, or talk. The lyrics to "You Belong With Me," "Breathe," "Red," "I Knew You Were Trouble," "Bad Blood," etc. are not conducive to these activities. Some cafes play song selections that are... I wish this location was one of them!

Sad location! Don't u dare drive your full size truck into that compact size parking lot! FAIL!  In a hurry? Drive on by! Does not matter that they have a drive thru, it is just an illusion that you think you can really drive thru ! Super tight, and super slow! No convenience at all! Don't ask for any food item to be hot, as it would serve better as an ice cube in your ice coffee! Summary? Act like it is not even there!

I love this place.  I spend a lot of time here (yes, I drink a lot of coffee and tea). To add a "needs improvement" comment, I think this location would serve its' customers better if they spent more time on cleanliness.        - They REALLY need an employee to address the trash cans, which aren't emptied until they're overflowing.      - They REALLY need an employee to walk the floor with a dust mop, picking up paper scraps, dropped napkins, and the like.      - They REALLY need an employee to wipe down each table.      - They REALLY need an employee to address the restrooms (refilling tissue, seat covers, soap) and sweeping the floor. The manager is very nonchalant and appears to not notice these problems.  In a place with such a revolving door of customers all day every day, cleanliness should be priority one.  Add the recent virus outbreaks in the news, and I'd say sanitizers should be everywhere. If I were managing, the above items would be addressed 2-3 times a day, between rushes. I still love them though.
The workers were rude, stuck up, unfriendly, and indifferent and the drinks they made weren't very good. Although the manager did give me a free drink. I won't be back.

I've been to several Starbucks across the U.S. but I must say this one was not very clean, had no real pastry selections and thank goodness the drinks were pretty simple avoiding a bad experience with the major aspect of why I go to Starbucks.  Maybe I just went at the wrong time.  Maybe next time it will be a better experience.

Dave and Danielle at this location are the best!!! I went for my free birthday drink.  Both told me Happy Birthday as soon as they saw it on my app. Of course I ordered the venti with no ice using one of the hacks I read.  Both were still super nice. Danielle gave me a cup of ice too. She told me lots of people do the same thing!   The drink was really good. It was a little sweet for me but once some ice melted it was better. It was refreshing on a hot humid day.  It was my first time at Starbucks since the pandemic started.  The store was clean. Staff wore gloves and masks. No seating is available. When I was done, the counter was sanitized.   I would definitely go back to this location!!

I find it unsettling to be denied the use of the restroom without making a purchase. I understand all the arguable points on both sides. Natalie was the associate. I get her commitment to upholding policy. This reflects on management in my humble opinion. I generally stick to positive reviews. You guys are not playing for good reviews anyway
Leonard, our barista, was awesome! Great service, big seating area and clean restrooms. This has plenty of parking off of De La Vina.

This place is 3 Yelp Stars (A-OK). This isn't a bad thing. It's just a normal starbucks. It's what you'd expect. Its nothing special. This is what people expect from starbucks. Coffee, seating, and free bathroom. There is another starbucks in the supermarket so not quite sure why this one exists. but it does. and here we are.   The staff is nice. The drinks are starbucks. This review is over.

Okay I go here all the time because it is my closest location, however this Starbucks needs like a complete re-haul. It's disgusting. I don't really enjoy sitting there because every surface is worn down and sticky- the chairs, the tables, the window ledges. Everything. It is just beat and I don't know why Starbucks won't update it. They need all new furniture too the couches and chairs are gross and beat and peeling. I also feel like there is a huge staff turnover. I used to come in here and would see the same people now I just keep seeing more and more new faces. Their coffee is good yes, pricey, but that's a Starbucks problem not a location specific one. But it's good. This location is hit or miss with items in the case as well as how fast they sell out of drinks. Also don't ever go here on a Friday night unless you want to be a part of a pre-teen group hang.

This Starbucks is a little difficult to get into due to the small and busy parking lot. It also does not have a drive-thru. However, they are always very fast and efficient on the inside. We have never had service issues at this location. I typically order an Iced Vanilla Chai and it comes out perfectly mixed (it can be grainy if done incorrectly) and delicious every time.

This used to be a nice starbucks. Just a few months ago there was a different manager and different staff. Now it's terrible. The customer service is awful and on Saturday the coffee was so bad I gave it back twice and then asked for a refund. No one likes burnt coffee that's been sitting in the carafe for hours and hours. If you brew new coffee into the old it's still going to taste bad!!!!!!!!!

19th Nov at 2:43 pm. The crew attending drive thru left a bit to be desired? I ordered a doppio with a little cream and was handed a cold tall cup filled to the brim. When I complained there was the usual sort of  we can make it again for you this is so beside the point if you're working at a cafe get a clue what an espresso shot means it cannot be a tall cup filled to the brim it's not a question of making it again it's a question of making it right the first time!

3 stars for the quality and the service-  Here's another review of a Starbucks. Since it's a chain the quality always seems to be the exact same and like a lot of Starbucks this one has pretty decent service. They also have a large selection of bagged coffee, coffee/tea mugs to purchase, and delectable food in the case. The bad news is that it's in the mall so it's usually really busy.
They're a little on the slow side, but the service was friendly. They don't have very long hours, they close sometime in the early afternoon. They make a decent tea.

Just went there and staff was extremely sweet. My family and I had Joy at the drive thru window. They actually covered our drinks, thanking us for being patient. Funny thing is, their service was EXTREMELY fast and no patience was needed. Thank you to Joy and the rest of their amazing staff for such a sweet gesture and clearly being so caring to their customers. We love you!

If could give this Starbucks no stars I would.  I have been on various occasions and have not been pleased.  They have been out of items, felt like I was bothering them and take forever to fill an order once it is placed.  Tonight I pulled up behind a car that had just gotten to the drive thru.  About 3 minutes later they had still not gotten their order taken.  They weren't busy.  I finally went around and left.  They need to get trained by the Starbucks at 44 & 141, where they are quick, friendly and efficient.
The staff is great friendly  I love getting my coffee from here always ready on time when I order from mobile always generous about my orders love the staff.
Tiny place inside the casino with a beautiful interior and ceiling. Employees are so friendly.
I was very amazed the coffee and services was the best my coffee was fixed just the way I liked it. 5 stars or better today

This location is only a few blocks away and a straight shot on my way to work in the morning, and yet, I will be going out of my way to find an alternative from now on.  For the third visit in a row my drink has been made incorrectly. This time (see pic) the "venti" had 2 inches of empty space before you hit coffee. And no, it didn't have whip cream that melted.  Sure, complaining about complex, gourmet coffee being mixed incorrectly is probably going to come across a little.. high maintenance. But here's the truth: if I'm going to pay $5 every morning for something I could essentially make at home, it had better be consistent and well made. Starbucks ALWAYS used to hold to a very high standard, repeat your order, spot-on every time. Lately, none of those things are happening. And at this point, it simply doesn't make any sense to go there every morning, and drop cash to get... whatever the barista chooses to make me.

It's a sad day when a Starbucks has bad customer service. Her name is Jo and you'd think with a name like that she'd understand mix ups between Erin and Aaron but she wanted to blame me for handing me my sausage egg sandwich and nitro cold brew. The receipt was a mobile order and I am Aaron, then after her coworkers asked her she tried to blame me EVEN MAKING ME GO TO THE TRASH FOR THE RECEIPT. Yes, I had to go into the trash to get my receipt. Never again Jo, I pray management sees this and other poor reviews and decides to do something about it.

In my experience the last several months since moving to Metairie, this location is very hit or miss. They're either awesome or awful depending on the day but their drive thru has become extremely slow these last few weeks no matter what time of day. I understand rushes which is why I don't mind waiting in line 20 minutes because I know they're doing their best. But when you wait 20+ minutes and your drink comes out wrong twice with a line backed up to the street behind you (which has happened several times), well I'd rather just go to any of the other locations on Veterans. Shame because this one is 2 minutes from my house.

Have been coming here a few times a week for the past two years. It's Starbucks, it's the only one close to the hospitals and such here on S Grand so it's a popular spot. They do get busy but it's always service with a smile, they have been hiring some new great baristas recently as well!  Always enjoy time spent here.

After my fourth visit at this Starbucks, I'm done. The baristas are always grumpy and act like your inconveniencing them. I chalked it up to maybe just being a rough or busy day, as they are human too and shouldn't have to always be peppy. However, this isn't the case and my experiences have always been unpleasant.

The service is lackluster, slow, and just not good.  In a prime place like this should be doing so much more.  It seems like this place is on the decline at least with the people running the place.    The employees are more interested in serving their own coffee rather than serving customers.  I come in and they take my order, the guy hollers the order and the barista states "I got it", then she proceeds to make herself a drink and take a break.  I others are just standing around and having a conversation with each other.  My drink and others were just waiting to be made, so I had to ask them to make my drink.  Then they give me a look like I had the audacity to ask for the drink that I actually paid for.  This place is terrible.
Great customer service. The drinks are always good. I'm Usually in an out the drive thru pretty fast besides during the peek hours. Will definitely go here again.

The staff here are always super friendly. It can be quite busy at times, but you never have to wait too long to get your order. It's quite a big store with adequate seating. It's one of my favorite Starbuck's locations.

This location is very clean, however, the coffee was not good. I typically visit Starbucks (other locations) at least once a day and I'm very familiar with the flavor of their Pike Place coffee. The coffee I purchased here was so weak I couldn't even drink it. It was just not good.

My husband and I go there on a weekly basis and they know my husband...as soon as he walks in the door, they start making his coffee!  When he first went in, he told them his name was Jerk, and they didn't know what to make of him, but now they know him...they are the best!  Service is great, location is perfect, coffee is perfect!  Thanks Starbucks!
I come here regularly due to location and staff. I have to sit outside because this is one of the coldest Starbucks ever. I get the corporate controls the AC but it gets ridiculous!

Busy and central location makes this a coffee stop and social event.   This location is open longer than the close-by Peets' or the other Starbucks, in the La Cumbre mall across the street. The staff always keeps a good pace and sets a high standard for the slower Starbucks.

I absolutely hate going to this Starbucks, but I go occasionally because it's on my university campus. It is literally awful. The line is always very long. Then, when you get to the register they are ALWAYS out of everything. The staff is so rude!!! Today when I went, they were out of all alternative options of milk, which I am allergic to. They did not have almond, coconut or soy milk. This is a terrible place!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I visited this location Friday morning 2/28/20 at approximately 8:30 am. I ordered a sausage and egg biscuit i also had ordered a drink i heard everyone was trying. Which was a "Cookies and Cream frappe" so i got my items got to work. Then i realized i wasn't given the drink i ordered i was given a "Chocolate Chip frappe" which was not what i asked for. Not sure if my order wasn't entered properly because of the line. But that shouldn't matter i should've received what i ordered now. I'm upset because i can't enjoy a drink i was looking forward to trying!
Always love to visit this location.  They are always so helpful and accommodating.  They never seems to disappoint.  This is probably my favorite Starbuck locations in Tucson!!!

Gotta say... I've been in this Starbucks for no more than 15 min and I find it filled with very friendly staff. Leana gave me a free double shot of a white bean/white chocolate frap. Little barstool area at the barista counter makes it a very comfortable replacement of drinking with a bartender!

Baristas work hard as this location is always SLAMMED with customers. I used to be able to put a mobile order in and count on it being ready when I got there. I don't know what happened in the last few weeks but every time I have put a mobile order in I've had to wait up to 20 minutes in the lobby for my order. So I'm taking a break from Starbucks for awhile. It's really disappointing.

I watched in horror as the girl preparing my food was eating as she's putting it into the oven.  While its in there she takes the last bite of her food, licks her fingers, and then goes back to touching food coming out of the oven without washing her hands.  Then some guy is sauntering back and forth behind the counter, no apron, not working, wearing flipflops drinking his Refresher and eating while talking to the employees. The place was a mess despite there seeming to be an overabundance of staff.  Very unprofessional and not what I've come to expect of the Starbucks brand.  The sad thing is this is normally the case at this particular store based on my personal experience and all the other reviews.  I'm going to head to Coffe Bean which is only 1/2 mi away in the future.
This is a cute Starbucks tucked away within the Willow Grove Mall. There is plenty of seating, and it's right next to Auntie Anne's! The service was excellent, and everything was clean.

Starbucks is one of my favorite places to go. Usually I go to the Smyrna location which is rarely a bad experience. I have tried the Mt Juliet location at least 5 or 6 times now and it's a bad every time.. so today I ordered a "venti caramel cloud macchiato"...  she never told me my total.. and when I look over to my fiancé handing me my coffee I got a "tall plain caramel macchiato".. didn't get a receipt or anything. Not happy. I would have been more understanding if they were busy..but we were literally the only people there.

First time in this WC location! So happy it finally opened, my boyfriend lives 5 minutes down the road so it's convenient for all the times I bug him for a coffee run! This Starbucks is huge, clean, spacious and has lots of outlets unlike other Starbucks! The workers were working very hard and still remained friendly :) I enjoyed my visit here and will be back soon

We pulled in the drive-thru line and the line was not moving for a solid 5 minute. We parked and went in but waited 15 minutes for our drinks and 7 minutes more for simple egg bites. The people were very nice but awful service, and the cold foam on my drink was not made properly.

They are always closed! No reason just hit or miss if they are closed. It's 1045 am and cones in front of the drive thru and they are locked. This is the 10th time I've been so irritated that they decide to close shop. Get new managers!
It's Starbucks, you all know and love it. Great seasonal drinks, location is friendly and speedy, for your average cup of Joe this is the place.
Great service and lots of smiling friendly baristas! This is my favorite Starbucks in town!

Ladies and Gents, this Starbucks just recently relocated from their Lithia Pinecrest Road and Bloomingdale Avenue location. It's now directly ON Bloomingdale Avenue, with Chick Fil-A, Wal Mart Supercenter and Bloomingdale Regional Library being nearby.  This new Starbucks is large and has a drive-thru, which I sometimes really appreciate when in a hurry, like I was this morning.   Service was friendly and quick. Definitely a plus was that my drink was correct! Looking forward to this new Starbucks!   9/100

I've got to update this review to five stars. This Starbucks, and the people working at it, are truly awesome.   I can now legitimately call myself a regular (much to my wallet's chagrin), and I've had a chance to see the staff in action on a daily basis. They are extremely friendly, know their customers by name, remember drink orders and have them ready by the time you've made it to the register, etc. -- all the customer service stuff you expect from the best.

Short line on a Friday evening and they have my favorite in stock! Trento sized strawberry acai with coconut milk. Lots of places have been running out with the new trend so I'm glad they have it it cool me off on a hot day in St. Louis! This store also has snacks and cups and gifts to pick up. I collect the you are here mugs and they have the St. Louis one in stock for under $12.

Went there on a Sunday at 7:10pm to order three drinks. There was no one else in the building or drive thru. We walked in and there were five employees. The lobby was dirty, tables dirty and the bathroom was gross. The lady that greeted us took our order and while the rest stood around one employee made the drinks. All the drinks were incorrect and the taste of them was not right. The reason I chose not to go back was because there was one of the girls continuously cussing in front of my daughter while we were waiting. Not one employee reminded her that their were customers in the building. I have been in customer service for years and this was not a pleasant experience. For a company that prides themselves in customer service it definitely didn't show on Sunday.

Super busy location with challenging parking.  Decor has been upgraded over the past year.   Still not enough seats for the demand.   A bit slow at times, but the usual fare at the usual inflated prices.  Not your traditional WIFI here.   Had issues logging on.
Always friendly, mostly timely, coffee pretty consistent. I enjoy going here for a daily latte.

This place had one single person working all alone taking orders and then making them. She was overwhelmed and being pestered by guests the whole time. I went to give her a tip for our drinks and she told me she isn't allowed to accept them. Why not? Hire more people or at least let them take tips they're basically doing 3 jobs for the price of one.

Yes, I hate to admit it but these baristas here- Sure know my order by now. Living from 1600 Walnut to S. Philly, I still come back every night to order my Paleo Version'd Coffee Black with a Stick of Butter. They get it right every time. Why wouldn't they? Many people look at me like I'm nuts but this Starbucks makes me feel at home. My location at best! Great workers, in fact I'm friends with a few now! Drinks expensive? No way. Same price as DD. I'm such a fan of this Starbucks!!
Really awesome location. Not real hard to find parking, and building has kind of an industrial chic flavor. Employees weren't real friendly but I'd def come here again.

I've never written a review before but my experience at a drive through in this location was EXCELLENT! the representative YADISHA was super helpful and even explain throughly the details of how to order the drink my husband and I enjoy to ensure I don't have any question the next time.  Thank you Yadisha!!

I was really excited that they FINALLY built a Starbucks for the Jersey to Philly commuters down route 38. For years driving to high school and now medical school, there was only a gross Dunkin donuts. I stopped here this AM and was pleased with this Starbucks. It has a drive through for the days you feel really lazy. The interior is cozy, with a few high tables, couches, and a gorgeous long wood table, perfect for communal studying. The wait for my drink was longer than I had expected, but that was due to the flurry of drive through orders they were filling. Next time I will order ahead on my app! For the location and interior of the place, 4 starts is warranted!

This place is good for lounging around and studying while sipping on coffee or whatever your favorite Starbucks concoction may be.  Drinks are what you expect from Starbucks: overpriced but made to order.  The downside to this place is they play music more loudly than I can remember any other Starbucks playing theirs.  Also, the staff is usually friendly, but the manager always seems to have a stick up her butt.  I always see her annoyed at someone or something and she does not hide it well.    All in all, not a bad place to hang out and talk or study.

Typical Starbucks. Good coffee lousy food. Staff was a little more friendly than most Starbucks but I couldn't help feeling I was in a corporate conglomerate. Place was clean overall. Will be back if I'm needing an afternoon jolt.
I love Safety Harbor and this Starbuck's is right in the center of everything. The baristas were super friendly and my skinny mocha late was just right.
This Starbucks is amazing. Shout out to Austin, Emily, Kathy & Kayley for making my experience every morning nothing short of flawless. You guys rock!

I'm usually not a Starbucks fan, except for the very occasional dessert drink where the lower quality beans are masked in a swirl of sugar, milk and flavorings! (salted caramel mocha, pumpkin spice latte, java chip frappe.. mm)    But this location has got to be one of the best designed ones I've seen! A huge breath of fresh air compared to the small cookie cutter Starbucks stores littered all over NYC! The ambiance is warm and cozy, and has the feel of and eco-conscious cafe. (Unfortunately the temperature is not so warm, brrr... its freeeezzy up in here! It's not so bad early am, but dang wear your thickest sweater and fuzzy socks if you plan on staying)   The girls that work here are super friendly and sweet as well. The last time they wrote my name down as Angel even though she clearly heard "Angela" and saw it on my card.. lol.  I really like all the wooden decor and large table in here. I could imagine that if I had gone to school in SB, this would've been one of my prime study spots. They have plenty of outlets scattered about the store, and hidden under the long table so you never have to claw and fight your way to one like at some other spots.   They have some cushier chairs and sofas too that provide an ideal spot to gather with friends to catch up, read a book or the paper.   If its a nice day outside and you don't want to be subjected to the frigid temps in here, there is plenty of seating and tables right outside.   Lots of parking and even during peak hours in the morning the service is still quick and the space is large enough that it doesn't feel crowded or cramped.
Super friendly , awesome consistency with making my coffee! Peaceful good vibe place!

3 stars because the service here is dismal at best. I've been to this location more than 10 times because 8th very close to my apartment but if I have the time I head west 6 blocks to the next closest location.   No matter what you order or how many people are in front of you you will wait at least 5 minutes for a very simple drink (ie black coffee) and if it's busy I've waited up to 15 minutes. Normally I'd walk out but when I'm craving iced coffee magic the more hip local establishments fail miserably every time.  The only saving grace to this location is the upstairs area, which is bright, with a good view of South St for people wqtching, and has a lot of seating and tables to read relax or do work.

I'll give at least 2 stars to any place that caters to vegans.  I'm not crazy on the service at this location, but it happens to be closest to me.  As for Starbucks as a whole, I am thankful for the "use your gift card for purchases and we won't charge you $1.20 to get your latte with soy and a flavor shot for the rest of the year after your 5th beverage" deal.  Comments on parking/drive thru - it get's INSANE here at times, it is an incredibly small lot and if the drive thru is backed up you just have to wait it out, there is no passing the line of cars to leave the establishment.

The customer service is at it's finest at this Starbucks!  The manager, Paul, is one of the kindest people that I have met in Philadelphia.  His service goes above and beyond.  You definitely leave with a smile!

I absolutely LOVE Starbucks! I do think it is a tad pricey, but it's quality and I love it! I really am not a fan of coffee, necessarily, but I love their teas, pastries and sandwhiches! They also have juices, milks, fruit, and gift items sold at every location!  I also love the feel when you walk in. It's really calm and welcoming with the couches, tables, chairs and free internet access. It's definately a perfect place to go have your favorite coffee while cramming for a big test!

Every time I come to this Starbucks they always screw my order up and the funny thing is I'm there daily.   My boyfriend goes in as well but they happen to always give it to him for free.  I always order no whip somehow they always put whip. This last time they put soy milk and whip. There employees need to start repeating orders to there customers.  Drive through is always busy around 7am-10pm so that could be why. I see it's a great place for meetings due to the business area

I've been here a few times and I keep forgetting to review them. I have never had an issue with this Starbucks. The staff are always nice and cheerful. I am somewhat particular about the drink I order and they never roll their eyes or make me feel like a "difficult customer".   Today, service was fast and easy with virtually no wait.   I wish I could review Starbucks as an whole company because my experience, no matter which store I go to, is always pretty great.

I've been a loyal customer to Starbucks for years and never been so dissapointed. For the last 2 days I've had bad experiences with 2 employees. The guys are always extremely friendly but most of the girls are rude, helpless, and don't have customer service skills. I ask for a bag and I'm expected to bag my own purchase. My drinks are made and just set on the counter by the barrista without her calling them out when there's not that many customers. The only reason why I gave 2 stars and not 1 is because there are some employees who know what they are doing. Overall unnecessary experiences.
Slowest Starbucks drive-thru in America. If you're in a hurry and need your coffee fix you should definitely go elsewhere.

I come here because it's the closest thing I got to my work place at the moment. It does the job, you know what to expect because it's Starbucks.  However, I don't think this is one of the better locations.  I mean, I've been to busier locations with fewer staff that move the drinks faster, and I'm taking into account the drive thru line too. The staff are generally very nice and accommodating, but not so much when they are busy, that leads to grouchiness and sometimes rudeness. This location has very nice indoor seating and the perk is a drive thru, but I go inside more often.  Downside, slow in delivering drinks, even when it's a downtime, and they've also gotten my drink wrong on several occasions.
many peaple just come use the wifi end didn't buy any thats crazy stupid.they ocuped nice seat n buy nothing .

Place won't give change to a paying customer. Ridiculous, and the coffee is garbage.   I bought 6 drinks here with the family, after finishing I went back up to the counter and asked them to break a 20 and was told they wouldn't break a $20. I rarely complain about a place, but this place is terrible.  After speaking to a manager and reminding them I paid for 6 drinks they gave me change. I will increase my rating by 1 star.

I'm not too sure of her name was Justine or Jessie she was the manager on duty but anyway she was absolutely rude and I'm sure she's a racist , not sure what's up with Starbucks hiring nazis but it's not cool . You just lost a long time Customer

Okay, lets start with the cleanliness of the store.   There is trash overflowing the trash receptacles, there is trash on the floor, the tables are dirty, the area where the employees are preparing the drinks and food looks like a bomb went off.  Now on to service where, I could allow the cleanliness of the store to slide a little if the service had been outstanding.  Sadly it was not.   For some unknown reason this Starbucks has decided that the person working the register should also be the person preparing the order.  Note to management,"THIS DOES NOT WORK!" Just like ALL the other Starbucks I have been to around the world, your register people take the orders and keep your line moving.   Having people stand in line waiting endlessly for someone to take their order pisses people off more than people having to wait for their orders.  Let's move on to the Special giveaway today - When asked about the Reusable Cup giveaway Dustin (WHO IS A STAND OUT AT CUSTOMER SERVICE) asked the harpy who appeared to be the manager about the promotion. Her snippy response to him was We're out. Now I could see if I cam into the store at 1-2 in the afternoon and asked about getting a promotional item but when you get to the store at 10am the expectation is that they should have had sufficient stock on hand to accommodate at least the folks who are in the store early.  Shame on you!  Get it together people, this is no way to run a Starbucks.   Wanna see how it's done?   Go to Justison Landing store.

Usually the service they provide at this Starbucks location is great. However, the past 2 times that i used my Starbucks app and ordered my food and drink ahead of time so that i could just run in and pick it up, (because im running late for school) they didn't even check to see if they had any orders so it wasn't ready for me at all. Not cool guys! So be careful if you're using the app.

I'm a former barista, though for a different Starbucks, so I tend to judge them a bit strictly. This one makes the drinks correctly and quickly. I'm not always thrilled with the customer service as sometimes they can be a bit rude. But most of them are friendly. I'll go back knowing that my very specific and complicated drink will be made right. For a busy Starbucks, they do very well and have their jobs down pat.
The worst customer service ever. Go to another Starbucks if you can. This is the second time I received attitude from the employees for absolutely no reason.

Went through the drive thru and employee Drea was the absolute most rude employee I have ever met from Starbucks. I go to that Starbucks almost everyday and have never been disappointed till now. Never have I ever had a person rip my debit card from my hand and she slammed the drive thru window open and close and said nothing but "HERE YOU GO".
