Makes me never want to go back to Starbucks off sunset hills THANKS DREA.

I've been going to Starbucks for a long long time. I even went to the original in Seattle just because I could. Tonight, I found my ideal treat!  The store was mostly empty near closing time I suspect so we had a relaxed time chatting with the barista. My wonderful wife ordered a chili mocha with whipped cream. As I watched her make it, my my mind was thinking "Mmmmm. Whipped cream. Good. Must have some now."  Never being one to resist an impulse, I asked "Could I have a small cup of whipped cream?" She said, "Sure, you can order it any time! It's called a puppaccino!"   I was kinda stunned having never heard this ersatz-Italian-word before. She explained, people order this for their puppy! Not really being a dog-person, I said "I'm going to call it a Daveaccino instead!"  Mmmmm Whipped cream Good

Who doesn't love coffee??? We visited today because it was the first day of the "holiday" seasonal drinks and I LOvE the peppermint mocha & couldn't wait to get my hands on one! Then, to my surprise they had free reusable holiday cups today! This location has a drive through and is right off of Eastlake road making it the perfect pit stop to get your energy fix. The drive through had a long line but, the coffee was totally worth the wait!  The seasonal offerings provided by the baristas at Starbucks help to keep things new and exciting. Just when you think, how sad no more pumpkin spice lattes.... bam peppermint mochas and toasted whites chocolate lattes!! They also have delicious seasonal cookies with this amazing icing that my kids just can't get enough of!
These baristas/managers deserve a raise! They are on their sh*t!! Service and drinks are consistent every time!

Everytime i come to this location, Staff are extremely rude. There is no courtesy. Its like a  like a bar.  the only sound I hear in this coffee shop are the employees constantly talking to each other. So loud! Nonstop loud talking amongst each other! Very unprofessional.   I go to starbucks everyday and this is by far the worst experience. Had to post after several bad experience at the same store.

Slowest, most non-customer service oriented Starbucks I've ever visited.  Only 1 person was making drinks and the priority seemed to be the drive through lane.  This is the most inefficient process/approach I've ever seen in a Starbucks.  I visited this location on multiple days and different times during the day, always to find the same inefficient service.  During my afternoon visits, the personnel were definitely not focussed on getting folks served and out the door...unbelievably slow!

Happen to come across this location for the first time  and was beyond impressed! The baristas were awesome - so friendly and very fun. Who doesn't want a good laugh with a side of delicious drinks. :)

Service was fine and the Starbucks quality was the same, but those airport prices are crazy as usual. When you order a bagel, they give you a knife and a small pouch of cream cheese that's barely enough for one side of the bagel. It's also a $1 up charge when you inevitably ask for another one...
Never been here before today but they were very nice. Just a little slow and there was no drive thru otherwise they'd have gotten a 5-star for the java chip I redeemed with my points!

Extraordinary slow. I counted 7 people working and only one person was making drinks. They were having a great time laughing and talking to each other while ignoring the customers in line. It took 15 minutes from the time I ordered for them to make a latte.

This Starbucks is in a prime location--complete with a shared outdoor courtyard and some good, adjacent restaurants. The inside is also very spacious and welcoming. I always go here assuming it's a great place to study and have a delicious cup of coffee. On both counts it misses the mark. Not a good Starbucks but also not a bad Starbucks.  The cleanliness is my main issue. I thought all Starbucks tried to maintain a well-kept environment for their customers? I figured this was standard procedure. Somehow this place is just dirty and unkept every single time I visit. The bathroom was so disgusting I literally walked in and walked out. This is really unacceptable, in my opinion. There are much better-kept local coffee shops right across the street (The French Press) that are crowded but manage to keep their space spotless.   As for the coffee, the blended and espresso drinks are all overly-sweet. I have sometimes not been able to finish them because of this. On the bright side, they have a Clover Machine (for the black coffee lovers out there)! You can get a personally-crafted cup of any of their standard or reserve beans. This is something I highly recommend!   Lastly, the employees are all really friendly! They always ask about my day and make a point to be cordial, even when they are swamped with orders.
The most convenient Starbucks on campus. You'll have to try to avoid the crowds though.
Every day I go for coffee before work. No complaints. Faster to walk in than use the drive thru
This is one of my most favorite Starbucks, as these ladies work hard with care & fast! They are just lovely!

My usual Starbucks is the greyhound pass location which I love, but I used the ATM outside of this location and decided to pop in quickly for an iced espresso. My drink is super simple and quick so I'm typically in and out.   I walked into this location and waited behind two women who were together. There was a barista making drinks and a cashier. The two women in front of me who were ordering clearly knew/were friends with the cashier. The cashier was letting them sample cold brew, discussing food, going over literally the whole menu, etc. I was never acknowledged as someone waiting to order. The barista kept looking at me like she was feeling my pain or something. I was in a rush and I kid you not - I waited behind these ladies talking to their cashier friend for fifteen entire minutes. I almost walked out several times but didn't have the guts because it would've been so awkward. The ladies kept walking back and forth to the food display and just "couldn't decide". And just chatted away with the chatty Kathy cashier. Like... I HATE stereotypes. Especially the Carmel one. But wow. This was the first time since moving here that I over and over again thought how much these ladies fit that stereotype. Rich older women taking their sweet time with no care about anyone else. It was disappointing. Anyway - when it was finally my turn, I expected a "sorry for your wait" comment of some sort. Or just some genuine friendliness since she was SO overly friendly to her friends. LOL nope. The cashier acted annoyed that I killed her friend time. At work. In those fifteen minutes the line behind me drastically grew and there were several waiting. It was just an awkward experience. Noooooot a fan.

I love Starbucks.  I like this location, but it can be hard to get in and out of sometimes.  Especially if the traffic is congested.    The crew is always friendly. My order is always right and hits the spot.

This location has been flaky for the past few months. For a number of weeks this past summer the opening hours shifted unpredictably, and for the past couple of weeks it's become the slowest Starbucks in the area. Whatever the reasons are, they're not prepared for morning rush times.

So happy that there was a great Starbucks drive-thru to hit when I was on my way to try the delicious Shapiro's Deli. The Circle Tour lady got a big Starbucks drink as part of her tip; I finally got a nice strong cup of java, as opposed to the muddy water that passes for coffee at some hotels.  This was an expansive Starbucks and not cramped at all. A decent parking lot and generous interior. I am so used to that terrible confining feeling that most California Starbucks suffer from. Not here.  Nice friendly employees also. However, 1 YELP STAR deducted for a very nasty bathroom experience. One thing you can generally count on, in addition to the fabulous coffee, is a decent potty. So surprised to see that this bathroom here was in a total state of disaster: over-flowing trash, paper towels scattered all over the floor, and NO toilet paper at all. But that's what seat covers are for in emergencies. I pointed this out nicely to the barista when I got a refill and he promptly mentioned it to the manager so I'm sure the bad bathroom was attended to.  But Starbucks needs to realize a good clean bathroom is a symbol of how the brand is perceived in addition to their high end coffee and convenient drive-thru's. It should not take some out-of-state tourist like me to point out the TP is gone and the trash cans are full in the Starbucks throne room.

It would be great to have the hours posted!  I am frequently traveling through this area in the evening.  The Starbuck's is conveniently located just off the highway.  They used to be open until 11:00 pm, but the last few times I have tried to stop by at night, they have either been closed or in the process of closing.  It's a nice little shop and a great location. Please post your hours, so that travelers can determine whether or not to exit here!
I go to this location when I am in the 109 Street area. I wish it was a bit larger as it is difficult to find a place to sit when it is busy.

This location is medium-paced, which is perfect, as many Starbucks can get overwhelming with all the customers. Of the four visits I have paid them recently, three have resulted in an incorrect order. They were very small mistakes that shouldn't bother anyone, but 25% success rate should be noted. All of my visits have been inside and "for here" orders. No drive thru, no take-aways. The important factor is that the mistakes were always promptly corrected, and usually with some form of reimbursement. The staff is fantastic, friendly, and fast.
The service was terrible here I waited 10 minutes for a coffee and.....it was wrong, such a disgusting disappointment. I know this isn't the best coffee, but it could at least be consistent

Pro's: Great service Clean Plenty of outlets Large table for studying Familiar faces at Starbucks whenever I'm three  Their generous with using rewards Quick drive thru, one girl I had was very cheery - def liked that since she was the first person I saw that day  No smoking in the outdoor area Good volume of the music and selection of music, kinda helps drown out conversations  Con's: Slow internet when it's busy, I've only had trouble a few times Can get packed sometimes and there is no room to sit, but that's a good thing for Starbucks  This is my favorite Starbucks in town. Great place to congregate with friends and study partners. Never been disappointed with my orders.

This Starbucks location is located inside of Target just walk inside in to Target look left you'll see Starbucks in plain sight. This location what all other Starbucks locations offer to the customer such as Coffee, Teas and etc. Some seating keep that in mind. The staff are nice and friendly.  Over all experience is great!

Seriously worst Starbucks experience so far. Ordered a pour over, the male cashier didn't have proper change, 3 other employees just standing there... I asked if someone else could start the pour over while he saw to the change. I was given just a regular coffee being told it was a blonde... this is ten minutes in. Then I have to wait for 1/2 and 1/2. 15 minutes in no change yet OLD COFFEE THAT turned grey when adding the cream. My son comes in to look for me because It has been so long. I tell them the coffee is old, I get told it's not, bologna, smelled old, looked old... none of them looked like they were going to do anything but let me leave with old coffee, I asked for the originals nail order of a pour over... but from how slow it had already been and how long it had already taken I asked for my money back. Girls leaning against the register... waiting for change for ten minutes! Manager aloof and childishly laughing about it with employees.  Not good Starbucks! No wonder at all why this is rated a TWO!!!

Renovation is a positive , my only negative is that there is always a long line during peak times and there is no urgency of the team that people have things to do. Moving at a glacial pace. I've been to many SB in NYC and Phila and they run like a German train. Not sure what the issue is ?
Always friendly staff. A few of the baristas have been here as long as I have lived here which is now 5.5 years! Love the drive-thru!

consistently the slowest service and lackadaisical staff of any starbucks. even when a line of 5-8 people are present they did not open a second register. strangely  there were at least 5 people behind the counter

Would Gladly Give 5 stars! Great Barista's Here,On my last several visits ALL my special orders have been ON-POIINT! I'm Always glad to wait for an order that's being made correctly. JUST DONT WANT TO JINX THEM lol! LOL!

It takes a lot for me to get mad about customer service, because I work in it and I understand how hard it is sometimes. But If you're working the cash register, get off your phone and pay attention to the customer trying to place an order. When I walked up he didn't even look up to acknowledge me and instead of placing my order I had to stand there and wait a solid minute while he was texting away. Also complaining about other employees in front of customers- not professional at all. Coffee was good though. But this location needs to work on customer service.

I'm here just about every weekend to get a caffeine fix before adoption events across the street at Petco.  They get my order right almost every time but this parking lot stinks.  I love the convenience of a drive through but it's very easy to get stuck in a traffic jam when you're trying to leave.

Very convenient location in Town & Country, so this place gets very busy since early morning. Nice interior remodeling, new look but not so comfortable furniture, who knows why..? Some staff members are lacking basic knowledge about standard gift card activation procedures, with an attitude against customer, as it happened in my case. The good part is that there are so many other stores to go to get the gift cards. No big deal.

Do you want to wait in line only to find out most of the drinks on the me u are out of stock and that being the case everyday then this Starbucks is the choice for you. There is only one person that works there hat is polite and has work in well run establishment before. Everyone else it's as if this was their first job at the age of 35. I'm sorry but this place needs new management. You've lost my business to Saxbys

This Starbucks opened last year during the pandemic. I'm not sure if it was intended to shut down the one near AMC Highwoods and transfer the staff to this more spacious location before covid or it was  done after.  This location is located opposite the main entrance to Hunter's Green and is in a plaza with Sprouts, and other businesses like yogasix, and Grain and Berry, with First Watch, and Fresh Kitchen imminent.  All employees were wearing masks and there are social distance markers on the ground which customers were mostly following.  I came here twice to try the new Oat Milk Brown Sugar Shaken Espresso. I'm surprised  to find that it wasn't as super sweet and the brown sugar /oat taste was relatively subtle. A grande was $5.92 which is usually a venti drink price which is annoying. Even with the high price, crushed ice 2/3 of the volume.    3.5/5  Pros:  Compared to the one at AMC, this one has more space inside and a small outside space. Though interior dining is disabled due to COVID for now.  There is a drive thru available.  Since it is in not in the middle of a Y, the parking is more convenient.   Cons BS Oat milk price  In terms of just distance, the SB near the VA is closer to me, but that one is on an incline and the parking is more narrow. This one will probably be my main  one.

The Nugget gave me $10 credit for the starbucks in the lobby. Now I know why. It's freezing out and a nice hot cup of coffee sounds good. I order a salted caramel mocha. After the drink is made, I'm told that I'm getting the last drink of the day. I'm happy they took my order, but then I see the drink.  Salted caramel mocha is what I ordered, but I'm given a salted caramel frappuccino. The last thing I want is something cold. However since it was a $10 credit from nugget and they just said they closed, I told them I'll just take it. They offered to make a new one, but I need to be somewhere.  I take one sip and it does taste like it's the last drink of the day! It was disgusting. I just dumped it.   I'll just drive to get some real coffee. It may be convenient to have a Starbucks in a hotel lobby, but when the drink is wrong and disgusting, I'd rather go elsewhere

Every time I come here I'm disappointed. I've gotten a cool lime refresher that was only filled to 1" below the rim and even then it tasted watered down. I've gotten frappuccinos with too little frapp base so they didn't blend right. I've ordered extra extra extra bone dry cappuccinos and gotten a full on latte instead because they don't know how to make foam. Not even 1/2" of foam - pure milk all the way to the top. I've never had them remake anything because I never have the time afterwards because they take so long. I used to be a Starbucks barista so I know what the drinks should be like, and I know that it's not hard to make them, especially with the large number of baristas they have at all times. But of the 5 times or so I've gone to this location they've screwed up my order every time. Stay away.

This is one of my fave Sbux in town. I also frequent the 6th Speedway location as well as the Kino Marketplace location. Parking at this one is less ideal but service is more consistent and drinks are served quickly. The newly locked bathrooms in all location are a pain but these ones are always clean. Also, there's usually ample space here to spread out and and work as well as a large shaded patio.

The drinks here are pretty typical of any Starbucks location and the staff here do a good job of working in cramped quarters and getting those drinks out in an expedient fashion....  So why only 3 stars you ask? The size and setup of the store is absolutely painful. It's kind of like a long aisle at a grocery store but not as wide making it incredibly difficult to move past other customers. There is also only two tables inside to sit at meaning if it's way too hot out or really cold you can not take advantage of their outdoor seating and are stuck walking around the mall with your drink or sitting on a bench somewhere.  I am pretty surprised that Starbucks would opt for such a small awkwardly shaped space in a premium mall like Southgate where they likely draw lots of revenue. Overall it's not bad but I like Bear Mountain way better when I shopping in Southgate and craving a beverage.

The Starbucks is closed to Greyhound Bus Station. We order some coffee when we wait the bus. The baristas made Caramel Macchiato taste good. I like it very much. The cheese and fruit protein boxes & bowls is also delicious. I sat by the window and have a good street view. Let me have a pleasant time before I left the Philadelphia. Thank you very much!

Least favorite Sbux. The staff looks at me like I'm crazy/dirty/cheap when I ask for a refill on my iced coffee. I never know if they will be charging me for the cream in my americano. I no longer order bar drinks here because it totally depends who is making them. My order is right half the time I go thru the drive up window. I'm ignored for a few mins at the counter when I walk in b/c everyone is focused on the drive up. Yup. I think that's about it.

Before I asked for non fat frappuccino and no whip but they put whip cream on it and then they restarted it but ended up giving me light frappuccino which had the light syrup and tasted really bad I just threw it away.  Today I asked for non fat latte with no whip the same barista added whip and when I told him I don't want whip he did not do the latte again just scooped some of the whip off the top. When I asked him are you sure this has no whip cream in it he said it is a drink that has a lot of chocolate in it and a lot of fat in it but I assure you it does not have whip cream in it. I know what I'm doing and I calculate the calories of chocolate and everything else. Wow lots of attitude for so much money put into a cup of coffee.
They know the drink by the fifth time you walk in, and your name by week 2. Laurie there introduced me to the Skinny Mocha WITH a shot of Toffee Nut. Im hooked.

I walked to an empty counter: no biggie as 2 employees were standing by, one sweeping, one just standing there. They then argued for over a minute whose job it was to take the order. One said that "Megan" told her to only do something. They talked on their headphones to someone in the back to ask for assistance. The one doing nothing finally walked over and took my order. Then a guy walked out from the back and they continued to discuss whose job it was. Then Megan walked out, and they started talking with her. In the meantime, noone is working on my order. During my wait, I bryshed an annoying fly away a few times and noticed how messy the counters - restaurant and kitchen side - looked. Very disappointing.

Let's just get this out of the way- it's open 24 hours. This means that, after 1am, you're going to run into drunks or people in recovery. The locals usually clear out by midnight.  The staff is pretty great. As you might imagine, cleaning up vomit in bathrooms on a regular basis tends to take the edge off of a person, regardless of how caffeinated s/he may be.  The outdoor seating is ample. and rarely unusable, as the weather tends to be agreeable.  Sure. there might be a lot of weirdos, but you can usually just pull up a chair and engage in whatever inappropriate debate is taking place.  Just don't try to talk with the guy intently watching the game outside on a portable television. If Ohio's losing, he's pretty cranky.

Recently, I have had a few issues with the service and this particular Starbucks, I never have a problem in the morning when I get my coffee, but towards the end of the day and on certain days, the service seems to go down hill. I really like this Starbucks because I usually always get what I want and my order is always on point, but recent trips through the drive-thru have not been the best. The past two trips I have ordered two drinks and my first one always comes out, then they always forget my second one. The other day I ordered two coffees, the girl gave me one and left mine sitting on the counter and walked away, I waited about five minutes before another worker realized I was still there. Just yesterday the same thing happened, and I had to remind her I had another one waiting behind her, I don't know what it is, but I go back because the morning staff always takes care of me right, I hope the customer service doesn't keep up this way because I don't want to find a new Starbucks.

I visit this Strbcks almost every morning, and for the most part the service is spot on....however, on occasion I will get a middle-aged barista that always seems to have an attitude with my simple "Pike" order.  When paying with my mobil app. (today)I didn't know if the transaction had gone through; never wanting to walk away not paying.  I asked her if it went through and she answered me as if I was a "child", repeating her thank-you and restating my name (as if a classroom teacher was disturbed w/a student).  I have witnessed the same type of service with other older customers before....treating them like children!!!  All Starbucks employees need to treat customers equally, with respect and dignity, no matter how upscale the location!!

Ordered a frap with coconut milk no whip! They put whip. When they re did it they took it around the corner and took the whip off. I trashed it. Unacceptable if you have a dairy allergy. I will go to another location for now on.

The barista could barely speak English, and just mumbled out the drink names. She was the slowest barista I've ever witnessed, I ordered two of the EXACT same drink and she legitimately made ONE AT A TIME. Like made one drink, called it out, and then proceeded to look at the next cup and remake the whole thing again. My caramel macchiato had way too much milk, and not enough ice (my drink was pure liquid within 5 minutes) or caramel. Hands down the worse cup of Starbucks I've ever had, save yourself $5.

I'm writing this review while sitting in a line that hasn't moved in 8-10 minutes. I'll give them one thing, they're consistent..consistently slow. The workers are always very nice and drinks orders are correct. They are just not efficient which is resulting in many unhappy customers. Block off 30 mins if you plan to grab a drink from this Starbucks.

Thanks to our local coffee roasters, I've become a coffee snob. I'm not sure if it's a good or a bad thing at this point because now I tend to snub my nose at most of the coffee I find offered outside of their shops.   That said, I can still enjoy Starbucks, though my preferences have changed quite a bit.   My selections are still typically black coffee offerings, but they trend toward the light roast these days. By many standards though, Starbucks' light would be dark. I can taste that dark, bitter, and deeply roasted character absent from most specialty coffee. It still works, but it's not what I really enjoy.  So combine the coffee with the fact that this particular store is small and located in a pretty densely packed strip mall, and you've got a location with less than awesome parking and slim pickings on the seating. Not a great combo.  The most redeeming factor for this store though is the staff. I've always been greeted with a smile, my orders are delivered correctly, and all the appropriate questions are asked.  Starbucks nailsl the consistency game again, and keeps them squarely in my wheelhouse of coffee shops I'll visit on occasion.

You will never want for coffee in this area of Philly!!!! There are three Starbucks right here around me. This one just happens to be facing my hotel so its the easiest to access. When you walk in the people are friendly. They acknowledge you as you walk in. Yesterday the service was a little slow but this morning i had my coffee right away.

What can you say about another Starbucks? This one is a popular joint for the Lakeview crowd even though NOLA Beans is just a few doors down.  The locals seem to like this place for its coffee and outdoor seating. If it gets too crowded, just try NOLA Beans... or just go ahead and try NOLA Beans first.
Service isn't as efficient at the Marsh Road location, but it works.
Great location and staff. Very friendly and great memories too. They work quickly and pleasant. Cheers

This is the best by far coffee place I've been to .i love the taste of everything in there.their pink drink is one of the best refreshers they sell.all of the food is good and all of the staff are very nice and friendly.no matter what type of line they have then tend to get out drinks fast .liked Dunkin' donuts before but know I believe there is no other better place to go than Starbucks.

What a horribly misplaced edition to the neighborhood.  I even hate looking at it when I'm driving by.  This is not a great new addition to the neighborhood.  It's commercial greed at it's finest and takes away from local businesses who are intricately involved in the immediate community.    Their coffee tastes like burnt toast, their food comes from a box and is microwaved.  What a disgrace.

This is the worst Starbucks I've ever been to.  The girl working there tonight at 8pm on 8/14/17 was so lazy and slow!!  She didn't feel like doing a pour over so she served me coffee out of a coffee urn that wasn't even turned on.  I tasted it and it was room temp.  I've never experienced that at a Starbucks.  I returned it and she did make a pour over.  It only took her 15 minutes to figure out how to make it.  Come on Starbucks.  Your customers expect better.  Your employee was way more interested in talking with her coworker than doing her job.

Love this location! Was my go-to when staying in Nashville a few weeks ago since it was walking distance. Always greeted when I walked in and felt welcomed. The barista Kourt (I think is her name) was really nice and awesome, and made my drink perfectly and promptly! Even had a small conversation with her about Nashville since I'm not from the area. Keep up the good work!
This Starbucks is never as crowded as many others...  Don't worry, it still serves the same highly caffeinated beverages as the rest.

This location is conveniently  located  on Market Street near City Hall and Fashion District.  It's usually busy. Some seating is located in front and several high top tables are in back. It's inside a Marrriott. Sometimes I just sit in the lobby instead.  The baristas here were nice.  The pop music playing was also gods. It made an afternoon break more relaxing.  I love that Starbucks has Happy Hour once a week! It makes the high prices more reasonable.  My chai tea latte was good. I would've like a little more foam because isn't that the reason for a latte?!  I like the flavor of a Starbucks latte. It has just the right amount of spices without being too sweet or overpowering.

I tend to go to this Starbucks when visiting back home. They never disappoint. The staff is friendly and courteous. I never seem to have a line or long wait either which is nice. They are always in stock of the classic and new features too. There's plenty of room to dine in or work all day. They have outdoor seating as well. Plenty of room to park since it's attached to other businesses too.

This is the most convent Starbucks for me to go to, it's the closest to my house, so I can't describe my happiness when it opened. However, since Starbucks opened in 2017 I have yet to receive a correct order. I have on multiple occasions gotten into a back and fourth with an employee about not steaming the milk in my tea. I never knew getting regular tea with sugar was such a complex order. The reason I'm writing this post today is that I have had enough rude interactions and messed up order , today I ordered tea and a breakfast sandwich all of which we stark cold. I'm fed up with the lack of respect and incompetence the employees at this location has consistently given to myself and my family after almost a year of patronage.

We ordered two pumkin cream  cold brews and they where really watered-down. After trying to explain to the baristas she said "maybe all the coffee is down in the cup and your just tasting the sweet cream" after I stir it up it still tasted like water and pumkin cream. We informed her about it and she never once offered to switch our drinks or even a refund. So after making us look stupid infront of others customers,We politely left the drinks on the counters and left. We will never go back to this Starbucks.

After waiting several minutes to even be acknowledged, Polly tried to charge me double for a tea without ice "because it's like getting two drinks." $7.50-something is what she tried to charge me, but in the app with mobile order it's $3.22. I might have laughed in surprise, explained that I know that's not how that works since I'm a partner at a corporate store, and told her there was no way I was paying for that and walked out. Extremely disappointed and will not be returning. I also will be telling anyone who asks not to go there and to go to a real corporate Starbucks, not this licensed store. This isn't the first time I've received as service here, but this is the first time it was so bad I walked out empty handed.

Saw three people in there but yet they didn't want to answer the phone. All of them were aware of the call and just ignored it. They shut their drive thru down with no explanation so we were just calling to see if they were even open.
One of my fave locations. Always available parking, the baristas are awesome, and this one is perfect to come to and get some work done or meet up with a friend.

This Starbucks is rumored to be the highest grossing Starbucks in the Midwest.  If the drive-thru and in-house lines are any indication of the accuracy of that statement, then they truly are!  There's nothing I can say about Starbucks here, that hasn't been said, written about, etc.  But I've been to many a Starbucks, and this one is, well, just a great experience at any time of the day!  The staff is wonderful, and always greets the regulars by name.  Seldom do I even have to tell them what my order is.  They will usually ask if I'm having whatever my usual drink is for that period of time.  And promptly serve it up for me.  On the very rare occasion, that your drink isn't up to your taste standards, they want you to tell them!  They'll gladly remake any order to satisfy their customers.   The store is clean and well maintained.  But the configuration could have been given a bit more consideration.  For example, the drive-thru lines up right in front of the entrance.  It creates a considerable amount of traffic, and presents a potential hazard for patrons going in/out of the building.  Similarly, there are a few parking spots right in front of the door.  Beware if parking in these spots due to the way the drive-thru wraps around the building and crosses behind these parking slots.  If you are parked there, and need to get out quickly, hope to God the drive-thru line isn't too long.  Or you're going to be stuck in your parking spot until the line passes by.  The good thing is, the baristas in this Starbucks are fast and accurate!  They generally keep the drive-thru line moving at a pretty goods pace.  That certainly is appreciated when you are short on time!  They have an outdoor seating area, but again, it bumps right up against the drive-thru line!  So consequently, your conversation is constantly interrupted by customers placing orders, or loud cars and stereos!  Enjoying a nice latte right next to a running car isn't the most pleasant experience.  The indoor seating area is less than adequate.  Sure there are tables and chairs, but on a Saturday/Sunday morning, they fill up quick, leaving you no other option but to sit outside, or take your order to go.  But as I stated, aside from these physical shortcomings, the exceptional staff more than makes up for it!  And the product quality speaks for itself!

This is easily my favorite Starbucks for many reasons, the main reason being that it's the most convenient--I love that it's right down the street from the University (where I work and study) and that it has a drive through. Despite the lines, the workers at this specific Starbucks are always quick and greet me with positive attitudes. I've never had an issue with any of my drinks at this Starbucks, and I've been going here since I started college in 2014. I go here at least 4 times a week and don't plan on changing that this next semester.   As for my drink today, I decided to try the new blonde vanilla latte (iced) with almond milk. It was absolutely delicious and the perfect amount of caffeine to get me through the afternoon. Come to this Starbucks and you'll never be disappointed!

We travel... a lot. We always hit starbucks. Some are beautiful, some are busy, some are perfunctory. This is near our home, so we tend to drop in often. This is the most polite Starbucks I've ever been to, more than Pike Place, Disney, South Carolina, New York, Cali, etc.  it's not the most updated highest tech or most beautiful, but the significant respect, professional courtesy and cheer by the morning staff cannot be overlooked as a step above. The manager seems very professional which seems to trickle down to the staff.   Staff turns out consistent coffee.
All surface areas and floor were dirty.  Was a late Sunday afternoon and not crowded.

Least favorite Starbucks unfortunately. Almost every time I put in an order something is wrong. They forget the little details. Venti water no ice- get a grande water with ice. Changa bagel extra toasted- barley toasted. Wet cappuccino- barely. Customer service was pretty good for the most part until the gal told us to move cause we were standing where the mobile orders go. Oh well.

I love this Starbucks..I love their flat white!! Rich and sweet! I have been to all of the other ones this is the only place that gets it right!.. However... I would give five stars but I feel like I'm at the bank on Friday at noon.. 20 people in line and one teller The wait is ridiculous!!

The young lady at the drive thru Emma made my day she was so helpful and positive so friendly.  To be honest the whole team is always great but  this young lady EMMA  is so amazing.. thank you for making my day so much better and for the great customer service you gave ..  With a lot of businesses short staff you don't get that kind of customer service . The way they are  happy to be at work and we're happy to help the people that come in. so I appreciate her I appreciate it great work and great customer service..

This place is good when I can get in. Unfortunately the parking is awful so it's rare I can actually get in to this one. Once you're in don't leave because you don't know when another spot will be open.

I'll have an order of the swift kick in the ass.  I rarely find myself at a Starbucks unless I'm traveling and without local options. This location offers a drive thru and your standard Starbucks menu. The only reason I would hold off on the fifth star is because I find it slightly pretentious when the staff corrects me for saying "small" rather than "tall" every time. I know, I know, it's all a part of their branding but please don't act like a dummy for saying "small".   Oprah Chai all the way. Much lower on the calorie count than your average chai. Bonus: its spicier too.

It's a Starbucks, pretty standard. Nice people work here, and they do have a snazzy fireplace that gets turned on during colder months.  This particular location gets wicked slammed on weekday mornings, so you're much better off going inside that sitting in the never-ending drive through line. Mind said line when you're backing out though!

Ok so BECAUSE I have "The Andrea" for a drink or "a complicated", (write ALL OVER THE ENTIRE side of the cup with instructions) drink, the TOTALLY friendly (how the Starbucks employees USED to be all perky and cheerful) remembered me!  She listened carefully and was actually concerned that I like and enjoy my coffee. So when she handed it to me with beaming pride, she asked if I wanted to taste it first. Well of course there is NO WAY for her to know that *I* know if it's right just by looking at it!  So I taste it and tell her it's perfect and she gives a good ole "YESSSSS!"...... I think I may have made her hour!  Lol.  Evening folks!

Honestly this Starbucks is so awesome. Confused at the bad reviews because I've never had issues. Frankly, the drive thru service has been nothing but great. The person taking my order is always so sweet.  My cappuccinos are always perfect.

Coming from NY and being around a multitude of coffee shops, I know a good one when I see one.   They are incredibly efficient when a lot of these people order weird crazy drinks, and I just get an iced red eye and call it a day.  The drive thru is actually amazing.  I never heard of a drive thru starbucks before, and this is the only one I know like that.  It's really quick and everyone is so friendly.  The only thing that kind of is annoying is the parking lot and the location of it, how it's not really easy to get into or out of.

This particular Starbucks has the typical food and fare you would find at other locations. Could possibly benefit from a bigger food display case, but decent size. I would say this Starbucks location is above average because it is in a cozy attractive stand alone building with ample parking and easy access from Street Road. Patrons are usually respectful quiet locals.Not too many noisy kids thankfully.  Friendly smiling help for the most part. Could use a couple more soft cushioned seats instead of those hard wooden tables and chairs, but there may be a space issue I guess. Has outdoor seating if you want to enjoy the Java outside on pleasant days and evenings.Nice cold AC if you want to escape the heat for a couple of hours. Very good place to meet a friend, or go on a blind date, so all in all, one of the better Starbucks in Bucks in my opinion.

This is my neighborhood location and naturally out of convenience, tend to frequent it. It is typically a mixed bag in terms of service, and last night's visit was especially unpleasant. Not sure of the hiring criteria, though an absence of former customer service experience seems to prevail. To top it off, I stopped in yesterday and was greeted by wise remarks from one of the baristas for placing a simple mobile order. Seemed to play it off as a joke, but came across rather as an insult, especially when the other workers joined in on the fun. Mind you, this was in a store full of patrons within earshot.  Management needs to overhaul their entire service approach. A customer should never leave feeling berated by the staff. Regardless of proximity, I'll take my business to another location.
Yehttps://itunes.apple.com/us/app/national-geographic-magazine/id418671597?mt=8https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/national-geographic-magazine/id418671597?mt=8

Drinks are always good here -- try the toffee mocha -- but the staff is a little on the raucous side. If you're trying to get any work done, I advise sitting in the comfortable couches toward the front of the store, the farthest point from where the drinks are being made. That is, unless you'd like to be entertained by a dissertation on why one manager is superior to another, and the other nuances of the interpersonal relationships of the staff -- a kind of soap opera, really. As The Coffee Bean Turns...OK, I'm a dork.  In fact, takeout might be a better option all around. I would give this location a 4 for takeout experiences.

I go to this Starbucks frequently, usually always pleasant. However, this time around I go there to get lunch. Ate the pb&j sandwhich and cheese stick then I noticed the cucumbers. They were covered in mold. The best by date was 5/10 and it seems that they weren't checking their food case. The drive thru guy was in a rush and it was pretty busy.   But, I do pay to have food that's fit for consumption, and this is just concerning. It worries me that they aren't cleaning the machines that brew the coffee properly either. I'd say, check you drinks and food before consumption here.

This usually is my favorite starbucks,  but today got a Frappuccino with soy and it was not filled to the top of cup and was a bit thinner than normal  Glad this wasn't my first visit cause I probably would not go back.  When you pay 6 dollars for a Frappuccino you expect it to be consistent. I felt this was about like McDonald's quality with Starbucks  price.

I am a fan of Starbucks, but not of this location. In the past I have had issues with drink quality, and didn't want to come back, but with the Los Altos store being closed (temporarily) I decided to give this location another try.  On this day 12/13, I tried to do a mobile, order but the store was not listed. I went on the webpage, no mobile orders available (really?). I decided to head to the location, drive through only, so I go through drive through. Half a dozen cars ahead of me (not uncommon). I try to place my order (no peppermint syrup), ok so I try to get a tray of cranberry bliss (they were out), so I don't want anything, but have to sit in the drive through for awhile. Side note: I went to the Los Altos location on Wednesday, mobile ordered the items I couldn't get at this location, went through the drive through and received them both as expected.

When I lived in the neighborhood, I enjoyed spending time here for multiple reasons.  It's a cool space for hanging out, and the people who work there are always friendly  It's actually not a bad Starbucks for studying either; there is always a solid presence of students from the nearby high schools, colleges, and medical schools.  I'd say this one has more than the average number of seats and tables, but do expect it to get packed like any Starbucks would be throughout the day.  Also, the fireplace is a game changer! :)
Starbucks I ONLY go to the one @Capital Ave. Service, appreciation, kindness, caring, & true making me happy with a smile

These people are the rudest starbux stAff ive ever encountered.  It was a sundAy morning at 10am and there was a line out the door.  THERE WAS ONLY ONE REGISTER OPEN.  When i called them on it, this nasty looking broad with a mustache started talking back.  So i told the manager who basically gave me the same attitude.   I called the district manager who said they would handle it after i told them how long they made everyone wAit.  Apparently this one girl Ashley (fat and obnoxious) is upset About and told me not to leave my water around.  Never have ive ever been told this at any starbux.

I always come here for Starbucks before a shift before work. Every time I order mobile, it takes about 15 minutes to get a drink with the barista making everyone in-store drinks before mobile orders. They usually only have 1 barista and 1 cashier during the weekend when this is a very busy Starbucks. I can't depend on coming 10 minutes before my shift because it typically takes more than 10 minutes from waiting in line, to ordering, and then getting my drink made. They should definitely have some more people on staff.

Time to update past review.    The service had definitely improved!    Being on the way home every day, I pass by and have been swinging in for a quick drink.   Much quicker than the past 3 months!!    The afternoon barista's are getting better.   The large selection of holiday beverages keep me wanting to go back and try new things.    Make sure you ask for it to be extra hot if you want to feel some warmth going down your throat.   The new gingerbread latte has just the right amount of ginger and cinnamon. They have no problems giving your pup a cup of whipped cream "pupachino".   Just ask when you get to the drive up.

Literally the worst Starbucks EVER!!! Totally avoid!! I ordered my usual - a venti London Fog and a turkey bacon sandwich. They handed my "tea" to me without the latte part. When I said it wasn't right they tried to fix it and handed it back to me with curdled milk floating on top. I handed it back and they couldn't figure out why I didn't want it!! My breakfast sandwich NEVER showed up and the employees didn't care or try to fix it by putting a new sandwich in the oven for me.....paid almost $10 , waited 30 minutes and walked away empty handed and hungry!!!

Great customer service!! Polly is always happy and cheerful. There's great teamwork in there. A young lady named Haley appeared to be under the influence of some type of drug or alcohol or I would have given them 5 stars...

I felt like I was annoying the staff whenever I asked for anything. They didn't have any cream out so I asked for some and the girl just turned around to get some. Then I also had to ask for sugar and the guy was almost arguing with me that there should be some at the carousel. I had just checked and it seemed like he didn't believe me.

Outstanding customer service from V.W., came in with my elderly mother, she came out from behind the counter to make sure my mom had a clear enough path to walk to the restroom.  Very mindful, after that excellent customer service at the counter. Eden made terrific drinks for me and my mom, thank you Starbucks
It was ok.  Big stuff f God did so discuss icy rigidity d do d TV etc day has u do odd Suzy trust

The partners out here are super great! my family is visiting from California! I just so happen to be a partner from California. The partners out here are amazing and I love the fact that this Starbucks happens to be around the corner from where I stay!!  Nelly Nel was the first to show that good old Louisiana southern hospitality, Mo and Sarah were awesome as well! Thank you guys for making my experince here an fantastic one!!! #tobeapartner!!!

The people behind the counter here are always pleasant and nice to talk to.  The layout might be a little cramped and it's not really a "cozy" starbucks...but Starbucks is starbucks...oh and parking can really be tight as well.

What makes this location so fantastic is its Katrina line - a reminder of how high the water went on the building to serve as a constant check.  This Starbucks is small and quaint (as quaint as a Starbucks can get), but very clean and populated with students.  I enjoy the doors, as they are not the typical push/pull with the same metallic bar handle, but an actual door where one has to grab the knob and really REALLY push or pull.  Anyway, this Starbucks is down the street from Chase bank and surrounded by a Rite Aid and a local eatery....sad to say that despite the fantastic face life of Harrison Ave, it's still empty..but that may have been b/c of the tons of parades going on....  Drinks are standard (passion tea lemonade..yum) are the baristas are "friendly" albeit a bit clueless on social tactic....they tried.

I love Starbucks tea & get it often @ this location. I don't understand why this location is extremely slow. It doesn't matter the time or the day there is always a line. They r not very friendly @ this location either. Therefore, I had stopped buying Starbucks tea. I recently started back hoping things got better its been about a year. No luck. I went this morning around 10:25am there was a line wrapped around the building & flowed into the side road where traffic is pretty busy. I parked to go inside thinking it would b quicker. It was not. A large group was waiting inside. After waiting to place my order I asked the very young cashier who the  the manager was & he said he was. I said to him, I just want to let u know there is a line around the building & is even flowing in the side road of traffic.  He said, yes we are busy and asked what I would like to order. Very Disappointing. Ive never had this bad service at another location.

If you're around the Intech Park area, it's nice that there is a Starbucks.  This is a smaller strip location with no drive thru, but it's fairly quick to run in for your caffeine fix.  It can get full quickly during peak times, but they are usually good at handling the rush.

The service at this location was awful and so was the drink they tried to pass off as coffee.  I ordered a short decaf Americano, I basically received a warm cup of water.  I gave it back and told them about it.  So they offered to put in another shot.  But they didn't.  They just gave me back the same cup of disgusting coffee.  Perhaps if you want a fancy coffee drink they'll get it right.  But it seems they cannot get a simple cup of coffee correct.  On top of that I had to wait a long time because she was training a young man how to actually make the coffee but did she say "thank you for waiting" or apologize for the wait, NOPE!!

It's a Starbucks, plain and simple.  What I will say is that they do keep it nice and cozy when it gets cold and snowy out and that is greatly appreciated. There is a large student population there due to its location near Vandy.

The staff is very friendly at this location, and the drink quality is excellent. I do agree, however, that the staff does not seem all that concerned with fast drink production. Not the place to go if you are in a hurry and there is any kind of line.
This starbucks is pretty dirty compared to most, it's kinda hot sometimes, and the music is a little loud if you're working, but with the new management I heard about I'm hoping things will improve.

I was completely appalled as I watched the girl in drive thru poke her fellow associate in the back of her head with a straw that she then stuck into my coffee.   Disgusting lack of etiquette toward her fellow worker and my the customer.

After spending way too much money at Starbucks in my life, this is the one that has made me decide to drastically cut back.  After they made my $5 order wrong (pretty basic - mocha w skim milk), I got a condescending lecture about the Starbucks vocabulary and how to order properly so they dont get confused.   Or, I could just go just about anywhere else.

Order shaken green tea w 4 Splenda, they shake the tea, meanwhile the Splenda is in the cup, pour the tea in and hand to me with the Splenda all on the bottom. I ask why it's not shaken together. Without a word just a dirty look the girl picks up the cup shakes it and walks away. I had to ask it be remade by the manager. VERY RUDE after I just spent $11. I travel the world usually this is the service you get in airport Starbucks.

This Starbucks is a perfect example of why I DONT frequent Starbucks, but I had a gift card to use.   Only one person working - making both the drinks and taking orders. Obviously this is a set up for a shit show!! He was unable to get anyone's drink right and did not listen to the orders. I asked for regular milk, but instead get skim. It was BOGO from 2-5, and he had no idea. Person next to me ordered an iced latte, and he makes an iced coffee.   Very annoying and frustrating. Will definitely avoid at all costs.
uhm no. coffee tasted burnt and chemical laden. I actually dumped it out and have never gone back.  SUPPORT YOUR LOCAL COFFEEHOUSES!

This is a happy little coffee place in Irvington that just happens to be named Starbucks.  The baristas really know their stuff.  I like just the plain ole Pike Place but sometimes I will venture out to a Caramel Macchiato.  Today I tried the Spicy Chorizo Monterey Jack breakfast sandwich and beware of the spicy.  This really made my tummy feel nice and warm.  The music is so soothing.  I could definitely stay for an hour or two.

Stopped into this Starbucks because it's on the way to West Chester and has a parking lot (rather than street parking like the one in downtown West Chester).  They served my tea quickly and efficiently and the salted caramel cake pop I ordered along with it was divine (except for when it fell off the stick).  The store was busy but still clean and well maintained.  I'll be stopping here regularly moving forward.

I love the Caramel Ribbon Crunch!! However, this morning I went into the store to get one after dropping my child off at school. So I ordered on the app so I could just walk in and pick up. Today it was pretty busy. I don't know if it's because it was a Monday and everybody needed a fix or want. But I needed one for sure.   One of the young ladies making the drinks seemed to be new because as she was making mine I saw someone helping her. I'm ok for the new person leaning because we all have been there. I just think with the rush it may not have been a good idea for her to learn speciality drinks at that time. Because more people were bringing drinks back in to be remade then ones actually getting made. Not saying they they all are being remade because of her. Because I don't have a clue who made the others.   I got my drink and walked to the car. Took a sip and all I could taste was ice, no flavor. So I called while in the parking lot to see how long a new drink would take to be made. The person on the phone stated "around 3 minutes". Perfect I had 3 minutes. Took my drink in told the barista what drink it was and through the other one in the trash. Because the previous customer had came in to retire her drink and tried to hand it to the barista and she said "I can't take that one you can just trash it".   So my drink is finished and the barista says "here's the new Frappuccino" looking for the person because she didn't remember it was me. I was solid at first because I didn't see any crunch on top so I'm thinking "is that mine". Like she didn't say "caramel ribbon crunch and I don't see any crunch". So I say "I think that's mine, but there is no crunch on top". She says "crunch doesn't go on the top only on the bottom". I say I come here all the time and I don't have to ask for crunch on top". The one I just through in their trash has crunch on top and I didn't have to ask. She says "yeah if you would like some that's fine". I say "I won't like anything I would like my drink to be made how it's suppose to be". She says "whoever has been putting the crunch on top has been doing it wrong".   Now mind you I just returned the one that was made from that same location 5 minutes prior and the crunch was on top. And there is a picture that displays the Caramel Ribbon Crunch clearly on top. So to have to debate about something so small in the morning instead of her just making the drink how the picture behind her showed... she wanted to debate. Now my time was already wasted because my first drink had no flavor. But now the drink has flavor but you don't want to put the crunch on top..I have ordered this drink too many times to remember in state and out of state. They all make it the same way. But this lady could not be wrong. As well I come to location plenty of times for the same drink and never have a problem. People have to normalize being ok with  saying "I made a mistake". But this lady wanted to argue me down about a mistake she made. It makes no sense to on put the Crunch on the bottom. If you had a Caramel Ribbon Crunch with crunch at the bottom, and then a regular Caramel Frapp next two each other. If the there was no paper on it telling which is which. You wouldn't know which one was the ribbon crunch. If I didn't want the crunch I would have just called it a caramel ribbon.  Please educate this barista for I take it as maybe it was too early for her to think.

Every time I order from them they always mess part of the order up. The first time they gave me the wrong sandwich. The second time someone stole my drink so I asked them to remake it and they made me the wrong drink lol. I'm just trying to have breakfast before class and they mess me up every time.

This is right by my house and I go here ALL the time! :) It is in a convenient location and there is always ample parking. Their drinks are always made properly and they always have a great food selection! :) The people who work here are also very friendly.   Their service can a hit or miss depending on who you get. Usually, things are delivered in a timely manner but there's been times they have just forgotten that they will forget my order all together. Two examples- 1) We ordered a bunch of bagels for work the other day and asked that one be heated and given to me to eat now. The barista made all the other bagels first and THEN decided to make the one for now. 2) A couple of weeks ago, we ordered 2 sandwiches to go and didn't get it. When we asked in about ten minutes they acknowledged forgetting. In the former case, we were the first people in the Starbucks that morning. In the latter case, it was extremely busy.

This is my hometown Starbucks and I visit about 3 times a week.  The staff is really friendly and the drinks are pretty much always good.  You know you visit too much when they know what drink you get. They do have a drive-thru here, so that is convenient, but sometimes when you go inside, it feels like they're only taking care of making the drive-thru drinks, but I get why they would not want those people waiting long.   The atmosphere is good and I sometimes go there to study to get out of the house.  Compared to other Starbucks, they don't have too much seating though.

Be ready to wait 45min, line wraps around on to Gravois. They closed the cafe so you can't walk in and order nor can you order ahead on the app for pick up. Way too busy of a location, they need to do something better.

This Starbucks is fast, but the people that work here are kinda dumb. I was giving the cashier a $50 one time and she was like we don't accept anything over $50, I was like yeh I'm giving you a 50, nothing over, she kept saying the same thing I just gave up and left. Also my name is Karim (Kar-eem)  they shout out Carmin. They spell my name right and call it with an N. Very uncultured.

The employees there are great and very respectful. Very down to earth and serves the customers well. Newtown is a very high maintenance area, and I give them props for how they deal with complaints!!!

My first time here was not great. The service was just ok for me but I saw other customers being treated worse. As one customer, who had been there awhile before i came, approached the counter "what's up" is how he was initially greeted. Eventually he was asked how he could be helped but it wasn't a very friendly exchange. I also had no wc marked on my cup and the order came with light wc. So overall it was just an ok experience.

I like Starbucks because of the consistency between stores all over the country. This one is close to my house and the drive thru is always fairly quick! I get the Strawberry Açaí refresher with lemonade instead of water  with a chocolate croissant!! Yum!! The guy working the drive thru this morning was super friendly and upbeat, which really gave a great start to my day!

Nice group of folks work at this location. Friendly and work hard to take care of customers!  Always enjoy stopping by this location!  Drive thru is always busy but due to Covid that is expected. Inside is always clean.   The location is tricky due to a busy corner. Parking is tight at times also.   Hope this helps you make an informed decision!

I always come to this location after a workout at the L.A. Fitness for a Venti Iced Green Tea with 2 Splenda.  The employees are always nice, professional and kind.  My Iced Green Tea is always made to perfection.  What more could a man ask for?  There is seldom a wait, yet the place is frequented by lots of the locals to study and catch up with friends.  B. Major Grade:  A+

I've only ever been inside this location once. And I found it a bit of an odd experience if only because it was more lined up than the average Starbucks location usually is. Perhaps this is because they seemed to be training new staff, or rather because the staff they had were busy asking questions about things that are normally rather commonplace at the other Starbucks locations I normally visit. Regardless, my food was delicious, and I didn't need to spend much time inside, so I'll call this visit a success! :)

This is a very packed Starbucks right off the freeway in my hometown.  On weekdays you can count on everything being good--the baristas take a lot of care with the drinks. My wife is very picky, always asking for Extra Hot, and this location gets it right. On weekends the backup teen crew is less strong, slows down the drive-thru at times and makes the occasional mistake.   Overall the coffee is done well here.  Not enough seating. People like to hang out at this location, and it's hard to get a seat except when it's nice outside.   Parking--just not enough spots and very poorly designed.

I go here and act like I'm intently working on a project on my notebook, when I'm actually jus jackin around!  No, actually, I do that at Paradise Cafe in Clay Terrace!   This IS the nicest Starbucks in Carmel. I mean, all Sbucks have the same hyper-inflated drinks, however, they don't all have the "atmosphere." If I'm going to pay the price, I at least want a nice space to drink it in. The Clay Terrace one sucks. The one across the street from Clay Terrace sucks. The one in Meijer sucks. Hell, I've never been to the one on Carmel Dr. and Keystone, but it probably sucks.  I'm actually surprised Carmel hasn't provided me with a "local" coffee shop like that of Noble Coffee. Co. in Noblesville.  I mean, one would expect the affluence of Carmel to support such a place. Oh well....  In the end, I'll probably end up going to Panera or Panera's competitor that's not actually a competitor because it's owned by Panera, Paradise Cafe.

Needed some caffeine so dropped in for a pumpkin spice latte.  The place wasn't busy, but apparently I stood in the wrong place because one of the employees changing the garbage gave me the nastiest look.  The PSL was good, but $4 for a tall? I'll just go to Circle K next time.

The bathrooms were absolutely disgusting. One had no toilet paper whatsoever and the other one had an explosion of trash on the floor. When I informed the worker at the counter, all I received was a heavy eye roll and an "okay."

Starbucks does a few things right.   Where this location is concerned:   The company identified the best location available on a busy thoroughfare (S. McCarran), then made a very well designed drive-thru that is pleasantly landscaped. In addition to all this, Starbucks has a pretty and pleasant outdoor seating area that you can see when approaching the drive-thru. From your car, it's the equivalent of looking at a nice painting of a picturesque Seaside landscape. You aren't exactly going there, but you can imagine yourself being there, and it takes you to a happy place in your head.   Bearing these things in mind, more often than I care to admit, this Starbucks beckons and I respond by driving through and giving them my $3-$4.   That's what I call good marketing.   My nonfat Chai Tea latte was perfection. It was cinnamony, with a generous dollop of clove, nutmeg and the other pumpkin pie spices that make it such a good treat. I love that the barista adds the splendas for me so that I don't end up wearing it on myself. I also appreciate the relative speed with which this drive-thru moves customers along.   Another plus for this Starbucks, they actually fill your drink right to the top of the cup (no cheating you out of that last 1/2 inch you paid through the nose for). Therefore, if you yank your drink towards yourself (as I did), you'll be wearing a nice splash of whatever jumps out at you through the little drinking-hole in the lid.

I am a huge Starbucks fan generally, but this location just can't get it right. The biggest issue has been that they always seem to be out of something I want to order.  Near work + a drive thru really should = Starbucks Heaven! They are so close to doing things well and I wanted to love this location but it just wasn't meant to be. The staff is somewhat friendly and does a pretty good job. There is seating inside and out. The parking can get a little crowded, but not too bad.   The deal breaker is that they never have what I want! I drove thru for breakfast and they were out of oatmeal toppings... twice. I finally convinced my non-coffee-drinking co-worker to take a Starbucks break and they didn't have either of the teas she ordered, then they were out of honey. Today our coffee machine died at work and I went to Starbucks with an emergency order for the whole office - but they had no cup holder trays!  This place isn't terrible, but it's not up to the Starbucks standard. If you can avoid this location, do it.
Always great to find a SB close by when I'm traveling. This location has a drive thur which I like.

We have a "Starbucks" in my office building. In reality, it's an Aramark stand that tries to brew Starbucks coffee. I'm a coffee snob and refuse to drink it. So, I walk to this Starbucks when I'm in need. It's a 10 minute round trip if there isn't a wait (usually) and they give select local companies discounts (so make sure you show your ID and ask).   I've never had a bad experience here. 5 stars in the world of Starbucks ratings.

Just tried the new refreshers and that is just what they are refreshing.  The lime reminded me of a mojito and what's not refreshing about that.  Very berry hibiscus was also good.  The barista recommended trying the orange said it reminds her of tang.  And I can't forget to add... Who doesn't get excited about the pumpkin spice latte- it just screams FALL IS COMING!
No point in mobile ordering when you wait over  20 minutes anyway. This place has been a total mess for a few months in the morning. C'mon people.

A coffee shop is only as good as your last visit. It's Saturday morning, I have been sitting in the drive thru for 15 minutes and haven't moved. I think they are waiting on Juan Valdez to roll up with his donkey with the next shipment of beans.

Busy, busy, busy...it is a Friday though...at the mall.  The line is a little disorganized - not sure what can be done about that.  Would've left 4 star, but they FORGOT to make out drinks from mobile order.  Mine and a lady next to me.  We stood there and watched 10 customers go by that ordered after we were waiting.
The customer service is always bad, this is the second location I've been to and the people at the drive thru or inside the store are just so snobby.

Girl at the counter had an annoyed at life,  expressionless look on her face as she spoke,  eyes barely open, almost as if she had nothing but mere contempt for the human race.  Asked for the vanilla sweet cold brew (a featured drink this month). She had no clue what it was and said,  ummmm, we gots (yes, gots) vanilla frappucino.  I didn't even bother any further.  I'll hit up the one across from my house.

This place is the absolute worst. They must think the beauty of the inside can make up for the terrible service you receive. Every single time I go there, they either a) don't have all the things to make my drink. b) make the drink too hot after I ask for a specific degree. c) always give me a the wrong milk when I ask for SOY. This place would have been better off not existing and the small one next to Sally's should have stayed open.

I came to Starbucks more or less everyday for 3-4 years. Just came today after a long time. Look like nothing have changed. Terrible service! It's an busy location. Right on USciences campus. There is always a long line. I don't mind the line however, staff could be a bit more friendly.

You'll find this location attached to the Hilton Bayfront Hotel. They have an entrance off the street and one inside the hotel also. They have shorter hours due to the location as well so be sure they are open before coming this way. They do not have a drive thru.    They have the standard food, mugs, snacks and beverages of all the locations. Staff was friendly and the store was clean. They do not have a scanner for the phone app so the cashier had to manual input the number, and I felt bad for him having to do that!  They has my drink finished before I was done at the register. They also had the Florida ornament mug, which I picked up here as well!   No bathroom inside, odd location, off site parking and no drive thru being this place down a bit.  Overall: A Great service. Short hours. Plenty of seating!
Soooooo slow, over priced, undermanned, and chaotic environment inside.  Poor parking, minimal seating, and odd location.

This is my favourite SB location! There's not really too much to say here, it's a pretty consistent place.   I recently spilled my entire drink (my own fault) and they immediately replace it at no cost! That really made me smile!   They also always remind me to keep my receipt for my "treat receipt" in the afternoon. I'm here a lot... What can I say?

My love affair with Starbucks began in college, when a budgeted weekend-only soy latte remedied any hangover, amped me up, and helped forge my way to a 4.0. Now, 5 years later, my latte love-turned-lifeline has spiraled and my discretionary income takes a hit more mornings than not. There's something very special about a beverage that is a symbol for industriousness and also the reason why I hike out in two feet of snow, in freezing weather, sometimes with the flu, to get a beloved boisson.   As an aside, this location is the best S-Bux ever! The team always manage to exceed my expectations.

Filthy! I would rarely leave a negative review , but this place looks like it hasn't been cleaned in months. The floors look absolutely disgusting, everywhere ! Dust and hair balls built up under tables. The scum and junk has built up on the the floor edges. I have never in my life seen a Starbucks in this state, I was shocked honestly. I asked for the manager. He said 'I've been gone a few days , yeah , I noticed it too.'.   No way this got like this in days , you can tell it's just the way this store is run. Badly.
Super nice people, always get my order right and they're fast as hell in the morning. Totally my preferred 'bucks!

Starbucks in my home town reminds me of a place that the community can go to and meet up or do work there.  This Starbucks is similar - soft music, open space, enough room to spread out but not take up a lot of room, drinks that don't feel half empty, and the location is not crowded in a traffic-y strip mall or grocery store.  Bravo, favorite location.  Plus, it's right off I-10 (with a large drive-thru sign) so friends passing through can meet me here.  It is not chaotic even with a drive-thru.  Yay!

First of all I love Starbucks. My husband and I were looking for coffee the day we left Reno. We figured any Starbucks is the same. I was disappointed with this location and had to wait over 25 minutes  for two coffees!   The way it was set up it was awkward to say the least. It seemed we had to push chairs out of the way to walk out....the one and only door we had to enter. The workers were friendly enough but it just seem to take forever and was awkward.
Typical Starbucks - consistent product, friendly service and clean establishment.
This place sucks. Go to Nono's or zen den or the other place up the street.

I consider this to be "my" Starbucks, although I do visit 3 others. I generally drive through on my way to or from somewhere else.  They're always friendly, never pushy, and when I order a caramel macchiatto (sp?) when it's 105º out, they considerately ask if I want it cold or hot. (The answer is hot. I'm not a fan of iced coffee.)  My only concern is the entrance/exit. It's very close to the intersection, and I've seen a couple of near misses when cars try to pull in/out of the lot. But I'm just a little extra careful.

Every time I have gone to this Starbucks I have nothing but great service today DJ took my order he was charismatic funny and just straight up awesome. He even tried to upsell with an extra shot him since he was so nice I almost took it but I don't like my coffee too strong so I turned it down. A couple weeks ago I ordered a tall and got a venti I told them it was wrong and they gave it to me free of charge. Another time I wanted a plain bagel and they ran out and I was able to chose something else that cost most. I feel like these people put the customer first every time.

Well, Starbucks is Starbucks...for the most part.  I have never had a bad latte here or anything like that.  But I am more concerned about the parking lot situation at this particular location.  It constantly causes issues that make you think to yourself, "CAN'T THERE BE A BETTER WAY?"  And then you cry out in agony.  The situation of this Starbucks is that you can only pull into it if you are turning "right."  If you are turning left, then you have to turn at a stop light and then pull into the parking lot.  Well, this is quite the issue because you are creating a situation where you are pulling head into about everyone else...the entire drive through line...which is so tightly wound up in that tiny parking lot that you can't even swing into a parking spot half the time.  Which, for that matter, there aren't that many of.  Even writing this review stresses me out.    This Starbucks location is also tricky because it is quite small inside, seating-wise.  And yet, every business man or woman without an office is determined to come here and take up an entire table to him or herself for a few hours at a time while drinking "H2O" as their cups are labeled.    So here is my constructive advice for visiting this location:  make sure you are not someone who gets anxious in awkward parking lot situations and if you are planning on coming here to actually have a coffee in the store, beware, there may not be room for you.  But, I mean, my favorite White Chocolate Mocha is just as good here as any other Starbucks.

This Starbucks on average does a piss poor job.  There are some folks that work there that do a great job but being a Starbucks it's too busy for the good people to do everything.  We have had at least 10 messed up drinks over the last couple years here.  Iced teas that weren't mixed right or improper proportions of the various ingredients, they put raspberry in a salted Carmel mocha once, They even "accidentally" put a few shots of toffee nut in an iced tea!!! What if we had a nut allergy to that stuff?  It may seem petty to complain about this stuff, but at $4-$7 per drink times multiple drinks per visit, that adds up.  We now go exclusively to the voorhees location where they make our drinks with care.

My partner and I are really sick, sore throat and all. We stopped at this Starbucks and were delighted to meet this amazing barista named Natasha. Five stars for her amazing personality, beautiful smile, and exceptional customer service skills!!!
The drive-thru line at this location is always extremely long. Be sure you have time to wait!

This is our go to spot in New Tampa for coffee!  Always friendly baristas and the drinks come out how we like them. Some Starbucks have baristas who don't really care, but these guys do!  They've even re-made a drink for my son. He forgot to say no coffee in his frapp and they re-made for him even though it wasn't their error.

Employee friendliness was great and all but when I see THREE people get their drinks even though I ordered before them, then I ask where my drink is. Fourth person gets their drink and I don't have my drink. Not Cool Beans! Fifth person that ordered after me gets their drink.... oh now sixth. How unfortunate I had to be somewhere soon. Disappointing service today especially when I spend about $15.

There is nothing wrong with the coffee, but the attitude that was displayed today was not very friendly.  I stop here now and again and everyone tends to be pleasant.  I made a mistake on my order...it was my fault and I was apologetic for my mistake, and the order was caught before the drinks were made.  The girl who was making them gave me an eye roll and a snide remark.  When she paced over the drinks I said sorry again and I got an unpleasant "whatever" in return.  Everyone makes a mistake sometimes, like messing up while ordering and I am going to take it that she messed up with the attitude.

Every time that I come here I have a very good experience. I'm greeted, and the coffee tastes good. They have also never ran out of anything when I have asked for it, which has happened to me a lot in other states recently.
I just a iced Carmel coffee. Delicious refreshing , made perfect. Starbucks is the best.
Fast service and the Unicorn frap is fantastic!! The drink is extremely refreshing and not too sweet.

This is the worst Starbucks I have ever been to!!!!! The employees answer the drive thru saying "hey" or "sup". THIS IS A COFFEE SHOP FOR CRYING OUT LOUD, so be friendly!!! Don't make it seem like I am inconveniencing you. Brandon is the only employee here worth mentioning in a positive regard. He is always so friendly and seems happy to be at work. Thanks Brandon for not sucking. The rest of you SUCK!
