They still have no lids for their cold commuter cups. The barista rang up two of them last week before she told me they had no lids.   I guess everybody's entitled to an off day. Ordered two caramel frappachinos and wanted to buy two of the new reusable cold drink cups that Starbucks is now selling. The sign says three dollars each including a straw. When she rang me up for eight dollars and some odd cents I questioned it and she advised me the straws were a dollar apiece additional. I pointed her in the direction of the display where it said three dollars for cup and straw, and additional straws for a dollar. She apologized, but didn't leave it at that, she went on to explain it was the first one that she had rung up and she didn't quite know what the price was. As an employee it seems it's her job to know what the latest and greatest promotions and pricing are. Simple apology would've gone a lot further than trying to explain away her ignorance of her product line. Frappechinwere fabulous    Great location, friendly baristas, always receive correct order. Parking can be a challenge because the lot is small and usually full. Drive thru is my favorite way to go. Convenient, efficient, and if you order ahead you can zip through the drive thru as well. A win-win!
Giving it one star because I found a rat running around there in the morning. Really gross

This is the second time I've been to this Starbucks location within the last 30 days.  The first time I visited, we were waiting for relatives and their incoming flight was delayed several hours so I had occasion to order several drinks.  They were friendly and quick to take my order though they seem more slow than other Starbucks in the making and delivery of drinks.  Quality is very good and I enjoy the open courtyard atmosphere of the airport.  You can almost always find a relaxing place to people watch and don't feel rushed to give up ytable!

I love walking into this place! Everyone is so friendly here. They always make me feel right at home. There is a really tall guy named Jameson who knows me by name and always has great coffee recommendations and something funny to say. Ask for him. (Everyone else is pretty great too)  The speed of service in general here has picked up on the last few months, which is a plus.

The best in town. Love the Clover brewed coffee. I can get any coffee either hot or iced. They take time to get to know you when you come in often. If you are so inclined, you can have a beer or some wine.

I had a wonderfully welcoming experience at this Starbucks. It was their first day open, which you would think would make everything hectic and crazy. On the contrary! Trevor, the manager, greeted me with a big smile! Blaise took my order and the 3 of us did a happy dance when I realized they had my favorite, limited-edition coffee (Casi Cielo for the win!) in stock. Taylor made my drink perfectly, with no issues with my requests. I never saw Kelly stop cleaning for even a second! Starbucks may be a big corporation, but the staff treats this store like they own it themselves. So friendly and gracious, and their excitement to be serving the Harbour Island community is contagious! The store itself is HUGE. There is no issue finding seats, tables, or outlets. I particularly love the artwork, which is a faux chalkboard design. There is even ample seating outside and thankfully even the outdoor space is non-smoking.  Welcome to the neighborhood!!!
This place is awesome...the service is always fast and they have a bar to sit and wait at..........During the day its quiet, but during the morning its a busy crowd...nice place..

I really want to give them more than two stars but nine times out of 10 I end up frustrated with the sweet people that work here. I've learned to mobile order at least 15 minutes out just to make sure I can get my drink at a reasonable time.   Yes, I understand all Starbucks are busy in the morning but I've been to busier Starbucks that seem to manage mobile ordering better.

It's Starbucks.  There is no need to review my Caramel Apple Spice because you should have already tried it by now! This location seems like the only Starbucks on this side of town.  I pick my grandmother up from church in Irvington and I usually stop here on the way home. I have read in other reviews that the wait time can be pretty long, so Sunday afternoon is a blessing because I never have to wait.  I go to the drive through every time so as not to leave my granny alone.  We are in and out pretty quick and I am sipping my fave Starbucks drink in a couple minutes after it cools off a little :)

After 10 customers that arrived and ordered after we did, we asked about our drink orders, we were told that the 'stickers' didn't print. (How did we get our food order?)  No acknowledgement or apology.  Bad form store 21373

First and only Starbucks where I ordered online and my order was not ready when I came in. Generally not that big a deal but there's only a few people in here and probably 5 to 6 people behind the counter. The whole point of ordering ahead is to grab and go, but I'm still waiting here 15 minutes later watching people who came in after me get their drinks ‍

It's a starbucks, but unlike some others the baristas here always have a frown. In the city that has housed amazing cafes since the really 19th century it's hard to jump on the Seattle-coffee train, we have our own style of cafe and it rocks. Anyone remember Kaldi's?
Friendly staff but the drive thru lane is unfortunately extremely slow. I usually drive out of my way to go to a Starbucks that is much quicker :-/
Thirty minutes and counting in the drive thru line on a Sunday at 6:30 pm. You guys need to get it together. Seriously.
No one is here but us and yet they still got our order wrong It's typed correctly on the label but not correct Come on people

Starbucks is my go to for coffee but this location I will not be re visiting. They are not friendly, never smiled and on top of that made my drink incorrectly. It will be worth it to travel down the street for better service.

My experience was terrible here. To say I was baffled would be an understatement. I understand it's Christmas Eve and probably super busy. The lady at register could not hear me..: which is fine so I spoke up louder and made sure the order was said and repeated correctly. I ordered a peppermint white mocha which was made as a regular mocha.... could not taste the peppermint and it was filled like 3/4 of the cup. My tea bag was all over the place. The only drink from my 4 orders that was ok was my nitro cold brew which I would have been even more shocked as it would be hard to mess up a simple drink like that. I tried calling back a few times but no one answered and their was no room for a voicemail. I LOVE Starbucks and this particular store usually does a good job at the drinks but today was just EXTREMELY disappointing to say the least. I hope I can get a refund for these drinks.

My excitement to see a drive thru Starbucks in the neighborhood quickly turned to disappointment after waiting in a line of 3 cars for 20 minutes. When looking in while I waited 10 minutes at the window for my drink it appeared that there were only 3 employees. They looked extremely disorganized, nothing was stocked and there were only 2 customers inside of the store so it's not like they were swamped. My peppermint mocha latte was delicious as expected but something has to be improved at this location.

What an absolute pleasure going through this drive-thru this morning! The gentleman passing our coffee out the window and taking payment was such a sweetheart. You know it only takes a moment to be really nice, make a joke or two and completely make someone's morning. This guy knows it.   Plus it's just a short jaunt from the ridiculously busy drive-thru at the Speedway/Wilmot store where I sometimes worry I might run out of gas waiting in line because the line is So. Freakin'. Long.  Not an issue at Broadway/Kolb plus they're super nice and speedy!

Love, absolutely love meeting at this starbucks! I will say however, that the service is very mediocre. They just make your drink and keep it moving. I am the type who likes to be sold or engage with others.

UPDATE: I've not been to this location in a while. Customer service has greatly declined. I waited at the menu/order intercom for a minute before anyone said anything to me. Long waits, friendly staff-most of them-I do not think are here anymore :(
Very nice and modern, quick service. Great place to go to study, parking was a little tight though, other than that, great service and atmosphere.

YOU SUCK!  This is the second year in a row that your restroom was conveniently OUT OF ORDER on marathon day.   Shane on you! Don't you realize that the same people that support you every day are there today to enjoy coffee a treat and a rest room.  Food insdustry rules state that you cannot serve food without an operating restroom...maybe we should shut you down for the day

I go to this Starbucks regularly since it opened to study. Wait times for drinks have gotten progressively longer. The interior of the store is freezing, I mean freezing!!! I wear long pants, long sleeve shirts and bring a sweatshirt and I'm still cold. I've asked them to turn the air conditioning temp up a little and said they couldn't because it is controlled by corporate. Don't know how true that is. I have had to leave many times because the Internet was ridiculously slow and could not support the live lectures I needed to view for my classes. Looking for a new place to study.
Closes at 9pm in Saturday night on the beach, summer time. According to google the are open till 10pm.

The drive-thru line wait is always so ridiculous. Also, make sure you're ready with a second option for your drink or meal , because it seems like they're always out of something. The staff is friendly. The inside is clean. But if you're running late to work and need a quick coffee, stop at the McDonald's next door.
Super slow service and the pike roast tasted like it was burned or old.  Sad since Starbucks is my go to safe coffee place.

I really love this Starbucks location!! I don't live near it but it's on my way to school and I always choose to stop at this one. Everyone is always so friendly and are willing to work with complicated drink orders which I know can be frustrating! All in all great location and friendly staff!

This Starbucks is my favorite one in the Nashville area.  Most of the staff here are extremely friendly and even remember my name, despite being one of the busiest Starbucks I've ever been to.  It isn't unusual to have a long line inside and out and to have difficulty finding parking, but everything moves through fairly quickly, despite the volume of customers their serving.
Baristas are always friendly and greet you as soon as you walk in. They're helpful with options and accommodate when I'm being picky with what I want in my drinks :)
They're fast and friendly.   They got all 7 orders correct!   They don't water down anything. Generally, everything tastes the same.

This location is actually the location right across the street from West Edmonton Mall, right beside Boston Pizza.; It is not located inside the mall.  I've gone to this location a few times and to be honest, it lacks in customer service on the occasion.  There are a few barista's that not only provide a quick service, but also provide a friendly attitude while on other days the service is extremely slow coupled with a rude attitude.  Depends on the day.  There is a drive thru at this location as well, which is convenient.

I come to this Starbucks often because I'm frequently in the area.  I really want to be a fan of this Starbucks, after all they do have friendly baristas but here's what annoys me:  They almost never have decaf brewing so it's impossible to get a decaf unless it's a decaf Americano or on the clover press- I've asked for a pour over and they don't have a pour over system- what?!    The seating is very limited here and one person, an older man sleeps at a table in the corner repeatedly day after day for hours on end while other people don't have a place to sit or have to share a table- I even complained about it to the baristas and they defend him- seriously?  No apologies or asking the man to please leave after a half hour or hour so other people have a chance to sit down.  I even called customer service who finally was apologetic and I hope the issue finally gets addressed!    I really hope that things can improve because I want to be more of a fan of this Starbucks but these issues really get on my nerves...

I come here regularly before work. I enjoy the setting, even when it gets busy. But, the staff is inconsistent. There's one girl that works on Saturday mornings at 5, and every time, she makes me feel like I should leave. (REALLY short hair...) Doesn't say "good morning" or "hi" back... won't even look at me.   I tried to engage in conversation once with her, "how are you?" and she scolded me for expecting her to put the honey in my coffee. No one ever told me, until that morning, that it was wrong and against health code. I certainly don't mind putting it in myself, but the honey packets aren't out for customers. She went off about how wrong it was to put the barista in that position... I just smiled and said that I would be happy to do it. However, all other baristas put the honey in. In fact, when I asked to put it in myself because of what she said, they laughed and said she's wrong.   I would give this place 5 stars, except for the way she affects the environment.

The only thing good about this location was that the two ladies working it were very friendly. Long lines and slow service. The prices were so much higher than any other Starbucks. They crossed out the prices with black sharpie in the display case and increased them when putting in the register. An egg wrap and a latte was $20.

I love the people that work here and their friendly demeanor, but it takes wayy too long to get things in the morning. It has been slowly improving over the past couple of months, but definitely not somewhere I can stop quick in the morning. Great drinks, great people, long waits.

The drive through is incredibly too long every time I'm here.. this time I was in line 30 min. It's just coffee what takes so long? NormAlly it takes anywhere from 15-20 min in line and that's a good day....

The staff here waters down ALL drinks, even if you ask for no water (waters down coffee, hot drinks if they don't make enough to fill the cup, ALL teas so they don't have to keep making more, lemonade, etc.) If you catch them watering it down they give you so much attitude and end up wasting more product in the end since they're so often asked to remake the orders. Moreover, it's very rare that they're fully staffed or even friendly. This is honestly the worst Starbucks I've ever been to and would not continue to waste my time or money if I didn't work across the street.
Always great service at this location. People who mark off for humans not being perfect all the time strike me as sad little people. I love this location.
Does drinks perfectly most of the time and good customer service. This Starbucks is also in a nice neighborhood! I wish there was more parking space though.
This starbucks is consistent, and ever present in the world from London to LA it's all the same experience. Some locations are slower than others!

We went to this Starbucks on a Tuesday around 9 am. While they were busy we waited a long time for sandwiches that were just reheated. My husband ordered coffee and sandwiches. The coffee was ready right away. He then ordered a couple of more sandwiches. The first order of sandwiches took 10 minutes. The second order took 30 minutes! Ridiculous!

This review based on multiple visits. I DON T LEARN! Be prepared for chaos and unacceptably long wait times. At one visit I ordered a sandwich which staff person forgot about! Today there's a super long line and one worker making coffees until another showed up. Why would anyone tolerate this AT STARBUCKS PRICES??? Unless you own its stock. This is my worst Starbucks experience AND MY LAST
The pumpkin sauce makes all the difference in ordering the Grand pumpkin spice latte!
Not trying to hate.. But the black dude that works here is so rude! Good thing I was in a rush and a good mood or else I would've bitched him out. Ugh

Fantastic Starbucks for getting work done! Outlets are a little spotty, but lots of spacious seating, and a nice atmosphere. Baristas are not bad (Starbucks quality here, if you know what I mean), and they usually play good music- a mix of all sorts of stuff.
Horrible service for mobile orders. They have no concept of what to do. Yeah I actually want my drink in under 15 min.

Let me start off by saying im not from here. This is my second time down here in the last 4 months, first time at this SB. Normally goto the one in the Brandon mall.  But glad I came in, apparently the guy i think his name was Cory now works here. I kno him from the other starbucks. Anyway, its been about 3 months since i last saw him & he remembered my drink.  Now that is what I call customer service

I don't know why folks go nuts about Starbucks. It's just a better version of a Dunkin Donuts. You go in the store and you're going to leave with a nasty smell in your hair and clothes. Most of the stores, including this one, are not clean and you can easily tell.   I see a lot of people trying to have a meeting here or try to study - but I don't know how they do that because seats are so close to each other and the music is quite loud. Last night, I was meeting someone. Right next to us was a couple sitting and there were two other tables occupied. In total we might have been 8 people. One of the workers comes out yelling: WE CLOSE! WE CLOSE! WE CLOSE! WE CLOSE! She said that to each and one of the tables. Mind you that some of them people just sat down. I saw the person I was with in the eye and we're like what in the world just happened?! She goes in the back and puts the music volume even more up! I "apologize" (not) for not knowing the closing time of your cafe, but Jeez, what kind of way is that to announce it?! Moreover - when the other customers ordered - why didn't you notify them that you close in a little bit? Thankfully I'm not a coffee addict - and good for me that I won't return here anymore. No one has time to deal with rudeness.
A Starbucks is a Starbucks, but this is definitely a much better alternative to the hellhole on 34th/Walnut.

Smaller location at Five Points.  It's not fancy, and the front patio is utilitarian and not the least bit visually interesting.    There is a large wall graphic inside that was intriguing.  It is notable for its size.  I was greeted by a smiling employee, and my PSL made quickly, which is where it's at when stopping at Starbucks.  Dear Hubby and I sat out on the patio, sipping our drinks and catching up in the day.  Noted that the interior was filled with patrons working or studying.  Clearly, this location fills a neighborhood niche.

Gosh darn lousy experience...dirty interior, xtremely slow drive-thru, no smile employees...nine of them behind the counter.  What a disaster of an operation and certainly not well managed.  Tried the drive-thru first and line wasn't moving, so we went inside.  Chaotic looking scene, with full garbage bin overflowing the counter top of the cream/sugar area, dirty floor, littered table tops. Two employees at drive-thru window busy talking to each other, not doing anything?!  Employee at inside register must have been new.  Hesitant on questions, taking our order, etc. Asked for no whip on cold drink, got about half normal amount on top anyway?    All this at 11AM on a Sunday.  We were not impressed.  Too bad they've got a monopoly here...a little competition would do us all good.
Out of many ingredients. Frappuccino has texture of iced coffee. Out of whipped cream last minute. Took 10 minutes for a frappaccino

I think this is the nicest Starbucks I've ever been too! It looks like it was recently renovated ans shiny sparkling new. I was pretty surprised to find it done up in such a rustic style, comfy couches, different shape of chairs. It's very restoration hardware.  Can't comment much on service and food quality. I was here at 7pm and it was dead. My order was taken quickly and served just as fast.  As for the coffee, well, I try to avoid Starbucks at all costs and don't think it would be fair for me to review based on that.  My review is strictly based on the "looks".

Huge upgrade from the Starbucks experience we had at the location in Harrah's. Our drinks were correct, tasted delicious, and they were so very nice. They were busy, but that's to be expected because of their location. It was strange to have to enter a code on the bathroom door to be able to use the facilities. I thought that was strange given the public ridicule Starbucks recently received. A public apology from the CEO recently about how Starbucks was started with the idea of people being able to come and rest, visit with friends, etc. Here is a thought, don't lock bathrooms.

This location is so convenient for me because it is right near Center Square and it is on the way to many places that I go. The staff here is friendly and the service is quick. Their strawberry Frappuccino is so good especially with extra strawberry syrup. They have nice places to sit while you work too. I would definitely recommend this Starbucks and it's nice that they have a good place to get drinks in this center because my experience with Nektar juice bar was terrible.

Full disclosure. This location is the closest to my house and I'm a regular. The staff is always nice and friendly. If something is wrong they will fix it.  And a few know my order by heart     Tip: can get a little crowded if you're planning to come do work. There are plugs along the wall but window seats you'll want to have a full charge. This location isn't as big as others but definitely great for a meeting or a few hours of work  Getting in an out can be tricky during rush hour but they do have a drive through and the other nearby locations don't

Worst Starbucks EVER and Iv been to many..They never greet you ..they always get your order wrong ..then when they have to remake it they're upset. I love Starbucks but this location needs real help!!

The worst Starbucks I've ever been to. I avoid it at all costs, chestnuthill coffee co is much better. I only go here when I've forgotten my wallet because chestnuthill coffee co doesn't take Apple Pay.  This Starbucks is the worst for the following reasons: - very long wait times even when the store is not busy  -rude and unresponsive staff. Terrible customer service. One of them handed me a hot americano when I asked for iced. I said "I'm so sorry (because I'm nice), but I asked for iced, the barista said "yeah your bad" and proceeded to pour the hot americano into ice. -poor drink quality, I've noticed a lot of my drinks have a funky taste at the end. Overall : if you're in chestnuthill try chestnuthill coffee co instead of Starbucks. It's a little more expensive, but quality trumps price every time.

You must have about three drinks in mind if you are going to order from this Starbucks because they never seem to have any ingredients. I am still confused on why they never seem to have ice - an explanation that I have heard on more than one occasion. You can wait about 20 minutes for one drink even though there may only be three people in line because some employees like to socialize with each other. I think some of the employees need some training on how to make the drinks because my friend ordered an iced coffee with cream and received a black coffee with whipped cream. The Starbucks was clean, but I would drive down the street to the other Starbucks to have a bigger chance of having all the necessary ingredients for my drink and in a faster time.

Nice Starbucks inside Harrah's Casino. Only problem, you've got to walk through a cigarette-smoke-filled casino to get to it. Harrah's is an old-school, dirty, smelly, old casino. To my surprise, the Starbucks was clean and relatively fresh.
Drive thru was super quick! Took me 5 minutes to get all the way from the road to the window! Kara was super friendly at the window asking me about my day!

Today this is my rating because the rude blonde with the weird makeup was once again rude.  She really makes me hate this Starbucks.  Asked for splash stick and that seemed like alot and she laid it on the dirty counter that's goin in my drink lady give me me a break!
I go to Starbucks everyday. And this one is my fav place. Ppl work there are pretty friendly!

I love Starbucks but this location has horrible service! I walked in with my husband one day and went to the counter and 3 employees just walk by us and completely ignore us. There we're no customers except for us so they weren't doing anything but we literally stood there for a few minutes being ignored by the 3 young girls working behind the counter. I was very surprised to get that kind of service at a Starbucks. I will not come back to this location.

I went here on 10/20/15 at around 6:45 am. I ordered 2 pumpkin spice lattes and a butter croissant. When I got to the window, the a lady with short curly dirty blond hair was making it obvious she was angry that we wanted to reload our Starbucks card through the drive through and she began to basically tell us how to order in the drive thru. Then because our card didn't have enough to pay off the final bill, she slammed her hand down and asked for the remaining cash. Me and my wife felt very uncomfortable. I would not recommend this Starbucks to anybody. If you want good service, GO TO DUTCH BROS. I'll never come to this Starbucks again.

I moved to STL from SoCal two years ago.  Started going to the Starbucks at Chippewa and Landsdowne because it is within walking distance of my home.  For 1 1/2 years, I put up with the poor customer experience because I figured that's just the way it is here in STL.  Then, I happened to drive by this Crestwood store and tried it out.  I couldn't believe how clean, friendly and professionally trained the staff is and the store is very comfortable.  So, now I get in my car, drive by the Chippewa store and head to the Crestwood store each morning.  Believe me, it's worth the drive to start my morning with a friendly store with exceptional service.
Very nice staff, but holy moly is it slow in there.  I waited 20 minutes for a simple order.

My first Starbucks hot chocolate and pumpkin scone of the season. It is truly fall now by my calendar. This is the new Starbucks location in town so I thought I'd give it a try. The weight was longer than normal; not sure why but it seemed like forever until I got to the window.  My hot chocolate was wonderful as always, just enough chocolate and a very drinkable temperature. Slightly disappointed in my pumpkin scone, however, it was dry and almost gardened in some places.  It tasted as if it had been frozen and they microwaved it? Y'all know what I mean, when you microwave something frozen and the inside gets warm, but the outside edges are hard and crusty.   At any rate, five stars for my hot chocolate and one star for my pumpkin scone. Averages to a three for Starbucks today. I'm thinking I can probably find a recipe for their pumpkin scones as I've got one for their cranberry bars.

My daughter was on a 10 minute break and decided to get a drink.  She waited over 30 minutes from the time she ordered.  Not enough people working only 3 employees on Labor Day weekend at the outlets?

A very busy Airport Starbucks location! Stopped in for an iced coffee to caffeinate the brain cells prior to boarding my flight back to Dulles.  Service was very fast, coffee was really good. They do need to spend a bit more time cleaning the seating area, quite a bit of trash and a number of tables needed to be cleaned.

Scott is one of the best, friendliest, most accommodating and considerate baristas I've ever encountered. Really deserves a shout-out for making customers feel welcomed and valued! Went out of his way to make me a specialty drink similar to one unusually order that's only available seasonally. Genuinely exhibits optimum customer service skills and grace. Usually a very busy location, opens earlier than all other Starbucks between Montecito and Goleta. Lots of sitting areas, sometimes hard to find seating so arrive early to get yourself a space.

Fantastic baristas!  This is one of my go-to Starbucks locations near my house and it's one of the best in the whole region. The baristas are so friendly, know your name and your drink (and how you like it exactly); all while being fast and efficient. It's a great addition to the Rittenhouse neighborhood and is a good place to run into celebrities too (Dave Matthews! Ryan Howard!) which is a nice perk! ;)

Over the years this Starbucks has been hit or miss, but for the most part it's been great. The customer service has gotten better over time and they are always really nice in the drive thru. Sometimes they are a bit slow and the frappuccinos are a little watery. Other times I can go through the drive thru pretty quickly and my drink is good (I usually get a frappuccino or pink drink). It's definitely worth it to go here to get a special drink that they don't have anywhere else.
I've been standing for several minutes and no one has got to us. There's multiple employees standing there doing nothing. Will not go here again. Don't support this location.
Worst customer service and wrong mobile order. For being so expensive, they should hire better staff. Rude and not personable like other locations I've been to. Will never order from here again.

I needed to buy breakfast treats for the office, so I went inside this new location one weekday morning at 6:30 am. There were 4 or 5 people working already, and the drive-thru area was very busy, although there were only a couple of us inside.  I was greeted promptly at the register, and in short order had my Pumpkin Spice Latte (venti, cold, with almond milk, no whip), and breakfast for the office.  I bought an assortment of breads: iced lemon pound cake, pumpkin bread, and banana bread, and I love that they were all individually packaged.  Each one was fresh, moist, and very delicious - I highly recommend treating yourself to the pound cake!  The interior is clean, large, and nicely decorated with plenty of seating. I would definitely come to this location again to hang out.

I recently moved to Brewerytown and searched for a coffee shop and Starbucks is my first choice but this one is exceptional.   Seriously everyone in here is friendly. Yeah employees and patrons. I always have a moment to connect with the baristas it customers.   The quality of coffee is superb!!!! We all have our special java concoction and mines is a tough balancing act since I like my C.Cap a little dry. It's been made to perfection here.   The environment is quaint and the air is clean. I know. How do I know. Well because it's always fresh and seems well ventilated. The coffee is overwhelming so you could do work in the space without feeling consumed with aromas.   The location is great too and it's not far from where I live. Technically I could walk but then I wouldn't get my coffee as quick...so I drive for the

Nasty, messy, gross place. Icky icky icky. What a mess.   Unfriendly explanation of the Starbucks app. Rude employees. How do you ever stay in business. Oh yeah. The name.   Out of whip cream. How are you out of whip cream?  Will not be returning.
Typical Starbucks. The line was wrapped all the way through the room but they got us through pretty quickly. Staff were very friendly.

While I regularly support the locally owned coffee shops during their open hours, I admit I am a frequent Starbucks consumer after work. They are very friendly in this location, some of the staff members know me when I walk in the door. There has been some turn over in the staff recently but the new people are also very nice. When the young lady made a mistake she caught, with my coffee yesterday, she offered to make it right then wanted to give me my next one for free.  I told her that was not necessary. It was good to see she cares about her customers.

Standard Starbucks store. Nothing to stand above, beside great location. On the other side, as I pointed out earlier, more and more stores are lagging in cleanness. This store is one of them. Hope that Starbucks Managment will consider and make some changes in the near future.

Never coming back here again.  I've tried many times. I'm The only person ordering a drink., a ice coffer. Three people are working, talking. It took 10 min and there was no one In front of me. This wasn't  go too.  I'm not someone who writes a lot of yelps.  The last few month when I come in I always order a ice coffer.  All they do is scoop ice, walk over to where the ice coffee is in a big pitcher and pour.  Why must I wait 15 minutes for that when people in front of me who order warm coffee gets it made right away. This place what is my go to Starbucks but now I'd rather just make my own and save myself the trouble of waiting forever when it only takes 15 seconds to make.

Horrible, my daughter and I had just got done at the hospital for her foot, and the cashier just stared at her. The Cashier ran my car for all of our food and then smiled at me and told me my card was declined. My card is a debit and has plenty, I feel like we got judged because we went feeling good and I wanted to treat my daughter to a strawberry shake (she's only 6 yrs old for crying out loud) my hair a mess lol, but really not serve us and make up a silly excuse all the machines are not working the cashier politely explained. She made faces at me when I asked for extra whip cream on both of our shakes and was just rude like she didn't want to help. I hope this gets noticed asap. I feel bad for sick lik kids or kids who get judged too soon by adults that are getting paid, because there is a hospital and this is life..you would never expect horrible service or rude behavior but I suppose it happens and it happens here. :/

Location has a bad fly problem and service definitely needs to be better.  My mother-in-law mentioned the flys were horrible inside and seating wasn't the best. So benefit of doubt we went anyways and at first was in drive thru which was going very slow, so we went inside and ordered the coffees and asked for banana loaf (they didn't have any). Had to wait over 10 minutes to get our drinks.  That is when we saw dead flies everywhere and in the window seals.  Very disappointed since the location is newer expected a lot more and the team seemed overwhelmed.

This is primarily the Starbucks that I go to when I drink Starbucks. I have been going to this location for over two years and every time I go I received excellent service. The staff is nice and welcoming and always offers you options if you don't know what you want. The drinks are cool and refreshing and I always get a double shot of espresso over ice or a latte on my way to work for a little pick me up. Look out online for specials during the pandemic I know they offered specials for nurses and Frontline workers. Will be back soon!

Typical Starbucks in the heart of Philadelphia's tourist area, however during this visit... the baristas were slow and unfriendly. There are still customers out there that are unfamiliar with ordering at Starbucks. A gentlemen in front of me seemed nervous with ordering and when the barista asked for what size he wanted, he waited a few seconds said he wasn't familiar with ordering, but wanted a medium. The barista seemed to be annoyed with the customer. The gentlemen had to go through the same ordeal with the cashier as well.  My drink order was correct though... I'll give them that.

Staff were friendly , this location is One of 2 remaining Starbucks with a drive through in the area.  So dad that Most of the others are inside stores like Albertsons or Target now and that's super inconvenient when you have a car full of kids or running to work and need to grab a cup and go.  The only reason we stopped here was that we were at a neighborhood business. Cafe mojo was opening next week down the street. I ordered 2 drinks a tall pumpkin spice latte with an extra shot and a vanilla latte with 1/2 toffee nut syrup and 1//2 vanilla ,whip and caramel drizzle.  They charged me $1.00  extra for both drinks so just under $10  One drink was ready and was not full all the way and the other they forgot about so I reminded them and then I checked it and it was not made as requested. It was also sitting in their drive thru window line and I was inside? Not that big of a deal, but honestly they were not that busy !   I would've asked a customer standing waiting if they wanted something else or if they were waiting on something ? I left and we both tried our drinks ( my daughter who has been a Barrista) immediately said this is not even hot enough they must have someone there in training , what a big dissatisfaction and waste of $10 definitely not consistent with other locations.  You would think that due to so many of the locations closing and all the cute little boutique trendy hipsters coffee shops in this town that are booming, that the Starbucks crew would be on their game.

This location is fine, but they seem to frequently be out of stuff.  No napkins today...no lids for 2 days...sorry you need to use an extra long straw for your tall iced latte today...   Friendly staff though.  Just order that shit before it runs out..mmkay?

Hot coffee served like warm.  Could not take Starbucks card.  Had to Apple Pay, then said I could go to website with receipt later to try to get the stars they owe me.  Manager not very friendly. Ultra caramel frappaccino was good.  Vanilla sweet cream cold brew did not seem to have any sweetness or vanilla. Had to add sugar packets in order to drink it.
No complaints. Visited this location while on a trip and was pleased. Keep it up guys.
Prices are ridiculously inflated. Over $18 for a tall pink drink , grande matcha latte , chocolate chip cookie and blueberry scone. That is almost double other Starbucks locations!
Worst Starbucks I have ever went to very unsatisfied with the service. They were out of everything I tried to order. The girls had terrible attitudes and were beyond rude.

honestly, for better coffee and better customer service, go to caje or even the arbor. i've been here three times and every time i had to wait for the employees to finish their conversation before i got hit with a rude "are you ready to order or what." I would've given an extra star for making my order correctly but they can't even do that! ordered a chai latte and got watery mud. don't even bother coming here.

Is there anything different to say about this Starbucks?  Yes, three things.  1. It has the Old City vibe and is in a beautiful old corner building. 2. Parking can be a bit of a hassle but it's worth it. 3. The Baristas are significantly better than most.  Kudos to management!

This is a pretty crappy Starbucks... as far as Starbuck shops go.  The outside is a orange red color that is atrocious to look at.  I was trying to find the place after driving off the 95.  I expected a shop with "the starbuck colors of green and pale yellow.  The location of the store is crowded with little stores and has lots of traffic.   It is not a restful place.  There's the subliminal fear that at any moment an early model sedan will come crashing through the windows into the area where people drink coffee.  Well, that's how I felt.  Whoever decorated the store didn't go out of their way to overcome that ugly look of asphalt and parked cars.  Maybe it doesn't matter.  They have a good business and the coffee is good; but if  you read those Starbuck books by Schultz you'd get the idea that just because all the other stores are scary, drab and dreary doesn't mean Starbucks has to be.  Oh well, grabbed the coffee and the 800 calorie "fruit bar" and took off.

Probably one of the best Starbucks I've been to! Most Starbucks are usually super loud/ crowded.. but to my surprise I've been able to get a good amount of work done here. Only issue I had in the beginning was the wifi not working. However, the problem's solved. There are a bunch of outlets around the store for your laptop, phone, etc  I've never had a problem with my orders here and always get it in a timely matter.  The employees are friendly and the customer service is good.

There are a few things I like about this particular Starbucks location:   1. It tends to draw a sizable crowd, but doesn't get too rowdy.   2. They had the good sense to add electrical outlets to the window seat area.   3. Staff is cordial and swift. I also find that they tend to be friendly-chatty, but not in an annoying or obnoxious way. And they know their regular! Asking them about family, work, church, etc. Pretty sweet.   4. Tidy space and tidy bathroom.   5. Outdoor seating!   6. There is this little nook off to the side of the bar. Only two tables there, but it is a bit more intimate if you prefer not to sit in the main area. They have a similar location on the other side, with sofas.

This particular Starbucks received from myself a collection of succulent plants and agreed to have them planted for the Isla Vista slain students of the recent past.        The shop is small and crowded, true , but the coffee is up to standard. Fresher tasting than other Starbuck's I have visited.
Slow slow slow.  They don't serve you in the order that you arrived.    Make sure you have plenty of time to wait

Probably the worst Starbucks experience yet. The barista was too busy talking to her friend to make the orders. They took so long to make a simple small caramel macchiato. There were also almost no stock in for the baked goods. Would not recommend going to this location even if it is closer to you. Very disappointed.

Your typical well run Starbucks! We needed a coffee to keep us going this morning and we were nearby so in we went. Clean, efficient and delicious - like most all Starbucks!  I'd come back here anytime!

This location was packed on this beautiful Sunday so we had to wait a little longer than normal. Because if this the wonderful staff gave us our drinks for free. Everyone should go to this Starbucks if you want incredible service from an amazing and hard working staff. Thank you for making our day so special!

Worst Starbucks ever.   If you are going to take up valuable airline terminal space, you better be good.    Not even worth one star.    Worst service.   Lines for 30 minutes.   Horrible staff.   Avoid at all costs.

It's an alright Starbucks.   There's a huge lobby! Lots of space inside, but not much outside.   The lobby was kinda dirty when we came in.   The service was quick, but the baristas were a little snippy and werent totally prepared with what pastries they had and what they didn't. Almost like the baristas weren't talking to each other until they needed to. It felt very disorganized.   There is parking in the back, but not a lot.

The worst Starbucks I have ever been to. Drive through takes way too long. Even when ordering an iced an iced coffee, they START when I pull up to the window. I understand that if it's busy, but when I pull up to the window see the staff AND managers looking at an Instagram post before completing orders, it's a bit unsettling. On top of that, their iced coffees are always watered down and the few times I've order through the app, I always have to ask them to top off my drink because they'll be about an extra inch of empty space in the cup. I'm not one to be petty about one more fluid ounce, but knowing this location it's because the workers just don't care. I have never had a good experience here and the ONLY reason why I return is because it is right next to my home and the only one on the way to work. I have NEVER had a good experience, NEVER received decent customer service, and never left satisfied happy. In fact, I've gotten yelled at by the employees, given attitude by the employees, and asked to wait to order while someone finished writing a txt message on their phone. The owner of this location needs to do a serious performance assessment.

Ordered coffee and a breakfast sandwich. Received my latte in a reasonable time frame. Waited and watched several sandwiches go out via the drive through. Kept waiting for mine - about 5 min. Finally I was given my sandwich with an apology on how long it took ... Started to eat as I drove off and the entire center was still frozen. Not edible. Had to toss it out. So I waited too long - for nothing. Wasted money too.

This location next to the Westin entrance and Lululemon is still really understaffed on multiple visits morning and afternoon. Often only one person making drinks and nobody there to prep the cups and orders to speed things along. Needs to have two baristas actually preparing orders at the same time. I left without getting anything this morning before my family left on a tour.
I usually go drive through and it's usually pretty fast.  I live nearby, so it's on my way home.  I have nothing to complain about. I can't say that the service is slow.

The location is really nice and so is the coffee. However, do not come here from 10:45-12:00 p.m because they are always packed during this time and you will wait no less than 15 mins for your coffee. I usually go around 1:30 p.m and they always make my coffee right, but for the past week I've been coming in around 11:00-12:00 p.m and they have consistently gotten my coffee wrong and I usually wait around 20 mins for my coffee. I really do love this location and the workers but don't expect to be in and out of this coffee shop within 10 mins if you go between 10:45- 12:00 p.m.

Horrible service! Overworked and not happy people working here...Our 2 drink order took 20 minutes.  It was made twice, they attempted to scrape off whip cream on a vanilla bean,  add java chip,  no whip... really.  Please retrain!!
Love this. Love starbucks. This location is always clean, service is friendly and fast. I study here all the time. Great stop.

Slowwwwww. For a Sunday morning, or anyone service should not be this slow. They either need more help or better help.  No real interaction from the person that took our order...  It just took way too long to get an iced coffee, iced latte and two sandwiches.  Rough!

it was so bad experience for me because i want to take my birthday gift drink on my birthday but they said that ''we can not give it, we are not Starbucks''. if you are not starbucks why your brand name is starbucks and you sell starbucks coffee and stuff.

I had the best drive-through attendant I have ever been helped by today. I ordered a cold brew with almond milk-- they were out but the attendant happily offered me some other non-dairy creamers and gave us two cups of water to go since it was a hot day. Delightful service at a typical Starbucks.

What are the hours of this Starbucks??? And who in the world owns this location?!? It feels like the employees just pick the day they want the store to be open and what times they want it to be open. Thank to a Sunday at 11 am and it's closed. I have driven here at least 5 times around 2pm in the middle of the week and the store is just closed. Is this location closing down? I'm just very confused. Don't waste your time driving here. It will probably be closed anyway. This picture is from a month ago. And it's still up.
I love my Starbucks!! Baristas were friendly.. The atmosphere was good.. I spilled my drink and they were happy to remake it
I love this place is very nice the frapuchino is wonderful and there is variety in drinks I enjoy this place

I can't say that I ran into the same issue as the previous person that reviewed this Starbucks. In fact, I had a rather positive experience.  Driving down the road, I had a hankering for a Soy Chai Tea Latte right about the time I passed this Starbucks. I swung in and realized I had to go inside because there isn't a drive thru. I also realized after I parked by the door there was a big sign with an arrow that said "use other door" right where everyone else was parked.   There were a couple of people in line ahead of me, but the cashier kept the pace moving and before I knew it, I was ordering and swiping my app to pay. There was only one barista crafting the drinks, so there was a little bit of a delay in getting my beverage, but it was perfectly prepared (I ordered it extra hot, and this was the perfect temp) and tasted wonderful.   A solid 4 star review.. I just can't give a 5-star to a Starbucks without a drive thru. I debated on sitting out front and honking until someone came out and gave them my order, but that sounded rude.

Drive-through was slammed and early-morning took about 15 minutes to get coffee. The coffee was delicious I tried the new Punkin and a salted caramel. Got to the drive-through to pay and the employee was very friendly made the experience worthwhile will come back again soon!

I know, I know starbucks is the evil CHAIN!  But this is a great neighborhood find.  you walk in and they know not only your name but your order.  You see the same faces all the time so not a big turn over kind of place.  Consistent, good (over roasted) coffee ...

Stopped in here Sunday at noon as was surprised at how empty it was.   Service was fine and the place was clean with all tables empty except for one.  Convenient location at a busy intersection.   Exit from the back road that parallels York to get onto Street Rd east or to get on York going south so you can take advantage of the traffic light at the next cross street.

I never get why people bash some of these locations. Where else are you going to get your coffee on I-95? I mean sure the Starbucks that is outside instead, but why bother? Go inside the welcome center, get in line, and go to this Starbucks. The staff was fast and friendly.

Its an average Starbucks location.  I think the best quality is the amount of outlets along the glass wall.  Maybe I'm spoiled but the staff at the location closer to my house is really friendly - the peeps here not so much.  Not that they are rude or anything, just not friendly.  The wifi kicks me out about every 15 minutes which is annoying.  They've recently rearranged the seating area to allow for more space but it feels pretty cramped now.  They do have a large outdoor patio area which I think is awesome weather permitting!!

Nothing wrong with the actual business, just Too many townies loitering outside. They creep me out.  Constantly cops hanging out and people drinking liquor and smoking the devil's lettuce outside WITHOUT EVEN BUYING A DRINK from Starbucks   It makes me consider driving the WHOLE EXTRA 5 MINUTES to the drive thru Starbucks just to avoid all the lunatics because I fear for my life.   One time I was walking around town late at night and I was new to the area and someone in the Starbucks parking lot asked if I wanted to trip on HALLUCINATION DRUGS and I had to run. They chased after me with a knife but I got away and hid behind a parked car.  Since it was night time they didn't see me. I was safe.   For now....

This is one of my favourite Starbucks.  The location is close to the mall but away from the mayhem of trying to find parking close by.  There's natural light except a bit darker in the one area that is not by the window.  The service is always efficient and quick, and friendly.  The last visit, they did not have my usual tea that I like to order but I found a good substitute.  There are a few armchair types seats, wood chairs, and a few higher seats.  I've been lucky to always find a table and chairs available.  Right beside the door is a recycle bin labelled for paper, and cups and lids.  I haven't seen this at all Starbucks but they should have them! Some locations might not have room for it but it's better than trying to jam your cup into a full garbage.

Essentially another Starbucks cut from the same mold as all the others.  However, I think that either this location is smaller on the inside than most others, or it is constantly crowded with teens who like over-milked, over-sugared not-exactly-coffee, but want to say they drink coffee.    Not a place I would stop to get coffee, or anything to eat.
This is by far THE slowest Starbucks. It's not just once in awhile, they are consistent. It's as if the manager is never on duty and the workers give zero f@#ks.
The coffee is trash its fucking off it tastes like a fucking ashtray filled with sawdust the service is fucking horrible would not recommend

I got my coffee and hoped to sit and relax in a quiet environment, but the staff decided to play loud moany-groany R&B music like it was their private nightclub.  -- update: months later, no change. Turn it down!!!!

I've been to Starbucks all around the US, few outside the states. This Starbucks has been THE best I've ever been to. Fast, friendly, quality, location and skill. Thank you! All our drinks were made right the first time. Sandwiches were warmed evenly and toasty. I'm not a review type person, but this place I just had to rave about. I've had a lot of bad experiences in many many Starbucks so I'm pretty sure that I know a great one when I see one.
Don't know why this location doesn't have reviews. It's a clean shop, pretty quiet but uber quick service! And the macchiato was up to my standard!!!

Today I went through the drive through before work and I ordered a trenta iced green tea non sweetend. They gave me a venti and I decided to let it slide. I noticed the color of the tea looked weird so I asked the lady "this is unsweetened, right?" She shook her head and said yes. I should have known better and tasted it while at the window, but since there was a line of cars behind me I drove off. Once I got to work I tasted my drink and it was fully sweetened and disgusting. I'm really bummed as normally they get my order correct as it's not a hard order.  This is an *update* I went back in for another drink and after explaining that they keep messing up my green teas, they gave me a free green tea. They said they are training new people and mistakes have been happening quite often lately. They really do want to make their customers happy and I appreciate them giving me a free drink. Thank you!

Okay so I have been ordering the same coffee the same way for years!!!    So here we go folks:  I go thru the drive thru, I place my order... And what do I get in return, a lecture on how I don't need to say this when I order it, and how I only have to order it this way...and on and on for 2 minutes to only confuse me...I was so irate and confused...I will NOT return here!!!. I think this guy working here either takes his job too serious or needs another job where he can tell people what to do. When I want my coffee, I want it with a total and a smile...no lecture on how to order a Starbucks coffee, when seriously dude, I come from Seattle where Starbucks was established!! Gezzzz...annoying!!!!   FYI: a iced coffee means cold, not luke warm... That means you serve it cold, shall I lecture you on how to make a iced coffee???
My local Starbucks. Good space for some reading or studying or a small meeting. Lines are never too long. Drinks are made in fashionable time, overall great service. Definitely recommend.
Y'all lit as a bih drinks are bus employees are dumb lit except for Lindsay and brandy but other than that keep doing y'all
This is my local Starbucks and it's amazing! New staff is super nice!! Drinks are yummy!! Glad there has been a serious change!! This is what I expect when I visit Starbucks!

Usually go to this location with no problems whatsoever. Today, however, I will NOT be returning.  I ordered my normal black iced tea, the employee even asked me if I wanted it sweetened or not and for some reason when I was done ordering I heard her laughing over the speaker. Whatever, brushed that off. Waited about 10 minutes to get to the window. When I pulled up she asked me if I had something completely other than what I ordered...told her no and repeated what I ordered 10 minutes prior. Then she says, "oh, we're out of black iced tea". WHY WOULDNT YOU TELL ME THAT IN THE FIRST PLACE?! The worst part was she brushed it off like no big deal and my time doesn't matter. I will be only going to Dutch Brothers where they respect their customers and their time.

Starbucks Ratings: 3.8/5  Food: 3.75/5 - Decided to grab some coffee from Starbucks when we are waiting for brunch at Ruby's Slipper. I got the iced coffee with a pump of sugar free vanilla syrup and light ice. We were hungry, so I also got two bakery items to share with my siblings. Blueberry muffin is good and moist, a classic at Starbucks, but it was a bit too sweet for my liking; wished there weren't that much sugar crystals on top of it. The banana nut loaf was better, once again super moist with a strong banana flavor. We all love the crunch from the nuts on the loaf, and it was a solid size too. Pro tip, always ask them to warm up your bakery items.   Ambiance: 4/5 - Located on Canal Street, probably one of the biggest Starbucks in the area. It is also a tourist area, so expect many people there. Interior is very spacious with lots of seats and room to work or chill, similar to most Starbucks locations but just larger. Inside is very festival, bright, and pretty clean.   Price: 3.75/5 - Got two bakery items: blueberry muffin and banana nut muffin for $2.75 and $3.25 respectively, and a Grande iced coffee for $2.95. The total came up to be around $12. This Starbucks's bakery items and coffee are probably cheaper or around the same as other local coffee shops, but if you're visiting I'd recommend try out a local shop instead.    Service: 3.5/5 - Staffs were okay, not greatest like most Starbucks locations. I ordered through the mobile app, and got called when my orders are ready, but there weren't as much acknowledgement from the staffs when we walked it, so not really amicable to the customers.   BLUF: If you are staying on Canal Street in NOLA and want to grab a quick morning coffee, this is a very convenient location for you. Very spacious with seats against the windows and middle section. Essential pit stop for a dose of caffeine and small snack.

We pulled up at 8:59pm having no idea what time they closed and walked in to see two guys cleaning up the counter area. When I asked if they closed at 9pm fully prepared to walk away, they invited us in to serve us and reassured us it was no problem at all that it was 9pm. Wow... this kind of service rarely exists and these two young men are true stars! Thanks Zachary and Carlos!!! Merry Christmas
Stopped in for a quick drink on a hot day. The location was pretty busy so it did take awhile for the drinks to be made. Definitely expected though!

This is the Starbucks inside Target. I enjoy stopping by to grab a Frappuccino before I shop. If you get the Cartwheel app, there are occasionally coupons that can be used. The other day I had a coupon for 25% off a Frappuccino.

It seems that the drive throughs at Starbucks lately have been taking much longer than usual. This location particularly is always slow in the afternoon when they are the slowest you would think that their service would be better. But I even just went in the morning and the line didn't move for quite a few minutes and I ended up leaving the line.

The Starbucks is as good as any Starbucks in Southern California and South Florida! I've never been in a Starbucks that I didn't truly love, and this one is a "Class Act as well; so I'll continue going and/or stopping on the way when I'm going somewhere for more delicious Starbucks coffee!!! Thank you for the great service, too!

Note: I promise I'll come back and write an overall review of this Starb, because most of the employees here are great, but this particular barista was so rude, I feel compelled to highlight this experience:  As a Starb addict and daily patron, I know I'm bound to run into poor service from time to time. At lunchtime, as the only customer in the store awaiting a drink, the barista hands me my "no foam" tall latte with1/3 the cup full of foam. I ask her if she can remove the foam for me. She's already put off and wordlessly takes the cup back. She scrapes off the foam and returns the cup to me - without filling the cup up. So now, I just have 2/3 of a latte.   Me: "Can you fill the cup with more soy?" Barista C: "No, that's all the soy there is."  Me: "There's no more soy in the store?" Barista C: HEAVES A SIGH. "Well, it'll be a few minutes." GIVES ME ANNOYED LOOK. Me: "Sorry, it's just that I always order my drink with no foam." Barista C: "Well, I am not here every day."  Me: (Admittedly with a bit of sarcasm at this point) "I'm sorry this is so much trouble for you." Barista C: EYEROLL. "It's fine."   So, let's recap. First she makes my drink wrong. No big deal. But then she tries to lie and tell me there is no more soy. THEN, she proceeds to give me dirty looks and essentially tell me it's not her fault because she is not there every day. Never once did she so much as say she was sorry, or even just give me a pleasant smile.   I'm just so floored by the entire exchange. Maybe I do have a very particular way I like my drink, but given that I spend nearly $1,500 annually at Starb (YIKES), I think it's ok that I have a preferred drink.

I thought I would try it this a Starbucks despite the bad reviews,since it's close to my house, and I get a free birthday drink. I started telling the lady my order and she started talking over me  to someone else, rude, but she rang up my orders. Then she started working on someone else's drink behind me while I was trying to tell her I have a birthday reward and a Starbucks reward.she kept cutting me off and just told me to scan my card code. I try to tell her again and again "Just scan your code " not even eye contact. So whatever I just scanned it. Then after she was done being an arrogant a**, I asked her if  there was anything else I had to do since it was my birthday and Starbucks reward ." Oh we don't accept those cuz we don't have a Starbucks system". So I had to wait there for my refund and  she had to call a manager , they had some trouble figuring it out, It was awesome. Never going back . Happy birthday to me lol.

I went to this drivethru. Convenient. A young man tried to take my order but was confused so 2 different girls took over. I wanted 2 Cold Brew with foam. I wanted it sweet and definitely not Pumpkin flavor. I ordered tall. I had not been to a Starbucks before on my own. Friends were always buying me coffee while I did them a favor. Getting the order almost correctly now. They were not served over ice but still good. The Talls were short squat cups, not Tall at all. The total price was 962. I rounded up to 12 with my tip. You know these kids are making Lousy wages at the drivethru. We drove away and I said to my husband Starbucks Stock Holders are LAUGHING all the way to the Bank. This is a MULTI BILLION dollar company. They could lower their prices so that MORE people could AFFORD it and INCREASE the wages of their young employees. They WON'T!!!! Maybe in a Perfect World. I seriously doubt that their employees can afford to buy the coffee they are selling. Just a thought.

This Starbucks has the most RUDE employees.  They need a class in how to talk to people! My friends and I walked in and forgot our mask in the car not realizing it..."do you have your mask"? We said "oh were sorry,  we can go get them", response,  "do you know where you are"? How darn rude!! She said it with a terrible attitude.  Followed with "you can leave now"! Wow!! Do they want service or not. We were going to get them and those words were unnecessary. We got our mask as we wanted coffee and had been traveling.  Attitudes continued and its the last time we will EVER stop at this location again as we go past here on our road trips frequently.

This place needs help.  I ordered an almond croissant.  There were several on the display shelf.  They gave me a chocolate croissant and told me they were out of almond.  "But there are like 4 on display", I said.  The response: "Well, those are like 10 days old, so they are for display only and were actually out."  Then, they only filled my tall mocha 3/4 full.  I had to ask them to fill it up.  There's also no scanner at this location, so I had to read the 16 digit number out loud (for everyone around me to hear). And I had to do it twice, because the guy didn't get it right the first time!!  They got my friends order wrong too, giving him two of the same pastry, when he ordered two different items.  Very disappointed in this location.

Before deciding to go to Starbucks, I grabbed a cup of coffee from Dunkin' Donuts the day before. The coffee was very bland and the service was not spectacular...no comparison here with Starbucks.  Upon arriving at this Starbucks location, I was impressed by the cleanliness and the fast service this location displayed.  This location was very serene and relaxing. It provided an atmosphere where one can write a business letter or even draft a paper with little background noise.  Some locations allow you to use your laptop but they have background, non compromising noise that interferes with focusing and concentrating on a task.  I ordered a tall Peppermint Mocha that was topped with whipped cream and peppermint bits. This coffee was rich in flavor and very inexpensive. The customer service here was great!   I will definitely return with my laptop and order another one of their flavorful coffee drinks in the future. I have been to other Starbucks locations, but this one was the best yet, by far.

My local a fav store.  Now 5 stars since they began serving their Nitro Cold Brew and got it on the App.  This version of coffee is by far my favorite.  My orders are always ready when I get there, and totally correct.  This is Starbucks people, not WaWa or DD.  So expectations are different.  No drive thru  at this location, but there's our door seating and judging by all the customers I see on their laptops, the WIFI must be good.  Almost a daily customer.  Keep up the nice work

I have had nothing but satisfaction at this new location. Quick, efficient, never messed up an order. Today however, is a different story. The drinks were satisfactory, sure. But what came through the straw of one of our drinks was appalling. What I thought was maybe a remnant of blueberry was in fact a piece of rubber. It was the most unsanitary thing I have seen from a Starbucks. I sincerely hope this was a one off and nobody else at this store encountered such. We will not be coming back to this particular Starbucks that is just minutes from my home.

UGH!!  Just ate (breakfast?) at this particular place. I asked to order the sausage sandwich with chicken sausage instead of pork and the clerk looked down her barista nose at me and said she could not possibly do that unless I purchased two sandwiches. WELL.   I discovered why, the sandwiches are pre-made and they just nuke them. I got a mocha ( which is what they are know for right?). Double UGH. She must have been pissed at me for asking for a substitution and stuck her bitter finger in it to stir it because I have never had such a bitter cup of coffee in my life. I've been to Starbucks before....I even went to Starbucks in Seattle, so I know what it's suppose to taste like and baby that ain't it!

The Good: consistency  The Bad: not for people that want to "drink in" on location. In the morning you will wait in line forever as all their workers prioritize mobile orders over people in line. Even the "cashier" will work  five mobile orders in between every person in line while those in line just stand there and get angrier.  Two people in line behind me striking up a conversation and complaining about the same thing. They may as well just make it a drive trough or mobile pick up only place..
This is the newest Starbucks on the Main Line! It's the cleanest, has the friendliest, most informative staff, and the layout is perfect for working, studying or doing art!!!

Okay, Starbucks management in Montecito, it's time to eavesdrop on your employees ringing up customers. It's one thing to be social,It's quite another to be engaging customers in conversation when there are customers behind them. I don't really care where you took your girlfriend last night or what's a great place to dine cheaply. Seriously, if a customers asks, that's one thing-but keeping customers there while you-the employee-ask them more questions? Not good...less is more.

Stopped in this new Starbucks for a coffee after visiting my niece. The starbucks is easy in, easy out, and the people that work there are just fine. I ordered a vanilla latte iced, it took about the time I placed the order until I got to the window to pay for my drink to be ready.

I stayed at the hotel across from this Starbucks and walked to this Starbucks. I am from the west coast and was looking forward to going to Starbucks and talking to the barista about things to do around town. Well that didn't happen! One, this is a very busy Starbucks even at 830am. Two, not one employee smiled or looks at you. And three, when you finally get to the register, you don't get a greeting, just a ' what can I get for you'. Now let me say, no one is rude but for a southern town I was very disappointed how unfriendly this Starbucks was. I will stick to the hotel coffee for the rest of my trip.
This morning I saw a barista add cold water to my drink because she failed to steam enough milk to fill the cup. Appalling. They aren't about the coffee here. They're about moving people through.

As far as Starbucks goes this is a pretty decent location. I haven't been here in quite some time since I no longer work in the area but I decided to grab an iced latte on my way to my shopping outing in the Bluffs. The drive thru line went quickly and my drink was already ready to go when I got to the window. They had made my drink wrong originally and already corrected it before I got to the window. They offered me the incorrect drink for free in addition to my drink which I took for a friend. I will definitely come back to this location when I'm in the Fenton area which isn't too often but now that there is a Pottery Barn Outlet nearby I can see it happening more frequently.

My girls and I stopped here for a little birthday celebration for my daughter. We got cake pops and chia lattes. We stopped in just 15 minutes before they closed and the barista was great. I didn't get his name, but he was the 2020 ASU graduate. Probably the best customer service I've had at a Starbucks. Thank you for great service! The cake pops & lattes were great too!

I have a big secret.  This is my favorite Starbucks location and I go here 2 to 3 times a week.    Okay, that's not a big secret... My secret is that I have been going here for a few years now and frequently of late and I have never been inside.  I'm usually in a hurry when I go to this location; I am either headed to work in the morning or getting a quick caffeine jolt in the afternoon or evening and I always use the drive thru.  The baristas here are incredibly friendly. Come to think of it, I can't remember a time when I encountered an employee that was rude. We all have bad days and I would totally forgive one or two bad experiences, but I can't think of any.    My regular order of a triple venti white mocha (and shhh sometimes 3 petite vanilla bean scones) always comes out quickly and consistently.    I guess I need to rethink this Starbucks.  I need to remember to think about it not just when I am in a hurry, but also when I am looking for a coffee shop to sit, relax, and read a book.
Slowest Starbucks ever. I only come to this location because it's close 2 the highway,  I have to get my coffee black because they rarely get it right!
Delicious drinks and friendly barristas! Love the new Creme Brulee Frappuccino. It is delicioso!!

Worst drive thru ever. If you have an extra 30 minutes to wait for a drip coffee go for it! Why does it take forever????  Paoli Starbucks just as busy and drive thru moves just fine!  Maybe you need to hire better more competent people

I love this Starbucks for no other reason than the fact that the barista doesn't bat an eye when I order a Iced Skinny Caramel Macchiatto alongside the biggest piece of coffee cake that they have.   I'm such a walking contradiction.
Slowest Starbucks on EARTH. 20+ minutes wait for an online order iced coffee with almond milk. Had to go to work so I never got my coffee :(
I woke up at 7 in the morning just got a soapy Frappuccino!! I would give it 0 stars if I could !! But the whip cream was good.

SLOWEST Starbucks I've ever been to! And I visit at least one daily, no matter where I am. The wait was ridiculous every time we went, regardless of if there were only three other people in there. Went to this one more than I checked in, and never waited less than 20 minutes. With pretty much no other customers.  This one was closest on the drive back to our condo after a days excursion, so we still went there, but I would have chosen another if it would have been an option.  Probably won't be back for the wait alone.

If I could give one star. I totally would. I ordered a bagel. The woman grabbed the bagel with her bare hands. I didn't see her wash or hands or put any gloves on. I'm kind of disgusted. With all the food issues going on lately. I would've expected better service and cleanliness.
This place is gangster. Light of my life. Best Starbucks ever. If you're a hater then go somewhere else. This place doesn't need you making the line longer anyway.

Yes, this is a busy Starbucks, but even when the line is very long (peak hours) they move people through quickly.  They always prepare my drink exactly the way I ask and I have never had a problem with them getting my order wrong.  There is one male barista (I wish I could remember his name) who is super entertaining and nice to all of the customers and puts a smile on my face every time I come through the drive-thru.  I always hear him say, "THERE she/he is!" when someone comes up to the window.  Even though I am so tired when I roll through at 6 am on Fridays, this guy cracks me up.  I also had a great interaction with a female barista there one time.  She was a very nice young woman who accidentally dropped my credit card on the ground next to the drive-thru window when she was handing it back to me.  I had to get out of my car to get it because it had fallen underneath the car, which was no big deal to me at all.  I kept telling her it was no problem, but she insisted that my venti iced coffee was "on her" that day.    Overall, a decent Starbucks that seems to amazing baristas!

I stop by this Starbucks nearly every day. The staff are super friendly, quick, and I have yet to have a bad experience here. I usually order ahead on the app, but even when I am in line the service is quick. Not to mention the fact that I have a custom order that I love and they always make it right. I have been to pretty much every Starbucks in our area, this one is my absolute favorite.
What a difference! The employees actually smile and say hello' the space is smaller, however, everyone here is so nice! Someone really needs to manage the marlton location as it is day and night!

More often than not, my drinks have been incorrect.  There's also rarely any seating because of the same large group of old loud foreign men there all night every night.  This is the closest to my home, but I gladly drive to two other locations a few miles away.

As a former employee (partner), I can say without a doubt that the drive thru window staff is a nightmare. In the morning, add 10-15 min to your drive thru order time. @Management, please train new employees during day shift so we're not all late to work for bar drink orders. The baristas deliver, it's the order being taken slowly/incorrectly and slow handoff that delay the process. Go to another location.
Darren and Brianna are very good baristas and very helpful. They always deliver great service every time I go there.

Cool Starbucks by Asana Climbing gym...walking distance. Friendly staff and clean inside. Everything seems organized and well managed. My order came out perfectly and no long wait. Their drive-thru is constantly busy.

If you have to go to a starbucks I would go to this one. It is my local sbux so I am a little bias. That being said, the staff is extremely friendly and never mess up an order which is what keeps me and my coming back to this location instead of any of the other locations that may be closer for them.

I give this place a 3. It's a solid 3 though. Every time I go in the people working there are fantastic to interact with. The place is clean and the products are consistent. It's everything you want your franchises coffee shop to be.  A well oiled mass produced machine.

When it's early morning (11am) and you are about to get into a full day of festing, you are gonna need a jump start. Enter Starbucks. We grabbed some coffee here on the way to French Quarter fest, and even though they were busy, the staff got our coffee to us quickly- and with a smile! The iced coffee is as good as any Starbucks. This spot is pretty small, and we were in and out so fast I didn't notice if there are places to sit, but if you are downtown for festing or shopping, this is a good place to get some brew.

I've given this location a few tries after initially being unimpressed. At 5:30 AM, I was one of two customers in the store, and there were four employees behind the counter. After five minutes I was finally acknowledged. I had placed a mobile order 10 minutes prior to walking in for a simple pour coffee, black (app said 3-7 minutes). Order was not ready, but worse was the less than friendly encounters with the employees. Also, this location has done away with straws for cold brewed coffees and you will be berated if you ask for a straw. Will go slightly out of my way to a different location in the future.

I walked in around 3 pm to get a coffee. The manager and an employee were leaning over the counter laughing while customers waited in line.   I ordered a iced americano and waited patiently while 5 people that ordered after me got their drinks. I said "excuse me" to the barista and she told me that they were fixing the espresso machine and it would just be a few minutes.   I told the barista that I needed to leave so I would be okay with an iced coffee. She told me I needed to get back in the long line for a refund because iced coffees were cheaper. I declined and waited longer.  Another employee came out and vacuumed the coffee beans out of the machine, then without wiping the inside just stuck the top back on and made my espresso!!! Gross!!!! Never going back there again.   The customer service and obviously sanitation is deplorable.

The crew is pleasant, walk in service is good. However there breakfast sandwiches are consistently cold. Not sure if its a oven calibration issue or if there trying to get things out things out the door quickly and not letting the food get hot.
