Impropriety cooked breakfast sandwiches that contain egg, pork or poultry could lead to illness. I would not recommend purchasing there breakfast sandwiches at this location.

Not a fan of Starbucks. Coffee is adequate in a pinch. Had a business meeting here. This review is limited to the iced caramel Machiato which was quickly served and prepared as you would imagine. Environment was clean and reasonably well kept, but honestly I really just don't like their coffee. Probably just me but I think Starbucks coffee taste like strained sock water. Not going to ding the rating as the taste of their product is obviously way out of any employee at the local level's control.

The benefit of this location is solely parking availability. I don't like it as much as the old one/some other Starbucks because it doesn't have that variety of sections to sit. I prefer locations where there is an open section, a more quiet section for studying, etc. This one is all open--it's an old fast food restaurant and is laid out as such.   The set up for ordering can be a bit confusing as it goes right to left. When I was in there, after being stumped myself, about half of the people who walked in after me asked me where the line started (I guess I was looking particularly friendly and knowledgable ) Staff was friendly, busy as usual, typical Starbucks with indoor as well as outdoor seating options and a drive through.
The service was great and I received my drink quickly. The iced coffee I ordered tasted like how I hoped it would :)

I don't know where to start... I worked at a Starbucks years ago so I know first hand how difficult it is to keep up with a big crowd, especially if you're a new employee but something is just not working here.   Although this is the closest Starbucks to my house, I try to avoid it. I can say with confidence I have never had a quick visit to this Starbucks, even if I'm the only one there. In fact, multiple times I have waited in the drive thru 20-45 minutes before receiving my drink. Again, I feel bad because I KNOW this job is not easy but the wait times and the amount of times my drink was made incorrectly or not filled to the top is a little ridiculous. If I'm paying over $5 for a small drink I would like it to be more than 3/4 full (I realize I get lattes but the foam is not even filled up to the top). I took away 3 stars for these three reasons. I'm sure the managers know their wait times are an issue so I just wish they could figure out a way to speed things up!  I do give the staff credit but they are always very nice and apologetic about the wait times or messed up drinks and typically if you go inside the wait is not as long, but I can't get over how many frustrating experiences I've had.

I ordered from the Starbucks after leaving my nearby gym. It is in the center of Clayton making it really hard to just park and run in for a mobile order. I had to circle a couple of times before I could find street parking. The inside of the store is really big and very clean with a lot of people hanging out on the weekend. I got a cold brew and pasty which were ready when I got inside. My drink and food tasted good but I would probably avoid this location just due to the parking. However, Im sure ill be back sometime since it's so close to the gym.

At this location you have to deal with a**holes who block traffic to go into the drive through the wrong way.   Circle the building people and get out of people's way.  The staff and service has always been outstanding. The coffee is just as good as any Starbucks location.  3 stars due to the jerks who block traffic.

Stopped by the drive thru this morning, about 6:20, and noticed that the car in front of me was taking an extra long time.  The car finally drove off.  When I pulled up to order, no one ever came on to ask to take my order hence, why the previous car drove off.  I went to the window and no one ever came to the window and I noticed there were people outside banging on the door.  The employee inside looked at them and kept doing what he was doing.  I went to another Starbucks.  The employee/s could have placed signs at the drive thru and the door to inform customers why the store wasn't opened.  BAD business practice!!!!!

Great study place! I got to enjoy a caramel brûlée frapp. Best part of winter. One of the employees even walked around giving free samples of a dessert (Cranberry Bliss?) that was absolutely delicious!

I ordered my coffee and breakfast eggs this morning. Coffee came and then finally 15 minutes after I was able to get someone's attention about my food being skipped. Barista was unhelpful asked me to order again, I showed him my order then proceeded to fill other peoples orders after mine. After 30 minutes left after demanding refund and nothing happening. I go here 6 days a week very disappointed

The 3rd of the 4 locations I frequent - this location holds that cozy "catch - up with friends" feel to it. And, literally, that is what many of the regulars do here - especially on Saturday mornings. It's the place where many take their "me" time after a monotonous day of work. The partners at this location just add to that ability. April had been at this location since she transferred from the Willow Lake store years ago. She and the other partners have truly given this location that feel of comfort and their attention to detail in customer service is outstanding!
This is legitimately the slowest Starbucks in America. Maybe don't have 1 barista working during the 8am rush hour on a Friday.... I waited 25 minutes for a latte.

First a Starbucks is a Starbucks no matter where you go, so the drinks are 98% the same.  The big difference I have found, is the customer service and the cleanliness of the store and bathrooms.  The customer service here was very good, they treated us, a group of 6 very well and there was some odd things ordered.  The only issue that came up was sadly my drink either didn't get made or someone took it, not sure which.  But I walked up and they made me a new drink right then and there.  The customer service here made my day.  Highly recommend this location.
Friendly and kind staff. Mobile order was not available. Always has a bit of a line but it moves. Staff is conscientious

I recommend this location as they r pretty consistent & most importantly always clean- inside & out- incl bathrooms. I really value that I can count on Starbucks standards at this store. Also- the staff is almost always super friendly! I do wish the bike rack was located closer-
Usually Starbucks is always spot on, but today my boyfriend and I bit into a cold breakfast sandwich. You could imagine how happy we were.

I've given this location several opportunities to change my mind. But every time i come they disappoint me. I'm a pretty basic coffee drinker, just a basic java chip trap is what I get each time and every time they have made it has been terrible. From a compete mess covering my hands in chocolate to completely watery, or flat no chocolate. The only reason I even placed two stars is because of a funny gentleman running the drive threw admitting his faults and making it laughable. The rest of the staff I've dealt with im lucky if they even make eye contact with me and I'm a pretty friendly person so I'm surprised.

I don't usually gush about customer service, but I've not met a snarky or disaffected barista here. Period. Everyone is super-chill, efficient and friendly to boot.  They absolutely rock -  over ANY Starbucks I've visited (in at least 12 states AND Puerto Rico). Thanks for always being great.  Oh, and making a kick-ass cup of coffee too! Thank heavens for Clover!

This location is consistently the dirtiest Starbucks I visit. I've also never waited less than 15 minutes for my coffee. I know they are busy but they need to make customers inside the store as much of a priority as the drive-through. If you visit this location don't bother mobile ordering or going inside, stick to the drive-through.

You would think that Starbucks is a Starbucks is a Starbucks and that since they are a chain, you could pretty much walk into any Starbucks and receive a relatively similar experience.  That said, I've been to hundreds of Starbucks in numerous states and this location is consistently the worst.    I've had issues with this Starbucks going back to 2007.  For years I refused to frequent this particular location even though it was very close to my former office because for some reason their coffee tasted bitter and they frequently messed up my and/or my boss's order.  That said, I recently moved temporarily into a rental in the Mira Loma area as my husband and I go through the home buying process to move back to our neighborhood in Damonte and unfortunately this is the only Starbucks on my route from my home to the freeway in the morning.  I figured that since so many years have passed, certainly they have had ample time to correct any inconsistency issues, but I was oh so wrong.    On at least 3 separate occasions in the last few months I either got the wrong coffee or they forgot to put any flavored syrup in the coffee at all, that's fine, I called they fixed it.  It didn't really fix my crappy morning without my caffeine fix, but at least they fixed the issue.    Today I went through the drive through as I do most Friday's and lo and behold, once again one of the drinks had no flavored syrup.  It was bitter tasting coffee and milk.  I tried to call and the first time they answered the phone and then set it down so I could hear the cashier taking orders and the chatter of customers and staff etc.  I sat there for a while thinking someone would get on the line, nope, they just wanted to silence the phone, but heaven forbid they speak to the customer who just paid $5 for a cup of crap coffee I could have made better in my own kitchen.  I called back twice afterward and they just let the phone ring and ring.    From this point on I will either wait until I get to Carson City or go out of my way to go to the Damonte Ranch Starbucks who are always consistent.  It's not about getting a replacement cup of coffee, I have the funds to pay for another cup of coffee, its the disappointment of waiting in line, paying $5 for a cup of coffee to brighten up my Friday mornings and then getting swill and being inconvenienced by having to call and go back.    I don't want a replacement, I'd like for your baristas to pay attention to what they are doing and to be consistent.  If they have messed up that many times with just my orders, I wonder how many other customers are consistently disappointed by the lack of accuracy and/or poorly made drinks.    Sorry for ranting, I just wanted to give the back story on why this is so frustrating, but for one location to have continually screwed up for years is just incredibly annoying and I feel that management should be aware that they have the worst location in South Reno or any Starbucks I've been through throughout the U.S.

Literally paid for a Venti just to get more bubbles than the actual drink. I order at 6:30 AM and didn't get my order until 7:10. And once I finally remind them I get this. I was already late otherwise I would've said something. But literally the worst location they take forever

I'm not a daily regular, nor do I visit during peak hours, so I can't review the service at it's busiest. However, I have experienced consistent quality and service each and every time I've visited this location.  My coffee is always hot and my iced drinks are always cold.  My orders have been accurate on every visit, and my barista and drive thru attendants have been consistently friendly.   I have yet to visit the interior of the shop, but the drive thru and parking facilities appear to be well maintained and clean.

Took them 3 times to get my drink kind of right. After the 3rd time I just accepted the drink. It was close enough and I needed to go. While waiting for them to fix my drink, I realized 2 other people were waiting for the baristas to fix their drinks as well. I recommend to order something simple like regular coffee. It seems like that's all they can get right.

I frequent this particular Starbucks location on a weekly basis - every Sunday after my long run with my running club.  Our (sometimes large) group comes in, hogs up the bathrooms as we each take a few minutes to freshen up after our runs, add wait time to the line, which can get long, take up usually at least 2 tables, a bunch of chairs, and the ledge by the fireplace, and stay for a good hour and a half.  The staff, I suppose recognizing our loyalty and business rather than our nuisance factor, is always very friendly and provides fast, efficient service.  I've never had to wait an excessive time for my drink, and I've never gotten an error with my order.  They  usually don't run out of things in the baked goods case either.  The store is kept fairly clean, considering we are there at the prime hours on a Sunday morning.  Someone is always emptying the trash and refilling the milk and Half and Half on the counters.    It would be easy for the staff to look at our group, week after week, and roll their eyes saying, "oh, this group again, hogging up the whole store," but that has never been the case. We always feel welcomed.  I always receive friendly service, my questions are answered, and some of the staff members even go so far as to ask some of us how our runs went that day.    5 stars for this location, for sure.
The cashier was in no hurry and chided customers "don't come to Starbucks if you don't have patience". I would have left if I hadn't already waited over 10 minutes just for a regular coffee.

I gets coffee here on the reg. Service is tight. Food is grrrd. Drive thru is sick. Other patrons are legit. I like this store. Just one mega ish that my wife got is their inability to keep cold brew flowin. What's up with that?! Besides that, I consider the Irvington Starbucks to be the best in Indiana and I've been to several other ones.
Awesome.   Favorite Starbucks!  Barkley gets pup cup nearly everyday here.  Team members are friendly and do a great job.
I got a grande mocha latte with a shot of espresso, and this is the second time where they didn't fill up the cup' If you're paying 5$ for a coffee it should at least be worth it.
This is one of the best starbucks I have been to in sparks Nevada.  They are efficient, friendly and a joy to be around!

If you plan on getting a cup of coffee at Starbucks before your flight, you need to budget at least 30 minutes. The lines at this Starbucks are LONG. The good news is that 8th & Roast just opened another coffee shop in this part of the airport, so if the line is super long at Starbucks, you can go there. But there's nothing great about this Starbucks. I guess it's nice to have one, but I've flown out of this airport dozens of times and there's always a craaaaaazy line.

A convenient Star$$$$ for this part of the world, with access from both Street Road and Second Street Pike.  Its proximity to some of my favorite restaurants - Aroi Thai, Blue Sage and House of Pho - make it a perfect quick après dinner stop for hot brew on the way to the movies or home.    It is not the most attractive franchise unit in the area, but it serves a functional purpose.   Unlike a box of chocolates, you always know what you are going to get.
everyone here goes above and beyond my expectations. my drinks always come out how they are supposed too. thank you staff!

I had a really bad experience at the Starbucks inside Macy's from beginning to end. It started with the person at the register not knowing how to ring up an order.  I told him i was using a reward, showed him phone that showed the reward, and ordered a venti peppermint mocha with 3 shots.  After a number of confusing and voided transactions.  He then charged my card $7.94.  I told him - "no, remember? I am using a reward, why did it charge my card?"  He then gets the manager to reload $7.62 back on my card. I told him "no - you charged me $7.92, and that's not even the regular price for these - it should be close to $9"  I asked where are the receipts - he only shows me the $7.62 ring up.  I told him i know it was $7.92, but he rings it up and it's $7.62.  I told him again show me the original receipt you charged me 7.92.  He doesn't have it.  I couldn't pull it up on my phone yet.  Sure enough, now that i look, - there it is - a reload of $7.62, and a charge of $7.92.     Then the manager said  nevermind the drink is on us.  My reward is gone; I did not get all of my money back, and THEN when i get my order (already made and starting to separate, he pours it into a Grande.  I said that was supposed to be a Venti peppermint mocha with 3 shots.  He then goes to the blender and pours the bottom remainings into a Venti cup and adds the rest and gives it to me.  .  Never mind the drink tasted more half liquid icey water than peppermint, mocha, or espresso!!!  I was so frustrated i didn't want to start all over again as i was in there for 20 minutes.  It was not busy.  I don't understand how this all happened.   I go to  Starbucks all over the world and have not had as silly of an interaction or worse mix-up and taste of drink.

I love this place! Staff is amazing and they are pretty quick in the mornings. My "Peppermint Mocha" is always tasty and hot! :) I've even had one staff member announce my order at the drive through before I even said anything!

i've always had a great experience at this location Unfortunately today was not one of those days. i was sitting in the drive through watching my drink be made as the girl drops the cup and spills 50% of my drink. instead of remaking it she dumps what's left of my drink into the blender and adds tons of milk. totally lazy and very unsanitary.
Really enjoy this location. It's drive thru line seems to be wrapped around in the mornings when I go by but it is pretty quick. In and out usually in less than 5 minutes. Worth the time...

The slowest Starbucks I have ever been in. Ordered an iced coffee while on the go. Stood waiting for 7 minutes waiting for my simple drink while one barista fumbled around steaming milk and pouring it in the sink (what are you doing?????). Not sure why the other 3 workers are on the payroll because they are standing around doing absolutely nothing.   Will not come back, plenty of local coffee shops in the area that are better, faster, and likely cheaper.
Bathrooms not open to public   This is ridiculous. Covid doesn't spread by going to the bathroom

This location used to be my daily Starbucks stop. The girls were always awesome and the food/drinks always amazing. So I decided to stop in while in town today.   The line moved so quickly that I was at the front ready to order before I'd said hi. As I was paying the barista looked up and said hi, commenting that I'd changed my hair. It's been 8 months since my last visit do I was impressed that she remembered me but she also remembered my drink AND my name.   Such awesome staff and their drinks are perfect. No having to explain 3 times how you like your drink.

What can I say? I've been waiting for a Starbucks drive thru forever! The staff were friendly and the inside of the store is laid back and relaxing. It's very spacious so doesn't feel too crowded when there are a lot of people. The one downside is a small parking lot that can get backed up with cars coming in to park and cars leaving the drive thru. They also have two 10 minute mobile order parking spots! So cool!
Horrible. Slow and poor service. It seems like the baristas are not trained properly so they are very inefficient and don't make the drinks the same. Never will come here again.

This is a good Starbucks, but always SOOOO crowded.  The line is always RIDICULOUS, so I honestly avoid it unless I'm desperate for caffeine.   And good luck finding a table, any time of day....  However, they are friendly staff, even though you're surrounded by annoying suburbanites...

This was a wonderful Starbucks. First of all, it is very large. The service rivals any that I've been to around the country.  I was absolutely taken by the way they decorated it. As one would expect, the decor is very much New Orleans - lots music and jazz related items. Great fixtures and chandeliers, and a terrific mural that depicts the French Quarter.  Starbucks did a great job with this one. Our group spend some time sitting there, enjoying our coffee and the surroundings.

This place is a bit small, and can be a little cramped with too many people in it. It's conveniently located amongst the historical sites, but this causes many people to come in. The bathroom was really dirty, which was really shocking because it was locked with a code and we expected it to be cleaner from Starbucks. The bathroom smelled and the ground was really wet as if someone had peed in there. The cashier was really nice, but the drinks could have been made better. There were grounds in the coffee for one of our orders as that was slightly disappointing. However, I don't think it's anything that the establishment can't improve on!

Seriously lol the attitude of these servers is really something. They won't even address you when you're standing there waiting to make an order. I've had countless burnt lattes and coffee. Stick with the ice drinks. They're a bit harder to mess up.
Worst service ever! So freaking slow I would fire all 3 girls that worked today if I were their boss
The staff is quick, pleasant and seem to love what they're doing. It was a pleasant surprise to be greeted with such enthusiasm after a disappointing plane delay.

Walked in before they closed at 6:00 pm, there at 5:55 pm and they said they were closed and wouldn't serve me. Stopped at this exit just to go to Starbucks. Need to have a talk with their employees about closing early!

Another Starbucks. Typical drinks, typical snacks, typical decoration. So what makes this one different from the rest? I can point out 2 pros and 1 con:  Pro #1: Location. This is located on a busy corner of one of the busiest foot traffic streets in Edmonton (Whyte Ave; also known as 82nd Ave). So the location makes this Starbucks a great place to meet up with friends while enjoying an afternoon out and about. Another great aspect about the location is the ability to people watch. Whether you sit inside, or at a table on the sidewalk, this is a great location to soak in your surroundings.  Pro #2: I like the bar space set up inside. This is something I haven't seen at any other Starbucks. Just to the left of the barista brewing centre there is a bar with about 4 stools set up. It is fun to watch the barista's work behind the counter and I love the space available. The round tables at typical Starbucks' are quite small if you are going there to do work but this particular bar is very wide which is ideal to spread out books and papers.  Con #1: I guess this is related to the first pro. The fact that it is located on a busy street means lots of street noise from vehicles driving by and also can make it difficult to find parking. Hopefully this encourages people to take public transit but I know that option is not always feasible.  Overall I do really like this Starbucks, especially in the summer while the city is abuzz with all of the festivals taking place.

This Starbucks has all the Starbucks-iness of any Starbucks -- coffees and beverages, snacks and bakery treats, mugs and etc. But what makes this store really stand out is the great layout. The long central tables are spectacular for working while there's also comfy spots to relax or hang out with friends. It's also easy to get in and out quickly. Plus the staff is warm and friendly.

Oh Starbucks.  How do I love you?  Let me count the ways.    You have helped me numerous times during long shifts at work and more recently, during the post Thanksgiving sales in which I had to show up at the mall at 5 am for work and for that, I thank you.  I can not wait until we meet again for yet another 10 hour shift.  Yay for seasonal flavors!!!

We arrived at this Starbucks right as they were closing during a severe thunderstorm. We had our screaming baby, but the employees at this location welcomed us in, even as they were preparing to close, gave us free coffee, and waited out the worst part of the storm with us. When we were ready to leave one of the employees even walked us out to the car with our diaper bag and helped us get safely in to the car!  They went above and beyond and it made a big impact. We will definitely stop at this location the next time we are close by!

Great location right inside the GSR and the staff is always usually very friendly, but during times of the year especially holiday events the line service is way to slow. They need to figure out how to bring in some extra help, cashiers and overall people movers...the last thing you want to do is wait 15-20 mins to place your coffee order. You need coffee not to be grumpy and making people wait for coffee makes them grumpier.

My drink order was incorrect and I didn't realize until I pulled away. I immediately went back through the drive through and asked for the correct drink which the obliged. The staff was nice but the drink is not the best I've had. I wish they would have let me keep the other drink since they were just going to throw it away anyway and I had to wait in the drive through 2 times.

I used to come to this Starbucks more often with work colleagues but now I'm here occasionally for meetings.  The line ups can be long but they tend to move quickly and the staff are efficient to get my drink fast.  But the spacing is definitely lacking.  There are tables and chairs but the people at the next table are an arm's stretch away so it's not good for any personal or confidential conversation.  Last time I was there, there was a person in a wheelchair and there wasn't enough space for her to maneuver around the people, tables, and chairs.  People also sometimes stand by the area where you get napkins as they wait for their drinks so it gets crowded.  The staff need to buzz people in for the washroom and I have seen people come in just to use the facilities without making a purchase and staff don't seem to mind.   Since most people take their drinks to go, I can usually find a seat but I still find it's not very comfortable compared to another coffee shop half a block away.
The staff are generally kind and very professional. This location is also particularly clean... a perfect place to get some work done or talk with friends!

Last night, a friend and I got together just to visit, sit around and talk, and really not to do very much. He said that we should go to this Starbucks.   This is a typical Starbucks like almost all of the other Starbucks establishments in the metropolitan St. Louis area. Their coffee is not bad but it's not good either. They have their menu of drinks posted on the wall like the others.   When we got there, it wasn't crowded, and the employees were helpful and friendly, and I always appreciate that. I just wished that the coffee at Starbucks was better in general, not just this Starbucks. It was quiet and not busy the entire time we were there, so I suppose that it's a good place to study or read your latest good book that you bought.
This is THE BEST Starbucks on the planet. Every single employee is so nice, efficient, helpful, cheerful and awesome! Seriously the best ever.

This is open for a while now and one thing they need to work on is maintaining the condiments counter.    I'm there often and almost always its out of supplies.   I'm also somewhat disappointed that they don't fill the coffee to the top...   Why should I get 3/4 cup of coffee?   Happened too many times to not say anything about ...
Food and coffee is standard Starbucks issue. Problem I have is ambiance. It's always cold and baristas can be loud. Not a good place to hang out.
I love this place! My cashier, Adam, was so nice and funny. Drink tasted perfectly. Rock on guys!

Some people may hate chain retail coffee shops, but this is as good as it gets in Arnold! I particularly enjoy Starbucks because the service is almost always enjoyable and there is free Wi-Fi in the place.  There are a lot of nice cozy chairs and I sometimes manage to plop here with a good book instead of the local library. (The library does tend to be a bit loud, but that is ok.) I can order a variety of drinks that can be syled according to my preferences. I always order the skinny version of an iced caramel machiatto. I always get my drink pretty quickly. I agree with other reviewers, they do need to add some more parking spots, but this is not a huge negative, because the store had to do with what was given to them.

The staff here is very friendly and polite.  The inside of the store is clean and organized.  With that being said, they are extremely slow.  There's generally a long line in the drive thru and it doesn't move very quickly.    All in all, it's Starbucks and if you're willing to wait a bit then swing by.

MUCH better experience this time - very friendly service and almost NO LINE - but it was 4 PM on a HOT (100 degree) Summer Saturday.  My 11 year old got a TRENTA aka "vat" of green iced tea.  We needed this!

Stopped in approximately 7:15am to order a green tea. The cashier stated they were out of both varieties of green tea.  ...What? How does that happen? Tea packets are the cheapest thing to stock and store. I drove down the street to the other Starbucks and ordered without any issue.
What can I say, it's a Starbucks. I needed a pick me up while I'm waiting on a layover. My latte helped me feel human again lol. Only regret after drinking my coffee, I'm feeling hot now lol.
This Starbucks has the friendliest drive thru workers. Always so happy and upbeat. It makes waiting in line for my coffee tolerable.

I came to the Starbucks one fine day and I treat myself once maybe every two weeks to Starbucks and I just like a pour over coffee dark roast cream and flavor.. The guy took my order and then turned around to make my coffee which had already been made no fucking way up or over could've been done in a short amount of time... and I don't know who the lady was but I was standing there watching the whole thing and she told him don't worry about it and give me the coffee... if you drink a pour over and dark roast coffee you like a specific taste and Coffee so for her to do that in front of me I would never go back to the Starbucks.. This Starbucks has issues with steaming the milk. my coffee is never taste good from this location

This is a great Starbucks to visit. Even if they are busy the people working there are super nice, and super fast. The speed and good attitude I don't find at every Starbucks. These guys are the A TEAM.

I wanted some coffee so badly I just pulled up to this Location and I asked the manger she's a girl the one has the scarf on her head . I asked her at 9:23 pm which is right now of the store still open she said no closed with the most bad attitude ever seen

This location is my favorite one to go to. They are friendly and make my drink exactly the way it should be made. I have even gone to them on a few occasions to have my drink remade because another location butchered it. Thanks for being so awesome!
I stop in here every morning on my way to work ! I think the staff is very social and they are always able to recommend me a good drink when I'm in the mood to switch it up !

My drink order is pretty simple (iced coffee, light ice), but it's been screwed up in pretty amazing ways.  It's had hazelnut, extra ice, regular sweetener, and various milks put into it.  If you count two drink errors in one trip, I've actually had my drink made wrong more times than I've even been there!  Also, the morning people have a habit of calling everyone "Steve."  I walked through the door, and was greeted with a "hi Steve!"  I figured they just thought I was someone else, until they greeted my friend who came in right behind me ("hi Steve!") and then the woman behind us ("hi Steve!").  I'm not asking for stern professionalism from every barista in the world, but that's just dumb.

I created this account just to write this review, Becca is the unfriendliest person I've ever met. She should never have a job where she would have to interact with anyone. Talked down to staff and customers. Never will I go back if she is working here.
Customer service is always on point and they make good suggestions if you ask. If you want sweets/food you just gotta get there in the am. Love this target as well it's pretty spacious.

This morning while visiting Reno I was surprised to find very few Starbucks offer a drive through.  I finally found this locationthe line was long, but  it moved quickly. The employees were professional and friendly. Thank you!

This Starbucks seriously lacks a sense of urgency. Too many baristas are working behind the counter to be efficient. I regularly go here since I am a hopeless coffee addict, but the experience is always awful. I order ahead using the app they encouraged me to use if I wanted my order done on time and they still deliver late. I only ever order a cup of pike place brew and they treat me like I ordered a frappe. I do not recommend this Starbucks.
Slow. Slow. Slooooooooow. The sbux in Frys across the street is 10x faster and they're not even trained baristas. Done here.
Even when using the mobile order app we still have to often wait for my drinks... And that is when we order it 15+ minutes ahead of when we arrive.

This Starbucks is located in Harrahs on the second floor. It is a fairly small Starbucks, and there seems to be wait most of the time. I ordered a medium latte, with nonfat milk at 140 degrees, and it came out so hot. I could not drink it for about ten minutes. I really think they burnt the milk. The service was OK, more like cattle being herded in and out. Will I be back, probably not.

I have went to this location more than enough times that I can say this place is ok. The drinks and food are great but the service is so slow. Workers are friendly but it seems they're short staffed all the time! I have went in and went to the drive thu.  Just be prepared that you may have a long wait and I don't recommend when you are in a hurry
This Starbucks is in a great location--right by the Southwest Airlines gates.  The service is fast and usually friendly.  They are often well stocked on baked goods and snacks.

Decent Stop Before Going Through TSA  SITREP  I got to Nashville Airport about three (3) hours before my take-off, and after I saw this spot, I really wasn't jone'sin to rush to the gate, not knowing what I'd find there.  So I decided to post here for about 90 minutes, and I'm glad I did.  THE SETUP  This is a large lounge area, and it's right next to the lavatories for men & women, so there's that.  It also had plenty of room to set-up my laptop and chill for pretty much as long as I wanted to.  NO WIFI  The first disappointment, though, was finding out there was no free WiFi at this Starbucks - nor in the airport for that matter.  So I popped out my WiFi Cellular card and made do with catching up on emails, uploading pictures, and surfing the internet.  EXPENSIVE COFFEE  The second disappointment was finding out that there is no normal pricing here at this Starbucks as you would find at a downtown Nashville location.  Just like the stops in Las Vegas and Phoenix, you will pay about 25%-33% more in coffee prices.  Ce la vie.  THE LOWDOWN  A decent stop for coffee & chill time when you are pretty much a "captured audience" on the airport grounds.

This is a very nice location with a great layout to sit, study, work, or converse with friends. I love the open payout and segregation for an additional semi-private area for sitting quietly. The parking lot leaves a bit to be desired as it is not conducive to parking or getting out of the parking lot. But, this is a busy intersection as well.   My one criticism of their customer service is that when customers are present employees should pay attention and serve guests instead of continuing to talk with their co-workers. Even though the lady was very nice to me when she was taking my order she ignored me for about 30 s Vonda while she spoke to her co-worker about personal stuff. Other than this it was a great visit.

The service given might not always be the fastest, but as a customer and retail worker, when customers are not friendly it really doesn't help anything, in order for the customer experience to be improved people have to stop being such buttholes, you get what you give. As retail workers we always try hard to please the customers and make sure that they get the best service, but honestly, as a customer we also have a choice. I read a couple reviews on here, and to be honest if you go to a restaurant and the line is long or the wait time is too long, would you really want to sit there for more than 10 mins?! I know I wouldn't, I can't count how many times I've walked out of places because the line was too long or the wait time was too long. Let's be realistic if you are not patient enough to wait in line, we as customers have the choice to go else where, so don't take it out on the workers, that doesn't help at all. Now if you were not pleased with the product you got, speak up, I'm sure they would not have an issue giving you something else, and if the workers are doing a great job, give them feedback, don't just complaint when something's wrong, also let be known when people are doing are great job. With that said, let's all get a cup of coffee, and call it a day.

I'm a long time customer of all the different Starbucks. This particular store is the worst. The workers are very slow and unfriendly. The only person to acknowledge the customer is the cashier. Sometimes the store isn't very busy and still no smile or personal touch.  I'm a reward card holder and the stores that I mostly deal with are: (Magazine, Harrison, St.Charles and Slidell).

I have been to many Starbucks in Canada and by far this is the most friendliest one. Every customer was greeted as the entire and left by multiple barista. The gentleman at the register even check to see if I got my order. This is how a Starbucks should be.

Who doesn't love Starbucks!? This by far is my fave location. It's close to my parents house when I'm Visiting and I can easily grab them a treat when visiting them!! The location is always kept clean, the customer service is always appropriate and especially since Covid they have made arrangements to remove some tables and spread out so individuals can come in and work! My normal go tos  are iced coffees and skinny vanilla lattes! They make them just right! The parking can be somewhat full at times and the lines can be really long but I'm patient so I understand I may not be able to drive up and be served immediately as others should understand lol. There was a time when I had to get my drink redone and the employees were so nice about it to fix whatever was wrong.   Pros  Customer service  Covid friendly  Drinks are perf   Cons  Parking at times  Lines are long   I'll be back. Probably today  love me some coffee!

Horrible customer service in the afternoon. I do not recommend this Starbucks. As the customer I except friendly kind service unfortunately the afternoon crew does not offer this. The tall blonde girl and girl with the nose ring are rude. I called the Starbucks manager and left two messages still no returned phone call. In the future I will go to a different location to get good customer service. Huge let down.

On the way back to home, me and my husband stopped here for coffee break. We ordered one almond milk latte,the service was quick and latte was great. It looked clean and the staff was managing customers really well.
You should check to make sure the lids on your coffee you serve are on properly. Spilled all over the suede and leather in my 70k Lexus.. thanks for that.

The drive-through here is always full and slow. However, I cannot believe the difference a year has made in the barista turnover department. Last year was a very unimpressive bunch at this locale. This year I am happy to report a much more upbeat and friendly bunch. The drink makers could use a boost in the speed department. However, the friendliness of the order/money takers somehow makes up for it.

Slowest service I have ever experienced. 6+ people waiting for their orders. Witnessed an employee swamped with 5+ drinks he had to make. He asked the manager who else was supposed to be helping him & she said there was no one else and proceeded to be not helpful and argue with the employee. Instead of being a manager & team player she watched him as he struggled. Arguing in front of customers is super unprofessional. I felt bad for the employee. Won't be returning !!

Although the people who staff this location are very friendly and welcoming, the accuracy of the orders is below acceptable. I once ordered a lemon loaf and the label on the bag said lemon loaf and when I checked, it was a banana bread in the bag. Another time, the lemon loaf had a large discolored spot where it looked like something spilled into the sealed bag. Today, I ordered a grande vanilla latte and later found out that the cup was completely just foamed vanilla milk with no coffee at all. Again, in all instances the label was clear as to what the order should have been. It's unfortunate because this is one of the closest Starbucks to me. Can NOT recommend this location sadly.

Wanted to redeem my birthday drink reward, so the cashier started my order on a Venti cup, to later find out I couldn't redeem b/c it was a casino-Starbucks. I'm (sort of) fine with that, but for some reason it was VERY hard for her to transfer my drink to a Tall cup: (first time ordering the new...) cinnamon almond milk macchiato, extra hot, with 2 pumps of syrup.  I confirmed that she "accidentally (?)" wrote 2 pumps of "CD" but she said Cinnamon Dolce is the name for the syrup. When I found her holding my cup by the lip I should have said something, that can't be proper "food" handling.  I got my drink in a decent amount of time, but it was a cinnamon dolce latte. I had the (friendly) barista fix my drink, but it didn't come back extra hot, the way I order all my drinks at Star'.  After waiting about 8 min. in line, going through the bday set back, having her remake the drink, and paying $4, needless to say, I can't give any more than one star. I pay for a custom cup of coffee and it's got to be right for the price.

Great location. Roomy and aesthetically pleasing. Awesome friendly staff. Very accommodating, even with strange drink requests. All in all, I'd say it was a great experience. And pumpkin spice started early, score!!! The girl with cool glasses was great! :)

This is my favorite place that I love to hate. I only go here cause I am a starbucks phene, and I get a consistent flow of gift cards and the Barnes and Noble does not accept Starbucks gift cards. The staff for the most part is RUDE RUDE RUDE. I have to ask a zillion times for my cup to be filled more, they always roll their eyes at me when I hand them my refillable cup, sorry my dad bought me a refillable coup and a giftcard, I'm a broke college student who needs caffiene. It is in a pretty convenient location on campus so thats why I keep returning.
Everyone was so welcoming and cheerful here. I usually don't write reviews for Starbucks visits but this experience (and of course, the coffee) made my day.
Always good service. With no attitude, unlike the Starbucks at Garden State Park on Rt 38.
Friendly staff.  Can't beat $1 drip coffee refills when you bring your mug.

I love the fact that when you walk in the door, most of the employees greet you by name! They are quick and always get my drink order right everytime!  I have 2 or 3 different drink I usually order and they will immediately ask, which one are you getting today and they gladly make it!

My expectations are lower of Starbucks at airports, so I'm delighted to report this location in the C terminal was strong.   I stopped in around 515 AM. In true Starbucks fashion a line of tired travelers was beginning their day and needed a caffeine fix. The line moved swiftly from approximately 15 back. It was my turn in less than 10 minutes which I thought was excellent.   The clerk was friendly enough, it's early! I could see three staff working the small kiosk. My order a sausage, egg and cheese on cheddar. My coffee a Venti White Chocolate Mocha. Payment was processed flawlessly through the app.   My sandwich was called out in minutes and delivered hot. My coffee took less than 5 minutes, but I was close enough I could see the barista work. She did a magnificent job and was quick.   Around six two seater tables are out front, I luckily scored one. I found the area clean. The sandwich was so hot I waited several minutes. Once it was cooled it was tasty with the bread perfectly crisp. The coffee was on point and I should know since this is my normal usual drink.   If your interested in merchandise they have a small selection of mugs etc to browse while in line. The state mug selection is available. This Starbucks visit was as good as it gets for any store, let alone an airport.

I am not sure where the others that have written reviews for this place have gone, but this store has the friendliest staff of any Starbucks I have been too. They know most customers by name, greeting them as soon as they walk in the door, and normally have their drink started before they even order. Steve, the manager, has done a great job with this crew, especially Toya who makes my drink perfectly every morning.

Great service and the drinks are very consistent. The #1 thing I love about this location is they have more than enough outlets for anyone who needs to charge their laptops. They also have plenty of inside and outside tables, which I think is a plus.   2013/#30

I love this Starbucks. Really close to Intercontinental Hotel and the mall. It is crowded in the mornings as people from the office buildings are coming to get their fuel, however the stuff is really friendly here, they have beautiful mugs and cups for sale, inside seating area and parking right in front.

This Starbucks is not only poorly run, it is dangerous to be in. The sign in the store reads they support social distancing and want only 6 persons in the store at a time.  The picture tells a different story. There were 12 people crammed in this store some of who h you don't see in the Pic.   They also got 3 orders completely wrong just in the time in I was there.   They need a total clean out of management here.

This is my wife's and my favorite Starbucks location.  Close to the house and covered outdoor seating makes a great spot to our pup. Lola loves her puppuccino (whipped cream in a Starbucks cup). It's always nice and clean here and the customer service is always top notch!  Although Starbucks is on the expensive side for coffee, or whatever you buy for that matter, it's the "Starbucks experience" that brings us back. Mellow music, other "like" customers, great free wifi, and of course great coffee. We've also met a lot of other dog lovers here who like to sit outside with their pup which keeps us coming back. So stop by and bring your pup and maybe we'll meet up someday!
Slowest Starbucks I have ever been too in my life. Sat in the drive thru line for 30 minutes. Do not recommend going here.

The drive thru is usually pretty good and the people are nice. Great location for sure, super convenient.   Word of advice: never ever go inside. Every time I've been inside they've messed up my order or forgotten to make my drink completely. I get my drink in my personal cup and every time they've set down the cup and forgot to make the drink. I waited 20+ minutes once. Horrible horrible experience inside and they weren't apologetic. Drive thru is convenient but never go inside

Everyday I drive past River and Campbell and drive the extra distance to both this location at Wetmore or the Sbux on Stone for quality service.  The morning crew here is probably the most cohesive team I've seen, completing beverages quickly and accurately, and it only took the team a few weeks to memorize my drink and my name.   Impressive, especially considering that at a majority of the other locations the baristas still look at me blankly while holding their Sharpies, even after 6-7 years.  I really feel valued as a customer at this location, and that's what makes all the difference.

The Starbucks was really quite disappointing. The coffee took forever to get in was, just lacking. It didn't appear to be fresh and the products I don't know just seemed off and flavor. The muffin that we got was stale and dry and very crumbly. It was not an inviting thing to eat so we passed and after tasting it left the rest in the trash.  The interior was not as clean as most Starbucks. And the staff was fairly slow and not efficient.  What's a girl to do? Well, you want a good cup of coffee sometimes you have to try a few places.
Friendly service, good coffee, but I would have chosen a local business like Black Crown Coffee instead had it been open on Easter Day.

The staff is great and the quality of products are up to par as well. My only complaint about this location is the amount of flies and gnats buzzing around. Living in Florida it is expected to deal with these bugs but it just seems they can be doing better in terms of insect control.

They say you have home, work, and "third place".  The Deptford Mall Starbucks is my third place.  The store is fantastic.  Despite being in the center of a -very- busy mall, the service is excellent.  The employees are friendly to me and to other customers in line, and my grande white chocolate mocha latte is made just right every time.  Professional and well-run, if you're looking for a great Starbucks experience, you'll find it here.  As an added bonus, there's lots of comfortable seating right behind the kiosk.
Horrible starbucks! The owners are assholes too. I recommend walking an extra block to the other Starbucks over the bridge.

Ridiculous. As a provider of paper, plastic and other items that eventually will need to be disposed of, there are NO TRASH CANS outside of the establishment. On my way into this location, I picked up a paper bag and a couple of cups (not from Starbucks), that some piglets had left in the parking lot. I asked the guy at the front counter if I could drop them in their waste can behind the counter and he said no, they only allow Starbucks trash. When I explained to him that this trash was in the Starbucks parking lot, and that there were no trash cans outside, he shrugged and said it was "company policy". W.t.H.??? So I asked him what he suggested I do with the trash, another shrug. Had no choice but to leave it where I found it outside. Did I say ridiculous?

I love this starbucks. I work in the mall and I go every Monday. The people are fantastic. I always order the same thing and they have it made before I ever get to the register. I think they stay busy because of the mall traffic but I have never seen people make coffee so fast and always consistent.

Increased prices b/c location is inside the Hyatt building. Staff are all very friendly, but seem like they're all new-hires - 2nd drink was made wrong (wasn't decaf) and the re-make was still the wrong size.

I've never had to wait so long for my drink. Only 1 person taking orders and 1 barista at 2:30 on a Saturday. Looked like a manager came out (wasn't in a standard uniform) after my drink was made, but there were 5 people still waiting to order and 7 people waiting for their drinks.

Ordered online and got there and only my drink was ready. I asked if my other item was coming and the lady had the worst attitude about it when I just asked very nicely if it was coming. Will be returning but hopefully I have a better experience next time.

OK, I know that I spend too much time and money at Starbucks...if they would follow the practices of this one, I could quit in one day!  We walked in and both employees were cleaning. One was dancing and singing to his music. He kept looking at me as if I should not be there. After 5 minutes we started to leave...another person came in, was greeted as a friend and immediately helped.  I guess that there is some sort of members only policy or similar norm here. Please, post your policies at the door.  Additionally, this place is filthy! Hense the cleaning, but why not help us?

I came here during a slow time when the staff had all the time in the world to leave a lasting impression. The service was mediocre and the lobby area was a hot mess. Not very good representation of the company.

Update: I officially dislike this store. I went in the drive thru this morning and ordered an exorbitant drink, which I meant to use a free drink reward that I have been saving. Went to the window to pay and notified the cashier before I handed her my card that I have a free earned reward on my Starbucks card, but went to check my balance tonight and found out that she didn't redeem my free drink on the card and charged me the full amount!  The customer service at this location has been going downhill, the cashiers are downright depressing. Won't be back.

I don't have a favorite starbucks, they've all seemed the same, until i stopped at this one the other night. I could tell by the warm greeting the young lady taking my order had a smile on and seemed genuine. Marily was waiting at the drive thru, she made quick conversation and I felt went the extra mile to make sure my order was right and even offered a suggestion, I was pleased!! This location is out of the way (i have one walking distance from my house) but i will always go the extra mile to stop at this location, customer service was superb!!

Good cappuccino. Terrible environment. I was enjoying my cappuccino and working on a research paper when a loud customer began a dialog with the Starbucks employee (Joshua). The conversation turned to Joshua's co-workers and supervisor. Joshua began bad mouthing fellow staffers and his boss. It began with Joshua talking about a co-worker who continually clocks out ten minutes early. It went downhill from there. I had to listen to all the nonsense for approximately 20 minutes. It was terrible. Joshua was loud and the customer was even louder. Ugh!  UPDATE: Nobody reached out to me, after my review was submitted several weeks ago, with either an apology. I can only surmise staff matters aren't important to this business.

By far, my favorite Starbucks, anywhere in the world.   I miss it every day.  Cozy fireplace on cold Tucson mornings.  Open early, by 5:30 am, like clockwork.  Dappled sunlight on the patio, lazy locals on Sunday morning.  Colorful bicyclists vying for the key to the bathroom (thanks, Bridget, for your kindness!).  Oh, yeah, and great coffee, too. ~Tracey, from Tokyo

I frequent Starbucks but the company should be worried about British, relative newcomer, Pret. Its small batch makes SB's coffee taste like motor oil and the simple, fresh salads unfortunately highlight the inadequacies of SB food. I say this because I'm rooting for SB as an American cafe - although Pret deserves to do well too....hoping SB pulls it together, especially especially with the food. I mean this in the most loving way, "it is awful" ...but you are American. Adjust accordingly.

Stopped here over the weekend for a pick me up.  The drive thru line was ridiculously long with over 8 cars waiting but rather than wait I took my chances and went inside.  I'm glad I did because there was only 1 person in line and they were already being served.  I ordered a grande iced coffee with skim milk and Splenda.  It was prepared well and tasted exactly how I expected.  The cashier was friendly and made small talk.  When she heard my dog was in the car she offered  me a free Puppacino .  For those who are unfamiliar with this a Puppacino is a cup full of whipped cream for your furry friend.  My girl Bambi was a happy girl!

I just love the Starbucks location they are so friendly and the manager Tiffany is very helpful. My favorite of all time has to be the green tea frappe on warmer days or the flat white on colder days.  They always seem to have a good stock of pastries sandwiches and salads and are seldom out of any item except very late in the afternoon which is very understandable.

Starbucks is my go to place for a latte. You pay a premium for the customer service.... but this store absolutely does not cut it!  The staff is rude, it's like they are doing you a HUGE favor by taking your order and making your drink! They are always out of some key ingredient.... and no "sorry is there anything else we can get for you" its a matter of fact "we are out" I have been to plenty of Starbucks stores around the world this is probably the rudest place and they don't even make the lattes correctly or maybe the rude service has left a bitter taste in my mouth. I can walk to this place from work but I would rather drive a few miles away and get my latte.
The staff is very friendly however the young man making the orders did give me the worst VBM i have ever had. I guess he was having a bad day, it is a simple drink to make so I'm not sure.
Good standard Starbuck's service as usual, with the standard menu.
Was OK until received rude treatment from an employee. Guess what Starbucks? There are other coffee shops around!  Bye.

This Starbucks often has a line wrapped around the building but they do a good job trying to move folks through as quickly as they can. The logistics are a bit messy, though (not their fault), though when it's busy. Don't forget your mask, and don't forget to tip your barista!

The building is nice. The baristas are kind. Furthermore, there is n't a plethora of people so if you want to get work done then definetly come to this Starbucks. It isn't as popular since the storm because it took a while for it to get back into its regular hours.

Probably the worst Starbucks in Santa Barbara. The Staff is beyond clueless and rude . 4 out of 5 times my order ends up wrong even though I usually have to repeat myself several times to the staff member taking my order. Is there no manager at this location?? I am honestly curious at how such ridiculous customer service is allowed at such a big named shop.
Got to the store 7:55pm - realize only 5 minutes to close, but the Barista told me they could only make me a cappuccino because they were "out of coffee".
Great location, fast service (even at their peak hours), and friendly employees :) This is usually my go to SB & I'm never disappointed.

A great little Starbucks to come check your email if you're hanging out in Reno.   As someone who refuses to pay for internet when I'm already paying plenty for my hotel room, I like to seek out somewhere to catch up on my messages and make sure the world is not collapsing. Oh right, and have a nice pick-me-up cup of caffeine. This Starbucks fit my needs perfectly. Plenty of parking, really friendly staff, quick service, and a great inside atmosphere. Oh yes, and free internet!

This place changed my life. I've always thought that yelp was for older woman who leave petty 1 star reviews. I just downloaded the app so i could leave a review to this place. I was going through a messy long term relationship break up. The day after before school I came here, and Deb, the lady at the drive through window was the nicest person i think i have ever met. I was doing poorly financially at the time, so I went here because the water is very good and free. I also ordered a blueberry muffin because i felt bad. Deb noticed this and said that if I just wanted the water that's okay and i shouldn't feel obligated to order something else. This put me in the mood to change my life for the better. Immediately, i made a list of goals that i wanted to accomplish. And since then i have worked out every single day, read more books, became a better brother, friend, and person. The only thing that hasn't changed is that i come here every morning to say hi to Deb. To deb, thank you so much for making the world a better place.

Easily the friendliest Starbucks I've ever been to! Everything is as you'd expect from a Starbucks, so no magical coffee - but I definitely appreciate the kind and friendly staff before my work commute!

Meh, parking nightmare. There are better locations but honestly, I'm getting a little tired of Starbucks in general. Anybody tried Dutch Bros? They're awesome. Better customer service (and not because Starbucks customer service is bad, but just because Dutch Bros. is THAT good) and the best rewards program. Starbucks should, at $4 a cup, have a better rewards program for frequent drinkers.  I will say I like the ability to sit in the seating area with a laptop so I can hopefully someday be the target for a piece in The Onion.  I'm sorry, this review sounds so negative. Starbucks isn't bad and this location is not to blame for all of Starbucks' issues. There are just better, less chain-y options out there.

It's so nice that Starbucks makes alternative employment opportunities for people incapable of even the most basic customer service functions. While I do kind of wish they weren't so close to the gates so I wouldn't think "I'll just grab a quick coffee before my flight," because they don't actually sell coffee here. They sell apathy and an ignorance of basic math skills. They give the coffee away for free.

Alright, so I know I should be all about the evil empire of Starbucks.  And for awhile I avoided it like the plague!  Until that fateful day that I entered my local store!  I love it here, the baristas see me crossing the street and have my drink ready for me in the morning - Grande Bold with Room!  It's great, I don't have to speak some mornings, which is probably for the best.  If I go outside of my 7:05-7:15am window the baristas ask "regular morning" and lately "regular afternoon" - I'm addicted to the Low-Fat Mocha Frap in the afternoon!  I enjoy Komodo Dragon, Verona, Sumatra in the morning!  A frap or seasonal late in the afternoon!  The staff at this store are exceptional!  I have gone to other Starbucks and been very annoyed and disappointed!  I wish the Pumpkin Spice and Gingerbread Lates were on the menu 24/7!  If you're in Old City, check this place out...  It's cheaper than Old City Coffee and the staff are a lot more fun!
I waited 20 min at the drive thru....at 6:03 on a Sunday morning...for a GD cup of coffee.  They must have forgotten the recipe.

From what I've been told, this is one of the highest grossing Starbucks outside of Philly. For as busy as they are they can have a lack of urgency and an attitude of boredom but typically have their act together and getting a drink is usually a smooth process. Attractive and comfortable inside. That being said, I have been going here twice a day since I moved here six months ago and not once has an employee ever used my name, which is a major faux pas in Starbucks culture.  Parking can be a challenge. By the time you get all of the employees parking their cars it leaves a few spaces for customers thus I end up going through the drive through more than I would like which itself can be "challenged" at times.

At first I was skeptical about the location of this new Starbucks. But it turned out to be perfect! Looks small from the outside but very spacious (and clean) on the inside with plenty of seating. Also has a very nice outdoor patio. Nicely designed, large drive-thru. Very friendly baristas. Great location & great coffee!

I'm always in a good mood when I go to this Starbucks because I know my day is about to get better. I walked here to pick up a Vanilla Latte for my friend. I didn't feel like getting coffee and wanted to try something different. Kenzie immediately sold me on the Mango Dragonfruit Lemonade Refresher! Her enthusiasm was infectious. I could've bought anything from her! But she was right! I loved my drink! Jeff and Ashely also provided great customer service and positive energy. I spent $10 for the two venti drinks.  This Starbucks has a nice outdoor seating area. They also have a drive-thru that can be very busy at times but the baristas are all friendly and try their best to get you through fast.   It's convenient for picking up breakfast sandwiches, pastries, fruit, snacks or boxed lunches if you need something quick. I frequent this location multiple times a month.

No reason not to give 5 stars but they didn't have grilled cheese.  Otherwise, nice people and apologetic.  And people, I always love when Starbucks has a drive-thru.  Especially if it's connected to a bank.

Service was not good. Waited 30+ mins for a drink. When I asked about my order, the so called shift supervisor, brushed me off by saying it'll be soon and didn't even check for me. At the end which was more than a 40 min ordeal, they came to me, handed me money and said "We don't have the ingredients. Here's your money. There's nothing else we can do for you.". Very rude staff; not friendly.

I thought this was a pretty decent, if small Starbucks.  The location at an oddly shaped intersection can be a little tricky, and the parking lot is of course too small.  I didn't have any trouble with the service, I was greeted when I came in and my coffee was ready in a minute.  Couldn't believe how crowded it was at 9pm on a Tuesday night!  I guess free wifi is a huge draw with a college nearby.  Loved the outdoor patio option.. perfect for a nice night!
I smellllll.... Cough-feeeee.... Say it with an New York accent, it's fun! =]

This location is my EVERYDAY stop.. everyone at this location is very friendly and they try to get to know you.. I feel very welcomed in here there is a couple of the employees that know my order n always greet me by my name I feel at home here :)

The coffee was made with little to no care at all. I The attitude from the employees there is basic. You'll be lucky to get any eye contact whatsoever. The coffee was so hot that the cup it came in was becoming flimsy. It's just not a pleasant experience   And at the prices they charge...

Lovely location but it's lacking the convenience of Drive-Thru which only allows the store 4/5 on the Christy Scale.   Service is pleasant  Pastry case nicely stocked  Comfy seating options  Private area for meeting  Patio bistro tables  Beautiful artwork  Merchandise for sale   Creve Coeur gem right here.  ~Christy

Geesh!  OK the girl was new but the guy working w/her was no help.  It was 8:30ish and there was one person in line in front of us.  We ordered 2 iced teas & 1 order of bacon egg bites.  When we turned around to go wait for our order there were 11 people in line behind us!  The guy told her she needed to multitask as he walked through the door to the back where it sounded like a party.  Turns out at 9:00 "the party" went on shift and the poor girl at the counter got some help.  Out of the then 4 baristas only 1 knew what hustle means.  I get that the new girl hadn't found her groove yet but other than the one who knew what customer service is, the other 2 were just there at their job!
Great staff located on campus huge plus if you go to the school. If you are a partner they are a fake bucks so no deals. They have lots of treats. Baristas won't burn your shots they are legit.

I ordered the strawberry refresher. Service was slow. When I finally received my order, my drink was so full of ice that there was hardly any liquid in the cup. Definitely the worst Starbucks experience I've had in a long time.
I've never had a bad experience here. I just wish they didn't close so early.  I mean, it's never too late to have a Vanilla Bean Frapp.

This Starbucks has turned into a place of positivity over the last several months. The employees are happier and the new layout helps brighten the store. They have new interactive displays where you can read good news. I'm excited to return when normalcy return.
Workers were nice, but didn't really know what they were doing. Lots of little errors that make a big difference.
Anybody Starbucks starts at a very mediocre position, however this one consistently delivers better quality and customer service as opposed to some of the other Tampa Bay locations.

This was the largest Starbucks I've seen in Nashville.  It has a barstool area, table area, small conference room and an outside porch.  Service was on par with other Starbucks, meaning it was efficient and friendly.  It was cool but not cold and felt great after being outside in the sweltering summer heat.  Go Starbucks!!

Went to this Starbucks today through the drive thru since there was no where to park. It wasn't a busy time either.   I order my drink and there was only one car ahead. When I get to the window, I pay and I am given my drink. It's a typical paper cup with lid but coffee has been dripping out on the sides. It's messy and sticky. And I notice it was not the size I ordered (Venti). I hand in back and tell them, and he takes my drink back- within a minute I have a new drink.   I have to rush off back to college at that point and I am not really expecting much from my drink. But when I get to school and I open the lid, I notice the drink wasn't at the top. Instead it started over 2 inches down.   Did they really do that? Make me the wrong size and when I complained just put my drink in a bigger cup so I would shut up? Looks like they did that too me.

I don't even know how to rate this place because I can't stand this location but I love starbucks. Every time I come here they never have what I want on the menu!! If you like tomato basil mozz paninis, dream bars, ham and cheese or spinach pastries, you better not come to this location. Every time  I've gone they are out of these items, got my drink order wrong, and they are slow af. I swear they hire the most incompetent people. Whoever is managing this location just doesn't know what they are doing or just does not care because it is the only starbucks in the area, that we are forced to come here.  They don't know how to hire competent workers and don't know a thing about taking inventory. Get it together Delran starbucks!!! Thank you that's all

The ability to get anything right in this location requires a genius. My cup filled with ice after my asked for no ice. My boyfriend got a coffee, it came without creamer. We had to go to the grocery store after sitting in SB line for 30mins. Food missing, drinks with no straw. The service here is just atrocious every single time

Stopped in for my morning earl gray latte.  The store was clean and service was fast.   They staff was taking care of the customers at a very good pace.   It looked like the drive thru was moving at a good pace.  My tea was made correctly and I was out the door in a few minutes.  I will visit this store again because of the customer service and speed.

I frequently buy coffee at Starbucks in Memphis, Jackson and Nashville, TN. This was my first and last trip to the 21st street location. The counters and floors were not particularly clean, and there was a bug crawling on the brownie in the display case. I called there clerk over to tell him about it, and he told me that the food in the case was only for display because they couldn't get rid of the bugs in the case. After that display, we didn't order any brownies,  and I am not sure if I will be able to choke down the coffee I had already bought.

worst fucking service ever. i walked in and was completely ignored. 3 ppl walked in after me and were greeted immediately. i would've never gotten my drink if i hadn't spoken up. i ordered my regular drink and it tasted like shit.
Drinks are fine but service has been awful the last few times I've been in. Definitely needs to improve
Very fast and efficient.  Only thing lacking is space to sit.
Can't even get a simple order right. Visited the place twice and both times they messed up my order.
I normally love this Starbucks, but my last visit not so much. Not happy when I get my drink far from full. I will admit I ordered it without ice and using my star points, but a venti is a venti.

I'm really over this SB. I go to this one out of convenience, but for some reason the concept of "no foam" eludes them. Today I ordered online, "Grande, Non Fat, No Foam Latte". When I walked in, I received my drink with an inch of foam. When I asked them to fix it, he took the foam off the top and handed my drink back to me. Don't get me wrong, I normally appreciate my over priced coffee, but when you are missing an inch of that pricy drink, it's just absurd. Worst. Starbucks. In. STL.

While relaxing in Starbucks at Sunnyside and Pendleton Pike, it was delightful to observe  employees greeting customers with a smile, recognition by name and speaking to co-workers in a respectful manner.  When you think others are engaged in conversation and preoccupied. ...take notice...it is a pleasure to be surrounded by good food, drink and company!  Keep up the great service!

Excellent friendly staff.  I go there every day.  My only beef is with my fellow customers who think it's fine to take up two or three seats with their bags and coats  some don't even purchase anything and management doesn't seem to mind that regulars can't get a chair or outlet.

With a great Downtown location, this is a great spot to step in and grab a coffee or tea. Their staff are very friendly, and great at giving recommendations if you aren't a good decision maker like myself. They have a small pastry section if you need a small bite of food, and are attached to the Courtyard which is great for hotel guests in the morning.  Keep in mind that this isn't the kind of Starbucks that you bring your laptop and backpack to. There's one tiny table, and it's not necessarily a comfortable place to just hang out in. There's one small table in the corner of the room, and that's it. The only downside to my visits here that I've encountered is that it's not necessarily sparkling clean. Often times the counter will be a bit messy or full of crumbs and spilt drinks (granted it's a food shop, and customers tend to drops things).

Horrible Service unable to complete the simplest request without having to repeat it 2 if not 3 times. Apparently a Venti Vanilla Latte no foam and 120 degree causes great vexing on the person working the drive thru. The staff is incredibly slow inefficient and indifferent. The mgr has no more interest in customer service than the staff. It is quite unfortunate and at $5 a cup of coffee it should b an incredibly BIG DEAL .....

The WORST Starbucks I've been to! Customer service is obviously not a priority at this location. I ordered the tuxedo mocha and they claimed not to have it until next week but on the app and everywhere on the website said it was offered this week. The person behind me ordered the same exact thing and was able to get it...um what? Was it because I was brown or because the cashier just didn't like me? I was so confused and upset about this encounter. Never have I ever received such poor quality of service. I can understand the long lines and the wait time but I definitely cannot understand the quality of customer service I was given!
I am not sure they know what they are doing both in ringing the register or making the drinks. Worst latte macchiato ever.

This is a full-sized Starbucks, not a cart or hut. I love this location! The employees are all super friendly and have been from the get-go. The various batistas quickly learned my order and always make me feel welcome. They have typical Starbucks fare...cups, mugs, snacks. And a pretty good selection of breakfast and lunch foods. When new drinks come out, they are quick to offer samples. There's lots of coffee competition on this block, but if you're a diehard Starbuckser on Poydras...your coffee dreams have finally come true!

Was at a friends birthday party when we left in the morning to get coffee and a pastry. We arrive at the location and all is smooth until we received our second order, a double chocolate mocha. My man Jordan took a sip and though it was single chocolate. After further inspection we realized tbat the crackhead barista spelled JordAn with an E, JordEn. How drunk was this guy to make a mistake so simple. Will not be returning for at least 3 weeks maybe more. Thanks for listening

Loving this Starbucks!  Elegantly situated in the Omni Downtown, the chandeliers and cozy tables set the mood.  Our order was cheerfully taken and completed in record time.  I especially appreciated the patience and knowledge of the barista, as I asked many questions looking to try something new.    Outdoor seating available and shaded.  Great place to sit and watch the activities on Georgia Street. Great work space for your internet needs.  Quiet and charming this also makes a great meeting place for friends.

EDI: I'm making this edit today to say that an employee was extremely rude to me. I sat there for a few minutes, looking at my mobile order. They kept walking past everyone waiting for mobile orders. It was so frustrating and I was about to be late for work (which is why I did mobile order!). I finally asked an employee "can you please just hand me my drink, it's sitting right there" and he was very rude. He threw his hands up and said have a nice day. I was not rude in the way I asked, it took him 5 seconds to hand it to me. This Starbucks continuously disappoints me. It does not do well for the Starbucks brand. This is the longest line I have ever experienced in a drive through. Takes a minimum of 20 minutes no matter the time of day. Not only that, but it's so hard to get someone's attention to give you your mobile order if you go inside. Not only that, but at one point I ordered a mobile order 20 min ahead of time and it still wasn't ready when I got there. It's just so frustrating.
We were staying nearby and needed to get up early the next morning. I called to ask what time they open and they gave me the wrong time so it was closed when we got there.

I've been a customer at this Starbucks since about 10-15 days after they opened some 15 years ago.  As an everyday (literally!) customer, I feel I have a duty to point out when there seems to be a problem.  There have been rumors that the newly appointed district manager (Rose, I've been told) has taken it upon herself to flex her managerial muscles and make BIG changes to "my" Starbucks.  This surge of managerial minutia / micro management was first apparent when the well-liked and seemingly very effective manager was shown the door. The next catastrophe was the mass exodus of FOUR employees that have been there since the store opened and one who had only been employed a few years.  I apologize for making the following rodent based analogy when discussing an establishment that serves food . . . BUT I believe the rats leave just before the ship sinks! (not that I'm referring to Missy, Kelly, Ed, Ng and especially Carrie as rats but the analogy fits!)  I can't be the only one who sees a problem here . . . ROSE!
They're closed until further notice ): there's a sign blocking the drive thru saying not much more than that, hopefully they will reopen soon and I'll change my stars! I hope y'all are all good :)

I love Starbucks and this location is very nice.  Ample parking and large interior.  I've never had an issue here!   I also wanted to compliment Tiffany Mann.
