Watching her interact with and manage the other employees and also the customers has been a joy.  She's so upbeat and happy, very personable.  She helped make someone's day today for a regular customer and seems to genuinely care about people.  She loves her regular customers and truly loves her job!  Tiffany is a great leader and asset to your company.  Props to her!
Best Starbucks around. They always have a dog biscuit for Piper  super friendly staff

Probably the biggest Starbucks in the touristy area of New Orleans . Stop by at night to get their special New Orleans mug as a souvenir. Be prepared for a lot of homeless people, slow and Confused service, I paid for my mug and the guy forgot to give it to me and walked away. There's trash everywhere on the floor and on the condiment bar. For a upscale Starbucks they need to step this up.

Went in for a coffee, while there were 3 people behind the counter, besides the drive thru person,  I was left standing there for over 3 min.  If you are going to stand in a circle engaged in conversation while clearly seeing a patron waiting then I will drive out of my way to Fenton, never an issue there.

Over 25 minutes. Never got my coffee. I was stuck in the drive through with no way out. They didn't seem to make anything until you got to the window. We were then late for a show that we had plenty of time for. Horrible start to our evening!!!
I frequent this location often and Kingston is always there making sure my order is right with a smile!! Hope to see him promoted soon within the organization.

I was coming in the drive through and it was around closing time and I was greeted by Josiah he proceeded to give me my drink. I soon noticed my Lemonade Dragon Fruit was made with water when specifically told him to make it with lemonade, that was four drinks. I asked politely if i could get it with lemonade as he went on to say some bs about closing. His manager approaches and he became less rude then he proceeded to say how he told us he could give us a separate lemonade. As you could imagine i was pretty frustrated cause it was 4 people and one cup how would that possibly make anything better, so, i got my money back after i paid with CARD they gave it to me in cash... if i could give zero stars i would. To add to that he had the worst costemer service, rolling his eyes and looking me up and down as if i hurt him, not my fault you FOUR drinks wrong... "but we were closing"

First time at this location. Would not go back. Paid for a grande, got a tall and the staff did not seem to know how to make the iced tea/lemonade drink my son ordered. Service was extremely slow and we were the only car in the drive thru. Disappointing.
Went here today for my morning drinks for my husband and I. The baristas did a fantastic job. Ris is one of my personal favs. Thank you Burlington starbs!

Typically, my experience with Starbucks is perfectly fine. However, I wasn't too happy with this particular location. Having Starbucks inside Target is convenient, but I was disappointed by the slow service. This was surprising because it's not really a busy location and there was only one customer ahead of me.  In addition to wait, my simple drink order was messed up. When I informed them that they gave me the wrong size, I had to wait even longer for a new drink and the barista did not express any apologies for the inconvenience. The next time around, I'll probably just go to another location and bring my drink into the store to enjoy as I shop. I hoped to give this place the benefit of the doubt, but it looks like other customers had negative experiences as well.

This location is literally next door to where I work so I visit often, haha. Sometimes I work at 6:30am, sometimes I work at 7:45am. Either way, the baristas in the drive thru are helpful and friendly! I believe they recognize me too, haha. Anytime I order a food item, they will ask if I would like it warmed up. I also know that my coworkers are treated well when they go too. Our drinks are made to order.  Depending on what time you go, the drive thru can get pretty busy. I personally have not had a problem, but I have driven by and seen a long line before. It definitely depends on day and hour. The Medicine Ball is a wonderful beverage! Made of hot tea, lemonade (steamed) and other healthy items, it helps when you are sick! Not a cure, of course, but it will ease your symptoms so you can temporarily function for work. One last thing about this location, there is plenty of space to sit with friends or conduct a coffee meeting with peers! Nice little patio in good weather and large sitting room inside! One of Reno's best Starbucks locations.

The staff is very nice and pleasant. But the drive through service is awful. I sat in line for over 15 minutes this morning and there's no way to leave the line if it gets too long of a wait. I can't see how any order takes 10 minutes to wait for but this place makes that happen. Just frustrating that once you're in line you're screwed if someone's order takes 5 tries to get right.

I have to say of all my experiences as Starbucks, my travel through the south Starbucks have been horrible. The drinks just don't taste the same as up north. They aren't made with that same flavor and they get very frazzled with changes of drinks!

I am not a big Starbucks fan, but when a friend suggested a meeting at Starbucks, I agreed. The location is perfect for all the business people in the area. It is located on the corner of lakeside and McCarran in a business park. The store is fairly good size with a couple tables to sit at outside if you wish. There is almost two separate sections to this store, the front where you order and a back room where there are several tables and chairs. Business meetings and computers were going on all over. I will have to say there free Wi-Fi is really good. The coffee is OK, it did not taste burnt, I asked for my latte at 140 degrees, and it was served to me correctly. If you are looking for a place to do business or have a meeting, this place will fit the bill.

Great "neighbourhood" Starbucks. Some of the SB shops have become kind of cold and sterile. This one located in North St. Pete is warm and welcoming, cozy with a neighbourhood coffee shop "feeling" as the original owners intended.

The lady 'Sam' always screws up my orders. Ex: i specify soy milk and am are charged for it, but she forgets to write on the cup. And i get dairy after being charged for soy! I say 'No whip cream', she adds whip cream even though it is written on the cup. It seems like she may be day-dreaming on the job!!  The guy with long-ish hair  gets it right and does not mess up the orders. Hence this place got its 3 stars, else it would've been 1 or less.

While I may not be the biggest fan of Starbucks - you know, it's the whole "giant chain" thing. I just prefer to support local! Also, I don't love their cappuccino, the espresso just tastes bitter. Of course you can't taste it in a big, fancy, 900 calorie frappuccino... Another reason why I don't like Starbucks, it's yet another American chain that is geared to getting people f-a-t! But enough of what I don't like about SatrB's and on to what I really like about this location: I just love the location! High Street is so quant, full of adorable shops and free parking. Then there is the patio situated under the gigantic sprawling trees of the east Glenora address - shaded and breezy in the summer, gorgeous in the fall with a plethora of falling leaves. In the winter you can sit in the big windows and watch the snow fall as you work. They also have 4 stools up at the counter which is a cool spot to park yourself and your laptop. So, if I'm going to be at a Starbucks, this is definitely the one!

Coffee and food are good- what you would expect from any other Starbucks.  However I wanted to review because of the employees- every time I'm here they are nothing but sweet, polite and smiley.  Wish more places would act like that.   Line moves fast.

Dear Starbucks , As usual you draw me in on a near daily basis! Never disappointed by my regular  cinnamon dolce latte. Always tempted to buy the newest folk rock indie cd , grabbing Ethos bottled water I don't need, chocolate Graham crackers , granola bars , protein platters ... Get me out of here!!!!!! The music piped in must have subliminal messages to influence you to Consume , buy, ....ok, drive through from now on. Oh Starbucks .... Till we meet again! Tomorrow you say? Ok.. See you then!
Very busy store you can get and right out! Lots of parking in the rear of the building!

AJ has a fantastic persona! She is friendly, informative, and witty. She helped out with my London fog latte (in Proper English :D) and provided a thoroughly good Starbucks experience!   The London Fog Latte is a bit less sweet then I imagined, but still tastes fantastic!

Starbucks is definitely a place where you know what you are going to get and its process is in place to ensure that you receive exactly what you order and get great customer service. They also have processes in place if you don't get 5 star service. Visited this location through the drive-thru just as they were closing. Great service!

Two words...Barista Baby!  So you think Starbucks coffee is bitter?  Wrong...it's better and you wanna know why?  The obvious of course is that you've gotta start with some pretty awesome coffee beans...after that it's all about the Barista. Doesn't matter which Starbucks I visit...whether it's a drive-thru, in a mall, airport or Target.  Those Baristas are highly trained professionals who know what they're doing.  I've been to many other coffee houses and time and time again, my #1 complaint is lack of consistency.  I mean it's not like they don't have great coffee, but please make me a non-fat latte that tastes the same all the time.  Until then, I remain a Starbucks devotee...peace out.
I love this Starbucks, and the people are great! As long as the coffee is this good, what else can I say because it says it all for me in this convenient location!!!
Great Starbucks! Could have been cleaner but the barista was super nice and made conversation with me which is a great example of how customer service should be!

I stopped in here to get a Macchiato and to have a little time to myself to read. It was pretty busy inside and a new person was still in training. After waiting a few minutes and everyone behind me already gone another young woman noticed me  waiting so she checked on the person making my drink, since  he seemed to be having a little trouble  she happily took the macchiato he was trying to make and dumped it and started over again showing him how it was done. I'm so happy she did start over again to make sure my drink was just as I  had ordered and expected it to be. ( to keep it vegan you just hold the caramel drizzle and use soy milk)

I purchased 2 hot peppermint hot chocolates without whip cream on 11/8.  When I got to work took the lids off one was barely a half full.  The other was missing 1/4 a cup from top.  I was so angry and disappointed specially after paying over $7. for both.  I've included the photo.
Really great service every time we go to this location. It's nearly always busy but the employees are always friendly.
New "no straw lid"on my iced coffee doesn't fit!  Way too loose.  Of course drink spilled as the lid fell off.  BS.

The staff here is super sweet and helpful, I haven't had an issue with customer service at all. However, I recently have started doing my daily mobile order because my job moved locations, and 4/5 times my order is either wrong or late. It seems like this store is incredibly busy, which Is totally understandable, but they also seem to have some very serious staffing/management issues. It's definitely not the employees fault, they are just trying their best, but when I give them 20 minutes to get my drink done via the mobile app and then I show up and it's STILL not done, it's clear that this store is operational deficient.

This starbucks is just okay. There isn't really anything special about it. Sometimes they mess up my orders here when I do the online mobile ordering. They have added the wrong milk to my coffee and also gave me the wrong food once. Everytime I order in store my order doesn't come out wrong only when I order on the app. There is always a huge line here hence why I do the online ordering. Sometimes the staff here is in a good mood but other times they aren't. You can tell by the way they are talking to the customers if they are or not.   Overall I will continue to come to this starbucks because its convent for me but I hope that their customer service improves and they stop messing up my order.

Honestly shocked this location doesn't have higher ratings. I go through the drive thru when I come here during early mornings of the week and am always impressed by how upbeat and friendly the staff is. They are always so energized and kind, it makes it a great place to stop.   I've also always found the drive thru to be quick and efficient and have never waited more than a few minutes. People have no patience nowadays, and I bet that's the case with those that have complained about the speed.

I have to say this location never gets my order wrong. I do mobile orders several times a week and they're always accurate and ready for me. Food tastes fresh and delicious every time.  There is plenty of room to sit, but the parking lot is a bit annoying. The morning rush is so intense, you sometimes can't access the spots but I guess that's a common concern with Starbucks.

Good product, poor customer service. The wife and I came to this particular location on St. Patrick's Day around 1:15pm to order our usual; while we're sitting in line we hear the window attendant taking the lady in front of us' order in what seemed to be a cheery mood. As we pull up to the speaker their tone is completely different, snappy, and pompous as they (oh and their name is Stephen) tell me I can't order a Frap because "they're brand name and call them Frappuccinos" which normally would t bother me because it's a common occurrence when I try to order via shorthand with the Starbucks franchise but Stephen in particular was just seemed to be in a bad mood all of a sudden; seeming like being petty toward his customers made him feel better. Then as we pull up to the window to pay he rips my debit card out of my hand then proceeds to hand me our order and say "here's your stuff" and closes the window.  I've never had an issue with this location before but I hope proper actions are taken to make sure Stephen doesn't feel the need to treat his paying customers like a nuisance anymore.

This is actually the place I tried my first Starbucks coffee, so it holds a special place in my heart. It's not perfect, but really what place is? They do a great job for being constantly so busy, and I appreciate that.

This place is always busy!! I recommend getting the Starbucks app; you can rack up stars to get free drinks and you don't have to wait in line. I normally place my order on the mobile app 6-8 minutes prior to getting there. It's definitely one of the smaller Starbucks around. There are only three 4 top tables outside and a hand full of seats inside. My drinks are always made well and plus it's a minute from work.
Extremely slow service along with not enough inventory to create the drinks you want.

I try to avoid this location as much as possible as I find them slow and they always seem to be out of ingredients. Yesterday was no exception. I was the ONLY person in line and I had to wait of 5 minutes for someone to take my order.  I wanted a Very Berry Refresher nope had to have Cool Lime. I asked for light ice and I got 3/4 of ice. I added a photo showing clearly on the sticker light ice so I clearly didn't stutter.
Maybe it's because I love SB, but this Starbucks rates higher for me than most others.    It's in a great location, friendly service and clean.
Enjoying my birthday reward a venti PSL. Good place to chill or work. Friendly and fast baristas. I'm tankful for this Starbucks it's within walking distance of my home.
Good Starbucks in the area. The baristas are friendly, service is pretty good for having a drive-thru, and it's in a fairly nice location.

Horrible customer service - I keep giving them another chance, but it never improves. Waited 10+ minutes this morning for a small latte. And that was before the Monday morning rush. Ask if your drink is coming up? They'll purposely prep others before yours. A bit too much ego for my taste.

Every time we go here they forget our order they will serve the people before after and everyone in the drive through. I speak up and they say oh yeah what was your order? Every dang time! 8 people working and they can't manage a black tea?
I like the location. I order on the app and either go through the drive through or go inside.

I have to change my rating unfortunately. There's always an issue at this location lately. We ordered a mobile order the other day, and it took over 20 minutes. This time they're down 1 espresso machine. But of course! If it's not the espresso machine, the oven is broken, they're out of all teas, they're out of espresso all together... The list goes on and on with the mesa location. I miss the staff from last year!!

New Starbucks location right in lobby of my hotel which is super convenient while I am staying here for a convention and need my morning caffeine.  This spot is kind of fancy looking with a nice counter area and crystal chandeliers hanging in front of it. Full menu as with most locations.  Lots of seating - high-top tables, window counter, outside patio seating street side, and even a little nook with comfy leather chairs.   Free wifi.  Very friendly staff that welcomes everyone who enters with a hearty hello and will engage in cordial conversation. Quick and attentive with the drinks.  Adjacent to the Omni Hotel lobby so you can take your drink to one of the nice couches there and relax while watching some tv on the flat screen.  Plus, my hot coffee does not have that burnt bitter taste here as other locations seem to have. That's a win in my book.
The best moca frap I have ever had! Nice people! Will come back here for sure! Although like all the Starbucks stores it a bit expensive

I'm in this Starbucks a lot and I've really got to hand it to the staff here, they're ALWAYS pleasant. They work really hard and I've never seen any of them mess up an order.   In regards to some of the other reviews: If you see a homeless person in a cafe and your first thought is "They don't belong here!" maybe it's time to check your privilege.  A muffin is like $2 and I bet that "homeless" "unpaying customer" is probably hungry/thirsty.   Tip: If it's over crowded, there's a beautiful library four blocks away (on North Church) with lots of seating and a quiet teen-free environment, perfect for reading and studying.

My only Starbucks on the way to work, staff is super friendly and only once in the last 2 years has my order been incorrect and was corrected with no problem when I called about it.   The only downfall to this location is that the parking and drive through can be a nightmare depending on the day.   There's no consistency of when or what time in the morning so I only know when i get close to driving by if I should stop or keep driving!

I didn't realize that the stores at the mall don't open until noon on Sundays, so I got there before the stores opened. Luckily, Starbucks opens earlier, and I stopped by to get a coffee while I waited for the stores to open. Just a Starbucks, but it was big enough and had seating. What else do you need in a Starbucks?

Valerie was such a sweetheart! My friend and I went in to try a tiramisu frappuccino, and we left with that and a tall flan frappuccino as well since we were curious about what it tasted like. Awesome customer service, and both drinks were pretty great!

The staff here is awesome. Always treated well, whether in the drive-thru or in store. Fast service even if the parking lot looks full and there is a line of cars at the drive-thru. Glad this location is so close to my house!

I witnessed one of the baristas wiping down a toilet(not using gloves) with a wash rag, she lifted the seat and really got it clean. Then, check this out, proceeded to wipe down all the mugs and cups for sale with said pee rag. That's foul.

Conveniently located for some people. Contains its regular crowd and occasional monkeys. That's right,  I saw some lady with a monkey, just looking around and exploring. So much for "No more monkey business".  Employees are skillful and nice, unless you come in 5 min before closing, then they just might be skillful.

This been a regular stop on the way to work for me going on 5+ years now. Up front, the three stars are for the staff however, a couple drag the rest down, one with a regularly flippant attitude.   Another common occurrence is at the point of sale when hot food items are ordered. The patron's name is not asked and when the order comes, a weak callout is made with only the name of the food and consequently not heard in restaurant over the often loud music and sometimes the sound level of the staff having a good time at work... I love seeing happy employees so perhaps this is a fair trade-off. However, a half warm breakfast sandwich tastes like hell. Often, they do not have in stock breakfast items like the heated sandwiches. Who does the ordering and for that matter, is there a manager even present seeing to these and the following glaring indiscretions?  As for the condition of the restaurant, I'd have to say it's a bit dirty and unattended. The window exteriors appear to not have been cleaned since the remodel, the window interiors are not far behind. The tables don't get wiped on a regular basis through the morning as well as the floor swept and debris remaining throughout the morning, it doesn't appear that it's mopped nightly either. The supply station is often out of items as I have observed often on my numerous visits at 6 am, the condition of the floors as described herein, unswept and still showing the signs of the previous business day. I did ask about this once and was told "Oh, we got hammered this morning". Again, 6 am and I look around seeing that I was one of three people in the place and but two cars in line outside, the staff kids partying it up... Really?  As said, there is good people working there that take a personal pride in the job they do but all that goes out the door given the aforementioned issues. A manager that isn't busy behind the counter preparing orders to oversee that the place is taken care of would be a start. A good hard cleaning of the entire restaurant, inside and out including the filthy paved areas outside, including the furniture is more than overdue. I suggest steam cleaning and sterilization. Can anyone say "Reset"?

It was a cold morning and all I wanted was a white chocolate peppermint mocha. I was on my way to school so I stopped at this location, no drive thru so I was forced to get out in the cold. It was a packed house.  I order my drink and they are out of white choc. Bummer. So I ordered a peppermint mocha=yuck. Tasted like dirt, not sweet and just when I thought they must have given me the wrong order, I tasted the peppermint on my last sip.  Never again will I waste my time getting out the car and paying $8 for a coffee and a danish when I can go to my local drive thru coffee shop and be way happier.    Lets remember why were NOT friends.

Small location inside a rest area but with a full menu. The milk and sweeteners are done by the baristas, not at a station, so make sure to ask for what you need. Service was slower than at dedicated locations but still fairly friendly. Not bad for a quick pick me up on the road.

Placed mobile order. Phone said would be ready at 4:06. When I pulled up, saw mobile line wrapped around the building. No problem, though. I parked in front and waited until 4:08. Imagine my aggravation when I tried to go in to pick up my order and there was a sign saying everyone had to go through the drive-thru. So, now it meant waiting until 4:25 for a coffee I ordered at 3:57 because I had to go sit in that long line.  Had I known, at least I could have gotten in line when I arrived.  If there is a problem at the store, the app should not accept orders! What's the point of a mobile order if you have to wait in a long line? They owe me a free coffee at the VERY least.

The Starbucks on 56th and Illinois is the best Starbucks in the area, no question. My daughter's current favorite is the double chocolate chip creme frappuccino, which is apparently challenging for so many locations except this one. Mine is the salted caramel mocha, with my personal modifications, and I am never disappointed. I sure wish we lived closer to this store. I may have to make the drive, though, because these baristas and the drinks they make are awesome!

For many years we suffered without a Starbucks drive-thru on the west side of town. All of the Starbucks were closer to work and had spectacularly long lines.   Suddenly, like a mirage in the desert the Starbucks on a hill appears! Conveniently located next to the interstate, this Starbucks is PERFECTLY located for a Frappuccino or Latte on the way to work!  It hasn't been open long, but in all my visits so far, I've been greeted with friendly baristas who promptly delivered my coffee beverage.  I look forward to many more visits to this Starbucks in the years to come.

This Starbucks has a great location and friendly staff however they never have enough people working to keep the line moving. The store has two registers as well as two espresso machines but only one of each is used at any given time. This causes really long lines. That's all - it's a Starbucks with a long line.

My wife and I submitted a mobile order after eating next door at PDQ. It was my birthday and if you have the Starbucks app installed, they give you a free birthday drink. We submitted our order and I was pleased to see that the birthday offer chose the most expensive drink on the order (and didn't really seem to care that I added some extras like mocha drizzle). Anyway, the app said our order would be ready in about ten minutes, at right at ten minutes, we were sitting in the drive through. The person in front of us had a long complicated order (FOR ONE DRINK) and we sat there listening to her chat with the Starbucks employee for about five minutes before finally pulling forward. When I got to the speaker, you could hear the sense of relief in the order taker's voice when I said "Hi there, mobile order for James."  Anyway, we pulled around, and quickly picked up our drinks. They were made exactly as we had specified and were absolutely delicious. Lemmie tell you, Starbucks has a winner with their cold brew lines. I love mine with a bit of sweet cream and some mocha drizzle and this particular location nailed it.   Note, it may be easier to walk inside and pick up your order as the line for the drive through tends to wrap around in weird places and block traffic.

Never really go to starbucks but this visit was much needed since I had a long 6 hr drive. Big time credit to Haleyanne who had the best advice and the best customer service. Definitely going back to this location. Thank you!
Mixed feelings. My drink is usually good but they are sloooow. I saw a sign that they will be closed for training hopefully that includes putting a rush on making the drinks.
Very nice people at this Starbucks and I felt the love with my latte and bagel. Everything was correct temperature and bagel was toasted to perfection. Thanks Again and Happy Valentines Day Y'all!!!

This place is hit or miss but today took the cake for me.. ordered my favorite skinny peppermint mocha and while I was waiting the girl at the window started sneezing and the she began blowing her nose....then proceeded to hand me my drink...so I asked her didn't you just blow your nose...she says to me I'm not sick... then I told her that I wanted my drink remade and someone else to give it to me...so I wait she leaves the window... then I'm watching this other person make my new drink... next thing I know she tried to hand me my new drink and informs me she washed her hands... (don't believe she did)I lost it by then and went off on her and the manager. I just asked the manager for my money back... I won't back to this Starbucks anymore..  that will save me money in the long run....

Every time I decide to go here, either their Frappuccino machine is "not working" or they happen to not "have anymore" of the drinks I wanted. I only go here cuz it's closest, but def this Starbucks is a disappointment.
I went to this location every morning before going to work. Sometimes 2 a day. It is super busy always better to order ahead of your there in morning rush.

So they have 3 people working.  Only one car in the drive through but I decided to go inside to use the restroom while I waited on my coffee. First their restrooms are closed. Second no one said hi or even acknowledged me when I walked in. Then the two guys stood there with their backs to me talking while girl was on the other side with her back to me. It literally took 6 minutes for them to even notice I was there. Worst Starbucks ever

OK, this is not a bad Starbucks. I visit occasionally. It is small. And occasionally the line moves slow and you wait a little bit for your drink. Most of the barista's are nice, quiet and polite. Then there is Omar. He is completely unnecessary. It seems he is always goofing off and just obnoxious.
This is the slowest Starbucks and I've been to a lot of them. They spend too much time talking and joking amongst themselves.  They continually get orders wrong.  The need new staff in this store.

Management sucks! Poorly trained staff.  Inefficient service, lack of customer care and  not clean.  Workers are inexperienced and don't understand the basic concept of customer service. They'd rather gossip with visiting friends and ignore the  long line of customers. They also DO NOT wash  their hands or use gloves after touching their faces, rubbing their noses, scratching their heads, coughing or touching surfaces. You might be better served bringing your own coffee in a thermos to this mall or going to one of the many other vendors for better service.  This location ranks low in customer care and cleanliness.
Pastries are yummy staff is nice.    I have issues with my laptop battery and it was a bit of a chore to find a table for two and an electrical plug, but once we got settled in, everything was great!
I love Starbucks...always will....it's my addiction of choice. :-)  Tonight they were giving everyone in the line free drinks because the line and wait was so long.  Thank you so much Starbucks :-)

Stopped in for some go-go juice on my drive to Virginia. Staff was nice. And they were polite to everybody. There was a lot of people from all over the world here. And they treated everybody kind. People left sticky notes for the staff to support them. Kind words go along way!
World's slowest starbucks. Always the same. Never get fast service here. Plan on a fifteen minutes wait everytime.
Although busy FABULOUS! Staff is attentive and organized although a big line in and out with coffee stirred in about 10 minutes faster then pumping my gas

I was waiting in line and one of the employees walked up to my window while I was placing a mobile order and rudely asked if I had a Mobil pickup order and I said I'm in the middle of placing one then with an attitude said "it's not going to come out any faster". It made it uncomfortable when he kept walking past my car. He continued to bring out new mobile orders when he told me not to place one. Not satisfied!
Sunflower mask takes joy saying no to people, in a condescending way. I watched her be hostile with the customer both in front of and behind me. Incredibly rude- woof.

I go to this location almost every morning. Staff is really sweet, especially Laty (I hope I have her name right). I am not sure if she is a manager or not but she always remembers me and is super friendly and welcoming. And the store is always clean.

This Starbucks store is perfectly situated in the middle of town.  It's quaint interior decor fits the area as they seem to have used the existing structure highly in the build.  Their coffee good as expected and I believe they have a Clover machine and usually have a bunch of single origin coffees  if you are a coffee enthusiast.  Both a front and a rear entrance make getting in an out easy and they usually have enough Baristas to keep the line moving.
Rude manager and way too much ice in my 'ice' tea. Better to go to the Wegmans across the parking lot. Less expensive and more seating.

Conveniently located in the Central Business District.  Good place to get work done and get a drink while you are at it.  Barista wanted to be sure she got my order in correctly and definitely out in the extra effort to make sure it was accurate. Overall a clean location with plenty of seating for a downtown location.

I've been coming here for sometime now. This Starbucks is in a great neighborhood and parking usually isn't that difficult to locate. The employees at this location are very friendly and work efficiently. Theirs seating towards the rear of the store which seats a good amount of customers with  additional seating at the bar area looking out the window. The store always looks clean and well kept. They also have various mugs, instant coffee, coffee beans for sale. They had a 30% sale for Christmas time and ended up purchasing a mug.   I liked the decorations they put up for Christmas and the music being played was great. I'm happy when I come in here it isn't too busy or I probably wouldn't stay. I feel a lot of people will take away from the overall environment. I feel some people talk too loud and I don't enjoy hearing other people's conversation. I would stop by here if your in the area and check it out.

This Starbucks is pretty clean with a couple of tables to the side. It located at a very convenient location next to HUP and CHOP. They are pretty fast with completing orders, with 3 people working on drinks. The ambiance is pretty nice. However it's pretty busy with people coming in and out. If you are looking for a place to complete some work, this is also a good location because the tables are off to the side away from the crowd of people flowing in and out.

One of my favorite starbucks to go to when I get a chance. They always remember me and my drink. They make it perfect with a hint of love.  It's perfect location makes it easy to grab on your way into target and out to go shopping. There is no better way to spend your then drinking a Starbucks while shopping and looking around. The only issue I ran into is a new lady working there gave me a hard time about a birthday coupon and she got really sarcastic and rude and didn't even say happy birthday or try to make the situation better. The worst experience I had. Other then that everyone else is lovely. Also sometimes I feel they are short staffed, most the time I only see one person up front.

Although it has Starbucks in the marquis, the experience ends there.  The two stars as follows  1) clean  2) it's a Starbucks.   There is another option on the delta side.  Smaller but better.  This one has a sitting area and tables.  The delta side does not
Came for Veteran's Day. We thought it was one of the nicest locations we have been into. Super friendly staff. Planning on coming in again soon :)

This Starbucks always does a great job but today I was watching an employee ask the manager a question where something was at, the manager Michelle responded "it's right there in front of your face" then again "IT WAS RIGHT IN FRONT OF YOUR FACE" the employees definitely didn't feel good about that response and it was very sad to watch and made me uncomfortable. Definitely makes learning difficult when you can't ask a simple question.
We mobile ordered And our food and drinks were ready ASAP. This location is  so nicely kept. The staff is always great.

Cute, clean Starb in the CWE. Love the outdoor patio seating - great for people watching and catching up on Vogue. Only downside seems to be the limited indoor seating, which may prove to be a problem as the weather cools down. Even my pumpkin spice latte won't keep me warm enough!

This Starbucks is located in the 2nd floor west wing of Harrahs casino. Very convenient if you are staying in the casino and it's too cold to go outside. It is decent in size and the staff was very friendly!
Nice Starbucks with a lot of seating upstairs. It was a little crowded and loud when I was here since there's so much seating.

[780]  The large Starbucks location is located at the Gloucester Premium Outlets.  No drive thru as it's part of the outlets.  It's a busy location but they were able to work the line quickly and prepare the order correctly.  Plenty of indoor and outdoor seating.  We actually spilled our coffee outside before taking a sip and the staff replaced it with a smile.  We will definitely be back.

This Starbucks used to be so great and i used to enjoy going here. Since the new manager has taken over it has completely gone down hill. He has no idea how to communicate with the customers and is so aggressive when he speaks. His new assistant store manager is just as bad as he with with her customer service. I've talked to her about an issue i had with an employee and she seemed uninterested in what i had to say, the old assistant manager was always making sure the customers were happy and satisfied if an issue occurred. Aside from that the employees are all new and have no idea what they are doing. I usually get the same drink and i had Grace, an employee, tell me the drink wasn't something they made when i regularly got the drink. She was rude and made it seem like i was getting a complicated drink. So i just ordered something else because there was a huge line of cars behind me. They also are constantly ignoring the customers and it seems like they purposely do it. I was surprised to see a few familiar faces recently, i thought the employees had all quit because of how terrible the service there has gotten.
quick starbucks that has a good staff to move the line... the inside is a little tight but there is plenty of room outside on the patio

I've given this Starbucks location two chances. The first time I came here I placed my reusable cup in front of the cashier and asked to have it in my own cup. She forgot to make note of my personal cup for the baristas so my cup continued to get passed over by the other cups lined up. After 16 minutes I finally asked the barista of mine was coming soon. He had made my cappuccino but not in my cup. So not only was my cappuccino cold from sitting there, I was forced to use a disposable cup. The second time I came here the shop was nearly empty yet it took 21 minutes for my cappiccino. The baristas were favoring the drive-thru customers which I understand but I shouldn't have to wait so long in an empty shop for just 1 cappuccino. Overall, this Starbucks location is one of the worst I've experienced.
Been here twice in the past month. Both times good experiences. The only con is probably a pro - they are always busy!

Yeah, this is the worst Starbucks in Reno. I went in for an iced tea, waited around 30 minutes and got nothing. I finally just walked out. Enjoy your $4, guys. You definitely did not deserve it. I understand that there is sometimes a buffer with online orders but multiple people who ordered well after me got their drinks just fine. Do yourself a favor and save your time, money and effort. Disgusted.

Great staff. Fast service. No place to sit due to the old men there that gather everyday combined with the suits that must work and steal wifi. Occasional hotties drift in and out along with a familiar face. Drinks are good but like any Starbucks expensive. All too often the bathroom is like a Hershey Highway massacre!

In the heart of downtown IV, this Starbucks is always buzzing with students. You have to come relatively early if you want to snag indoor seating; this is a popular study spot among many, so you won't find too many available spots. On the bright side, there is additional seating on the patio outside. In addition, the baristas are usually quick to crank out drink orders. Anther thing I really appreciate about this location is that it stays open until later on finals weeks, so there is no shortage of caffeine for those trying to pull all-nighters. I only wish they'd serve drinks on tap, like the nitro cold brew!

Four stars just because the drive through people are awesome.  The guy was super nice and when I pulled up to the window --- best smile.  I love it when people just have that happy sparkle.    My latte was hot and the service was fast.  I will have to check out the inside one day.

This store is extremely slow and that parking lot is a NIGHTMARE! I would never park in there because the drive thru line is so long that you would never be able to back out of the parking spots. Sometimes the line will be so long coming from two different entries into the parking lot an street traffic will be backed up. I usually park across the street and walk there so I don't get trapped. Whenever I try my luck and go through drive thru I'll be stuck for at least 15 minutes. Way too slow

I enjoy this Starbucks as I have yet to go to a really bad one.  I say the building is nice and clean, the customer service is pretty good.  The smell of the coffee in the air is always a good fragrance to me.  The dining area is clean and free Wi-Fi.  My coffee drink or espresso has always tasted so good.  The sandwiches and pastries are great to and very healthy and delicious.  I can find no fault in this Starbucks as it lives up to the reputation as a great coffee house in my opinion!

I like the very well covered outdoor seating and the crowd at this location. But Laura D. is not alone in thinking wow, how are the bathrooms here so nasty? It's like what you'd expect at a remote state park.
Extra slow service, soggy doughnuts, cold blonde brew, dirty bathrooms and dirty tables. Will not come back. Also, the Internet would not connect.

So I've been coming here for a while. I've had average experiences. I usually come on the weekends and for some reason they can't get their stuff together at the drive thru. Today I ordered a tall nonfat mocha frap at the drive thru. I pull up and the guy who took my order notifies me that he forgot to put the cup on the station so they didn't make my drink and that he's sorry and I need to wait. 2 minutes later he is about to hand me a paper cup that clearly isn't a frappuccino. I tell him what I ordered, he pulls the sleeve on the cup down and shows me the sticker that says tall nonfat mocha frapp, and says "this is who I'm dealing with". At this point the manager comes over a minute later and says she very sorry and hands me a card that says $4 off my next drink. If I remember correctly those drink cards used to cover any drink now it's limited to $4. Anyway finally after sometime he brings me out a venti and says "they made a mistake twice and they made a venti, so we will just give it to you"  Thanks to the smug guy and the slow service they get two stars. They would have gotten one but the manager seemed apologetic.

This is your standard Starbucks. What's notable about this location is the cute building it's in - since it's in the historic district in Philly. The doors to enter are super cute and it's not too busy.   The downside of this location is they moved really slowly preparing drinks and food. My typical Starbucks is in the heart of Manhattan and they move significantly faster than this location.

For some reason, this one is always disappointing. The male baristas/cashiers are usually unpleasant/mildly surly. I've repeatedly gotten the bottom of the barrel coffee, meaning the last bit with all the grounds in it and they have made a few busted drinks for my wife as well. The condiment counter is always dirty and the trash is always busting out of the top full.  Now I'm fairly easy to please, and if the coffee was $1, I would have no problem with lousy service and grinds in my coffee (multiple times), but for what is charged, I think things should be better. Honestly, this is the only Starbucks in the city I have a problem with. The rest are all awesome.
Usually when I go to this Starbucks they never get my drink right, but yesterday when I went there they were so much better then normal!!

AVOID THE DRIVE-THRU ON SUNDAY MORNINGS.  I thought that maybe last week was an off-day but I was extremely annoyed this morning as well.  I would like to point out that it took 20 minutes to receive my order this morning with 4 cars in front of me. I ordered a hot green tea- and received it without the tea bag. I saw the gentleman take the tea bag out- which is unusual since I've never had that before. As a result, my tea was too mild for me. And my cup- it was not double cupped, and the cup sleeve was inadequate for the hot drink. The order for the gentleman in front of me was wrong as well.  Do I sound like a crazy lady on caffeine withdrawal? Maybe. I wish that this Starbucks would add another person to assist with the drive-thru during the busy times.

I'm a huge Starbucks fan and have come to this starbucks 3-4 times a week on average for about 4 months now. They keep the place nice and clean but aren't as personable at this one as I've experienced in other starbucks stores. I'd usually be called by name by now and my drink would have been memorized.  My drinks are always great as usual though.  My biggest thing is that it's not as cozy here. I'd like to see some couch chairs in the near future.   Sincerely, The girl that orders the raspberry white mocha every time.
This is a great Starbucks. The staff are fast and friendly. My drink is right every time. I love having them so close to home!

The friendliest Starbucks in the city. Always gets my order perfect and always making an effort to ask how customers day is! Super clean and definitely handling all COVID19 precautions as far as I can tell.
The service was amazing and the girl with the blue hair was so nice and make my drink perfectly

Yooooo WTF!!!! Yoooo how's that even possible! Yooooo! They have only one background music and yooooo it's so terrible! Yooooooo! I stayed here for 30 minutes! Yoooo that stupid song is driving me crazy yoooo! WTF
Horrible. I ordered today a white mocha coffee and it was so so bland. I don't usually leave reviews .. but YUCK! Go to a different location.
So personable ,friendly and polite I am welcomed with a smile everytime. I really enjoy the cleanliness, but most of all the sweet staff

The service was great miss my buddy I usually have coffee with had my regular 4 venti soy 2 Splenda iced latte. Yummy to the max today, great service. Only thing to make it better would be if my friend was here with me.

Consistently, the SLOWEST Starbucks I have ever been to. Prepare to spend anywhere from ten to twenty minutes waiting on your order if you go through the drive thru. I don't know who trained these employees but they have absolutely zero sense of urgency. Even when the line is literally wrapped around the building, they will be moving at such a glacial speed that it's quite frankly unnerving. As a former Starbucks employee, I gotta say, this location really needs to step it up a notch! Super disappointing!

They are closed during this tough time, not the issue, but left every light on.  Pulled in with a trailer this AM because every light is on(inside and out).  TURN YOUR LIGHTS OFF so people don't think you're open.

Hot coffee. Quick service. And 2 desserts for $3 AFTER 4PM!!! Why am I just finding this out?  It's Starbucks? What do you expect?  This is going to be my preferred location in DE because the other 3 always having some kind of problem. In order from worst to least worst are: -Concord - unnecessary long waits; poor order accuracy, pastries aren't pulled from the display when they run out (this isn't a problem if you like playing the guessing game) -Marsh - unnecessary long waits -Kirkwood - unnecessary long waits; poor order accuracy  ^Disgruntled patron

My drink is a ice soy latte. I've gotten many many times, but never like this at Bordentown location. Twice it's happened to me in a week. The bottom of my drink has a white chalky chunky substance. The first time I did not notice it will I was drinking it and it was off. The second time this morning (7-24-18) I looked before drinking and again the same white chalky substance. That's disgusting!

A while ago, it was a very rare event when I went to a Starbucks drive-through. That changed when Starbucks moved into the old Burger King building near my house. While the service is always prompt and friendly despite the long lines, the quality of what they make can be spotty.  I'm a big lover of matcha green tea lattes, and all kinds of brewed drinks. a big part of what I love about brewed drinks is the distinct flavor of what is being brewed. So when I come here, and I order a matcha green tea latte, or any other selection, and I get a cup of warm milk with only a splash of what I actually ordered, it grinds my gears. I get that some of the only reasons people drink Starbucks is to show off to the world that they're hip and cultured, but some people actually like the flavor of what's being brewed. I could easily warm up some milk on the stove, put it in a paper coffee cup, and lug it around with me, but that isn't what I'm playing for.  If this location fails to quit serving warm milk, I'll have to start going to another location, or coffee place all together. These establishments are far too costly to only serve warm milk.

Nothing like a vanilla chai tea latte with oatmeal & brown sugar. Hearty and semi-heavy on the tummy. I needed a nap afterwards. Their breakfast cage free egg white chicken sausage or turkey bacon is rather tasty as well. Stole a few bites from my friends. The wraps are light with low calories so there is no guilt associated with eating 'em.  This location is very crowded most times so be prepared for the wait.

Stop here quite frequently on my way to school or home from work.    This location is nicely tucked away in the Mason Woods shopping center, you can do some shopping, banking and even hit up the 'bucks.    There frapps are yum, but I love the holiday season because they have pumpkin spice, peppermint mocha and also their Christmas blend coffee, which now comes in via!  The staff are incredibly friendly, and depending on the time of day, you may have to wait a bit, but no big deal (unless you are those suit and tie types that have no patience and really need your double soy no foam asap)!

This is the newest SB in NWReno, and it has all the things you might expect... and that fancy CLOVER machine. https://www.starbucks.com/coffee/learn/clover  I may not often get a cup from the Clover, but DAMN it makes the place smell better than most Starbucks.  --- Also, very kid friendly, and the crew is nice, even when I show up to meet with my boss, and not my cute kids.

Horrible service ... !!  After you ask for your order  , they tried to put you on the spot by screaming to the person who took the order (cashier ) ... it is so unprofessional and so upsetting !! Never coming back .. if you walk a few blocks up .. there is a better Starbucks with so much better service !

My drink tasted great. The workers were nice and were working hard. My issues are not with this particular SBucks, but all of them. The evolution of their app orders has caused me to lose interest in visiting their stores. There must be a better system, and SBucks is a wise enough company to be able to figure out that it is not working.   I walked in and right up to the counter to order. There was no one in line. I noticed 5 or 6 people standing around, waiting for their order to be made. I ordered my drink and walked over to near where they were all standing and began waiting. As we are waiting, workers are calling names. "Bob!" No one moves. "Karen!" Nothing. "Molly!" Still no one. There are now 9 or 10 drinks sitting on the counter that people have ordered but haven't picked up. Finally, names of people get called that were actually standing there the whole time. One guy ordered regular coffee and probably waited 15 minutes. Finally, I get my drink after waiting between 20-25 minutes.   I don't get why people would order a hot coffee on the app and let it sit there for 15 minutes or more and let it get cold before you pick it up. I also don't understand why people would order an iced coffee, or a Frappuccino and let it sit there and melt before strolling in to pick it up.   Starbucks, please fix this issue. Your workers are doing their best, following your broken system, but it's terrible.

I love the location and drive thru option of this Starbucks but the coffee has recently been less than satisfying.   The atmosphere is pleasant and clean. The staff are friendly and polite. The drive thru is clutch. Food is meh-right. The coffee is unpleasantly watery like there isn't enough coffee beans for the brew. Add more beans and this place will shine again like it did in it's hey day.
I've been waiting in their  drive thru line for 20 minutes!!!  They really need a better crew working weekends.  This is ridiculous.  I have no idea what could take this long.
This is the slowest Starbucks I've ever been to. One car was waiting for more than 5 minutes for their order! That's horrible.

Usually this Starbucks is pretty good, but today the workers were being obnoxious and not very friendly. I asked the barista if the sandwich on the counter was mine and instead of offering any sort of assistance she shrugged her shoulders and walked off.

This Starbucks needs so much improvement. They usually do not have the ingredients to make so many popular drinks or they do not have straws. I avoid coming here unless it's just a must. The management at this store should be re-evaluated. The Panini's are always out of stock. All of the refreshers are out of stock. Most of the syrups are out of stock.  The straws are usually out of stock. I won't mention how many times they just close the drive thru altogether. I prefer to go into the Starbucks inside the Fry's grocery store.
Visited here on 7/24/2014 at 3:10 PM. Jessica took our order.  I will cut this short and simply day that Jessica was not only curt and short with us, she was rude.

Worst starbucks location ever! The drive through takes forever.  The people working have no sense of urgency.  Twice they have been out of all coffee except dark and I've had to wait 5+ mins for a plain cup of coffee.  It's insane!  I can go through chick fil a and get food faster than a coffee here.
We went through the drive through and it was a great start to our morning, everyone was wonderful and speedy! Not only did our drink look so lovely but it tastes... *chefs kiss*  Thank you :)!!!

I love this Starbucks and that's coming from a chick who said " F U Starbucks!" a few years ago because I thought they were such a rip.  I still feel like they are a rip but I will gladly pay for the spectacular service I receive every single time I come to this particular Starbucks (and I've been coming for well over a year).  I honestly receive better customer service here then I have at some of the best restaurants in the city.  Everyone is quick, FRIENDLY, POLITE, and the place is always clean.  I have also noticed that there isn't much turnover which I'm sure is a rarity in the coffee business.  Shout outs to: The guy with the curly hair and glasses who works on the weekends- He is very fast and always asks people how they are and says it in a way that he actually cares.  Skillzzzz The little chick with the glasses on the weekends - she is also quick and is very nice and knows what the regulars drink.   The little chick with the short red hair who works during the week - she is always brings her A-game and is also very nice.    Love this place!

Quick breakfast before boarding our flight.   Everyone had on gloves and masks to serve customers quickly. I don't drink coffee, but they have many favors to choose from. I had an egg sandwich with lemonade and my husband and son had eggs, cheese and bacon sandwich.   We will return soon.

This Starbucks is my favorite one in this area. No matter how long it's been they know my drink and always get it right.  Even early morning service was professional and friendly as well as quick.  The Carmel Latte was made just for me with whip cream on top and Carmel drizzle great to the last drop.  This drink was finished so quickly until I wanted another one.

The staff has always been nice.   The drinks are made correctly at least 75% of the time.  The drive through has always been crazy busy which is no reflection on the Starbucks itself. It's to be expected often but this location especially is normally quite busy.  We order mobile order ahead and have started going in to pick up our order. It's always ready within about ten minutes from when it's ordered through the app.   Quick, easy.

I visit this Starbucks often (if not everyday, then every Thursday). Not only are they very efficient but they are always very welcoming.   I prefer to visit this Starbucks over the one local to me because of their service.
I had the best experience here out of any Starbucks I've been to. They are fast, nice, and always make sure your order is right.

Like many Starbucks locations, this store utilizes the automatic printed ticket for marking drinks. While this is no doubt an effective way to correctly craft your desired beverage, it often times comes at a lack of convenience as technology often frustrates users.  On Saturday morning, I came in when there were two people waiting on drinks and no one in line. It was a very moderate morning in terms of customers. Nonetheless, it took 15 minutes to receive my drink because the ticket did not print. Three people received their drinks even though the had ordered after me. Of course, the real issue is that I had received one of my drinks very quickly, yet I was waiting on another. I eventually had to let the barista know why I was waiting. Overall, this was an unpleasant experience.

I have to say that I have been through the airport many times , and I have not stopped in here till I noticed the prices were all the same for a sandwich.   I do not normally buy sandwiches from Starbucks but decided to see what was there and had the chicken chipotle with the passion tea lemonade which is my favorite.  The service was on par and was treated very well. I have always loved going to Starbucks because of the friendliness and the bathroom. This one did not disappoint

Need a reward after my 5k! I so earned the grande cold brew with sweet cream this morning. I received fast, friendly service with a smile. The environment was clean and extremely nice. The staff seemed like they enjoyed where they work. The car line was moving so fast, too. They cleared it out in no time and kept it small.

Starbucks opened today.   I do not like the location but I do like the inside and the patio.   I just hate that it is so close to local coffee shops and also it is right on the main road.  However, it is only a block from my house so...   Looks like yet another coffee shop where I will be the Duke!     *nothing special about this Starbucks other than it is new.  Otherwise it is the same as others.

I've been coming here for about 4 years pretty regularly during my time as a Penn employee and student.  I always noticed that I would get my best service in the morning, during the normal hospital/school/work rush (730 am - 9 am) and at night service was slow as they must put the new people on at that time to train (because who is rushing for that 6pm cup of joe).  I went away for the summer and now I have returned to school to find that this Starbucks has apparently put their night shift on during the rush hour. Two days in a row I stood waiting at the counter as someone forgot my very, very simple order of a tall pike with a shot of espresso.  Literally I am standing there, starring at the workers as they hustle to make everyone else's mocha-choca bullshit and here I am just waiting for my drip coffee.  Both times I asked what was up after about 7 minutes and they are like "Oh, what did you want again?" WHAT?!  And before you ask, yes, they apologized and as a consolation prize, I got one extra half of a multigrain bagel... after waiting 20 minutes.   WHAT!?! x 2.  I have never ever experienced anything like this at a Starbucks and especially not in a high traffic Starbucks.  I will literally never come back to this location and I will steer people away.   I will also post this review on your Facebook, once I find it.
Favorite location I've ever been to. I sit at this Starbucks everyday to do homework and the baristas are nothing but superb. Greatest environment and service!
Waited a good five minutes just to get an iced coffee. Baristas seemed irritable and were rude to customers. Not a good Starbucks experience will not be going back

When you make the wrong drink admit it. A skinny cinnamon dulce is nothing like a macchiato. There is no drizzle and it's made with skim milk not whole. Thanks for wasting the time it took to stop and the money I prepaid with my app. Glad I can go back to my own neighborhood soon. Oh... and there was NO ONE in the store.

I came today 2/19/21 at 8:50 the door was already closed! And they should be closed by 9pm and also they ladies was inside and they saw me and no one opened however they went out from the store after 9 so I don't find any reason as they closing early and also every one of them was having more than one drink! and was laughing and no one cared as the three ladies closed early than 9pm as the door should be closed by 9!  Last time i will come to this store and I don't recommend it to anyoneyou can save time and go to another store.

Went through the drive thru yesterday (4/5) and the person at the window was not wearing gloves when they took my money AND handed me my drink. They were also not wearing a face mask when the mayor advised everyone to wear a mask. During this pandemic I expected the Starbucks Corporation to have better guidelines for serving their customers and protecting us all from the spread of the Covid-19 virus. I feel it is dangerous for my family and the Reno/Sparks community to patron this location and all Starbucks if they are going to continue these practices.

So... five employees... One working as a cashier, one making drinks one squatting down I assume looking for some thing, one sticking his butt in her face laughing then going on about "blah blah blah bullshit, blah blah blah bullshit", and the final one stuffing his face with food and visiting each individual employee. On a positive note, they got the order right so I guess that deserves one star at least. ‍

This is my local Starbucks and I must give them props because I've been going to this one for years as it was moved recently to a closer place by my work! It is very convenient I will say the parking lot is a war zone and I get scared for my life by all the drivers who ignore stop signs here! Other than that there is no better Starbucks location in the Montgomeryville area! I literally will go out of my way to go to this one as opposed to many others in the are because I know my coffee will be perfect here as I've had bad experiences at other Starbucks in this area! Please look no further for the best Starbucks cup of coffee in the area!
Best Starbucks in town. Great staff and great service.

Went to this Starbucks location twice in one morning and had terrible service both times. The cashier was rude, they set drinks/food down without announcing it was ready. I stood for about 10 minutes waiting for a breakfast sandwich and when someone finally asked me what I was waiting for the girl finally noticed that my sandwich had not even been put in the oven. Everyone was busy fumbling around with stocking the kitchen and not with customers. Disappointing as service is usually great at Starbucks.

What a pleasure as Emily was at the drive through and as she handed me my drink I drove away smiling because of her Great Personality!!! Look forward in seeing her again at Starbucks on Los Altos in Sparks!!!
My favorite Starbucks in stl always friendly and kind. My order has always been fast and hot. They are awesome
Teeny tiny location. Quick, friendly service and oddly not busy during the mid afternoon. Loses a star for not really having anywhere to sit inside.

This particular starbucks is always hit or miss with me. I order the same drink at many different starbucks and for reason  it tastes so much different.  Very disappointing seeming I'm an addicted coffee lover and I tend to spend a good amount of money here. However,  Lauren the manner is very efficient, friendly and knows how to make a good drink and the establishment is always clean.
african american girl very very rude!!! i will never visit this starbucks again. she has a nasty nasty attitude. she should not work up front bottom line!
This particular starbucks is always clean. Staff is very friendly,  fast, and know how to make a great coffee! Always my go to location to hit up right before work.

Large Starbucks right near 476 - so fairly convenient but the trafic at rush hour is bad.  Drive thru is available but we opted to order inside (and use the restrooms).  No issues.  Got in and out fairly quickly.  Parking lot was PACKED.  Alex (cashier/barista) was very good. Seating area was clean and comfy.

The quality is the same as every other Starbucks and staff are friendly but the wait time was way more than typical!   It was a good 10 to 15 minutes waiting in the drive-through lane... only a few cars in front of me

Another family favorite. It used to be a Long John Silvers and in the 7 years we have lived near by we probably went there 3 times.  Since it was turned into a Starbucks I'd say we are there 3-5 times a month. Plenty of seating. Bigger than the typical Starbucks.  The kids and I are addicted to the cake pops!

I am currently boycotting the Starbucks by my house on Royal Oaks for this one! This is without a doubt the FASTEST drive through around. They are friendly and always accurate with my order. This is what Starbucks should be!

I love my iced caramel maciato it's a wonderful way to treat myself especially after going to the gym with matches after the gym I like to stop by the mornings and sometimes after work I don't drink coffee too much but when I do Starbucks is the place for me. It's nice I love the fact that they not only so different types of coffee is whether it's iced hot or the wonderful balloons they also have cakes breakfast sandwiches and different types of snacks this location is always busy but they're pretty quick to get you in and out without many mistakes got a love Starbucks

Staff was incredibly rude and impatient. Their interactions with themselves and each other were unprofessional and hostile. This is NOT the Starbucks experience I'm used to. Greg, the manager, was particularly inappropriate.

This location has recently been remodeled and is now a truly awesome location with a large community table and plenty of two-seater tables inside. The location has very nice staff too. Not quite a replacement for the defunct Ike's Coffee down the street, but a nice place to chill out after work with some reading or laptop time. Great WiFi connectivity and plenty of power along the walls and the community table.

I would like to give a negarive one rating ，but this is the lowest I can submit，being an royal Starbuck fan for eight years is not easy and always ordering the same items repeatly，the worst location by far if anyone would like to stay as Starbuck fan ，it's a disgrace for this particular business to be in operation ，if the management team happens to follow this ，please fix this problems and make sure the employees are properly trained to serve.....food not heated up properly and the mixed drinks were not according to the right taste

I've been here for 20min waiting for 4 drinks. I have watched 3 phone in orders come in and go out ahead of me.  I have also watched 4 customers who ordered behind me leave and there have been at least 10 cars go through the drive through as well.  I guess I should have stayed in my car..  Not sure what the hold up for my ice tea is, is but I've never been inside a Starbucks this long. Boo... (The drinks were made well...so there's that)
They just can't get it right in the drive threw!  Every time we go there they either forget the sugar completely or no cream!  Mind you we are asking for light and sweet coffee!
Use to like this Starbucks but they have went downhill. Always getting orders wrong. Speak to customers in a condescending tone. Very disappointed. Do better.
Standard Starbucks. I think they are over priced in my opinion, but the coffee is good enough

Keeps getting worse. Today, 8:30. They took my order for pike coffee. And I had to wait because it was not brew. It is 8:30 in the morning. Nine people working, it is a coffee shop, who is monitoring this. Not the first time, and who ever is brewing the coffee is also doing a terrible job. Steve, come back please.

I have gone to this location 5 times and out of the five times my coffee has only been right once. So four times I have had to throw out the coffee because it taste so bad I can't even drink it. Also every time I try to get a pink drink or anything with strawberry's they are "always out" kind of ridiculous at this point and it kind of sucks because it's right by my work and house but I guess I'll drive to the one near largo mall Instead because they always get my coffee right.

This is a very busy Starbucks, so running out of drinks is going to happen. My last visit at this Starbucks will probably be my last at any Starbucks. I ordered three different drinks options, after being told they were out of all three... I was finally told they had the forth option I ordered. I requested an iced drink two different time...after waiting 15 minutes (no big deal, they were busy) my drink came out hot. When I tried to tell one of the employees that it was supposed to be iced, she walked away. I'm hoping she just didn't hear me but nonetheless I left this Starbucks with no drink and a 6 dollar charge on my card.

Okay, four and five star reviewers... Have you ever BEEN to a REAL Starbucks? Or are you just buying bottled water here?  This is a franchised 'bucks, not a corporate one and it shows. If service was a vacuum cleaner, this place would be a Dyson. Seriously. Rude people that can't get a venti tap water right. Heaven forbid you try to correct them. Can you say stink-eye?  The store is beautiful and comfortable, but I drive all the way up to Marsh Rd where they know how to make drinks properly, they remember you after one or two visits and they're some real friendly folks to boot.
The drinks were good. But when you order and are charged for a venti I also expect the cup to be filled more then half way.

Extremely friendly and fast drive through service! Trevor was extremely kind and even gave suggestions on what might taste good with my drink. Thank you! Strawberry açaí lemonade with peach juice tastes amazing! 10/10 service will definitely be going to this Starbucks from now on

I went in this morning and ordered an iced mocha. Tatiana greeted my friend and I with a smile and a warm welcome even though it was 6:50 in the morning. Brandon greeted everyone  with a good morning welcome to Starbucks even thought he was prepping drinks. This locations stafff has never failed  to impress me. Whenever I need Starbucks I will go to this location because of the great customer service that I receive from here.

I've now been here twice for afternoon work sessions. It's spacious, there's a decent-sized parking lot, and the wifi seems to work pretty well. Service was friendly, but at the same time, they got my drink wrong (I asked whether they only had white chocolate iced mocha, or whether they had regular iced mocha--and very definitely asked for the latter, but I think asking about the former confused them). Also, floors could use a bit of a sweep, and tables--at least in some cases--could use a wipe. I understand sometimes a place gets busy and this isn't always possible, but once the crowd dies down, that's something that should happen.  Still, it's a generally comfortable environment with lots of plugs; it could just use a bit more curation (and cleaning). All in all, though, this is a solid option in an area without any independent coffee shops open past last afternoon, and I'd certainly return as needed.

I come to Starbucks waaaayyyyy more than I should. I know this location very well. The drive thru is always courteous and prompt but sometimes I like to go in. The restroom is ALWAYS DISGUSTING.  I know these coffees are costly and I know Starbucks can afford a cleaning service to clean their facilities.  Their baristas clearly do not think they get paid enough to keep a clean work environment.  Not only was the restroom disgusting but so was the lobby. The floors had massive amounts of trash along with what looked like weeks of yuck built up on the floor. I'd love to offer my company as some relief. Please contact stellar cleaning services LLC if you need a professional to clean this place up. Now let me start on the baristas... there were some AWESOME  workers about 6 months ago but now they are all young and worried about hanging out and how much fun it would be if they could get another of their friends to work here with them. OMG please split these people up! They do not get the job done well when they are busy talking about the weekend coming instead of listening to the order being placed through drive thru!! They definitely need a class on how to do your job and do less socializing.  Bottom line, starbucks costs too much money to not be taken care of in a top notch manner!

There's not a whole lot I can say about Starbucks that the general public doesn't already know. If you're getting a brewed coffee (hot or iced) make it at home. For all of you lazy or complicated people like me, Starbucks is well worth the money. ChipLand's baristas are very detail oriented when I order obnoxious drinks (especially the night crews, probably because there isn't the morning rushes), and they are always sure to ask me how I'm doing.

Wholly agree with Jenny!  This place is filthy. The condiment table needs to be cleaned. Have frequented this Starbucks for a long time. Recently it has fallen off particularly in cleanliness and seemingly service too lately. One barista a couple of weeks ago made me feel sorry for asking for a bold pour over. Tonight put the icing on the cake. The hot coffee is frigging COLD!

I have decided that I hate this place. I am not a fan.  The only saving grace of this place is the interior décor; however, the coffee still is not good and the service is lackadaisical from employees who seem uninterested in what they are doing.   I'm never going back.
I love this Starbucks. I usually use the drive-thru and they're always so fast and friendly, even when it's super crowded. :)

New New location, I likey.  Then again I like all things "new".  Located at the corner of S. Grand and Sidney; across the street from Tower Grove Park. The decor is an industrial, rustic feel.  There are 2 leather chairs but no sofa which was a bummer but the long tables and steel stools made up for it.    Full stock of your Starbucks favs; tins of tea, coffee, tumblers, sippers, pastries, sandwiches, parfaits and more!  There's a "What's Happening" board at the front entrance if your interested.  Patio seating is available with plenty of shade for you to work.  The drive-thru looked forced. Tight. Sharp turn-ish but hey whatever works.    Lastly, the coolest thing I've ever seen at a Starbucks was the "Customer of the Week" incentive which shout-outs a random favorite customer who receives their next drink on the house.  I asked how this was made possible and Jeffrey explained "It pays to get in good with someone; it's totally random."  Made no sense but at least we know its a possibility.

Brownsburg finally has a new Starbucks--which has had no problems finding customers.  The new location on the northern side of the freeway has several noticeable improvements over the old one. First of all, the parking situation is way better, with a circular set up for easy access. Second, there is more space in the interior for hanging out. It is way less cramped. And way less noisy.  The crowd here seems less uptight than the group who frequent the older branch.  Because its new, there is an efficient and fresh-looking checkout area.  No exterior seating, though. I like this option at various times of the year.  Service--friendly and error-free so far

I stopped into this Starbucks near closing time, and was told that they were "out of coffee beverages". I ordered a Frappuccino so I can only assume that they meant THAT and not that they were actually OUT OF COFFEE. It was close to closing time, and its possible that the staff just didn't want to bother, but I had to drive to Mt Juliet and a Frappuccino would have really helped.
Friendly, attentive staff, great location and cozy atmosphere! Perfect for getting work done. Best drink is the cold brew!

I stopped here for a drink without co-workers on my way to the airport. I was surprised how crowded it was at 10:30 on a Wednesday.   It was a pretty standard Starbucks. I got an iced chai tea latte and a chorizo breakfast sandwich. This one actually had the low sugar chai mix here which is hard to find now. The breakfast sandwich was pretty good.   It was so crowded that I think we got the last table there. Even though it was crowded, there wasn't a line to order and we got our drinks fast.   I would come back again.

I went with some co-workers to use the coupons on Retailmenot for free caramel or hazelnut macchiatos from 3/1/13-3/31-13. Retailmenot is a reputable site from which I have successfully gotten several other coupons or coupon codes before. The coupon has no limit aside from not using more than one in the same transaction.   I used the coupon from Retailmenot at this location and other locations the week prior with no problems. Today, a few co-workers and I went in to use the coupons and there were two employees at the front register. When we told them that we had coupons, the male said, "Oh, we're not taking those but I don't know why, talk to my co-worker." We went to speak with the co-worker and she insisted that they were a scam. The first week the coupons were available they were accepted but then all of a sudden they were a scam? Especially considering the first employee "didn't know" why they were no longer accepting them, it seemed to us as if too many people used them so they didn't want to lose out on more money so began telling people they were not valid.  I doubt Starbucks is hurting for money, however, if they can't afford to have a month long special, they probably shouldn't offer free items with a coupon for a long period of time.

Apparently this Starbucks is closed during the majority of the day.
