I attempted to go through the drive thru today around 3pm and there was a sign stating the new hours of the location were something like 5am-10am and 7pm-9pm. What?! Very very bizarre for a national chain to be closed for the majority of the day. Hours are also not updated on Yelp, Google or the Starbucks website so there was no way for me to know ahead of time. The only other time I was at this location i purchased 2 hot chocolates which essentially tasted like hot water, almost no chocolate flavor. We had waited over 20 minutes in line so didn't feel like going back through for new ones so we just tossed the drinks. Not sure what's going on there but I will absolutely not be returning. Dunkin Donuts down the road is much better.

Come here regularly to study. Good atmosphere with friendly staff. Just today, an employee (with whom I'd had a lovely conversation) accidentally rang in a cold brew instead of a nitro brew. They replaced it super quick no problem and gave me the cold brew for free. Another employee even strained the cold brew so it wouldn't get watered down while I studied and I could have it later. Highly recommend this location!
The Starbucks at the Southwest terminal was speedy & friendly. They would be outstanding if the Gals would wear hair ties & the kiosk was a wee bit cleaner.

This is the worst Starbucks I have ever been too, and trust me, I'm a gold star member who is obsessed, so I've been to quite a few. The cashiers either don't understand or just make A LOT of mistakes. It's not uncommon to ask for a cold drink only to find a hot one waiting for you at the end (happened 3 out of 5 times in 1 week once). The wait time is a ridiculous 10-15 minutes, and people after me in line are often served before I am. I always get an iced vanilla macchiato which consists of like 4 ingredients and it never tastes the same each time I get it. Unfortunately, this is the only Starbucks on my way to work so I either take a chance and go here or stop at Dunkin. I've been going to Dunkin more often then not because they never mess up my coffee and it tastes the same every time so I know what to expect. This place sucks!

Not enough staff. This Starbucks takes longer than any other store I've been too. Even longer then when I went to your New York location. I live across the street and i can't even get a cup of coffee. Inside and drive through line is so long. Never a full staff to meet the demands.

I love Starbucks food & beverage products. I'm a regular at those near my home. Today, out running errands, I stopped at this location for drive thru. Slowest service ever - for those in front of me as well as my wait. When I got to the window (only 2 vehicles in front of me), the window barista asked if I had ordered a peppermint fru-fru drink. In short, they lost track and had not made my drink. A bad day or SOP?  Don't know, but not up to expected performance.

While I love Starbucks today I'm in the middle of ordering and the girl who's taking my order stops and starts talking to the others casually as if I wasn't still standing in front of her ordering. So rude! If was important fine, say excuse me and then proceed. Manners people, especially in this industry.

I go to this starbucks all the time, Most of the staff are very helpful and useful and even offer discounts when they are available, everytime they make a drink it always comes out just about to perfection, they make it better than some of the stand alone starbucks locations.   This Starbucks is a smaller one from being inside a Target, but the staff is always courteous, working and won't keep you waiting.

This rating is more for the employees than Starbucks. I am probably the only person in the world that special orders a pink drink; I like more acai than coconut milk. Megan prepared my drink. She called me up to verify what I wanted. (I am sure there's not a button for what I want). She wasn't bothered at all, even though the store was packed with people in a hurry to catch a flight.  She double checked everything with me all the while smiling and a pleasant attitude. Starbucks might not be her career stop but I bet she carries that positive, friendly attitude wherever she goes. You might want to see what would keep her Starbucks! She's a great reflection of your company.

This Starbucks is a diamond-in-the-rough.  The interior decor is what I'd expect from a nice coffeeshop that is looking to add atmosphere.  Some old-world style furniture, etc.  The lineup moved quickly, though the barista was a bit behind (and forgot my order initially)... the atmosphere made up for it.

THIS IS THEEEEEEEEEEEEEE WORST STARBUCKS in the country, in the world and possibly the galaxy (if they exist :) ).  The management staff has no clue how to run a restaurant.  The cafe is closed half the time - and gee the mobile app accepts orders for cafe pickup which they DID AGAIN THIS MORNING.  If you haven't been to this location, the drive thru is a nightmare. The excuse by the nasty person at the pickup window for the cafe being closed - we are short handed and we are focusing on training. BBBBBBBBULLL.  You may be short handed, but training - total BS.  NEVER GO TO THIS LOCATION
Plenty of space to chat. This is a larger location than most, I think. It's not too far off the highway, and there are lots of other businesses around. Also, there's a drive thru!

This is our neighborhood Starbucks and I have to give it 5-stars. We never have any issues with our drinks and of course going here for years now, you get to know the staff. We take our dogs here on the weekends, and sometimes during the week, in the drive-thru, and they are very dog friendly. They love to give treats to our dogs and even ask about them if we don't have them with us. It's a great location, I think it's easy to get to, and the drive-thru is a bonus.

I love Starbucks but I avoid this one. It has some of the slowest service I have ever experienced at a Starbucks.   I was there 2 days ago...The guy who was doing the drinks loves to talk to the costumers and his co-workers, which is fine...I just noticed he does a lot of talking and no working too. He will stop working and just start talking and talking. There was only one person ahead of me and it still took almost 10 minutes to get my drink.   I also got over-charged at one point. The price on the menu was lower then the price I was charged. I added nothing special to my drink...I was going to ask for an explanation or my money back but there was a big line at that point so I let it go.  Small coffee shop, not much sitting. Music can be too loud sometimes. On the plus side there is a Sweet Cece's and a Chipotle right next door, that's a bonus.
I never review starbucks'. This one was fantastic. My cashier was perky as hell and friendly asf. She really made my day and I hope to spread her joy. My coffee tastes better because if it :)

I always have such a great time at this Starbucks.  The baristas are all super friendly and great to talk to. I usually place my order through the app and pick it up at the drive through.  Most of the time they know who it is as soon as I pull up and say "order for Ryan."  They have a personal touch which has been lost to most other Starbucks locations.

The service has gotten better. One of the better starbucks in the area. I'd go to this one over the Target one down the road. But avoid the drive thru at all cost if there are any cars in line. One time I saw 2 cars in line at the drive thru and I thought ok this will be quick but nope took me about 10 mins to be out.

Awe Starbucks! I have only visited via the drive though recently to get my warm delightful steaming cup of goodness!  I appreciate the app and the new rewards system that gives you credit even if you don't pay through the app!

The baristas are friendly enough, the coffee is.. well, Starbucks coffee, but the internet connection is absolutely dreadful - comparable to a cell phone and what ultimately led me to look elsewhere.   There's usually copious amounts of outlets and seating though with about twelve 2-tops lining the giant wall of windows looking out upon Latimer St (read: dumpsters and a parking garage).  Occasionally bums will coddle up next to the window outside and attempt to entertain you with their histrionics.  You could add or subtract a star if you are or aren't into that.

Probably one of the worse experiences I've had with a Starbucks. Went there around opening for a quick breakfast. Had a long drive to Chicago.   Ordered iced coffee and got a regular coffee. Ordered an egg cheese and sausage sandwich and got only bread and cheese. Didn't realize it until I was already on the road.  There were over 5 workers and they all seemed lost. Place was otherwise clean.

Literally the slowest Starbucks I've ever been to hands down. I travel to this area for business and stop here since it's close to my building, otherwise I would drive to a different Starbucks. However I'm considering switching to Dunkin Donuts. The drive through line is ALWAYS like a parking lot. It's never a quick stop here.

needed a place to sit down and study and found myself on this side of town (away from my usual starbucks).  i sat in this starbucks for about three hours and the team here really handles a busy crowd! all the staff was super friendly and the barista made sure my drink(s) were tasty, just the way i like it.  i also like that there were quite a few places to sit down at. some starbucks just don't have enough sit-down room.
Ordered using the ap a free birthday beverage and the Venti pumpkin spice frappe was ready for pick up on arrival and did not disappoint!

Had a great experience being helped by Brad at the counter. I'm not used to this heat (in town for a wedding) and was sweating like crazy and needed hydration. I asked for a surgery drink (back off coffee snobs .)   He recommend two great options to spike my blood sugar and I was on my way! Fast and pleasant service.

This review is less about the place - Starbucks is Starbucks and they make great coffee - and more about the staff.  I'm a regular at this place and the people who work here keep me coming back.  They remember who you are, what you drink, even my crazy custom breakfast order, and are so friendly, even when it is busy.   This place is lucky to have Steve, Jay, and Debbie.

this is without a doubt the worst starbucks ever  i have yet to ever get a drink order correct. its so simple: venti vanilla latte almond milk no foam   today no vanilla i wa the first or second person thru the drive thru i had to repeat the order twice , we had a whole lol momnet about no vanilla creamer at home i mean seriously at $5.50 a cup get it together. i usually drive tot he 71st and bindford location because the location  by me on 56 st. is so slow and they always alway always mess up something in the order or you have to repeat it 3 times.  get it together

I avoid writing about chains because they often seem the same, but they really are not.    For one reason or another some different locations make the drinks wrong like burning espresso or wrong pump ratios.   This starbucks on Anderson has always met my expectations.  I forgot how much history I have with this location until they finally messed up my oatmeal with soy this morning.  The cashier was new, but the girl on the hot bar remade it perfectly.  I came here to relax before my first interview for my current job and here to relax before my first day on the job.  We shouldn't underappreciate the hard workers who make starbucks an epic experience.  Shoutout to Suzy who remade my order!

I'm sorry, but it shouldn't take 20 minutes to make a latte, considering there was about a 30 second wait at the counter to order. The coordination between the order takers and the barista (if you can still call someone who just pushes a button to make espresso that name) is seriously lacking here. At the time, I was one of two people whose drinks were totally overlooked while others who came in later received theirs. Avoid this location, it should be easy to do as there are about a dozen or so Starbucks in the vicinity. In particular, the Callowhill store has been doing an excellent job since the day they opened.

I love that this location is walking distance to the Clearwater Harbor Marina and Coachman Park. They accept mobile ordering. The cafe is a good size for a Starbucks and the patio has amble room for several guests. Patio is dog friendly!

Disgusted!!!  Racism!! Can't sit in Starbucks while black and wait for the rest if your party.  Gold Rewards Status will not patronize another Starbucks until some firing take place and I don't mean relocating and a public apology from corporate.  Until then Starbucks will not get another dime from me.
The Starbucks itself was okay, coffee was as normal. I was unimpressed with the service. There was a lady, Nancy, who was pretty condescending, which was annoying.

This Starbucks located in the Winthrop plaza has a very nice design to it. The workers inside are always friendly after I come and always quick to fill orders and make them just right. It's located close to Publix and some restaurants so it's a nice place to stop by after visiting these places. There are plenty of chairs outside for those winter months when you want to have some hot coffee and read a good book outside. Inside, the couches are extremely comfortable and people always seem to be on their laptops looking all cool and productive.

This is a tiny Starbucks location with very limited seating. It's located in an office building. The service was quick and friendly. My guess is during the week it's super busy. We visited early on a Sunday morning. We were in and out in a flash! Even though it's a smaller spot, they still offer all the same drinks and food items in the case. Both baked goods and refrigerated items. We ordered s green tea, vanilla bean frappacino and  a skinny macchiato. Homeless people were outside and even Inside this location too. Parking is tough too!

Honestly one of the worst starbucks I've ever been to. Staff isn't friendly the place isn't welcoming and chances are every time you go in here you'll be waiting for 10+ minutes in line and for your drink. Good luck.
Crazy busy and seemed understaffed on a Sunday morning at 11:00. It is a beautiful space with free WiFi if you don't mind the 12 - 15 minute wait.
The very best Starbucks, with the greatest staff. Has a drive through, also. Nice interior.

My FAVORITE Starbucks!!! ...sometimes.  I've been coming to this Starbucks since it opened, dealing with it's overly-small, pain in the neck parking lot, because it was the closest one to my house. The staff is what keeps me coming back, and no matter how much time passes between my visits, the veteran employees always remember my drink, and genuinely ask how I have been, and what I've been up to.   I usually order a grande or venti iced Americano, with hazelnut syrup, soy milk, and extra, extra, extra, extra caramel drizzle, and they NEVER disappoint me here, especially with the extra caramel drizzle, so long as it is one of the veteran employees making my drink.   They have had a lot of new people come and go, who are typically just... mediocre. They seem unenthused, and never, ever make my drink right. Like, really.. How hard is it?! Extra caramel drizzle doesn't mean one small drop of it in my drink, especially when you charged me for the EXTRA caramel drizzle... Twice!! But, I know my drink is safe when I see one of the regular, veteran employees. I love going in and catching up with them, and they're all awesome. A few of them go to my gym, and we always talk about working out there. It's nice, and adds some personality behind the baristas who previously just made my coffee.   Now that I have the option of the drive-thru Starbucks on the other side of the expressway, it truly says something when I continue to come to this one, with it's horrendous parking. The veteran employees make me excited to return, and pick this Starbucks over the new one. The latter CONSISTENTLY does not make my drink correctly, and the cups are always sloppy there, but I'll leave that for the review I'm going to write for them.   All in all, this is my favorite Starbucks. Great employees, good coffee (as long as the right person is making it), with horrendous parking (hence the docked 5th star).

This store is consistently among the worst Starbucks I've been to.  Their service has gone down since my last review, and if it weren't so dang close to home, we'd never come back.  They have trouble making drinks correctly, are ridiculously slow, and service is never delivered with a smile.

If I could give 0 stars, I would. This Starbucks really needs to up its game with service. Employees seemed abrupt and unfriendly at the least. It literally took 20 minutes just to make a grande chai latte. We then ordered a hot expresso that turned out to be a COLD double shot of coffee. Staff never asked for our names and QUIETLY announced our orders. Luckily, we are from Seattle and will never be stepping foot on this property again!

Ordered drinks for "here" to drink in the restaurant on a slow day. Just a few moments later I clarified that meant I  wanted the drinks in coffee cups insTead of to go cups. The real poner of the employee was, " well we've already started making the drinks" and made them in to go cUps anyway. $10 for two drinks. I think this place has  forgotten that what makes the drinks  worth that is the service and atmosphere. Disappointed that this store seems to have the attitude of a glorified McDonalds.
There has simply got to be a management problem at this place...I have never been to a place where the employees are consistently joyless seem to sort of care, but with not much enthusiasm.

I always get fast service  (unless it's 7am) the people who work here are always very friendly.  I did once find a bug in the show case thing, they removed the bug but didn't dispose of the food that was in there.

I just have to rave about the employees at this location! I go to MANY different Starbucks locations and this one is by far the busiest due to its location right off of 75 and 301. However, the staff here is just SO kind. Every employee I have encountered here has just been the sweetest & so hardworking even in the midst of fast-paced chaos. I intentionally do not go to the Dunkin across the street even if it has a shorter line because the employees here are SO nice and seem to really care about their job. Definitely some gems at this location!
Great place to study or relax! Seating area usually has available tables and a decent amount of outlets! Gave it four stars because there's never parking :/

Good luck moving around in this clutter claustrophobia nightmare! If one wants to chillax at their local starbucks and do what one does while chillaxing at a Starbucks, this location is NOT THE LOCATION FOR YOU! If you wanna be crammed in a little space around a ton of rude people and hapless employees doing their best to serve said rude people, then this IS THE LOCATION FOR YOU! I choose to do neither, I'm out!
Ordered a $4+ medium hot chocolate with extra whip cream and I get home to find it maybe 2/3 full and with no whip cream!

They served me cold coffee.  As soon as it was handed over I could feel the chill.  I ordered coffee with cream.  Come on Starbucks, keep fresh and hot coffee ready!  You are a coffee company and if you can't serve a good hot cup of coffee you should be ashamed.  I handed back to the barista and she replaced it and said the person must have gotten it from a pot that was turned off.  WHY would that coffee be available to be served.  I'm a Starbucks fan and this was downright disappointing.  Do better!

We wanted to sit outside because of the pandemic and also because the weather was gorgeous. The outdoor tables and the whole outdoor seating area is FILTHY. Garbage on the floor, and tables not wiped. I happened to glance down at the  great amount of Starbucks garbage and noticed a Starbucks receipt from two days ago. That shows you how often they clean and sweep the outdoors. Too bad. We were very disappointed. We will find somewhere else to go.
writing first ever review because mean people suck.  just witnessed someone I assume to be the manager being unnecessarily loud and condescending to a new employee who made a mistake.

Really big communal  table is great.  A good selection of small tables, comfortable for studying and meetings.  Seems to have a lot of regulars, shoppers, and students.  Outside seating too.  Employees have always been pleasant here.   As all Starbucks go, really good coffee and treat selection. Love the organic soy milk hot chocolates. SO GOOD!
"Pup cup" delight!   So cute!  Great customer relations!  It made his day. We will continue to go out of our way to visit this location!!

Made my drink poorly and use the wrong milk so I asked them to remake it the guy says yes I'll make it and then use the last of the milk I needed on a drink he had not started yet knowing full well that that was the last of his milk. Completely disrespectful and lackadaisical of my time. This is not my first bad experience at the Starbucks. There is serious attitude problems at this location

Good morning Yelpers.   So I am here to get my morning coffee. and the customer service I just have to help about.  The barista that works the drive thru is phenomenal. Her name is Mia.  Now when I say her customer service skills outweigh any others that I have encountered at this location I mean that sincerely.  Thank you Mia for your awesome service. it is because of people like you that I continue to come to this location.
Friendly staff, never a long wait, and always make my drinks correctly. If they mess up, they apologize profusely! Love this location.

Update: still horrible wait times. Even inside. No one seems like they have any ounce of hurry.   I love Starbucks. A lot. But this drive through on the weekends is so incredibly slow. I don't know if they're understaffed but sometimes a car won't come out of the drive through for over 5 minutes. Sometimes it seems almo
Really....not sure if it's one or two people working..6:15 am and must be 60 people waiting...ridiculous

UPDATE: decided to give this place another try and they failed . I ordered on the app & when I get to the window, they give me my drinks and tell me to have a good day.. I say, I ordered a lunch box and two cake pops.. they give me one cake pop and a lunch box. And they gave me the wrong cake pop. Back around, she apologized and gave me the right one. But idk why they can't get things right.   This place needs some work.. ordered a caramel frap and a cookie dough cake pop.. I was told the cake pop was out of stock. I asked which flavors they did have.. and she said cookie dough or chocolate. So I said okay I'll take the cookie dough. It was silent and she goes "so you want a cake pop?" I confirm, yes the cookie dough one like I asked three times now. Get to the window- I ask if I can pay cash but still use my rewards, she says yes. Takes my phone and then charges my card- WTF?? Come on Starbucks.

I came here randomly and actually tried the online purchasing option. It worked very well and walked right in and was handed my drink. The place has a decent amount of seating options. The employees here very nice and overall place is clean. I'm not too sure about the bathrooms though.   The drink was very good and the parking here is great unlike over crowded Philadelphia. I felt comfortable hanging out here and enjoyed relaxing and consuming my beverage. I will for sure stop by again when I'm in the area.

I really enjoy starbucks and appreciate it when I'm out of my area but after REPEATEDLY waiting extended times for my coffee I do not go here anymore.  Their drive-thru, and walk-up, are absolutely the slowest of any Starbucks I have ever been to.  EVER!  Speed it up please!

As someone who used to work at Starbucks, I feel I have enough authority to say that this is one of the best Starbucks I have been to.   My drink is always made exactly right, and all the employees are very friendly. I am known on a first name basis by several of them, and I visit this location 3-4 times a week.   The store is very clean and spacious. Even when they are busy, there are enough seats to be had and the service maintains a steady 5 out of 5.

Good enough.  What I would expect on an early travel morning. No wrong done here.  Just decent lattes at early hours.  Did the trick.  Did its job.  No harm no foul.  Keep churning them out.  Keep the machine running.

This location ruined my image of Starbucks years ago. Hadn't been here in well over a year because the customer service sucks. Regardless, people in O'Fallon/Shiloh must not care about the bad experiences or reviews here cuz even still today, the drive-thru line was wrapped around the store as it always is, blocking the cars of the people who decided to brave the hot mess nightmare inside of the store. I'll go to the Fvh Target location if I have to have Starbucks or I'll goto Circle K gas station and use their espresso machine and get treated way better and much quicker.

New location for this Starbucks. Garbage overflowing inside and out. Coffee is lukewarm and when we went to get a hotter one, we were told they were out of the dark roast and couldn't heat it up. Seems like poor management.
Great experience this morning with the young gentleman at the counter.  The only person to ask how to spell my name. Thank you!! A great way to start my morning:)
I come here at least a couple times a week, it's close to the gym and has a great atmosphere.

If there were a minus star I would give that!! The person behind the counter couldn't even look up to greet me. I could hardly hear a word she said and never once said thank you!!!  Than the girl who put my iced tea and sandwich on the counter mumbled to let me know my order was done and again grunted a thank you. Wow this Starbucks sucks. I've been to many and never had such an experience....blah

This Starbucks is small but very cozy. Staffed with a caring & generous crew, my experiences here have been wonderful! I usually find myself popping-in to grab an espresso drink, but occasionally I sit down and browse the internet, chat with friends or do some research for work. All together this Starbucks is one of those rare gems in a generally sparse coffee desert.

Everyone in this Starbucks is very nice and friendly, but it has a horrible stench inside.  There are a lot of homeless people in the area and they tend to camp out in the store, which makes you want to grab your coffee and get out as soon as possible!

This starbucks is right by my place and while it lasts, it has been an essential establishment for me. Unfortunately this location is closing very soon due to Covid-19 impact I assume. I really enjoyed chitchatting with the employees there as they were the best seriously. I have never had a single bad experience at this location. Special thanks to Josh - he truly was the best. I am going to miss you all so much when you vacate.

I used to come to this Starbucks almost every morning but decided to drive a little further due to the incompetence of its baristas and staff especially Emely who justifies the employees when they over charge or give you a shaken expression when you ordered a chai latte. On top of that the lines are ridiculously long. This Starbucks needs to get it together.

This is hands down the best Starbucks in the Santa Barbara area, and there a lot of them!  I go in there many mornings and some nights and even when it's busy, they're cool. Gets a little nuts sometimes, though and they go through cream so fast! The bathroom passcode thing is annoying.   What happened to the singing barista though?
This Starbucks is awesome.....  The staff here is just unbelievable and it the customers are so great... It a pleasure to sit and enjoy great company of a cup of joe in the morning

I found this Starbucks to be exceptional when my husband and I were in town. We were staying at the adjacent Marriott hotel so coming down the elevators to get a cup of coffee was a huge perk for the hotel.   The staff here is awesome. Let me tell you why. I collect the Starbucks mugs from places I've visited and unfortunately, this store ran out. The two guys that were working the first day we were there was super apologetic that they didn't have any in stock but "thought" they might get a shipment overnight. They were nice enough to give me directions to other Starbucks around that they thought I should also try. Unfortunately, I struck out there as well.  When we went in later that night, I told the same guy that we were unsuccessful in finding the mug but he was still hoping that they'd get a shipment in that night but urged me to check the very next morning.   The next morning rolled around and the two guys weren't working but a new staff came in and I told them the story of how we were anxiously awaiting the arrival of these mugs. The gal went into the back and sadly, they did not arrive in the shipment. I was bummed because we were leaving that morning and it was clear that I was going home empty handed.  I was disappointed and gave my husband the "sad face" look.   We proceeded to place or our for a few pastries and our coffees and when the gal told us our total, we thought she had missed something. Turns out, she comp'd our coffee because they didn't have the mugs we wanted. How sweet!   I thought that was a nice gesture and it sent us on the road to the New York with a smile on our face. When a store is super busy as this store was has the time to go out of their way to find a bunch of silly tourist a mug, I would say that's as good as it gets.
We went through the drive through and I orderd an iced pink drink.  They weren't kidding about iced. It looks like I spent $5 on ice. My drink was about 90% ice and 10% drink. I won't be back here.

This has got to be the worst Starbucks Iusually come here  every Friday after work and a few months ago they changed all of the Baristas and people that work here and the service has completely downhill the wait is long it's dirty in there and it doesn't seem like anyone really knows what they're doing I hate to say this because I've really enjoyed stopping here every Friday on my way home as my personal treat I think I have to find a new Starbucks because unfortunately this has just not gotten any better I waited a while to give it a chance but nothings changed I'm very disappointed and I think Starbucks as a corporation would be very disappointed with this particular Starbucks  café

Came in right around lunch time, and it wasn't too crowded. Available seats for a good conversation with a friend. Barista/cashier was very kind and friendly. My vanilla chai latte was yummy, though it took a little long for them to make it.

Always busy, always fresh.  First time I've noticed a board with pics of the variations on drinks.  Great idea.  It sold me on the Danica.  A blend of coffee, caramel.  Recommended for bold flavor.  Try it out!

As much as one tries to avoid eating at a non-local chain when in New Orleans, sometimes PJs just doesn't cut it. For when that happens, this Starbucks is fine. The Northeasterners from Tulane flock here.

Went today bc I worked at home - live at SoBro right next door.  Coffee and food was ok but the service is abominable.  Line almost out the door yet there was one guy taking orders and a young girl who looked like she just got out of bed standing around picking at her nails.  When I asked the cashier what she does you would've thought I committed the unpardonable.  Was reprimanded by the cashier, her and even another customer.  "She works very hard" - vehement defense of the indefensible.  Anyway I ordered 5 coffees and  5 sandwiches - what a deal, only $50!!!  I generally avoid Starbucks bc of mediocre quality and high prices - guarantee you I won't visit this one ever again.

Don't bother using the app to place your order, its faster staying in the drive-thru line. I've used the app multiple times and I am always waiting for my drinks. The app told me 2 to 6 minutes and I waited 15 minutes for my drinks. For the past three times I've ordered a bagel toasted with cream cheese the first two times they're out of bagels and the last time they had the bagel but no cream cheese or butter. What a waste of time! Completely frustrating and annoying.
Worst customer service. Barista ignoring  customers refilling coffee machines. I left not paying for that.

Starbucks is A-OK Starbucks the only thing I do not like about Starbucks they need to keep their counters clean and keep your floors and bathrooms clean it gets mad when you make the drink wrong and asked to remake it just my point of you other than that I love it

I go to Starbucks every day (sad to admit) but I find myself trying to make my way to this particular location. There are so many work stations available and the guests are generally quiet. It's a perfect place to open up your laptop and get into the zone. The staff is really friendly and accommodate requests with grace.

Fast, efficiently and friendly. I was in and out of this little location on a Tampa visit and my order was perfect. The only slight gripe was about the cafe that needed some tending to, but other than that it was great!
This is my absolute favorite local Starbucks location! It's always clean and my order is usually correct. I really like the employees.

I wanted to visit this Starbucks several times before providing a review to make sure I was offering feedback for more than just one experience.  I have to say, this is a great location!! Each time I have visited inside, it has been very clean and the staff is upbeat and courteous!  I've been through the drive thru as well and experienced a little bit of a wait, but I find that to be normal since they make these delicious drinks to order.  Pro-tip:  If you're on a time crunch but need to get your coffee fix, order ahead using the Starbucks app and you can pop inside to grab your ready to go brew from the front counter!
ordered coffee from a nice fella who was surprisingly fun.   Drink came up fast and the store is clean and in a great location.  pay parking btw, but there's plenty of it.  go!

When the purchase is courtesy from others (gift card), then why not male your way to Starbucks. I really only purchase flavored iced coffees such as caramel and mocha and vanilla. Some times the iced coffees have been very flavorful and enjoyable to drink. Other times I have gotten iced coffees from here, it did not leave anything to be desired. Hoping for better flavored iced coffees.

"Cookie Monster," or the incredible drive through employee formally known as Ryan, made my Starbucks visit Sunday absolutely spectacular. He was funny, kind, quick and turned an otherwise average visit to Starbucks to above and beyond. I believe it's employees like him that make a difference in someone's day by spreading joy. THANK YOU RYAN - Coooooookies!

I rarely write reviews, but the customer service I received from Katherine today at the drive thru was the best I've received in a very long time. It took a little longer than usual to make a drink I had ordered, but she was so kind and friendly I didn't mind the wait.

[Out-of-towner Review]  Thank you and you suck...are how I feel!  Given that you are in a convention center, you'd think although it was the weekend, you'd have better hours of operation. Conventions run 7 days a week. My convention happened to run Sunday - Tuesday. So opening day (Sunday), everyone was jonsing for coffee all day, but after 2PM you were screwed! It was closed...you could endure the gross humidity and/or pouring rain and walk a few blocks to the Marriott Starbucks, but theirs closes early too! Annoyed...to say the least...  BUT, for the two remaining days was their saving grace. Although the line was continuously 40+ people deep, these automons kept it movin'! No lie, they were quick and efficient for a very small set-up. They weren't the friendliest, but they were damn good and efficient with their time...you can't complain about that...  If I walk the length of this convention center, you better reward me with a caffeinated beverage. Your wimpy hours just won't cut it for all the various people who saunter through here...step up your game, but keep up the good work! It's a Catch 22, sorry guys!  Keepin' convention'er's caffeinated on their timetable...

I come here all the time and I asked for a iced coffee with vanilla sweet cream foam, sweetener and caramel drizzle. I get the drink and it's just sweetner and caramel. So I go back in to add the sweet cream foam and they gave me a black coffee with foam no sweetener, no caramel, so I ask her to redo it and she puts sweet cream foam, sweeter, and no caramel. I shouldn't have to ask them to remake my drink 3 times when I just paid 5 dollars for a coffee and it's still not correct.

Nice looking Starbucks, friendly employees who don't look down at you for not being a coffee expert and asking questions.    Their hours are short compared to other local stores, they close at 9:30 but the food gets rolled up around 8.  Get there early if you want a muffin with your coffee!
Very wonderful service was in and out in under three minutes (during a busy time) and my coffee is absolutely perfect. Totally made my day keep up the good work!!

Two Words: Study Spot  Out of all the Starbucks in Santa Barbara, this one is my favorite!   I accidentally ordered a chai latte(I meant to order a brewed chai tea), and without any hesitation they changed my order. They did not give me any attitude (unlike at SOME Starbucks location).   Sometimes, there is a long line, but I find that common at most of their locations. However, I must note that they try their best to get the line moving along.  This is a great study spot because there are plenty of tables. There are even comfortable chairs and tables outside.  I always find parking - there is a 2 hour limit.   Best service, Best Starbucks.

The ladies were awesome today. They were calling our name to board and we had to leave without our drinks.  With our flight pushed back on a delay and terminal changes and then changed back to the original gate.  Big communication going on, we missed our flight.   Going back to Starbucks knowing they  tossed our drinks the manager remade them no charge for us.  Thanks for turning our day around. This was true customer service. Thanks ladies! I'll always remember this.

The customer service from the younger woman with brown hair in particular, was horrible. Headset on, trying to bring food to customers in the room, while also taking orders. She was rude to me as well as my father, and people like her can ruin an entire visit. Saturday, July 10 at 7:50am.

This is my favorite location on the Westbank, a zillion times better than the one on Terry Parkway.  The employees are super nice and friendly, and they remember my drinks and ask about my day.  The atmosphere is good, perfect for work, study, reading, or light conversation (inside voices and no screaming children... usually).  They were recently remodeled, and I'm going to be an old fart and say that I liked it better before.  They added a "community table" (that's what I call it), which is long and sits a few people at the one table.  As someone who cherishes personal space, I'm never going to sit there.  Now that I've finished whining, I do enjoy this location, and I appreciate the pride that the employees have in their store and work.  It's mega-obvious, especially when compared to the craphole that is the Terry Parkway location.
Starbucks in Harrahs gets an A. Cozy little spot on the second floor. The coffee is hot and fresh. The lemon pound cake so yummy. The staff is friendly. The banana art made me smile. Good job!

Hello folks,   After picking the kids up from school we have a weekly Starbuck's day. It typically varies and we frequent the Rock Hill store. One afternoon after a Sunset Hills Home Depot visit, I mentioned to the kids there was a new Starbuck's across the street. Let's give it a shot..... Surprisingly my daughter said dad this is the best Starbuck's period I have ever had. My son replied equally.... I then tasted mine and there was a fullness/richness about the coffee... We thought maybe this was just a fluke. The following week we went back to our normal store. It was ok but just not the same... Therefore, the next week we made a special trip back to the Sunset store to see if there was something truly different... In short, absolutely!!!! Everything was made to perfection... Not sure what it is, maybe a new store, new equipment?   In summary, we are now making this our primary store and even going out of the way to make it happen.... Keep up the good work Sunset!!!!  Thank you......

Parking can be difficult at this location but thats the same song for most places in the village. Parking garage is available two blocks away on College ave plus taking a stroll is what broad ripple is all about. Even though its not the best location to grab coffee on the run, I do prefer this one if I want to chill and hang out with a friend especially in the winter because they have a wood burning fireplace.  Haven't seen another starbucks with a fireplace...very cozy and an average amount of seating is available.  Staff is always helpful and friendly. Great spot to people watch as it is in the heart of broad ripple with window viewing galore.

Cashier seems nice enough, but the young black girl who fixes the drink has an attitude, and made a mess of my drink right in front of me. Considering the location, I would think they would have a much friendlier staff.

Wow this is a first! I am not a person to write negative reviews but after my experience here I had to... I visited this Starbucks at approx: 6:50pm on 03AUG2013. I just ordered water at the drive-threw.... Now I am not sure about you but I love the water filters that Starbucks uses... nothing compares. I don't usually just order water but when I do I always TIP because Starbucks doesn't charge for water. So first thing I noticed is the girl who served me "Cortney" disappeared with the cups for a brief moment then returned to the water station that was in view and poured my waters:( then handed the waters with a condescending "Enjoy your waters" I had already given her my TIP and I drove out into the parking lot. My wife was already pointing out a lot of debris including fibers, particulates, and an unidentified substance. Upset I took it back in. I will spare you the details but I will tell you the "their" lack of surprise and remorse said it all. BAD BAD BAD employee's. I feel that Starbucks is a great business and they have even better products, its to bad their employees don't feel the same way.

Honestly the worst customer service I have ever had in a Starbucks. Can't even name names of i wanted to because nobody wears name tags.   I walked in wanting to buy some coffee beans for my new ninja coffee machine. There was a guy putting some bags up. I asked him if he could recommend one of the dark or medium roasts for sweetness. He ignored me. I asked again louder. He walked away and went behind the counter.   I picked one out myself and approached the counter to pay and get a drink. I smiled and asked him if the were any chocolate chip cookies left. He did not respond.   Frustrated at this point I asked if he was ready for my order. He grimaced at me and still said nothing. I told him my coffee order and asked for a chocolate chip cookie, heated up. After ringing me out he threw a cookie in a bag. It was still cold.   I made my way over to the barista making my order. I asked her how her day was going. She ignored me. She put my drink on the counter and I said thank you and she shrugged and started to walk away. I took a sip and said "hey there is no flavoring or cream in this like I ordered, could I please get some?" She put a bottle of milk on the counter and walked away. I put the milk in my coffee and gave up on the flavoring.  I don't feel like I was rude, and I was trying to be smiling and friendly so I don't know why everyone seemed disgruntled. They didn't seem very busy, and I don't expect a grand conversation or red carpet treatment or anything but a smile would be nice I guess.   I don't know, it just really threw me off. I don't even care about the coffee being wrong so much as everyone glaring and ignoring customers. :(

Out of all the Starbucks. This one is actually one of my faves... half because It's right next to the lab I work on my thesis in.    But on a more real note - this place is PACKED.  8 am coffee in 10 minutes? Forget it.  Order ahead is a must!    They do have a wider variety of snacks, mugs, and sandwiches but parking sucks since it's on campus and you have to really be *on campus* to get it.
Is great place for read book .. and cofee the space is magic and g re at i love the university and love this special place

Just picked up a Luke warm Carmel ribbon crunch frap. They 100% made it for someone from a mobile order 10 minutes before I got there and since they didn't pick it up yet gave it to me. I called and the woman was nice but told me the time stamp was from when I ordered online, I didn't order online... they offered to remake it if I went back and I was gonna make it 2 stars but this was deliberately shitty service and honestly gross. I'll drive extra for a Starbucks that doesn't give me someone's old drink that was sitting out.

Well they finally moved across the street to a little bit larger building. This one is a standalone building and it's nice, it's modern, but there still no comfy chairs inside. Same standard drinks and food.   However, I will say before I left, I asked for a glass of water and it wasn't triple filtered which shocked me. I had to throw the water out and I could tell the ice was good. Not sure what happened there. But if you're coming to study and have a coffee, it's new and luxurious. There's a patio; but the area is limited and a bit crowded if your looking for privacy. If you wanna sit at the counter, it's roomy and there are lots of seats. But if you want a nice comfortable chair to sit on and talk to your other guest... Move on down the road as there are no comfy chairs in this place.

It pains me to write this review but time and time again I've been burned by nasty drinks at this location. Where's the consistency?   The interior is beautiful, I used to love coming to this location when I worked in the Camino Real Marketplace. Every morning I was greeted and they knew my drink.   Now they don't, that's understandable. My drink is a white mocha, hardly a difficult one. Why doesn't it ever taste like it is supposed to?

This is a really good Starbucks. I love that it has a drive though and carries nitro cold brew (which I am addicted too). I see Danny almost every time I visit and he always remembers me which really makes my day!   The only downside is that this place is usually packed so the wait can be a little longer.

Awesome staff. Always friendly. Rember names and drink pref.   As with most Starbucks consistent quality and if not right they will make it right. Atmosphere is great lost of parking and seating. My fav coffee house in Reno

Service sucks, came here multiple times and was constantly disappointed. This time the lady seemed to get frustrated with me because of how I was  pronouncing the word "acai". She bluntly corrected me which was annoying. At least be nice about it. Another time, i got an iced caramel macchiato. It was absolutely horrible. The barista probably was drizzling the caramel in my drink for a good 15 seconds. I would sip clumps of caramel....it was so bad

I love that this Starbucks is on my way to work.  Whenever I go the Batista's are always friendly with a smile on their faces.  The line inside and the drive through always go quickly even in the morning when everyone seems to need that cup of delicious coffee.  This location is also a great place to stay and read, study, or just sit and enjoy your coffee.  It is always quiet enough with a very pleasant atmosphere.  Thank you for keeping me awake and alert at work!

I've never been to this location but the floor is filthy. It's 3pm and crumbs are all over the floor. Everywhere. Have no idea how long it's been like this or how often staff are supposed to sweep, but I haven't seen a Starbucks this poorly kept up in awhile.

Coming from the Northeast, I can't believe there are so few Starbucks locations in Nola! I believe there's only two!  This one on Magazine has a great parking lot. Plus, there are so many plush comfy seats, tables for working, and they always make my drink just right.
It's great! It's so close to where I live that I don't even have to drive there. The service is also good, I've never been disappointed from this location and I have been here many times.

What this location has working for them:  The fact that they have a drive-thru.  Sometimes, I just need a quick cuppa and I haven't the time or inclination to park and go in.    They are speedy.  I have come during rush hour and even with the crazy volume of people, they get you in, out, and caffeinated in no time.    The service is always friendly.  I haven't had a single experience where this has not been the case.  What I don't like:  The convenience of the entrance.  Which, I'm guessing is less their fault than the city's...but a word to the wise--the only way to get into this joint is if you are heading east on State Street.  If your heading west, you must go a bit up State, turn around, and head back east (and at that point, you might as well just hit the drive-thru at Cafe Capri and be done with it).  You also cannot access it from Veterans Memorial...though you can exit there.  Really, not the best set up.  So, if your heading east on State and need a quick caffeine pick-me-up...they'll take good care of you!!
Really nice cashier, service was fast and location was in a nice spot. I would come back to this spot.   I would recommend this spot

This is a nice location. I come to this Starbucks almost everyday because all my classes are online and it's nice to drink coffee while I work. It is always clean and for the most part quite. Out of the all the Starbucks in this area this is my favorite one to work at and study. I took off a star because I literally come here everyday around the same times and the baristas still don't know who I am and they usually seem caught up in talking to each other. But at least they always get my order right.

I happily live less than 2 miles from 2 Starbucks but this one is the closest. I am thinking, walk up here on a nice sunny day type of close because it is only about 2.5 blocks away.  The staff: They're always friendly and accomodating. They don't rush you.  You can always expect greetings when you come in and smiles on their faces.  They always get my orders right and they do it in an efficient manner.  The storefront: Clean and well-maintained.  There are several tables and comfortable seating inside. Some outdoor seating as well and a drive-thru.  You can get the usual drinks available at any other Starbucks here as well as the pastry/food options.  You can hang out and enjoy your beverage inside where you will find a few tables and comfortable chairs or take it to go.
Hotel 2nd floor location. 1st place WINNER, by far the MOST EXPENSIVE Starbucks location I have been to. Expect to pay a premium.
This store is a disgrace. Employees are rude and have no regard for their neighbors in the nearby apartments. It's also dirty.

This Starbucks has always been there for me whenever I need a little pick-me-up in the morning before I start my shift. Its the fastest mobile ordering Starbucks around University City area. I can place a mobile order while I'm on the way to work, and bam 5'-10' later, my drink is ready for sippy sippy. Regular morning staffs are amazing and super efficient. They're giving out orders like boom boom boom, super fast UNLESS they have a new hire that requires training or something goes wrong with the mobile order system. Other than that, my coffee is always ready whenever I'm in the store to pick it up.  After morning rush hours slows down, the amount of staffs are also reduced, everything is going with a slower pace. The weekend also has fewer staffs compared to the weekdays.  The disadvantage here is that there's no parking on the street, so its very challenging for customers to stop by or even pick up with mobile order. It's only more convenient if you're visitors from the hospital or employees from the hospitals.   Staffs are friendly. They remember your name if you're one of the frequent customers. They also make me happy whenever they greet me by name and ask how I am doing.

Wow. Quite possibly the worst Starbucks experience. Came in with a couple friends, one of which lives down the road and usually avoids this terrible Starbucks. I asked the cashier if the "salted cream cold foam cold brew" could be made as nondairy. He said of course and gave me my milk options. I went with coconut milk. My friend then said she wanted the same thing but with soy milk. He then asked if she wanted it hot or cold. Idk how cold brew can be hot  he then said he thought she wanted a salted caramel mocha. We clarified what we wanted.   As we waited for our drinks, the barista sneezed in his hands and had a huge snot rocket. Gross. He then walked to the back, wiped his hands and came back out. Def wasn't back there long enough to wash them. He stood around behind the bar and a girl I assume was the shift lead motioned to him to wash his hands. So he rinsed them. Kept sniffling and touching his nose. Not who you want making your drinks, especially when you have a 7 week old, as my friend does. He should not have been working in food service. Point blank. If you are sick, do not work around food.   My friend asked the supervisor if someone else could make our drinks because of what we witnessed. As she was asking this, I see him blending heavy cream for our drinks, which of course, does not fall into the nondairy category. He hands us one of them and we don't even want to touch it. The supervisor sends him on break and comes in to remake our drinks. She asks if our drinks are the "salted caramel mochas", which clearly homeboy rang in wrong so we let her know what we actually want. She then says it's made with heavy cream so I point out that the cashier told me it could be made nondairy. He says "oh I thought you wanted salted caramel mochas". I asked if I could get something else since i don't want dairy and they say they'll try to make it nondairy. Which btw, totally possible but might not be as foamy.   After being in here 15-20 minutes for 3 drinks, to say i was frustrated was an understatement. There was no apology, no service recovery, nothing. Definitely need to retrain this staff, never seen such an incompetent bunch. The shift lead seemed to be the only one who knew what she was doing, understood food allergies, and cared about her job/customers.

Bf ordered a coffee frap and got an iced coffee instead---? It's quite a piss off when you go out of your way, and can't go back to complain because you're in a rush. So drove for nothing to get the wrong drink. Great.

I have probably visited 4 or 5 Starbucks' in the city, and this one is one of the better ones.  The staff has been extremely friendly and helpful every time I visit, and there is a nice upstairs seating area to relax and browse the internet while drinking your whatever.  On a side note, I picked up a box of their new instant iced coffee, and I think it is really good.  One box is probably enough for 10 or more small sized ice coffees, so the price isn't too bad either.

The staffs were very friendly. That's why I am giving them 2 stars rather than 1. I ordered grande caramel mocchiato and paid $5.45 for it. ($1.00 more than a regular Starbucks)The barista did not make my drink right so I had to add an extra shot. Usually, Starbucks offers for free to make it right but I have to pay 92 cents. So my coffee is almost $7.00. I have Starbucks gift card but cannot use it here. They were too pricey and didn't make a go I like that they are inside the hotel. Anyway, I am not going back to this place.
I don't drink coffee, but sometimes crave a green tea or egg sandwich.  It's good for a random pit stop but I think Starbucks prices are too high.
So rude. Not a friendly bunch to start your day with. Additionally weird music playing. I would recommend a neighborhood coffee shop.
It's Starbucks.  The coffee is always consistent. The great thing about Starbucks is you always know what to expect.    The staff was friendly, the store was clean.  A good visit.

The girl that's in the drive-through every morning is completely rude. Told me today after going there for years and  getting a Venti ice water With my $10-$20 orders, that they don't do that. Just went there yesterday different person they did that. She also told me they're no longer giving out straws but didn't give me a lid with a non straw friendly hole in it.  When I asked for a straw she basically threw it at me. Every other Starbucks in town will give you a Venti ice water and a straw if you ask if you need one. Unless they give you the right lid are you supposed to pull your lid off when you're driving a car and drink your coffee I don't think so! I'm writing to the corporate office as well.
I never can understand why they can't make the drink right (that I pay more than $5 for) and when you ask them to fix it , they act like It's my fault they made it incorrect.

I have been here six times now and all six times something is wrong. The top two issues are incredibly slow service and inconsistency with the way their drinks are made. I mean I've been to Starbucks all across the country. Big City Starbucks and small city Starbucks and this is the slowest one by far. I waited 27 minutes for an iced coffee! Safe to say...I will not be back.

"Caitlyn" like to close the store 4 hours early and tell potential customers that their hours are "adjusted" this week. She likes to try and act condescending when asked why the drive through appears open and is not even blocked. "There was a sign; what more do you want?" There is no sign at the drive thru. She then proceeds to shared her long tenured 1 year knowledge of how restaurants work. I'm sure it won't be long before this Starbucks is under new management allowing employees to speak to potential customers in this manner. The reviews will speak for themselves. There are plenty of Starbucks out there. Avoid this one if you must have overpriced coffee. Better yet; head to your nearest Dunkin.

Usually, this is my go-to Starbucks. They do always take a little bit to get your order ready, but USUALLY polite friendly people... Unlike most of the staff, there's ONE lady that's just PLAIN RUDE.. I stopped by at 8:07PM, drive thru.. (checked online and it said they were still open).. pulled up to the drive thru, "hello?" Nothing. "Hey are you guys open?" Nothing. "Helloooo?" NOTHING! Waited like a good 5min and no answer at all. I drive up to the window, (keep in mind they don't even open the window to answer my question), and ask the lady are you guys open? She literally looks at me never opens the window or comes over period. Just stood there and said, "Does it look like we're open??!" Making rude face expressions, never coming over, never opening the window, AND she kept acting like she couldn't hear me after that. IGNORING me... Plain nasty and RUDE.

There is almost always a wait here. The staff here are hit or miss, but mostly nice. They always have at least three people working the bar, but they still run behind. My Venti iced latte was good, as it usually is. They also carry hot breakfast sandwiches, paninis, and salads, along with the usual bakery items.

A nice Starbucks. Parking is easy, although I do prefer the drive-through Starbucks. This one is clean and appears well-managed. The staff are friendly and my chai tea latte was made so quickly I thought maybe they had read my mind as I was standing in line!

When I lived in Goleta this was my favorite Starbucks to go to. With me moving out and staffing turnover I didn't know what to expect. I went in today (11/12/2012) and purchased an Iced Venti, no whip Gingerbread Latte. The lady who took my order was friendly and the person who processed my transaction was even friendlier. I received my drink and when I took a sip I noticed that the Gingerbread flavoring was missing. It was basically espresso shots and milk. I hate having to have anyone at Starbucks remake my drink. So I went back in line and informed the person who took my order what had happened. She took my cup, said "oh..." and remade my drink. No apology was offered and I took my remade drink and left. Poor customer service in my opinion.  I've been to other Starbucks where they apologize profusely for making the drink incorrectly. They even get to the point of handing out a "Your next drink is on us" card! I don't know if this is still implemented but it would be nice if Starbucks was consistent across the board. Then again, maybe it's because of the staffing turn over at this particular Starbucks. I might just have to go to the Camino Real Starbucks next time around.
In line right now for drive thru. It's going to clear 30 minutes fir a coffee. Going to be late to work. Crazy.....

Now it's taken me quite some time to actually write this review because I wasn't quite sure what I wanted to say..but here goes.........  Don't get me wrong, I do like Starbucks but I finally came to the realization that it's overpriced and overrated.  I was a Gold status for quite a few years as my favorite drink was the hot or iced chai tea and more recently, hot green tea.  I began boycotting them when they had the holiday red cup release in 2018 for the fact that I received by red cup but with no lid since the store was out --- the barista @ that time advised me to just visit another location or call and ask Starbucks and they should be able to give me one because they sometimes just ship to few to the individual stores.  I went around and asked other locations who were sold out of the cups and didn't have any extra lids so I proceeded to call Starbucks as well as post an inquiry online.  I was not given any help or support for this...just basically "oh well" and they couldn't do anything about it.  Now my concern is how can Starbucks send a store a certain number of red cups but not enough lids for the cups.  Is the expectation for guests to have their HOT drinks in lid-less cups!? I could spill my drink on myself and it's not cool to have my Starbucks HOT drink in a lid-less cup because most of the time you're on the go when enjoying your overpriced drink.  Based on the level of service I've had at other establishments when there are shortfalls or issues w/things when being a committed customer, I was surprised by the level of service and just lack of caring I received when I spoke to someone at corporate about the issue.    When I look at Starbucks now,  not being intrigued or care to be a Gold star status anymore, I don't know why consumers are so crazy about this place!  It can't be that the drinks are THAT amazing that people are willing to pay so much for their iced coffee.  When I used to get the Chai tea --- it was never made fresh --- the chai comes out of a cardboard container, heated or iced.  Yes, I used to be one of these people but not anymore .... and now I would rather save that $5 that I used to spend on a drink and get a smoothie from a place that tastes much better and is not overly sweetened and unhealthy. I'm always amazed at how any Starbucks establishment always has a crowd --- you almost never see one that's empty or not busy in some capacity.  And as I look at other reviews on Yelp, I can see that mine is clearly one of the very few that isn't 4-5 stars.

Awful Starbucks location. My wife waited in line for close to 30 minutes.. only to find they had nothing vanilla and basically no food. They could only make caramel, peppermint, or cinnamon drinks. Ever heard of inventory management? It would have been nice to know they were literally out of pretty much everything prior to standing in line for half an hour. When my wife got to the counter and politely voiced her frustration, the cashier just had a blank stare and acted rude. Terrible service, and whatever drink she ended up with was inedible.

This is Starbucks at Poydras and St. Charles. It is an awesome and smaller location than the one a few blocks away at 700 Canal. A bit less crowded and touristy. The beverages are amazing. Got my red holiday reusable cup though I missed the launch so I am happy!!!! Baristas are kind and friendly. Location is great to work in or pass time enjoying a tasty beverage. You'll love this location too!!!
Working online!  Great place to work!! This Starbucks has alot  To choose from coffee drinks snacks..great layout With different seating areas clean restroom...friendly enough staff!
Always busy at this location esp because of the drive through option. They all move fast here though so they've all been crisp every time I come here. Friendly cashiers

I'm a huge fan of Starbucks and have never had a bad experience at one before now. I didn't know there could be so many incompetent, rude people in one spot at one time. And the line for wrong orders was longer than the purchasing line... You're better off walking a block and a half to Academia Del Caffe at Penn Square.

This is a very busy Starbucks. I had opportunity to spend an hour or so here while waiting for my daughter and her entourage as they were shopping for a wedding dress. Though busy, it was never crowded. The only drawback was the counter staff. As with many of the other reviewers, I also experienced a rude/snippy tone. Customer service is key for Starbucks. At some point, if this isn't addressed, people will choose another option.

I LOVE this location. This is possibly my favorite Starbucks in the Edmonton vicinity. There is just that something special about the Highstreet area that I can't quite describe. It is a feeling and until you visit on a sunny afternoon you may not know what I am referring to.   Anyways the Starbucks itself is in the end unit of a strip mall giving it access to a great patio space. Well the patio isn't as large as it could be; they make the best of the space available. I like that the patio feels somewhat secluded and has a mix of trees (providing shade) and sunny spots.  Inside is tight on space too. Again they make the best of the space available but there are not too many tables and chairs. It is always clean and tidy and there are bathroom available for patrons. As this is a busy location I appreciate that the service is always stellar - the staff are friendly while remaining efficient.  The location is what makes this Starbucks special - you must try it out!

Although this particular location does not have a drive thru but local patrons do make an effort to come in. Why? Because of the service! They will know your name and drink order right when you walk in. Always friendly and service is speedy.

I have been to this Starbucks everytime I come into town and I have to say, it is one of the most cleanest and nicely ran establishments I ever visit. I stay in harrahs frequently on business and always get my morning cup of coffee here . The people are absolutely wonderful and their lobby is always clean clean clean! I also appreciate that they have a wide selection of drinks and pastries for everyone to choose from, especially at odd ends of the day! Overall I am always pleased and highly recommend this place is anyone is ever in harrahs and is looking for their morning or mid afternoon coffee and pastry!
Tried the drive thru. It's wrapped around the building so I go inside only to find nobody in there but 4 people just standing behind the counter talking. Good job guys. Way to run a business.
Great service during Covid! Drive though was super fast and easy. I ordered like normal, at the first stop. Then I paid and got my drink at the second stop. Girl was friendly.

This Starbucks wasn't bad at all. Though we've been trying to get in the habit of patronizing local eateries and nothing else, every so often, for convenience's sake, we have to pop in to the occasional chain. What made this experience memorable was the polite, genuinely happy, adorable barista that served us. She exuded positivity and ensured that our order was correct. And we don't often order food from Starbucks, but on this particular visit, we ordered the egg and chorizo patty sandwich... WHAT?! They had me at chorizo patty. It was piping hot and SOOOO yummy! I'm not sure that it was enough to pry us out of the hands of our local coffeehouses and eateries, but it was definitely something to scratch our itch at the moment. Great job :)
A great little place to just relax and enjoy some chit chat.  SBUX coffee is the same everywhere but the locations are what make places more special.

This the worst Starbucks we have been to. After waiting a long wait time, I placed my order for just a chocolate croissant. The cashier didn't greet customers and wasn't friendly despite being early in the morning. She at least took my order quickly and handed the wrapped to someone else (usually the cashier takes it out and warms it up).   Well after a while, I started seeing people that were behind getting their drinks and breakfast items. I then proceeded to ask where was my croissant because it was just a pastry. Then a woman said it was next and that she was busy making sandwiches and it would take 18 seconds. I guess she forgot or didn't get to drink her coffee this morning because she didn't get those sandwich order after I had placed mine.   Then I asked to speak to a manager and she said she was one. I believe she said her name was KG when I asked. I then let her know it was poor management on her end not to know how to prioritize which orders should go out first. I let her know I was before those items that she choose to do first. Her poor judgement is poor reflection of her leadership role to her employees.  KG
The morning staff is fast friendly and consistent. Pretty much how your typical starbucks should be

So disappointed in this Starbucks. I thought it would be so cool as it I a drive thru on a major Highway but the bathroom was very dirty and the garbage cans were overflowing with garbage. The bathroom also was extremely dirty. I don't like it when these places are filthy

While I don't have any problem reviewing "chain' establishments, with only an exception or two I don't tend to review places where you can pretty much expect the same thing from location to location.  This is, for the most part, the truth with Starbucks. Starbucks is Starbucks, ya' know? If you don't like their coffee in one location, there's a pretty good chance you won't like it in another location and, of course, vice versa. Now then, you do get some variations in service and I do think those are worth mentioning.  So, here I am.   I was spending the evening at this corner of 56th & Illinois - dinner at Byrne's Grilled Pizza, a visit to Kids Ink, ice cream at Graeter's and, at the last minute, a stop at this Starbucks location for a shaken iced tea for the road. I mean, heck, even the fire station in this neighborhood is pretty good to look at.  As has been noted, this location is located on the corner and it's in an older, fairly upscale neighborhood. While not all the buildings in this area are wheelchair friendly, Starbucks built a decent ramp into their place and despite it being an incredibly small location it's also pretty well laid out for a wheelchair.   I was actually wheeling by Starbucks when an employee appeared at the door. As I haven't known them to be incredibly hard-sell with their coffee, it quickly became apparent he believed I was on the way in and because it was raining and a bit chilly he was there to make sure I got in alright.   Great move, Starbucks.   There's actually an entrance right on Illinois and an entrance on the 56th Street side. The Illinois Street entrance, while appearing to be the main entrance, isn't adjacent to the ordering side. It's not a huge deal, but worth noting. As I entered, another friendly employee poked gentle fun of my Gold Club card and the fact that I corrected myself when I referred to my drink as merely an "iced tea" and not a "shaken iced tea." She was like "I'd have known what you meant." I almost said "Yeah, well, I've heard Starbucks can be kind of militant about the whole drink name thing," but I decided to appreciate her friendly demeanor.   My drink was prepared quickly and correctly. The same employee who'd opened the door on my way in opened it on the way out. While this isn't particularly rare for someone in a wheelchair, it still was a notch above on a night when it was raining. It was appreciated.   As others have noted, the interior is small and I could see where it would easily fill up. This location is fairly close to Butler University, though I'd imagine Butler has their own options and I can think of 1-2 other Starbucks locations also close to Butler along with a Hubbard & Cravens.   This place fits the neighborhood feel of this corner. They were warm, friendly and inviting and their interior reflects their upscale area because you do have some coffee options you may not find in the more urban settings.

Starbucks! It's random that I come to this location but when I do I usually go through the drive thru. When I go through the drive through it's flawless. On Monday 11/25/19 I decided to go inside‍.The girl at the register..ughhh let's jus say RUDE!! Aside from placing my order I asked for a large cup of water with ice. Sounds simple right... so when I went to pick up my order the guy gave me my food, coffee and a little cup of ice water. Since the girl who took my order wasn't busy I went over to her and let her know I asked for a LARGE cup of ice water. Her response was.. well you have to let us know you wanted a VENTI cup.. I wanted to say, really fool I don't work at STarbucks to know their terminology! Instead I just let her know that whenever I go through the drive thru it's not an issue.. Anyway.. she takes the cup pours it into this VENTI cup n then fills it up with just ICE! Her attitude was straight rude definitely not what I would expect at a Starbucks in CHERRY hill. So for her negativity this Starbucks gets a SUPER

Came in three straight days while staying at the nearby Residence Inn and was able to get my wife's favorite coffee(Peppermint White Mocha) right every morning and my daughter was equally happy with her Frap Mocha. Even with a line was able to get in and out less than ten minutes. I had the artisan bacon breakfast sandwich and exactly what I expected. Just wished they had a drive through.

Second time coming into this Starbucks. I try to stop in this one over the others downtown. The staff is so kind and my drinks are made quick! I'm writing this review because I tried the Tampa Sunset and it didn't disappoint! It's a great summer tea option!

Generally id just go to a different location. (Which i will most likely do.) But this location is closer than most. Im not sure what the deal is but this location just CANT seem to get it right. There is ALWAYS something missing. Now im not just some karen freaking out. I actually work in a kitchen. And in turn i read the tickets fully so i understand what im making. I ensure the food i produce is what the customer asked for. So i feel it should be the same in a "coffee kitchen". Ill be buying from local coffee joints from now on. Like Black Crown or Coffee Times.

More people making coffee than in the store buying coffee and after a 10 minute wait, I was told that my order would be another 10 minutes.  20 minute wait for the limited number of people in the store seems crappy. It really seems we need to come up with a better balance of app order fulfillment and in-store patrons.   Or at least have the cashier manage expectations when taking orders.  They really didn't seem to care though.

This place is hit or miss with service. I'm trying to give them grace because I bet it's because of being understaffed or hiring new people. Every time I go there and get a simple order (grande Iced coffee with oat milk, Carmel syrup and the classic syrup) they get it wrong. One time in the drive through the person said "did you say soy?" And I said "no" and then repeated my order. Drove away, and they had put soy in it. Every time I go it's made differently. NEEDLESS TO SAY, just check your order before you drive away and you'll be okay.

This is a Starbucks, so we know what to expect when it comes to coffee and tea drinks and that's a good thing if you like Starbucks. The people are friendly and helpful, almost offering a smile all the time...the seating area is big, clean and bright.... and they have a drive thru for people in a hurry. They also have outside eating area
Very nice and cozy starbucks location. Everyone here is just so nice and friendly. Got my caramel frappacino fix today and now im happy.

On the door it says that you must have shoes and a shirt for service . Unfortunately we had neither . But thanks to the great employee for coming outside and letting us know that we may come inside to order and that people do it all the time . So just Incase you're like us , you can go inside .
We LOVE THIS STARBUCKS!*!  Everyone is friendly and very very fast at concocting delicious drinks! :)  Try the vanilla bean creme ! YUM!

For an airport Starbucks, this place was perfect! The staff was super friendly, service was fast and the coffee tasted great!  There is plenty of seating. They have a full selection of food and drinks! The only bummer was I wanted a Starbucks tall mug with the saint Louis logo and there are zero to be found  .... so if you ever get a restock! Please send me one! - a Saint Louis native

Gentleman at the counter was friendly and correct order taken. Filthy and stinky bathroom. The gal making our drinks forgot the milk. She did add milk after we inquired. My decaf was bitter and not fresh at 5:00 in the evening.

This is possibly the worst managed Starbucks that I have ever visited. Inexplicable and inconsistent pricing.  Good customer service is seriously lacking at this particular Starbucks.  When I brought this to the attention of the store manager, she insisted on blaming everyone else. Yet she was the one that charged me differently, on three separate occasions, for the exact same drink.  Honestly, they really need to find someone to run this Starbucks that knows what they're doing.

I'm not sure if something has happened during the last 6 months since the last review was written, but I really don't understand why this particular Starbucks has such high reviews. The good: it is a block away from my apartment, it is always clean, and there is never a line of more than just a couple people. The bad: the employees who work there get under my skin every single time. The men seem to be fine but the women...It's not uncommon for me to have to stand there at the register and wait for them to finish their conversation before taking my order. The number of mess-ups that happen considering the low volume of people is pretty astounding.
