I kept going there because I figured that sometimes things aren't perfect, people sometimes make mistakes, etc. I'm a pretty understanding person. But after today, I will be walking the two blocks to 13th and Chestnut. There are always a lot more people waiting in line and it is always crowded but the baristas are amazing, they are super friendly, and I get the impression that they actually care. 13th and Chestnut is not always the cleanest (due to the high volume of people) but at this point I would rather put up with a little trash if it means I get excellent customer service.

Service and quality at this Starbucks are extremely uneven and clearly depend on the staff at any given time. Some baristas are very good at making great lattes and frapucinos.  Others do a slap-dash job.  They seem to be saving money by not filling cups in the drive-through line, by the way -- you might want to check before you drive away.

Great service. Always a welcome warming smile from the team, and they really know me now. Just what I want. The atmosphere is cozy (although it gets cold with the door opening and closing with nothing in between). Has the gingerbread latte. Not a fan of it. Smells like an old wood shed, but tastes like a cinnamon dolce latte.

Sure, "Starbuck's is Starbuck's", but it's ALL about the customer service.  One time I ordered a venti iced soy chai tea latte and got an "Are you effing kidding me?" look from the guy behind the counter.  What's wrong with a chai latte...iced...with soy. I don't think I'm being that high maintenance.  I did have to smile to myself, however, when he spilled it just as he was putting the lid on and had to re-make it.  Also, for some reason this location does not make iced coffee in winter.  When I ordered it I was told they don't make it in winter, but she could make it another way for me if I liked, so I said yes - and I don't know what she did, but it was about 100 times better than their regular iced coffee. (OK, so this is a plus, but I still hate the place).  Also, I've witnessed who I assume is the manager berating an employee on the phone for being late once again, right in front of the customers, which I thought was extremely unprofessional.  Overall poor service... it's never speedy and I'm usually worried they are going to get some aspect of the drink wrong.  Saw a guy wait so long for his misto he had to leave before it was ready.  Lame.

As a Starbucks partner myself I expect to be treated exactly how we are trained to treat our customers when I go to a store. This Starbucks has the worst customer service I've seen in any Starbucks.  I went through the drive thru.  My order was rushed, put in incorrectly and misunderstood. When I got to the window the barista didn't have his name tag on and he was uninterested. I was charged for a drink I didn't receive. The shots were dead. The sandwich's egg was still frozen as well. This store should be ashamed of themselves.

Stopped in to Starbucks before we head out to this building for work. No coffee for me today, went with my favorite Green Tea Lemonade for the Win!  When we arrived in the parking lot, apparently we were at the rear entrance, so it's a little confusing set up from the outside. However when you get inside, you witness this amazing setup. Like probably one of the best designed setups that I've been to.  They have this area that looks to have been setup for meetings and getting work done, it's like it's own room. The work benches have electrical outlets and USB ports on them! That is a great setup. My colleagues and I were amazed by this as we hadn't seen any like that one.  It had a great feel to the place, the barista was nice and my beverage is delicious as usual! Man I love Starbucks!

If your into a good cup of coffee then STARBUCKS is the perfect place for that. And a good breakfast sandwich to go with it. Personally I go to 282 Los Altos Park way. The staff is amazing they will literally go out of their way to help or please you. I highly recommend the Chicken Dubbe smoked Bacon!!!

When I'm driving on the 101, this is our stop to make to stretch the legs, use el baño, and get a drink.  It's more about the strategic location than the actual location.  It's usually dirty on the floor, the service is at a minimum, and it is average on speed.  Too bad it's not known for the better! I hope they raise the bar soon.  This does not mean that the employees don't care (who knows? only they do) but it does mean that it is sorely lacking when it comes to other more cheerful and clean Starbucks.

I asked for non-caffeinated drink the barista was kind of snarky and looked over at the other Brista and they shared a small laugh and told me that all the drinks are caffeinated and then when I said that they were able to make me a drink yesterday that was non-caffeinated she said well no because everything is caffeinated and I showed her the photo of my drink on the phone and she said that's dragon fruit and it's caffeinated long story short she didn't want to help me at all she didn't want to take her time she didn't want to do anything for me so I ended up with the stupid strawberry lemonade, thanks. I really really do not like it when people treat me stupid I believe in being super super nice and I like it when my kindness is given back I do not like being made to feel stupid.

This has to be the worst coffee experience I've ever had. The employees are rude, and the coffee is downright disgusting. I have no idea how one can make a coffee so gross. I ordered a simple dark roast. It had a thick sludgy consistency. It was extremely bitter and sour, nothing like coffee. I rarely dump drinks, but this cup tasted so rancid that I had no other choice. I definitely would not recommend this business.

Conveniently located inside the Marriott near the elevator for the parking garage. This Starbucks is like an open counter and you can just sit in the lobby of the hotel to enjoy your drink or food.   I came down around 8:45 am the line for Starbucks was long! There were about 30 people waiting. Once the line died down I was greeted and got a nice strawberry acai drink. So good and the ladies there were so nice.

By far the worst Starbucks location.  I asked if my order was made yet because I've had my drink stolen before from this location and the girl gave me an attitude and said I don't know I'm cleaning... the store was empty I could see if it was busy. Then they took their good ok time making my drink.  I'd give 0 stars if possible

The line here is ridiculous. Drive-thru  is always long and wrapped around the building. The line inside is alright if it wasn't for the mobile orders which seem to take precedent, which is a fault on the company as a whole.
I really like this location.  The staff is nice and helpful.  I have never had a bad experience and it is a typical Starbucks.  It is just a normal experience...
Caetlin was so kind!!! Stopped in before work they were out of some milk substitutes but she was so sweet about It and honestly made my night with her kindness!!!

Starbucks in my home town! The future is now!   Like Yelper Steve P, I too was very skeptical when I saw the Starbucks sign outside of the building that once was a mom and pop pancake house way back in the day. But I was pleasantly surprised by the large, inviting space when I walked through the doors. This would be a great spot to get some work done or have a casual meeting.   I ordered an Americano and asked if they had the egg white breakfast sandwich, since I didn't see it on display. The employee at the register looked through the refrigerator but, unfortunately, they didn't have my go-to. I opted instead for the bacon, gouda and egg sandwich which was tasty if not as light as the egg white option. My Americano was HOT - I couldn't drink it for a good twenty minutes.   Overall, glad to see this new location so close to home. No more driving to Delran for my Starbucks fix!

I expected poor service due to the reviews and it was quite the opposite. Pleasant, efficient, patient staff. I can count on one hand the number of times I've been to Starbucks so I don't know the coffee "lingo." The cashier was really patient with me as I changed my order and helped me order my coffee just the way I like it. I will absolutely return to this location in the future.

As a former Starbucks partner (not this locations, another) I was extremely disappointed. There was a line forming and four baristas ALL MAKING DRINKS?? Seemed unnecessary. Then I saw a male in a gray thermal and maroon beanie come out of the back, start taking orders and making drinks NO HAT NO APRON, I can only hope he washed his hands. The eggnog lattes my boyfriend and I ordered seemed a little off and the pastries we had warmed didn't feel warm at all. I'm a true Starbucks fan and rarely have a bad experience... very sad.

I've visited this location on a few occasions and various times of the day. I've never visited and received prompt service, it's always an extraordinarily long wait for basic items. I visit the other Seminole location off of Bryan Dairy and receive outstanding service. I don't know what it is about this specific location but I hope they improve it soon or many people like meal just visit other Starbucks in the area
Great place. They have very clean bathrooms and trust me I've seen quite a lot of bathrooms in my day!
Love this Starbucks! Always has fun, upbeat music and love meeting clients here. The employees are nice and super friendly!

I had a decent size order from here in the drive thru. However, when I received my chai tea latte...it was missing the "chai tea" part and I received a cup of milk...since then, their business has gone uphill and I do return.

Slowest drive thru ever. Try to park to go inside and it's chaos getting in and out of a parking space due to the drive thru line. Inside service is not any faster. I never found Starbucks coffee or food especially worth the wait.
Thank goodness for Starbucks!!! This one is efficient and the folks were really nice.

This location (22nd and Kolb) has hands down had the best service each and every time! They are consistent with their great team work, superb customer service and awesome quality/speed of the menu items. My Momma raves about this location and the few times I've been here, they have lived up to her praises!

Went in this morning to get my coffee that was almost $10 (which I'm aware Of) handed the cashier a $50 as she handed it back and immediately said "I can't break that". Without even trying to see if a manager could check the office or help in any way. So I leave to try to go to the bank next door but that was closed because it is a holiday. So I go back to Starbucks to attempt to see if someone else can help me and end up getting the manager. Who WAS able to help me. I've worked in hospitality for the last 15 years now and there really seems to be no such thing as customer service anymore. If I'm paying almost $10 for a damn coffee why couldn't you take two extra seconds out of your hourly paid time to help me.

I went to this Starbucks located near the airport with the intent of working on things on my laptop after my dinner plans while waiting for my parents' flight to come in. I had no intention​ of buying anything because I mistakenly thought the new rule was that anyone was allowed to come into any Starbucks without having to buy anything. I would later find out that the rule only applies to use​ the restroom and not to stay there to do work. However, none of the employees said anything to me! In fact, one complimented me on my backpack. I'd go back to this Starbucks location, but I'll definitely remember to buy something first if I'm going to stay there longer than a restroom break.

They open at 5:30 AM. 5:35 AM "Order ahead not available."    So I drive up to the location to order the coffee at the store, less than 2 miles away. THEY ARE CLOSED!!! Yet when I look on the mobile app, it gives the impression they are open.    On a usual daily basis, after 9 AM, order ahead is usually not available.   Of all my travels, this is likely the WORST Starbucks ever.

Haven't tried everything yet....definitely a fan of the cold brews, haven't had all them but my go to is Irish cream cold brew , it's fantastic so is salted caramel cold brew. They both fire me up like an 8 day alarm clock

There are a lot of Starbucks locations around me. I try to avoid this one because every time I have gone, my order isn't up to par. If I am paying $6 for a drink, I expect it to be perfect. I ordered my usual (strawberry lemonade refresher with no berries and 3 scoops of matcha) from this Starbucks yesterday and it was gross. They didn't shake it up so my matcha was just sitting at the bottom and they put berries in at first and i'm guessing pulled them out because there were random little chunks floating in it. Disappointed. Won't be going back to this location which is unfortunate.

We have been here multiple times between 9 and 10 pm when the app says they are open and they are closed or they shut off there lights when we are trying to open their doors or go through their drive through.  Please update your app and the hours on your door so that you close according to your hours.

This is the slowest Starbucks ever!!! Second time trying and orders always slow waiting 5,6,10 minutes in line at the drive thru is absurd!! Don't bother going here just go across the street to the DD

Disclaimer - I ran the Honolulu airport Starbucks overnight shift for a few months several years ago.  Why do they train their employees to swirl the F'ing cold drinks!!?? When they do liquid gets in the rim of the F'ing lid and it will drop in you. Maybe that's why I got so many tips from the flight crews in HNL - I used to point out how clean their beverage lids were when I made them drinks.  My barista today slung my iced latte at me and closed the window. I waited u till she noticed I hadn't left and asked for a napkin to wipe the sloppy cup and she happily gave me (literally) a stack 4 inches thick.   It's the little things that used to make Starbucks a great place - now it's just a McDonalds with a different name...

Half of the time I go they get my drink wrong and I end up getting sick. Today they got my breakfast wrong. They offered to fix it but they ended up taking the wrong food and just sticking it somewhere (which is severely against health code as it went into my hands and now it should be thrown away.) they finally gave my the right order and it was still 1/2 frozen. I was running late to work so I couldn't go back and have them fix it.

If I was to leave a review before today I would have been 5 starts, but today is the day Starbucks lost my business for a very long time or for good. I frequent this Starbucks at least 3-4 times per week. With the virus scare they have shut down the store to drive up only. My medium duty wrecker can not fit in drive through. Today I was refused service bc I was standing in the drive through, which is funny bc last Thursday the staff told me I was always welcomed to walk up to the window. Not very good service to someone who is essential to people and i am not refusing road services to anyone

Worst experience ever the manager James is quite rude! Saying I can't speak to people, me knowing it's a free country continued to do so and he threatened to call the cops! Lol must be new to managing
So nice to see Emily at this location. Very friendly. The staff are all very nice here.   Lots of seating and very clean. A nice change from some of the other locations.

The new "makeover" is HORRIBLE. Cold, dark, ugly,  and clearly they just want to hear people through. They even took away any comfortable seating, not even a single couch! No more local art, not even a community bulletin board.

This place is very slow. I placed an online app order @ 4:08pm and it wasn't ready until 4:41. I had to go to the cashier and she had to make itAnd they need all hands on deck on days like this. The line was to the door and it was at least 15 people waiting.  I will never go there again!

Starbucks is my favorite coffee place and this location is always popping! I usually attempt to mobile order just because the location is crammed into a small shopping plaza and there isn't a whole lot of room for drive thru traffic but it works! When I've mobile ordered it's always been ready quickly, when I go through the drive thru it's usually packed but moves fairly quickly and the employees take multi-tasking to a whole new level! I don't know how Starbucks finds such amazing employees for each location, and I mean that I have literally never went to a Starbucks with bad employees, but they knock it out of the park! My drinks are always made great and if I order good it's always made to perfection! Don't let the traffic scare you off! I promise you won't wait long!

I'm a fan of Starbucks. I am a huge fan of the new feature in the Starbucks app for ordering ahead. You just order, go to the location, and get yours off the counter. Efficient.  Here in #Nashville, a couple of local #Starbucks spots have AWFUL lot designs. Starbucks (West End) and Starbucks (Dominican). West End has a massive and dangerous drive-thru line sitting in the road all morning. Dominican has not nearly enough parking, creating a free-for-all need to invent solutions on the fly. Sometimes, this results in a cranky customer yelling at another, like I was yelled at this morning. Surely causing your customers to bicker was not part of the brand promise, Starcrack. This lot also has a dead-end into the oncoming drive-thru traffic, complicating the discovery of the lack of adequate space even more. (Starbucks Smyrna is also guilty of this one.)  I realize the previous Councilwoman from my area of town is credited with securing the Starbucks (and neighboring Lexus) deal, but clearly the planning was poor. Kinda what you'd expect, given her reputation. I was actually going to take this issue on if I had run for Council, especially the West End part.  So the question is, what are you guys gonna do about it??
Terrible service. As Starbucks is all about connecting with customers, you wouldn't expect a partner to give you a nasty attitude.
With this pandemic I don't understand why the employees were not wearing gloves today! We have been to this location before but today I didn't see anyone wearing gloves

I'm not really a Starbucks fan but sometimes, it works. The employees at this location were all super friendly and engaging. You know when you have a bunch of questions about something and you can tell when the person behind the counter is losing patience? Not this crew. They were happy to answer the surprising array of questions I had about their iced teas.   Speaking of which, I ordered some type of berry concoction that was bright pink and fruity but very, very tart. Walk the extra block to Cafe Ole for their amazing raspberry iced tea if that's what you're jonesin' for (sorry, Starbucks). The only reason I didn't do that was because I had already been there just an hour earlier and didn't want to be that weirdo going back twice in an hour.

Best s'bucks on town!!!  Iced au lait to fight the heat.  Often has free pastries in the afternoon. Great crowd od respectful regulars.  New glasses for in house cold drinks. Me likie!  French press. Komodo Dragon blend is not so great for me...citrusy.  The French press coffee is phenomenal and not expensive!  Lots of parking, fun atmosphere, reasonably quiet. My current favorite s'bucks in Nola!

This Starbucks has most inconsistent coffee preparation. I have ordered Cappuccino and Latte with extra shot  at same time just to test them and I was not surprised when they made both under a minute and they both tasted same and at the same time horrible. This happened at 2 times in 3 days. After that I ordered mango dragon fruit refresher and they ended up giving me a pale yellow colored drink and as soon as I saw it I said it's not what I ordered as the refresher should be pink in color and the barista ended up telling me it's the same drink minus the mango dragon fruit as they are over with it. I ened up telling them I am not paying for the drink which is not having the main ingredients in It. So be mindful when you go here as you might not get what you want.

Conveniently located if you're walking around Haddonfield (if you're driving, good luck finding a spot). Staff is friendly and the store is clean and contemporary, sitting room with fireplace and stools by the windows to people watch. It seems like the school kids are always here at 3, but otherwise the line is short.

Closed at least 30 minutes before their scheduled time (10pm) with no note on the door or drive thru. People inside were just cleaning and taking things to a mobile storage unit in the back, giving each other "rides" on the dollies. Would have been nice to know they were closing early and have staff act more professional. I had a long drive I've ahead of me and needed a pick-me-up for the road...

This Starbucks- inside Frys- is the best Starbucks on the planet! The ladies are beyond awesome! Even when dealing with the jerks they maintain their professional demeanor and do their job! Jada and Bre are the best!
I'm a regular Starbucks drinker but this location disappointed. I don't know if they were having an off morning but the coffee tasted like nothing.
This place literally messes up my drink every time! I'm surprised the customer service here isn't better considering the upscale customers coming in there from Montecito.

Just your average run of the mill Starbucks. There's only a few nice comfy chairs with the majority of seating located outside. It's tucked away in Clearwater mall and never gets terribly busy. There's limited parking right outside the store but you shouldn't have any trouble parking somewhere in the plaza. So far all the baristas here have been very genuinely nice which I appreciate. More of a Starbucks you'd stop in for something quick than a place you'd spend several hours at.
Its a regular Starbucks, Nothin fancy. Right inside the casino next to the slot machines. Was never a fan of starbucks. I was here just because I couldn't find a decent coffee shop around.

It can be a hit or miss depending on the baristas. I always get my alternative plant based milk steamed because it makes it creamy and reminds me of regular milk. Some just feel like it's extra and won't do it at all. Never seen lazy fake nice service at any other Starbucks I've been to.  One time I feel like I was given regular milk because after drinking my stomach was upset. It is just convenient won't be going there anymore since I graduate in May!

You would think that the baristas would be a little less loud and obnoxious when the majority of customers are trying to work. They were all young men so boisterous that I could not concentrate and had to leave. The Christmas music playing during the first week of November didn't help either. Sorry, but this was a very annoying experience.

No line in drive through and super sweet employees.  After my son's 2 1/2 year old wellness check, I told my friend we'd stop at Starbucks on our way to see her. This was on our way home, so it was a quick stop. Usually I go to the Starbucks on Racetrack, because it is a tad bit closer, but I'm starting to question my decision. That one always seems to have a line of cars (but it does move super quickly). At 10:40 a.m. on a Thursday, there were no cars at the drive through! The entire time I was there I didn't see another car. That's bananas to me. I'll have to come back and see if this holds true.   I was thrown for a loop when I tried to order a pumpkin cream cold brew and a chocolate chip cookie. They didn't have either. The pumpkin cream cold brew is seasonal, which makes me sad. The employee was seriously one of the most helpful people I've ever met. She kept asking me about my taste preferences and offering suggestions. With other drive throughs, the employee probably would have just given me time to look over the menu and make another decision, but she seemed invested in helping me choose something I would like.  I ended up getting chocolate covered madeleines (for my 2 1/2 year old) and an Irish cream cold brew for myself. Both were huge hits. The Irish cream cold brew was the perfect amount of sweetness for me while still packing a caffeine punch and my son ate all the chocolate off the madeleine (which is what I expected).   Since this Starbucks is so close to Costco and ALDI, I expect that normally it is more packed, but I'll do some field research and come back a couple more times. For Yelp, of course.

Probably still the worst Starbucks I've been too. They make you wait a 20min long line just to be told that they're out of what you want to get regardless of what time of day. Manager is rude and no help either.

I'm giving this Starbucks one star because of the ridiculous convoluted drive-thru. What a joke. If more than five cars are in the Drive-Thru, nobody can enter or exit the entire parking lot. I know, I know, first world problems. #nopumpkinspicelatteforme

2019 update: Baristas are still sticking their fingers in cups as they move them down the line. Does Starbucks care at all about sanitary handling of drinks!! This is the newly opened SB in Oro Valley. It was packed. One of the baristas, I think named Kaitlan, kept sticking her fingers in the glasses as she moved them around over and over again. That's disgusting. Then I got a refill of my unsweetened green ice tea and it has this gross sweet taste. I was watching them. They were barely washing out the mixing cups with those spray wash station. I think they need to tune up the training.

Dropped a couple stars because I've been waiting for 20-25 min. In line to pickup my Starbs and they should've posted by the Drive Through window that they are not allowing customers to pickup the order inside because I put my mobile order in, shopped at Aldi and tried to hop in to grab my drink but no luck - they're not allowing people inside for picking up their orders until May 23 FYI! I hopped back in line and it's awful - still waiting for a good while...I have to remind myself to never come here on the weekend!

I don't drink coffee but occasionally visit Starbucks for other items.  I paid more than six bucks for a Strawberry Acai Lemonade Refresher at this location and after a few sips through the straw, I started getting air.  It turned out that the glass was about 80% ice (see photo).  What a rip-off!  I had a better experience when I ordered hot chocolate.

I went through the drive through this morning and girl who took my order and who was at the window, was extremely nice! It was 730am and she was so bubbly(idk how she does it) but it made my morning. Also a plus my coffee turned out great! Always have great experiences at this location!

This is without a doubt the slowest, most unorganized, and inaccurate Starbucks in St Louis. No line in the store, and a short drive thru line...took them 15 minutes to get my order ready. Horrible service

I really wish they would tweak a few things at this location. They're always under staffed which results in a really long wait for a horribly made drink. I keep going here because it's right across the street from where I live but it's so upsetting because they never get any of my drinks right. The staff always seems overwhelmed and just confused. I really hope management makes some changes and properly trains the staff before putting them behind the bar.

Brand new staff for a brand new store - I was extremely disappointed with the service.  It took about 5 minutes to reload my Gold Card and buy two drinks...then another few to make them.  There went my 10 minute afternoon break....their business would be better served with knowledgeable baristas and shift managers.

I really hate this place. I really do. If you just want an ice coffee you must wait inline for everyone getting a fancy warm milkyatto.  They never have the Trenti lids in stock. Took me 20 mins to get a simple xtra large ice coffee. I think plain ice coffees should not have to wait with the fancy milk people.

Best service I ever had!!! Manager on duty was more than helpful and she was on point!!! I just think that they get such bad reviews is because people are so miserable that they need to be rude and non understanding toward the fact that a lot goes into keep things clean 24/7 not to mention that they are always so busy which to me means that there are way more satisfied customers than the rude miserable people that post bad reviews!!! Keep up the great work!!!

The new location that they moved to across the street is sooooo dark. No windows. I love sitting next to Windows when enjoying coffee. It is also very loud (concrete floors and high ceilings). The only thing it is good for is that has a driveway. Otherwise not a fan.
Absolutely my favorite coffee shop in the area. Amazing staff, great coffee and plenty of seating.

Quite possibly the worst Starbucks I have ever been to. Not only is this location very dirty, but they also are not helpful or friendly. I ordered through their app and went in to pick-up my drink. I had to wait for at least 20 minutes for them to tell me they were out of Pink Drink ingredients. Do NOT come to this location. You might as well just go to Dunkin' Donuts. They will do a better job.
Best service ever from a Starbucks.  Super friendly service and extremely clean space. I will definitely be coming to this location from now on.
My favorite Starbucks in town. Valentina and Cornelius are so welcoming and always smiles. I only go to this one over others.

Ordered a strawberry funnel cake Frappuccino. The barista wiped some errant whipped cream off the bubble top with a dirty rag and handed me the drink. She then proceeded to wipe the counter in front of me with the same dirty, wet rag.  Gross.  Emailed Starbucks and was credited $10. Greeeeat.  There's a Starbucks a couple of block down,  across the street that doesn't contaminate your drinks and let me use points.

It's Starbucks so by now unless you live under a rock you should know what you're going to get. So with that said, the x factor is the employees, the restaurant space itself and the cleanliness of the restaurant.   The employees at this location have always been kind in my multiple visits here. Today I received a coffee and the barista asked if I could wait for her to pour a new batch because the coffee I received was on the stale side. That goes a long way because just the other week down the street at another location, I did receive a stale Starbucks coffee and it's pretty disappointing. That's your pick me up at work that you've been craving for the last two hours and then you finally get it and realize you should have just had the regular stuff in your work's cafeteria. So I'm appreciative that this staff goes the extra mile and acknowledges your presence when you enter.   As far as the space goes, it's not a large space but this location is never crazy busy so I've never seen a seating issue here so it appears to be just right. And cleanliness has also never been an issue here, there's never packets of sugar thrown around the condiments counter or trashed tables.  Overall, it's a Starbucks location that has good employees, always seems to have seats available and is clean. Definitely recommend if you like Starbucks.

Too slow! I've been coming here for a few months now. This is my neighborhood Starbucks. From the long lines daily at the drive thru, to the full parking lots out front. Inside a sea of people. While fully staffed. I'll still manage to stay in the store for almost 15mins. Insane. Not to mention the high prices and mistakes on drink orders.

I am not a fan of this Starbucks. My friend and I go here almost daily and it's always a gamble. Some days the drinks come out perfect, other days they're bitter. But what can you really do when you're addicted? Also the employees have RBF about 90% of the time. Not friendly at all. Going to this Starbucks is like a bad relationship. You hate them but need them.

This a very busy starbucks, so if you want a quick cup of coffee, be prepared to be there early because the line is quite long, with all the conventions and events hosted by the casino, I do like that I could use my Starbucks giftcard, it was clean and organized,  and they were able to cater to the busy crowd,  and didn't mess up the orders.

Pretty crazy that we are in a time sheets it's exciting to go out just to go through a Starbucks drive thru but that's reality at the moment.  I was very impressed that they handled the rush very well and were prompt and efficient.  We were able to put our card into the chip reader and did not have to touch any buttons at all or sign anything .  Very cool.

Honestly every employee should be like Connie. She is the sweetest, friendliest, overall greatest employee I've ever encountered at a Starbucks. Her service is unmatched and the store overall is awesome!!

To say this is a Starbucks is giving Starbucks a bad name! I frequent Starbucks an average of 4 days a week. I ordered an iced almond milk latte and it tasted like burnt wax! I haven't ever had coffee taste like this. Five dollars for something this unpalatable is sad.

Typical Starbucks coffees.   I only write to request that someone feng shui this place. The flow is so strange in this Starbucks that I actually made a metal note to say something about it later. When you walk in the side door you walk smack into a huge display that completely hides the register. Then you're stuck going around this gargantuan display, but there's no clear path so you have lines going in both directions around the display and no one knows who's first. Then when you're waiting to pick up your drink, you're standing directly in the way of the other main door. And to top it off, the cream/sugar station is right in front of the front door, so you're getting bumped while you're trying to stir and the whole experience is just awkward from start to finish.
What's not to like about Starbucks!!! Yummy!! The only thing I can think is that they are a bit expensive. Staff is usually friendly and it's clean.

I used to work right on 4th & Market and parked around this Starbucks, so obviously on my walk to work, I would randomly stop in for a morning latte. I LOVE this staff. I have been to many-a-Starbucks and they are top notch and my absolute favorite. They were always so friendly and though I'd only stop in once a week, they remembered me and always had a warm welcome. I seriously miss this group and every time I am in Old City, I make sure I stop in because I love dealing with them. Just thought I would let Yelpers know how wonderful they are!  P.S. - Like another guy wrote, be VERY careful with parking. The PPA will nab your car within 3 seconds. Also, peak hours, it can be a little tight in there.

I asked for my white chocolate mocha at 130 degrees. It is boiling. My friend, whose name is Sue, was asked how to spell her name. "Ts...    they spelled it "Zue" . Really. They also did not leave her room for cream and sugar.

Probably my favourite location in St. Albert... which sucks for me, because it's the furthest away from me hahaha.. just my luck.  The staff are friendly and they never seem to be out of stuff.  Well, at least not the things that I want.  Service is fast and efficient and orders are always correct.  What else can you want?

I am not really a Starbucks fan due to the prices of coffee. But I have to stay awake when I am at the hospital waiting for my father. My sister never goes to Starbucks and doesn't do sugar. The girl up front was not helpful and very short. The girl making the coffee was way friendly and made up for the rude girl.

It is 3.9.17 I have visited this place a few times since my last review a month or so ago. I will bot be back. There are too many other SB locations worthy of my business. I have read the other recent reviews. They are spot on. The customer service here is completely lacking. The employees are slow (it was NOT busy when I went), unmotivated and unfriendly.
This Starbucks is great. Fast and very friendly service every time I have been.  Love it.  The employees really go the extra mile here. Thanks

Decided to go through the drive thru and get breakfast on my way to work, took a bite of my ham and egg sandwich and the egg/cheese were frozen while everything else was warm. :/ Ended up going to work hungry. Human Bean is 1000x better.

Stopped in here during a conference we had in Nashville. We were staying at the Omni so this location is on the opposite side of the entrance from the hotel. It was a quick walk and our service was friendly and fast. You cannot use Starbucks rewards here though. Nice little seating areas too.

I'm not the type to write a bad review. Much less about Starbucks, I mean how hard can you screw up something so standard nowadays. Truthfully the food and drink that I ordered weren't bad. Iced grande mocha and an egg & cheese (oh wait! That's not what I wanted, they were all out of the ham and swiss. Ok, that's fine, I came in late, shortly after noon, but come on at least know that your out of stock for the day). Now my major issue, I had to wait 15 minutes in line behind wait for it...TWO people. The employees work with no sense of urgency whatsoever. They joke around and have side conversations while I'm mid order!  What the hell! I understand team dynamic should be good, but finish taking my order before starting to talk about your children. Listen this is Starbucks. I want my order in 5 minutes, especially when the store is empty. The service is unacceptable, yea the employees were nice, but you have to do your damn job in addition to being sweet. It might be cause it's a new store, but these folks need to step their game up.

Came to Starbucks to grab a quick drink but no one seemed to be manning the drive through. I was throughly disappointed and did not get coffee. Then unfortunately, I got so tired from lack of coffee that I crashed my car. Thanks Starbucks.
They were busy as can be and completely killed it with customer service and quality!  Great job Big Bend crew!

It's too hit or miss. I'm not a big coffee person, so I only ever get the caramel macchiato when I need a pick me up. Sometimes it's good, sometimes it's nasty. It almost tastes burnt the last few times I got it. Recently, I brought it back because it was nasty and burnt and I heard the lady say under her breath "she gone drink half the cup then bring it back." I had only taken like 3 sips! And even if I would have drank more of it so what? It's nasty and I want it remade. But I still come here every so often I just hate that I have to have my fingers crossed like I hope it will be good this time.

If you're in University City and you want to go to Starbucks, this is the one to go to. The baristas work so hard to keep that long line moving along quickly and smoothly. And they are so cheerful while they are at it. It CAN be hard to find a seat, sometimes, because the place is small and it gets really crowded. But that makes it lively. Drinks are made right. Cream and sugar table is always well supplied. Despite the crowd, they manage to keep the place clean.

Clean, straight forward Starbucks. For some reason the green tea does not taste like the ones I get in CA. I know, I know this is blasphemy for a corporate chain ....... I'm so sorry. I guess the problem is me, I am monotone - I just get one drink over and over.

This Starbucks is attached to the Marriott across he street from Reading Terminal. I've been to this location many times for drinks on the go. The staff is usually very kind and pleasant. Due to the convenience in location, it is relatively busy and doesn't leave much room to seat groups. Not to mention this store is pretty small. It's not really too clean either but usually okay enough. Overall, I'll probably be coming back for their drinks!

Took the grandkids here the other day.  We've been to many Starbucks - G-d know there are enough of them - throughout the country.  This was is basically on par with others.  You can't always find a table, since this place is fairly small - this can be a pain. Otherwise, this place is pretty good, but nothing great.  Does the trick though. Plenty of parking for cars but not enough seating for humans.  Has outdoor seating, which is a nice extra - only when the weather is good, of course.  The grandkids really like coming here.

This Starbucks is a hit or miss with staff. Some can be super rude and don't listen. For example, just left and asked for light ice.. pretty sure they put extra. But sometimes when you get the right barista, they make it perfect. Joshua, I think is his name, is pretty awesome and one other younger guy, but I can't remember his name. :/
Pretty good location, it was a busy day and they got backed up s little, but handled it well. Coffee and good was its usual goodness.

This a very friendly and clean Starbucks.  My favorite one close to me and have always had great service and always greeted every morning by name.  Parking is difficult in the small lot right in front of the entrance, but I park in the main parking lot on the side and have had no problems.   No drive through available otherwise one of the better Starbucks in town.

Not only is the parking lot horrible- but the service hers is horrible! It takes 10 minutes or more for the drive through! And most of the time they get the order wrong! I ask for whole milk and 9 out of 10 times they get it wrong! So disappointing ! And I pay 5$ or more for a cup ! So disappointing

Last Monday I was having a crazy, rough day.. my phone was picked up by someone by accident and my whole life went blank.. You know how it is when you're cut off from the world, and you know no one's phone numbers by heart, LOL! But Brandyn, an exceptional human being at Starbucks, took my order and said, "It's on us."  I was so taken aback by this... it immediately calmed me and allowed me to sit down and get centered. Then another employee came over with a small brown bag with "I hope your day gets better" written on the outside, and a pink cake pop on the inside.  What an incredible personal touch! These guys ROCK!,  and they definitely made my day so much better.  Great job Starbucks.

This is such a reliable starbucks location. Tonight Byron was working at the starbucks window and he made our night! My sister and I were taking a break from caring for our Mom, and finally got to run out for some coffee. Byron was so kind and friendly that we were able to laugh and have a nice convo and just take break from the chaos!  We hope Byron gets a raise he's amazing! And the coffees were perfect yay!!

"Vergina? Vergina your coffee's ready! VERGINA!" -- Is what I WISH the barista had said. Instead she looked at the sad-ass attempt at my name on the cup and said "Uh... the iced coffee is up?".   If it were me? I'd've yelled it. I'd've yelled it out with a pointed tone of "you've gotta be kidding me" while staring judging-ly at my dingus coworker who can't spell the name of a state (plus about 16253516 street signs in Reno + a city + a lake) making her cringe.   As a former barista, I get that after a long morning full of tripleshot-halfcaff-twopumpsvanilla-extrahot (readmymindandgetitright bull hockey that turns what should be a 30sec cup of coffee into a goddamn act of Congress) ....you're a little exhausted. I also GET that you probably didn't have to take a spelling test to get your job. I also get that there are some names out there that are impossible to spell correctly phonetically. Like Lynnea, or Mirahndah, or Westynne.   But there is just no letting this one go because the cheat sheet is practically right out the window. I mean, there are more "Virginia" signs in this town than in friggin' Richmond.   But that's okay because you know what? I do like your coffee. And when I'm in a hurry and can't make it to one of my favorite local spots (because Bibo and Hub are my first two loves) I'm fine to swing on in. This location is convenient and the Baristas are very sweet.   I just read that Starbucks might soon be contributing to its employee's college tuitions? I'd say.... that's a good call, Starbucks.   Good call.

Absolutely the worst coffee/cappuccino I've ever had.  Should have taken heed when didn't see cappuccino on the menu. I didn't want the amount of milk of a latte and not a fan of the oily burnt taste of  their regular coffee, so I asked for a cappuccino w/almond milk; (forgot they use sweetend almond milk).  After removing 3"of the sugared flavoured almond milk froth, there remained 1/8 cup of luke warm very sweet, over milked, very weak espresso coffee (if they even used espresso).   Definitely not worth the $4.  Never a fan of Starbucks, but was meeting someone who suggested it.  I'll pay good money for a small roasters specialty coffee, or pay alot less when settling for not so good coffee at WaWa or Duncan Doughnuts.
Went in to get a cup of brewed coffee. Berista said, "it is 45 minutes old and did I still want a cup?" NO! Is this Starbucks? Won't ever return to that store. So disappointing!

This Starbucks is easy to get to for me on the way to work. The parking lot is a little tight tho. I order in the morning through my app and pick it up on the way to work. It's so convenient!   Certain times of day they are a little busier and what should be a quick in and out for my mobile pick up takes a little longer. Sometimes people who ordered in store are all standing around the end of the counter and that's the same place you pick up mobile orders so it's hard to tell who is mobile and who was in store
Best Starbucks crew in the area. Always friendly with a smile even when busy. Too bad they are temporarily closed for a few days. Go to the rt. 70 Barclay Farm SBs. There are nice there too.

Its not that hard to get the simple orders in right. I didnt not ask for special order, just what they got in the menu. . Ugh I had to explain the drink for the person.  Also, cant they find morning people to work in the morning who can be happy to work in the morning. ?! This was not my first time of experience grumpy crew in the morning.  It is the worst thing to face the grumpy people in the morning as I walk in to grab a simple drink of latte..   And yes, they are ALWAYS OUT OF SOMETHING !!! Not impressed.
There are many Starbucks choices in he area, but what makes the experience worth it is the staff. They treat you like family.
Super fast and friendly. Come here all the time and the line moves fast and serviced is exceptional.

This place has horrible customer service. I'm always in line about 30 minutes to even an hour every time I order. I went through today, waited 30 minutes for my order just for them to not accept the $100 I had. I asked if her manager could get change from the safe because it was all I had and I had already waited in line for 30 minutes. (every single store has a safe for this reason when they need change or to make a deposit) and the woman working the drive thru says  mockingly "No. I don't know what to tell you there's a bank right over there." I was mortified and drove off. I will never be going there again.

I have had three bad experiences in less than a week with this store. The first wasn't really the workers fault, I got there right at opening and my preordered drink wasn't ready because as I was told their espresso machines were still warming up and not ready for use. So maybe corporate should look into that. The second was again early in the morning. My preordered drink was sitting right there. I pre order because I have to be at work very early most days and at every other Starbucks it's never a problem. So there is one barista talking to a lady about her drink that he's already making her. And instead of multi tasking like any good service industry worker should be able to do he makes me stand there and wait until he is done talking before he can bother to simply hand me my drink that is ready and waiting for me. The. The third was when I walked in today, again to pick up my pre ordered drink. The baristas were standing there talking to some girl , and I wasn't in a hurry so I stood at the end of the counter and waited quietly. The baristas never greeted me or said hi to me or anything. So I stood there and waited patiently while they socialize. Then another girl walks in behind me and the barista immediately looks over at her and tries to take care of her and give her the drink she preordered. I finally said excuse me but can I get some help to because I was here first and I stood here and waited patiently. I also forgot to mention the time I preordered from this store and got here and they weren't open like the app said they were supposed to be. So I had to haul ass all the way down to chesterfield by my work just to get my order.

I drink Starbucks daily! And Starbucks is a disgraceful company! You are not a franchise! Your privately owned! so you are responsible for hiring managers and rude staff! That cannot and will not understand that supporting you local community is a proper way of conducting your self! This locations is lower than scum! by not allowing a police officer to NOT use the restroom being denied! You need to retrain your staff and have some protocols standards and procedures for when the police officers needs to use a restroom!

I am trying to give  a zero stars to this location. Very very disappointed. We ordered a double espresso, a Teavana, a chocolate brownie and a Turkey basil pesto...almost not Turkey at all inside the sandwich and the Total was about $ 18.00. Beside that the table was very dirty. Not sure what's going on with this location but it definitely needs to be managed in a proper way.
Employees turn people away before posted closing time. As a former Starbucks partner, this goes against company values.

This Starbucks is a gem. I've always had very good service overall and appreciate the friendliness. I especially enjoy the desire to remember what my name is and what my order is. I'm generally here for meetings and coffee with friends and would say 9/10 I get great service and my expected order.   And that's what I expect out of Starbucks - consistency and speedy service. I give it an extra star for the overall staff friendliness.  Additional bonus - they have a small assortment of board games

Cashier with the dark pink and blonde hair has NEVER been friendly. As the first person you interact with in the morning....this should be unacceptable coming from someone working at Starbucks. Disappointed.

Starbucks is Starbucks but I have now had 3 bad experiences at this location.  1. Nonfat milk in my latte was stale. Walked in (no way to get back in drive through/ explained there was really something off and after one more attempt all agreed that the milk needed to be tossed. Received a $4 gift card. 2. Drove up at 9:54 and heard recorded announcement that store is closed. Parked and walked in. Store was open and they knew nothing about the recorded announcement. This was especially annoying as I have a broken wrist and it was hard to handle the 2 drinks I ordered. 3. Ordered 2 drinks this evening. After several repetitions of my 3 drink order. I pulled out. Took a sip, and there was no Splenda in my latte. Drive through is a great convenience but this location needs to improve customer service!!!
This Starbucks usually has a long line but every time I'm here they do a good job of managing drink orders and getting everyone moved in and out quickly. They even got my drink perfect!
New Starbucks on the block at 39th and Walnut! Always packed with Penn students and their macbooks. Has a plenty of food options. Great service

Everything from my previous review holds true (copy below), but the service here really is stellar so I had to bump them up another star. The issues I have with the location (small venue, horrible parking design, exorbitant profit margins built into upcharges) are all larger company issues...but on the small side, the staff here really does a fantastic job.   I've been in this location enough to see what employees have to deal with. Yes, customer turnover is high, but think about the irritability level of some of those caffeine-addicted customers having to pause in their flurry to get their fix: they want their specific order EXACTLY the way they imagined. Perfectly. Instantly. Even if they didn't say the order correctly (because baristas should also be psychic, too).  I've posted up here with my laptop on occasion. I've never seen a barista lose their cool; they're unflappable. They're deft and efficient speeding orders through both the drive-thru and the counter. They know the menu inside & out (and I'm definitely one of those people who asks a bunch of questions so I can order exactly what I want). And they're smiling, calm, and share that soothing vocal timbre reminiscent of a shrink talking their client off a ledge...probably the best quality for a barista working the a.m. hours. I even trusted my last barista, Josiah, to "surprise me" and have me try something new - if you know me in person, you know I like to do exhaustive research and then make a educated, conclusive decision; unless you're one of my inner circle, surprising me is often a recipe for disaster - but Josiah got it right and I have a new favored drink!  I didn't expect to write this much about a simple chain coffee shop, but I really do think the staff here is undervalued. The service alone would rate this a 5-star location for detail, consistency, and speed. Unfortunately, the other problems at this location are such an issue that I can still only rate it as 3 stars.   Since I referenced it and in case it's no longer available after I post my new review:  MY PAST/PREVIOUS REVIEW I like that the baristas know their coffee culture and can answer any questions.  Whoever set up this drive-thru, however, is an idiot.  If you come from the cross street rather than entering off Brentwood, prepare for some tight finagling (and the blaring horns of irritated, coffee-deprived customers) to get to the lane. And you're going to sneak in a sixty cent surcharge for adding soy milk (no upcharge for dairy) to my coffee?  St. Louis Bread Company is a block away with better coffee & no extra fees for those of us avoiding lactose.  I won't go out of my way for this one.

Horrible service. Walked in as the only customer with one order ahead of me. Stood there for 15 minutes while others got served first. Interaction from employees was less than friendly or understanding. Walked out with one drink instead of try two I ordered. At least I asked for a refund. Will NOT be back to this location.

I've never had this happen before but today I was not happy with my starbucks. I asked for a hot latte and received an iced latte, I bought a chicken wrap box......it was terrible!!! The wrap had barely any chicken it!! While the flavor was good, I had to eat a plain wrap with nothing in it BECAUSE I WAS STARVING and already back at my job. NOT HAPPY!!!! There has also been times when I have ordered my latte and got back to my job (which is in the same parking lot as my Starbucks!!) and when I sipped by drink I could feel how light it was, opened the lid and the cup was just over half full. Seriously!!!!!! So now I ask for light whip cream so they don't fill half of the cup with whip cream. Last time I checked....whipped cream was just a topping.

The coffee is fine, but the early morning staff is horrible. The early morning barista is condescending, rude and just overall nasty. She doesn't make time for the line of customers, but does make time to chat with her friends and talk down to you when you order. Overall, good place to get coffee with an attitude.

The last several times I have visited this location, the staff is very different. This used to be one of my favorite locations, but not so much anymore. One of the baristas made my latte as a macchiato, then mis-made it again as a macchiato, and the third time got it right. No problem, but not one "sorry" or even a "your welcome" when I said thanks. Last weekend, she made a SF cinnamon dolce latte instead of a SF caramel latte...again no apology when I noted it wasn't the correct flavor.   The staff used to be very friendly and on spot. Not so much now.

I love that this location is so close to me.  I recently started delivering for Uber Eats as a side job.  This is one of my favorite spots to pick up deliveries from. They almost always have the order ready and if it's not ready, I never have to wait more than a few minutes. They package the items to perfection and write the customer's name on the bags, which helps me out if I'm picking up multiple orders.  The employees who work here are fabulous and always very welcoming.   I always look forward to stopping in!!!!

I wish they had the breakfast sandwiches since the airport selection is so limited.  Otherwise service is speedy and the coffee is your typical Starbucks...  Kinda bitter and super strong, but sometimes that's just what a girl needs for a am flight

I go to many different Starbucks throughout town to do music promotion. Each time, I order something different. My favorite thing, is the Egg & Cheese protein box and a Mango Dragonfruit drink. But I will change it up often. I won't review the drinks or food unless I feel I need too, because they usually are the same each location. I want to focus on the atmosphere, in these reviews. This location is small, but good to work in when not many are there. Its quiet and the staff is friendly. Seemed clean.
My coffee was delicious, but unless you have A LOT of time on your hands, you might want to go to the one down the street.

This was a terrible experience. First we ordered our drinks and sat down. we saw them spill a drink on somebody else and all over the counter. they messed up 2/3 of our drinks and when i walked back in to ask if they could remake it, they said i would have to wait a "long time". we got our drinks finally and my daughters didn't even have flavor. the last two times we have come to this Starbucks they have messed up. i do not recommend.

Give yourself extra time for your order.   Right across the street from the convention center and a decent option for shorter lines for coffee than at the Starbucks in the center. The staff is super friendly. Like ... I fell like I want to be friends with them friendly. That makes up for the slow service. I chalk it up to the southern style of not being in a hurry. Granted all of us at a conference want our coffee now! Probably not a bad thing to make us slow down for a minute.
This is not a Starbucks they told me they said it's owned by the Hilton and my Starbucks card will not work there this place sucks don't go there they are rude and customer service is poor.

Super rude. Got a iced green tea, Saw what looked like tea from a busted teabag and white stuff at the bottom of my tea and asked them to make me a new one and they initially refused and said it was just the sweeten low that was in there. Then I went up there and showed the lady the black stuff and what looked like white food crumbs in my tea, and she then proceeded to snatch the drink from my hand and started to say under her breath "the black stuff is just tea." And then proceeded to be rude the rest of the time and when I went back for the remade tea, she was also rude about that. Not to mention the fact of how long they took!  Got in there 2:15ish didn't get my drink and leave till 2:40ish
I was very disappointed today. I went through the drive thru and had to wait forever~! They gave the car in front of me the wrong order not once but twice!!! I have never had to wait so long!

i have found that this particular starbucks location is one of the better ones compared to the other locations in santa barbara. david and the rest of the weekday morning crew are the best - awesome customer  service and mad skillz in making a perfect drink every time.  iced tall soy 4-pump toffee nut latte, please!

Retrain. Including manager!! Simple birthday redeem can't even process! And tried to blame me for it when I mentioned many times before I even ordered!! Didn't even bother being nice and didn't even get the correct beverage!!

Messed up two drinks out of a two drink order. Brought drinks back to be remade, handed them over and told them what was wrong. They said they would remake them. 10 minutes later I come back to the window to see the same drinks sitting there not remade. Very disappointing service.

The service is slow.  The music is too loud, to the point where you cannot concentrate on studying or working. Junkies come in there and hang out.  Customers are yelling.  It's more like the atmosphere in a dive bar, only coffee is served instead of beer.   The one that closed on Main Street was so much better.
My lattes always taste perfectly delicious here. I've been visiting this Starbucks since 2010. The girls are nice the drinks are always prepared just right.
This is a great little Starbucks that I enjoy coming to before or after work. It can get very very busy though, especially on a Saturday.

I came in here for a little post exercise treat. This store is a little smaller than other Starbucks, but I thought that made it feel cozier. There's a long snack bar counter facing the parking lot, and the traditional comfy area with tables. The employees were really friendly and everything was made fast and correctly. Even my boyfriend's blonde roast that had to be done specially had hardly any wait time.  This starbucks is definitely one of the better ones I've been to, so stop in if you're in the mood for some yummy caffeine.

This Starbucks didn't cater to my needs, so needless to say, I won't be back:    1.) I needed to put in three different BOGO drinks and they wouldn't allow me to do that. They said the offer is only one per customer, but the Westshore/Cypress location has always allowed me to do multiple orders in one transaction in comparison. I told the cashier I needed to ring up 3 transactions, but she must've not been listening...whatever - chalk it up to a misunderstanding & incompetence. As a result, I didn't order my Carmel Macchiato or get a BOGO Iced Black Tea.   2.) I waited 10 min. for my friend's Ham & Cheese Croissant...they forgot and I even got there early before the 2pm BOGO rush. Whatever - this location feels scatter brained to me.  I appreciated that they filled the Venti Passion Tango Lemonade (no ice) to the brim (I paid my coworker to have her second tea as a result)!  Parking isn't too bad - it's shared between Panera, Petsmart & other businesses.

When I first started going to Starbucks, I would never come to this one. The drinks always just tasted funny. This sucked because this is the closest one to my house, and the next closest one was at least two miles away and I walk everywhere. But as of late I have started coming to this location again and it's been amazing. There's been a few hiccups where my drinks would be bitter, but I would talk to the barista about it and they would fix it without complaints.   The barista's at this location are amazing. They are always in good moods and there's one in particular where every time I walk in, she always seems to be so excited to see everyone. And I actually gossip with her about barista's at other Starbucks locations and she always tells me to call them out for being lazy. She's the main reason I love going to this location at night.

Not friendly at all! Didn't ask the normal things you ask when you are in service like, " can I help you"... Just looks at you until you figure out she is ready for you. I would have at least appreciated a thank you for spending over $10 for two coffees. This used to be a good place to come, now just a bunch of unhappy, entitled people that are obviously unhappy with life.

I honestly don't know why there are only bad reviews for this Starbucks when I've had nothing but positive experiences at this location. I mostly order my drinks through the app and it's always been ready by the time I get there and if I made a mistake ordering, they quickly fix my drink without hesitation. The customer service at this location is on point. I always make an effort to come to this location even if it's a tad bit further because the customer service is so awesome. Hopefully everyone else who had bad experiences will have better ones in the future.

Your experience here might be different from mine. But here's what happened to me. Ordered a regular old pike place drip coffee though the app and the workers here never called my name. Stood there for 20 minutes while others got theirs. Workers never thought to ask me what o needed. My coffee had been sitting at the pickup counter the whole time I was there. Are the workers blind to that? The coffee was cold by the time I got it. I had to stand in line to get to the register to ask for my coffee. Then there was no cream a sweetener. The workers didn't ask if I wanted some. I would avoid this Starbucks if I was you, or be ready to make yourself noticed in here because none of the workers here apparently are trained to notice a drink just sitting there and a customer standing there. Seriously.

I just went through the drive-thru and it took forever.. No one inside either ordering I don't know why it took so long today and then she tried to tell me a mango dragon fruit was a creamy drink and she wanted to sit there and argue with me... To the little teenage whatever chick that was so rude and had an attitude... know your drinks honey because I sure do.
Good service, speedy and always busy! The product speaks for itself but this location has quickly become my favorite in SB.

Consistently a 20-30 minute wait for a mobile order AND the landscaping is unkept and outside dirty. Way to show pride in the neighborhood. I try to go to the Vets location even though it is far less convenient. They really need to get it together.
The most hostile, rude Starbucks experience I've ever had.  These people don't like their jobs and it shows.

This location is generally quite busy since there isn't really another Starbucks nearby that's easy to get to (there is one in the shops at the metropolis at Barnes and noble). For that reason, I usually go inside and order or do the mobile pickup-I like to think I wait less than in the drive thru but I'm not sure it's actually true. Staff is always very friendly. I wish they were able to be faster but I think it's just a volume issue as they always seem adequately staffed.
Friendly team. Quick service. Music is loud and a bit wild. Loud dubstep. Team seems pleasant behind the counter. Smiles all around. Great job.

Starbucks. The usual sort. A seriously crappy parking lot kinda sucks the awesome out of the joint, and I'm not a fan of the velvet chairs (the leather on S Howard is superior)  And the phalanx of loser smokers out front kinda ruins the entry... but thats everywhere these days, so I can't blame Starbucks for idiot addicts killing themselves...  Friendly and helpful staff make it all OK. :-)
The coffee was good but I was waiting for 15 minutes for food i ordered a half hour before I came
I am assuming this is the four-bucks inside the Target in Glen Mills. Went there today for a large order for a group and they were pleasant, helpful & quick.  No issues and so glad they are there.

It takes a lot for me to give a Starbucks a low score but this location is not my favorite. I ran into an issue where one of the drinks I ordered required "steamed lemonade" the barista was very rude when replying that "we don't steam lemonade" and when I asked why he simply asked the manager. The manager stated it was a "health code" violation and stated they couldn't make my drink. They didn't offer to sub the lemonade for anything or even to NOT steam the lemonade, they just moved on to the next customer. I contacted their customer service and they said there's no reason the lemonade couldn't be steam and apologized profusely for the situation. To this day I still avoid this location, it's worth it to drive further down the road.

Have you heard? The manegercall the police on 2 young men who "wouldn't buy anything " and sat at a table to wait on a friend. I'm so guilty of going to Starbucks and using wifi, and not even ask for water. Horrible service... there's many Starbucks to choose from people. Don't choose this one

I mean, it's a Starbucks, amirite? You know what you're getting yourself into when  walking into the arms of that ever familiar green mermaid.  I do want to comment on the efficiency of the staff in the mornings. I often drop by here before work when it is super busy. However, the line goes by super quick. I have often been the fifth to sixth person in line and I'm in and out in less than 5 minutes. Awesome.  This staff is doing a great job at getting me to work on time and caffeinated!

Just went through the drive thru, and it was awful.  I ordered an Iced Americano and a breakfast sandwich. The lady who took my order, Kim, screwed it up completely, which is tough to do with an Iced Americano.  I was halfway home when I took a sip and realized they messed it up, so I had to turn around.  I suppose that's on me, but again, an Iced Americano should be pretty easy (if you're not visibly hungover, that is).  So I go back, and the lady at the bar made it for me again.  Still not right, but at this point I'm pretty frustrated and my sandwich is cold, so I accept the drink and leave.  I'm pretty sure if I complained again that my drink would come with a side order of saliva and mucus.  Normally I go to the one at Franklin Mills, where the girl Brittany gets it right with a smile EVERY TIME.  Now, when it comes to coffee, perfection is not to be expected UNTIL you charge almost 5 dollars for a singular drink.  At those prices, you had better get it right.

These super sweet baristas just made me a delicious drink in a jiff! :) The pastry case was full of amazing (& very tempting) treats, and there was plenty of seating & good vibes. I highly recommend stopping by here for an espresso drink and snack!

The coffee is good but the service at this location is always horrible. The staff looks dazed and confused especially if you ask for something like more half and half since the container is empty. No reason to wait 5 minutes for a cup of decaf, gave up on getting cream. I could understand if this place were busy.

This has been one of my best starbucks experiences, and I've been to many starbucks. The customer service was amazing. The barista took the time to explain every single tea to me, and other baristas, who were busy working, even happily told me their favorite teas. They spelled my name correctly, which is very rare. In addition, the barista gave me the extra tea that had been made for my brew. They made my day in Santa Barbara that much better!   In addition, there are many large chairs, as well as tables, indoors and outside. Gorgeous location too.
The Starbucks was filthy all the trash cans where over flooding with trash the restroom was pretty dirty as well. Disappointing  customer complaint about restrooms customers left upset

It's Starbucks and this is NOLA which means there are lots of local coffee options but this location is great. Fast, friendly and drinks prepared properly, what more could a girl ask for...ohhh yeah she asks for establishments that are conscious of and follow COVID rules, which they do THANKS STARBUCKS
No tops for drinks, service sucks, drink order wrong.... Just horrible not going there again!!

I really only came here once. It was on our way to Cape May and I was having a serious crave. We really only popped in and out (definitely less than 10 minutes) which much to my surprise is a GOOD THING! Anyone knows its VERY rare to do that at a Starbucks when you're not using the mobile app.   You know the saying, first impression and all that, well the atmosphere and cleanliness definitely made an impression... a good one. I can't comment too much on them as we didn't order anything other than beverages so there wasn't much interaction. But the customer service I received was nothing to complain about. I definitely see myself returning if ever on another road trip in that direction.  Until next time, ~~You may pay the bucks, but you're treated like a star.

The  barista   seemed bothered to take my order, didn't ask me my name, which was Olay, because they were not  busy. I was standing waiting for my drink and she put a drink up on a higher Stand, didn't make eye contact with me so I  didn't think it was my drink, and then went back to making another drink that I naturally thought was mine , she made that drink and then with a big smile called for "Justin "and said  your drink ready!  Then looked at me and with her eyes pointed at my drink like "there's yours".,but she never said a word.  I will say that she was  RUDE RUDE, RUDE!  Drink tasted fine.

A bunch of confused employees, over staffed yet slow and no one knows what's going on. Manager is rude and the food on display is moldy. Response to telling them said they're display doesn't reflect the provided food. Lmao come onnnn

Consistency and friendly service is the hallmark of any Starbucks. This location has lived up to this standard. My order is always ready at the time specified on the app and given to me with a smile (you can tell even when masked). Thanks Starbucks!

This Starbucks is one of the most hit or miss establishments that I've ever patronized. Sometimes you get a nice person helping you who makes your drink well, and sometimes you get...uhh...something entirely different.  There have been countless times where I went in for a beverage and was told that they did not have the ingredients necessary to make it. There was a straight week where they were out of ice. Like, really? Ice?   Usually the service is terrible, usually there are 1,000 people in line, usually the cups aren't filled to the top, and usually the employees spend more time talking to one another than they do talking to the customers...but sometimes everything goes off without a hitch.  So, this review isn't really helpful.  Just know that this isn't *really* a Starbucks. It's run by Sodexo, which is why they don't take Starbucks promotional materials (coupons, star redemption, etc.), and also why they get away with everything they do.

I love Starbucks and always get the same thing. I am glad to see a Starbucks so close to home so I don't have to drive all over to get to one. The only thing that has always been an issue of mine with Starbucks is they're prices since they are a little high. Other than that I love them.

Probably the worst Starbucks I have ever been too.  Maybe because it's at the college but doesn't mean you shouldn't have a working bathroom.  Told the person behind the counter and she wasn't surprised.   Took 20 mins for an ice tea!

My name is Tara and I am a Starbucks addict... its my one corporate crutch... Also I am a NYer (Brooklynite actually) so I am used to a get in - order - get the h out kind of pace at Starbucks. This was a pleasant change of pace. The girls were so sweet and made my drink perfect (unlike some NY store that do not comprehend "very little ice"). They even held onto my umbrella for me when I left it behind.
Closed  at 9:45pm Thursday. They are not open the hours advertised. Beware don't waste the drive.

After 18 visits, I figure it is time for a review. Why? I like this unique Starbucks...  It is tricky to get to. If you are coming in from Curlew, you can get here by cutting through a huge parking lot to get to Tampa road. Through much practice, I have found the optimal route that avoids every speed bump that causes my car to bottom out. Try left, right, left, right, left, and then right.   Regarding this location, they have plenty of pleasant outdoor seating as well as indoor chairs and a beautiful solid wood communal table. Unfortunately, it is too close to the register so I can't edge it out the door and in to my house.   As always, the espresso is consistently Starbucks and does what I need it to do. Wake me up!

The promise of Starbucks drive through is that they get your order right every time. Out of my 5-6 visits here so far my order gets messed up twice. This location is logistically great for me to and from work but I think I will go back to Quik Trip if I have to break for coffee.  Yesterday there was a mumbler at the drive through. I could barely hear him and he did not confirm anything I was saying to him. Of course the drink order is wrong, I asked for soy milk and got regular milk and when I asked him if it was soy milk, I was told I did not tell him that I wanted soy milk. Yes, he did offer to make me another drink and asked me to wait in front of the Starbucks instead of the drive through line. That's fair. And 10 minutes later I had to walk into the place to ask for my replacement drink. I give up on this place. There are so many other coffee shops in the area with better service. Sorry Ferguson Starbucks, you need to up your customer service game.

I have had nothing but issues with this location lately. When they first opened they had a good group of people working and they've all seemed to disappear. My first issues came in the form of them never having my food order ready when I ordered through the mobile app. And this was consistent every time. They were an hour late to open one day and made me late to work waiting for my mobile order to be prepared and then when they didn't receive an order through the app that I had placed they begrudgingly and slowly made my drink and then when I told the manager this was the second time in two weeks that they had made me late to work he shrugged and said sorry. I reported all these instances to corporate and the last one they acted like I was just complaining and that these things happen, which I agree that they do happen sometimes...but it has turned to every single time I order from this store. I will be going to another location or no Starbucks at all from now on.

This is one of my favorite Starbucks in town. Everyone is very sweet and on top of it. Once in a while they send someone from Lemmon valley that I don't go to that location because of her but it's fine I love the team up there! Kim is the best barista I've ever had the experience of ordering from and her customer service is top notch. If she were to leave this location I wouldn't be coming by. Lindsey is just as amazing and I'm stoked every time I see the two! I'll keep coming back way too often for their amazing drinks!
A typical Starbucks. Friendly service. A bit slower than others I have visited but my order was always made right.
Worse Starbucks in the St. Louis metro area. How can I wait in line for ten minutes and my drink still isn't ready?

Me and my mother have been going here for over a half decade, and it's a great Starbucks!  All of the employees are very helpful and hardworking, and if you have any issues they will be more than happy to assist you with anything. It's a great place to do your homework, or just read a book! They have free WiFi, and if you stay in store, you can get .50 refills for certain drinks, also it isn't a loud environment, so that's a bonus. Overall, I've had nothing but great experiences here, and I would recommend it to anyone!

It's Starbucks.  They all look alike to some degree, all have the exact same menu.
