Ones such as this one always has a plethora of hipsters on their Macs doing some graphic design project or blog post....  Little coffee trivia I found out there : Cold brew takes 20 hours to prepare and is smoother than regular iced coffee.
Great staff. I am there at least 4 days a week.  Parking is not the staffs issue. It's the east side and or north shore parking is always issue. I would put this Starbucks against any in the city!!

This is your typical Starbucks, however I'm giving them 2 stars because they are incredibly slow. Some mornings you may be waiting in the drive through for around 15 minutes. Also, it seems every time I go to this Starbucks they short me on my drink. Just like this morning, I ordered a venti but when I opened the lid it was the amount of a grande... So disappointing. Too bad this is the only Starbucks I pass on my way into work.

Never have I had an issue with this Starbucks the only reason they are getting 3 stars and not 5 is because I ordered on the app so it would be "quicker" I could have came in and ordered normally and gotten my food quicker, there are so many mobile orders sitting here as I type and mine is still not done very upset about this.

Every Starbucks should have a drive thru! I go to this one specifically because it's to much of a pain to get both my kids out of the car to get coffee in the morning. Every time I go the person who takes my order is courteous and quick. Expect to have a bit of a wait here, if the line is backed up in the drive thru it can take at least 10 minutes to get your order. It's one of their busiest locations and for that reason it can take a bit longer.

It's okay. I asked for a drink that was a promo that just ended and was quickly dismissed. Then I asked for a normal drink. I asked for skim milk and she repeated the name of my drink. I don't think she was very interested in listening. I don't think this is a Starbucks I will come back to.

Hands down, this is the worst Starbucks location I have ever been to. Sadly, it is the only within a reasonable distance from my home so I continue to go. Every (and I mean EVERY) time I go the wait time is ridiculous whether I'm ordering a more intricate craft beverage or a tall blonde. This location gets backed up at all hours because the employees just really don't give a shit about the customers. I've gone inside to order multiple times to find one lone employee taking orders and literally no one making drinks regardless of how many people are in line. The employees have no sense of prioritizing the customer and are usually stocking, half assed cleaning, or socializing amongst themselves. The only semi-positive thing I can say about the staff is they are typically not rude, just oblivious. I cannot stress enough how much I despise this location and wish I had the willpower to stop throwing my money at this place. Oh, and the selection of whole bean coffee sucks if you're into that.   Tl;dr - Avoid if possible.
This is the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. It doesn't matter if you do a mobile order or order in the store, it's going to take forever. The employees seem so stressed all the time.

Definitely not one of the better Starbucks I've been to.  They get CRUSHED during morning rush hour.  Currently, I've been waiting over 10 minutes for a simple cold brew.  Ever since Starbucks started doing mobile orders, this store in particular just can't keep up.  This morning when I ordered at the counter there were at least 15-20 drink orders ahead of me, most of which were comprised of mobile orders.  I'm not sure if the baristas here are slower than at other Starbucks or if the demand is just so high that they can't keep up, but I can't wait 20 minutes for my coffee every day.

I don't review chains.   I would always give them a three,   McDonalds; 3, Subway; 3, Taco Bell; 3, In ' Out; 3 (ok, 4).  Anyway, you get the picture.    But this Starbucks is different and while every other Starbucks in the world would probably get a three this one gets a four.  Let me explain:  It's not for the service, in fact the girl who gave me my coffee was kind of rude.  The coffee and the pastries look like any other Starbucks so no extra points there.   It is the people watching!   Wow!   The place is packed and it is full of a very diverse and amazingly entertaining group of people.  Think people who are staying at the Biltmore for a bachelorette party having their coffee before they jump on a fancy wine tasting bus.  A few Westmont students, some CEO of some company, an old guy who is always there, a few assorted crazy people and maybe a celebrity or two.  Some think that they are privileged and seem to be annoyed to be waiting for their coffee with all the common folks.  So if you are REALLY reading Yelp to find out if Starbucks is a good place to go for a cup of coffee, go there (or come here since I am sitting here now.)  You will certainly be amused.    Wow, and as I sit here, some old guy just asked me if I would mind moving, he told me I am in his seat!!!!   Can you believe it?    I LOVE this place!

This is now my go-to Strarbucks and is nearest on my way to my new job.  It's a 4 star place but I am knocking a star off gfor the horrendous parking situation.  It is located next to the Paoli train station which is jam-packed night and day and clearly there is just not enough parking for ommuters and locals alike.  I parked in a seafood restaurant next door and walked over.  Meanwhile, my Grande Nonfat Caramel macchiato (at $4.82) was pricey but delicious.  Nice gals running the show. I wonder where they park???  Excellent Service, Crappy Parking
this is a great store, i live a little far from here but work in the area and this is my favorite stop on my way in to work! always brightens up my day and the employees are always pleasant and kind!

It is 8:15am and the store isn't that packed. The parking spaces are very few and people have to park illegally just for a fix of their Starbucks. The staff is also few, and SLLLLOOOWWW.   Why Starbucks? Why?! Even when I come at other times of the day, parking is awful. Wifi is slow.

Monday night 6/25 at around 8:57pm I pulled up to the drive through after a hard gym session and needed some caffeine to get me home. I was greeted by the most genuine, friendly voice asking me how my day was and what I would like to drink. Later on, I was told her name is Elizabeth (blonde ponytail working the drive thru)...and I just have to say THANK YOU FOR YOUR AMAZING CUSTOMER SERVICE. If only every Starbucks was like that...I didn't feel rushed even though I asked for future when they closed and she said 9!! Oops...once again thank you for the great customer service!!

Terrible customer service at the Drive thru! I'm gestational diabetic and have to order a drink that is "odd" I'm sure, however a barista being condescending and rude while I'm placing my order is not appropriate. I order this drink frequently at other locations and have never been questioned or "cut off" while placing my order. Luckily this is no longer the closest Starbucks to my home or work! I will definitely visit Starbucks again, but not this location!

There is not much to say about Starbucks.  Listen, when you are a Colombian, and you drink Colombian coffee everyday, and you have been harvesting coffee while being a child, a plain coffee in Starbucks won't surprise you much.  The consistency of the coffee is the same every Starbucks you go. I even become a petulant woman if I have to talk about coffee in Starbucks.  However, Starbucks have two items I really love and for which I return to it.  The chai latte, and the espresso shot.  Moreover, the workers are usually really nice people, super attentive and smiley.  They have something in common, their looks, they have tattoos, and piercings, and crazy glasses; they are happy people.

The area for this Starbucks is great- it's at the center of Germantown in Chestnut Hill.  There are plenty of chairs and lounge chairs to sit and do your work.  It's quite so you can be really productive.  Just like any Starbucks, the staff are friendly and attentive when you walk in the counter to order your drinks- other than that nothing special with the service.

Boyfriend and I went to grab some iced coffee drink for him and water for both of us. Spent about 10-12 minutes in drive-thru with only 3 cars in front of us. We come from a congested area in the suburbs of DC where drive-thrus are usually 10-15 cars long and the wait won't be much longer than experienced today.   When we pulled up to the window, we saw 5 employees standing around chatting and were told that someone will be right with us. Boyfriend gets his drink and a minute later, we get one water after ordering two. No receipt, no straw for coffee drink, and no second water. If we weren't in a rush, we would've come in the store and demanded to speak with the shift manager. But for some reason, I feel that wouldn't have resolved or changed anything.  Stay away. Go to Dunkin for quick coffee fix, or small local coffee shops for small business support and better service.

Of all of the Starbucks in the city, this is the best one. The staff here are so efficient and most of them now know me by name (says a lot about my coffee habits). Denise and Fernando especially are so awesome. Love this place.

My visit today was horrible!  I entered the drive thru at 9am made it to the order speaker at 9:28pm  I waited 5mins at the speaker & no one said ANYTHING.  The cars ahead of me we pulling up so I began to think perhaps the speaker wasn't working.  (Although there was no sign about the speaker not working just a sign that says they would closing at 2pm) I proceeded to the pickup window as to not continue to hold the line behind me.  I explain to the young lady I wasn't able to order at the speaker she calls another associate or manger to the window.  This female had dreds and double nose rings unfortunately I didn't get her name so this is the only way I can describe her but she told me they would take my order at the window but next time I would have to get back in line because they are backed up inside and outside with orders.  I was taken aback & explained I have been in line for over 30 mins but no worries I would not be back.  How rude of her not to just apologize and say sorry for the wait and sorry that we didn't get on the intercom & say one moment.  Overall I was annoyed with the time it took but extra annoyed with her response to me about something that was clearly their fault.  Poor service all around!

It's Starbucks.  Drive thru experience: This location seems to be incredibly quick. I did order a turkey pesto panini. The turkey tasted  bad. It made me wonder if they had checked the date. I had to toss the sandwich away.  The coffee's were good. The barista was quick, kind and very nice. I can't seem to remember her name.  The location is nice as it is before a freeway entrance. It makes for easy access. Plus it is far enough down that it is out of the construction zone.
By far the best atmosphere and floor plan design of all Indy Starbucks. Great service and fresh everything - Today

Stopped at this Starbucks on my way to a doctor appointment. I went inside, did not go through drive-thru so I cannot comment on that.   I was heartily welcomed as soon as I walked in. It's spacious, with lots of tables, a few couches, and comfy chairs.   I got my order within five minutes, it was correct and hot. I worked on my laptop on the table.  Bathroom was clean.   No complaints.

The dude working the register was rude and the service was terrible. They were slow and at least 3 people that ordered after me got their drinks first. When I finally did get my order it was the wrong drink, but at that point I just wanted to leave.

this starbucks is shit. first off the line took way to long for 2 drinks. second the girl taking my order repeated it to me 7 times and still didnt get it right. then not only did my drink jist taste like water and no flavoring. the water tasted like swamp water. literal suer water. disgusting

This is my local Starbucks. The Food Girl and I have frequented it for a few years. I must say, in the last few months, the quality, service and overall experience has plummeted off the deep end.   Where do I even start?   Is it too much to ask for a "Good morning" or "Thank you?" The snarky, snide service gets under my skin. A few new baristas have been nice lately but there are a number of morning-regular workers who are obviously jaded and bitter. It shows. The environment in this Starbucks in the morning can only be described as "dreadful."  Customers feel like cattle, moved from line to handoff station and finally out the door. And frankly, that is the best part of the visit.   The store itself is a damn mess. The condiment bar is perpetually dirty. Sugar everywhere, spilled milk and empty containers. Look, I understand that this is a coffee shop and it goes with the territory, but it seems like the staff doesn't even give a damn.  I have never once seen someone cleaning up the condiment station, and I go to this location at least twice a week.    As for strengths, the drinks are generally good. Still, the service is extremely slow and often gets my order wrongwrongwrong. I was even overcharged last week, not realizing until I got into the car. there was no way I was going back in there to get my $1.25 back.   My advice? Go to Black n Brew. I should listen to myself more often.

I have ordered online multiple times form this Starbucks location. However, every other time when I arrive to pick up the order, they will tell me that the sandwich(es) that I've ordered are out of stock.  Any other Starbucks, when ordering online, will only let you order the sandwich if they have them in stock. This location needs to do better with updating their sandwich availability for online ordering - it is a consistent problem! A manager needs to figure this out and fix it.
Aways great service..  everyone was very happy and excited to serve me.  Wasn't too busy when i stopped in around 9:30am
Incredibly slow service, even for a Starbucks. The staff here do not care. If you got through the drive thru, it's a minimum of 10-15 minute wait

I work right around the corner, so i am always going to this Starbucks. Today was the day that made me want to write a bad yelp review. Around 1:30pm went in the drive thru, backed up. took almost 30 mins. I was locked in otherwise i would have backed out and went to pjs. I had to repeat my order 3 times. Grande coffee w/ soy. it shouldn't be that difficult? got my coffee and it was wrong. this happens more than it should. the baristas at the drive thru are never smiling. I will continue going to this Starbucks in hopes they get my coffee with soy right one day... and for the convenience!

I'm not a Starbucks fan, but if you're in the Willow Grove Mall looking for coffee, this it. Baristas were very efficiently working their way through a fairly long line. Place is clean, and it's in the "food court" with convenient seating, pleasant lighting under skylights, clean seating area. Very strong dark roast, almost with a burnt flavor. I don't drink it, but shockingly they were out of the cold brew, which must be a bestseller at a place like this.
This is one of my favorite Starbucks in Tucson. Building is relatively new, so it is clean and stylish.   Service is always fast and friendly. My strange requests welcomed with a smile :)

'We're out of everything! ' Seems to be the order of the day.  I was disappointed that they display the pepperoni  turnover in the display case but when I order it the product is discontinued!? Don't display product you don't sell.  Also all other fold over types are sold out. Finally-  It doesn't take 24 hours for a gift card to transfer to the Starbucks app, it happens right away.  I put an extra $10 on my app thinking that's what happened but when I refreshed my app the gift card had applied.

Today was an off day at Starbucks. Girl making drinks gave me a plain Grande Latte; label clearly stated Cinnamon Dolce' Latte. No topping foam only. I am just surprised because this location is always good & my go to! I handed it back to her & she put the topping on it. However, it stiill wasn't right because it had  no syrup in it. Yucky $4 drink plus $1 tip

This is still my favorite location. Just recently the orders have been wrong alot, most recently in the last 2 days. Yesterday wasn't a big deal because it was just lemonade missing. Today is an issue because I've only had 1/4 of my drink and feel sick because the wrong milk was used. Mistakes happen but 2 days in a row and people have severe allergies, luckily I didn't drink it all and it will pass but please starbucks pay attention a little closer. (Plus we pay extra for these things)  1 Star-   Total failure of an experience based on this experience would not return. Would not recommend.  2 Star-  Below average on several levels, not a total failure. Wouldn't seek out to return or recommend.  3 Star-  Average experience, minimum expectations met. Nothing that says wow or that stands out. Would return if someone else wanted to come, not a regular go to.  4 Star-  Above average experience, will come here again and recommend to friends and family.  5 Star-  This will be a regular go to and will be highly recommended to people I know. Exceeds expectations on a consistent basis.
This was a typical Starbucks. Service was prompt. Staff was pleasant.

I really enjoy this Starbucks. The main reason? They always spell my name right on the cup!!! It may not sound like much but when you've got a weird name it's pretty exciting to have someone actually spell it right. My beverage always is more satisfying when it actually says my name.  The seating area is always kept clean. Since it is inside of a Chapters there isn't a whole lot of room but it's a cozy little area with a nice bit of natural light. If this location gets outrageously busy and you don't feel like waiting in line, you can hop on over to Target since there's a Starbucks in there too (although it doesn't have a seating area).   The baristas here are always nice and reasonably quick. I've never had a bad experience here. (Unlike other Starbucks... Cafe Vanilla frappes are NOT the same as Vanilla Bean, but I swear 87% of the baristas don't know that).

Starbucks is not my first choice in coffee places, but because they are everywhere they are convenient, especially drive throughs.   Yesterday I placed my order, sat in line for 5 minutes, pulled up to the window and held my phone out for the girl to scan. I said "this is a free reward." She got mad because she could not get her scanner to work, snapped at me when I asked her if she wanted to take my phone (which I guess they are not allowed to do- this is fine but explain it, don't attack me for asking). It finally scanned and then she said that I owed her the $5.40. I repeated that this was a reward. She then got mad all over again, snapped at me and was just rude because she did not hear me and had to scan again.  I will not be going back to this Starbucks. The coffee is just not worth the wait or the lousy employee attitude.

Starbucks runs America, that is unfortunately the only choice of coffee that I saw around here at the Grand Sierra Resort. The line was of course super long and super slow, but surprisingly fast for them to make it. It was a supervisor on the coffee bar making it. The cashier that helped me had no facial expression but that he hated his job and didn't want to be there. He was emotionless when he helped me. He just kept saying "anything else?" And "Do you want your receipt?" Customer service is a biggie for me. They ran out of sandwiches and bagels at 10:30am, I am not surprised, but...... they should be a little more stocked? Being that they are at a big resort.
I love this Starbucks my boyfriend and I come here all the time and we pretty much always get a large chocolate mocha and a Large Dragon fruit refresher with lemonade and it's so good!!!

Who gets excited about another Starbucks? I do! Because we finally have the convenience of a drive-thru, and it's the only coffee place in the area close to my job. We no longer have to travel 10 minutes away during our 15-minute break to get coffee.  It will be interesting to see how the traffic situation is once people find out this place has opened being that it's located on the busy intersection of Turnpike and Calle Real.  The inside of this Starbucks is beautiful! I love the whole atmosphere and clean modern look it has. They also have outdoor seating which is pretty cool. The staff was very welcoming, and MJ was especially helpful on my visit. Thank you MJ!  Overall it's just another Starbucks, nothing new on the menu but again it's nice to have this place close by.

04/23/16  Expresso Machine Not Work... Has drown off my day... All I was looking forward to was Caramel Macchiato  with 3 extra Expresso shots.. To make My Sunshine bright... and to make things better after purchasing the last 3 mini vanilla scones they given then way to another customer  great... Not Happy!
Great Starbucks always friendly and willing to do.what ever they can to please.  Parking is a bit of a problem but worth the effort.  :)
Great location and quick service. The employees are always smiling and it is clean.  Always a joy.   Awesome outdoor seating in the afternoon.
No long wait, great service and made just the way I like it. Scones are amazing.

This Starbucks is always packed due to it's location in the mall. My mom and I decided to go during the Frapp Happy Hour. The line was insanely long and we waited over 25 minutes for the drinks. The bar where you pick up the drinks was very disorganized, people were picking drinks up that weren't theirs, some drinks were not labeled, etc. I'm pretty sure I picked up someone else's drink (they shared the same name as my mom's and it was the same drink I ordered), but my moms drink was missing. She had to go up and ask them where her drink was. I'm sure they ended up tossing a lot of drinks away and had drinks missing due to the volume the store was experiencing. I would try to avoid this place unless you don't mind waiting an inordinate amount of time for coffee. On the plus side, the cashier was pleasant and the frapps were bomb.
Hot food takes forever. Every time!!! I just don't understand. So many people working. Is there just one oven?

WALK TO A DIFFERENT STARBUCKS! This one is poorly run and very slow. My drink order was messed up and I had to ask several times about the status of my food before they realized they had never even started it. They acted like I was an annoying customer for asking, i was polite and I just wanted what I paid for. There's plenty of other Starbucks and hopefully this one is poorly run because it's new and still learning. For now tho, if you're in a hurry, you may want to skip this location.

Ordered a pumpkin spice frap and a mocha cookie crumble frap today. The service was great! There was a young guy who was working the front and was super helpful when I was trying to decide which drink to get. Great customer service and the drinks were really good!

I go to this Starbucks twice a week, without fail, and sometimes more! It's fast (even though it's always packed) and the employees are nice! I typically do mobile orders in the morning and my drink is always perfect and ready when I arrive! The only downside to this Starbucks is that parking is limited, so when it's super packed the drive thru may be your only option. But that's not a huge deal to me because I'm always picking up, not staying! However there's lots of seating inside for studying or working. Love this Starbucks!

This is the slowest and least friendly starbucks I have went to in Indy. I work close to this location so I frequent it quite often. They are slow. Even when the lobby is close to empty and the drive thru is slow. I have waited and waited and waited on basic drinks with barely a smile when my order was finally ready, let alone an apology for the extended wait. I am usually very patient and have never said anything to them except thank you but this evening was it  This has happened repeatedly to me at this location so I had to come to yelp. I have had great experiences at other locations and will make it a point to not come to this one anymore. I wanted to add that I work in the evening so I am coming close to 7p, not mid peak hrs.

I came here around the middle of May and had no problem using my Gold Card. I actually came here with a large group on Saturday, and they were quite efficient with our orders.   We were only visiting the area, so I am not sure I'll come back just because of that. But if you're from around the area, I'd recommend. They have board games too.

I used to love getting my morning pick me up here...the baristas were great and always accurate. Now they have a lot of new people and suddenly my order with "no foam" has a lot of foam. Maybe it shouldn't be a big deal, but it does make a difference. You get a lot less when there's foam, and I just don't like drinking air. And I'm pretty sure I've been jipped on syrup recently too, so I just stopped requesting it. I order the same thing everyday on the app, so I know what it's supposed to be. I still need that triple shot to get me going, but I might have to start picking it up elsewhere. (Side note, Amanda is awesome!)

This Starbucks is always busy. Expect to wait for a seat. The store is like any other Starbucks but with a few things that make it stand out a bit from the rest:  1. They hang artwork from local artist. The artwork is nice and it is for sale upon request. I like this.  2. The staff is a bit more friendly than other Starbucks I've been to. I think they might genuinely care about their jobs... Or, maybe they're just nice people.   This is a good rating from me... for Starbucks.

My wife and me have been going to this Starbucks for four years. The people that work there are kind, considerate, and fun. When I went through my cancer battle they sincerely wanted to know how I was doing. When my wife was in an accident they asked me how she was doing as I picked up her daily chai tea latte with no water and no foam! Lol! They are quick, accurate, and personable. If you are over in the Carrolwood area check them out. You won't be disappointed.

Lovely little Starbucks nestled in a Safeway, but feel compelled to review this one in particular because of the lovely lady who is working here today. Fernanda (I believe her name is) is downright awesome. knowledgeable about the product / recommended two new tea combinations to me. Bother were rad, incidentally.   Also, because I had to stay an work, I was able to observe her with other customers. She's the "poster child" so to speak for the perfect Starbucks employee. Whoever gives raises and/or kudos should give all of them to Fernanda.   Thank you!

I just love having a bit of heaven in a cup on my way to work in the morning.  Such a treat! This location is brightly lit, clean and roomy. Everyone we've met there has been friendly and helpful.  The store is gearing up for Christmas and have added some pretty nice shiny bits. Free wi-fi , nice location and great company while enjoying my hot beverage. What more can I ask?

This morning I decided to pick up an espresso at my favorite Starbucks on Speedway and Wilmot. Even though there were at least 6 cars ahead of mine, they managed to kindly take my order, get it right and with a smile. Thank you guys!

So I am a pretty easy person to please, having worked in the retail industry in the past and even in a restaurant for a brief moment in my life, I understand that most employees want to do their jobs and some customers are just plain jerks, so I am a pretty easy person to please I dont expect much, just that my drink is made correctly and that I get a Hi or some kind of greeting from the person taking the order.   Well i pulled up today and ordered a drink and the other guy on the intercom said Ok anything else? to which i replied I would also like a chocolate croissant, well the intercom went blank for 2-3 minutes and i was just sitting there and he comes over the intercom again and asks anything else, to which i then replied a chocolate croissant i thought he didn't hear me the first time which is understandable, he said no I got you i'm on my way to check, he did in fact hear me the first time he could of atleast said let me check or something, so he comes back on and says we are completely out of chocolate croissants.   I pull up to pay and the girl who rang me up was very friendly and said you had the drink and croissant right and I said well i wanted a chocolate croissant but if you don't have any then its fine, she said no we do have chocolate croissants and handed me my order, I give this place three stars just because of the attitude of the first employee, he needs to learn how to communicate with his customers better.

I order the same drink everyday and they always make it different or ask stupid questions. If I said no whip, why would ask do I want whip on the bottom. I'm going to ask to speak to the manager next time. Especially if I'm paying 9 dollars for a drink & Banana bread my drink should be made CORRECT
For a chain store very frustrating when staff doesn't know what they have sugar free and makes a drink with sugar when you cannot have sugar

This is one of the bigger Starbucks in town, so it is a great place to hunker up and do some work while enjoying your lattes or what have you. That is, provided that you don't come here during peak hours when it is filled with rich pretentious California girls and college students.  The baristas here are OK. Most of them seem a little too busy, or during slow times, preoccupied with themselves and their smartphones, to provide good customer service. But every now and then you will find a gem here.   I'm not a huge fan of Starbucks in the first place. I find them to be cold and uncomfortable places, which is probably the point, because if it were, nobody would leave.  Still, it's OK.   For a Starbucks, that is.

So I went into this Starbucks on a lovely sunny day making me very parched I ordered a large refresher and a protein box and was hoping for a large water. I was then told by one of the workers that they were only doing grande waters for the day... like honey pls I am not Arianna Grande I need me a big water so that I can play a good day of tennis smh. I was not pleased and wished that they knew the money I spend at this place and that I feel that if I ask for a large water they should give me one because I bought other things... not mad just disappointed

I was feeling light headed while waiting more than 5 mins for my drink. There was a chair next to a table. I sat down, the women working there told me  not to sit. I told her I already ordered I'm just waiting for my drink. She rolled her eyes and said NO SEATING!   Never ever coming back again!

Went in to the starbucks a week or two ago at the nugget to get a quick bite to eat. I was looking at the food in the display case and noticed there were fruit flies in the case so I let the manager Gabby know, to my surprise i was met with rude attitude from the manager as she coldly told me those are only for display so it didn't matter. I can't believe the attitude i received from the manager Gabby especially since i have known her father and mother for years. Gabby was far more interested in chatting with the supervisor on shift Taylor which is probably a given since they are family, which leads me to my next question why is there a supervisor working under her sister who's manager? Things like this is how you end up with poor customer service employing siblings in high up positions like this in the same department. They are far more interested in chatting with each other than listening to customer feedback.

This is my neighborhood Starbucks,which makes it the best!! (For me!!!) I love when I walk in and everyone says Hi! or Good morning!!   They are (mostly) so friendly! I think when they aren't it's because they're either a he trainer or trainee and their mind is exploding. Seriously, not sure but I feel like this Starbucks is the training center of Tampa!! How grueling that must be and they STILL smile at me!    This is a typical Starbucks as far as offerings, no wine, appetizers or fancy desserts like other ones I've heard of, but I've had my share of Starbucks around the US and Europe and have had to return a cup or two for burnt taste, wrong order, etc. Not here. I come here 54-5 days a week when I'm in town and have never had a mistake! Ever.   I'd say parking is the only issue because people don't want to park behind the store - but it's there, so my 5 stars stick!
Horrible service I was in drive thru line got out went inside and cars that was behind drove out before being served or even started so I asked for money back and walked out

They always have a new staff who are clueless, last time our order was wrong. Went back again & the car in-front of us got the wrong order so we had to wait as we watched them remake it. Then we pull up to the window and our order is wrong as well and staff tried to tell us it was right and we test tasted what they told us was a nitro cold brew and they lied. Not satisfied with this location.

So glad they moved down the street away from the train station where it was a nightmare to park let alone get back onto Lancaster Avenue. I fequent this drive through at least 3 days a week and 99% of the time they are quick, kind and drinks are correct. I try to stay away from any business during peak hours but this is my exception to the rule. 2 thumbs up for easy access and convenience.
Usually Starbucks is pretty good, but this one was awful. They messed up my order twice, and I never get anything complicated just a white mocha. I'm very disappointed

Review indicated the worst Starbucks. Totally agree. One person taking orders and making coffee on a busy Sunday morning.  Line to the door. Ordered a green tea cappuccino no whip. Got a grande with whip.

Gabe and his friend (I think Kyle, who has been here for five years but didn't want Gabe to tell anyone that, sorry Kyle) were excellent.   We were a particularly silly bunch and they handled our weird and delivered both a fantastic customer experience and delicious drinks & snacks. Especially considering the shortage of fresh bananas and only having smoothie bananas on hand. They were able to deliver an adequate smoothie experience with no issues.

Drive through is horrible every time I come even when there isn't a line. I usually avoid this location due to the long wait time but today I decided to come inside and received some of the worst customer service; the cashier didn't even acknowledge me until she was done with her conversation and just stared at me with vacant eyes. I will say the tall gentleman that gave me my drink had great customer service.

[945]  6/18/2019  It's located on a busy highway so many people stop for a hot drink.  This Starbucks is VERY slow.. I mean very slow...  It took a long time to get our order.   A few nice outdoor chairs.  If I come back I hope the service is faster.
Literally the slowest wait I've had at a drive through. Rude service, 20+ minute wait. Coffee was good, inexcusable service

I go to this location quite often because it's convenient and they always get my order right. I always get a venti white chocolate mocha with coconut milk no whip cream no foam long shot. I have gone to a couple of the other locations in both directions and it's always been hit and miss. Here it's is has been consistent so even if I am going the opposite direction I will go here if I need a quick caffeine. The staff is always friendly. On one day in particular I went in to get a gift card to do a giveaway. The barista helped me purchase the card and put it on my app so that way people weren't using my main card. It was very sweet.

I've worked for Starbucks for about 4 years, I understand the rush of people, etc..the person at the register and the barista on the main bar were great and had a positive attitude. The cold bar barista looked like she hated her life! I understand cold drinks take a little more work but man, she looked at my order and just sat there for a couple mins and said under her breath, "I don't wanna do this" it was an iced green tea latte. She didn't do it to standards and as a former barista, that's disappointing. Maybe she'll have a better attitude soon, probably not though.

Nice Starbucks located next to the I-4. Good sized parking lot means it's easy to get in and out, even when packed. Staff is nice, makes great beverages and this Starbucks has a nice vibe to it. This location is open 24 Hrs! I always stop here every time I travel to the west coast.
Stellar service during a power outage- felt like 100 degrees inside. Thanks for hanging in there!

YES i'm giving starbucks a review..  I am the typical caffeine junkie.  I drive to work grumpy, I open my store grumpy, I walk into Starbucks.  Instant smile!!  When it's warm out, there is nothing better then a ice coffee black eye in a larger cup with TONS of ice.  I have it unsweetened no cream if anyone decides they wanna drop off a cup of coffee for me.. =)  OR in the winter, Cap with extra double shot.  I told you I like my caffeine.  During those glum days I'll add a shot of peppermint.  Brightens me right up.  I love this starbucks in particular though.  The staff is GREAT, they remember everyone by name, and what you like.  The lines are quick and that extra buck in the tip jar is totally worth it.  Why I didn't give them 5 stars is because the prices now are RIDICULOUS.  I can't give up my daily starbucks, but that doesn't mean I'm not a little peeved that I'm paying $42 a WEEK for coffee.

This place has a couple key advantages:   1) Places like Monon Coffee close fairly early, and I often rise late - hangover or not - but Starbucks is always open.  2) Drive Thru - When there isn't a line snaked around the building, the drive through is fast... and when it's cold or raining, that's the tops.  3) Coffee - Intelligentsia it's not, but Starbucks is always consistently fair to good.

This is a subpar Starbucks with a poor layout. Sure, the staff is friendly, but something seemed off about the interior. It is walking distance to Vanderbilt University, which is nice, however. There is outdoor seating for when it's nice outside.

Love this Starbucks. It has a great view of the river and bridge from the porch and serves the same great coffee you'd expect in any Starbucks. Just make sure you know that around 7:30 am it's typically very crowded from people going to work or students getting their morning coffee. Also, going to this Starbucks around 3-3:30 on a weekday is dangerous as it is just about a 5 minute walk from a middle school and a high school, so often times you get crowds of kids coming in that take quite a bit of time to order and are very loud. If you avoid these times, though, this place is great.

Exiting Crate & Barrell, I needed a pick me up to continue on my day in Tampa and decided to stroll in. Everyone is always so friendly here & my order was taken with a smile and was prepared quickly. They have a drive through for those who are on the run and still deserve their caffeine oomph. The entrance is a bit weird because it's a double lane that cuts off into a weird yielding turn so if there's traffic, you can be stuck on either side for a while. The WiFi is always strong and since the coffee shop is very clean- it allows for a laptop session alongside your iced coffee. There's a hunch if outlets everywhere which is probably due to them being a newer and more renovated location. Starbucks is always a good idea so when you're in the area, stop by for a quick caffeine boost. You'll be glad you did!

Tremendously slow service. 5-6 people behind the counter all more focused on tasks than customers. Served the wrong size drink, did not call any names when they put drinks up. Might work if they were proper size and you were checking. Dismissive attitude when mentioned to them.
Zero. really. Perhaps Starbucks was good ten years ago. But now, dirty, unfriendly and just another Corporation that forgot how they got started, us the customer made you.

I just left this Starbucks and immediately pulled my car over into some random parking lot because I HAD to write them a review. It's Tampa..on a Thursday morning..at 7:30 am. I got into the drive thru line behind 7+ cars thinking to myself, oh joy.. this is probably going to take a while. I kid you not, I was ordering within 3 or so minutes and they had my cheese danish and latte (which was made to absolute perfection) to me *in under a minute.* Don't know what kind of magic these barista wizards are using back there, but I have never been so impressed/mind-blown in my life. Keep up the awesome work you guys!

This location is such a delight to visit. The store manager here really cares about his customers, his employees, and the brand. You'd think that being in the middle of the mall would cause this location to be crazy loud and congested, but they do a great job keeping lines short, and sharing smiles with customers. I've been here many times and will keep coming back to enjoy my standard coffee and breakfast sandwich.

Drink was good but it took 22 minutes for me to get my drink upon entering the door. No apology for the delay and no manners by staff whatsoever. After the cashier yelled, "Next!", I walked up and said, "Good morning". No response. I said thank you for my drink. No response. RUDE!
Nice guys here but very slow. Very slow. Can't even go here on a Friday morning because it usually takes so long.

Most unprofessional Starbucks I have ever visited. I only go to this one because it is close to work. The employees are too concerned with talking about their late night excursions than doing their job. I have also heard them, on multiple visits, talking about the customers in front of them. Incredibly innapropriate.

I like the set up of this Starbucks and it's usually full of people reading, studying, or chatting.  It's location is convenient in that it's near the Green Hills Mall and shopping areas, but beware of the traffic to and from the place!  Use the light to get out if you need to turn LEFT.  Service: Speed (Good), Quality (Good) Taste: Green Tea Frap (really tasty)

I didnt like this location because is was dirty inside the store and the bathroom was nasty to the point that i couldnt use is when i really needed  to use it!!! i hope the management will see this review and will take further action to solve this issue

I was on the road from San Francisco to Los Angeles and stopped by this establishment. The reception was non existent for me, but the white guy behind me got the "welcome sir". I waited in line like any good citizen. Then came another person,a white girl this time and she also got the "Hi and welcome" and the server asked her almost instantaneous if she had made up her mind. Of course she did and she got to order while she stood behind me!!! I was totally ignored. I mean..whats up with that?  If you have an explanation i would really like to hear it. I was well mannered, well dressed...khakis and shirt. I hope to god that me being asian had absolutely nothing to do with it!! I finally got my order and i didnt even get thanked for leaving a 2 dollar tip while my purchase was only 8 bucks! I mean really?

bad service today, when I order the drinks inside the cafe,I just standing in the pick up area to wait, there was skinny girl very crazy ， she think I order them on the app,and keep bothered me,I feel uncomfortable ,I mean why you care ? Just do your job !  She also yelled at the cafe (hey guys)said who knows her order? Really? Why you need to care? If you do want to know what I order  Is that means that you gonna make my drinks first? So crazy people!

My favorite Starbucks! They don't have a drive-thru, which was originally a disappointment. However, the morning team moves faster than most drive-thrus! More importantly, for someone that stares at a computer screen all day, it's great to get some human contact!   The staff is so friendly. I especially appreciate their understanding when my mind doesn't function well in the morning. I feel comfortable asking for crazy and unique combinations of yummy drinks!
Never been inside but the drive thru staff are always pleasant and apologetic if they are busy. Great customer service for sure!

Service was utterly and unapologetically awful. Wait time was long given the amount of people in store. What was an issue is after 23 minutes I was given the wrong order. I suggested the order was incorrect and I didn't want it. Server would not apologize for the fault. Said she'd make another but at that point almost 25 minutes later I asked for a refund. While there in the 25+ minutes this happened no less than 3 times. Getting orders wrong and either having to give away free drinks or throw away drinks. This is potentially the worst managed Starbucks I have ever been to. Unfortunately awful in every regard.

I definitely like the layout of this Starbucks.  It has a drive-thru.  You walk in and it looks like a long, wide hallway without space to spread out and the table means to do so.  The music selection is comforting and there is artwork on the walls.  The employees are super energetic, and there are a lot of them, so maybe bring headphones if you are concentrating extra hard.  Drink was okay - I think the liquid came to the halfway point of the cup which is sad.

The numerous times I have been here the drinks have never been right. It's upsetting because it is such a convenient location. My cold brew didn't even have ice one time and the cold foam is never made correctly!
First time here  Quiet  Lots of tables  Friendly service  Outdoor tables too  Big parking lot  Will return

I've stopped in here a few times recently. It is such a busy location. The first time I came in and ordered--big mistake. I was there for nearly 25 minutes waiting in line and then waiting for my drink to be made. The next time, I ordered ahead on the Starbucks app.  This saved a lot of time, as it only took 10 minutes and I ran other errands as they made my drink. I'll definitely do that from now on.

Sure this place has its flaws, but trust me when I say this: they are trying their best here! Each and every partner at this store comes to work In hopes of a better day, they are trying to make every customer happy. Even the ones that are complete trash to them. GIVE THEM A BREAK! Instead of complaining about the wait take a minute to look around you, see that line of customers out the door? Did you also see that drive thru line wrapped around the building? Did you know that this is the only Starbucks with a drive thru in this area? Did you notice how this Starbucks is located right off the highway? Expect it to be busy. Now remember at the end of the day it's JUST COFFEE and those baristas are humans with feelings so be kind to them. 5 stars to those hard working baristas and 1.5 stars to the many customers that come here.

Proably one of the best place to go and eat or drink in Edwardsville. I have only been here for 2 and a  half days and Ive been here twice already. Workers are friendly and my drinks have been delish!

I am disappointed in this Starbucks. I ordered a large coffee with cream and sugar, a very simple order. They handed me my coffee which was piping hot, I paid for it and walked  a few  blocks to my hotel giving the coffee some time to cool down so I could drink it without burning myself. The girl gave me a black coffee, no cream or sugar. I'm not sure if she messed up or if she just didn't care because she had a attitude like I was bothering her by ordering coffee.

Allow extra time if your headed to this Starbucks, gotta be one of the slowest in town.  I do drive through and order only already brewed coffee (Pike), but still wait long periods (10 min at the window earlier this week) for someone to even come to the window and take my money.    Not especially busy at 6:30 am but boy are they slow.  I think I'll purchase a coffee maker (I'm on extended work stay) and brew my morning coffee in my hotel room.  The coffee isn't that good to have to deal with this kind of frustration so early in the am

The Starbucks here in the KOP mall is actually a great place for a relaxing cup of coffee, particularly for those looking for some quiet time during the middle of the day. Unlike other Starbucks, this one can always be counted on for good coffee and pleasant music.   I must also say that I love the nice decor here. They have great seating around the glass windows and doors, which is good for some laptop or wifi time. That was worthy of a nice picture. Check out the photo I quickly took here.   The only real downer is the crowd that tends to aggregate around the mall, which spoils the quiet time that one might try to grab at sunset. If not for that, I'd say that it's a very pleasant place to be at after work or to catch up with some friends.

Ok regular customer comes in talks to the lady working up front says where's everyone at today the worker tells him you mean the homeless people? He's says yes she says I don't know where they are today they're always here during business hours seems !!!   They keep going on about this issue whole time I'm listening and learning what they're speaking of and particularly who?  So I guess Starbucks didn't learn anything about talking down to customers or profiling a set of customers that ain't even in the store?   I'm gonna report this to the higher ups that be cause I pulled out my phone and video the whole conversation tired of people thinking they can talk about whoever they want to if you don't have anything nice to say just don't say it

This is the highest grossing Starbuck's in Tucson, no joke.    They're at a busy intersection, but VERY convenient when you're headed to the beloved healthy grocery store in the same parking lot.    This establishment is also open on all major holidays (including Christmas).     It's a bit cramped inside and sometimes I feel like I'm playing frogger if I walk in, but the drive through is very convenient.

This Starbucks is pretty typical in terms of layout.  However, I would recommend the Starbucks at either Delmar and North & South and Hanley and Wydown over this Starbucks for interior space.  Also, when you are enjoying your beverage the views are poor, both inside and outside seating offer only views of the parking lot.  One advantage this Starbucks has is extensive parking, as it is in a strip mall.

Went there this morning for a get me up. They have the nitro on tap. Only problem is when I pulled off and looked to take a drink the nitro cold brew was half coffee and half foam so by the time the foam subsided it was half a coffee. If I'm paying $5 for a nitro it would be great to get a full cup

Came here on a rainy day. The staff was overworked, yet managed to prepare my Mocha Latte very quickly while still maintaining conversations with customers. With  all of the computer space I plan on staying longer next time. The rain was so deep my service dog had to stay at home limiting my time out.  The walls are decorated with nice artwork. It actually enriches the experience here, unlike some places,where it distracts from the ability to drink and eat in visual peace.

Unfortunately this place just simply cannot handle more than a few customers at a time. I just waited 10 minutes in line and 15 (FIFTEEN!) minutes for an iced coffee.   Oh and I'm writing this while I'm still waiting. Let's see if we can make it 20.
Only two employees on a Saturday morning ??  Lines out the door.  Gotta find a better place to grab my joe.

This location has a drive-thru...but if you use a reuseable cup for a fancy drink, they still use a disposable cup until you get to the window. Go inside instead if you want to not waste.  The staff seemed a bit distant and not too customer friendly. In fact, the barista seemed a bit impatient with me while I was deciding how to make my however-you-want-it Frappuccino.   Overall, nothing too noteworthy, but does the job.

If you want slow service with warm coffee this is the location. Miss Nadeine doesn't care if there's a line or not. She's going to get her conversation out! Waited for literally 3 minutes before she finally finished with her conversation and finally place her friend order.  All that talking she was doing, she also got the person order wrong and the person was now also mad at her SMH.

It's a starbux so its the usual - good quality coffee, consistent.  I'm here in the evening & it's not crowded, but steadily busy-no waiting.   Had a cranberry bliss bar that didn't seem to be quite as good I remembered, not sure if it's my memory or it just wasn't as good.   But hey I like starbux, & this one is as good as others- but no  special "hi, how are ya?" 's
This is a okay Starbucks located in the Silver Legacy Casino, however they ran out of breakfast items during my stay. The baristas had a bit of attitude also because they are not a corporate store.

I really like Starbucks. It's convenient, reliable, and consistent... usually lol. I travel often and it's a great place for a small meal or a caffeinated beverage. Through my travels and daily life I've been to Starbucks all over the USA, literally. 22 states? And I must say this is the most unreliable, inconsistent, ball-dropping Starbucks I've ever been to. How do you screw an order up every single day? HOW DO MESS UP A STANDARD COLD BREW WITH SWEETENER - TWICE?  Recently I've been in the area helping family and working, so I have come here about 2-3 times a week for about a month. Of that I'm guaranteed to have at least - AT LEAST 2 botched orders. The line is normally 13-15 cars long (yes really) so you think "I know, I'll order ahead!"  WRONG lol. Best way to waste all your time and mess up your order. You actually wait longer! For real!  This has happened a few times.. you're standing in line, which is of course in front of a glass window.. and half the time customer service rep just helps whoever walks right up to the door. Really lol? YOU EVEN HAVE SPECIAL STARBUCKS TAPE OUTLINING THE WAITING LINE WITH BOXES FOR WHERE TO STAND. AND *YOU* CANT GET THAT RIGHT AS EMPLOYEES? Hahaha wow.   Over the last month I've had: -6 mobile orders out of an item, once 2 of 3 -sizes of the beverages incorrect 2x -missed items from my order  -COLD FOOD THAT SHOULD BE HOT 4X -orders made wrong, like an iced coffee and a cold brew  Hey look I get it, stuff happens. It's a constantly busy location. These are crazy times. but it's a Starbucks, not an international space station.   Best part of all? Sometimes you don't even get a simple "I'm sorry". It's just a shoulder shrug and the standard 'oh we're out of this or that. Or I forgot this my bad. 1 time and 1 time only I was compensated: I was given a free coffee as compensation for waiting 39 minutes lol. WOOOW   Hey corporate- please get control of this store. It's atrocious, it's a disaster, and a terrible representation  of Starbucks.

This is the Starbucks I go to most often.  It's usually very busy, and the space is L-shaped and narrow.  t's often hard to get a place to sit down inside, and there's plenty outside, if that works for you.  It usually doesn't for me.  Why am I giving this Starbucks four stars?  Good service and good location.  I often go to Trader Joe's to shop afterwards.  It's often on the way to or from home, and I came here for trenta-sized Passion iced teas to counteract dehydration during cancer treatment.
Barista this morning doesn't know how to fill the cup. $4.00 for coffee, it should be full. In a hurry and no time to wait. Thanks guys! Happy Monday.

My favorite Starbucks. Friendly baristas. My drinks are always well made and staff makes an attempt to know you're name and make friendly conversation. Have experienced the "pay it forward" drive thru train a couple of times here which leads me to believe we have some kind customers and neighbors in this area. I love that even when the line looks crazy it never takes long.
Pretty much the same Starbucks as all others.  Consistent coffee drinks.  Very nice that there is one right there in the hospital.
Chapters gets me here, coffee is nasty and bland, stick to the cold drinks.  -Yelpku #1

I like this Starbucks because its right in the middle of Landsdale town. The staff are friendly, and the only criticism i have is that the seating is a little tight and outkets are hard to find. Better opt to go to the North Wales location if you're looking for space and places to plug in a laptop!   Heres a link to their nutrish info!!  http://www.starbucks.com/menu/nutrition
My fav coffee. Won't go anywhere else for my cup of joe.

I don't even like coffee and i'm horrible at remembering names of drinks and sizes, but because i work at Old Navy early mornings (4/5am), sometimes i need an energy boost. The baristas are ALWAYS nice enough to take a minute to help me find a drink that i might like and give different suggestions. I highly recommend this starbucks location. :)

Rude service at counter. Unfriendly and just miserable greeting. Website says they have unicorn drink yet staff said they didn't ( probably didn't want to make it ). Took forever to get two drinks while 7 employees are standing around. Avoid at all costs.
This Starbucks gets 5 stars because I got like the very last Lilly Pulitzer x Starbucks Swell water bottle here in St. Louis. Yay me!
Love the Starbucks, the staff is super friendly they always know what I want. Green Tea Latte Soy no classic yum!!!!!!

I needed a late afternoon fix and slipped into this store because it is the closest to friends with whom I hoped to connect for dinner. There was an unbelievable downpour and it seemed prudent to get off the road and "just chill!"  This Starbucks is like hundreds I've been to all over the country -- cozy, modern, busy and predictably, friendly.  I didn't experience anything above and beyond and I didn't observe anything Starbucks wouldn't be proud of... What I saw was exactly what Starbucks set out to create:  a comfortable refuge from the rest of the world where you can get a good cuppa Joe, maybe something to nosh, and either get stuff done or "just chill" with (or without) friends.

Service is fine...but the quality of their drinks are mediocre at best. I would NOT recommend getting their iced coffee. I've tasted better iced coffee when I made it myself at a hotel. Their iced lattes are ok, but not worth the price. Only satisfying drink I've had there has been the pink drink. But..way too much ice, even when I asked for light ice. I never bought food from there, because I always saw flies buzzing around the food in the display case. Unsanitary much? And my final thought applies to Starbucks overall: their drinks should be cheaper. Or, at least make the size difference between a tall and a grande greater.

Abysmal service. Will never go here again. They are inside the Sheraton Hotel and apparently feel that is all they need to get a steady stream of customers. There are a lot of other coffee shops in the area.
I just want to say thank you to the gentleman working the drive through today at 1:51 on 1/21/2019. Small gestures go a long way. YOU ARE A BADASS SIR!

The drive through was hanging out the entrance area and another car was trying to exit so we just had my husband drop us off  so he could get gas while we got my daughter coffee. It's a very crammed location, under a dozen tables and not much space for a spot of 4 grannies to get their order in, but very fast service with a crew of all smiles on a Black Friday..

My little sister and I frequent this location and we love it. We always go through the drive thru and we have never had a problem. The people who work the drive thru are extremely polite and nice. Great job to whoever does the hiring there. The coffee is delicious as it is most Starbucks. Quick and friendly service every time always equals a 5 star review from me! Great job!

Terrible service by Barista too busy chatting. Manager was rude! The place was empty and I've been served quicker in extremely busy Starbucks.  I overheard the barista say 'it's my last day' so hopefully things might improve but I'm hugely surprised by the managers attitude because they are usually so good in Starbucks!  My drink was wrong but it wasn't even worth mentioning!!

Always a pleasure to get a cool drink or some warm coffee related drink from Starbucks can't go wrong when they have what you're looking for lol... I understand that all of the shortages are causing problems for their business as well as others so I can't complain.

The drinks are always made correctly, even when substitutions are made. the staff is generally friendly, with the exception of a few discontent  personalities. I'm in here quite frequently, and have to say that the biggest downfall for this place is that the staff doesn't really seem to care that the line is out the door. The wait is generally obnoxious  and sometimes not even an option for me. I've been into MANY Starbucks over the years that are just as busy, if not much busier, and have not experienced such a wait...EVER. I don't know if perhaps the baristas get overwhelmed, or of the store is significantly understaffed, but this wait time is unreasonable. Many days I've taken the longer walk to the other end of the mall to get Dunkin Donuts, because the 15 min round trip walk is still faster than waiting for sometimes 30 mins or more.

Oh man, it was way too early in the morning when I rolled in here to take a picture while driving back from Las Vegas to Portland. It's a pretty standard Starbucks with a drive-through window that gives the standard Starbucks fare. It is exactly like any of the other million Starbucks across the United States although the staff was slightly friendlier than usual. Certainly more friendly than they had any reason to be at such a god-awful early time in the morning. Anyway the coffee is fine.  The only other piece of information to note of interest is that if you're coming off of the freeway to this Starbucks, you will not be able to directly get back on the road toward the freeway. You will need to exit from the shopping center it is in and make a U-turn regardless of which exit you take.

Starbucks is overall a great spot because you can not only grab coffee but you can order Frapp, hot chocolates, lemonade, food items plus utilize Wifi!!!! Plus if you have a Starbucks card you can obtain points for each of your purchased items.

So everything about my coffee and the majority of the service was fine, except that the cashier doesn't know how to do basic math. My bill came to $11 and change. I handed her $22. She turns around and throws two dollars into the tip jar and breaks my $20 for my coffees.  Somebody better go back to math class.

Let me first say that I have not yet been to a Starbucks that wasn't super busy but this one is ridiculous.   However,  it is to be expected because of it's close proximity to the Westgate Brandon Mall, the AMC movie theater and the I-75 exit.  When they first opened they were extremely slow getting orders out, especially for the drive thru.   I thought I might run out of gas while waiting.  They have gotten a little better with time but still too slow.   Keep in mind the location if you plan to go to this one.   You WILL wait.
I drove through this Starbucks chain. My order was right. Coffee was tasty like yum.  Did I just 'bad haiku' this review?

This Starbucks is one of the worst Starbucks I've ever been to in my life. The cashier was super rude and couldn't hear me. He had to ask me several times for my order and I said it loud and clear. Didn't even bother giving me a receipt either. Do not go to this Starbucks, there is a better one literally 5 minutes away.

I've been studying at this location for a couple years now while also occasionally checking out other cafes/coffee bars and I always end up coming back here. This Starbucks has an awesome layout when it comes to seating but more importantly, the staff here are amazing. Small gestures like remembering to get me a cup of water with my coffee even though I always forget to ask, or patiently talking me through what comes inside a Pmkin Spiced Latte without rushing me out the way, will definitely make you feel appreciated and welcomed. Thank you!

Stopped in at 11:30a. Not busy. Quick service by pleasant staff. This is a small store with strictly table & chair seating (no sitting area) serving the reliably consistent high quality Starbucks products. As I'm watching my diet, I had The very good Cold Brew iced coffee and low fat turkey and egg white sandwich.  I was very happy with both.

Hellooooo.... renovation!! I walked into this location last week after taking a break from my expensive Starbucks habit. When did the interior change? I love it! It feels more open and airy. Very woodsy feeling too.  I try to avoid this Starbucks before 8am and during the lunch hour, because it is always waaaaaayyy busy. If you use the order ahead function on your Starbucks app - that helps immensely.   So why the three stars? They always, always run out of food items like the protein boxes. Just keep more in stock, m'kay?
Great service!  I had a birthday treat that didn't show up on my card and the friendly barista gave it to me, no questions asked!  I appreciate such a friendly face and a nice welcoming attitude.
Service was great and my drink is delicious. I order a Java Chip. Probably the best one I've had. The man with the beard working the drive-thru was very friendly. Overall great experience.
What can I say I am a sucker for a good latte. Today it was on ice. This is a new Starbucks. Staff were friendly.

The service here was the best I've had at a Starbucks in a while. The guy behind the counter was quick and didn't give me the funny look I usually get when I don't order coffee or tea. (I can't have caffeine in my drinks and have gotten paranoid about ordering decaf after several disastrous experiences elsewhere). He was more than happy to ring through my bottle of water and banana loaf. This location is particularly bright and spacious. There are plenty of seating options, even though on this particular day two older ladies had five bags of clothing unpacked across all the couches. So, why the three stars? One of the things I used to love about Starbucks were the baked goods. Every time I get a piece of cake or banana bread it's starting to go stale, and for the prices they charge, that really shouldn't be the case. It still tastes good, but I like my cakes and breads to be moist and not hard and dry at the edges. They haven't lost a customer over it, but it is something that I hope they work on for the future.

I came to this Starbucks this morning and was trying to find the cheapest coffee I could so I could go to work 30 minutes later. The lady behind the counter saw I was kinda off and asked if she could make me a drink of her own. I agreed, 2 shots of espresso 2 pumps of vanilla an 1 pump of hezelnut and I filled the rest of the cup with creme. I wish I remembered her name,, she made me the best coffee ever.
This is one Starbucks that completely fails to deliver on the brand experience Howard Schultz set out to bring to Americans years ago. On top of that, my beverage wasn't that great.

I like my coffee black,hot, and served with a smile. I am never disappointed by this Starbucks.  It doesn't matter if it's morning, noon, or night. They are always friendly and they always do their best to make you feel at home.    I know Starbucks is big on customer service anyhow but I just wanted to give a bigger shout-out to this particular place.   You really know the true nature of a business, and its employees, when you go there often.  These guys are super.  Especially love the always upbeat Christian and Cristina show (I hope I'm spelling their names correctly) in the mornings.  They are a great happy start to any day--and of course a superb cup of coffee!

I've always enjoyed stopping by this Starbucks. The ladies are always so nice and their customer service is excellent - definitely an extra perk in the morning when I'm still groggy before having my coffee!

How much is a cup of Grande coffee exactly?  I stopped here for a refill and after the long line of waiting, the waitress charged me 2.6 for a grande (refilled) coffee ? So even it is not a refill, why I am getting it 2.24 else where... god , if I'm not too tired to argue and the line is still too long....  don't know what's the feeling. Thought it should be a comfortable rest stop. Now I almost felt asleep waiting in line.. and btw, tried to order drink on app, the stupid map on the app only find UD Starbucks the closest.. ahhh ...

Everyone at this establishment is so incredibly happy and energetic and friendly whenever I come in! I'm from Michigan but I visit franklin for a week almost every month and even though I'm gone for a month at a time the ladies and gentlemen baristas still remember my name and my drink order! As a barista myself I know how hard it can be to always maintain a smile while working behind the counter but these baristas never seem to have a problem keeping their smiles and attitudes happy and friendly! Definitely my favorite Starbucks I've ever been to!
The employees were so loud and careless for any of the customers studying or reading... Horrible ambiance. I suggest just grabbing ur beverage and leaving.
They are all very rude and not nice i've never had a good experience ever going into here. They also look like they are hating life.

Incredibly slow service.  Under staffed or staff doesn't pay attention to the customers unless it is a mobile order.  Allow about 20 to 30 minutes if you stop for a coffee and even longer if you are having a drink made or getting something to eat.  I would recommend going to one of the Starbucks in a store at the Chemical road shopping center.
The unnecessary wait time at this Starbucks undermines the excellent service normally provided at other locations.

The staff here is actually not bad. This is a starbucks in a target so you have to have an open mind. You get what you get with no other Starbucks in the close vicinity. They do close earlier than their listed hours on the website. They staff shut the lights off 30 minutes before the listed closing time. Had to go get a smoothie from the cafe instead. Which was pretty mediocre.

I used the Starbucks App for the first time to order ahead, and my coffee was waiting for me! The staff here are all super friendly and nice! Whenever you want to try something new, they are very helpful with finding your next favorite beverage!

My favorite Starbucks EVER! I come here at least once a week and the service is great. They recently remodeled the bar area and I love that there is more seating. The parking lot is shared by a publix so there is always plenty of parking. This location does have a drive thru and also a large outdoor patio area. This location is very Military friendly and will give you a discount if you are active duty!
Don't let the long line fool you in the morning. These cats are fast! Seriously, a line of cars that wraps around the building takes less than 5 minutes to clear. Very impressive.

i go to this starbucks all the time and haven't really had any insane issues that would make me want to write a yelp review but girl what is this lmao. i ordered a cold brew with cinnamon almond milk foam, and i can tell you one thing it doesn't have an impenetrable later of cinnamon on top

For anyone who drinks Starbucks more than a few times per week, we all know how precise and proficient they are. Unfortunately, this specific Starbucks is not. They have longer than usual wait times (6-10 mins online ordering), the drinks are never right or never taste the way they should. Yes, the staff is friendly, but not efficient. If you can avoid it, please save your money and go to one closer!
I really feel they have fallen off so much! Now you get average product for the same inflated prices.

The system was down today and couldn't take money. That's okay. They gave me my coffee and did so with a smile. The lady ahead of me was a complete douche. She was all cranky about it. That's okay douche lady. I tipped them $10 and will continue to go to this Star Bucks. Minus one virtual star for not having a drive through in Safeway for my lazy moments. Plus one virtual star for the employees being fabulous! I love you to the moon and back coffee. I mean Starbucks.

[933]  6/16/2019  This Starbucks is located inside the Philadelphia Premium outlets.  No drive thru  This place is packed most of the times when we visit.   There are outside tables which is nice.    Staff is nice and drink was good   I'll be back
Pretty empty for a Starbucks at 8am. But a larger sitting area than most and they have outdoor seating.
This is a super normal Starbucks. Nothing really pro or con except he outdoor seating is quite nice.

Brand new location located on Carruthers Parkway. Great service everyone welcomed upon entering the store seems as just opened a few weeks ago. Service was very very good everyone is very knowledgeable. Lots of smiles around the counter very technically sound barista's. Great new location off of brothers Parkway located the chicken salads to check. I would highly recommend this to any of the people that have to take Carruthers into cool Springs.

Teams cans all overflowing ... Six workers... I was the only one in line. Told them about the trash and 10 minutes later, with no additional customers... Trash still overflowing. One trash can was directly in the view of the counter staff. Skip this place, find another coffee shop.

Your standard run of the mill Starbucks. The staff are friendly and the baristas are good at what they're doing. I came during a pretty slow hour so I got my cold brew instantly. Everything else you needed at the side counter for sugar and cream were in stock and organized which is usually a rare case. Definitely a convenient location for people staying at the Marriott hotel.

Love, love, love, LOVE this location.  99% of the time I use the drive through, and am greeted with a perky voice in the box and a smile in the window. Obviously we all know Starbucks prices sort of suck, but when that grande iced skinny vanilla late touches my lips, I know it was all worth it (lol).  Anyway when I did go inside I was surprised by the nice decor and comfy seating! Also has some of the fastest wifi around! No joke!  I will say the layout of the parking lot kind of sucks, so if you park and go inside you better not be in a hurry because chances are your car will be blocked in by everyone in the drive through lane.

Small Starbucks with a connecting convenience store. Every time that I've gone, they have plenty of space to study and sit and the line is non-existent later in the evening compared to the other cafes around Penn and Drexel around the same time. Friendly workers and quick service.

We came early morning on Mother's Day pouring Rain drive thru. They were top Notch no issues and we even asked for Drinks,Pumpkin Bread , and gift card. The staff was friendly and efficient even on the holiday! Cheers to your staff!

I work down the street from this Starbucks and come here somewhat often. It's always pleasant, fast enough, and they make the coffee exactly how I ask :) I'm actually writing this review though because I want to specifically sing the praises of Ali, who made my day last week!!! It had been a really rough and stressful week and sleep was lacking. I drove by here to get an afternoon coffee so I could make It through the rest of my to do list. After I ordered my coffee and was waiting in line, I looked into my purse only to discover that I had left my wallet at home! I thought I just might lose it considering the week I had had, this was just the cherry on top of things! I knew when I got up to the from they would probably roll their eyes and be frustrated that they had made my coffee and now I could not pay for it. When I got up to the window I told Ali that unfortunately I would have to come back because I had left my wallet behind. She was so sweet about the whole thing! She told me that I could go ahead and take the coffee, and I could just get them next time. I feel like this is so rare in big chain places these days, and there was no reason whatsoever that she had to do that, she just simply went out of her way to do something nice and she honestly made my day! Starbucks is lucky to have Ali :)

This store has been slipping for a long time but I feel like I need to write a review with my terrible experience today. I spent literally not exaggerating 20 minutes in the drive-through waiting to get to the window. She took my credit card but did not tell me the total and handed the card back to me with no receipt. I know this is the modern age and a lot of people pay with their phones but I don't and I expect to be told the total and be given a receipt which they did not do. I finally had to ask for the total. When I drove away I realized that the drink was made incorrectly. Am I going to go back and get another one? No, I don't want to wait in line again.  The store needs some serious up training and some help in the organization.

This Starbucks location is the best location I have ever been to. Being vegan, Starbucks is the only major coffee corporation that I have encountered that has options for someone like me. What makes this location stand out from all of the rest is their positive and caring staff. Amelia in particular, has always gone above and beyond, knowing my voice in the drive through line before I even order. She never fails to disappoint me, and if I'm having a bad day she can always lift my spirits by her kind words or by writing me a note on my coffee cup. I'm always leaving here with a perfectly brewed coffee and a smile.
This is the first time I have ever posted a negative review, but this is the worst...Starbucks....ever. Extremely slow service, rude and incompetent employees.
Terrible service - SLOW!!!  Unless you're retired or on vacation, not a good idea.  Not up to Starbucks standards.

Out of the 3 locations I frequently visit, this is the only one I ever have an issue with. This time I waited 20+ minutes for one drink and it wasn't even busy. after the 15 minute mark I asked one worker if my drink was coming up and she told me it was next. Another minute or two goes by and the man who rang me up asked if I was still waiting and I told him yes. He looks and says someone must have given it to the wrong person. It took him all of 30sec to make it and I was out the door. The two female workers were complaining about being short staffed and such. Not only did they give away my drink but then the one lied about it being next. I work in the restaurant industry and this was not the way to handle the situation.

This is a busy location with a drive through. Parking can be an issue in the busy 7-9am window. Orders can take a while in the morning due to volume of orders, but you can order online and then pick up. Staff are friendly, and they do make tasty drinks!

It's a Starbucks. What makes this one different from all the other Starbucks, is that they are slow as molasses that has been kept in the ice box. I don't even get complicated drinks.
