I order cold brew, black and it still takes forever. However, they do provide me with a laugh. They can't spell my name right. I could understand if they spelled it with a ph instead of the v, but here they spell it with a b. The last poor barista who tried to call my name out had no idea how to pronounce Steben.  I give him credit, he politely asked how to pronounce it. Since the v & b are next to each other on the keyboard, I just smiled and told him it was a typo.   This Starbucks is in my hood, so I'll continue to support them. I'll just take a deep breath before walking in and make sure my patience switch is flipped to the on position.

Idk. Any joy I get from visiting this Starbucks diminishes at the annoying light that takes forever wish I could chose a different way out, but not so.  The store is nice and quick, an friendly, everything I enjoy about the brand, its just I don't want to visit because of the hassle to get out of there.  What this intersection needs is a pretty round about. That way everyone keeps moving and doesn't have to wait forever at that damn light.  Maybe I'll go again and try to get out another way. Ughhh. Im getting old !!!
Very friendly lady behind the counter. Sadly I had to add water to my beverage once on my flight b/c it was too strong....

This is the only Starbucks that always gets my order wrong. Was just there and ordered a Coffee Frap with Java chips. Instead I get a Java chip frap and I repeatedly ask if that was a coffee frap and they continued to say yes. It was so chocolaty I had to dump it. Well either the people need some more training on how to make drinks or they need to clean their ears out so they can listen better. Thanks for making me waste my $7.

This is probably the worst Starbucks I've ever been to.  It's a shame that this is the one I now live closest to, because I frequent Starbucks.  But some of the employees here are so rude that it makes me never want to come back.  I have been coming to this Starbucks fairly regularly for over a year now.  Orders are frequently forgotten/botched, drinks have been poorly made on more than one occasion (I'm talking the same old crappy ice in an iced coffee refill or very little ice in a venti latte, to the point where you take a swig of warm latte), and some employees are downright rude.  If I had experienced this poor service one or two or three or four times over the course of a year then fine; that can happen anywhere.  But I'm talking cross your fingers...you don't know what you're going to get when you come here.  You get a bad experience about 30% of your visits here.  It's important to note that this rudeness does not apply to every employee at this location, however.  I have experience the entire staff there and a handful are nice, cheerful people who do not give the customer any attitude.  Many, though, are the exact opposite.  Unacceptable.

Love Starbucks.   Hate the location of this store. While I love that it is in walking distance from my work, the location is awful. Parking is horrible and there honestly isn't enough space.  It is a smaller shop, but food and drinks hit the spot.  I do feel it takes a little longer to get items ordered, but this location is always booming.  I don't think I've ever seen it slow down, it's almost always packed inside. There is no drive thru at this location

This morning was the worst experience that I have ever had at any Starbuck! I ordered tall coffee 1/2 regular and 1/2 decaf plain black coffee and put it in a larger cup and fill the rest of the cup with hot water! After four attempts she still couldn't get it correct. I was so embarrassed for her and so tired of me trying to explain it that I took what she gave me and left. I didn't order anything extra to be in my cup except those three items, just decaf and regular coffee with hot water. I walked out with a cup of watered down coffee, so weak! Maggie of Harahan
Always busy, and usually pretty quick service. The parking lot is too small, but that's not their fault. Thank goodness for drive-through!

Yay...finally a Starbucks open late and SUPER close to work! This night owl is happy. Wish it was a 24/7. I've been twice now and each time great service and delicious coffee. I love the local Felicitous near by too but this is closer and they make it so easy to grab a coffee in a hurry!  Felicitous needs an evil drive thru...but this is about the new Sbux...It's great.
I love this location, the only downside is the parking lot. It can be difficult to get in and out when there's a long line in the drive-thru.

Spanking new Starbucks, open less the a week. It has everything you expect from a Starbucks, and that's good... people are friendly and helpful  Coffee is good, cold brew is on tap..... Big store with plenty of seating both inside and outdoors They have a drive thru as well...

Quick service, must be the time of day usually they a little slower. Quiet atmosphere to work on my studies. The people here ( employees and customers ) are really friendly. This is becoming one of my favorite hang outs.
The flies!  Please please please put up a fly trap or something inside.  Love this place, but it's annoying.

Plenty of seating, great coffee and pastry, and strong wifi. Oh, and friendly and efficient staff here. Great way to start the day when you are exploring Reno and the surrounding areas. My wife and I were in town for four days exploring and looking at area real estate. This Starbucks inside our casino was very convenient. Yes, there were lines in the morning, but consistent morning kickstarts were what we were looking for (and needed) so this location was worth the wait. Kyle took great care of us today 8/14/16 and was very friendly and efficient when I ordered my venti non fat mocha WITH whipped cream. Ironic...I get it. Thanks for the morning food and beverage Kyle!
I've been here for 30 min. and the staff have ignored me standing here. This is not the first time. There's really only one staff member here that has good customer service.

This USED to be my "regular" place. I loved the DT window.  Well I moved daycares and started going to the one on Hazel Dell.  Annnnnnyway, fast forward two years and I have missed this one so much and recently relocated daycares AGAIN so now I'm back in that area and so this is my regular stop again.  Like everyday - every.single.day.  Somehow, they "never" really recognize me (and I drive a car that is wrapped in the Caribbean Sea, sand and Sandals), order the same thing and somehow, they still often mess up my drink and are not what I consider to be "friendly" at all.  It's very disappointing.  I am actually almost OVER Starbucks because of THIS location and the OTHER location on 116th (at Keystone). They both suck.  Because I need a DT and because I love espresso, I'll continue to spend my $5, but it's annoying me more and more each day so I yelped it.  That's all folks.

My coworkers and I were hosting a meeting at the Westin by the baseball stadium but there was no coffee shop in our hotel so we walked on over to the Starbucks in the Hilton, located only a short walk away, to get our caffeine fix.   It appears to share a space with a frozen yogurt shop, so where to enter Starbucks is a bit confusing. There was signage for both the Starbucks and the fro-yo joint, so it was not clear. We ended up walking into the front of the hotel and in that way rather than through the outside entrance which is where we ended up exiting after we had our coffee.   The staff was super friendly, helpful, and quite efficient. Our drinks were made within minutes, and we were on our way! They tasted perfect and were just what we needed for an early morning pick-me-up. I highly recommend the location!

I came here when I was waiting for my oil to get changed in hopes of getting some studying done. It was freezing inside, there was reggae music blasting, and the kid working the register didn't acknowledge me for a couple minutes. I got my drink and headed outside to find a table, but outside there is no lighting (fair enough). I am not a huge Starbucks fan, and usually only go there out of convenience but there is definitely a "Starbucks standard" which this location fails to meet.
Bacon, egg, and cheese, grande Carmel frapp, and a cake pop... No better way to start your morning. And since I'm a regular they always have more order down and ready to go!

This Starbucks is a perfect example of what happens without corporate or proper supervision... Either the staff or the manager has this figured out... the way to get out right at closing time is make it so unbearable to be in the store that everyone leaves... It's the evening of the Summer Solstice Parade... State Street is packed with people and yet this Starbucks is empty! Why? It's freezing in there...  One of the biggest complaints is that Music is so loud... there are tables galore and many electrical outlets... (One of which two months after reporting that someone broke off their plug in the outlet, is still not repaired).  But to be in there more than 5 minutes... you will need a bottle of Excedrin I am sorry... but management this store is a fail...  The people smile and talk with you... but if you ask to use the bathroom they will say (and I'm not kidding here)... even if you're the only one in there they will ask have you purchased anything? are you a customer! There is a young mgr there he thought this was funny after I said that Yes I have been a customer here for some time... figuring as we have talked a few times he would remember me... but Nope! I understand that transients use restrooms and leave them a mess... but I'm sorry... I'm 59 yrs old and I do not look like a street person.  I love the vast majority of Starbucks... but I would not recommend this spot at all.
I always love to get a Grande, Vanilla Bean and a Venti, Black Ice Tea.  It's a great place to study and do homework after school.

It should be a must-go place for all the international students at Wash U who live in Delmar. It is an international chain and most of the items are standardized. You can guess it even if you don’t understand the menu. The egg and ham burgers for breakfast are good.

7/25/17 Came back a little over a year later. Same location, same time, much improved experience. Service was great.  6/9/16 The Gate A location... literally the slowest Starbucks I've ever seen... literally. It took longer to get coffee from that Starbucks at 5:30 AM than it did to get through TSA. Did you get that? It took longer for their "baristas" to make espresso than it did for the federal government to make sure I wasn't bringing a bomb on the plane.

Nice Starbucks with outside seating. Too bad they don't have a drive thru like the one at Linebaugh and Dale Mabry, but this is in a strip mall and not a free standing building. They currently have a deal until May 10th where from 3-6pm, Frappucinos are half off! There is a new frappuccino called the "S'mores". I sadly was underwhelmed. I LOVE s'more flavored things. This had a burnt marshmallow taste and it was way too sweet and not frozen enough. I'd be willing to give it another try with another barista though. It was busy and the girl seemed overwelmed and "in the weeds".

My 300th review and it's for a Starbucks?  Are you kidding me? No up and coming restaurant? No killer new NY Style pizzeria? A Starbucks? Seriously?  Yup.  It's been that kinda month.  My boss LOVES Starbucks so when we get together for early morning meetings, we have been getting together at this beautiful,brand new Starbucks in Brookhaven.  The location is perfect - right there in the new Giant Supermarket plaza, next to the AT&T store and the Smashburger,closest to Middletown Road.  This is one of the biggest Starbucks in the area. We love the large tables but there is plenty of seating all around the coffee shop. Wifi is free and it connects quickly to all my wifi eating devices: pad, phone,laptop.  The coffee is typical Starbucks. I am more of a Dunkin Donut's coffee guy but here there are three choices of regular to blend into the largest cup they have - that one cup lasts for the whole meeting.   There are many more coffee choices as well as some food choices as is typical of a Starbucks.

In the past this Starbucks has been nothing but drama for me. Today was a completely different experience. The baristas were ridiculously friendly and the service was wicked fast. The order accuracy has always been a triumph here.   I usually find the cafe orders to be quite slow. It's possible that there is a completely new team. It's possible there's a new manager. I don't know what it is but boy it's like a whole new store. The drive thru is usually more reliable for speed.   As of September 30, 2020 they have recently reopened the cafe for some socially distant seating. The restrooms are still closed for guests.

You think you've seen one Starbucks, you've seen 'em all, right?   Wrong.   This is absolutely the fastest coffee shop I have ever been to.   On a busy Wednesday morning, there were 2 cashiers and 2 (maybe 3) baristas moving at warp speed.   I ordered a coffee and a breakfast sandwich, and before I even put my wallet back in my pocket, the cashier was handing me my coffee.   Then I walked around to the sugar/cream station and as soon as I had put the lid back on top of my coffee, I heard "slow-roasted ham & swiss!" and was out the door.   Total time inside: less than 2 minutes.   Amazing.

Excellent Starbucks location! Everyone is friendly and they always have a smile on their face. They have a pretty good selection of bakery items and sandwiches to go along with your coffee or tea. I recommend trying their Banana Nut Bread, it's quite tasty.
Amy was completely rude to me and the lady in front of me. If I didnt live so close I wouldnt come back. Other ladies were nice except Amy.

went there again to give them Another try.. outside in the customer seats sat a street person asking for patrons going in and out for money.  Inside I was second in line, barrista took the order from person in front of me, then as I approached register she turned around and made Herself a cup of tea and checked her smartphone for messages , really not caring I was in line. Never again at that or any Starbucks locations

It's a Starbucks. They are all the same, that's a good thing. When you go there, you know what to expect, Starbucks coffees, friendly service etc.  It has a big outdoor seating area.. inside is small and a little cramped. No drive thru.

I am a palled at Starbucks partnering with a gentleman to approach people for money and donations for bully and for some school timberline I can't believe they're allowing this to go on I feel like donations have been becoming more aggressive and forceful and I don't agree with it I don't see why that worker can't just ask if they'd like to donate when they pay for their coffee I would recommend nobody going there

I've been here a few times, all in the morning hours. The drinks and food have been on par with other Starbucks in the area. There are 8-10 spots to sit and eat, study, etc. Seems fairly well maintained. Service is a little slower than other stores in the chain. I have never gotten my coffee drink in under 10 mins.

This location took forever to serve a cold nitro brew, which I don't understand since all they have to do is to pour it.  This should not have to wait for the barista.  The guy who took the order should be able to get this rather then stock shelves as he did.  Then when we got on the road I found that it wasn't even cold but warm, which is pretty disgusting.  $5 for a small warm coffee that is supposed to be cold.
I was blown away by how friendly the Team was. The store is super clean. Tommy. Adorable. And amazing customer service.  The Barista Alexandra reminded me of the good ole days. Awesome.

I mean it's Starbucks! Need I say more lol. Came here for a guava green tea lemonade. The line was so long and took what seemed like forever to get thru. When we finally got up to order, they didn't have lemonade! Ugh. Oh well I just ordered the drink without the lemonade and it was still good. Staff was friendly and got our order right.

DO NOT EVERY GO TO THIS STARBUCKS if you intend to enjoy yourself and have a nice conversation or study or do anything else for that matter.  There are these obnoxious assholes that hang around there every single day.  They are Armenian?? Russian??.  I don't know what they are saying but they scream at the top of their lungs.  They are very disruptive and rude. They block the walk way where people are trying to get by.  I asked the lady there if she could tell them to quiet down so I could study.  No dice.  They literally shout when they talk and could care less how they affect the people around them.  It is worse than a biker bar.    I've seen them chase dozens of people out of there with their rude, disruptive behavior and foul language, and the people that work there could care less.  They cuss in English for some reason??  Worst Starbucks I've ever been to, and I've been all over the country.    If you are going to get your coffee in the morning to go, you'll probably be fine.  But, if you are intending to sit down and relax.  Forget about it!  Ain't gonna happen.

I have never been in such a loud and uncomfortable Starbucks. Not only were they blasting hip-hop/rap at full volume. They decided to ignore my request to turn it down and make rude smirks from behind the counter. it made me incredibly uncomfortable and unsatisfied I traveled across town only to spend 6$ on a shit experience and disrespectful customer service.

This Starbucks is the where it's at Starbucks. It is smack dab in the middle of downtown St. Pete and has competition from three pretty close ones, but that doesn't seem to be a problem, for business is always booming.

So I've been to this Starbucks a bunch of times over the last 5 years. I got two iced drinks today and some food for my friend and I. We changed the milk option from 2% to Whole Milk. I took a few sips of my iced chai tea latte, and I was immediately sick to my stomach, and I stopped drinking it. My friend drank some of his iced coffee (way more than I did of mine), and his stomach is also hurting and is very sick right now, up and down to the bathroom non stop. The drink is still sitting in my fridge right now still because I've been in bed for the last 4 hours since we got Starbucks, throwing up a lot and having the worst stomach pains and bathroom problems. I did not eat any of the food and I'm absolutely 100% sure it was the Whole Milk in our drinks. It was the only similar thing we both had and now we're both very sick. I've never had a Starbucks drink bother me until now.
Very friendly staff and they don't mess up your order! I highly recommend this starbucks over the other ones!

Kelly is the best barista at this location and I believe she's the best barista overall for Starbucks. But darcy the short hair small girl.... I can't even. One time I ordered a iced Carmel macchiato it was made totally wrong, I understand the drink is mostly milk but this particular time there was a lot more milk than usual. The drink should be brown but it looked like straight up milk and I told her there's too much milk but she went on to argue with me that it was made right. And another incident she gave me back my change and the coins fell out of her hand when I was trying to grab the bills with it but instead of putting it in my hand she just threw the rest of my change in the tip jar when I was picking up the ones that were on the counter. I don't have a problem with giving tip but don't just assume that's what I was doing. She definitely ruined my past couple experience going to my neighborhood coffee shop.

Rudest Starbucks ever! You say how are you and their too much in a rush to reply! Drinks are always messed up. Employees never in uniforms or aprons. Always out of items and things. Employees are mean! Not sure why some still work here how they've treated customers.

All of the partners (employees) are so kind at this location! Caetlin made me the BEST dirty chai I have ever had! All the baristas are incredibly friendly and wonderful - I 100% recommended this Starbucks location!

There is no worse Starbucks... The drones that work here have no clue of what customer service is. It is 8:00am and I've walked into this store when there was only one car in the drive-thru and I am the only person standing at the counter. Four drones are behind the counter talking to each other through the headsets they are wearing and appear to be ignorant of my presence at the counter. No smile, no acknowledgement, and no greeting... I'm the only person standing at the counter...  After what seemed like two minutes to me, I couldn't take it anymore, I left and flipped this Starbuck's the bird.  A place to be avoided.

This is my local Starbucks. I tend to hit this place up before heading to work. Since I've been here a 100 times, it's hard for me to really say much about getting in quick, as I'm used to driving around the Pavillion's Center/Walmart parking lot; meaning it's not easy to get in and out of.  The staff always say hello when I walk in. It's always clean and tidy. So far, they have always had the extra's I like to order; protein boxes and salads so I've enjoyed going here. It's not a higher end store, but they seem to get drivers like myself in and out of the drive thru fairly rapidly. It meets my expectations every time.

I know what you're thinking ... "She's reviewing a Starbucks? Everybody knows what Starbucks is like!" Well, you don't know MY Starbucks, do ya? Yeah, that's right ... so shaddup! (please keep reading my reviews)  My Starbucks has more than just the slightly overpriced coffee, and the long counter at the window where you can sit and watch people go by, trying to creep them out with your staring. What, you don't do that?  My Starbucks has three (that's right, THREE) plushy seats in this little semi-private corner, and, get this -- a singing Starbucks girl. I call her that because I don't know her name. She's a very happy person, my singing Starbucks girl. And she doesn't care that everyone can hear her, she just sings whatever. Some ppl may find it annoying, but I like it ... it kind of just blends in with their music.  The coffee-shop also arranges their tables in a way so that not all the tables are smooshed together. I like that too, more privacy for each table. You can write or read whatever without feeling someone is peeking over your shoulder. Now if only they would lower their prices a bit, I could give them a full 5-star rating ... just kidding!

I come here because this place is convenient since it's near everything. Expect a long wait time in the mornings at the drive through. Every time can be anywhere from 15 minute wait. if you want your coffee and are running late, go to Starbucks at Damonte

Slowest Starbucks ever!!! One cashier who had no idea what she was doing. Very long line. They had one person making coffee and four other people that the best I can tell were supervising the coffee maker and the cashier. What a joke! Took almost 50 minutes to get an iced tea with only 10 people in front of me. Avoid at all cost if you have a flight to catch

20 minutes in the drive through at 2:30 on Saturday is ridiculous. Ordered usual green tea and an extra blonde shot of espresso. Tea  was Excellent but shot was cold. There are more than enough Starbucks around to go to. Avoid the highway 19 next to Moe's unless you have a half hour to kill

I have nothing against the drinks or food, but the way they conduct business. The baristas are super sweet, but the lack of attention to all of the different ways of ordering is bewildering. If you are going to offer curbside then make sure you get it done. After waiting 30 min, finally I go in to be told that my order wasn't even noticed by anyone. Waited another however long and still didn't get my sandwich so I left. The physical location already makes this place trash.

Horrible service. If you are not a regular they make you wait. The customers who received their drinks before me actually ordered their drinks after me BUT I saw the man hand the girl a tip... this is so wrong!!! Won't be coming back!!!

My husband and I just drove into town from Tennessee, after 12hr - we decided to pick up a KETO FRIENDLY DRINK. The cashier at the register was extremely friendly, professional and even offered us a sample of one of the most popular drinks. She has been the best Starbucks server ever met, and we drink a lot Starbucks! Thanks to her, whenever we visit, we'll make to stop by!!!
It's annoying every time I go to this Starbucks they put coffee in my caramel ribbon crunch cream. I'm shy and don't like to say anything but it's still annoying
The coffee is standard. The employees at this location are overworked and poorly managed.

In search of a quiet place to work on a weekday morning I decided it was time to check this newer location out. Sadly, I only stayed a few minutes because I had serious trouble finding a seat and the line at the counter never seemed to move.   I'm not sure if it's the shopping center, more people living in the area, or what, but the few experiences I've had with typically predictable national brands in this plaza have not been up to the standards for these businesses.   This location is much bigger and nicer than the location that was previously across the street. The crowd inside was all business too, which is a great indicator of who's living in the area. All that said, the store doesn't seem set up to handle the increased volume they're experiencing. I stood in the line for what felt like 10 minutes and only saw it inch forward. I just wanted a cup of black coffee and a seat to do a little work, but it looked like that could take 15-20 minutes so I went up to the store on S. Virginia by Total Wine (notoriously bad for seating too)  and was seated (with power nearby) in 5 minutes.   Giving three stars for benefit of the doubt that this was just an off day. It certainly isn't what I've come to expect from Starbucks.

I like this Starbucks.  I actually prefer it to the one off of Ina when I am heading out of town and it is my last convenient Starbucks stop before I hit the long drive to Cali.  Always clean, friendly staff who write my name correctly on the cup, clean bathrooms and they never mess my drink up.  Way to go!

I come here often because its right on my way to work, but some mornings it's a headache. About 1/2 the time they seem to get my order wrong or mixed up with someone else's and there's one woman who works here that I swear just hates her job!

I love going to this Starbucks. Every one of the baristas are very nice and accommodating. There are, however, some problems. The other evening was at Starbucks and used the bathroom. Now, I have to say that this is one of the most used Starbucks, just for bathroom use, whether one is a customer or not. When I went into the bathroom, it was obvious that the sink had not been scrubbed. There were layers on layers... That same night, while three people were on, only one was out on the floor serving customers. This particular barista had to do the drinks and the cash register. I image that the others had work in the back room, but it wasn't fun to wait while this poor person had to deal with the orders.   It seems to me that in most stores, the staff is really pressured to get the cleanup done before the store closes. There is mopping (which can be dangerous to customers) and cleaning of restrooms, which, sometimes, are locked for the remainder of the night. The baristas (overall) say that they are treated well by the company, but it might be better to do the closing cleaning after the cafe closes.
OK! So i realized that once i broke down each step of how i wanted my drink (coconut milk mocha macchiato-iced), they actually executed it perfectly!
Worst starbucks in the city...maybe the world. Insanely long waits, no air conditioning. No bathroom (you have to go to the bar across the hall)

I love this place... it is my favorite Starbucks store!!!  I happen to love Starbucks... Terrible thing to admit, I know... But especially at this particular Starbucks, I always get great customer service and the employees always seem to actually ENJOY doing their job. Try finding that at other drive-thru's. The employees are are always very nice and quick to fix any problem I might have. They recognize me by face now--I usually go two or three times a week. I have to say that like most other people, I want to "fight the man" and give the local coffee shops a try. But I can tell you--I'm always disappointed with the other guy's coffee--I guess there's something to be said for big business because Starbucks just simply offers superior coffee. I hate to admit it, but I have yet to find a better cup of coffee anywhere... no matter how hard I try. I did give them only 4 out of 5 stars due to the expensive price. But I guess you get what you pay for.

Cannot believe this location is at a great location and the baristas not so much.  Thought I was in a DD, simple ice tea was all wrong. Got a grande instead of a venti, extra sweet, instead of what I asked, and light ice, when asked for extra ice.

I hope this is the right location. The Starbucks outside security between A and C gates is great. Well, the woman working this morning is great. I arrived at the airport early and stopped in for a coffee and breakfast sandwich before I went through security.   It's early, she's working by herself, and for the 45-ish minutes I was there she was the most positive and helpful Starbucks employee I've encountered in a long time. Multi tasked with the drinks and sandwiches, was kind with people who repeated their orders or checked in on things assuming she had forgot (she hadn't), & was super efficient. A great airport coffee experience

I love Starbucks and  was excited to try a 50 % off macchiato on  Thursday night.  GSR Starbucks is not a corporate store so the offer on my Starbucks card could not be used. Disappointing.  On a better note the store is very nice inside a casino. Plenty of seating.  Great employees.

One of the nicer Starbucks in the West Chester area.  This place gets fairly busy and seating is limited, but the barista's all work very fast.  I find service to be great, and drinks to be consistently made.  A good choice for a local Starbucks!

This is a lovely, clean, spacious SB, located in a great neighborhood (near Trader Joe's!). However, the staff there is so young and uninformed. Perhaps there is too much turn-around and they cannot train employees fast or well enough, because I have had such poor experiences there. They do not know how to answer my questions about drinks. They do not know how to make a return or exchange. They have had to pause the transaction, and call their manager for help. I have also been overcharged twice, and getting it corrected is not worth the time and effort! I learned this the hard way when I waited over 15 mins for an error I discovered on my receipt, all while holding up the line and making all of the customer's behind me very cranky.

I love this Starbucks it's always really fast and all the workers are super nice! Especially the barista Sydney she's recommended so many delicious drinks that I've never tried before, she always took her time to make sure the customer was happy with their order and always promoting a happy attitude. Please promote her she deserves it!
It's my local Starbucks. The staff here is a riot! They are always happy, cheerful, and at times goofy. Today's shenanigans was a HUGE fly swatter in back.

I'm writing a review and a little tidbit from the review prior to mine. This location is wonderful. The crew here is always so nice and helpful and all the drinks are wonderful. Lots of parking and easy on and off access to the freeway.   By the way, Carpinteria has TWO Starbucks... The other location is located in the Vons grocery store located on Linden Avenue.  Check them both out, you get excellent service at both locations

Definitely not the best Starbucks I have ever been to. The first time I ever went there, they got my order wrong. The last time I was there something was definitely off with my drink. When they get it right it's good though. The workers are generally nice but as someone who loves Starbucks, I'm pretty disappointed that this is the one closest to me.

This Starbucks is always super crowded, especially late in the evening. I like that it has lots of seating and now it just put in a community table, but, of course, always filled with students using their laptops. The staff is decent, but not extremely nice. And lastly, it's always freezing. Then again, ALL Starbucks are freezing.

I so badly want to give this location a good review but I have visited too many times and been disappointed-have to say that this Starbucks has such a high turnover and maybe this accounts for their inability to make a consistently good drink, specifically their lattes. I really think it's the lack of training. Almost every other location makes their drinks consistently, but at this one, they either let the espresso shots sit too long ( causing a burnt taste) or fill it overflowing with milk (which dilutes the taste) or serves it lukewarm. They are also 5x slower than any of the other locations in town. Whatever it is they are doing, more times than not, I walk away frustrated and vowing never to go there again.

I just left this location, the service was not friendly at all. We stood there for five minutes with staff right in front of us without so much as any acknowledgement. When the young lady came over, not so much as a smile or any form of pleasantry. The caramel grande latte had no flavor, none of the brûlée topping at all. Just will not be back.
I had the best customer service Leah was amazing! Stopped in for an afternoon pick me up and was greeted with a friendly smile!

DON'T WASTE YOUR TIME OR MONEY!!  I went shopping with my family at the outlets and decided to stop and get my usual caramel frappuccino. After placing my order, I then waited about 20 minutes to get my drink. They proceeded to tell me they are out of whipped cream. The drink came and it was watered down and tasteless. I requested a remake and they did so somewhat willingly and the drink still tasted terrible.

This was a perfectly typical Starbucks, like 100's of others. They provided me the caffeine jolt I so desperately needed and sent me on my way, buzzing around like a fly on meth. Ok, it wasn't that exciting but it hit the spot!
It's consistent, clean, spacious, and friendly staff. What else do you want from a Starbucks. The only pain are the lazy asses who can't park.

This is the second time I have been to this Starbucks and ordered a Vanilla Cream Cold Brew which tastes like it has no vanilla at all. This is always my go to drink in the morning and have never had issues when ordering it anywhere else.

Showed up at 7pm and the place closes at 9pm. ALL the food was pulled, had to wait about 5 minutes for someone to come up from the back, was told they are not making Frappuccinos any more tonight because they already cleaned everything. It's 95 degrees outside and that makes no sense. So my suggestion would be there at least 3-4 hours before they close or else you can't get anything.

This is a great location. I really like it here, and I would love it if they had more seating. I always order to go and my drinks are perfect. If anything isn't 100%, they correct it immediately. The staff are friendly and efficient and the location is pretty clean.  Also, if there is a promotion going and the place is packed, I usually get my order in minutes. Product shelves with their mugs etc are well stocked, plenty of treats and sandwiches, and I don't think I've ever seen them run out of the iTunes freebie cards If you need a place to sit, you may be out of luck. The tables are usually filled with patrons from local businesses having a lunch meeting or students catching up on some projects and collaborations before catching a bus at the transit centre. Always reliable and consistent, this location is a regular stop for me.
I just drove by and how can this place be open during the Corona Virus outbreak? Do better Starbucks. Close your store.

If this were your first experience of the Starbucks franchise, you would walk away neither overly impressed nor depressed.  For those of us who've easily been in dozens of them, the feeling is about the same. In our particular case this week, it was a necessary evil -- we were trying to locate yet another tourist attraction (this sounds like complaining, but trust me, we were having a fabulous time) and the iPhone and its handy GPS were nearly dead, so we ducked into Starbucks for a drink and a good long battery charge.   I'd never had a Starbucks smoothie before and I'll never have one again. It was just not like a 'real' smoothie -- no fruit chunks, no bold fruit flavors, nothin' interesting, and a strong preservative taste to seal the deal. But chances are I'd have said the same thing if I'd gone to any other Starbucks nationwide, so don't take that the wrong way.   The girls behind the counter were LOUD and goofy, but at least they were having fun at work. Plenty of parking out front.

I swear there is nothing like "Starbucks" to me. Yes there are other coffee shops with different styles. Bu when you look at the long haul Starbucks is still at the top. This is 1 of 3 that are near me and I love all 3 of them. The staff here are amazing, the coffee is never off (no matter who makes it for me) Great job training the staff Starbucks. Love it.

I wanted to start by saying the hours here on yelp are not correct. I'm not certain what the hours are but I came through/passed by multiple times between 5-630 am on my way into work and it wasn't open.  I was able to get breakfast here one morning but that was after 7 am. The line moved very quickly but their menu is severely limited. I had to settle on my 3rd breakfast option because they were out of the other 2. I got the feta wrap but it was still frozen cold in the middle which sucked. The good thing is my iced chai latte was very good but took awhile to get made. Would be open to return next time I'm in town.

Very green apron! Nothing to complain about here.  Partners moved fast and were always friendly despite the sour faced rude customers who ordered in-between text messages and ongoing phone conversations.   For those who find their store too small and the their service too slow, trying getting off the phone and look the person behind the counter in the eye when ordering and stop treating them like a barely tolerable app on your phone.

The service here is excellent. The parking is still pretty awful - and now there is a "Super" Wawa next door. Nonetheless, I still come in every month or so for a $5 latte. I love it, it's expensive, I feel guilty when I do it - but I gotta have a starbucks fix every once in a while.

Staff are very friendly, drive thru is quick, and they recently remodeled. The updated layout makes better use of the space & gives it a more open feel. Probably not good for large groups due to space but a very friendly place.

I work near this Starbucks location, so I am there quite often. What I enjoy most, is that there are a few employees that take the time to get to know their regulars, remember their orders, and really just put that extra effort. Even after not visiting for a few weeks, the first day I came back I was greeted by name and my preferred beverage was getting started before they finished ringing up my order. It is that extra effort that makes this Starbucks location stand out from all the others.   As far as food and product go, this location is pretty standard to all Starbucks. The coffee is strong, and the espresso beverages very customizable. They offer the toasted breakfast sandwiches and lunch sandwiches here. The product selection is pretty small since the location itself is quite small. There are only two spots in the store that offer the "comfy seats" but there are many more seats just inside the office building for Starbucks patrons.   The reason I'm not rating five stars, is there is pretty high employee turn over. This effects service, and when the new employees are behind the counter the service slows. But it is that small core of employees who have stuck around that make my trips for my daily caffeine fix very enjoyable.

Ok, so its a Saturday evening at 7:15pm, after a Cardinals home game and this location is closed, WTF????  AND its located in the lobby of a Marriott hotel!! And there are NO signs with their hours posted on any doors, weird!!!  Was VERY disappointed!  Thankfully their competitor,  Kaldi's was open!!

Very busy location with limited parking and the first one west of Philadelphia on Baltimore.  Thank goodness I can order ahead because today, I walked in, saw the line was 8 deep, and ordered on line through the app.   That put me to the front of the line in just a couple of minutes.    Coffee is always fresh here due to the volume.
One of the best Starbucks' in Edmonton! Always consistent in making the drinks, and the most polite staff! This location will NEVER let you down!

Literally the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to in my entire life AND THEY WEREN'T EVEN BUSY!!! There were four freaking baristas working, two people ahead of me in line (one didn't order a drink, just a slice of lemon pound cake) and it still took 20 full minutes to get my tall iced coffee which literally requires no fancy mixing. You just pour the coffee from the pitcher and some cream and viola! I almost asked if I could make it may damn self at one point. So frustrating, every time I come here it's slow. I have to remind myself to not come to this one.

Just had another awesome visit. I used the new Mobile order and Pay app. My order was ready to pickup when I arrived. Awesome! It was delicious... And they have a new beef brisket sandwich that is yummy.i also purchased a small bag of Almond Rocca caramel popcorn. This store is immaculate and the baristas are so friendly.
Kept getting my order wrong. Charged me a second time for one of my drinks. I wouldn't recommend anyone I know to this location.
My all time favorite Starbucks now for over 10 years.  Well trained employees with great customer service every time.  The ambiance at this location is relaxing and inviting.  Love it!

This store is the worst Starbucks that I've been to. There is an employee named Dev. She's one of the most horrible people I've ever encountered in my life. Dev made my drink wrongly and when I politely asked her to remake my drink she flipped out on me and told me I was being rude and ganged up on me with one of her fellow employee. She at last snatched my drink from me, dumped it in the sink, gave me a refund, and told me to get out. I recommend that you avoid this place at all cost. Dev if you read this... Your mom's a two dollar prostitute.

I always stop in this Starbucks when I'm in Tucson. The staff is always on top of things, they have the fast drive thru of any Starbucks I have been too, and they have never gotten my drink order wrong. My only complaint is that they are sometimes out of the spinach wrap I so love.
Drive thru quick and kind. One girl remembers I always want a water with my coffee and has had it ready before I asked!
Very efficient and clean. They are particularly good with the mobile ordering. My order is always ready by the time I get there!
I hope the blonde was fired immediately after refusing to let the officer use the restroom!!!!

Favorite location to go to personally. Although they're always busy (location is right by 69), they always seem to get you in and out quickly.   Tip if you're in a hurry: if the drive-thru is long (it almost always is) it's worth running inside real fast. They're usually not as busy inside and get you out faster! Ordering ahead on the mobile app is awesome as well if your drink is available!

I don't really have anything negative to say or anything positive. This place is like most Starbucks- you've got your coffee, food and merchandise. The seating is limited, but they have a decent porch space. The employees weren't rude or mean, but they didn't go out of their way to be super welcoming either. This was truly just an "Ok" Starbucks visit.
One of my favorite Starbucks locations conveniently located on 96th street easy access in and out the staff is always very friendly the only downside is there's no drive-through.  Near the Nail Bar

My first time at a Starbucks reserve store. It doesn't seem any different than the regular starbucks except for the upgraded interiors and also, I think that they have more coffee/upscale coffee options which doesn't matter to me since I just drink cold brew.  The staff here is extremely efficient, the line up here moves quickly so don't be deterred by the line-up. The only downside to this starbucks was that the ladies' washroom smelled horrendous.

The drive thru location is HORRIBLE!!!!Everything else is great! Just a suggestion.......switch the drive thru around so that the line starts on the other end of the window and have the outgoing vehicles come out where theyre currently going in. The way it is now. cars line up right at the entrance and block the on coming traffic. It causes a lot of angry people. This shopping center is already a mess with coming in and getting out.

Newly remodeled beautiful store with ample parking. My only complaint was the employees seemed exhausted and didn't want to engage. I also needed a cup of water and waited at the pickup area for 5 minutes without being acknowledged so I just left. Hoping it was just a fluke!

Always friendly service, albeit a tad slow but it's usually very busy there. Parking lot is a tragedy, the worst I've experienced. Bottom line: due to the lack of parking and the entitled folks who park illegally all over that lot (making it really congested), I usually go elsewhere for coffee.

This Starbucks is fine. I would agree with other reviewers that there isn't a real sense of urgency with the baristas. However, I've only been a few times in the late afternoon when I'm not in a rush and there isn't a real line.   Not a good place to work- lots of teens hang out around Gay Street and they want their Starbucks fix too. Might be different during working hours? I think the non paying customer issue some reviewers were talking about a few years ago doesn't happen as often anymore.   Kind of a shame that a Starbucks is in a nice location on Gay street vs. a local business. Fenn's Coffee is a short walk away and has a homier, local vibe (without kids) and I've seen people working there.
Love Starbucks!! I always get the free tea crap with extra matcha. All the Baristas are super friendly and my poodles Love going with me they always get a pupachino

We have been coming to this Starbucks since they've been open. We've seen managers come and go, staff changes and remodels. Consistency has never failed. The Baristas learn our names, drinks and faces quickly. They are always friendly. Though at times it could be chaotic and an ocasional drink gets messed up overall we love our backyard Starbucks. Great job to our new manager "Brittany".

New Starbucks in Tarpon, Previously down by Publix, it's now in the walmart parking lot, where the KFC used to be.  The location is clean and bright, but way too loud on the inside. The older location was dark and intimate, this location is very open.   The counter is reversed so you order on the right and pick up on the left, the baristas were friendly and warm, they got our simple order correct, on this past Saturday night.   I like the place, even if I'm not the biggest fan of the over roasted coffee.
Well I wouldn't know how this Starbucks is. The rude ignorant parking lot attendant who bullies patrons back into their cars is to blame. Stay in your little hut and leave the customers alone.

It took 20 minutes to get a simple glass of black, brewed, iced coffee. A terribly inefficient location. People traveling on the freeway don't have the time it takes to stop here. I thought the Starbucks I stopped at yesterday in Barstow was bad, this one was even worse.

Compared to other Starbucks this one always has the same issue - really dirty, gross restrooms. I stop here on occasion to work before appointments downtown. Nothing makes me lose my appetite more for a delicious cup of joe than smelling urine while washing my hands.  Please fix!!! Begging you because you have good wifi. You are kind of my office Starbucks....  Most recent visit was yesterday 6/28.

Went today. It was busy since in the mall. I ordered the new cold brew. It seems to move slowly here and when I wanted to get a tea later I skipped it since it seemed busy and I couldn't stand the wait, the staff was friendly though and my drink today and many other times has been done right, it maybe deserves 3.5 stars. A better starbucks than some others.

This is my favorite Starbucks in St. Louis.  I'm reviewing it as a Starbucks, since that is the sign on the side of the building.  I didn't expect to be blown away by some baristas creative take on a latte... but I also didn't expect to be underwhelmed by some hipster's weird interpretation of my favorite beverage.   I go to Starbucks for consistency, friendly service and free refills.  I come here when I'm visiting my parents to get work done.  The staff has always been super friendly.  The decor in the Starbucks is nicer than some of the other stores I've been in.  This store is also a little less busy than their Kirkwood Rd. counterpart, so be sure to head here if you're going at a busy time of the day.  I think the drive thru helps manage the crowd.    Overall, a great Starbucks.  You'll get the drink you want, and you'll get it with a smile from friendly staff.
I stop in here every morning to get my coffee. Fran and Alayzgia are the NICEST and most polite ladies. They know my drink, they're always smiling and it's just a pleasure each morning.

This location is great!   It is a very clean location and the staff seem to work quickly and move the lines and people out quickly.   It is a normal Starbucks like any other one. The staff were friendly when I went in and were happy to accept my military ID and give me a discount on my purchase.   The coffee was tasty and I also had a muffin that was pretty good.   I would come back to this location because of the staff being so helpful and friendly.
Awesome service, great management, very friendly and efficient people. It would be nice if all Starbucks were this good.

I like this location, the staff and even the patrons are nice here.  I don't know that there is a ton to say about Starbuck's in particular.  I think it's important to point out bad service or a dirty store, but truly I have yet to see a Starbucks in those conditions.
Great customer service on every visit.  Everything is always delicious.  Ive only used the drive thru at this location, but they are courteous and accurate.
Great Starbucks.  The staff is pretty friendly.  I enjoy stopping here and doing some reading and studying.

I stop by here quite often on the way to work.  They are fast and accurate most the time.  Whoever is working the window is super friendly.  They make suggestions for you to try.  The inside is quaint and has seating for you to work or just enjoy your tea or coffee.

Wanted to give them a chance to redeem themselves from my last experience and they failed yet again. I hold Starbucks to a higher standard they charge enough to at least hire individuals who are willing to do their job to the best of their ability

I was in there with a friend and he has frequented there several times.  When they call the customers name to pick up their order I could barely hear the name called and I have good ears. They only say it once and if your talking to someone you might miss it and your drink will either get hot or cold.  My friend ordered a cold coffee drink that he usually orders. This time they got the orders wrong 3  times. Almost making us late for church. They don't know what customer service is. I won't go back there again.

I love the location and this is a great starbucks, but the access drive to get to their lot is in bad shape.  I worry for my tires.  I wish they would put pressure on the company who owns the mall property to resurface the access drives.  Really, all of those businesses in there should be complaining about it, and especially new tenants should be complaining.
I'd rather come here to study rather than the library. I enjoy a nice caffinated drink while listening to smooth jazzy music (most of the time) Infested with college kids, it never gets boring here.
if you like adventure, you should go here. The drive thru SUCKS. Just left this dump late for work and without my coffee. Thanks for nothing

Even in Boise, there's a Starbucks open on Christmas day! Who'dathunk?  For being a major holiday, the staff was super cool, and the drinks tasted just like any other Starbucks location (which is to be expected) and the line moved pretty quick despite a holiday rush. Ho ho ho!

Timing is everything? Yup!  Of course had to stop today to get the free holiday cups!  Of course you need to get the grande specialty drinks.  As we were waiting, the line started to increase.. four cars behind me and three cars in front.  Hubby uses the app and says we needed to spend $20 to get 75 stars...   Our purchase total was $10.25... but he always falls for those gimmicks.. like buy one get one free etc..  When I got to the window and showed her his phone, she said the car in front paid for ours!!!  Perfect timing!  Thank you to the car in front!

I know there's some negative reviews but every single time we've come here the service, product, and speed of service have been exceptional. Came here News Year morning and expected half drunk hungover employees that didn't want to be there. I was pleasantly surprised. Great job folks!
Service could be much better. The barista Glenn was not very helpful and somewhat rude. It took over 15 mins for me to get my coffee and bagel and this was not at a busy hour. Disappointed.

This is now my go-to 'Bux as I work at 18 & Market (I used to frequent the one at 20 & Market).  During the morning rush it gets absolutely mobbed - use the mobile ordering if you can and you can swoop in and out to get your over-priced #DiabetesInaCup.  Meanwhile, I treat myself to a latte about twice a week.  Trying to cut down but I love them.  I know people claim to not like Starbucks but the long lines here (quick moving) seem to indicate otherwise.  Gotta have my FiveBucks aka Charbucks!

Fortunately, coffee drinkers are addicts so they put up with a lot to get the coffee.  Very rude workers.  Petty.  No customer service.  They don't even try to be decent.  Convenient location is best attribute.  Usually there's parking, but wow, putting up with the disrespect and rudeness of the "help" is very hard.  90% of customers are using Apps.

One of the worst customer service experiences I've had a Starbucks was at this location in Septmber. My boyfriend and I stopped by on a Saturday afternoon and ordered a drink each. The girl at the til did not even smile at us or seem enthused when we ordered our drinks. My boyfriend wanted a cup of blondo coffee (his favourite) and she immediately responded "we don't have that available right now - only the Pike roast. So I immedately asked her if they wouldn't mind making him his own cup (I know other Starbucks do this with no problem whatsoever). So she huffed about it and said "fine". Oh I was so mad at her rude disposition. We stood off to the side afterwards, awaiting my drink (chai latte) and in the meantime, there were a few more customers who were in line to order. About 5 minutes later there was no indication that his coffee was ready, so I walked back up to the til and the girl was not there. Instead, she was busy chatting with a male employee just inside the doorway as I could hear them. I stood there for a good minute until she decided to saunter back out to the til. She smugly asked me what she could do for me and I told her we were still waiting for his coffee. She turned around and realized she hadn't even started it so she said "oops, it will be a few minutes". She didn't even apologize. I was so mad at the whole situation, it was my boyfriend who had to steer me away from the counter and calm me down. So, bottom line, the staff working there that afternoon provided me with the worst customer service in recent memory. I will NEVER go back to this location again.
Best baristas in town. Remember my name and my order every time. Well run and very efficient all around. Pretty convenient location as well right on the corner.

Starbucks Starbucks...oh how so many people love you and hate you. This girl is a regular at this Starbucks. Some are good and some are bad this one is just at home.   You ask how can this be ? It is so close to the mall and it is hard to find parking in peak times. Let me tell you why . This Starbucks is a great place to unwind from your daily troubles and relax.   Yes it is busy and yea it is hard to find parking at times but it's worth the wait because the staff is always friendly and remember you by name. They make you feel at home.

Staff at this location are really friendly. I love going early mornings because they have tons of open seating as most of their business comes from the drive thru at this time. Even though I typically prefer local coffee shops this is a perfect place to work a few blocks from where I live. Make sure to sign up for rewards so you can get to free refill status on black coffee then get a coffee for a few bucks and refill it a handful of times.

Went here to get a coffee and a breakfast sandwich ..They got the order completely wrong, The sandwich never was put in the oven, I ended up waiting and waiting. When I complained they refused to refund my card and restore the rewards to my card. Barrista said she was stressed and wasn't paying attention to my order.  The place is dark and very noisy.  They need to get some bright lights in there

This Starbucks seems like an older location.  There is no drive-through, and electrical outlets are in short supply.  Either the seating arrangement keeps you away from the outlets, or they're just not there.  There was only one outlet near the three tables I was sitting at.  But it's still Starbucks.  Quality drinks, friendly staff, and good service.  It's always a pleasant atmosphere, and great for a little mid-day break.

Brand new great location and layout. We've been here several times per week since it opened and love the staff. The cafe is larger than typical Starbucks and is open late, which is great for us local residents!!

This starbucks is great, the people here are very friendly and the location is a nice place to just sit back and watch the traffic. My friends and I love coming here for our bike meet ups and catch up while watching nice cars drive by.

We come to this Starbucks at least once every two weeks if not every week.  It is conveniently located in between our house in Orlando and my parent's house in Tampa.  This is by far the slowest Starbucks we have ever been to... Consistently.  Service is poor, you would be lucky if you get much more conversation than 'what can we get for you'.  We thought that this time we would go inside to speed things up and get a better experience but that didn't change anything.

Rude staff.  Took forever to get our order, guy in front of us had to ask twice to get his order. Our caramel fraps didn't get the caramel drizzle. Waitstaff was obnoxiously flirting with a client with kids in the store. And it looked dirty!

This is the worst Starbucks ever. There were 3 cars in front of me in the drive thru (a short line for this location), and it still took 15 minutes. And if you order on the app they never have it ready when you get there. I'm literally done with this location.

I like the location and the layout of this store. Right in the heart of Downtown, a block off the main strip of the bar scene, opposite from Jannus Live (RIP Jannus Landing). The decor inside is very modern with some soft leather chairs along the far wall. The staff is very friendly despite the fact that you know they have to deal with a lot of drunks and the downtown crazies. You'll have to pay to park most days (all days?) but you have to do that anywhere downtown. I'd go here more often if not for the parking problem. But it's still a great Starbucks.
The location is convenient and in the morning the outside area is nicely shaded. Service is okay.
This is a nice Starbucks coffee store in the middle of down town Santa Barbara...I'm really impressed with It's decor,space and location..I will be coming here again when I visit Santa Barbara

So from the last time i reviewed this location years ago they have improved and are a million times better now. My drink of choice is a Venti Americano with heavy creamer and vanilla. If this is done right its one of the smoothest drinks you could imagine, drinks like water practically but you still get 4 shots of espresso. If done wrong it taste like instead of using espresso they used coal from an old fire. Its very hard to get right. That all being said, my past few years coming here they've done a flawless job of perfecting that drink for me, most of the people working started to remember me and my order also which is huge for me. Now granted were all just human, occasionally during extremely busy times it may not be totally up to par, but everyone working has always been more than happy and willing to fix the order. This is one of my favorite locations in the city without a doubt.
Absolutely the best staff around. I review not just on product but on staff. Highly recommend Mocha Latte. Definitely awesome.

I've always had great service. This is definitely a busy store! The drive thru is usually pretty quick and the employees are friendly. I love all the tea options that they have and the rewards that I get. It's always been clean and decent parking.

Went to this Starbucks location this morning to order my drink and got THE RUDEST barista I've ever met in all the states I've been to and all the Starbucks I have been to this one was just a total jerk!! I didn't even get to finish ordering my drink as he cut me off multiple times. I understand not hearing me clearly but after he ignored my request for light almond milk... I ordered a venti coffee frap with light almond milk and he says "a coffee frap with almond milk ok" before I could even tell him I wanted extra frap roast pumps. So I tell him I want LIGHT almond milk and he snapped back well it's a pre measured amount so what you want extra ice????? Like no you jerk!!!!! I want extra frap roast but you wouldn't let me speak to even order it!!!!! I ended up leaving because I was so upset about it. I've never been treated that way before at a Starbucks. I spend a lot of $$$ there and to be spoken to like that was OUT OF LINE.

Kira has been so very kind and her customer service is exceptional. No matter how many times I come in a day, she makes me a fresh cup of coffee and puts all the things I love I. Them. Always smiling, and gets it done right!!  Thank you Kira!!!

This is a standard mall type Starbucks on the end of the mall near Wetmore.  indoor and outdoor seating facing parking lot. pleasant service and kind of slow on weekday evenings.  Currently the holiday items are very cut priced but they are OUT OF SALTED CARAMEL AND CARAMEL BRULEE!   Well maintained and close to decent shopping and the Tucson Mall.

This place was crazy busy. Steady flow of people coming through. Thankfully they kept up pretty well & we received our drinks  without waiting too long. This particular Starbucks is the smallest one I have ever been into. It is also set up strangely, but maybe that's because of its location & tininess. Ha! As soon as you enter you are standing at the order counter. Then you move around the counter in a line, finally to end up on the other side. There you will find some seating if you choose to stay. There are also a few tables outside. We left & enjoyed our drinks at the monument.
Cute Starbucks location right in prime location. This location is very small but the customers were friendly to share the large community table. Staff was friendly and coffee was good as usual!
What is with all of the flies? On multiple occasions there have been swarms of them throughout the cafe. Need to address the issue ASAP.

Generally I go to the Starbucks close to work, not home. But I was in the mood for a tea on my day off. I am a generally happy person and get along with everyone in service, as I understand that it's not the best job. I, however, expect at least somewhat friendly service. I went into this location yesterday and there was a familiar face mopping the floor. I apologized that I was interrupting her and that I could order from afar so I didn't mess anything up. She very rudely responded "Well you're going to have to pay, aren't you?". I don't think that was called for at all, so I walked to the counter where she made minimal eye contact and continued being sassy with her co worker.  As for my drink, it was Starbucks. Nothing more or less.

Super cool interior! I am a big fan of the vibes of this Starbucks location. It is different than any other location I have been to. The outside and inside have a unique look.   My drink was also delicious and was a recommendation from their "favorites" board. I can be indecisive at times so I liked that they had this board right when you get to the counter.
My favorite Starbucks In the area now. They are super friendly and quick. I also like the setup and ambiance of the store. I will have to go more often.

I've been to a lot of Starbucks in my 30 years on this earth. But WOW!!! This one sucks. Ordered my normal grande latte. They didn't have hot lids for grande or venti cups. Said I would take it without a lid, but they wouldn't give it to me without a lid. So I settled for a tall. They don't accept rewards because they are a licensed store, and then barista messed up my VERY simple drink.

The staff here are just ticket takers, I think. You don't get ANY personal service like you would at an exterior Starbucks. It kinda smells like dirty sweat in the back lobby. They don't have much of a food selection like salads and other items. And for the amount of business folk that stay here, not impressed with the amount of places for people to "set up".  I would go out of my way to Not come here again. I wasn't impressed with any part of it. They do have lots and lots of seats.... Just not the right configuration.
Don't bother coming here unless you want to wait 30 minutes for a simple drink. The drive through takes so long that it's almost a joke.

Maybe they were low staff. I know Starbucks can get crazy inside but I feel like people should try their best when they can. I wanted to try the new mocha cookie drink and I asked for no whipped cream. There was a new girl working there who put the whipped cream on, when I asked someone about it they made me feel like it was my problem so o proposed a solution of scooping it off. The lady who took my order took my cup and sloppily scrapped the whip cream of. It did not look appealing at this time but I took it and went on my way. The drink wasn't ruined but I felt the rudeness.

Always great coffee. If you like chi this is the Starbucks to go to, I have to drive past the one closer to me bc there chi is mixed wrong, to go to this one! Always great service! They are one of the quickest around

I can't shop or go to the new Apple store without a Frappucino after-wards. It's just one of my unwritten rules. It's a refreshing treat to celebrate a new purchase or resisting the urge to make a new purchase.   I like the new store location over the old kiosk version. However, I disagree with John H., I think the line moves slower. Perhaps my line was longer then his, because they were moving slow. I found it odd that two associates were taking orders and only one making all our drinks. I may be crazy, but wouldn't the line moved faster if two were making the drinks?  We all know the baked goods are shipped in to each Starbucks. There are no Kebbler elves in the back stockroom baking. Even though they are shipped in, I love the petite vanilla bean scones.   Staff is always friendly and pleasant. They should be, Starkbucks treats their employees very well with benefits and $.

I love this Starbucks and I am not alone. This coffee shop serves the community with a perfect location!   Starbucks employees are  so capable, and service minded. They really know how to juggle the busy crazy times and still bring a sense of speed and calm. Try that combination!  Its always friendly I disagree with some of the mean comments from others. The staff does such a good job keeping it clean and ready to be used all day long. I got some personal help from Matt on the new Starbucks app. He was just so kind and went out of his way to patiently help me, a grandmother from out of town struggling with technology to get my reward!  If you haven't tried Guatemala yet its great too.

Whenever I would to Reno for the weekend, I stopped by this Starbucks at least daily. Parking lot is small and limited but plenty of street parking if need be. It never really seems too busy, so I don't see this being an issue. The folks behind the counter are extremely friendly, and my drinks have been perfect every time. I frequent Starbucks and have specific favorite locations because I'm so picky... and this is one.

Ok I used to HATE this location. I continued to go only because it was right across the street from my office and I really need my Starbucks fix. Why did I hate it? The customer service SUCKED! When I go to a Starbucks I have a certain expectation of friendly baristas who Don't care about anything other than making you laugh while putting your drink together as soon as humanly possible. Well there were these two women that were working there -everyday. All they did was talk to each other! I'm not talking about talking to each other while making my drink... I mean talking me while there's a huge line waiting to order and another line waiting for their drinks. It's 7:45 in Center City! We are cranky and do not want to be late to work! Do not take your time making the only thing that will help me survive the next 8-9 hours. I don't see those girls much anymore not sure if their shift changed or they got rid of them. But if they're gone then you will get your coffee on time and with a smile :)

This is my favorite Starbucks in Delaware. The inside is always clean. They are always stocked with my favorite brownies when I want one. They are always professional.  I like their adequate staff and this is the only drive-thru that I have seen in DE so far so that is a plus! However, the cleanliness around the perimeter of the store needs help. I saw the same iced grande half-filled latte sitting on the curb (between the 3rd & 4th parking spaces on the right) for about 3 weeks. This sounds strange but I have been coming here weekly with my daughter since the middle of November.  All in all I just picked it up yesterday and kept moving. I love this company and recommend this location.

There are a few reviews that negatively characterize the staff here, but in my experience the staff here is one of the most charismatic and energetic units in Tucson. However, this location is insanely busy and the staff seems to be under constant pressure to move as quickly as possible, which causes mistakes. This is more of a location issue - I think that if there were another location maybe a half mile down the road the service may improve greatly. I have been here many times over the past year - on one occasion the staff forgot my order and I had to ask them again to bring it out. The thing is- the line was so long that it extended out of the door, so it is tough to be too angry about it. If you're craving Starbucks, just go here. And if the line is ridiculous, the nice thing to do is tip the staff for having to deal with it, instead of getting angry at the inevitable mistakes.
I go to this Starbucks a lot because it opens really early, has great tea, has friendly, competent staff, and a lively clientele. Food is ok.
Slowest Starbucks I've ever been to--- drive thru is never a speedy process but this location is always packed.   Enjoy their happy hour deals!
I love the new design of the place with a couple of more outlets which is desperately needed at other locations. Great atmosphere as well and the baristas are customer friendly.
Honestly not a great Starbucks. Every drink here feels half assed over the last couple years. I always feel like I wasted my money when I come to this location :( Robb drive Starbucks is better.

If I'm going to go to a Starbucks (primarily for the lemon pound cake), this is one of my favorite locations in Tucson. Convenient parking, generous seating areas inside and out,  and friendly staff. The lines can get kind of long, but generally my drink (and lemon pound cake) are delivered quickly enough.

Super friendly. I usually go to the drive thru and it's always the same guy there. I forget his name. He's young, kinda tall and has dark hair. He's always very nice and doesn't laugh at me even though I'm usually putting on purple lipstick when I pull up. Lol. I know Kathryn is the manager there and she's awesome too. This location definitely has the best staff of any Starbucks I've ever been to.
quick service.. nasty bathroom though.. par for the course for a starbucks

I was never a coffee shop goer until I moved into a condo with a Starbucks on the ground level.  It started with me requiring internet access before I had it installed in my condo, and needing to buy something from Starbucks in exchange for that free internet.  Now I'm addicted to lattes, and about $25 poorer/week!    Generally, I find this Starbucks location to have poor service and sometimes poor product quality.  Oftentimes I will be waiting to place my order while the staff members are chit chatting with their backs turned to the counter.  I find this to be a customer service problem at many retail establishments these days, and I'm always taken back to my days in retail when we were taught that the customer comes first, and if there aren't any customers, you need to busy yourself with cleaning, stocking supplies, etc.- anything but chatting.  When it's finally time to place the order, the staff are sometimes unaware of their own products, or I have to repeat my order several times (I would think that Skinny Vanilla Latte is pretty standard!).  Also, upon ordering decaf coffee on behalf of someone else, I've been questioned several time as to whether I really want to wait for it (perhaps because they don't want to take the time to make it?).  Usually if someone orders decaf, it's not for fun and giggles... it's because they can't have caffeine.  Therefore, yes, I did want to wait for it.  I guess the real indicator of how I feel about this location is that despite the fact that it's on the main floor of my condo, I'll often pass by it in favour of going to another coffee shop to get my beloved latte.  I'd rather wait out the 10 minute bus ride to Second Cup or Le Prep to get my beloved latte than be set in a bad mood after dealing with this particular Starbucks location.

Parking can be a chore but this is your typical Starbucks.  This place gets quite busy and seating is very limited.   Sometimes you have to sit on someone's lap, and vice versa.  So if there is nowhere to sit.. just pony on up... "Excuse me miss...mind if I sit on ya?!"  (best to use a wee irish accent)  otherwise yeah.. it's nice and refreshing to go here and realize you got a proper coffee with espresso and the right flavor, whipped cream like you asked, etc etc.  and be happy you are not at Dunkin Donuts!  I'm giving 4 stars because its a franchise, chain, boring run of the mill deal. Nothing here more exciting than a Venti Caramel latte.  For fun I always order the "supersize" and they look at me wierd.  I tell them to supersize every frickin thing... they just look at me again, wierdly.    Meanwhile, many of their baked goods are actually mini's.  Small teeny tiny versions of their counterparts in the non-starbucks world, yet cost 2x as much! hooray! Who doesn't love blowing their wad on a cup of coffee which is mostly ice, if you get the iced which I always do, and an assortment of mini bakery items.       Someone once told me Starbucks was founded by a hobbit.  Suddenly all the mini baked goods make sense!  It's by little ppl for little ppl!

All I have to say is yum!! Starbucks is delicious. Their coffee is warm,tasty, and how coffee should be. Their passion tea lemonade is sweet and very good. I also enjoy their pumpkin and banana walnut slices of bread. The location is great.  I'm fond of Starbucks.

I live too close not to come to this Starbucks but I will not anymore. I know Starbucks has their own price but when I order a flat white and it's not even 3/4 full, I'm upset because I want what I paid for and I know how it should be made and the one on 91st and keystone can get it right and are so much more pleasant!
I didn't realize that this was downtown Wilmington's only Starbucks.   Picked up two cappuccinos.   Great service although one was a bit drier than the other.

Starbucks food and drinks are pretty much the same wherever you go... We like that.  What sets this location apart and gives it that extra special Midwest flair is the insane drive-thru line. Consistent 18-23 minutes waiting.  Fine for those who have plenty of time on their hands to sit and do nothing while spitting out car exhaust and burning gas.  Just for fun, no one seems to know what to do when the line extends into the Best Buy parking lot and entrance.  If I could, I would open my own coffee/food location right across the street and show live video of the Starbucks line on an app.

I came through the drive through at 9:55pm. Yes I understand they close at 10pm but they could've at least told me sorry we shut everything down or something so after waiting at the order microphone for a bit I go to the window and the worker IGNORED me. I'm sorry but this is ridiculous. This was the first al straw with them. They have always been rude and had an attitude with me. The Starbucks on pyramid in the shopping center with the car wash and the one In Spanish springs has much better customer service!

This was the worst starbucks I've ever visited. Service was terrible and slow.   The staff working at the terminal c location were rude,  loud and unfriendly. When you pay $6 for a coffee you expect  happy polite service.  It took me longer to get a coffee than it did to check in my bag and get thru security. Visit another starbucks if you can. This one was BAD!

Maybe it's because this Starbucks feels right at home nestled neatly on the boutique corridor of Illinois and 56th, maybe's it's because there's no drive-through or busy highway to deal with, or maybe it has that certain coffee shop feel that you get from the depths of a city neighborhood, instead of a strip-mall.  Whatever it is, I like this version of Starbucks.  It's pretty straight-forward; we all know what we get - tall, grande, venti, grip - frap, whip, skinny, sip.
