It's quality bean at a fair price, that's why we all go.  So what does this leave me to review?  Service, of course.  These baristas are some of the friendliest around; every time I stop in, they are very welcoming and put me at ease from my chronic condition of running late for work - at least if I'm generalizing about myself...  Parking can get a little crammed on this side of town.  There's not much room behind the boutiques and there's limited curb-side spots.  There is outdoor seating when the weather permits, which is great for people watching  This is a great little pedestrian/bike friendly coffee shop in Butler-Tarkington, one I can raise my cup to.
Stopped in and waited 10 minutes for the drink to be made, a single person making drinks, really?!

Ah Starbucks. Well once again brand recognition is in full force, looking at the outside I could have been anywhere (Madrid? Penang? Sydney? - nope I was in St. Albert) and the inside has the same cozy, casual feel to it. The store front faces west which is great for our area because that means they get the afternoon/evening sun.  The service is good - not great as sometimes you have to repeat your order or the staff feel the drive-thru takes priority. The food and beverages are exactly what is expected from a Starbucks (that's what you get from a standardized chain) but I find this fact comforting because I know what I order will taste the same no matter where I am in the world.   I like that there are multiple plug-in locations - on the walls and underneath the bench seat. This is great if you do need to work for a long period of time because then you don't have to fight other people for the limited power.  There are 5 Starbucks locations in St. Albert and this is by far my favorite - for some reason, even for how "out of the box" the store is I really like the feel of this one - it is less busy then others, the staff are always welcoming, and the parking lot is very user friendly. I love to meet up after dinner for coffee with friends throughout the year here.
They were friendly. Went through the drive through to do our pickup. Love my morning Starbucks.

I don't typically go to Starbucks. Actually, I don't go to coffee shops in general. But this one is right down the street from my office and I stopped in one day because I was very early to work on a pretty day and wanted to sit outside in the sun for a few minutes before going to the office. There was a long line of cars waiting for the drive through but only a few people in line inside the store. The line moved significantly faster than I had expected based on previous experiences at Starbucks and after I paid for my drink, it was ready in just a minute or two. I've always thought people considered long waits at Starbucks to be a badge of honor. Not the case here. And the people were so friendly. They called me by name as they gave me my drink and as I was leaving. I decided to go back a few days later and they actually remembered me and anticipated my drink (I'd been in there once before!). Three different people working there called me by name. Each time I go in is like that, despite the fact that I stop in sporadically and occasionally. And the line of cars for the drive through is always moving, which I've also never seen at a Starbucks. I've been into Starbucks shops around the world and this is the best one I've been in. Love this place.

This Starbucks is pretty nice, and the service is pretty good. There is a fair amount of inside seating as well as some limited outside patio tables available, a drive-thru is also available for those times that you just want to grab a drink and go. The rest is pretty much what you would expect from Starbucks.

They do not let you use your Starbucks card to pay, however you can get Cheesecake Factory. =) They do still make good drinks and it's inside the Barnes and Noble. I wouldn't go out of my way to come here, but I'm a book nerd so I will always be a customer at least until someone messes up my coffee (Even if I don't get my stars for being a Gold Card Customer).

Came to this location 15 minutes before close (so sorry!) and they were still extremely friendly. I got their new promotion - double shot on ice, and it's not bad! I was looking for a new favorite and I might have found it. Their outside seating also looked very nice.

I witnessed an appalling display of customer service. A male employee treated a customer terribly rudely even though the customer was in no way acting badly. The Starbucks jerk got the man's order wrong and the customer actually apologized to the employee. Rather than respond like all employees are taught, this moron actually insults the customer under his breath. The old man couldn't hear and asked the employee what he had said. The employee said, 'nothing', and then muttered another insult. I was too shocked to say anything. The old guy never knew anything was wrong and left just as happy as when he came in. Shameful.
I am not a fan of the coffee.i usually get latte ir capaccino..i am a fan of the bakery items..just personal preference.they also have a small gift area.

I really like this location. I usually meet up here on Wednesday mornings before work for some discipleship time. The employees are always friendly even when it's 6:30am, and I like that there are a couple of tables outside to sit out and get some fresh air.
Terrible service ! They are slow as molasses ! Every single time but I have gone there to order a chai latte they forget to make it !
Not enough electrical outlets.  They need to install places to plug in for those who need power for devices.  It's 2015 SB peeps---one or two outlets right next to each other just don't cut it.

I love this location. It's spacious, has a wide variety of seating, from comfy couches to various sized tables, but the best part is that the employees are so personable! Definitely a Starbucks that you can plan on spending awhile in with good company.

When it comes to Starbucks, the speed and quality of service and taste of drink may vary per store, so at this location...  Service: Speed (Average), Quality (Good) Taste: Green Tea Frap (regular deliciousness), Pumpkin Spiced Latte (a bit watery)  We'll see what I try next!
Nice people.  Don't bother with the egg nog latte because they have the flavor of warm milk.  The pumpkin spice latte misses the mark as well.
The drive thru is ridiculously slow here. 4 cars in line and over 20 minutes on an afternoon-not the busiest of times. They also don't have the nitro cold brews at this location.
Fairly easy location to get in and out of on Charlotte Avenue. In my several visits I've experienced fast service in a friendly atmosphere both by going inside and through the drive through.
Best Starbucks location in our area! Employees are always providing the best possible customer service, including custom recommendations and making customers feel special and appreciated. Thank you!
High turnover, poor training, bad management, SLOWEST Starbucks on the planet. I will be going elsewhere for my coffee.

A great experience here yesterday, and almost every previous visit. This is not my main go-to Starbucks (I live in Edwardsville even though I work in Collinsville) but they don't disappoint. A little bit bigger dining room than E'ville (they should switch since E'ville is usually busier!) but usually clean. The staff is very friendly. Barista yesterday was super patient and very helpful with a non-coffee drinking member of our group. Michah (manager or asst. manager?) is very friendly, too.

I really like this Starbucks, it might just be my favorite Starbucks!  I say that because all of the employees are very friendly and sweet.  I came in dripping wet from the rain and one of the baristas offered me a trash bag so I wouldn't get more wet, how sweet!   This Starbucks is conveniently located to my gym and work so I'll plan to come here again.  They definitely give service with a smile, which goes a long ways these days!  I think I'll choose to come here more often!
The service at this Starbucks I would think that the owners would be embarrassed!  Incompetent and took half an hour to get a drink.... At an airport!? Unreal

Let's start with the good! The staff are really friendly, their bakery items (I had a browny) were really good, I didn't have to wait too long considering how busy it was, and got the correct order (although with way too much cinnamon). On the down side I have a few issues that will result in me never returning to this location. I get the logic of being busy and cutting little corners to get customers through the door, but throwing dirty used steamed milk jugs and whatever else that needs cleaning across your clean work space into the sink is gross. Re-steaming milk can get people sick. Not cleaning down the steamed milk spout and pushing through your lines can cause some bad cross contamination and get people sick. When pouring milk into a steamer jug and managing to pour it through your hands somehow you need to learn how to properly pour milk. If your work station is more milk then stainless steel, take the second and wipe it down. When lifting your steamer jug all the different milks are dropping into whatever is sitting there. If for whatever reason I couldn't have lactose I would be one sick person. All this being said, I didn't mention anything to the staff because the barista looked a bit overwhelmed. So I won't go back knowing that if the visible corners are cut who knows what else there is that I didn't notice.  But again, the staff were super nice. :)

This place is always so packed with cars going to the drive-through. Since the Starbucks it's only drive-through right now. So that wait can be a little bit longer just because they are not doing anything inside. So if you plan to go to the Starbucks here just make sure you give yourself enough time. They always seem to get my order right which is super nice. The barista's are super friendly. And I know they are trying their best with how busy they probably get. I hope one day they can up inside again, it would make the wait less for me since I only order through the app for the most part. And it makes it pointless to do that if you have to wait in line with everybody else. But it is what it is.

This location is one of the more busier Starbucks that you'll ever see. The drive-thru is always packed (even during the supposed slow hours of 3-5 pm), and the employees behind the bar are moving so quickly that you'll more than likely not have even the briefest conversation with them (not that it's a bad thing).  Exterior seating seems to be plentiful, while interior seating is of a reasonable amount. Don't be surprised to see half of the seats taken up by those on their laptops, a few locals, travelers, and salesmen pitching an MLM to an unsuspecting sap that just came in for a Frappuchino. Just your typical Starbucks crowd.  Altogether, this location is one of Hendersonville's best within a very busy shopping area.

I was so surprised at how close this location is to me! Like most Starbucks, the menu and setup is the same. I like how the tables are spread out and that there is a long, spacious table in the back, perfect for studying and underneath there are plenty of outlets to plug in your electronics.   The staff when I was there was very friendly and attentive. The area for milk and sugar was clean and the garbages weren't overflowing. There isn't much more that I can mention about this place. It's a great location, good atmosphere, lots of parking in the shopping complex and great service.

Not much to say about it, it's your typical Starbucks however with more mess ups than usual. Go there almost every morning and I'd say about 3 times a week we have problems with order mess ups and just them forgetting our order in general. That's bound to happen anywhere you go but when it comes to be an occurring thing can be pretty frustrating. They usually have a loaded staff too and considering most people have to go to work and other events, would be nice to get some better service.

1 Star for this location only because I love Starbucks! I have been to this store several times...after yesterday I will park my car and buy from the Starbucks in nearby Frys, or drive to another location! I'm puzzled as to how a Starbucks store can get their service so WRONG!!   A few times, I have had excellent service via the drive through usually around 6am. Up until yesterday, I thought the worst store was in the Las Vegas airport. That being said, in the past 3 weeks I have experienced terrible service here on 4 separate visits. Isn't anyone training these people???   Yesterday I ordered my coffee from the mobile app (at 2:36pm), when I drove up to the store (a good 15-20minutes later) the drive through was packed but the parking lot was nearly empty, so I parked and walked in. Two or three people were sitting at tables, and I was followed in by an older couple. I walked up to the counter with the sign, 'pick up mobile orders here'. I saw the guy was completing a drink, and I saw my little red cup ready on the counter behind him. I asked him if he could grab my mobile order, not looking up he replied, 'yes'. Even though he walked past my drink several times in the next several minutes to take drinks to the drive-up window, my coffee sat there. Over and over he walked past it to take an order to the guy at the window, then back past it again (this time with two empty hands) to start filling another order.   I continued to look in the direction of the other guy behind the counter (by the till) who seemed to look my way a few times. The couple, who came in after me, had already ordered and paid, but were now standing at the counter chatting with the guy.  Finally another woman came in, I thought this was a good sign, assuming he would acknowledge her and the other couple would see someone else was waiting behind them. Nope, he just continued to chat with the first couple while the other woman was now waiting. At this point, the guy filling the drinks was done with the order for the older couple. He motioned that he had their drinks, grabbed a cup and began to fill another order!!  Let me say again, I did not stand there 'quietly', I asked a second time for my mobile order, I knew the guy had heard me and he still would not make eye contact. I paced, stood on my tippy toes, looked at my watch several times (it was now 3:25pm). I considered walking out, the whole thing was bizarre, but I had paid for the stupid drink and I wasn't going to leave without it.   Finally, after the 2nd woman paid, I walked over to the other guy.  He didn't look at me or acknowledge me in any way. I asked him loudly if he could grab my mobile order for me??? He said, 'oh you have a mobile order, you need to go to that counter (pointing to where I had been standing, pacing, etc!! Really buddy...you can't tell me you did not see me there!! ). I explained I had and refrained from commenting on his snarky remark. So he FINALLY handed me my coffee.   One of the other bad experiences here was about 2 wks earlier. On that day there were literally 5 employees behind the counter. I walked up to the girl filling drinks and asked for my mobile order. She said, 'yes, just a minute', she was not only filling orders but also attending to the cars. She glanced repeatedly at the 4 other employees huddled behind the counter, as if hoping they would help (she was the only one working). I felt sorry for her so I waited assuming she would grab my coffee on one of her trips, nope.   She continued to fill other mobile orders, (no other customers were at the counter) while glancing several times at the other employees. They were deep in some kind of conversation never looking at her or me.   Finally, one broke free from the pack, so I walked over to him, he did not acknowledge me, I asked him if he could grab my mobile order. He was rather rude and then I realized he was the SAME Snarky GUY who gave me my order two weeks prior!!!   I just feel stupid for waiting so long especially after several other bad experiences at this store.

I visit this Starbucks every morning before work. I order the same thing every day. I wish I received what I ordered every day. Lack of consistency. Today my coffee looks like milk with toffee syrup - forgot the coffee? One day my toffee nut syrup was replaced with gingerbread?!!  What if I was allergic ?!! I stopped going to Dunkin in part because of a lack of consistency.. This is just getting absurd.

Great atmosphere, and great on a rainy day. Employees were friendly. Tables were clean. The employees didn't ask for my name for the order. When it was ready it was just sitting there and I was oblivious to it. But not a big deal since it wasn't busy. I would hope on a busy day they would pay more attention to this important detail. They had free samples of their pastries so that was welcoming.

This is the fastest Starbucks drive thru I've ever experienced. The stores in Clarksville need to take a page from the Smyrna store's manual! I've been about 8 or 9 times and this store is always fast and my drink accurate.

I am not that active on this site, however I decided to write a review after the terrible service we had this Starbucks location.   I have to add that I am a gold card member of Starbucks and  I have 2 pounds of their  Thanksgiving blend bought up for the holidays.    Tonight in route to a meeting in Lakeland, FL, we decided to stop at this location for coffee, water and a snack.    We initially requested a brewed coffee and preferably Thanksgiving blend if they had it.   The lady in the drive thru was very  very short with us and  blurted out  they were no longer serving the Thanksgiving Blend for the season.   We then started to change our  order and before we could finish, the lady cut us off and told us our order total.  We asked for several items  such as muffins,  cookies, snowman cookies etc and  each item we requested they were out of them.  She never made suggestions as to what was available.   She acted as if she was doing us a favor  just by taking our order.     I rarely get too angry with a sales person however tonight I wanted to  to drive off without our order.  However we  followed thru and endured their slow service.   There will be no more stops at this location!

It's a Starbucks - you know what you're getting.   Notable of this location: - clean - has good parking - relatively quiet and good for work.  - locks on the bathroom doors (not a huge fan...)  Overall, I'll probably be back just because of the convenience.

Of all the Starbucks in the St. Pete area this is the best...! Great staff....good patrons & a lot mof fun..!  Parking is available across the street at the shopping cneter when the tiny lot fills up!

Usually all is well here at this store, and I usually frequent it everyday on my way home. Today was different. I stopped in around 6pm and I guess there was training going on where the store cleanliness was neglected, even though there was very little traffic (since the drive thru was closed too). I got my coffee then went for a refill, where i was told that coffee doesn't get refills. This is the first time I've heard this "company policy" since it could "give me a heart attack." The barista really didn't care that everyone else but her has done refills and have me a similar drink without asking if that's what I wanted. It not like I'll never come back, but I'll avoid that barista for sure. So much for the Third Place.

What a shining example of how a Starbucks should be ran. 2 days in a row, the drive through was quick and the people friendly. One girl started up a quick friendly conversation while we waited for our coffee at the window. Super sweet girl (I didn't catch her name, but she has a couple tattoos on her lower arms). I truly wish ALL Starbucks operates this efficiently and this friendly. Thanks!

I have going to this store for almost two yeas now. They are favorite store in STL. I will go out of way to go to this store. The staff is always so nice even before 6:30AM! They know my name and my order (unless I change it up.) I wish I knew all their names. But each one of those staff members needs an A++ for their smiling faces.

When I come here I can always count on fast and friendly service! Their baristas always get my order correct and it's brewed to perfection.  Today I enjoyed a grande non-fat iced latte and it was great! They have a large selection of bakery items at this location and I'm always tempted to get something. Their cake pops are delish!   A bonus to this Starbucks is the outdoor seating. The atmosphere is not that special because it's in a parking lot but it's great to enjoy the nice springtime weather.

I really don't understand it. Of every time I have been to this Starbucks location the staff is so rude, so inconsiderate, so disengaged from their customers it really begs the question that this must be the fault of the management. You ask a simple question and get a reply that might as well come with a snarl and growl. I would avoid this location at all costs (I will be driving out of my way) and go to the location on Oracle and Rudasill just down the road. Hopefully something will change there, because the entire staff should be ashamed of the culture and history that they are letting down. Maybe call this a re-do (part 2) Starbucks and move on!

Love this location! Super clean and the staff is always wonderful. You're always greeted with a smile and the service is very prompt! Conveniently located next to Target so I can shop and stop in for breakfast, lunch or a quick drink & treat! They keep everything well stocked. Though the Target has a kiosk for Starbucks inside, I always prefer to come here! It's also next to an urgent care and when you have an unexpected visit with one of the kids late in the day it's a nice place to stop and get a pick me up to get through the wait times.
Slow slow slow, never enough people working, brewed coffee never ready in the am.  They really need to get it fixed here.   The location is great but corporate needs to get on the manager

This place has always had my food and drink right, but man it needs helped. I've worked at Starbucks and been to hundreds of locations. I go to one almost everyday. This place....you need a solid 30 extra minutes if you plan on waiting to order, then waiting on a drink. I've even ordered in my mobile app while at home (over 10 minutes away) and still came to the store and waited.   I'm not sure if it's the way the work flow is...there always seems to be a lot of workers. I hope they can get this resolved. I started just ordering a minimum of 15 minutes out which has worked out well the past few times.  **If you need an iced coffee refill, also plan on that taking at least 15 minutes - this is probably the worst part because I'm just wanting to leave at this point**

They were out of a lot of things (product wise), but other than that they were friendly. I sometimeS always feel guilty when getting water with my drink. But, they were totally for it and even offered. One awesome thing I saw was, a business card in Braille!!!

Dumb butch gave me the limp wrist when I handed him my change cause my broke ass did not have bills.  I specifically counted and divided my change and verbally told him so it would be a time saver but no of course I'm trying to steal smh  I asked for no ice and these bitches in the back ground laughed.

Walked in at 6:30am.  I asked for Evolution pure celery juice.  They have the brand Evolution just not the celery juice in stock.  They said Whole Foods has it.  Well, I went to 2 locations and neither had it.  I looked at Starbuck's website first to see if they carried it and they did.  As I was in here I figured I would order a coffee. I am looking at the menu and nothing looks familiar...I said where is the regular menu? They said they NO longer have it only promotional items.  Well, I don't frequent Starbuck's often and now I know why.  I ordered a caramel coffee regular.  Total $5.61.  It was a HOT coffee and they did not bother to put a sleeve on it for me.  i got so aggravated I threw it away and left!  I will NEVER go back!  Horrible service and everything in between!

This is the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. And I have been to many many Starbucks. I have come here twice and waited almost 20 minutes in the drive through. The first time was during the morning rush so I can understand being busy. The second time was a Sunday afternoon. Not sure what's going on here but something is off at this Starbucks.
This is a really good local starbucks with great staff.  I enjoy the neighborhood and its a great place to have a coffee and read a magazine.

It's very hit or miss at this location, both with timeliness in being served as well as the employees making your morning beverage. For the most part, the better crew does tend to be there at least 3 out of 4 visits, so that's a plus.  I will second what Christopher said about the line though; it is super confusing for people not familiar with the area and I constantly see people asking "Is this the line? Or do I go back here?" What's the point in having a side door that opens half-way through the line that someone has to walk all the way back to the regular door to enter line. Why not just keep that door locked and cause less headaches? I've seen many a newcomer come in that way and look as dazed as a baby bird while they catch their barings and realize the actual line zips way back to the other door or catch them standing in the drink line when they have yet to place an order.  Signs or no, I've seen many people have issues with this; just saying, unless headache is one of many things you plan to pick up from starbucks during your morning routine, just enter through the regular door (across from Blue Mercury!)

Ok, I am not one to write bad reviews. Especially for chains because I feel like you'll generally lose some quality somewhere along the way. But this Starbucks, I just will not ever be wasting my time here again. The past 3 times here, I've had my ordered messed up.  First time, I got the wrong sandwich but correct drink. Second, wrong drink and wrong bagel. Today, wrong drink, right sandwich. In all of this I know I've been "overcharged" in the sense where I should be getting some money back no matter the amount. But I just can't even bring myself to stay there any longer. The person at the window has always been sweet but I honestly would just like a correct order for once.

They know my order as soon as I walk in. I appreciate the barista's effort here...makes me feel special because this isn't even my "home location" lol.  It could be that we share the same first name, whatever the reason she pays attention to detail and makes sure my cup of Joe is made to perfection.  The inside is the size of a large walk-in closet but the fire place and friendly service is more than welcoming.   Pull up a chair and stay a while. ~Christy
Prime location and pretty fast service.

So there's slow and there's Starbucks slow.  Really.  Been here 17 minutes with 6 drinks coming over walk in counter and 11 going over drive they window.    This Starbucks is so slow it is ridiculous.  Safe bet here - go they drive thru.  They have a timer and they are in task.  Walk in. - slow.
I wish there was a way to give a negative star. How is it possible to be stuck in a drive thru for 40 Minutes and still haven't received your coffee? DO NOT COME HERE !

This location needs a complete staffing overhaul, especially during nights and weekends.  Not only do many of the baristas make beverages incorrectly, but there is this attitude about them that is just plain rude, like they can't be bothered.  So many of them also play on their cell phones while working.  There's no sense of customer service or building relationships, and often times the wait is ridiculously long for orders.  The restrooms also are almost always filthy.  They really need some help.
Not to burst the Starbucks bubble, but their pastries are just old and mediocre. How come they are so second rate?

The Starbucks on Magazine Street is one you can count on for a great beverage. I always get the chai latte, sometimes with vanilla. It's usually made to perfection, at the perfect temperature (not too hot that it burns my tongue, and not too cold that it doesn't warm my soul). They have a large selection of food and snacks as well, including my favorite breakfast sandwiches. The pricing is standard for any Starbucks. The staff are friendly enough, and try to make your experience the best they are able. The building is large, with a lot of seating, that's often taken by locals... I'd advise you to get there early so that you can get a seat, and if there's no seating already by the time you get there, don't be upset. All in all, the Starbucks is in a great location and serves what you crave, caffeinated, sugary beverages!

The employees here treated us so well. We ordered our drinks and the staff noticed my five year old had a birthday shirt on. She asked her how old she was. Then when we were waiting for our drinks one of the baristas gave our whole family free cake pops. They even upgraded our kids drinks to Venti's. Super sweet staff! Other Starbucks could learn from them.

This Starbucks is located in the Garden District of NOLA. it's really beautiful compared to the ones in the Vegas area. I love how they decorated for their city's feel. The store was clean, including restrooms and they had a small selection of gift items (including Texas mugs?).  The service was pretty quick but they messed up two out of three of the orders. One was the wrong size and the other was the wrong flavor.  Weather was beautiful while we were there so we took advantage of the outside seating which there was a lot of. I would definitely stop here again if I'm back in that area.
Writing this review leisurely as I sit in drive thru going on 15 min now. Way too long to wait for a 5$ coffee.  Ok. Update. 25 min. Order was wrong. Yikes. At least they gave me a free drink coupon.

I like this location. The staff was friendly. They had a fair amount of seating and the place wasn't crowded so it was easy to find a seat- that being said I went mid day during the week. They don't have any fancier seating like some starbucks but I don't mind that. The bathrooms weren't great but they weren't bad either- better than quite a few Starbucks I've been to.

I have never had such rude service at a Starbucks before. Quite shocking for the area of Nashville. I walked in used the restroom and went to the counter where I was the only person in line and waited while being ignore for at least 4 minutes while the lady carried on a conversation with her co worker all while Counting her tips. Once she finally finished and announced her grand total of tips she finally recognized that I was standing there patiently waiting to order. She was not very friendly nor apologetic for the wait.   I order my drinks and my brothers (all the same drinks) and once received walked outside and took a sip of my drink and recognized that it didn't taste like it normally does. So I walked back into the store where the lady who made the drink basically told me that it's not normally a sweet drink and do I want her to add sweetener? My brother also at this time tried his drink and verified his wasn't sweet either and came in behind me as I'm speaking to the lady. The other lady jumps into the conversation and basically says that his normal Starbucks is not making his drink following the recipe. So they are not normally sweet. However they can add vanilla sweetener to our drinks if we would like. Instead of arguing with them we accepted the vanilla sweetener and went on our way.   I felt this duo are just lazy and argumentative and did not want to remake the drinks properly.

Don't order a Cappuccino. They don't know how to make foam. I specifically requested an extra dry cap and got extra bubbly milk that resembled a milkshake. Customer service was good just need more training on how to make proper foam. It's not that hard.  Don't think I'll be coming back since it was $4.25 for a drink that wasn't authentic. That's conglomerates for you.

This is considered my "local" starbucks. I've frequented this one numerous times over the years and I haven't had a bad experience. The place is filled with more tables and seats than most starbucks. They have 4 lazy boys which is nice to relax in. Since its located near CCC and Highland, i feel that there are a lot of students in here.   I don't like that they don't brew blonde after a certain hour. The staff have always been nice to me. One time, i took my nephew here and he doesn't speak english but was learning. The staff was patient while he was practicing his english ordering a drink.   The bathrooms are usually clean as well as the rest of the store. I've used the drive thru on numerous occasions and its good they offer it since the parking lot is a bit tight in that shopping center.

Starbucks in Newtown Pa. seems to be slacking in its cleanliness -  the trash is always overflowing and the counters are always a mess.  It doesn't take long to clean up every once in a while - not once a day.  Also I have been to other Starbucks in Hamilton NJ - Doylestown & Yardley and why is the Newtown Starbucks the only one that takes away the milk containers from the section where you fix up your coffee.  If these issues have been taken care of that's great if not, there is always Dunkin Donuts, or maybe it's time for a Saxby's to come to town.
Got a vanilla iced latte with oat milk, a chai tea late, and a caramel brûlée latte. They gave me an extremely small cup for the latte and it was still about $4. The cups were also sticky.
Friendly service, this Starbucks is right next to the Kroger on Highway 70 S. You won't miss it! No drive through available at this location.
I love the way this works. The design os more like the Christmas one it's better than the Tampa version of Starbucks and it's not that bar in the inside. What i like the most is the parking spot.
Consistent Starbucks food and great staff. A great place to have coffee and watch the neighborhood regulars. Come here every morning when I am in Philadelphia.

Worst experience at Starbucks in awhile. I get it's a licensed store (in a hotel) but don't punish your customers for it. As a former Starbucks employee maybe I'm over critical but here are my issues with today's experience. 1. No signage on one of the breakfast sandwiches. I had to ask the curt barista what it was.  2. I ordered an iced chai with soy and a shot. I saw the cup was marked incorrectly and reminded the barista about the soy. She said she got it. She did not so I asked her again to put it on the cup. She finally realized her mistake and told the barista - soy. 3. She rang me up and the total was around $13. A breakfast sandwich and a drink. Ridiculous. I knew it would be high and I was planning on using a free drink reward from my gold card. The barista on the till the said "we don't accept rewards". Crazy because I could pay with my gold card. Insanity. I know most licensed stores are able to accept rewards so not sure why this one is lagging. 4. I get to the handoff plane and my drink is called without the shot espresso. I corrected the barista and she had to take the drink back to add the shot.  Charging customers $13 for a to go breakfast means I shouldn't have to hold your baristas hand throughout the entire process let alone no one working smiled or said thank you.
Drive thru is the slowest in the land. Drink is usually right.  The location is really great, with great access to the highway. Don't forget to join Starbucks Rewards for your free drink!

Every time I go to this Starbucks they end up taking a very long time to make my drink even if there is not wait. They also manage to make my drinks wrong, either forgetting an ingredient or shorting me on the amount of coffee or espresso in the drink.  I don't expect a lot but I feel like at the very least I should get what I paid for and get it in a reasonable amount of time.  Unless there is no other Starbucks around you I would recommend to avoid this location as they are sub-par at best.

This is my favorite Starbucks ever! The employees are always so nice & always make my drinks perfect unlike other locations I've been to! The service is always very quick & they are always very inviting! Thank you Starbucks on Chippewa & Landsdowne for being excellent!

I don't usually review chain restaurants, but this Starbucks is absolutely adorable!   Conveniently located at the Omni Severin hotel (where I was staying), the staff is friendly, it is always clean, and it is one of the cutest Starbucks/coffee shops I've ever been in!
It is Starbucks. Coffee is just okay but we don't have many other options in town for a cup of caffeine. Five stars for this location because the workers are always super friendly.

Panic attack in a parking lot.  I feel like this review is long overdue.  Every single time I leave the Brentwood Starbucks I feel like I've been through a battle.  Not only is the parking lot a nightmare, making it difficult for the people going in as well as the drive thru, but the service is unbelievably slow, they get orders wrong all of the time, and the staff either doesn't want to wait on you, or wants to talk to you all day.  There are problems here.  Most people don't have 15+ minutes to dedicate to waiting for coffee!  I hate writing negative reviews but being that this is the Starbucks that is literally 1 minute from my house, I've been coming to this location for years and It's only getting worse, this review is justified.  Lately I've realized it actually takes less time for me to head to the Rock Hill or Webster Starbucks than to wait on Brentwood.  The other locations have me in and out within 5 minutes no matter how long the line (which is awesome!)  Brentwood needs to take a cue from their fellow stores and step it up a notch.
Twice now I've tried this location and they've messed up my order. Pretty disappointing. Just save yourself time and disappointment and use a different location.

I know that all Starbucks locations seem to be the same, but what sets this location apart is the Clover brewing system and the Starbucks Reserve coffees. The Clover system is designed to create the perfect temperature and brewing time for any coffee bean to give you the best cup you've ever tasted. If you are used to drinking coffee, this isn't just hype, you'll get a delicious cup. The selection varies, but you don't have to get a Reserve blend, they will brew any of their standard blends with the Clover. In addition, you can buy a bag of the reserve coffees to take home. They may not always have the blend you are looking for, but it gives one the opportunity to sample different varieties (albeit in a fairly pricy manner). There are usually little cards that describe each Reserve blend to help with the decision making process. I'd have to say that the Clover makes a great cup of coffee, and is the reason I choose this location over the myriad of other Starbucks in the area.
It may be the biggest Starbucks in the area but SO SLOW - waited 25 minutes TWICE for one coffee. Won't come here very often
Gross. A simple cup of coffee was watered down and burnt. Hands down the worst cup of coffee I've ever had! They had a nice staff but they lacked any knowledge of coffee. Won't be back anytime soon

I usually never write bad reviews but today I had to. One employee was  so rude. I was getting a coffee and an employee named Michelle was so mean to multiple customers and gave a nasty demeanor when calling names for drinks. Which is sad because multiple employees there are very nice.

I'm not sure what happened, but this Starbucks used to suck majorly.  I used to wait sometimes 15 minutes in the drive thru with 3 cars in front of me!  And, if I went inside, I used to feel like I was intruding on the staff - the attitude was sort of like "oh crap, another damn customer".  Now, however, something has changed.  I went there today after lunch at Aroy Thai and the staff was friendly, fast, and great!  I actually felt welcomed to enter the store, rather than loathed, as before!  And, the service was quick. I felt compelled to hang out with the fun staff, rather than grab my drink and run the hell away, as before!  I am a renewed fan and can't wait to order my next quad tall cappuccino!  Yousa!

I've quite honestly experienced better service at other Starbucks locations. That said, I really do appreciate the fact that I can use my Starbucks Gold card (or app) here just like at any other Starbucks location. This location isn't one where you would be likely to sit and enjoy your beverage: instead you are expected to buy one and go elsewhere to enjoy it. On my most recent visit, the staff member serving me didn't understand what I wanted until I rephrased it twice and even after I answered her questions she provided me a slightly different product than what I requested (which really wasn't a problem but it was rather odd). I do appreciate having access to Starbucks in this location but I wish the service were better, especially when it is summer and not very busy, and that there were more of a seating area available.
Friendly staff but filthy. Orders often wrong. Few comfy chairs. The best part are the people who camp out in the ladies room.
Twice going here for iced coffee it is either to sweet or not enough. It doesn't come out the same each time this pisses me off my coffee is as sweet as a soda this seams like it was on purpose
busy busy busy I always use my app to get stuff here.   cause man there is a line. but thats a good thing. standard starbucks filled with customers and great employees.
Convenient location & had coffee to dose up the mom for mother's day. They even wished her happy mother's day. Prompt service, friendly & plenty of pop cakes (my fave!!)

8am mobile ordered that was prepared promptly! That's incredible considering my order & how busy they were.  I ordered something in error & they happily corrected my mistake & no cost.   Great staff & very pleasant!!!!  Items ordered: 2 cheese danishes One banana nut bread Grande white chocolate mocha  Tall americano
Literally the slowest Starbucks I've ever been too. Both times I've been here. Don't go through this drive thru unless you have 30 minutes to spare.

Everyone knows Starbucks, and either you like it or you don't, so there's not much sense in reviewing the menu.  Well, EXCEPT!  I am currently OBSESSED with their chicken sausage breakfast wrap.  This wrap (which the location closer to my job is often out of by the time I get there) the drive through (which aforementioned location is also lacking), and the friendly workers make this Starbucks my first choice for breakfast on my way to work or a caffeinated pick-me-up when I'm tooling around town on the weekends.

Customer service was horrible today! Left my phone there today. Realized what I did and came back for it 20 minutes later. Baristas were totally uninterested in helping me when I asked if anyone saw my phone. It wasn't even busy. I called back later to ask again and whoever answered said it could be in the safe if it was there but he didn't have access to it. I asked who did and he said he didn't know and that they were extremely busy. Didn't ask me to call back or try to help at all. My phone was stolen at their location and no one really cared to help a customer out.  Let me clarify one thing again...not all of the baristas have horrible customer service. I'm talking about the ones that were working there on 12/2/2012 from 1:00-3:30.

Starbucks is Starbucks. I prefer the locally owned brews, however in a rush you can't beat the perfect same consistent coffee from Starbucks. Their 2am--2pm treat receipt is a great deal right now. (make a purchase before 2pm then show your receipt after 2pm same day to get any cold grande drink for $2)... And besides the coffee, I am really liking that Berry Hibiscus tea. I can't get enough of that this summer!

Today I received excellent customer service. Jerame took my order and educated me on the best possible coffee that would keep me up for school. He also greeted my father and I as soon as we walked through the door. Both Kyle P. and the other Kyle was polite as well. The coffee shop was spotless and so was the bathrooms!!!! A-Plus  to your staff on today! Exceptional customer service!! I am a very satisfied customer!

Not only is this the nicest Starbucks in Tucson (in my opinion), the baristas always greet everyone with a genuine smile and are friendly. This store is always busy but they manage to sling out orders in a timely manner.   Side note: Kudos to Scott for wishing me a good day in ASL. That made my morning!

So...I love this chain. The variety of quality coffee drinks, the app, the rewards. This store though... Need to prepare for busy weekends. I've been waiting for 30 minutes for online order. Unacceptable.

Very quick service. Very reluctant to get dark roast at any Starbucks especially at end of day. Usually taste burnt. Not at this Starbucks. Nice clean and fresh I got the Gold Coast dark roast. Will for that reason  and heat service make this a frequent stop when in the area.  Would of given 5 stars but parking is too limited

This starbucks is the worst ever.  I always order  from here yet almost every time I order food they say "we actually don't have that today"  they should always have food ready to go to a customer and not run out its just bad business. And to add their lines are always long I cant believe this location sometimes.

I only give this a 3 star because of the decor and location. I live in Wilmington, and until this opened only had the one on marsh road and the one in the Nemours building on North Orange (which have excellent Barista's and service - Not open on sunday). I digress.   When this place just opened they were severely understaffed with very untrained and unprofessional Baristas. Not only did you have a long time to wait, but then the attitude they gave you was unacceptable. Tonight I went there walked in and about 5 or 7 employee's where just standing laughing and talking conversing behind the counter. I actually felt like i was interrupting there conversation by placing a order. Same RUDE service. What is it with this location Starbucks why can't you hire QUALITY employees! I actually don't go to this Starbucks anymore despite how great it looks the service is just awful. I'd rather goto the one on orange or marsh road.

This location isn't bad. Usually the specialty drinks are made well. I've never had a bad drink from this location.  The drive thru gets ridiculous. Usually long and slow. I would avoid it unless you happen to get there when there's no line. Everyone seems friendly here, I've honestly never dealt with a rude barista at this store. The location is convenient as much as it is a pain. Getting out of Regency, unless making a right onto 60 from the side road next to the plaza, is so frustrating.  Parking is ample, no issues there. All in all, this is a good Starbucks. I'm a Starbucks addict with a Gold card since 2007 and a daily trio or 2 everyday of the week so my Starbucks review credibility is good lol Enjoy!

Serves it purpose when I don't have a Peet's to go to.  Service was fairly friendly. I just needed a pick me up to take on my drive back to SLO after hanging out in Santa Barbara all day. Starbucks coffee is just too weak to ever earn more than an average rating.

"COFFEE COFFEE COFFEE COFFEE COFFEE" - I chant to myself while I drive down Damonte Ranch Parkway.  I am only three minutes away from my office but I'm not walking into work without my coffee.    As I turn the corner coming out from the freeway underpass I pray that the line in the drive through isn't long.    Sometimes the line looks like this:  "SON OF A B&%#CH"   Sometimes the line looks like this:  "Whoo hoo!"  This Starbucks can be either incredibly busy or dead.  There is no rhyme or reason to it.  I have not been able to pin point a day of the week or a time of the day during which the peaks hit.    That being said - the line always moves very quickly.  This morning I had 9 cars in front of me and I was out of the drive through in under 4 minutes.  The baristas are always smiling, they give you napkins without having to ask and the breakfast sandwiches are always cooked perfectly (other Starbucks location in the area like to burn them).  This is one of my favorite Reno locations for sure.  I give this location an A+.  Don't be afraid of the line.
This Starbucks is way way slow..... I was disappointed in how long I had to wait. The service however was good - the person I came into contact with was pleasant.
Dirtiest Starbucks I've ever been in. Barista got my order wrong and was rude about fixing it.

I kind of want to give this Starbucks three stars, but it's thanksgiving... I'll give an extra one, and here's why...  The location is great. I happen to live in the area, and there isn't much here at all - so they have the monopoly. The staff is very friendly, and they usually are pretty well staffed. It's inside the Westin hotel. For me, parking isn't an issue because I usually walk there, but there are only three or four parking spots. I have occasionally stopped there on my way to work and asked the valet guys if I could just pull up. They are very generous and allow for that.  The downside - at times they can be a little slow. Since they're in a hotel, they'll often get large orders, and I've been behind that large order. No fault of theirs, but beware! Also, at times they don't adhere to their hours, and they don't have the "typical" Starbucks hours.   All things considered, this is a great Starbucks for people on rocky point!

Young lady with pink hair offered some of the worst customer service I've ever had at a Starbucks. "What you gettin'?" is probably not in the employee handbook of how you greet a customer when he walks to the counter to order.

This is a small Starbucks located in a shopping center. The staff were friendly and welcoming, also very fast and professional. I decided to go to this Starbucks because it as the closest in my travels and I was in dire need lol!   I ordered my favorite, the Carmel Macchiato. The barista had pre-made espresso. However, my drink was delicious, and not watered down as I expected since the espresso was pre-made.   If you're in the area and in need of Starbucks this place is cool to go to! It's cute, small and near other stores/restaurants! It's right next door to Qdoba!!!

I really like this location. It's located on the corner of Washington. The staff is very friendly and there's always plenty of seating. Definitely one of my favorites spots to stop in and drink a cup of coffee with a friend or to get some work done!
I loove this Starbucks. It may take a while, but everyone is really nice and it's great for people watching!
Ordered a quad shot latte with almond milk, due to my milk allergy. Confirmed when I placed the order and when I picked it up. Nothing marked on my cup. Spent my flight puking, thanks for the milk :(

Exceptionally poor service. While waiting, 3 orders were made completely wrong, including mine. Staff is unfriendly and very disorganized. Great attention paid to drive-up, while in store customers wait exceptionally long times. Have never seen so many employees in any location so inefficient. Store is in need of major management overhaul. Will visit Panera right down the street next time.

There are a few local places i frequent more often than Starbucks, but as a member I receive great rewards and discounts, so every once in awhile I'll pick up a venti nonfat double shot no foam this that or the other without breaking the bank.

The worst Starbucks. Ive ever been to. No problem with the employees. But, my latte was really bad, spinach wrap was black - it was so burned, there were no newspapers of any kind, there were no coffee stirrers available, internet was beyond slow and spotty.

Some of the previous reviews on here or just plain silly no one at Starbucks ever asks for a tip. Yes it was a little on the slow side, but it's a small stand in a large mall food court . Its pretty much like every other Starbucks very busy and very efficient.

I've been to many Starbucks in my life and this one was the most disgusting. Don't go to this Starbucks! Not only are they slow but the area in which they make drinks was filthy. So was the restroom. It was disgusting! I also watched a barista make four fraps in one blender. I don't think they are supposed to do that.

We frequent this Starbucks because the staff is always very polite, cheerful and seem to all be having a great time! and generally very quick with the service, too.  Thanks you for your cheer and smiles!!!  and the coffee, too!! :)

I come to this location very often!! It's right on my way to work on 96th st. I usually come in the morning and go through the drive thru. Usually this lady Laurie is working! She is the absolute sweetest!!!! She always seems to be happy even like today when there is a drive thru line soooo long!  I have never had any problems at this location! Very spacious! Also has lots of outdoor seating!!!!

The worst caramel macchiato I've ever had. I am a frequent Starbucks drinker and caramel macchiato is my go to drink before work almost every morning. I was on a road trip from Tennessee to Chicago stopped by this Starbucks location to pick up my regular order and I don't know how they messed up one of their most popular menu items but it taste disgusting! I'm not sure if the milk is bad or the coffee was watered down but it does not meet Starbucks standards. I have never felt the need to leave a bad review anywhere until now. So disappointed!
This Starbucks is great! They always pour my coffee to perfection. Bake my breakfast sandwiches to delight. Always happy in the morning. Great customer service. This is my spot!
Great spot for morning breakfast and coffee.  Usually very busy but quick moving. Overall good service and drive through usually moves well.
Lots of room to work. Not very busy at 8:54 am. Friendly staff. Loved my first dirty chai!
It is a Starbucks, you know what you get from a Starbucks, but the service here is always 10/10. Even at 6 am the employees are smiling and always greet you! Great experiences at my local Starbucks.

By far the worst starbucks on the West Bank. My drink is made wrong every time and the baristas are extremely rude. Not one person smiling or even attempting to pretend they want to be there. The manager is even worse, she not only is nasty with her employees but customers as well. This is not the customer service starbucks claims it has and they should be ashamed of themselves for letting this woman treat people so badly

I really hope Yelp doesn't kick me off for reviewing Starbucks. But, to be fair, it's the LOCATION that I am reviewing, and not just Starbucks in general. Let's be clear.  I just love these guys. They are always super duper friendly and I've never had a bad experience. I usually rock the drive-thru (please don't kick me off, Yelp), but the few times I have been inside has always been pleasant. And not just the "Thanks for coming!' pleasant, I'm talking light banter pleasant. I love light banter. Especially when it's done right. And now that I'm pregnant and have a laundry list of a single coffee order, they never seem to judge me. It's ok, I'm judging myself for the grande iced decaf nonfat vanilla latte.  The only reason for the 4 instead of 5 stars is that sometimes their service is a tad on the slow side. But even this is ok with me. Once, when I thought I was having my first bad experience there because the dude was taking forever, it was actually him remaking my drink because he couldn't be sure he'd used decaf. And to top it off, he just gave me the "screwed up" drink in case I knew someone at work that would want it.   Damn I love these guys at the COLLINSVILLE Starbucks.

Starbucks on South Street. They're a standard Starbucks and the people-watching is good. They've got a couple tables outside and Whole Foods is across the street. Never had any complaints about this place, but I don't get complicated drinks for the most part. Coffee or the salted caramel hot chocolate. Mmmm.

I think it is because we spend so much money for coffee these days (it's amusing to think about the times in the 1920's when you could buy a cuppa joe for 5 cents!) that places like Starbucks, Peets, Blue Bottle and Handlebar are judged on another level: a higher level than most places you can buy an espresso drink or a coffee. This makes sense and this rings true. My experience with this La Cumbre 'bucks has only been positive. From the manager to the Baristas and everyone in between the service and interaction between the employees and myself has been pleasant, fast, committed. There are a number of bad reviews about this place yet i have never received bad service here and i have been here maybe 100 times over the last several years. I have to be honest and say something that i have been wanting to say here on Yelp for a long time:  you must take control of the service you get in a place in which you are paying good money; particularly when the people behind the counter are young people who are in the work force for the first time. I say all of this to counteract, even in a small way the bad reviews of this Starbucks in my adopted city of Santa Barbara. If i have an issue with a 6 dollar cappuccino, i speak up and they will make it over. "They" can like it, they can not like it. Not my issue. I am the customer. I am the one paying. Typing this here it does sound a little harsh but in reality, is it really? I am polite yet firm.  In any event this Starbucks unit is one of my faves along with that in La Canada, Studio City and the drive thru in south Glendale. The people here are, once in a while mesmerized by the beauty of SB, the beachiness, the surfer-like paradise and the loveliness of the land  but a little firmness in how you order and how you react to bad service goes a long way. I mean paying $2-5 plus for coffee is a serious thing.

Best Starbucks customer service ! This is the first time I come to this location and the stuff members are awesome ! We went through the drive-through and the girl on the speaker understood my fiancé accent , he usually always struggle , also the lady at the window was very nice and caring! I really liked coming here !

I don't like whats happened at this location. Why not treat all customers the same. If no one was bothering anyone then what was the big deal. Everybody sit and mind their business and relax at coffee shops and when the entire group is there then people will order something together , nobody likes to order or start a meal when a member isn't present yet. That's kinda rude. I really just want all my money back that's loaded to my card because at this point I do not want to visit places like this. I Just can not support the disease called racism.
A simple espresso over ice took 10 minutes. I was the only one waiting for a drink. Strange. Not sure what was happening.

Don't go to this Starbucks. It sucks. The decor is nice but it's always super packed and the baristas do a very crappy job with your drink. Not only that but they don't have some of their advertised drinks in stock -_- I ordered a grande caramel macchiato and I opened the lid because it felt lighter than usual and yes it was not a "grande" probably just a tall in a  grande cup...to top it off it looked really gross. Go across to the street to beyond bread or anywhere else

How many people standing around does it take to make a strawberry smoothie? There were at least 7 ppl standing behind the counter staring at you first off. I felt like a chicken dinner. End result this was the worst strawberry smoothie ever from starbucks.

Like another reviewer said, it is very hard to review chain restaurants do to their corporate standards that each restaurant must follow. I will say that there was a mistake in the ordering process when I went. I ordered a peach green tea and waited for it. I never heard it being called but they gave it away to someone else who had been standing there. That made me a little upset because how could you possibly miss or forget the person who ordered something five or 10 minutes later? The woman who was working at the counter made up for it by giving me a large peach green tea instead of the small that I ordered. I guess it was her way of saying sorry about that. I really appreciated it! I also met some medical students while I was studying there and we had some interesting conversations. That's the nice thing about the Starbucks. It's right across the street from the medical school campus so there are a lot of other medical students studying there if that's what you're interested in or if that's the crowd that you like to hang out with. I also like to bounce between getting drinks there and going to the chipotle a couple doors down.  The parking is so convenient. There is a very nice and clean parking garage right across from it. Not sketchy at all

This location is probably my favorite location so far. For being in the heart of Downtown St. Pete, it is never overly-crowded, which I definitely appreciate, seeing as I'm usually needing to sit down and do some work. I'm not sure if it's because of my purple hair (it probably is), but the staff seems to remember me, which just gives this place a home-y kind of feel.  As for the menu, it's standard. Just like every other Starbucks.  The reason I go to this Starbucks instead of any of the other locations in St. Pete is because it is clean and a friendly environment.
visiting from LA for the Hot Air Balloons, I stopped at this location about 5 am and the service was amazing, I will comeback next year to this location.

Friendly service. Coffee was good. Online order to 20+ minutes. Watched people walk in, order and leave before me. Only one person working on online orders and several for the counter and drive thru. Lesson learned, online order is low priority at this location.

Typically I avoid drive thru lines when they are really long but this place was so fast & on point with my order it was crazy. There was at least 10 cars ahead of me & I kid you not I waited only two minutes for my order. My Carmel macchiato was so good I finished it within minutes. I'll definitely be back!

The guy taking my order in the drive thru messed up my drink. I don't know if he heard the right thing or not. When I told them that wasn't what I ordered, I heard the guy say "what does she want now?" in a bitchy tone. Umm, rude much!

Pretty typical Starbucks.  Comfy sit down area, usually full of people studying, chatting, or on their computers.  The free wi-fi is nice and the location is great.  Nice place to hang out, check your email, or pick up a quick drink and a snack.

Every time I come here, I plan to wait. This is by far the slowest Starbucks in the area. Today I skipped drive through and went inside to order my coffee, and discovered the source of the slowness. Staff are moving at snail speed! They show no energy and perform every task in slow motion. So frustrating. I get it...food service isn't fun, but take some pride in your service. I have 2 stars because the drink tasted great...15 minutes after I ordered it.

I gotta give a shout-out to my favorite Starbucks location. Staff are very friendly and have been very accommodating during the pandemic. I always leave here feeling satisfied and well taken care of by your staff.

I really like the layout of this Starbucks. It has ample seating for the area and lots of different options. I have found myself here more frequently recently since I've had numerous business meetings in the area. It is always busy but not crazy. I also appreciate how tidy the bathrooms are!

Poor layout. And how do you run out of your signature pumpkin spice syrup? Not one of their better locations. I hope they consider some improved operations.  I go to Starbucks for consistency. If they can't do that they have little to offer. We have awesome independent coffee shops in the city, I'll be spending more time there going for forward.

I would give half a star if i could.. this starbucks should be named starSUCKS!! went in around 6pm- slow time of night.. waited 20 min for a drink b/c they never filled out a cup for me... then the barista said "settle down folks i'm working on one machine with 20 cups"... well where was my cup???? i expect better service from an institution such as u of a! 20 min for a cup of coffee!! could have brewed one myself.... disappointing.

It's high time I reviewed my work neighborhood Starbucks. Over the last 6 yeas there have been periods of time where I've been here at least once a day. Sometimes I just need to get the heck out of Dodge so my friend and I walk over from the office. Then there are the "I'm starving because I didn't have breakfast" days, and the "I am in serious need of caffeine days" and the "I'm totally craving something sweet" days or maybe even the "I didn't bring lunch and don't feel like driving anywhere days".   I will admit that sometimes the staff isn't always overly friendly right up front; however, I've definitely seen improvement in this area over the last 6 months. Lately I don't think I've walked in the door that I haven't been greeted. They are always friendly at the counter and sometimes even remember our usual orders. My friend and I do our best to provide comedic relief for them. Most of the time we get a laugh. There was the whole "hot morning buns" conversation with the guy at the register once but maybe that story is better left for another time. I don't think they've ever messed up my drink order. They are patient and accommodating when my friend wants a chai tea latte made with a tea bag rather than the mix, and I want a caramel machiatto made with coffee rather than espresso.   As for the consumables, the coffee is still Starbucks coffee - enough said. I do like the tea offerings. I also like the breakfast sandwiches. The pastry case, as in any Starbucks, can be hit or miss.   I border on the Yay! I'm a fan, but then I ask myself "if there was another option within walking distance would I still choose  this Starbucks?" Probably not, but this place is A-OK.

I stopped by this Starbucks on my way to work today and I left very happy for the first time.  They must have fixed the machine to pull correctly because my coffee was great.   The watered down flavor was gone and I enjoyed drinking it. The new Barista was very friendly and temped my coffee perfectly.  A couple more visits like this and I will give this Starbucks a 5.

my favorite location. they ran out of the breakfast sandwich i wanted and it wasn't updated on the app. the barista was super apologetic and overall very nice! it was fast and painless to order. my drink was delicious.

Excellent customer service - even when they're packed! The drive-thru line comes in waves, sometimes quick, sometimes not. I always order inside or via the app to make my visit quicker. But I'm always greeted with a hello and cheerfulness - even if it's behind a mask!

Well usually I have a great experience at Starbucks. Not this time. I ordered my Frappuccino on the mobile order. I showed up to pick it up, and 15 minutes later asked where was my drink. They said I'm so sorry I did not print out and she was happy to make it for me. She then finds the cup on the counter and says are you Mike, and I say yes. Apparently the other  gal started making it and got pulled off and had to go do something else and just left it there. Nobody finished it and my cup was just sitting there.  I used the Mobile app to save time, not to stand there for 15 minutes and wait for my drink.

This is one of the slowest sbuxs I have ever been to. They frequently mess my order and never repeat the drink order back to you. I go out of my way to go to the sbux at 71st and Binford because they are amazingly quick and so efficient.

Friend service and very knowledgable. Service is relatively quick most of the time. The crew is competitive here and like to challenge the others stores about things they do not know.  Today a gentleman named Brandon was working the drive thru, he was a very upbeat individual. My drink was made perfect. Keep up the great work Starbucks.
Why does it take forever to get out of this Starbucks? Why is it not possible to run, get a cuppa and be on my way?

Quick drive-through service, friendly staff.  Interior is nicely decorated, and there are several tables for those who like to enjoy a beverage outside.  You can get a decent egg salad sandwich here, too!

This is my favorite place for my Starbucks coffee and more. The manager and staff are very friendly and accommodating to me. Most of the time I use the drive thought that is  very  nice .  All of my visited here have been delightful and fun. The inside and outside is very clean.  The bathroom is unsex  and it's always clean too. I love excellent service. I 'm a person that will keep coming back when I'm treated with respect. I asked for suggestions on trying new products that I haven't try .( everyone of my  coffees and more  drinks has been great.) If you are in  the Ballwin area try the drive thought or just stop in for some amazing customer service while picking up your favorite coffee. I have use this location for my business meeting too. This location is very comfortable for all your needs.  Gloria~
Slowest Starbucks ever. No advantage to ordering with the mobile app, you will still wait a long time for your drink.
So small inside. The staff are very indifferent,  we are in plant city people, no room for snobbery!

Welcome to the schoolyard, where first impressions are all that matter. You trip on your shoelaces running down the hill and forever you'll be known as the kid who rolled headfirst down the playground slope. To that end, I agree with Kristine d.'s recent review of her interaction at this Starbucks.  If this Starbucks isn't careful, they'll forever be known as the location with that one employee who finds the most basic tasks too complex to complete. His name, according to the several grumbling regulars behind me in the growing line, is Alberto. He will perpetually ask you to repeat your order, to scan your phone again. It's not a bashful, flustered "Sorry, I didn't hear you." It's an expectant, dismissive, "Again? Speak up." There is no apology in his tone, only entitlement, as if by ordering a tall coffee we are standing in the way of his efforts to do as little work as possible.   It's always easy to tell when this particular employee is working register - which is all he ever seems to work - because the line will stretch twice as far as the average morning. Even his coworkers seem put-out by his enervated and apathetic pressing of buttons and mumbling of orders.   "So that's a grande mocha, which comes to a total of..."  "No," a customer says, "I ordered a tall latte."  The manager in the background rolls her eyes and offers an apologetic smile.

Drove thru for some tasties.  I think they forgot to put syrup in my macchiatto.  :(  Still she managed to get our complicated order right and they were quite Efficent.  It is Starbucks what is not to love?
By far my favorite coffee spot in the city. The staff is always friendly, always clean and fast service. I just love the atmosphere that the staff creates here.

My friend is a healthcare worker who serves trays to 50+ patients in a COVID infested hospital, putting herself at risk every day, while being even more at risk due to her asthma. I was very upset to hear she went to this Starbucks and they told her the free coffee for health care workers is only For nurses doctors etc, when she is making a sacrifice as well. This was very ignorant. She had to explain to them why she was a healthcare worker when they are the ones offering it?? She deserves better after the risk she takes everyday.

Earns a star for betting the only corporate owned Starbucks in City limits and thus earn and use rewards. Earns a star for its cleanliness.  Earns a star for staff being friendly.  Remove a star for closing at 5 PM weekdays.  Remove a star for BEING CLOSED ON WEEKENDS DESPITE BEING ONLY CORPORATE STARBUCKS OF THE CITY AND NEAR THREE HOTELS!!  Ever since the riots/looting of 2020 and pandemic closures, in Central downtown we have brew ha ha market st closed fully while the brew ha ha across from this sbux closes at 1 pm weekdays and all day weekends. The non corporate Starbucks is 7-2 weekdays only. IM coffee weekdays only (but HIGHLY recommend)   But joke is on them because the former Loma Cafe REOPENED to now be Milk & Honey Coffee and food and art and oh yeah IS OPEN on WEEKENDS.  But still, Milk & Honey is on 4th which means those in the teens have a long walk.  Wilmington, the dying city. (Also, never visit pine street unless you want to be shot)

The worst Starbucks I've been to. I never had a problem with a single Starbucks location until this one. I did a mobile order, and got there a couple minutes later. I took a seat in front of the pick up counter. I sat there for some time, and decided to check on my order. Turns out my Venti, Hot, White Chocolateuu Mocha was sitting there. Not at the pick up counter, but next to the machines.... forgotten. When I finally get my drink, I realized my venti cup, was filled as if it were a grande. Nice. Took a sip and sure enough. It had gone cold. While I was waiting, I noticed someone come in saying their food order was wrong. The floors were dirty, and the staff wasn't the most inviting. Will not be returning.

The staff is great. God bless them for putting up with all the homeless people that seem to linger all day long, hogging up all the seating and adding an unpleasant odor in the air.  One that actually overcomes the beautiful smell of coffee that you would Expect to smell in a Starbucks. Where is the manager? Why isn't this place in better shape considering its central location ?
located in a strip mall in Montecito it's a larger Sbx - the emplyees are genuinely nicew and the clients are kooky!
Used the mobile app for ordering and they forgot the Carmel syrup. Thought the app would save time but not when they can't get your order right
Awesome Starbucks location, great staff and friendly place to get a coffee and hang out. Great new Starbucks location, check it out.
I love Starbucks.. you could say i'm a starbucks freak.. but this location tends to not satisfy my drink.. 5 out of 10 times they get it perfect..

This is the best coffee shop in Nashville. The crew here knows what they are doing; they'll take good care of you. They are fast, courteous, and service-oriented. They make good coffee, always fresh, always hot.   It is worth your while to make your way over here for the best coffee in town. It shares parking with several shops, so just grab a spot in the garage behind the building, and come in the back door.

Best starbucks around!! Even if Im at giant down the street that has starbucks inside, I make the effort to stop here instead because the staff is amazing and makes the best coffee! I come here all the time and they all know my name, my favorite drink, and even know if i want decaf/caf depending on what time i come in! They always have bright smiles on their faces, and ask about my day, I can say a few of them are practically my friends i talk to them so much when Im there. They will always make the drink exactly how you like and are happy to fix it if not to your liking. Best baristas around! That is for sure!

Great location in the business district.  I was staying at the Ritz a couple blocks down for a work meeting so I'd walk over in the morning to get some coffee before the day started.  The staff was friendly every time I was there, and when I redeemed my birthday drink one morning the cashier gave me an extra smile and wished me a happy birthday, which was a nice little touch.  Beware, though the lines are long.  The first morning I went I think I waited almost 10 minutes to order my drink and then nearly another 10 to get my drink.  They were working fast, there was just a lot of people ordering drinks and food.  I cut down on some of that the next day by using mobile order and pay, so I'd order when I left the hotel, but even still I had to wait a few minutes once I got there for my drink.  It wasn't a huge deal, and once I knew I planned for it, but the lines were long every morning I went, so be prepared.

Worst Starbucks ever. I stood there for several minutes before I was even asked if I needed something. You can watch the employees and see they don't even talk between themselves.  The mood is very depressing. Take my advice and drive a few more miles to a Starbucks that cares about their customers.

I'm not a coffee drinker, but I love this location.  The staff is very friendly and I love the inside of place is awesome. There a long bench with charging posts (USB) and lots of maneuvering room. It's in a great location that Speedway has done a fine job developing!

They make great drinks here, but this is honestly the slowest location in the world!  Great people, and I never have to add to my drink when I pick it up.  I'm not sure if it's a staffing issue or what but every time I go there it takes forever.  You often can't make online orders there, which is severely needed since there is no drive thru.

It's convenient to have a Starbucks drive-thru on my way to work, but the wait can be pretty bad in the busy morning hours. I can't comment much on the staff or the feel of the inside because I only stop by the drive-thru@this location.

They just remodeled this Starbucks and its much nicer now. Very open and lots of big tables to share with fellow coffee shop junkies. I'm not a huge fan of Starbucks coffee, but since its basically across the street from where I live, it makes it very convenient when I need my caffeine fix. The staff is very friendly and accommodating.

The service was alright; nothing to write home about, but this review mostly deals with the upkeep of the shop. I was here on a Saturday evening when it was not busy. There was one couple sitting on one of the tables and the three male employees in behind the counter. The shop looked like it had not been cleaned all day. The bathroom was dirty and there was no toilet paper available. The counter was sticky and dirty. I have never been in a Starbucks so unkept before.

The rest of the reviews pretty much nail it. The parking is terrible, I know the layout was already in place when they bought the property, but there had to be a better way to put in that drive thru so the line wouldn't block parked cars. Plus, the traffic for the drive thru spills right in to the street.   The music is way too loud and the staff is not friendly. The last time I went inside it took about 5 minutes before anyone even spoke to us. The 2 people behind the counter were working on drive thru orders, but it doesn't take but a second to say "hello, I'll be with you in a moment". They do, however, have enough time to carry on personal conversations with each other while you're waiting.   I don't like giving bad reviews, but I've been here 5 or 6 times already trying to find something positive (and it's the closest coffee shop to my house). It's just the same every time.
