1st time here and will not return. Simple order "fell thru the cracks". Waited 20 minutes for two coffees. Expensive time wasting experience. Also got no receipt for my order or I would have asked for a refund.

Nasty help. I stepped inside the  door while putting my mask on and suddenly i hear this girl scream PUT YOUR MASK ON!!. Terrible extremists. I will not be back . You have to give a person the chance to put on the mask as you walk in the door. Also place is dirty and maybe they should be more concerned about spreading germs to others. Not a nice place to grab a cup. Go elsewhere!

Every single time I have come here, they are out of the salted caramel salt. I thought that maybe it was just a bad month, because it happened like 5 times in a row. But at this point it has kind of become comical. Don't ask for it, because they don't have it. Or maybe they don't like my face. The world will never know. That being said, I have rated this 5 stars and will continue to pay outrageous prices for sweet delicious coffee.

Always very friendly people working and they heat the croissant better than any other location to where it's actually toasted and not just warm. The Lattes have a great foam, too! The interior and exterior of this Starbucks also get two thumbs up. I'll keep coming back to this location.

Surprisingly the store is actually pretty big. I love that they keep the restrooms locked but also very nice tidy neat and clean. Their staff are very friendly and patient. I love that the customers are also patient in return considering we all still have to mind the covid 19 health measures. My order was taken very easily and payment method was my Starbucks app on my phone. I had no problems. My order was made quickly and correctly. The only thing that was a little confusing for the staff was that I had ordered a drink that typically came with black tea but instead I requested their passion tea or passion tango tea. No matter, they were able to figure it out and make my drink very quickly. Delicious.

Knowledgeable barristas who don't hesitate to give you good service; its what you really are paying for at Starbucks - not the coffee :) They are always busy, but being so close to the movies and off the highway has that effect.

This review is for the new (2017) Starbucks that opened in the Town & Country crossing shopping center. Featuring its signature tea and coffee drinks and sandwiches/pastry case fare, this location also includes a drive-thru. The baristas are friendly, albeit beleaguered when orders at the drive-thru pile up. They were out of the egg and turkey bacon sandwich when I came here, so went with a gluten free option that was still tasty. The interior is dark and a bit smallish in comparison to other Starbucks so probably more of a grab-and-go option.

Patio overlooks a parking lot.  However, the beautiful Pusch Ridge overlooks that stark parking lot.  The lines are typically short, since the OV is sparsely populated.  It's Starbucks-- you know what to expect

Don't arrive on a Sunday when it's not busy.  The only visible barista was lounging at the drive thru window chatting up some guy.  She seemed to think that her conversation was more important than a customer.  Asked me to wait. I left with a photo that will find its way to the manager. Giving it one star for looking like a Starbucks even if they don't sell coffee.
They need to figure out how to serve customers that only order a pastry.  It's not rocket science!
the latte was the worst I've ever had in my life..  . I don't normally frequent starbucks but for a chain they make somewhat decent coffee... .  I even got 2 shots and it still just tasted like milk

Everyone knows what Starbucks offers at this point, so I am comparing this location to others.  The parking lot parks four cars and is super cramped so you will most likely need to find parking on the street or one of the neighboring lots.  It was impossible to get a seat and the people that were there looked like they were camped out.  There was literally a knitting club there that had earmarked about 20% of the store's "real estate" to sit and chat and knit.  Very annoying since I just ordered a Chai tea and had no place to sit. The person who served me was polite enough but if you are looking for a place to sit head down the road to the Starbucks in the Kohl's shopping plaza - - better energy and lots of seating.

They are always super friendly and really good at completing your order properly. The Internet is usually really good. The patio has loads of seating and a handful of outlets. The inside is usually busy with some seating and a lot of outlets and aggressive CA (bring a sweater).

SERVICE Rocks!!  Stopped at this Starbucks location after checking into the Hampton Inn just a few blocks own 34th Street and my wife wanted an Iced Soy Latte.   Despite being close to 4 pm the store was crowded but the line moved very quickly. The gentleman working the counter was super friendly and helpful, they did not have any dark roast brewed and I ordered a Pike but he virtually insisted he would make any pour over I wanted. I ordered a Sumatra and he had it ready for me very fast while keeping the rest of the line moving.   Very clean, well organized and comfortable store and SUPER clean including the rest rooms.   If every Starbucks was like this I would be a regular!

Ew. Inside was decent, but the outside area smelled like urine and there were multiple people walking around barefoot or sleeping in the chairs. I sat down without realizing that I was actually sitting in a chair that stank of  dried pee. Never going back there.

This is my coffee shop to work at daily inside or on the patio. The employees are always so nice (in addition to the amazing drinks). Recently, I left my purse on the patio when I was moving inside with my computer bag. They were so kind and quick to take it and hold It in the back for when I realized by mistake I left it. Great employees at this place.
Condescending flamboyant employees. Out of specials every time I ask. Hate this place.
It's a Starbucks in kop what do you expect ... I like the one on gateway shopping center but that's me. Love the mobile app,
The drivethru is soooo sloooooowww....I've actually left this line and driven to the Starbucks on 301 just to save time.

Pretty good Starbucks despite the sometimes questionable location. I never know with south st..you either find a gem or you come across crazies.  The staff here are very nice in the mornings, so I can't vouch for the later times. They're friendly and professional and the wait is not bad compared to other Starbucks locations. They also make my drinks right. Parking is a hit or miss but in the mornings it's no problem !

Good service...inside moves faster than the drive-thru in the AM. They make some of the better Chai Tea Lattes. But my reason for three stars is that they are consistently out of the Petit Vanilla Bean scones by 8 am! I can understand being out around late morning, but 8 am? Stock more, people!

It's Starbucks so you know the consistency is always there.  There was no line and the gentlemen taking the orders was fast so I appreciated that.  The only thing that irked me is two people behind me got their blended drinks before my quad espresso with no milk. Really? Ok. Well, either way it's cheaper here than el colombe around the corner.

I was informed that the girl that my complaint was about in my previous review no longer works there anymore. It's a good thing bc she will definitely drive customers away! If I'm in the area, I will stop by! Otherwise I will stick to my Starbucks inside target location!

Located right down stairs from the American Airlines club lounge which made it very convenient for us to head down and grab us some of our fav drinks while waiting for our flight.  Walked right up w/no crowd to place our order -- friendly staff and drinks were wonderful.
It's fine if you want a run of the mill starbucks item. If you want an amazing coffee look down the street for Volo.

normally I do not go to any Starbucks but I am meeting a friend here and I've checked the place out and it seems to be ran pretty smoothly the staff was friendly and courteous.   I think the only thing that needs some help is the bathrooms and they should probably open the canopy covers outside because it's Florida and is about a hundred plus degrees outside other than that it's a great little spot to relax
17 minutes in the drive-through ridiculous I'm going to Jupiter donut to get better coffee a better service. 4:53 pm 12-21-18

Its a starbucks, so unless you have been living on Mars there should be no need to describe much. However,  what makes this location stand out more than the average location... they have great consistency. My drink tastes the same almost everytime, and its good.

This is a great Starbucks location! Different seating options (sofa, tables, bar stools), Clover brewing for signature "Starbucks Reserve" coffee from around the world, free wifi and a drive-thru. On top of that, nearby eating options make this the perfect place to sit down and have a drink after your meal (if you don't want to eat at Starbucks, that is). Definitely recommended. Oh, and since they brew Starbucks Reserve, they also have reserve coffee bags for purchase.
Waited 20 min for an iced coffee.  People came and went and I still couldn't get my drink.  Terrible service.
Does it get any better great coffee and books? Standard Starbucks fare and good staff.

Starbucks is so over priced it's ridiculous. That aside, the oral Valley Starbucks has now changed the bathrooms to be coed. It's disgusting to have to put the seat down every time you go to the bathroom. Starbucks bathrooms in general are filthy. I give most of my business to Quiktrip. Inexpensive fresh brewed teas and super clean bathrooms that are separate for men and women. Give it a try.

Mornings are always insane at this location. I work in the area so this store is my go-to. This particular morning was chaos. I placed a mobile order thru the app and it said the 1 drink I ordered would be ready in 5-8mins. Incorrect. It should have been updated to reflect the circus show going on in the store and said your drink will be ready in approx 50mins-never!!!!! That's how long I had to wait until I realized I was never going to get my drink. Everyone was looking at each other and stating how crazy this was! Others had been waiting just as long and the frustration in everyone's faces was brewing. The staff was calm and really tried to maintain some sense of order. There was a girl going thru the older mobile orders stacked up on what appeared to be a book shelf. She tried her best to pull orders for ppl waiting but at some point I wised up and asked for a credit because I couldn't wait any longer. They issued a refund on my mobile app and a credit towards my next order. The 2 stars   goes to the recovery effort.

Nice little location by the Eller house. Staff seemed a bit new and explained my drink selection (French vanilla latte) is usually three shots of flavoring, and asked me how I wanted to split the hazelnut and vanilla shots. Which I thought was weird, but what do I know.  The latte was lacking, which is why I knocked the one star off. I found a table and used wifi for work and making work calls and it was easy.

This is my favorite Starbucks in Reno!-I am greeted by the friendly employees every time I walk in, they promptly get my order in the car line or walk-in, I have been happy with my coffee, and they are always quick to right an order on the rare occasion there is a mix up.  My hubby and I go there 4 days a week-When we could sit inside I liked that they have a large seating area

Starbucks is Starbucks, that much we know. So what sets each cafe apart is how well they make their drinks, and how good their service is.  For being a busy cafe in the heart of Clearwater Beach, the crew here sure do know how to pump out their orders quickly and accurately while being courteous simultaneously. And whenever I have issues with my orders, they're quick to fix - of course, I'm courteous to them which, really, is all they ask for. Because when you're on the beach, it seems like anyone who isn't a local are always rude.

Yes, I know, there is a Starbucks practically at every corner or major intersection.  This one; however, is my go-to Starbucks on the way to work.  Yes, it is packed every day.  Yes, parking is a nightmare and getting back onto Hillsboro (especially since I have to make a left) can be a challenge.  But it is a consistently decent cuppa joe and although it is busy, the line moves rather efficiently and quickly.  There are days I don't feel hip enough to be in there and there are also days I seem to be the customer not wearing pajamas - but that's really neither here nor there - just an observation.  Is Starbucks my favorite caffeine spot?  No.  But if certainly beats the office grind (pun most definitely intended).

Starbucks is about at typical and predictable as it gets.  This location is nice, clean, pretty spacious, the drive thru is always speedy, the staff is always happy to serve you (even at 6:45 am- I don't know how they do it).  My drink almost always tastes the same- which is nice when a tall skinny vanilla latte is a staple in my diet during days when I have more tasks than time.    If you're in the Brentwood/ Rock Hill area, this is the Starbucks I'd reccommend.  The one on Brentwood Blvd is kind of a logisitcal nightmare in terms of getting in/out and finding a place to park.    Also, DO NOT order the new Vanilla Spice Latte.  It tastes like your grandmother's perfume.  It's bad... really bad.

Really a horrible experience. I came here 20 mins before my shift because i know it's busy. I waited 25 mins for my one drink before asking if they had my order. The lady said no, and then went back to making drinks. A different employee then approached me and asked if i needed anything, and i asked her also if my order was coming up soon. She said they did not have my order, even though i placed it in person. They gave me a refund, and after checking my account it appears that instead of refunding me they charged me again. I will not be returning to this location.

I was the only one in the drive thru.  Through the window I could see several employees just standing around which leads me to believe that they weren't busy inside. All I ordered was a kids hot cocoa and it still took well over 5 minutes for me to get my drink. When it came out it wasn't even warm it was cold. Don't think I'll be visiting this Starbucks again.
Close to the Free Library (which has its own little cafe inside -- great but tiny and fills up quickly)

Usually pretty fast and I have never had any problems here. 4 stars for a Starbucks doesn't ever make much sense, but this is the most convenient drive through when I find myself driving a lot during the work day. That means I end up coming through this drive through pretty regularly. With that regularity, you would expect at least one issue. Never ever!   McDonalds next door is constantly slow, but these workers must actually be drinking that coffee they are brewing, and even if the McDonald's workers are, that stuff just is not of the same quality, I don't are what the commercials say. The staff is fast and friendly.   Whether it is a White Chocolate Mocha Frappe with extra shots of espresso or just a regular coffee with cream and some espresso shots, it will do the trick and it never takes long.
Despite the fact that this place is insanely busy the staff is cool and makes a good cup

I used  to think all Starbucks were the same. But boy was  I wrong. I have come to this one several times before but mostly just through the drive through. Nothing to brag about but I did get what I ordered until last time. I did my order on my phone and already pre paid so all I had to do was run in and grab it off counter. I was in hurry and on my way to the other side of town. I never order whip cream on my Fraps so I didn't notice their was no Carmel on my blended frap. As I'm driving down the freeway I took my first sip and did not taste any caramel so I looked at my cup and did not see the usual caramel stickiness on the bottom or sides so I called the guy to let them know my Caramel Macchiato did not have any Caramel in it. He told me to take the rest of my coffee to another Starbucks and they will fix it for me. Like I had time to go to another Starbucks with my half drank frap to ask someone to make it right. I was shocked at such bad service from a 6 dollar drink that they messed up. I have never been back to this location since. And no. I did not take my half drank Frap to another Starbucks either.
Typical Starbs but hit and miss if you get your danish within a reasonable time. Friendly baristas.
Decent Starbucks but definitely would be better if they had a staff that actually knew what they were doing and you didn't have to explain to them what kind of drink that you wanted....

I'm wondering why the service at so many Starbucks in Tucson is deteriorating. I haven't been to this location for 6 months. I met a friend on a Sunday afternoon which is not a busy time. Only two people working which isn't a problem as I got my drink timely. The reason for my rating is the young man who made my drink said nothing and didn't even look at me. He said nothing! The truth is it takes the same amount of time to say hello and thank you as it does to ignore your customer completely. I will choose other Starbucks locations.

I am an avid starbucks drinker. Not a day goes by that i DO NOT drink starbucks at least twice. The East Norriton location is the closest to my house but i go out of my way to go to the Plymouth Meeting location because the East Norriton location is constantly disappointing. Yet again today, My husband comes home with our coffee and it is wrong. The flavor is missing as are my additions that I ordered every day on my app.  This is not only the LEAST friendly starbucks I have ever been to, but I have never seen a manager even present out in the front and when your coffee is wrong you always get the eye roll. It is poorly managed and the staff never stay. In Plymouth Meeting I know the manager by first name and see her almost everyday because she is out supporting her team!  Coffee cannot cost this much and continually be wrong at the east norriton location. It really needs an overhaul!

Not much to review ... it's a Starbucks; what can you say, except to be glad they have one in the mall! You get your drink, and hope it's up to par. If not, they'll make you another one. So relax, and hope they get your drink right. Also, prices are a little higher than other stores, the barista couldn't explain why. But that's ok- maybe they're a privately operated.   There are few tables and chairs, so it is convenient to stop, have coffee, and strategize shopping plans.   They use the app, and it's convenient.

6/26/2018:  When traveling somewhere around the Planet, there WILL BE several Starbucks franchises practically everywhere in every city from Reno, Carson City, Minden, California, New York-all fifty US States, Canada, Alaska, Singapore, Philippines, South Korea, Macau, Hong Kong, Japan, Thailand, Malaysia, Europe,   etc..  I like the Starbucks' ice cream scooper I bought in Beverly Hills CA a few years ago; however, while traveling to Lost Wages, I put my prized Starbucks traveling, stainless steel, coffee press on a table, and I turned the other way. . .  Ppppppfffffttt~GONE!  The nerve of people taking my coffee press inches from my elbow!  Well~It's time buying a new cold coffee beverage container . . . I just wish the one I bought today was safe keeping hot coffee inside but, oh, well . . . NOT.  AND~The number one reason I keep returning to Starbucks:  Drumroll, please . . . Peppermint coffee with a double shot of espresso surely makes me into a nervous Chihuahua~LOL!  Arf-arf-arf!!!!
Ordered tall coffee got just half a cup instead. Ordered muffin which felt like a couple of days old probably...
The staff was amazing!! They took extra time with my nephew and made him feel so special!  Parker and I will definitely be back, cause Seth was wonderful with us!

This Strabucks is inside the Target on City Ave.  I am not a coffee drinker however every so often I get the hankering for a yummy chai tea latte.  As I meandered up to the counter I couldn't help but hear three employess in a heated conversation.  I suppose I wouldn't have minded so much if they were helping me at the same time but I was disregarded for several moments.  I finally placed my order for a tall, skinny (made with skim) chai te latte.  It is my habit to always ask if it is made with skim because the consequences of me having whole milk can be quite detrimental! So once my order was made I asked  "Made with skim?" to which the barrista replied "did you ask for it with skim?" I looked at him like he was crazy because he was rude and just assumed that I didn't!  In any event, I told him I had and he made it correctly.  When it was ready though he just placed it down and walked away.  So overall the service here leaves something to be desired.  On a plus note, there is ample seating as well as free WiFi.

I love Starbucks! I've been a fan since the chain started popping up in Indianapolis. This location is small and cozy. The staff is always friendly and fast. If you're new to Starbucks or want to try something new, I recommend the Green Tea Frappuccino, Mocha Frappuccino, or Pink Drink. For food I would recommend the Chicken Caprese or Chicken & Double Smoked Bacon for lunch. Then the Chicken Sausage & Bacon Biscuit, Sausage Cheddar & Egg Breakfast Sandwich, or Classic Oatmeal for breakfast.

Was really excited when this Starbucks opened because it was so much more convenient than the Glasgow location.  Was hoping as time went on, they would work out the kinks and get in a groove.  Couldn't have been more wrong.  I almost always have to repeat my drink order 3 times because the person taking my order is also making drinks.  They are constantly running out of something...brews, cups, etc.  Today my egg bites came in an oatmeal cup.   I thought it was oatmeal so I circle back and go in only to be told "we were out of the egg bite trays..we should have told you".  They never have enough staff and the drive thru takes forever.  Find another Starbucks on your route and skip this one.
Mass confusion... no names on cups and with a long line hard to tell who is getting what and in which order. A few people working, no organization
Just a few minutes away and always quick (for Starbucks). If they do mess up they let me keep the mistake drink and make another right when I ask them.

Frequent here. There is usually a line in the drive thru and subsequent wait. Today was a it long though I was the only car in line. Customer service was poor today as well. When I did get my non specialized cold beverage the cashier did not look at me and leaned it our the window half tilted. This rubbed me the wrong way. Service is normally better. Perhaps he was having a bad day.  Inside the store it is clean and cozy. The bathrooms are also very clean.

This place is the worst Starbucks I have ever been To. The manager Scott work behind the counter like a he knows what he is doing but for all the moving around. The coffee maker has no drinks on it and he is not getting anything done. I have waited several time for up to twenty to twenty five minutes for a single drink. At least once a week my ices coffee is hot. It just not a good place .

Friendly staff, awesome Barista, very helpful staff who I notice tries very hard to remember their customers and what they drink. I have never had an issue with them, and I have been going here several years. I sometimes come to study and it's always very comfortable. Will continue to go here, as long as they are here!!!

Pretty disappointed with this Starbucks. I moved to the north valleys a couple of months ago so I started going to this Starbucks. A couple months ago I stopped by close to closing to get a coffee because I was working the graveyard shift. I pulled up to the drive thru, placed my order, then the person who took my order told me "it's going to be a while because the person in front of you literally ordered 15 drinks." Closing was in 10 minutes, and there was no one else in line. They had the person who ordered all of the drinks park. What was stopping them from making my 1 drink? If someone orders 15 drinks in the drive thru, obviously they are a jerk and should wait....Why wouldn't you make the 1 drink first? The other person is going to have to wait forever anyhow. I didn't get coffee that night, I left.    This morning I went to this location to get coffee before work. I waited in the drive thru for quite some time without anyone taking my order. I decided to call the store, while I was in the drive thru and the employee who answered told me it would be about 15 minutes because they were running late. It was 04:45 and they were supposed to be open at 04:30. I'm not up at 04:45 because it's fun....obviously I don't have 15 minutes to wait around for coffee! :)   The time I spent sitting in the drive thru could have been spent getting coffee somewhere else. Starbucks is my go-to for coffee. It's usually convenient because it's a quick stop on my way to work. I go to Starbucks almost daily. As a result of the events that unfolded this morning, I didn't get my Starbucks, I am sipping on some crappy gas station coffee as I write this unfortunate review, and I was almost late for work :(    NOT COOL STARBUCKS...NOT COOL.  My other 97 visits to this location have been good but the fact that you were not there when I needed you this morning gives you 2 stars.

This is a great Starbucks.  The employees are good, and friendly, too.  The inside has the modern but comfortable look and feel of new Starbucks locations.  And they keep the place (including the bathrooms) clean. Most importantly (for the purposes of my Starbucks rankings), they have a number of electrical outlets, including a pair of outlets (plus a pair of USB charging ports) in each of the four legs of the communal table.  This location has a respectable food selection and the surprisingly good nitro brew.  They have consistently gotten my order right.  It's everything that I look for in a Starbucks.  FTR, they also have a drive-through.  I don't do drive-through anything, so I can't rate that, but they seem to manage it decently since it never spills over to cause problems for the customers inside.

Most of the barristas are friendly and competent however there is one female, who makes the absolutely worst iced tea.  For a venti 1/2 passion, 1/2 black iced tea she put in about 2 fingers of tea then about a cup of water, then iced and shook it.  It barely had any color!  When I questioned her on all the water she replied that it is made extra strong and must be diluted.  She was not pleasant.  The other employees are great but when I see this one making the coffee and tea, I leave and go next door to Dunkin.

Terrible, terrible service. I waited 20 mins in line at the drive through to arrive at the window to realize they had not captured my order. Matthew, a Starbucks employee, was extremely rude and told me he could not take my order at the window and that i would need to go inside. I explained I had an infant in the back seat and that is the reason why I didn't go inside in the first place. He finally agreed to take my order at the window but did it in the snottiness way possible. Starbucks typically has a friendly staff so I was super disappointed to run into such an unpleasant person at the edwardsville location.

I really like this Starbucks location.  It's quite small compared to others, but I find that this makes it feel cozier.  It's not usually too busy, either, so there's always a seat to be found, and I've never experienced a long wait.  The employees are super friendly and helpful, and they've always made my drinks exactly how I've ordered them.  The condiment bar is always stocked up nicely and is very clean, which is a huge plus in my book.  This is an overall great Starbucks!

Generally, always pleased with the service here. Always incredibly friendly.   Careful, though, don't over salt my Salted Caramel Mocha frappucino. Too much makes it sink to the bottom and can get overwhelming.   I wish this were a larger Starbuck's but they are clearly doing something right as this place is ALWAYS packed.

This Starbucks is located in Regency Center, and most of the time, entry and exit is smooth.  There is ample parking, but if you just wanted to hit the drive through, this location is pretty quick.   Other than that, it is your average expensive high calorie coffee joint.  So far, it's kept pretty clean, and the service is friendly.    My favorite is the Java Chip.  I get it every time I go to Starbucks, morning, noon, or night.  Yeah, it's a milkshake.  Don't judge me.
Just your usual Starbucks. Quick, expensive, and of course the obligatory people on there laptops seemingly deep in thought. No complaints here.

First and last time coming to this Starbucks. There are currently about 18 people waiting for their drinks while one person makes them and 2 people stand at the register with no one in line. Also no sense of urgency at all to get out drinks. Most Starbucks are usually not like this so it has to be poor management. 9 minutes to get an iced tea is outrageous, will try other Starbucks in the area until this one gets its act together.

I'm confused as to why this Starbucks doesn't have a complete 5 star rating!  For starters, there's actually a parking lot...in PHILLY! Mind you, it is shared with a few other stores and a TDBank, but dont worry, they wont tow your car for parking in their lot for 5 min. to run into the Bucks for a morning latte.  Secondly, this location received a complete reno, not so long ago.  The difference is great, and the flow of traffic to the register is much better.  The real reason I love this Starbucks however is the people.  The associates are so nice, know you by name, and can spell out what YOU want before you can tell them, simply because they remember.  Because of these reasons, I will go out of my way to stop there, even though there is a Starbucks 3 minutes from where I work. :)

I love Starbucks but specifically THIS one. Why? Well aside from the fact that my drink is always perfect the only other reason I go out of my way to come here is because of the staff. I have never before met people who consistently greet with you smiles at 7:30am and always leave you with a happy faces on your cup or some seemingly genuine words to get your day going. Thanks Starbucks staff, you always make my day.

Want to be late to class or an appointment? Come to this Starbucks.  You know you're desperate for GOOD coffee when you're willing to wait in a 20 minute line, when there are other places that sell coffee around campus.  The coffee, was good, the staff moved as quickly as they could, but they had one person working the register and a line of 50 people at a time.  Starbucks should hire more staff for this location to please customers and ease the burden on their employees.

The baristas are very nice, great environment and drinks are pretty good. However they don't seem to know how to make a lot of the drinks on the app. The other thing is they turn the AC wayyyy up and you end up freezing sitting in there longer than 5min.

Coffee culture used to be so much simpler. I like the phrase "redhotsteamin'cupOjoe'. I almost never use it, but I like it. Starbucks are fascinating. I'm not talking about the millions of variations of water run through the dried ground seeds of the ubiquitous Rubiaceae family of plants. I'm talking about the subtle variations how people move in and out of every Sarbucks in every city. The navigation of the que, where one stands or sits to wait for brew, how staff and regulars accommodate non-regular interlopers like I often am. Ok,,, maybe its not that fascinating.   Anyway, I appreciated this smallish Starbucks in the center of Philly. They moved people in and out real well and still we found a place to camp for an hour or so in front of a momentous meeting - sort of reminded me of the hallway scene in Pulp Fiction. Go figure...

I had absolutely no idea that this location was even here until I stopped by Cheeseburger Charley's. It was a lot larger than I thought it would be. But it didn't seem to have a lot of lounging room. I rate all coffee shops on how good they'd be for studying or reading. They had a lot of seating but it wasn't really a studying environment. I don't think I'll be going back.
We only have one kind of coffee right now and we're not gonna brew anything different, but there's another Starbucks 2 miles away.
If you are looking for a quick drive thru---keep driving. Slowest Starbucks drive thru in the area.

There are very few times where I think, "Man, this airport Starbucks is awesome!!!!" And this definitely wasn't one of those times. So cramped, so loud, so slow. It took the cashier like five minutes to take my order of two drinks and two sandwiches...... I was pleasantly surprised to see everything came out correct, honestly. After waiting ten minutes in this dirty location though, I can see why this place only has like 2 stars.

This is the fastest drive thru in America! It blows my mind. There can be a line of cars wrapped around the building at 8am and I have never waited longer than 5 min in the line. It's magical and everyone is always friendly. Nothing better than a delicious, well-made coffee, breakfast to-go and a friendly face telling you to have a fantastic day when you are on your way to work. Great place!
My favorite Starbucks in town. Near my Casa and is well staffed.  Even with a line they are quick and friendly.
The workers there seem to treat every customer with disdain. Every order was incorrect and they were soooooo slow. Terrible service.

Was diverted here after the location on Shadeland decided that even though they are posted to open at 6:00am on Sundays, that means they have the lights off & are still prepping at 6:15...while customers are driving away, including an Indy Metro Police cruiser...that's another review.  I pulled up to the drive they, placed my order & was on my way in less than 5 min. Awesome!

Never seen a 24hr. Starbucks before. Great stop on the way back home from a beach weekend in Clearwater. While our drinks were being made David was very friendly and chit chatted with us. We appreciated his humor.
A good little Starbucks inside of Target.  I have stopped here a couple of times and they have consistently done a good job at fulfilling my order to Starbucks quality.

Decent Starbucks. We got there 15 minutes before they were closing and it seemed like they were already closed. The food case was empty and the servers seemed bothered that we came in.   My colleague had a Medicine Ball and they didn't have enough tea bags to make it correctly. My coffee was old and I wish they would have said something about getting something other than old coffee.

Perfect location for those traveling on I-44. We were able to grab a sandwich at Jimmy Johns then head next door to Starbucks. The line was a little long, but it was the guy in front of me being difficult.

Need a caffeine fix before jumping on that flight? Well Starbucks has you covered at Terminal F (and all the other terminals, as well as the landside building). Staff is friendly and a limited selection of gift items are available for sale.   FYI, hours vary by location. Here's the breakdown:  Landside: 24 hours Terminal A: 4:15 AM to 9:00 PM Terminal C: 5:00 AM to 9:00 PM Terminal E: 4:30 AM to 8:00 PM Terminal F: 4:30 AM to 8:00 PM

Of all the Starbucks around Mill Woods this one is my favourite. Very cheery staff and it seems to move very quickly. The coffee and such is a normal Starbucks experience....good stuff. Only downfall is the atrocious entrance to the area through the parking lot. Constant traffic jam trying to get on/off of the main parking lot lane. Take the super wide route (turning right when you leave the drive through) to avoid the headache that will 100% happen if you that the jam route. Avoid the jam route I say.  TLDR - Great, avoid the jammy parking lot zone right by the entrance to the drive through by turning right when you leave the drive through and go over a few lanes to exit.
It's a college crowd. Obviously you're not here because you're looking for psudo-intellectual chat. You're at the Starbucks on Gay & High.

I've given this Starbucks multiple opportunities to redeem itself, and it has yet to do so. While the location is impeccable, products are inconsistent.   As someone who has been a barista, i can see drinks being made incorrectly. The staff as a whole is rude (a couple exceptions, but not enough to persuade me to go back).   The nail in the coffin was asking for a skinny drink, receiving a full fat/full sugar drink, and the barista wanted to argue about what i had asked for.   I get that this is a busy location, and i can overlook the occasional issue, but this location is consistently inconsistent.
Hey went threw the drive through at 6 am and the girl was super nice and made our drinks perfect

Yesterday around 4:40 pm I went in to buy a frappuccino, the guy who took my order was so rude. Making me feel stupid just because they weren't understanding me when I said I wanted light caramel drizzle.  Then the girl making the drinks joined in and kept going along with the guy the taking my order talking about how I didn't say it correctly.  I've never had this issue before at other Starbucks locations .   Don't plan on coming back to this location.

I love this location. I use the mobile ap to order my coffee right before I leave the house and by the time I get there it is ready. It is also always just the way I want it. It is perfect.   They do have a drive through, but I think mobile ordering is faster than the drive through. I usually come during the week right around 7am and most days there is going to be a line in the drive through then.    They have outside and inside seating. It is nice inside but I never just come here to sit and chill.

When in doubt, and need a WI-FI, starbucks is always there for the rescue. This is a nice location easy to spot right off the expressway and has a pretty decent outside area. I love my starbucks drinks, but even better when I spotted this one, I knew I could kill a little time till my next meeting. glad I found it, and know it is around.

While Starbucks isn't the greatest coffee in the world, I have coke to be really attached to this location. The mid-day crew has come to know my order when I come in. I spend hours in here reading my bible and doing sermon prep.   I  the venti iced coffee, heavy cream and 3 stevia.

As a traveler I frequent a lot of Starbucks. This is by far the worst. I waited in line for 30 minutes and watched everyone behind the counter move at a snail's pace. When it was my turn I ordered a coffee, oatmeal, and a water. I reminded the barista to charge me for the water, but she just waved it on. This happened to a few customers so I'm sure the company would want to know they're losing money (not that I mind). It then took another 15 minutes just to get the oatmeal, which didn't come with any type of sweetener (no honey, agave, brown sugar, etc). I didn't notice until I opened my oatmeal on the plane so I ended up with very a bland breakfast. Save your time and go to La Colombe a few steps away.
Took forever and what we ordered didn't taste like normal, the barista did give us a free coffee to try which was very good.  So five stars for that.

I've been here 2 days back to back and yes there's always a wait. One day they managed the line really well having one of the baristas come out and take orders of ppl waiting and writing the orders on drink sleeve. I really appreciate this at any and all Starbucks they do this at! It REALLY helps! Good job ;) today the line was about 10 mins but no one took the orders in advance...   All baristas are friendly and nice.   Can't comment on the mixed drinks much I'm a dark roast drip all the way. Kids enjoyed their fraps though.

Excellent, friendly service every time. Like a breath of fresh air! You're always treated like a guest and a friend. So much nicer than your average Starbucks! The employees here are doing a really great job.

Prob the absolute worst Starbucks I've ever been to in my entire life. Mobile orders average 15-20 mins and if you order more than one item you wait 2-7 mins before receiving each item. I submitted my mobile order at 9:42 and didn't receive three items until 10:02. Forget asking the manager, the few times she's actually out there she's rude to the customers, as well as her staff.

Reading these reviews made me realize people only take the time to comment on things like this store when they've had a bad experience. ANY Starbucks you go to is going to have moments where you catch baristas at a bad time.  Needless to say, every time I've come to this Starbucks I have been greeted by the baristas behind the bar with a "Welcome in". Anytime I am indecisive about a drink the baristas always work with me to help me find something that I'll love and then let me know that if I don't like it to bring it back and they'll make me a new one. They are so friendly and make an effort to get to know me. I highly recommend this Starbucks to anyone looking for a quick drink.

The staff here is hands-down one of the friendliest you'll find in Boise.  Unlike the places you get forced smiles and fakey greetings Starbucks sometimes generates, the staff here seems to honestly be happy to see you. At least in the evenings, they're never overly busy and I've never had to wait more than a minute or two for my drink. I've never had a quality issue, and they seems to always properly follow recipes. The restroom is always super clean, which probably takes a fair amount of work on their part. If you're on Eagle road, this is the best of the four Starbucks to drop by.

Wow- I'm no Starbucks expert, but they're usually nice. This one is gross. For starters, upon entering, at least 7 employees were buzzing around, but no one was taking orders. The woman who finally assisted me was very nice & helpful. Drink was fine.  I needed a space for some computer work. Had to put napkins down as a barrier between my computer and the table. The bench to sit on is completely broken, dented in, and uncomfortable. Only use the bathroom if you are wearing a hazmat suit. I would definitely not recommend this place unless you use the drive-through.
The girl working here was completely rude and there's always a long wait. But the drinks come out average so some times the convenience over the other Springfield Starbucks wins out.

I'm not going to write a review based on the Starbucks products, because I don't know anyone who hasn't tried them for themselves. So I will just review this location.  If it were just Starbucks coffee: Fast, Fresh, In my mouth immediately I would give it 5 stars because I love that stuff.  So these guys get 5 stars. I only go through the drive thru, but that's all I need. They are fast. I once waited ten minutes, and they gave my sister and me our coffee for free because of the wait. Even with 5+ cars in front of me I have never waited that long again.   They are convenient if you live in the North Valleys because you don't have a lot of other options. This is why I think these guys are always packed when I go there. But honestly, I feel their speed is appropriate to the amount of people they are serving and they can't really do anything about that. In fact, having less customers tends to be worse for your business so I guess I can't push for that!  Also they serve Starbucks coffee... go here if that's what you want :)

Commercialization is everywhere in this area of South Jersey, but it's almost necessary to talk up the recent Centerton Square businesses and particularly Starbucks. Three words:  Twenty-four. hour. caffeine.  24-hour caffeine served in a comfortable setting (although all Starbucks could use more counches/comfy chairs) with the New York Times at your finger tips and muffins and cookies of all sorts. Granted it's pretty cold inside for whatever reason and it IS Starbucks (hence 4 stars instead of 5) but it's nice to have around.   And, in attempts to mutate into something with a more personal appeal perhaps, this Starbucks hosted a night of local musical performances the evening before Thanksgiving. Doubtful that Corporate came up with the idea, but they agreed to it! Kudos for that at least, as perverse as it seems.
Always great service when I come here. And the baristas offer free samples to make sure I like the drink. My favorite Starbucks in Reno!

The location at 601 S Harbour Island in Tampa is awesome. Today especially so.  Always friendly and prompt, remembering repeat customer preferences after just a short time.  Most days I put in a sizable family order in person and after paying I wait maybe 1 minute, or 2 at most before it's all ready.  This allows me to handle 3 work emails before heading home.  Today I ordered, paid, and just barely had time to open email app and bam - order's ready!  And even when they recently had a system issue, they still figured out how to open and handle simple cash orders, when most other locations would have just kept the doors closed.  Great team here!!

Great location! However, there are certainly some kinks to be worked out. The drive through line for me has always been prolonged and inefficient. My last two visits have taken 15 min just to place the order, to say nothing of actually receiving it.  (It's my own fault for not wearing clothes I can wear in public, haha!). The baked goods are always sold out (but that makes sense based on the volume they're facing).    I suggest they retain Chick-fil-a's management for drive through! They always have that line hustling!

The absolute slowest Starbucks! I've been here about 5 times now and every time I've been here they have messed up on my order! I've asked for a manager and they said they don't have one. Do yourself a favor and go to another location!
very efficient and speedy Starbucks. even when the line is long, you won't have to wait too long for your order. also, the staff is very friendly

So, I'm ranking this Starbucks as a 3 compared to others in Tampa.  Why so low?  The parking here is a nightmare as is getting in and out of the actual parking lot.  They are seriously out of two of my favorite things....vanilla roobois tea and rice krispy treats (or rice dream bars as I think Starbucks calls them) at least 75% of the time.  I've asked them multiple times why they are consistently out of these items and no one seems to have a great answer.  The bathroom is normally pretty dirty too.    Giving them the benefit of the doubt though this is a very high volume Starbucks and their staff have always been warm, attentive and pleasant.  They are efficient as well for the volume of people they push through on a daily basis.

I never came to this location. I was in the area, it was snowing and it just seemed right. Smaller location not the smallest and it was very busy, like most Starbucks I go to. I got my White Chocolate Mocha and I was out.

The service here is good and they have a cute outdoor seating section. But as others have noted, the parking lot is awful and it is often 100% full in the morning and at lunch time (the only times I've ever stopped by).

My investigation leads me to believe this is the store now known as 639 W Ina, not 7151 N Oracle.  I've hit them up several times through the drive-thru and service is average. Not the typical uber-nice Starbucks customer service experience but still pleasant.  I was mistaken in thinking this location opened at 4:30AM like the one at Ina/Thornydale. I had to be at work super early and came to the drive-thru at this location and stopped at the speaker box but could only hear what sounded like someone wiping down the mic or something. After a couple of  "Hello? Hello?"s without response I pulled up to the window. I was noticed within about 30 seconds and the woman went about unlocking the window and opening it. I asked if they were open and she told me no, they open at 5AM. I said, "Oh, OK, I thought you opened at 4:30 like Ina/Thornydale and Grant/Swan." She then said, no, 5AM and pulled the window shut.   As I put my car in drive and glanced at the clock in my car I saw that it was 4:58am. Really? She couldn't have offered to serve me 2 minutes before opening? I realize she didn't have to but c'mon...this is Starbucks, and they're known for great customer service.   I drove away disappointed and hit my regular store at Grant/Swan and headed in to work.

I just wanted to say how much I absolutely love this Starbucks. We drive 15 minutes to come here all the time and study. Every barista is amazing! I wanted to give this review because of the service I got today. They accidentally made me a Hot Latte instead of Iced but one of the Baristas made me a second one and then gave me both!! He was super nice about it. Love this Starbucks

Poor customer service. Went in and purchase 2 holiday collection cups and a drink total was about $50. Dionne short blonde hair girl was extremely rude the moment I walked in. I was amazed the holiday collection that was released a day early. She started saying comments underneath her breath as she thought I didn't hear her and right away said you can't use your point which was no problem. Treated all other races in a nice respected matter but because I was Hispanic she was extremely rude. She also didn't take my order down correctly. When the person that did my drink told me try it. I said it doesn't taste right he said no problem ill redo it she comes back saying oh I must of not heard you correctly and rolled her eyes. Once again poor customer service. Dionne treat everyone equally with respect if you are unhappy at your job try looking somewhere else or leave the attitude at home.

Service is good, but if you have to use the restroom, I would definitely go somewhere else.  I've seen gas station bathrooms that are 10x's cleaner than this one.  You even need a code to get in, you'd think it'd be cleaner
Pretty much standard for Starbucks. Smaller format with about six 4-person tables and 2 small bar height counters. Nothing bad, but nothing fancy about this location.

Not attentive to customer needs...ordered coffee, kept me waiting and never asked me what am waiting on. They just kept looking at me until I said where's my coffee?....they forgot my order but never bothered to ask me what am waiting for

Went in tonight RIGHT before they closed and the workers were sooooo awesome! Marley and James were so welcoming and fun to come in to I love when I can come into a store and feel like I'm friends with the workers. Lizzy also made sure that I was taken care of after my order was taken. The store looked very clean. Service was fast, coffee was good, overall a good experience.

Service is ok, but it's crowded with people who buy one coffee then decide to homestead taking up all the seats. Doesn't your college have study areas? Instead of sitting to enjoy my coffee for ten minutes, I'm in my car. Thanks Millennials!

Why do you ask for names when you aren't going to call out names? Baristas creates the drinks and places them on the counter. Am i going to just assume it's my drink and go ahead and touch someone else's drink? With covid still being an issue, how is this not being addressed. Kept saying excuse me and they couldn't hear me over their personal conversations that's the workers were having. I asked for a double shot and they only gave me a single. When I asked who's drink that is, the barista said "idk there's no make" what the hell. Who's managing this location? Clearly nobody.

I came here en route to Houston. I asked for a bacon Gouda sandwich. They made it, the egg was cold. I asked them to remake it the egg is still cold. At this point I am like wtf. They can keep the $4 how hard is it to reheat a damn sandwich? Never getting my money again at this airport. Only getting two stars because the coffee is somewhat decent. There was a racist review below in which the user Nash M. (Who is a negative yelped per his profile) who states the "wrong demographics" works here because the staff is African American. Don't pay attention to that, because that comes from a place of Godlessness and hate. Pay attention to the fact that products were not cooked or made correctly and service sucks. I have been to Starbucks in major cities with all demographics: African American, Hispanic, Caucasian, Asian you name it! It is the work culture of that particular location which fosters poor service not the race or ethnicity of the workers. Good news is there are multiple Starbucks in the airport I went to the one right at the beginning of the C terminal. There is one some ways down you may have better luck with.

This store is consistently slow and dirty. The employees DO NOT care about customers and seem either lost, disinterested or both.   The ONLY reason I come here is that it is convenient.  Maybe they know they have a captive audience and take customers for granted.   How does management allow this to continue?

This Starbucks is slightly below average, I would say. The parking is very limited, and it can be tough getting on and off of 82nd St. The inside is nice, but on the smaller side. Most of the seating is for 2 people. The noise level here is not too bad, though - I think the higher ceiling helps. Service is average - baristas are friendly, but this is consistently slower service than at other Starbucks in the Indy area.

The worker at this starbucks (inside Grand Sierra Resort) told us that they couldn't switch out chocolate to white mocha LIKE EVERY OTHER STORE COULD. Though another worker last night has made that change to my drink (the drink was great by the way), the lady working today (December 26, 202112:00pm) has been really stubborn and given us an unsatisfactory service. If you're lazy just say that.
Great location.  Friendly baristas,  Coffee made to order.  Would definitely go back there next time I'm in town.

I like this Starbucks because it's on my way to work early on Saturdays and Sundays. The place is usually empty and I'm always pleasantly greeted. I come almost every week, so the baristas remember me and my order! That has never happened and I love it.   I tried the other Starbucks nearby (the one with the drive thru) for a while, but I like this one more for early morning.   I did come here once in the afternoon and it was beyond crowded... I think I'll stick to the early morning pleasant experiences.

I love Starbucks but this location has not mastered the recipes for chai tea, green tea or the ginger ale.   The locations in Gonzales, Houma, and even the nearby Barnes & Noble are a lot better. The lines would move faster if your total was given to you prior to approaching the drive thru window. Customer service is fine but the drinks need to improve.
This Starbucks is excellent. They respond quickly and cheerfully no matter how busy they are. I prefer this over the others in the area

This Starbucks it one that is worthy of a review purely because the staff is amazing . Being in the customer service business I can tell you that they make my stop here 10x better. On multiple occasions that I stop here, the staff has been so nice especially Sharmaine and Erica ! You can feel that they are genuinely sweet .Although our encounters of me placing my order with them is small it really does change how I feel when I walk out .

Needed something refreshing after playing disc golf at a park nearby.  I came in here to get a bottle of water, and then the add for a pumpkin spice Frappuccino caught my eye, so of course I got one, haha.    Too bad there was only one size for bottled water, which was huge and way more (and more expensive) than I would have preferred paying for.  But I guess it was good to quench my thirst for the long drive home from Indiana to Illinois.    I like how you can choose the Frappuccinos to be caffeinated or decaffeinated.  I opted for decaf, because I didn't want the caffeine in the afternoon.    The pumpkin spice Frapp is sweet but delicious.  Yum!

I was more of a fan when the gold card got you free soy, especially as I don't like too much of it. Now at 60 cents even a SBUX fan such as myself seeks alternatives. Shame but better for the wallet in the long run...
The place is really dirty. Garbage all over the floors and bathroom needed cleaning. Would make sense if they were really busy, but it was slow and nobody was cleaning.

Reminded me why I never go to this location.  Was ignored by staff so they could talk to co workers.  They actually told me I would have to wait until they could finish their conversation.  The girls behind the counter were talking so loud to each other the entire 2 hours I was there that multiple people commented about it and left.  They were literally screaming about gossip.  Totally unprofessional and distracting.  Serves me right for even coming here.
The fat white girl wears looks, which is very racist, and she treated me with contempt because I am not an American.   806 Manhattan Blvd Harvey, LA  70058 United States

Standard Starbucks experience. This one located inside the Marriott, so be prepared for long lines at peak times, especially during conventions. They do a pretty good job getting people through the line quickly with a small staff, though.
The staff here are, on most times, amazing. There's limited seating, compared to how many people line up during and after lunch time, but they have free wi-fi.

I am pretty much a tea drinker but when I want coffee I prefer Starbucks.  This location is convenient for me and as typical has the little coffee house seating area with some outdoor seating.  The only downside (not Starbucks) to be concerned about is the parking lot is small.    This place always greets you warmly whether you walk in or use their drive thru.  They offer free Wi-fi and is a great setting to get comfortable to check email, surf the internet, or do some work (or write a Yelp review).  Aside from liking the coffee my favorite is the Caramel Frappuccino and it comes in two sizes of calories (a whole bunch or almost a whole bunch).  I always find it humorous to order because I don't drink enough coffee drinks to know all the correct nuances of ordering especially the specialty drinks.  So the staff is always very helpful in understanding when I order it with no bad stuff but want to still taste good.  This particular drink is cold and is a blend of caramel, coffee, and milk topped with whip cream. If you think cold comfort drink then you have the picture.  This location is quaint and is more suitable for its drive thru and smaller groups.  The kind of place for a quiet conversation.  Definitely a "Yay I am fan" kind of place.

i just started coming here and for the second time in a row they took 15 mins to make my two coffees and i i usually stop by when i know i have time as my job is 3 mins away. they took my payment and left me there waiting for a while and ended up being late.... not reliable and most likely not going back.
Just what I needed after an early flight to Tampa. It's conveniently located inside the airport next to the escalators.

This place offers a very comfortable environment to meet with people.  The Starbucks regional office is next door to this location, so of course the staff are the best Starbucks have to offer.  They are on their toes and do a lot to help make your experience the best.   This place is very LBTQ oriented and you will see many family members while on your visit, both working and enjoying the experience.

Once again there is a massive line at an airport Starbucks. These should always have at least two registers open, plus baristas should be taking drink orders while people wait to pay. Why is this so hard?

I agree with Stacy L.  I've been staying at the Marriott for a conference since Sunday, and every day I go in, I feel like I get nothing but bad news.  They don't honor the Gold card free refills, soy, or syrups.  They're usually out of something every day...almonds when I want just a light snack - "oh sorry, we ran out."  Very small selection of sandwiches, and haven't seen a protein plate at all in this place.  Staff usually acts like they don't give a damned.  They close super early...what Starbucks in the US do you know that closes at 5pm? Do yourself a favor and bring your business to the Sheraton across the Street, or one of the smaller, independent coffee shops nearby like Rue de La Course (too many in NOLA to miss) who actually care about their customers and selling a good product, rather than passing the time doing as little as possible until they get off work to go party down the street.

I had a terrible experience here and will not be returning. I ordered the new iced  brown sugar oatmilk shaken espresso and what I received was nothing like it. I've ordered the drink before at other Starbucks and it was always made perfectly , but this one was nothing compared to it. It was sooo watery and tasted like milk and water. I even went back in to let them know so it could be re made, still received the same exact drink. I posted a picture of what it's supposed to look like since I had it before and a picture of what I received. Two different drinks. Do not go here. They do not know how to make their drinks.

If you have a dairy allergy this location is one to avoid. For the past year this location has consistently ruined my latte. Every. Single. Time. Having almond or soy as a base is not that hard. It's actually common. I wonder how many times a day they actually ruin an order.  Own your mistakes. Correct them. Train your employees. I will be sick for the evening because of the milk. Thanks.

Not bad. One of the barista was talking to friends instead of making coffee at the right time. No biggie but maybe keep in mind that you're doing coffee and drinks for people not chit chatting. (I don't mind the chit chat but please do that when there are no customers or something).   Price is good. Not bad. Good drinks though!

I was so happy to see a familiar friend open sonearly for my flight home. The baristas that were working at 5am were so friendly and worked quickly to get each of us waiting in line taken care of quickly and getting us caffinated for our trips. I ordered a venti blonde cinammon dolce latte and it was delicious.
I love this Starbucks. Maria is the best ever. Her coffee is on point. Makes my morning.

This is an average Starbucks near the entrance to Terminal A and the baggage claim area.   I have been a couple times while waiting on delayed luggage. My order has always been correct so no complaints there. There are a few nice chairs to lounge in while burning time.

What sets a Starbucks location apart from another?  The peeps behind the green apron...THATS WHAT!!  This location rocks !!  Quick and friendly exemplify this downtown Reno location. We enjoyed our daily jump start here during our recent ski trip. Visit this location to enjoy FIVE STAR service if you DARE!!

I've been to this Starbucks location couple of times and the experience of getting coffee here always been the same, HORRIBLE! Minus the dirty counters and grumpy baristas who visibly hate their job including the coffee they made you they also will make you wait for it. The tipping point for me was when I ordered a simple drink (Tall Pike) went to get Half & Half at the dirty counter but found it to be empty, skim milk - empty, whole milk - empty, I asked barista if I could get more Half & Half, she looked at me and said sure! then ignored me for good 10 minutes until I left, other baristas also ignored me while being busy hating on their job. I went to the near by gas station to get me Half & Half for the coffee I purchased at the Starbucks. (Anyone else see anything wrong with this?)  So If you still decide to come here, get in line for slow crevice and free humiliation.   Next time I'll come here, but who am I kidding I will never come here again.
A new stand-alone Starbucks at the Delaware Travel Plaza... a welcome addition to my usual mid-way stop to New York.  Easy in, easy out, drive-thru!

Well it is Starbucks.  How interesting can this review actually be?  This is your standard Starbucks.  They are friendly and fast.  They run out of stuff sometimes though and it pisses me off.  Other than that I guess you could say they are pretty comparable to all of the other 5 gazillion 'bucks out there.
The slowest drive-thru ever! Definitely need to work on efficiency here. It is easier to drive to Paoli than wait in this line.

HORRIBLE customer service & to boot $10+ they gave us 2 coffee and milk when we paid for specially lattes....Not impressed.  I've been a "Starbuckets" drinker for years and always looked forward to the Holiday flavors.  Was in a giving mood & decided to treat a co-worker after lunch.  OK so she has a complex coffee order (I zoned out somewhere in the cinnamon, triple, no foam, whip...)  The gal in drive-through had to ask a few times herself to get it right....no biggie.  Get to the window and waiting for order I could sense hostility....the gal makes a comment about "head-set not always working" co worker said it's OK you got it right, insert awkward pause, then "you seemed ungrateful"  HUH?!?   I was FLABBERGASTED thought WE were the customer and your goal is to get my order that I paid for.  I was floored. After a "manager" gets snarky with us and rolls her eyes we pull away at this point scared to drink coffees and realize they just gave us coffee and milk not the drinks ordered.   NOT coming again to this location and suggest unless you are into S&M don't go there yourself.  Thank goodness the folks at corporate are decent and understanding.

Whoever was running the register today told me the wrong amount of espresso shots for my drink.  I only found out later when I asked the person making it.  You may want to be cautious if you are limiting your caffeine for heath reasons. You have basically one job at Starbucks: Know how to make the coffee.  If you can't do that consistently, you should probably seek employment elsewhere.

I go to this Starbucks often and every time I am impressed by the speed and accuracy of the employees. The long drive thru line may be scary, but I often do not wait longer than 5mins.  I have no gotten the wrong order, and even when they were out of almond milk, I was given a ton of apologies  - heads up coconut milk is just as great.  The employees are super friendly and helpful!

Really liked the space inside this Starbucks. I often use Starbucks to study but some locations don't offer a good amount of table space. This Starbucks has a decent amount of tables/chairs. Employees are nice & they cleaned the bathrooms twice while I was there. They also have a drive thru, which is a great perk.

All brand spanking new of course, this new location shares a space with a Mattress Direct and features two separate vehicle entrances and a drive-thru. Outside seating totals 4 tables while inside has a large table of 10, a 4-top, 4 couch seats and a couple of 2-seater stools. Free wifi of course and standard menu items, staff is friendly!  The place is right across from the Sunset Hills shops like Home Depot, Old Navy, Bed Bath etc. so it's convenient during shopping trips, it looks like they are also catering to the business park community in the vicinity.  I'm not even a Starbucks fan but I like trying new places. The Vanilla Sweet Cream cold brew isn't bad although I thought it would be sweeter. Everything here looks to be in order and clean, and I hope it would be seeing as they just opened their doors.

Thank you. Doth such a place share such an idea with a complement reader? https://twitter.com/DwKleckner seeks a very refined, polite, female acquaintance who may further an incomplete Introduction to Society, though discreetly. BACKGROUND... https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/103051966613676983821/reviews/@33.8243044,-114.1629745,4z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m3!8m2!3m1!1e1

I just recently went to the new starbucks that opened near McCaffreys and I went through the drive thru. I ordered a drink and a panini and as soon as I got to the window, my drink was ready and my panini was out only a few seconds after that. The panini was cooked perfectly and wasn't cold. I hope they can keep up the fast pace unlike the other starbucks in town where you wait 15 minutes to get a grande drink.

Friendly baristas and they work hard.  In the morning between 8 and 10 am, the line, and the wait, can be pretty long.  They get a lot of app orders. This is a very busy location and not too large a store.   Drinks and food are standard Starbucks.  My wife loves the Carmel Macchiato and their donuts.  She drinks and eats like there is no tomorrow, even though it isn't cheap.  Grandkids really like it here too.  Always seem to have a Starbucks coffee cup on my desk.   Generally recommend but be prepared to wait in the mornings.

Closed "until further notice" on 1/24/21 after I left my house early in order to get coffee for my night shift and get to work on time. Very unprofessional. Very disappointing. Pissed was not the word. No real explanation to their loyal customers or care.

What IS the deal with this location?!? While the baristas are always nice & polite and my order is usually correct, I am flabbergasted EVERY TIME I come here by how long it takes to get through it. Inside or drive through, it amazes me every time.   It's the only one on my way to work however, soooooo.....

Don't go here often, but stopped in recently two consecutive mornings. The staff's too laid back - seems to be a South Street culture of "easy does it". They don't know how to move a long line (or a short one for that matter) like most Starbucks.   The 2nd visit one person in line (me) at 8:40am. There's three employees behind counter two working on drinks. The third, a manager standing 3 feet away, sees me waiting and then looks away - she's too busy and concerned with doing the inventory with a hand held electronic device. Apparently customers in line are less important than completing inventory.   From now on it's Chapter House cafe just down the block.

Look I know that people love Starbucks, and I know its only for the name. look Mc Donald's, they have great coffee and you can spend 4.00 on a meal with coffee or just get coffee for 4.00 at starbucks with no sandwich. Just somthing to think about.
Walked all the way here for a much needed early morning cup of coffee. They don't open until 5:30am even though listed at 5am ever day. Really!?!?
Service is always fast a efficient. And my items are always prepared to order with freshness!

Not a review about their delicious coffee..  Staying at Starbucks makes me wonder about life... What are my hopes and dreams? Did Howard Schultz have the same dream in transforming this incredible brand into "america's third place"? Who are these people sitting around me and what are their hopes and dreams? This is one of the few Places I can soak in my own presence and mindfulness; to slow down, embrace and appreciate life. I thank everyone who works here for their professional and prompt service; especially this cool Barista Chris, who has an incredible positive and bright personality. Sweeter than Frappucino.

Do yourself a favor and drive to this Starbucks.  Quick Drive-through, clean restrooms, fast internet and great side tables for meetings. Great outside seating.  Best of all is the STAFF.  They're happy to be there and always have a greeting.  They suggested a "London Fog" or Earl-Grey Tea Latte with sugar free Vanilla syrup.  I did mine with will almond milk.  Great place!  I'm a Starbucks mug fan - check out the new Nevada cup..!  Winner!

It's Starbucks, but anything in CWE has to carry its own swagger, and this little spot, despite being near Coffee Cartel, manages to squeeze together plenty of seating in a small spot and has some great service.  It's also conveniently across from the Chess Club and right next to the Chess Hall of Fame.

Came here for my usual iced venti half-caf Americano with three pumps of sugarfree vanilla and room for cream.   There was like -2 parking spaces so I had to put my hazards on and be "that person" to get my drink since I sure as hell wasn't driving to find a spot for a 5 minute coffee run.  Cute place, deck looks out on the river which is nice.

This Starbucks is always busy! for a long time it was the only one in this area. I know they have added one inside the Publix which shares the same lot as this location, but I do not think a lot of people know this. Anyhow, when I stop by here it's typically an in and out situation as I will put in a mobile order. I will say these baristas work fast. I have never had to wait and also have never had any issues with getting the wrong drink. The taste of my drinks are consistent with my expectations. Personally I love the ice chai latte! The drive through is another option, but at this location this will not get you your order any sooner. I am not kidding when I say they are busy!   I typically will see people sitting inside and outside on the patio. A lot of them look like they are studying or just catching up with an old friend, typical coffee house stuff.

Spent $4.91 on steamed milk with caramel sauce. Not a single shot of espresso anywhere, definitely not what I consider a caramel latte. I came in around mid-afternoon when it wasn't very busy either. Very amateur mistake from an expensive coffee franchise.
Decent service. What I don't like is the store is far too small and you have to park at mcdonalds alot. Coffee is great but not the best location or size.

Yes. It's a Starbucks and as such, you have a certain degree of certainty in your experience, whether you like it or not. So, going to Starbucks is a lot less about the coffee than about the location of the store. Each store's location has it's own flavor and flair, and this Starbucks continues that tradition. I think the highlight of this location is the outdoor seating (obviously, when it's nice outside!). In the middle of a ton of traffic on Clayton Rd, this place somehow cultivates the sense of being set away. The interior is large and good for studying (lots of plugs).  To reply to Peter B's review, I was able to redeem offers at this location yesterday, so I guess it's changed from a licensed Starbucks to a regular Starbucks! Yay!
The service here is aweful! The blond haired barista with the crazy dark eyebrows is a total witch... I think she forgot the customers, like me, pay her rent....
Jake is easily the best barista Ive ever seen. It was so busy,  but Jake maintained a great attitude and worked very hard.  He deserves recognition by management

This Starbucks is fine.  It is, however, small, and it gets very busy at certain times of day, with a lot of people picking up coffee on their way onto the blue route or the turnpike.  During rushes, the employees crank through orders more quickly than at a lot of other Starbucks, but you can still have a long wait in cramped environs.  Also, the parking lot is small and getting out of it can get complicated.  The product is the Starbucks usual and the employees are good, but it's not the fastest or easiest place to stop at busy times and the seating is limited so it's not the best Starbucks if you're looking for a place to have a conversation or to sit back and sip your coffee.

Worst Starbucks I have ever been to. One worker on a Saturday morning, stuff all over the place, out of caramel and other items, nothing in the cases. Looks like the remnants of a hurricane hit this store. Will never come here again as I don't have time for this nonsense. Going on 15 minutes waiting for my drinks now.
Love this place. Very cozy, though I never sit down. I always just grab coffee and go. The staff here is almost always great
The already over priced Mocha quickly turned $160 cup of Mocha, when the lid was closed on the paper seams of the cup. Realized it dripped all over my pants and shirt when it was too late.

This is a great Starbucks location, with plenty of indoor and outdoor seating. The new CITRUS GREEN TEA LATTE smells like a lemon drop and tastes like some lemon gummy is lost somewhere in the bottom of your cup. It's not bad, if you like that sort of thing. They didn't skimp on the green tea, it's got lots of great green tea flavor.

The parking is horrible at this Starbucks. I'm surprised there aren't accidents.   The staff does a good job and they hustle, but the drive through line STILL gets super backed up in the mornings. It takes forever.   The inside is small, I often have to wait for a place to sit.   But--drinks are good, staff is competent and friendly, and if you do a mobile order, it's ready quickly.

Going by the reviews, I must have a horseshoe in my pocket.  We have never had an issue with this location.  I like to chalk that up to not being a drive thru person.  Face to face changes a lot and if it ain't right at the counter, its no big deal.  That and I do seem to visit at very off times.  We are usually the only ones inside so all the seats are open.  It's a new building from ground up so it's got what you expect from the current 2020 generation of Starbucks.  I'm a fan of this location till I get burned.
Really nice staff and comfy location.   They were really friendly and had a good variety or food  and drinks for all.  We returned again.
This Starbucks drivethru has a long wait of 7 cars and the staff were rude and unpleasant. We will not be back to this location.

This Starbucks is kind of a mixed bag.  The drive thru is slow but I think it is do to the location.   It is on a major street and is very busy in the morning.   Getting in and out of the parking lot can be a little tricky.   I recommend going right and around the block down to 31st where you have a light instead of trying to turn left out of their parking lot.   I decided  to park and go inside. The service inside was fast and my tea was made correctly, which is what I expect from Starbucks.
Twice I've done a mobile order with plenty of time and an extremely un-busy hour only to arrive to pick up and the order wasn't even taken off the machine/ never using this location again

Love this Starbucks. Excellent customer service. Lawson is the manager and has such an awesome staff. I come here on a daily basis because of the atmosphere and awesome customer service I get. They have plenty of seating both inside and out. I come here to drink my coffee and relax. Love this place.

4 stars for the location, cleanliness, and service-  One of my favorite Starbucks for sure. Yeh, not a lot of parking right next to the store but it is a standalone building in a shopping mall parking lot so there is parking available. The store is usually busy especially the drive through but the lines move fairly quickly. I've always had good service at this location and the restrooms are maintained well. Also, a rarity is the cleanliness of the self-service bar. It's not only clean but it's usually orderly as well.
