That's a difficult accomplishment with how busy this place can be so they obviously pay attention to the details. Typical Starbucks trinkets and sweets for purchase.
So many good drinks in menu.. Yes a bit pricey but we all know this already..  This particular location was clean and staff was very friendly! Great coffee break!

If you've been to one Starbucks, you've been to them all, when it comes to the food + drinks, prices, and the overall appearance. We stopped by at around 8pm to do some studying + work, and just bought a cookie. It was perfectly toasted and delicious. What I love about this Starbucks is there is plenty of seating. There's large shared tables, bars, small tables, etc. There are also outlets everywhere.  Like some of the Starbucks in my area, the staff try to make the atmosphere more welcoming. This Starbucks also has a riddle of the day (where you can ask the barista for the answer) and a bookshelf in the back to leave & take a book as well as some games.   The parking lot is not proportional to the number of seats in the Starbucks, but luckily it was not busy so we were able to get a spot. They also have reserved parking spaces for those just stopping in for mobile orders or anyone who was only parking for 15 minutes.   For any night owls that prefer to grind + do work later in the day, I would recommend this location!

The barista was making a drink for a target employee and was talking me and a girl got in line  The target employer left and she took my order then proceeded to take other orders before starting any.  My point is you were sitting there chatting with a target employee you could have done that while taking my order and starting it. It wasnt even that hard an order. Waited 15min for an iced tea.

Starbucks seems out of place in an academic library. And there is nowhere to sit, The drinks are on par with any other Starbucks - but with two Tim Hortons just outside the library on the same floor, this seems redundant.

This place is always busy. Half of the staff were standing around talking. Two of the girls were distracted by some guy talking to them. I saw my drink ready and the lady proceeded to stand right next to it for about 5 minutes. All she had to do was put the lid on. I wouldn't recommend if you're in a rush. My order was fine it just took so long to get something simple.
Great new space and they have a Clover brewer!  Always friendly here!  Try your next cup of coffee using the Clover machine!

This Starbucks is clean inside and I have had decent experiences in the past. However, I recently used (day of this review)the drive-thru for the first time and was in line for probably 25-30 minutes. I was originally behind 2 cars and the car in front of me had ordered two drinks. I should also mention this was around 2:30pm which is not a peak time. I am not saying to avoid this Starbucks, but it is lacking that consistency factor which is their hallmark. I am giving them a 3-star review for now and will update as my experiences dictate, but I do not see it changing.
Always a table open.  Great Wi-Fi signal.  More cushy chair and sofa options than any other Starbucks in the area.

So, I'm driving on Sunday afternoon heading from Tampa to Orlando, looking forward to a good jolt of Starbucks coffee to keep me awake. After waiting about a half an hour looking for a Starbucks sign, I see one for this location.  I pulled into the lot, and it was pretty busy. I figured I could live with that, knowing that my local Starbucks moves pretty fast through the line. I was sorely mistaken. When I walked in, probably 15 people in line and virtually no one sitting down. After standing there for 10 minutes and not seeing any progress. Myself and a few others bailed. The person in front of me said they seemed more focused on serving the people in the window than people in the building.  I walked over to McDonald's and got a couple of apple pies, coffee and was on my way. The people in front to me still had not been served.  This is not typical of my experience with Starbucks.  Caveat emptor!

I have never done this but I am finally over the arrogant, non helpful attitudes at this location.  A handful of good people work there but this is directed mostly at the "upity" little guy with brown shoulder length curly hair working this morning.  I asked him a question and as always, he responds with an attitude of why are you bothering me, I don't know anything.  I asked another employee and the attitude was better,  but nothing.  Three customers in line helped me.  The Starbucks employees need to remember customers who buy from them every single day.  Remember they make coffee and there job is CUSTOMER service.

This Starbucks is pretty spacious and has larger tables!  However, with the larger tables comes the probability that you will be sharing with someone you do not know.  Honestly, I think this is great, because it fosters a sense of community.  On the other hand, you better hope the person next to you does not smell.    There is also a comfortable couch and a couple of chairs to lounge in (about 4).  There is a bar in the front overlooking U.S 19 (not the loveliest of views, but you get a ton of light being that it is an entirely glass wall in front of you).  This Starbucks is often full of college students studying.  I've actually been told this is the Asian starbucks (meaning there are more Asians here than you would find most places).  Surprisingly, this is true.  I can honestly say I have seen more Asians here then most places in the Tampa Bay area.  This Starbucks is in a pretty good location, next to a Target, movie theaters, other food places, etc.  However, the traffic on US 19 can get pretty backed up so choose your time to come wisely.

It's convenient when you have a Starbucks on base and you don't have to drive far, but the management aspect is struggle busing. The manager is slow as molasses, I literally just walk out if I see her at the register or making drinks. I will be late for work guaranteed despite getting there 15-20 minutes early. They consistently end up being too watery and...just not good.   I will say the most of the baristas are very sweet and accommodating. Some are better than others with modifications with drinks, I absolutely adore Desirae (I think that's how her name is spelled). She's so bubbly and sweet and is the ONLY ONE  that consistently makes my drinks spot on. So, I'm on cloud 9 with my elixir of life.   Two stars for me on the quality of the drinks is inconsistent and slow service. Not always the case, but it occurs enough to be noticed. However, if you don't do modifications to your drinks, you should be safe to order! It wasn't always this way, just went a little downhill progressively over a 20 month time span.
An interestingly shaped Starbucks location in a strip mall. There is good amounts of seating here, but service is slow.

The customer service is always superb. However, they are always out of something when I go there. It's beyond frustrating. It's not the employees fault, but it drives me insane because I drive out of my way to go to this Starbucks and then to hear they are out of the drink I want... it's disappointing. Theres also always a huge line at the drive thru. And it's annoying to navigate because you must drive around in a circle to get to the drive thru.  Then it always moves very slowly. Sorry but i prefer the Brookhaven Starbucks!
Rude/indifferent staff, sloppy service (e.g., hot coffee lid half off), parking lot is always tight. The Broomall Starbucks just a little farther east on Route 3 is much better on all counts.

This is the Starbucks that I frequent most often as it's right by my office.  Everyone is really friendly (like most Starbucks) and service is fast.  The inside is not huge and there are only a few tables, but they do have outside seating for nice weather.  My one complaint is the tiny parking lot.  If there is a long drive-thru line, as is often the case, the poor saps who are parked in the lot aren't even able to back out because the drive-thru line blocks everything else.  Other than that, good stuff.
Hands down the best Starbucks. Staff always goes beyond to satisfy customers. Great group and coffee!

Speedy and efficient. Sounds good right. Perhaps for some but not me.  Not many in the store. Only me in line. Short line at drive thru.  When I bid the person good morning and asked how she was her reply was what can I get for you. So much for pleasantries.  I was trying to decide what to eat but she looked very impatient so I ordered my  drink to calm her down.  That didn't work. Immediately she asks what else I would like. So I picked something quickly.  I get it this is fast food. But it's not cheap fast food. And it's nice to feel appreciated when I'm dropping coin somewhere. I suppose it's being in the north instead of home in the south.  Solid Starbucks but unfriendly.

This location is disgusting. Ordered a latte with soy milk and was given a drink that consisted of floating chunks of white all along the bottom. When I addressed the barista all she did was look at me with attitude and say "I can make it again but that's just how soy is." That's weird...because the hundreds of other times I've ordered the same drink at multiple locations I've never had chunks of "soy milk" floating all along the bottom of my drink. So gross!!! Stay away from this location.

Slowest location ever. Staff is very friendly and seem to all be working hard so I am not sure what the problem is with this location. The parking lot is a nightmare. This time they had to brew more coffee as well.. not sure why we are out at 4 pm on a Saturday.

My DISABLED child came home and told me today that the lady at Starbucks at the Mall was very rude to her when she was there yesterday. She (not sure what her name was)  made her feel so rushed to give her her order. And was very nasty to her. This is the most disgusting behavior, and whoever you are you should feel really ashamed of yourself.  And maybe find a job where you aren't in SERVICE.

Supper loud music. 2 people standing around just asking if you want food with a line of 11. If they say your order half was and you finish it to confirm it is right they will get an attitude. Horrible customer services

This is my Starbucks of choice, my Zen, my happy place. My best friend and I have been coming to this particular Starbucks for 5 years. Starbucks has seen us laugh and cry, it has heard our stories of heartache and butterflies. Starbucks has comforted us when we felt down, and listened to our gossip and seen the first's in our lives. When ever there is an emergency there is always "time for Starbucks". Maybe I'm giving this particular Starbucks a 5-star rating because it holds a little place in my heart. The drinks just always taste so much better here, but let's be honest coffee beverages just taste that must better with my best friend. I love Starbucks, I have always loved Starbucks, and I will always continue to love Starbucks. It just holds a place in my heart.

This Starbucks has some of the friendliest Baristas in town. I visited right before they were about to close with a group of friends and there was no rush whatsoever. In fact, they were very lively and were playing a game with their manager to see if they could get a customer to do the Zena Warrior call. Well, luckily they picked the right people because my friends and I are always up for a good challenge. In return for participating, they gave me and my friends all a free drink. It was the most fun I've personally experienced within a Starbucks. Definitely made my night!!

Stuff is awesome the service is fast and amazing. This Starbucks is always busy but you never wait to long.  Starbucks coffee is a little over priced but once in a while its good. Love the holiday flavors. Pumpkin spice one of the best of all times.

Cashier didn't seem to know what she was doing. I asked for a peppermint mocha frap and she kept asking if I wanted it with coffee or vanilla. I told her I want it with coffee and oatmilk. When I received the drink I noticed it was significantly lighter in color but thought it was because I asked for oat milk. After leaving I realized she gave me a vanilla frap . I obviously asked for a peppermint mocha. And said peppermint mocha repeatedly.

Starbucks usually have their acts together. Morning prime time and things are humming. This Starbucks, people standing around looking lost. Long line with nobody being helped.  Coffee that is mundane. With so many great coffee shops in town (shout out to Handlebar), why did I come here?

8:30am with a line in the drive through - but out in less than 5 minutes! WOO!  Tried the new Cold Brew w/Pumpkin Cream after the barista talked me through the ingredients  (Vanilla Syrup, Cold Brew, Cream Topping w/Pumpkin Flavor & Pumpkin Pie Seasonings). Great service + they write jokes on the drive through window for much needed morning laughs

I just picked up my order at Starbucks when I realized I ordered from the WRONG place! Such a stupid mistake on my part. I felt so bad and  thought they'd be mad or salty, but they were SO incredibly nice!! They really made my day. They made my drink for me anyways and LOOK they even put a little heart on my cup! I'm really trying not to cry in the office rn. I LOVE THIS STARBUCKS! I noticed that they are so nice to me every time I go (i go probably too much for my own good ) this time was just so extraordinary I wanted to share. I think kindness goes a long way no matter how big or small, and the people working here really do their best to extend that. Tipping them every time I go, and I encourage everyone to absolutely tip them! They deserve all and only good things

No two Starbucks are alike! Well... they're close but this location definitely stands out. There are two locations within walking distance of my work and I would choose this one anytime.  Why? For one, the staff are always upbeat and friendly. They're always joking around with each other and it seems like a fun work environment. Also, while the lines can get long, they always seem to go quickly. Sometimes I order ahead on my cell phone and my drink is always ready to go when I get there. The drinks here are always made well and the few times they've run out of something I wanted, the staff always have a similar back-up to suggest.   The seating inside is cozy and comfortable and they have a nice outdoor seating area for those hot summer days. I think I'm going to head over there now for an afternoon pick-me-up!
MorNing Crew at this Starbucks is absolutely awesome!!! Happy, attentive and great service. Plus the Baristas make my coffee to near perfection of how I like it.

This is the newly opened SB in Oro Valley. It was packed. One of the baristas, I think named Kaitlan, kept sticking her fingers in the glasses as she moved them around over and over again. That's disgusting. Then I got a refill of my unsweetened green ice tea and it has this gross sweet taste. I was watching them. They were barely washing out the mixing cups with those spray wash station. I think they need to tune up the training. I'm not going back for a while.

It won't replace Peet's in my heart, but it's a better cup of coffee than the other chain places in town. The staff is quite friendly and seem to work well in keeping the line moving at a brisk clip.  I've also become attached the weekly iTunes app giveaways.   Dammit.
I love my Starbucks. 99% of the time I used the mobile AP which I absolutely love.  The barista know me and have it ready when I arrive.  I've tried a variety of drinks and will purchase them again.

I love this Starbucks. I come here almost everyday and order the same thing, it was a little sweet and they added whipped cream and regular caramel (which I don't like, just a little mistake, I like the dark caramel). Thankfully I was able to just walk in and request it a little less sweet and with no whipped cream or regular caramel !!! They made me a whole new one!!! This one is perfect like everyday. Thanks so much! Don't forget to tip!!! Sorry for bringing my drink back but it's so much better now !!! Love the staff here, always so nice ! I also love coming here because there is always a shady parking spot where I can smoke and drink my coffee while listening to music. Very clean restrooms also I might add.

It's like people at Mc Donald's left to work here. Seems like they can't get an order right . Ordered the simple Vietnamese style coffee - quad expresso with 5 pumps white mocha with splash of heavy cream.... got one shot expresso 5 pumps white mocha and 3/4 of a venti cup of heavy cream .

Top notch first class Starbucks! Customer service is excellent and always feel welcomed. You know the workers are good when they remember your order and begin to make it at the sight of your face. Only negative is parking!

*AVOID* this place at all costs!!!  They gave me green tea instead of black tea...i mean...how can you make such a mistake? You make coffee/tea all day...that is your job! After I put milk in my tea, it turned GREEN so I asked the barista if she had given me the right kind of tea.  She LIED and INSISTED that she had given me black tea.  This is intolerable! Such employees should not be hired if they are not interested in their work! Sad! The drink was DISGUSTING!  If I could give zero or a half star, I definitely would.  I have always drank great Starbucks drinks but this was a horrible DISASTER.  I would have been better off drinking plain WATER. Please double, triple or quadruple check your drink before you drink it b/c you may not know what you are consuming!

They are now a ghost town as the shopping center is being renovated.  Parking is horrific, if the entrance is even open.  Combined with my experiences here I've pretty much decided even though it's convenient and I like the drinks I'm not going back.

I have been to over 100 different Starbucks all over the Midwest and South and this is the only one that consistently gets my order wrong.. I,get an iced white chocolate  mocha with caramel drizzle, not a hard drink to make, and yet they never get it right.. It is either way too sweet like they added extra sugar or it is soo bitter you can't drink it.. Just wish this store was as good as all the other ones I go to in my travels.

My first visit to this particular Starbucks was not the best. I normally stick to what I know especially with place that make my food or drinks to perfection. I was not in my normal area so I decided to give this location a try ( all Starbucks are not the same). I was there fairly early due to my commute to work. I ordered my normal Salted Caramel Mocha however I got what tasted like ONLY steamed milk. Imagine my disappointment considering I was on the road and away from the establishment when I discovered this. Although my drink was not correct my barista was very pleasant and nice for it to be 6:30am. I will give it another shot, I haven't totally canceled out this location yet. I still say it's worth a try

I love this Starbucks for so many reasons, but mainly because the people there genuinely seem to enjoy working there.  The seating area is also larger than most other Starbucks in city limits and everything in here is well-maintained and very clean.    Never had a problem with drink consistency or the time it takes to make them.  I've definitely been to a couple Starbucks along my travels where they have no communication between employees, leaving my marked cup sitting on the counter for those few extra minutes while I'm trying to rush out the door.  Luckily, this is my neighborhood Starbucks and I'm fortunate to have such friendly employees greeting me every time I walk in.

Remodel looks okay but this place is always backed up, even when it's slow.  They have 2 cash registers but one is obviously just for looks.  Now, please do not be confused; there will be an employee standing in front of both cash registers, but they cannot, under any circumstances, ring you up using the register on the left.  Meanwhile the general vibe I get is that I am lucky just be served there.  As if asking for a coffee in this starbucks is some obscure out of line request.  These are my thoughts after 4 visits, which were all eerily similar in experience.

The Employees work really hard. They are friendly. Coffee always seems better than other locations. I see them keeping the building clean. When there isn't many Customers in the store. I love the Caramel Apple Spice Cider  Yummy

So... It's a hotel lobby Starbucks. Pretty typical. Small and limited seating.  Today, however, the crew seemed to be somewhat flummoxed by the whole process. The two young men at the counter were trying very hard to provide good service but they seemed to be a little overwhelmed by the process - not providing bags, forgetting if they had rung things up, etc. They both were very friendly - welcoming each customer and saying "thank you." I appreciate that.  The coffee is fine and better than the mass-produced brown stuff in the conference urns!

Menu wise you know what you're going to get but with me, I order the same thing- hot peppermint mocha and a plain bagel, toasted WITH BUTTER. 80% of my orders are mobile orders and they still get the butter wrong and give me cream cheese. Some mornings, it is okay because I'm going back home but most morning I'm on the go and to have to return to their site for BUTTER is an inconvenience. I've ordered from other Starbucks throughout town and they never have an issue, not sure if this one is just understaffed and so busy they're unable to follow WRITTEN instructions.  After 7 months,  the above (original post), still stands true. Because of that I drive to the next closest Starbucks location at Marketplace where they're always pleasant & my order is accurate.

I live closer to the Starbucks on Brentwood but I prefer this one. It is the best neighborhood Starbucks around! I love to study here. I also love that the baristas will let me study for hours, they never ask me to leave, and sometimes they even give me samples! How can I not like this place?!  On a side note, there is a drive through, it's clean, the bathrooms always clean, and there's plenty of parking.

Nothing like sitting in the drive they for 30 minutes to find you missed the teeny tiny order screen and instead of them helping you at the window anyway- they tell you to drive back thru to the end of the line and order! Poor service. I will now become a Dunkin Donuts patron for life!

This is the worst managed Starbucks in the country. It has to be. They never have products, even before the world's supply chain went to heck, they staff is routinely rude & condescending, and their practices of reusing equipment with out properly sanitizing should at least warrant a health inspection. Corporate's quality control needs to pay this place a visit, and have a new pen filled w/ink and 500 sheet notebook to list issues.

My daughter and I ordered 2 coffees and 2 muffins, they didn't ask if we wanted it warmed and I didn't think to ask until after it was handed to us. So we asked nicely if they could warm it for us. Oh boy, the person working there who should not be in a customer service position says to me with a glare " you should have asked"! Ok, well whatever. So Im standing there with my 12 yr old and she puts a bag down and my hand goes for it , Im still kinda shocked from her unnecessary meanness, and then she literally yells at me "is your name Nicole!?" Like 3 times. Her coworkers think it's hilarious that she is so disgusting to a customer and gets away with it. She clearly has no concept of customer service or just plain old treating other humans nice.
I'm just wondering why I got stuck in the drive thru for 30+ minutes with a total of 5 cars in front of me. I so wish I was exaggerating.

Soooo, this is me being nitpicky, but I found this comical and slightly annoying. Any time I order my specific drink, I have two customizations that are incredibly simple. It gets messed up occasionally because the baristas have a routine when making drinks and I get that. It happens. Normally, I'll let the barista know, they will hand me a new drink, and ask if I want to give the incorrect one to someone else. I always do to pay it forward cuz the drink is still good and who doesn't want a free Starbucks to make their day better. The barista originally asked if that's what I wanted to do and I said sure, she talks to her manager, and is like ummm I think I will just toss that one and takes the incorrect drink. It's not the end of the world, but it's just bad managerial skills. You would rather toss a drink that YOU made incorrectly to what I prefer, instead of letting me gift it to someone else to keep me from getting two? I didn't make the mistake and you just wasted a drink unnecessarily. Only Starbucks I have ever had do this and I won't be back. It was incredibly awkward and a like, really moment lol. I'll stick to my Murfreesboro locations especially after reading the other reviews.
While they make a decent coffee like most others, I wouldn't recommend coming here unless you want to hear some of the rudest starbucks employees complain about their lives

It's a nice Starbucks in a sense that orders are delivered quickly and efficiently. The staff is always friendly and wears masks. Food and drinks are about the same as in other Starbucks locations.  You can also seat outside, but I noticed that tables outside are rarely cleaned if cleaned throughout the day at all. Hence, minus one star.   Lots of parking on the back of the building, so it's easy to find a spot.
Always have friendly and fast service! The drive thru is convenient during the cold months when you don't want to leave your car.
Staff are awesome and remember regulars orders. The are always courteous and have sufficient pastries in stock.

So apparently I've just haven't learned my lesson with these guys. By far the worst Starbucks I've been to! So tonight me and my husband went out for a late dinner and afterwards we wanted a drink from Starbucks so I looked on my app and saw that one was the only close by that was open. So we took the chance and went and sure enough failed us. I got a venti pink drink and my pink drink was light pink that was it! It looked so plain!!!! Why?!? Because they didn't add any strawberries! There was so evidence of any strawberries! I'm not complaining about the price because hey i know I pay a lot for a drink but hey I love Starbucks so whatever! It's yummy! But anyways if I'm paying that much I should be getting what I paid for! What it's suppose to be! My husband got a white chocolate mocha hot and he said it tasted bland. No flavor! And on top of it I placed the order on the app so we don't have to wait too long. My husband gets his drink and makes him wait another 10 minutes for the pink drink! If I want Starbucks I'll just go somewhere else and drive a little further out of my way to get some I guess! Ugh so annoying! The worst Starbucks ever!!!!!!

I've experienced better at this Starbucks! this is my go-to Starbucks until now.. I went in yesterday and they were out of decaf coffee. No big deal, they offered a decaf americano instead, which i declined because I was in a rush to work. I go in today, and luckily they had some brewed decaf coffee. BUT, they only had enough to give me half a cup! c'mon now people.....
Pretty annoying waiting 15 minutes for Coffee and then when youre halfway home and try your coffee its wrong . Cant say this is the first time either

The service at this Starbucks is below poor, waited 15 minutes for my latte and never got an apology for it taking so long. No customer service AT ALL! Very disappointed for a Starbucks! In addition everyone working behind the counter had major attitudes, not one staff member smiled, nor acknowledged any of the customers. I thought when you gave your name it was to be called so you know when your order is ready. It was more calling out the drinks instead. 3 people ordered the same thing and we were left wondering whose was who. Management need to retrain it's staff at this location.

Every time I come to the play area with my son and my wife I go and buy some coffee while my son is playing but this time I didn't want coffee so I've asked the girl behind the counter if I can get change for 20 to use for the toy cars and she refused!! I will never buy a coffee again from any Starbucks location

This place never gets my drink right...and I mean NEVER.  Really sucks bc this is the closest starbucks to me.  I order at the drive through and it never fails...order is wrong. To top it off there's only one girl in there who is super nice.  Like, unusually kind. Everyone else is annoyed or checked out.  I have no idea what's going on with this location, this kind of service (or lack thereof) is new to me.

I have to admit the Google WiFi is a major attraction because it works perfectly with my Moto X WiFi phone -- so much better than the AT&T systems. BUT the service is excellent and consistent and friendly to boot. Currently my favorite one in town.
The barista got my order exactly right & was so accommodating about allowing me to use 2 free star awards. Very quick!

I don't know why I subject myself to the rediculousness of this place.  #1, how is it legit that people waiting to go to the drive through can back up an entire road.  Even if there are parking spots, you can get in the lot bc all the people waiting for the dumb drive through have the only entrance completely blocked.  Once you do finally get in the place, they take your order immediately, and then you just wait.........and wait for them to get the drive through line down.  Today I watched 15 drinks come up before mine and there was nobody in the store waiting but me.  It was all for the drive through peeps.  HELLO, I'm standing right here in your store and you're going to put me behind the 50 people waiting in their cars, blocking traffic.  When I finally asked about my drink, the girl making drinks totally ignored me and some other person said, oh I think that drink is sitting over here in the drive through line.   I think it is crazy that they don't have a dedicated person making drinks for people that come into the store.  Ok my rant is over.  I'll probably end up in this crappy starbucks again tomorrow bc, let's be serious, it is on my way to work.

This Starbucks is pretty small compared to the other ones In the area but I will say that they are very quick with the drinks are are extremely friendly. They have a few 4 person tables that are great when you need the extra room to do work or you are expecting to meet a few people. Both the dining area and the bathroom were very clean.
Long line, well staffed, reasonably short wait. Pretty much what you would expect at an airport location. One concern was the cream/sugar table seemed rather unclean.
You might want to rethink who manages this location and the team that works at this location.  Worst Starbucks I've ever been to!!!!!!!!

If asked, "How do I take my coffee"... I reply "Seriously. Very Seriously!".  Clearly I have had enough coffee today because I am on fire with these reviews! This was my first time visiting this location (only came through the drive thru) but I am overall very pleased. The baristas were quick, and pleasant at the window. My drink order was also correct ... VICTORY! I was in chipper enough spirits, between their quick speed and my holiday tunes, that I even had to pay it forward in true Christmas spirit!
Seems like it's pretty new and has a huge lounge area and outside seating.  It's connected with the Hilton.

Very rude people there. It's a nice place to meet people. But the people were very rude. They refused to remake a drink after "losing" our drinks. Then refused to be helpful. Told us that they aren't remaking when they gave a refun that we didn't ask for.
This is the Most understaffed Starbucks I've seen compares to the amount of customers.

Starbucks is the McDonald's of coffee shops, right? So, I should probably temper my expectations.  That said, the whole experience is corporate, lame and I can't wait for it to be over one I'm there.    From the other customers who would push you out of the way to get their Joe 30 seconds sooner, to the barista that mumbles the drink orders as they come up, the need to go there is a useful reminder to never let yourself go to sleep without coffee in the house for the next morning.
Dark Roast sucks. I think I'll stick with Dunkin' on this one. I love the atmosphere though, I go in with my Dunkin' cup and study. Food is okay too.
Friendly employees, and well stocked location with inside and outside seating.

Skip this store if you can - and have another store on your drive - otherwise double check your order before driving off as I've found order error high.  This has been a great store in the past - with friendly service and a strong desire to get it right. Staff (in the past) would great you by name and even offer suggestions on something to try when I would sit in. I trust they get it right side up - as it has a great location.  Today I get 3 shots of espresso in my Americano Verses 4, poured milk into the half-and-half creamer, and warm Verses hot water. Luke warm - week coffee is the worst! And I did not realize just how far a friendly hello from a familiar face goes in feeling welcome. But the turnover is such that I rarely see the same person week over week in the past year. It has never been this bad  But I will check into Yelp and will be glad to give them another chance once they start delivering the cup of coffee that was ordered - and turnover slows down.

Elizabeth is EXTRA rude ! Not the 1st time, not the 2nd time but always has a pissy attitude.  She has a smart and rude mouth ! Should not be working if she doesn't want to deal with customers, she should not be working .

I've always loved Starbucks, but they are doing a great job during COVID that makes me like them even more.  Just order with the app and pick up, and they take all necessary precautions and seem to take the threat of this virus a lot more seriously than some places.  For that they'll keep getting my business.

Coffee was overrated and not very impressive. Barista added whipped cream even though I didn't ask for it. This location is very small and felt claustrophobic at times. I also do not like their selection of music for purchase. Nothing wrong with the selection itself, but why would I go to Starbucks to buy a cd?  The pastry offered was a bit dry as well. The panini bread was dry and they seemed to skimp on the meat. Again, I'm at Starbucks to drink their coffee, not get a sandwich. If I wanted a Sandwich, I would go over to Safeway or Quiznos nearby.  This location has a drive-thru window and wireless internet available if you choose to use it.

Super friendly baristas (which is becoming a rarity on the SBucks front)! The turkey bacon, egg white, cheddar sandwich was surprisingly delish! And the orange cranberry scones and dark roast were actually fresh here! All around solid SBucks on the Northside!

This starbucks is located in a big "Gateway" shopping center and is my go to after I hit up Saladworks or the deli or the Taco joint at lunch time.  In the morning it's swamped but at lunch I can usually get my four-bucks (try almost $5 for a Grande Iced Macchiato) caffeine fix.  Le sigh...

I continually leave this Starbucks location disappointed by the staff's interaction with customers. I ordered a Frappuccino, and the response from the cashier was "You can't order that." I replied, "Excuse me, but Frappuccinos are on your menu." Then, the barista interjected "If you want a Frappuccino, I suggest going to Wawa across the street." Appalled by these employees' rude statements and poor customer service, I am disgustingly surprised that this particular Starbucks location continues to thrive. After visiting both the original Starbucks store and the Starbucks Reserve Roastery in Seattle plus multiple East Coast Starbucks locations and having enjoyed the pleasant customer service at the aforementioned locations, why does this particular location continue to have such ill-mannered employees? Please address and correct their subpar customer service. Thank you!
By far the best customer service at a local Starbucks. I can get in and out before work without waiting.. Best thing recently was Kim suggesting the Chestnut Praline Latte!!! It's amazing!
One of the more poorly managed locations in the area - staff wasn't particularly helpful and borderline unfriendly.  It also took a very long time for a simple cold brew.
Customer service is horrible at this Starbucks rest stop. I think Starbucks should screen the rest stop Employees better like they do for the actual stores.

Typical Starbucks. We had vanilla cream frappe, cold chai tea and sweet cream cold brew. Barista was nice not overly friendly busy talking with other barista. Tables were clean. May stop by if back by in the mall

Aesthetically pleasing uptown location. Parking is a headache on the cramped Maple Street but there is a small lot available at an old gas station right across the street.   I brought in my reusable mug that had a bit of gunk in the bottom from letting old coffee sit in it and the nice guy behind the counter scrubbed it for 10 minutes in scalding hot water to try and get it out--how nice! This location is busy and crowded because of its close proximity to the universities so I suggest getting your drink to-go. I'd stop by again if I were in the area.

This is a great Starbucks to visit. They have a beautiful patio to enjoy the spring weather. It's typically busy but the wait is never bad. Watch out for annoying bicyclist that take over on Friday Saturday and Sunday mornings. Make sure to bring a photo of your dog to post on the community board.
I like this Starbucks because it's generally less busy than the others in the area. I normally don't have a problem getting a table and the wi-fi usually works.

This store is Absolutely FILTHY!!!  Floors, tables every surface looks like it has not been cleaned in a very long time. The tables are sticky, crumbs every where, small coffee spills.  I am shocked as I have NEVER been in a dirty Starbucks.
New location is now open - avoid it.  It's dirty inside.  Service is downright pissy.  And their drinks aren't made right.
Worst Starbucks in service and efficiency in America probably but it's the only one in the The Gulch so location keeps it open.
Best barista ever award goes to.... BURK! This guy had my entire family laughing at the drive through. Great service.

I came to this Starbucks yesterday around 10am. I ordered a cocoa cloud macchiato in the drive thru. I had my phone out the window to pay but the girl standing at the window was talking to another girl about pay with the window open ignoring me. She finally turned to me and offered a non-genuine 'hi how are you'. When she handed me my drink she commented about how pretty the cloud macchiatos are not noticing it was obviously the wrong flavor there was caramel all over it and not chocolate. I took it anyway since I had an angry toddler in the car that needed to get home. Definitely need to be more professional at the window and pay attention to detail.

Starbucks - what can I say. Morning, noon, or night this is the love to be! I have nothing negative to say about Starbucks at all!   However, this particular location has its limitations. It is one of those smaller stores, meaning less seating and a more crowded environment. I wouldn't recommend coming to this one to get work done. On the other hand it does have a drive thru, which is always nice.   Overall this Starbucks is just like the rest to me - one of my favorite places. :)
The worst latte I've had in a ling time. Looks like just coffee with milk. Tastes ok, but no foam. Ive been here previously and it was better
Employees here were extremely polite and when my drink took longer than expected they gave me a card for my next drink to be on them, if I lived closer I would defiantly be back!
very nice especially on the inside. they also have the latest clover machine and select coffees that you don't always get at starbucks. This is one of my favorite starbucks.
Solid coffee, like the skinny mocha... Usually make it with extra cocoa.  Wondering if it's a good idea to buy the $1 reusable cup?  Says it'll pay for itself but who knows.
Always hit or miss, but usually when Tia (?) is working my drinks are perfect.
Nice location but parking lot is kind of tight. No drive thru either but they usually get orders out pretty fast. Unique atmosphere.

It isn't the Venti Skinny Cinnamon Dolce Latte that keeps me coming back. In fact, I actually find my typical Starbucks order annoying. Yes, I annoy myself just ordering coffee. The coffee is okay, but the staff is WONDERFUL.  The staff at this location keep me coming back. It's not just that they are always smiling; it's the fact that the friendly demeanor appears genuine.  Also, this is a high volume location, especially in the morning during the work week.  Don't be deterred by the long line at the drive-thru. You'll be amazed at how fast it moves.

I stop in this Starbucks a couple times a week. The staff is always friendly and efficient, and I'm definitely a sucker for free parking. I love big coffee shops that you can go and sit for hours and feel relaxed..this is one of those. I was pleasantly surprised the first time I went in and now I keep going back!

I had great service there. Everything came out right. It's just it would of gotten a 5 star but there was this crazy bitch that was trying to not let me through to park I was trying to park to go inside to order instead of sitting in line.  But she wouldn't let me through. But over all everything else was amazing.
The staff here is always friendly, I use the mobile app and they always have my drink ready and already know who I am and have it up on the bar for me.

This Starbucks is HUGE but cozy. The staff are very friendly and quick. I really like the various types of seating, many places with outlets for charging laptops/etc, parking, and right near Vanderbilt.  Perfect.

This is a pretty solid Starbucks w a great staff.  They have never gotten my drink order wrong.  The restaurant for the most part is always clean.  My one huge complaint about this Starbucks is the entrance to the drive up.  It tends to become a cluster Bc it has 2 ways in which clogs the exit and people will not let you pass to exit nor will they let you alternate to get into the line.  Starbucks people are very possessive about their place in the line.

Been going here every morning for two weeks and can not comprehend what is going on. I usually get the same thing, latte and kind bar, every morning, and literally every morning get a different charge, ranging from below 5$ to almost 7$ for the exact same order. The barista had a lot of attitude when I mentioned it the first time last week, but it keeps happening. Maybe this will get their attention.

I feel bad using a specific name however I've been to this location 3 times this week and everytime Matt was extremely rude!. I even mentioned to him about being so happy to be there which he had a very crappy tude saying he was sick and its been a busy holiday week and he's worked a ton of hours.. While I was ordering everytime another customer walked in he would roll his eyes!! Ok I understand all of that to a certain point but come on.... On top of this two of my drinks were wrong! no apologies for the rudeness after I pointed it out or making my orders wrong! Everytime it was filthy as well!

I've been here many times and haven't had too much of a problem with the employees here. Frappuccinos are usually really good and the environment is relaxing, but this one isn't really for people who are short on time as it usually takes a while to make their coffee.

This is a Starbucks, so we know what to expect when it comes to coffee and tea drinks and that's a good thing if you like Starbucks. The people are friendly and helpful, almost offering a smile all the time...the seating area is big, clean and bright.... and they have a drive thru for people in a hurry. They also have outdoor seating.

I'm only a fan because they have the drive thru, but the drive thru was sooo slow!  And parking was limited so believe it or not, the drive thru was the better option.  Anyway, I still like it for its proximity to me and the fact that I don't have to walk in to get what I want.

Yes I know it's Starbucks!! I need my coffee and I am not going to drive all over town to get it.   The employee's here are really kind and thoughtful. Pretty sure there are specific ways to order latte's and the baristas never make me feel silly for not knowing the order of the words. So I like a breve hazelnut or caramel latte.   Went through the drive in on this day and ordered three drinks. No they weren't all for me. Ordered a Venti caramel latte, a venti coconut milk caramel iced coffee, and a venti white chocolate latte plus three double bacon breakfast sandwiches. The barista double checked to see if there was three and there were so I got them. BAZINGA did that make my day.   Went to pay and asked to use my charge card to add money to my Starbucks card. She was very kind and sweet and said it wasn't a problem. (People have the right to say no to our requests especially if the drive up is really busy). I told her I appreciated her kindness.  Everything came in a timely manner and our sandwiches were hot.

This is the best Starbucks I have ever been to! The service is super friendly, and the atmosphere is perfect for studying or for hanging out. They have tons of seating! It is the best. Always go here.

The worst Starbucks I've been in. Not sure if I just caught them on a bad day or not. I hope so. I came in around 4:25 because I need the pickup on a frapaccino can give.  No one was overtly rude or anything they just didn't seem to care.   There were three young ladies behind the counter but only one was making drinks. And even the lady making drinks seemed like she could use some help the other two were too busy: one doing inventory? And the other counting money in the tip jar. Even that wasn't too bad but then another came in. She never went behind the counter but she talked like she was an off duty employee. Unfortunately she kept engaging the sole girl making drinks.  My advice is to avoid this place unless you are very patient and have plenty of time to kill.

This is the most inefficient operation on the planet.  Last Sunday around 9, I drove up to this location, and as usual, it was completely backed up.  I parked my car next door at the dry cleaners to avoid having to wait in that line, which experience told me would take forever.  I entered the facility and there was no line.  I placed my order for 2 croissants, 2 blueberry muffins, and 6 vanilla scones, and because I was in a hurry, I told them to skip heating anything up.  All of the items were in the bakery display.  I was then directed to wait to receive my order because there were 15 orders ahead of me in the DRIVE THRU.  Nothing in my order had to be prepared; just tossed in a bag.  There were at least 5 people behind the counter working, but I was told "she is working as fast as she can".  So not only is this food overpriced (and frankly, most of it available at your local market), but the service is terrible.  When I told them I was not going to wait for them to service the entire drive thru customers first, and to either give me the food or a refund, the manager refunded my money and thanked me!  So, I drove to McDonalds on Olive where I placed my order, paid my money, and was out in less time that I spent at this Starbucks with nothing to show for it.

This Starbucks is located in a large strip mall area and has a drive thru.  I used the drive thru so my review is based only on that; the exterior appears well maintained & the employee I dealt with was friendly & efficient. I received my drink within minutes and had no issue.  I'd be back again, maybe even go inside for a further review.

This is a review of the Starbucks kiosk located inside Giant Supermarket at the Wynnewood Shopping Center.  I can't tell you whether the coffee is any good or not. I stood at the counter for a full minute, waiting to place my order, as the woman at the register studiously ignored me and carried on a chat with her colleague. After that point, I left as the woman carried on her apparently riveting discussion. All I can say is that I hope such "service" is not par for the course.

This is my local Starbucks. It's the usual haunt for meetings with business partners and friends.  Big patio outside but often the tables inside are full.  No leather chairs for relaxing - it's all wooden chairs.  There is a bar area overlooking the parking lot and a giant shared picnic style table.  Service here is quick and efficient.  Drinks are good.  The cream/sugar area can be a bit untidy but in general it's fairly clean.  Parking is generally easy but Friday and Saturday evenings can be challenging when the Hart's Kitchen starts to fill up.

Usually a very long wait and they never have coconut milk, almond milk, oat milk, etc. They regularly are the only Starbucks in America for years that doesn't have basic drinks and ingredients on the menu. Nice workers though - not blaming the staff.
This location is constantly understaffed .. wait time is absolutely awful ..morning , day of night ..
to the guy in front of me at the drive thru @ 1633. thank u for purchasing my drink. Happy Thanksgiving!!
The morning drive thru service at this location use to be amazing. Now the customer service is weak. They don't care about providing a quality product.

Convienient location.   Store is large and clean.  The woman taking my order seemed to be on auto pilot.  I tried to engage her in conversation (I was the only one in line placing an order) and she seemed confused as to why I was talking to her.  I asked her which frappe she recommend (I couldn't make up my mind between two) and she told me "I don't know, I don't drink any of this stuff."  Hahahahaha.....Ooooooookay.....at least fake it for me lady...hahahaha.  Good thing I love me some Starbucks and could finally make up my own mind. My Skinny Salted Caramel Frappe was delicious and the hubs Americano was perfect....which is most important anyway...Great Job Barrista!!  I'll be back and I promise not to ask any questions next time.

Friendly, courteous staff. They always do their best to offer quick service with a smile. I have never had a problem with my order being wrong. As a semi-regular, it's rare I have to give my name when placing my order. Small space, but that is beyond their control and certainly an upgrade from the kiosk they used to occupy in the mall.

This is definitely the best Starbucks in the Goleta area. The baristas are fast and make the best drinks. Also, their iced coffee is much better and much stronger than the iced coffee at the Fairview location. This one is also huge and has great wifi. They have really stepped up their game in the past few years.

The location of this Starbucks is not the greatest. You'd think with it being right off I-10 it would be but it's very difficult to get out, especially if you have to make a right hand turn onto Ina.  The staff is very friendly, though, and that makes up for the traffic problems.

There is no seating in this Starbucks. And it's always cold in the winter and hot in the summer.   The staff is helpful but a little on the slow side. Merchandise selection is super small. No drive through. Most of the parking is in the rear which makes it a little difficult to just run in and get your coffee.   But--if you're shopping at Clay Terrace and just need a caffeine buzz, it's perfect.

Come here often, but today we ordered a fizzed tea. Person at the counter took our order. When we got to our car, I notice the tea wasn't fizzed. Went back in  and they told me the machine was broke.  Why would you not tell us when the order was placed. When ask to exchange it for a Refesher. There was none on display and the claim there were none in back. Terrible service.  They always act like they're too busy. Won't be going back to this location.

After recently moving, it is my local Starbucks. It's a typical Starbucks atmosphere. I have been in several times and have always been greeted with a smile. My classic white mocha has always been made correctly and even stirred so all the flavoring isn't hanging out at the bottom of the glass.  I pass three other Starbucks on my way to work but I will happily come back to this one.

When you place your order online and people that have ordered after you IN LINE get their order first, something isn't working. I ordered online and 5 people, who ordered after me, got their drinks before I did. Apparently, the barista never checked for the online order. WTF?! Hey Starbucks! Don't push online ordering unless your people are trained to check for the orders. Again!!! WTF?!

As a previous Starbucks barista, I was shocked at the uncleanliness, unprofessionalism, and poor customer service. One worker was (hopefully) on break; however, she stood next to the espresso machine licking her fingers of hot Cheetos. Additionally, there were five people in uniform; however, only one was doing anything (taking orders and money and then proceeding to make the drinks without washing hands). I realize people have lower expectations for airport Starbucks, but this one was remarkable.

Me and my husband love Starbucks!!! We stopped at this Starbucks on our way to Brandon we ordered what we normally get but the price was significantly higher than what we normally pay! So of course we asked the shift supervisor MASON who said we can charge whatever we want at this Starbucks ... then we asked the cashier GENNY and she said it's an up-charge to add extra sugar. So we asked her to take off one drink she took it off the shift leader had to come and take it off so he was rolling his eyes being absolutely rude! Then GENNY added the amount on a gift card when I asked her to put it on my card she said well you have to wait for the shift supervisor. In total we were waiting over 15 min for a small drink. The drink was sitting on the counter and we had already paid so I asked her to get me the drink and she said "well if I didn't have to deal with your receipt You would have your drink!!!" I will never be back to that Starbucks that was the worst service I have ever received!!!!

As a global customer, this is consistently the worst location that I have ever been to. Sadly, this is very close to my home. The location is great but is functionally a nightmare! As soon as the drive through starts to back up everyone trying to get in or out is blocked. If you like to smile and enjoy a stress free life, avoid this place! You have been warned. Cheers.
Grabbed a quick lunch before my mani-pedi. The Thai-style peanut chicken wrap was just divine!

This used to be my favorite Starbucks location. However recently I have been extremely disappointed. I use the mobile order to get in and get out and skip the line. Within the last week I have placed 2 orders and when I arrive not only is my order not ready( I have no problem waiting a minutes) but it hasn't even been started. Disappointed Customer

I like the location and the walk up window of the Starbucks; but, I've noticed that the service has deteriorated over the past couple of years. I thought that it was just my random visits that resulted in bad service until I've heard other people talking about it. Staff has been less than friendly at times, and the quality of the beverages has been poor. Capital and 16th St., Starbucks is far superior.
Quick efficient, it was far enough away from the convention center to get a latte fast!

I wish this location was cozier and more inviting. There are no soft chairs or areas to sit and visit. It is loud, cold and hard. This atmosphere just makes me want to get my coffee and leave, rather than catch up with a friend or do  some work. There used to be a areas with big leather chairs, but they've been replaced by a counter that has people with their backs to the room. Disappointing.

Went to this location in the mall and ordered a mint mocha frapp.  The waiting area, which is a decent size for a mall location, was full and the line was almost out of the door.  I decided to order from the mobile app since the line wasn't moving. It was a good choice, because I was able to get my drink while people who were in front of me in line hadn't even ordered yet.  The barista that made my drink was friendly. I didn't observe any of the rude behavior that I noticed in other reviews.  There was a slight adjustment that needed to be made to my drink and the young lady promptly fixed it with a smile.  Considering it was a Saturday, the visit wasn't bad at all.  For the volume of people that they had, I think they did a good job.  My frapp was great (first time trying this flavor).

Way too slow!  The girl at the cashier was cheerful and friendly, but drinks were made way too slow!  This Starbucks is located on one of the biggest intersections in Clearwater. They should know to have enough staff to get drinks made for the general flow of customers that pass through here.

Any place that makes my coffee and other drinks amazing. Will always get 5 stars from me.  Not to mention it is walking distance from where I live. So just a hop, skip and I'm there. Guilty of being a Starbucks lover. Although some of them aren't the best  this place here rocks.   It can get a little busy since it is located within the outlet stores. So you get creative and download the app to order ahead so when you walk in your order is right there awaiting you.   No complaints about this Starbucks, kudos and keep the coffee's coming.

Tried to make sure they had the iced tea I wanted before I ordered it.   They said they did.  Charged me.  I waited and waited.  They announce they are out of it.  Offered me something else, but I don't like the alternative.  I asked for my money back and they did.  I also unfortunately paid a 26% tip on my app for the before getting the drink.  Mentioned that.   The gal with out even giving a darn that I didn't get ANYTHING from them except a wait, announces "can't give the tips back".  So note to self do not tip them assuming you'll get something before it actually comes unless you like tipping to wait

I like coming to this location. I usually order on the app and then go inside to pick it up. It's always ready by the time I get there, unless they are busy which is understandable. The drive-thru takes a very long time so if you don't like being in line for a whole, I would order ahead or go inside. One thing I have noticed lately is that when I order hot drinks, they aren't filled up all the way. With how pricey it can get at Starbucks, I wish they would fill the cups all the way. The tables are never clean either from what I've noticed, but I never dine in. Just a heads up for other people. Other than that, I've had pretty good experiences.
For such a small location, they're always prompt and friendly.  I've never had a bad drink and the pastries are great too.    Target & Coffee - all in one place - what more do you need?

slow, always out of stuff, poor customer service, not clean, not typical starbucks workers. i've never seen a starbucks who always gets the drink wrong. its always too much of this or not enough of that. the location just sucks!  Taylor is DELIGHTFUL and a pleasure to order from. Very well spoken and pays attention to detail!

Went to this Starbucks today, we got a grande Caramel Macchiatto and a tall Skinny Vanilla latte in the drive thru. When we got home we found out our coffees not full, they didnt fill it up to the rim, there was only about 3/4 of coffee with the foam in them. We were disappointed.

There isn't a lot new to say about Starbucks. I happen to really like Starbucks and this particular store does a great job. I haven't run into any problems with drink or service quality and the store itself, given that it's on the newer side, is really beautifully decorated. There consistently seems to be a lot of people hanging out there working and passing the time and it has lot of little nooks to accommodate everyone. Also, since they're in a shopping center, tons of parking! Hooray!!
I love this Starbucks! Also, read the other reviews and Jerry is awesome and so friendly. The whole team!

Starbucks has closed its bathrooms to paying customers at least 4 hours before parades pass today. Boo!!! I blame the store manager on duty. Why not see to it that the employees spend a little more time on actual service and less time trying to charm customers into feeding the tip jar with lame humor?  Headed to the Canal Place store from now on....smaller and more crowded but at least they get the job done.   Shame on you Starbucks.
Literally some of nicest people I've ever met. They go above and beyond every time. The drinks are great as well. The overall experience is awesome and I'm glad they are my neighborhood Starbucks!
Starbucks has to do better. This is the second major story in two years that I have seen regarding Starbucks managers descrimating againsts blacks. I spit on you.
Great Starbucks. Helpful and friendly staff most of the time. Consistently great coffee.

Stopped here on a cross country trip needing some Joe for the road.  First negative - no drive through.  Second negative - small parking lot that was very congested.  I think this must be an old Dr. office or something.  The building itself was unique for a Starbucks and was plenty busy but getting in and out kind of a pain.

Again and again, Starbucks on Country Club and Speedway continues to amaze.  My favorite store in Tucson, I will go through any lengths to go out of my way to this store.  Consitency, Friendliness, and promptness make this Starbucks a shining jewel.  I have been coming to this very location for 5 years and I'm stil a loyal customer.  Keep up the good work!   Tip:  Lots of students come here, it gets pretty packed and hard to find seating after around 2 PM.    The outdoor patio is dog friendly!

This location is logistically in a ROTTEN spot.  A busy intersection.  The true bonus is that (unlike the Starbucks further up State Street) it has a drive thru.  They have outdoor seating, but who in their right mind wants to sit outside at a busy, often congested intersection inhaling fumes?  The really good thing is that since Starbucks is generally AWESOMELY the same for product, you can get your caffeine on the go and be satisfied with the product itself.

I mean...this is your typical Starbucks.  The people are friendly, the drinks are consistent, and there's lots of room to plant yourself if you're meeting a friend or trying to get some work done.  What I don't like is the parking.  Obviously, this isn't something they can really help, but it does deter me from visiting.  There are SO many spaces, but with all the other businesses in the complex competing for spots, it's hard to find one during busy times.  But, hey, if you're looking for a solid Starbucks experience, this is your place.
Delicious every time...great service, clean store, fresh pastries....everything one expects from Starbucks.

I have fond memories and a great long-standing relationship with this Starbucks. Every time I drive in from Phoenix, I stop here to get my caffeine kick before driving to the in-laws.  On this current visit I realized this location moved over just by a building. The drive-thru is even better! They added a parking lot across the street if there isn't any parking right up by the door.   It's a popular location for a sure scrumptious beverage. By the way, no more Einstein's...sad.

This mornings crew was garbage except for the strawberry blonde girl. All gossiping and standing around. I ordered a tea, and two food items. They ended up being out of the sandwhich and tea that I ordered, so I just asked for a refund based on the lack of customer service. Then, I was told they could still make the tea that I wanted (strawberry açaí) even though they just told me they were out? Incredibly rude, inconsistent, and childish. It was like being in high school watching them stand around and talk while neglecting customers. For the messed up tea and the egg bites I ordered, it took us 25 minutes. There was no one behind me and they waited 10 minutes before starting anything. After 20 minutes, they asked if we were waiting for something (egg bites) and proceeded to JUST START THEM after that. My advice? Get people who take this job seriously unlike the girls that worked this mornings shift.
Too much staff none of whom understand the correct names for routine Starbucks items. Just say no.

This is my local Starbucks and I often stop by here when out running errands since it's close to both my usual post office and my usual grocery store. Same old story as every other Starbucks, right?   Right, except for some reason their wireless Internet hates me lately. Whenever I try to connect I get weird error messages, which is a total bummer when I've stopped in to work over a decaf Americano and order only to discover when I sit down that I actually cannot do any work. In fact, I'm there right now, siphoning radio waves from some other random signal. I may even fall off the Internet before I get a chance to hit 'post' on this review. Fingers crossed.   PS: Lauren's review comments on the three plush chairs at this location; I'm happy to report that that number has been upped to FOUR.

5 from my perspective but i saw a couple instances where others would rate at the 3.5-4 star. I used to come to this one a lot but sense my Luv works in another one i rather go to that one. Kiss two birds with one heart shaped stone. I can see the difference between a regular SB store vs Drive through one like this. The time window is huge! I think like most places they are understaffed to. Holiday week can do that to any business. Nice line of cars outside for window service n a medium one inside to. The staff was doing what they do best n were on task. Well mannered n positive vibes. The madam before me had a hiccup order but it wasn't written in the cup as she wanted it. No biggie the barista doing the mixes fixed it on the spot. Place was packed by the time i got my orders coming out. To my knowledge i think it's faster to just get out of your car n walk inside. I like coming inside anyways. I love the human interaction better. Cuz whats the point without it? Might as well just stay home n make yourself a mean cup of joe your way. Be in your pjs n cuddle up with a nice bottle of inspiration n the dogos. Lol i saw the place was well kept inside, they had an unfinished creative board with a high quality Japanese Manga style barista being painted in pastels. Don't know who did it but here's a boat load of support from another Artist n professional illustrator;  "your art is superb quality, even if it's a hobby? N / or if not? Chase that dragon with full intent of making it yours! You have a beautiful talent that to my very steep n high expectations of this style of art? It matches with my own standards. It is very well done. Make it to sell in posters or even prints. You will get that money fast! Pair yourself with a writer n build your dreams together or if you are just like me? Write draw, paint n publish your beautiful art, i would love to see more of your work!" Blessings to all n hope you all work n flourish this coming holidays!

By far the worst Starbucks in town. The only reason to come here is that it's conveniently located in the UA bookstore. Things I hate about this starbucks:  1. Incredibly long lines, especially between class times 2. They don't carry the full range of pastries--"La Boulange" is unheard of here 3. Cashiers consistently flub it: clueless about drinks, can't operate the cash register, can't navigate the gold card program, frequently mischarging and then not knowing how to fix the situation...all mixed in with an occasional snippy teenage attitude 4. Drink quality is inconsistent. Want too much ice? Check! not enough ice? Check! That's probably among the most minor of the drink faux pas i've experienced here.   Canyon cafe is a much smoother transaction, and the pastries are good! They just opened up a starbs in the library, so there's another option for the UA set. If you must have Starbucks, the one on University ave. is much better.

This location went from a 2 star to a 1 star.. If 0 were an option I would have chose that. Today I bought a coffee thru the drive thru. Americano, sugar free vanilla with cream. Tasted completely burnt and sour and normally it's smooth and sweet. The rest of the day I was physically ill and had to stop everything I was doing and go home.. I'm guessing the creamer was spoiled cause they leave everything out, and didn't feel like getting a fresh one for there customers. I'll drive a little farther next time to go to a better location.

Once again, I'm here to give this Starbucks a bad rating. I frequent this location as I live across the street, but today, i heard the manager come over to a customer and tell him he had to leave if he didn't buy anything. I get it, but also, having worked at Starbucks, I know Starbucks is supposed to be a "second place" and by kicking someone out.. that doesn't exactly fulfill that "second place" saying. I'll add that there were about 4 other customers in the store, so in no way was this man taking up space as there were plenty of open tables. Ridiculous.
Super slow service made sense when they made my drink wrong three times. Driving a little further to the suncoast starbucks probably would have taken less time.

Walking around this mall can sure make anyone a bit tired.  Enter Starbucks.  Located next to the food court there are plenty of seats to sit down and relax.  Add a cup of your favorite coffee or tea and you'll recharge your batteries in no time.  I went during holiday shopping and the gals behind the counter weren't phased even with the long line of people.  Drinks were made at the same rate orders were being placed.  Doesn't get any better than this!  Great folks!

This location is a danger zone for drivers and pedestrians alike, thanks to their drive-thru that has cars lined up in connecting streets. Drivers who can only be considered narcissists or morons will block traffic moving south from the busy immediate intersection to wait to make a LEFT turn---effectively cutting the line of more 'correct' cars blocking northernly traffic to make a right. Employees report the drive thru is 'always crazy' with police being called to deal with accidents and fender benders--sometimes more than once a day.  On my visit today, I parked across the street and was nearly hit by an angry truck driver who weaved out of his lane to go around a car waiting to make a left turn---the blocking driver had only shrugged her shoulders at all the incessant honking: 'golly, what can I do?'   Get in the proper line; go to another starbucks; lock your door cuz someone is going to kill you. On purpose. With their bare hands.  Inside, customers inside waiting for hectic employees to service the drive/thru, which remained relentless (ok) and dangerous (not ok).   Anyone see that story on how often police visit Walmarts due to disturbances? Add this Starbucks location in this affluent neighborhood as a contender.  Bottom line: Instead of walking to Starbucks, I either get into my car and drive to one further away, or realize my fondness for their breakfast sandwich and pumpkin latte is neither not worth dying for or increasing my carbon footprint and just, you know, make coffee at home like a sane person.  Also, manager Allison? It's unacceptable for you to talk that way to an employee in front of a customer, then interrupt his service to the customer and demand his attention and mea culpas while the customer watches and waits.   I know the drive thru is stressing you out but still...

In my opinion the only thing you can review at Starbucks is the people that are serving you, the cleanliness of the facility and the location.  The coffee and the "snack" assortment is what it is...  At this location the staff is average.  They do their job and do it fairly well.  I think it would be better if the staff paid more attention to their customers.  The facility is clean and neat.  The location is convenient and has a drive through.  My only caution is on the drive through, watch the turn as it is very tight.

They have the rudest most disrespectful staff I have ever met. They constantly refer to me as an old bag and refuse to take my order. We don't take orders from old women and don't come back. The manager always seems to have her dirty mucky boots on the seat. She constantly disrespects the customers and the furniture. This nazi woman should be fired.
This location does not make drinks correctly and always gets my order wrong, even if I'm ordering 1 drink. Other Metairie locations are much better.
Great place open late (11pm) on a weeknight, I think even later on the weekend.  Needed a traveler late (family pack) and they were very gracious to get it ready for me right at closing time.

The store was clean and service was very quick. I love that Starbucks has switched to paper straws now! It's great for our environment.  As far as the drinks go, this was the absolute worst Caramel Frappe that I have ever had. It was watery, flavorless, the caramel was thin and the whipped cream was melted. Very disappointed.

What is different about this Starbucks is that it's situated next to a lake. There's outdoor seating, and it's got a nice view.  However, the parking is terrible. There are only four or five spaces on a tiny stretch of asphalt next to the shop, and the parking is terrible if you enter the lot from the "wrong" direction. Exiting is also a nightmare.

More than once I have walked to this Starbucks on an impulse because it is in the same shopping center as Kroger. Who doesn't need a delicious coffee treat after a long grocery trip??   This is a big open Starbucks inside with good outdoor seating (though it overlooks a giant parking lot) and no drive thru. They always get out my coffee quickly, and have plenty of parking which is nice compared to some of their other West Nashville locations. I don't typically hang out at this Starbucks; this is more a stop in when I'm on my way home with groceries, but might be tempted by their cozy leather seats next time! :)

This is a licensed and not company owned store.  They do not honor gold card refills.   It's ok but Starbucks should require them to hold their same standards. The WiFi is also pretty slow at only about 2mb/sec clocked using speedtest.

Probably the worst Starbucks ever been too. Took forever walking to even find it. You would think in a touristy town of Nashville they would have Starbucks on Main street. Where even many open deserted spots could be. This was all uphill and then very crowded and tiny. The server taking order was nice but girl that made my tea left it to me to add my own raw sugar in tea. Usually they always do it. It was so cramped to even wait for order. Many groups of people walked up and saw wait and walked away. So losing business.

My local Starbucks is pretty consistent with service and drink quality as well as being on time for app orders. If I were to find one thing to nitpick it would be - examples like this morning - where I wanted to order a drink ahead of time to beat the rush hour traffic and my local Starbucks did not have the pistachio latte available on the app. The Starbucks app suggested I drive out to a further location, about 10 minutes out of the way. So skipped the convenience of app ordering and drove out there and order through the drive-through, the old fashioned way. The young man that helped me via the drive-through was very nice and pleasant for 7:30 in the morning. I will reiterate, I have no issues with this location they are always on par with my expectations!   Pro tip, always try the new drinks or the Barista's secret menu suggestions. You might actually find something new you enjoy! And do try the new pistachio latte (Jan 2021)... I highly recommend this new addition!!!
Good place, scenic view and nice coffee. You can sit out and enjoy the calm pace of the nature. Parking could be a issue

This Starbucks is a little slower then others I have been to but they always get my order right. The drive thru convenience is awesome. There's no other drive thru in the area. Be prepared to wait a min or two longer, but your order will come out just as it should. We need more drive thru locations in this area.

First time at this location and Jared made my day!  Received a Verizon free drink,  but was having difficulties pulling it up on the phone, Jared saw my troubles and said I will take care of you.   Because of Jared kindness, this will be   my Starbucks of choice, please visit this location.

How hard is it to make an Iced Caramel coffee with coconut milk? Seems pretty hard to this Starbucks location, I've been coming here for the past two days to see if they'll get it right but nooo, and oh not to mention that I ordered and payed for a venti coffee and got a grande
We had never been here and randomly stopped by on a road trip. Go through the drive thru just to see Bob's smiling face. You won't regret it, he's the man!

It's one of those stores that you either love or you hate, as witnessed by a lot of the reviews on this site.  Most of the employees are really awesome folks, and the two stars are for them.  The neighborhood is inherently self-entitled, and that sometimes makes for a difficult dynamic.  A tendency of management to listen to and accommodate the vocal minority is frustrating, and it leads to systemic changes that alienate regulars - as also witnessed in many other posts on this site.  The more this store tries to look and feel like any other Starbucks, the more it loses its charm.
They actually are not open until 5am if you go to their store early morning be mindful of that. Good service, odd set up for dealing with traffic inside when it is busy.

The system went down this morning three customers in front of me. The woman who had to deal with it was frustrated, understandably so as the line was growing on a busy Saturday morning. She finally gave up and anyone who was paying with a credit card, the two people in front of me at that point, got their drinks for free. I told the truth and paid with my app instead of walking away with my already made drink for free. No thank you, no apology for the wait and inconvenience.

I love this Starbucks. Every time I go, the baristas are super nice and very open to talk. They also recommend good drinks and many that I don't know about! Prices are good for the sizes. Word on the street is that they are finally using better ingredients while decreasing the amount of artificial sweeteners in their beverages. The layout is comfortable and there are plenty of tables to occupy. Wifi is great, they now have Google wifi which is 100 times better than the old one. I am definitely coming back here for sweet caffeine trips or simple college studying.

Parking is pretty bad here so unfortunately lose a star. And if you are on Germantown PK its really only accessible if you are heading EAST.
