And the place could use a good dusting and window cleaning. Staff is professional and very nice. The coffee was hot and good and the cinnamon roll was light, airy and tasty.
Very nice and freindy and and very quik,never in there longer then 5 minutes getting my coffee.
Starbucks!  At Tampa's airport!  Cue a Kermit the Frog-styled "YAAAAY!"  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tv4KQbDaCfM

Very Rude person as a barista! Went up as others that came after had been served! And she just barked what are you looking for! Told her and she still proceeded to make as she wanted not requested. Very rude and very discriminated against! I didn't think that is the culture that Starbucks would like to be known for! Surprised For sure!

I love this Starbucks! It's very close to my house which is an obvious plus. The staff is amazing they always take the time to say hi even when they are so busy!  I love that this Starbucks had a drive thru for those days I don't have time to go in, even then they know its me. Love this place!!
Great location and friendly staff. Whenever I'm visiting Downtown Reno this is the only Starbucks I go to.
I go here about 4-5 times a week and the staff is always very quick and friendly. They always get my order right!  The only problem I have is the drive through is hard to turn out of.

I'll admit this location is very "cozy", but we all know that is code word for small.  I arrived around 4pm on a Tuesday PM, looking for a 15-30 minute respite with a nice cup of Chai tea.  There wasn't a place to sit.  It had a very nice atmostphere with the usual holiday decorations and wifi.  A very nice large casual table that could seat 12 in the middle.  The Barista's were very nice but seemingly overworked.   Unfortunately it did take me almost 10 minutes to get my drink.  I'm sure it is much less crowded before school is let out.    My tea was good overall.  Will come back if I can get that corner leather seat!

This is the closest starbucks to my house. Not bad most of the time, standard. They drive me crazy cause I request light ice on cold drinks as they fill the cup and you end up with about a third of a cup of fluid. If I don't get light ice the 4 dollar drink is gone by the time I get home. Ordered light ice today and they filled the cup. I think I'll just skip this starbucks aND stop at one where I'm not paying for ice instead of a beverage.

I'm not sure I have much to say that hasn't already been said by others - the staff here is the friendliest and cheer up your day when you come to get coffee/tea before work. The inside is one of the largest Starbucks I've been into - ample working space and they have a small patio too. It does get some traffic in the morning and I think the parking lot is a bit strange as the drive thru is a weird figure around parking spaces. I highly recommend this location!
An impeccably clean Starbucks with a drive-through. Employees are friendly. Other than that, typical starbucks fare.

This is the friendliest Starbucks ever! They had free samples  of their peppermint mocha and they were delish. They carried on a conversation with you and didn't just write your name on a cup and forget but actually remembered my name.  If I lived on this side of Tucson I would be here daily!

I had such a welcoming experience this morning at this Starbucks, which is so hard to come by. An older women was very accommodating and helpful during our order and even made us a free drink for me to try. A younger lady was talking to us about college and making sure she got our order right :). I love this location!! Truly made a great start to our day

If you enjoy breathing carbon monoxide while sitting in the drive-thru for 20 minutes, then this is the Starfux for you!  My aunt was in from St. Louis and friending for some of their Joe, so I had no choice but to stop.  20 minutes later, she got her used-to-be-hot latte and I got my used-to-be-frozen frap.

My family and I called this Starbucks to see if they had they Unicorn Frappe since we were by it. The man on the phone was very rude and said NO Starbucks has them in this area! BS. I found 3, but we were by that location. I'm glad they were out! Will never do business at that one. Never doing business with Starbucks period! It's pretty shitty they roll out a new product and most places ran out Wednesday
Super awesome staff! They are very fast and welcoming. This is the only starbucks I will go to...the others don't even compare.

This is one of the most rude coffee stores in Sparks NV . He only employee who was working at the time we drove by never acknowledged us as we drove to the drive thru she flat out ignored us for 3 minutes! Ugly baristas get ugly reviews!

This Starbucks is spacious and well laid out. The drive thru is an added convenience. I visit several times per week and the staff is always courteous. I prefer espresso and related drinks and am always pleased.
This is the slowest Starbucks I've ever been to. It's been open for long enough that you'd think the kinks would be worked out by now. Please do something to fix this issue.

I know Starbucks is known for their coffee, but I love love love thier Spinach Feta Wrap!!!  The coffee is pretty consistant from location to location, and I've noticed the staff is always super friendly.    The Edwardsville location is no different.  Good coffee.  Good food.  Friendly staff.  = AWESOME.
Your coffee beans are ground using cancer causing chemicals yet you felt the real threat was a pair of black men waiting on a friend so you call the cops?  Disgusting!
Don't shutdown the Clover machine early just so you can get out early. There are far too few Clover machines in town. Americanos and pour overs are not good substitutes.

Good service, but for some reason the past two times I've been there my drinks have been kind of "meh."  My husband's coffee was also filled to dangerous levels, which facilitated the making of a huge mess as coffee leaked from the cup.  With that said, the store is modern, clean and new and it's in a great location.

The barista was friendly and efficient.  My Americano was made properly.  I was able to use reward points to purchase my beverage.  My issue is when I went up for my refill I was told that they do not provide refills (not even with a $0.50 nuisance charge).  It seems like more and more Starbucks are not honoring refills. Certainly in my area.  This a loyalty program. It works two ways.  I purchase and Starbucks rewards...  You know; a quid pro quo!  Where is the quo?  Ok, a franchisee can do what they want.  Starbucks should at least make them post a sign and list this on the web app so we can decide not make the trip if we don't like the limitations.  Used to be if they redeem rewards, they provide all benefits.  No more.  There are some interesting coffee shops nearby.  I will try them out.  Starbucks is not a discount chain.  Without the rewards, I will give my business to independent shops.  It seems that is the only way to fight back.

Tell me why the white girl barista was all smiles and friendly to every customer until it was my turn to order. That smile turned to sideways glances and nervous laughter with the quickness. Maybe my shaved head and brown skin were too much and shattered your fragility? If seeing people like me scares you, you probably shouldn't attend a UC in Southern California. I promise to never again cause you anxiety with my presence.

This has been my staple place for Starbucks for the passed three years! They always get my order right and once I lost my debit card while in the drive through and the cashier still gave me my coffee! Talk about pay it forward. It's always clean and kept nice. The drive thru does get a little backed up in the morning! But it's worth it! I love this place!

Came on a late Saturday morning.  Not crowded, right before lunch time.  People behind the counter were much more interested in their very involved (and loud) conversations than actually serving the customers.  Very long wait time for my drink, and then, when I finally got it, it was wrong.  Baristas here are clearly not paying too much attention to what they're getting paid to do.  Several other customers before me had gone to sit down to wait for their drinks because it took SO long.  This poor service is unusual for a Starbucks, in my experience.

Ya, so this was the first place I went to get coffee today (if you read my other review about my coffee haul). I opted for a frappe, and it was nothing out of the usual for me as far as starbucks. The customer service was nice.  I like that their pastry bar was fully loaded with tasty looking treats. (no flies an urchins like ive seen at some locations). The restaurant is very clean, prompt professional service and a nice ambiance. I'd def go here again.  I agree with Rusty, this place could get 5 stars if it was a little less expensive.
Omg I love the coffee here.  Now I can see why Starbucks is so popular.  Only one coffee in my lifetime has been better than Starbucks, and that was Scot Coffee out of Allentown....
Big fan. This place was busy. They were fast, so the wait went fast. The employees were friendly. And as busy as they were, the was very clean.

we've been walking here at least twice a week with our dog for the last several years and i'm happy to say their coffee has improved (it somehow tastes less scalded)--either that or my taste buds have fallen prey to their insidious corporate homogenization. the baristas are friendly, though they quite often have a  bunch of young gals that are pretty clueless and may have to retrain their listening skills--at least they always make sure you're happy with your order.  this place gets crazy busy sometimes, which is half the fun if you're into people watching. we sit outside and watch the hectic parking lot and can help you with two simple TIPS: 1.) it's often faster to go inside 2.) don't park your car next to the drive through line when it's busy. lastly, you can ask for the misters to be turned on when it's hot out.   although this in not the coffee shop my PNW heart yearns for, and something inside me dies every time i come here, they're conveniently close to me and to other fun shopping spots, have a nice patio and their drinks are now more consistent. my detraction of stars stems mainly from some of their corporate practices (no recycle, still using #5 plastics and no effort to reduce waste, stupid lingo, price, froo-froo menu items that take forever to make and hold up the line), rather than their occasional sub-par barista.

Randomly locked up at 8:09 with no sign on the door and employees still inside when the posted hours are open until 9:00.... and for some reason my drink always tastes like a kiosk Starbucks from Albertsons or something compared to other locations. Friendly service, though.
This is the best Starbucks in the world! My wife and I love going to this location. The staff is always great and treat us like family when we come in. And they make great suggestions for drinks.

I realize the Carmel Apple Spice is probably the least exotic drink on your menu...but if you can't be bothered to make it correctly...then stop selling it...today I paid $3.54 for a cup of warm Apple juice..No...whip cream No...carmel sauce....Come on guys...Really????

I've only had positive experiences here. But then again, I come during non-rush times so that could be why. Baristas are always friendly and my orders never take too long. Shout-out to the gentleman helping me with my tumbler purchase on Saturday December 5, around 2PM. He was the best! Even remembered our names to say bye as we were leaving.  Parking can suck. This location is usually busy when I drive by. And that drive thru line can wrap around the entire building at times. My drinks are usually correct. Sometimes not as strong as I order but eh, it's whatever. I take my order to go so can't comment on seating situations.

What can you say about Starbucks. They have their quality down, their coffee down, and their pastries too down! ;-) So I hold the staff's feet to the fire. These people rocked it. They were friendly, polite, and extremely helpful!  I was tongue tied in ordering my "Skinny Venti Vanilla Latte! After we all laughed, I was schooled on the right way to order it! Their restrooms are always clean. That's huge to Gigi, the Mega-Princess! :-P The local bulletin board was filled with resources. I have to say this is one Big Corp Cuppa I enjoy! And this particular store rocks!

So tired of their uncooked bacon in their sandwich's. I ask them to cook it separately and they over cooked the entire sandwich, left the bread on top of the bacon to cook, and the bacon was still uncooked! Bread almost broke my tooth because they cooked it for soooooo long with no common sense that the bacon can not cook inside the sandwich!

This is supposed that be a peppermint mocha Frappuccino which normally contains coffee. It was so light. No coffee and definitely no mocha. The worst drink I've ever had at a starbucks, will not be coming back. Highly don't recommend.

This is one of my favorite Starbucks. It's fairly close to Whyte Avenue but less chaotic. Perfect when you need a break and a caffeine reload ;) Definitely less noisy and great service here. They were able to accommodate my drink without making funny faces that I often encounter on busy shops. I am just missing the outdoor sitting area on the south side of the store on sunny days though.
Amazing Starbucks with amazing employees.

I needed to use the bathroom at this location and for some reason I had to wait until an employee was free to escort me and open the door. This is not only embarrassing but dangerous for a 70y/o with limited control. The wait was 10 min. Needless to say I'll get my coffee elsewhere from now on!

Pricey Airport Starbucks  SITREP  They have the basic level 2 Starbucks products & service available at this location.  THE SETUP  I am not a fan of a Starbucks - whether it be in an Airport or a Stadium, etc. - that uses the location as an excuse to charge up to 35%-50% more for their product.  Paid nearly $3 more for the Venti Blonde & Sausage Breakfast sandwich I could have gotten for less at my home Starbucks.  THE LOWDOWN  They got most of the goods you want at a Starbucks, but they will make you pay for the "privilege" of being in an airport.
Nice little satrbucks in Carpinteria.  the staff is friendly and they make it a point to remember your name and what you ususally drink
I go here pretty regularly because location is convenient. But I find the quality of service here to be consistently poor compared to other Starbucks.

Love that Starbucks has a drive-through! Makes it seriously convenient for anybody on the go, especially with young children Intel. Great service, friendly staff, always a pleasure to frequent a Starbucks.

Clerk working the interior customer service counter - she was sick as a dog this morning. Is Starbucks not taking temperatures of employees and checking them? This girl (Caucasian girl with bright green hair) was so sick there was no way she should have been interacting with other people - she looked like a super-spreader. I am staying away from this store - hope she didn't give me Covid.

This Starbucks is still just a solid 3 Stars. They get the job done, but without any extra pizzaz in customer service.   I stopped in to pick up a Grande Cold Brew before my class over at the Apple store. The cashier was very dry, to be honest I do not remember her even thanking me or ordering. The wait for my beverage was minimal, considering two others were ahead of me in line. The young lady who presented me my drink, did thank me for coming in. So I figured, it was her job to say, "thanks".   I wish Starbucks would go to paper straws or the new lids that are like a travel mug and do away with the plastic straws.   Will I return? - Only if I am taking a class at the Apple store.   When: February 27, 2020 Why: Liquid refreshment for class

Came in last night and ordered a Rewards drink for my son. It was a customized butter beer recipe (Harry Potter). I found the batista to be at bit short and not very friendly. He seemed to be in a rush when I was trying to pull up the recipe on my phone even though there was no one behind us. When the drink was done, he slid it across the counter to my son, who is 11. Son whispered to me that it felt word to be given a drink like that.  I do frequent the St. Pete stores almost on a daily basis. Never got such a cold feeling in an otherwise warm place. I didn't bother ordering anything else. Is rather just go to my local Starbucks, where I am treated with a better attitude.   The drink was made correctly, so I did give 3 stars.

I'm with the first reviewer that this is not the best Starbucks to stop at. There is a nicer one down the street. I stopped to use the restroom and ordered two tall strawberry frappachino's. I'm not sure if they put any strawberries in here. The drink was not the usual pink color that it is at other Starbucks and it only tasted lightly of maybe strawberry syrup. Not worth the $7 I paid.

I have stopped here on several occasions with each and every visit ending on a poor experience. Out of all the SB stores in Tampa this has to be on the bottom of the list. I have been given luke warm coffee on one occasion and coffee without sweetener on my last visit. I only complain due to the fact that as we all know first thing in the morning when your in that morning wake up funk all you crave is a great cup of SB wake me up. It's a total bubble buster to leave the drive thru with your expected cup of happiness and end up tasting some blah tasting cold java....I don't believe I will be giving this SB a third attempt..Sad to say I will be avoiding this location and opt for one I have had better luck at...
I wanted a drink that I saw on tik tok and the girl in the shift made fun of me and I ended getting something completely different from what I wanted. Not nice

This is one of my favourite places to meet friends or colleagues for a coffee/tea when the weather is nice.  The outdoor patio is great with lots of tables and chairs that aren't crammed close together that you can hear every conversation around you.  It's in a great are too.  If weather doesn't permit to sit outside, there isn't a lot of seating inside and it can get cramped.  Some of the table and chairs are close together and most are for tables of 2.  It also can get busy during coffee break time and I have been where the line up is to the door.  The waiting area for drinks can get crowded too.  Will keep coming back when the weather is nice to enjoy the patio!

Nice service, nice coffee, and very quiet and comfortable environment. Free Wifi, and sufficient chargers for laptops if you wanna stay here for a while. I love the sunshine coming through the windows.

For a place that sells coffee which runs through most, they're so stingy about their bathrooms! It's on the news all the time and manifests localmly as well. We'd had coffee on the patio and someone was still inside though they'd locked the door. We had to use the restroom so badly, and everything had been closed.   Now, I know they had closed, but I've worked at retail, restaurants, etc before and worked the late shift. And have been put in the same situation multiple times. Sometimes you get caught and have to go! There is nothing wrong with that. Yeah  it's late, yeah I want to go home or not deal with people, but to deny someone the basic urge to use the restroom has always been such a disgusting act to me. Especially two women at night. We ended up having to go to a dirty bathroom across the street, where we couldn't even stay long because the parking lot was littered with creeps saying disgusting things to us.   Honestly, it's not hard to be a decent human being and the things you do reflects on the establishment you work for. I dont know who the guy inside was, but he ignored our knocks, and rolled his eyes at us when I finally knocked hard enough for him to have to acknowledge us.   Starbucks really needs to get their restroom situation together I've never seen an establishment with employees that act this way about the bathroom.

Good. I had a strawberry shake with a almond croissant. Man was it expensive, but that's why I do not come here often nor pay for it (gift card used :P). I normally do not drink coffee, especially here at Starbucks. Once you tried coffee outside of US, especially at a coffee plantation, Starbucks is disgusting compared to real coffee. Service was great, ordered my food and drink and it was ready in about 5 min. It was not so crowded on a Sunday night, so, that's great. This is a chain, so price will never go down, just up, but I only stop if I have a gift card. Otherwise, I avoid Starbucks anywhere.  Overall: Great service, ok price, good food and junk

I went through drive thru and the first two things I ordered they were out of. The women that was taking my order was rude and rushing me after I asked if I could please have a moment to look at the menu since the two things I ordered they did not have. She had an attitude and I wasn't very happy with the service from placing my order to paying at the window where she seemed annoyed to even be there. So I ordered a strawberry refresher and cold brew with pumpkin. I didn't notice until after I was about to drink the strawberry refresher it had rotten strawberries in the drink that looked moldy. I love Starbucks but this particular location is horrible I've had problems in the past but since this is the closest Starbucks to me I decided to give it another try. Definitely won't return to this location I rather go out of my way it's a shame because other reviews that I've seen now are just as bad.
Good morning! I hear this every time I come in. The staff knows my name and my order before I reach the counter. Nice. I prefer Starbucks over the others.  Good place for good coffee and good people.

Unbelievable.  I sent two of my kids in (12 and 15) to get drinks and when one of them asked to change the order for one drink they returned to the car telling me that they were not permitted to make the change.  I had to go in personally and show my face, at which point they acquiesced.  Really?? We spend $$$ at this place each month -- many people do.  What a blatant effort to bully young people .. why?  It was early evening and the place was deserted.  For the record, this was 6/8/19 around 6:30 at night.  The young woman who was working that evening should be fired.
My favorite Starbucks. They never forget my order. They make it with smiles on their faces
I love this place, my daughter and I go twice a week at least. Nice people who work there, I bring my daughter to work in the morning, so we stop on the way.
Pretty big with outdoor tables. Convenient location at a mall complex with a drive thru window. Free wifi is nice. AC is blowing. Floors and tables are dirty and trash needs taking out.

Worst Starbucks I've ever been too! This place was filthy and the restrooms were disgusting with no toilet paper. When we let the baristas know, they said they were too busy to worry about the restrooms even though there was only 1 person in line. In addition, when I asked if they made a been there mug for Santa Barbara, they said that the been there series didn't do any cities just states which is obviously false. Avoid this location at all costs!!!

Wah!  The local coffee house I used to visit when I was in this area went under!  Bah!  Well, a man has gots to have his mocha, if you know what I mean.  Hmm.  OK, that sounds dubious at best.  Anyway, I went to the local Starbucks instead after discovering the local independent was gone.  Service was actually friendlier than the last time I visited a Starbucks.  But the coffee was not up to even Starbucks normal standards.  Espresso in my mocha was off...did they change their espresso roast since my last visit to this chain?  Or was it just burnt or something?  Is Starbucks losing consistency?

my drink was ready quickly and accurately. baristas were friendly and efficient. the starbucks area was tidy and organized and you didn't have to venture so far into the store to get your coffee. it was really busy at 7:30 am on saturday because of a huge kids' soccer tournament in town (parents were picking up coffee for themselves and their 10 closest friends). the baristas were able to keep up with the volume impressively well. for me, there's nothing wrong with this starbucks.

These folks move through molasses with seemingly little sense that they work in an airport and have planes to catch.  I've been in line already for 10 minutes with little progress.  Staff need more help and need to step up their game.
My favorite Starbucks in Tucson. I love the extended hours, location and layout with a choice of shaded or in the sun seating outdoors. They have always made my selected drinks correctly.

This is a lovely Starbucks in a cozy and homey neighborhood. They have indoor and outdoor seating and the outside decor is cute. The interior is large with ample seating that includes sleek, black leather armchairs.   There are small tables as well as a long community one and I personally find this location to be a great place to get work and reading done.

Stopped in to get coffee for my long ride back home as I'm from out of state, and was very happy with the customer service from the staff! The young lady (wow that makes me sound old, lol) who took my order was very pleasant! The store is beautiful & if I had the time, I wud have loved to stay awhile & enjoy the atmosphere!! And of course my coffee was simply to die for! Thanks for giving me a really nice moment in my day!!

So this is the Starbucks in Tampa's F terminal, which happens to be a Starbucks reserve! They have the reserve clover-brewed coffee. Ultimately it was a little disappointing because I mistakenly thought all reserve locations also had the nitro cold brew. I didn't snap a picture but it seems like they've also significantly downsized from their renovation a year ago? There wasn't any Starbucks-specific seating and I felt squished waiting in line for my drink. Other than that it was on-par with any other location.

I Usually drive through Starbucks but today I had to use the facilities so I went inside. I ordered my usual iced blueberry black tea no liquid cane sugar two pumps sugar-free vanilla. It was not very busy. I used the restroom, came back out and waited and waited and waited for my ice tea. While waiting, another  lady asked where her egg bites were. She already had drank 1/3 of her venti tea and still no food. It appeared that the 2 young men "working" were too busy talking to a young girl to fill orders. One girl was working and she was doing everything. I'm not sure they left out the cane syrup, as I watched her make my drink. By that point I just wanted my drink and to leave. Not a happy customer today. I'm hoping Sbux sends me a request for a survey, as they often do.

I fully admit I came here after the May 29 company-wide shut down to talk about new policies that said anyone can come in and hang out without having to purchase anything in the store.  To be honest, I was waiting for a friend who was running late for dinner down the street, and I had time to kill. So I tested the new policy. I sat down, used their free wifi, spent about 30 minutes, and left. No hassle by anyone.  Let's be honest, I spend enough money at Starbucks at a monthly basis that I don't feel much guilt about using their free wifi for a few minutes.

Holy crap! I walked into this location today to get a latte and I thought I was in the wrong place.  They have completely renovated their store and it is beau-ti-ful! They've installed lovely dark floors, moved the two sided fireplace to be accessible to more completely new leather club chairs, added a wall divider for a more private room and (probably most importantly) added a TON of countertop seating WITH PLUGS for studying.
Love this place. It's the old long John silver's but coffee smell has taken over. Good thing, coffee/fish probably not a good smell. Just need more parking like all the others
Cain and Phoebe were the sweetest! Honestly really made my day and I'll continue to pass on their kindness throughout my day
Worst Starbucks ever. The workers are So slow. And they Never have anything. Save urself a 30 min wait just find something in the fridge
What to say - Its a Starbucks with great service.  Usually hang outside after cycling on the weekend - can't beat it.  Baristas are very friendly.
Had to have my daily Starbucks! It's not for luxury it's a necessity!! Love this store since it's so close to my house. Clean store, yummy drinks & a nice crews! Until next time,...probably tomorrow!

This is my all-time favorite Starbucks location! I have been a loyal Starbucks Gold Card member for some years. There have been a lot of Good, even some great locations... but this one by far is my favorite. It is very convenient for me because it is on my way to work, but there are times I have had to go to other locations for different reasons. Every time I do, I am disappointed because of the HIGH BAR this location has set. The manager is exceptionally helpful, friendly and professional. The staff is AMAZING. Friendly, upbeat, proficient. I have never had problems with my order. They're smiles make my day! KUDOS! I'm a fan!  ~Annie Kidd

We were staying at a nearby hotel, mobile ordered, and picked up our food at the hand off plane! Our order was not quite ready for us but almost! There was not much of a connection at the handoff but our food and beverages were great! We will return on our trip!

Went for lunch since I work 12 hour shifts, coffee is needed to get through the day. Only get a 30 minute window to run out. Traffic can be rough depending on when I go due to the mall and turnpike. Got there and ordered. Was very nice and energetic. Coffee was good and I was out in minutes. They were all attentive and the place was clean. Perfect place for a quick trip at lunch if you're in the area.
Literally the worse customer service. The only thing that is consistent here is the lack of good service.

"Now, this is your first lesson in coffee. Without anything in it, it's black coffee. Can you say black coffee? Then, there's coffee regular. That means two sugars and milk, which they have forgotten, so can I borrow some of yours? Ah, coffee regular." -- Look Who's Talking (1989)  What a great little Starbucks - and I mean little. We walked in after eating across the street and were in need of a cup of coffee and a sweet snack for dessert.  We ordered and then looked around and realized there was no place to sit - at all... then the young lady behind the counter said there was more seating upstairs... what?  Wow - we took our coffees and snacks upstairs and had a great space to sit and chat for an hour until it was time to close the upstairs for the night.  The service was good, the drinks were prepared properly and the overall atmosphere was nice.  Also, it was quiet, but it was also at night - so not a true test.

This location is fairly small -- not a lot of indoor seating and it can be crowded while waiting for your drink. But, they do have a fair amount of seating plus a "walk up" window for people who are out walking their doggies.  They have a Clover, which they use to brew their specialty coffees. I am CDL girl, so I don't have a well developed palate for coffee. But, I saw the machine in action and it looked pretty cool.   Yeah, Starbucks is a chain, and there are a lot of great local businesses, but sometimes Starbucks is just so darn convenient.   If you are riding your bike on the Cultural Trail, there's a bike rack nearby.

This Starbucks is extremely busy. Literally every time I've been here, different hours of the day. Expect a long wait whether you go in or go through the drive-thru. But in good news, they have never messed up an order that I've placed, and I'm very picky with my coffee. If you like Starbucks and are willing to wait, this place isn't that bad. I recommend the drive-thru because the inside is pretty tiny and everyone packs in there, so seating is very limited and it's pretty loud.

Took a little coffee break coming from Hearts Castle back to L.A. This place was busy but the staff was very friendly. Coffee  was delish as usual. Bathroom is clean. And it's easy access from the shopping center where it's located.

I've been to this specific location a couple of times, and I've liked it just fine. The coffee is good, and the baristas are friendly. Today, I went in for a free drink with a coupon I earned. The girl behind the counter was super sweet! She helped me figure out what drink I wanted (I wasn't sure) and threw in all the extras since she knew it was free. When I picked it up, she said if I didn't love it they would make another one. The drink itself is delicious, obviously, but what stuck out was the phenomenal customer service!
Typical Starbucks experience. I'm a simple order Americano w/ room for milk. Can't really mess me up.
The atmosphere is nice but the staff was not helpful and did not take my name. They appeared new or understaffed as they did not know what I asked for. Not what I expect out of Starbucks.

This is a nice Starbucks, but it's always very COLD in this location.  The staff doesn't seem to care that everyone who is sitting there is wearing their coats, scarfs, and gloves (me included) while sipping on their coffee.   I understand some coffee shops do that to shush people away when it's crowded, but this store is usually pretty empty.  So if you are one of those people who loves to sip your coffee in a 38 F degree place, this is for you.  But if you are like me and the majority of people who rather be in a 68-70F store, run away from this place.
Yes the coffee is good but an additional bonus is that the employees here are for the most part very attractive. A+

Congratulations to this mindless group of assholes that work at this location for losing a lifelong Starbucks customer for good! After ordering my drinks with my mask on, I went and sat down in a lobby full of maskless customers and removed my mask while I waited for my order. I was promptly told to put my mask back on or go wait outside in the cold. When I asked what the difference was between me waiting for my drink in the lobby with no mask on and the other paying customers sitting in the lobby with no mask on, she said "they already got their drinks". When I said "fine can I have some water?" She told me no, and told me to get out. This is not about keeping anyone safe anymore. Not that it really ever was. This is 100% about keeping people in line and making them follow absurd rules for no rational reason. There is absolutely no difference between not wearing a mask while you're waiting for a drink and not wearing a mask while you have a conversation with your friend and a drink on your table. I demanded a refund and told her that her customer service is garbage and that because if her the company is losing business. She then says "the company is doing just fine" and handed me my money. All while her cloth mask was hanging half off of her face. Screw Starbucks.

I don't add cream to my coffee, so logically, I do not want my Starbucks barista to prepare my coffee for me with the top third of the cup empty to make room for cream.  I learned quickly that people are encouraged to tell the baristas "room for cream" if they want it, and "no room for cream" if they do not.  So that is what I continuously did.  And the baristas would nod and smile and it would seem that they were listening, so it was awfully surprising when I'd open my cup to put my Equal in and see that they have graciously made room for cream.  Gah.  The first few times this happened, I was willing to let it go; I'm not one to rock the boat, and at the times I'd be visiting this Starbucks it was always five minutes before I was starting work for the day, so I wasn't really interested in waiting some more.  Additionally, I don't think I have much drive in me prior to my first cup of coffee of the day to even formulate a good conversation, so it was all for naught, really.     However, one day I finally had enough.  Ever the optimist, and coupled with a naivete that somehow didn't get squashed while growing up in New York, I ordered my coffee with no room for cream, and a ready-for-cream cup was returned to me.   I gave the cup back to the barista after my discovery and asked her to please fill it up.    She told me I had to wait in line again to order.   I got a little feisty and told her that was a page in the unfair book that I had highlighted and earmarked, and that it doesn't take a lot for her to please just take the large container of coffee located just to her left and use the next three seconds of her life to put it in my cup.   She then rolled her eyes and made some remark to a fellow employee that people like me are the reason she wants to 'quit the coffee business forever.'  I couldn't contain myself and proceeded to laugh at her.     I mean, it's just a lot of headache to simply procure a coffee!  I gave the situation some thought for awhile afterwards and my conclusion is that there is absolutely nothing SO great about anything Starbucks has available that I would be so terribly lost without it and have to return.  I can get better tasting coffee *anywhere* in the city, even right next door.  And I can get better pastries elsewhere.   And so on.  Though I guess I am a little sad that I won't be able to buy those weird CD compilations Starbucks sells off of their record label or whatever the deal is with that.   Sarcasm meter on.

*I ordered ahead of time by using the Starbucks app... my order wasn't even ready at the window.  * I had to come back because I realized they forgot to give me my coffee cake and I asked for it not to be warmed on the receipt  * on my receipt, it states "shopping bag... $0.00" At first he made state out everything I had ordered and I looked at my phone for verification but then he took it (the bag) away from me. Super awkward because I needed to carry my shit.

This Starbucks location is just okay. I prefer the one on walnut and 34th much more. This Starbucks location definitely is one of the busiest in the area but I feel that they're always a bit under staffed. Baristas are always struggling to fulfill the online orders.  I always get a tall americano but I know that they almost always just pour dripped coffee which is not what I ordered.   Though, I am impressed with their selection other than coffee which include variety of dessert items, sandwiches, mugs etc. Cake pops cures all.  I'll probably come back to this location just because it's convenient for me but doesn't mean that I'm happy about it. Meh

It's nice to be able to sip and an enjoy a Starbuck coffee while my wife shops at Sephora at Keystone Xing.  This Starbucks is a little small and has an odd layout considering the two entrances and exits.

Unfortunately, every time I've come in to this Starbucks, the customer service was pretty horrible. Not only does it take forever to get a drink, the coffee was not very good (compared to other Starbucks) and the baristas were not friendly. Definitely not my fav, and meh, i've definitely experienced better.
You will sit in the drive thru for at least half an hour between 12-5pm. If you don't, you got lucky. They are slow and still get a simple order wrong.

Literally just left this Starbucks drive thru. I drove through yesterday morning and was specifically looking for the raspberry loaf to have with my chai tea latte, unfortunately they were out. I was craving Starbucks again today so I went back to this same Starbucks cause they make all the drinks THE BEST. Anywho, drove through, asked for the raspberry loaf again and they didn't have any again which was no biggie but I was craving one so I got the next best thing which was their classic coffee cake. Drove up to pay and get my items and the lady who helped me at the window remembered me from yesterday and apologized for not having the raspberry loaf, shortly after our encounter the store manager came to the window to apologize and inform me they'll make sure to order more raspberry loafs to be safe and because of the inconvenience (which it wasn't at all) they comp'd my classic coffee cake. True class, professionalism, and customer service at its best. BEST STARBUCKS in town...on all levels!!

Very slow service inside. It seems that they pay more attention to the drive-through customers. Coffee was okay but they needed to restock the milk.  Spacious interior but I didn't care for the loud music playing.  Outdoor seating but noisy as next to busy road.   Too bad no one was smart enough to put the outdoor seating to the side or back Where it would be quieter.

I'm giving this location and this brand 5 stars because it serves its purpose well.  But let's have some real talk for a moment.  You're probably on Yelp to complain about some absolutely insignificant thing that insulted your ego; like the grown woman a few comments below who was upset because she couldn't get what she wanted on her birthday. Lady, are you kidding me? Or the other person who was upset because the decor wasn't what they wanted it to be. Do you hear yourself?  I, just like you, was lucky to be born into a typical, middle class, American family. And I grew up just outside of a major, international city; NYC. I'm a hardworking professional, and when I spend my money I want to feel like I got what I paid for. So I understand how others might feel the same way.   But some of you take it too far.   I get it, when you were a kid your mother and your teachers told you that you were special and your fathers called you a princess. But that was B.S. you're not special. You are one of 350 million Americans and one of 6 billion on the earth. You mean nothing to people outside of your family so stop acting like a soiled, bratty, juvenile.   If you feel like you have been insulted by a product that you've received at a Starbucks or some other coffee place or restaurant, you are shallow and have no grasp of reality.  While you take to Yelp to reap vengeance on this company, children are literally dying all over the world. Children and adults in Haiti are making "cookies"out of mud because there's nothing for them to eat, and you're complaining about not being treated special on your birthday. Get a clue. There are children all over Africa that don't even know their own birthday because their families are too concerned with priorities like, where to get fresh water and nutritious food.   At a certain point in your life you have to realize your age and your responsibilities and you should understand by now that people respect a hardworking, self reliant, ethical adult. Not someone who is easily upset by a Starbucks promotion or lack there of. Those whom take to Yelp to attack a company, or worse, an underpaid employee of a company, are seen as hysterical and without merit or importance.   To those of you whom write nasty, negative comments on Yelp....  I would be embarrassed to be you.    I do not work for Starbucks or Yelp. My opinions are just that, mine. And it should not be assumed in any way that Starbucks or Yelp share my perspective.

This starbucks is always out of everything!! Management really needs to do a better job of keeping sure things are stocked up because EVERY time I go to this starbucks they're out of something. Kinda ridiculous if you ask me. There was atleast 7 people working and it took 15minutes to get a tea and coffee.(this is hands down the slowest store in tucson no matter what day I come) and the store was absolutely DISGUSTING how dirty it was.
I bought a sandwich and they serve it to me cold. They didn't offer me a new one. The service sucks here
When I get a refresher it's always way under filled and the music inside was cranking. Sounded like a club in there. Very hard to study.

I came to this Starbucks with high expectations I've been an oro valley native my whole life and I've not been pleased. I went with my girlfriend and they took 35 minutes to get her drink prepared even when we reminded them that they forgot and that hasn't been the first time they forgot a drink order, as my parents went a week ago and my mom ordered a regular coffee and didn't get it either until she complained that they forgot. The atmosphere lacks a coffee shop vibe as well as it feels like I'm in an auditorium in the open portion towards the left side. So far I prefer the old Starbucks that we had by papa johns and probably won't be coming to that Starbucks again.

If ever a Starbucks needed a location makeover, the Paoli Starbucks was it. To actually have adequate parking at the Paoli Starbucks fulfilled a desperate need for them.   But sitting here, I feel like they didn't quite get the seating area right. I've been in more comfortable Starbucks before. I feel like they missed an opportunity to add more comfort seating, instead they went with harder tables. They could have added more outlets for laptop use, but I don't see as many.   There are a lot of improvements. But they could have done better.

Very nice location which will be celebrating its 1st anniversary shortly. Baristas are all friendly, location is nice and clean and so is their restroom.  I learned that they were trained by my pastor's elder daughter who works @ the one on 611 in Jenkintown - haven't gone there yet since it's out of my way and whenever I'd go by commuting to my former work location there was never any parking in their lot and am unfamiliar with Jenkintown's twisty, turney streets.  Like another reviewer said sometimes they can be a bit on the slow side.  One day I was very lucky and my friend who works @ the Jenkintown location filled in that day - I must say that my Frappucino that great day was extra tasty.  The only food item I tasted (since they tend to be pricey) was their Bantom Bites (little balls made out of bagel dough) which were OK in taste but not worth the value.  I like to come here when am looking for a special treat @ the end of the week since I don't live around the corner.  It's in the Huntingdon Valley Shopping Center which is just a few feet away from the building where my primary care doctor and my gyn are located - also a Pet Valu is a few doors down - also turning 1 year old soon.  At the other end of the shopping center is the Giant that I do my grocery shopping in, get stamps and prescriptions filled.  The reason this is a 4 instead of a 5 is that they have a $5 minimum limit when reloading your card (whereas the Starbucks located in said Giant has no such limit) and the service can be sometimes a tad slow.  However, you shouldn't go wrong if you give this location a shot - and there's plenty of parking to boot and the Route 24 bus stops at the shopping center.

Service here is fantastic no problems with customer service , but got a protein box today with a chicken wrap , apples and cucumbers and got so sick . Had it for my lunch break and was so sick at work with it

The workers were welcoming and got my order right. However, I just have to say that this was by far the dirtiest Starbucks I have ever been to. Mind you, at 5:30am the place should look fresh and clean. It was not. The night shift definitely did not clean the bathrooms and there was a pile of stuff on the counter between my server and I. It just had a dirty feeling to the place.

Recently I've seen an improvement in service. I also have noticed several new employees and so am happy that management must be listening to feedback. If the service levels continue to be positive I will update my rating. Keep up the customer focus......it matters!

Service with a smile!  Went through the drive thru because my dog was with me in the car. The line went quickly and they made my drink perfectly. I ordered a puppaccino for my dog (whipped cream in a cup) and they served it with two little dog treats on top. He was so happy!!  If you're looking for a great coffee alternative try an iced green tea latte with coconut milk. It's like a dessert in a cup without all the guilt.
This location has greatly improved over the past few months. They always make my order right and the line moves much faster. I appreciate management taking action and improving the service.
waited in line at the drive-thru for 30 minutes at 2:30 PM. No option to leave because of the curb on both sides. Absolutely ridiculous. Will not return.

I love this Starbucks!  I agree with some other reviewers about the decor not being as fancy or modern as other Starbucks, but I don't really care about the decorations as much as the customer service.  We ALL know that Starbucks isn't cheap, but when you have great service it feels worth it.   Consequently - I've had HORRIBLE service at the one across the street in La Cumbre Plaza....literally the barista called out "Venti iced coffee sweetened for Mary" and I stood up and apologetically gestured "Oh, I said nosweetener actually" and she glared at me while dumping out my iced coffee (continuously glaring at me) and then refilled it and pushed it towards me.  Her coworker was standing next to her and I smiled at her and said "Wow, what great customer service, huh?" She just smiled and looked away.  I mean, if you hate your job it definitely shows through to your customers.  I've never had a bad experience here at this Starbucks, but I avoid the other like the plague.  I come for the coffee but I come back for the excellent service.  In fact, at this one in particular I've had baristas tell me that if I'm not happy with my drink choice to please let them know so they can remake it.

Located on Whyte Avenue along what some have dubbed "Coffee Corner" this place is packed, overflowing, loud, and too busy. I generally head to the independent Block 1912 across the street rather than a chain place if I can help it.
Staff is very friendly in the morning but when I come in in the afternoon they are always playing abrasive rap music. Don't really want to hear nwords and fbombs when I get my coffee.

This is my regular morning and sometimes afternoon stop.  Great atmosphere and a little larger than most other Starbuck locations.  The staff is pleasant.  Sometimes there is a wait, but typically not too bad.

The music is always literally blaring. The girl was so rude at the counter. I simply asked her how much something was and she just says "I don't know"... then ignored me. This Starbucks has the worst customer service I've ever experienced. Again WHY IS YOUR MUSIC ALWAYS BLARING...

Loved the convenience of being inside the hotel and not having to walk into the city. Always quick service but as previously stated, they do not accept rewards/coupons from the app. Also it closes at 5pm during the week which was disheartening after sitting all day in classes at the hotel and not being able to enjoy the cafe afterwards. Clean place and friendly staff.
I go to this location every morning before work.  I find the baristas friendly and engaging.  They always remember my drink and are cheerful at the ungodly hour of 4:30 AM.

I'm a diabetic and before I ran my daughter there I researched online my drink options. Satisfied with my choice, I ordered the drink I found online only to be told this location didn't have it. ( some sugar free vanilla spice option). However, the barista said she could make something very similar that would meet my sugar free requirements. Delicious! Thanks to her, I am a sugar free vanilla latte with 3 pumps chai addict. I've gone to other Starbucks and placed my order and have had comments made by the employees on how that must be yummy. Kudos to her!

This is a VERY small Starbucks location, but it's just as good - or better - than others!  Because of the small size, it feels more "coffeehouse" and quaint, and when it's not too busy, it's a very peaceful place to enjoy a cup of coffee and read or relax for a bit.    The employees are always very nice and really helpful, and willing to make your food exactly how you like it.  The condiment bar has always been fully stocked, clean, and large enough for multiple people to use at the same time.  The tables are usually clean and tidy.  I'm glad there's a Starbucks location towards the outskirts of Tucson!

Good location off I-80 for those of us traveling though. Friendly staff. Typical Starbucks coffee. Location a little small if you are looking for a place to hang and spread out. Perfect for a grab and go and service was fast.  Also has a drive-through.

Have been coming here consistently on Sundays with my sister as part of our weekly bonding issue. I've always really appreciated that they take care to get our orders right. Both of us order some strange things, mixed with weird dietary things, and they've always been great!   Drinks are on point!

I love that this location has a drive thru! I wish all Starbucks did. That way I wouldn't have to go inside and see all the douchebags hanging out all day pretending to write novels and screenplays. This location always seems to be busy, probably because there aren't many Starbucks in the area. They still manage to keep the line moving pretty quickly.
This place is fabulous. I am in love with the frappucinos. Java Chip is the best. Or I get the banana chocolate viviano if Im feeling healthy.

Rude Rude Rude Rude Rude baristas!!! No hello, no good afternoon, no thank you, no anything.. honestly just told me my total and walked away!! I said thank you too the other barista that made my drink and she said nothing either!!!  Made my drink pretty fast, that's the only reason they got any stars. Welcome to Brentwood...where we're too good for anyone.

During the week, it's fine. On a Sunday morning, absolute mess. I get it, it's busy. But when I say light ice & they give extra ice or ask for a hot latte and they offer me a cold one, I get a little annoyed.  The line is long which is good for them, but the staff is clearly disheveled.

My daughter loves the spinach feta wrap and I like all of the varieties of the sous vide egg bites especially the bacon one. Generally we order a custom fru fru drink and a very classic coffee with 2 breakfast foods and almost all of the time they get it correct. So let's say that we go to Starbucks four times a week. That is approximately $85 a week. I  am not sure why a super simple iced coffee with heavy whipping cream only was incorrect. It had sugar in it. I know the difference when something has sugar in it. My cheeks  get hot and I feel funny. It was so sugary I think I lost a toe over the excessive amount if sugar that pulsed through my body.   OK I understand that Starbucks is like anyplace else understaffed and overly busy.   We almost always preorder and we always have to wait at least 20 minutes, which is fine.   Today we ordered With a person standing outside taking orders in the drive-through.  If  i am able to  correctly navigate the Starbucks app and get my order correct, I would expect that they would be able to do the same as a Starbucks employee taking my order in person.  What is concerning is that when I called to let them know Of their mistake and that I would be back  after dropping kids to school, in about 20 minutes, that I really didn't have time to wait. The person was RUDE and told me they couldn't Guarantee how long it would take and then I may have to wait. I asked  i could give them the order number and have them make it and I would be in shortly,  20 minutes. Again I was told she couldn't guarantee the wait time. I asked if she could just make it and I would be in  and it didn't need to be hot or cold it just needed to be corrected without sugar.    Somehow the phone call was disconnected. It was rude and I haven't ever had this happen at a Starbucks where they custom make your order. Just grumpy that I still don't have my coffee and I have to check my blood sugar and  likely correct it with insulin as I don't consume sugar/sugar substitutes. Thanks Starbucks.     Really it's a blessing because I am going to save my 85 dollars and make my own coffee.  I hope this teaches my daughter that if you pay for a service or product the expectation is that it's correct and if it's not, then advocate as a consumer, to have it corrected without rudeness.

Some say that Starbux is Starbux no matter where you go but I have to disagree. Out of the three or so locations in the "greater Northeast," this is definitely one I prefer. They seem to have the least waiting time (if you're getting something inside), they have enough of the outside seating (3 tables for them and 3 tables for the mexican place next door which are shared), wifi always works gret (I go there a lot when I am working from home) the staff is always smiling and they actually don't burn their coffee as much as some of the other locations.   Now, some things could be a little better: 1. The wait at the drive-thru is often much longer than I anticipate and, for that matter, prefer... I am not sure what the cause is but once, I waited for 18 minutes while there were only 2 cars in front of me) 2. They could do a better job cleaning outside after those very nice people who neglect to throw out their trash

If you enjoy really horrible, condescending service, you'll  love this location! Got lectured on how to make a Starbucks Double Shot on Ice by some 19 year old pot head snob after he made it wrong. Little did he know, I was a supervisor at Starbucks for 2 years. Other than that, it's Starbucks. You already know the rest of the story.

Great location. Staff is friendly, place is clean, drinks are ready quickly. Easy off the highway location (we stopped en route to Iowa, and were happy we didn't have to drive miles off the exit). The shop was busy, but there are plenty of places to sit and the temperature is nice (hate when a Starbucks is 100 degrees).

Slowest Starbucks in the history of the planet. It's 2:20pm on a weekday, there's literally 9 people in the entire store, and I'm going on 12 minutes waiting for a latte I ordered for my fiancé. Sadly, this is a reoccurring problem here. I come here to grab her coffee a lot, and what should be a 5 minute stop turns into a 20 minute adventure, every single time.   Other than that, the store is clean, and the employees are nice. Just your typical (slow) Starbucks.
Bathrooms could be a little bit more cleaner, but this Starbucks is gorgeous inside! Cute barista there too ;)

Let me preface this by stating that I rarely reivew things.  I have only good things to say about this stabucks, I frequent many starbucks and this location is by far my favorite.  I am a very consitent person and like to go in when i know my favorite barista will be there.  The service is wonderful and they really do make an effort to get to know you and to be friendly.  A special shout out to Kevin the barista for being such a positive person and always brightening my day!  I am always happier when I leave this location than when I come in.  They have nice tables that have excellent light for studying and it is always very clean and welcoming.  Additionally there are rarely lines inside of the store, but the drive through is usually busy because of the proximate location to the highway. Best, Reagan
Baddddd Attitude !  Customer service  really poor. Other then this ... Same old same old ... Coffee
This is true Starbucks - unique, lots of tables, music - making it a great place to hang out. Line can get long sometimes but it usually moves well. Parking is convenient but can be scarce when busy.
This new location is notoriously slow in preparing mobile orders. They also served my wife burnt coffee beans recently in her eggnog latte. Friendly, but certainly lacking in the production side.

This Starbucks is staffed with lovely folks who provide quick service and a smile!  And they're open until 10 pm, which is a big plus as well.  And have you tried the Peppermint Brownie Cake Pop?!?  So yummy, and a nice *wee* treat in this season of overabundant treats!  The only bad thing?  The parking lot is teensy and difficult to maneuver when there's a line for the drive through.  It's too easy to get trapped.
Love this place. I always get a ice mocha with no whip. But they give me the whip in another cup for my doggie.....

First let me say I am a fan Starbucks. However, this particular Starbucks is one of the worse stores that I have ever visited. This place was dirty, I ask for paper towels for the men's room and I was looked at like an inconvenience (never got the towels), I ask for two packs of honey in my coffee, so what did she do? she slapped two sticky packs on the counter and said, here you go. I paid for my coffee with cash, instead of the clerk handing me back my change, she slapped 6 dollars and 86 cents in coins all over the counter and kind of shoveled it over to me. I understand that this is the electronic age but surely this young lady knows what cash is and that it will not bite you. Coffee shops are popping up everywhere now days, if Starbucks management isn't careful the stores are going to continue to decline. Competition is tough.

Quick and easy-- unless you are driving. This Starbucks is in the heart of tourist traps. There is no parking around. I walked here from my hotel. The line was a bit long so I was worried it would take forever. They were quick and got my order right. That's really all I can ask for.
Manager and staff are awesome. Same good stuff as every Starbucks. Best staff I've seen

I drove back from Fort Lauderdale to St Petersburg today, and stopped at Naples for coffee.  I wanted a Bullet Proof Coffee, and was told they don't make it. So Starbucks! It's time to get with the times and offer bullet proof coffee! It's the latest greatest coffee and as a coffee company you should offer it, or you will lose business! Or, let's put it the other way: Offer it and you'll gain a ton of business!  Here's the recipe: 1 tbsp real salted butter, 1 tbsp good quality virgin coconut oil. Whisk up in a blender for one minute, and VIOLA! Boom! Bullet proof coffee! Let me know when you have it, and I will be back!

I was over on this side of town to shop at the local WinCo last Friday.  There was a queue going all the way around the building like no thanks I will pass! I noticed this Starbucks across the street, so I thought what the heck why not treat yourself since you wasted a ton of gas coming over. I noticed they had a drive-up window, but the line was long. I went in and they were very nice to me. I got my usual order. I get a skinny vanilla latte. I found it interesting how different the order tastes depending on who is making it. I found it super annoying that it's not the same every single time. Like it's a basic recipe for goodness sake.  When I go to a juice bar and make a order, it's almost always tastes the same. I know it's silly to be annoyed but I just don't get it. That's why I love my coffee at home! It tastes exactly the same every morning. I also think it gets pretty expensive to get it everyday...  The waitstaff guy was super friendly and accommodating to me. So I want too disappointed that the coffee was different tasting once again. Home is better :) Cute location and clean, well organized store.

We come to this Starbucks every time we are in town it's always a great atmosphere but, today it was not.  I'm assuming it's the store manager not wearing her apron and has the nastiest attitude I've ever seen.  She's giving the most horrible customer service to every single customer apparently because the morning shift did not stock prior to them leaving.  She's letting out her frustrations at the customers.  I ordered my drink at 1.31p and just now got it at 1.54p and it was made wrong.  When I mentioned it to her all she said was "my bad" no I'm sorry no I'll remake for you, nothing she just turned around threw the drink away and had another associate make my drink.  This is not what you should expect when walking into any Starbucks.

ONCE AGAIN - I ordered an UN sweetened green iced tea. Really stressing the UN. But no, it's sweetened.  Blah!  Why can't they get a simple order right??? Does no one pay attention? Or is it always a new batista that doesn't quite have if down yet? Very dusappointing.
Great place, friendly staff. Rarely get my order wrong. Definitely the preferred place to go instead of other coffee places.

I was looking for a local owned coffee shop in Florissant and realized that they do not exist. The three I was able to find on Google maps had all closed up. But go figure there were two Starbuck's locations within close proximity so I picked one and there I headed.  This location was impressive. Not the physical building, but the staff and the people. It felt like a local owned coffee shop. After mentioning that I just moved here from Los Angeles, I was told that my first cup of coffee was on the house! We proceeded to chat about a few local places that I must visit. I met a local business owner, the local self-proclaimed "Crazy Dave" and a few other great people - and I felt very much at home.  The coffee was, well Starbucks. Other than the fact that they do not have any of the breakfast sandwiches, this was everything you'd expect from a Starbucks, and more.

This review is not for the quality of the product but for the customer service, or in their case- lack thereof. This Starbucks location continually has crappy customer service, and from the way it looks- it's all coming from one cranky bitch.   I went in one morning to get a breakfast sandwich (Ham & Cheese Artisan sandwich with no egg)...and noticed that another type of sandwich had bacon on it. Mm, bacon...right? So I asked if bacon could be put onto my sandwich. The answer: No. Let me be clear, it wasn't the "no" that was the problem, per se. It was the snarky attitude of the woman behind the counter. It wasn't 'no sorry' or 'you'd have to purchase both sandwiches'..it was "No" with the implication and attitude that the answer is no because she'd rather go stick her chest out and flirt with that married man over there than dick around with something for me.   I prodded as to WHY it couldn't be done, and got the *sigh* and even an *eyeroll* from the woman behind the counter. How STUPID of me for not knowing that the sandwiches comes pre-packaged, right? *slaps forehead* I totally forgot I worked for Starbucks last week! I pointed out that if egg could be taken OFF of a sandwich, then surely something could be put ON a sandwich. I also pointed out that I had no problem paying for the other sandwich so that the ingredients could be combined into one. Apparently a ham & bacon merger was imposssible for her. When, let's be clear - every other Starbucks is capable of doing what she is telling me she cannot do.   I am the customer. I did not ask you for anything for free. If you have bacon in the building and I ask for bacon on something- then do your frickin' job and give me the damn bacon! If I ask for a Bacon Frappacino I expect you to start blending. I have no clue what this woman's issue was...maybe she needs to get laid because her bitchery was off the charts. So I grabbed my drink and told the manager at the time (who is now retired) that I would not be back.  And I meant it. Until I went back. UGH.   I got in a situation where it was either stop there for something, or starve til morning. Blah Blah blah I go in at 6:20pm and order my sandwich. Miss Stick-Up-Her-Butt was there and couldn't make my food because she'd turned the oven off. Um, WHY? Why did you turn off the oven 40 minutes before you're supposed to close? Simple. She wanted to leave and instead of serving customers she just turns off everything and turns people away. Lame.  .  .
Every time I have tried to come get coffee they are closed. Closed on Sunday and only open until 3pm?  I get closing inside but why close drive thru??

It's very convenient to know that I could get my favorite coffee drink in the middle of the mall while shopping. The barista here is always ready to listen to your order and every time I've been here they get my order correct. They also make your order fast so the wait is not long at all. Who doesn't love Starbucks!?
Wait times a little long but the employees that work there are super chill and friendly. Thumbs up for them!

They run out of small stuff often (lavender, honey, dehydrated fruit, etc) but the drive thru staff are always helpful and nice. I got my drink today because someone recommended it and it's goood. Line is typically long but it moves at a decent pace.

Tracy is the sweetest lady there. I go there most mornings before work, and we always chat and talk. My breakfast is always delicious and made quickly. Even if it wasnt good, I would still go here just for the people who work here. The customer service is beyond compare.

This place forgets so much... I don't know if they are hungover students or just don't care... one day I can get my drink and have to wait for food and another the opposite. There isn't any consistency in this Starbucks. Jake is one of the rudest baristas I have ever encountered. I asked to see if my food was ready as I was waiting because I'm en route to work and don't have time for inefficiencies, which is why you pay a premium for world class service. Unfortunately, this store misses the mark and that the only consistent thing they do. If Jake could have he would have thrown my bagel at me as I was in the lobby waiting with my drink and he said here is your bagel and tossed it on the counter. Definitely not the level of service I'm used to. I will choose circle K coffee before returning to this store as Jake does not represent your brand well and this store is not operationally sound.
The angry older woman has a rotten attitude . She behaves as if she s doing you the customer a favor . Not spending my money on Starbucks again - much less at this place
Cappucino made with heavy whipping cream = keto greatness  Friendly service, clean location.
Yup.  Its a Starbucks alright.   Good cofee, always way too crowded with hipsters abusing the free wi-fi, and this one is in a book store.

Everytime I come I wait in line for about 30-45 minutes and 9 out of 10 Times they tell me they are out of the food item I choose. It's frustrating to wake in line for so long and then be told the one thing I actually really want is out.

I'm dropping my rating because lately it's becoming impossible to get my coffee without a half of a carton of soymilk in it.  I have tried to explain on several occasions that I just want a super small amount in my coffee and more times than not I get a half a carton dumped in there. Everything about this location is fine. I mean it's clean, the staff is friendly and they move people quickly too.

Friend just moved to this neighborhood and he loves to go out for coffee.  We found this place and it was crowded.. Every table taken. We ordered and then stood for a few moments to see if a table would be available but no one moved. So we left.  Until we find another coffee shop in this neighborhood, I guess we will be getting a cup here now and then.
Annoying girl that answers the drive-through I need my coffee before I hear that kind of stuff. Needs to work on her comedy somewhere else
Another standard Starbucks. I think it's staffed with UIndy students, perhaps? Anyhow, nothing that I've experienced here is out of the ordinary. Just standard quality Starbucks service.

This Starbucks location was very crowded for a weekday early afternoon.  Primarily outdoor seating available however high tables and a small area with cozy arm chairs was open. I would not recommend this location for a business meeting or quiet place to work.

Typical Starbucks, but this one have outdoor seating under a roof. Also very convenient parking right in front of the store. You must get a coffee from the Clover. A ver unique way to enjoy your coffee.  Another great location is it is next to Belle Meade Premium Cigar. Get a coffee and a smoke and enjoy life, its short.

This Starbucks has become one of the worst! The wait times are ridiculous, even for mobile orders. Shortly after I made a formal complaint about their handing of mobile orders, they just flat out stopped offering mobile orders. I no longer get coffee here because instead of fixing a staffing issue, they just turned off mobile ordering. I only do mobile ordering and I'm sure I'm not the only one. Poor management in my opinion.

This is a Starbucks. I am a huge Starbucks fan, don't get me wrong. I absolutely love a venti bold brew, black, and their coffee is deep and rich and just like my father forced me to drink (none of that "fru fru stuff"). The thing is, being a frequent bucks-er, there's a few things you come to expect and this location misses the beat on one key aspect.  1) get me my coffee so I can get the f*ck out and on with my day.  I have NEVER not seen a snaking line at this place. It is, without a doubt, the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to and I grew up in the southwest. Good god people, you know your address is Philadelphia, right? We are city people who walk at a city's pace who want our coffee at a city's speed. If I wanted my cashier to nurture me and call me "baby" I would drive my way out to the 'burbs. If you're looking to grab a quick cup of coffee on your way to work, find somewhere else, because this place will undoubtedly ensure that you arrive late to work like a jackass, too embarrassed to bring your Starbucks cup in with you...  I will say, parking is great, location is nice and the interior bodes well to coffee enjoyment. But it frustrates the living hell out of me.

Staff here is always friendly and efficient. Great location- very convenient and although they don't have a drive-thru here, it is sometimes faster here than other Starbucks in the area with a drive-thru. Pet-friendly outside and this store is always clean and well-stocked. Enjoy coming here.

Decent coffee chain, though it lacks the charm and quality of a small town cafe. The Frappuccinos are icy drinks perfect for hot southern summers, however almost everything here is very sweet. Often, I get items 1/2 sweet with only 2 pumps of syrup, including their iced teas.The oatmeal and breakfast sandwiches are their best food options. The service is usually quick, and it's a clean place to use WiFi and have a cup of coffee.

Thank you Jill for arguing with me about a caramel macchiato upside down vs caramel macchiato v caramel late. Your professionalism is astonishing.  Instead of arguing with a customer, why don't you actually attempt to complete my order and LOOK UP THE RECIPE

This is the only Starbucks I've been to since moving to Reno. The service is always wonderful with staff that goes above and beyond. The establishment is clean and distancing is enforced. The coffee is always made as ordered and delicious. They even have outdoor seating. Why bothering finding another spot if this one is always great? :)

It's a Starbucks- No better or worse than any other, really. I liked that the staff had nametags identifying where they were from! Dominican Republic, Korea, Laos etc. And they were very friendly. The baked goods here are fresh, and hey, sometimes when you're in the hotel for a conference and you have 5 minutes between panels this is the only breakfast you can get! If you don't want Sbucks, the hotel has coffee in the lounge area, but it's even pricier (as in $4 for a cup).  I do think this location was more expensive than any other I've been in. Maybe it was my imagination.

This Starbucks Location doesn't seem to be run very well; the employees were more concerned with taking pictures of each other and talking about customers who had placed orders on the phone than making drinks. Just got an iced coffee with nothing in it and was standing there in the crowded waiting area for like 10 min. It's worth it to walk to any of the nearby coffee shops

The new girl you hired does not know how to make coffee ! I've been coming here for years with my co workers because it's down the street from our work and have always had Stellar service everyone knows your name consistently good coffee but they got a new girl making the coffee and myself and my coworkers have all noticed a massive difference. Even going as far as to dump our cups out two meetings in a row ! We've noticed the following problems from tasting like someone dumped a half a cup of sweet n low to being almost all milk no coffee . Today I sent my co worker to get me a venti coffee with a little extra ice and it was so full of ice I  was done drinking it after 6 sips I felt ripped off plus the sickening sweet n low taste again !! We've all thrown our coffee out or barely sucked it down felling like I've wasted a lot of money lately . Please retrain this new girl so we can enjoy our favorite coffee place again !
It's a Starbucks, so you know the offerings. The staff here are great, which sets them apart from other locations. Super friendly and helpful.

This store is too narrow and is poorly laid out, without any consideration given to what customers experience.  Specifically, when I visited during Winter, it got extremely cold in the front of the store because whenever anyone opened the door, gusts of cold air rushed in over the seated customers. The management made no attempt whatsoever to shield patrons from the cold wind.  Only the customers in the back were shielded from the cold, but back there you have to listen to the inane barista chatter.  I once witnessed an ugly scene at this store. One of the female employees sitting out front noticeably began crying. I was inside and I overheard a male employee backstabbing her, assaulting her character.
Racism is alive and well at Starbucks! Boycott Starbucks!
Staff is great, very friendly. Service is quick. The only negative is the size of the location. Otherwise it's my Starbucks of choice.

I'll preface this review with the fact that I'm a tad biased when it comes to this spot. I've been coming here since it opened, almost 7 years ago, and haven't found a reason to frequent another location, however, that could change.   The location of this spot is PRIME as it's on my way to work, baseball games, and countless other functions. Since the doors have opened, the staff took a while to get settled, but once they did, things were seamless. Recently this store has lost 3 employees in the past 3 months to other chapters in their lives - Tyler, Erica, and Nicole. All three were integral at helping maintain the balance of how things ran.   There have been other key ingredients here as well - Tia, Jason, Bethany, Tamara, Yumi, Eunice, Adam, and a couple others who share the duties, but in the end, the three mentioned at first, are the focus of why this store might take a hit.   VERY long opinion short, this store's Barista's have ALWAYS maintained a sense of hard work, efficiency, and the will to reduce long lines and turn out quality drinks. I just hope that the absence of Tyler, Erica, and Nicole don't affect things long term.   I wasn't lying when I said I am biased.

I really like this Starbucks. It feels new and is very clean for an urban downtown location. Friendly staff fast service and clean restrooms are part of the 5 stars. Open til midnight! Lots of seating and at night you can sit outside with a latte and watch the club scene go by. One of my favorite Starbucks so far in the US. If the women's bathroom could have a hook that works going solo would be easier.
