By far the worst location I've done business with.. The issues add up, the customer service is lacking. I mobile order every day and can wait up to 10 minutes for my drink.. My home is 5 minutes away! Today was my final straw with this location! I did my usual mobile order and get there to pick it up and see what looks similar to a bag in my cup.. as I look at it further, I found the cup was smashed in on the bottom, so as opposed to switching my coffee into an appropriate cup, they chose to use the smashed one and an additional cup on the outside. It's simple things that these lazy people choose to look past. I pay $5.85 every morning for the same exact coffee. There are days it's perfect, too strong, or extra sweet. I've addressed the situation and was charged for an extra shot to correct my "too sweet" coffee. My ingredients don't change. So the fact that there's inconsistencies, says the training is not up to par!
The best Starbucks!! The staff is always so warm and friendly!! Line gets long but it's a busy area so what can you do.
Awesome service Christina is amazing Remembered my drink from last time I was in today, in fact remembered it so much that she reminded me I left something out! Loooovvvveeee great service like that!
WHOA - stopped by on the way through. This place has a deck that looks over the water. Very cool!

This is by far the worst Starbucks ever. I normally bypass this location (despite it being the closest) due to their terrible service. Today was no different. I ordered a grande Mocha Latte. It was ready when I got there. Fabulous. I (stupidly) got in my car and drove off. About a mile up the road I pick up my cup and feel like it's a little light. Open the lid...its 3/4 FULL. Look at the photo. I didn't take a SINGLE SIP. Like this is what I'm talking about with this location. Either one of three things is going to happen. 1. You'll wait forever. 2. Your drink will be made wrong (in taste or just a completely different order than what you asked for) 3. You'll pay for a grande and get a tall.

Poor service.  Went through drive through.  Allergic to dairy  and soy. Starbucks menu boasts of coconut so attempted to orde a drink with coconut milk. Girl on speaker acted like we were talking a cryptic when we asked for coconut milk.  Then when asking for sparkling berry drink was immediately told they were out of apple juice.  Wow... horrible prices and horrible service. I won't be coming back here at all!!

It is a Starbucks, what more can I say. It has the same menu items and quality service as all others Starbucks. What makes this one good is the size - it is about twice as large as a regular Starbucks. It has seating at community table, sofa and other 4 top tables.  If you work downtown or are meeting someone downtown, this is a great place for a CoLab meeting.  Plenty if space to hang out, including extra seating inside the adjacent Hilton Hotel.

Once in a while my husband and I would stop in this Starbucks and enjoy a beverage while relaxing in one of the comfy chairs in the large back area. Recently though, we couldn't tolerate the extremely loud thumping music so we had to leave. The next time we wanted to get a drink and relax for a little while there we had to leave because there were so many flies buzzing around. This Starbucks has terrible acoustics so if they choose to play music they should keep that in mind and keep the volume down and try to realize that all of their customers are not fans of thumping bass.
Usually pretty good. Today they forgot about my mobile order!  A cleaner Starbucks.   Busy drive thru.   Busy little place. A Starbucks.

I can always rely on them to get me my caffeine fix. The Baristas are always courteous,friendly, and go out of they're way for the customer on a regular basis. Even when it gets crowded, the baristas keep the line moving. The muffins are also made fresh everyday.
Very dirty outside and in. Very slow service also sold out of Tea. This location has been closed 2 times for employees with Covid. By far the worst starbuck I have ever been to.
I love this location, the chairs are so comfy and the baristas are funny.
Come here almost everyday on the way to work! I love the quick service and fresh coffee/food. The very best part of this trip is they call me by your name everytime you walk in the door!

First, it's a Starbucks.  They are pretty consistent in drinks and flavors across the board.    The staff at this location is why I return.  When my mother died, I needed a sense of normalcy.  Something to help me get back into a routine.    I went to this location every weekday for a year.  The majority of the morning staff know my name. I walk in and hear from at least two "good morning Nessa!" I never have more than a how are you? How's your morning conversation with them, but the simple effort of remembering my name and greeting me helped me keep going everyday.    I've since moved and still try to hit this location a couple times a week.  They may just be doing their job, but when you're in a bad headspace, that kindness makes a huge difference.
I love the service I get from this Starbucks I like the management ,& workers are excellent especially the morning staff could not be happier
Nice Starbucks. Locations within stores and airports are always noisier. Service was good. Friendly barista.  My chai was perfect. Plentiful table space to fit snd relax before or after shopping.

This is my favorite Starbucks... Starbucks is by far one of the best coffee places in town and this outlet has it's uniqueness for me...it's a place to go if you want to be treated as a friend, not just a customer. The Baristas are amazingly awesome and work hard to make everyone feel at home. The caramel frappe chino which was refreshing and above average, java chocolate chip which is tasty...the tomato basil mozzarella sandwich which is hearty, healthy and yum, chocolate with raspberry filling pastry which was exceptional.  Okay before everyone goes crazy and talk about the price...make it a treat to yourself you don't have to have Starbucks every day me I do it twice a week most of the time I would use my Keurig at home to save money because let's do the math if you brought Starbucks Coffee 5 days a week for 52 weeks that's  $1,300 a year   Overall It's expensive but worth spending every penny for the betterment of your taste buds (Don't judge LOL )  and outstanding customer service.

Gorgeous new location! It's very trendy and looks unlike all other locations in Nashville. The store is still very new so they may have a few kinks to work out, so I can overlook a few mishaps (extra wait time and not adding almond milk to my coffee...definitely not the end of the world!). They have a cooler for a greater variety of cold to-go food options. It's an odd layout because the front of the store is the drive-thru ordering station so the windows are on the back of the building. Give it a try if you're in the area!

Okay so let me preface this with a positive. Mino is an amazing baristo and kind human and he is the reason I come back to this location! Something about his demeanor and personality brightens my day! Besides that, this location is constantly disappointing me! There's been two instances where I've gotten such poor treatment, granted, it's while they're super busy, but I too am in a service job where I get slammed and am super compassionate and professional with my guests.  Today, I ordered ahead on the app. When I came to pick up, the woman that handed me drink was super dismissive and mean. I told her I was waiting for another drink as well and she said "I know" and walked away. While waiting, I realized my drink was made wrong and I asked if she could remake it, but the lid wasn't on all the way and the drink fell. She was furious and looked at me like I was an imbecile and I got really embarrassed and just walked out, my sister was in the car and knows I have really bad social anxiety and the drinks were already paid for. So she went in and got the drinks for me. This barista in particular really went out her way to make me uncomfortable and it really ruined my mood and stressed me out.   Probably will visit during Minos shifts and on a slower day but really sad about the service and people they chose to have on their team. I wish a manager would talk to the employees about how they treat guests.

If this review was based on 1 sole employee at this location, they'd get 0 stars but I like a few of them here so I'm giving 2 stars. There is one lady at this location that may be the rudest person I have ever met. I ordered a coffee, she made it wrong and blamed me for not ordering correctly and then proceeds to tell me how to order it. My jaw dropped bc she spoke to me in such a demeaning manor. I've order this coffee the same way for a year and a half. I have complained many times about her and nothing has been done. Pretty sure she is a manager so it's rare I go to this location anymore. Oh and she charges extra for NO ICE!!!

Close friendly and convenient! They have a drive through and the staff is SUPER friendly and make the visit delightful each time. Coffee is consistently great and they have BREAKFAST!!!  Just wish it was a bit bigger due to the traffic when it gets busy, but it's a small complaint.

Nothing bland or regular about this Starbucks.  Starbucks drinks and treats are Starbucks, but the people that work there make it a pleasure to start your day or stop by to refuel.   A great place to have a quick meeting for work or check in with friends.

I'm not really a Starbucks person, but this is a great location.  There is lots of seating, the staff is so nice and knows you, and they make it seem like it's not a mega chain.  As with all Starbucks, the drinks are great, and it is a little $.  It was /the/ after-school hangout in junior high - how lame - but brings back good memories.

This Starbucks is close to my house so it's convenient but the time it takes to get through the drive thru is ridiculous. It's usually pretty slow, so I go with patience but today, 8:30 am on a Sunday, 6 cars in line and it's taken me 30 minutes to get through the drive thru! Crazy!  However, the baristas are friendly and the drinks are perfect. This would be the perfect Starbucks if speed was improved. Honestly, I should have used pre order but I didn't realize I would be in the line this long.

I would stop by this location more often but the parking lot design makes it frustrating. The drive thru line blocks the parking lot. The entrances are poorly designed where cars block each other in both directions. If the flow was improved they would probably ove a lot more cars through.   My other issue with this location is the consistancy of the drinks. I usually get a vanilla latte and I often wonder if I got the wrong drink.

Starbucks is Starbucks. The coffee is always good. I only wished they would keep the half and half out in the afternoon. I hate to have to keep asking for it. If they're so worried about it growing warm, put it in ice! Jeeeez.
Great and convenient Starbucks, always fast and good coffee. On this most recent visit, I was lucky enough to be staying in the Marriott, so it was REALLY convenient!

It's a Starbucks - the drinks are standard, the barristas are friendly.  The line is often crazy long.  But there are usually seats to be found (unless it's a weekend morning, then I'd go to the other Starbucks on Hwy 100).  Plenty of outlets for plugging in laptops and other devices.  There's always parking somewhere in the lot, but you might not be anywhere near Starbucks.  The outside tables are nice, but you're basically sitting in the Kroger parking lot, which, again, is less than ideal.
I grab my work coffee everyday here. The staff is super friendly and personable! If you're looking for a quick pick me up, this is a great spot!
Great way to start the day! Smiling baristas and delicious coffee!! Such a nice place to meet up with friends and enjoy a good cup of coffee!

As far as Starbucks go, this is one of the locations I prefer. I tend to go through the drive thru at least a couple times a week and despite the high volume of business, the service is fast and very friendly. Not to mention- I don't think my order has ever been messed up here which is kind of amazing. They recently just got the nitro cold brew here too... which makes it even better. Overall, love driving through here on my way to work or school since it's so convenient.
The Chile Mocha addiction is real my friends. I can't shake it. Glad this is on I-44 on the way out of town.

Ted and Patrick are the best baristas! I am so pleased with my experience with them! Went in to get an extreme caffeine fix to study for a nursing exam. I asked for their expertise on the most caffeinated beverage and not only did they have a great suggestion but it was incredibly good! They were excellent and very personable! Thank you soooo much for helping me get through my exam!   Also thanks for making my new Starbucks go to drink (: soooo yummy!
I frequent this location weekly. Extremely friendly staff, consistent drinks, and quick service.

Nice Starbucks, good atmosphere and not as cramped up as a lot of Starbucks I've been to. The deal breaker here was the restrooms. They were FILTHY! Dust build up on everything in there was a dead giveaway that they are not cleaned regularly, thick brown urine buildup on back of toilet.  Nasty sink. The best way to judge how well employees keep a restaurant is by the bathroom. If they can't wipe down a toilet, how well do you think they are going to clean those machines they have to fully breakdown? Those bathrooms haven't had a thorough scrubbing in at LEAST a week. Buildup as thick as what was under the toilet seat takes a while to accumulate.

Definitely an improvement from the previous Starbucks stand in the Galleria, though it can get slow and packed with customers. I do appreciate that they offer ice water for patrons as you wait though - a plus for hot St. Louis summers!

they were out of just about everything and that is NOT okay. i couldn't get anything because all their options were awful. i would not recommend. service was awful and cookies were not fresh. this is a disgrace.   update: i ordered a drink and it took 20 minutes to come. it wasn't even the right drink. starbucks has to get it together.

STILL the slowest Starbucks on the planet.  They got my drink order wrong and then told me it was because of a new person (which is what they told me last time too)  maybe hire a few more advanced baristas?

Love coming to this location. It's very convenient. Coffee is usually consistent, once last week it was very bitter but it's usually very good. I just wish they wouldn't forget my blueberries for my oatmeal. This morning was the 3rd time in a month.  I know I should have checked...which is my bad. I really wanted my blueberries this morning :-(

Ladies and gentlemen, I implore you to find a better Starbucks than the Flourtown PA Starbucks.  YOU CAN'T DO IT, NOPE!  I've been coming here at least 3 times a week for the past year to do work and I have to say that the workers here are exceptional. What can I say, they run a tight ship.  Howard is in here every day making coffee and keeping everything clean like a barbarian! His work ethic shows and he seems to know everyone's name.   Jon is also killing it. He's dedicated to his craft and is a helluva guy.    All the other workers are true warriors as well but I forget their names.  Come here for coffee.
Always friendly, fast service and never a problem with my order - even though there's regularly a line for the drive-through. It's the one Starbucks location I know a line shouldn't mean a long wait!

I've given this place so many chances, but the times I leave unsatisfied outweigh the times I leave happy. Every time I get my drink it seems to be made incorrectly or stingy on the amount. I went a few days ago and ordered a grande Starbucks double shot on ice. Didn't pay attention when I left. Turns it out was less than half way full. The majority was foam. I realize this is the case with those drinks but it's hard when you pay for a grande and I got less than a tall. Thought I would try this place one more time this morning. Ordered a venti double shot on ice today. Same thing, too much foam and made incorrectly. The drink usually comes with classic syrup to sweeten it up. No classic syrup and barely any skim milk. I love the staff,very friendly, but drinks are awful quality. We all know Starbucks is pricey and when you pay that much for a drink and it's made wrong or has half the amount in it, it's frustrating. I did have a few sips of the drink below, but it did fit in to my grande Starbucks mug. First bad review I've given.
Stopped in for a Quick Tea. Love the Green Iced tea. Good location and fast service.
I went back today and it's much better. The staff is nicer and very welcoming. I'm glad they made changes because I will keep coming to this one. :)
Slowest Starbucks in the universe!  Don't know why I'm surprised each time I go there and it takes 20min to get my online order that said would be ready in 5-7 minutes.
Great friendly staff! Located st the corner or inside the Marriott also across the street from the courtyard Marriott
Orders are consistently wrong, extremely slow service. Stay away  How this place is still in business is beyond me.

I just went through the drive through after ordering a trenta iced coffee. The barista handed me a venti and I said I asked for a trenta not a venti (and was charged for it) . She told me it was a trenta, again I said I don't think it is. She took it examined it and said yes it is. Clearly it isn't a trenta when you see it next to the correct cup (I posted a comparison pic). I didn't have time to go in and argue about receiving the correct drink that I paid for but damn what terrible customer service. I drink multiple Starbucks a day and will definitely not come back to this location.
Typical Starbucks. No complaints here. My drink was good (I always order the the chai tea latte) and the speed of the service through the drive through was about average. Overall a decent stop.

Perhaps im spoiled coming from Seattle where Starbucks are almost always excellent, but this place was consistently awful.After about 2 months of orders being botched, and blank stares when it was pointed out to the staff, we found a good Starbucks on Broadway. My favorite here was being told "we ran out of bagels.thats why we didnt bring it out to you". Oh i see.I was just expected to pay for it:.No apology.  AVOID THIS PLACE .
Regular Starbucks coffee at the airport. Entrance of terminal B. They actually have quite a few choices and get really busy.

Thank you Starbucks for making my low carb Venti iced coffee PERFECTLY! I've been to a ton of Starbucks in the city of Chicago and not one has mastered my order. Shame I have to drive 5 hours for a good low carb coffee. This made the end of my weekend in Edwardsville. Thanks baristas at this location!

I always forget this starbucks is there.  There is always seating when you go at the right time of the day.  I get my hair done at circa 79 so it's a great location to grab something sweet before getting beautified!

I pass this Starbucks everyday on my way to drop my daughter at school, and while it's super convenient for me, I generally do not stop there due to the time it takes. The staff has always been friendly, but it's such a busy place sometimes it would take 15-20 mins to get through the drive thru line. Well today, I desperately needed a coffee after the long weekend and thought I'd give it another try. And boy I am glad I did! They had a team member out at the front of the line taking orders, who was so pleasant and professional! She asked for my order and my name, told me the total and asked me to pull around. IT WAS THE QUICKEST VISIT EVER! I pulled to the window and my order was ready! It was such a nice way to start my morning, and I'm so happy that I can incorporate this store into my daily routine more often now that they have an expedited ordering system! Great job and thank you!

This is one of the best SBUX around, and it absolutely comes down to the management.  I spent several months, regularly going to the one over by the Mont Mall, but the place was consistently dirty and the staff was pretty ridiculous (read - immature, loud, and...just didn't allow for a great work environment).  I am not going to give my full opinion of that store (I know they have a new building - sadly, the people will be the same), but I have to mention that one in comparison because it is so close to this one.  As I sit here, working, the store manager is sitting at a nearby table LEADING a couple of new employees.  She knows how to do it.  She's professional and her employees exude that. She takes the time to really teach her employees how to be great at their jobs, and I absolutely think the success of this particular store is because she's awesome.  The store is clean. The bathrooms are usually clean - even smell decent.  The trash is rarely overflowing.    I took one star off simply because I'm amazed that none of them seem to recognize me (let alone remember my name) even though I am in there most days, making at least two separate purchases each time.  That just seems odd to me.  But, hey, I guess I don't make much of an impression. It's also not the fastest service, but, again...faster than the other SBUX down the road.

Other than traffic on Overland making getting out of the parking lot being a bit dicey at times, I ADORE this Starbucks location.  It is my reward spot after surviving shopping at the HELL that is WalMart, which is across the street.  They are quick to take your order and on par with wait times at other Starbucks locations in Boise.  I've had some great short chats with the folks working the drive up window.

I've been to this location, inside the airport, many times while on trips to Philly. I love Starbucks because the experience and choices are always consistent. -This is my go-to when I'm trying to be as healthy as I can, essentially "on the go" while traveling. I can get an oatmeal, some fruit and a black coffee and I'm as happy as a clam.

This is a problem I do like Starbucks and if I want to get something quick I can always go there. But this is like the fourth or fifth time already going to the Starbucks in Franklin and they never have what I want they always tell me when I'm ready to pay after waiting five minutes or more then that. I'm sorry we are out of this or that. I mean come on when you were taking my order you should say hey sorry we don't have this do you want to get something else. I mean it's easy to say yeah is this much and I know Starbuck it's not cheap. That's why I'm paying for what I want before you  finish get my order make sure that what I'm ordering is on there don't make me wait and then tell me you don't have any of it.
Love going to this Starbucks. Always greeted by Bradley's great personality.  They always make my drink exactly how I like it. Drive thru however slow at times.

I came here on my birthday and explained to them that my free drink didn't pop up on the app. They didn't bother to help besides scan my rewards. They still charged me for my drink and I still ended up paying $17 for the stuff I got. They also ran out of the sausage and cheddar sandwich like what is this? Popeyes? Thanks starbucks for helping me on my birthday! NOT!

Superbly sensible, impeccably ordinary Center City Starbucks. They're appropriate opposite a Target.   Typical pleasantries. Free Wi-Fi, they assume praise cards, and so on. Upstairs seating. Loads of normal light amid the day.   Inviting administration!   In case you're staying, request a mug. Furthermore, make certain to transport your very own mugs to the counter when you're set; there's no table administration, normally.
Great experience yesterday at the Citrus Park mall Starbucks. Quick line at the register and my s'mores frappucino was correct the first time. Used the app to pay.

Product  was as good as always (and over priced ) but the design and detail of the interior is amazing!  Gorgeous walnut wood. trim and baseboards, colors are very complementary and a very cool brass musical instrument  hanging sculpture in the adjacent sitting area captures  the true essence  of New Orleans.

After 25 minutes of waiting in line, the 3 staff members forgot to make our drinks. The head barista, Arumi was very rude while talking to her cousins that had just walked in. Instead of making our drinks, after I reminded her of our order, Arumi proceeded to introduce her family to the rest of the barista staff. There were 12 people in line to order and that did not affect any pace of the Starbucks 5 points staff. Several people waiting in line began to comment on the poor service. I will not come back to this location ever again. Horrible customer service.

I am a big fan of Starbucks, always have been and always will be.  I love their coffee.  This location is friendly and the folks who work at this branch are helpful and cheerful.  I probably stop here at least once a day. My big problem here is the parking.  This is a VERY popular Starbucks, but the lot is teeny-tiny and people are parked all over.  I always expect to see an accident here.  There is no street parking near-by and the almost empty lot next door has a guard to shoo off Starbuck's customers.  They really need to figure out something to do with this crazy parking situation. I also love that they stay open late in the evenings.  I am a fan - but the parking drives me crazy!

The Good: Every worker I encounter at this location is friendly. Stopping at this location multiple days a week, my order is 99% correct and in the 1% chance that it's not, it is fixed with a smile and apology. The location is clean.   The Not-so-good: The app often lists that they are out of items when they actually aren't so always be sure to physically go in or go through the drive-thru to order if you encounter this issue at the Blue Bell location.

Best Starbucks in Reno!! All the baristas are wonderful and recognize me when I pull up to the drive thru or go inside. They know my usual and I have NEVER been disappointed. I rarely go to any other Starbucks and I pass Human Bean to come here. Would give 10 stars if I could. Love love love this Starbucks!!!!

Everyone was so rude here. I work at a Starbucks and if I acted the way they did I would have been fired a long time ago! It was only 9:30 am and no one was in there...it was like we were bothering them by being there.

What's Joe Pesci's line from Lethal Weapon, "you get ****ed at the drive-thru?!" Needed a lift to make it home on my daily commute between St Pete and Orlando, so I stopped at the exit 19 Starbucks and ordered a chai tea latte.  Two exits west on I4 I went for my first sip and it was coffee!! I hate coffee! Joe Pesci was right, I wasn't turning around, so they got me.

Love Starbucks coffee and their service. Even more so as this one is in our back yard just about! Their staff is very friendly and are willing to help out if you are not sure what you want at that time.       My only issue with this one is that they are a bit slow at getting your coffee and getting you on their merry way. If they stepped it up this would be an easy 4-5 star place. Just be cautious if you're on a short time limit!

It's worth mentioning that the employees at this Starbucks location are all fantastic. Management has really established a great team of individuals and they all function like a well oiled-machine, getting drinks out quickly while still making customers feel welcome. The drive-thru is often way too long (the only negative)... so I'd recommend going inside and ordering. You'll get your drink faster and the folks inside are great.

The crew working Memorial Day was fantastic! Very cordial and efficient. In particular, Jack, who took our order, suggested to my fiancé to get a pump of classic syrup, as opposed to granular sugar in his iced Americano. This has solved an issue in our relationship, for this had been making him grumpy and sad. Thanks, Jack!!

There's nothing like quiet time at Starbucks. Good coffee, great music (at this location at least), and decent artwork. The baristas worked quickly and were friendly. I like that there is a good selection of seating, from couches to individual booth seating. FREE WI-FI is always a plus as well. My coffee was made correctly and tasted pretty good. I didn't even use sugar like I usually do.

This is my favorite Starbucks in Center City.  Service is fast and friendly.  I don't go to Starbucks more than twice a month, and the cashier in the mornings still remembers my name and my order.  I'm always very impressed with her recollection.    Occasionally, they run out of spinach and feta breakfast wraps, so get there early to make sure you get the food you want.

I've been to this location about a dozen times. My husband and I enjoy going in on a rainy day and reading for a bit. The inside it pretty big and they have outdoor seating but it's usually pretty crowded. Unfortunately the last 2/3 visits have been negative. We stopped on a Sunday morning before church and went in because the drive-thru line was super long. I ordered the same thing for both myself and my husband. It took 15 minutes for our drinks to come out. Not kidding. I requested the drinks have sugar-free syrup because of dietary restrictions. We rushed out once the drinks were made and didn't try them until we got to our car and were out of the parking lot. One of the drinks was SF and the other wasn't. We had to throw the one with regular syrup away and couldn't go back because our drinks took so long. The woman who made them was clearly flustered because they were super busy but she made them back-to-back. That mistake shouldn't have been made.  Fast forward to this morning at 6:50 am. No one was in the drive-thru. I ordered my drink (the same one that was messed up previously) and gave the woman my money. A man made my drink and set it on the counter and then went to the back, I think. The woman also disappeared. Literally. FOUR MINUTES PASSED and my drink was sitting there. She came back to take someone else's order in the drive-thru and then proceeded to get a cup and start making their drink. I kindly said "excuse me, I have somewhere I need to be. Can you please give me my drink?" She didn't say anything and just handed it to be. I was in shock. I'm not kidding when I say it was four whole minutes. If I could've reached in the window and grabbed the drink myself I would have.  I tell this long story because both of these poor visits involved the same woman. A little bit older with short blonde hair. She can be very nice but tends to be a little out of it at times. I hate to call someone specific out but in both accounts she was the one to blame. I still plan on visiting this location but hope that this particular employee gets the hang of her job.

When we take the motorcycle out for an early Sunday morning tour of the Tucson Mountains, we love to end the trip here for breakfast so we can sit out by the bike and enjoy.  The staff, as at all Starbuck's, are friendly and the food is quick and good.  Thumbs up!

This Starbucks is so slow the woman behind me left. She travels out of BNA regularly and she still didn't have enough time to place her order and get a drink. They only have one person at the register and one poor person making all of the drinks. I see another employee is in the drink area now but the line is still barely moving. I got my drink, a grande iced latte, but it tastes a little off.

This is a very convenient Starbucks location and a great store, though the quality of service is a hit-and-miss!! Depending on the baristas on duty and how busy the place is, you're likely to have either a 5-star or a 2-star experience. I'm not particularly happy because they've messed up something on my last four visits there... forgetting the extra shot of espresso, giving me a small drink instead of a medium, giving my drink to someone else, ...maybe they'll finally get it right on the fifth try! :-(

**UPDATE- There has been a dramatic improvement the last two visits! Good job! Lately this Starbucks has been really disappointing. I have often come in and watched my mobile order get pushed to the back of the line for someone that ordered in the drive thru or in the cafe. This is not the way it's supposed to go. I used to work at Starbucks so I am very well aware of the procedures. I have also watched baristas get out of sequence on their drink making. I think some training needs to be done to get this very busy store up to the standard. It's frustrating to have to wait an extra ten minutes or more for a drink that you ordered on your way in the store. There is a reason for mobile ordering. The people here are great and I love the atmosphere but please work on your standards.

I just left this place and the two baristas were so rude, I felt really embarrassed. Here is what happened, I'm on a road trip and I always order through the app then go pick up my coffee. Most of Starbucks have a section where they leave your coffee and you grab and go. Today I did the same but it turns out that's not how they do it but I didn't know. I grabbed a coffee and the barista was so rude and I understand we are in the middle of a pandemic and they must be mindful but there are better ways to treat people especially when I wasn't trying to be malicious. I am a faithful Starbucks customer but I'll never be back to this one, they should just close the little window and call names. They probably have a sign and I should have paid more attention but there is no need to make your customers feel bad. I felt super embarrassed because this happened in front of other customers so 1 star for the two baristas this Saturday morning. PS: I'm just visiting St. Louis so I don't really know how things are being handled here, I even apologize to the baristas but then I was thinking that it is not the way to treat your customers.

Yeah, so I bet Seattle is thrilled to hear we all equate a Starbucks with their fine, rainy city.  With Xoconocho two doors down, and the Listening Room across the street, I'm not sure how this Starbucks gets any business.  Even Merridee's down the street offers free wi-fi.    Enough ranting.  The coffee here is identical to all the other Starbuck's coffee I have ever tried. If it were not for the urge of people to give me Starbucks gift cards and holidays and birthdays, I would not be here.
Asked for raw sugar and they told me that they were out. Hmmm to charge over $5 for a drink and the amount of traffic in here you think they can manage to order extra sugar.
Is a very small-busy coffee shop, but has a large seating-working area.  Very well located inside the mall.  Excellent service and always great selections!

There were two registers. When it was my turn, I walked to the second one and he was preoccupied with something else. So now, I was in the other line, with no cashier. The other cashier didn't bother to ask me anything as 3 people skipped over me to order. I would've spoken up if I wasn't sick and lost my voice. I work in customer service/food. Speak up and do better.
Got to stop by every morning......I try all the new blends......summer time iced coffees are great.  Have my own starbucks cup !!!
Quick friendly service inside, and never a long wait in the drive-thru.  Only 4 stars because of the lack of inside seating proportionate to the amount of space inside the store.

Ehh, this starbucks is ok. I came here mid afternoon on a Saturday and the place was packed. Waited in line for at least 5 minutes to order and then another 5 for my drink. Luckily, my drink was prepared well and correctly. The service was average, nothing to complain about but nothing spectacular. The barista making the drinks was disheveled in appearance and had liquid all over the front of his apron. Granted, I get that spills happen but his apron was gross, he should have changed to another one if he was going to continue making drinks. Overall, average experience. This place seems to have pretty good reviews so perhaps I caught it at a bad time.
One of the best Starbucks I've been to. If you do not understand what to drink is they will explain it to you. Very patient employees

Sometimes you need a little bit of generic basic bitch things in your life. This place is one of those places for me. My favorite Starbucks in the city. Everyone is friendly ( usuallyI only go in the mornings, the late staff looks miserable at times but I understand).

This location does quite a good job. I have gone inside as well as through the drive thru. The drive through can take a bit of time when it's busy, but if there is a line up and you're short on time, park and go inside.  Service is always fast inside, but parking can be an issue every now and again due to limited spots.  I usually get myself a soy vanilla latte, sometimes I get an iced one. They are usually great but every now and again I get one with burned espresso- meaning the shot has been out over 30 seconds before being put into the syrup or dumped into steamed milk/soy/almond etc. If you go back and tell them, they will of course make you another.  The interior is that of all Starbucks, modern, clean and has a good amount of seating.  The staff is always friendly and helpful, they keep their goodies well stocked and aren't usually out of too much.  This is a good location.  Enjoy.

Why would someone do a review on a Starbucks ?? They are everywhere ... Unless you are near west shore of Tampa. This is the only one around and it is tricky to find  This Starbucks is INSIDE OF THE WESTIN lobby. Pull into parking lot and around to the right side. It has the typical green Umbrellas outside to mark the treasure.  They do have their own parking.  This SB is one of those special locations that does not except your free drinks that you have earned. They do accept payment via the SB app though  They do have a nice assortment of sandwiches, snacks, merch etc.  My Venti iced upside caramel macchiato was made perfectly.  I bought some low-cal jalapeño popcorn that is insane goody good good.  Staff was very nice and the location is clean.

The next time I think it'll be fun to watch a scary movie, I'll think back to last night when I stayed awake until 5am, anxiously waiting to either be dragged out of my bed by evil paranormal forces, or fall into a possessed state and kill my dog, my neighbors, and ultimately myself. Finally, when fatigue overwhelmed fear, I slept, but woke up only an hour later to the sound of my dog's muffled dream-barks.   Thankfully, I knew holy humans with warm, reviving beverages were a mere 2 blocks away. I ventured out into the crisp morning air and despite the darkness at dawn, I thought it better to be mugged than remain captive to my racing imagination.   One cup of coffee later, I returned to my doomsday domicile, curled up on the couch and thumbed through my roommate's coffee table literature: a photography book aptly titled, "Women Before 10am." Better dreams next time. Avoid "Paranormal Activity;" it will haunt you!

Old favorite spot to get coffee. Debey has been rude to me on 3 occasions, making me never want to go back. Once she ignored me as I waited for her to never look back at me after handing me a half full cup of coffee in the size I ordered. Another time I asked for a drink to be split, never was a problem in the last 2 years for this, but she acted suddenly angry I asked and gave me a small hot cup and no lid for an iced drink. I waited for a lid  but she ignored me then also. I am tired of feeling this rude person's passive aggression. I wonder how many other have fallen victim to this. She should not work with the public

I'm not sure if this Starbucks goes rouge when following the standard of other Starbucks stores, but everything done here is extremely inconsistent. Every Starbucks I've ever visited will gladly give you ice waters when asked, however this store refuses to give them to you unless you purchase something. I even had asked to purchase a venti ice water since they would refuse to give me one for free, and I was refused to be sold a venti ice water (even though they clearly have venti cups- ice- and water) Saturday I bought a coffee and asked for a venti ice water, and was given one no problem. Today i purchased a coffee and asked for a venti ice water and was told they do not give out venti ice waters anymore, that's it's a new policy, which I find completely ridiculous. Being a mall employee, I contribute to the sales of this location almost daily along with my co workers, and I think after my experiences at this store, I'm going to have to stop supporting this company completely since this issue seems to be unresolved, and will continue to be unresolved.

This is not a bad coffee place for a national chain. Good location in a very active business part of East Carrollton. Parking is tight but it is no problem if you live in East Carrollton and take a pleasant walk for your coffee.   Staff was friendly on my first visit and very helpful to me. There is free Wi-Fi and that is always nice. Electrical outlets are plentiful for your electronics. No TV and that is good and the background music was enjoyable and easy to listen to. Another plus for me is segregated rest rooms according to gender. Not one for unisex restrooms anywhere.  Nutrition information on all their products is a welcome addition for all who are counting calories.  There are additional tables outside and with covering.   So I feel this is a good location for any Starbucks fan. And not a bad place to take a break with coffee, tea, or bakery items.

This is my least favorite location in Tucson and I've been to them all! The staff here is unprofessional and slow... Currently I've been standing here for FIFTEEN minutes waiting on a breakfast sandwich. That's not counting the ten I waited for my six drinks. They keep asking if my order is complete and I keep telling them I need my food! Some of the employees are hustling and a few of the staff are clearly trying to hold this place together.   I would strongly recommend going to the Grantand Swan location where everything runs smoothly, they are professional, and are able to get drink and food orders out in a timely manner.

Really love this Starbucks! The employees here are super nice and I have always had really quick service. I also love the fact that they have a drive thru (YAAAY laziness!!). All around a solid starbucks and a staple for my caffeine fix

The baristas are nice, but the service is very slow. My drink was fairly uncomplicated (unsweetened iced coffee with nonfat milk), but the person who took my order wrote it down wrong. Unfortunately, I didn't realize it until I was halfway home.

I would give this place a 0 if I could. The customer service here is horrible. The cashier, Krystal, rolled her eyes at me the entire time she took my order and was extremely rude. They were out of almost everything at 9am on a Sunday. Instead of the cashier communicating nicely other options to what I wanted she just snarked and rolled her eyes. I was very caught off guard by this because I rarely right bad reviews, but had to after this experience. Horrible service, long wait time, and they are unprepared.
Last time, a month ago, was terrible. Today I am here for lunch and I'm impressed by freshly brewed coffee&cream, bagels and cookies by the cashier, all fresh and service was fast!

I really don't know where to start.  The service is bad and slow.  The last time i was there they completely forgot my order and I noticed after about 10 customers go by..  I understand mistakes happen so I go again today.  This time I looked to see who ordered before me and I thought  i will get my coffee after her, surely.  Nope, again about 7-10 customers get their coffee that came in after me. I  speak to a staff member and ask where my coffee is and he said it's coming.  He said they  make the brewed coffee first??????????? (I was having an iced Americano)  , so i point to a guy walking out with an iced drink and said , "what about him?  He just walked in and got a drink.. THE GUY SAID,  "WELL HE'S A REGULAR CUSTOMER!  ?????  REALLY?????    THIS IS THE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER!   It always has been.. Finally, another server, grabs my cup to make it,  stops , puts a pasty in the oven and walks in the back room and doesn't come out.  I had to ask again for my drink.  There are so many excellent Starbucks, that i can't understand why this one doesn't care about customer service.  I'll never go again..  too many excellent Starbucks.  The one on Edmondson Pike, the one on Nolensville Road and the one on Murfreesboro Road. .. all consistent in giving excellent service.

I just waited 10 minutes for my mobile order to be completed. This is after placing my order 10 minutes prior to walking in the door. It took 20 minutes to make my drinks, from the time that I placed the order to the time that I walked out of the Starbucks.  Let me reiterate, it was a mobile order. When I arrived there was no one in line, the baristas were making two drinks. There were two baristas and a manager. The manager finally asked what I was waiting for and I told him that I was waiting for a mobile order. He took the mobile order from the printer and put the stickers on 2 cups and told the baristas that they needed to be made right away. The baristas were making drinks, the manager was wiping off the counter. The cups sat there and the manager continued to fiddle with other stuff behind the counter. Here's my question. Why wouldn't the manager just step in and make the mobile order. BTW the mobile order consisted of two iced teas. Not brain science. And the barista finally picked  up one of the ice tea cups, looked at the sticker and began to make the ice tea. Whatever he did, he did incorrectly, because he then threw that drink into the sink and began again. He finished that drink and then picked up the other cup. There was no immediacy in any of this. If this is the worst thing that happens to me today, I am happy, however the reason for placing a mobile order is to have the drinks ready when you arrive. If the Starbucks have been swamped with people, I would understand. If the manager had stepped in to help, I would be a little more forgiving. But all three of the employees seemed to be inept at accomplishing the goal of getting to teas onto the counter. And I am writing this review, because this is not the first time that this has happened. If any corporate Starbucks people are listening, it would be great if there were an alarm of some sort, a bell, a beep, buzz, that would allow the employees at the Starbucks to know that there is a mobile order waiting. Just a thought.

I have been to this Starbucks about five times. Each time I've been here it has been an extremely frustrating experience.  I tend to go through the drive-thru because I am a graduate student and I am always in a hurry to get to my next place. However, whenever I am in the drive-thru there is always an excessively long line and the drinks always take a long time to make. Obviously, the person who frequents the drive-thru tends to be in a hurry. But, instead of making my drink quickly and efficiently it has been made incorrectly about ninety percent of the time and takes about ten times longer than it should. The first time it happened, I thought it's just the drink, it's semi-complicated, I get it. The next time I chose something as basic as a Iced Caramel Macchiato. It looked like the correct item so I was thrilled, but it tasted awful and still took ten minutes to receive it.  I know how tough working in the service industry is, I work it in, but when I timidly asked them if they could remake my drink or brought attention to the fact it wasn't what I ordered they failed to take responsibility for the matter and made me feel horrible for bringing attention to it. Customer service should be high on the priority list for a well-known coffee place, but instead it was more than lacking.  I vowed this morning that I will no longer be frequenting to this location. Save your money and go to the Central West End location, they always have a good attitude and they are coffee connoisseurs.

Disappointed in this Starbucks. Ordered a sweet vanilla cold brew. After waiting a few minutes an employee came over and told me the vanilla cream wouldn't be ready and they could give me regular. I said ok, but I paid more for that specialty drink and only got a basic cold brew. No offer of reimbursement or to make a different drink.

I love it when I come to a Starbucks and everyone knows everyone! Makes the town seem so friendly and nice which it is but it just gives it that Homie environment.     Time to come here with a friend just to do coffee and talk and they're really sweet!    They messed up my order one but were really Kind in fixing it with no issues!!!

Im happy to find this Starbucks as an alternative to finding parking to go into a Clayton location. I went through the drive thru in the middle of the afternoon with only one car in front of me. It was a little slow but not terribly bad. I got a Venti iced matcha latte my go to afternoon drink. When I got it it wasn't filled to the top and had too much ice. This happens sometimes with the shaken teas but it's nice when the baristas are aware of it and make sure the drink is full for the $5+ you pay for it. Overall it wasn't bad I'd definitely use it instead of Clayton.

Pros: I always hit this place at low traffic. Never waited more than 2-3 minutes for my tried and true Iced Coffee with cream, no sugar. Of course that -is- probably the quickest prep item there. The wifi is quick, regularly see downloads of 100-200K/sec from major sites. I do spend time here looking at my Serato Whitelabel releases and have no complaints.   Cons: Store is anchored on east side of strip mall, which means the slim layout of the place + most of seating on the east can make for TOASTY morning visits during the summer even with the blinds down. The parking lot can feel be tricky at times, people drive way too fast through the narrow lanes so triple check before you back out of your spot when it is busy.  Baristas are all nice to me, so over time I've gotten to like this location even more. It's walking into a Cheers episode.

Centrally located in the meadows mall, Starbucks is an oasis in the crowded shopping mall. With all there favorites, espresso, cappacino, latte, chat tea, frapachinno and more there seems to be a small line all day. The coffee is OK, not great, but the padded seats and free Wi-Fi make for a good people watching area and to get work done.

Great location! The employees are very nice and they work fast. This location gets pretty busy, but I've never had any problems. As some of the other reviewers pointed out, parking can be a challenge due to its location in the shopping center, and yes, there are insects outside (people, we live in the desert!), but that has nothing to do with this Starbucks in and of itself.

I decided to not make my usual fast rush home after running the rat race all day and take a moment to remember why I work like a demon.    $6.00 craft IPA with an espresso shot on top along with $6.00 truffle mac and cheese is where I decided to throw my hard earned dollars at.  Keep in mind the evening menu is called small plates so do not expect a big heaping portion of food nor a trough of spirits but for the evening kick back it's just the right amount of everything.    The baristas usually serve the truffle mac n cheese on a real plate with real silverware (not the cheap plasticity things) so do not let my photo fool you.  I actually wanted to take mine to go and not eat it all in a sitting.    This was a hard feat because the mac is seriously good coming from a coffeehouse!!   I mean wow!  And I was thinking I would not get my mac b cheese fix because the place I wanted to go is closed on Tuesdays.   The mac n cheese has a nice lasting taste that keeps you craving another forkful that you have to wonder how did Starbucks pull this off and where can you get the recipe from.    It is cheesy and moist, not dried out and not lacking flavor with its complex flavor profile.  I admit for $6.00 its expensive but the taste had me hooked and throwing my dollars at it like a well deserving....um yeah probably should not say who or what lol!!  Somehow the Espresso Cloud IPA paired perfectly although they suggest a wine with it.   I normally would never think beer and mac n cheese could pair so well.    The Espresso Cloud IPA has a nice smooth taste that keeps it from tasting like a regular boring IPA like some I have came across as of late.  The smoothness also mellows on the finish to where you may be tempted to have one more before leaving.  For an evening kick back and maybe even a get to know you date, Starbucks Evening menu definitely will have you seeing stars of delight.

I love this Starbucks. It is great for studying and all of the baristas are really friendly and kind. I love coming here to work on homework and anything else I need to do. I would definitely recommend this Starbucks to anyone in the area
This place seriously takes forever. I've been to Starbucks all over the USA and this one is the slowest! They take 15-20 mins in the drive thru when there's only two cars! Workers are nice

Starbucks is my go to place for coffee, but I was very disappointed when I stopped here this evening. I ordered on the app just a few minutes ahead. Of arriving. I asked about my order five minutes after the app said it would be ready, and the barista asked what my name was. My order was ready in another 5 or 6 minutes (not really sure because she didn't call out my name when it was ready). We ordered d a tall and a grande cappuccino. The barista said she put the grande in a venti cup because they were out of grande cups. That would have been fine except that they filled the rest of the cup with milk so, of course, it was no longer a cappuccino.
My iced caramel macchiato has no caramel?  How is that possible?  Cafe seems to be doing well- lots of customers and friendly team. We just need caramel in the caramel drinks!

Went here 3 days ago, just me and my boyfriend. The cashier was rushing us to order and they werent even busy then every time we talked to give her our order she would cut us off and start asking questions without letting us finish what we were saying. Got there at 4pm and it seriously took 30 minutes to make 1 peppermint mocha frappe and 1 green tea frappe with mint added to it!! 30 minutes!!! No lie!! The drinks were VERY watery and nasty. They were not blended well at all! They forgot to put the chocolate things on top of my whipped cream for the peppermint mocha frappe and also forgot to put the chocolate drizzle on my boyfriends drink that he asked for. I have NEVER had a bad experience at a starbucks and this was this first. We only stopped at this location because it was on the way to where we were going. We will NEVER go back to this location. Not to mention it was dirty inside, straw wrappers everywhere on floors and tables, crumbs on tables... people just stood around instead of cleaning in their down time. Carrollwood is 100% better, we will continue to go that one because the service is fantastic and always clean

One of the most efficient Starbucks I have been to. Not to mention the baristas are amazing, and really seem to love making conversation with their customers, which makes us feel more important! If Ryann or Logan is working, they will 110% make your day.

I frequently visit this Starbucks location and the establishment is always clean, shelves orderly, and the staff is extremely nice.   Today when I went into Starbucks with my husband, the staff went out of their way to make sure everything was great. My husband ordered his usual and I ordered a water and a white mocha.   He received his coffee and my water  pretty quickly and we sat down waiting for my mocha. We waited for a few minutes and when he went to check on the status, an employee had already made it and placed it on the counter and someone picked it up.   Realizing this, they apologized (totally wasn't their fault) and quickly made me another. I really appreciated the fact that they immediately made me another drink and apologized- even though the apology wasn't necessary.

This Starbucks is slow as Christmas. I've sat in the drive thru for 10+ minutes on numerous occasions and had to just pull out. Another frustrating thing about this place is the hours. They don't abide by their posted hours. Ever. That's not a covid thing. They say they're open until 9 but I've been by at 7:30 before and they have said they're closed. Horrible Starbucks.
The place is dirty and small. Too bad for the most important plaza in Indianapolis. The cashier was ill-mannered. She didn't say a word to me at all.
This place has the best staff of ANY Starbucks anywhere in the world (and I've visited plenty!) Kudos to Janet and the rest of the AM crew - she might be the world's friendliest barista!

As a former "partner" (Starbucks employee), I just can't kick that SBux habit. I know the standards, and I hate the stores that don't follow standards (looking at you, Canal Place location) because they are truly there for a reason. I will call you out on it gladly. That said, these guys can make my iced venti 3 pump classic no water passion tea (summer) or my triple grande nonfat mocha (winter) correctly every time, and with a smile. I truly appreciate that. Plus, if you can grab a table, it's a great place to study or get work done.

WHERE'S THE FLATWARE? The only "cutlery" this Starbucks has is plastic. And they don't have plates. When I asked for one they had to pull a demi from the display case.  I BROKE MY PLASTIC FORK ON THE MUFFIN and then spotted my dress shirt when I had to eat the stale breakfast sandwich by hand.  IS THIS WHERE THE INDUSTRY LEADER IS TAKING US? Not to be grumpy (I seem to be writing a lot of sub-3 ratings lately) but this Starbucks is located in a business hotel. We bleary eyed patrons in $1,000 worth of dress wear are dodging grease dribbles before meeting clients.   (thankfully I remembered to blot not rub!)

This place is usually mobbed on the weekend, but on the other hand, there's nothing better than strolling down Main Street with a hot cup of coffee on a cool fall day.  Drinks are made with care, and some of the servers are really friendly (Jess was so nice and called us by name, which I wasn't expecting).  They've got a public bathroom you can use too. It does usually have a line though (of course).
The two guys who helped me were super nice and quick, stirred my coffee !! Which no other one has ever done!! So i definitely will be returning for sure. Clean, quiet environment. Enjoyed it.
The Barista Mark always has a smile on his face! Super friendly staff and awesome set up
Great coffee and pastries, they add alot of ice so I suggest asking for less. A little pricey too. But overall great place to stop by in the morning.

What can I say?  This is Starbucks!   Unfortunately it's next to my other favorite place to get a smooth and rich ice cold frozen coffee, so I have to choose.  Cons: The parking is REALLY tight and is always busy with being in the middle of Vanderbilt University and Medical /Hospital.  Even though the line is long, I get through very quick. Finding a seat isn't always easy, but this is the largest Starbucks I've ever been to.  It does take more time to find where to park than it does to walk in and get my coffee, but it's worth it.  Definitely keeps busy with its location, unfortunately its location that keeps it busy and crowded.  I'll keep coming back for more coffee here.
U cannot even enter the mall until 10 am! I guess they open for local in mall store staff to use between 9-10 am!

We have lived in 6 different states in the last 10 years, and this is our 2nd time in O'Fallon. This drive thru by far is the slowest out of all the Starbucks we've been to. Hoping it would have improved since 2015 but it hasn't.   Took over TWENTY minutes to receive our mobile ordered drink. WHY IS THIS LOCATION SO BAD?!?

Cant complain too much about this place, they have pretty good coffee and great customer service. I prefer to go through the drive thru when I can cause there's less pressure to order and you're not rushed as much.   When I go inside, I always feel like a fool cause I'm unsure of what i want or what to ask for most of the time. Still learning the lingo on the coffee and how to order it!   You cannot beat a military discount too which is pretty awesome, for both active, and retired military and veterans. I also enjoy their cake pops and when I go I either get their iced coffee or their iced tea.   If you're anything like me and like to avoid the crowds or aren't a fan of being in crowds, go to the drive thru instead.

This once was my favorite Starbucks location, but recently they've fallen behind on keeping the place clean - there always seems to be overflowing trash, or wrappers/crumbs on all the tables and floors. On a more positive note - There is a lot of seating, including a long table.  And the baristas are usually very friendly. And the coffee - Let's be honest - Starbucks Coffee, is Starbucks Coffee - that's why we like it. It is standardized; no matter where you go, it's the same brand and pretty much the same product. I am your typical Grande Caramel Macchiato extra hot no-foam latte girl, and this place never fails to give me that inspiring jolt of caffeine infused inspiration.
Always get great friendly service at this location whether it's a quick stop for my morning coffee or I'm heading over on an afternoon break for a pick me up.

I have truly struggled w/writing a review for this location because I am such a frequent visitor; but I feel it's time.  The physical location by far is the worst. The parking lot is often a mess. Best advice... Don't park on the drive through side. Secondly, the line is ALWAYS long. No way in the world I should be sitting in my car for 20mins and you're not making an entire gourmet meal. I'm sorry, I get the service industry but the expediting is off or something.  Lastly, please pay attention to what you're actually making. On a few occasions my order has been so terribly wrong I had to keep asking myself if I made a mistake. No reason I should have to take a sip, look at my cup and talk to myself. It hurts my feelings :(
The barista Michael Petillo was amazing! He made my grande latte to PERFECTION! Yet, I am still a Dunkin' Donuts addict, I will return to Starbucks for a latte by Michael!
Services are prompt. Like any other Starbucks. Their selections are limited tho since is not a store by itself.   They have the cups and coffee stands for sale. A little sitting area as well.

I would give no stars. I never do this,write a remark. BUT this was bad service. Simple order two coffees and three breakfast sandwiches. Waited and waited. Got my wife's coffee waiting on mine. Customer behind me got his three coffees and breakfast sandwich. Still waiting and not a word from the employees. Second customer behind me got his two coffees and food. Still waiting, wife's coffee is now cold so I asked what's going on ?  They said they are just making the other coffees now. So I asked them to make my wife's coffee again. Third customer got their order. Still waiting.  I figured it out at the end, they saw my badge when I paid    Got to my car and the coffees they made weren't even hot. I love starbucks BUT I think I'm done. I'll find a mom and pop coffee shop.

I always enjoy Starbucks coffee and whenever I'm in St. Charles, we usually stop by this particular location, though usually just the drive through. I think today was the first time we actually went inside.  Big fan of the drive through here. The employees are always extremely upbeat and never try and rush you. Sometimes they're a little too helpful when they ask if you want to add an extra shot of espresso and end up bouncing off the walls :).   On this particular visit, we had some extra time so we went inside. We got our coffee quick but they were out of their breakfast sandwiches. Not a big deal since the barista explained that yesterday's snow storm affected their food situation.   I'm particularly fond of the blonde roast and if I happen to catch it on sale, I'll buy some and have them grind it for me. Iced green teas are great too.

My first experience here was a pretty good one: I had the Strawberry Acai Refresher and the ice only came up to the 40% mark of the cup.   I was being only semi-sarcastic with my use of the word "only" in the previous sentence: When I had a refresher at the Mandalay Starbucks, the ice filled up about 80% of the cup.  So I decided that this Residence Inn location would be my #1 Starbucks in Clearwater Beach.  One of the problems here, however, is that they close at 3:00 pm on most days.  They are open a couple of hours later on Fridays and Saturdays.  Also, on a recent visit, they were out of the ingredients needed to make the Strawberry Acai Refresher.  When I asked for a Kiwi Starfruit instead, I was told that they didn't have that either.  So I went with a Very Berry Refresher, my third choice.  And then the only straws they had were the "paper" ones that have a too-strong cardboard-like taste which interferes with the taste of the beverage.
hewwo   dis starbucks is weally nice and clean  the staff are fwiendly   the pwink dwink is also the best dwink

The Gate A location... literally the slowest Starbucks I've ever seen... literally. It took longer to get coffee from that Starbucks at 5:30 AM than it did to get through TSA. Did you get that? It took longer for their "baristas" to make espresso than it did for the federal government to make sure I wasn't bringing a bomb on the plane.

I don't know why this happens but when it's only the male employees working they mess up Orders, forget orders and don't call out name.  I'm on a time limit. Last time I waited 10 minutes I walk up to employee area and said if you can't make my drink I have to leave I'll take refund. The guy said oh you didnt get your drink. He said to other guy where's his drink. He said I am didn't get order. What the Hell they are 3 feet away from each other and using ear pieces.  I'm done with this location.  I will say when the females employees work no issues.  Management need to be updated on this and  changed if they can not train correctly. Maybe giving money back on this issue. How will the store ever learn.

I frequent this location. And the Santa Barbara carrillo street location. The carrillo location gets it right everytime .And awesome workers that are happy your there.Im also near The Carpinteria location.Not because the nice workers or clean restrooms. But because of the convenience. Sorry,but this is the only thing going for this Starbucks. No one looks like they like their job and they turn their back when people walk in the door. Carol ,one of the workers ,is always rude and frankly gives out half empty cups of coffee. Also incorrectly done. Always acts as though you are a lot of trouble if you step foot in the store. And if you return the drink she only corrects the coffee half way. It's either cold,sugar syrup dripping down the side,or partially full. She will show you where the napkins are to clean it up yourself though. Some workers are slightly better.Its So sad because of the great location.
I love the curb side pick up option! Also the barista named Roger was absolutely phenomenal and very friendly and kind.

Today I went into the Starbucks on terry pkwy before going into work and I ordered something simple; tall coffee with milk and vanilla. The coffee sucked and I just don't understand how you can mess up such a simple order. I also felt like the woman at the register was kind of rude. I'm really disappointed because I hold such high standards for the Starbucks franchise in general and the fact that my neighborhood location can not live up to those standards is really disappointing. I feel like the headquarters would be really disappointed as well.

I wouldn't recommend this place for studying or having a meeting. The tables are very very limited due to COVID but that's normal. My issue is that they are very very tiny for workspace. I barely got my computer and notebook on it and I was out of room. Thankfully my Computer was charged bc just so happens that the tables that are available don't have outlets near them. If you care about anything than care about this: THE CHAIRS ARE THE WORST EVER. They're half-backs so you Can't lean back at all unless you want your spine sliced in half and they're SO WOODING AND UNCOMFORTABLE! Honestly, I think this is done on purpose... I ordered an iced latte w mocha and Carmel and almond milk. Same thing I order at my other starbucks, same thing I order and dnd, same thing I order at buddy brew, same thing I order at cozy coffee cafe, and same thing I order at blind tiger....HERE IT WAS SOOO GROSS. After tasting it, I even had to go up and ask for extra mocha in hopes to get rid of the taste. I paid WAY MORE for it than I do at other coffee shops but threw 3/4 of it away!! Ewwww. Something must have been wrong w the milk or flavors??   It is also very loud. The workers weren't quiet at all and they're blending frapps every two minutes.  There's a very nice coffee shop LOCAL, that's like 1 minute from this place called Cozy Coffee Cafe. CCC has an easy to get to parking lot and will bring your drink curbside QUICKLY. Most importantly it's COMFORTABLE seating, relaxing atmosphere, and quiet!! AND LOCAL

This Starbucks always have fresh coffee.  The staff is very pleasant and I have made this location my go to place.  I love the convenience of the drive thru. And the cleanliness of the outside and inside.  It's located in a popular location which makes it easier to get to where your going.
I always get the same thing--grande caramel macchiato. This location skimped on the expresso. My drink tastes like hot milk.

I went inside to order for 5 different people at work and was so disappointed by my experience. The coffee was great and Lisa (the one who took my very lengthy order) was phenomenal but the rest of the staff left MUCH to be desired.  As Lisa was taking my orders,  occasionally she would have a question.  When she would turn to look to her peers for guidance,  they would blatantly walk past her or ignore her completely.  It's obvious that this group has no respect for team work or their customer's time. None of them were smiling or friendly other than Lisa. Very disappointed.  I'll be going to a different location from now on.  Great job to Lisa for providing excellent customer service despite the negative environment!!!

Best Starbucks in town. The store is always clean, and the team members are always polite, attentive, enthusiastic, and friendly. I stopped at this store several times over the past few months, and I was consistently impressed with the quality of services. The coffee's good, too : )
Consistently excellent service, and the espresso has bite. Best SBUX in Tucson.

I am a Starbucks fan, even though I usually purchase traditional coffee. There's many different locales better than this one. I went through the Drive-thru this AM, no picture on the screen which was odd. Requested a bagel, all out, fair enough, it happens, I took it as a sign to cut some bread out of my day. Apparently the barista or cashier was a mute! Not one word during the whole transaction, not even a "here you go", "ok" or anything. Just handed her my money, she handed me my drink, I was the only one in line. I said Thank You, to her and she looked like she wanted to tear me a new one. There's a much better one in Exton, super people, and the one in Edgemont makes me feel like part of the family. Not this place, is it the manager? The coffee was tasteless, and lousy, I made myself a K-cup later and reused the cup, had to be good for something. Definitely not the Starbucks norm. Devon, better, Edgemont, fantastic, Exton, supernice. Newtown Square and Broomall, very warm. Paoli...go somewhere else if you want a good start to your day.

I have seen an employee, I believe she is actually the manager, coming back from a smoking break and start working without washing her hands. I have been going to this Starbucks often since I've been staying at this hotel and I have seen this happen more than once. How do I know she was coming from smoking break? Because her breath smells like ashtray! Disgusting!

Let me boast and brag. I've been coming to this Starbucks since it opened. I've seen manager after manager, barista after barista come and go but the level of service and quality has been consistent. I've had my share of favorite managers from Paige, Dan, to our current. The current barista staff of guys and girls are by far my favorite crew.   I'll have my triple espresso with steam cream please!

Starbucks is starbucks and we all decide for ourself if it's for you. This review is specifically for the drive thru. My first experience. I was impressed. It was fast, they go my order straight and I was still able to use my starbucks app with ease.

Okay, my previous review stands but things are getting better.   I started going to this location late night (because they have the latest hours) and their late night staff has converted me. There is mostly college-aged ladies working when I go in and they're AWESOME. They are friendly, quick, and they remember me AND my drink. I rarely have mess ups, no more rude staff, and no more waiting for them to find time to acknowledge me at the register.   My one singular dislike about my new experiences is that they put away the milk starting late afternoon so I have to ask them for it. It's not a big deal, but semi-annoying.  ****** updated AGAIN***** Drink mess-ups are getting on my nerves. Not including the messed up drinks from my original review, 3 more times I've had to return my drink because they never put the espresso in my drink. Once again, they got it wrong and once again it was made my problem. Starbucks employees used to apologize, re-make your drink and give you a free drink coupon when they made mistakes. Obviously, I expect the drink to be fixed but it would be nice if someone would apologize when they eff up.   I really don't know if I'll ever go to this store again. I'm fed up.

The first 2 visits I kinda thought the staff was rude. 2 more times prove it. Guy at window definitely rude. I said thanks and have a good day, and he said nothing!! He heard me! Name starts with an "I". Also name of a country!!  Also guy in ponytail is rude to us when we go inside. What's up with this place??
Waited 20 minutes even after ordering online previously...I am still in line with two cars in front of me.

Sometimes I wish they'd add minus stars.    I frequented this Starbucks while I stayed in Indianapolis big city with NOT such a small town feel.  Yes you see correctly, I changed the sentence there.  They are very rude here with all that covert racism.  Anyways.  Each visit to this location was during after peak hours.  In fact no one was in line.  Yet, they managed to be very rude and very slow every single time.
Typical airport location. The coffee is fine but you wait and wait if you catch it wrong. Typically not staffed properly to meet demand.

This location is so hit and miss, and it's one of the most inconsistent stores I have been in. I live right around the corner so this is my local "didn't have time to make coffee before I had to leave for work" drive thru shop. Each time I come, the drinks I get taste different, a phenomenon that is so confusing because of the nature of what this nationwide chain is built on. I suggest management have some training refresher courses to up the standards for all of the employees.  I've also had a few awkward experiences with some of the baristas' customer service skills. The baristas seem to be more friendly and talkative to each other, and usually ignore any kind of conversation with customers, even when I'm standing right there staring at them.  I'll still frequent this place, only really out of occasional necessity, but I hope management reads this and takes some action to change the culter and vibe of the store and the employees.

Rude, slow, and inaccurate. They had an attitude when I walked up, ordered, and when they handed out my drink. They also gave me a completely different drink from what I ordered and were entirely unprofessional throughout the experience, they were yelling at each other behind the bar and ignoring the long line of customers. And this is coming from another Starbucks partner.

Omg, 3 employees for this kind of volume? Are you frigging  kidding me???!!!! Service sucks, no parking whatsoever, dirty, empty milks, no honey, blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah. Horrendous Starbucks, stay away if possible.
coffee is awesome of course, but what I don't get is why they close so early!?!?! I don't know if it's an Arizona thing, but usually in NY you can be in the burbs and the coffee joints are 24hrs.

I have been to many Starbucks over the years but never seen a place like this. This is the Starbucks in the Nugget Casino in Spark, NV. A barista uses a whip cream container, she hangs the container on edge of a trash container(see attached picture. I circled). After seen that, I walked out of the place. That makes think twice to go visit any Starbuck again. Is this how Starbucks trains their employees? After many many years of their customer I am so disappointed. I hope they change their training procedure so this type of incident do not occur again.

It's your typical Starbucks, so no complaints here. It's pretty spacious inside with plenty of seating. They also have a small patio outside with seating, and of course a drive through. The staff has been efficient and friendly every time I've visited.
The drive thru service is so so so slow it's comical. Something needs to be done to make it more efficient
Always great service and drink made right,  Drive thru Lane always moves quickly, baristas are always friendly and engaged with customers.  They also show their support for those who have served.

Just as good as any other Starbucks! Sometimes I stop by here before going to school, and I come here almost every day during Dead & Finals Week. Only flaw is that it is very small and crowded, so it is a bit hard to study here when I need to! I also wish that it would stay open a bit more late!

This is not my usual Starbucks, but I visited them via the drive-through after an urgent care visit (don't worry! I didn't have covid!) I ordered ahead, and when I went through the drive-through I explained that while I didn't have covid, I was sick, and they were more than happy to hand my drink to me! I will definitely stop by again when I'm in the area!

This is a nice big Starbucks with lots of seating as well as a few kiosks with Starbucks drinkware and gift idea items. The staff are very friendly. I was afraid there'd be a long wait in the morning, but it was fairly quick and I was off on my way.
The folks are friendly and they don't mind that I call the Venti "Large". Very good!
