This Starbucks is not for people who like a friendly staff. They were not rude but they were not like the type of staff I am used to having at my CA Starbucks. So if you don't mind that, then this is a good Starbucks.

Best mango-hibiscus drink! I'm not a regular at Starbucks but I've been in several & this one was by far the best one I've seen! Very open with tables or comfortable chairs and tables by window. Bathroom was clean and had baby changing table. There's also a small semi private room/area for work or studying or possibly nursing. All staff friendly! Also had many more choices for a non-coffee drinker like me where others I've been in typically offer much less. I recommend this location! Did forget to get my free birthday item though-oh well maybe later:)

Since this is the relocated Lithis Pinecrest Starbucks, the great staff moved with the store. Yeah!! Great lattes guaranteed!!  This is a beautiful new store, much bigger than the old one. Much more seating.  Nice to have a parking lot dedicated to this store and not shared with other businesses. However, having to take the road to get to the new Walmart to get here, can be a nightmare. Tons of traffic to get in and out of the new businesses cropping up there. This Starbucks has a new drive thru which should make many happy.  I would give it five stars if it weren't for the difficulty getting in and out because of traffic.  But, perhaps the most consistent Starbucks, so therefore my favorite!

Only thing I don't like is the traffic and now there's no direct turning lane to go straight in anymore, have to make a u turn now. Other than that great service, nice staff and wonderful tall mocha Fraps and pastries ( that's what I always get there)

This is my favorite Starbucks in Brandon. The customer service is very conducive and welcoming. I also like the availability of a drive-thru. The one on Causeway is closest to my home, but their staff sucks! I always love grabbing my coffee and frappucinos on the go from here!

This Starbucks has probably one of the rudest barista's ever. Slamming the oven, throwing our food down. I get that it's early but if you hate your job change it.  Three stars because the coffee is good.

Pros: new starbucks location, new chairs and tables, and now with drive thru! Ughs: Longgg wait times, single worker making drinks, slowest Wifi ever (tested), flys flying around.  So this is the new starbucks that moved across the parking lot. It went from a corner unit with one restroom and moved into a duplex with two restrooms. you would think the upgrade would help, but I feel like its still the same old environment in the store, in terms of speed.  I order simple drinks like a shaken sweet tea or a coffee. Usually at other SB stores since its so fast it can be done immediately. But here, nooo, they put you in a queue with the rest of the cups and that queue apparently takes second priority to drive thru customers. So even when there is less than a handful of customers that ordered, my drink will take 10 minutes. To dispense coffee. or shake some pre-mixed tea. what. the. hell.   Also, did they move the flies over from the old location? There are fruit flies that used to buzz around the tables, indicative of not keeping the environment sanitary. I figured with the new store it would not be a further issue. But now the flies are back. I killed two. I'm sure more will be back.  Finally, what is the deal with the Wifi. I thought this was google starbucks wifi. Or whatever that means. Before the speed was decent that I could watch at least a youtube video. But lately that has stutterred and buffered to a crawl. So I did a speedtest.net at the location, and lo behold, the downspeed bandwidth turned out to be 0.76Mbps. WTH? I'm yelping right now from the starbucks down the street and the download speed here is 8.06Mbps here.   So, yeah, I think I'd rather go to this starbucks that I'm at right now, or drive real far to palm harbor. because this trinity one is blech.

Clean Starbucks with lots of comfy seating inside and a nice outdoor seating area too.   Baristas were friendly and since it was slow they even rinsed our hydro flasks and kids before refilling our reusable travel tumblers with iced lattes.   The double smoked bacon sandwich toasted open face was delicious.   Conveniently located right off the freeway.   Lots of parking!

The line at this Starbucks is always horrendously long and today, after waiting in line for 15 min, they're out of cold brew and the strawberry refresher that I ordered is basically water. Not to mention the lid had drops of coffee on it. Anyone who has ever ordered the strawberry refresher can look at this and tell it's just watered down. It's disappointing when you wait forever, pay almost $5 for a drink, and this is what it looks like.

This Starbucks location is and a continent location just off the freeway. There are two reason of giving this Starbucks a three star. One because I have not been in the facility. And two there was trash all over the ground outside of the buildings.  I ordered three drinks, when I pulled up to the drive-through and the lady at the window was nice. They seemed to get the drinks out pretty fast so there was minimum waiting time. Overall, the drink I had the Vanilla bean frap,non fat no whip.   It's nice to have a coffee place that sells other drinks besides coffee for those non coffee drinkers.

Went there for a business meeting. Order both snacks and drinks, everything was going well at first, we stayed in the corner and minded our own business until we realized that the AC was lowered so low that it was unbearable to stay one more minute in. And given that there are not outside chairs, I couldn't help but think this was a tactic to make us leave given that we were there only black customers in the store at that time.

So there really is only one person working here who is always on his game and gives phenomenal customer service. He's been there for years, so this excludes him. They mostly cater to mobile orders, and can't multitask. And if you don't get there before Felix orders his 8 shots of expresso, you'll be waiting for 25 minutes.

This Starbucks is super close to my home, although convenient, not worth the effort or time. I have tried to regularly attend this Boise Ave location for about 6 months. Within the 6 months I have notice the following:  staff is usually rushed.  Staff usually not in a good mood, no matter the time of day. (few are fine). The product produced and services is usually sloppy.  Never quite what you order, if you attend the drive through.  Occasionally this location will be out of a product, such as cups or a particular blend, then charges you for "what you get, rather than what you ordered" and quote "we are out of small cups, pay for the medium or don't get the coffee".  After today's visit, I will prolly never return. Too bad this location is a stone's throw away.  Dutch Bros will now receive 100% of my business.

I frequent this Starbucks almost daily.  On the downside, it can be crowded and slow.  On the upside there are always a fun group of people to watch coming in and out.  I find the staff to be friendly and always willing to please.  In the mornings you most likely will see a celebrity or two if you watch closely.
As far as Starbucks drive thrus go, this has got to be one of the slowest in Boise. Staff is nice... but being 10 minutes late to work because you get trapped in the coffee line is not worth it.

The customer service needs a little work and the place is not very clean, because when I sat on the table it was dirty and o should not have to say something in front of all the customers after having a cashier with little manners.
Friendly, clean, drinks as expected. Love love that they're open late. A bit more room on the inside and outside to sit and chat or to get work done.

Never fails. Every time I'm there the drive thru window line is insanity.   But most times I wait and order the same thing a large unsweet green tea lemonade.   The last 7 times I've been there.  5 times the order has been wrong.  They charge for a GTL and I always receive a watered down green tea  Today I decided to go inside.  8 people were working.  No one was in line.    It took 13 minutes to get my beverage.     Ummmmmm....

Love this Starbucks! I have an unusual name and they bent over backwards, offering to spell my name correctly and phonetically on my drink. They do a good job with their drinks, and there is so much space inside to study, play games, or have a great convo with a friend (even though it looks small from the outside!)

How long does it take to make a bagel? I ordered in the line? And now my frap is melting because they had me pull over to the front. I didn't even get curbside service even though I clearly wanted drive thru service since that's where I placed my order. I got out of my car twice in this heat while waiting. Ridiculous guys. Wtf.
Didn't quite listen to my order. Got my tea wrong. Drank it anyway because I was on my way to work.

Ordered hot coffee in the drive thru. Coffee came out cold! In a hot coffee cup. I'm really confused because it tastes like iced coffee it was ridiculous. I had to go through the drive they once again and return my 'hot'coffee for a hot coffee. It was a waste of time and we did not get anything from them for our wasted time or our inconvenience. I will be writing to Starbucks about this. Not good business.  The location was 5425 US Hwy 19 New Port Richey, FL.

I go to this location daily mostly because it's convenient. Service is always slow and drive thru is chaotic. Today hit the limit for me. Someone put a pump of some kind of sweetener, tastes like chocolate, in my black americano. Do better.
We just stopped for a quick coffee and expected normal Starbucks, but it was great service and a short line. They even helped me pick out the perfect drink. All in all great service.

The staff at this location is wonderful! Today, as I was rushing to eat my salad while working on my laptop at he same time, I spilled everything on the floor...after taking one bite.. While I was strugglin to clean up the mess (also embarrassed, frustrated, and hungry), one of the staff came up to me with a brand new salad and cleaned up everything on the floor for me. Best customer service ever! Made my day!   Anyhow, besides this personal anecdote, I also want to comment that the location itself is really nice! They have plenty of small tables that you can study or work. Much better than the one on walnut if you are planning on sitting around for a while. Also, the music is the right volume and the couches are super comfy...

I hate to see that this Starbucks has such a low ratings Yelp. This is one of my favorite Starbucks locations. I grew up in Greenville and I think that this place has well-integrated itself into the surrounding area.  Yes, there are better local places nearby (like Brew HaHa for example) but I must give this Starbucks credit for creating a great space right on the Kennett Pike. They have a wonderful seating area and the exterior in beautiful.  The DO NOT have a drive-through, which I kind of enjoy. This can mean that wait times can be longer but at least you have a nice space to wait.   If you're craving Starbucks in Delaware, come to this Starbucks. There is ample parking and a beautiful space to wait for your brew!

BY FAR THE WORST STARBUCKS IN TOWN TO STUDY AT! ***  ***That is obviously a false statement- I'm hoping people who don't really read these see it and it keeps them from studying here and taking up my tables :)   This is actually my favorite study spot in all of Philadelphia... and I am NOT a starbucks person.   Reasons: 1. Free wifi 2. WITH PLENTY OF OUTLETS 3. Nice staff 4. Only tables made for two people- so it prevents annoying crowds that talk a lot (yet somehow they still miss the handicap sign on the front table) 5. I don't get phone service here... which you may think is initially a problem... until you realize how productive you are!  6. It's steps away from public transit, yet tucked away enough to keep it from being swarmed by the 9-5'ers 7.  I'm not sure if it's because it's located next to Fox and Hound or that it is close to the gay district... but it would be a lie if I said I didn't enjoy the man candy that walks by between 4-6pm every weekday :)  8.  Also, it seems to be people who actually study... i'm sure the two person seats and unlimited triple white chocolate mocas don't hurt that any tho (not that I order those...) 9.  They don't care if you bring outside food in... as they realize no real person could sit there for 12 hours straight and eat the crap they have

Starbucks has the Worst Coffee Ever! Seriously, why would anyone want to waste their time and money on a place like Starbucks? Their coffee tastes nothing like coffee. Don't bother going there for a cup. Bob Evans has better coffee than this place.

I am definitely not the type of person that leaves reviews for places, but I wanted to for this Starbucks! I come in here to do homework every now and then and the employees are always super friendly and kind to everyone! They always try to keep the tables clean when they have downtime. They take the time to ask you how you actually spell your name, ask you how you're doing and have a legit conversation with you (which may not be important to some people but it is to others). I just appreciate their kindness!

Average Starbucks with incredibly nice staff. Good amount of seating, free WiFi, we like to study here.   Just a hint about coffee shop etiquette for patrons: if you don't plan on using the electrical outlets, pick a seat without them.

This is a nice Starbucks in the heart of Irvington.  It stays fairly busy because it attracts everyone hanging out on or near the main drag, which is the stretch of Washington that runs from Arlington to Ritter.  In addition, it attracts people that live in the area who need a coffee fix and want to just get in and out without incident.  The staff does a good job of keeping up, and they are friendly.  They keep stocked on all the standard Starbucks products.  I have to be honest and say that sometimes the standards come out a little inconsistent on taste or how it's made.  It's as though someone is using a different measuring cup or something.  Nonetheless, it's still Starbucks.

This place sucks. No good customer service. The coffee they make is not upto par to what they charge for it. Been to many starbucks but this particular one sucks.   Don't waste your time and money here. Drive a mile down to and there is another coffee shop. Far better!!!
I love going to this Starbucks because once I go in Hillary already know what I want and she such a happy person makes me feel so welcome..! She should get a raise

Zach is the best!  He leads the tribe of hardworking co-workers who aim to please the customer with superior service and a smile! Celebrating your future visit with a smile and a double espresso purchase!

Mmm..White Chocolate frappuccino here is the best I've ever had! maybe it's because  ordered it with 1 pump of frap and the rest was white chocolate LOL.  People here are friendly. Service was fast too!   -1 star because they don't take phone orders.

I have been in Starbucks all over the country.  Media Pennsylvania is a funky little fair trade town nestled into the county seat with its courthouse and lawyer class.  The Starbucks lacks an identity.  It is on the outskirts of the town center. It feels less like a coffee house than a strip mall fast food coffee shop. Plus the sour smell of old bitter coffee hanging in the air here is killing me. I get the feeling that this place is open only because it can, and no other reason.  Too bad. Starbucks is such a great brand.

Sparing readers a detailed description of what Starbucks is or what they serve is pointless: anyone who hasn't been under a rock since the late 1990s has probably been into one.  Maybe you like it; maybe you don't; your opinion is your opinion.  Personally, I prefer the local coffee house to a Starbucks - just as I prefer a local burger joint to Hardees. Despite my preference, Starbucks is tasty, ubiquitous, and convenient.  This Starbucks location, which has operated since I was in high school (hint: it isn't new), has always satisfied me.  It is a five-star location.  A five-star Starbucks?  Yes, especially as I'd only compare one Starbucks location with another Starbucks location.  I'll tell you why: It's the service.  I've experienced nothing by exceptionally friendly service at this location for more than 15 years.    Story time.  Very recently, I found myself with a great urge to purchase a French press coffee maker.  Since I had ventured to Northside, I figured I'd try Starbucks, since I knew they sold them.  After visiting one location and striking out, I telephoned this location to see if they carried the item.  To my satisfaction, the friendly young barista not only said that she had them, but that they were on sale!  When I arrived at the shop five minutes later, I found the she had already neatly wrapped and bagged my brand new French press.  The barista, who herself is a fan of the simple coffee makers, gave me various tips on how to brew the best cup of coffee on earth.  I'm not sure I'm a master yet, but I'm a work in progress.  It's the small things that count.  86th and Ditch Starbucks: I'll be back.

I am so glad that Starbucks mothership has decreed the outdoors space around their venues nonsmoking.   Previously you could not get into this store without cutting the smoke with a knife.  Now it is breatheable air!   The Baristas are very good, service is quick and friendly.
Very nice Sbux. Love how spacious and plenty of plug in outlets there are. Next to the Arch, so perfect excuse to make a stop and rest a bit.

This place is HUUUUUUUUGE!!!! There is so much seating, they should just set up a projector and screen some blockbusters! Not only is there a ton of seating, it comes in all different types: round, four person tables; longer tables with power outlets; solo chairs with footrests; booths along the walls; and backless, puffier, ottoman-like chairs.   The area with the solo chairs is slightly removed from the rest of the store, so you can get your introvert on! Grab that prime seating behind the merch shelf and get some work done!  The staff was super friendly. The barista did make my flat white a grande instead of venti, but he fixed it in no time and gave me the messed up drink as well. I LOVE when they do that instead of throwing it away!  This place is going to be a local haunt for me for sure. I hope it stays this great as it gets older!

This Starbucks just won over my heart. During the rush there was an autistic man that came in who the employees greeted by name and made feel so special regardless of how busy they were. I was so impressed by this establishment and the manager on duty!

Restrooms & Facilities are not cleaned throughout the day.  Lovely & engaged staff.  Bathrooms are filthy.  Store is well stocked with a variety of sandwiches, baked goods, drinks, etc.  great music selection.  I really expected Better from Starbucks today
The staff is always pleasant whenever I come here. Which isn't very often. Unless it is during the winter season. Hazlenut lattes are unbeatable.

Waited 35 minutes in the drive-thru on more than one occasion when there was about 6-7 cars in it each time. Drinks were okay but they have got to get a better system for the drive-thru. Inside might be faster but the drive-thru is not quick by any means.

Spacious respite to get away from Eldorado's gambling or the downtown heat. Lots of seating and actually temperature cool for being close to a large open entrance of the casino.  Popped in a couple of times. Got a nitro coffee, wonderful. Doesn't need sugar or cream, I know right? Also the dragonfruit tea is quite visually stunning.  Service was a hit for the tea but a sorrowful miss for the coffee. It looks like the most quiet and relaxful Starbucks to be employed at.

I have to say that I am generally a big fan of Starbucks for a) their customer service and b) their 'go green' mentality. However... The workers there seem more rude than I usually am when I wake up without caffinee. Also the lady who glared at me for my 'complicated order' decided to charge me extra. When I said she charged me for the wrong thing... she just said 'it's how much it normally costs'. On top of that... she made my order wrong.  I'd rather go to Caje... that place tops all the other java and tea places here in IV.

First off, I will still unfortunately be going to this Starbucks out of convenience as it's right by my office. However. Since Tiffany left there is approximately one nice person that works there. Lazy, slow baristas. I get it, your busy. But when you have to move, move! There is a newer lady that is doing the drive thru for who knows what's reason. She can't get or make drinks right, overflows cups, and takes forever to get you order even when nobody is behind you. This Starbucks is great location wise and I know that it's bound to be better, but the people that work there all need a reality check.

This is your typical Starbucks store. However the staff is very friendly. When the store is busy expect to wait a bit longer for your drink. This store is located within a shopping mall so it's busier than some normal stores.
Today I ordered a grande iced tea port over a Trenta cup of ice. All the staff was friendly the product they delivered was lacking in ice as my picture shows. Two stars today.
The WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER. Total attitude from the time we walked in.. 20 minutes to get a pour over!!!  Pathetic.
Love this new location. Various seating arrangements and the Baristas are always friendly.  My Tevana Chai tea in the bags is wonderful. Thanks Joel!
Took kinda long for them to realize I wanted Cheese Danish. They brought me a bagel. Luckily I noticed on the register before that

Always fast and courteous. Rarely mess up my order (and I am a pretty frequent customer) unlike a lot of Starbucks in the city. Line is never very long. Mobile orders are usually ready before I get inside. Pretty much all you want or need in a coffee shop!

Hands down, this is the WORST sb ever!  My husband and I always get the same drinks... this place NEVER...EVER gets it right!  It's either too bitter, too diluted, or too much breve. I've even received my ICED coffee WITHOUT ICE!! After many... MANY complaints to the customer service department ... even an in-person meeting with the AREA MANAGER, we have officially given up on this place. We have been very patient and they just can't get it right. There have been numerous times that we've even brought our drinks up the street to the SB on Robb & Mae Ann for (what I have defined as) "coffee repairs".  Simply awful :(
What I expect from a Starbucks. Fast, friendly, clean, and a great coffee! And drive thru for those on the go, was quick. Close by several hotels, ours included. Yup, going back there tomorrow to.

Great location and easy to get into, go at least 3 times a week. Staff is very friendly and always remembers my favorite drink. Manager "Tiffany" is friendly and always engaging. Seems like there are already regulars there and a great place to check your emails.........keep up the great work.  Btw.....this is the stand alone store not the one in the Target..

Today, I had a horrible experience at this Starbucks. I'm going to call out the particular shift because I think Managers should know what their Barista's are doing. I was in around 11am on Sunday. The Barista started my drink, and another customers drink. During this time she decided to walk to the back..get a mop..proceed to mop the floor...and than put the mop back...Finally getting back to finish off our drinks...Really????? Horrible customer service Starbucks....While I will be back...I hope to get a Barista who actually cares about their Customers.
Lots of tables to sit and work. Cool and airy. Great place to meet for informal interviews or coffee chats. Staff are friendly.

This place is always packed with people playing (or working) on their macs.  Seriously, what is up with that. I'd feel completely left out if I bring my PC to this store.   Besides that, the store is big and cozy. The barista ate nice and helpful.  It's always been a pleasant experience when I buy coffee from here.

I came in with my friend for a drink and breakfast sandwich after we ordered I got my sandwich but had to wait almost 10 mins for my drink. To add icing on the cake my friend received all his items before me when he ordered after I did. Everyone who works at this location looks like they hate their job and could care less about the customer..

This starbucks is very nice they have a drive through and eat in area which is very convenient. The food you order is always hot and fresh and they always get your coffee order right. All the employees are very nice and will do anything (like remake your drink) to make you happy. Sometimes the drive through can take take but only if there's a lot of cars in front of you.
I have always enjoyed this spot. The staff are nice and the place is clean. Plenty of places to sit and the music is pleasent. I would recommend this place to anyone.
Very extremely rude staff! The female at the drive through acted like she hated her job and took it out on others! Won't go back to this one!

This was my favorite go to Starbucks when Stephanie managed it. When she left to open her own business it went down hill. The garbage cans are ALWAYS full. My drink is usually wrong and the staff just isn't in sync. I hear the staff complaining about something most of the time, be it personal or about work. Doesn't make for a very positive experience. The drive thru is slower than molasses in November and most of the time, after that long wait, the drink is wrong. The store is not neat and tidy any longer and just lacks that warmth it had when Stephanie was manager.  Sorry to give such a negative review but, unfortunately after having tried to stick it out through the transition of management, I just don't go there except for ground coffee and only if I'm I'm the area. Very sad to see this store go down hill.

A Starbucks like any other Starbucks. This is the one I visit when I work in the mall, and there isn't anything special about it, but there's nothing I have found particularly awful, either. I mostly visit here as oppossed to Tim Hortons or Seond Cup because of the My Starbucks Rewards program.  The lineups can get pretty ridiculous, obviously, since it's in a mall. They also tend to have only one person on till, even when it does get crazy, which is a bit annoying, especially if you're going there on your break and don't exactly have 15 minutes to spend standing in line to order and again waiting for that order. But most of the servers I've had have been very friendly and good at their jobs. There are one or two there that I dread getting, but that's the same anywhere.  My coffee usually tastes just as I expect it to, and only once or twice have they gotten anything on my order wrong (and they are always happy to fix it). A good option if you're in the mall and want a drink or snack. (Though don't hold out hope of them having the new-ish bistro boxes in stock - they're sold out more often than not!)

I havent done it in a WHILE Due to this awful recession id rather buy bags , booze and more booze, However working until 1 and not falling asleep until 1:45 and having to be up again at 6 is not something I can do @ my feeble old age. I needed a boost and red bull is more of an after 9am pick me-up .  Im thinking and thinking and thinking THE BUXX  pops into my head like a lighting bolt from GOD on this magical ASHE WED ( oh its a sign)   Tall NONFAT extra shot latte w/ 2 splendas has given me the power to crunch these #'s until 4 today   GOD bless those baristas @ the starbucks on marsh and silverside!!!!
This Starbucks is super cozy. All the employees are very friendly and it is a cute little spot for coffee

Awful coffee! Ordered a hot coffee with a cold foam topping. I have ordered this multiple times at various locations and never had an issue. I ordered through the app and picked up at the drive thru. Was never told they made it an iced coffee up til I was at the pick up window. When the young girl handed me my drink and I noticed it was an ice coffee I asked her why my coffee was made are used instead of hot like I ordered. She proceeded to tell me that they cannot add the cold foam to a hot coffee. I told her I have had this ordered at various locations and had no issue. She never offered to remake the coffee nor apologize.

This is the unhappiest appearing staff of any Starbucks that I've patronized. The baristas are rather unpleasant, in fact. The drinks are hastily placed on the busy counter, and the customer's names are inaudibly announced. After watching a series of unhappy customer interactions for 15 minutes without my drink appearing, I inquired about it's status. I was told "we made it twice, someone else must have grabbed it." As I was standing within 10 feet the whole time, I knew this not to be true. Also, why would they remake a drink if it had been picked up, and no complaint registered??? The drink was then promptly made, and was a Starbucks beverage, just as expected. My experience does not seem isolated, based on other reviews. I would seek out other options, if available.

Right around the corner from the popular donut store..  How can anyone resist a vanilla latte, extra hot to go with a devils food cake donut??..  Fast, and efficient!  Perfect location!  Employee was friendly, and energetic!  Asking for your first name, to make a more personalized  visit...  It works!
Awesome place. Cool Baristas. Very convenient located in a hospital when you don't want just regular coffee. Wi-Fi reaches in here. Clean and organized. What more can you ask for ?

(4.5) ~ Very Good Overall Coffee & Service.  This is one of the few locations in the area to have "Nitro Cold Brew" on Draft:). You can either get it Regular or with Sweet Cream.  Because I like strong coffee, I prefer the Regular;). Now the only reason I didn't give them a (5), is because they don't currently have a Clover Brewing System, which is the best way to brew hot coffee buy the cup and allow you to try their Reserve beans;)

Smallish location partnered with a bank on the interior.  Everyone was friendly.  Nothing spectacular here but the promo of extra stars for buying a baked good.  3 stars for a small coffee and brownie!

I've been putting off this review in the hopes that they would get better with some staff turn over but they haven't. I will drive to five points and go to that Starbucks over this one any day. And I do. I only go here when I'm shopping or a friend is driving and I'm always disappointed. My drinks are consistently made wrong. And when I'm getting a coffee it's because I have a crap ton of stuff to do that day and don't have time to wait for 15 min for a drink that easily could have been ordered and made within 5 if made right the first time.

Its hard to believe that a company this big allows it's employees to dawdle and move as slowly as they do. I work in the service industry, I know first hand that what they do is not hard, and should not take long at all. I waited 8 minutes for my frap with nobody in front of me. Insane. I love Starbucks but this locations lack of speed and general nonchalant attitude makes me fear being late for work every time I go in.

I visit this location at least once a week (I work nearby) and have never had a problem with my drinks or food.  The baristas are pleasant and work fast.  As another reviewer mentioned, this place often gets slammed in the afternoons by dozens of students from nearby schools.  However, even at seemingly max capacity, I've never waited for more than 5-10 minutes for a drink.  Definitely wish they had a drive-thru but then I'd probably go even more, which my wallet certainly doesn't need.

I've given the new (late summer) baristas time to adapt but they are just horrible.  Yes they can make the coffee but they always seemed completely overwhelmed.  They can't be bothered to look up or greet anyone that enters even when there is as little as one customer.  Getting their attention requires a song and dance show.  Mobile orders require shouting just to receive and can often take longer to get then just standing in the line because of this.  Its like they intentionally ignore any sound from the floor.  There are a few really great people still working but these new ones really outnumber them and with Kroger's store now open that may be the better option.

Coffee was OK. The wait to even order was over 20 minutes at 7 am this morning. I can't seem to understand why only 2 people were working for a busy time: 1 making beverages and one on the register. There was a third employee on the phone but she did not seem too concerned with the line almost out the door....

New location same service.   Why are there 5 people working and 3 people in line yet not one hello, please hang tight?   They all look busy but there is 1 person at the drive through?  I really hoped the service would get better, last time i visited in a Sunday around 3 pm they were out of Pike coffee! Out of coffee at Starbucks, so the girl filled my cup 1/2 way and handed it to me yet wanted to charge me full amount.  I was like.... mmmmm no, are you okay?  I asked if they could make more coffee she said no.    I really am so frustrated with this place.    I'll go to the local Panera and not aggravate myself.

The service painfully slow and the employees seem clueless. They were out of my first two beverage choices. I ordered an iced green tea latte. What I got was a cup of almond milk with clumps of matcha. I asked if this was how it's usually made and if there was anything that could be done about the clumps. An employee, who informed me he was on his lunch break, said there was nothing that could be done that the clumps are normal and perhaps I should just shake it up myself. He said he would re-make it for me but, again, he was on his lunch break. Great. Thanks for your help buddy. The best they could do is blend it into a frappacinno. Another employee begrudgingly tried to fix it but it didn't help. This store is just a mess overall.
This was the closest Starbucks to where I am staying. Service was fast and efficient. There was plenty of seating. This location is at the Oxford Park Centre.

I like this starbucks because it's not usually filled with the chaos of any other starbucks usually found in other busy cities.   I like to support local cafes in NOLA but once in a while it's nice to come here and just study for a bit. Plenty of light, I like to come in at the slow times during weekdays

Came to the same location at a different time and the experience was 100% better! Patrick, the barista, was super helpful and top notch customer service. I got a Vanilla Roobois, which is really good and even better with a little bit of milk.

This Starbucks WAS my favorite in Philly, the workers were friendly, fast, and the quality of the store was very good. This was a place I could get work done, and didn't feel like I was in the way, the store was also very clean, and comfortable. Lately the quality has dropped, the drink I usually get (Hot White Chocolate Mocha) doesn't even taste the same, the staff is very short with people and will often get rude with you. When you ask a question they act as if educating and informing customers is not apart of their job. I will not be visiting this location in the future because the new staff lacks substance.

The only reason I even go to this starbucks is because it's the only one on my way to work, but other than that the manager is horrible!!!!!!!! She was unpacking boxes when i went to get my drink the other day & literally threw the box over my head & if i didn't move it would of hit me. another time she told me i didn't know then importance of masks & was rude to me all while i was wear scrubs to go work my hospital job during a pandemic.  there's one employee here that is always pleasant & nice, he always tells me have a good day & says good morning, but the rest are rude, & honestly the customers are rude af too. i wish they would make a starbucks closer to my house so i could stop going to this trash can of a strarbucks
From start to finish had the best experience here. Jeff helped me and was in an amazing mood which made me feel great! Loved this location!!!
Great location and always fast! Even if the line is long I'm still through quickly so the staff here is very efficient and friendly.

OK place to stop in when walking over to Harbour island.  Pet friendly patio with a large water bowl for you fur babies.  Staff was fine but they could not honor my Starbucks rewards.  Not sure if that is a one time thing or all the time.  There is another coffee place by the docks with bakery and gelato .  They give Starbucks a run for location and sweets.
Great location! The workers are always nice and friendly. There's never a mistake with my order. This location always seems to be busy but there is never much of a wait.

This place is ALWAYS dirty! You would think being in the heart of DOWNTOWN Indianapolis it wouldn't be but IT IS!  I only stopped in this location because it was 1000 degrees outside and I love their cold Strawberry Green Tea. Well,  along with the nasty store the employees had NASTY attitudes. A woman asked for a cup of ice  after she finished her drink and you thought she asked for a million dollars!!! The barrister loudly proclaimed that "It's hot outside and we don't have ice to spare!" What,  they don't have a ice MAKER? Is the ice bought in from City Ice?  And as for my STRAWBERRY GREEN TEA, I may as well have paid  $4.00 for WATER!  The flavor and color of my drink neither  tasted or looked anything like green tea, let alone STRAWBERRY GREEN Tea!

Service may be fast but not so friendly. And can someone please answer me why they charge $.60 for chocolate drizzle on top of my frappé? I WILL NOT be coming back to this location.   The drive thru was packed but they get cars in and out. There are a lot of workers inside the restaurant but they aren't smiling. I went at 8am and no one seemed to be happy to be there.   I give this location 1 star because I frequent Starbucks quite often, and I am a Gold reward member, but this charge is ridiculous and just made me question my loyalty to this franchise.

Went in today around 8:40pm. Y'all guy with blond hair and receding hairline seemed to be very upset and it showed. I understand thing happen in life but you're at a job and you shouldn't let it affect you. Anyways I ended up leaving and not getting anything cause he made me feel like me ordering a drink would be a bother to him. Hope his day gets better. If he's there just walk away he clearly doesn't want to be there. This gives Starbucks a bad name since usually employees are super nice.

This Starbucks is the slowest I've been to yet. They are out of nearly everything and won't get a new shipment in for weeks and then tell you that it's been discontinued. I've never gotten a sandwich fully heated here, it's always cold in the center so you have to ask for things double toasted. I give it 2 stars only because the people working there are always very, very nice!
I frequent this Starbucks since it is close to my house but the drive thru today set the mood for my wife and I as the chillest experience we have ever had. It truly set us up for a relaxing day.

It was kind of a surprise to eye a Starbucks in a shopping complex just off one of the seedier strips in the city, but a very welcome one, as my need for a warm wake-up drink was high after hours of sifting through dusty thrift-shop merchandise.  I ordered a cappuccino, which was the usual tasty treat, and a fruit and nut bar, something Starbucks has been carrying recently as a healthier option. They're basically a granola bar, lightly sweet, and what really makes these stand out is the addition of dried apricots and cherries. Yum!  Maybe it was just the time of day, or the fact that coffee shops in this area are few and far between, but this Starbucks appears to get quite a bit of business.

Friendly and efficient staff. I've enjoyed all my crafted drinks here. I'm always able to find a seat for me and my family.   My last visit I tried the blonde roast. It was extremely bitter. My spouse's thanksgiving blend was much tastier. Go with that brew!

Sometimes it's crazy busy here, but the people working are always so pleasant! Of course they are pretty good at making our drinks...just takes a few minutes longer some days.  We always come inside because the drive thru always has a line...they are pretty well staffed so they keep both lines moving!
Love starbucks drinks but I will never go to this location again. Waited 45 mins in the drive thru only to get the wrong size.

My girls love Starbucks Every time we pass a Starbucks they want to stop. Yesterday we were celebrating my daughter's birthday weekend, passed Starbucks and had to stop in the drive up to get their favorites. Well, to our surprise and disappointment. The cookie and cake pop was delicious as usual, but the 3 drinks were not good at all. They lacked flavor, half the drink was liquid, and the rest taste like slushy ice, with lumpy deflated whipped cream. There was only 1 car at the window, which left pretty quick. We were next, but waited at the window for a very long time. It appeared the barista was making several orders while we waited. Now there is a line around the building ‍‍So so disappointed. A total waste of my $22, then they kept my bank card and ruined my daughter's birthday shopping spree because we had to leave the mall to go back to get my bank card in order to shop. I don't recommend 2705 Veterans Blvd.

I've been to a lot of Starbucks locations. But this one specifically always have problems with mobile orders. Either extremely late or if you get there "someone" must've taken the wrong mobile order. Happens way to often. I minds well just go order in person.
Its a starbucks. You know you will get good service and drinks. I had the Green Iced tea. Yes it was good stuff.
Great staff and never mess up my order. It's the first place I go to when entering the mall.

Christmas Eve and a very good atmosphere. Staff were very busy but maintaining a very pleasant demeanor. Taking orders as they came and delivering with a smile. Made the stop for Spiced Chai Latte all the more pleasant. And of course the drink was spot on!

This is the Starbucks I frequent due to convenience. They have remodeled the inside providing a more modern aesthetic. I enjoy Starbucks because of the store hours which close late I like other local coffee shops. There is seating with outlets, and comfy chairs. The only negative is they don't have decaf coffee ready. You have to Ofer Americana or pour over. Overall great place to work and get a cup of coffee.

I love Starbucks and I got to drink my first Pumpkin Spice Latte out of the red cups. Location is very convenient. The staff members were courteous to my friends and I, while we took a long time to decide what to order. We didn't have to wait long to get our coffees. Also something to consider is how there are empty seats on a Sunday, considering how everyone drinks coffee on Sunday Afternoons.

This is the only company owned Starbucks in downtown St Louis, and it is far superior to the others, especially the one on Washington. The baristas are friendly, incredibly efficient, and seem to care about the customers. It's actually kind of amazing how quickly they can prepare a drink. Unlike the nightmare on Washington Street, I've never had an incorrect order in this store. And, they stock had breakfast and lunch items as well.  The only thing that keeps it from being 5 stars is that the location is kind of small. It's not too small, there always seems to be a place to sit, but its far from the most luxurious Starbucks I've ever been to. One of the great things is that when the weather is warmer there are plenty of tables to sit at outside. That relieves any overcrowding, and is actually pleasant considering the giant empty department store you look at across the street.  Its out of my way to go to this Starbucks, but it's worth it because of the service, staff, and quality of drink and food.
The girl in here had a real attitude problem. She is obviously miserable working and she is being slow. She throws stuff around without a care and is rude to the customers. Don't go

Clean and friendly baristas. Drinks consistently have the same flavors. Seems there's some hate about he location and staff but My experience was positive and I'd return. Shoutout to staff and management! Feel like anger in some cases may be due to high expectations... when there is an obvious worker shortage and a tiny pandemic going on... lol
Friendly workers, of course expensive. I can't understand why they don't put up a fence by the drive through. The view is all of their garbage, very unsightly!

One of my favorite Starbucks locations, especially since the fantastic remodel this past Fall. Very easy-going and useful layout, great options for mobile devices and laptops (Goggle WiFi), and very friendly staff. BTW, I rarely give 5 stars for reviews - this store does everything right!

Never would I think I'd make a review for Starbucks cuz come on, it's Starbucks. But here goes; So made a Starbucks run and came in here with my 2 friends for 7 drinks. 4 hot lattes and a hot chocolate. They didn't want to give me sleeves for my hot drinks cuz they were running low and wanted to reserve them for "really hot drinks". I told her we did have hot drinks but she said they really aren't and really hot drinks are like Americano's  I mean really? I've never experienced this and don't think we really need to be ordering $5 lattes and have a cheap Starbucks be stingy with sleeves. Kinda ridiculous for a company that makes millions.  On brother...

This place is so slow I've had more than enough time to write multiple bad reviews and tell friends about it... the line wasn't even long I don't know what the deal is! If only there was a convenient Dunkin!! I'll never come back even if there is no one waiting...
I really wish this Starbucks was bigger. It's such a nice place to study but it gets so crowded. However, it does have good (working) wifi!
This location has no drive-thru which seems to result in faster counter service and makes it a pretty quick stop on the way into the office!

This location is consistently efficient.  They seem to be one of the busiest stores in the city in that they get a lot of downtown morning traffic and lunch time, too.  The drive thru can be backed up into the street, but they keep it moving fairly quickly.  The drawbacks are that they can run out of things fast such the pastry and food products.

Much better than your average Starbucks!   First, it's much larger with a very pleasant upstairs seating area.  Second, it's open until midnight, unlike most other coffee shops here.  Third, the service here has been very good with friendly baristas who actually seem to enjoy working here.  Its definitely one of the best Starbucks I've been to!

There are no plugs at this Starbucks. I came here while visiting Reno with the intent of spending 2 hours working before hopping in the car to work some more on the drive home (while my husband drove of course).   While looking for somewhere to plug my laptop in so I'd have a full charge for the drive (and because my laptop doesn't hold a charge very long), one of the employees let me know they don't have plugs anymore because people would post up too long. There truly must be a better solution to this, like, a time limit on the plugs or something along those lines.  I work remotely so I spend a LOT of time and money (much more than I probably should) in their stores, and I really feel it should be safe to assume reliable WiFi and plugs will be present.   Booooo!

there are several starbucks in the airport but I am referring to the one located outside the US AIRWAYS gates.  I was treated to rude and disrespectful service. I had a reward on my card and attempted to use it, when the cashier didn't know how to work my phone, she gave it to another worker, who took my phone and attempted to enter my card into the machine. She turned me away immediately and told me there was no money on my card, umm lady I have 5 different cards you just happened to enter the wrong one.  She gave me this look at service as if I was not good enough and after a red eye I really wasn't having it especially since I did not get any coffee, because I refuse to buy coffee from such rude service, HMS HOST will be hearing from me.
The baristas here are welcoming & friendly.  The lobby is large & clean. Love this Starbucks!

Now this is a fantastic Starbucks.  Fast, friendly service with employees who remember your name when you walk in!  Pretty impressive.  They are super fast with the line, which can get long in the morning.    Lots of outdoor tables to talk about the local news of the day with the downtown loiter squad.  They also have the "Clover" system if you want a uniquely brewed coffee.  Try it out if you are curious - you can ask for any coffee to be made in the Clover.  These are common in big cities but DTSP has one!    Even though it's "just Starbucks," the staff here goes above and beyond the call of duty to get you your much needed caffeine fix.  Also, they shut people down from using the bathroom if you don't buy something.  I personally like this philosophy because the general Starbucks public bathroom policy makes most of their bathrooms end up being where bums shave and do other various activities, some more hygienic than others.

This is my favorite Starbucks location. Yes, there's a Starbucks on every corner it seems, but here the floors, tables, and bathrooms are always clean and the staff are very friendly in comparison with the indifference that feels so common to a Starbucks visit nowadays. Even when my app wasn't functioning one day, preventing me from paying, the barista gave me my drink for free and was so kind about it. It's my go to spot to get my favorite coffees and snacks on the go, or to sit and work peacefully.

I have always said I didn't like Starbucks; the coffee is too bitter and I never know what to order. This morning, I was rushed out of the house due to my alarm being set an hour later than it should've been. I ended up flying to work because unexpectedly, there was no traffic and the only coffee shop near is Starbucks (I needed coffee to get through the day), so I decided to just go. I told the barista I wanted a minute to think and then that I wanted advice on how to get the sweetest coffee. He was extremely patient with me and told me his opinion, which I took. When I got to the window he told me to try it before I left to make sure I liked it and it was sweet enough. To my surprise, it was perfect. This barista (His name was either Erin or Devin) was so kind and personable, he made my morning so much better. He even printed out a second receipt for me so I would know what to order in the future. Personally, good customer service is my "make it or break it" with most restaurants, being someone who worked in the food business for over 2 years. He is the reason I will now be returning to Starbucks frequently. Thank you!

Do you ever order an iced coffee and then an hour later you're wondering why you are bouncing off the walls?  Talking so fast?  Walking so fast? Can't concentrate?  Constantly OUTRAGED while reading the Reno Yelp talk threads?  The super friendly barista here confirmed our suspicion that a Starbucks iced coffee actually has  more caffeine than a normal coffee because it's so concentrated.

This is one of my favorite stops on my way to work. They take the time to get your order correct and they greet you every time. The inside of the store is always clean and the staff are always friendly. What I like about this particular store is that you can usually order and get through the drive thru in a reasonable amount of time. When you go to other drive thru's you could sit there for a good amount of time. My drinks are always made correctly and never had any issues. The other thing I've noticed is that their baked goods always seem to be fresh and not dry or stale!! Way to go you all rock!!

Unfortunately we were disappointed  in our experience at this  Starbucks location.  Ordered a latte with almond milk and when asking the Barista if it was the answer was "it should be" in a rude tone......it wasn't it was milk and was subsequently thrown out out.
Worst Starbucks I've ever been to. Parking is terrible, the service is slow and inaccurate, and the employees are rude. Don't bother asking for decaf because they make it regular despite the request.

They charged me and then skipped my order and eventually somebody asked me why I was standing there and somebody else said oh sorry. And then it was too late and I gave up.  But it all worked out because I went to Denny's and I had my food in front of me in less time then I waited at the Starbucks for my espresso.

Most of the time there is someone friendly, efficient and aware of the customer at this location but not today.  I have been at Starbucks everyday of my life for probably 5 years and things have changed.  No longer do they wear the uniforms, allow you to choose at the customer station which cream/milk you want in your coffee (self serve) and no longer do they feel necessary to give impeccable and friendly service.    Love the coffee, love being able to sit and work in a nice environment but starting to lose interest in asking for milk versus getting it for myself and waiting for pleasantries.  Today I waited 5 minutes (long for Starbucks to be acknowledged) and another 5 minutes (with no one waiting) to have a basic ice coffee made with equal and milk that I had to ask for....  Disheartening for a big fan.

LOVE this Starbucks because there is an outlet wherever you sit. If you sit at the community table or at the bar, the outlets are right under the table/bar, facing down. Outlets are on the wall for the rest of the seats. Of course, it wouldn't be Starbucks if the temperature isn't at freezing, so bring a jacket. There are also lots of seating outside. The baristas here are nice and very accommodating.

Usually I love coming here. But last Thursday morning was some of the worst service . I placed in a mobile order , which was a grande cold brew it took close to 30 minutes to get it . Since it was taking a while to get it I ordered some drinks for some friends ; they ordered drinks from Starbucks Xmas collection and I was not given them on the reusable red cups, although I saw they still had a stack of them since it was still around 11 am . I didn't think much of it until I was later told by friends that with those cups you could get discounts until January. Seems like the staff were overwhelmed and did not care about their customers.

They closed earlier than the time posted with all the lights on and I was sitting there waiting in the drive thru. The workers couldn't even bother to say they were closed which would take less than 30 seconds.
Great Starbucks location. Wildly efficient drive-thru. Nice staff - very friendly and helpful.
Awesome and quick service! Whoever Snow White was on Halloween was so friendly and had lots of jokes! Store was clean and food was great

I give 5 Star's Batista's are absolutely awesome and extremely Fast! They make me smile every time! ya ya ya the parking lot is awful cares as long as your safe! put on your smiley faces and enjoy that coffee :)

this starbucks does not support or care for any philidelphia police officers, fire fighters, and emt's.  boycott the business, any coffee establishment that will not alow a police officer to use its bathroom does not belong in business.  what happened to simple life respect for others?  go to dunkin donuts.... the coffee is better anyway!!  http://www.nj.com/news/index.ssf/2015/09/starbucks_employee_to_police_officer_you_cant_use.html#incart_river

Love this location! The employees are consistently friendly and helpful! Puts a smile on my face.   The location is newer and very clean. They have some board games, which is nice. I love coming to this location!
Just burnt the roof of my mouth with a latte that I ordered with the modifier "Kids Temp", which should mean less hot than normal temperature...imagine if I had given it to a child...

I love this Starbucks location and staff! They are always happy to take my order and have genuine smiles for customers. No matter how I want my drink they listen and have great suggestions when I want to try something new. The store is clean with a nice atmosphere inside to sit and enjoy.

This is the worst Starbucks location I have ever been to and unfortunately this is the closest one to my house. I have been to Starbucks locations all around the country and even around the world, yet still nothing compares to the horrible service at this location. The staff is very friendly but you will be in there for 20 minutes waiting for your order only to be given the completely wrong order. It is consistently bad.

Called at 5:59 a.m. to order box of coffee to go along with pastries for a board meeting. (I actually was only going to order the coffee but the associate asked if I wanted to add pastries--YES!) My order was taken by a pleasant and helpful young woman named Emily. It took all of two minutes. Awesome!! The Dunkin Donuts across the road won't even answer its phone or return calls--so it's Starbucks from now on.

When we ordered there were 10 people ahead of us so it took 20 minutes to get our coffee.  we ordered iced coffee; I asked for Splenda but they only had stevia, then I had to ask to get some napkins.  The coffee was more bitter than usual, even with an extra stevia. 10 minutes after we got our coffee, the came around to say that they would be closing in ten minutes (3:00PM),  we sat outside at a table which was sticky. As we were leaving we noticed that there was a lot of small trash in the parking lot.  This place should be closed until they can stay open and  Maintain their parking lot.
This is not a Starbucks.  If this were a Starbucks, then I could pay for my coffee with my Starbucks stars.  As I can't, it's rather a waste of time and effort, and false advertising.

I was in the store earlier today & things were busier than usual. A couple customers were being extremely rude to the employees, but they still kept smiles on their faces. Ashley & Andy were especially helpful, despite the stressful situation. My coffee was good, as usual.

Went here this morning and i'm 99% sure the main girl was drunk. Also my salted Caramel mocha was not sweet at all. Will not be revisiting this Starbucks! Stickin to my main Albertsons starbucks, for the win!
Very straightforward and fine service, traditional mediocre Starbucks food. Kind of observationally dirty and unkempt.

Awful customer service. If you don't fit there mold they treat you like crap.  Avoid this location. And I now will avoid Starbucks all together. Go to Dutch bros or somewhere they treat there customers like people.  It's no wonder they are in the news all the time for judging and profiling people. It's not like there coffee is all that good either, just convenient. Don't bother.

Great shop. No issue there.  2 stars and can attest to the other reviews.   If you would like to damage your car in the tiny parking lot or drive over a traffic sign that has been "laying down" in the middle of the only entrance or exit for weeks while braving the tight corners as traffic enters/exit, highly recommend. One $180 tire later...won't be back.

Absolutely the worst Starbucks in town ! I have never seen such rude baristas in the 18 plus years of going Starbucks ... which we visit on a daily basis! While waiting for our drinks, two customers complained about their drinks being wrong and the three idiots working there acted like it was the customers' fault and were EXTREMELY rude to them ! We waited for over 15 minutes for two lattes to be made while the three idiots talked. Do yourself a favor and drive 1/2 mile to the location on Damonte !!
Open early even on weekends. Friendly, big, clean. Has a drive through and ample parking. The music is good. Outdoor seating too. A prime example of what's good about the chain.

Manager Ariel has very poor customer service skills. I have never felt like a situation was so poorly handled as it was at this location with this manager. She had a chance to make things right after the baristas made a huge mistake with my order, but chose to offer nothing to make things right and keep a customer happy. Not only that, but she also didn't apologize for the error or huge waste of time. In fact, the whole staff was completely nonchalant about the mistakes they made. This store is a regular stop because of the location, but after the way the situation was handled I will make efforts to go elsewhere.

They have a secret spot upstairs to read away from the hustle and bustle of State Street. I know it's not baked here but the pumpkin bread tastes like it just came out of the oven.   The people here are wonderful. They just seem to go out of their way to make sure you are happy. Can it be they slip something in the coffee?
Hands down the best!!! Love walking in there!! Today they went above and beyond and even left this sweet message which made my day!!!

Most Starbucks are the same however the folks at this location are VERY nice.   Generally the store is clean and so are the bathrooms.   This is for sure one of the better Starbucks locations in Nashville.   I am always amazed by the number of people on their laptops at almost every Starbucks store go into. Every coffee shop for that matter. LOL

Parking can be problematic, but the staff is really nice. They are also really creative--I love the displays they make out of cups and other found things. Lots of space here, and nice people. The service is usually pretty quick. Busy corner.

This is one of the best Starbucks I have ever been to in my life. I recently went back to school for nursing - I come here almost everyday when not in school or clinical to get work done. The staff is amazing - and never irritated with me being there so much  the seating arrangements of this Starbucks create such a wonderful work environment- it's my favorite in the area. Plenty of individual and group space space. Large seating arrangements for one or a few. The rest could all take tips on their furniture layout! It's a key note  on this Starbucks .. I get ALL the coffee beverages, and they always come out amazing! Thank you all so much !   It's Andrew

Was my first time at this Starbucks my wife's favorite coffee place since the others are closed she came to this one was nice fast friendly and order was correct then we went to another place that was open now just so you all know lol I prefer coffee bean
If your moving like I am right now then there's no place on earth to chill with the free WiFi then this Starbucks.   It's big, clean, nice, and awesome. Did I mention the free WiFi.

I'm a regular at this location because of the Ina construction but now that you can cross the freeway I suggest going to the location in front of Target. By far the SLOWEST service ever. Every time I go it's 20-30 minute wait no matter if they're busy or not. The drive thru is the absolute worst. Went inside to see if the wait was better and ended up standing there for 25 minutes. What a joke it is to have 5 people working the line but having 1-2 drinks leaving the counter every 7-10 minutes because employees are too busy talking amongst each other.  Seriously, drive to a different location. It's worth the extra drive.

The line is always sooooo long and all i see is one person at the register. It's like this every time i come through. It's 3:44pm on a Monday. Please hire more employees. It's always always always an issue

its my local Starbucks :) Love it because its pretty close to the house. I go in for my passion iced tea, unsweetened :)  Friendly staff, its clean :) Its a small one, but I think most of them are. Nice place. I like it. Its a typical Starbucks, but this staff is friendlier than most :)

This is the Starbucks that took over the well-known Flying Saucer building on Grand, near the SLU campus. It used to be a gas station, then a Del Taco. They were going to demolish it and then Starbucks came in.   You'd think the Starbucks would be huge but it's only half of the building. There are 2 community tables, some counter space, some random chairs around the sides, and only 2 small private tables.   Lots of light from the huge windows all around the building. Full of students.

I'm a frequent visitor of this store. Every time I go. An employee named Nora exceeds my expectations. She has a so much personality when she is interacting with me..I'm sure other patrons feel the same about her. My visit doesn't feel like just another transaction.

I love this Starbucks at Tampa Premium Outlets! They are so cheery and nice. They are open a lot longer than the usual outlet hours from 7:30AM to 10PM.   This last visit I got their seasonal Caramel Brûlée latte which whip cream! It's so good, & it's just like a dessert to me.  They have a nice outside patio too for some perfect Fall time weather which we have right now.

Supervisors: Kathy, Stephanie, Josh, Chris, Ashlie Staff: Iszel, Katrina, Blake, Bridget, Jesse, Kellie, Jordan, Monique, Austen, Dakota, Jeo and Mars.   This Starbucks location has truly become a home away from home!!~! I have been a loyal customer on and off for the past 3 years, and each and every associate has completely welcomed my hubby and myself into their store with such a friendly attitude. They facilitate any and all questions while assisting every order with grace and patience. They certainly have  that "je ne sais quoi" that keeps you coming back for more! We feel like valued customers and handled with superior care.   Kathy, Stephanie, Josh, Chris, the current Supervisors, all sustain superior leadership. A staff is only as good as the leadership provided; they are exceptional leaders.  All staff members are so informative about food items and their prices based on our caffeine needs. They also offer their expertise and suggestions for new coffee items/seasonal products.  This store is utterly impeccable! Each associate provides a service containing a higher standard of excellence, "wowing and delighting" us with their professionalism and knowledge. They are TRUE ambassadors for the Starbucks Brand (which we take quite seriously since we used to live  in Seattle). Because of them, this location is my FAVORITE Starbucks in the Sparks, Spanish Springs, Reno, and Carson City areas. They have retained a loyal customer in us and we look forward to see them with every impending visit!  Thank you Sparks Starbucks on Pyramid/McCarren for providing such wonderful customer experiences each and every visit!

Awesomeness! Another Southside Starbucks near Costco. Unfortunately you have to drive into the Tucson Marketplace to a little strip mall on the Left side near the Costco, but it has a long drive thru, large interior and exterior seating area. It is Brand new.    Here's a tip: When you drive into the Marketplace you can see the back of the strip mall and see how long the line is so you can decide to park or do the drive through.  When you pull in the Starbucks is on the Left side of the strip, but the drive thru is on the far Right side.  I do like the large interior and exterior, some Starbucks can be so cramped, the parking is sufficient as well.  Since it is NEW, the lines are getting long in the morning.  So Read my TIP above to get in quick.

Josh made my experience great such a friendly upbeat person in your business goes a long way I got the most amazing caramel macchiato with extra caramel and extra vanilla today and it was made with love will definitely be back thanks for a  wonderful experience ps thanks to whoever made the coffee the coffee was sooo great

Pretty much your typical Starbucks. Nice outdoor seating area. They get my order right and are quick.  It can get quite busy when the school kids all storm in the store at once.  If the weather is good, venture down to Wydown Park just a block east on Wydown. Its usually very quiet and has a lot of nice benches.

This has got to be the worst Starbucks ever! They are slow and don't make the coffee right, I asked for two pumps of vanilla syrup and they gave me one and charges me for three extra pumps! They have also "accidentally" charged me for two mocha lattes and a strawberry lemonade all on the same order either get yourself a new drive thru speaker or get some new employees because this Starbucks sucks. And before I get hate I do realize I was going through the drive thru and that it could be different if I were inside but wait I did go inside and I have never seen rider employees they gave me dirty looks when I was on a coffee run for my employer and just seemed ver annoyed with me because I ordered 3 coffees and 3 pastries. All in all I don't like this Starbucks and will never be going back.

The fact alone that I'm taking time to review this Starbucks speaks volumes. As any seasoned Starbucks patron can attest to, each Starbucks has its own culture, if you will, and climate. this lively, upbeat location is staffed with awesome individuals who really go the extra distance to make your drinks and experience pleasant. This is not the standard at every Starbucks I have been to and I appreciate the climate of this one being so positive.  Although normally bustling, the staff never falls short of providing  exceptional service with warm affect. The store itself is well lit, with industrial styled high ceilings and plenty of indoor and outdoor seating. Despite being busy, there is usually a chair or table available for those individuals that like to work or study in a public atmosphere. The music selection is always great, and kept at a reasonable level. There is a broad variety of individuals who patronize this location so people watching here is stellar, if that's your thing. I enjoy sitting outside for some fresh air and sunshine and there are even outlets outside for your electronic devises. I also occasionally bring my pup, as they are dog friendly. The store itself is usually kept rather cool (great AC) so bring a sweater/sweatshirt/jacket if you get chilled easily and expect to spend some time here. The ample parking lot is in the back and at the side of the store and there are handicapped spots available near the front door. On the times I've used them I found the bathrooms are always clean and stocked.  On my particular visit today I was greeted with a smile and other pleasantries.  I decided to try the cold brew coffee and I was not disappointed. On the website it claims that the coffee is brewed over 20 hours, resulting in a smooth taste. I definitely recommend. The staff is efficient and makes their orders rather quickly so if you need to grab a drink in a hurry this should be your "go to" spot.    Thank you, Soho Starbucks, for consistently exceeding my expectations. Kudos to you.

Great coffee. Great service. But I just overheard two of the baristas talking about getting the booster shot and the one making my coffee said he hadn't gotten any of the shots. Starbucks isnt requiring vaccines or covid tests of its employees until February 9. I guess I'll have to wait until then for my next latte. I can't believe how many coffees could be responsible for passing on the virus.

I live closest to this Starbucks and my gym is also in the vicinity.  I run miles and miles around South Reno and before I typically walk home, I usually stop in and ask for a large ice water.  They get it for me without a problem and they're always so nice.  It's free, tasty, clear, cold water.  Dunkin' Donuts once asked me for 50 cents, pshh.  Bucks is where it's at.

The employees of this Starbucks work for the supermarket in which Starbucks is located.  They have always made my drinks correctly.  When I came here with children, the employees were very nice and made the kids feel very welcome. Lots of free samples for the kids. Also, the employees took the time to talk to the kids and act interested.  That's what I want in a coffee shop. Good service and friendly employees. I noticed a comment from Felicia about an incorrect order. This is obviously a minor communication error. Was the mistake that bad that you needed to just award a single star?  Perhaps Felicia was not clear. Sorry, but I question the communication skills of someone who doesn't distinguish between "their" and "there."

Just left Starbucks drive thru without my order.  Stopped off while on lunch break had two cars in front of me.  I ordered a latte and moved up behind the next vehicle in line.  The first car immediately pulled out and the car in front of me pulled up to the window.  The window person bumped fist with the driver of that car as he leaned out of the store window.  I waited thinking this was going to go fast just like the first car.  After a few minutes the server left the window then came back to the window and gave the customer his drink then bumped fist again.  They continued talking for over 8 minutes when the car finally moved on.  I told the server that it took over 8 minutes worth of conversation until the vehicle it left.  There were three cars waiting behind me.  He said," I wasn't going to charge you for the drink, he was a friend".  I told him to keep the drink as it wasn't fresh and I would not be back. I called the manager who apologized saying they were old friends and it wouldn't happen again.    This location is a hit and miss go to Frothy Monkey in downtown Franklin.
Stopped by the other day for mothers day and I must say that their Valencia orange refreshers are great

This will be the last time I come here. The newly-remodeled restaurant is understaffed as usual. It should not take 20 minutes to get a coffee. I've also had issues with the quality of the food and drink here, the staff, and incorrect orders. This place needs better management.

This was the only time I have ever felt like walking out of a Starbucks in my life. The employee was a shorter woman of asian descent around 30 years old and had a horrible attitude. Nasty, no smile, and slamming things down as she put together orders. My advice to the manager is to assess your staff and get some pleasant baristas. To top it off while I was trying to get work done, she was walking around slamming things down as she cleaned as well. Horrible attitude. I will be sure never to return to this location.
Often very backed up and sold out of tons of stuff. I recommend a different location. Twice I ordered a latte and they didn't put in the espresso, it was just milk and sweetener. Twice!!

I stopped by on a Sunday morning, and the drive thru line was super long, so I went inside. The line was shorter, but it was moving SO slowly. I probably waited almost half an hour to get to the front of the line, and they took another 10 minutes or so to make my drink, which was pretty basic. It seemed like this location was severely understaffed. Unless I don't have another option, I probably won't come here again.

love the personal treatment received by each barista. wasn't a huge fan of the male manager/register. not sure of his title. all window staff at drive thru are awesome! I look forward to seeing Nicholas.  he's a crack up. makes me laugh. couple of the girls are super bubbly and fun too. love this location as my favorite drive thru
Love this Starbucks! Very nice staff and they're pretty fast! It can get busy on weekends with all the bike riders and people out and about but this crew can get the job done.

Idk what's wrong with this location. Either the workers are too lazy to make the right order or they simply didn't have enough training to make a decent tasting coffee. Every time I get an iced coffee or frappuccino it tastes so watered down and bitter. I couldn't drink my iced coffee last time bc they forgot to put the caramel syrup that I asked for so it tasted so bitter. They just always seem to forget something in my order that I paid extra for and I always go back to see if it'd be different another time but no it NEVER tastes right.
Great service. Friendly service. Clean operation. Yes, you can feel the midwest oozing from this place and it is all good.

Only me second time in this location.  We had to wait a little bit before somebody asked for our order, but it was a little busy, so it was understandable.   The two grande mochas were made to perfection as was the strawberry refresher.

Starbucks as far as drinks go is... Well.... Starbucks. They're always exactly the same. I know what to expect and that's what I get. So that's the reason for the solid three stars. What baffles me about this location is that I have been going there consistently since I moved to this side of town about three years ago. And by consistently, I mean at least three times a week, every single week. They still do not know my name. Every time I go in, they act like its the first time they have ever seen me. They're never rude, but they're not really that nice and even when they're nice- they definitely don't recognize me. The QT employees next door recognize me more and they have only been seeing me for about 4 months... Step it up Starbucks. Please.

After visiting the Starbucks at Ina near Oracle, I'm happy to say this is definitely our favorite.   It's clean, my arms don't stick to the tables, I don't have to lay down a placemat of napkins and the garbage is never overflowing. Most of the staff are great!  Some of the newer baristas need to get a rhythm going when taking care of customers.
Our fav. weekly "hang out" place to wait for an hour when my daughters have piano lesson! Nice store, well kept & nice staff! We'll be back next week!:))

Great Starbucks! Good location and plus there is a shared parking lot behind it so you don't have to worry about parking. The parking lot does get full if you go during the prime going out hours like Friday and Saturday nights. But that's the same for all the parking lots on Howard during those hours.  This Starbucks has the typical selection of food and drinks. There's plenty of seating inside if you don't mind sitting at the long table or the bar-like counter that faces the outside. There are several comfy leather seats and also several smaller tables. As with any Starbucks, there's always people in there working on their laptops.   They do have lots of seating outside. They have tables that face Howard ave and also a couple of tables tucked on the side that faces 717 South (which is more quiet than the street noise).

I got two large Venti Caramel Crunch Frappuccino's and as i was drinking it I realized i was tasting mostly whipped cream so i take a closer look and there was whipped cream filling my entire drink. I love Starbucks but I don't want to be just drinking Whipped cream i paid $14 for caramel flavored whipped cream. I would reccomend getting an iced drink which is just as good but not full of whipped cream
