Not nearly as good as the service and atmosphere of the one in the middle of Doylestown - manager at Warrington never seems to be happy to see customers and frankly seems like she's in a bad mood all of the time.

Another great Starbucks.  Although they are a chain I simply don't think you can go wrong.  This one has some outdoor seats as well as a fire place and some comfy seats inside!  I would return to this location.

I try my hardest not to come to this location. It's just plain terrible. My most recent experience was 2 weeks ago. I came in placed my order. There was only about 2-3 people in front of me. After me was a stampede of people. I ended up waiting for 20 min for my order. I understand they were busy. I saw the people that came before me got their drinks and left already.  I finally came up to the counter and asked about my one measly grande white chocolate mocha and the worker said "well we have a lot of orders that just came in"  I'm thinking "and??" I think they finally realized they forgot my order because less than 5 minutes they called my name. Other times, the drinks would be made ok. Like when the cloud macchiato came out, they wouldn't make it as well as the other place.  Not recommending this location at all until I have a better experience otherwise.

This has always been my absolute favorite Starbucks, but the last few times I've been here they've been EXTREMELY SLOW!!!!  I even tried using the mobile app and Still have to wait!!!  Today, they told me they never received my mobile order!!!  Funny, that's never ever happened before.  Last time they said staffing was bad...whatever!!!!  I thought Starbucks strove to be the best of everything in customer service and drinks!  Also, last time they prepared my drink on top of everything else and she gave me a $3.00 coupon off for next time, of course my drink was over $7.00 but who cares! Would someone let me know if there is a better option on the south side of Indy???

This Starbucks is my absolute favorite in town. I love the layout and large windows, making this coffee shop always bright and open. They sport a drive-thru and were crazy quick with my online order (even though I live 5 minutes away), so really great if social interaction makes you cringe like me. The only thing for me is that one of their male employees is always a little TOO cranky for me; I'm sure he's already had his morning coffee unlike me. Oh! And they have doggie treats!
I stop by this location on the way to work almost every day. I order my favorite drink, iced caramel macchiato, and it's always made right! This particular location has extremely nice staff.
Trying out the new Apple pie Mach, the flavor was delicious!!! Tasted apple with a hint of cinnamon and espresso! I love this little starbies it's usually quiet and the service is always good
Service here is always soooooo slow. A bunch of lazy kids standing around doing nothing.

I understand everyone is busy for the holiday but get it together Starbucks! I'm sorry I don't have a fancy app with money stored on it to pay for my drink like all the other 9 people in line, but when I give you a $10 bill for my $5.35 charge, I don't expect over $2 in change in quarters and other coins. You should probably go check if you have some more ones

Generally terrible customer service. Unfortunately I think the location is what keeps them in business - close proximity to 295 for travelers. I'm guilty of stopping there during travels to PA because of the location and every time I kick myself - mediocre beverages and food that's either sold out or never heated all the way -- certainly not a good representation of the Starbucks brand and consistency.
This newly remodeled Starbucks is great. The barista's are always very pleasant and the coffee taste great. It's kind of expensive but it's Starbucks right.

This location is pretty awful compared to other Starbucks locations. Almost all of the employees are rude and impatient, except for a few. Today I heard one of the employees complaining about a customer who was ordering and said "she should just go to a gas station if she wants regular coffee!".  No one should be made to feel unwelcome in a public store. On top of their terrible customer service, the drinks are often incorrect or only half full. It's the only convenient location by my work but I've decided it's just not worth it anymore. It's not worth paying $5 for a half empty drink, especially when they're being rude on top of it.

I go here a lot because it is so close to my house and right next to Trader Joe's which I frequent but every time it is so crowded - with a line around the building for the drive thru and a line inside as well. The parking situation is terrible. You basically can't get to their parking because of the long drive thru lines so I park in the main parking lot but you have to walk across the drive thru line to get into the store (they've now put fencing up so you have to walk around). At least half the time I am in there they either completely forget to make my drink or make me a frappuchino instead of a latte (seriously why has this happened on more than one occasion?? Latte sounds nothing like frappuchino). *Sigh* but i'll still keep coming here because #imlazy and a #coffeeaddict. Positive notes? The coffee is good when I get it, sometimes I get a free drink out of them messing up my order because otherwise they just throw it away and they offer my pup a free puppuccino usually!
Slowest service on the planet. Only 3 people on the line and had to wait over 10 minutes. I left the place without any coffee and nobody even cared. Incompetent employees. Worst experience ever.

Super slow service on a Wednesday morning at 8:15 am. One lady just plain left, but I stuck it out and got my $4.50 iced half-caff latte.    The good news? Close to my office. The bad news?  Don't come here if you are in a hurry.   Clean restrooms.

I came in to this Starbucks and their mobile ordering had gone down, so there were a handful of angry patrons. The staff here handled the rude crew with grace and patience, and kicked into high gear to get everyone taken care of. Not every patron was grateful for their hard work, but I hope the staff knows that it didn't go unnoticed. It's almost like people get crabby without their coffee! :)

I visited this shop a number of times during my hotel stay for a conference. I was very pleases with the fast service. On one visit I was given an extra syrup shot for free. Nice touch. There is a small but comfortable seating area with couches. The only thing I did not like was the design of the store positioned the pickup bar in a corner of the store which causes a bit of a traffic jam during busy times.

It works for moms to bring their kids to, but it isn't there for any man to do anything but be of service at. No self service coffee stations, and door codes at the bathrooms make this a rather exclusive to the mom-with-child kind of setup.
Unfriendly.  Forgot part of order. Half full drinks. Asked for extra hot - didn't get that either.

I love almost everything about this Starbucks. The layout and space available is conducive to studying and/or meeting with others. Service and employees are always delightful. There is ample parking. There is really one sole reason for this 2-star review, their wifi connection is always HORRIBLE!!!!! My music stream is constantly cutoff and My I internet connection is lost. Every other time  I conduct an internet search I get an error message that my connection is lost. It is incredibly frustrating and every time I return to this location I hope it's fixed, but I'm batting 100% here - 5/5 times I've come, this has been the experience.
Always pleasant folks here.  Nice place to come and do some work if you don't work in a typical office environment.   Next to shopping/eating area as well.

Worst Starbucks I've ever been to!  They can't seem to get an order right!  I am a regular (I know, stop going if it's so bad) since it is the closest Starbucks to my job.  At least 50% of the time my order is made wrong.  It's not a hard order - Peach Green Team Lemonade.  It's on the menu.  Maybe they should hire some people who can read what the order actually is!
Certain baristas consistently place my coffee on the counter edge nearest to them and furthest from me. I'm barely five feet tall; I'd like to not have to climb across to retrieve my caffeine.

This Starbucks is awesome!! It is the most beautiful Starbucks that I have ever been in. However, when I told them that, they humbly told me several locations worldwide that I need to visit  to really be blown away.   They are incredibly efficient! They need to be. It is a VERY busy location. You should order ahead on your Starbucks app and walk in to pick up your order. Otherwise it could take you a considerable time to go through the drive through. You may have to park in the Freddy's parking lot because often their lot is full in addition to 10-15 cars waiting in the drive through line. This should tell you how amazing this  'Bucks rocks it. Julia is always amazing and my wife and I are looking forward to getting to know the rest of the all star cast as we are hopeless addicts to the best coffee house in the country. KEEP ROCKING WEST CHESTER!!

It's right off the casino floor and there has been a line every time I've tried to get coffee but the seating area  is mostly lounge chairs so it's really comfortable to work. It's also nice there are lots of plugs. It's pretty noisy with the slot machine noise. The coffee is a lot higher than a free standing store

This is one of the best Starbucks I've been to in a long time. The food comes out hot and fresh and the drinks are made by expert baristas. The speed is good for the area they're located in and it's amazing!!!!

Reading other reviewer's reviews on this place confirms my experience tonight, that the service has gone downhill at this particular location. I have been a Starbucks customer for over a decade and all over the country (and a few places overseas) and have NEVER been treated so rudely by an employee. I wasn't watching the clock (though clearly they were) and was yelled at that "Sir, its 10 o'clock and I'm locking the door!" Really? Was that a threat to lock me in or a euphemism for "get the F*&^ out." I'll go with both. I worked as a barista at Sbucks years ago and they trained us to professional and courteous and quick to please, not treat customers like mangy dogs. The staff there needs to 1. Do a better job at hiring employees who care about thier customer service and 2. Do a better job at training to be customer service orientated. The higher ups at Starbucks went through a lot of trouble to train and maintain high quality standards years ago because sales were dropping, and history is going to repeat itself if this crap keeps up.
Avoid during the mornings.  Wait time in drive-thru typically is about 25-30mins.  Either it's the only Starbucks in a 15 mile radius, which isn't, and super busy or it's super slow.
This Starbucks is suddenly the best part of my morning. Clean and friendly, too.  Heard they went through changes, and whatever they did made this like a Seattle store.

I went through the drive thru just after 2 pm on Wednesday (12/14) afternoon. I ordered a tall chile mocha. While I was sitting at the window for about 3-4 minutes I heard the lady at the window comment to the barista that there might be an issue with the chile powder. After an eternity she opens the window tells me it's $4.65. I hand her $5.65 and she hands me my drink. After 30 seconds she opens the window again and tells me I didn't give her proper change. I mentioned that I handed her $5.65 and that one or more of the coins are now sitting on the ground between the car and the drive thru because she dropped them on the way in. Another 60 seconds later she hands me $1 and makes a sarcastic comment about how annoying the customers have been today. I drive off. Guess what? No chile powder. Chile Mocha indeed!

The inside is just WAY too small.  You walk to nearly the back to place your order, then step a few feet to the very end to wait for your drink.  Then you have to walk all the way back through the store to get out.  So you have to excuse your way through the people placing their orders, and the people waiting in line.  And with it being so narrow it's a pain.  They desperately need a larger space.  I won't be back.  Maybe I'll use the drive-thru but I won't go in.  I'll visit other locations instead.

What the hell, Starbucks?   Lately, I've begun going to Second Cup more often. Not because I like their coffee, but because they're quieter. I used to like this Starbucks a lot. No more.   I went in here yesterday. I just wanted to sit and relax and write. I usually have my earphones with me, but had forgotten to throw them in my bag.   Lately, it seems like every Starbucks I go in has one barista who has to be SUPER SOCIAL, the loudest, most obnoxious person in the entire joint. Yelling to be heard over the coffee machine? They're not that loud. Everyone in the entire place DOES NOT NEED TO YOUR HEAR YOUR EVERY WORD. Customers do not need to appreciate all conversations between baristas!   It's going to be a LONG time before I come back. And that's sad. I used to like this place a lot.

We are going on over 40 minute wait in the drive thru for a drip coffee. This is the slowest store and the least busy I've been to. We are finally at the window and it took 8 minutes to get our drip coffee. We asked if we were still being charged after the exorbitant wait and the employee, Lea, as polite as could be said "absolutely". We then proceeded to ask for the district manager card only to have the manager come over and apologize. Really?! I worked at Starbucks all though college and it was never an issue to keep our customers happy and issue a drink comp. apparently the policy has changed? My father that is terminally ill and at the near VA hospital asked that we bring him a Starbucks drip coffee. It took over an hour for his coffee to come to him with no sincere apology or try to remedy the situation. Management, do you read the yelp reviews? This location has a problem and needs to be addressed. This is not a busy drive thru it should not have taken more than 5-10 minutes. We are utterly disappointed and upset that an outing away from my father's bedside turned into an ordeal.

Hello. It's Starbucks. What else do you need to know?  This one does have more space than the average and a nice outdoor seating patio.   Otherwise, order your normal caramel nowhip crapuccino and enjoy!

This is the second time I have been in this city at this location and had to walk out.  The staff is more concerned with sweeping floors, stocking shelves and everything else but servicing the client.  I waited 10mins with only one person in front of me and no one ever took either persons order.  I will never stop at this location again.

There was a single drink order ahead of my single drink order. They promptly cleared that and spent the following 15 minutes ignoring my order, and serving other, later customers. When I inquired about the status of my drink, I was thrown shade.   I'd had enough, and decided to get a refund, and go. But wait! They *just* made the drink, and tried to shame me into taking it. Really. You made me late, and I have to go.  On the way out the door--"HAVE A NICE NIGHT!" Real loud, very immature; you embarrassed yourselves.

Out of all of the Starbucks in the downtown Indianapolis area, this by far is my favorite place to come and meet up friends, get some work done and just to hang out. It is well kept, has friendly baristas and there's not too much of a hangout spot for panhandlers.

The staff here had gotten pretty consistent; there was a huge turnover rate but the workers have been constant factor so that's a big plus. The manager here seems really really invested in what she does and I love that passion. Even though Starbucks is a corporation, I can see that the manager here really likes what she does and she seems pretty well educated on coffee (I've seen her so seminars and stuff). The biggest thing I have against this place is they run out of things which is fine if it's towards the end but, it's been pretty frequent that they will run out of things in the menu by mid-noon.   I understand that coffee is popular but that being said... Once or twice I understand but this many times this early?   Overall though, Horsham location wasn't the best and I preferred to drive out to other place even though this place is the closest to me. I like it now; staff is good, coffee is ok (aside from what I mentioned).

This is a really nice Starbucks. Get service, always good coffee. The only reason I gave it a four star is because of its location and drive through, it is slow moving and I've had to wait in the parking lot not the drive through lane until It starts moving. But over all it is a very good Starbucks.

This place really went downhill. They are messing the Starbucks reputation up. Just expect to wait at least 10 minutes if there's at least two cars in your line drive thru. They have zero emphasis on speed.

At first glance, and being an out-of-towner, I couldn't tell that this location had a drive-thru. It's situated behind the location in what looked like the Bank parking lot. It was very busy and the line slow, but the excellent service and my drink (recommendation of the cashier) being so good made up for that.
Grant and his crew are awesome. The only things are the lot sucks and some of the customer who come in are rude. I used to work in the building when it was Roosevelt Bank. Always good service

Horrible service--was completely ignored when I tried to place my order while the cashier browsed her phone. Then was ignored a second time asking a question while the baristas were engaged in conversation. Generally, they gave the impression that they didn't want to be there.

Computer isn't able to properly process payments through the app. Don't ever have problems using my Starbucks app but came here two days in a row and suddenly I'm having those issues. Don't have problems at Elmwood location or at a Covington location in which I frequent OFTEN. Won't be back.

This sleek modern architectural design of a Starbucks building, invites you to pull on in.  And if the Starbucks itself doesn't pull you in, then the connecting Chipotle may!   Yes, I'm a sucker for some Starbucks and Chipotle.  I don't think I've ever consumed the two in one sitting, but I'm open to the idea now.  Neighboring the Saint Louis University campus area, this Starbucks can become very busy at certain hours of the day.  Students flocking in for their caffeine fix and plopping down with their Mac Books.  That being said, I wouldn't count on every visit being a breeze.  You may end up waiting a little longer than had you choose a different location.    There's not a vast amount of parking available for the Starbucks in particular.  In fact, there's barely any.  You may need to end up parking in one of the neighboring lots, and turn an eye to the signs informing you not to park there.  Hey, sometimes the rules need to be broken in the name of a latte.  Luckily, there is also the drive thru option available at this location, which nicely wraps around the building.  *Drink of the moment: Iced Soy 2 Pump Sugar Free Cinnamon Dolce Latte.  Is that a mouth full, or what?  Yeah, a mouth full of deliciousness!

Hours suck. Close early. I get COVID but seriously? Never open, closes at random and out of everything. All other Starbucks are not like this one. Should just go out of business completely. Clearly the employees don't want to work and when they do they obviously don't want to be there anyways. So over Starbucks and it's inconsistent hours

Go here frequently and the baristas are always very nice and friendly. Drinks are good also, one if the very few Starbucks I have found that add the chocolate shavings to the peppermint mocha (and I have been to numerous ones in other states) so that alone deserves 5 stars!!

Just a convenient place to get "STARBUCKS" because its just there!  It's a good and bad thing to this, just like any other Starbucks, some just don't have the same consistency to drinks.  I ordered a drip coffee and it seem like its been sitting a while like some other people said in the reviews.  I asked if they changed the coffee and they told me yes we have and it just tasted off a bit.  The lines can get long sometimes especially in the morning, afternoons and night time.  Overall its Starbucks, it'll do its job, sometimes might taste slighty off and just ask them to remake it.  *parking*  outside of GSR hotel casino & resort  cash / credit
Love Starbucks!  Must say that the some of the Baristas at the location near Loft need to learn their menu & pay attention!

Order was placed at 3:06P via mobile suppose to be ready at 3:12P didn't get served until 3:25P after I arrived at 3:10P waited and watched 6 people get served in front of me!!!! Called after I left talk to manger he apologized and let me know they were short staff.

I have equally come to this location and the location further up 4th Street closer to Gandy. This location seems less attentive. Johnnie seems like a good worker today, but he started my drink and turned around and started taking orders without finishing it and I stood there and waited for my online order. I don't care what the sign says and whether or not there's a bell there for online ordering, the employees should be more attentive to the customers. I think I will continue more of my business at the other location. Especially since the girl today sounded like she was trying not to have an attitude.

New Starbucks on the block. As it's pretty new it's still pretty clean and quick moving. Good amount of staff working today, quick service, clean counters etc. They have cold brew on tap if that's your thing.  There's a good amount of tables, but lots of people in and out so might not be the best place for you if you're looking for a quiet space to work.   Overall, pretty good as far as Starbucks goes.
No decaf, no sugar in the raw or syrup, trash cans over flowing at 11am.  Didnt call when order was ready.  Not impressed.

I was so exited the other day to get my morningvcoffee, but was beside myself when i saw a hastily written note saying temporarily closed AND with no explanation as to why!!! It leads me to question if a certain virus was involved.
Hi. Best Starbucks Ever. Forever and Always. Simply because of one thing.... COFFEE. And ONWARD. And Max B. For the McRussell clan of the Clan McRussell

Ordered a strawberry açaí refresher and received a cup of ice with a splash of refresher. Three swigs and it was gone. Normally I go out of my way to go to this location But I don't know that I will anymore.

Worst burnt tasting coffee I ever had in my life. Expensive is an understatement. I'm told you have to get one of the flavored coffees or some fancy drink and you'll like it. If you can't just get the base coffee right they shouldn't be selling it. Anyone can dump a bucket of sugar in something to make it taste like desert.

I'm not a frequent visitor to Starbucks but I don't mind this location when I'm in the mood for a drink.  I came with my coupon for a free drink to celebrate my birthday.  You can sign up online and they will mail you a card for a free drink with a 2 week window to use it.  It was a sunny day and I was craving a sweet drink.  I was glad to see no line up (this location seems to be a lot quieter than the one 2 blocks west) and I asked the barista what drink would be like a frozen hot chocolate.  I didn't catch the name of the drink but it sounded good so I ordered it with soy milk.  The only thing was, he should have asked what size I wanted instead of giving me the grande.  I probably wouldn't have been able to finish a venti though as it was a filling drink.  The barista who made my drink was friendly.  I enjoyed my drink as it wasn't too sweet but it had little pieces of what seems like chocolate bits.  I found it was very filling as well and I wasn't hungry for food after.  I wish I got the name of the drink, but I'm sure next time I can ask the same question and likely get the same drink.
Every time I go there and get serious strawberry they always have someone new and they always mess it up they need to just close down
Drinks were made really well, super convenient to stop for gas and grab a coffee. Mocha Frapp & Vanilla Latte on point!

Busy time at the airport. This location really needs an express line for beverages only. Sandwich orders take a lo-o-ooong time holding up the line. People have flights to catch.  Workers could use an incentive to speed things along.

I go to Starbucks every morning and get the same thing. There are 2 on my way to work and 2 on my way to class. I pass this one on both of those routes and have since had to force myself to NOT go to this Starbucks even though it is the closest. I spend at least 8-10 minutes in the drive thru WAITING each time and usually my coffee order isn't even right! I also always tip when there is a jar but why would I tip y'all if you can't ever get my order right and it becomes quicker for me to get out of line and go inside to get my stuff? I will not give this Starbucks another chance again and will drive to another or go to a better company of coffee shop.

Quick Tip: if you're a Starbucks connoisseur or have any desire for consistency in drink quality drive to Marsh Rd Starbucks!    I had a really disappointing service experience at this new Starbucks location on a Saturday around 11:30 . I was excited that Delaware had more options for Starbucks now! ( Marsh rd and 896 95 rest stop are only real alternatives) The store location looked great inside and out. I was informed later, however, that the money put into the appearance and not into hiring REAL baristas is because this is not a REAL Starbucks, privately owned and operated.    As soon as I received my drink I asked the Barista if it was foam free (as originally requested) she said it was. But when I picked it up the weight of the drink made it completely apparent that it was full of foam. I eventually posted the picture on twitter (below). When I asked the Barista to take out the foam I was shocked at her response! Wordless she grabbed the drink while I held the lid. She attempted to pour out the foam (which for obvious reasons did not work). She then scooped out the foam... then handed the half empty drink back to me. I asked that she just remake the drink. She once again surprised me, she just walked away! I couldn't believe it. Baristas have never been anything but kind and courteous even on their busiest days. I was the only one in the store, except for patrons sitting in the lounge (so it's not like I was monopolizing her time).   The manager on duty then came over to make my drink from scratch a few seconds later. Guys, I can't make this up. When she handed me the drink it was so full of foam I begrudgingly asked AGAIN to have the foam removed, but this time I was explicit. I asked the foam please be scooped out and more soy milk steamed to be poured to the top. She smiled and started the drink. But then as I feared, these Baristas truly did not know how to serve a "no foam soy latte". The manager's attempt was half full and still had foam somehow. (Picture included ) This is when I decided to post the pictures to twitter.   Then the manager proceeded to tell me I was wrong, when she saw me taking pictures, soy lattes "always produce foam" the manager told me. I drove 8 miles out of the way to go to a tried and true Starbucks where they made my drink. To add insult to injury the Baristas at the Riverfront Wilmington location didn't even offer me a refund WHEN I DIDN'T TAKE EITHER DRINK HOME.   This was so unbelievably uncharacteristic of Starbucks I was shaken by the first Baristas aggressive nature and refusal to help with my drink and the manager's lack of ability to correct the situation. I really appreciate the Starbucks Twitter team reaching out right away after seeing the pictures. But ultimately Starbucks corp doesn't own this Starbucks and can't correct their obvious shortcomings.

This is my least favorite Starbucks location of the dozen or so Starbuckses within a half mile radius of it.  They don't deal well with crowds, the bathroom is dismal, and they are often out of things.  When you have the tantalizing signs advertising my seasonal favorite gingerbread latte, you cannot be out of gingerbread syrup.  I have heard a rumor that their new manager hails from the dynamite Starbucks located at 10th and Chestnut.  I am hopeful this may to lead to some improvement, so I will give this one more chances and update as warranted.

hanging out in a coffee house/cafe to do some work while sipping a drink and possibly a snack?  Having to feed a meter the entire time is not the best plan.  This place you have to find a metered spot and then feed it.  Meters are expensive here.  The store is not as warm and inviting as many other of these and it's not as nice looking inside either.

Friendliest Starbucks staff ever here. The baristas were so sweet to my grandson today.   His "baby coffee" (aka hot cocoa) was done in a snap and served  with a smile.  He was delighted.  The drive through can be difficult which is why I took it down a star, but I do think that is in large part because it is located on the corner of a very busy street.   Inside...super quick.  Drive through...not so much.  If you're in a hurry, step inside  The drinks are consistent here as well which I have noticed isn't always the case with some other locations.  When I order my sugar-free vanilla latte...I know exactly what it's going to taste like.
The product is fine, but the drive thru can be exceptionally slow.  Fifteen minutes while watching people walk in, get served, while still sitting in the drive thru.

Awesome service, other than our drink longer than we expected (order mix up), and the barista was so apologetic and tried to make it up to us with a gift card for a beverage... It was nice and thoughtful.
Sad  all the dead plants on the patio. No more couches on the patio. Doesn't look like too many people frequent this location at least on a Saturday evening.

Came in at 4pm yesterday and they only had Pike Place brewing. When I asked with genuine astonishment if that was the only option the cashier said yes and did not offer a pour over option which every other Starbucks would offer if they choose to only brew one blend. Also imo Pike Place is the most generic blend you can offer cmon man.   If that was not enough for the one star review they did not have any cream or milk out at the self help station?? I went by the location several hours later when I was in the area and stopped in just in bewilderment to see if they do not put any half and half out or accompaniments and to my shock they were still not out! Cost saving measure in a Starbucks or are you hiding the cream and mixing options somwhere I am not looking?  That will be the last of my money this location gets and luckily I used a giftcard.

I've given this place so many chances, but the times I leave unsatisfied outweigh the times I leave happy. Every time I get my drink it seems to be made incorrectly or stingy on the amount. I went a few days ago and ordered a grande Starbucks double shot on ice. Didn't pay attention when I left. Turns it out was less than half way full. The majority was foam. I realize this is the case with those drinks but it's hard when you pay for a grande and I got less than a tall. Thought I would try this place one more time this morning. Ordered a venti double shot on ice today. Same thing, too much foam and made incorrectly. The drink usually comes with classic syrup to sweeten it up. No classic syrup and barely any skim milk. I love the staff,very friendly, but drinks are awful quality. We all know Starbucks is pricey and when you pay that much for a drink and it's made wrong or has half the amount in it, it's frustrating. I did have a few sips of the drink below, but it did fit in to my grande Starbucks mug. First bad review I've given. DRIVE THRU IS AWFUL. At 5:30 in the morning I shouldn't wait 18 minutes For a drink. Do not go here if you think you'll be in and out quickly. I could have ordered a dinner at restaurant and got my food quicker than I got my drink at the drive thru. And I was the second car in line.

I have literally been coming to Starbucks my entire life and I have never had such amazing service as I have had today. Jessica is so sweet and attentive, she set my morning up for success. Everyone else was super nice as well!

I rarely used Yelp but today was a must. The drive thru girl with the fake lashes was the rudest employee. I could sympathize with working the day after thanksgiving, but there's no need for an attitude with every question. After all that unnecessary hostility... she gets our order wrong. What an embarrassment, I hope the manager recognizes who they employ.

As packed as this location always is, being in the middle of a business district area, they move remarkably well. The service is as fast as it can be but if you have questions they take the time to answer them. They are kept immaculately clean and as busy as they always are there's always a seat to be found If you want to just take a moment and relax

I waited 11 minutes (after ordering) for an iced tea this morning and almost missed my train. For about 6 of those minutes, my ice tea was already "shaken" and left to sit in the container they make it in. Why could they not just throw it into a cup for me? Instead they prioritized the blending of 4 frappachinos, while the ice melted in my tea and became watery. Thanks starf***s. Unacceptable! They cannot handle the morning rush. If you are wise, you will go elsewhere. Try 19th and Chestnut - great service.

1/11/19 - VERY disappointed that they didn't have my favorite sandwich today, chicken and artichoke.   11/20/18 - So disappointed today. Not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on   Once again we are here. Our favorite place. Love our Baristas. Jessica, Treigh, Caleigh, Alex and Taylor. If I've forgotten anyone please forgive me. So many great people work at this location. :) And of co

I come to this Starbucks every day and they're always very nice. I work down the street so I place a mobile order and it's usually correct and everything is ok.  Tip: every other Starbucks has a sign or a bin that says "mobile order pickup" and they don't. After 2 months I still stand there for a while waiting or they hand me one thing and forget to give me the other so I wait for that.  Typically everything is great but since I order through mobile and leave with it, I have gotten back to my office to find that they didn't warm something or they gave me cream instead of coconut milk. I don't have the opportunity to say anything after I've left so I would just ask that you pay closer attention to each order. A bad coffee can ruin someone's day when they don't have time to take lunch.

I agree that the view probably wasn't an issue when they leased the building.  The store is ALWAYS CLEAN, and the Staff is ALWAYS FRIENDLY.  As a regular at this store, they remember not only my name, but my favorites.  I think this is why I see a lot of "regulars" in this store.  The atmosphere is warm and comfortable, and a lot of young people hang out there to use their computers/internet.

This Starbucks has the worst drive thru, doesn't matter what time of day the line is always wrapped around the parking lot. Seems no one inside cares. If you have the option go to another one until they change management or staff.
Great Starbucks visit. Made my Frappuccino perfectly perfect and my barista was friendly and cheery.  My order was ready when I arrived at he window.  A five star visit today.

I am very pleased with this Starbucks location!!! They've made my drinks to my perfection many times! Not only that...the staff here are super over the top friendly! When I'm at west county...I definitely have to stop here!

Welcomed me when I walked in. Parking is limited and right off of a busy street so don't park in convenient spots if you don't want your car to get scratched or bumped.   Bathroom is clean. Not a lot of seating. It's in a plaza so there is no drive-thru.   They have a lot of staff and seem to get the job done quick.   Popular location for plugging up for productivity, so get there quick!

The girls here and couple of male baristas are wonderful! Always super sweet about refills with my gold card while I shop and they are always patient with me when I forget to recharge my card before ordering! The girls are also so great about explaining new drinks and or replacing drinks if you're not 100% happy! Thanks for my delicious pink drink and tea today! Hopefully one day you will carry the deli sandwiches and other cold items they carry at the Starbucks stores!

Inconsistent drink quality and you all too often get TERRIBLE service. Most of the staff are far more concerned with their conversations amongst each other and with chatting up their friends who come in to mooch free drinks to serve the paying customers.   I can't adequately describe how consistently terrible the service is. Loosing orders, charging the wrong amount, having to repeat your order over and over and over again all while you are made to feel you are interrupting the staff's social hour is no way to run a business.  Until I hear rumors that there has been a massive change in the service, the TECH Center Starbucks is in indefinite time out with me. Starbucks HQ really needs to step in and deal with the poor quality seen at this Sodexo-run hell hole.

Super friendly staff and being from Philly I definitely love and appreciate that...but when you make my drink wrong and try to justify it, that just irritates me to no end. Vanessa clearly needs more training or to be pulled off the morning crew

Possibly one of the friendliest overall staffs of any Starbuck's I've visited! They make you feel like family if you are a regular and know many people by name. They are always smiling; no matter how busy. Manager is a friendly fellow named Aaron and is always ready to listen to any comments or questions. They've never gotten an order wrong. Lastly, I love the utilitarian vibe here!  Note: I didn't take off any stars for parking but like the other Edwardsville location, it can be tricky leaving. Remember that you can't get out by going around to the driver-thru entrance. You can get out by going back to entrance or through the hotel parking lot.

After waiting for nearly 10minutes for somebody to take my order. I then watched 4 people who ordered after me get their drinks. I ordered a simple latte. I spent a total of 32 minutes between waiting to order and receiving my drink. I'm beyond disappointed. However they did me a voucher for my next drink free.

They are taking Covid-19 protocols seriously. They added plexiglass by the cashiers and they are enforcing everyone to wear their mask properly. They opened up about 3 tables recently. Their bathrooms are still closed. The staff here is really friendly and they're all hard workers. When they are busy, everyone seemed very focus and organized. For how busy they are, they are very efficient. For the amount of people they have, they get the drinks out quickly. If you come here during the weekdays and if it's around 7am to 8am, I suggest to do a mobile order. Order 15 minutes in advance before you arrive, to ensure your drink is done when you get there. Sometimes they get slammed and there's usually a really long line in the drive thru. If you weren't aware, you can also add a tip on the app, by viewing your receipt. Select the dollar amount and click done. I didn't know this until recently. I think Starbucks should ask if you'd like to leave a tip before checking out. This way, it's visible to everyone.

This is the best Starbucks I've ever been to, and I've been to every one in pinellas county. I have been coming here almost daily since they've opened and I love every person that works here. They make the experience even more than just getting coffee. They call me by name, they know what I like, they all are so sweet and fun to be around... I've never had a bad experience here - ever! They are very efficient yet mindful and they always make my day!! Thank you, All!!!

This review is about this location, not Starbucks in general. I was motivated to write this review because they are the definition of "efficient" in the morning! I ordered by phone about a mile away and when I arrived, my drink was just ready. I pulled into drive-thru one morning, running a bit late to work and not knowing if I really had time to stop by. There were 5-6 cars in the drive-thru section, so decided to check the time and leave if it took too long. I pulled into line and left with drink in 3 minutes! Fairly friendly, too.

I stop here a lot when I am heading to San Diego for my pre-road-trip caffiene.  The staff are always super, the joint is clean and they always get my quad venti drink right.  BUT, this is only get three stars for the sole reason it is in a terrible location.  Hard to get in and out of this one, period.  While I love the location in terms of being right off the freeway - if there is any traffic happening at any point in the day it is a pain to turn into, and get out of to put you back on the I-10 heading west.  If you are a patient person, then it will be okay and likely not bother you.

Its a good 'bucks, a little busy with people at laptops, but I can't complain because I often do work there too. Its the place to have your office.  They have a few more items than some of the local smaller locations. They also have a drive through.

Typical Starbucks exceptional staff. Cleanest public bathroom anywhere. Like all stores, the menu is the same as all the others. Sit outside on a nice day, inside is on the small side, but comfortable.
I've never spent less than 30 minutes at this Starbucks. If you want 2 normal drinks, expect at least 30 minutes.

I also work at a busy shop on Magazine so I know how it is, and I know what it feels like to get treated like subhuman by patrons. Everyone at this Starbucks is usually so sweet despite it all, and I commend them for it. But today, on a chill Monday afternoon, I ask one of the baristas with pigtails, a beanie and glasses if my order is up, and she doesn't even look at me, shrugging while turning away. Thankfully, a much kinder barista gave me my drink not even a minute later. Moral of the story, folks: it takes more out of you to be rude than it does to be kind. Save that nasty energy for the people who actually deserve it, not someone who's literally just asking for the coffee that they paid for.

I'll start by mentioning I am a regular at Starbucks, I am there everyday. As I ordered my drink today an employee decided work was the appropriate place to talk about a protest she participated in. She also mentioned how she spoke poorly to the police that were present at the protest. You are free to have your beliefs, but work is not the time to publicize those beliefs, especially when all you patrons aren't anti- police. I won't be visiting this location again and I'll be sure my pro- police friends and family don't either.
I would not recommend stopping for coffee at this Starbucks unless you have an entire day to waste. Unacceptably slow staff. Stopping here tacked on at least 30 minutes to our journey.

I've never had an issue with them messing up on my drinks, but honestly this is the worst Starbucks in SB by miles. All of the girls have a huge attitude and seem like they hate their job. They're also the SLOWEST at making drinks. I've waited 15-20 minutes before and wind up late to where I'm going. I've tried to keep giving it many chances because it's the closest one to my house, but I'd rather drive a little out of the way to have friendly faces to see before I go to work and quicker service.
Love this Starbucks. They're always busy but they are always very courteous and friendly. Oh! The coffee is delish

It's no mystery that Starbucks is by far my favorite stop for coffee and other selections that are spontaneous dictated by my cravings at any given moment. I have visited literally hundreds of Starbucks' location since they've come into existence and although they all seem to be pretty consistence with the quality of the products they offer; the service, quality of the product, wait-time, cleanliness and accessibility of the location are the five points I will rate all of them on going forward - so to make the point I've recently incorporated a rating system based on this short list to calculate the rating of the locations I visit. I feel all these criterions are controllable by the corporation, the franchise owner or store manager.  Service - 4 Stars Quality - 4 Stars Wait Time - 5 Stars Cleanliness - 5 Stars Accessibility - 4 Stars  Rating 4.4

This location is awesome. The staff is consistently friendly and the best part is this place has a bunch of comfortable places to sit, and tons of outlets.   There's almost always a shortage of outlets at any cafe but this spot has you covered.  Also I just like the way Starbucks tastes. I can get a cold brew for $3.19 here where at the Well down the street they're $5.  I understand they are some kind of nonprofit but for a small cold brew that just feels too high. We're not at an airport or movie theatre sheesh.
Great staff. Always friendly. Wish I could smoke a cigar outside to enjoy my coffee though.

This is by far my favorite Starbucks ever!! I love this place. The drive-thru is always quick and the baristas are always friendly. And welcoming. My order is always prepared just the way I order it and always comes out in a timely manner. The inside and outside is always spotless and clean--great place to hang out and do my work in my laptop. Free wi-fi connection is just another one if it's best qualities (like every Starbucks). I honestly avoid going to any other location.

Do usual Starbucks menu or usual Starbucks just interested but polite Customer Service surrounded by potholes that could destroy your car recommend going to a different Starbucks if that's what you want if you just want to get a cuppa coffee stop by Speedway or Circle K

11/20/18 - So disappointed today. Not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on a Tuesday!  Once again we are here. Our favorite place. Love our Baristas. Jessica, Treigh, Caleigh, Alex and Taylor. If I've forgotten anyone please forgive me. So many great people work at this location. :) And of co

If I could I would give a 0 star Ordered from here a hot mocha drink with raspberry flavoring, (go to drink all the time)paid and waited. Not too bad of a wait but when everybody started getting served that was behind me, then I started getting annoyed went up to the counter asked hey have you made my mocha with raspberry flavoring yet? And the barista said oh we don't have raspberry flavor at this time. Soooo why tf wouldn't you tell me that from the start??? She then said I could make you something else. I said another drink and she said ok another 5 mins went by and nothing. Finally my drink was given to me by another person saying there's a drink she hasn't said a name for it.... well thanks a fucking lot Starbucks. No one go to this establishment, unless you like shitty customer service and you wanna wait forever for a drink

Not the best at their service. Out of the cookies I wanted which is ok, but out of raw sugar too?? Went through the drive through and she even forgit to give us the stir sticks. She was however polite.
Taylor is a very nice and polite barista. He is always in a good mood and looks you in the eye. Besides that, it is a normal Starbucks with everything you would expect.

My sister frequented this Starbucks location more than I did while we were living in Tucson, but here's what *I* have to say:  It's clean, the employees were friendly (except one who laughed at my Visa Card picture, but maybe I deserved it--I had my picture on my credit card), the deserts they had the last time I went in were delicious (okay, I didn't try everything, but the chocolate cupcake was good), and they made my Caramel Light Frappacino perfectly (because I like my drinks light and my sweets packed full of awfulness and sugar--I'm full of contradictions like that). When my sister went, she said they had her and her ex-boyfriend's order down to a "T," which I thought was kind of cool.   Otherwise, it's a decent, typical Starbucks. Nothing awful, nothing exceptionally noteworthy...yet, here I am...making a note.   Ha.
Went here at 8:30 on a Friday night and the doors were locked with staff inside even though posted hours are until 9:00. Guess they don't want my business.

5 people behind me got their drinks and I had to ask if the barista had my drink order- she non-chalantly says "yeah, it's over there"....hello?  If you know it's there why the hell aren't you preparing it?  I know it takes all kinds but you are in the service industry (SERVICE!). Additionally, she prepares the worlds slowest Americano and proceeds to dump cream in it!  I tell her I wanted light room not cream and she looks at me confused not offering to make the drink correctly. I'm pretty sure this isn't the Starbucks standard.  Worst Starbucks experience to date.  Rude/ unprofessional staff.

I love this starbucks. The staff is friendly and always has a smile on their face. The usual for me is the white chocolate mocha hot with an extra pump of syrup so that I don't have to add sugar and a slice of banana bread but today I tried the mango dragon fruit tea and omg was it amazing and refreshing I will definitely be trying it again

Nestled in the heart of academia (i.e. the University of Arizona campus), this Sbux is humming with the quickened pulse of anxious undergrads second-guessing their preparedness for the exam that will soon determine their fate, while professors sadistically toy with the idea of adding challenge questions to said exams. Baristas draw the focus of students and professors alike back to the matter at hand by collecting their orders: students going with an extra shot of caffeine in hopes of sharpening their focus; professors saying 'yes' to the whipping cream in a celebratory fashion. Ah yes, the bittersweet blend of the aroma of fear and coffee beans perfumes the air. *cheers*

This is a great Starbucks location. It's right on West End, perfect for being checking out the Parthenon, heading downtown, or going to Vanderbilt. There's 3 other Starbucks location right down the road, so if you miss this one you aren't far away from another one. Their drive thru line is always fairly long, but they get through it pretty fast! It has a large indoor and outdoor space, great for studying, working and meeting with friends. The staff there are all very friendly, and helpful if you are unsure of what you'd like.   See you soon Starbucks!
Great customer service first thing morning...helped us recreate home with specific requests! Thanks!

Poor service. Waited minutes just to be asked if I placed a mobile order. Then when I received my beverage it tasted odd, but I didn't think anything of it. When I got home I looked at the bottom of the cup, and there was a white substance that I didn't order in my beverage. They need to get better.

Ordered two skinny vanilla lattes. Took 20min to get the coffee on a Sunday evening. Got two lattes with whole milk and only 1 pump of sf vanilla syrup looking back at the receipt. Won't be going back to this Starbucks.

Was here this AM and Ashley helped me out so much.  I'm vegan, so I hate being "that customer."  She was super knowledgable about the drinks and suggested something different when I said I couldn't have whipped cream, since it "made" that particular drink. She didn't act like I was a hassle or anything, and made me quick experience great.  Just wanted to share excellent customer service when I see it!

If you've ever been to any Starbucks on earth then you know what to expect here. The coffee is consistent and satisfying (except that one bad mocha I got at the Starbucks in LaGuardia, but I don't like to talk about that...)   The great thing about this location is the customer service, especially in the drive-thru. They're always so friendly it leaves me wondering if the constant smell of coffee and car fumes combine to make some kind of mood enhancer.   I don't branch out much beyond my tall mocha (Ok, fine. Venti.) and the seasonal salted caramel latte, but they're always good enough to make me want to come back for more.

So they got my size wrong and the drink wrong and no espresso shot when I paid for it and not double blended when that's what I asked for. Really annoying and no receipt...? Very rude and seemed unorganized. Won't be going back anytime soon, Dutch bros it is.

This is the only Starbucks I go too! I'm in love with all the staff! There's one in particular his name is gizz and he's the sweetest person I've ever talked too! He's a tall boy with dirty blonde hair and he always perfects my drinks and when I don't know what I want he makes it perfectly!

Confrontational !!!!!!!!!!! & can't make the same drink right EVER!!! should be closed down for 3 hours to retrain to make drinks correctly & learn customer service!!! & learn how to ring up a discount rewards card!!  thank gawd for Indy stores that get it right
Ordered puppacinos for two puppies, along with a Flat White for me. Dogs enjoyed theirs a whole lot. Thanks!!
I avoided this location for a solid 6 months then tried again.  It has certainly improved and is very much in-line with the Starbucks locations.
Fast & friendly. A big plus was they were open until 10PM which is a huge for those working later in the evening. Coffee was spot on. Not sure what the complaints are all about but I will be back.

I utilize this Starbucks too much throughout the work week. It's so incredibly close to where I work that the drive through is pretty much begging for my business. And I cave. Every time. The staff are usually very friendly and quick with my order. The decor inside this particular Starbucks? Eh. But, for being attached to a gas station, it still manages to keep me coming back for more. Love me some Starbucks!
This is my favorite Starbucks ever. I live across town, but every barista I've had here has been so nice!!
Can you please start cleaning the cups. Espresso was all over lid and spilled in/out my car and on clothes. This has happened multiple times. Drinks are not consistent in taste.

The staff here is hit and miss. Sometimes you get someone friendly and sometimes you dont. The manager here is the worst. The last time I went to the drive thru here I had to have her at the window. Even when you go out of your way to smile she is cold and bitchy back. Wont go here again

Worst drinks and service EVER!  Their Frappacino's are weak and watery.  Now if you  want a creamy, rich tasting Frap, go to Nordstrom's coffee bar and order an Ice Storm!  OMG they are like drinking  a shake!  There are a variety of flavors, but my husband always orders the Caramel Ice Storm.  Since I watch my calorie and carb intake, I'll have a sip or two of his - it is so delicious.  If you try one, you'll agree with me that Starbuck's is the worst.

I don't understand why there's negative reviews about this Starbucks location when honestly, all I've had was amazing service with the very accommodating staff. It's located next to Penn Hospital and down the street from Jefferson, in a busy intersection where doctors and students come in for a cup of coffee -- you're going to have delays. If you're already late for classes/work, then why stop for coffee to add more to your lateness? It's YOUR job to prioritize your time, people. Not the staff.   Stop being an entitled primadonna that huffs and puffs because they didn't make your very important coffee fast as possible. Anyway.   I work around the area and got to know the staff fairly well! It's very busy there but the staff is always cheerful and make sure they've heard your order correctly, even apologizing when technology decides to backhand them out of the blue -- my coffee is always made perfectly and handed to me with a smile.  The store itself is clean -- I always see staff members either cleaning or keeping their bar area free of spills or answering any question a customer has, even when they don't get it line. They have impeccable customer service when I sit there and people watch from time to time, even striking up several conversations with a few. They care about their customers as well as providing them with a tasteful beverage.  2/5:00 PM is when there's heavy foot traffic so if you're in a rush, make sure to order on the app!

The place is nice- in addition to the standard Starbucks look they have a mini sofa room and a patio. What i dislike is that the staff changes a lot and the drinks are not always the best. But it is great if you want to enjoy a cup of regular coffee while enjoying a nice weather, especially because bensalem doesn't have that many patio places.

The only bad thing about this place is the lot. I curse aloud every time I turn into it! I come here for my caramel macchiato and very berry fix. The barista Aliyah makes a mean and delicious caramel macchiato! Had one tonight and it changed my evening! Best I've had at any Starbucks so far!
Dirty place, counters are filthy, baristas are not attentive, all tables needed to be clean

I came here to pick up a order that I made online but when I got there there was nothing waiting for me. the absolutely amazing barista asked me what I ordered and instead of sending me away to the other location I actually ordered, she fulfilled the order and I'm very appreciative!! Thank you so much!! Now I am on time. Thank goodness because I accidentally ordered at a location 20 min out of my way.

This is my favorite SB in town. It's roomier and cozier than the others. And IMHO, much friendlier too! When I want quiet time to work or zone out, it's just what I need. But when I stop in for a pick me up and I'm open to chatting there's always an SB regular hanging out.   The drive-thru is easy. Definitely not as crazy as the greentree/egg-harbor location.   It's a cozy little spot. Stop in!

When you have a broken machine customers who aren't having a drink made from that machine shouldn't be told it will be a wait. An iced drink should take a matter of seconds to make. Maybe having a plan to organize your workers for a situation like this would be more efficient.
Always get it right. Stop here almost daily. A great crew at this location.

My Vanilla Latte was great and the wait was minimal. The barista temped it to 175 just how I like it.   I have noticed the lids not fitting tightly like they should at a few Starbucks lately  and this was also a loose lid / cup combo. I only got caught by this fun event 1 time. Now I double check the lid and I never tip my cup back, just to be safe.

This place is always a disaster. Too many orders, not enough workers. Everyone seems to work at their own pace not worrying about the back order of drinks in a swarming cafe. Not many smiles. John seems to be the only worker that holds this place together, always in good spirits. The way the manager Tressie treats her employees is disgusting. Her attitude seems very contagious. I'd consider myself a regular due to working nearby & nothing ever changes my opinion of this Starbucks. GO TO ANOTHER ONE!
The slowest Starbucks I've ever experienced, it takes 10 minutes to get a coffee every time

I give up on Starbucks.  A few weeks ago, I ordered a mocha.  I waited for 15 minutes while the barista stared at me every chance she got and completed three other orders of people who walked in behind me.  The only thing I different that day was I was wearing a USO cap with a Marine Corps pin on it.    Only when I went to the register for a refund did she hold up a cup a and said she was making it.  I sent an email to Starbuck customer service and have not received a reply.  Now when my neighbors give me gift cards for watching their pets, snow blowing driveways and everything else neighbors do for each other, if they insist on giving me something I will ask them it not be a gift card from Starbucks.  And I won't be getting Starbucks gift cards for the service men and women when the USO has events.
Not happy at all they gave me warm and not hot coffee.  Tf. Too pricey to screw that up.
This is an excellent starbucks location. Beth who works here is always inviting and an amazing person. I would highly recommend this starbucks location to all. Wonderful new building and very clean.

The Baristas are very friendly and welcoming every day.  It can get crowded but the line moves pretty well.  Yes the parking stinks, but lucky for me I can walk here from my house!  This is a go to study spot for a lot of local students ... including myself.  Get there early if you want a seat to study at for a long period of time!

Not sure why all these negative reviews on this page. They are always friendly and nice despite being super busy almost all the time. (I'd be miserable) LOL. Always great with mobile orders and have them ready when I get to the store. Today specifically I was having a bad Monday and just wanted my coffee,  I called the store cause of course I forgot my mask and spoke with a gentleman (not sure of his name) I was asking if he could meet me at the door with the mobile order , then he met me at the door with a mask instead. I was happy about this because I needed one the rest of the day and I was thinking of where was I going to get one for the day?! Made my Monday much better with the kindness of this employee, also made me feel like it was no big deal I called the store telling them I didn't have one. He was happy I called and let them know. Recommend this Starbucks not only for the service but people working there who are always friendly and willing to go the extra mile. :)

Honestly, reviewing a Starbucks is like reviewing a McDonalds. You know what you're getting, and the quality may vary depending on just how little the employees care at the precise moment you're there, but it will never be the best culinary experience of your life, nor will it be the worst. That said, the staff were friendly, seemed competent, and made their way through a fairly long line of customers in a reasonable amount of time.
Best Starbucks in South Tampa. Staff are always cheerful and amazing. They always make my white mocha perfectly. Love this Starbucks.

I love starbucks! This location not so much. Stopped in thinking it was going to be fast. Ordered my venti iced latte with coconut milk and paid.  While i was waiting i saw people cleaning because they were not busy. About 5 minutes pass and my order wasnt even started. I ask if they got my order and they were like no, the machine didnt print it out.  After that my order was made. I didnt get my good vibe in this starbucks. I will return cause it a good location. Its off the plant city exit. Easy in and out of the interstate.

I arrived and the drive thru was long, so I decided to go inside.  I waited for 45 minutes for one black grande coffee!!!!  As I got bored, I counted the cars in the drive thru.  50 + cars were served before your inside customers.  This is unacceptable.  I am highly disappointed in my experience.

They always are very welcoming and know me by name. They know what I order and notice when I try something different from time to time. Very quick service. It's smaller than a lot of other locations but I like that atmosphere and my order always tastes great.

Drive Thru Disappointment  I frequent this Starbucks because its close to my house. It used to have a trash can accessible to drive thru customers, but apparently according to a staff member I spoke with it was "getting used too much" so now the trash can is still there, but its too far away for use from drive thru customers. Why you would remove a feature that was getting used is completely beyond me.

They were kind and attentive to my needs and wants as a customer! Great spot!   They offered to press instead of brew my coffee. My breakfast sandwich was hot, and I didn't know they had Sriracha!!    Feel bad for their location, but if you're in Millwoods, this is a great location!

This is a typical Starbucks. There aren't any cushy chairs which is kind of sad and it's very crowded because it's right near a lot of classroom buildings for Penn and Drexel. However, the baristas are always very friendly and they try their best to move people through the lines. I would recommend going here during off-peak hours such as early in the morning or in the evening where you can usually get a seat.

The checker was a young girl the only one that was not friendly. Because of her they get the one star. The food is always good at Starbucks or for the most part But her attitude and lack of customer service was not good. The rest of the people working there nice.

Convenient location and friendly service. I stop often and my pick up / drive thru orders are always correct.  Today I purchased a gift card in the drive thru and was helped by Juan. He brought several gift card options to the window and completed the order efficiently.  Thank you Juan for another great experience.

SLOWEST STARBUCKS EVER!  I popped in for a gift card, decided on a coffee to go as well.  Time stamp on receipt was 2:17p; did not coffee in hand until 2:24p.  The inside service was completely in the weeds; drive-thru a priority for service (average time seemed to be 3+ minutes).  I recommend drive-thru if you're in a hurry.  Knew I should have gone to the one in Target...waiting sucks.

Least favorite Starbucks in the area. Both the guy taking our order and the young girl barista seemed distracted & not very accommodating. The Americano with 6 pumps of flavor & 1-inch steamed cream ended up being good, but I had to ask twice to get it right & barista did it with a snarl. Also was charged more than any other Starbucks for the same drink.

Usually the only time I end up at a Starbucks is when trapped at an airport terminal for a morning flight. Luckily, I don't fly often so years can pass between Starbucks experiences.  $3.95 for the largest size cold-steeped coffee is ridiculous, being that at least half the volume is comprised of ice. Watered-down medium brew at 40% mark-up over the same stuff they serve hot.   On this occasion I had been up for over 24 hours awaiting the arrival of my first grandchild. With any luck I won't have to avail myself of week-ass cold brew and harshly hot "fresh" brew until the next grandchild comes along.
Fen hands down makes the best espresso Frappuccino. Love this location and people like Fen make it worth the wait.

The bald guy manager whoever he is, he has the absolute customer service I've ever experienced. I don't know how this man has a job. He needs to be trained again or something because he is awful. I feel so bad for anyone who has to deal with him. All the people that work there look so miserable and don't even try. This location is by far the worst in the area. You're better off going to another one. I can't stress this enough. How can one Starbucks be this bad??? !!!

I love Starbucks! Especially their Café Lattes, they are to die for. Back when I was in University a year and some months ago, I would buy it every morning before class and it perks me right up. And I'm no longer grumpy. Without coffee I'm cranky. Now that I don't have any part time job for awhile, I had to cut down my Starbucks purchases of coffee.

This particular Starbucks is on the ball every day and I visit coffee shops on the regular. The lady in there by the name of Heather is a machine. She even remembered my order from my last visit. Now that's what I call customer service. All around I'm going to be making this my regular stop in the mornings.   Still all of the ladies there are very chipper this early in the morning and my drink is mad perfect. Well done.
Awful service, the drive thru wait is 10 min each time with only 3 cars in front of me. Do better

Visited this location for the first time and really appreciated the assistance from the manager Abby. She was really helpful with providing information on their new one step coffee brewer. Will definitely recommend this location to others.

I love this Starbucks, unlike many of the nearby locations, this one seems to keep their coffee machine clean. I typically get a mocha frappe, often with a shot of espresso. Many of the other Brandon locations including the ones in the mall, do not have a good track record with their frappes. They just do not taste good every time, and for $5 a drink I want consistency. I don't know if they're not changing out the frappe mix often enough or not cleaning the coffee machine often enough, (as I get an added shot of espresso.) Either way, if you're looking for an excellent frappe, this is the perfect location!

A lot of students at this location but they have a great selection of outdoor seating. Even benches. We went in, it was really busy but our drinks were made quickly and we were in and out in less than 10 minutes.

Slowest Starbucks ever! Every time I go (and today will be the last) the wait to pick up your drink after paying is horrendous. This morning there were 5 staff behind the counter and most of the time at least two of them were just chatting. I watched one of them make my drink of an iced coffee black. I saw him fill the cup less than half way with coffee and then the rest with ice, then instead of putting it on the counter for me to access he just set it aside. I then asked a girl behind the counter if she could "dump out some of the ice and add more coffee as it was mostly ice. " So naturally she scooped out all of the ice and filled it to the rim with coffee. When I saw there was one lone ice cube in it I said "now there's no ice." She responded with "you want more ice?" I just got her to give me a side cup of ice so I could leave as I could see this going on all day - all ice or all coffee. Apparently they can't do moderation in anything. Did I mention that they are horribly slow? Never going back!

Drive-thru line is always wrapped around the building and moves at a snails pace. Decided to do a mobile order instead and just go inside. Found out it is so slow because there are only 3 people working (1 at the register...making drinks and food too, 1 making drinks in the back for the drive-thru primarily, and 1 running the drive-thru window...while also making drinks and food). Seems really disorganized and understaffed. The woman asked what I was waiting for, and I told her a mobile order, so she fumbled through the receipts and went and made it...and then brought it out saying it was "hiding" in the back...when it was obvious she just made it.

Grabbed my first Pumpkin Spice Frappacino of the season and it was as heavenly as I imagined! The drink was fantastic, but I'm giving 4 stars because the line was long and it took a while to even be able place my order and even longer before the drink was actually ready. Though it was busy the environment was still pleasant & there were plenty of seating options both indoor & outdoor. Really enjoyed my time there today.
This location has consistently performed below expectations. They not only are the slowest, but often do not produce the drink ordered.

I used to go to this Starbucks all the time when I was in grad school. Service was always fine. However, this last time, we stopped in the morning after Thanksgiving on our way out of town. We seriously waited 3-4 minutes in the store before anyone acknowledged us verbally. It was pretty ridiculous. There were 7 or 8 people waiting and finally, once the guy making drinks made a move to talk to us, the woman working the counter finally just stared at us at the register until we ordered. Really? Then she made a big show out of writing our names on our cups when we were the only people waiting for drinks. Pretty surreal.   I do remember that people tend to camp out in this location and set up shop for quite a while. If you're meeting someone socially, it can be hard to find a table. In warmer months, there is ample outdoor seating, however.   The drive-through can be tricky as there are two entrances and there always seem to be issues with the two lanes merging.

This place sucks. You offer three brewed coffees but you only have ONE available? And it's the stupid pikes roast? That coffee is terrible. The reason why you have it is because no one orders it. Ugh. Starbucks. Stupid leftist company. It's a monopoly at the airport. Annoyed.

super slow there were 3 cars ahead of me and it took 20 minutes to get my order. that is super slow service for any drive thru. to top it off, the "all day menu" ran out of many many items. really really disappointed.

This is easily one of my favorite Starbucks. Whenever I order via phone, my drink is always ready with no mistakes. The staff is pleasant and they work quickly. Also, I adore the location since it is near where I  work, and it is always on the cleaner side considering that some many students hang out there. Although the students are well behaved and quite pleasant, I don't think I'll be there working.

I don't go to Starbucks for the coffee. Whaaaat? Yep. Caffeine makes me jittery, but the Cheese & Fruit Bistro Box is the bomb! Their sandwiches are pretty tasty, too.  The Bistro Box is stocked with a nice portion of Brie, Gouda, two-year aged Cheddar cheeses, nine-grain crackers, apples, dried cranberries and roasted almonds. It's a nice blend of crunch and freshness and #cheese.  You can't go wrong with the Roasted Tomato & Mozzarella Panini either: roma tomato, mozzarella, spinach and basil pesto on a piccolo roll.  They also carry my fave boxed Horizon chocolate milk, though it comes with a steep $2.50 price tag.

First time coming to this location . Cashier was unfriendly and had an attitude. I ordered a berry hibiscus with light ice and my drink came out quickly but it was definitely not light ice , it was heavy ice . 90% of the cup was ice even though it even said light ice on the cup.

I did an online order. I arrived there 10mins after I placed my order. They were busy so I waited when I looked at the time, I had waited already 10mins. I went in line and wait. Once I spoke with cashier, my order had not been completed. They said they would take care of it. In total, it took me 30mins to receive what I ordered. I waited because I did not know how to cancel my order from online and get refunded. When I left, I realized my order was incorrect as well. I did not return back since I did not want to wait anymore additional time. I do like Starbucks, but I will not be returning to this location.

We hung out at this Starbucks in Harrahs at least once a day during our 10 day visit to NOLA. If not here, then we visited the one on Canal & St Charles at least twice during our stay. On a hot day, their iced coffee is a welcome relief. We usually cut thru Harrahs on our way to Riverwalk for Cafe du Mode beignets. It's a smoke free casino and AIR-CONDITIONED so we stop, have an iced coffee and watch people playing the slot machines. The employees are okay, typical of Starbucks, friendly, courteous, fast & efficient and the iced coffee usually pretty good though not consistent  (with refills). They have decent rewards program (star rewards).

Everyyyyyy sinngggllleeeeee timmmeeeee I go they mess something up to the point where they mention it at the drive thru "I know this happens often" uh no every time update your mobile app so orders are current so you don't get pissed off people and have to refund the entire order then provide a courtesy " next ones on us" gift card
It's somewhat hard to park during the morning rush, but there is plenty of comfortable seating on the inside.  Staff is prompt and friendly as one might expect in any Starbucks shop.
I never leave reviews but this is the only Starbucks that makes my drink orders right, everywhere else is bland/watery. The line could be long sometimes for drive thru but thats expected

I have been doing mobile orders for the last 3 months, weekly orders - sometimes every few days. The customer service is horrendous here. The baristas and cashiers blatantly ignore every customer in the cafe. My mobile is almost never done when I arrive, even though I make sure to order at least 10 minutes before arriving, these days 15 min. Yet once again I stood in the cafe and waited for my incorrect coffee order. I inquired twice about my sandwich and I don't know why I was ignored and pushed to the side but I finally couldn't wait anymore because I was already going to be late to work because of this that I asked for a refund. Once I asked for a refund - suddenly my sandwich came out of nowhere, it was ice cold sitting behind the counter where I couldn't obtain it, and I now didn't have time to ask her to remake it. The service here is absolutely terrible. Put your order in anywhere else.

This is easily the worst Starbucks location ever. Whenever I visit a Starbucks, I usually order a peppermint mocha. But whenever I've gotten one here, it's pretty much just steamed milk and a shot of espresso. Incidentally, I've stopped getting coffee here altogether unless it's just something plain and already brewed. Last week, I got a hot tea and the girl made it with water from the tap instead of the hot water. When I asked her to remake it, all I got was an attitude. Similarly, I tried to have coffee drinks made with the same result. Needless to say, I hike down to 34th-ish street now if I want something that actually tastes good. Also, the staff here is terrible managing the lines, which get incredibly long. I'm relatively certain the only pace here is turtle. To wait 15 minutes or more for crappy coffee isn't worth it. The Starbucks close to the UPenn campus is way nicer, has better coffee, the staff is friendlier, and even with the walk, you'll still get your drink faster.

First time at this new location and went through the drive thru. No one in front of me but it took almost 5 minutes before someone came to the window to take my money. Once she came she was super friendly but the wait was a little surprisingly. I got a java chip with an extra shot and it was amazing as always.

Isn't the point of mobile order to get your order in line for preparation?  Twice this week I placed my order 10 min prior to arrival. The app said would be ready in 6-8 minutes both days.  As I entered I noted who was in the regular line.  Both days people who placed their orders after I arrived (about 10 min after I placed my mobile order) were getting their beverages and mine hadn't even been made.  Yesterday my order was ready 20 min after originally placed and today it was 17 minutes.  Very frustrated.  I get it is a busy location....but both days it looked like they were picking and choosing what drinks to make...not necessarily in order placed.  And to top it off today they were out of vanilla syrup at 7:30am...poor planning.

My favorite Starbucks! They make the best coffee drinks! Especially my cinnamon dolce iced coffee. Today I went in there hoping the could resolve my horrible drink that I got at the Starbucks in the target across the parking lot. They so awesomely do fix it and I was very grateful for it.
