I promise I will stay faithful to my TRUE Starbucks .  Thanks again for fixing my drink

As far as Starbucks goes, which isn't that far, this one is pretty much as good as you're gonna get. In fact, as a coffee shop it has pretty much everything I need: coffee, ample seating, friendly and fast waitstaff, proximity to delicious eating and drinking establishments (ahem Percy St.), free internet, and a lot of cute girls walking around outside (warning: can be distracting from homework). The bathrooms are clean and don't smell bad, as are the baristas.   However, the place unfortunately gets docked two points. The first reason, some might say, is really strange and perhaps picky, but I couldn't imagine not mentioning it, as it is something that really bothers me every time I go there. Particularly when it's late out, I am often sitting there happily along with my fellow coffee-drinkers and girl-watchers drinking and watching in peace, enjoying the light music playing on the speakers overhead. The lights are dimmed and we are just enjoying the evening before closing time, when I will stumble into the nearest bar and get my drank on. All of a sudden, the peace is broken by an EXPLOSIVE sound coming from behind the coffee counter. What is this sound? I have no idea. It sounds like someone dropped a 1000lb bag of coffee grinds onto a bass drum. Or perhaps like a 400lb barista just suffered a cardiac arrest and fell into a pile of mousetraps. But let me tell you, my friends, this sound is rather upsetting. And it literally happens at least 2 or 3 times that I've gone there. What the hell are they doing back there???  It's also too cold at night.  Other than those reasons, this Starbucks is above and beyond most that I've been in.

I have been going to this location of Starbucks for years. in the beginning they would zip you in and out real quick so that you could get on to work in less than 5 minutes. Now it takes almost 20 minutes and the order is wrong. I have had this experience several times and finally this morning was the last straw so I am writing this review. I am very unhappy with the service at the drive-thru and it doesn't go any faster if you go inside to order. The baristas don't know how to hustle and evidently don't know how to read because my order is wrong a lot. And they question me like I'm the one that changed my mind mid order. They remake the drinks but then of course that adds more time to the wait. Ugh! So disappointed.

I ordered a Nitro Cold Brew with sweet cream. The first attempt came back as an iced coffee. I returned that and it was replaced with a poor facsimile of the advertised product. I have ordered this drink at least 10 times in the past from other Starbucks and it is always properly prepared and delicious. Please do some training to ensure product delivery that justifies charging $5+ for a coffee drink.

New location and expect it to be nice. Horrifically dirty bathroom from the outside door and handle, to paper all over the floor, to water, messy mirror, and just stuff everywhere. Seat up, changing table down. Very disappointed.

This location is cool because its the 1st starbucks with a 2nd floor loft, which looks comfy for studying or working on your computer. On a saturday afternoon, it was busy but there were lots of seats to be had.  Parking is not good though and you'll have to pay if you go over 75 minutes parking at the shopping plaza parking lot. I guess that's typical for downtown Santa Barbara.

If you're looking for one of the nicer Starbucks in town, this is the place to go.  The location is perfect if you're a South Countian - right smack in the middle of a busy area, hospital, food establishment and grocery shopping.  The staff is super nice but I think that's just the company in general as I can honestly say I have never gone to Starbucks and not had a nice employee.  The company has started to add more items to their food offerings.  I have yet to try them but I cannot say no to their pastries as it is.  Something you may not know - if you buy the Starbucks coffee bags to make at home, if you keep the bag and take it to one of the stores, they will give you a free cup of coffee when you turn it in.  Very rewarding!

Consistently receive terse and curt treatment, especially by Jenny. The sultry attitudes here are better suited for a dive bar. Most Starbucks around Nashville attract quality employees by it is impossible to relax at this one after receiving negative treatment on several occasions in a row.

Maybe it is the volume of traffic, but this SBUX seems habitually backed up and just hanging on by fingernails.  The staff is friendly which is great, and they make up wrong drink orders but a stop here isn't a 5 min quick in and out.  It's an ordeal. Even if you mobile order and show up at the approximate availability time, you are still waiting upwards of 10 minutes.  It's like the bar needs to be bigger, with less seating because nobody wants to hang around and add a few team members. Maybe a visit from the process folks would help.

I am a huge Starbucks fan and was thrilled to see that this one was built right between my gym and my home! I went to get coffee the first day they were open and have been back several times since. I was disappointed on my first visit to discover that the employees were not as friendly as all the other Starbucks that I have been to. I decided to give them the benefit of the doubt since they had just opened and were possibly unfamiliar with the store. Unfortunately, this unfriendliness has not changed on any of my return visits. Until now, the quality of the coffee was just as good as any Starbucks so I just overlooked the unfriendliness, but today something happened that forced me to write this review.   Today I ordered a latte Macchiato with an extra shot. I told the cashier my name, she wrote it on the cup, and I went to wait for my drink. Christin the barista, called my name when it was ready and announced my drink but it was not what I ordered. I politely asked, "what did you say"? She did not reply, and my partner said he couldn't hear her either. I leaned in and asked again politely "excuse me Miss, what did you say?" She looked at me and impatiently and said the name of the drink again which I still did not understand. I smiled and said "well that's my name but that's not what I ordered". To which she replied petulantly "well then I guess it's not yours". She then condescendingly proceeded to explain to me the difference between what I had ordered and what she had made. So I replied again politely "well you can understand my confusion since that is my name and there's no one else here waiting". She snapped "yes of course".   Nothing else was said, she went about her business, and then after about two minutes took the mistake drink and threw it away. In reality, she had made an error and was making me a new drink. Shortly after, she gave me the correct drink and said barked "here you go"  I'm not sure why, but for some reason the management of this Starbucks tolerates unfriendliness and bad service. This Starbucks is conveniently located for me so I will continue to go there, but I will recommend that you be ready to be treated poorly when you get your coffee.
It's bad enough that Starbucks has a one person only bathroom, but this one takes the cake for being the worst! They have ONE bathroom ONLY now! This is so inconvenient when you have to go!

I stopped going here over a year ago.  Today I was forced to try again.  Pulled up to a drive through line 20 cars deep.  I placed a mobile order to save time.  Over a 40mins later,  I made it to the window.  And I STILL HAD TO WAIT for my drinks to be made.  Good thing there isn't indoors service right now.  I'll go without, before I go here again.

Only been in twice to grab coffee and treats, but it's really pretty inside and there seems to be a lot of seating. They even have an outdoor seating space. The staff is quick and friendly as well. I'm glad this is right near my new house!
Reno, NV  Great service. Awesome baristas............... ........... ........ ...... Skinny vanilla latte one shot two shot and no no no sugar!

The Starbucks I avoid going to. I had to stop there while I was in the area multiple times, and unfortunately, it was the slowest service ever. A few of the employees seemed like they could care less, were joking around and making fun of each other, while people are in a rush trying to get their coffee and leave. I understand waiting 5 mins for my drink, even 10 mins during a busy hr.  But 20 mins of waiting for 1 simple drink is uncalled for. I wouldn't have written a bad review, but it was like that already several times.

Almost every time (maybe 8+ times) I have gone into this Starbucks, the staff seems less than interested in helping me or anyone, they are always chatting with each other or attending to other work seeming related things, but leaving the register unattended. One employee even snapped at me bc I didn't understand that adding a shot of vanilla to something meant it was 'sweetened', dumb I know, but I thought sweetened meant adding simple syrup. Live and learn. Atmosphere is usually pretty good, big tables for work and play. It's your typical Starbucks experience, just not nearly as friendly.

This has got to be the dirtiest Starbucks in Tucson. The floor was full of dirt and pieces of what seemed to be some white debris almost all over the floor. People were stepping on this and carrying it to other places.   Every single available table needed cleaning and there were a lot of hangers-on concentrating in their laptop and doing maybe homework. Some of these had nothing on their tables that were sold by SB.   What seemed to be homeless folks just walked in and out to just to walk around. Gladly the restrooms were locked, but any employee could have given them the numbers to get in. Restrooms needed some minor cleaning.  My grande coffee was really a short with foam taking 75% of the cup, the weight of the cup gave it away. Should have complained but I just wanted to get out of there.   We enjoy SB from time to time and I must say that except for most tables always needing cleaning and folks just using their laptops, this on was a rarity. Thankfully!
Coffee was ok. Selection of other food items was very limited. But that would not make it a 2 star yet. However this was by far the dirtiest bathroom I have seen in any Starbucks I have ever been.
ETHAN IS THE BEST!!! Every time I order he always puts a smile on my face and is literally the cutest, nicest, most polite employee there!!   HE IS THE BEST!!! #ethanforpresident

While the parking isn't ideal, the staff are incredible.   They're talented and caring! They remember your name and have an interest in serving each customer well. Holly is the manager and she's active in the store and does a good job of picking the right employees.   The people behind the counter are just incredible. Lucky to call this "my" Starbucks!

The only reason I am giving this 4 and not 5 stars is because of how busy this location gets. Sometimes when you plan to "dine in" it is hard to find a seat. The wifi is also slower when there are more people inside. The staff is great! They really enhance the experience and you can tell they love their jobs (or they are great actors.) They really make it a personal experience from the cashiers, to the people who make your favorite beverage. Sometimes, however, I do find it frustrating when they only have one cash register going and there are tons of people waiting in line. Overall, a great location with great employees who continue to enhance the Starbucks experience.

I never write reviews but I was so absolutely impressed by my service today that I would be remiss to not to share my experience! The barista who checked me out--I unfortunately didn't ask for his name--was the nicest and most helpful person I've ever dealt with in a coffee shop! He even made me a special drink that he recommended (which was delicious by the way).  I will say I was very upset that this location didn't have the option to tip on my card. My barista went above and beyond and the staff as a whole seemed to be working hard to get people's drinks out and keep the store clean. I never carry cash with me and I wish I could have offered additional financial compensation for the exemplary work of the staff   I will definitely be returning to this location (with cash on hand)!

Today, I went to Starbucks. Starbucks is literally my favorite place to go, I ordered my usual today then got up to the window and saw that my drink; Had to cups on it, I wondered why. So, I took the other cup off and it was leaking!!! They could have transferred it into another cup on top that it had way too much ice in it, I couldn't even get my straw in. The lady offered to help and poured some of the ice out, she was very nice. But, I've had problems with this Starbucks before. Both of us (My Mom & I) received drinks that did not taste like usual.  Step up your game starbucks!
Been here over the course of the summer. I've ordered 5 of the same drink, and they all have tasted different. staff are sitting around on their phones. Disappointing customer service.

Always excellent service ! I stop here often before work nearby and it is one of my favorite locations. Drinks always taste good and even when there's a long drive thru line, it goes quickly. Baristas are always so friendly!
Super standard Starbucks but good staff and clean area. Clayton is super nice to walk around too!
The service is great. The amount of homeless inside and out is really a blight on an otherwise great monument circle experience.
I just spent 23 min in the drive thru here and no free drink coupon offered! Seriously Guys!!@
As far as Starbucks' go, you could not ask for a better team. This store is always running very smoothly - even when the line is to the door and there are 20 cars in the drive-thru!

As Starbucks go, this place is solidly below par. This assessment is based on below average ease of entry and parking (although the latter has improved with a neighboring business moving out) and more importantly, the staff here is just less friendly than other stores.    As an aside, it's definitely on the smaller side seating wise, and does not really have any quiet nooks for working, studying, etc if that is important to you.  Bottom line:  it's not horrible, but I try to hit other stores when possible

The JW Starbucks is rude and unprofessional.  Ordered a cappuccino and venti water, it was $6, come to find out they charge for water in a cup. I said I don't want the water. After the third time of telling me they need to scan my phone app, I said why don't you train your people to warn about charging for water so I don't have to scan my phone three times.   I went back to sit down and some SJW employee says In front of my seven year old that she's going to call security on me. Why is a low brain SJW scaring my kid?   I contacted Starbucks and if they don't make this right, I'm done with Starbucks and their SJW nonsense.   I spend $200 month in starSucks and they'll let homeless people use their toilet water for free but charge a good customer for a cup of water?   I've had enough of this SJW company treating me worse than a homeless person.   This JW Marriott has nearly convinced me that I'm done with starSucks  And their new bonus system sucks as well.   I'm really starting to hate starSucks
When i ordered coffee they told me the coffee machine was not work,when i ordered bagels, they told me the toaster was not work too! What amazing place!
Pretty disappointed Starbucks. I ordered a Grande Chai Tea Latte this morning & when I opened it up, a quarter of it was empty. Well, that sucks. Next time can I pay a quarter less of the price?
This location is OK. Mostly just use the drive-up window on the way to work. The big downside, the parking lot is designed very badly!!!!

I went into the Starbucks in the Target after shopping.  It was 7:45 on a Sunday night and the cashier had told us that Starbucks closes at 8:00.  There was only one barista and when I asked if they were open, because she was sitting at a table, she responded that she would be on break for 10 more minutes. We decided to leave since it appeared that she would be on break until closing time.  They either need to have two people working at least or someone working by themselves needs to realize that the customer needs to be waited on and not expected to stand there until you are off of break.

Just opened up. Nice addition as it is directly on my way to work but there are a lot of cafes in the neighborhood. It is a nice size overall. I'm curious to see how busy it is going to get. I feel like I watched this place get built in a few weeks. I love a nice iced americano for the morning walk. The staff was friendly.

This is one of the coolest Starbucks I have seen! Love the interior! Very modern and clean! Fast and friendly workers. They still have salted Carmel mocha for now! They're out of pumpkin spice though :(

Friendly staff. Convenient location... Often there is a bit of a wait.... My only real compliant is they run out of Cold Brew 60% of the time... Why not plan and make more cold brew? I feel so petty complaining when there are way bigger problems in the world today... this just seems like an easy fix.
Closes early without warning.  Extremely dark outside.   Lights are never on outside.  Logo light is never on when open...very rude staff

This is literally the worst Starbucks I have ever been to in my entire life. I live in St Charles, and it takes less time for me to drive all the way to St Peters to go to Starbucks, than it does for me to drive down the street and get my coffee here. The service is awful and slow. The servers are rude and they yell at each other in front of customers. It's like watching the first week of Hell's kitchen when no one communicates and everything they cook is crap. You can tell that they're so used to messing up drink orders that it has become status quo, so they don't even bother apologizing anymore. Expect to sit in the drive thru for 20 minutes if there are more than 4 cars in front of you. If you brave the poorly planned parking lot to park and go inside, their hand made signage directs you to stand in line in the wrong place and they'll not hesitate to point out the error of your ways. Is there a leader or a manager of this store? It seems like every one that works here is on their first day on the job.  Update: I'm back here in line. I'm a glutton for punishment  Update: Back again on April 22 for a unicorn frapp. They stopped making then two days early and refuse to make one for my 12 year old.

I'm giving this place four stars due to them never getting my order wrong. I've never had an issues with that aspect. But this place gets packed and I mean packed at times. It's in the middle of university traffic and Tucson HS, so expect to wait. Use the mobile app here if at all possible. The location is nice though. I like the outside areas to sit and at times the inside is pretty nice accept when it's really busy. The staff have been pretty friendly every time I've come here and move quick (because they have to due to the volume of customers). It's not all sheik like some other Starbucks around town but has that homey feel to it. Make a stop and try it. I've also seen dogs here outside too.

Unless you order your drink ahead of time via the app, don't expect to get your drink in a timely manner...even if the place is dead and you are the only person waiting for an in-store ordered drink. There is no sense of urgency to any of the aforementioned drink-making and they seem to be consistently understaffed, or the staff isn't managed properly. You've got three people taking orders/warming food, but only one making drinks. The renovation of last year is unwelcoming (probably done on purpose) and it no longer has the small-town feel it once had. Oh, and the flooring they chose to put down shows everything and it is beyond dirty every time I go in. This store is going to take a hit once the new one opens over near McCaffrey's, especially if they can't get their crap together.

The doors on this place are worth a 5 star rating all by themselves!!!  Got my fav Iced Caramel Macchiato.  Very yummy, one of the best I've had flavor wise. Could have used a bit more caramel on top, but couldn't we all want that?  Staff was great. Prices too high, which we already know when we go here, but we all deserve this treat now and then !!  Love it !!

So I am going to have to knock a star off because I tried to venture inside one afternoon.  It was crazy busy.  The way the store is set up disorganized the entire line so it was confusing to know where the line began or ended.  Maybe I will come back when it is a bit warmer outside because of the additional outside seating, but until then, I am sticking to the drive thru.  This is not the place to sit and do some work or have a little meeting.

Super friendly staff. They make you feel like your business is welcome, and never make a sloppy $6 drink. Definitely worth the inconvenient parking.  Last time I was there they even offered a puppy whip for my pooch. I only wish every Starbucks was as friendly as this one.

I'm a big fan of Starbucks, but this particular store m is the only Starbucks I go to that doesn't have my mobile waiting for me when I come to the door. Everything just takes longer here. Not sure why, since they have more baristas than other places.

Wonderful Starbucks that uses the old gas station turned del taco space wisely.  They have a great selection of coffees here including the new rare/exquisite line.  I was able to buy a bag of Jamaica Blue Mountain ($30 for 1/2lb) and was thoroughly impressed by how bold and inviting the beans were.    The layout of the location is unique as well.  Less outlets so there aren't too many stragglers.  There are a couple larger center tables that multiple students can sit at, but I believe they are meant for more of a social gathering type atmosphere than a study atmosphere.   This Starbucks also features the Clover brewing system which is a technologically advanced system that seeks to brew coffee at the perfect temperature for the perfect amount of time.  I tried it (it costs about 50 cents more), but I did not taste an immediate difference.  Maybe I should have bought a normal coffee to do a taste test?

Can't live without my coffee fix, usually day and night! White mocha is my drink and they do it up right!!! I really don't like the drive thru set up at this one, but they are friendly and get my drink right.
Brand spanking new with a very scary fruit fly problem in their display case, in their restrooms and seating area. Like whoa...

This is one of my favorite Starbucks.  Not because the drinks are better (I'm actually pretty sure that Starbucks strives to have your drink taste the same at all locations) but because the people at this Starbucks are quite possibly the friendliest baristas I've ever met.  They are always cheerful, smiling and you can tell that they enjoy what they do.  Plus they all seem to like each other with the jokes that they're telling as they prepare your order.  Even in a large super-chain like Starbucks, they manage to make it feel like you're in your little neighborhood "everybody knows your name" kind of coffee shop.

Visiting trinity this past 10 days. Love Starbucks coffee and was there twice a day. Problem was sugar and cream at counter was never attended to. Very frustrating.  This seems like the simplest thing that manager should stress to it's workers

Right on Main Street in New Hope.  Nice outside seating, although your view is of the intersection.  Quick service with a smile and the typical Starbucks quality.  No parking at the store since it on Main Street so don't think you are going to just pull into the parking lot and run in.

The staff gets a 5-star rating.  The abuse of the handicap spots in front by mobile-app patrons gets a -4.  To those that think a quick in&out to get your trenti sized quad shot soy milk pumpkin spiced latte warrants parking your Range Rover in the handicap spot because you're too lazy or entitled to walk the extra 30' from the nearest available parking spot.

This review is for the location in the red baggage claim area at the Southwest belts. Ridiculously long wait! One person working left that line gave up and went upstairs to the third floor main concourse and was told by employees at that Starbucks that they don't care downstairs. It doesn't matter if there's a long line because as she said, we're closing that location soon anyway. I was horrified! I have never gotten anything less than excellence from Starbucks employees things have really gone badly here at the airport for them.

What a great way to start a day than a good cup of coffee and a friendly staff that greets you by name.  Just when the emails start rolling in I just twist my cup around and enjoy the hand written "Have an Amazing Day!" Thanks for starting my day with a smile.

We thought the service was great. The lady who took our order was new. However considering how busy they were I thought she did excellent under pressure while maintaining a really friendly demeanor! Even though I had a complicated drink order, my drink came out correct and delicious. It took about five minutes for our drinks to be ready, but like I said, they were busy, and Carla did a phenomenal job juggling all those orders.
This location is really a hit or miss. You never know if the people working that day are feeling lazy or not with the quality of work they put out that day.
Pulled up to buy and was told I had order online. "ain't got time for that!"  This store is off my list.

I live walking distance from this Starbucks so instead of driving to one I have the convience of just going here. I have not always been a fan of Starbucks because of their coffee but I started getting adventurous and started trying new things out. I fell in love with Starbucks Iced Green Tea unsweetened with honey and the Mocha Green Tea Frappuccino all because of the help of the workers! The staff are always friendly and since I am always there alone or with friends socializing or doing homework they basically know me. I am always welcomed with a friendly smile and whenever I ask for help about a drink I feel like they always go above and beyond even telling me if I don't like they can make me another one! The mornings and Sundays can get busy and super loud which might cause you to wait a bit for your drink but that isn't the workers fault. I prefer sitting outside around the evening since it is relaxing and more quiet compared to the inside sometimes. This is by far my favorite Starbucks in the area!

Most of the baristas are nice but there is one guy I've run into that is kinda sassy and I cannot tell if it's personality or if it's a displeasurement of being there. I was given a snarky comment when my ordered drink was made hot instead of iced. Which weirdly, the ice was given to me in less than two minutes but the hot took ten? My main issue is this location is really slowing down. Waiting in line for several minutes, 20 to 30? I might as well shut off my car to save gas and the planet. I'm going to start going to the other location on 135 or even the in store ones.
They never disappoint. I had a bad experience at the Algiers location, but this location made up for it.

This is, by far, my favorite Starbucks location. It's conveniently located on my way to I-64, so I frequently visit on my way to work. The drive-thru staff is friendly and fast - even if the line is all the way out of the parking lot! The food is always hot and the drinks are made-to-order.

Never coming back to this location ever again. A mobile order for a white chocolate mocha was ordered at 8:37a and at 9:15a I had yet to see my drink appear. I asked the young man nicely about the status and I was told we are busy and we will get to you. I patiently waited HOWEVER I had to address the young man again after I noticed folks who walked in after me got their drinks before me. I thought the purpose of the mobile order was for my drink to be ready when I showed up maybe I am wrong. I didn't appreciate the lack of customer service experienced and the young man acted like I was being an unreasonable and impatient customer. After waiting over an hour for a mocha I knew it wasn't me. When mg drink was ready they still didn't bother to let me know my order was ready. In the words of Trillville "Neva Eva, Eva Eva" will I go to this location.

I am at this Starbucks ALL THE TIME. I work just north of downtown (between downtown and Germantown. This Starbucks is the nearest coffee shop I can walk to (or a coffee shop inside the farmer's market, but I prefer not to do that). It's in a hidden little area within the Doubletree Hotel. Walk down the diagonal sidewalk thing and you'll find it. The lines can get very long (a lot of state employees frequent this location in large groups), but they're pretty fast most of the time. They also do a happy hour (between 2 and 4, I think), when it's buy one get one free on drinks. It's just a Starbucks, but it's gotten me through many an exhausting day!

This Starbucks is my go-to since it is nearest to my house and they always seem to make my coffee right. My order is simple: a basic White Chocolate Mocha. Today I went through the drive-thru and ordered just that. When I received my cup of "coffee", upon taking a sip, I realized it was literally just hot water and milk. When I walked inside a explained to the barista that it tasted like hot water, she laughed and said that "they were just playing with me" and that "the pump was broken". So, they KNOWINGLY gave me a steaming cup of water out the drive-thru window, fully expecting me to drink it. I'm quite disappointed, this is not the Starbucks customer service I am used to.

The morning staff at this Starbucks are phenomenal. Just great, happy attitudes start a happy day. I loved visiting and the staff already knowing that I was getting a "Soy Mocha no whip" everytime! The only thing is, I'm pretty sure everyone in Oro Valley knows how good this place is, so sometimes they are slammed but the wait even on a bad day is only maybe 10 minutes.

This place is pretty suave. I like how they have a variety of coffee and pastry. The food is good except for the "Budget" you need to buy it. Basically, what I am mentioning about is the price of the food when you try to put it in kid size.  My son's second review :-)

Slow service.  I usually have a great experience here when i go into the store.  Just did drive thru and waited about 10 mins  for my drink and was offered no apology or any real expression of concern for the wait by the cashier.  Thankfully my drink is good!
Good for relaxing.  Nice comfy couches.  Hard to study as they blast music over the speakers.
These guys are awesome! Super friendly and super accommodating for unusual requests! The manager here rocks! Thank you!

I used to the Starbucks app to order ahead, went inside after eight minutes. My order wasn't ready. I was given the drink after he finished now about 15 minutes after I'd ordered  I had to ask for the food I ordered, he told me to go talk to the cashier. And stand in line behind 6 people to ask for help to get the food that I had ordered ahead. So I had to be an asshole, and ask loudly if he could please help me out (I wasn't nasty just dumbfounded) so I could have the food I had ordered ahead. Never apologized for any delay and gave me a sarcastic have a nice day.

Fantastic customer service! I'm a regular at Starbucks and their kindness and great energy never disappoints. I wasn't sure on what drink to get today. So a barista helped me while being patient with me. While letting me try 2 drinks for the price of one. Awesome awesome awesome!!!   Thanks!

First try at the blonde espresso and I can say I liked it.  It has a sweeter and smoother taste to it, caffeine wise I cant really tell if it has a stronger kick.  As for the rest it was normal but I like that starbucks is trying out new things with there coffee even when they have 800 different things you can get and then customize on top of that (not complaining).  As always using mobile order has made picking up coffee on the go so easy and probably the best feature.
Very nice baristas! Great coffee! No line... had a great time.  Paid for it with my iphone which made checkout a snap! Will go back often! Outdoor seating available.

I occasionally come here to get drive- thru oatmeal for breakfast. This location seems to mess it up a lot. They dont include things like the spoon, or blueberries when i order blueberry oatmeal. It doesn't bother me too much as I've come to lower my expectations. The drinks are typically over sweetened. Maybe this is to cover up the over roasted, bitter, burnt beans?  Lighthouse Coffee down the road is wayyyy better. The way espresso and coffee should be. If Lighthouse had oatmeal I'd never come back to this store.

Oh boy, my visit here was really bad. There was no parking available and an exhausting line at the drive through. I really needed a coffee and was about an hour away from home, so after a very long wait at the drive through I decided I'll step out from that hough line and look for parking, it was worse.. I've placed a Mobile order and waited for about 15 more minutes until someone pulled out. Until I finally parked my car and stepped in, my mobile order wasn't even ready!! I gave them 2 stars only because the place inside looked very nice, clean and organized. They need to do something about the parking and drive through lines.

There's a reason this location only has 2 stars. This is Quite possibly the worst Starbucks I've ever been to... I ordered my Traveller's for a big work meeting a day in advance, came to pick them up the next morning and they had no record of my order. No apology. Just looked at me like I was the crazy one. This the second time this has happened. I will not be returning.

The long was so long this morning, and nothing ruins my day more than slow lines and a dirty coffee shop. BUT! This Starbucks is exceptional! First time at this location and the line is moving so fast, drinks are being called out so quickly and efficiently! Place is super clean even with a lot of people! My husband and I were so impressed by how well this store is run! Awesome!

We were here shortly after opening day.  Service was super friendly and prompt, and our drinks were yummy and correct.  Plus, TWO CONDIMENT BARS!  This café is also really roomy, and the parking lot is much larger and more well-planned than most SBUX DT locations I have seen.

The drive-thru line can block parking spaces if it gets super long, which it has a tendency to do. This can be very problematic if you're inside and come out wanting to leave your parking space.........  Other than that, it's your typical Starbucks. The inside is fairly small, but there are a decent number of tables and a couple comfy chairs where you can read, chat, or work.
Grazie JC and Kelsie for being epic baristas! You almost make me wanna live in PA rather than in North Carolina....
It's Starbucks.  It's on Tucson's Southside.  There is wifi.  The staff are friendlier than most other Starbucks.  There is rarely a long wait inside.
Yum! What a good Starbucks. They make good drinks and are friendly. Good seating and service.

It's a Starbucks, $5 coffee and a chill atmosphere. This is a relatively small one so there aren't a ton of tables. The people who work here are good and this isn't a super busy location so you can get in an out. I wish more Starbucks had drive thru so you didn't have to make a trip out of going to get a cup of coffee but it is what it is. My only knock is that the tables and floor here are consistently dirty. I know these are high turnover business but I have never been here and seen a clean table.

I love this Starbucks... Probably one of the busiest in the Spanish Springs East Sparks area. Always friendly and never got my order wrong... Sometimes the sand which might be a bit cool but heck I just press on.. The team does their best and probably can use some LEAN Management, heck they are always awesome.

Horrible customer service. Girl at the register talked to her friend as they made his order so they couldn't get anything started for anyone else. When they were done making my sandwich the girl literally threw it on the counter and walked off without saying a word. I had to walk up there and see if it was mine. Guess that's Starbucks for you, though.
Not my regular Starbucks, but convenient to where I was.  Tried the toasted coconut cold brew for my birthday reward to enjoy on a nice spring day in Reno.  Nice patio seating outside

I always order thru the app and I give them plenty of time to prepare. Half the time the order is not ready at 7 a.m.  Today I received the wrong breakfast sandwich; what I ordered was expensive.  Customer Service refunded my order; which was great.  This location is hit or miss on wait time and correctness of the order. The other Starbucks in the area are much better but unfortunately, they are not close to where I live.
24 hour Starbucks. Near the interstate. One time I had to wait a couple of minutes for my coffee, so they gave it to me free. Pretty sweet.
Worst Starbucks I've ever been to. They managed to screw up a 3 item order twice. We only went there out of convenience but big mistake.

I've been to this Starbucks many times, and I've never had a good experience. There are certain things that I know are out of the control of the baristas, such as:  1. Only one employee working at a time, therefore he/she works both the register and as a barista. With a long line, or even just a line of 2-3 people, this can take up to 20 minutes.  2. Running out of ingredients/the blenders breaking - both of these happened on my most recent visit.  3. They don't accept birthday rewards (the free drink you get on your birthday as a Gold Card member).   What IS, however, in the control of the baristas is the quality of the beverages and their attitudes. It's just impossible for them to get a drink right. Even the most common and the most simple of drinks (i.e. Caramel Macchiato, vanilla latte, etc.) are always so way off, that I end up just throwing it away... and that's after having them re-make it.    Yeah, if you can get to an actual Starbucks, don't waste your time here.
Took 20 minutes to get fucking oatmeal. No one asked if I had everything. Zero communication. And now I am late.

As others has said this is by far one of the slowest locations I've ever been at.  One person working taking orders and working as a cashier.  The majority of my time in the drive thru was listening to her taking orders. A definite need for more help or training.  My frappe was very good but melted a little while I waited to be served.

By far the best Starbucks in Florida!  I travel a lot and there are good and bad Starbucks......  and then there is Starbucks at Suncoast and 54. The employees are friendly and hold conversations.  The bar moves much faster than any other Starbucks I have ever been to.  Thank you Joey, Christine, Sherry, Demi, Just Jake, Phoebe, Kevin, Chelsea , Sania and anyone I missed!
Shirley has stupendous customer service. It's very hard to come across someone who is so nice and so welcoming in the mornings. She is such a dear and would like to thank her for being so kind!

Great food. Occasionally they do not have almond or oat milk. This becomes frustrating when my vegan friend wants a coffee and it is not very possible. I wish they would stock up more. I love Starbucks, but also enjoy sitting down to enjoy my meal, there Is not much seating at this location. Overall, I'll still always come back, but I would like more stock in popular items.

No ice in the ICED mocha expresso.  I swear it's warm, not even remotely cold.  To add insult to injury, they never called out my drink order once it was prepared.  Probably waited an extra 10 minutes twiddling my thumbs.    It's really shocking because no one else had ordered a drink after me and it was obvious that the drink order was mine.
7 employees working a few customers and not one clean table. In addition to that there is a nasty odor that make you want to leave. No excuse for this!

This is one of the busiest Starbucks in town but luckily it has a drive-through. It's actually a great spot for meet and greets and job interviews during the day as it is located in the Westshore business district.  Peek times are very crowded though.

Personable staff. Very friendly. Always willing to go the extra mile to make sure you get the drink you want made correctly. It can get busy at times, especially on the weekend, but they work as efficiently to ensure quality every time.

ONE star because theres no choice for ZERO .. wanted a nice cold brew drink, they didn't have the white mocha syrup it included, ordered their current special (kiwi star fruit refresher) they didn't have the star fruit syrup for it. Lost my patience, stuck in drive thru, avoid this Starbucks at all costs!!!

When to this Starbucks after having lunch at Pumpernick's restaurant.  There was no line. It was happy hour. My daughter and i were able to get a drink for the price of 1.  Great deal! My drink was made as a requested. I will continue to go to this Starbucks  when I have a craving.

This is probably my least go-to Starbucks. I was trying to complete the Bingo boards of visiting five different participating locations. The location is pretty good, it is right off of Horizon Blvd with parking lot. You can either park and eat-in or you can choose their Drive Thru.  The moment me and hubby walked in, the vibe is just off. As if "we don't  belong":( Idk if is just bc I'm too sensitive that day or ........ The male barista is just not very nice. I won't be returning unless I MUST.

Avoid this Starbucks like the plague. I've been to this Starbucks twice, both times waited faaaar too long. For example, we were in drive through just now, with only two cars ahead. The two cars ahead ordered a few drinks each. We waited a solid 15-20 minutes... smh!
The staff is very friendly. The store is always clean and even though it may be in a horrible location due to parking, etc. that's not their fault.  5 out of 5 for me.

Love this little starbucks! Every time I come here there is always the sweetest girl at the drive thru window. Never had a problem here or waited very long for my order. My only complaint is that the parking lot is designed poorly and if people are trying to back out they cannot because the drive thru line is blocking them. Besides that it's probably my #1 favorite starbucks in Saint Petersburg. Happy coffee sipping!
It took 15 minutes to pour black iced tea. I know this isn't the big city, but please have a bit more of a sense of urgency. Yikes.

Hands down best crew here!!!!  Miss Abbey you were the first one to remember me. thank you and congratulations on your new location and I'll miss your face!  Ronnel, Megan, Sara and Issac.Thank you for your hard work, your smiles even when you've had a bad day. You guys have been like my little family! Without you guys I'd be lost. My coffee has always been on point except one time but, she was a newbie cough..cough Sarah! Lol then the next time I seen her she mastered my drink. So, with all that the entire crew is pleasant, fun and great atmosphere!
This place is so filthy. I get it, it's a chain coffee shop. BUT, The bathrooms are always out of order or just nasty and gross, and the service is just the slowest.
Love Starbucks, love their coffee. But crystal was extremely rude at the cash register today. I hope I never come here again if she's working. Definitely needs to be trained on customer service.

This is the most inefficient Starbucks I've ever been in. I've never seen so many people walking around doing nothing while so many people were in line and the staff had a nerve to be chatty with each other while we stood in line. Terrible

Just had a 25 minute waiting experience inside. We went inside because the drive-thru line was backed up onto Westshore. We waited so long and were going to be late for work that we decided to get a refund. We waited in line for that and then at the last minute thought our drinks were next to be made so we decided to wait for our drinks. Ended up being inside just over 25 minutes. What's wrong with you, Starbucks? Hire more people. Expand your facilities. Open up a second location across the street or down the block. Or have a sign that says "your anticipated wait time is 25 minutes."
One of the best and nicest Starbucks I have ever been to.  Rarely mix up my order and are almost always friendly.  Really solid neighborhood Starbucks.

I regret it every time I come to this Starbucks. The employees seem to move very slow and appear poorly managed. I also see different people every time, which makes me think people don't want to work here. Today I had a mobile order that took 18 minutes to come out. Their napkins were completely empty, the trash was overflowing, and the bar was filthy. They also altered multiple customer's orders because they were out of iced coffee. I don't understand how I have such terrible experiences at this location when there are so many employees behind the counter. I love Starbucks and will continue going, but it will not be at this location.

A little too busy -- and the staff are a little too frazzled to really give the normal Starbucks experience. I don't want to be given a cup of coffee by someone who looks like they just want to clock out and go home :( That being said - the pours are good quality and the store itself is usually nice. Their food case is almost always empty of the really tasty things too.
They seem to run out of thing's early in the day which makes me rarely come to this location.   Service was fine. I love the Lemon Ale!
Went through the drive thru; the staff is very friendly, service is quick & the frappucino is delicious! I'll be back if I'm in this neck of the woods! :)
This is the worst starbucks i ever visited. People are rude and lazy. They were letting the person on register drown while others are just chatting. And to too it off, my drink is completely wrong.

I try to avoid this location as much as possible.  I love Starbucks  and will frequent many locations but this one is sooooo horribly slow.  Not sure what's going on with their management here.  Unfortunately it's one of the closest to me so at times I'm stuck going to it.  Don't go there unless you want to spend 20+ minutes.
Being from Seattle/Tacoma comes with certain expectations of a Starbucks team. Very under-trained staff. Very Slow. Unusual for every other Starbucks.
The barista won't let me order a soy latte with cinnamon powder... She insists it is for the cinnamon dulce latte only.

Received horrible service. We waited 30 minutes to order than another 20 minutes for our order. Drinks came pretty fast but the bagels that we ordered took 20 mins. The male worker that was heating the food ignored us when we were following up on our food order. He told us that he would do our order after he finished the order of the 5th customer behind us. We had to wait 5 customers after in order to get 2 bagels. Worker was very rude and lacked customer service. When asking to speak to a manager he said he would get them. We followed up with the other workers and they too said that they had called the manager to come up front. We waited and decided to call the Starbucks on the phone, only to find out that the manager was not on site. They upfront lied to us and intended for us to wait for a manger that wasn't there. We were extremely frustrated and upset with the service received.

Disappointing. Almost every single time I have gone to this particular location I order the same drink and they give me something different every time. It is really frustrating trying to explain to the barista what they made a mistake on. I have gotten the correct order maybe two times out of seven. Each time I order I said the same thing and they got the same information. Somehow it's something different. I enjoy Starbucks very much but, again this location was very disappointing.

Service was friendly enough; the condition of the store was not to Starbucks standards. I left the store hoping that I would get a email generated survey for this location. The bathroom was a mess, toilet paper all over the floor, the back of the toilet behind the seat didn't look like it had been cleaned in a very long time. There appears to only be one trash for the the entire (large) store and it was at the connivence bar which was jam packed and overflowing.

If I could give this place zero stars I would. It's the slowest and worst Starbucks I have ever been to. They're terribly inefficient, unorganized and walk around like lost children back there. The girl taking orders kept losing her badge for the register and she's making food at the same time. There was only one person making drinks. They were talking to each other and completely oblivious of how long their line is. We stood in line for almost an hour. They ran out of food. For being an airport Starbucks, it's horrible. Don't waste your time here
Best Starbucks on this  particular road trip across county.  They have excellent customer service for EVERYONE... they  also have free grounds for the garden. !

Y'all are litttttterally the slowest drive thru in Pinellas county. Do not go to this location if you need to get to work, just go to Ulmerton location.  Update: literally sitting here for 20 minutes again. I'm counting. Y'all need to get it together. Every time, not just once in a while.
Great drinks here love the Frappuccino  But it's busy here so I do mobile order all the time or you'll wait in line

I love this place! Great environment to hang out. Especially being from a church in the same parking lot, I spend a lot of time here through out the week meeting people, and on days to get away and study. All beverages are rad, and friendly staff. The manager is truly welcoming as well.

Depending upon who is working is how great the customer service is. Either great or sucks. No in between. Parking is always a nightmare here. Having other customers park where ever they feel like doesn't help the situation.
This is a place where you get your coffee and leave. Sketchy people hang out there often and the chairs inside are very uncomfortable. Not a great place for a conversation in my opinion.
Only medium size by Starbucks standards. Cool Portland Brew coffeehouse nearby on Murphy Road at West End is preferred alternative.
Jeremy is the best! He loves his customers, is attentive, quick, and always happy! New patio area is done! Go get your coffee at Starbucks in Bashas.
Horribly slow when it was almost empty and a bunch of degenerates hanging around

On one hand, it's pretty nice to be able to get Starbucks at such a central spot on campus.  I'm fairly new to the school and the area so there aren't a lot of places that I know how to get to fairly easily to grab a drink or something quick to eat.  Starbucks fits that bill.  I'm not ashamed to say that I LOVE Starbucks.  I like that I get consistently well made drinks, rewards for drink purchases, and something familiar pretty much anywhere I go.  I do have to say that their prices do strike on the high side of things.  Fine.  This isn't a surprise.  The surprise was that because this store is run by Aramark to some degree (my receipt said Aramark, not Starbucks) they have jacked the prices up even if further!!  If you can believe it!  I have a go-to summer drink -- venti, unsweetened, half green, half passion iced tea, no water.  They were able to make that for me no problem, but whereas I usually spend $2.81 at at normal Starbucks, this store charged $3.95.  That's crazytown!  I can't say I won't be back, but I'll be purchasing my drinks off campus whenever I can help it.

Extremely busy at 8 on a Saturday night. They only had three people working ... A cashier, a barista, and a girl pacing and cleaning and putting things away. The wait was ridiculous, especially when this third employee could possibly have been making drinks for customers instead of cleaning up... So she could go close up quicker I'm guessing. We ordered on the app and our drink was made well after the line behind us had all been served. When we do the same at the countryside mall  location, they are always consistent with the order in which they make drinks.

Nice location if your on your way to class at Webster. The workers have always been nice and never messed up my coffee. I just wish they had a drive thru. (Do to the "farmer's Market" I doubt this will EVER happen.

So I have only been here once but I came her with my sister after having an awesome time and a cat lounge/cafe and wanted a drink so we came here and I ordered a dragonfruit lemonade thing and she ordered a caramel Frappuccino. We ordered it with no espresso(cause we need to sleep at night!). We came in and I noticed it was actually a bit small and warm but the exterior is gorgeous. There are tables and plants outside. It wasn't too crowded and there was no line, staff was nice and it took like less than a minute to get our drinks. THEY ARE QUICK!! So overall the experience was pretty good. I will would come her again but get something else!
The coffee here make me wonder if Starbucks really cares about the taste of their coffee.

I only go to this location when I'm over at my mom's house nearby. There is a small parking lot that is usually overrun in the morning and people are aggressive trying to get spots. There is no drive thru option so when I have my baby with me which is 90% of the time it makes it a huge hassle to try to get inside. There is generally a lot of people around the door waiting for drinks and it's very busy. I ordered a cinnamon roll Frappuccino through mobile ordering during happy hour and they made me the non coffee version which was way too sweet to drink. I didn't want to go through unloading my baby and dragging her back in to have it remade so I just left and trashed it when I got home. The only bright spot of the experience was a man in army fatigues noticed me struggling to get my baby carrier around all the people waiting for drinks and opened the door and helped me carry my drink to my car. So nice.

Very fast and friendly service.  This morning I tried the blueberry oatmeal for the first time.  The flavor packets mix together in such a way that can only described as 'perfectenshlague'.  If you watch the show 'The Office', when @dwightshrute becomes acting manager, he defines being in the state of perfectenshlague as the optimal happiness and fulfillment. Perfectenshlague is how I would describe my experience today.    I give this 4 stars, because they accidentally charged us and fulfilled the order of an extra reduced fat turkey bacon sandwich.  Of course I ate it, but now I'm over my morning calorie count so it's going to force me to workout today.

Starbucks is Starbucks.  Always pretty good.  I went through the drive-thru, and was not busy too busy unlike what someone said previously about always being busy.  I guess it just depends on the time and day you go.  I went on Sunday around 2:30.  I'm not sure what the one-way issue was from the previous review, but everything I saw was 2-ways (in parking lot, the streets) so this place was easy to find and I didn't have a problems going in and out.

Stopped by to grab coffee and saw a familiar face behind the counter. Tyler worked at another coffee shop in town my family and I used to frequent, and needless to say we were all excited to see a friendly face at our new regular coffee stop. The entire staff was working so hard during what appeared to be a crazy rush, but they all had a smile on their faces. And the same could be said for Tyler. He was working just as hard as we always remembered, and we can't say enough about his attitude, politeness, and eagerness to help.

Still the WORST STARBUCKS EVER. I give one star because I eventually get my drink but it slow and usually wrong. The staff rarely looks up. When you walk in - the whole demeanor of the store is as if the customer is disturbing them. I have given it a second chance and a third chance and a fourth chance and it has not improved in the last nine months. For sure stay away or choose a different Starbucks if you are in a hurry.

This location is one of the best locations I've been to. The employees are very helpful and I never have any problems. I recently went during happy hour and the computer systems were down and the gentleman manually gave me the bogo offer whereas other locations don't try to help you. I will come again to this Starbucks. Wonderful service.

Did a phone order for a cafe latte and a hot tea- said 6-10 minutes. It took 5 minutes and order was correct. Not sure why others complain. It's way easier to do online order and pick it up than wait in a car line.

This is one of the ones I go to a lot. They always make my Soy Chai Latte just how I like it.   The parking situation can get a little crazy sometime. Don't get confused if you go to the drive thru. One lane is for Starbucks the other one is for the bbq place next door.
They gave me the most crap unprepared iced latte I have ever drunk!!! Don't come here

This Starbuck's single handedly feeds my Starbucks addiction. Why? Because they're always awesome! Rarely does the drive thru have a line and the staff is consistent and friendly. I have never gotten a bad drink or food item here and they are never out of items. They are open to making off the venue items and patient with me when I come in with 4 kids, order 7 drinks or try to pay with 3 different gift cards and redeem my points. I ordered several large catering sized coffee "boxes" here last year for a Christmas party and the staff was so helpful in deciding what I needed and what types of coffee to get. Avoid going between 2pm and 3pm or before 9am if you don't want any line at all as those times can get a little busy for this location. But this is, hands down, one of the best Starbucks locations in Reno right now. Believe me, I would know ;)

The cashiers usually charge me wrong and apparently they have no clue how the rewards cards work. If it weren't for hour breaks and their convenient location I would go somewhere else. Seriously canyon in better.

Giving only 1 star for 1 reason... The cashier was extremely inappropriate and rude. She told me what I owed and walked away to gather stuff, which is fine. I gathered the exact change and laid it on the counter. When she came back, she repeated the amount owed. I told her "oh it's right here" and she replied "NEXT TIME IF YOU COULD JUST PICK IT UP AND HAND IT TO ME, THAT WOULD BE GREAT". I was dumbfounded. Everybody deals with things in their job they don't like, but we let it roll off because we are providing good customer service. I say she needs to find a job she is suited for.

The workers were very rude. They were ignoring me even though I was the only one waiting. They look at me and completely ignored. Afterwards I heard one of the workers saying thay she didnt feel like talking to people that day. I was in shock. I will never go back tk thsi Starbuck ever again.
Highly disappointed it's 38 outside and this guy is telling me they don't make medicine balls and people have colds and it's one of the coldest days in history.Wow I'm not coming back here .
When I am in town, this is my place for espresso. Curtis even knows what I want without asking, triple shot on ice.

Honestly, I've never been to a Starbucks with such awful staff. I just moved to the state, 5 days ago. I've lived in NYC, Boston, Pittsburgh, Ann Arbor. Never, have I ever.. and I mean ever...had to deal with such disrespectful people in my life. I was extremely excited to have a Starbucks location  so close to my new home, not anymore. What a waste for the company. Such a prime location but they have filled their store with employees that should be no where near the public.
Great people, hard workers! Always gets drinks right, cares and positive attitudes! Pleasure coming here! 100 stars!!

This Starbucks use to be the fastest!  NOW is takes atleast 25 min in the drive thru and I couldn't get out of the line.   Its ridiculous. I'm not sure what happened but it's a total bummer. The girl at the window said they are really under staffed. But damn. Where is management?????
Trid Starbucks is amazing. The employees are so sweet. They wrote a note on my cup that said " You are loved. Have a wonderful day!" They have great service and basically no wait.
If you have a gold card, be prepared for them to give you a hard time about what you are authorized. This place is good at telling you what you can't have

I'm addicted to the  1/2 blueberry black tea  1/2 lemonade this summer. I love it. I get it without the cane sugar and it's not too sweet and very refreshing. I stopped into this location for an afternoon pick me up. The drive-thru is always busy here! I'll be back!

I really think Starbucks has gone down hill in general over the past few years.  I really wish there was a good, local coffee shop like the ones I see popping up all over other metro areas like in downtown Chicago, NY, LA.  Some whole foods are starting to have coffee bars and they serve a far superior product.  This location is OK to work at.  Lots of seating and AC, but service is very slow at times, especially in the AM.  Drinks are thrown together.  Shots are inconsistent.  Nobody takes time to add the shots to the water for an americano anymore which makes it bitter and destroys the crema.  And, last time I was in, 1/2 of our breakfast items were missed so 1/2 of us were done eating, the others were just getting food.  Anymore, bagles are warmed but not really toasted.     I was there last week and I watched 7 people wait in line for some time, then leave.  A few more just turned around after looking at the line.  Looking for a better place.
Free Wifi, Free Power for devices, over priced drinks. Exactly what you would expect.

I am a lover of Starbucks and going on here is always comfortable. It is big enough to sit down and have a nice chat over coffee and efficient enough to quickly run in and grab the drink of your choice.  Staff is very friendly and when you are a regular they often remember your name and order.  Every now and again you will find a bad apple but also will find those that go above and beyond to make sure you are a happy customer. If you love Starbucks, this is a great location.

Got a strawberry Frappuccino. Delicious as always. I also found the staff to be friendly to my kids. It gets kind of crowded in this Starbucks, but it wasn't too bad this evening.   Great place for. USI was meetings over coffee or tea. Especially if you can sit outside.
This location blows. Customer service was god awful. Everyone working there seemed like they hated their job. Maybe I just came at the wrong time of day (11 am) but I shant be returning ever again.

Hands down the nicest, cleanest, friendliest Starbucks I've ever been to. That alone would give it at least 4 stars,  but the fact that my drink was properly made, with good espresso and streamed (rather than burned) milk means this place gets all of the stars. A+, Seminole Heights.

There was this one employee she just started working there. Was very rude and started yelling at me and arguing with me. I thought that Starbucks employees were suppose to be very nice and always give what the customer wants. Customers are always right when I worked there if I had an employee that did that we would've had to reprimand her and if it happened again we would fire her because of her actions so I hope she doesn't work there any longer because that was not something I would like to deal with every time I want a coffee.

I've been here a few times now and I've never been impressed. They've gotten my drink wrong every single time. The first instance was a few months ago and I ordered a grande iced coffee. Plain. Nothing fancy. The coloring & taste was off which I mentioned to the barista. She was persistent by telling me that my drink was iced coffee so I left. I didn't believe her but she obviously wasn't going to change her opinion. I ran into a friend who is also a barista at another location and he too noticed my drink was too dark to be an iced coffee so he tasted it, and he deduced that they gave me an iced Americano with skim milk. The second time wasn't much better: I asked for an iced caramel macchiato and it was made horribly. It was very bitter, not proportioned properly, the ice was melting VERY quick, etc. I'm not asking for perfection but I've been to various Starbucks locations and my CM was never that bad. The most recent time I went there was today. I don't know why I did that. I should've simply went to the one at Westshore Plaza or beloved location in SoHo but I was being lazy. Guilty as charged. I used the drive-thru after hearing a lot about it. It was an hour after the morning craze so I figured it wouldn't be much of an issue. I had to repeat my order five times (iced toffee nut/vanilla latte) but it wasn't a life or death issue. The barista handed me my drink very, very quickly. It looked too light to be my drink so I assumed they put too much milk which has happened before. Oh well, right? I couldn't even check the printed label because two cars in back of me were vigorously honking. I didn't taste my beverage until I was halfway home. I noticed something wasn't right. The same designated Starbucks employee who took my order gave me an iced chai tea latte. I could've went back and returned it but it didn't seem worth it and I didn't have the time. Amazing. My advice? Avoid this place if you're able to. It has a nice scenery and the staff is generally very friendly but the drinks aren't made very well. You deserve better for your money. We both do.

This location has good seating and I'm usually able to find a chair quite quickly. However, I don't go here anymore because the Internet has consistently been slow. I don't get it, but 3/3 times the connection was ridiculously snail-like and I ended up leaving to go to the other Starbucks across town, where I had no problems. I don't go here anymore because I don't want to risk waiting in their long lines, getting a chair, setting up, and then discovering I can't even make any progress because their internet is frozen solid. Now I'll either stay home to work or go to another location.

I have such a love/hate relationship with this location. On one hand, the staff members are always friendly and more than willing to make sure the customer is happy. On a few small occasions, which I am not proud of, I have fumbled in my usual Starbucks fluency and forgot to say "iced" or whatnot in the midst of my ritualistic request for a "grande iced light caramel macchiato" and they were more than happy to make it iced instead of a latte when I realized my own unhappy mistake. Hey, when you have to remember FIVE fluent words to properly communicate your order, it happens, and they understand that. I appreciate that.  On the other hand, the line could NOT MOVE MORE SLOWLY in the mornings. So many times I've given up and drove right past this location at the sight of a line of cars dangerously backing up onto Veterans Blvd. and blocking traffic. The most baffling thing about this to me is I have (stupidly) waited in this long long tedious line many times out of pure desperation for my 11-syllable-long coffee order, and the line always moves painfully slow. Not sometimes, ALWAYS. In fact, I've timed it eagerly while on my way to work. On average, between 7:30am-10am this line may take you anywhere from 10-20 minutes on average to get through. Usually this averages out to about 15 minutes on any given weekday but there have been times I've waited for nearly 25 minutes in my car questioning the purpose of my entire life that I don't seem to have the decency and pride to say "forget it" and drive away like a normal functional human being with due priorities. It's a pretty consistent statistic. Unfortunately, I usually I don't have that kind of time before work to wait that long for my amazingly addictive grande iced light caramel macchiato. But I admit, there have been times in the past where I was a part of the problem (*shame*).  Anyways, by the time I get up to the window it's routinely about a 5 second exchange of, "That'll be $5.38, here's your drink, and here's your straw, have a great day!"....and I'm off! Sooooo my question is, WHY is it when I get up to the window it's all easy breezy but yet I'm sitting in a gridlocked parking lot for 15 minutes waiting on the people ahead of me?!?! I really WANT to understand!!! I really do!!! Are there an exceedingly large number of people in the drive through line with excessively complicated Starbucks orders? Are they fumbling through their wallets at the check out window because they failed to make their payment ready while they were sitting in line for 15 minutes? Are they all approaching the window and then suddenly deciding they changed their minds and now they DO want that whipped cream on their skinny double frappuccinos? WHAT IS IT?!?!?! WHY?!?!?! I NEED TO KNOW STARBUCKS!!!! Ok...sorry rant over.  My point is I really love the service and the Starbucks product in general but this location is a clusterf*** to get in and out of during peak coffee hours. I am forever highly faithful to my Starbucks coffee splurge but BEWARE if you are trying to hit up this location on a weekday morning with a time constraint. It might not be practical for most people to endure en route during busy mornings.

The staff here is great. They are always friendly, and helpful. It's one of my favorite local spots for a meeting, or just to work when I need to get out of the office for a bit. I wish they had more seating, which is the reason for the four stars.  But overall, I think this is the best vibe of a Starbucks in Fishers. As far as the drinks...it's Starbucks. They may not be the best or most creative, but they are consistent every time.

This location is the Starbucks closest to home - so when I do have a hankering, this is where I usually end up! I've always had a good experience here. The drive through can get very backed up during peak times due to limited space, so I usually order ahead and park/run in to grab my drink. There is a small but nice outdoor patio that has a great mountain view. The location further north on Oracle is much bigger, but I prefer this cozier shop that is far more manageable and well run (at least in my experience). Thanks, crew!

I hate to be that guy. Leaving a bad review is never fun, but this place sucks. The staff has an attitude, the drinks are poorly made, & always have too much ice. You're better off supporting local businesses, especially during the pandemic.   I'm from out of town & made the mistake of trusting the chain. I've been here a few times now, asking for really light ice only to get a drink that's 1/4th actual coffee, 3/4ths ice. Today, I got a drink that was literally half empty because I asked for no ice. The barista on staff was so stuck up about it that I felt compelled to mention how terrible this place is. You work at Starbucks, man. It's not that deep. 1 star

Though i know it goes against the grain with most hoity-toidy coffee aficionados: i happen to love the 'bucks! You've heard it: "oh no, Starbucks? They're too commercial or their coffee tastes burnt...and on and on." I do not like every venue nor every menu item but i dig the the espresso and when product and service blend harmoniously (as they often do): very few can match the Starbucks experience. Since i am writing this about a Santa Barbara venue i must mention the Handlebar downtown and of course the almost sublime Handlebar on De la Vina: both serving great coffee, beat the 'bucks food-wise (Roman-style pizzas one night as a special??) and thrill you with terrific service. (isn't it sad that we often expect bad service in coffee joints or other joints...for that matter?) So since i was in SB for a few days i knew i had to check out this venue particularly since it is a Drive thru. I know. I know. Sounds a little  kid like. But i can still engender, pull up from my gut the thrill i used to feel as a kid checking out a new store. And oddly enough it was my little niece and nephew who wanted to go here to experience the drive thru-ness of this place. And so of course we checked it out. The kiddoes were mostly interested in the Cake Pops, i wanted to see if the 'bucksters here could make a Triple Grande Soy Capp, Dry in one try as it doesn't happen often. I am happy to say that they got it right: perfect blend of primo espresso, soy froth and soy milk: all in the correct proportions. The espresso here tastes of cherries, citrus and yes a bit of smoke. But the blend is a beautiful thing. I am not a big fan of the brewed coffees at the 'bucks but many many people are. What i am a fan of is the French Roast beans which i brew at home. At home, with my coffeemaker i can extract a big fat coffee, slightly vanilla, slightly flowery, slightly citrus cuppa Joe! Odd, i know. But the 'bucks should look into how they brew...if you ask me. The Birthday Cake Pops disappeared inside the kids seemingly in 2 gulps. The Pops themselves are a secret vice for most of us. The cake is soft and buttery and the hard shell frostings are good, the flavors taste natural. As in the advent of the Muffins, i think that cake Pops were invented so that we can eat cake in the morning and not feel guilty. But the Soy Capp is for slow drinking: letting it dwell in my mouth in order to pull out all the inherent flavors swirling around. The young dude at the window went out of his way to be nice, talked to the kids, was reverent towards me (ha!). He repeated our orders to make sure he understood.  Pozzy experience here on product and definitely service but it is the first week of being open. How long will this last?. Good Stuff, though! I will be back and next time i will actually go inside to check out the latest version of Starbucks merchandising and marketing.

Girl in drive through window dropped the two quarters I put in her hands, asked me to get it for her when clearly i had no room to open my car door but I attempted.. ended up just giving her more quarters I had instead. She was not very friendly. I asked for my frap to specifically be made thick with extra ice and it was liquid):
The only thing that could make Starbucks better would be if a coffee/green tea didn't cost almost five dollars.  They always have the best staff and great drinks

I really, really hate how this Starbucks' parking lot is designed. For a location in a sprawling strip 80% dedicated to parking (of which maybe 20% is used on your typical day when looking at the entire parking), Starbucks has only one way in and the drive-thru makes it very difficult to park here.  Why am I leaving a review of a chain we're all pretty much forced to use for coffee? Well, I guess because there is some public art that kind of sort of resembles the "old" Starbucks logo.  Oh, also, there's a Pokemon Go gym and a Sponsored Pokemon stop.  So there's that, I guess...  Oh, and I'm pretty certain the Sheriff's deputy at the next table over was on a blind date. So come here if you like blind date awkwardness?
Best bucks around   Super service and nice employees  Highly recommended to new visitors

the worst starbucks i've been to.  it was great to have a location in the airport to stop in after a 6 am flight but i should have kept walking vs going in.  there wasn't a wait but i did have to wait for the baristas to finish their conversation before i could order.  there were 5 of them but no one took my order for a couple minutes!  again, should have kept walking vs going in.  i ordered a decaf cafe mocha but i didn't get it.  the baristas said it was decaf but of course it wasn't.  won't be back!

Love this place... I hate the limited seating though. Line moves quickly but you always have to take it to go because many seniors come and take over the place and stay forever. I can't complain about my drinks though, always on point
The employees were so friendly it made my day!! My order was prepared quickly and to my exact specifications!

I've always experienced fast and friendly service at this Starbucks location! I often order from here from the mobile app and have my assistant pick it up for us. It's always ready when she arrives.   I will sometimes go through the drive-thru as well and again, they are very quick! The parking situation is a little cramped, and I can't exactly review the inside of the store, since I've never been in it myself. The five stars are for the staff and the quality drinks they create. I have another Starbucks closer to my home and I will drive the extra couple of miles to get to this one. Not once have they messed up an order, when the one on Prater-Sparks Blvd have, repeatedly.   Also they are always well stocked when it comes to bakery items. Not once have they been out of whatever I've wanted.

This is ridiculous. A lot of people want coffee before work but it is bad that it takes 30 minutes. Why is there not another starbucks in this area? And on top of that, this one is out of half their items!! I want change!

It was a Sunday morning. 20 plus people in line. Tyler was the at the cash register, slowly walking and talking and acted like he just had a huge breakup or something . About 3 minutes just to take an order and payment. Severely unhappy guy not even trying to go a little faster for everyone waiting in line.  The barista making the drinks was faster and trying to speed things up, but her team member was holding her up.  Unfortunately there isn't another choice for coffee in the am inside the hotel.
It's Starbucks and it gets it right- all the time  ideal location  walkable from Chase Plaza- generous hours well organized with good high volume flow- attention to the brew

My visits to this location (W. 38th by Target) have primarily been through the drive thru but this time decided to walk in and order because the drive thru was way too long to wait; and I'm glad I went in!! It's spacious for social distancing, the staff was very friendly and plenty on hand. I went in a little after 7am, on the day they were giving out the new holiday plastic cups (they had plenty at that time and they were prepped and ready to be picked up at a table, you just had to show your receipt to the associate) I got my bakery item quickly and I didn't have to wait that long for my coffee either - I ordered the peppermint mocha and it was perfect! I would actually liked to have sat down and enjoyed my coffee inside if I had the time because the ambiance was just that nice! I also like the location as it's right off of I-465.

This is literally the worst Starbucks I have ever been to in my entire life. I live in St Charles, and it takes less time for me to drive all the way to St Peters to go to Starbucks, than it does for me to drive down the street and get my coffee here. The service is awful and slow. The servers are rude and they yell at each other in front of customers. It's like watching the first week of Hell's kitchen when no one communicates and everything they cook is crap.
