You can tell that they're so used to messing up drink orders that it has become status quo, so they don't even bother apologizing anymore. Expect to sit in the drive thru for 20 minutes if there are more than 4 cars in front of you. If you brave the poorly planned parking lot to park and go inside, their hand made signage directs you to stand in line in the wrong place and they'll not hesitate to point out the error of your ways. Is there a leader or a manager of this store? It seems like every one that works here is on their first day on the job.

Corporate office needs to evaluate this store! Slow service throw drive thru. Don't get orders rite the first time, I have been coming to this location nearly 6 years and the last year or so has been with soo many problems occur

Funny how we're writing A review on a chain that prides itself in consistency. The day before coming here I had gone to a different location and had a wonderful peppermint chocolate holiday frappe. However, I had a Vente frappe here with the same peppermint chocolate flavoring and was sick the entire evening with some kind of stomach bug. I don't know what they put in their coffee or possibly just dirty fingers is what my partner and I were thinking, but it wasn't a pleasant experience. What is more.... The flavor on my partner's drink which was the chai latte had to be doctored up. I thought to myself it's crazy that we spend five dollars plus onto drinks that are on sale and have to come home and add ingredients to make the flavor at standard. Probably better to order inside then to go through the drive-through as you can taste, watch them prepare it and ask for any extras. At least they do offer to redo the drink as she had way too much cinnamon and her latte. Luckily the cinnamon was on the top so I could skip it off before I had to add the extra honey to sweeten the mix.  AND the seating is so sterile. Nothing comfortable. Just hard wood chairs and a HUGE desk like table with metal military style stools.

I been coming to see TJ to make my salted caramel cream cold brew for months. He is the ONLY one who can make it right. I have literally went to all the Starbucks locations and no one can make it right. Thanks TJ, you rock!

I was here for the free coffee special, it is a decent size store with plenty of parking.  It was packed.  The ordering line is long, but the staff is very helpful and efficient.  The waiting time is 15 minutes but is understandable.  I do see the  barista got frustrated with the workload, however, the drinks that I got didn't drop the quality.

This SBUX is great!   Nice location (close to my office) and they have a drive through.  Inside is small but warm and inviting store.      Mornings can be busy here since there is a school right up the street and teachers are in line getting their coffee (had one yell at me to get out of the drive through line because she was in a hurry but thats Galena High School not this sbux).  Highly recommended - they are one of the few SBUX I drink at frequently and they have NEVER gotten my drink wrong.

Good service! This review is based on a drive-thru experience only. I was served quickly, the staff was friendly and the coffee was very hot and fresh. I didn't get anything fancy today just a cup of Pike...

Of the local Bux, this one is great for two reasons:  Drive-through service and very speedy service (a necessity with a drive through window).  The beverages are consistently good, and I have never encountered a surly Barista here.     I do not find this location terribly hospitable for lingering because the seating is very limited.

Drive thru is fast and the outdoor lounge area is so cool. It's a good place to meet a friend or something.  If I lived closer to this location, I would be here daily. Also, parking is pretty good. Lots of spaces.

Okay. So, it's Starbucks. I get that. However, this one is just high-key terrible. When I visited recently, they gave me the wrong food (twice) and messed up my drink, all while taking 20 minutes to get the stuff I ordered to me. They only had one overwhelmed person on cash register, and there were at least 7 employees in the store. I will likely not patron this Starbs again, even if I'm desperate. Their only saving grace was they had a Beatles' album on the
Pretty slow Starbucks.  Ordered my venti iced green tea sweetened and waited about 10 minutes for my drink. Note that there were only three other people ahead of me and the place wasn't busy.

I love that they have a drive through! I work in the area and it's very quick and convenient to just pass through. My favorites are the pink drink, caramel macchiato (with an extra shot when I need one lol), and the almond milk cinnamon macchiato! So delicious I know it's new but I hope they keep it around. If you have the Starbucks app you can place your order ahead of time and that way it's ready when u get their to pick it up fast. And when I'm hungry my favorite treats are the birthday cake flavored cake pops or the rice Krispy squares. I come here at least twice a week and have never had an issue, the drive thru line moves pretty fast and it gets me caffeinated on time! Thanks Starbucks!

Came before 8am. Great service at the register but terribly slow at making the drink. I ordered an iced green tea. I waited over ten minutes. Will go out of my way to another location versus coming back.
"And can I also get .." "Do you want whipped cream with that?" "No.. and can I also get.." "And your name?" "Mike...." *Awkward silence* "And can I also get banana nut bread."
Three $8 drinks Not even close to being made correctly in three days. This Starbucks has gone from being the best to the worst SAD STORE

I went here a week ago to get two iced coffees for my boyfriend and I. We went in and took them out to the car, only to realize they were (somehow) not made correctly. Mine didn't have any vanilla in when I had asked for it, but I don't mind black coffee so I just decided to keep mine. My boyfriend's had vanilla in it, but it tasted absolutely disgusting. He took it back and they happily made him a different drink, so that's why I'm giving them three stars. If not for that I would definitely give them less because we waited a very long time for the two incorrectly made drinks.
This is by far the best starbucks in the area. The atmosphere is good and the people making the drinks are even better.

A disservice to all the good Starbucks stores out there.  This is a "licensed" Starbucks, so it's not managed by Starbucks.  They give points but you can't redeem rewards here.  I was here last night and they were out of decaf.  They were also out of napkins and wood stir sticks.  I returned today (it was close by) and they are still out of napkins, and refused to even give a paper towel to someone with pastry goo on their hands.  They are still out of wood stir sticks as well.  Go elsewhere.

This I why I love Starbucks: First, the coffee cake! The best! Good with coffee, but without it, it's great also. Second, the frappe chino and teas. They are so flavorful! It also goes well with the food there. Also, the service is good, because they get the order right and don't take a long time... (usually). Lastly, they are definitely kid friendly. A lot of drinks kids can have and they are delicious . All in all, Starbucks is amazing. Enjoy!

I can 't believe the people here actually say the service is good. The eye rolls and less than friendly atmosphere makes me drive a few extra minutes to the one on Jefferson Highway.   Also can we keep mentioning how many times espresso shots are dead in your drinks? LOL I CAN'T EVEN COUNT ON TWO HANDS. Its been that MANY times. The wait in the drive thru on a week day? 20-30 minutes.   This is BY FAR the worst location in the WHOLE WORLD I have been to. WORSE THAN AIRPORT STARBUCKS.

I go to this starbucks occasionally after nightshift at the hospital. A few months ago at Christmas time I ordered a new menu item "Holiday Flat White" which I read straight off their menu on the wall behind the counter. When the cashier told the barista my order her initial response was "I don't know how to make a f*cking flat white" right in front of me. I received a drink none the less. Today I decided to go in for a green tea latte. I've had these before from starbucks and also have matcha at home which I prepare my own lattes with. To my astonishment their "matcha green tea latte" said it was over 400 calories. I noticed another menu item that said "matcha soy milk latte" and it read around 200 calories so I thought I would try it. The cashier was so rude and insisted I was wrong and said "it's the green tea latte. Matcha is green tea ma'am." Even after I gestured toward the menu board, she looked up at it and STILL insisted they were both the same thing. Did not even ask the barista. I wasnt arguing over tea after a 12 hr shift so I got the matcha green tea with coconut milk. Terrible customer service.

Quick stop on the way back from lunch for a chocolate croissant. So warm. So buttery and so chocolate. Delicious!  Staff was super friendly and very quick. I'm not a huge Starbucks coffee fan or I would have gotten coffee while we were here. I am a coffee fanatic! Don't get me wrong. Just not a fan of Starbucks. But I do always love their pumpkin loaf bread, their protein packs and now their chocolate croissants. First time trying one and it really just melts in your mouth!  Yum!

I've never been the one to review restaurants but Ive never been to such a disorganized Starbucks.  My order was completely wrong, they forgot to give me my other items and when I spoke up about it they made it seem as though I did not pay for that item. I was just in complete astonishment. The cashier was rude and did not greet me as I approached the register.  I am so disappointed. I will not return here.
Yeah I was clearly say they're open till 10 PM on Friday nights but I pulled up after 9 and they said they were closed. Weird.

The service here was great and the lady working the drive thru was very sweet and polite considering how busy they get, especially around the holidays.  The decor is very cute, cabin type feel, which is a bit different than the ones near downtown Tampa. I liked it. The drive-thru is a bit tight, so make sure to go slow.   I ordered a light frapp with soy milk and it came out perfect and yes, this location does Puppucinos! Which is really just a small cup with whipped cream, but there are locations that won't do it. My dogs loved the whip and I loved my frapp. Will stop back to this location next time I'm in St. Pete, because not all Starbucks offer the same level of service.
Visited the store twice and they messed up drinks in those visits. Staff is rude and not willing to serve the costumer professionally. NOT RECCOMEND

As many reviews I read about this place...they're all true!!!! There's always a newbie in training!!! I wonder if they're able to keep their employees? Anyways, I came here on a Saturday morning, no one was in there so I didn't care. But there was a new girl and a guy. I guess my drink orders can sometimes be complicated because she had to ring it up 3x. I noticed that I was overcharged too for no particular reason. I had a grande caramel macchiato and it's like $4.45. My total was $5.45 so my tax was literally a 1$? Wonder why....I didn't ask because I was running late. As usual lol but 3 stars for this location because my drink was super delicious!

I was thinking about where I could get a nice sandwich today and figured I would think about it over coffee. Stopped by my local Starbucks and, low and behold, they have updated their food as well as their snacks/cakes. Well, updated is a bit weak since I just ate a turkey sandwich that had the kind of turkey you get from the day after thanksgiving. It was amazing, simple and delicious. The havarti cheese just put it over the top.   For those who enjoyed the snacks and cakes before, they have updated them but you can still find close matches to your favorites - except they are fresher and tastier.   Bravo Starbucks for moving forward.
Super quick service, wish there was more places to study on my laptop (ie more outlets), but it's pretty much my go-to Starbucks :)

Headed for flight and stopped as I normally do.  Line was 40 people deep, wrapped around the store next door.  Stood for 15-20 minutes and did not move. No coffee is worth this aggravation.  They need a better system for folks who just want a cup of black coffee.

I love this location.  It's bigger than their old location down the street.  The staff is very nice and respectful.  They are fairly fast at making your drink, depending upon how crowed it is.  I'll definitely be coming back.

Was almost late for work because I didn't plan on the 15 minute wait. I loved the walk-up window, but not sure it was worth it to me. The guy behind me told me the wait is always this long and there are no more than three people working. There were two working that day during the morning rush.  I realized after I checked my app that I was not given a cup discount.

Small SB but very inviting.  They have plenty of people working so the wait is short. Music is playing softly in the background so it's not overwhelming to have a good conversation with others. Nice place to get some writing done.
So I went in there this morning and told the barista I wanted a large coffee that didn't taste like coffee with a lot of caffeine and im not sure what she gave me but it was dank.

I tried to do some work here today and I couldn't even with ear buds on.  Maybe I should have brought my larger surround sound headset instead.    One of the reviewers who said that there a lot of ladies from nearby who like to talk really loud and obnoxiously to draw attention to themselves or talk loud on their phone inside was spot on.  I left this afternoon after about 30 minutes after this couple of bleached and Botoxed middle-aged women with their trendy yoga pants were way too loud at the table next to with a seemingly never-ending torrent of petty complaints, gossip about other women and their kids, and name-dropping.  I came here for some coffee and to get some work done.  Not a headache.

Honestly there's nothing bad to say about this place. It was clean and it did the job if you're thirsty. The only thing I would say is that it's extremely overpriced for a small coffee. Overall I would recommend this place if you ever need a quick drink.
Worst starbucks of them all. Hours are not consistent,  slow on taking orders and preparing everything. Staff us not friendly at all.

If they would stop chit chatting with each other they would probably get my order correct.  I go once a week get w scone and a venti sweetened green tea and most of the time they do put the sweetener in but I'm done they were too busy chatting with each other they screwed my order up.  That's it for me with this place

Dear  Starbucks Co.  I drink your coffee when I'm in a pinch due to your baristas' lack of coffee knowledge and poor attitude. The lack of customer service has only increased with current Covid-19 regulations.   You know... I'm so ticked off, I'm not even going to bother further.
This Starbucks is close to my house and can get very busy if you just come to the store. The line takes forever so best bet is to perfect on the app and pick it up which I do and is more convenient.

This location never messes up my order! It's right near where I work and I can always be assured that my mobile order is perfectly made. Other locations will occasionally mess up my cold brew with cold foam, but this one never disappoint! Thank you staff!

As expected, this location lived up to the hype. Nice to finally see a Starbucks on this side of town. The service was quick and accurate. They have the absolutely delicious cold brew on tap. I will be a regular at this location.

This is my go to Starbucks location because I live close to there. Never had a bad experience coming here. The people that work here have always been friendly and courteous in my personal experience. They keep the inside of the store clean and organized and service is fast and efficient. I got the Iced Caramel Macchiato and they made it well. Sometimes, some places will put too much ice in the drink and it feels very watered down or it feels like you're getting way less than what you paid for. But here, they don't over ice. They're also generous with the caramel and you get a ton on both the bottom of the cup and on top of your drink. The only real downside to this location is because the store is located on the corner of a busy intersection, the small parking lot means that parking can be really limited at times. During a busy afternoon, the drive thru line can snake around the building and really spill over and hold up traffic. But other than that, good location. Will keep coming back here :)
since my drink at Starbucks is the same everywhere I go this rating was purely based on the staff. The gentleman in there needed to drink their own product as they seemed either bored or tired.

The line can be REALLY long in the drive through.. going inside seems to be punishment for not waiting in the drive through! I tried that once and they filled 5 drive-through orders for every body inside it seemed! Drinks are always yummy though!

I was pretty disappointed to find out that this Starbucks is closed all summer after advertising that it would be open all summer online. You have to actually go down to the store to find out that it's closed over summer. They need to update the hours for the summer stating that it's closed as well as the signage around campus saying that it's closed..
Low key think someone cut the wires. Didn't get my serious strawberry. Still entertaining. I'm about it.

Used to be my favorite SB to go to! Now the staff changed or management or something because getting a drink in the drive thru is about the most frustrating thing u can imagine. I have been in like for 25 minutes before! Don't let the number of cars in line fool you. That doesn't seem to determine your wait time. I hate that it has declined in efficiency and staff friendliness. There are other SB around and actually have had more success getting out of my car, walking into target getting the drink and leaving than sitting in the drive thru line at this one!

So you now have to wait in line to get the code to use the restroom and then wait in line to actually use the restroom. Oh and the barista who has the code miraculously does not have a pen to write it down so you can remember it. This is after the remodel where they though it was brilliant to serve you out of a trailer with no food and half the menu. Maybe go to Largo or something?

Went to this Starbucks this morning.  Placed my drink order... My name was never called so I went up to check on my beverage... sure enough it was sitting there my hot drink was cold! The Barista didn't bother to ask if I wanted it remade. Terrible customer service.

One star because the parking lot here is terrible. It's that bad. Traffic from the drive-thru line backs uo out onto Brentwood Blvd. When this happens, which is frequently, it's extremely difficult for cars exiting the drive-thru to leave. Nearly impossible to get one of the very few parking spaces that they have, and overall just a mess. This place was planned extremely poorly.

Wonderful uptown atmosphere. There are a lot of college students, business men and neighborhood locals meeting here. Some of the staff are friendly and others are defiantly not.  I love the beautiful patio and the atmosphere this Starbucks offer.

I drive out of my way specifically to come to this Starbucks location! The baristas are always friendly and quick, and my order is always accurate, consistent, and tastes perfect! The only downside is that there isn't a drive-thru, but the service is fast enough that it hardly makes a difference. Love, love, love this Starbucks!!
I wish I good give negative stars -2. This place is bad. They are not following Covid guidelines. I will not go back. Stay away people if you don't want COVID-19. No gloves, no mask

Not my favorite Starbucks   I ordered a iced venti decaf sugar free caramel latte.  When I got my drink it was definitely not decaf (believe me I can tell). But the barista insisted it was decaf.  We went back and forth with this until he offered to remake it.  I then got my new drink and I noticed this one had 2% milk while the original was fat free.  I tasted it, no flavor.  He finally added more caramel and it was then ok, not great.  I don't like what this store looks like either.  Felt crowded and cluttered. Staff was nice but I doubt I would be returning to this particular store.
Ordered a Venti Pile Place and the coffee was cold... not even warm! There needs to be a temperature check system to ensure customers are getting a hot cup of coffee.

I'm not usually a Starbucks guy.    I wanted a coffee and was on the way to visit friends; if I showed up with one coffee, I'd have to put up with sad puppy dog eyes, so I stopped at the local Starbucks.  There really aren't that many other options in the suburbs of West Edmonton. I actually think the coffee and the espresso are pretty average and certainly not worth what they charge for it.  But, this stuff is way popular and well branded.  I mean walk into a real Italian café and try that Tall, Grande, Vente thing and watch the retarded child look you're going to get.  Only Tall means something to a European barista and it ain't what you get a Starbucks.  A "tall" coffee is a single expresso made with twice the normal amount of milk as a "short".  It's not a "size"; it's more volume because you're asking for more milk versus the more common "short".  Similarly at a bar, you can ask for your highball in a "short" or "tall" glass; same drink, more mix.  If they get that much wrong, how good can the coffee really be?  The really annoying thing is that this location has a drive-thru window.  Who's brainwave was that?  They have customers both inside and outside and one barista.  So, guess what?  There is a long suffering line in front of the counter and one snaking around the building.  Additionally, you have no real sense of where you are in line or when you can reasonably expect your order to come up.  The staff look stressed out; the bartista definitely is and there isn't a customer with a smile on their face for a good thirty paces.  Oh, what a good time.
This location is always a friendly place! I stop in here whenever I'm walking along Mass Ave. It can be busy because of its location, but usually staff are quick and friendly despite busy crowds.
I mean if you went here for coffee, you blew it. But there are a lack of public restrooms in the area, so, you know.

Literally the worst drive through Starbucks experience ever. It takes so long to get even a single drink, that I was able to put my car into park and slowly write up this particular review. They don't even acknowledge how bad it is or that you've been sitting and waiting for 11 minutes.   Go literally anywhere else.

Asked for a Light low calorie beverage and was told they didn't have it... they didn't even offer to make a beverage with half the flavor or attempt making a low calorie beverage for me, so the rest of my family got their coffee while I couldn't enjoy anything.

Starbucks is good. It is always reliable and I can get my iced late just right no matter where i am.  Some indie coffee shops can't pull an expresso shot to save their lives and this is something i do not have to worry about with Starbucks.

this one i located inside DMAFB at the BX so it's convenient for our military and their family who needs their coffee fix. It can be crowded at times and service can be on a slow side. But so far they only messed up my order once. I ordered my son's fave (java chip frap) which is not that hard to make. But we waited for at least 15 mins for our order and it never came! The people behind us got their orders quick. I went in the counter and told them about my order. The girl behind the register talked to the barista and told us they would make our drink and did not apologize for their mistakes!. Anywho, the only time we go to this place anyway, is when we shop on base and my son or hubby craves for it. Other than that we go to a different location where they pay close attention to their customers orders.

The manager Emma was AMAZING! She was the most kind barista, and made me a personal drink based off how I told her I was feeling. She took the time to make sure I was feeling fantastic after leaving Starbucks with my drink, to make it through the rest of my day. By far my favorite trip to Starbucks, and would recommend everyone to to this location.

Stopped by on my way to work about 8:40am- the drive thru line wrapped around the building, which blocks a couple of parking spots in front.  If you are parking and plan to go inside, just remember that you may have to wait for a drive thru car to move out of your way before backing up.  Service inside was speedy.  Cashier knew what he was doing and was very friendly.  My drink was finished by the another barista and handed to me before I even left the register.  My oatmeal was warmed and bagged about 2 minutes later.

I drop by here from time to time to see if perhaps the drive-through lane has gotten faster.  After all, I heard about the Red Sea and Pharoah and all.  But no such miracle today.  I was the third car in line.  Time from order at the clown's mouth to touching my cup of joe was 11 minutes.  The only thing I can say to those who previously reviewed this as a "fast" drive through lane" is, "should employees REALLY be reviewing their own restaurants?"    Meh.  This place is a turtle's best friend.

Honestly, I came to Starbucks to study. I ordered a regular s'mores frappuccino that took some time because I think the barista forgot my order for awhile so they made it a bit late, but they thanked me for my patience. Starbucks has outdoor and indoor seating available. There are sofas, a long table near the window and at the center, and bunch of small tables. There are outlets available near sofas and some small tables but definitely outlets available right under the table near the window. It is a good place to study if you don't mind some background talk. And yes, the frappuccino was good :)

Incredibly busy Starbucks location so I do understand that the employees may get short tempered or cranky.  It's unfortunate that customers suffer for their moods.    I was ordering my drink and with the noise of the airport announcement behind me and steamer noise behind the counter, I didn't quite catch the cashier's question but thought she asked if I wanted anything else.  I said "no thank you," but that wasn't an appropriate answer to her question so she said "you gotta stay with me."  I apologized and asked her to repeat her question.  I answered and let her know that I didn't leave I was standing there the whole time but just didn't hear her question.  Ok.  Moved on.  Then the barista serves up my drink and I'm certain I heard "green tea latte, extra soy."  She was speaking pretty fast.  Anyway, after she returned to the counter I asked is she said "extra soy."  She seemed annoyed and told me that the size of the drink is what I ordered.  I explained I understood that but is the drink extra hot or extra soy?  (The flavor isn't the same if it's with extra soy and not what I ordered).  She said "I said extra hot, soy."  Ah.  Ok.  Great.  Got my drink just as I ordered but please keep your attitudes to yourself.   It's unfortunate that the area by the airport gates is noisy.  I'm sure customers really do want to make the ordering process efficient and friendly.  A little understanding, please.  Hope your day gets better Starbucks IND Concourse B18!.

The lady works in the counter for making drinks in the morning on Sunday seems to be very mean or racist. I said to the male employee that I wanted the drink to have no ice, and he wrote it on the cup. It was understandable that she mistook it for having ice. But after I asked her to take the ice out, she didn't even say a word with the mean face and took the ice out silently. I said thank you to her, and she still didn't want to say a word to me with the mean face. But she said "have a great day" with a large smiley face to the white customers before and after me. I'm wondering if this is the overall starbuck employees' attitudes to people with different color.
This is right by my house. I go there usually during the hottest part of the day for their Very Berry Hibiscus drink.. The staff is always really nice.
Nice, new open floor plan!  Would have been 5 stars but they don't have a single cushioned seat or couch available for relaxed seating.

Best starbucks in Reno. The drinks are always made right (and the baristas there make great recommendations for new drinks to try also) and they are really really fast at making the drinks for you. They're pretty nice there too.

Again typical of this Starbucks location.  Sat morning with 1 person on counter and 5 working drive through.  Line out the door and saw 5 leave already.   Never a manager around to balance workload.  Lol... love watching the robot workers not making decisions.  *update*. 40 min to get a hot tea and microwave sandwich.  WOW

Honestly this is my least favorite Starbucks to go to. It's always crowded, doesn't ever really seem clean and there is always so much staff there and sure on busy days they're  needed to keep up with all of the customers, but otherwise they just converse with each other the whole time they're making drinks and barely stop to announce your beverages. I also hate their beverage pickup spot, it's so small and I can never tell which drinks are mine or if they're for pickup. They need a better system in place because I don't have as many problems at other locations with the confusion over drinks. I also ordered a white chocolate mocha the last time I was there and it was sooo bad. That's not the first time I've had bad drinks from this location specifically. Not sure why they are always missing the mark. I do have some good experiences but those are usually few and far between and I'll opt to go to a less crowded and cleaner starbucks.

If you like Starbucks drinks then there is no issues since they almost always take the same no matter the location.   This location is smaller but has excellent use of space.  The staff is extremely helpful.  I find the wifi to not be very good here but that is the only downside.   Also usually very crowded

The Goods Cannot function without a latte. Leboulangerie is the new bakery and it is much better than before   Ambiance 1. I like the faint lights in the ceiling kind of rustic chic  Service  1. These people know me all the time!!!  Remember me by name!!  MoJo 1. Anything drink, I love them all but sugar free coffee is my fav
This Starbucks is the best location on the Westbank! Drinks are always consistent and perfected. The baristas here are super friendly and provide the best service.
This Starbucks is a nice little shop close to my office.  Staff is friendly and beverages are always very good.

Arnold, MO has a new Starbucks! Yay! The service here is wonderful and they are fast!  This location is similar to the Fenton, MO location but the decor is upgraded and more spacious.  They also have a drive-thru.

This location gets crazy in the morning and around lunch times, even in the summer. Because of the high volume, they are often out of some things, like sandwiches and baked goods, so you can only really rely on the drinks. Some baristas are sweet, others are there just to get the job done. There are two levels which is nice for meeting with a friend or getting some work done.

Rude workers who doesn't care about your drinks. I asked for a Frappuccino and wanted to ask about how many pumps of caramel syrup and I asked for 4 and when he was talking to the barista he just told her to do whatever. And when I got my drink, they forgot the whipped cream and when I told her she just messily put some on spilling the whole drink on my cup which is now sticky. Also they didn't give me friend the right change. My other friend also received the wrong order and she asked them to remake it and they gave her a grande when she ordered a venti.

Pros: It's a Starbucks. Nice seating in the back and outside. They appear to have brought in some new baristas that are a little more on top of things. My dad buys me starbucks gift cards.  Cons: Close to the worst starbucks I've been too. They are consistently slow and have messed up or forgotten my drinks several times.   Call me a coffee snob, but they always let my shots sit for a long time before adding the milk to my latte, resulting in a bitter drink. (Starbuck's "policy" is to never let a shot sit more than 10 seconds--the layers combine and it goes bitter).  I'm used to west coast starbucks, and the drinks definitely taste different here.
Worst Starbucks I have ever been too every time I've gone service is always good but the drinks are terrible tastes like burnt coffee every time

Definitely not your normal, everyday Starbucks. This is unidenified flying coffee experience! Even the interior looks different. I'm not sure what it's like in their drive thru, because when I make the drive out here, I have to go inside and sit for awhile. The ambiance is perfect. There's tons of room for the students from SLU & surrounding universities to come and study and...*drum roll* THEY HAVE A CLOVER. Oh my goodness, when this store opened with a Clover -- I literally screamed. It is the best tasting brewed coffee you will ever have. Unfortunately, that is reflected in the price. Around $3-ish for a tall? Kind of makes you feel bad when you put your creamer in.
This has got to be the worst Starbucks in Canada. Always new people, the drinks are inconsistent and the service is horrible. Definitely needs new management!

This is my favorite Starbucks. They staff is always super nice and it's busy in the morning of course... What coffee place isn't?  The seating is nice and there is a lot available inside and out. It's ways very clean and even the bathrooms are always clean.   My order is right all the time and usually pretty fast but I order the iced chai latte so that's pretty easy. Sometimes I order a snack too and they usually get that right as well despite being so busy.   Overall this Starbucks is my favorite and I go there often. One thing Santa Barbara/Goleta is missing is drive throughs!  So annoying but oh well.   The location is also pretty perfect for me as well and always a lot of parking available.   There is also a Starbucks right inside the Vons but less selection for food and no seating.

So, let's start this off in the proper context. I'm not the biggest fan of Starbucks coffee. I think it's a bit too acidic, and I prefer a generic breakfast blend over anything they make.  That being said, this location has great staff, it's clean and I have no complaint other than my coffee preference. I generally rate Starbucks as a chain, so 3 stars is what I expect I expect if any chain. This place gets 4 stars, so that tells you it's cleaner, friendlier and more worthwhile than other locations.   Order, add Splenda, drink up and enjoy!!!

Someone was kind enough to spend their money buying me a Starbucks gift card so all I ask is that my drink be made correctly. Apparently, this location does not comprehend the value of a correctly made Starbucks drink. I ordered a tall blonde vanilla latte, hot. The first time, they made my latte iced. The second time, they made my latte with regular espresso instead of blonde espresso, and then had the audacity to get an attitude with me because I asked for my drink to be made correctly twice.  I also wanted a reduced fat turkey bacon sandwich. I was told that they were out of breakfast sandwiches, but someone in line after me ordered a ham and cheese croissant. I politely brought it up to the barista that I thought they were out of breakfast sandwiches as per what I was told, to which she then replied that they were out of breakfast sandwiches so I asked if you're out of breakfast sandwiches why is the person behind me getting a ham and cheese croissant isn't it considered a breakfast sandwich? Then I asked, why is it that a reduced fat turkey bacon sandwich is considered a breakfast sandwich but the ham and cheese croissant is not, to which the barista reply that she doesn't know and that whether or not the turkey bacon sandwich is considered a breakfast sandwich depends on who you ask. Then she had the audacity to get an attitude with me yet again saying that regardless they were just out of the sandwich. Hi definitely do not recommend this location of Starbucks because of the rude Barista's, and the horrible communication that takes place. Save your money and buy Starbucks coffee beans from your local grocers or go to one of the licensed locations like the one in the Hyatt regency on Perdido. They have great customer service, make great drinks, and have much better communication skills.

\Very disappointed in this Starbucks on Washington and Magazine in New Orleans. Used the restroom as my husband was ordering a drink and was really shocked at the filth of the place. As I made my way to the back of the Starbucks, I had to wind my way around many bags of garbage stacked near the entrance to the bathroom. As I managed to get into the bathroom I was really surprised at how little the management cares for cleanliness. Too bad, it is a really good location. It certainly needs new management and some lessons on how to clean.

They have people working within inches of each other.  They give out no gloves or masks.  Poor window person getting dirty money & cards within a foot of people in cars.  Really?  Is this an essential business.  Do not patronize these greedy corporate entities.

I was working in the area and stopped here to caffeinate myself for my 90 minute drive home. If you're not college student age (the place was packed full of kids doing their homework, which I respect), you will pretty much be ignored at this Starbuck's. My 20 year old colleague was proof of that! I saw him get treated better than my 40 year old self and my 50 something colleague. I was holding an empty canister of half and half to alert the staff that it was empty and needed a little for my Americano and the cutesy young blonde working looked right at me, and continued her conversation with her coworker until ANOTHER COWORKER from the back noticed me and took care of the situation (maybe I should've asked my 20 year old coworker to get me the creamer, I would've gotten it quicker) The fact that there's a Vons in the same shopping center WITH A STARBUCKS in it should make these people give better service so the clientele doesn't go walking to Vons for their coffee fixes!! I have a feeling that's what anyone over the age of 30 does in this neighborhood!!!

Located in Broad Ripple, it's always busy not to mention difficult to find parking for if it's peak time. I've always had good service here but the line deters me away most of the time. I love that I can mobile order to skip the crowd but since it's in the middle of the strip, there is no drive-thru available.

Conveniently located on the Galleria's second level, just outside Nordstrom's women's section and adjacent the Disney Store. If you can't find it with this navigation you're at the wrong mall lol..  It's a very inviting space; full of color, character and cool barista's. They have tons of merchandise for sale from tumblers to coffee beans. Tables inside and outside the store are in wifi range. There's an extraordinary feature at this SBUX, the exposed brick detail both inside and outside of the store. I'm a sucker for exposed brick.  Be advised its in a very busy mall so there is no peace to be had here...save your work for another location.  ~Christy
Plenty of space and friendly staff. Designed for the road warrior. Thank you Starbucks.
Slowest Starbucks I've ever been to- 2 cars ahead of me and I waited in line 20 minutes!!!

My review is not for the staff but rather the clientele and owners. Why do people always use the handicap parking spots to pick up mobile orders? Most stores only have 1 mobile parking spot if any at all. Are people so narcissistic that basic laws and human decency are cast aside for a cup of gourmet coffee? Maybe the management can start enforcing the parking or add more mobile spots. I have found this to be true at every Starbucks I have visited in 6 different states.

So, I wouldn't normally review this type of place, seeing as its just such a massive chain and you generally know what you're going to get...but the baristas here have completely exceeded my expectations and they alone get 5 stars.   Each time I've stopped in here, I've rarely seen the same employees for some reason. Regardless, they have all been very friendly and accommodating. I haven't had to wait in line for more than 2 minutes and the store has nice seating options, inside and out.   Now, what has inspired me to write a review, you ask? As aforementioned, their accommodation. I came in on a Monday, got my usual drink, and then also got my usual sandwich-- the spinach & feta wrap. As I'm driving to work, I get towards the middle and it was frozen. The egg was like an iceberg. Irritated, I had no choice but to throw it out as I didn't have access to a microwave at the time.   I didn't want to go back for a while, but I returned that Thursday as I saw that their Refresher beverages had returned. I placed my order for the Berry Hibiscus, and I was asked if I wanted a sandwich. I hesitated (I don't like to complain a lot), but I told her about my experience on Monday. She was very apologetic and said that my sandwich that day would be on the house. They made right my prior, lacking experience. Kudos to you, fantastic baristas!

The big Green Hills Starbucks.  Nashvillian Nicole Kidman has occasionally enjoyed the coffee here, when she's not helping hubby Keith Urban with his rehab.  Other celebrities and regular folk  enjoy sitting down for a drink of brew and some of Starbucks expensive desserts.

They never disappoint. I had a bad experience at the Algiers location, but this location made up for it.    Update- This location has nvr disappointed me until today. The girl at the drive through window has to be watched . From the point of ordering, she repeated my order back wrong. I corrected her and there was silence lol. She then tells me to just pull around. I get to the window and ask her to repeat what she has. WRONG. I correct her. She gives me my drink. Still wrong. It's like...  i give up... they're still a good location but this girl idk - and she has an attitude the entire time . Why are YOU mad?
My favorite Starbucks.  Great service.  If consistently order the same thing and stop in a few times, they'll remember you by name and your drink order.  Great people here!

Overall awful experience. Waited for my food for 45 minutes and in line for 15. Despite the fact that there were about 20 customers waiting for their drinks, the baristas still had time to chat and take their time.

3/01/19 - Eddie's last day. He has been a plus plus for this location. He will be missed immensely. ;(  2/12/19 - So disappointed to hear that our wonderful asst manager Eddie The Arizona Pavilions location will be leaving us.   1/11/19 - VERY disappointed that they didn't have my favorite sandwich today, chicken and artichoke  11/20/18 - So disappointed today. Not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on   Once again we are here. Our favorite place. Love our Baristas. Jessica, Treigh, Caleigh, Alex and Taylor. If I've forgotten anyone please forgive me. So many great people work at this location. :) And of co

The Starbucks in the Joe Crowley often messes up orders or takes forever to get out food. The blonde manager is really rude and doesn't seem to have a good control over her staff. My friend and I have ordered drinks there with curdled milk and when we went for new drinks they gave us the same old milk and glared as the drinks were announced. This is not the first time I've had bad milk so I will probably stop going to this location
2 stars for coffee.. sometimes it's very weak and flaw. 5 stars for customer service. Polite & friendly  baristas.

So , I order a peppermint mocha iced coffee, not a Frappuccino . I have ordered this drink many times over the holiday seasons . In Brandon , Tampa , Orlando and Jacksonville locations . Never had a problem.  When they gave me the drink it was light in color heavy cream vanilla with clear liquid on the bottom. Clearly not a peppermint mocha but I take a sip to see what it was, and it was just pure mint . I definitely didn't taste chocolate and I definitely didn't taste any coffee. So I went up to the cashier and I said "hey this is not my drink I ordered a peppermint mocha."  She then told me that was my drink and I pointed to the picture to point out that it was not a peppermint mocha . She said it was .  Unfortunately I had to go to work so I couldn't sit there and argue with her. I threw my drink out on the way out, it was a un-drinkable. One of the most disgusting drinks I've ever had. So I basically just gave  Starbucks free money. Please do not go to the Starbucks unless you just want to give away your money.

I love this Starbucks location! The baristas are always so nice. I come here to study and am never disturbed by noise or anything else that's distracting. It's also close to the places I frequently go. (:

This location can never seem to get an order right no matter how many times you repeat it to them. Today I ordered a certain kind of hot tea, I repeated myself twice, they still tried to give me the wrong kind even though the two sound nothing alike and I had to wait for them to remake it. I got home and opened my tea to let it cool and there's a long black hair floating in it. So there's my money down the drain, literally... again. Because this isn't the first time this has happened. I've ordered green tea and been served straight unsweet lemonade mix (which looks nothing like the iced green tea) and had to go back and waste my time waiting for them to remake it. I've had metal in my green tea latte because they are sloppy with opening packaging...and it's always an "oops sorry about that, if you come down we'll remake it." Like it's not a big deal, like I didn't almost SWALLOW a shard of some kind of metal foil, like I have the time to go back down there because of their mistake. They really need better training at this location, until then I'll gladly drive the extra 5 minutes to ina and oracle and stop wasting my money here.

My absolutely favorite Starbucks!! It's is always so clean and everyone is so friendly!! If I could give this location more than 5 stars I would!! There is a man working that was so kind to me last week it made my day, I think his name might be Joe, but love love love him!!

Why does anyone go to Starbucks?  Slow, dirty. The incompetence of the staff oozes throughout the entire store. So many better places close by. If you want long waits, poor service, dirty store, by all means this is your place. Try the Mustache Cafe for the best in SB.

Rude & inconsiderate staff. After waiting 20 min in line, we ordered lunch. One of the drinks was super sweet and my son needed some water. I walked up to the barista to ask for a glass of water and she rudely told me to get in line. So I waited another 15 minutes to get a glass of a water. I'm glad we're just visiting, our Starbucks at home is very welcoming.
Excellent location, Clean and app orders are ready promptly. Only suggestion would be more consistency in their brewing.

Typical Starbucks. Fast and efficient employees. Good amount of seating and my drink (Trenta strawberry Açai refresher with light ice) came out perfect. Even though there were a lot of drinks before mine I don't think I waited more than two or three minutes. Overall I left satisfied :)

I have been hesitating on writing a review for a month now, but I officially will not go back to this location ever again, so here it is....  The location is great, as it is right around the corner from my new job. I have gone here at least 15 times in the last month and a half, and literally, no sarcasm, they MAY have gotten the drink correct once...ONCE! After the first five visits of leaving there with something i did not order, i started to realize i need to double check, and triple check with the baristas if they are making it correctly. I know this is annoying, as I worked for Starbucks myself for six years, but i got tired of spending money on something i don't want to drink and just "sucking it up" every time.   This most recent visit, it was the same ole ordeal. I double checked with the guy taking the order, just to make sure he understood. Then i watched the guy on the bar making it incorrectly, but i thought to myself "give them the benefit of the doubt, maybe that is not your drink"....but...like always...it was. I had to say something this time, and the person i am, i felt bad about it....but I just reached a limit. Keep in mind this is not a crazy out the ordinary type of drink, like i know they get hit with often, or i would understand the amount of mistakes. I could understand once or twice or even five times not getting it right, i am a reasonable person, but 99% of the time?! Flabbergasting.  They are super nice people, so i do not want to bash them..but they just seem so clueless... The irony involved was how they were talking to each other about how they are "Coffee Masters"....in which i had to physically turn around to avoid laughing at.   In conclusion, if you want what your paying for...do not go to this location. It is clear management does not take the time to make sure the Baristas are properly trained. Or maybe everyone that works there is stoned?

They did a really good job they got my drink on the first try no questions asked and didn't even charge me for the drizzle being that I didn't want it inside the cup which a lot of the Starbucks are doing now and seems very silly to me since I don't have it in the cup and it's not using all of that product I shouldn't have to pay $.60 for something that is such a tiny amount.

Fairly rough experience here...  it could be pages of me explaining about the experience but I will save you the time.  The moral of the story is that I knew more about ringing up my fairly simple order than the guy working their did.  Sad but true.  If this hipster is working... I'll just about face.

Drinks were good. I had to wait to be acknowledged by one of the 3 baristas. They saw me, but continued doing what they were doing before finally coming over and greeting me to take my order.  Hospitality is one of the things Starbucks is known to deliver, so it's pretty much expected.  Otherwise, like I said, the drinks were good and the place was clean.

This Starbucks is a mess. From web orders to the check out, nothing is set up to be customer friendly. The web orders are housed behind the counter. That is totally fine, but the employees actively ignore people. What's the point of mobile orders if you need to wait to get drinks in line? Next, the barista there this evening was in no rush or concerned about doing anything quickly. He filled up the lids, stared at the blender for one drink, remade the drink, and did nothing else. The cups were piling up. Each time I go here, the experience is the same. It's very disappointing.
Pretty good Starbucks but they are busy so I think some things fall through the cracks. But all in all a good spot with hot coffee.

Today, my boyfriend and I went to Starbucks. We had to repeat our orders 3x before the lady took our order. Then she never took our name and had a poor attitude. Our orders were wrong. We asked for iced coffees with almond milk, was charged for such and given hot coffee with regular milk.   It was highly irritating to have to repeat our order and still have it completely wrong. We couldn't wait to get it fixed since we had an appointment. It was just frustrating
I tried to give them a second chance. They are very rude at drive thru and my drink was incorrect. Save your time and go across the street to Wawa to get your cold brews and espresso

So I have never reviewed a Starbucks before because they all seem exactly alike- In fact it's like they did it on purpose so you always feel like you are in familiar territory. This one gets a special shout out though because the barista was so gosh darn nice!   It's also much more open than some other SBs so you can sit and work without feeling like someone is breathing over your shoulder.

I rushed to Starbucks because it said they close at 10pm and I get there right at 9:30pm and they tell me their closed. I tell the barrista I thought you guys closed at 10pm and she said on Thursday's we close at 9:30pm they need to fix their hourly schedule I'm highly unsatisfied and disappointed.

Generally speaking, the people working at this Starbucks are friendly and get your drinks just like you asked for them, so I've never had any complaint. Up to this morning I would have given 5 stars. But this morning the male server who handed out my drink at the drive thru had the filthiest finger nails I've ever seen. No food service worker should ever have such nasty looking hands. I couldn't find a way to comment inside the Starbucks app, so here I am. If it happens again I'll stop going to this store.

Pros: Super friendly folks, and did not have to wait long.   Cons: They were pretty busy and the lobby needed some TLC.   Pro Tip: On your birthday, you get a free coffee, go for the Venti!   Catch this location in our Vlog on YouTube! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCdUdlMFpgU73KIRDdTvO45Q/featured?  Check us out on Instagram! @mkandtj

This Starbucks is very nice! In general I like Starbucks and it is consistent with others. My drink is always perfectly hot. When I order to-go from my app it is always waiting for me, which is super convenient!   I also like the outdoor seating. Although it's on a main road, they have a rustic wooden fence blocking the view of the cars going by. I always appreciate that they give free water if you ask as well, no purchase necessary!   My only critique is the price, obviously. I don't drink regular milk so I pay +$.60 for soy which is delish but makes my tall drink $4.33. That's a lot for a latte. However, I know it's a luxury and I have learned to expect it, which is why I still give it a 5 star review.   PS pumpkin loaf rocks my world

Well it feels good to give a Starbucks 5 stars again. This Starbucks is huge!!! And the employees here have been trained great....finally some very personable and helpful employees! I've been longing to try the nitro coffees and the barista at the register recommended one for me to try. She wasn't bothered that I asked her and didn't rush me along. When my coffee came up, the other barista realized she poured me the wrong one and gave me that one plus the correct beverage. So now I've been sitting here for two hours very caffeinated! I will definitely be coming back...especially with the non-claustrophobic feel that this store has. I could hold a dance class here with all this open space! The only complaint I have is that parking is pretty slim, but they can't help that. I'll fight someone over a parking spot to come here and work!

place is an icebox all day and night. way too cold to sit and drink coffee......staff is ok. starbucks is the problem.  place is an icebox all day and night. way too cold to sit and drink coffee......staff is ok. starbucks is the problem. their stores are ice cold across the country

I get my daily coffee from here, and I'm glad that I do. The staff is very personable and welcoming, and their store is very decked out for holidays and special occasions. Like any other Starbucks, they're busy, but they handle rushes and large crowds very well (especially under the pressure of cranky middle aged men and women throwing a fuss over coffee beans). If you're in the area and interested, get some coffee here!

The customer service is great and what can I say....Starbucks has the BEST COFFEE, cake pops aroumd! I LOVE Halloween time, my favorite Pumpkin Spice Frap is available. I wish they'd bring back the Unicorn Frap. I was unable to get the sour sauce in mine when I had it since everyone ran out of it but it was still delicious. The insides cozy and they have two lounge chairs by the door.
The gals making the coffees were great but the gal heating up our banana bread was slower than molasses in January. Minnesota s January that is.

Great customer service .There was an issue with my order a few weeks prior, I was in a rush and the have me a recovery card. I wasn't sure what that was so I called and talked to the manager . He told me to come and he'd take care of me since the card didn't cover the mistake. I wasn't able to come in right away but when I came in and explained it to the other manager he took care of it and let me keep the recovery card. Super understanding and I appreciate that he took care of the issue with no hassle. I'll definitly be back.

Really? In 2017, are we really denying pregnant women access to public restrooms? And at a Starbucks no less?  My wife is 30 weeks pregnant and was seeking relief after a fair amount of eating, drinking, and walking along South Street.  She walked into the Starbucks at around 8:00pm and asked for a venti cup of ice water and the restroom key. The barista (a white male) told her restrooms were for paying customers only. She showed her belly and explained that being pregnant she really needed to use the restroom and he told her there was nothing he could do because it was store policy.  My wife walked out visibly shaken and told me what happened. I walked in with her and asked for clarification about the store policy. I've never known any other Starbucks to do this. I was told by the same barista that there was nothing they could do and they reiterated the store policy, saying it was a "Philly Starbucks policy".  I told them that was ridiculous and asked if corporate was aware. Another employee (a white female) stepped up and told me that the reason they have this policy in place is because it is a "safety issue". My wife and I looked at her perplexed, so she continued explaining, saying that they let people use the bathroom and they end up shooting heroin and there's been an uptick in crime.  This made me livid... I asked if she really thinks my pregnant wife is coming in their bathroom to shoot heroin and she just kept repeating it's a safety issue.  Fed up, I said that I would be calling corporate to complain. She laughed and said I could go ahead and call corporate because they are the ones who made the policy and give them direction. I asked her for her name and she said she was not required to give it to me. I asked the other two employees and they also refused to give me their name saying they are not required to do so.  Frustrated, I said, that I would take a venti Cafe mocha and the restroom key. I figured policy aside, my wife needed to use the bathroom so I'll just buy something. The female employee said that she is choosing to refuse me service and that I need to leave the store or she would call the police.  Shocked by her brash reactions and the fact that the other two staff seemed to think this entire exchange was funny, I said this whole thing was crazy and headed out the door. As I was walking out, the female employee cursed at me saying, "have a great f'ing day". I walked back in and asked what she said and she kept threatening to call the police if I didn't leave. I took out my phone and snapped a picture of them (see attached).  I called corporate to complain about the situation and they indicated they would follow up with me.   All in all, I am incredibly disappointed in this particular location and the workers here. They were rude, condescending, and took every opportunity to insult and demean us. And given that the three employees and the few customers in the store at the time were all white and we are black, l can't help but conclude that race played a factor here.  We spend entirely too much money on a daily basis at Starbucks to warrant this kind of treatment. And given their location on South Street (which gets busy at times), I can understand a customers-only policy, but they should consider expanding that to pregnant women and also people who are regular Starbucks customers. They should also evaluate individual circumstances and make informed, solutions-oriented decisions.   Very disappointed. I look forward to a more detailed response from management/corporate.

I ordered a Caramel Brulee and they had it spot on. Delicous on a cool Friday night. Quick service as well. All the tables and chairs were occupied with people staying warm and enjoying Wi-Fi on a late Friday night. If I need a coffee (or Caramel Brulee) fix, I would come to this location again.
Quick service with a smile.  Staff is really good and products are also very good.  Nice seating for a quick bite.  Never had a bad experience at this Starbuck's.  Keep up the good work.

This Starbucks is, unfortunately, on my way to work ... sigh. There usually is a line for the drive-thru; I almost always park and go inside. It's much quicker than waiting in line. Good solid service; I rarely have issues other than sometimes they put the sprinkles on my skinny cinnamon dolce latte and sometimes they don't. Another reason to go inside ...  I used to study here when I was in grad school. It's small but you can usually find a table.
Just waited in the drive thru for 9 minutes and didn't even get to order. Multiple cars ahead of us gave up and left as well. Tried to call and no one answered.

So I go to the counter and order a coffee  Along with what I think will be a huge slice of lemon poundcake  :-)  But instead of the counter girl grabbing it from the counter display like she had with other products for other customer she pulled a  different piece out of a draw and it was a sliver in comparison to the display piece. So I said  "oh man I was hoping for that one"  She just kept quiet and finished the transaction,I let it go  thinking  well I don't need the extra calories anyway.   So I sit and start drinking the java and bite into the  sliver I was given and think to myself " I took the bait"  it Wasn't about cheating people on the size it was  stale product they where moving... NEVER AGAIN will I think I'm getting more then status quo from a corporate ran place!

This location is smaller than most locations I've visited. The layout is galley style, quite narrow, with a couple of leather seats by the entrance. During warmer months the outdoor seating is great if you want to avoid feeling crowded inside.   The staff is friendly. I can't remember a time when I wasn't greeted even during peak times.   The only downside with this location is getting in/out during rush hour. The traffic on Pendleton Pike can be challenging to navigate. If you're familiar with the area the "back way" will save some time and sanity if you opt not to cross multiple lanes to go east.

I am a fan of this Starbucks, they always get my order right and the are fast about.  Friendly and nice that's important to me!  I get the same thing every time a house brewed coffee room for cream and sweetener....    There is handful of Starbucks all over the valley, this is a good site!

This is a good Starbucks. It's always busy, the wi-fi is strong, and the baristas are generally moving at a very quick pace. It can get a little too busy to make sitting there too crowded but I'm rarely one to sit at a Starbucks.
Slowest Starbucks ever.  There is not a single person that works there from Cashier to the Barista.  Absolutely terrible.  Shouldn't take 20-30 minutes to get a coffee.
Friendly. Caramel macchiato. This starbucks has a great location, that is what makes it special. Grab a decent cup of joe and enjoy the park seating.

It's Starbucks...need I say more!  No I'll be serious,  I visit this store three times a week.   I have said before I am a coffee junkie.  This place does it right every time.  I'm never let down by the staff or the products.  The goodies in the bakery case are awesome too.  The only thing negative I could say about this store would be the restroom. It's a male/female shared space.  I hate that! I'm sure they did that for lack of space,  but if it's community shared, they need to keep it really clean and, one time I had no paper towels to dry my hands,  oh brother,  we have all been there!    Otherwise,  I'll still be going back, but will use my "facilities" before leaving the house!
This Starbucks is tricky to get to but as for service and cleanliness they up hold the Starbucks name!  Unfortunately nothing unique that stands out just your basic Starbucks but that's a good thing!

I have just recently moved to the area and have now been to this Starbucks twice. As a former Starbucks employee, I have to say that this store fell below the mark. Both times I ordered, the same woman was at the register and both times, she had little to no personality. I ordered a Venti iced coffee and when I got to my drink, it was sweetened. Now I know what you're thinking...as a former Starbucks employee, I should know that their iced coffee normally comes with the classic sweetener. But what I also know is that whoever takes the drink order should ask whether the customer wants the iced coffee sweetened or unsweetened. So when I waited patiently to catch the barista's attention to tell her that I was sorry, but I'd like it unsweetened. She proceeds to tell me it comes sweetened as she dumps out the coffee and huffs to make a new one. If I had never been to a Starbucks before, this would have been a complete turn-off. Starbucks employees are supposed to connect with the customer while making them feel at home in their café. This Starbucks has yet to do any of that. If it wasn't for the great location (walking distance from my place) and of course the coffee/food products, there would be even less stars above this lengthy paragraph.
Justin at the drive-thru window is fantastic. He's great at his job and is very nice.
Who knew it was company policy not to brew decaf in the afternoon?! Really?! Isn't that when most people want a decaf?

Happy New Year!  It's the fist day of 2018 and I'm very grateful for this Starbucks.  The staff Rob, Christopher, and Ashina were very friendly. They kicked off the new year with somewhat of a rude customer (I'm sure that person had their reasons for being a bit rude), but they handled it very gracefully!  It's tough starting off the first day of your new year having to listen to customer complaints, but thank you for being open on New Years and being patient and gracious to that customer. You guys are awesome!
We waited in the drive through line for 23 minutes for an iced tea!  There were only five cars in front of me I think it's ridiculous!

this location is kind of a pain to get in and out.  This corner of Lindberg and Lemay has always been an odd location to me. I was sad to see Gingham's close, but I am glad that SoCo has a stand alone Starbucks. The one in the mall is terrible, and sometimes I don't feel like driving all the way to Tesson. Plus this location has a drive thru. always an added bonus.

This is by far the WORST starbucks I have ever been to! Their customer service is horrendous. People are NOT at all organized and don't even know how to make a drink correctly. Why hire someone when they can't do their one job correctly? Highly disappointed!
I ordered a pumpkin spice latte for my mum. The barista was so kind to write MUM on her cup.
I come to this Starbucks Weekly and they never disappoint! The staff is always polite and never gets my order wrong.

This seems to be a fairly good Starbucks. Shady parking if you can find it, handy so that your car doesn't sit in the sun all day. Plenty of power on the community table by the bar. Power is underneath the table along the entire adage. Again, very handy and well thought out. The Baristas here are pretty typical, however two stand out, one for his inappropriateness overall who I will not mention by name, and another, Suzie, who seems particularly smiling, and rather bright. She has made some good suggestions that have saved me a little bit of money by using a coupon code for something I didn't know I could use it for, which has brought me back to this location over other stores. There seem to be quite a few regulars here. Same people every time I come here. To me, that is always a good sign.
No decaf was ready and was told I would to wait for it to be brewed, isn't that the whole point of Starbucks.
Very great customer service! The staff was friendly and warm welcoming! They try their best to make your day happen!
Service here is so quick!! Always use the drive through and even when it's loaded with cars.... They still get you out of there fast! It's impressive.

Just left here and the staff was so friendly and sweet. All had smiles on their faces and wished everyone happy holidays. So nice to see happy employees late at night. Girl at the register tonight exceptionally nice!

The drive-thru for this Starbucks is mobbed at seemingly all hours of the day, but they are usually pretty good at keeping the line going. Some mornings, there is a barista taking orders before cars get to the intercom, which helps speed up the line by giving the baristas inside a head start on orders. The employees are all very friendly too.

I went to this Starbucks a few times and some of the staff are, not very friendly not saying it's horrible place but they stand to be a little more polite.still i would go back,. Im confident they can do better.
Nice patio, automated machines (not good), coffee ah, ok.

This Starbucks is really great. I guess it's a pretty high volume store because of the intersection it sits on. I recently went in because I heard they had a renovation (within the past year according to other yelp photos) and man is it beautiful in here! I love all the tufted leather, wood paneling, wall paper, and gorgeous art! Not to mention the Edison bulbs in the bathroom. I'm not sure if they just redid it or expanded the size as well because it looks much larger than I remember it. This location was already nicer than other locations and the remodel just tipped the scales.  As for coffee they have the same usual Starbucks menu. They did appear to have a large wall with other snacks and gifting items though. I got a venti PSL and it tasted delicious although they don't fill the cup nearly as high as you'd think because of all the whip they add. My drink did take awhile to make. I feel like I was waiting much longer than other patrons. Maybe they cater to the drive thru first? There were a lot of people working here though. All of them running back and forth. If you're not in a hurry this is a great Starbucks to sit and stay awhile.

I visited this particular Starbucks on my way home one Sunday evening. I walked up and proceeded to order a drink (one of 3) because i was placing the orders on behalf of people that were not present. So naturally i had questions. The barista that fixed my drinks had an immediate attitude towards me. I asked her if the drink i was ordering was a 'hot' beverage or a 'cold' beverage, and she responded with a ( terrible condescending tone) it's 'blended.' I was very confused and asked her again, 'is it a hot drink or a cold drink.' Her response and tone did NOT change. I ordered the drinks, paid and sat down while i waited for my drinks. Alexis stood by her register when she should've been making my drinks, because ultimately that's what i paid for, correct? Shortly after that, two customers walked in and i know based on their interactions that she knew them. She proceeded to have side conversations while i waited for my drinks. Finally, when she 'got around to doing her job' she decided to make one of their drinks first ( which need i remind you they walked in after) while i waited. I was irritated. She sloppily, and angrily made all three drinks all while having a conversation about something totally unrelated. On my way home i decided to call the store. Alexis picked up and well i asked to speak to the manager. And guess what? She said 'well, im the manager. How can i help you?' Long story short, i told her my thoughts, she hung up on me and i rang corporate. Her only defense was that she said, 'well, the reason i made you wait was because that's how i was trained.' Take away her title and give that job to someone more appreciative, deserving, less condescending and more geared towards customer service. Pathetic customer service! I hope she gets reprimanded!
Quick and pleasant service! Conveniently located RIGHT by where I always park!

I love this Starbucks location.  The staff is super nice - not to mention quick and efficient.  I've never had a problem with my order not being right and I've never had to wait more than a few minutes to complete my order from beginning to end.  I highly recommend this Starbucks - one of the best in Tucson!

I have been there multiple times and the managers have treated me horribly. I didn't want to write a review about it but recently I've tried my luck again and ran into an issue! The barista Moe really helped me out, how is it that a barista is way more professional and more for helpful to me than your management? I recommend you have kind people that are good with customer service in a management position rather than people that refuse to help you. Thank you for the help Moe I will be back because helpful barista's like you!

It's a Starbucks in an off-the-highway rest stop.  Hard to win from a service or atmosphere perspective.  That said, the extraordinarily long line moved faster than I expected and the drink was as expected.  A solid choice in a pinch.

Anna! 5 Stars! Give this young lady a promotion! Wonderful customer service! Stopped in for a large iced coffee black unsweetened and I had my son in the back seat, he's ten months old! A little terror! But oh that coffee! Hot damn! So good! Allright!

Disappointed. Waited about 40 mins for my coffee and still left without it because the wait was ridiculous. About 15 people waiting inside for their coffee and every order that was being called out was mobile orders. What's the point of having an inside for people to enjoy their coffee right then and there then?

I am a huge coffee fan! Its an everyday thing for me : ). I have been going to starbucks for years!!!!! I have been to so many all over the world its unreal... This starbucks location is great! It is always poppin with people! If you are a fan of the sport people watching, such as I am... This is the place for you : ). There is a great outdoor area to sit and chill. Its large as well! Large interior, with lots of places to hang! A+++

Faster service for walk in customers durning rush hours. The drive up needs better traffic management due to a small parking lot and a short over all length of the drive up lane. Drive thru vehicles often spills out into traffic while waiting. Consider adding signs to let your customers know they can enter Starbucks parking by continuing down to North Martin Ave and taking the first right into parking.

Both service and quality are consistently good. I felt like a regular after only a few trips. Like any retail establishment, Starbucks especially, when they hire a new employee there's a good chance this employee will not know all the subtle nuances of the pricing or understand the strange ordering habits of the patrons.

This Starbucks is the worst Starbucks in the Tampa Bay Area!  They close whenever they want... I've tried coming multiple times, and it's been closed during their "business hours".   Also they are the SLOWEST ever. I waited in line 30 min for our order. When I got my order - they handed me my drinks in a drink carrier. In the carrier were to VERY HOT americanos... neither of them had stoppers in them. Sooo the HOT americanos were leaking naturally- and extremely hot water spilt all over my legs burning me.   The girl in the window told me it was her third day. Makes sense. Because there was 0 customer service... and clearly she had no idea what she was doing   Absolutely ridiculous.

This drive thru is literally my nightmare. Can't help the horrific parking situation due to location, however it is by far the slowest drive thru I've ever been to. I live around the corner so I go this Starbucks on my way to work, and I have to plan to leave extra early just in case the drive thru takes an extra 15-20 minutes, which happens almost every time. I just don't understand it. Three stars because my order is always correct and the staff is pleasant.
This location is the best.  The baristas are great & so pleasant.  They never get my simple food order wrong (bagel toasted with butter).

I am so sorry to see so many bad reviews for this location.  I have been coming here ever since it opened and maybe I am just lucky but I have never had a problem.  Coming here before 7:30 can be a little slow but only because there are three high schools up the hill and if almost looks like every student stops at Starbucls first!!   Great and friendly staff, clean surroundings and fabulous service.

Okay, I downgraded my stars to two. One for location/building and one for a consistent product. Zero stars for service! The guy that was friendly and singing must have been beaten down by the sour puss employees that now work here. I get it! I'm not a morning person either; however, if you're in the service industry and plan on staying there, you might want to kick it up a notch.  Now, even though the service is subpar at best, it's the product I go for. It's consistent and fresh and I have found few local establishments that can compare. I've had latte/coffee products and CC's and PJ's and it was the worst cup I've ever had. Except CC's in Old Metairie... They have an awesome iced pumpkin pie latte!   Previous Review:  They are always so friendly here. This morning the barista was singing and very enthusiastic. I'm not a morning person and was struggling to get going today. He helped me start my day off right with a perfectly made pumpkin spice latte and a kind word.  I love this Starbucks location.  It has charm and the building has that "old" feel.

When I came in they were short staffed and incredibly busy, when I went to get my drink another person took it and ran off before I could stop them, but the barista saw and immediately remade it for me. A long wait should not negate human decency.

Every single visit I've made here since it opened (which have been several), has been fantastic.  The drive-through is speedy, orders have been accurate, and the best part is the friendliness of the staff.  They actually SMILE when they talk to you and seem pleased to be there, a very welcome change from the Robb Dr. location just down the road.  Hands-down, this is my new go-to morning coffee stop on my way to work in NW Reno.

This is a very convenient location and close to the Selmon and I-75 on/off ramps. The staff has always been very friendly for all of my drive thru and inside orders. Yes, the wait time can be rather extensive, and understandably so.
