For the most part everyone is ordering a craft beverage of some sort, not to mention perhaps a suit item so the way is to be expected. If you're looking for a quick experience this is not the place and I would suggest getting your coffee elsewhere so you're not stuck in the drive thru lane filled with regret.   This location does have one detrimental flaw and that is the drive-through lane. It wraps around the building and parking spaces making it difficult for people to reverse out and leave. It is not always crazy busy and we have had some good luck with short wait times on weekend mornings. Pro tip: Treat the employees with respect and kindness, do you never know what they are going through that day!

Kind of a hot mess. They made a s'mores frappucino without any coffee in it. Not sure why, we were only customers ordering so not like it was busy. They were friendly but didn't know how to Starbucks. Ordered coffee drink and got a lot of ice and milk but no coffee in it. Didn't satisfy my coffee urge. Attached to a Sheraton hotel and it's very stuffy, like they are trying to be high end but miss the mark. Dunno. Double "meh".
Great place with fabulous people behind the counter. Always cheerful and go the extra mile. Great coffee and awesome conversion;)
Worst service. They are totally confused. No manager in sight. If you are in a hurry dont even think about it.

This was the worst experience at a Starbucks I've ever had. Although the management went above and beyond to remake the drink, the first associate (trey or deontrey) was so rude and didn't offer even the slightest form of customer service. I will not be returning to this location even though it's the closest to my house. I would rather drive further and get better service at a different location.

The best Starbucks in bucks county. Been coming here since I first came to the area. They are friendly neat and make my day every morning and will stop by in the afternoon also that is how much I love this place.

I am not a Star$$$ fan.   I love coffee that tastes good at its base and does not require extra processing (read, latte, capp, etc.) to make it palatable.  So, it is always somewhat surprising to see these little coffee caves sprout up in supermarkets and box stores and do moderately well.  I admit to having been a happy stockholder for a while, but that did not change my opinion of the underlying product.    So, I was skeptical when I walked into Target a few years ago, and several times since, to see this outpost hanging its shingle within.  It is still there, but seems to be scraping along with only one person running the operation.  Some people like to sip java as they push their shopping carts around the store.  A few actually stop in only for the coffee and depart.    It would be great if there were more coffee alternatives around here.  Until then, this place and the competing Dunkin Donuts and diners of the world will duke it out for my coffee dollar.

As much as I try to make coffee at home, some days you just need Starbucks. I knew I had a busy day at work today so I decided to stop for a pumpkin spice latte. It's a little earlier than I usually get one in the season but there are no rules anymore so why not.  Pre-Covid, I used the app a lot and would send in my order as I was leaving my house and it would be ready to go when I arrived. Starbucks was closed for for awhile and then finally opened back up, but I have just been using the drive-thru since then. Both ways are equally quick and I get in and out quickly.   The staff are very friendly and genuinely seem like they want you to have a good day. I would say that is true for all Starbucks locations but this is the one I frequent the most.
Always have relatively quick service and their employees are very friendly and with great customer service.
I don't recommend going to this store on weekends. been to this location on Sat & Sun

Terrible experience. I put in an order for Hannah and saw it come out and the barista insisted it was for Dana. I saw 5 people come up and spin the drink around looking at the name. Waited 30 mins, called it quits and asked for a refund

I come to this Starbucks a few times every summer and fall because of my job doing recruitment at Ben Davis high school and every time I go, they are a wee bit slow. I usually go to the Starbucks on 15th and Capitol and they be havin their ish together - so I guess all Starbucks are not created equal when it comes to speed.   I ordered a grande iced Carmel-machitto for $5+ (I'm like did prices go up again?!?!?) - that's neither here nor there. I waited in line for a record-breaking 7 minutes and could not pull out of line due to the concrete median guarding the drive thru. Those things are the ultimate customers blockers and protect Starbucks from losing money.  Anyways, when I get to the window the girl was SO nice and the drink tasted as expected. So that made up for the long wait. I would give 4 stars but this is my consistent experience at this location. Sub-par.

Why review another Starbucks? Well, for 2 simple reasons:   1. This store is unique for being a PLUSH one and for being located inside an ER Hospital building, which itself looks like a deluxe hotel!   :-)  2. Yelp urges me to do a review, possibly because I have posted a few pics and a tip.  - Go enjoy! It's a beautiful, full service store with one exception that it does NOT redeem your earned rewards, although you can still earn stars for your purchases.   DETAILS you can skip:   While visiting a friend in Reno, he suggested that we should come to this Starbucks. I was totally surprised that he brought me to an ER Hospital building, though, which by any standard looks like a luxury hotel with large atriums, waiting lounges with artistic sculptures, flowers; patios overlooking beautiful gardens! I thought my friend was probably going there to visit a friend in the ER. But no, he promptly brought me to this very stylish Starbucks, which had a very plush interior decor and a comfy outside seating area in a lobby overlooking the charming garden! Now, I understood that my friend brought me here to show off their ER hospital and this Starbucks!   So, we got in the long line for our brew and pastry. The baristas were friendly and helpful. They moved the line rather quickly with 2 cash registers open. We happily enjoyed the goodies in its charming "lounge" area.  :-)  I liked this place so much that I went back there the next 2 days for more. I liked its seating area in the lobby, too, at a table by the large picture window overlooking the small garden below.   However, despite its appearance of a genuine, big Starbucks Store, it is only a LICENSED one by the Starbucks franchise. That means it CANNOT redeem your earned Starbucks rewards, nor give Gold Card members free refills, although you can still earn stars for your purchases.  :-(  Oh yes, they have FREE parking in a parking structure across the Tahoe Tower Wing. :-)  July 2015
I was the only person in line and they intentionally made my drink wrong (tall half calf vanilla latte)

I have been to Starbuck's stores all over this lovely country of ours, and usually receive excellent service, and delicious coffee.  Unfortunately the same cannot be said for this particular location.  From the management down through the ranks, rudeness and condescending attitudes abound at this store.  It's as if they all could care less if you patronize the place or not.  Nearby locations that meet the usually excellent Starbuck's experience are in St. Clair Square Mall, and one located in the Target Store; both located in Fairview Heights, IL. Just a short drive down the road from this terribly awful hell hole of a store. Please understand that this review was written after many and repeated 2nd chances.  Oh, it is filthy too!

This Starbucks is the worst ever.  Although Starbucks policy is no smoking within 25 feet of building, the smokers are puffing daily at the tables outside.  I've asked the staff to get the smokers to stop.  On one occasion I heard the Starbucks worker come out and make fun of the person who complained about the smoking...me...to the smokers.  There are cigarette butts all over the place.  Even the inside smells of smoke and dirty clothes.  I've called corporate several times, but does no good.  I say close this location and open one on Beach drive or at the Sundial.  I thought Starbucks was about the experience...but not here in this rat hole.

I go to this Starbucks at least 5 days a week to get a drink and study. I have always received the best service. I never thought to write a review until today because I witnessed the most amazing situation. An older couple who communicate through sign language came in to order. The barista who assisted the gentleman took time to communicate and made sure he got the order correct. He wrote down questions and pointed to the menu on the wall. The man sat down in one of the chairs when he finished ordering. The barista brought the drink to the gentleman when it was ready and the barista made sure the drink was perfect before returning to work. This level of customer service was amazing. The barista could have easily become frustrated because of how busy the drive through was and the number of customer in line but the barista remained calm. I love this Starbucks and all the employees!
This is a nice open location where you don't have to knock down tables, chairs, or other patrons to place an order.  Otherwise, it's a Starbucks...nuff said.

I came in 30 minutes before close for a mobile order of pikes coffees & when I got here the gentleman working apologized because the pikes  was a little cold and he was making a new batch for my order... that's NEVER something to apologize for in my book. Most places wouldn't have cared and would have served me the cold coffee. I know because I've had it happen maaaaany times. I appreciate the honesty and the caring!!! I'll definitely be back to this Starbucks in particular. I asked the guys working name so I could mention him here too.. So thank you alex!!

This little gem is often overlooked as it sits inside of Sheraton City Center. Over the years I've seen two makeovers, and I believe this last one may provide optimal space.  The hotel itself has added a seating area outside of the coffee shop, just in the hall area. The shop had removed several couches and put tables where they use to be... This is helpful to the business travelers that mostly frequent the hotel.   The Starbucks isn't as artsy or involved as a free standing Starbucks. The staff isn't as hipster or as talkative as some of the other shops. They are mostly younger, high school or college students, who are friendly and upbeat. The service could be a little better, some of the lines get too long and at times the baristas are unsure of certain drinks. Especially ones that aren't on the menu...  I don't mind the wait, considering it's not one of the busiest Starbucks. Go here if you have time to spare, and are in the mood to lounge around in the lobby.

If I could give this location zero stars, I would.  It's a cluster, 99% of the time.  Don't bother complaining, even for the benefit of those treated poorly--the management does NOT care and will make excuse after excuse about firing people, transitions, how hard the job is. Things we all know and are patient about, just not when it's the norm, which at thiis place it has become. People don't have time or motivation to complain after waiting a half hour for a cup of coffee, but please vote with your feet.

This is the Starbucks attached to Macy's. We really needed a "fix" so we stopped here on Black Friday and the place was crazed.  Meanwhile we got through the line and were duly caffeinated.  Glad it was there.

I think a little t depends on the time of the day for this location. I've been here several times and mostly good experiences. I've used the drive through and when I got home I had the wrong order and was upset but too far to drive back to fix the issue. I have my usual order at any Starbucks and it's an easy drink and 95% of the time it's on point but today my drink was a bit off, but not enough to ask them to remake it. I come here because it's a convenient location.   This is basically a free standing location but there is a Chase bank connected. Plenty of parking and a large seat area albeit full most of the time. They also offer an outdoor patio which is nice when the Florida weather cooperates!  I'd recommend this location as much as any other. The baristas aren't overly friendly but they do what is expected - making a coffe drink or a tea beverage.

This is the most personable Starbucks crew I've ever met. Pretty much everyone is extremely great. The only reason I didn't give a full 5 stars is one employee I believe named Sally is extremely rude. I come in almost everyday and am always extremely pleasant to her saying thank you and all I get is a stank face and ignored. Aside from that these people are amazing. Would recommend highly.

Starbucks do you see the consistent one star reviews. Just now, teach the lady customer service. Wouldn't toast the bagel; told me they didn't have something, I then showed her the sign saying they do. She said, "ya that's wrong and never heard of the pound cake you're asking for."  Wouldn't make specialty coffee, just regular. So I go in for a toasted bagel and mocha and leave with regular coffee and stale donut from yesterday.

Everytime I have gone there or I send someone with my order written down for them to show the bartista I get my order wrong.  I literally write  Ice blended mocha Soy milk  Chocolate drizzle  Whip  Like I don't understand why my order is always wrong I get a hot mocha soy milk or mocha ice blended regular milk. Yesterday they added 2 shots of something my drink was so strong I ended up having to throw it away I am so disappointing, this also happens to my coworkers I would rather drive to Camino real shopping center for my drink. Or coffee bean. Also yesterday I waited 20 mins to get my order. The man who took my order was really nice tho, what a shame

This is my go to Starbucks location in the area. Every staff member I have encountered is friendly. My order is always correct & freshly made. Mobile order is easy & efficient at this location. The drive thru can be long on some days/times but that's expected. There are 4 other locations within 5 miles but this is the best.
Clean bathrooms, nice staff, burnt lattes. So disappointing...  Won't be back to this one.

This is my favorite starbucks location BY FAR. The cafe is always clean, I always get my drink in a timely manner, and it's always correct. Even if they mess something up, they're always more than happy to remake the drink. The staff here is wonderful as well. Everyone is very friendly and always try to spark conversation. Emily and Savannah specifically are the nicest baristas I have come in contact with at ANY Starbucks. I live in Collinsville and always make the drive to this location because it's just THAT GOOD!

This store is always busy. However the baristas are always sweet and apologize. The last 2 weeks I have witnessed some customers being extremely rude and abhorrent people and they have dealt with it with grace. Last week during frappuccino happy hour I was behind a car who was grossly rude and frankly out of line. They were yelling at the barista at the order box thing, and then verbally abusive at the window as well. They ordered a million drinks and then sent them all back, they threw money at her and called her a fat ass white bitch and demanded she crawl out the window to grab the money they just assaulted her with. This held up the drive thru line and made it a mess. They finally left after 20 minute with their million drinks. The girl at the window was so sweet and apologetic for the wait. She was near tears (rightfully so after the outlandish treatment she went threw by this woman and her passengers) she gave me my frappuccinos for free and gave me drink cards for the next time for my wait. She was the girl with blue hair and she is ALWAYS sweet and professional. I have never seen her be rude or nasty to a single person. Then today I witnessed even more horrible actions by a woman causing a scene from the start. She was attacking a barista calling her names, going after her appearance and faith. Even calling her racist because her drink was wrong. She was video tapping the whole time and just saying the worst things ever. The barista just kept smiling, apologizing and remaking her drinks over and over while dealing with her verbal abuse.  Both these girls were professional and polite and never once gave into these entitled trashy people. They smiled threw it and never gave in. Their manager should be thankful to have good help.

First, service is too slow. It takes forever to get a drink.  Secondly, the "Trenta" size is basically a Grande with 2 extra scoops of ice. Rip off.  Lastly, I don't think this place is hygienic. While waiting the additional 20 minutes for my drink after placing the order online; on more than one occasion, I saw a barista grab the cup from the inside after scratching behind her ear. Disgusting. Luckily a different barista made my drink.

Conveniently located just off 70 in case you need a fix in the middle of suburban hell.  I grew up just a few blocks away in Barclay Farm and it's interesting to watch as suburbia has eaten away at all the mom and pop shops and chain stores have popped up.  The parking is very tight here due to the amount of businesses in the area. Back up S  L  O  W  L  Y.  Don't forget to ask for a puppucino in case your beastie is with you.  Service was robotic and fine.

I normally seek out locally owned small businesses to review versus a new Starbucks location, HOWEVER, this is a big deal...  Santa Barbara + Starbucks Drive-Thru = Happy, Caffeinated Locals!  I know, you're probably not nearly as excited as me and other folks in town, but I can only count on one hand the amount of drive-thru establishments within the boundaries of Santa Barbara. I know I know, us Californians are spoiled for having such beautiful weather most of the year that there's no excuse to not get out of your vehicle and walk inside. Yet, once in a while you want the convenience while en route to run errands, meet a friend or pick up the kids. The closest Starbucks drive-thru locations are 30 minutes away in Buellton and Ventura.  Now that I have stepped off my soapbox, I ended up parking my car in one of the 20 or so spots and walked in. From the minute I entered, the store manager Arumi introduced herself and welcomed me.  I guess not all Starbucks are created equal...  The interior decor is modern rustic with a fair amount of seating but also offers an outdoor patio too. The staff is excited to be open and made my basic Iced Vanilla Latte with light ice (they got my request correct, Genezaret C.--give them another try!) just as I remember and expected it. They offer the normal packaged food options, fresh pastries and some Starbucks merchandise.  Bottom line: Santa Barbara, there's a drive-thru! Hooray!

This is a great Starbucks location. I don't do reviews too often because I try to do thorough ones, and the experience needs to be worth it for me to get the ambition to do so. But today for example I placed a mobile order ahead of time for a coffee and sandwich. It took me a bit to get there and because of my own doing they were cold. I asked if I could just quickly have them microwaved and he immediately took both them back, dumped the coffee and gave me a full fresh cup and even a whole new breakfast sandwich which took a moment while they cooked it up in their oven.(Sad to see them discarded, but it's this service that matters here). A local disabled woman that often wanders this area came in while I was there too and all the staff were so nice to her, complimenting her on a new dress and asking what she wanted when other people avoid and ignore this woman in public. You could tell they cared for her as a regular and treated her well.  This time, and all other times here staff greets you with smiles and very friendly accommodating service.

This location just reopened for the fall semester in UNR's Joe Crowley Student Union. It's a large store with lots of space inside, which was very organized and clean. The employees were very friendly and I really enjoyed coming here. I'm glad this location reopened and I will be coming here more often. My drink was very well-made and it was delicious. Also, it wasn't busy at all for the first day of the semester and I'm looking forward to coming back.

Apparently spending close to $200 a month isn't enough (yes it's an addiction) to get a refill on your ice coffee. Apparently, It's against company policy to get a refill unless you are finishing your coffee in the restaurant. Someone might want to tell the lippy manager that  we are in the middle of a pandemic and people don't like drinking their coffee in places where dozens of strangers come through every hour. Instead of being a corporate robot, maybe be a human instead. Or use your common sense, if you have any.

I'm only giving this store a 3 because their drive thru employees are super sweet every time i've been here. Today i came into the store to sit down and drink my coffee and get my breakfast. I waited in line to order and when it got to me, she seemed very rushed. i ordered my drink, AND FOOD. she didn't ring in my food, which is fine, i just waited in line AGAIN, i ordered my food and paid for it. then she tells me after i paid she doesn't have anymore of what i wanted. she then said "what else do you want?" in an aggravated voice. i told her nothing, i simply wanted what i ordered, she was very rude about it and told me she could just refund my money then. which is fine, but DONT BE RUDE. i work in the customer service industry and i would NEVER treat my customers like this. customers MATTER. i should have been told "sorry i apologize, we don't have anymore" rather than "what else do you want?"

Bottom line, the slowest drive thru with absolutely no excuse. All times of the day, no matter how many customers. Slow. They never apologize for it either. Even when it takes a solid 30 minutes. Idk why i keep trying this one, in fact I just left the drive thru after waiting for 15 minutes not even getting a chance to order, with the same car at the window since I arrived.

Employee named Riple was very disrespectful, worst Starbucks I ever been too.  Slow service , employee Riple was arguing and was very disrespectful. Terrible service and experience at this Starbucks location. Wont be back to this location.

A buch of racist ppl work here. Walked in no one greeted me. A white lady walkes in every one (baristas)told her welcome. I was next in line the barista/cashier finished the order with the customer told them thank you, turned around and start3d to make them their order. I wad standing there for 5 min made eye contact with two other baristas and felt like they were looking right thru me. The barista/cashier that was making the order to the two customers that had just paid turned around looked at me and loked away. Damn never coming to this starbucks again. Been a gold member since 2014

This is my daily stop. My singular complaint is this:  there is one barista who is most unfriendly. He never greets you, never says "thanks" or have a great day. He shoves your items through the window and immediately turns away as if he has better things to do. Didn't catch a name, but he's a tall man, close shaved (or possibly bald) head. Someone needs to teach him what good customer service looks like. Every other person at this location is upbeat and friendly...except for him.

Reading the reviews, I came across many yelpers complaining about the layout and the size of the space. THE EMPLOYEES CAN NOT CHANGE EITHER ONE OF THESE SITUATIONS. If they could change this, I'm sure they would in a heartbeat.   Anyway, service has always been great for me! In the morning, it could be absolutely dreadful, due to the line. However, the employees try their best to stay chipper and calm in these hectic situations. I could NEVER be that chipper in the morning, so it makes me appreciate the service even more.

This short and sweet review has been brought to you by me. This Starbucks is located on the UNR (University of Nevada, Reno) campus within the confines of the CSU (Crowley Student Union).  The bad: It's crowded. Rarely can you find a time when it's not busy. The good: I've never had bad service there and all their products are good and tasty. Not cheap, but you get what you pay for.  This is the only Starbucks on campus. But, it's not the only coffee vendor on campus.  ---------------- Bottom Line  Yes, I will be back.

This is my neighborhood Starbucks and I it visit frequently because it is close to my house, and being the coffee a Holick that I am whenever I don't have fresh brew at the house this is my go to stop. Your typical Starbucks decor,  friendly atmosphere, free Wi-Fi, great place to come and read the paper or a book or catch up on some work that you just can't get done at the house. During peak hours, mornings and afternoons the drive-through line can be kind of lengthy but if your Starbucks Conniseaur such as myself it is worth the wait. I like Starbucks because it is a consistently good cup of coffee. Always know what you're going to get in you can always rely on the rich bold flavor.  The baristas here are the bomb. Super nice, super friendly, and very accommodating.  What can I say, I'm a creature of habit and if something is good I'll keep coming back. Way to go Royal Oaks Starbucks.

I love the convenience of this particular location but someone needs to explain to me why a full two hours before they close they had all of the pastries out of the case. I understand the need to start cleaning up before closing but two hours? Have they never heard of impulse purchases? Their manager needs to get the team on schedule because we had six people who all wanted pastries. It's ridiculous.

Time is not of the essence at this Starbucks watching everybody lollygag behind the counter they are more concerned with filling  ice or working at other things rather than filling the  20 people who want to drink orders

I love this starbucks as everyone is so friendly and very helpful; however, I was sitting at the bar doing homework and a little cock roach crawled right up next to me. Yuck! I know it's common but there is this thing called pest control. That's the only reason for 2 stars. Everything else is perfect about this starbucks
I enjoy that it's right next to my house but they are a little slower than other Starbucks, still friendly but always so busy...which I guess isn't a bad thing!

This is a high volume tiny Starbucks.    The staff is friendly and they do a good job of making sure the endless line keeps moving. It can take a bit of time to get your drink since it's so crowded but there is plenty of seating around the kiosk, so you can sit while you wait for your drink. You can also use the Starbucks app here.   It would be better if they were able to expand and open a storefront to better accommodate the crowd.  All in all, it's a nice place for a shopping coffee-break.

This is the slowest Starbucks ever. Everytime it never fails. Do they train someone here everyday?? They are never short staffed so I can't put my finger on it. The people are always nice but geez it's always low. Guarantee when you pull up the first thing is , " welcome to Starbucks please wait for a moment and I'll be right with you.  So annoyinnnggggggg
Extremely long wait but grateful for the drive thru. Staff are pleasant. Got my drinks right.

It took forever to receive my caremel ribbon crunch frap! By the time I got it, it was watered down and unsweet. I wanted either refund or them to remake it, but the line was too long. Totally unsatisfied and will never choose this Starbucks location again.

*BEWARE RACIST STARBUCKS* DO NOT COME  My friend and I came here and the employee Madison was extremely disrespectful!!! She was complaining at us when we asked to use two forms of payment and rolled her eyes then threw my card back at me after I paid. I asked her what was wrong and she began yelling and telling me they were understaffed and told us they were understaffed- like that's my fault how? She then called her manager when we didn't say ask to speak with anyone and began rolling her eyes when we asked her for sugar. She was being extremely disrespectful to people of color and blaming us for being understaffed!

Gotta say that I've never had a worse experience than at this starbucks. Went in and it wasn't very busy but it took ten minutes to get my drink because of mobile orders.  As I patiently waited with the other walk in customers  all of the mobile orders were handled without the customers even in there.  Then my drink was made wrong. When I pointed it out the barista said she would "fix it real quick". By that she meant she would finish the other two mobile orders. When I said to them it was the worst experience I had recieved at a Starbucks I was ignored.  Not normally one to write a negative review but...  Anyway, novel idea, maybe someone should handle the drinks for the customers that are actually there and one for the mobile orders.

First off let me say that they are always busy, BUT they have good service and if you go before closing time, which I believe is 11pm, you could get the samples they didn't give out during the day!!! Love love LOVE Starbucks. It is pricey, but for their coffee I do not mind. If you can go, you need to bring your friends/ family cause it is AMAZING!

I had the coldbrew with cinnamon oatmilk foam and it was delicious! The oat milk foam sits at the top and you get a rich nutty and creamy bit of foam with every sip of the coldbrew. As someone who normally drinks black coffee, I was looking for subtle flavors instead of something super sweet, and I was not disappointed! The baristas were very friendly too! I also really liked the extra effort taken with the seasonal decorations. I look forward to coming again!

Recently given a nip/tuck.. This Starbucks brings the new single-serving Clover brewing system and Starbuck's new Reserve beans. Not to mention the great new Starbucks decor and look!  Back to to Clover system. It's so great! Especially for an iced coffee lover. I now have so many more options for iced coffee instead of just the one typical pre-made "iced coffee blend". They can now make any bean they have in store into an iced coffee. It's fantastic! Try them all!  My favorite right now is an iced Indivisible blend coffee!  And the people are nice too. Very friendly.   Something else that makes this unique is the outdoor walk-up window. Very cool.  My go-to Starbucks!
Slowest service.  Small space.  Indifferent  staff.  Drink quality inconsistent....in other words I've had better.

Very tiny Starbucks in downtown. Only two 2-top tables inside and a counter for three more people. This is not the *$ you'll go to to do work. But it is consistently Starbucks and so there's that.  In my opinion, there's better coffee and better work space nearby. But some people love their green siren lady and for you all, here you go.

Parking here is murder. They only have 4 spots which are almost always occupied. This is easily remedied by parking at the CVS across the street or on Penn Valley Drive. Both are fairly reasonable options.   Other than parking issues, this is a great branch. The lines are small, the staff is quick, they have a few tables out back overlooking Lake Afton, and there's also plenty of free seating available inside.   It can get crowded from time to time, but overall, this is a nice, pleasant place to get your AM coffee.

This place is tiny inside and by tiny I mean claustrophobia-inducing. But the saving grace is the lovely sidewalk seating. And, of course, there's free wi-fi.  I enjoyed an iced latte here Sunday morning while reading the news on my phone and it was a nice setting. The egg white, turkey bacon and white cheddar breakfast sandwich was better than I expected it to be - it was just enough to fill me up without making me feel too full.

Jilliana was not very friendly- first I waited at the drive-theu window for 5 minutes and she never opened the slider to acknowledge me.  When she did, it was 8 minutes past when my mobile order should be ready and she said "what's your name?".  I had told her my name in the drive-thru 6-7 min prior and I was the only car there.  They hadn't even started my order.  They didn't have the syrup, despite the app showing it as available.  After selecting an alternative, she made my drink.  When delivering it to me, she never apologized for the delay or so much as smiled.

This Starbucks is downright unpleasant to be in. It has very little seating and has entrances that open both onto the street and into a hotel lobby. I felt like the doors were constantly opening in this place. The store is also far smaller than your average Starbucks. If you need to get a drink, it's okay, but it is not a place I would linger.
this Starbucks is utterly disgusting; truly gross. It was infested by gnats which meant that everywhere we went had bugs. My food had gnats in it, the bathroom had gnats in it.

This used to be one of the best Starbucks locations around! However, in the past 6 months it has turned into the worst. The majority of the staff are rude and have an attitude if you ask for anything. My drink is very simple but somehow is never made right. The only reason I am giving it 2 stars instead of 1 is because it is a Starbucks location and not a Dunkin location!!!

Well, i love starbucks but I'm done going there. I went inside to order & was waiting for my drink and sat down for a second at one of the tables & this male employee goes " you can't sit there so you need to get up" keep in mind im over due so I'm super pregnant, I get that the table was blocked off but damn way to make me feel like shit, when all I needed was a second to sit down and wait for my drink. This was my favorite Starbucks & I was a loyal customer to this specific location, just sucks I felt they had no care for my situation at all.

Starbucks are all the same, sure. But this is the worst one. Takes forever, make drinks wrong, staff unhelpful and not cheerful. More worth it to go to the Target next door, go in, and use theirs. You would probably save time doing that too.

It's 40 degrees in Philadelphia. Why do you have the air conditioning on now? Is that really what Starbucks corporate wants? What a waste of energy. Not very good as a corporate citizen. Besides the fact that you guys barely recycle (correct me if I'm wrong).   I guess you want to freeze us out of your location. What's up with that?

Beautiful facility but this team suuucks. They just don't seem to know what's going on. Drinks are coming out slowly and out of order. Watched a Batista make a screw up order into an order for someone else. Waited ten minutes for a cold brew. Fail. Restaff!!

I'm extremely happy that this has opened in my neighborhood. The place is well laid out with a decent amount of sitting room. It has a community table that's perfect for bigger groups. There is an outdoor area to catch some rays as well  The staff here are great and are quick in getting drinks finished. Even thought the wifi can be a bit of a challenge sometimes, this is one of my regular hangouts.

I continue to frequent this Starbucks even though they continue to drive me crazy.  They are always out of dark roast coffee...I mean it's 9am how do you not have another pot brewing??  The line is always 5 or 6 people deep with only one register open.  The staff is very friendly, but I feel like the efficiency is lacking. I now went at 8pm, they close at 9pm...again no dark roast coffee.  With a hour till close the place was out of cream, no sugar and the floors were being mopped.  I just don't get how this happens when you still have 1 hr till close.

I frequent quite a few Starbucks in St. Pete to buy my favorite bag of coffee beans; but as of yesterday, I will only be patronizing this location on U.S. 19 and 22nd, and here's why:  I had gone through the drive-thru to purchase a bag of my favorite Italian Roast beans and gave the girl at the window my credit card.  She came back with a bag and scanned my card; but when she handed it to me, it was a bag of Verona.  I told her I only wanted the Italian Roast and asked her to issue me a refund, which she did and gave me the receipt.    When I checked my bank, I saw the initial charge but not the refund.  I waited a few days but still no refund.  So I called the store and asked to speak with a manager.  The manager was wonderful!  She listened to my story, looked up the transaction, saw the charge but no refund and said the employee apparently had forgotten to put the refund through.  I told her that every time I go to that location to purchase Italian Roast, they're out of it, which she said she was surprised to hear because she has 6 bags.  She asked if I wanted a refund or a bag of the coffee.  I told her I'll be in for a bag and thanked her for how she handled it.  She said she'd have a bag waiting for me with my name on it at the drive-thru.  Later that day, I went through the drive-thru and lo and behold, there was a bag waiting for me with a Starbucks gift card and note saying "Your next one's on us!"  Wow!  That is outstanding customer service!  I wish I got her name to give her credit because she exemplifies excellence in customer service, problem-solving, management and leadership.  Thank you, Starbucks manager!!  I'll be back!

I just want to say that all of the employees handle the busy-ness of this store very well. This morning, one of the employees (Paige) stayed very patient and friendly even when a customer was being rude and upset about the store being out of certain things. You all do great work.

This SB is close to my job, so I visit often. I appreciate the staff knowledge of the product and overall friendliness. Generally always clean a well stoked. They introduced me to youthberry which is my new obsession. Only concern is that they are soooo slow at times and not just during peak hours.
Oh my god. Slowest place ever. Late for work because of them. Staff seems completely aloof and not even trying. This is simple to fix with some management help.
Horribly slow. I would have left but I was let trapped on the drive thru line. Never going to this location again

This is THE WORST Starbucks I have ever been to. They ALWAYS SUCK no matter the time of the day or the day of the week. They will 100% mess up your coffee. Do not trust them. They are always in a bad mood and will rush you. Absolutely terrible. Last resort.

Haven't  been to a Starbucks in a long time. Had the Grande Pike Place coffee. It was good and free from my VWS account. Was going to have a bagel but there were bugs flying around in the enclosed case that was a big turn-off.

I'm willing to bet that there has been a change in management. Long ago (2016-2017) this Starbucks (Near L&T) was ran really well! Long lines were handled quickly and smoothly, I saw the same baristas all the time and the baristas were creative(I can't get certain drinks I love because these new baristas couldn't imagine making a Caramel Brulée cold, think milkshake consistency ) Whomever working here now is very uninspired and the store looks like it lacks staff.  I visited this location 2 mornings ago, with a pretty empty store it took 8 minutes to get a Venti Passion iced tea lemonade! I opened my store almost 2 minutes late   They were knocking out the mobile orders, but what about the customers in store? Would you answer the phone and chat with a client for 8 minutes when there's one at the register with 50 things ready to purchase? No! Retail 101  I just left this location 30 minutes ago after waiting in line for 5 minutes with 1 water in my hand. The 2 guys working clearly can't multitask, there was a group of foreigners in front of me taking forever. 1 of them could've easily rang me up, sent me On my way!, made a drink, rang the other 2 customers behind me, made their drinks and MADE THE MOBILE ORDERS LAST cause the store wasn't busy AND the average mobile order in the mall sits for at least 3-5 minutes (huge mall, long walk)  I've had a long day... I'm done with SBUX for now
Okay boys, time to spend the money and get the electric outlets fixed at the big table.
It's starbucks, you don'y need to read a review on yelp to know what to expect.   Clean place, nice staff and my pup loves the pup cups when he rides around being basic with me :)

Never fails..disappointed Everytime but yet I come back to give them a chance... And YES got disappointed AGAIN  Ordered the new Berry prickly pear frap.. Looks nothing like the the pic. Its a venti full of vanilla bean frap and a couple spoonful of berries...
Tell me why I asked for light ice and they fill the cup up with more than half with ice. 3 sips and my drink is gone lol thanks!
I love the drive thru. The old Starbucks up the street was not drive thru and difficult to park.  The staff is sweet. Their faces pop up at the ordering center. And they remember you!

We had a terrible experience. I was with my friend to order drinks on our way to Orlando. First time at this Starbucks. We had Lucien as our cashier. She got our order wrong and forgot to put in one of our order. Our order seemed to overhwhelm her significantly despite asking for no modifications except oat milk instead of regular milk. She was either very soft spoken or irritated. Took 5 minutes to just get our order placed. She didn't even confirm our order. After the order, only two out of the three drinks came up. One of the drinks was wrong. So we had to ask to have redone. Then, we were waiting for a long time for a third drink watching other customers who came after us get theirs. When we asked if they were making our drink, they said they never got the order. We had to bring it up to another barista who was a bit older with white hair in a ponytail. He was sweet and helped refund us. On a side note, the bathroom was not very clean. Toilet paper all over the ground. We are never coming back.

Staff is unenthused to the point of appearing beat down. Maybe it's the night crew or maybe they shouldn't be open till midnight if they are already prepared for closing at 10:00. Who knows, maybe all those things, maybe not. One certainty is that the experience here is forgettable.  The conversations had by the staff, not so forgettable. Especially if you are sitting upstairs directly above the chatter.

One of our Santa Barbara-area stops for many years.  Especially when heading south on a Sunday, the 101 can be a virtual parking lot.  Being able to stop at this Starbucks allows for a view of what's happening with the freeway, which is useful for deciding when to brave the road again.  This location's been here for many years.  It's one of the smaller stores.  Not completely cramped, but not that large, either.  Since we normally stop on Sundays, the competition for parking spaces in the lot is not usually an issue.  I imagine it could be intense during the week when the other businesses are open.  It's a tight lot to begin with, so add some traffic and chaos could ensue.  Usually we both go in together.  This time, I was doing something in the car and didn't go inside.  Dear Hubby ordered us a beverage for the road.  He was back before too long, and the Frappucino he selected was made well.  Just the right dose of cool tastiness for the drive home.  I prefer this venue to one of the really busy, cramped ones on State Street where finding parking is near impossible.

This is by far the worst Starbucks ever.... and its not all the employees that make the experience awful, but the one girl they have working almost every Sunday is SO completely rude to the customers AND her employees she works with.... it is absolutely ridiculous. Her name is Cassidy and I made it a point to look at her name tag today when I ordered my drinks. She belittles the people she works with right in front of my boyfriend and I as we were ordered our usual drinks and this has not been the first time! It is everyyyyyy time!! I apologize to the person at the register for HER behavior because they are the ones who have to work with her..and they ALL just look at me with a sad but shitty look as if they feel sorry for themselves as well.. i cannot blame them for that. Every single employee there is very very nice EXCEPT for her... I would much rather bring my business elsewhere and boycott this Starbucks in International mall until they get rid of this bitter, unenthusiastic, rude employee because she is giving Starbucks an AWFUL look.. especially if you are a first time customer!
Slow employees long line, all the girls seem to work at their own paste  bad customer service, no rush what's so ever
Ordered a drink via Mobile Ordering last night and the drink was ready when I got there and made perfectly. Service was fantastic!! One of the better Starbucks in the area.

I've ordered from this Starbucks a couple times during COVID through UberEATS. They make the order super fast and always get it right. Great customer service (I had to call once to inquire about something).   I also love Starbuck's company policy of allowing anyone to use the store and the bathroom. That's an amazing contribution to the public and I am thankful they made that policy a few years back.

All the girls working this morning were completely rude not just to me but each person that came in. Oh, but while taking my order finally, they yelled "hey" to a girl cheerfully and went back to being unfriendly to everyone else. I think they purposely forgot the sweet stuff in my drink it's supper bitter.
Closest Starbucks to my friend's house. Fast efficient. Baristas were friendly. I walked in and it was empty. The drive-through had a long line.  The parking lot is a bit precarious. Be careful!

I'm not going to review the coffee because we all know what Starbucks coffee tastes like.  This review is more for the overall vibe of this location. There are a few other local coffee shops in the area, but if you want to use your rewards or just really need a frap, this location is friendly and always full of locals. The patio is dog friendly and makes for good people watching.

I've been here a handful of times. I've driven through, but they are usually backed up and so I ultimately decided it was nice inside and my daughter and I enjoy sitting and hanging out. It's clean and the staff are friendly, and they even seem to have more food items on stock then the surrounding Starbucks in the general Tucson area. I literally searched for the sugar plum pastry everywhere and couldn't find it and came here and got it two times here in a row! They are all out now though (limited special item). One day when I arrived, there was a mishap and they forgot all about my drink. I waited patiently, and finally went to remind them. They were apologetic. I was not in a hurry, if I had been I'd have been annoyed probably but it wasn't a big deal for me. It did however make me decide to come a couple more times before reviewing them to make sure I didn't rate them poorly if they didn't deserve it. I have since maintained an otherwise good experience with them, so I feel I can give them the 3/4 stars. This has been my new favorite location. I think that's for personal preference really. They are generally busy and a little slow though, so don't stop here if you're in a hurry and you'll be alright ;)
Great job keeping up during corona virus limited service.   Had drive through yesterday. Fast and friendly.   Order ahead was correct and on time. Pick up was easy.   Good job guys!

It's a Starbucks.  New and shiny, but still a Starbucks.  They closed the other location, just a few blocks down next to Ceol, and they built this new location in the parking lot of the 1 Liberty Building.  I've heard that one of Starbucks' goals is to have every location have a drive thru, which makes sense since that's likely the most profitable transaction they can make.    Parking is a bit strange in that they'd added spots specifically for Starbucks in the 1 Liberty lot, but no entrances for Starbucks on the east side of the building (which is where the lot is).  They've got a fake planter/barrier/divider thing to keep you from taking the shortest route to get to the entries on either the south or the west side of the building (which also happens to be right through the drive through lane).  Drive thru was doing solid business at 6:30 AM, probably 3 or 4 cars deep.  Tip: don't try to use the door on the north-east of the building - looks like it's stairs into the underground parking and is not connected to Starbucks.  The space is standard Starbucks - if you've been in any of the modern Starbucks you'd recognize it, and likely if you took someone in blindfolded, and had them identify the building, they'd know it was a Starbucks, so kudos to their branding and look and feel folks.  High ceilings set this place apart slightly, and there's more seating than the old location, with a mix of high tops, built-ins, etc.  I stopped in mainly since I wanted to try out the Reserve line of coffee, brewed on the Clover system.  The Reserve line is their new "premium" line, and the Clover is some fancy new brewing machine, single cup at a time that lets you program in exact water temperature and brew times, and uses a vacuum more than pressure.  It's a single cup system, and they grind and dose the beans after you order.  Interesting for a geek like me, but I think for most people it'll be seen as more of a novelty than worth the price premium.  The Reserve coffees ranged from I think 2.75 to 4.00 depending on the beans for a 12 oz.  Starbucks will probably have a bit more variety going through their doors with their Reserve line though, so that might make it worth while . . .   Of course, the whole Reserve/Clover bit is Starbucks attempt to drive their bottom line up, trying to constantly grow as any publicly owned corporation must do to keep the stock market happy.  I think it is a better cup of joe than their Americano, albeit a bit expensive for what it is.  I think the cup I got this morning was good, but I also believe an Americano from the Hub or Bibo are excellent and comparable choices, for a buck or two less.  I won't be making any future attempts to stop in here explicitly, especially with the Hub on Cheney so close, but if I'm in the vicinity and in a hurry, the drive thru convenience could win out . . .

First off, let me say that the staff here is awesome. I have never had a complaint about a drink. It's like they all went to a Carnegie course because they remember my name and they are pretty fast considering the volume.  I'm not a fan of the atmosphere or clientele. People stand in the way of the logical pathway to the order station. The area to wait for your drink is overcrowded. I saw a Mercedes with a Trump sticker which should tell you that people inside have more money than brains.  Great staff and coffee with a poor shop design.

I often go to this Starbucks. Today I was disappointed and didn't notice till I got home.  I ordered a strawberry lemonade acai with 3 pulps of raspberry syrup.  But where are my floating strawberries?

I get it, you get busy. That's normal. It's how you handle it that matters. 25 minutes for a coffee is unacceptable. What's worse, when people complained to the manager, they were dismissed. The young woman taking order would say she had no idea how long it would be! You better have an idea!  You know when the orders placed, you know when the name is called. Step it up a Cville!

The best Starbucks in existence!  On Sept 11, 2015 I was car 6 of I think 77 that paid for each others drinks that day before the chain was broken!  The people are sooooooo friendly which is crazy when you realize its one of the busiest fastest paced places around, but they still treat you like family and will make anything that might go wrong - right with a smile.  I just love everyone there!

the worst star bucks around.  everyone is inept and the drive thru on a good day is super slow. i just waited 30 min to be told at the window to pull my car up because my breakfast takes a while to cook. so in other words we just started your order now. it's consistently late and consistently slow.

This is an amazing Starbucks, I ordered a Violet Drink with 2 pumps of raspberry syrup, it's good and something I recommend trying,  The staff were so kind! A worker who made my drink, was off break, so when he went down the sidewalk he saw me and asked me if it was good, this is DEFINITELY, a good quality Starbucks, it wasn't to pricey, and it was definitely clean, and child friendly. Bathrooms are gender neutral, and they are very sanitary. They are #1 on my Starbucks list. If you are looking for somewhere to study on your computer, I recommend here, they are quiet and they know how to keep a place clean! This is one of the best places I've had Starbucks in awhile.

Love the location because I literally live 3 minutes away so that's a plus. From what I experienced, this particular Starbucks is very generous. On the few times I get my orders wrong/drink out of stock, they'll graciously offer me a free drink - with a positive attitude as well. :)  I like to study early in the mornings but it does get crowded so if you want to have a quiet space, come in the afternoon. If you like positive vibes and an overall nice atmosphere, this place is on your list.

This Starbucks does not rock my world in any way but I have to stop here when I am in Tampa because I am always driving by it on my way back to Orlando and convenience matters more than quality.  Service is generally fine -- good but unremarkable.  The speed of service, however, costs the location its stars.  I find that the wait time here is often far too long, even when there is not a huge line.  Still, where there are no other real options and time is of the essence, you will find that you get what you need and sometimes that is enough.

I used to buy Starbucks every morning on my way into work.  Thanks to covid I'm saving money on coffee.  However, this time of year brings my favorite blend, the Christmas blend. They have a blonde Christmas blend and a dark.  I made a special trip out just for the coffee.  In addition to the coffee they also have my favorite scone, the seasonal cranberry orange scone.  There is no better coffee & scone combo!

First off, I should admit: I don't drink coffee. (Whew, I feel better putting that right out there) But, I have a wonky schedule, which means at 6am, I'm looking for supper-or something supper'ish. Starbucks, as I'm finding out, has a great hot menu. Today I learned: the impossible is possible. The impossible sausage, egg and cheese sandwich is really good. Tasty-yes-great texture, not greasy-and the roll it's served on is divine. (Oh, the crunch!) I'd eat my shoe if it were served on this roll! I will definitely have the impossible again. And bonus: it's also filling.   I'll be back to this location, too. At 6am on a Saturday, there was no wait at the drive-thru and the service was amazing. (She didn't balk at the Elmo pj pants I was wearing) Yes, it's across SR 60 from Brandon Mall and Target, in the busy Michael's/AMC theater parking lot-but early in the morning, it's a breeze. They have tons of parking and a little outside eating area. For $10, my Saturday is off to a good start.

This Starbucks!  It's been here since I can remember back in early 2000's (the mall opened in 2001).  And the only time I see t slow down is during closing time and opening time haha.  They are a very small shop but the staff must be trained very well for waves and waves of people.  They always do an amazing job remembering and lots of other times to go out of their ways to keep their customers happy.  I've experienced it a few time when I forget to add something or tell them something specific and they will make it work.   The only thing is that "US" the clients make a mess around their work stations and sometimes is a little bit hard to find a clean table, clean milk/half and half/ napkin/straw area.  So if you are a conscious person please let's help this guys make their job easier by cleaning up after ourself.   Friendly staff, good service, and location within this mall makes this Starbucks an awesome one.  (Well sometimes the lines are never ending but that's why you have Yelp! Read, write, comment and like while you wait)

Great location (meaning it's right downtown St. Pete) and really have to love almost any Starbucks! The employees are really nice and does a good job. However it can be hard to find a parking and this Starbucks is pretty crowded at most times (downtown, huh). There are only few nice tables and they are taken for hours by people who sip on a drink and study on their computers. So it's a no no for finding a nice table, but yes yes for getting my morning coffee... until I get my own Starbucks machine on Black Friday sale for $59. Yeeeey:)

Kind of dirty. They get my drink wrong a lot. However I still continue to patronize here. The last time I was there I got diet vanilla syrup instead of regular, but didn't bother to tell me that until after I drove away and had to come back.

First of all, we were not greeted by the person at the counter. We could not understand a thing they said because they mumbled. When my mother tried to reload 20 she went to use a 100 to pay and they couldnt take it because it was too big of a bill which is beyond my understanding. And there was no apology no trying to reconcile and no "sorry about that enjoy the rest of your day" terrible customer service.

Horrible service. I waited a good 3 minutes to have my order taken while the 'Mary Jane' talked on the phone. Of the other 4 employees all of around aimlessly nobody could help me and I wasn't even acknowledged by any. Mary Jane, the woman who was on the phone, was using her service gloves to talk on the phone and use the computer/ take cash.  Not only did she touch food with these same gloves she couldn't understand why I would ask her to change her gloves to get me a new bagel. She argued that she had used something else to touch the bagel. If you think it's acceptable to talk on the phone and touch cash why would I trust your judgement on anything at this point. Disgusting.

This location at 5pm seemed pretty chill and quiet . Only a few Petrona dining in and/or studying/working .  The bathrooms are neutral and generally in a sanitized state which is always a plus for public restrooms for me . The baristas and cashier were friendly and efficient .

This is the worst Starbucks.  The last two wines I have been here they have screwed up my order.  Last time they didn't put syrup in my frap and this time they gave me a mocha frap instead of a salted caramel.  Not again!  Wasted 11 dollars on something I didn't like or want!!!!
This is close to the hotel that I stay at in town a couple weeks of the month by far my favorite one to visit.

For the second week in a row I've ordered my drink 10-15 ahead of time for pick up, I walk in no greeting, I had to wait an additional 10 minutes for my drink.  Today was the last day I come preorder my drink.  The manager comes out the back room wearing a head rag smh this used to be my favorite location.  So far 2 people  have returned their drinks while I'm waiting. #Not anymore

This Starbucks always seems like an after thought in Terminal B and I really hope an upgrade is coming soon. It's tiny for the space it covers & there never seems to be more than 1-2 employees and only ever 1 at the register - although there are 2 registers. Their Starbucks app seems to never ever be working - so you choose to wait over 15 minutes for $3 coffee and don't get to reap in any of the rewards. Well here's the good stuff (because I know all that sounded like whining and of course first world problems but hey I'm serious about my coffee and am an extreme grouch when I haven't had it - see above and it's 6am) ok the good stuff The staff is always nice and friendly no matter what - even when dealing with grouches like me (this morning the woman at the register had the most impeccable eye make up). If you want Starbucks and are flying out of terminal B arrive 2 hours before boarding - you've been warned.

Ok so I am a diehard Starbucks fan. I was way excited when they opened this one because it's super close to me and now i don't have to go inside target to get one of my fav morning pleasures. That said, I am really sad to say this Starbucks has consistently fallen a short of the excellence Starbucks is known for. There are usually extra long wait times (regardless what time you come in), the last 4 of 5 times I've come here they got my order wrong or just given me someone else's order accidentally. I've ordered via the app, walk in and drive thru but it doesn't matter - they are consistently inaccurate. Literally every time I've come inside, they were out of raw sugar at the bar, so I have to go to the counter and ask for it. I hope they turn it around because the way it's going now, I prob won't keep coming back much longer. I am not one to complain and I don't put in difficult orders - but there comes a point when even the most faithful customer will get tired and walk away. I'm almost to this point and it sucks.  PLEASE GET IT TOGETHER Starbucks. Please.

The second I place my 1st Dr Marten boot within this holy espresso bean Temple, I hear John Doe (Starbucks Barista) welcome me as if I was Danny Zuko returning to Rydell High after a long summer vaca. The Barista, if not busy at that time, comes from around the counter just to truly express his feelings towards his customers making me and the other caffeine junkys feel simple at Bliss. Many times I get to the location and my drink has already been started and by the time I lock my noble steed(bike) to the pole outside the drink is awaiting me. All the baristas are swift, social, intelligent, beautiful beings that offer something more than most Baristas. That something is the reason why myself and other colleagues will ONLY go to this location, they are my reason for REASON!!

I've been a fairly frequent customer of this particular Starbucks since it first opened.  I've watched it go through the speed bumps of a management change, staff turnover and COVID-19.  The staff, with few rare exceptions, is pleasant, professional, and seems to truly care about their customers' happiness.  All this while working in what can be a busy, demanding industry with a sometimes-difficult clientele.  Over the past month, I've noticed that my drink order seems to take longer-and-longer to be fulfilled with each passing visit; even when I have ordered ahead.  In some cases, it's taken as long as 30 minutes.  (My drink is one of the fastest, easiest ones to make, by the way).  I'm the type person who, if I know WHY something like this is happening, becomes less annoyed by it.  When I inquired about this trend, it was made known to me that this store has recently been experiencing a rash of call-outs.  So the shifts frequently wind-up being understaffed.  I also learned that this store was just made into a training hub so alot of the staff that are manning these shifts are inexperienced, newly-hired trainees.    The 3 stars are just to call attention to the problem.  This store used to be very reliable in terms of knowing when the busy vs. slow times were but it could be a slow time of day now and I'd still risk a 30 minute wait for a drink.  Hopefully, the situation will be addressed and rectified soon.

There was one time they didn't forget me or screw up my order, so that's why they aren't getting one star. The staff is nice, at least, so that's why they get three. But seriously, get coffee from somewhere else if you want what you ordered and in a timely manner.
Every single time I visit this Starbucks I have to wait at least 15 minutes tops for a drink.
Awesome service by Scott and Chara- not only were they kind and friendly but provided top notch service! Kudos

Nice run of the mill starbuck.  Cashier let me try the nitro coffee before I purchased it. I never had it before and wanted to try something new. Less bitter than any of the other coffee from Starbucks. I ordered it with sweet cream in it. Yum!!!  The employees were attentive and great and treated everyone equitable while I was there.

I have ordered a couple of times here and sometimes their coffee is good, but on occasion you get a cup of milk with watered down coffee. On one occasion I asked for a caramel macchiato and it tasted like milk with caramel. No coffee whatsoever, and I was disappointed because I love Starbucks! But overall it's a good way to grab a quick coffee if your on the go or if you're late. And the next time, I might ask for an extra shot in my macchiato :). "Happy Eating!"

I live nearby to this store and I come often, I can't recommend the service because it took 15 min. To make my Cappuccino and the guy didn't call out so I kept waiting, too much of college casualness not enough professionalism! Don't go during afternoon rush hours they are too slow. Highly not recommend it.

Ordered my drink at 12:39pm, did no receive it until 1:00pm; when asking about my drink, the lady rudely informed me that they were making them as fast as possible. However, the person that ordered after me got her drink first.....   I get Starbucks every morning, but this location has to be the worst by far. Very disappointed.... Every time I come to this location it is never a pleasant experience.   Almost told them just keep my money but not make the drink because I was beyond annoyed at how long I had to wait for a walk in drink.

This is probably one of the rudest Starbucks I've been to. This morning, they messed up my drink and when I told the person making it - she acted like she didn't have the time in the world for me and asked "me" to take it away and give it to someone. When did that become "my job"? Instead of apologizing for messing up my order she asks me to get rid of it. What's next, I need to jump behind the counter and make my own drink?

I love this location. The coffee is great, my orders are correct, and the baristas are quick and friendly.  The interior was just refurnished and looks great.  They have a long conference style table now with electric outlets.  It can be really busy between 7am and 8am; if you have trouble parking, there is an overflow lot around the back.

I love my Starbucks on 10th! The employees are already friendly and fun. We've done business meetings here, come through the drive up, and just hung out with our kids between sports.  The staff takes their time to get it right but also understand coffee drinkers need that caffeine stat ha! Great job!

Slower than slow today! Four people in the front. One mopping. One guy looking lost. Two other girls going back and forth getting stuff. Looked disorganized. 10 min to get a tall cafe Americano w steamed cream. I also didn't have anyone in front of me. Rational?? Couldn't tell ya.

Every time I order a frappucino in this location, I always get a snobby look and an "ugh". I mean, if you don't want to make it, why have it in your menu. The rudest baristas ever and not to mention slow service!!

wow they made my coffee 3 time amd all wrong waited 30 min  to just get my money back very unorganize  the look like new people. and had no idea wat is there job  they need training  and order extra  they usually out. of things. I will say never coming back to this store
Super sweet barista. Always happy to make my drinks, custom order and dairy free. Simply delightful.

The employees are so nice and work very efficiently. They're never rude and disrespectful. If they mess up my order (which is rare) they're really nice and they fix it right away without giving attitude. I always love coming here it's such a welcoming environment
No real complaints about this location as far as the drinks or service. It's just always incredibly packed. You're lucky to find a seat when you go in. Best to get your order to go!

Review for starbucks located in Terminal E- Delta. Visited this kiosk around 4.45am prior to a 5.20 flight so I was impressed the baristas were friendly and quick at that ungodly hour. However the Grande cold brew I received was the worst I ever received. For the past year I've always ordered a black cold brew and this was so bitter that I needed to add a splash of half and half to cut the taste. Had I had more time prior to getting to my flight that was already boarding, I would have returned it for another coffee
Super clean super delicious coffee and sandwiches. Absolutely my favorite Starbucks to go.

Spacious inside, nice outside area, I give this Starbucks two thumbs up.  I never worry about finding a table at this Starbucks. There is a nice long booth seating towards the back that has a couple tables plus chairs lined up along it.  This is very effective seating.  Of course there are some comfortable chairs and additional tables and chairs scattered throughout the Starbucks.  There are about 6 tables outside.  A couple are underneath covering which is a godsend in the summer months.  They keep the air conditioning on the colder side, but not enough that you freeze.  Just be prepared for it to be a bit chilly.

By far the WORST STARBUCKS maybe in the entire state of PA. Not sure how this places stays open the way it's running. Every single time I go here you wait no less than a full 10-15 minutes for your drink and today she hands me the wrong drink after waiting 15 minutes and she just laughs about it. This place is a twilight zone.

When you climb out of bed in the morning and you're all like "must... Have.... Coffee...." And then there's that beautiful white and green mermaid a block over, singing her over-roasted, sugar-laden siren song and you're all like "I'm gonna get the diabeetus but its sooooo good" so you go in there and you're like, "wait am I at Cheers? Because everyone knows my name. Oh and my drink is ready before I've even finished paying" and then you come home and all your friends and family are sitting in a circle in your living room and they're like "have a seat, we're worried about you" and you're like.... Okay, hold that thought I need some coffee people....   But yeah, this is a good SBUX. Go there. You need another addiction, right? (Who doesn't?)

This Starbucks is close to my work and I probably should have learned my lesson by now. They are slow and it's loud. I came in today to grab a quick latte between meetings and waited well over 15 minutes when there were only 4 people in line in front of me. The service is only Ok. I will not be returning.
This location has officially CLOSED very sad day it was in a very convenient location quick off and on access

Wow... this Starbucks inside of the Target Store has great service! Love the free wifi to share content  while having a pumpkin spice latte! It  is back!  Ms. Alexus is always happy to serve you with a smile! Great experience!

Went on a bagel run to another establishment , but I had to have Starbucks.   Still no seating inside. This location is geared to keeping drive thru moving, so I think the drive thru is quicker than walking in and getting it at the counter.

This location was not accepting cash due to technical difficulties.  A very busy airport and all they did was tape an easily missed 8 by 11 inch sheet of paper sign someone whipped up on a computer.   There were over 40 people in line most of who were not aware of the cash only issue.   Absolutely unacceptable to hear from the cashier when I finally got to place an order "we made a sign".   Starbucks - thanks for wasting my time - it will never happen again. You lost my business for good.

This is by far the worst Starbucks to stop at. Avoid this location, rude rude people work here!  After ordering 4 drinks for my family, one of the drinks was a peppermint mocha iced and it tasted awful. I went inside to ask to exchange it for something else and the 2 woman working there literally said "maybe you shouldn't have ordered it" you'll have to wait until I get the 8 people in the car line first, then I'll get to you.  I literally had to wait for everyone else's drink to be made first. Once the drink was made she literally tossed me my frappe, it even fell over.

Sad to give my old favorite only 3 stars but man it is just the slowest Starbucks. It's so small that they really have to move the drinks faster as there isn't a comfortable area to wait when it's crowded like today. There isn't a drive thru which has never been an issue for me in the past but it's been 2 years since I've visited and I could tell there was a major difference this time.  I think with as much business that this location does, they need more than 2 people working the counter. Our drinks were good and hot. I just hope they figure out how to speed up the process.

For me, the perfect location on way into the University, with early-opening hours that fit my schedule well. Wifi is great; the place is bustling but still a good place to work. I like the large communal table. I find staff to be efficient, if not overly warm.  This location (sadly) benefited from the closing of the very-popular Ike's Coffee down the street, but staff do a great job keeping the line moving. They have a Clover machine; I tried the product once and the product was excellent. They're now applying for a liquor license.  Liquor doesn't seem what a Starbucks is all about, I'll be curious to see how it affects the character of the place.  I ding them a star only because interactions of the seemingly-ever-changing staff with customers is a bit stilted. The staff's friendliness seems disingenuous, as if derived more from a directive in the employee handbook than a genuine appreciation of their customers.
I loved this starbucks until one guy tried to teach me there is no mocha in white chocolate mocha frappe after he gave me white creme frappe and then offered to drizzle mocha on top

Great and friendly service, especially considering that it's a small store along an interstate. It's in an odd location that's hard to get in and out of and attached to a sort of run down gas station. With the area exploding in growth it would be nice to see Starbucks build a proper stand alone store in Whitestown.

I came for the Wi-Fi as PJs Coffee has lacking wifi power just a few shops down the road. There was ample seating and my regular coffee was promptly handed to me. Creamer bar was well stocked; clean floor and loud music like all the other homogonize Starbucks you have ever been too.

Will never stop by this location ever again. I usually go to the one that is on Loughborough and never has a single problem over there and they seem to always make my drink great and "full". What I meant full is the meaning of a full cup, not missing almost 3 inches in a venti cup. Today I stopped by this one and it was around 5pm. It literally like nobody was in there, its not even a rush hour so I dont know why this girl barista was so rudee. I stated that I wanted my ice green tea latte to be shaken up instead of leaving all the green matcha powder settling down at the botton like that, she poured the whole cup out to the sink and start making another one for me while giving me an attitude. When she gave the drink to me, it was missing like 3 inches, like wow & the drink doesnt seem to have any sweeter in it . I think she forgot to add in any syrup. Well my bad for wanted it shaken up I guess. On the other hand my sister wanted a venti black ice tea mix with some haft & haft, her drink wasnt even full. So she asked that barista if she can have some extra black ice tea to the full cup? She came and pour it into my sister's cup & my sister was being nice and said " sorry about that". She literally didnt even say anything back, turned her back and walked away. Like what was that?! It surely ruined a nice day for me.

This location is horrible! Wait time inside and in drive through is outrageous. Some of the people there are on top of their game but many of the employees are incompetent. I literally waited 10 minutes in the drive through with only one person in front of me and she only was handed one drink. Let me say this there are definitely some employees there who are awesome but a few I've dealt with who are horrible and ruin it for the good ones.

I tried the Bacon, Sausage, Egg and Cheese Wrap today. What I did like...the wrap was hot, the bread/wrap itself was good and flaky, it was full and it did taste good. What I didn't like... the sausage was all down in one end, the bacon was one strip of half cooked bacon ( bacon crumbles would be so much better ). Still great service and my Trinta Black Tea gave me my caffeine boost for the day.

I'm giving this location two stars only because I've never had my drink come out wrong. However, this is definitely the SLOWEST Starbucks I have ever been to. Going inside to get your drink takes at least 15-20 minutes and the drive-thru is literally a 30-40 minute ordeal. It's ridiculous. Not once has an employee apologized for the long wait. There's always plenty of people working. I'm completely baffled by how slow they are. It certainly should not take this long to make coffee beverages.

Sooo, let me preface this by saying I love me some Starbucks but this location is hit or miss and is probably one of the more difficult locations to maneuver in and out of. You are battling traffic from three different establishments and it's just dangerous. If you are ever in a hurry I definitely wouldn't take my chances because for some reason this location takes awhile to get your order done. Sometimes you appreciate the extra time and others it's like you leave wondering why it took so long for you to get something you didn't order. Ahh well, the employees have always been very nice and extremely pleasant but maybe they are just overworked? Not sure, but I can say I just got my pumpkin cream cold brew and it is extremely tasty so today I'm appreciating the wait. If you're going to stop in just plan accordingly!

Gone to this Starbucks two times and have ordered the same drink, a quad espresso. Both times it was made wrong and the expresso was bitter. Not one of the best Starbucks. The employees are nice the coffee just sucks.

Thought I'd try it out again.   Disappointed, again.   How can there be four employees, in the only one in line, and no one says a thing to me for 5 minutes?  I came in the store because the drive thru was ridiculous and long, but still just as frustrating.

It really shocks and saddens me that this Starbucks is rated so low! I have been going to this Starbucks almost everyday since it's opening. Back in the earlier days, my grandmother and I would go before I would go to school and the baristas knew both of our names as well our drinks. Ken Whan was the manager for years and just recently retired. He had numerous articles in the paper about his phenomenal customer service which would make customers always come back to this location. Even though he is retired, this Starbucks still has a great reputation and those customers still come back because the baristas are still great.
