This location has a really nice patio space in the back and comfortable and open seating due to the large windows. Seriously people, this Starbucks is awesome!

Wow.  4 people in line ahead of me.  Waited 10 minutes before realizing they skipped my drink.  After I reminded them no acknowledgement.  They did apologize when they delivered the drink, but the drink was terrible.  Tasted like pure milk with a bit of coffee instead of espresso.  Worst latte ever.  Wasted my money and time.

This location is super small and you will have to wait a bit for your drink. Unfortunately I have gone several times already and each person that does my drink does it wrong or it doesn't taste right. Also their lemonade is super sour. Other locations use a premade carton so I don't know what crappy homemade lemonade they use but it taste pretty bad. I saw they pour it from a pitcher..that's how I know they are making it inhouse. Also most of the employees don't understand the concept of light ice. By the way watch out that they don't over charge you on refills. It happened to me. I won't be going back. I keep wasting money everytime I go and I always leave unhappy.

Horrible service.  The manager needs to be fired.  She was abusive and berating with her staff both Saturday and Sunday mornings.  On Saturday, I stated that it was very hot.  Her answer should have been, "let me adjust the temperature."  Instead, she said, "I know it is crazy."  Her employees tried to help but she was not managing properly.  Almost passive-aggressive with her criticism.  I suggest coffee bean up the block instead.
Ordered a iced white chocolate moca just wanted to try something new.The Drivethrough was fast and the order taker was friendly.
Trying to figure how service can be so slow when there are 8 people working behind the counter. Worst Starbucks I've ever been to.

Horrible food inventory control: multiple times I have ordered a breakfast food item and 5 minutes later been told they have run out.  Horrible atmosphere: too small, too many people with mental illness issues hanging out.
As one of the only coffee places in the area, I was excited to try it and I was not let down. The coffee was great, the little cafe itself was cute and well-equipped, and the staff was friendly.

After receiving terrible service at Dunkin Donuts with our food, we came to Starbucks to order drinks. The attendant who  Ibelieve was the manger George took our order and he was amazing ! Answered our questions , listened to us , and explained the difference so we made sure we made the right choice !   We always appreciate great service ! Thank you !

I'm usually a local coffee shop kinda gal but every now and again I go into a Starbucks because they are conveniently located. What I generally enjoy about Starbucks is their speediness and consistency throughout the business. This Starbucks is certainly the exception to the rule. It's taken me four years of working in the green hills mall to write a review because I always hoped that an improvement was just around the corner. Always understaffed, sloth like and poorly stocked. What's going on with this shop that's different from the others? Can some one figure it out so we can all stop being late to work while waiting thirty minutes for our grande drip brew.

I get coffee here once maybe twice a day sometimes and they started my order like 5 minutes later and no one was even in the lobby and it didn't taste good at all like my coffee usually has the last 10000 times not a happy customer
The drinks are awesome and well done! Only complaint is that alot of college kids go there so theres never anywhere to sit!
Racist employees. Wearing BLM espousing their views.  Not in uniform. Corporate policy is not to and yet he was allowed ?   Why?
I normally don't write reviews but I've had a great experience at this Starbucks. The staff here are friendly as well as professional.

This Starbucks location is hit or miss. Mostly miss. Since it's in my neighborhood I visit often, which makes me kind of depressed to think about now that I'm sitting down to write this review.  They recently re-did the parking lot, which seems to have made room for an extra space or two. I know it's not their fault, they don't have much to work with. But when I have to park in the handicapped spot because all of the other spots are taken (even the make shift spots that hang almost into the road), I get dirty looks from other customers. Give me a break people!  As for the coffee, it's your standard Starbucks brew. I don't usually purchase drip coffee, I'm more of a latte gal.  I'd have to say only 1 or 2 regular baristas are friendly. The rest act like there's someplace else they'd rather be, and that's to put it nicely. One of them is super talkative and it takes forever for her to get through a line of customers because she wont shut up. I wouldn't mind any of this so much if they made my latte correctly at the end of it all.  But they usually do not. And you know what? I'm tired of asking for it to be remade because it makes me feel like a snob. And i'm not a snob. So I just bought a french press. And I'm looking into purchasing my own espresso machine. C'est la vie.   It's a shame that there's not a nice independent coffee place in Jenkintown that has a chill vibe and plenty of seating. I guess there's not market for it outside the city.

Consistently terrible service. A few weeks ago I placed a mobile order consisting of a few cold items from the case and a warm sandwich. The whole point of placing a mobile order is that so you can just run in and grab it quickly. The only thing that they placed on the pick up counter for me was my sandwich(which was hard like they used one this someone didn't pick up instead of making a new one because I was there only a few minutes after placing the order) and my drink. I asked the shift supervisor where the rest of my order was and she said yeah it's in the case just grab it.i have never had a Starbucks make me collect all of my mobile items. Then when I asked for a bag she acted like it was the end of the world. Then today I went to pick up my mobile order through the drive thru because I had a little extra time and when I got to the window they didn't even know what my drink order was.. but they had my food? So? After telling them what my drink was they made it incorrectly not once but TWICE and the girl must have thought I drove away the first time she handed me the drink because she came back tot he window and said sorry for the wait the person in front of you was complaining about everything. Wow... maybe you should verify which customers at the window before you open your mouth. Someone made my drink incorrectly twice and I'm the burden? Would never go here again if it wasn't the closest one to my work.
Probably the fastest service I have ever received at a Starbucks. The baristas were extra nice.

This Starbucks is always busy, but they know how to get customers on their way in the drive thru. Every employee I have talked to has been super nice and my drinks are always perfect!  I always pay using the Starbucks app so I don't have to mess with my debit card. I love this location!!!

I've only recently gotten into Starbucks, and while I have a favorite location to go to, it's near work and I thought I would try this location near my home so I could enjoy my drink during my commute instead.  For this location, they never have skim milk out and the baristas fill the coffee to the top. I have a very simple brewed coffee order and just ask for a little room to add my milk, and they can't seem to comply.  There are better locations on the west side than this one (Avon, Brownsburg). This one is usually not busy in the morning, which probably should have been a tip-off.

I come here multiple times a month. I always get excited when Rhett is working or helping me. Always makes great suggestions; like changing my milk on my dirty chi tea, or how many pumps to put in it. Or adding nutmeg or hazelnut to my iced americano.    I work from home or at different Starbuck, I would say I got to at least 5 different locations throughout the month or not more. From St. Charles to downtown STL and everything in between plus some in IL. This is my favorite location. They just seem to be more on top of it and caring about their guests, mainly Rhett!
I've been to many Starbucks in my lifetime and this is by far the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to... inside and drive through.  I've been here multiple times and it's always the same.

I've waited far too long to write this review. This location has consistent bad service. Each time I come here I regret not taking the time to go to the Avon location. Starbucks has me where they want me, though, I'm so addicted to their product that I'm a captive audience. First of all, my biggest complaint is their lack of quality control with their drink production. They frequently burn their espresso shots or "skunk" them by being too slow with pouring them into drink, the ratio of syrup to milk is never right, often you are handed a very light grande (in other words they are too lazy to make sure each person consistently gets adequate steamed milk because they are stretching out the one carafe as long as possible), and if you order a drink that calls for stirring (i.e. Mocha) just forget it - that mocha sauce is just sitting at the bottom so be prepared to self stir. Second of all, they are not efficient. I'm willing to wait for an excellent drink when they are busy, but instead I'm made to wait while they stand around complaining and socializing & then handed a sub-par drink. There are a few people that work the drive through that are just rude. One is a young female who is so emo that she depresses us instead of greeting us. The other is a very tall middle aged man who is not funny despite trying very hard to be, not motivated AT ALL - very lazy in his presentation AND the dude ALWAYS hands us the wrong drinks first. Also I need to note that when I'm handed a completely wrong drink and I ask you to make me what I ordered - telling me what you handed me is better anyways is NOT customer service. Bottom line is every single time we visit this location we are reminded that we keep saying we should never come back there. Well, this time I think it'll stick. We will not be back. Sucks cause we are both currently grimacing over our mediocre coffee drinks that we just paid $12 for. Not happening again - Brownsburg Starbucks lost our business for good this time!

I love the morning crew (+ Rae!) at this location. They are always so warm and welcoming AND greet a regular like me by name. I am going to be so sad when I move and have to find a different Starbucks by my new work in Chandler. But honestly I'm going to miss how kind this staff is and how much they pride themselves on their work.   This part is more for the developer/planner of the location who clearly has never before designed a parking lot for a popular drive thru coffee shop. It can get pretty congested and I've even witnessed some rear ending fender benders with the way people pull into center so quickly. You would think they might expand the lot to share with Wells Fargo (because they're clearly using up all their spots next door).

Went in for a shake and they were short the banana because they ran out, so I just took some strawberries . It was still very good, but the awesome part was they gave me a card for my next visit! Super sweet! Thanks for making my Monday! ! They know how to run a business! Xoxox

Let me start by saying that I love the ambience of this Starbucks. It's in the new Target/Petsmart complex on Broad Street and they really have a pretty cool space.  However, this is definitely a NEW Starbucks. I got a tall black coffee (like I always do) and it was cold. I did not get a cold coffee. It was later in the day and they were not busy but part of being the largest premium coffee chain in the country is always having hot premium coffee.   I don't know the area well enough to recommend a local alternative but it is definitely worth finding one. Considering how many young people live in this area now, I'm sure this place is swamped around rush hour. Do yourself a favor and find some cheaper, better, WARMER coffee somewhere else.

Time to update my review. The line can still be long at the drive thru just like any Starbucks, but for me who goes here a couple times a week mid mornings, they definitely have improved their wait times. The staff is friendly and they are one of the few Starbucks that makes my Black Tea Lemonade consistently yummy and actually shakes it (mixes it) like they are suppose to. Wish they were closer to our home as the drive thru at the Starbucks at Buck and Lemmon is horrendous.
Friendly staff and nice place to hang out! Starbucks seems to be able the same everywhere so the atmosphere and environment really makes a difference. This one's a good one to stop by!

Starbucks, A place that is, quite literally on every corner.  Now o'days seeing it can be an eyesore, and the coffee quite lackluster.  But man there is no way I can give two stars to starbucks, not after all the times it has saved my life from a near coma during work.  The staff is alert and friendly, always on the ball, even better they are fast (fast food... coffee style).  I can get a regular cup of coffee, exactly how I like it, at a pace that will make your head spin.  So what if the coffee comes from some mans diarrhea that gets sprayed out of a machine, it wakes you up... all i can ask for when in a hurry.  Overall:  the coffee aint the best... but its good enough. lol.

Morning treat. Store is in a shopping center. But the actual store is not as clean. Alittle disappointed because of COVID store should be kept even clean due to the cdc guild lines.   However they made my drink perfect to my liking. Actually all the three drinks we ordered were good.

I used to love going to this starbucks on the way to work but unfortunately the new girl ruined it for me entirely. I order the same drink every single day, a grande iced caramel latte. The new girl made it hot and not to be rude but I cannot drink the steamed milk-it makes me stomach sick all day. So even in the coldest of winters I order an iced drink. I said I ordered it iced can you make an iced one please? She looked at me and another girl-who I had never seen there said sure no problem. She must have taken the hot caramel latte and put it on ice because I have been feeling sick all day. What kind of service is that? I would think when I pay $5 for a latte it should be what won't make me sick. NEVER going back. Watch how they make your drink carefully, absolutely awful!

If for no other reason than they replaced the old Long John Silvers... I am a fan. Love the fact that there is a drive through. As I said in a previous review of another Sbux, I come for the hot chocolate, not the coffee. Great place to go as it is right on my way to work!

I was so excited that they were moving because that would mean adequate parking and an easier in and out. I didn't realize that meant that the fabulous staff at the train station location would be gone. I've gone in several times and each time the staff has either been rude or messed up an order. Unsweetened black tea is not a difficult drink, but they manage to. I'll be going to Exton, which is saying a lot because they're slower than molasses in February.

I've been here a dozen or so times because it's convenient, as this one is located inside the Target Store in Burlington Township. Each of my experiences was perfectly fine/good. No problems whatsoever. The wait can be a bit long in the morning because it is on the small side. However, now that the stand-alone Starbucks across the parking lot (with a drive-thru window) is open, that problem is likely to go away.
Tried a peach green tea and it was refreshing and they gave my puppy a puppichino! Staff was more on the friendly side as well. Cute spot to visit while I'm out here

I love this Starbucks at UNR's Joe Crowley Student Union, aka "The Joe." Staffed by students, they hustle and make a damn good cup of coffee/frappucino/specialty drink of your choice. Friendly, fast, efficient and always a cheerful word. Yeah, sometimes you have to wait in line when it's a between-class rush, but I challenge you to find any other Starbucks in Reno that is more efficient and ALWAYS gives you a very hot cup o'joe.

Most unorganized and dysfunctional Starbucks I have ever been to. As a gold member, nothing makes my day more than my afternoon coffee. Workers are rude and appear as they don't want to be bothered, staff is overwhelmed with the massive drive through line and there have been several times they have told me they can't make my drink due to them being out of certain things such as cold brew and sweet cream. Not your typical Starbucks that's for sure... something is always off at this one! Very disappointing!!!

The drinks are good, but I've noticed some of the baristas talking about customers at times. I understand that it can be stressful to work in such a small place, especially when there is that morning/afternoon flood of customers, some of whom can be extremely rude (seriously, who do these people think they are?!). However, as former regular, it does turn me off a bit when I hear them talking about a customer in the open after he/she has left. Hopefully, I'm on their "nice customer" list.

Horrible. And will not be back here. After the experience this morning I have to say I will never be back to this location. Every single person that I spoke to that worked there was the WORST ATTITUDE. If you do not like your job, find antoher one because that place will lose customers over their HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE. I work in customer service everyday of my life and if I acted like they did, I would never have any customers. Everyone was rude and very short. I had no idea what the cost of my coffee and muffin was because the lady at the window never said anything to me, just took my card and handed my stuff to me. Sorry but it is 8 AM in the morning and starting people's day off like that is terrible. Needless to say I will be getting my morning coffee elsewhere from now on.

This is my family's usual (and favorite) Starbucks in our neighborhood. They are quick, courteous, and usually remember you and/or your usual (even if you regularly just go once a week).    I appreciate the fact that they all work together so well to get you your items quickly and accurately whether you are in the drive through or go inside. Another nearby Starbucks focuses on the drive through much more and it can take you quite awhile to get your items inside (even if you are taking it to go and the only ones waiting). It's like night and day with the service and friendliness of these two and this one most definitely wins!   If you are parking to go in, I highly recommend that you park on the part of their parking lot that faces out and away from Starbucks and over on the half that is in front of Fed Ex. It is much safer pulling out of that area, because many times people come racing around that corner over by where the drive through comes out. I've heard of others who avoid this Starbucks because of this, but you only need to park in the correct spot.  This is the only negative, so when you choose to take my advice on where to park... there is no reason not to go here.    I also highly recommend their rewards type program. Like I said, we usually just go once a week (and also often on road trips), but we still have earned free frappuccinos several times.
great place to socialize and the staff is always friendly, sometimes a little busy so finding a chair could be hard.
Probably the friendliest Starbucks I have ever been to! I walked out there with a smile on my face. Such a pleasant way to start my day!

This Starbucks is quite convenient for me to stop at on my way to work since it's close to the 44/270 entrance.  I usually send my order through my mobile and pick it up with zero wait.  However, I have ran into a couple of issues almost every time I order from here.  The first time, they've completely forgotten my pastry and I had to tell them and show them the receipt on my phone.  Then I waited for them to heat it up.  The second mishap, my caramel macchiato was made cold when on the app, it doesn't say "Iced caramel macchiato."  They did remake it and were very apologetic.    The staff are usually very friendly here and the storefront is always kept in great condition!  The staff is what makes me stop by this place before work over the one that is 10 seconds from my house.  They are, overall, more pleasant to all their customers which is the reason why I'm still giving this place a 4-star rating.  Nothing really major has ever happened with my orders and they've always done the best they could to alleviate the issues I've had with my orders.

My husband and I have been working from home together since the pandemic and decided after some appointments we'd grab breakfast to head straight home and eat while we started working. I ordered on my app which is super convenient and says ready ASAP. Their estimate was like 15 minutes or so for a croissant sandwich, egg bites, and two hot drinks. We pulled up with 5 minutes left of the time estimate but I went inside anyway so I would hear my name when it was ready. I walked in and it was being put on the counter at that same time which I was pleasantly surprised about. Everything was great but I can tell the food was pre-made and heated in some way. It was fine but I'd probably just get a drink next time.  Also, people need read the drive thru signage and not block the entire entrance as well as blocking the road to pass to other businesses nearby. It leaves an extremely unsafe setup for others. Be careful coming and going!

Do not go here unless you want to wait 45 minutes for a drink that's made wrong. Sorry but this store needs to get rid of the old lady in drive thru if she can't take orders the right way and in a timely manner.

Lack of staff and busy morning commuters can make for a crazy and stressful day for baristas. The team members of Starbucks really step up and maintain such a lovely attitude when dealing with some less than pleasant uncaffinated humans waiting for their items in a long line. I have been so impressed with the staff and they move fast and efficient and keep up the cheer- I will always back people who work hard when they are in a tough spot. I can't make the grumpy people nicer- but I can say that the people at Starbucks are amazing and I am thankful for all they do!

Customer service was very nice but they did not fill my coffee all the way. I asked for extra hot foam and I got less and cold. I asked for caramel drizzle and when I got it there was no caramel drizzle on top, but the service of people was very nice! I would go back but I just hope they get this message to make my coffee right next time. I think I just dont like starbeeez ‍

Convenient Starbucks on campus, they offer pretty much everything every other Starbucks offers.  The line gets super long in the mornings before students get their day going. There's a big lounge area with some booths, and many tables.   It gets pretty loud some times, if you like to study at a quiet area, this is not the Starbucks you should come.

Pretty much exactly what you would expect from a Starbucks. Quick service, super friendly. There's a ton of extra seating in the back, which is so nice. Very clean inside and out, bathrooms also super clean. Whether you just want a quick drink or snack, or you want to sit and stay awhile, this is your place.

Worst service I would avoid going to this Starbucks because the drive through line takes forever and even walk-ins make you wait. I usually get a black, unsweetened iced coffee and it amazes me how long they stall to pour those drinks when I see workers taking breaks and talking to each other or Even sipping on their own drinks! One time I order through the app and walked in 15 mins later for the same ice black unsweetened coffee, it was still not ready! And I looked around there was 1 customer in line for the cashier!  I would rather go to another Starbucks near my job in the morning, look for parking and walk in and out within 5 mins. The city ave location really convinces me how bad management is because it's been at least a year I do not see improvement at all but it's literally on my way to work so I would rather make an extra effort to not just stand there and wait for my drink!

Worst Starbucks ever that consistenly screws up even the simplest of orders and does not understand their rewards program or prepaid coffee mug product. In one instance they completely forgot to put in our sandwhich order, then asked us if wanted them (hoping we'd say no). They later apologized and gave us a free drink voucher. However when I returned and used it-they messed up that order too. How ironic.

Ugh!!! ((Eye roll)) Worst. Starbucks. Ever. Ok, I think I finally had my last straw with this location. I come here fairly often because of its location between the 95 exit ramp and where I usually head to in the NE, and for the sheer fact that it has a drive thru which for a mama of two littles, is sooooo nice! Especially coming from the city where drive thrus don't exist.    It is certainly a pain in the tush to get into, and then once you do make it in, there's a line. There's ALWAYS a line. Doesn't matter what time of day, there's a line. A slow moving one at that. I probably could've written this review while I waited in it.   Besides the logistics, they're always out of at least one item someone in my crew wants! ALWAYS!! I don't know who does the ordering for this location, but they're terrible at their job. How are you ALWAYS sold out of something. And it's not just the same item every time either. Ugh!  Then today they serve me the most disgusting cup of cold brew! It wasn't drinkable! Glad I just ordered a tall when I usually get a venti. Someone up there was looking out for me! How do you mess up cold brew?!! And a black cold brew nonetheless!! Seriously? This tired mama needed a bit of something today.   Service is very hit or miss and I've had one encounter where the cashier at the drive they window scolded me for not telling her I was ordering my free drink at the speaker. I mean, she was really nasty about it! OVER. IT.   Not one single redeeming quality about this location except for the drive thru which isn't enjoyable either!   Unless you're desperate, save yourself the hassle!

Great coffee. Line super long! Baristas friendly but very slow at completing orders. Lots of standing around. When it was my turn for a simple plain coffee I was told I had to wait since they were brewing. Don't bother.

Starbucks in general is bad enough, but they've turned the awfulness up to 11 at this location. The space is cramped, with hardly any seating and inadequate parking.   The workers are the worst - the combination of phony excessive friendliness and disregard that is worse than just the typical Philly-area coldness.   In spite of being so small, however, the store is completely filthy. There's a layer of grime on every surface, and it looks like it hasn't been swept in a couple of months. Your shoes stick to the floor as you walk through because of spilled beverages and food that has been left to dry out. Just, yuck.   Did I really just spend 3 bucks on bleah coffee at this dump?

Not a fan of this location in particular, the location promotes unsafe foot traffic routes through the parking lot, most of the baristas here either don't seem to be trained properly and/or have absolutely no sense of urgency. Its not a leisure thing grabbing coffee before work or a meeting and having to feel pressed for time by a barista that feels like they can take their sweet time with every single drink. Take an espresso shot and get to work.

The service at this particular Starbucks is fast and very friendly. I'm never disappointed at how helpful the staff is. When the line is long they have even compensated by giving me my coffee free! This is my favorite Starbucks yet! The inside is clean too and that's important.

It's Starbucks you know the chain you either love it or hate it....I'm a HUGE fan.   This one has all the perks *drive thru  *outdoor seating  *full offering of bistro boxes & food options -- I've done the leg work not all Starbucks in Reno carry the Edamame Humus Bistro Box  *Free wifi *Lots of indoor seating options -- bar seating facing the window, 3 bistro tables & large communal table.    Linda is my favorite barista here!  Actually they're all pretty nice!   Yup it's pricey but an addiction is an addiction!

It's a Starbucks located inside the Albertsons in goleta. Standard faire Starbuck items so nothing really new to add. Since it's so new they obviously have some growing pains (starbuck app not working properly on on visit, longer then normal time to get drinks made).   The one thing I like about this location is the convenience factor as I can get a drink before or after I shop and not have to drive a block over to the other location for my caffeine fix.

This Starbucks never gets drinks made to order, the drinks are always super watered down and they never taste good. Feels like a "half-assed" Starbucks, including employee effort, overall food, and customer service. You definitely don't get what you pay for. Go to a different Starbucks.
This place gets the order wrong every time. Tea tastes watered down. I'll travel the extra distance and go to neshaminy or oxrord oaks.

This Starbucks is a hit or miss. Sometimes I get the most amazing beverage and sometimes it's way off mark. Today I asked for an Iced caramel Macchiato stirred and it tasted like milk with caramel flavor and came unstirred. *SMH* :-( I would have asked for them to make me up a new one but I was worried that it would be extremely heavy on the espresso. So I just stuck with it plus I was to lazy to ask. So my bad I guess but if you pay $5 for designer coffee shouldn't it be perfect??

This Starbucks has always been terrible. I hate to sound so mean, but it's the truth. There are never enough people working and there is always a line due to the fact that it's so understaffed. In fact, I avoided this Starbucks for a long time due to some serious maintenance issues. When I say issues, I mean tiny flies were everywhere and it smelled of sour milk. I've recently come back to this location since my friends live near this one.   To be honest, it has gotten better. Maybe it's just because it isn't very busy anymore and people are avoiding this Starbucks, lol.
I am not big on Starbucks but this location I like as a traveler - large space, cool decor like trampet celling light, big communal table, free wifi, bathrooms and outlets to charge your devises.

Perhaps one of the worse Starbucks I been to. I went through the drive thru and ordered a tall green tea latte. It took forever and when I got it, the green tea powder was in chunks. Clearly not a well made drink . By the time I turned around, the drink had already separated and the green tea powder was all settled at the bottom. So I go into the store and ask them to remake the drink. The cashier was friendly but the barista, all she did was pour my drink into a tumbler and shake it a few times. That is clearly not the definition of "remake." Definitely not returning to this location. They also seem to have charged me extra compared to Starbucks I usually go to.

Super busy location right next to CHOP and HUP. Often packed between shifts at both hospitals, so be prepared to wait 5-15 minutes. A few times, I have bypassed this location based on the snaking line inside and nearly outside. When I have ordered, the coffee-based drinks and offerings are pretty much on par with other Starbucks locations. With such high-traffic, the cleanliness of this store suffers as well. Not sure how the employees can really address this. They have seemed super busy when I have been there. I think most people who go here love Starbucks products and are willing to put up with the lines and lack of cleanliness around the store. As a plus, sells the Starbucks Reserve brand coffee.

This is my new go to Starbucks as it's the closest location to my house. I wish it had a drive thru but not every location is equipped for one. I usually only do mobile orders as this location is super packed most of the time with local students sucking up the free WiFi.   I highly recommend doing mobile orders on saturdays as the weekend soccer mom crews hit up this location in droves. Give the mobile orders double the estimate time as the baristas are usually swamped with the order lines going up to the entrance.   Must say the people working here are very friendly and cheerful. They have never fussed about some of the creative concoctions I try ordering with the free drinks I get from the Starbucks reward program (love trying out the secret menu items found on the internet)

Literally waited in the drive thru for 12 mins at this location off Speedway and Pantano. I would have gotten out of line but I was blocked in with cars behind and in front of me. Ordered two grande coffees that came out to 12$, defiantly more than I wanted to spend. The staff wasn't rude, but wasn't the friendliest either. I ordered two medium Carmel Macchiato's that were pretty dang good so I'll give them that. I may return to this location in the future.

The service at this Starbucks was absolutely terrible. I ordered a pumpkin spice chai tea latte and instead of  the cashier asking me if I wanted a receipt, she crumpled it up and threw it away. I waited for a good 30-40 minutes. I then went up to the cashier to ask for my order and they didn't even have my order up! So I waited another 10 minutes for my drink. And then one of the cashiers accused me of stealing the drink and was trying to get me to pay for it again. I told him I already paid for it but I didn't have a receipt because the cashier crumpled it up. Then I left.

I love this Starbucks.  It's my go-to attagirl prize for getting my grocery shopping done at the nearby Walmart. I've always received prompt, friendly service and it's nice that they have a drive thru.

I used to love this place because the employees are so welcoming, quick, and super organized. There was one employee who always remember my usual drink and always make it quick for me. Even when it gets busy, the line moves very very quickly. Now I don't see the old employees working there anymore. This place has changed. It is now very disorganized. During 3 visits, I was in line for 10 minutes. The drinks took awhile also.

I really like this Starbucks. 1. it's right by my house, 2. the staff is typically kind and sweet, 3. the wait isn't too bad. The only downfall is that sometimes my drink is spot on and sometimes they don't blend it well enough so when I get home I have to mix it myself. I always do the mobile ordering and they're on top of that! One of the perks of Starbucks. Definitely a good starbucks other than the drink mishaps from time to time.
Was super late for our train and they were able to give us the speedy service we needed (with a smile)! Solid Starbucks ;)   Thanks!

They coffee here is good, like at all Starbucks. The offerings to eat is quite limited. What is astounding is how indifferent the people who work here are to how loud the music is. At times it is impossible to talk to someone on the phone without using the speaker phone. I have had to talk to people who work at various Starbucks about this, requesting that they turn the music down, mostly with bad results. They say they will gladly will turn the music down, then they don't. And they will not turn it off. I can only surmise what the reason is for this is: They think they are a music venue, and the music they are playing is simply too important for people not to be exposed to it.

I think this is the last time I'm coming to this location. Every day this week I ordered a Blonde Vanilla Latte and every time I receive a plain latte. Today I decided to call in and ask for a refund. The woman who answered the phone said to come back and they'll fix it. Unfortunately I'm well on my way to the office. So I ask how about tomorrow? She said that fine but when I asked who I should ask for she seemed bothered by the question and said "I don't know who is going to be here tomorrow" and then started to talk to another customer and hung up on me.   Sorry I'm not going to participate in the lack of customer response. I've been going to that Starbucks every day , I mean every day, for the past 2 years. Every morning and I never complain but I can't take paying 6 dollars everyday for something I didn't order and didn't want on a consistent basis. I'll try the Clayton location for now on.   Keisha
Terrible service inside  They waited on everybody but me then they told me all we have to make more coffee were out I sat there for 10 minutes very frustrating

Coffee and pastries were fine. Issue was the wait due to inept staff. There were two customers in front of me and it took me 10 minutes to reach the register. Unacceptable at an airport. Girl at the counter was friendly but didn't know how to properly work the register. I asked for my coffee in a double cup - got a single, ordered a coffee cake - got a pound cake. This happened to everyone in front of me. All this while a good 2-3 employees were not doing much of anything(let alone helping the confused girl) and another 2 were making drinks slower than I've ever seen at a sbux. Manager was busy counting money instead of dealing with the situation. Nice seating area - maybe I would've used it if I did't have to run to my gate!

This was my first trip back to this Starbucks since moving to Maplewood. When I lived in South City this was my go to location on my way to work each night and on my days off. In the past I always ordered ahead and they were great about always remembering that when I ordered nonfat I still wanted whipped cream and they always greeted me by name. Today I went through the drive thru and ordered a nonfat drink with whipped cream. When I got to the window they had my drink and the girl questioned why it had whipped cream when my ticket said without. The manager John told her he was sure it was supposed to have whipped cream and greeted me by name. I was really taken back by this since I haven't visited here in over 6 months. There is something to be said about someone that remembers your name and order after all that time. I may have to go out of my way more often to go to this location again.

This is the most comfortable, and well-managed Starbucks I've been to in Philadelphia, hands down.  One might be hesitant to go in due to the current construction in the building (rendering the entrance a bit hidden) and the seemingly long lines, but rest assured, they know how to move a line along and you won't be waiting more than a minute or two.

Can't believe that I haven't taken the time to write a 5-star review about this location thus far; that is, it would have been a 5-star review up until my latest visit last weekend.  Let's first talk about the 2-ton elephant that I just plopped into the middle of the room: this used to have about 6 super-comfy chairs in which to come here to relax and drink in. The seating really set a wonderful tone as the awesome corner neighborhood coffee shop, in this quiet section of Rittenhouse. But the comfy seats are truly gone, and replaced with the typical wooden chairs and tables. Granted, there is a bit more seating for this busy location now; but this quaint neighborhood spot just became a bit more sterilized. So there's that.  Now on to the great things about this location: it's still a great neighborhood coffee shop for this quiet section of Rittenhouse, with ample (now more) seating, has a great open atmosphere, it's one of the very few area locations open through 10pm, and it may well have the coolest baristas in town - they've been the nicest, down-to-earth gals around (I'm sure there's guys too, but I haven't seen any thus far).   So this is still my go-to location for when I want to get out into my neighborhood; but it's now less cozy than it used to be, is all.

The employees here are not great... Management should really think about hiring associates who clearly aren't morning people when your store opens so early! I thought the store opened at 5am, when it actually opened at 5:30.  I tried to apologize to the associates for getting angry about them not being open and no one would even make eye contact with me.  One woman in general was extremely rude, should not be working in customer service at all!  The other woman working charged me to add cream to my iced coffee? Since when is that an additional cost?  So glad this isn't my local Starbucks...

It's no mystery that Starbucks is by far my favorite stop for coffee and other selections that are spontaneous dictated by my cravings at any given moment. I have visited literally hundreds of Starbucks' location since they've come into existence and although they all seem to be pretty consistence with the quality of the products they offer; the service, quality of the product, wait-time, cleanliness and accessibility of the location are the five points I will rate all of them on going forward - so to make the point I've recently incorporated a rating system based on this short list to calculate the rating of the locations I visit. I feel all these criterions are controllable by the corporation, the franchise owner or store manager.  Service - 3 Stars Quality - 4 Stars Wait Time - 2 Stars Cleanliness - 4 Stars Accessibility - 2 Stars  Rating -  3.0 Stars

Ordered ahead and still waited over 20 minutes for my food item which I never received. I asked several times and was told "it's coming, we are really backed up". When people behind me received their items before me, I had no choice but to leave without my sandwich as I was late for work due to waiting 20 minutes. Starbucks has gone so downhill
What's up??? I waited in line 10 minutes to order, then waited another10 for my drink. If you are understaffed, put more people on!
The worst coffee in general at every store bitter and overpriced, it tastes like they never clean their urns and pots, I only went here BC it was my only choice for coffee
If you come to this Starbucks, be prepared to wait F O R E V E R!!!! In line OR inside.

This is a well designed, large Starbucks that is a great escape from the summer heat. It has everything you expect from a Starbucks, including a Clover machine.  It's a Starbucks, you know what it's like here.

The service here is absolutely terrible, I will not return. I have came here like 7 times now and gave them the benefit of the doubt because they are a new location. I've ended up in the drivethru line stuck for 30 minutes 2x when there was only 2 cars ahead of me when 1 order each. It's wild how long it takes to get through when they are not short staffed.

Damn long night can not sleep well ...I real need a cup of coffee , lucky my place near by starburst . I order ice coffee with cream milk and sugar . The waiter is the girl , she was very nice with beautiful smile ....I told her make sure not put milk in my coffee because I have allergic with milk ....she said ok 'I will make sure ' and she gave me black coffee ...oh my god , maybe I spoke not real good English so she was not understood ... I told her I want cream milk in my coffee ....then she was putted cream in my coffee but my coffee was not real good . And price $2.56 for regular cup ...     I gave 3 stars because they are nice but coffee not make me happy.

Playing the Starbucks stars game today. Need to keep my gold card status for the little one in our life. The service today was excellent. When I went up to the counter to get a free refill I was asked how I liked the Pineapple Black Tea and I said I didn't. They happily switched my refill to strawberry green tea. Much better.

One star because they're there. Sucks the big one. Across the street from my job, and was so excited because the one by USF is awesome. Seeing the drive thru had such a long line, I went inside-for quicker service, ya know. But, they had me wait for my order until everyone in the drive thru was served. Really?! Well, I did, i did.... I threw a hissy fit.  What's the use? I'll stop at Mambo's. Cheaper, faster, tastes better.   Hey Starbucks MLK! You suck!

I'm seriously taking it personally that this Starbucks only has a 2.5 star rating. As it may be my favorite Starbucks. Here's the thing, upon perusing the below reviews, the folks that review it negatively are basing their review off one or two negative experiences. Take it from a girl who frequents this establishment multiple times per week - it doesn't get much better.   I will say there isn't a lot of seating room. So if you are looking to post up and get some work done, maybe don't. This is the perfect location to come in grab and go or come in, grab, eat quickly then go. The staff is super nice and friendly. They remember me and my  order - what more can you ask for???   Negative - this shopping center parking lot STINKS.

Be warned.  Starbucks on saint josephs university is money hungry.  They will over charge you for anything if they want.  Employees Always on the phone.  Not a real starbucks.  No customer service skills   Go to the other Starbucks on 46th and city Line Avenue or on City Avenue and monument rd.

I think I may have arrived after a mongol attack.  Upon walking in, there were wet footprints about (it was not raining), one of the employee's check stubs was laying in the entrance (and man, I hope that person was a part-timer).  I found the source of the wet footprints being an overflowing and caution-taped off wide-open men's bathroom.  Coffee was fine, place was dirty, people were nice what I saw.  I'll try again some day as it's near where I work.  It was typical Starbucks employee formation of 3 non-stop coffee-slingers never catching up with the unending line of customers, so I can see why the place was a bit dirty.  The choice was a line out the door or stopping to clean.

The Asian barista made a great iced double shot so we ordered another. But the young (teen?) barista lady ruined the next one. Strongly suggest baristas taste the drinks they make, it will give an idea how good or bad you make them. Great place to read and has extended room for customers!

I have to say that I am a bit of a Starbucks but and typically don't have an issue with any of the service that I get.  The Bryn Mawr location is the exception.  First of all it seems as if all of the employees are on a major "slow down mode". It takes them forever to ring people up and forever to make the drinks.  I can not figure it out.  No other location is like this!  May be the management - who knows.  But I dread going in there but it I convenient from the train station.   Their parking sucks, but hey there is nothing they can really do about that in downtown Bryn Mawr.  I just really wish they would start drinking the espresso so that we can be served in a consistent amount of time - and with a SMILE!

We stopped in here to grab a sweet treat after a tough day. Our barista Bryan welcomed us with a smile. He was very sweet and caring. He asked how our day was and we briefly explained what we had just experienced.  He said he was sorry for our loss and really made us feel better. He went above and beyond and that was outstanding customer service for me. He didn't have to care but he did. I hope @Starbucks knows how lucky they are to have such an awesome employee working for them.

I sent my order to a store that why 10 miles away. They tried to void it but couldn't and just offered to take care of it. Many complain this is a slow Starbucks, it's also busy as Shit all the time.   Mobile order and waiting is not an issue ever. (Just send to the right store!)

I never complaint on starbucks..but this time i have to! Mobile ordered: exactly 18 mins till i get my drink when i order 5 mins down the road! I understand theres situation where everyone ordered at the same time...but c'mon, 18 mins for a coffee drink,, i really dont have time for it! Whats the point of mobile order anyway...Really have to rethink about going to starbucks every single time now.  And on top of that..my drink was wrong...didnt follow my order!! ask for no ice, when i get ice..before that I ask gently how long or how many drink are ahead of me when i said i have been waiting 15 mins or more? maybe i could come back bc i have an urgent place i need to be..the barista guy smile and said with a tone "you are not the only one waiting, but the whole franklin town is waiting this morning!" wth...i am still shaking my head with the whole starbucks experience this morning!

Fix the drive thru!  Quality of service - 5 Friendliness of employees - 5 Speed of drive thru service - 1  If someone gets a complicated order or one that takes a lot of time have them pull forward and keep the line moving. Easy fix.
Good quality service, friendly employees and always an enjoyable experience.  I recommend this establishment everytime
Predictable, solid service. Nice that your drive-thru drink orders are served to you on a tray to minimize touches.

Because Starbucks has the same drinks...well... everywhere, I base my Starbucks reviews on customer service and ambiance/seating areas. As someone who likes to use Starbucks to get writing done, this location is pretty okay.  Late Saturday afternoon, is a little less crowded than going on a Sunday. There are plenty of seats, although I wish there were more electrical outlets. The weekend lines tend to be long, but the baristas are friendly, and they are usually pretty good at getting through orders fairly quickly, even when busy.  What I don't like?  It's always FREEZING in the seating area of this location.  What's up with that? I mean, I always have to wear a heavy sweater or hoodie no matter what the temperature is.  I guess that will come in handy during the summer though when it's hot.

I ordered an iced latte and got an iced coffee. I asked if it was supposed to be a latte and the barista looked at the sticker and said no. I assumed I mis-ordered and took the drink. I took a sip and just randomly looked at the sticker, IT SAID LATTE. So I took it up and asked if I actually could have the latte I ordered and the barista seemed super cool about it. When my order was called , it was a hot latte even though I ordered ice. I didn't feel like wasting another drink, so I just dealt with it. I've never been inside this Starbucks, it's clean and spacious but I most definitely wont be heading back to this location anytime soon.
These people refused me service and I saw them spit in some poor mans drink! Please consider ANYWHERE else to go this place is disgusting!!!!

Love this Starbucks. It's a community Starbucks, so they have poetry and entertainment at times. The manager is awesome and really turned things around. It had a rough start in the beginning. It's very very spacious. Great for meetings!!! Parking can be tough although I always luck out.

Now 5 out of 6 visits the store is out of something. I don't know who is in charge of their inventory and/ordering but they're doing a terrible job. Tried giving benefit of the doubt but now we're going to make it a point to not come here anymore. Totally unacceptable.
I have good experiences here. Just happy it exists honesty, in this area.  Drive through is my preference most visits here, and energy one is friendly. I have had no issues with this location.

Been using this Starbucks for the last year since it is on my way to work, sadly since they shut down with Covid and opened back up again their drive through experience has went extremely down hill. They used to be super fast and great on orders, however this is the third time I've gotten in line (not a long line, maybe 5 cars max) and had at least a 20 minute wait in line. If you are in a rush I would suggest another store... people are very nice and helpful so if you have time they are great!
Always clean, good coffee, great service, a d easy to pay on the app. Starbucks really gets it. Bathrooms are usually clean and nice. Good parking and friendly service.

I love coming to the Starbucks. The staff is always so friendly, drinks are made quickly and never really a long line for mobile orders. With the pandemic and every thing going on in the world, the little things make a difference. "We Love You" written on a cup just brightened my day!
They got my order wrong BUT got me hooked on something NEW & tasty! I've never had my coffee without something creamy but this is very very nice and tasty. YUMMY!

Ordered two drinks from the Starbucks in Grand Sierra Resort. One of them still had large chucks of ice that wasn't fully blended. The other was only 2/5 filled, and when we asked why, the worker just told us we could get in line again. Unacceptable because we always order the same drink at other Starbucks without any trouble or inconvenience.  Service here is lacking, as well as the coffee. Deserves better!

Usually come there often, but service was beyond rude didn't even greet me, be honest didn't recognize the girl serving too, spend most of the time talking to coworker so just left. Time is money and if I'm not getting proper service definitely not giving my money.

Real disappointment. Not clean. No bathrooms operational.  Not enough seating.  Took way too long just to make one latte. Glass doors were full of fingerprints and I was here early in the AM so obviously not cleaned from day before. Advertised on I-75 but was 3 miles off the highway. Deceptive.

I tried this location a couple times before leaving this review in hopes that the service would improve, but unfortunately that's not the case.   Pros: This is one of the larger, if not largest Starbucks in the area. It's pretty much brand new and has a drive thru. The inside is very modern with plenty available seating. It's a free standing building, which is very nice especially since most Starbucks are in strip malls, or located in other businesses.   Cons: This location gets very busy, especially the drive thru. I've had it take over 20 mins to get my order, but unfortunately going inside isn't much quicker. This location suffers from poor customer service. I have been at the counter and waited almost 10 mins before being greeted, then another 10 mins after ordering to receive my order. The parking can be a little tough as well, since Starbucks shares a parking lot with Panda Express and many other retailers.   Unfortunately, I do not recommend this location.

Having a full functioning Starbucks inside of a hospital was a great idea. There long lines in the morning but it moves really quickly.  If you go to Starbucks, I would suggest getting the app because you can get points on your purchase and eventually get free coffee.  You can't redeem your free drinks at this location.

The Slowest Starbucks in tri state area! Expect 15-20 min drive thru times. Doesn't matter time of day or night.  If you do order ahead 10 min out and go inside to pick up the drink still wont be ready.

Super friendly staff! I typically drink basic black coffee at other places but they have a lot of different roast so they are very knowledgeable when it comes to flavors and such! Love the Verona blonde roast now!

I ordered a skinny mocha frap and a regular mocha frap and the barista made a big deal about it to where she actually said she couldnt deal with it because her lazy ass couldnt make two different fraps. First she made both fraps hot and then added whip cream too both even though i asked for no whip. That's when she got frustrated and had to pass to the other barista to make it.

I've never been much of a coffee drinker. I'm more of a hot tea kind of girl. However, when I stumbled into this Starbucks looking for Christmas gifts, I have to admit I was really pleasantly impressed.   Not only did they have a great display of gifts for sale, but to my surprise I found a nice little "nook" that I see myself going back to to hang out. They have a few comfy chairs, and several decent sized, sturdy tables set up where you can meet, study, and plan your day.  My favorite surprise was the book nook - they have a little alcove of books that you can read...YAY.  While I was there, I thought what the heck. I got a Salted Carmel Mocha Grande - YAY again. I have decided that this is now my go to beverage when I stop in. This drink alone may have converted me into a coffee drinker! No one is more surprised than I about that, let me tell you.   I always envisioned Starbucks as having long lines, cramped space, and crabby people needing their next caffeine fix. I've been there 5 or 6 times now, and I always get waited on right away, and the barista is über friendly and helpful.   I like this spot. A lot. The only drawback I've found so far, is that it's in a strip mall, and the parking lot can be full right in front of their store. But for books, a comfy chair, and and a Salted Carmel Mocha, I can fight for a parking spot.
When the free Monday Holiday Treat didn't come off my bill the barista went to fix it, He said my whole bill was on them. I love when they're so kind to their customers.
Friendly staff and accurate orders. Can't ask for much more from Starbucks :)
Literally one of the worst Starbucks ever. 2 stars only because it's busy but that's no excuse for consistently getting people's orders wrong.

A-ok... Unless this is your first SBUX experience, you know what to expect. Today, 6/25/16, TIA is not too busy, and neither are the shops. Flying Spirit, so decided to BYOC. Tasty brew and friendly staff. I will be back... But then again, this is a favorite stop when coming to FLA.  PS. Free Wi-Fi via the airports network!
Starbucks always has good customer service! The best variety menu! Comfortable and never disappoints#
It was good. Really like the vibes I was getting! Was busy, but understandable because all the drinks were heavily modified, I like my drinks simple. Please support our baristas by ordering simple.

Very rude, dropped an espresso cup  on accident and was planning to pay for it. It was my fault, but Employee looked at me rudely and rolled his eyes... had an attitude and for no reason. I will not come back to this Starbucks EVER! If you do not enjoy your job then don't work for the company. You never know who's having a bad day & you treating someone worse ... does not make things better. I did not order anything from Starbucks.

I always stop at this Starbucks in the way home from work on Fridays- it's never crowded and always stocked full of Pastries in the case and other refrigerated goodies. The baristas aren't as personable as my 'regular' Starbucks but at least I get in and out fast. There's always a fair amount of people utilizing the common area with their laptops so I think it's a conducive environment to get work done!

A dysmal Starbucks by all standards besides maybe speed. It's impressive they're able to move the line along and complete mobile orders as one of the busiest locations in the city. However, if it weren't for that, I would say it doesn't even deserve one star.  I've tried iced americanos and cold brew from here that never fails to taste burnt and today decided to order an iced green latte which ended up being weird tasting milk (didn't smell spoiled but definitely tasted off) with green powder at the bottom. My friend has ordered iced tea and a frapp here which have both been extremely watered down or 80% ice. I'm not sure if it's because it's the morning, but there hasn't been one order I've been happy with. Skip this location and go to any of the ones nearby.
Nice cashier, but they forgot my sandwich. Manager said "it should be coming up," but I had to get back in line so they actually would take my request seriously.
It's always busy here, but the staff works really hard and they are always super friendly.  When I visit, I usually have four drink orders and they make them very quickly.

I mobile order here before work all the time, today i placed my order when i left my house (it's a 20min drive) i get there expecting my drink to be ready when i got there... it was not instead i had to wait 30 mins for my drink and then be late for work because no one was doing their job correctly.

Typical Starbucks - good drunks, great service.  Only fails in attention to facility privacy.... Ladies restroom lock hasn't been fixed.   Instead the have a note!!!  Whoopee!   They need to fix the lock!  Like YESTERDAY !

This Starbucks is extremely rude and prejudice. I came in and all three of the staff weren't doing anything but walking around pretending to do something. Two looked me right in the eye and didn't take my order. I was the only one in line, but a white man walked up and they took his order while he was standing next to me. It was extremely rude, and they won't ever have to worry about seeing me here ever again.

This is my local Starbucks and I've seen it all. They've clearly gone through management changes in the past couple years, and I have to say they're always getting better!  Example: they just moved the milk/sugar station (what is that even called??) closer to the door, and for the first time in months it was fully clean and stocked! So they're definitely paying attention and trying to give the best experience possible.   I typically mobile order from this location in the mornings, and they usually have a pretty short prep time. This is perfect since I can order on my way down from my apartment in the elevator, drive over, park in the 20-minute spots just up the street, grab my mobile order, and head to work. My orders are always accurate, and the only problem I've run into is when they're out of sous vide egg bites (low carb woot!) - this has happened several times, though not recently, and usually they're on top of checking so when I arrive they can substitute something else for me. It's unfortunate they can't respond via the app, but this is a corporate Starbucks issue that has nothing to do with this store.

Walked in at 8:25 was told sorry we are closed. I asked what time do y'all close... the girl said 9 when I told her it was only 8:30 she said sorry we are closed as I said. She walked me out and locked the door behind me. 11/06. Rude as all hell! Closed over 30 minutes early and was nasty.

Conveniently located next to my place of work and full of the friendliest baristas?  Perfect.  I typically like any place where I frequent enough that people start to recognize either me or know what I usually order.  I wish Starbucks wasn't so expensive, but that is not any of the workers' faults - I'm sure they wish their paychecks were larger, too.    I don't think I have to say anything about the coffee.  This location gets full of college students during school season who are faceboo, err... studying.    While I would love for you to be open 24 hours, my pocketbook would not.

Love this starbucks, the staff is quick, friendly, and patient. This is one of the few starbucks that I've encountered that always give out free samples nearly every weekend. It's nice to go to a coffee shop with friendly baristas that always take your order with a smile on their face:-) My drinks are always good from here.

A Starbucks is a Starbucks is a Starbucks. This one is reliable, with friendly employees, even when I stop by before 6 AM, a usually quick drive-through, and a clean store. The drawbacks are that you can only really get in going eastbound or southbound (not their fault) and that the outside drive-through ordering sign is almost always broken.  Not a coffee standout, but if you need your go-to espresso drink, this Starbucks always suffices.

Just had my first coffee from Starbucks in years  I didn't buy! Was horrible still ! Coffee just had cream nothing fancy got home took taste it was so bitter  tasted like last cup that sat all day! Even had extra extra cream! Guess i wont order again!Wawa has better coffee and not near the cost!

Like every other Starbucks near campus, this place gets BUSY! The surrounding are quaint, like your artsy friend's living room, but they get crowded like a house party in no time. There's a back room with a long trestle table and several smaller ones well-lit by generous windows...prime study/work spot. The front patio is small, but gives a great view of University ave and the baristas here are not as ditzy as the on-campus Starbs.

This isn't just your average Starbucks. Walk through their doors and feel like you're home. I will go out of my way to see my favorite barista's at this location, they have been known to put a smile on my face even during a rough day. It's more than just my coffee, it's a cup of sunshine. A little cheesy? Yes. But to top it all off, if you are not as into the feel good stuff as I am, they make a perfect drink here.

Fast, friendly, courteous service. My drinks are always prepared exactly how I request it to be made. This location is always busy , but there is always room to sit since a lot of the traffic are to go orders.

Shocked!!  Most of the time this place is excellent.  Why do you shut down your food display before 8 PM when you are open until 10 PM?  Two hours before you close you don't want to sell more product that you actually do have in stock? Can't make profit from a turned off empty display.  Does corporate care about the unwelcome feeling a customer gets with this experience? Nice people, clean store, very weird business practice.
I was hopeful they would have my regular drink. The barista was very short and cut me off on my request. "Don't have that!" Will not visit this location again.

This Starbucks was conveniently located off the interstate. Everywhere is out of almost every syrup made because of shortages in production. I went with the only thing available. Vanilla latte. Bathrooms were clean. It was a little slow getting the coffee and they weren't that busy.
Ok. Service. Not a corporate location, so they don't accept Starbucks coupons. Not cool. :-(

Great New FAST Starbucks in Four Season's Shopping Center.  This location makes Drive Thru a breeze.  Enter the Starbucks lot from N Woods Mill @ Olive Blvd.  Traffic from this store exits onto Olive Blvd.  This Starbucks is in a brand new building on a corner lot that has been empty for years (in front of Dierbergs).  Standard Starbucks Products available.  I ordered a Flat White and it was a delicious as always.  Extra friendly service.  Check out this new store!  I will be back soon.

I love this Starbucks the people here are extremely nice. I come here at night mostly to do homework and there are plenty of places to sit. The staff will also go around and ask you if you want a free sample of a drink that's in season. LOVE IT!!! I've been thru drive thru as well same type of service A+
This location is absolutely bonkers. Waited a pretty long stretch for a tall black coffee. I think they were a barista short or something. Grab coffee BEFORE the mall!

The customer service needs to be improved, our order was missing food and when we called to ask them They were very dry and rude. It sounded like they didn't care that THEY FORGOT TO PUT OUR FOOD IN THE BAG

I came here for a really quick visit, as I placed my order through the drive through. The drive through situation is somewhat abnormal as Starbucks is on the right end of the shopping center, and you have to drive around from the left end behind the stores. Not sure if I would drive through if it was late in the evening, as it seems like it would be pretty dark. The employee who helped me was very friendly, and for it to be peak time on a Saturday, the line moved relatively quickly, and my food and drink was made in a pretty efficient amount of time. I will probably visit again. This parking lot can get crowded, so hopefully next time it would be crowded and I won't mind as much parking to go inside.
Great neighborhood coffee spot with an incredibly friendly staff.  If there is no street parking in the front, there's always space behind the building.

I used to live in Seminole Heights and frequented this location often.  The service was great and the drinks were awesome as usual.  This is a 24/7 location, which is awesome.  My only complaints about this location is that it's too small, gets crowded and there is not enough parking.  I gave it 5 stars because the staff is always attentive and take customer satisfaction very seriously.  Although there are long waits at times, the people that you get to meet make up for it!    B. Major Grade:  A

Convenient location with drive through for my work commute. I usually end up here around 6pm (night shift!) & 10am on Sundays before church. Drinks are good but the service is painfully slow with my average drive thru wait at 15 minutes. Inside service is not any faster. And I know rush times are challenging but maybe they need more staffing during these times?? I've been to Starbucks at the busiest areas in LA and sevice was very quick.... right now, I'm sitting in the drive through line, there are 2 people in line, 2 people waiting to pick up their drink, 3 people ahead of me in drive through, & I've been waiting 18 minutes and counting... sigh.

I do not prefer to go to this starbucks, they are always out of something, such as Carmel for the fraps, or whip cream, the girl at the counter is always preoccupied with something. its always crowded and the quality of the drinks are off. Id much rather drive out of my way to the startbucks on State than visit the mesa location

I'm not a starbucks fan overall but was forced to stop at this one. The breakfast sandwich was greasy and disgusting but the dragonfruit drink was really good. The staff wasn't friendly at all either. I'm going to continue to avoid them as much as I can.

This has always been our favorite Nashville Starbucks, but this is the second time I have been in recently and had an extended wait. They don't have enough people working. Staff working are doing their best but there is too much customer volume in this location for only 2 people.   My order was placed for a Mocha and a hot chocolate at 4:35. Drinks ready at 4:53. One questions if the desire for profit has exceeded the desire for a positive customer experience.

Come here if you're a fly.  Or a person who likes flies.  This Starbucks is conveniently located and usually seems to have tables open.  The employees are friendly and efficient.  But both times I went there to do work, I was grossed out by the flies buzzing around.  It's good to take out the garbage sometimes.  Just a thought.

I understand all fast food is over worked but when you do a mobile order. Your drink should come out as you placed it. I say light ice every time and every time is like they purposely add extra ice. When a drink is 7 bucks every day. You want it to be made correctly.

Best Starbucks experience ever! Not sure if I'm spelling this right, but Diane was the nicest persons ever. We came through the drive through and she was so perky and friendly. I go to Starbucks everyday and this by far exceeded my expectation and made us smile

This has been going on for a few weeks and I can't ignore it any longer... There's a new assistant manager named Amy at this store and her presence is intolerable any longer.  The energy she puts off is not pleasant, the staff vibe is ruined when she's on shift and I just can't stand being in her presence. I used to be a District Manager for a Fortune 200 company in retail and she would not survive in one of my stores, no clue how she's a Starbucks member, much less in a management or leadership role. I hope Starbucks doesn't lose staff over her as this is one of the best crews in Tucson and where I go 4-7 times a week to study with my daughter. Starbucks, listen to me, Amy needs to go, you're jeopardizing longtime customers and valuable staff because of one bad element...

I was staying at the Renaissance nearby for the weekend. Since I was up early, I ended up having a big lunch, taking a nap around 6, and totally missing dinner! Not feeling up for a big meal, I decided to take advantage of the billions of Starbucks gift cards that I have and get a little snack.   It was a mile away AND they have a drive-thru, so it was perfect! I pulled in and there was no-one there. Not sure if it was because of the time or if it's because it's still a new Starbucks (so no-one knows about it), but I was happy! I pulled up to the menu at the drive-thru and was immediately greeted by a VERY friendly employee. I asked her to give me a few minutes to decide what I want. After a few "hmm's" and "uhm's", she decided to help me out. She asked me if I was looking to get something particular, something big, something small, etc.. It was really helpful, and I appreciated her personality. I ultimately decided on the iced green tea and their oatmeal.   I pulled up to the window and she was still SUPER friendly! Seriously, the friendliest employee/barista I've EVER encountered at any coffee shop. My order was ready within a couple of minutes, and I was on my merry way.   The oatmeal was nom! It would've been even better if I was at my own home with my own microwave to heat it up a bit more but that's no fault of Starbucks. I asked her to "hook it up", so I got all of their toppings - dried fruit, mixed nuts, FRESH blueberries, agave, and brown sugar. I opted to not use the brown sugar (since it wouldn't have dissolved), but the agave was perfect. It was really delicious, and I'm definitely looking forward to getting it again. The iced green tea was as good as usual.  A great little Starbucks! Also, it's next to a Chipotle, so it would be perfect if you're looking to have dessert after a meal.   PS. I managed to sneak a few peaks at the interior. It is definitely bigger than it looks from the outside. Also, the parking lot is small but decent.

Don't go to this Starbucks. Pregnant with my third kid and barely sleeping with bad pregnancy brain. I stopped by the Starbucks while walking my dogs because my daughter was hungry. I went inside to get her a snack. Being human and a tired, sleep deprived mom I accidentally walked off without my dogs. Realizing this shortly after, I called Starbucks in a panic & about in tears and told them I was not far away and was literally sprinting back to the store.  I asked if they could make sure no one steals them or anything. They said okay I see them and sure. WELL After this they called animal control and the sheriffs department on me. The sheriff told me after seeing my dogs she realized this wasn't really an appropriate call & that I was obviously a sleep deprived mom. She said she could tell I felt terrible and that my dogs were extremely well behaved and well cared for. She then told me to leave before animal control got there. Luckily, the sheriff was so understanding and kind. I will never return to this location. In the past they have done annoying things like mess up my drinks etc but I understand that mistakes happen and have never gotten upset. Now, I'm MAD. Support a local coffee shop instead. I am so easy to please this is my FIRST negative review. I usually only review things I love.
Great starbucks. Not much more to say then that. Plenty of seating to get work done, very very friendly staff, and drinks are always made to perfection.
Not the best Starbucks. Usually get drinks right. Just off HWY 30, so quick on and off. I prefer the independent coffee shops but sometimes all that is around is Starbucks.

I'm a coffee snob and Tucson has plenty of places to scratch that itch. That said, for a Starbucks (huge qualifier there!) this location is my favorite in town.   Easy parking, nice redesign on the interior, good crowd of regulars, and plenty of seating all make this location work. It's the closest walkable coffee spot to my house, so it gets a visit from time to time. And it's even saved me once or twice when I've run out of fancy third wave beans from EXO or Cafe Aqui.

This location is terrible. No matter how many cars are in the drive through line, it will take at least 25 minutes. Most of the employees are unfriendly and seem put out to have to deal with customers. Going inside is just as slow. When I've been inside the employees don't let you know drinks are done. There's just drinks lined up and customers have to check the stickers to find their order. Often people take the wrong order, walk away, then come back over to get the correct one.

An incredible staff at all hours. They are to a man and woman friendly,personable,efficient ,and professional. They remember your name and favorite drink and deliver it quickly. If you miss a day they are genuinely concerned that everything is  ok. Coming to this store each day is a great experience. Highly recommended.

Horrible service. Very unprofessional attitude and very slow service. I ordered a drink from the drive through and when I got to the window, I got a snarky remark because I wanted to use my Apple Pay. I'd rather drive an extra 5 minutes to the other location.

This location tends to have longer lines then others nearby but they seem to move effectively most of the time. They have never messed up my order and I constantly amazed at the memory of the expediter (must be all the coffee?)

I really liked this place when it first opened. But the staff has become progressively more unprofessional. While placing an order the cashier was more interested in chatting with his coworkers. As a result I had to tell him my order (which was simple - a latte - that's it, nothing fancy) three times before he finally got it. They get 0 stars for service. One star for the decor.

This place is usually really good at service but today it wasn't the best Sam was the person who did my drink but he messed it up and then when I asked for what I got he gave me sass which didn't feel good and made me feel bad then he forgot my friends drink so we waited for like 5 minutes and he still didn't make it then we finally asked and he was definitely annoyed and then he Aggressively graded the cup and she said that the drink didn't taste as well as it usually did and that it didn't look right

This Starbucks location was a meeting point for a walking tour of the Garden District we did while in NOLA.  But, I also learned a valuable lesson too!  ATMOSPHERE: It's a long and narrow space with lots of windows letting in natural light.  No drive thru.   BEVERAGE: Iced Coffee  Here's where my aha moment occurred.  While I've been to Starbucks numerous times, I always order a Frappuccino or smoothie, hardly ever an iced coffee as I don't want to make (mix) my own drink.  In the past, I'm usually given coffee over ice and forced to head to the condiment station, open the lid, discard some coffee so I can add cream, add sugar, and try best I can to stir them well with a straw or stirrer as opposed to using an unavailable spoon.  No thank you.  I might as well make it at home!  But, on this particular morning, I didn't want anything heavy or overly sweet, so I but on my big boy pants and was ready for a mixology session, or so I thought.  After placing my order, the barista asked "Would you like it with or without milk?'  Dumbfounded, I said "With milk."  "Sweetened?" she asked.  "Yes!"
