Low and behold, I received an iced coffee, 100% made for me!  My labor?  Insert a straw and grab a napkin.  That's what I'm talking about!  It was perfectly sweetened and creamed.    SERVICE: Cashier service was fairly quick yet very thorough and their baristas rocked!  OVERALL: Starbucks Corp:  Thanks to this shop on Magazine Street in New Orleans, you've gained an iced coffee fan!  If I don't receive the same ordering questions at my local shop, I know now that I can "have it my way."

This location never lets me down. They always have my togo order ready within moments of arriving. I totally avoid the drive thru line as you probably already know, they are always long and I have no patience. Going inside almost feels like a life hack. Always friendly, consistent and efficient.

Often the line is really backed up in the morning and there's no parking anywhere. Keep in mind if you're stopping on a weekday morning.  As of recently I've noticed if I order a extra shot of espresso it doesn't get added into my beverage and sometimes they don't put my requested soy milk. When I notify them I can tell I don't have the extra shot of espresso they give me attitude and say that the paper says it so the barista must have added it, when it's quite clear it doesn't have more than the usual. Getting pretty frustrated since it's the only Starbucks on my way to work and this is happening more and more lately.

Staff is great and drinks are made well. But the WiFi constantly disconnects and there is not enough plug ins around the cafe. I spoke with the manager about a month ago about these issues. She said the would be fixed, but no improvements so far
Average location, wait time was okay. No complaints, the hotel lobby was a nice thing to walk into!
Very LOUD and obnoxious employees. NOT a place to relax in peace, drink a cup of coffee and study, read, etc. Do not recommend.

Huge shoutout to the Starbucks in the CWE!! I consistently get amazing service from the barista team when I visit. Every single time, without fail, I am greeted with a friendly smile that always cheers me up. The staff at this location are absolutely outstanding, and I have never once had negative experience. Mobile ordering is super fast - I highly recommend that you order on the app so you don't have to wait in line. This Starbucks tends to be fairly busy so if you plan on popping in and out, order on the way.  Something I love about the interior is that pretty much every seating option is situated by an outlet. One of my biggest pet peeves is when coffee shops don't place tables and chairs conscientiously. If you're coming here to work and need an outlet, this is the ideal spot to be! A unique feature is the "greenhouse" portion of the store, which provides additional workspace with lots of natural light. Definitely my happy spot!

After six weeks Tucktaway in quarantine, and not being able to find a Starbucks anywhere... It was like finding gold to realize the location had finally opened up! It's amazing how much you miss something when it's no longer there!

This Starbucks location is worth a stop just because it is one of only 3 (at the time of this writing) locations in the greater Indianapolis area able to serve a COLD FOAM CASCARA NITRO. It's a deliciously smooth, lightly sweet treat that is worth going a few miles out of your way.  This location is large, with plenty of seating and pleasant employees. It's a great stop to satisfy your coffee cravings.

The service at this Starbucks is super slow. From the moment you walk in, you feel as if something fishy is up with the store. The glass on the front door is cracked with no tape or warning signs (safety hazard). I know vandalism happens, but make sure your customers are not going to get near it or use the door.  Next the line was only three people deep (me being that third person) and it took a good 10 minutes to get to the front. The three workers were doing the following: one was making drinks, by herself; the next one was on the register trying to ring up orders and she seemed a little distracted chatting five of the ten minutes away with her friend who was buying one item; the last guy was chatting away with the girl on the register, distracting her from her job.   After we ordered our drinks, we waited another 10 minute for them to arrive. Now I know this is typical of a crazy morning, a lunchtime rush, or any busy period in the day, but none of this was happening. When we got our drinks, we asked for one cardboard sleeve for our own cup that we brought. The girl making drinks was not only rude about it to us, she was rude about it to the lady in front of us about the same thing.  A couple of suggestions to the manager/owner: first, put a manager in that team; second, if the cardboard sleeves are -that- big of a hassle, put out a dispenser of them and customers won't have to ask for them. We were about to spend 3-4x what we did spend, but since the service was terrible, we decided not to and almost walked out and went to our preferred store about two miles away.
Always a good day when I can get my vanilla latte. Even if it's a pickup or drive through. Friendly people too. Thank you for your service.

Watched an employee named Alex pontificate loudly instead of start my drinks. Shuddup and do your job. By the way I passed two local coffee shops that can make a better latte. I guess I'm not going to be so brand loyal anymore.

Super awesome baristas and also a super busy Starbucks in the AM. I probably come here at least 2-3x a week to grab a caffeine fix for myself and the office. They always get all the drinks right and are done on a timely manner. All the baristas here are friendly and usually always have someone on hand to hear food at the bar.   I just wish parking wasn't such a pain in the butt but that's not really a Starbucks issue than the property management. They do have quite a few comfy chairs to lounge at and a few tables for chit chat. The lines do get long during peak hours but the baristas can only do so much with an espresso machine.
I have been here several times for coffee and breakfast. The service are always fast. But They have never made the coffee and food right. Was neither too watery or too sweet.

I keep trying to give them a chance but the very young man behind bar needed more training didn't know how to pour a flat white and didn't care. Friday 645 4/29. He shouldn't be on the bar. Does the manager ever look at their 2.5 star rating and think hmmmmm maybe we should work on customer service????
Slow service. Ordered one iced coffee with cream and one Americano plain. Took over twenty minutes to make. And no apology for the wait., I don't care for Starbucks coffee, but was desperate.
Apparently, this Starbucks isn't open on Father's Day or really any other time I've stayed at this hotel.

If no stars was possible that's what they would get....this Starbucks has the most incompetent staff in town.  I consistently get the worst drinks from them as they can not follow directions.  Not once have they gotten my drink order correct.  I have even ordered simple drinks they should not mess up like a green tea latte and they do not add enough of the mixture and I end up with a milk drink essentially.  So incompetent!

Ann is one of the nicest people I have ever met. Starbucks is a place where a lot of people go to have conversations and particular people go to be specific and complicated to feel better about themselves but Ann made everyone feel like they were special, including those annoying caramel frappacino/caramel machiatto with extra caramel people. I was so incredibly impressed with her service and care. She is an amazing person and I hope she gets promoted to the highest position because she is what every Starbucks employee should strive to be, up to the highest position. Hire more people like Ann and your company will be nothing less than the best.

Slow service, not awful but could use some speeding up. And they need one person strictly dedicated to cleanup, restocking condiments/sweeping/ trashcans. The place was pretty dirty, even behind the counter.   On the bright side the coffee was good.

I am a daily weekday customer of this Starbucks location, so if this review were anything OTHER than five stars you'd better believe I'd be a walking contradiction.   The success of this particular Starbucks may seem to be due to its centralized South Tampa location, but that is only partially true.  The rest of its success can be attributed to its excellent management and staff.   The manager Ian and all of his baristas make a solid effort to get to know  you - and not just how to spell your name (an effort that I have come to appreciate given the unique spelling of mine), but they also make a considerable effort to get to know who you are as a person. I would love to report that this is just my experience, but I have personally overheard more than one barista ask various customers, "Hey (insert any name you can think of), the usual?" Sometimes, they're already preparing the drink as the person walks in. This type of familiarity is hardly achievable at most modern-day bars, let alone a "chain" coffee shop!   On a recent occasion, a newer barista forgot my breakfast sandwich order. Upon realizing her mistake, she offered me a $5 gift card for my wait and trouble (which I graciously accepted...my daily visits make for an expensive habit!) The aforementioned is only one of many examples that support my assertion of a five star experience.   One last thing - re: the bathroom code; I am honestly shocked that it took Starbucks so long to implement a secured bathroom system. While I lived in NYC, Starbucks was referred to as "the public restroom of the city." This is no joke and it is no surprise - heck, there was one on nearly every corner!  If you are actually a CUSTOMER and not just popping by in a drunken stupor post-Gasparilla or after your generic walk/run on Bayshore, then asking for the code shouldn't be that hard now, should it?

I don't understand the problem they can't seem to get my order right and it's a very simple order of sous vide eggs with Sriracha. They always forget the Sriracha and I put it on my order. And they hardly ever remember to give you a napkin. I don't know why I want food from a place that doesn't give you a napkin! How hard is that!

Ok  it's 5:20am and i have never been greeted with such nice staff. They went out of their way to make my morning pleasant. Not to mention the drinks tasted better than ever! Thank you Todd for a great Latte and Thank you Joel for great service!!!

Back for the bogo special.  It is still only for take out only.  All tables and chairs are blocked or removed (including the outdoor seating).  The instructions are cleared for the CDC guidelines and also all workers wear mask.

This should be renamed a Starbucks Express.  This is a smaller location with seating for about 10 people.  Also, they don't have an oven, which means they can't toast a bagel and their food options are limited to prepackaged goods.

I was never a fan of starbucks. If my friends car didn't break down half a block away in traffic, then I probably would have never come here. But, I am really glad I did. The people behind the counter were so helpful with listening to me and helping me find something I liked. It was new and different-- french press style coffee brewed over ice.  They even asked if I'd like a bigger cup so I could add soymilk. Very thoughtful and pleasant employees put me in a good mood. The willow coffee was great, as well.

I'm the ultimate NON-coffee drinker, but decided to stop in here today to get coffees for the rest of the office staff.  We've been working tons of hours and knew that they could all use it.  I bought myself a pastry (ok, but not the best I've ever had).  It's relatively a newer Starbucks, but it's definitely not a place that I ever frequent with the wife.  Everyone was very happy when I brought in the coffees and it was good for morale.

The service was fine. Two issues: Minor- the wifi did not work well. I was able to easily log on to the wifi from the yogurt store next to the Starbucks   Major-a panhandler came in. We were sitting.  He approached us for money.  We refused.  He persisted for sometime. The employees did not ask him to leave.

This Starbucks is one of the busiest you'll see in Bucks County. It's THE hang-out spot in Newtown. Starbucks is Starbucks, but at this one, the staff couldn't be nicer and, more importantly, they get the drinks right. There's a good amount of seating for the space for individuals as well as groups who'd prefer to sit at larger tables. The only gripe is that it attracts the teens late at night, so around that time it can get a little loud/annoying. But as long as you hit it before 8, you're in the clear.
Are we not open for business anymore? Multiple times I've went there during normal business hours and they are either completely closed or the lobby is closed.
Always fast service and this is one of the only starbucks I've been to that hasn't messed up my order (yet) and I've been to this location about 100 times since it has opened

A Starbucks is a Starbucks, right? Not really I have visited the good and the bad. What I appreciate the most about this Starbucks is the exceptionally friendly service. I have only had fantastic interactions at this location and the staff goes above & beyond to ensure you have a great visit. Plus it has a drive thru, what can be better than not having o get out of your car for your daily dose of caffeine.  My only complaint, they don't carry cinnamon chip scones. :(
The lady who doesn't brush her hair was being so rude and did not smile , was not polite. Made a nasty face because of my ethnic name. Rude. Definitely will not return.
Worst location for a Starbucks! Drive thru line always poorly managed and cars always hang out into street which precwnta those who wish to park and go inside from being able to do so!!!

They are wonderful! They know all the regulars by name and they remember your order every-time. There is sometimes a long line but they are so quick you very have to wait too long. Coffee is great for the bucks

The store feels ill to me. I waited at the entrance for the customer in front of me to finish. Manager said to close the door because of bugs. Ate outside across the way and no bug activity at all (too cool out). Last time here never saw them wipe down tables.  They should get an ionizer air purifier.

Okay first of all this place is good but one of the workers flipped me off I couldn't be more offended and I didn't even do anything I was trying to get my vente iced water my bad maybe she was having a bad day

This starbucks is always packed and working at its highest potential. Never had an order missed or wrong. Friendly staff that work hard to get things right. For the amount of pain that regular pain in the butt customers give, ya'll always shine through and make it happen.   Know that I hate to see what pain regular customers give to ya'll, but damn if ya'll just put people in their place by ensuring the product you serve is on point! Keep up the good work!

Can't recommend this location. Six employees and only two were working. Line of several people moved so slow cause the cashier wasn't getting help. The drinks were made right and the place was clean, but the struggle was real for the poor cashier who was doing it all.   There were about 5 people in line and 3 more waiting for their drinks. Most of the employees were chatting among themselves and ignoring the line / customers, while 2 were pulling all the weight. The cashier and barista making the drinks were hauling, but it still took time to get it all with the rush of people.  Overall: C- Props to the two hard working employees, I felt for them!!

This location can be a little hard to find (2nd floor of the Hyatt Regency -- and it's a little bit of a maze up there) but they have fast service, good wifi, and lots of seating.  It gets busy when there are conventions at the hotel, but they keep up the pace and the line moves quickly.   Tables/chairs can occasionally be a little messy (a few crumbs) but not bad at all.  One of the best Starbucks I've been to!

Ordered from the app, for pick-up. Waited a long time but no order showed up...the clerk admitted they could not find the order. Meanwhile, during my long wait, it was hectic, chaotic. The pick up orders were laid out with little notice, people had to constantly look for their order, the cups were poorly labeled, each person had to pick up each cup and pull down the cardboard sleeves that covered the drink name. Really!!!! You can't put the label in a more obvious place so 10 people don't handle my drink before I do??? But you want us to wear masks?!? The restaurant was sloppy, loud, tables had debris or liquid on them. The floors were cluttered with debris. Maybe they were understaffed?  It was just a bad experience, no employee/customer interaction. It seemed that no one cared and they were all rushing around. Overall, it was a bad experience but I hope the employees can find a reason to feel joy in their crazy, chaotic work pace.

Two days ago I was came in for an iced coffee, and while nothing was wrong with my drink, there was something terribly wrong with my service. The girl taking my order was not over friendly, and not outright rude, but less enthusiastic than I'd expect. After almost sliding my card before knowing it had a chip reader, she snapped "Hold On." at me, which kind of threw me off because as someone who works in retail, I would never treat a customer with that tone. But this is the real kicker- while my receipt is printing, I notice she begins looking down and what do you know, she pulls her phone out of her apron behind the cash register and STARTS TEXTING WHILE MY RECEIPT HAS ALREADY PRINTED AND I AM STANDING THERE WAITING FOR HER TO HAND IT TO ME. I will also mention that it was around 5pm and there were about 4 people in line behind me, so she stood there texting while we all waited for her service. I was appalled and actually couldn't believe how dumb someone could be to do that, right then, on the job.
Ordered grande cafe americano and server poured me regular coffee from pre made bin.   Guess I just should have ordered coffee and saved some $$.

Always a pleasant experience at this location. Baristas are friendly and helpful. Since I'm not a coffee drinker, I enjoy the hot chocolate, especially on a chilly December day. Customer service is alive and well here.   Just the right temperature and chocolate enough with whip on top. Nice to relax with after a busy day.   Plenty of parking at this location too, but no drive up as it's located in a corner of a shopping center. Five stars today.

I love rating Starbucks against smaller, more local, more interesting coffee shops.  Once again, this particular Starbucks isn't as good.  Stopped in at 11am, ordered medium roast.  It tasted not so fresh.  Service was reasonable but not very friendly.  Some Starbucks employees are cheerful, not here.    This particular Starbucks is long and narrow with nice decor and a decent selection of baked goods.  It is fairy new and looks that way.

If you like to wait for coffee with an attitude, this might be the Starbucks for you. Too bad the usual happy SB attitude is not present here as the location is good. I'll definitely stick to the local establishments in the neighborhood from now on.

This is our second time here in two days. To best describe it layout wise, it's long and narrow. The whole layout of the prep and kitchen area is a lot different from any Starbucks I've been too.   Yesterday, when we came at 10am, they were struggling to stay afloat with the massive drive thru lane and the line inside. We had ordered a bacon Gouda sandwich and were told after ordering that they didn't have any left. We had also ordered a warm plain croissant and when my niece bit into it, it was still frozen in the middle. So she was gifted a chocolate croissant to make up for it.   Today, we ordered 2 bacon Gouda sandwiches, mocha, java chip Frappuccino, strawberry green tea & a passion fruit lemonade. They were quick and efficient and got everything right. They didn't have any passion fruit for the passion fruit lemonade so we opted for strawberry instead.

They have absolutely no clue how to make a decent Vanilla Chai Latte. I went here once last week with a craving for my chai latte. I went to the drive-through because I was in a rush, pick up my iced latte and they forgot to put the vanilla in it I had to drive back around wait for the guy to come to the window. I let him know that I asked for a vanilla chai latte and he told me that chai lattes don't come with a vanilla and I said I understand that but I asked for vanilla. He takes it back pumps probably one pump of vanilla and gives it back it was still shitty but I had no time to go back around for the second time so I left. I'm driving by there today. Ask the guy for the same exact thing, get my iced vanilla chai latte and it still tastes like shit. Case in point, don't ask for a iced vanilla chai latte at this location.

Ahhh Starbucks, my guilty pleasure. It never disappoints when I need the pick me up only a skinny Caramel vanilla can give me. There is usually alone at this one because it's so close to the hospital and other off its but service is usually quick and friendly. The everything bagel with cream cheese, the savory squares, or the veggie breakfast sandwich are all great additions to any drink (yes that bill will end up being $9/$10)  If you haven't already- get the app and if you're not a fanatic, it will certainly make you into one.
Some of the best baristas I know. Friendly, fast, and drinks are made perfectly. Excellent atmosphere in which to study too!
EVEN TIME I come to this Starbucks there's a 30 minute wait. How and why does it take 30 minutes to make a cup of coffee?

The service at this location is bad. Waiting time for cup of coffee was about 15 minutes! The staff seemed to prioritize drive thru over walk ins. They have to work on the service improvement. Not a good location to get a "quick" coffee cup.

The employees here are so nice and go above and beyond the norm of customer service! Kudos to Jacob he definitely deserves a day off with double pay; never fails always greets us at the drive thru with kindness and energy; I wish I lived closer to this location for he is by far the nicest barista  (I visit Starbucks quite a few times per week) He should be considered a trainer. Tysm

We stopped by this service area. Decide to take a bathroom break. Then realize it's another 2+ hours drive so needed a coffee for sure. It's around 5:30pm as I remembered. There was like 4 to 5 customers in front of me. Only one serving the customer behind the counter. And 3 other staff behind doing the drinks, filling stocks and etc. First of all, total 3 counters, only one open. It was only like 6 to 8 customer beginning to form the lines. It takes this cashier lady 2 to 3 minutes taking each order. She was very nice, take time to listen to your order or making suggestions to the customer if they were confused by what the menu offers, and she would note down on the size of the cup that your ordering with detail words stating what they want. So I have no complaints on her performance. It's a great customer service for sure. But the problem I see was that if she is taking 2 to 3 min to place an order for one customer, that is a huge problem for this franchisee location. If I was in line behind 4 to 5 other customers, then that means I have to wait on the line for least 15+ min for sure. I know in fact it was slow moving line because 2 of the customer in front of me literally left the line and walk back to other shops to buy I assume its coffee. I counted the time I was on the line which is exactly around 15 min. So here is what gets me upset the most, the lady in front of me was ordering some kind of custom drink that had this new employee went nuts and kept showing her frustration. I kind of overheard she ordered something with 2 extra espresso shots and need some kind of cinnamon to be place on bottom of the cup beofr pouring the coffee as well as sprinkle more cinnamon on top of coffee when finish. The women who was responsible to make the order I overheard she said to the cashier lady that she doesn't sure how to make it because she didn't learn how to make this type of drink. She was also showing frustration towards the cashier lady as well as her facial expression to the customer who ordered the drinks. She kept saying that she never made this type of drink request before and she warned to the customer that she may not make the exact flavor she wants it. She kept showing frustration while making the drink. Then I heard that she said to the cashier lady that she should be making this not her because she was only here 2 days..and got trained for 2 days... I was kind of shocked when I heard that. First of all, how a big franchisee company like Starbucks can prepare  a well organized training to all its new employees? If this is a new employee that have  expressed her concerns that she cannot make this custom drink order, shouldn't someone more senior level employee immediately take over or at least show her how to do it? She was on her own and trying to figure out how to make that drink. Anyway, by the time she finished making the drink, she said to the lady who ordered the drink said that she is sorry if the drink is not made to taste exactly what she expect it. Then she turned around and make the next drink order. At this time I was like.. oh nooo. Because I also had at least one custom drink. So I ordered a grande ice caramel macchiato which is a normal drink on the menu, plus a custom drink which is 2 espresso shots on a big cup with ice. So the new employee lady happen to took that custom drink order and another guy who works in the back mostly doing ice smoothie type of drink took the ice caramel macchiato drink... I look at her and literally in my mind.. oh boy... she looked at the cup that the cashier wrote down and her face was like... wt.... what is this and how to make it? The cashier said just put ice in the cup and pour 2 cups of espresso shots over the ice.. she look at it and shakes her head.. I really want to laugh, but meanwhile upset because it took me 15+ min waiting in line and another 10+ min ordering and waiting for my drink to be made while watching this whole drama going on. Once she finished the drink she looks at me and said, I dont know if this is correct. I understand her frustration but gave her a warm welcome look and said yes you did great and  that is exactly what I need. I feel bad for this new employee women because she didn't get proper training before being place on the field to serve customer without additional support or supervision. I also felt upset because if you have total 3 cashier counter available, why dont they notice this and open at least 1 or 2 more cashier counter. They seems to have at least 4 employees in the back. One at counter, one in the front making drink, another 2 seem to running around trying to find something or filling stocks. Meanwhile they have a long lines all waiting to order their beloved Starbucks coffees. At the end, this location needs improvement in terms of organizing their staffs. I probably wont be back on this location even if I stop by this service station.

IF I COULD LEAVE A ZERO I WOULD. NOT ONLY CANT YOU BE A BLACK MALE AND WAIT ON A FRIEND B4 U ORDER YOUR COFFEE U HAVE TO BE IN embarrass and be walked out in handcuffs while your friend meet you I've never in my life had to witness something so awful to the barista that throught her life was in danger I hope you never truly are in danger. As a franchise Starbucks should have procedures in order what to do when a customer didn't purchase anything and it shouldn't be calling the cops. I'm sad the situation is sad and you guys as a company old those men an apology and the Barista should be fired.
After paying nearly $6 each for two Pumpkin Spice Lattes at the drive thru, I opened on to stir it to find that they underserved both by an inch!  Won't be going to this Starbucks again.

Nice & friendly staff but dangerously small and unorthodox parking lot. (Totally not their fault!)  Great location 1.5 miles away from my daily Rte 95 commute to and from Philadelphia. They uphold the Starbucks standards and I recommend this location if you're in the area.

Today is my birthday...I have the Starbucks app and have entered my bday in before so I know they have it....I went in and asked how I get my free birthday drink and they said I have to get an email...i checked and I was like ok but I didn't get an email can I just show you my license I promise today is my birthday... no... no free drink for me. Even though many ppl have told me they have showed Starbucks their license and gotten a free drink on their bday....Also worse than that I ordered a drink I order all the time it's the dragon fruit refresher... paid $6 on my birthday and today it tasted like pink sink water.. had zero flavor rad... thanks guys
Starbucks by E29.... customer service is terrible!! Rude and slow. If you don't want to work...quit!

Beautiful remodel! Nice new selection of sandwiches too!!! One of our fab spots.  Why don't they have someone to clean the tables at Starbucks all locations? It's not my job to clean a table before I sit down to enjoy my purchase!!!
This is a great Starbucks location just for the loft upstairs. Great people watching opportunities, and in a charming older building.  Minus 1 star because my latte tasted a little burnt.
Fast friendly service. Clean interior and exterior. Highly recommended. This store is easier to get to and more convenient than I4 store

The workers where very rude. I bit my tongue and still said thank you after being ignored for 5 minutes so the employees could talk about there personal lifes. The coffee is pretty much the same at all the Starbucks so the worker's make the experience and the suck at this one.
2nd time in a month they've gotten my food order wrong. I ordered eggs bites both times and got a sandwich instead with the egg bites sticker on it... not that hard to read

I've been to this Starbucks a bunch of times. There is no drive thru. It's in the nippers corner area of Nashville by Kroger, right in the corner. Outside seating and inside cafe seating. A little slower than most Starbucks but everything is made correct.

I don't often drink Starbucks, but sometimes I am in a bind and I need caffeine quick.  This Starbucks happens to be convenient to my work, but that is where the convenience ends.  Much like everyone else, I am not a fan of this Starbucks for the wait.  There could be only 4 people in front of you, but you will still wait about 20 minutes.  It isn't just a one time occurence either.  It happens every time.
jessica has been long gone and like many businesses, this starbucks is under staffed or regularly has longer wait times and just inefficient service.

Starbucks are usually Starbucks, except for the staff. They usually make the difference ANY place you might go.   Well, the staff here was VERY nice. They watched my laptop while I left for a moment. They made excellent suggestions while ordering.   The only complaint this day was that someone left something in or on something for too long. There was a burning smell for a while.  It was nice and cold inside while it was hot outside, and this makes ALL the difference when drinking coffee in the summer.

This is the third time I've been to this Starbucks in the last two weeks and this is by far the worst Starbucks I've been too. The customer service here is horrible and I will never come to this one again!

Went here last weekend and worth crossing the river. Order was correct, staff was polite, ample places to sit both inside and out and it was clean. Best of all the baristas don't argue with the customer over an order they make incorrectly. Unlike the manhattan blvd location. Which USED to be our go to spot for coffee on Saturday mornings. At least they got the hiring right at this location.

Wow............how is this place still in business. I came in at 6:15 am to get a coffee and get some work done on my laptop. I order a coffee and ask for honey. The girl immediately says they're out of honey. OK. So I take some agave. I couldn't help but notice the tall guy with the beard wrestling with a coffee machine appearing quite frustrated saying F this and F that and "this job sucks I have to get up at 4:30 in the morning" etc. after sitting down for a few minutes it becomes clear that all of the four employees are disenchanted with their jobs and do nothing but complain and talk about working elsewhere. Of course I go to sit down and the wireless internet doesn't work. I almost think they might do this on purpose so they don't have to serve anyone. There wasn't on other person in the place.  In my opinion, these are young immature kids of the entitlement age and someone needs to call corporate to come and clean this place up.  Worst Starbucks experience ever.
Still a VERY slow Starbucks.  I have found that this location takes forever whether you go inside or through the drive through.

SLOWEST. DRIVE- THRU. EVER!! They have definitely lowered their standards with this business. And yes, as mentioned by other customers, after school hours are terrible! But hey....at least there is DD across the street!

Ordered one of the new drinks and the worker said that it "irks her life" when people order it. That it takes too long too make, and they need too take it off the menu. She continued too complain while preparing my drink, and honestly made me regret approaching and ordering and that's a feeling I rarely get as I used too work In food and know how exhausting days can be, and dealing with customers can be but straight up rude employees.
Great Starbucks! The baristas are very nice and the place is very modern and clean. Mickayla was so sweet and even brought us our drinks to where we were sitting.

While I like the many independent coffee shops in Reno better than the chain ones, this Starbucks is always consistent and it's close to my home so very convenient. Customer service is always outstanding here and they are very COVID compliant. I love the seasonal offerings-peppermint mocha is my fav drink here.

I'm a traveling Starbucks Supervisor whose based in Salt Lake City, Utah but visiting for Thanksgiving and I was impressed with this location. They were quick, clean, and prompt on adding more sweet cream to my iced coffee. Kay was the one who welcomed us in, took our order, and wished us a good day as we left. Thank you for embodying what a Starbucks should be!
Bad service. 15 min wait mid day for a Mocha. A hassled barrister. Sweating. With attidude. Maybe just a bad day.

The WORST starbucks i have ever gone to. We ordered 3 espresso drinks and they all tasted like water. We went back in to ask for some more sweetener and all but one person was friendly. The coffee was lukewarm and none of the employees were wearing masks. Starbucks usually has great customer service but this place was terrible. Whoever the manager is needs to do better.

First let me say this is nothing against the store and Caughlin Ranch. The barista's here are courteous, helpful, and always merry in the spirit of the season.   My one star goes to calling customer service and ending up in a loop that offers no live person to resolve my problem. I just spent $83.74 on a wonderful hot chocolate and gift card but my apps locked up and would not allow me to use my Starbucks app to pay for it. Therefore,  I miss out on all my reward stars. When I called the misnomer "customer service" all I get is recorded announcements for their credit card or referrals to the website. Nary a live person to be had.  Customer service at Starbucks has continued to decline and it's no wonder their stock prices are declining as well. When you put shareholders ahead of your hard-working, paying customers it's always a recipe for disaster.  One star is more than deserved corporate Starbucks.

Customer service sucks ! Let me begin to say that if I want just regular coffee I would have gone to wawa. Nothing irritates me than someone who is so rude when people like me gives you business! I'm not the one to blame people and put them out of work. But damn if you're life depends on what you do maybe  it's a wake up call and this establishment better make it clear to people they hire what customer service is all about! I bought a latte macchiato from this woman who while she was asking me what I want has her back turned against me! You call that customer service? Order # 87682 inside target Springfield mall doesn't even deserve a star!

BEST Starbucks experience ever!!! A girl named Katie was working the drive thru. The entire time we waited for our coffee she hung out at the window and made awesome conversation. Usually people just shut the window or don't talk to you. She was the kind of employee that brings people back to a location. Starbucks is winning with service like hers! (She was from Roseville, in case there's more than one katie!) :)

I was very disappointed with my two experiences at this location. I stopped at this location this morning on my way to work. It was not crowded or busy, which is what was the most frustrating part of my experience. I waited over 15 minutes to receive my order of an iced coffee and a breakfast sandwich. The iced coffee alone took almost 10 minutes. I was not apologized to for my wait. On a weekday morning, its unacceptable to have that slow of service. I will not be visiting this location again. I love Starbucks, but I will take my business to Dunkin' Donuts for my morning coffee.

Went to this Starbucks on Friday afternoon. Only a handful of customers there. No one in line. I stood at the counter for two full minutes waiting to be served. Made eye contact with at least three of the employees none of which acknowledged me nor said they would be with me in a moment. Nothing.  Finally just sat down with my friend who apparently had fabulous service.   I went back to the counter five minutes later and was finally served.  Starbucks, you need to teach basic manners to your employees.

Clean and very friendly service.  Plus, they have a cute room with numerous tables, chairs, and sofas.  A great place to grab a cup of coffee (or the like) and catch up.  Parking can be a hassle but there is the metered lot in Union Square.
The nicest workers of any Starbucks I've ever been to! Always ask how you are at the drive thru and chat with you at the window if there's time. They always make my day!
Today I ordered lemonade. There were strange stains on the lid. And On my last two visits the staff was very rude
I've never seen such fast working Starbucks employees! They get the job done and move on to the next in lightening speed. Wish they were this fast at my local Starbucks.

3.5 stars.  It's a no frills Starbucks.  Nothing great, nothing bad.  Busy little location, 2 registers chugalugging, long line, and the highlight, a loft with seating.  When in the area, and you must get a fix, this works.

I was shocked that outdoor tables were so dirty. First of all this is Starbucks and I thought they had a better reputation and secondly this is Covid season and these tables should be cleaned for health reasons especially! The Staff seemed busy and competent, but what is the point if I catch a disease. So disappointed!

First time at this Starbucks in North Valleys.  The staff was very nice and service was prompt.  Their drive thru seems to be very busy.  I was there mid morning.  The dining area is quite small compared other Starbucks I have been too.  Chairs were comfortable, but tables were pretty small.  There are two larger tables for those with laptops.  You can function on the smaller tables, but are confined a bit against the wall.    The dining area was well kept and there are outside seating options as well.  The music was a little loud for my liking.  Any conversation you would have would have to carry over the music, thus increasing the overall chatter level in the dining area a little louder then normal. The men's bathroom was well kept and clean and all personal hygiene supplies were stocked for use.    Overall, I enjoyed my experience at this location.  If they toned down the music a little bit, then it would definitely be a 4.5 to 5 star rating.  Cheers!

Ordered a venti iced caramel cloud macchiato and this is how empty my cup was when they gave it to me. I asked the girl why it was like that and she said it was normal but I've ordered this many times at other locations and haven't had any issues.

This Starbucks was a quick pit-stop on the way out of Philadelphia to head back to DC. Not only is this a gorgeous location if you're considering going for the wifi and to do work, but the employees here are quick and friendly. We were very happy with our experience here, and I'd absolutely go back the next time I'm in Philadelphia.   Also, parking was easy. Let's be honest, in any major city parking has to factor into the rating of an establishment. Well, way to go this Starbucks! You're great with that. Street parking nearby, and we were leaving on a Monday morning at like 10. So there should have been lots of people trying to park, or so I'd expected. BUT the parking gods were on our side!  All in all, a beautiful and accessible Starbucks location in a fun, vibrant part of the city.
Shout out to the team this morning...one machine down, 25 people waiting for coffee and they kept cranking the drinks out.  Great group of people at this location.

It was 9pm. My 6 yrs old wants cookies. The told us we are close and not taking any costumer. We where the last costumer and I explained  we onky want cookie for my daughter  They still refuse to help.  Bad costumer service

Ok, as far as Starbucks go. Would love a little more friendly vibe but haven't seen that from here.....ever. Not saying it's unfriendly...just blah. Varied crowd if you want to people watch... I mean it is downtown Clearwater after all.
For how busy they were they got our drinks out quickly! Very friendly! There's so many Starbucks in the area but this is definitely not a bad location!
Great location and friendly staff but are usually out of the assorted sandwiches that every Starbucks should carry.

Came here for drinks today (2/23) and it was probably the worst Starbucks I've ever been to. The guy working the drive thru with the tattoos of naked body parts was so blah, rude and like he didn't want to be there.  We ordered an iced passion tango tea lemonade and the order came out wrong so upon telling him it wasn't what we ordered he genuinely seemed annoyed at us for his own mistake, no apologies and huffed and puffed away for the correct drink. After the correct drink comes it is literally watered down so much it tastes like water. Our other order was blonde espresso iced with sweet foam and no exaggeration the sweet foam took up 3/4 of the cup with only 1/4 of coffee. It was all gone within five sips.  Super disappointing experience from the service to the drinks. Would never return to this location and would definitely not recommend.

Though the line does go for days it seems before that first cup of coffee, the staff totally makes up for it!  They are super friendly and a great way to start the day - thanks for being amazing this morning!!

The guy working in the drive through put our change in the tip jar without asking. I wouldn't have minded giving him a tip if he asked, but the fact that he did it without asking is rude as fuck. We're all struggling dude. Ask before taking. I don't recommend at all

I'm not sure anymore about this Starbucks....  I stop by almost every morning before work, but here lately I've waited forever in the drive thru just to be told they just opened and two of their employees called in.  Then there have been times when the drive thru and front door is blocked with chairs on the outside...  I'm ‍ confused, what is going on at this location?  Ending on a good note, they always make my drink awesome!
Great, friendly staff. Helped me with a special drink request without a second thought!

Reason for one star slow service and I mean really slow. Took them 10 minutes to take my order plus another 15 to give my coffee! Waited way too long for a simple coffee! It happen to me before but I decided to give them another chance. Never coming back again!

The service was really nice. Very fast no wait at all hardly. I also had to purchase 13 5$ gift cards for a business event and the cashier made my experience so pleasant she was so kind! I appreciate this level of service.
HORRIBLE SERVICE. THATS ALL I HAVE TO SAY. But anyways the way they take order and do those orders before your mobile order while they say you are next. Stupid.

This must be a very busy story as it's on the main strip and across from the mall but they have yet to allow indoor seating or open the restrooms to the public.. how are we supposed to wash our hands without the restrooms?  The staff inside was very friendly, there was no wait for my drinks.
The nicest Starbucks that I have seen. Great place to meet with your friends or sit in the comfortable chairs and use your computer. Much nicer than many of the other Starbucks that I have seen

Well, I sure hope I'm not letting the secret out, but this Starbuck's is a hidden gem. The looks of it are deceiving as it appears to be a run-of-the-mill strip mall location without a drive-thru, but it does indeed have one--you just have to drive around the side of the building. Because it hides out, the crowds are virtually non-existent. Also, they get the orders right every single time.

I truly love this location! Everyone is happy to help and just an overall positive atmosphere amongst the employees! They're all very sweet and I'm glad they helped me fix my drink without any hassle. I always leave with a smile and the energy to power through my mornings! Thank you guys!!! Such an awesome team!!

This new location is very nice and the staff is very friendly. But this one is special...It has a drive thru! The first Starbucks location in this city with one. Maybe even first in the country, I'm not sure.  Right across from the Magnolia Marketplace and just walking distance from where I reside, I can come here and unwind with friends and have a good social time.

The usual Starbucks food and drink favorites located in a not easy to find strip mall just east of Street Road and Bustleton Pike. Recently remodeled to a newer, cozier, cheerier, atmosphere with a little more room to move around. Went from small to average size. The Baristas in here have always been very nice, efficient, and willing to accommodate. Service is never an issue. Lines can get long, but never long waits. Could use a bigger food display case, and like most other Starbucks they need more cushioned lounging seats, but space is always an issue. 2 single bathrooms that are coed and are clean  Plenty of off street parking and a small area to sit outside if you like.  All in all, a slightly above average Starbucks, but still missing the ambiance of the stand alone building Starbucks down the road west, on 2nd Street Pike and Street Road.

Best Starbucks. Worst parking lot. There's nothing they can do about it, I know, but still. We literally come here every single weekday morning. The baristas are always smiling, knowledgeable and friendly. Oh and fast. They give my daughter stickers sometimes. The have big, comfy chairs we chill in and we love. The walls used to be covered in local children's artwork. I miss that but it does have more of a sleek vibe now. And a farmhouse table in the middle and a bar by the window. They always have plenty of everything and it's fresh. I will go out of my way to come to this Starbucks. They are a breath of fresh air in the mornings. Franchetti and Erica are so incredibly sweet and I think his name is Gannon or something with a G (I'm probably blushing too much to read) is smiling and happy and super cute. Definitely doesn't hurt to look at him first think in the morning. But really he does make the best iced green tea lattes. When I go other places the matcha powder doesn't dissolve. He makes that happen. I've left my debit card here and they kept it for me. I went on a mad search for an specialty mug and called and they held it for me. It's home in the mornings. We love this Starbucks (especially compared to others).

Terrible parking and terrible customer service from one of the workers here. She's super rude and didn't even greet anyone coming up to her. Just assumed what size I wanted my drink (which was wrong).

This is one of my favorite Starbucks locations. It's always clean and the service is always great. I enjoy the seating both indoors and out. I especially love the outdoor seating, even during winter when the heaters make it enjoyable still. I haven't had a bad experience at this location yet and always enjoy spending time there.

This is the most convenient Starbucks for me on my commute.   My biggest complaint - and this is specific to the brand not this location - is that the cups leak from where the lid and the lip of the cup meet. My $2 coffee should not leak. I never have this issue with Wawa coffee, but ever other cup of Starbucks I get drips everywhere. Why?  As for this location - work on your employee morale. Everyone there seems miserable. Staff rarely smiles and service is not prompt. If there is a line, no matter how Many people are working, you're going to wait.
New Hope, Pa. Friendly and accommodating staff. Outside tables right on Bridge Street. The perfect place to relax, enjoy the great coffee and people and car watch.

While I do try to buy local as much as possible, there's a lot to be said for Starbucks' consistency. CC's has better drip coffee that I buy in bulk, but the espresso drinks at Starbucks never disappoint.   I love the staff at the Maple location! They're great about remembering regulars. My only issue with this location is all the undergrads here during the school year. But if you do want to be surrounded by stupid hats and girls in belly shirts having "like, totez deep convos," Maple is the place to go.

The baristas have no sense of urgency. I need my coffee. This is very urgent. It does get busy from time to time, but even when there's nobody in line, it still takes a good ten minutes to get my drink...  but hey, starbucks is starbucks and green tea frappuccinos call  my name~

Thank you, Starbucks employees, for continuing to let us get our Starbucks fix during this hard time! This location is now drive-thru only, allowing us all to stay safe and have minimal contact. The people working are friendly and driving here is my big event for the day during this quarantine! I have been coming here for a long time and they are always quick and efficient, even when the drive-thru line looks super long. I hope they can remain open as long as it is safe for them to do so! I love my Starbucks and I love this location!
Poorly run. Always a long than normal wait. Subpar drinks. Andrea is terrible. The workers seem overworked and are not friendly
My favorite Starbucks location :-) baristas are the best!!!! Service is awesome :-) This is my first time checking in (on Yelp) but my colleagues and I are here at least 2-3 times a week.

This place is just okay with me.  I live three blocks away so I go here out of a matter of convenience when I'm in the mood for something that I can't make at home.  But the service here really does just suck.  The baristas always seem way more preoccupied with talking to each other rather than acknowledging the people waiting at the counter.  And I understand that everyone has a job to do (drive-thru, hot bar, cold bar, registers, etc) but when the person at the hot bar is getting smoked and there are four of you standing around watching them struggle to keep up with tickets, jump in there and help a brother (or sister) out!  I work near the Starbucks on the circle so the vast majority of the time I'll just stave off my coffee withdrawals until I can get downtown where I know they'll make my order correctly and in a timely fashion.

This is one of the few Starbucks locations in the Tampa area with a Starbucks Reserve coffee selection and a Clover brewing machine.  What does this mean for you?  It means that you have a wider variety of rarer coffee blends brewed fresh individually by hand at the time you order and not a second sooner.

Although they've moved (they used to be next to the Apple Store), they've still kept their quality service intact. Pretty much every time I go here, I get fast and friendly service. I did have an issue once with an unfriendly worker, but I'm going to consider that a one-off.  Definitely gets busy quick, but they do their best to get you in and out with the right drink and a smile!

We went here on a Wednesday afternoon to get some work done.  The wifi was about the best I've found anywhere, and I spend a lot of time working from coffee shops.  The music was great (the smiths, talking heads, lykke li, nina simone, ...), the building is unique and interesting, and power sockets were easy to locate.  It is a Starbucks, so you know what to expect with the coffee and food selection.  It is the same as other Starbucks.

Love this Starbucks. I go thru the drive thru. Most of the time there is a long car line. The line does move quickly. I always order the Caramel Frappuccino. I am always happy with my order and with the service provided.  I will continue  to go to this Starbucks location.

So I am currently here at Starbucks with my daughter. We ordered 2 tall fraps. The people in front of us got their drinks and food and almost finished their sandwhich before we got our drinks. I had to tell Bridgette, the employee there, that we've been waiting 18 minutes for our drinks and still haven't gotten them yet. She looked for our cups with our names and they were nowhere to be found. That's odd!!! I physically saw the guy write our names down on the cups. Did they disappear? Did they throw them away? Or did they intentionally not make my drinks! After I had explained my situation to Bridgette which I hope shes not he manager, she simply said ok I will make them for you! With an attitude! No apology or an attempt to figure out what happened with our drinks. Poor customer service! If your going to work for the brand you have to be fast paced and still be friendly at the same time! Isn't that part of working at Starbucks????multitask!!!!!!

This Starbucks location is the one I probably frequent the most.  I am here almost every Sunday morning in the drive thru.  The employees are friendly and my drinks are always delicious.   Even with my picky order that seems to always change they are patient.   I also keep the corner of the bar company several times a month while I read or crochet.  It is nice to see the frequent visitors and hear there order stated before the customer even says it.  I have seen drinks remade without complaint when it is not quite what the customer wants.  The employees are constantly cleaning during any down time and seem to really care about the store.  I will continue to be here often.
The slowest drive through ever. Everytime I come here. Coffee is good staff is friendly, but for some reason with only a few cars it always takes longer than 10 minutes. So GO INSIDE is my advice

The rebirth of Starbucks at Tampa's International Airport! Out in the open and brewing up a storm, the new location is hustling and bustling as per usual. Stocked with caffeine-addict approved beverages and fat kid approved bakery items. The line during a busy Saturday afternoon moved relatively quickly for a Starbucks. The seating was all filled up but there's plenty of room to spread out inside the airport. The up charge was killer but that is to be expected at TPA. I like this new location better because it's contemporary, easier to find, and much more spacious. The line can't be out the door if there isn't one. Welcome back Starbucks! I will speak for everyone when I say you were missed!

One of the best locations in Indy! The staff is very friendly. I stop in almost every morning and I have a complicated order-- but it's made very consistently from day to day.   The one drawback to this location is there is a large homeless problem in this area of downtown and so almost always there are people asking you for money as you walk in or out. But that's completely out of Starbucks' control.   I pass 3-4 Starbucks on my morning commute but I choose this one because of the friendly staff.

Poor management! I waited 20 minutes for my order placed at 8:30 on a Saturday morning. To be fair, they were slammed with orders, so that wait was not terrible. What moved me to give one star is that apparently they were "all out of " a number of things posted on their menu on the wall. I ordered an Impossible breakfast sandwich, which they were all out of. I asked if I could have just egg and cheese and they said they would have to take the meat off a meat breakfast sandwich. I'm not an uptight vegetarian, so I said OK. They charged me the same and did not offer extra cheese or anything. During the 20 minutes I was waiting for my order, I overheard three people being told they were "all out of" what they wanted. When people ordered food, they were told they would have a long wait, and did they still want it. Most said "no" or ordered something else. I would say they should hire enough workers and have enough product so that customers are not discouraged from getting what they really want.
Well worth a visit. You can sit out back by a stream and enjoy your coffee. Coffee was good and the clerk offered to warm up my cookie! Had never considered doing that. It was yummy.

This is my favorite Starbucks! Always fast service even if the line is long. The people working here are always nice to me, asking me how I'm doing, etc. A lot of students hang out here and do their work. When sitting outside no homeless people have ever approached me. Downtown location this ALWAYS happens when seated outside.

Nice staff, nice facilities, but sloooowwwweeesssttttt service I have ever received in a Starbucks! I ordered a grande iced coffee (read not at all complicated) with no one in front of me and just one guy behind me. There were 4 staff (yes four people matched to two customers) and somehow it took us both 6 minutes to get our drinks. Not the end of the world but not very considerate.

This Starbucks is SOOOOOO disorganized. The staff behind the counter talk to each other instead of making drinks and leave you waiting at the pick up counter with your foot tapping. I asked what teas I could choose from and the  girl just said we have green tea that's all I know. I asked for earl gray and I was the only one customer waiting for drink besides the drive thru line which had 2 cars.... needless to say, I waited for an uncomfortable amount of time to the point where other folks walked in, ordered and got their drink before me. I just asked for tea!! They definitely forgot my order. Not cool! I had to grab the girl's attention to ask where my tea was. I said, "do you know how much longer on the earl gray?" And she says, "no" and I wanted to say, "well then who should I ask to get my tea?" But I held my tongue. Then I see her drop a random tea bag into a cup and quickly fill with water and push towards me. I thought they typically sweeten with some kind of syrup but I guess not for me or not today. I seriously think they need to hire and train folks not to be so disorganized even when things are slow. Just tried the tea and wow it is bland and tastes all wrong. I looked at the tag and it says "English breakfast" so I'm definitely not happy with the service but I already drove away bc I was so ready to get out of there. Next time, get the order right and don't forget customers.
Great location, and my order was made perfectly and quickly. So glad to have another Starbucks location in the City!

I absolutely love this Starbucks!!! I love to see the way they greet all the customers, even the ones they don't know. They all go the extra mile and we'll do what it takes to make your order right. They work well together -and they are all so friendly.  My favorite for sure  Yeah they're pretty awesome

Friendly staff, they get my drinks correct every time, the location is clean and spacious. Good place to work/study. Music at normal level. I've come here 10 continuous days and have nothing but praise for this place and the people working here. Thanks for helping me work remote while visiting family during the holidays

I go to this Starbucks about 3 times a week. Drive through is always slow because there are a lot of big trucks in the area and getting to the order window is difficult. They usually get my order right, and taste is consistent. I've found quicker service ordering on the app and parking to get my order.

Hey guys! Thanks for wasting my time by not fulfilling my mobile order this morning. Waited 20 minutes before leaving empty handed and with $5+ less on my mobile account   Hope you do better next time

This location is my favorite in Sumner Co or anywhere north of Nashville. They are great about having my order ready quickly and correctly. The regular employees are good about remembering you after a few visits and are very friendly.   It tends to be a busy location and at peak times it can be difficult to get into the parking lot, so if I'm in a hurry I always order ahead on the mobile app and it's *usually* waiting for me when I walk in. Sometimes I'll have to wait a few minutes. But it allows me to enjoy the small talk with the employees rather than feel anxious about my time.   No business is perfect all the time but this one is pretty close.   PS...At the risk of sounding like an ad for Starbucks (I'm not), if you're a regular and not using the app, you should reconsider. Building your drinks tells you how much of what is in each drink, it allows you to customize everything and lets you see everything that's available to you that's not on the menu board. Including whether or not they're out of something before you get there. You'll see what the cost is before you order and you can earn free food or drinks that you've built up to $12 or whatever. Perks and rewards are nice when you go somewhere regularly.

I have to say that this Starbucks that was new in the Mount Juliet, was like the best than any other location of this area. The reason why I'm giving this a 5 star is because of how the staff and the employees have been doing a great job, how good their Frappe-chinos come out, and how clean this place is.   Since they opened in 2016, I decided to go try them out and see if they do any better job than the one next to Home Depot. It turns out to be 4 times better than expected to be. I go to Starbucks a lot these days, but the one thing I've got to say is that whenever I come in to Starbucks, one of the employees will know my order if you come here a lot. I was impressed about one of them knowing what I wanted to order.   This is like one of the best Starbucks's I've ever been to in a while!!
My absolute favorite starbucks. The baristas are always friendly and very personable. I love all of them and they always make the experience a great one.

Store is supposed to be open till 8 pm.  I came in for coffee at 7:25 pm only to find that they stop brewing coffee at 7.  I've never had a Starbucks tell me I couldn't get any coffee before.  They didn't even offer a pour over.  I've been to starbucks at 7:50 pm and was still able to get a coffee but not at this location.  I'm on my way to the one a couple of blocks away.

This starbucks is BY FAR the worst Starbucks. I always wait a while and come back to give them another try and it never goes well. 9 out of 10 drinks I've ordered have been wrong. Very rarely do I have an interaction with a friendly employee there. Most of them have attitudes. Every time I leave this location, it's never been a good experience.
Speedy service through the drive thru.  Courteous people on the intercom as well as the window.  Excellent location on the way to the freeway on-ramp.
Not a fan of this starbucks.  It was just "ok" LONG wait for a iced mocha....   and no drive thru  But it is close to UTLA
Good place to hang out and get a quick cup of joe. According to my husband but I like the frapacinos

Love this Starbucks location. The staff are fantastic, and even though I'm not here as often as I have been in the past, they still remember me and chat as though I'm there everyday. Love that they treat their patrons as though they are over for coffee at home. Service is great and the cafe is always clean and well organized.
Under staffed, stopped for a quick cup of coffee 6:45am, lengthily Line, 2 people serving 15!

Essential worker in a van that wouldn't fit throuGh the drive thru. I walked up to the mobile app order window on suggestion of an employee who had left the lobby and got into his vehicle. I explaIned that I was in a vehicle that would not fit through the drive-through and asked if I could quickly get a large coffee through the mobile app window. The lady said I needed to download the mobile app and order from there on the phone. I explain to her that I had no room left on my phone to download any apps and asked if I could just pay very quickly. She asked the manager who tried to say that they are trying to illuminate cross-contamination and that's why they couldn't take my order through the mobile app window without me using the mobile app. Meanwhile everyone in the drive-through is handing over debit and credit cards and cash cross-contamination! Your logic is stupid and you just denied service to an essential worker that is driving for seven hours during this COVID-19 time. Congratulations you are assholes!!! During my travels as a driver I have been accommodated by everyPlace I have been to. Screw the snobby ass Starbucks and they're retarded employees. So I will use the Yelp app to write a review seeing as how I don't have room for any more apps on my phone and I'm sure as hell not going to stand around for a half hour deleting apps and adding the Starbucks app.

Update your hours!!!!!  Made an intentional trip here and you're not open.  Also, Kroger employee has no clue the hour of opening. We count on you guys to give accurate info and know what's going on!  Geez.

Here all this time I thought I was cool . nudge,wink,...... My son told me to order the grande,carmel frappuccino one day, I was hooked. So, on the occasion I get there at 5:25 in the morning, and they are taking orders, I will order my manly drink, and sip with delight and slurp the whip cream with my straw, when I get to the bottom. mmmmm, :)
The staff is always friendly and helpful.  Even though my order is pretty simple people don't always get it how I like it all the time ...today it was perfect which so made my day!

This is a busy fast moving Starbucks. There will be a line, it will move fast. The servers are adequately knowledgeable. Not the most, not the least. The food is fresh, sometimes they run out.  There is parking, yet it is midtown, it is crowded. However in the am, you may consider mobile ordering or go thru drive thru. There are people asking for donations at the front and the back.  Ironically on the weekend nights it is not to hard to find a place to sit or park.  There is a fire pit in front when it gets chilly.  Have ONLY had wonderful coffees and teas here.  Lots of good seating and wifi.

This Starbucks, in the Camino Real Marketplace in Goleta, actually deserves 0 stars. All of the bad reviews this place gets but nothing changes. The place is dirty, overcrowded, and the little shits that work there just don't care. The half & half containers are always empty and the last time I was there my "hot" sandwich was still frozen in the middle. This place blows big time. I'm going somewhere else next time I'm in the area.

This Starbucks has very nice customer service.  The Manager, Miguel, is a 5-Star manager. He's always in a great, mellow mood. They are very busy place. However, that tells me it's because their Good! They have plenty of tables inside and out. My husband and I go there almost everyday so I can start my day off right, with a smile!

The baristas are friendly here but they get my order wrong every other time. They always seem to go up a size and when I asked about it the manager said its only a small price difference. Nice scheme they have going on. Make sure you ask for a receipt. That's the only time they get it right.

At almost 80 years old, my beloved mother had never been in a Starbucks and she wanted to try it. We went inside to order so as not to slow down the drive thru. The person who took our order was a woman with short grey hair under her visor and the name tag "Jaye." She was impatient when we could not hear her over the ambient noise and through the protective plastic. When my mom struggled to say the order loud enough, Jaye actually ROLLED HER EYES at an elderly, first time guest. I stepped in and took over the order. Drinks were made properly and were quick, hence the 2 stars. But, the disrespect is unforgivable.

I really like this Starbucks. I can usually find parking pretty close by and I can't complain about the drive through because THERE ISN'T ONE! I usually get a drink, change in the bathroom for kickball, and then get on my merry way.

Normal walk-in starbucks... Staff was very friendly... Drinks were made fast but with care... Had a great convo with the bristia about differnt coffee's we liked and she suggested some of her favorite that I might like...
my peppermint  mocha taste like crap! bitter..no coffee taste..like plain ice blebded with milk ...suck

Today was the first time I ordered from Starbucks during quarentine. This location was quick, careful, and friendly. Everyone was masked and my mobile order required no contact, which was great.   I ordered 3 of the pink-drink alternatives (coconut milk creamed refreshes) for my housemates and all 3 were delicious!  Most of all, I really happy to see the very clear support for both the Black Lives Matters movement and the LGBTQ community. That alone will make me support this store.

I loved that the employee at the register greeted us when we came in. However, upon ordering the first of my drinks, she proceeded to close the order. When I explained I wanted to get another drink, she rolled her eyes, gave a big sigh and not-so-nicely asked me what else I wanted. She was extremely short for the rest of the transaction. Not what I've come to expect of Starbucks employees.

Busy with college crowd most nights, however coffee is excellent and the staff friendly. If you are a regular they remember your name and your usual drink most of the time. Would recommend for a coffee and some time with friends.

The service here is adequate.  The parking is so hard to use!  They have three spots by their dumpster and you may have to camp out waiting to get out of two of them if someone parks in front of the dumpster which is a legitimate parking spot.   They have signs everywhere stating you will be towed if you are not a Starbucks customer. I dare you to try and get a tow truck in the parking lot!

If it ain't broke, don't fix it!! If it's broke fix it ASAP. Waited 18 min for a tall Americano, about 15min to long. Absolutely no efficiency to get their customers a cup of coffee. I finally walked out empty handed after 4 people came in after me and left with their orders. Worst customer service but then again it's Starbucks. If your time is valuable, Don't waste it going here.

Back to being a Starbucks gal. I was going to Dunkin' off Missouri & the evening service is terrible. The moral & energy here is consistent. My only thing is the amount of espresso they give in a single shot is extremely minute. Not even a full shot. But I'd rather come here where the service is great.

Whatever Starbucks is doing during their employee training, it's the best business model to date. I've never had a bad customer service experience here in my life and I'm here weekly, for years.  Yes I can admit it, Don't judge.  I really want to know Starbucks corporate secrets. They're killing the customer service game. It's one all businesses should implement. I always leave happy after my starbucks employee encounters. They are all so jovial all the time to the point where I've thought to myself, should I leave my career to become an employee there. I even one time asked a barista what their secret is and she told me it's all the Americana shots they're doing together in the back.  Well it's working. See you guys tomorrow!
I love Starbucks! Iced cinnamon almond milk macchiato! Yummmm Pumpkin spice lattes Yumm! I like the way your local Starbucks gets to know you!
This Starbucks is the slowest I've ever been to, six people behind the "bar" and almost twenty minutes for TEA go toa different Starbucks you'll save yourself headache

This place is in the Bellevue so it's a nice location right on broad street. It's the first store on the right once you enter the building so it's easy to get to when you are in a rush. The seating is off in the back of the store (this isn't an "open floor plan" setup) so there is sense of privacy as you eat. The staff is friendly.   Downside: it can be packed during rush hour. VERY packed. This is the reason I avoid it in the mornings if I can't spare the extra time. Also, the selection is not large. This store has a limited menu and, despite being in a posh location near city hall, frequently runs out of items that are supposedly on the menu  Not a bad place by any stretch of the imagine. It's just that, in comparison to other Starbucks, it just doesn't compare.

This teeny Starbucks does a decent job with the small space it has.  Although there always seems to be a line, they make sure that the drinks are made quickly (and accurately) while still doing it with a smile.

What a great experience! Haven't been to a Starbucks in quite a while. Ran out of vanilla creamer at home so went to this Starbucks Drive-thru. The drive-thru went quickly. The order screen was a live feed of the person taking the order. This man was so energetic and nice!  Came up to the window to another employee who really seemed to enjoy their job. She offered my two dogs a 'Pup'-pucino (whip cream in a shot cup). They loved it. (A little messy but...). My large Skinny Vanilla Latte was delicious. Great start to the day!

This is one of my favorite Starbucks locations to get coffee from - the employees are always patient as I'm sometimes indecisive in what to get and ordering ahead with the Starbucks app makes things super easy; they always have the order ready within 5-10 minutes tops! Usually I only come to Starbucks to complete the star dashes and earn points...and check out the seasonal merchandise!  They've done a great job spacing out the tables at this Starbucks location and encouraging the use of masks. Pumpkin spice lattes, here I come! :)  Thank you, 17th Street Starbucks!

Sometimes you need coffee at a place that everyone knows you. (Queue the Cheers theme song) This is my place. Love or hate Starbucks all you want. I love them.   This location (96th and Masters) is the one for me. The manager, Gary, has a talent for finding baristas that get it. They understand community and building something out of rusher visits. This has been my Starbucks for the last 5 years and I love them. Kate, Jay, Bailey, Kristin, Laura... they kill in the mornings. My drinks are always tasty, they listen, and over time get to know you. There's always a smile and friendly conversation no matter how busy they are. There's even notes and art some days.

Love that they have a drive thru however their customer service is lacking. One employee named Reid (I think) has taken my order several times and had consistently been rude.  Have experienced this behavior with a couple other employees as well but not all of them. Don't mind paying Starbucks' high prices but not willing to pay for the bad attitudes.

The love affair is strong and deep. Which is surprising as I just started down the caffeine trail in the last 4 months.   This location is along the way to work every morning, making it hard to not stop by and put in a little quality time with you.   The drive thru is hardly ever packed and the customer service is awesome just like the whip I add on you every morning.   The place is clean on the inside but I hardly venture in as life and responsibilities force me to keep moving expeditiously on to the next thing like the natural born millennial I am.   You're awesome I won't leave you my hot grande caramel macchiato!   Until tomorrow morning!
I come here a lot. Always fast and courteous. I always think the tables could be a little bit cleaner, but there's a lot of space for seating and the bathroom is always clean.
I think they have new leadership at this location. It is still slow and alot of the staff is not friendly but it's better than it was.

Extremely rude staff at the drive through window. They were out of every dessert I asked for and when I asked what they did have the woman raised her voice and shouted at me. She purposefully took her time and made us sit at the window without acknowledging us while she spoke to other people.
Just like any other Sbux. No unique charm but my drink was up quickly.

I have a couple complaints about this Starbucks. My boyfriend and I live nearby, so this is the Starbucks we frequent, especially when we need a pick-me-up on our way to Phoenix. We usually get a coffee and a breakfast sandwich. My favorite is the spinach feta wrap, which is always perfection but twice now my boyfriend has gotten the sausage & egg sandwich and it's been cold in the middle.   Another time, we frequented this place was to get an iced tea and a latte. I ordered an unsweetened passion fruit and the bf got a vanilla latte. His came out at least 5 minutes before mine and when I finally got mine and took a sip, I could tell it had sweetener in it. I checked the cup label to see if it said unsweetened, and it did. I stood there for another couple minutes before an employee approached me and made me a new drink. This all wouldn't have bothered me so much if I didn't have to wait so long in between Erik's drink and mine.

Updated review. I've ordered a lot of the past few months and they've really improved with times of orders and accuracy. I typically always mobile order, I've learned to give them at least 10 mins in the mornings and it works out just fine.

Busy. like Starbucks is generally busy - but this place is hopping on a Saturday. However, there are plenty of seats and tables. If you want an outlet (this might be tricker but doable). But friendly staff, quick, and I do like the atmosphere where you want to get some work done for a few hours.

I am a regular at Starbucks.  5 days a week I was going for my morning breve latte, that's about $5.25 a day, times 4 weeks, that's  $105. a month.  And However, if my husband and grandkids are with me it is much more as they usually want something to eat and often times a thermos and reuseable to go cup! Recently, I went in and upon ordering my coffee I discovered it was twice the usual price.
