When I questioned the person behind the counter about this I was told "yes prices had increased", "by double?" I inquired (almost just paying it).  When another employee looked at the register and asked if I ordered one or two?  I said one.  He realized I'd been charged twice.  He apologized explaining the girl on register was brand new.  No worries.  We are all new employees at one time or another in our lives and mistakes are made, and then easily rectified.   I was told by this young gentleman that they made the correction (although I was never given a receipt; my mistake but I have literally been coming here for 6 years and didn't think it necessary).  When I arrived home later that day I was on line with my bank checking my deductions when I noticed that yes, Starbucks had charged me double, and then took it off.  However, I noticed that they than charged me twice for the same drink showing two separate transactions.   Again, knowing the girl at the register to be new I realized she simply made another error.  I decided I would call Starbucks to see what they could do about it.  I spoke to "Danni", whom I assumed to be a young manager.  I explained to her what happened.  She remembered me, as I said, I'm in there a lot.  She told me to simply come back when she was on duty and she would give me my next coffee making up the difference.  I told her that would be fine.  However, when I went back she wasn't there.  I explained to the cashier what happened and she told me to wait for Danni to come back in.  So I did.  Today Danni was in.  I went up to the counter and brought to her memory what had happened.  However, on this occasion she told me I needed to prove it with my bank statement.  Now I'm standing there feeling embarrassed asking for a refund for one cup of joe, while at the same time being looked at and spoken to as if I was just trying to get a free coffee.  Someone needs to speak to these young people about customer service, especially the regulars.  Not only did I spend what I believe was a nice amount of money on coffee and other items per month but I was also a generous tipper.  The only person whom I ever met at this location who knew anything about customer service was the night "Brandon" who has worked there for quite some time.  He is simply heavenly to deal with and knows the value of a repeat customer who has been nothing but kind and understanding towards your staff.   I am not saying I'll never be back but it might take a while for the sting to subside.  I'll also insist upon a receipt each and every time.  If you can't trust me why should I trust you?  Receipts should be mandatory, not asked "do you want a receipt" , if you are going to treat your customers this way. Today my star rating is going to be very low.

Pls do not waste your time going to this location. They can not and will not make your drink correctly. This is by far the worst. I brought a venti iced green tea latte and more than half the the drink was just straight foam! That is ridiculous. I did not just spend over $5 for just foam. Definitely will not visit this location again. I'll take an extra 10/15 min drive to go somewhere better.

This is the filthiest Starbucks I have ever been in. Gross. Gross gross.   Toilet was a filthy mess with toilet paper everywhere on the floor. The store itself needs a good scrubbing. Trash cans overflowing. Straws, paper and other questionable detritus on the floor. Sticky seats.   I get that Santa Barbara seems to have a burgeoning homeless challenge. Perhaps that's a contributing factor. I don't know.   I just know that this is a seriously gross Starbucks. There's a coffee bean and tea leaf across the street and an independent coffee house up the street. Go there instead.
Luckily I was one of the first in line but it was very slow,I have to say it was not the best Starbucks Ive been to.

I'm not a frequent coffeehouse patronizer, but this Starbucks is one of my go-to locations because its close to work. Located on the ground floor of the Marriott, this Starbucks has a very corporate vibe to it. There is always a ton of people in here, with pretty significant lines.   This is not a cozy-feeling Starbucks at all, being that interior space is very tight--like a thin, lengthy road. The amount of seating is sparse, and rather disjointed (a couple alcoves and a bar-type window seating area). This is not a place that you would wish to spend any length of time in.  However, you can be pretty sure of one thing - your food & drink will be fresh, being that there is a high volume of people that pass through here. In fact, I *highly* recommend that you get the Starbucks app to order/pay so that you can skip the crazy lines.   Theresa may be the best barista in town - we're talking Best of Philly. She obviously works hard, she always has my drinks made perfectly, and she is one of the nicest ladies in town. I think she is only here in the late morning, though.  I tried ordering through the app just yesterday, and it all worked flawlessly for me. I ordered and paid with my mobile phone, it gave me an estimated time of 3-8 minutes that my order would be read, I walked over to the Starbucks, and by golly I had my drink in-hand on the 8th minute!  Why this location is not open 24 hours, is beyond me; apparently Philly cannot sustain a 24 hour Starbucks. If any location was going to be 24 hours, I'd think this would have been it.

I love Starbucks, obviously. But it just seems as if this particular location is always extremely slow. I have to leave my house 30 minutes early to be able to stop and get a coffee because it takes so long to get through the drive-through line. I then tried ordering inside the store and it took just as long. For whatever reason this location just has poor time management.

Former SS and barista trainer here. I ordered an iced coffee w/milk and vanilla this morning. When I was handed my drink, it was an iced coffee with a splash of cream on top. I took it, but informed the woman working DT that it wasn't made to beverage standards. The only response I got was an eye roll and her turning her back to me while calling out that she needed a break. For a new drive thru, I would expect the team to be much more attentive to customer service than this.
Nice employees; quick service! My drink was delicious.  The only drawback is that this location does not accept rewards.
Its a nice Starbucks location. Plenty of inside seating in this small strip mall.   Order is always correct  Parking is tough so we park in the next strip mall and walk over

Rude rude rude. Not what Starbucks represents.  You must not step across The Line before they open, not even to look at the bakery case. Maybe it was just a bad day. Oh yeah, this was 10 min after their opening time according to the website and they still weren't set up.

Been going to this Starbucks since it opened and the coffee was always great but lately it's been going down. The last 4months I have been going here the coffee is just not the same. I feel like they don't add enough espresso to my macchiatos and they add to much Carmel and it's way to syrupy. I will not go here anymore I'd rather drive out of my way to cherry hill or Sewell. Thanks for now ruining my hometown Starbucks experience.

They are generally very very slow - it takes forever to get your drink here. The baristas are below-average, and my drinks are always just so-so. If I'm going to pay $5 for a latte, I will go to another Starbucks!! A decent amount of seating, including outdoor seating in summertime.

Worst Starbucks ever. Bunch of rude workers who messed up my order 2 times in a row, no apologies, and they were extremely rude about it. If you want to add more stress to your morning before you board your flight this is the place to stop.
This is the first time I have ever gone to a Starbucks and the person helping us was grouchy. She didn't say hello, thank you, or bye. Very disappointing.

The manager needs to learn how to manage. Employees seem to think playing around and yelling and laughing as loud as they can is acceptable, very childish and unprofessional. The manager needs to understand they are employees not friends. Customers have to yell to be heard over the music and employees. Takes forever to get your order. Very poor service. Like all Starbucks WAY WAY overpriced.

Workers are rude, no respect to clients, I normally come here when I'm shopping at nearby grocery stores. Taking advantage of COVID-19, they lock the bathrooms but they allow people they know. This will be the last time I will be getting my coffee at this location. What a customer service.

New Years Eve, the store front sign says they are open until 6pm - it was 5:20pm when I arrived and the drive through was coned off and the door locked. We were ignored at the door as the staff was closing up inside. I understand holidays, wanting to leave early - but as someone who works in customer service- that is poor practice.

This is one of the bigger Starbucks in the SB area, and also the one I frequent the most due to its location in the Camino Real Marketplace. It's a convenient coffee stop if you're out shopping in the area (Costco, Home Depot, Ross, Sports Authority, Ulta, etc) or if you're waiting for the showing of a movie at the theater a couple doors down. This place is quite popular so be prepared for a longer wait (than other Starbucks I've visited) for your drink...it's almost always crowded (a ton of college students studying), there's usually a line, and most seating will be occupied. They renovated a couple years ago so the place has newer furnishings and a slight rearrangement of the layout, but overall it seems pretty much the same as when it first opened. Their super friendly staff and prime location keeps bringing me back, even if it's not the coziest of places and doesn't offer super fast service.

Very friendly and quick in store service, store is always very clean and has great connectivity options. Wi-if is fairly fast and there are power outlets for charging your technology just about everywhere you can think of.  Drive through can be a bit slow, but this is by far the nicest Starbucks in the area.

Previously I had rated this Starbucks for poor service, however I have gone back to their location and found they seem to have a new crew of workers. The service is much better than it had been before, additionally the location is very convenient if shopping or grabbing food from the nearby restaurants. The downsides are that it is still not a "full" Starbucks, you cannot order online, redeem free drinks, etc.

While staying at UCSB for a conference, we would walk in to Isla Vista every morning for coffee.  I like Starbucks for the coffee and for the consistancy of product and service.  Our first morning, I ordered a grande coffee in a vente cup with an add shot.  The young lady took our order and sent the cups to the barrista (who didn't show up for several minutes).  Once our young gent (who will be forthwith referred to as Flock of Seagulls FoS) returned, he got my friend's latte to her.  Then he began working on mine, finished it up and topped it off with a different type of coffee.  When he brought it back to me, I stated that that wasn't what I ordered.  He stood there with the coffee in his hand as if I would change my mind and he wouldn't have to redo it.  After about 10 seconds of this stand-off, I kindly informed him that he would have to remake my drink.  All the while he was apologizing and creating excuses.  Dude, just shut it and make me my coffee!!!  I'm not sure what their system is there... verbal orders or written, but if it is verbal, then the barista needs to be in the same room when the order is made.  BTW, the lady behind me was not happy with her beverage either.  We left the premises to the whoosh of another drink being poured down the drain.  Day 2:  We come into Starbucks and FoS has his foot up on the counter.  Now, I'm not a germ-phobe, but I've worked in enough restaurants to know what is on the floor, and I don't want that on the counter..  We got the correct drink today, but only because the female worked both the cash register and drinks.  FoS was busy stocking sandwiches.  Day 3: Drinks were ordered by about 6 customers, and we were smack in the middle of the group.  The two before us got their drinks, and the two behind us got their drinks, and then we got our drinks.  There was quite a substantial wait between the first two orders and the last four because FoS felt a very urgent need to use the restroom even though customers were waiting.  Thankfully he did wash his hands!!  Day 4: Screw it!  I'm gonna use the Kuerig in the office!!
Worst service waited about 30 mins until I cancelled my ordered. Manger was very rude and rushed about cancelling order. Didn't even apologize. Horrible service

A Starbucks is a Starbucks. Usually the drinks are consistent and the service is friendly.  Here they were very polite and friendly. I was greeted right away.  My order was taken and my drink was ready quickly. It tastes just like I had hoped.   Initially I thought the store had closed.  Starbucks has shut down many locations at home. Although the parking area is small we had no trouble parking.    I saw a few of the more recent reviews and was surprised how bad they were.  My experience was the opposite.   I would recommend stopping here if you need a coffee.

The Starbucks experience used to be a unique and memorable occasion compared to other beverage purveyors. The experience would suggest comfort, productivity, inclusion, & community.   With the rise of the "buy local" campaign and the "social consumer" Starbucks had begun to lose its draw. Drive through experiences are rushed. Relationships with baristas are hard to establish due to the "efficiency" of the order and delivery process. Ideas and happening seem to have found a new home in our culture.  This store does little to help with the ordering experience. Cattle herding is more descriptive of the current experience. However, the fireplace inclusion & outdoor cove seating hold on to the ideals that this company used to stand for.

This is a brand new store- just opened 5/7/16.  There are 2 parking spots in front, and 2 handicap parking spots in front.  There is a huge parking lot behind the building (and there is a rear entrance in the store).  There is a drive-thru.  I was immediately greeted upon entering.  My drink was prepared before I was even done paying.  The pastry case was well-stocked (even in early afternoon).  There are 2 huge communal tables in the center of the store, lots of tables around the perimeter, 3 comfy chairs near the front door, and a huge patio.

When it comes to coffee shops and stands, I'm usually not much of a Starbucks type of person. It's nothing personal. The coffee is decent, but I just prefer to support local coffee shops.  On a recent mall outing, I was in the mood for some iced coffee and my choices were a long line at Cafe du Monde or no line at Starbucks. I guess I'm an easy mark: no line wins over line every time, no matter what. This isn't a full on Starbucks shop, although they offer everything you would find at a regular Starbucks. It's pretty much a coffee stand in the middle of the mall. The man working was really friendly and the iced coffee I got was good and decently priced. I'm sure an iced coffee with 2% milk was the most "normal" order he had heard all day.  The cool thing about this stand is there are plenty of sofas and chairs, in case you want to lounge around and enjoy your coffee and take a break from the rest of the mall madness.

Normally this small Starbucks is excellent. However, this morning we had to wait 15-20 minutes on two orders. A pumpkin spice latte and an Americano.   I finally asked the register person to credit our order.  He said "sorry". The basic root of the problem was a slow, untrained barista who seemed to go even more slowly after I complained about time.    So, I'm sorry to say I cannot rate this location higher than a 2

I live 2 minutes away from this Starbucks location, however I most often drive 10 minutes to get  coffee at another location.  In the 2 years that I've lived nearby, I've had far more bad experiences than good. On several occasions drinks have been forgotten and other times  I've received really slow or indifferent customer service.  What makes it annoying is that this location always seems to have plenty of people working.  It just seems like they often get in each other's way.  If I'm gonna drop $4-$5 on a corporate cup of coffee, I expect to get the drink I ordered and have the pleasant, cookie-cutter experience that should come along with it.

My coffee spot for almost four years now. Love the locals too, but this is close to my daily grind. The baristas here make it feel local for sure.  With so many people going in and out, parking lot confusion happens quickly - especially on weekdays. Not to mention the entire shopping center is a pain to get out of, regardless which direction you're headed.
Everyone is very nice & efficient here- admittedly tough to get I to their entrance unless u r heading the right way- but worth a circle if need be- as quiet, very well lit & kind staff.
great customer service by the lovely lady with glasses.
These guys are very busy. I always get an iced green tea. They made it perfectly. The wait was about 20 minutes on a Sunday evening.
Basic Starbucks experience. Nothing fancy. Had the regular items. Fairly small seating area. Had an outdoor seating but it was too cold to sit outside today. Friendly service.

Today I arrived in drive through and gave them my order at 1:55pm. I ordered  venti White mocha, and a grande caramel frap. THATS IT! I waited in the line along with 5 cars in front of me and about the same behind me. Until I finally revived my order in my hands at 2:13pm. Almost 20 minutes! HORRIBLE! Whoever the hell owns this dam Starbucks needs to fix this, because the other Starbucks on Los Altos and Disc to perfectly fine with large volumes of customers.      To the owner - ur employees are struggling or something is not right. If you happened to take 2 seconds of ur day and read my review but ALL OTHER REVIEWS AS WELL...you would be making more at the end of the day! #enoughsaid

It's a Starbucks.  It met my expectations: friendly service, consistent food and beverage, free wifi, relatively clean, lots of outlets for electronics, and convenient location.  I had never been to this one, and it is a good choice for stopping while in South Reno. It is bigger than the one on Mt. Rose, there is LOTS of parking (it is in the Sierra Summit Mall) and when I went (early afternoon) there was no wait.  Nothing stood out as being outstanding. Nothing stood out as being terrible - hence "A-OK"  Being average is not necessarily a bad thing for a chain.
Cute shop. Friendly staff. Located at the corner of State and Cota.  The End.  What more can I say...it's a Starbucks.

Been here two times and this is the second time they have forgotten my order. I waited over 10 minutes while others (6 people) behind me got their orders ahead of mine. I had to go over to the barista and tell them that I hadn't received my order after waiting another 5 minutes. They need to re-think their service order priority.

Might be the slowest Starbucks in the history of Starbucks. Only car in line and I waited 12 minutes for an iced coffee, which I asked for very little cream and watched her pour 1/4 cup of creamer in. They have messed numerous orders of mine up, which when you pay so kufj for coffee you expect good service. Unless you have 20 minutes to elsewhere.   Attached is milk water with a dash of coffee.

At least three times a week they screw up my simple order. A Venti iced coffee sweetened with light ice is what I ordered. What part of that said put cream in it? This is been repetitive enough that I will no longer go to the store.

I love this Starbucks!! Mackenzie, LeThu, Kate & Danica are wonderful baristas! They are so friendly as well. My drinks are always delish and they make great recommendations.   I always look forward to stopping in. I definitely recommend!!

We came to this Starbucks on our way to Bed Bath and Beyond in the same plaza and noticed it had a drive through! At first we wanted to use the drive through, but the line was too long, so we ended up parking and going inside. The inside was nothing special, but it was clean and the baristas were friendly. We ended up ordering an iced tea lemonade (not in the mood for coffee that day) and the order came up fairly quick.  Next time I go, I'm going to try the drive through!
There is a walk up window to order drinks if you want to beat the indoor line or if you're walking your dog in the summer time. Nice outdoor seating with a good view of mass ave.
This store is cool but the barista Fernando always hits on me and it makes me very uncomfortable.but Mariah to and Jordan are the best.
Nice place to read a book, TERRIBLE WIFI SPEED, if you plan to be online for more than 3 min, then don't even bother trying it here. It takes over 1 min for google home page page to load.

I will start with the parking lot. It fits about 8 cars max and you can squeeze like 6 more up the side and the front of this place. I have gotten off my  Starbucks addiction lately but stopped in today. I ordered my coffee, Iced Grande Americano with LOTS of room- I tell the barista to tell the guy making it because in the past it has just a smidge of room and they think dumping some  of the coffee out makes it all better. This  has happened so many times that I stopped going. AND guess what, it happened again I looked at the cup stunned and just took it and left. In a hurry to get to work I did not feel like arguing with the barista why he needs to remake it because I like it strong not watery. It is like the old days at Wawa before the computer touch screen you have to watch them make it so they don't F it up. Sorry Starbucks in Bryn Mawr with all of your Wifi internet users clogging your parking lot for hours and making it impossible to stop in quickly for a cup of coffee filled to the top - I gave you one more chance and you blew it.  ****I emailed my complaint to Starbucks and received a coupon for  2 free coffees in the mail. I will surely use them-  but at a different location.

I am a semi-regular at this location.  I often end up here when I have some time to kill before or after shopping at Merenda's Italian Specialties, which is one of the most awesome Italian groceries I've ever been to, and which I highly recommend.  I like this place, and I obviously keep coming back.  I like the ambiance, minus the view of the parking lot, and I like the location, again, minus the suburban sprawl that it lives in.  My main complaint about this location is that the staff seem really overworked, and I don't understand why.  I suspect it's the drive through that's the culprit.  I've been in here time and time again, when there is no line (inside, there's usually at least a car or two waiting at the drive through), and it takes quite some time, often over a minute, for someone behind the counter to even come over to me.  For example, today there were FIVE employees behind the counter, no line, very few customers in the shop, and they were all doing things behind the counter in a frenzied / hurried looking fashion.  All five of them!!!  What the heck are they doing?  Cleaning things?  Making drinks for the drive through?  I don't know, but I don't get it.  Maybe I have trouble understanding it because I always order stuff that requires limited effort, like tea, coffee, or a baked good, and maybe other people are ordering ultra-special-involved drinks that require 4 people to expend 5 minutes each just to make the freaking drink.  But I don't know, that just doesn't seem plausible.  I've been to so many other coffee shops where there are fewer employees, yet the employees like, walk right up when a customer comes up to the counter.  Some of my favorite coffee shops in West Philly have only 1 or 2 employees at a given time, and they handled more volume than this shop (no drive through though).  I don't like it.  It creates a stressy vibe.  When I stand at the counter, I feel like I'm absorbing the stress of the employees.  I don't feel like they're really 100% there, when they ask me what I want.  Like, I'm sitting here in the coffee shop as I write this review, and the employees are constantly running back and forth behind the counter in a frenzied fashion, yet there has been a total of one customer at the counter the whole time.  Ahh, here two more customers come in, finally.  I'm watching the interaction.  The employee isn't making much eye contact.  The two customers look so nice.  The guy behind the counter looks so stressed.  Oh, here someone comes out of the kitchen...she looks so stressed out too.  Seriously, Starbucks corporate management, DO something about this location.  The poor stressed, overworked employees...they don't look like they are having a good time, and they're making it a less pleasant place to be for the customers inside the shop.  I really want this place to have a genuinely chill vibe.  That would be like, really chill.  And I'd like that.  Heck yeah.
Same as any other Starbucks. The service is very pleasant here. The atmosphere is comfy and clean

Great staff! Always polite and helpful. Rarely do they get a drink order wrong.   However, space is almost always an issue at this particular location. It's not like the ambience is bad. It's just that a lot of people don't understand how to use proper 'line etiquitte', especially when the weather is cold. There are two entrances to the store, so I think the potential for awkward encounters is high. Personally, I've had some people think I was trying to cut them...when in fact, I was trying to manuever the line so the waiting line wouldn't trickle outside.   Honestly, if you can swing it, I'd go for a different Starbucks...IF the wait is long. While the staff's attitudes are great, at the end of the day, the environment is way too claustophobic. Thus, more often then not, you'll probably get a whiff of some of the negative demeanors that mark certain downtown employees.  On the other hand, IF the wait is short...then I'd still recommend going here...just because you'll have a great experience with the staff and open window seating (if available).

This is my home Starbucks at the moment. I love the employees, who are polite even when they are crazy busy. I've never gotten a bad product from this Starbucks. My order is a little complex, and they've never gotten it wrong OR looked at me like I'm insane for ordering. When they unveiled their new pastry menu, they were giving out warm samples, even to the drive-thru line!   I wish I could find a way to thank them for making my morning better each time, but I haven't thought of a good one yet. I guess I'll just keep going back.

HUGE shout-out to the morning shift working Friday October 9th! I came in the morning prior and the barista complimented me and I gave her a compliment back and that alone made my morning because who doesn't love a compliment? The next morning (this morning) I came in and was in the back of the line and she came over and asked what I was drinking and took my order even though there was like 5 people before me. After that she pulled me to the front and let me go ahead and pay. Its a simple act of kindness and it really impacted my morning!  If there is any managers reading this or Starbucks the company, her name is Tory and shes awesome! Everyone working this morning was extremely friendly and awesome but defiantly a big shout-out to Tory!
Waited 15 minutes in the drive thru. Got a smores and a waffle cone. They tasted nothing like they were supposed too and by the point in time we got them we just wanted to throw them away.

This was my go-to Starbucks.  This morning I got a rude barista who chastised me very publicly for trying to get my own drink carrier.  I assume it's a policy that when you order multiple drinks on a mobile order, you should be expected to wait in line and try to flag down a busy barista to get a drink carrier so that you can actually carry your drinks out of the store.  You know, waiting in line to get a drink carrier almost defeats the idea of putting in a mobile order, doesn't it?  I get the idea of not wasting drink carriers, but common sense and a little customer service would be appreciated.    In any event, there are lots of Starbucks and other coffee shops around town where you can get better customer service and you don't get a side of attitude with the $5 cup of coffee.  I'll gladly go there instead.

Normally a good Starbucks location. Was miffed when, after waiting in a long line, discovered they were out of my favorite, one and only item I order, Emperor's Cloud and Mist Tea. Not one barista offered to check if they had any more in the back. Poor order management and lazy workers. Wasn't any better at 16th & Walnut location.

I stopped here after discovering the Soho had sold out for the Chris Robinson Brotherhood, and I couldn't find the public restrooms. The barista(Katie) on duty was very kind and engaged me in conversation when I told her I had just driven 120 miles to see the show. I was quite pleased with her customer service and getting me on the list to the show)).   If all baristas could be as customer service oriented, the service industry economy would thrive. Having grown up in Ventura county and frequently going to Santa Barbara on the weekends I have forgotten what it is like to receive great customer service from a corporate run business.

S-L-O-W-W-W!  Drive up (while still at a turtle's pace) gets more attention than those who brave actually walking in.  And while I'm here, and speaking of walking in,the front door is ridiculously hard to open. While they have a "handicap" parking space in front I doubt if anyone needing to park there could open the door without assistance. Seems like Starbucks could afford to install an auto door device.

Just skip this and give your business to small mom and pop shops like Poor Richards Coffee down the street which has organic grass fed dairy, is cheaper and is much more comfortable than this Starbucks which has about 3 high-tops, 4 comfy leather chairs (the only comfortable seating in the whole place) and one huge wood table. You could also go to Gryphon Cafe in downtown Wayne. Im so sick of over priced Starbucks with chemical ingredients and dairy from grain fed cows full of hormones and antibiotics.

I have decided that this is the last time that I will give this Starbucks location my money.  Time and time again I have received sub par service from this location and due to convenient proximity to my house I have returned with the hope that they will get it right this time.  I've looked past the fact that the staff hasn't been as friendly as other locations, I've compromised when they gave me still half frozen breakfast sandwiches by heating them up at home and I even overlooked the time that they surprised me by saying they had no coffee but could make something espresso based.  I don't know how a coffee shop runs out of coffee but it was 6 a.m. and I guess I wasn't in the mood to ask such mind blowing questions.  Today after having not visited this location for over a year, I decided to give it one more shot how hard could it be to order an ice coffee and move on with my day.  Well after waiting for 10 minutes to get my iced coffee and watching 7 other people with specialty drinks who ordered after me be served before me, I went up to the cashier to inquire as to what was taking so long with my simple iced coffee.  Her reply "oh we spilled all the iced coffee and now have to make some more."  10 minutes later I got my coffee, no apology, no offer to comp for the inconvenient 20 minute wait. Apparently communicating with the customer who is waiting for their coffee is completely off their radar.  So I'm done, over it.  This location has the worst customer service and in the age of social media reviews and unlimited choices I won't be giving this place my business and I hope others will consider offering their money to locations that are friendly and aware of what it means to at least meet if not exceed your customer's expectations.

The service was great ! It looked nice and clean. And the people working there were so nice. The drink I asked for was complicated and she by accident messed it up and had no problem at all when I asked if she can redo it. I was so satisfied with my drink so far she was the best one to make it! I WILL be going there again it was a little far from home but it was completely worth it.

Walk in to get a drink. No hello or welcome. The workers talked to each other and ignored my existence.. after I asked where my drink was a worker rolled her eyes and said over there. Not coming here again..

This was a good Starbucks before it became a drive-through in a new location.  They are  always be out of everything. During Smore's season I ordered Smore's and they gave it to me. With no whipped cream. And I was like where's the whipped cream? And they were like, "oh we are out of marshmallow."What?? Then it's not a smores! It's chocolate and some other things! Was there a few days ago they are out of pumpkin sauce. Was there yesterday and they're out of cold brew. Was there today, they're out of cold brew. They also love to hand you cups that have coffee dripping down the sides of them. So, always double check before you take the cup to make sure it's clean.  Update! Today they are out of nitro and it's 6:00 PM ‍

Drinks were good as almost always are at any location. However, service was seriously lacking in several ways. Among the worst was when a lady next to us crashed chair into my arm causing my just received hit Chai Latte to fall and splatter all over the floor. It took at least 7 minutes for someone to offer a towel or show up with a mop. No inquiry about whether or not I was burned and no concern for the dozens of other customers slipping in the mess. I had to put a chair over it myself to protect people from stepping in it and falling.   Once someone showed up with s mop there was no offer to get me another one, free or otherwise. The lady who spilled it walked away as if nobody else in the place existed and I got bupkis. I'll be back because they get it right more often then not and I love their products, but just maybe not to this one.  I gave 2 stars instead of 1 because the two tiny sips I got tasted pretty good.

under slightly new management, which has caused a perceivable hit in quality. it's still a better cuppa than any other SB i've been to in the area (save the delmar one which has much the same staff).  people are friendly, too.

This one is a staple. Close to school, good hours, and plenty of seating. It's always filled with fellow students, and there's always a few cars in the drive through, a testament to it's popularity. All the employees (that I've interacted with) are very pleasant and the restrooms (for those that care) are well kept!
I've probably been in this store 12 times half of the staff is great but my order has been messed up at least 4 times now and I am hesitant to go in depending on who is working.

The only drink I've gotten from Starbucks the past year is the green tea lemonade with extra lemonade and literally every other time they hand me a PEACH TEA LEMONADE (gross) and when I try to correct them half the time they hand me just green tea. Green tea lemonade is on the menu so why is it so hard to get it right?

This location is always absurdly busy in the mornings. This is the main reason I avoid it - even though it's on my way to work. Typically I'll stop off at a local coffee shop on the Westbank, or the Marrero Starbucks, if I need a caffeine fix before work.   It would actually better for me if I purchased my coffee on the Eastbank, so that I would still have some left upon arriving at work... but here's why it's not going to happen:  If you wait in the drive thru, you're probably looking at a 10-15 minute wait on any given weekday morning during rush hour. Go inside? Add on at least another 5 minutes. I waited 20 mins from the time I walked in the door to the time I got my latte on Friday (needed some extra caffeine after Muses the night before).  It's not that the baristas are slow. They actually crank out a lot of espresso at a fair pace. They're friendly, too, which is why this gets 2 stars instead of 1.   If it weren't for the service, this would defintiely be a 1 star experience. I can't see recommending a 10-20 minute wait for the same beverage you can get at any of their other approximately 28196291 locations worldwide. Granted, I'm sure the wait time is significantly reduced at any other time during the day... but the only time I ever *want* a $5 coffee is first thing in the morning.
These girls will make laugh. Very nice and friendly people here.
Excellent customer service! Long line but it went Fast! Loved the decor and the coffee chats with other visitors!
Nice job! Really appreciated the service and special pour over. My chai tea was perfect as well. Nice to have good service when on a long trip. Thanks so much!

The coffee and specialty drinks were fine, and yes it's New Year's Day and any employee would want to be home today, BUT I'm still giving this Starbucks a 2-star rating because of the barista who served my group. She informed us that they would be closed in 20 minutes and we assured her that we wouldn't keep her past closing. She was very snippy with us as she rang us up and then "accidentally" forgot to give my friend her $20 gift card back after she used it to buy a drink. My friend saw the barista pocket the card and asked to see if it was her gift card, but the barista said, "i don't think this is yours." Needless to say, it was.

This is my favorite place to get my coffee. It's super convenient and my iced coffee is always made just right. A few of the workers even know my order as soon as I walk in. The staff is super friendly and willing to help you with anything. Clean, cool atmosphere. My number 1 place to get coffee!
One of the smallest Starbucks in the city. I've never had a problem with the staff. I just wish this location had better seating. Max is the greatest barista EVER!

This is my favorite Starbucks in Doylestown. The baristas are very chatty and friendly, and inside there are 2 rooms for seating, giving you a view of historic Doylestown. One of my favorite drinks at Starbucks is Iced Coffee, but this Starbucks always seems to not have it! They also run out on a lot of little supplies.

I missed happy hour, but I got my seasonal favorite.Erynne was the barista at the window tonight and was very nice.  She saw that I had my furry friend riding shotgun and made sure he had his pup cup.  The grande peppermint mocha frappe that I ordered was a the perfect choice the match this warm December evening.  They were also very generous with the whip cream .

I LOVE EVERYONE AT THIS STARBUCKS! honestly i come for the customer service besides the pink drink that i love so so so much but it's that personal interaction and rapport that they all built with me individually... KAT with her bright personality, Shaquille amazing very funny , JAS very outgoing , ALEXIS ugh love her cool vibes! ILL travel just to come to this Starbucks before i go to another and they truly care!
We were In the neighborhood & stopped by for coffee.  We ended up getting a mocha frappe & vanilla bean frappe they were both excellent!!Customer service was great also.

Oh dear god ... if you go to a "regular" Starbucks (i.e. not on the U of A campus), you'll have an awesome visit and it'll be everything Starbucks is suppose to be.  If you go to a Starbucks on the U of A campus, you'll probably have a terrible experience, especially at this location, right outside the Cameron Library.  First off, the servers only speak spanish.  Why?  I have no idea.  They also never smile or strike up a normal, polite conversation with you.  The one server who must be a manager of sorts always looks like she's ready to kill someone.  I actually don't think she's capable of a smile or even standard conversation.  The drinks and food are the same as any other Starbucks, and you early points on your Starbucks membership at this location, but you can't use your points at this location.  This is stupid and I think has something to do with how this location is franchised compared to others.  I worked at the library and in the summer this location didn't open until 8am, the same time the library opened.  For students, this is fine, but for staff it sucked.  If they even opened 15 minutes earlier it would have made life so much easier.  And in the summer their sign says they close at 3:30pm.  Personally I think that's ridiculously early for a coffee shop.  And for awhile their sign said 4pm, but they still closed at 3:30pm!  They're also incredibly slow.  In fact, I think I could be faster moving backwards.  They always seem to have a line, but it's mainly because they serve so slowly!  They have two cash registers, but I've only ever seen the one open.  They also seem to have a hard time keeping the condiments refilled.  Most mornings the milk isn't out right away, and most afternoons there's no sweetener available.  They're definitely letting the Starbucks reputation down.

Just like the previous reviewer, the staff is so friendly and that is the main reason I am writing this review.  I needed a late night pick me up and I had yet to have a pumpkin spiced latte so I thought i would drop by.  I literally walked in the door at 8:55pm, store closes at 9pm, and the lady behind the counter greeted me with a smile.  After I ordered, she continued to make small talk and ask me how my night was.  I told her I needed a caffeine boost since I fell asleep studying for the cpa exam.  While making my drink, she said it sounds like you need a little extra boost to get you through the night.  I was like, "that sounds amazing".  When she went to ring me up she said the extra shot was on the house since she hoped it would improve my mood for studying.... well it did and here I am writing this review!  Having worked in the service industry, I know how one eye's that clock come closing time.  I thought me entering the store so close to closing time would definitely upset the staff... that was clearly not the case.   Job well done!

This used to be my home Starbucks that I stopped at several mornings a week for my standard Starbucks drink (Iced green tea with no syrup!) up until I moved to West County not quite a year ago. I was in the hood the other afternoon and since I had tried the chile mocha (special fall drink) a few days ago it has been on my mind. I hit the drive thru here at around 5pm on a Thursday. There was only one car ahead of me. I ordered a tall chile mocha latte and was on my way in minutes. My beverage was hot and delicious. I'm not sure why this Starbucks has a lot of negativity directed at it in the reviews. They retain employees and have a core group of regulars who hang out there in the mornings. I used to pop in when the drive thru was too crowded and I always got a "hello" and "the usual?" from the same girl! Don't hate on the South City Starbucks...
Painfully slow, The management staff seems not to care if you are waiting for 20 minutes before they open another register. This location has gone down hill, it use to be my favorite.

I recently started going here vs the regular Starbucks where I was going.    For the most part, I have been very pleased.  The baristas and managers have been very pleasant and friendly.  It's not as big of a store compared to where I was going before, which is more relaxed, the music is softer and the loud music was a real gripe of mine at another Starbucks.  The only downside about the smaller shop is it feels like sometimes the large amount of people waiting for their food and beverages feels like they're on top of the people who are in the limited seating.  It would be nice if they had more seating but they're a smaller shop.    For the most part, I really like this Starbucks, friendly baristas.    There was an issue recently with an angry customer but the manager and customer service addressed it.  There are a fair amount of homeless in the area too.    Again, I like this Starbucks for the most part, these are my thoughts.

This is a solid Starbucks, but not really one of my faves given it's location.  The three stars is based on location alone.  It sits outside the mall, no entrance from the interior.  The staff is consistently fantastic... and always makes a solid drink (AND they do the Flat White like it is supposed to be done.)  It has a lot of seating which is nice and a little outdoor seating area as well.
The staff is SO warm and friendly at this location. You can tell they like their jobs.

The barristas were all very nice. The food was good. I like the variety of breakfast selection. We got our food in a timely manner. We didn't get coffee so I can't comment on that but I will say the breakfast we ate was good and the people were so nice.

Worst customer service!! Glad this isn't my regular Starbucks!  They do not try to help customers. They tried to over charge me, for two coffees when I only wanted one. ‍ Ended up leaving with no coffee.
Don't hang out here long if you need to use the bathroom.  Only 1 at a time for the men.  Awful.

I was unimpressed by this location; it suffers from the inner-city Starbucks syndrome patrons may be familiar with in other big cities like New York.  It had a resident homeless guy on the days I visited.  The staff is a bit defensive as a result.  The feng shui is lousy and there aren't a whole lot of good seats.  It's not a disaster, but it's also not a particularly good example of this otherwise reliable chain.

I watched someone maneuver their SUV in the most artful way to not hit someone in the parking lot. Their bumper seemed to be about an inch away from the side of another car that was parked in front of the store. Mind you, this Starbucks for some reason allows people to park in spaces that aren't actually spaces, making the already-tiny lot a complete and utter nightmare. We somehow got a spot in the 15-minute parking area and I was glad that the people in back of us finally moved. It was insane. That is my main complaint. I'm not sure what I would have done without an actual parking spot. Other than that... my frap was tasty and the staff seemed nice.
Surprising location for a Starbucks. Still very nice, big digital ordering screen through the drive through, and quick service on a Sunday morning.

This startbucks location was very understaffed, I mobile ordered 15 minutes ahead and still had to wait another 15 when I arrived. The staff was nice, but seemed very overwhelmed and stressed- seems like a management issue. They also have a drive through so I just don't think they can handle it all.

Best service during a packed Black Friday in the galleria!  The line was outside filled with impatient shoppers needing their fix.  Once you made it inside to order, the employee at the register named "Drew" was oozing with happiness and excitement to take your order and the employee "Cara" passing out the drinks and food was just as happy, cracking jokes, smiling and giggling!  Now that's how you run a business!  I give this location a 5 star!

theres virtually no parking and the drive-thru is often so backed up that it trails out dangerously to Stokes Rd. it is perhaps the most poorly planned parking lot that i can think of. add in the fact that they frequently mess up drink orders and you've got yourself a real treat. do yourself a favor and check out Lakes Coffee in the log cabin shops behind pj whelihans, you wont be disappointed.

Let me start by saying I love coffee and Starbucks (there's a local shop with better coffee but that's for another review). Some Starbucks are great with fab baristas and great relaxing atmosphere. Camby is not one of them. The seating is tight, often the bar is the only spot to sit. The crew behind the counter do a good job at making drinks and are usually friendly, but they are often rowdy and loud with lots of colorful language. In short, it's not my favorite location, but it's my closest so it'll do.

Don't come here unless you are using the drive-thru!  I have been here twice in the last 3 days and both times I had to wait over 20 minutes for a toasted bagel!  Terrible in-store service.  Coffee also inconsistent in quality and flavor.  I observed the staff frantically getting orders out to drive-thru customers while ignoring in-store clients.  Bad form , Bux!

Came in early morning before 7am on our way back to Portland, and the place was clean, staff were friendly, and although the drive through was slammed it really didn't take that long to get our order. When you consider just how few people are actually making the drinks I thought it was pretty amazing watching them work. Was a convenient location on our way back to the freeway, and appreciated the smiles from the staff.

I imagine this Starbucks as a ship. A marvelous ship that brings coffee and treats to all the hard working town folk. When I imagine the captain of this ship I see a fastidious young man with big dreams sparkling in his eye. That young man is none other than Julian, the handsome fellow with the great jokes and that signature ever-present smile. Julian will one day have a fleet of ships of his own but in the mean time he's doing a great job serving up beans to those in need.

Terrible service asked for a iced London fog tea. Tried explaining what is was because she didn't know, at mid sentence she stopped me and said she'd figure it out. Got my drink and it was just 2 bags of tea in a overfilled cup. Didn't even attempt to try to get my order right

Typical Starbucks.   Lots of people there on Sunday morning mostly getting takeout.  Large communal table, smaller tables and high counter seating at the window.   Service was ok.   No receipt offered.  This is in a huge shopping complex so parking during shopping season will be insane.  The fact that they are at the end of a row of stores helps with parking.

For the first time in my life a Starbucks barista actually asked me how to spell my name instead of writing something incorrect and acting indifferent (although it was always funny to see what they would put), and I appreciated the fact the gentleman took the time to ask me! I had a delicious frappacino - toasted white chocolate mocha - it was prepared quickly and was delicious! This location also has plenty of seating and lots of convenient parking.

This is by far my favorite location to goto. Just as every Starbucks serves the same menu, the baristas do a great job bringing me back to this location. Launie always makes the experience a great one for me. Keep up the great work because I will be back!

What is important to know about this Starbucks is it's just off I275 easy exit and easy return back on to the interstate. Important for travelers to know. Larger store size, lot's of outdoor seating, drive through and very busy most times. Other than that it's a Starbuck's nuf said.

I really liked this location up until today when they purposely gave me the wrong change back & lied about it! I was the ONLY car in the drive-thru (which neverrr happens here). My order cost $5.73 & I gave her a $10 bill + 73 cents...all she had to do was give me a $5 bill back as change! She gave me my drink as soon as I pulled up & handed her the money...all that was left to do was turn around & hand me the $5 change. Tell me why I had to sit at the window waiting for sooo long that eventually 3 cars had pulled in, ordered & were waiting for a while behind me before she gave me $3 + some coins. I tried to tell her the change was wrong & she kept ignoring me/pretending she didn't hear or see me, even though she was standing right at the open window doing nothing the entire time...so I waited. When she decided to acknowledge my presence again, I told her I got the wrong change back & it's supposed to be a $5 bill. She starts giving me some b.s. defensive speech & I got so fed up that I just drove off. If u want to steal an extra tip for yourself & are that desperate for $1-$2 then just take it & go buy yourself some nice chapstick at dollar general. Also let me just point out that I completely understand having to wait a while in lines because people are busy making orders or having a bad day, that's fine & when they apologize, I tell them it's no problem at all & not to worry about it. That being said, it was at least 5 minutes before the FIRST car behind me even pulled into the drive-thru and there were THREE cars who showed up & ordered before she even gave me any "change". She was 100% just being a petty lying thief on purpose!
Absolutely wonderful. 10/10. This is my go to Starbucks before work and I even drive out of my way on my days off because of the great service.

This place is always busy and packed but despite the busyness service is very fast and the employees are always nice and on point keeping things moving.   The couch towards the back of the store is very comfy too. My only complaint is the small parking.

The baristas here are quick and knowledgable. However, the parking lot is a nightmare and far too small for the popularity of the location. I've never stayed sitting in this's location, so I cannot comment on the ambiance.

I have given this particular Starbucks multiple tries and every time it's the same disappointment... Long waits, no parking and watered down awful coffee! I will not be returning to this location ever again. I completed a survey for that store and I would have given it negatives if possible!

Today is double star day!! That means much needed coffee. Luckily this Starbucks location was on near by, its my favorite location for respectful employees who get my order correct on the first try and speedy service!
Nice and cozy environment perfect for studying and just hanging out with friends and family. Food is amazing and the drinks are great. And the staff are very friendly too

Service was good but I ordered a tall latte and it tasted like a cup of warm milk.  There was only the slightest hint of coffee.  I know a latte has a lot of milk but I have ordered lattes at other Starbucks and have never had one this bad.  I updated my review from a 1 to a 3 because the staff fixed my drink without hesitation when I voiced my opinion of the drink.
The service is good. The coffee is great. And they donate to feed the hungry through Operation Food Search.  That deserves my loyalty.
For a new store they are always out of something!! Hate being stuck in a drive thru line waiting for nothing thus had happened 3 times now won't be back!

I give this location 1 star because the cashier was friendly. But otherwise my experience was sub par. I requested a dirty chai - but forgot to request almond milk so I immediately notified the barista before the milk was added - should have been an easy notification that I needed to pay extra, which I would have gladly done. Instead the barista acknowledged my request but still added regular milk (I thought it must have been someone else's drink), so I was shocked when she called my name. I asked her if my request had been too late - she asked her manager (Ms Raquina) who, without even acknowledging me, stated that I would have to get in line and pay for an entirely new drink!  No way am I doing that!  I don't normally complain about such small matters but the service was already slow which is not good for airport patrons. The time was around 5:30am, not sure if that should matter, but my recommendation is that a bit more time be put into customer service at this location. If possible - go to a Starbucks at a different gate!
Everyone at this location is really friendly. My coffees have always been spot on. This particular visit, tried the pumpkin spice cream cold brew. I loved it.

I have visited this store for years, but in the past few months have stopped around 7:30am for coffee. Each time i have waited over 20 minutes for my drinks.  As i watch the  staff stumble over fulfilling call ahead orders a big crowd is formed waiting for drinks.  I know its the morning rush but waiting 20 minutes for lattes is not acceptable. Plus my lattes smell burnt. Will not return to this starbucks in the morning.

This is the only coffee shop at Renown (spoiler alert) and the management here does not staff appropriately. I am a huge fan of Starbucks and my  complaint for this location is how long the lines are and how much time you spend waiting for your drinks. Oh.... and the staff is definitely not your typical Starbucks people. They don't smile often... or at all. It must be a "Renown" thing. Not a fan of this location at all!

This Starbucks has a first rate crew of baristas. Great Starbucks products delivered with a smile. I go out of my way to visit here.  I particularly like the tub chairs for comfort.  This store also has a consistent clientele of pleasant people. The Drive Thru often has a long line but very efficiently served.

They are very slow. They show no urgency when they are busy. Walked in to get our drinks on a Sunday evening, it's not outrageously busy but they are at least 5 groups here with us...should be taking 5 mins. This Starbucks takes at least 20 mins for drinks every time. It's just inconvenient & not a good experience.

The crew is awesome! Usually slammed (I mean, everyone needs coffee) but they get my coffee and name right every day that I go. Staff is by far the best part of this location. Nice and approachable, they get my daily fix of caffeine (which is worryingly high) right every time!

I'm not sure who Starbucks promoted as the new General Manager, but things are not being run correctly. The same crewmembers are still there- and still hardworking. Every day the line is wrapped past the front door and mobile orders take 15-20 minutes for customers to receive. Before COVID, I went to this location 5 time a week to order coffee and study. I spent about 10-15 hours a week in the lobby studying. Now because of restrictions, I just mobile order. The problem seems to be management. The store is not being run correctly. I enjoy this location and have had some great memories here. Hoping Starbucks corporate can fix said problems. Thank you.

While the staff here tend to be friendly, that doesn't make up for the excessively long waits and incorrect orders.  I've given them a chance at least a dozen times because it's the closest drive-thru to my home.  However, this place is the WORST when it comes to waiting.  And it doesn't seem to matter whether it's 2:30pm on a Saturday or 9am on a Monday - the wait is FOREVER.  And even after an order being completely inaccurate, I've never been offered a free beverage coupon like at most Starbucks locations.  We were in the drive-thru when there were only 2 cars in front of us.  It took 22 minutes for us to get ONE beverage! The car directly in front of us got so frustrated that she just pulled off when it was her turn to get her beverage -- and the staff didn't even notice!!! They tried to give us her order instead of ours!  On another trip there, I ordered a latte.  When I took a sip when I got home, I realized they had instead given me a black coffee! I drove all the way back to the store to get a latte and all I got was, "Sorry about that."  No coupon, nothing.  I've put in MULTIPLE complaints to Starbucks about this store but have seen little improvement.  I've been to a LOT of Starbucks stores with wonderful service - this is NOT one of them!  Unless you have 15-30 minutes to wait around for your drink, it would probably be faster to park and go into the Starbucks in the Target across the street.

This is easily the slowest service I have ever encountered at a Starbucks. It took 15 mins for our food order and they still forgot an item.  It took them another 5 mins. Best part...when checked, both orders were completely wrong. Only stop in if you are not in a rush and don't care what you get and want poor service.
This is to best place to get coffee and pastry and the employees are great especially erin she's the best goes above and beyond just a great experience

I come this Starbucks very often to do my computer work while I'm drinking afternoon pick me up.  I like dark roast coffee and I do not care for any other kinds.  Starbucks is well known for its strong taste coffee, but not this one.  They only have Pike Place and Blonde Roast.  There is a sign of Pour Over French Roast, but they tell me they don't have dark roast at all.   Because I need a coffee, I take Pike Place, but it's not my favorite.  Can someone bring Dark Roast Back???

nice décor but barristas have attitude they are superior to their clientele & didn't get right order; I requested 10 shots of expresso and given 4!  they did not allow me to pour the 1/2 & 1/2 & the manager takes out her emotions on her customers.  I was denied my birthday drink because I didn't have my card # with me; they don't answer phone! temperature too cold & employees gossip about me.  Not fun.

Carolyn was very rude the whole time , from the beginning when she took my order she was cold and full of bad energy , and even more rude after I asked for a refill; she was talking to me as I was trying to take advantage or something of the refill process.. I spent 30$$ the same morning on a coffee and a Starbucks speciality cup ! I don't appreciate the dirty looks as if I was trying to take advantage of the system when I've never had any issue getting refills within the same day. between me and my fiancé we spend over 2000 $$ a year just on Starbucks and this time around the employees were extremely rude to me and a friend, I regret giving you business!! From now on my fiancé and I will be taking our money to Dunkin' Donuts next door.

Oven not working for the little lunch pizza squares, no apples or bananas, crowded but half the floor full of delivery boxes blocking the inside line, nearly empty pastry case, one harried barista at the inside register. Will try again another day!

It's a Starbucks but on mass ave.   I've been a couple of times for meetings and it's super convenient. Although it looks small from the outside, it's pretty spacious.   On my last visit, the WiFi wasn't working which was a disappointment. Before that, there was one person working (making drinks and taking orders) so service was quite slow. Parking can also be tricky due to the location.
It is probably the worst Starbucks service in the city. Coffee is fine if they get it right. There are much better coffee places near by.

Possibly the nicest and most knowledgeable Starbucks staff I've ever encountered!  They made my coffee perfect they made what started as a bad day a very good one! We are so lucky to have them right on the beach it's nice for the people who live here and visitors alike! I will be back and recommend to all! Thanks guys!

I've never seen such inept Starbucks employees ever. Dear owners, buy your employees some markers or train them to use labels. They fd up both our drinks. I gave two stars only because they were nice when I asked if they'd fill up my water bottle.

Very clean and mobile orders are held instead of being out in the open. The employees were all upbeat.   Could use bit more light inside. Outside of that it's your standard Starbucks experience. One of the better ones in the area, in my opinion.

If I could give this Starbucks zero stars I would. Barista Heather was very rude and off-putting and Macy was like a deer in headlights and very confused. The training here is clearly subpar when compared to how well trained the staff is at the other Starbucks I frequent. It is unfortunate that this location has such poor quality employees.
A very nice young lady serviced me on 06/08/17, we spoke of Natalie's cakes. Very informational about company who makes them. Thank you for a delightful experience.   Thanks again,
Occasional long lines at peak times, but staff works hard to move customers thru. Staff always friendly despite being very busy.  I go here almost daily.

Drink was made fine, customer service was bad. The male cashier was nice, but when I went to grab my drink and say thank you the girl gave me a condescending look. When I asked where the straws were she told me the condiment bar not even looking up or pointing. I would probably come to this location more if you didn't have such rude female employees. Came here on 5/2/2017

On top of being a big coffee guy in general, having worked at Starbucks for several years makes me able to see all the good and the bad when it comes to a visit to Starbucks. I know that on any given day you could have a different experience at Starbucks, but this location in particular is always consistent on being great in. I often mobile order as it's perfect on my way to work, and my drink has never not been ready when I got there. I am always greeted at the door, and have really great customer service. I've noticed a trend in recent years of barista's being less friendly and not often greeting you... And until I was on the other side of the counter, I never realized how much better of an experience it is when you're acknowledged right away. There is a younger guy that works mornings, wish I knew his name, but he always is super friendly and on top of it. This locations consistency and drinks is always spot on as well, don't feel like my drink has been cut corners like some other locations. With so many Starbucks choices, I will always go out of my way to visit this one!
My favorite Starbucks in the county! Order always correct! Never a long line! Friendly employees! If I could only go to this location more often, I would!!

Horrible staff. Closes before 8pm. Refuses to serve guests. Don't even bother!  Countless number of people walking up to order and their door is locked and it's not even 8pm. Staff is trying to ignore customers!
I love this place:) the staff rocks and it is quick! By far my favorite Starbucks in the chain!!!!
Whoa! Definitely my favorite Starbucks. I drive out of my way just to see Mckenzie. She always greets me with a smile and most of the time remembers my name and what I like to order.

Got my coffee through the drive thru and when I pulled and got to the red light I went to drink it and realize it was only 3/4 full.. couldn't turn back.. when I tried to contact Starbucks, I got no response.. No more venti's from there for me .. great CS STARBUCKS !!! NOT

I want to give a shout-out to this location and the great people working today. I recently found out I am lactose intolerant and I am still learning to ask for milk alternatives. I forgot to ask for my drink with a milk alternative and they were super nice about it and helped me get one without milk. Extra points here!

Hey this place wasn't dirty like most of the Starbucks I've been to but the parking lot and drive thur looked like the maze in Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire. The women did give me the wrong milk, not trying to be like spoiled here just wish she would of asked. Again it's Starbucks the same it just the service and location that is the issue

Great Starbucks with great service. I've been here a few times after staying at GSR and I never had any problems. The drinks taste great and GSR sometimes has coupons where you can get 15 dollars in free food and it works at this starbucks.   Also right before closing, sometimes they offer their left over pastries for 50% off which is nice to snack on if you're hungry.

BLACK MOLD WARNING! DO NOT DRINK OR EAT HERE! I work in the airport. It has been confirmed by multiple workers that have been employed and hospitalized at this location that it has black mold everywhere due to improper cleaning. Stay far away from this Starbucks. They close every couple of months for "cleaning" but it never gets cleaned.

Just left Starbucks and they had 0 food items. No boxes, no sandwiches. They need to improve the inventory management here, causing missed sales and upset customers.  Hopefully management reviews this and can make changes.

This is a fairly new Starbucks location. I frequent it often as there is never a long line or fully occupied. The location fits perfectly in front of the Icon building of the Gulch. Parking is usually easy as you can park on the street directly in front of the location depending the time you visit. The location is very clean and the baristas are friendly and helpful.

You would think that this Starbucks would be awesome because they are usually busy, have great local and tourist customers, and there are several other locations in a 3 block radius. WRONG. This Starbucks is THE worst Starbucks I have EVER been to. I have worked on State Street for years and every time I give them a chance, they always let me down. I used to only drink Starbucks coffee, but this location has made me learn to love Coffee Bean, which is right across the street.  Here are the worst parts of the service they provide (all of these things happen 90% of the time, no joke): 1. They act like taking your order is a nuisance. They roll their eyes and sigh if you take longer than 2 seconds to order. 2. They take FOREVER to make your order. I'm not talking 3, 5, or 7 minutes. They literally take 20 mins to make you a drink. It is because they don't care. I really think they hate making coffee here. i have seen them, with a super long line, have 2 people taking orders, 1 person doing nothing, and 1 person making all drinks. They do not care if you have somewhere to be, if you are on a break, if they are busy, or anything else about your life. They move only at 1 speed: DGAF. 3. The reason I write my review: Today, I went to get a coffee on my break. I was too lazy to walk the few extra feet to a different Starbucks or CBTL. I always regret when I do this. I gave my order and endured the normal attitude and eye rolling that is expected. I waited for the baristas to continue their personal conversations with their friends seated at the bar. When my 10 minute iced coffee was finally ready, the girl did not even break eye contact, let alone talking, set my coffee out and THREW my straw at me. THREW it. like tossed it so that it fell to the edge of the table. She didn't say my name. She didn't say my order. The signal to grab my drink wasn't a smile or eye contact. It was a thrown straw. I left disgusted that I just paid $3.95 to be insulted!! I know that it's just coffee, but this day in age, when times are "tough", the one luxury we all have is to spend our money where we choose. When something is so prevalent and there are so many options, stores should not rest on the fact that people "need" what they have. Because the truth is, we don't. I gave this Starbucks 3 years and probably close to 100 visits to earn my business and today it was literally a straw that broke the camel's back.  DON"T GO HERE.

Let me just say that there were friendly, efficient people working this Starbucks the evening of September 2nd when we were stuck in the Indy Airport for hours due to a delayed flight. I was disappointed that they closed at 7pm, which felt extremely early for an airport location, but the overall experience was a very good one. Our order was received exactly as requested and even came with a smile from each of the gentlemen working the stand. We made a little small talk since they weren't busy and off my girls and I went to enjoy our tasty beverages! We got what we expected and a dash more.

Very crowded in the morning. They work hard to be fast and efficient though. The staff is very nice and friendly. Overall a solid starbucks. Also it helps that it is right by my work, so I frequent it often. Only thing that kind of sucks is that they run out sometimes of the breakfast wraps if I forgot breakfast that day.

Stumbled upon this location because we were trying to get our coffee fix, but mostly to seek shelter from the rain.  lol    The drinks are A-okay and the decor is awesome in here.  Lots of artsy things hung on the wall and from the ceiling, which is extremely different from the usual Starbucks decor.  Only thing really lacking is the customer service.  I asked for a water after ordering my coffee, and the worker just gave me a weird look.  Workers will do their job, but aren't the most friendly.  The world could use a little more smiling!

Nothing like going to starbucks and waiting 40 plus minutes for 2 cold brews to only see a 4th employee (army print crew neck) come out of the back and distract the other 3 workers (who were working hard).

Just had the WORST EXPERIENCE IN A STARBUCKS EVER!  We've been Gold card holders with Starbucks since 2010. Additionally we shop Starbucks daily. Multiple times Daily.   This location for a new location is one you should skip. There is a DD down the road.  I entered the cafe this evening intending to order our usuals in addition to some after dinner treats.  I approached the counter and was met by a very sweet young lady with red hair (i think). We began conversing on everything from the weather to Pumpkin spice goodness. I asked her when that becomes available etc. Immediately from behind her a woman ( who later I find out is the manager on duty Tasha) speaks up and rudely addressed me. Spoke over the employee as well as me.  She then walked away.   I was dumbfounded. Who treated her so badly that it was necessary to behave like this to a customer, disregard an employee and show so much disrespect. I was so floored I just walked out. I couldn't imagine spending a $1 in there. I will make it my duty to make sure no one I know even considers entering that store.  This is either someone who should be fired or perhaps training as a whole should be addressed for that location. If as a manager she can Behave this way who is holding the other staff accountable?   Are the drinks even made correctly? Are the dates of throw away followed? Are people charged properly for their drinks even? I mean with this type of employee at the helm on an evening shift I'd be scared to walk out of my business.

I normally wouldn't take the time to review a chain coffee shop that seems to have a location every ten feet, but my recent experience at this location left me with a smile on my face!  I stopped in on a Wednesday after dinner for a meeting and the barista (forgot to grab his name) was extra chipper and seemed to have a pep in his step. He provided great customer service and drink suggestions. I appreciated his sense of humor.  This Starbucks is more spacious than I thought, with a room separate from the barista counter to camp out on your laptop.  I can imagine this place gets busy during peak hours and it's Hunger Games style intensity to grab a table/outlet, though.  Parking isn't convenient, but it's Broad Ripple. If only it had a drive-thru...

Great location for picking up my morning pick-me-up on my way to work!  The baristas are always friendly, welcoming me with a smile (I can see it in their eyes since they have their masks on!), and a warm "good morning!"  My order is always correct, and they make my drink the best!  Thank you for consistent great service!
One of the worst Starbucks I've been to. I've had to wait 25-35 minutes for one drink i ordered on the app.  Slow service, rude staff.

It's Starbucks...so you know what to expect. Some locations are great...some not so great...and this happens to be one of the better ones. I generally hit the drive thru and they are very efficient - it gets BUSY here probably due to the location literally steps from the parkway. Yet, the line moves along quickly at any time I have visited. The baristas are friendly and I've never had a mess up on a drink here. One thing that I can say is awesome - more than once I have been the lucky recipient of a drink that the person ahead of me paid for. Even happened on a Monday one time which made that Monday so much better than usual. Clearly they have some awesome customers who visit here regularly. So whoever has bought my coffee here a couple of times - thank you! I repaid the favor onto the person behind me. We need more kindness in the world. And coffee. Pretty sure those things are related somehow :)
Love this Starbucks for my 6am weekend Venti White Chocolate Mocha  always a smiling face to greet you!

I'm a contrarian it seems with respect to my rating of this Starbucks. I was surprised to see a 2 star rating ... especially with my many experiences over the last few years. Yes, it gets busy with USF students camping at tables but generally easy to get a spot even at the communal table with power plugs. For those who complained about dirty tables, well if we all cleaned after we were done then ... just saying.   Today, the order was prepared quickly and exactly as ordered. Greetings and order processing. Solid. Very Berry Hibiscus with light ice and 2 berry scoops. Check. I had a previous instance they made the wrong order after someone got distracted when someone else was asking about it, but they took care of it right away.   Haven't tried the drive through but it always looks busy. But I'll take stepping in to enjoy my beverage of choice.

It's a pretty typical Starbucks.  Of course, I typically don't review major chains unless there is something worth highlighting.  This location has a few:  1) Yes, the Wi-Fi is free, but extremely slow.  It drops out on me quite a bit during my visits... still it is free.  2) The place is kept extremely clean.  Still, it is rather small and only has 3 outlets (2 by the bathrooms in the hallway and one in the other room).  Come with a full charge if you plan on staying a while.  Otherwise, think of every other Starbucks you've been too... yup, it is just like them.

This has got to be one of my favorite starbucks in Tucson. The staff is super friendly. Our order is always correct. It's the same as any other good starbucks except they are super friendly. It looks like young college students are working here and there is nothing wrong with that. If you are ever in this area, swing on by. You will not be disappointed.
If you've been to Starbucks before, you know the drill. The staff is friendly, and there's a decent amount of room to sit with friends, or by yourself.

I have been visiting this location about two times a week for about a month. The store is a good size with a drive-through. The costumer service is so-so. The purpose of my review is the absolutely disgusting restrooms. I've seem cleaner port-o-potty's. Every single time they are dirty, smelly, wet and unsanitary. There is no possible way they are cleaned regularly. I'll be going back to Cherry Hill where they clean the restrooms from now on.

They messed up my drink this morning, I called and they offered me a free one so I go back in just now ands it's even worse then before! I'm almost positive there's not any chai in here. It just tasted like milk. Very disappointed in my experience here today. Especially for spending $14 on 3 really poor drinks. Not sure I'll visit this location again.

This is my most regular Starbucks stop! Being across the street from my office really helps! (Doesn't help my wallet though...) The staff is very friendly, and I've never had an issue with the many mobile orders I put through on a regular basis!
