It's a small location so it can fill up fast if it's busy, but most customers are coming and going so there's usually a spot to sit. There's also a walk-up window outside if you're sitting in the shaded area next to the building. I haven't tried that yet, but it looks like a good spot for a meeting or just to hang out and enjoy people watching (perfect area for it). This location is also a "reserve" location so you can find specialty coffees here, and they have cold brew and nitro cold brew on top! I'm a fan.

Very pleasant experience this morning. I wish they had more inside seating/table space for working. The majority of tables are outside and being a hot and humid climate I value indoor working space. But, all in all the staff are extremely likable and the place is clean. Starbucks is one of my favorite places and I enjoy varying my Starbucks experiences.
I've gone here on multiple occasions. I order a hot drink and it's luke warm. I order a coldbrew and it's barely cold. This problem has been happening consistently. Staff is friendly though ..
I think this location is better off for not having a drive thru. The inside is very echoey, so if there are a decent number of people inside it's not a good place to have a conversation.

This is probably the worst starbucks I have ever visited in this ride creatively dubbed "Life". As I crossed the proverbial picket lines to enter this humble establishment for which I had preordered a drink online, which worked like a charm unlike the mysterious object aunt bertha picked up in India. However when I began to wait for my beverage another story began to unfold. This was so dirty and disgusting place I have ever been, and I took my daughter to a rap concert! I decided to photograph this event to show the world. Thank you for time

The local Starbucks in Montecito, located on the commercial street of Coast Village Road. My wife likes to have coffee first thing in the morning, so I'll get into my car and drive from the Four Seasons, where we are staying and just 5 minutes away. This is a fairly large location, serving the locals. Lot's of foot traffic, but the 6 baristas behind the counter are super efficient moving the line, with professionalism and a smile. There is a huge parking lot, so it's really easy to get in and out!

I ordered today a caramel macchiato. The worst in my life! No coffee,  no caramel just milk. They where lucky that i was with my husband and i didn't go back to make them taste what they sell. Take a taste before you left, just in case they gave you something else. This store have a problem. Every time the coffee is different. Especially caramel macchiato. Train your barista Starbucks!!!

This Starbucks is in violation of Philadelphia's law regarding gender signage on single occupancy restrooms.  I visited today and noticed gender signs on the restrooms.  I spoke with a manager, a young woman, and said they needed to come down in compliance with the law.  I was told "We tried that, it didn't work." I was told that when they put up unisex signage, men went inside and then left the restroom doors unlocked to be perverts.  I pointed out that this was unfortunate but it did not change the laws.  I was then told that upper management had made the decision and nothing could be done.  I pointed out that my only recourse then would be to file a complaint with L&I.  The response I got was "Hey buddy, do what you gotta do."  I might also point out that the two restrooms have DIFFERENT external lock codes on them, which means that it is up to the employees to attempt to ascertain the gender of the customer and then give them a code pertaining to the gender they've assumed.  NOT acceptable.

Nothing better on a hot day than your fave beverage - unless the staff gets it wrong and you realize that they don't know their products.  I ordered a Venti iced Earl Grey tea (I repeated this a few times as the screen said "BlkTea") with heavy cream and sugar free vanilla syrup.  I know it takes a few minutes for the Earl Grey to steep, so when it was handed to me nearly immediately, I asked when they started keeping iced Earl Grey on hand.  The girl working the window said "Uh, I don't know...?" and the barista who had made the drink said "we always have our iced black tea - it's an English Breakfast blend".  I responded that I didn't order black tea, I ordered Earl Grey, and the girl with the headset said "I thought Earl Grey WAS black tea". I responded that though they're both made with black tea, Earl Grey has bergamot and other flavors and is a different tea.  Instead of offering to remake my drink correctly, and since I had already paid, she just smiled and said "sorry, I thought it was the same thing." I had a guest in my car and a line behind me so I left, but I am disappointed that the staff don't know their beverages and I was not offered the correct beverage OR a refund - I was just smiled at like "have a nice day with your wrong drink!"

I'm always one that loves to sit at Starbucks and sip some coffee while studying. The wifi is good, the baristas are friendly, and plenty of outlets! Good seating however is difficult to get sometimes. Seating options are limited and I prefer the individual seats rather than larger shared tables. There is also window seating but the table is small and awkward to do work on. I like to spread out my materials and get to work so individual seating is important for me. Luckily I'm often able to get my preferred seats.  Another thing, this Starbucks is freezing! Yes I know Starbucks everywhere are always on the cooler side but this one in particular is the worst. I've come in during winter and had to keep on all my winter layers in order to stay warm!  Parking is limited! I fortunately live close enough to walk so this isn't an issue for me but during peak hours I know it's extremely difficult to find nearby parking.
This location is very understaffed.  Huge lines even before 7am.  Staffing issues getting worse

The best thing about this Starbucks on Main Street, Doylestown, is the front elevation from the street. It occupies a historic building that dominates a key intersection in the Borough.  Very attractive.   Sadly, that is where the positives end.  Inside, the layout is dreary. Dark on the brightest days, it feels like a morose ghost inhabits it, making for a sense of weariness. The coffee at all Star$$$ is universally bad, and it is no different here. It forces you to upgrade to an expensive alternative to regular coffee, a perverse upsell that seems to work everywhere. The pleasantness of the staff is helpful, but cannot overcome the down energy that is ever present.   Many better choices in town, perhaps why the shop was not overly busy while life on the streets around it was jumping.

The people who work at this Starbucks are literally the nicest people I've encountered. I walked in a probably five employees said hello to me and two of them struck up a conversation. Also they made my drink quickly and without any errors. I really don't normally review Starbucks because they are all the same but I was struck by how friendly everyone here was!!

Best Starbucks in New Orleans! I come here frequently, and not only do the staff members remember my name, they remember my drink order. (I'm boring and get the same thing every time) They are also the only shop in New Orleans that doesn't burn their espresso either.

Does this say ice anywhere? This is the 4th time! Jesus! Or if it's not this sometimes I ask for soy since I'm Lactose they end up putting regular milk and they act like they put soy but u can taste a different. So disappointed in this place now. They need the old barista back!
This store has not been open on time for two months. They either call off or show up super late to open the store. This store is located in the Marriott hotel!

I absolutely love this place. I have been here for almost 5 years and I have never had a bad experience. Everyone is friendly helpful and always on the move. They are very efficient and fast in their service of customers. I have never had a drink made incorrectly and they are always willing to honor my weird requests for drinks. I am there so often they only ask hot or cold which is a blessing because half the time I'm sleepwalking there. I give my whole hearted recommendation.
Absolutely the best place around!!  The customer service is amazing. Only had one bad server, but for the majority of the time EXCELLENT SERVICE.

My local Starbucks. Great staff, very friendly. The store is a bit small if you plan to hang out and do some work / have a meeting, etc. But, they have made the best out of the space. It is also right next door to the best doughnuts in town (Nicola's), so grab a Starbucks and some Nicola's doughnuts and you are SET!

for as busy as these folks are they have great poise & attitude while they grind and serve away to tourists, locals, employees and outside panhandlers   sure its sbux and the rep that comes with it but philz, sightglass or blue bottle is over 200 miles away.   if hub was closer for me there'd be one less review.   the weekends are a mess so come earlier or later  the inside of this smaller franchised space is gorgeous with a tip of the cap to the construction mgmt team.  i like the pictures of their humble beginnings storefront on pike place meets virginia street and the ceiling resembles the fox theater in oakland a bit  daily drip seems to have blonde, dragon and pike in the rotation as one is also able to get their la boulange fix
Hate to give a 1-star, but the response time at this Starbucks is the worst I've seen. I was staying at the Westin across the street and dreaded going each morning. Needs to be remedied.

This location is very small but they are quick to get the drink orders done. Every now and again parking can be a pain in the butt because there are quite a few businesses in there. They've got a decent sized outside seating area for the size of the location.

As many in the yelp community know, I am not a big fan of Charbucks (aka fourbucks) BUT- and this location deserved the big but... considering that this shopping area (near the Target on City Ave.) is the only walkable place near my new office, I come here for an occaisional Ice Green Tea Lemonade.  This Starbucks is clean and (aside from morning rush) not crowded.  A pleasant surprise.  My goodness - I may start (gasp) liking Starbucks again.
Great Starbucks, very typical. Really nice staff though. Very kind, very quick. Starbucks is always a great escape that's comfortable and familiar where ever you are.

It is the worst Starbucks I have ever been to, they always lack something, cream, caramel, the flavor, it is terrible that they never have anything to give you the complete order. I just moved to this area and it is the only one nearby with a drive thru...for me I didn't give it even 1 star

It's Starbucks, so I won't go into the menu. This Starbucks is great. Great customer service, they do their best to call you by name and thank you personally. There's multiple tables for those looking for a place to get work/studying done. There's no drive through but with mobile ordering, that doesn't bother me one bit. Love this quaint little location!

Sidenote: The best cup of coffee I've had in CWE is at Pi Pizza.   I think Starbucks excels at consistency of product. This location is no different in that the coffee is the same taste as any place else. That said, this is a great location to grab a quick cup and go. ...however, parking is tricky near Maryland and Euclid so this is easier said than done.   I rate this as 2 out of 5 stars comparing it to other Starbucks shops in the area. It is small and cramped and has one unisex bathroom which creates waiting for the bathroom an expectation. On a positive note, the bathroom has always been clean. Maybe the guy in front of you feels obligated not to be a sloppy mess knowing a cute girl might be waiting for her turn.   The business itself does really well given the traffic in CWE and their Apple -esque ability to make people feel special while holding the logo on their cup (myself included as I type away at my Macbook writing this review...).   A coffee shop as a whole and I might give it 3 or 4 stars. The staff is great (as great as all Starbucks staff) and you know what you are getting when you walk in the door. But when it comes down to going to a Starbucks, I'd rather go to one elsewhere.

This Starbucks has one of the longest lines with regularity that you will ever see. Plus it's a small Starbucks with a limited menu. I would only give a full-sized Starbucks 3 stars because their coffee tastes burnt and their prices are ludicrous. I know some people are obsessed with Starbucks but I've never understood paying more for worse. I far prefer Dunkin Donuts or Tim Horton's for quick and basic coffee, and if you want good coffee,you need to order online and have a good burr grinder and a Chemex or French Press.

I had a friend go to the Starbucks location to get a Venti iced latte for me. They were out of the Venti iced cups and my friend asked if they could do 2 grande for the price of the Venti. They would not and did not accommodate for their ordering or supply issues. I usually do not get Starbucks but today called for one. They were also rude.  Starbucks please understand that just a simple gesture will go along way.

Why are so many people so critical?  It's coffee and pastries.    Came here on Thanksgiving, and it was super packed.  Staff was super friendly, especially working on a holiday. Came to get coffee for my fiance who also got stuck working on a holiday.  Unfortunately only big box retailers get on the news about their employees working on holidays.  Other big companies are forced to cater to consumer demand and those employees should get some recognition too.  Short and sweet review. If you read this today, Thanksgiving, be nice.  You could've stayed home and made your own coffee and breakfast. These guys are here making your mid-week holiday easy.

After voting, I stopped by here when I realized I had just missed the bus.   For a mid morning, the place was filled with students and business folks and the general public.  Lots of table setups for laptop warriors in the back. A few couches and more comfortable seating up front.  The aisles of product for sale were sort of narrow, compared to the other locations I've been to. They were really cumbersome to pass between and next to people in line as I tried to get to the napkins/sugar/condiments in the middle.  Workers were quick. I didn't expect the woman doing inventory of the pastries to acknowledge me, since I don't care about that type of thing. She was too focused on her job to acknowledge me. The woman taking my order was quick, but I didn't get a friendly vibe from her, either.  Unfortunately, my pastry was stale. And I was too busy catching the next bus to do anything about it. I'm glad I don't drive, as the woman trying to get out of the parking lot took several long minutes before she found it safe to do so.

Probably the one I frequent the most. No complaints really. The staff, at the moment,  is competent. Always clean. Wish they wouldn't compulsively toss the newspaper so there would be something to read.
Grabbed a peppermint mocha and peppermint mocha Frappuccino while shopping on Mass Ave. As always it was delicious, the staff were very friendly, and we had a nice break from the cold.

This new, larger drive thru Starbucks is absolutely disappointing compared to the smaller one right next door that it replaced. I went to the previous smaller location for 10+ years.   The staff at the new "improved" Starbucks are sweet, but highly inexperienced. It's a bit of an issue when you're paying $7 once or twice a day for a beverage and it is not hot enough or not cold enough. They do not understand how to use their $12,000 espresso machine properly. They don't time their shots. They have no sense of how humidity affects the grind/tamp.  I'm not going to harass the 18 year old barista to re-make a drink three times when they clearly haven't received adequate training. I have thrown my drinks away the last two times I went because they were not enjoyable at all. I plan not to go back even though I live and work within a three mile radius of this Starbucks.  I know to some, it may sound like I am being some kind of coffee elitist, but this is not Dunkin' Donuts. Starbucks have built their reputation on making coffee and espresso drinks and they rightfully charge more money for their product. But, if they do not know how to craft and serve their product, there is no point.  Clearly, they aspire to be the Arby's of the coffee world because they do not train their workers on the basic chemistry of building drinks. For this quality of beverage, I find the Dunkin' Donuts much better. The staff there is well trained in what they do. It's quite sad when the Dunkin' Donuts can get their act together, but a Starbuck's can't. Sad.

Wow!! Savannah, what a joy it was to see the tag with your full face and magnificent smile!!  Grazie mille for an amaaaaaaaazing iced espresso made perfectly in response to my request, and even more so for your amazing smile and personality! Bravissima!!
These gals make my morning!  They are always incredibly friendly, quick and consistent (even with the new faces from the Broadway location)!! Thanks!

This place is always there for me when I'm at the hospital for my son.   I used to work for Starbucks and in the morning that I would have to leave the hospital to work, I would stop here. They would always give me my coffee for free.   Friendly staff, nice place to sit and bring a bite of food.
We went through the drive thru for a Sweet Italian Cold Brew. It took way to long to get through the line. The coffee was fair, will try a different Starbucks when in the area.

There are two Starbucks in Terminal 1. This one is for the one in the C Concourse/Southwest Airlines side. Not sure why this one always turns out so much more sweeter - in a bad way. I always get Starbucks when I fly and this one is consistently bad. The other one on the Delta side does a fairly good job. Unless you are short on time, I'd recommend going to the one on the Delta side (on the right once you get through security).

I give this Starbucks points for location. It is conveniently located right next to Cameron library. I am not a Tim Horton's girl, unlike the majority of U of A students (as evidenced by the two Tim Hortons located steps from each other in CAB), so I'm glad that this Starbucks exists to meet my growing caffeine needs.   If you, like me, are one of those people who ridiculously customizes your drinks, then be prepared for the possibility that the staff will either not understand what you are asking for or make your drink incorrectly. It doesn't always happen, but it happens often enough that it should be noted.   It would be nice if they had a better seating area so that you could meet up with friends for a coffee break. This is definitely a grab-and-go place. The use of the debit machine incurs a $0.35 fee and there is no mobile app scanner so they must enter your card number manually.

This location needs some serious retraining. Five baristas behind the counter, one making drinks. The other 4 yammering amoung themselves, quite loudly, how they didn't want to work the counter, another one complaining they didn't want to be there at all.  Never had that bad an experience in a Starbucks. Waited 15 minutes for a coffee while the line of people just grew longer. Plus this one doesn't even have a drive in window. The last thing a customer wants to hear is employees complaining. My drink was wrong and no, I didn't go get it fixed.

I've never been a Starbucks fan but had to settle for it this morning. I waited in line for 10 minutes to place my order, placed it paid for it then waited for another 15 minutes to be made. When I saw people who were in line behind me getting their order I asked about it and was told they're out of the skinny mocha.   This is horrible customer service!

I love this Starbucks!! It was well overdue for the area. The atmosphere is welcoming, love the designing, and the decor. The employees are very friendly, professional and helpful. I was disappointed to find the fireplace not working. It has been a month and  the fireplace is still not WORKING!!! Disappointing especially the days I really would love to relax in the lounge. Hopefully the owners are working on this issue.

For the past few weeks I've been ordering a (triple shot) venti chestnut praline latte, nearly every day at this (my favorite and closest) Starbucks.  The past few days, the latte has been less than 2/3 full. I know that whipped cream melts almost immediately in hot coffee, and I expect a little space, but this is STUPID.   I'm spending nearly $6.50 on one of these and I'm getting ripped off.  I'm fa la la la la la la la sick of this.
New Starbucks with a drive thru.  Busy place, cool hipster  types working the window doing the safe distancing thing.  Great coffee as always.

I love Starbucks but this location today just disappointed me completely. This location is always busy but they are usually fast. Today was horrible. It took me 25 minutes to get my drink via drive thru. After 25 minutes of waiting the danish they game me was not warmed up. I still had to wait another minute for them to warm it up. I get to work only to notice my coffee (size - grande) was only filled half way. Completely disappointed. I noticed the car I front of me also had to return a pastry. I am not sure what happened but judging from my experience they must have messed up their order as well.

I've fallen in love with the mobile ordering feature of the Starbucks app, and this is the store closest to my office, so when I'm driving in and I'm feeling the need for coffee and a pastry, this is the place that gets my order.   Unlike the location a mile north on 86th street, this place is fairly quiet ad subdued. There's no drive through, so if you want to get something, you've got to park and walk in and get it yourself. I'm okay with this... it means it much quieter than the others. The staff sets my mobile order on the counter so I can walk in, say hello, grab my order and be on my way before the my headlights have a chance to shut themselves off. They've learned that I don't like my pastry warmed up, which isn't an option to request on the app, but they know when y order comes through how I like it, and they have it ready. Love this location!

Starbucks is always good. I love the oatmeal and passion tango ice tea. My boyfriend had a coffee frap he liked very much!  The only reason I'm giving 3 stars to this Starbucks is because I looked all over for a recycling bin to recycle my cups etc and it was no where to be found. There was a whole story on the wall about how Starbucks is trying to be green etc. but there was no recycling bin?!  They definitely need a recycling bin and a compost bin!  Then they will get five stars!!

I love the stained glass ceiling at this Starbucks! It's located inside the Silver Legacy Casino and it looks out on the gaming floor. I wonder what used to be in its place before Starbucks.  There aren't many seats, but most people just grab their coffee to-go anyway. I went here with my mom for a quick pick-me-up in the afternoon. Nice to just sit and relax.

Friendly staff, but when we got our coffee, we both noticed that the cups were not nearly filled up. I asked for my latte with no foam and I got a latte with foam. I stirred at it for a while and when the foam cleared I had a cup of coffee filled to about 3/5ths of the way. My boyfriend's was the same. Here are pics of what I'm talking about. First one is mine, second is his.  Neither of us had taken a single sip of coffee yet when I took the pictures. I feel like I'm owed a dollar back on the coffee that did not fill this tall cup. :(
Honestly like yeah the lines are long but everyone is super nice. I mean it's a Starbucks on a college campus not sure what everyone is expecting.

It is Starbucks. Not much more to add to that. The space is familiar and the staff is quick here, which is nice. Since it is cookie cutter coffee, I say check out the mobile ap so you can order while parking (or sooner) and just roll in to grab your octane juice and leave. No reason to stick around here.
Basic, run-of-the-mill Starbucks. Have had issues with mobile ordering. Either the order was not received or it's been sitting behind the bar and I get a cold drink.

This place is AMAZING w Starbucks new mobile order option. They always have it ready in about five minutes. I give it 4 stars because As far as a study spot, I don't feel like this is a great place. There are not a lot of seating options and they are very close together. On occasion when trying to study here at had to leave because the music was a little too loud and I can hear it over my headphones.
This is the best Starbucks around with friendly staff and great barista skills. They always make my coffee just how I like it!

Horrible experience at this Starbucks. I came in during a time when they were carting inventory in from a truck, but the space to do that was in between where the line starts.  I was in line to make my order when the employee harshly demanded that I move out of the way. I would have been fine if he had politely told me the situation, but he literally hollered at me from behind the counter and was walking around multiple times (cleaning counters? Replacing cups?) before even taking my order. Really? It was a slow evening which is why I expected it to not take too long to receive my drink. Took a total of 2 minutes being hollered at and being told to move away while being stared at, 5 minutes just to place my order and 3 minutes for my drink to be ready.   The lag time because this employee was slow and rude was just not worth it.
Just ordered online, paid for it get to drive through wait to get to window and only then get informed they're out of it. Totally defeats the reason for ordering online. Big waste of time.

I am a really big fan of this Starbucks location. The staff is friendly, efficient, a little quirky, and they are already starting to remember my order (despite my only becoming a semi-regular about a week ago).   It's also one of the biggest, most wide-open Starbuck's I've ever been to, which makes me very happy. I'm used to going to Starbucks and struggling to get a spot at one of the four available tables. This location however, has something like 12 round tables (seating two to four people) , 10 large lounge chairs, a bar, a long table with lamps and several side tables. And there appears to always be available seating.   If you're the kind of person who enjoys doing work or reading in a location outside of your office or home, this is a great place to go.

Gave me a regular medium roast coffee when I told them twice that I wanted a medium roast ICED coffee. Also, the cup was filled to the brim and leaked out underneath the lid. Just another reason why I'll pick Wawa or Dunkin over Starbucks 10 times out of 10.
Really fast service and super nice people. I spilled my tea right after I got it, and they replaced it immediately for free.

I am currently at the Starbucks in Willow Grove mall - my idea is include more customer service courses for the team members or quality control of the team member. I was served by a team member who was an older woman with red hair she was wearing a purple Starbucks shirt and did not display a name tag. This employee did not greet me as the customer she held a conversation with another team member during my order and did not thank me for the business or say have great day. A TERRIBLE EXPERIENCE

I use to love coming to this Starbucks. Always had fast and friendly staff, who would go above and beyond most companies customer service satisfaction.  However for the past few months service here had gone down hill.  The last three times i was here my order was made wrong.  I than had to wait another 20 min for my correct order.  The drive thru is even worse.  Expect to be sitting in line for a min of 30-45 min, just to be given the wrong order.   The only reason why I still come to this Starbucks is because of Danny!  He always greats every guest by name and memories your order for you.   I'm sad to say if service doesn't get better soon I will be going else where.
They are fast and my order is never wrong. Very nice clean place to sit and eat or have your coffee there employees are very friendly.

The staff here is so incompetent. It takes 20 minutes for them to ring up a coffee. It's really bad. They need training desperately. A rest stop is supposed to be quick...not a 30 minute sit down ...they are losing money because people just leave the line. I watched 4 adults who work here just stare at the computer screen like Zoolander looking for files in the computer. It was unbelievable. And they did not get better with every customer..they got worse. Yikes

If you are coming to work on your computer, don't bother. There is very limited seating and it isn't very accommodating. Would only recommend this place for sipping on something while you shop at Safeway or to take back to your car.

Starbucks is what it is, and quite frankly, i like what it is. Having got my caffeine fix at Starbucks's all over the world, i can generally say that across the globe the service and quality has been reliable at best. And then there's this location. This is like the rebel Starbucks that must be led by a renegade manager who decided to just buck the system and deliver a poor experience across the board, for the sole purpose of delivering a poor experience. Getting any of the staff to actually smile or be pleasant is a certain impossibility.  I've found this location to be generally dirty and unkempt, and there are these odd little flies buzzing around all over the place.  I've literally stood there like an idiot while two barista's just chatted away, completely ignoring my simple order of just a venti Coffee.  Yes, i just drink black coffee. Venti. Nearly impossible to screw it up. And yes, they screw it up every time. If there wasn't a Bank of America in the same lot as this Starbucks i'd never step foot in there. I suspect the staff and management are just in a constant stupor because of the unbelievable volume they do with a drive thru.  But God forbid you step foot inside.  As my visiting niece said so eloquently "Wow, this Starbucks location blows"....Well said hipster niece.  Well said.

I love Starbucks, but after years of going to this Starbucks and it never getting better, I am finally writing a review. This is the slowest Starbucks in the U.S. It takes 30 mins sometimes to get through the drive through with only 4-6 cars and every time I go I hope this time will finally be better and it never is. They need new management or corporate to come in and overhaul.

I have been to this particular location 3 times in the last 3 weeks.  The service is usually slow and not terribly friendly but today was the worst yet.  The girl at the window never even considered smiling, she never spoke even when my order was wrong, and we didn't get napkins  Even though this is now the most convenient location, I will not go back.  Have to find someplace new.  I am a Starbucks Gold level member.

The worst Starbucks that I've ever been to. Who ever was working Sunday morning (1/2/22) at about 9AM is the most unprofessional person ever! I start giving her my order, a total of 4 drinks, and every time I would add a drink she would make a face. I repeated myself a few times because she had her AirPods in and I could hear her music, that's how loud it was. For a Starbucks, having an employee that doesn't care about their job like that is very disappointing.

Here on business, and I needed a caffeine fix this morning ... STAT!  Glad to see this Starbucks was open at 5:30am, and I was clearly the first person in the shop today.  I was nicely greeted by Gail, who proceeded to share some really cool new techniques with their Clover Coffee machine.  She suggested I get the 100% Kona Clover - she was absolutely right, it is delicious!  It's smooth, not bitter at all- and she was so enthusiastic and knowledgeable!  Thank you for my java fix Gail - you made my morning!

Trash days are Monday Wednesday and Friday. The first day I came I was not able to get into the drive-thru, I was determined to find another morning routine so I waited and found out their trash days. You want to come for their customer service and stay for their food!

The baristas are bunch beauties. Drinks are always made correctly and make me feel as good as some cocaine on a Monday morning. Sounds depressing? Actually it's the opposite. Me and a couple boys last week did a full send and nailed some broads. Woke up Monday morning did the white and it made me feel like I was born in a new light.
Drive thru usually pretty congested & slow but staff is ALWAYS friendly:). Buy one get one free salted caramel mochas today!

Slowest. Starbucks. Ever.  I have sat in the drive thru for twenty minutes.  The parking lot is not convenient.  You are basically trapped once in line.  If you go in its almost impossible to get out.

I guess it was ok, I am just use to the staff at my usual SB's remembering me. Spoiled yes. The staff was nice, and the coffee exceptional.  I paid with my iPhone app.  That's always fun. I will go back to my usual Starbucks from now on.

This Starbucks is always slow. Long drive through line that crawls and even in the store the staff is slow and unmotivated.  I have been at the drive thru window and heard them talking rudely about customers and staff. They seem super catty and not motivated by customer service at all.

Love this location!  Though connected to the Marathon gas station, store has ample parking and is well maintained.  Some outdoor seating for warm days.  Service is always above and beyond!  Other Starbuck's locations are within a few miles but I always choose this one because not as crowded, quick turn around on orders and very clean on the interior.  Bakery items always fresh.  Baristas go out of their way to make you feel welcome and serve you exactly what you want.    Mobile ordering and drive-thru both at this location.  Easy access to interstate.  Great free WiFi.

Absolutely hate Starbucks policy of not serving brewed de-cafe after 12 noon.  Makes no sense to me... Coffee is their product, I am the customer and customer is always right.   Otherwise, great products, good service

Not my favorite venue, but was in a hurry and needed a caffeine fix. 1 Starbucks, 2 Starbucks, I never see any differences except, location & service, even the staff seems the same. Expensive, but good coffee. What's a girl to do? Caffeine fixes can reach critical levels!

This is one the most spacious Starbucks in Tampa.  A long seating along the wall with personal tables, couple high tables, round table and seating - conversation area invites to work and relax.   The corner windows are great, provides a lot light to the store.  The parking lot looks small but actually work very well with the crowd.  Along the busy Dale Mabry  Hwy is a very convenient place!

If I could give more than five stars I would. Going to Starbucks is a morning ritual for our Suburban family. They make the best iced coffee in town! Hands down. It's perfect every time! They know us by name and always conversed with us as soon as we walk through the door. The manager joe is one of the most caring and friendliest managers I have ever met in my entire life! Really! Go there.

My daughter and I stopped at this location on our way out of town, I'm so glad we did. It's a comfortable atmosphere, there's no overwhelming loud soud of hipster music and the employees aren't pushy. They got our order right and was extremely efficient. Now let's talk about the restroom .... Very clean and gorgeous skylights!
Are you kidding me? I ordered large, look this cup, cup is large, but they offer medium size.   This is half cup of the coffee! Seriously Starbucks?

I like this Starbucks sometimes and then others I don't. There are good days and bad, but there have been problems sometimes with orders and sometimes the customer service isn't up to par with the others that I have been to. A few times my girlfriend was given and attitude and one of the cashiers rolled their eyes at her with her snotty attitude. I haven't had many problems I stopped in today and the barista, who was a older gentleman, made my drink was very friendly and is always offering other items to go with my drink. My drinks were always made well and I never had a problem, I know some people have had problems with yeh cashiers and I can't say personally I have had a problem. Good place to stop and grab a coffee, certain people make drinks good and others have made them not so well, give them a shot, love to read reviews!

With COVID-19, you can only drive through or mobile order. No standing inside. That said, there is always a line at this Starbucks in the morning. They always move the line along quickly; never making the whole process take more than a few minutes. I typically get my coffee customized and they have yet to get it wrong.

Wonderful Starbucks location. The staff is great and always friendly. I mostly go through the drive through and the employees are always sweet and funny. My favorite was when I asked if they made a different type of drink and the response was "heck ya, and it's super yummie" this response made my son and I giggle. Nice job!

Sure it's a global chain of cookie cutter coffee shops, but this one is clear proof that not all are created equal. My mochas are always a little off, the workers seem a little surlier than others, and the place is often packed thanks to it's awesome location leaving long lines. Totally skip this place!!  Surprisingly (hint the massive sarcasm) there's a starbucks a mere block away on Chestnut. Clean, friendlier, tastier drinks, nicer seating. This is the place to go..

An open letter, redux;       All of your great employees are gone or on a VERY limited amount of shifts. You are now, officially, a complete and utter epic fail across the board. Most of my co-workers have moved on to other locations and I'm trying to get some help from my GPS to do the same w/o compromising my AM commute time.  My hats off to your legendary ability to suck in such a well rounded way.  fin.

At the best of times I am not a Starbucks fan but I go with benefit of the doubt and rate against other Starbucks. The location is ideal but the service, variety & attitude of staff is ridiculously bad. They really need to employ actual baristas with the wait time and queues that happen here. I was queuing for over 20 mins to get my coffee (standing) and they didn't have half and half which was what I requested and tried to faux make it and it tasted terrible. The staff lied to me and said it was half and half even though I watched them pour something different (and incorrectly - I suspect none of them work for a creamery). I know this seems picky but this is my 4th and last experience here as none have been positive and mediocre is the best it has achieved but generally disappointing.

This Starbucks has improved a lot since it opened. It is a very busy location but they handle it very well. Very convenient for us but we still miss the crew at the Chadds Ford location by Whole Foods. It is consistent with every other Starbucks!! Very friendly staff!
The worst Starbucks, our drinks are never right from here and the place is always a disaster. It's in a great location so I wish it was good.

Starbucks is known for its flavorful blends and deep roasts- so the flavor and quality is always consistent. This store is set apart by the great staff and their amazing ability to work together! I picked up a quick latè on my way to the Dry Bar and from start of order to drink delivery I watched a seamless dance of the baristas who were very busy making magic behind the coffee bar!  My drink was great (as always expected) but the staff was great!

The staff was very friendly.  We stopped on our way to Busch Gardens for some snacks and drinks.  As expected, the drinks were quickly made, and there were plenty of snacks to choose from.  The bathrooms were also very clean, which can be hit or miss so this was a plus.   Overall, it's an easy in and out for a quick stop.

Coffee (of many variations), Tea, Hot Chocolate, cakes, lunches, snacks & gifts... pretty much what is at all Starbucks.  Not a big coffee drinker myself, I frequently stop by with my daughter.  And if given an option at hotels, I'd stop by for Starbucks coffee for my "daily cup of Jo."  I do, however, love their lemon pound cake... not gooey, goes well with that breakfast coffee when you don't want yet another egg sandwich.  They also have a few interesting lunch options.  Will probably stop at Starbucks wherever, whenever... good coffee, even for a one cup a day person.
Come on guys! Train your employees on how to redeem earned rewards. Other than that I love this location. Spacious. Great corner

This is a smallish Starbuck's compared to some in town but it is my local one and is perfect for the slightly out-of-the way, family oriented, slightly rural feeling Mesa neighborhood of Santa Barbara. There are a few chairs and small tables on the "pet friendly" front patio that is in an alcove sheltered from the weather. The atmosphere inside is cozy on a cold day and, being near SB city college, is often quiet and studious but is friendly and comfortable. There are two (quite well maintained) bathrooms which is unusual for a small coffeehouse, and the staff is quite efficient and friendly though the place does occaisionally get very busy in the morning rush.  This is a "big chain" coffee with a distinctly friendly, local ambience!
The pumpkin spice latte is back! woohoo!  The only reason I ever go to Starbucks.    This bread called the Lemon Loaf is also one of the greatest things ever.

Staff is always very nice here. Got out and got me a strawberry Açaí refresher which was suppppppper delicious and it was based off a recommendation from the staff! Sooo tasty! I needed a little pick me up but didn't want coffee so that was a nice change. This place is always super busy and they have a drive thru which is great! Especially if ya have kids and you need that treat!
Redid my drink for me when they accidentally messed up and gave me the other for free!

Very nice Starbucks located on the second floor of the hospital. Good signs once you get inside to the Starbucks. Don't be intimidated by the hospital Ambience, once you get to the second floor and find the Starbucks you'll be very happy. Large, clean, bright, friendly with plenty of seating both inside and outside the store.
Coffee good but I make better expresso. Their cappuccino is excellent but I find everything there to be kinda expensive.

I like.that they have added some things to their menu .The slow roasted ham and Swiss has.become my new favorite. Catherine the manager is so sweet and always on top of things. I usually get the same thing but from time to time i switch it up and recently tried the BlackBerry Mojito iced tea , boy was it refreshing . I'd also like to give special nod to Avery he's always so friendly and goes out of his way to be helpful. Great job dude :-)

I have gone to this Starbucks off and off.  I avoid it because of the uneven service that i receive.   Some , perhaps most, of the barista are excellent...many not as much.   Recently,  I dealt with an abrasive barista named Theresa.   What makes it worse is that I heard that she is a barista that had been there for many years.  She acts as though she is doing you a favor by taking your order.  She also seemed to feel that a partner that I was with was asking for free coffee..which was clearly not the case as he had a reward on his card that he earned from purchases.  This has been a consistent problem at this store.  Because they have a large homeless population at this store,  they tend to generalize ..especially towards people of color.   Theresa's sullen demeanor and her nasty treatment...and erroneous assumptions about my companion..confirms my decision to avoid this store like the plague.
This location has never messed up my basic order of iced coffee with cream but MAN it's slow now. It's not uncommon to wait 20 minutes in this line. I guess I'll blame it on the COVIDS‍.

It's my local Starbucks. The staff is always very friendly and has more to say than what do you want :-) Has a drive through.  If it wasnt out of my way I'd visit more often. Man I wish there was another one closer to my house. Can we get a Starbucks at the Beach?

In an update to my first review, I returned to this Starbucks during the holiday season and went through the drive-thru during the same time of day. The employees couldn't have been nicer. While waiting for my coffee, the man working the window asked how my day had been and laughed with me when I said how excited I was about my snowman cake pop. It's cute and delicious; of course I was excited! While I can't truly compare to my last visit as I went in the store that time, I do believe this location has upped its game when it comes to service. So I have no problem upping my rating as well. Nice improvement, Starbucks!
This is a very busy store, but always efficient and friendly staff.  Roomy both inside and outdoor patio.  It would be really great if there was a drive through, but not possible at this location
One of the best Starbucks in the area! Quick and friendly service with an efficient drive thru. Can't ask for much more when I'm on the go!

Slowest starbucks. Went to drive thru today and it was bad service again.  One time I waited several minutes for someone to just come on speaker at the drive-thru but ended up driving away. Told myself never to go back after that and I absolutely will never go back now.

Worst service here!  Sadly making me look for my coffee elsewhere. No reason to have such poor, unprofessional and generally unhappy/unhelpful service.  Every-time.  This is NOT a one time offense otherwise I would let it slide.

Great service, prompt drink ordering, quick drink pickup and wonderful staff. Glad you are sooooo close. Love you Starbucks. :) They had everything needed, very clean and great location!! See you tomorrow for another coffee.

Quite possibly the most disappointing Starbucks I've frequented. Time after time I walk in, place my order and have to wait in excess of 10 mins while there is no one else in the store, but drive thru orders are flying out faster than Usain Bolt. To add insult to injury, my drink order is often wrong -- light ice does not mean half a cup of ice. I go here grudgingly due to the convenience factor but I won't go out of my way to give this Starbucks my business.
What do you say about Starbucks..this location friendly service and good Americano..like the most
If I could give negative stars I would, the girls that just waited on me are rude and shouldn't be in customer service. I'll never go to this location again!!!
Love the flavor of eveything they have it's so good and plus really nice people everything us clean and tou can relax

I've been there twice & travel a lot to many many locations. No bad vibes. I'm specific how I want my coffee made and they get it just right. No drive through which can it may be inconvenient to some though unfortunately & I can understand that. They deserve five stars overall

I stopped in on my way to work, store was super clean, and hardly any customers waiting at all. I decided I needed to try the new Brown Sugar Oat milk Espresso Latte, but a skinny version. It was super yummy, and will definitely be ordering that again.

The people working here are extremely nice and always in a good mood! They are all fast workers and you never have to wait more then like 5 minutes to get your drinks. And most importantly they make very good drinks and the snacks are always really good!

I've been to this Starbucks a number of times and the baristas are always super friendly and cheerful, even at 6:30am.  I usually go through the drive thru, but the wait times have been crazy, so I used mobile ordering.  I am lactose-intolerant.  I always order soy in my drinks.  As soon as a took a sip of my latte this morning I knew it was made with dairy.  There is no mistaking that taste.  I was so disappointed because I was already half way to work, and with the wait times the way they have been, there was no way I was going back to wait in line and have it re-made.    Luckily mine is not a dairy allergy, just an intolerance, and I noticed it right away.  I had to throw out a $6 latte.  Super sad. I understand baristas are in a hurry but PLEASE check the labels on the drinks before just making them with dairy on auto-pilot.  I would have spent all morning in the bathroom if I hadn't noticed the difference.

I ordered a venti cappuccino here via drive thru and more than half of my cup was foam. I actually thought my cup was empty. When I shook my cup, there was a measly amount of liquid swishing at the bottom of the cup. I actually put my finger down inside of it, and it was almost all foam! There was barely any liquid in the entire cup! And I paid almost $6 for a venti sized drink. I'm never going to this location again.
The service and location is good. I've been coming here for over a decade, but the biggest problem with this location is SPACE. Never a place to sit. It's packed every time I come in.

The line was a little long but other than that this Starbucks was friendly and made my caramel macchiato perfectly! They have everything a flagship Starbucks dies including hit breakfast sandwiches and yummy pastries.

Small, quiet, attached to Hampton Inns.  All the handicapped sports were taken by enormous pickup truck with no stickers or disabled plates, super frustrating. Coffee was hot and strong  just like my husband likes. I like the free water to drink in the car. Good coffee.  Impossible parking.

By far and away the best Starbucks that I go to in the STL area.  I work right next door and frequent this location and it always delivers. Unlike some other Starbucks that I've gone to over the years, this location really goes out of their way to connect and get to know their customers.  That speaks volumes to me and is something that doesn't go unnoticed. Even on their extremely busy mornings you'll see the staff with a smile on their faces, still making great drinks. Great place to grab some espresso and great people working behind the scenes.
Happy staff. Running special during the time I was in St. Louis. Quick service. Will walk the extra blocks to visit this location.

If you like the clover brewed coffee,  this location has a clover machine.   Ask the barista for a coffee that is not already brewed and the barista will use the clover machine to brew it.  I ask for a decaf pike; the barista brews the coffee using the clover and I do not pay extra for getting a clover brewed coffee.    The location was recently remodeled with new wall murals and furniture.  There are convenient electrical ports right below the seating.    I like this location for their service, informed baristas and cleanliness.  Thank you Starbucks!

Every time I go to this Starbucks I leave feeling so happy. The baristas are great, always so nice and patient with my modified drink requests. My drinks are always made so perfectly. I was a partner and I became very picky about how my drinks need to be, every time I've gone anywhere but the one I worked at I end up a little disappointed with my drink, but not at this one, here I get a drink that is always just right, and the baristas always have the greatest attitudes.

One barista and one person taking orders and long lines. I only stopped in because i had a gift certificate. I prefer coffee bean and tea leaf.  I ended up leaving because i had to be somewhere and did not have 30 min to wait

My favorite Starbucks. They make a mean iced white chocolate mocha. Sweet lord. I could have one of these every day. The crowd that comes there is very business-like. If not business-like, they're cute girls. Just sayin'. If I had more time to burn, I'd hang out here more often.

Honestly, I was very happy to have this Starbucks in the area since I live in Seffner but it's inconvenient that one day they'll be open and the next day they're closed completely. Make sure you call before you go! Also when they are open they're always out of something.
This is my favorite Starbucks in the area to visit! I make the drive each time because of the amazing employees. Leeanna is always so kind and so is Quentin. I leave happy each time.

Very disorganized. Got up charged, wanted a black coffee and some egg bites: should've been around or under $10 but got charged $16. Then they forgot about my coffee and I had to remind them 5 minutes after that I just had a black coffee that they literally keep on hand and just have to pour it. And then as soon as I picked it up, spilled all over my hand. So good luck at this one  egg bites were good though.

Terribly Slow. 30 minutes for a coffee it's unacceptable. They treat you poorly and the drinks are watery down . Drive 5 extra minutes and go to the new one in highway 50 .  Don't waist your time here.   By the way. Not once nor twice. Every single time it's the same . I'm done !
This is "my" Sbux. It's busy all of the time so the drive thru can take For.Ev.Er.  I have never had a poor customer service experience besides the wait and I'm hear nearly every day.

The baristas are super nice and make all the drinks as they should be! It also has a full Starbucks menu, which is super nice because I feel that many smaller Starbucks in the area do not. Beware of the early afternoon though because it is super packed!

I'm not sure how this Starbucks earned poor reviews, but in the week I've been staying at my parents' place in Riverview on vacation, I've gotten my coffee here every day. For a corporate coffee shop, this location does a great job. All five of the staff members who've helped me were friendly, timely, and filled my order accurately. I didn't even have to ask for a puppuccino for my sweet hound--the barista offered one. Another staff member went to look for a pastry and yogurt flavor I wanted that weren't displayed. The outdoor seating area is convenient and plentiful, and the interior is clean and inviting. It certainly beats the Starbucks near my office in Raleigh, NC.

A Starbucks, is a Starbucks, is a Starbucks.... and this one is a Starbucks.  That doesn't say much, but simultaneously says it all.  I'm a purist with my coffee and always just order coffee.... no frills.  There should be a separate line for people like me who order, they turn and fill a cup and we are gone.... I don't even need to go to the separate counter to pick-up.  But I digress... this was coffee and it was good.

Disappointing experience at this Starbucks location. I'm an airline pilot and I visit a different Starbucks location nearly everyday. I always order a decaf americano. The last two days at this location the shots of expresso were smaller and not as strong as I'm accustomed to. I mentioned my concern to the barista and I was told the expresso machine was working just fine even though the shot glasses were only partially full on a number of servings. The americano I was served was notably weaker and not as flavorful as I usually get. At Starbucks, consistency is the name of the game. Unfortunately, not all locations are achieving the high standards of excellence and customer service I've come to expect.

Just went to the drive-through. It is 5 PM and I was informed they are closed for the day. There are no signs anywhere. No explanation as to why they are closed at 5 PM on Wednesday. Maybe they should get their shit together and put signs or something in the window so everybody who was in front of me and behind me didn't go out of their way to get to the drive-through for no reason. But while I'm here- let's talk about the terrible inconsistent service. Always always always test or Your drink before you leave because there's a good chance it will be wrong. Wait times at the drive-through are also  the worst in the entire Tampa area. If you can, go to the Starbucks down in front of the golf view mall. Quick and easy
Nice stop while shopping catch a feed minutes rest with a nice cold or hot tea but my fav is still a mocha.

NEVER come here on a Monday Morning unless you want to be late for work.  I understand that yeah it's Monday morning and everybody still half-asleep but honestly it should not take over 20 minutes to make somebody an online order yet you want to go in there you're sitting there waiting still and then to the point where you have to ask the person where is my order and they just have no idea all rude saying what's up. Im never coming here again

I'm a die hard Starbucks fan. The employees and the managers here are super friendly and pretty fast for how busy they are almost all the time. They do have a great location for before and after work for a lot of people.

My girlfriend and I decided to come to this location to enjoy a strawberry refresher over the other locations because this location is usually so spot on.  However, we order two venti refresher just to be told that they are out of strawberry juice.  Ok that happens, I guess, so we change to passion tea when we are told that they are out of lemonade.  Seriously?!?!  We decide to do do the new cold brew iced coffee and what looks to be a chocolate scone.   I hand the heated pastry bag to my girlfriend sitting outside and ask the barista if milk comes in the coffee. She tells me know but that they could add half and half. I ask for a splash The barista hands me two drinks what look closer to white mochas than coffee with a splash. My girlfriend then notices that the barista gave us a chocolate croissant.  I went to point it out but the girl walked away as I was talking to her about the mistake. My girlfriend then noticed that the scone we wanted was no longer in the case.  We wouldn't have ordered food today, but the scone was something new for us to try.

I love everything about this Starbucks except the lady named Dani! (at least that's what her apron says) All I want to do is come in, sit down and drink my coffee in a nice environment which is possible if she isn't here. If Dani is here get ready for noise! Narrating everything she does to management in a very brown nosey way. Being rude to other workers when they talk to her, "I'm deaf in this ear you have to speak up!". She gets flustered and can't keep up with the others but acts like she is better than everyone because she's a brown noser. Oh, you're cleaning something gross and bragging about being the only one doing it? Thanks for the info while I'm trying to enjoy my food and drink. Watch out management, don't be fooled. You don't want this person in a position of power, don't ever promote her. And while you're at it, can you tell her to shut her big mouth?  Everyone else is a super star though. Good job guys.

This is a pretty good located store.  The menu is consistent for other Starbucks.  The visit was not memorable.  There was no line and my order only took a few minutes.  I like how well staffed the location usually is, so even if there is a morning or afternoon rush time, they wait hardly varies.  They do have outdoor seating which was nice on such a nice and warm fall day when I was by.  I am a bit harder on stars with national chains, thus the average rating.  You will get the kind of product and experience you expect at Starbucks at this location.

This is a worse Starbuck's I have been to, nationwide. I ordered mobile order and my estimated time for 3 drinks to be ready was 7 minutes, I ended up waiting for 30 minutes and when I asked associate about it, he was rude and replied, we are busy, don't you see it. That being said, stay away. I waited total of 40 minutes for my mobile order. That is unacceptable

Only giving one star and only because Yelp makes you. I would give no stars! Just left this Starbucks. Crap service. Took 25 minutes to get my food. Ordered 3 sandwiches. Stood by the pick up counter. Lady put a bag up there and I said are these my 3 sandwiches. Girl said its Sara's. I said okay. Waited longer. I said are you sure.. Looked inside and the name said Gina. Thanks a lot.. Now I have cold breakfast. Schedule more employees if you want to keep customers happy and coming back.
Slowest drive through EVER. Be prepared to wait 10-15 minutes MINIMUM no matter the time of day. Pic on a slow day at 2:00 in the afternoon. Each stop at the window was like 3 mins. SO DAMN SLOW.
The drinks were fine but all 4 of my sandwiches are cold in the middle. Not room temperature. Cold.
Very small very busy but service was good ... I miss the big Starbucks the one I used go to where I can sit and read and have Carmel macchiato .

Typical Starbucks, which fulfills my Sheldonesque need to have my coffee be consistently good no matter where I am. Service was great, plenty of comfie chairs around the fireplace.  Good place to knit while I wait on others. Only downside is paying to park.

What a rude barista. My order was messed up & after explaining to the barista, she (mid aged Caucasian female)  was very upset that cashier hadn't ring up my order appropriately. It was a very simple order. She looked at me such a stranger way like I had done something really screwed up. I just flew 8 hours & this was my first stop in Indianapolis downtown, was hoping for a little bit warmer & welcoming response in the cold afternoon:(

Starbucks at Target closes at 9pm today... So why are all the baristas telling me I can't have anything hot to drink and its 5:30pm?? They said the only hot drink is plain coffee. So thats what I got.  Im christmas shopping and I cant sip on a hot latte. They are already cleaning up and shutting things to go home. Im super annoyed.

Drop your (friend, sibling, loved one etc) off at work at 7:30am, drive around awhile and end here.    Order a chai latte (or your own poison), and a crazy good zucchini muffin.  Then, devour the muffin top and the rest, while picking out the walnut gems which is strangely like panning for gold.  Repeat when your budget allows.
Favorite starbucks and a new favorite drink.....green tea frappuccino with soy!!! Excellent!!

If you go on a cold day, prepare to drink your Latte 'iced'!  Everyone around me is trying to bundle up to keep from shaking.  The seating is also limited for such a busy location.  If you have anything more than a very brief meeting, find another cafe'.  Everything else is your typical Starbucks cookie cutter experience.

It is an okay Starbucks. It has definitely become and chain store. I wished it was like back before it got so big that they would give you your coffee in an actual mug. Their were Starbucks store in the east coast that would do that. The ounces here don't. I don't mind their coffee it's still pretty good and the atmosphere is good. There is just a romantic memory that I had with that Starbucks in the east coast. It was raining out, you could smell the wet rain as it dripped from my clothes. I order some coffee and sit down. I start reading the news paper and as I was reading here came this lady politely interrupting me to serving me some coffee. "Here your coffee sir" the lady says with a smile on her face. I can just image all this happening in black and white hehe jk. Any how enough of letting my imagination take over. I just miss the days when Starbucks wasn't so commercialized. But I would come back here to grab a cup from time to time.

This Starbucks rates a review because, for better or worse, it has character. During the day it's so packed with laptop users you'd swear you entered a tech start-up's unorthodox work commune were it not for the baristas' familiar outfits. As dusk fades, it attracts a fairly regular crowd of Tampa's finest minimum wage earners outside while the inside seems a bit like a study hall. This is one of those rare Starbucks that seems to approximate the Platonic coffee house, with regulars either spending all day exploiting the free wireless connection alongside a cuppa joe or just living the slightly overpriced Bohemian life as they dread the limbo between when this once 24hr Sbux closes and when they can return to what has clearly become their neighborhood haven.
WiFi at this location is slower than a dial up connection for those who know what that is.
Once again stopped in got the best customer service from Gio got a nice ice tea after I couldn't decide on coffee thanks for the wonderful staff you have at this location

Although the drive-thru line routinely spills into main thoroughfare during peak hours (6-10 am), going inside saved me tons of time. Took less than 5 minutes to order and receive my drink.  Fully staffed with friendly folks! Very clean and cozy atmosphere inside.
You never know if they'll be open or closed. Business hours change too of. Can't rely on the hours published on the web. Ridiculous.

Starbucks is Starbucks. For a caffeine fix w/ regular coffee only TruBru @ Portland brew wires me better. Today they didn't have Bold so they hooked me up w/ an Xtra Shot Americano, which may replace my need for Bold coffee. This location has great outdoor seating. The Drive-Thru is always cramped and hard to navigate.
This is probably the slowest Starbucks. I order using the app and it's not ready or wrong.  This location is so close but service is not good.
Always seems understaffed. I was overcharged because the cashier was too frustrated to change the mistakes she made.
Friendly service, great attitudes. Excellent coffee per usual. The Bantam Bagels were great.
It's a regular Starbucks with a little-less-than-normal friendliness. The place had a number of people there, but seating was certainly limited. No drive thru either. It's attached to a plaza.

A nice starbucks with consistent quality of the coffee they put out. A great place to meet up with friends has a nice vibe. A little loud for a place to get work done, but a good place nonetheless. The baristas here are very upbeat and kind. They get the coffee out fast and diligently.
Friendly staff, quick delivery. I was here for several days at different times, never got dissapointed.
Decaf takes 15 minutes they quit prebrewing it.  Im changing to Panera.  Icontacted mgmt.  corporate office.  Ive come here for 13 yrs.  no more.  Its all yours.

Relatively speedy, efficient Starbucks location with a few dings for occasionally screwing up charges on a car full of people, but otherwise competent.    Be forewarned: the entire local business community and government office complex swarm this place during morning rush and right after lunch, and the ingress/eggress paths were NOT designed to accommodate that volume of vehicles.  It turns unto a rat's nest real fast here.
Quick little visit on the way to the dispensary then lunch on the coast at the brewery. #stpetebucketlist

Service is fast and friendly, they always have you in and out quickly so that you can start enjoying your drink. This is my closest and most favorite location in Tucson. This last year they had a remodel and it looks beautiful. I have never received a less than great drink from any of the staff even during a busy rush.

They're awesome. Except one worker, he had a name tag that said Jesus. He was very condescending and rude. Sometimes they take five years but probably because some customers order like 5,000 Frappuccino's.

I love Starbucks!  This one is absolutely horrible though.  I thought they all went to "sensitivity training."  I think they forgot to invite the employees from this particular Starbucks.  The store manager that works the morning shift, is by far the rudest manager that I have ever encountered at ANY Starbucks.

I go to Starbucks a lot and have been to most of the ones in the Reno area. This one is by far my favorite choice. Every time I have gone I have received friendly, fast , service. If they accidently make your drink wrong they do not get an attitude about making you a whole new drink. It's always very clean and inviting inside. This location also has a big selection of cups, mugs, and water bottles to choose from and several other coffee related items. I will go out of my way sometimes just to go to this location. The only bummer is they do not have a drive through.

I like this Starbucks. I have quite a few to select from near where I work. I'm not going to lie, I actually loove Starbs & convenience of having 3-4 close ones near me makes me feel spoiled rotten. The only downside is that my pocketbook isn't the biggest fan of my frequent coffee visits.  I don't like the crammed parking lot, so I rarely go inside. Drive through is the way to go @this location. Download their app. for BOGO drink specials, usually on a Thu. and alerts on innovative, new drinks...That's one consistent thing I love about Starbs, always expanding & creating new drinks - another reason I'm addicted!  I've tried nearly all the drinks on their menu, but I've really been obsessed with their Caramel Macchiato 'cause it's similar to an Iced Latte. I get a Venti with one pump of Caramel, light ice & it comes with 3 shots of espresso. Will I regret trying to sleep later on tonight after getting a coffee at 6:21pm? Maybe...Will my body thank me for the caffeine rush? YES, PLEASE! :-)

Slowest starbucks Internet I've ever used, to the point where often its unusable. This isn't an issue that only happens during certain times of the day, I've been there various times from 10am to closing, and it's always incredibly slow.  The drinks are fine, exactly what you'd expect from a Starbucks (which is a good thing!). The service is quick and friendly. It is freezing inside. Not sure why that is, but it gets to the point where my hands get that slightly numb feeling that comes with cold weather.

Tends to get crowder but I need my daily fix. I like this location because of the. CLose proximity to the turnpike and route 1. Also has outdoor seating and better parking than the starbucks that's closer to my office.

please read this especially the manager !!!! this place has absolutely no organization at all!!!!! they do not have enough staff probably because the pwner doesn't want to pay anyone i ahev never taken the time to write a bad review in my life but this is just unacceptable. corporate needs to grt notified of this place asap its a drive thru it should not take 40 minutes to go through hire more employees or close down!!!

Out of Venti Cups and refused to sell us a trenta because they only had 20 of those cups left for the day. I'm sure the girl working was just tired of explaining that, but her tone and sarcasm was unneeded. Her exact words were "I can only sell you a grande, because as you can read on the sign we placed above the screen we are out of venti cups" First world problems for sure- but just unnecessary to be rude.
Friendly baristas. Great location just off the freeway. Absolutely beautiful building. I will be coming here FREQUENTLY!

The absolute worst location! I wish this wasn't the location closest to me or I would never go again, but it is on my way to grab it, so that is the only reason I go back. First of all, the drive thru line constantly takes so long to the point where it gets me late. Like I'm talking I've waited for 30+ minutes and you get stuck in the line, so I can't get out at that point. It should not be taking that long ever with 5 or so cars in front of me. It has happened on many occasions. Also, my coffee order is almost never made right like I'm very clear with my order and they still manage to mess it up. The food is always burnt too, which is a big reason why I rarely get it anymore. I used to go daily and now I go maybe once a week because I'm sick of wasting money on something that is either burnt or not even made right and I'm also sick of being late for work because of how slow they are. It's ridiculous and this location needs to be managed much better because it makes Starbucks as a corporation look terrible.

Love coming to this Starbucks due to its close proximity. I have a favorite seat because it has a big table and outlets readily accessible. If I don't sit here, it's pretty tough to find a seat with a lot of space and an outlet. My drinks have been made correct each time I go here. Great place to do work.

We stopped by the Channelside District Starbucks today on Kennedy, places our simple order (just ice teas) and they served 8 customers who care after us their orders (more complicated drinks). We asked to be refunded, no one apologize or cared & we left. Very disappointed! Willing to spend not easily earned money & completely disregarded! Wish I could give no stars!
Great drive thru service. Smiles and polite. The coffee was made to order perfectly. This place is wonderful.

Yes, it's "just" a Starbucks, but when we stopped in for our morning coffee on our trek back to Boston, the staff were extremely friendly and even helped us make sure we had directions (although we had a GPS) to get back to the highway.  My beverage - a cinnamon dolce latte - was made perfectly and extra hot, just how I like it.

It's Starbucks so the coffee is what you would expect but,  seriously the agitation and craziness that ensues from the parking games played in that lot has made me leave several times. Then I park in the big lot and walk over to hothouse...   The worst part is it's really the only Starbucks that serves Bryn Mawr, Haverford and Villanova.... tired college students + mainline clients = disaster

Come on, we've all had at least a cup of coffee or tea at Starbucks at one time or another.  I'm a one cup in the morning kind of person.  Usually stop here with my daughter who's addicted to Vente Green Ice Tea (no sugar, no shaking, no extra water) or 3 shots of expresso over ice.    So yes, I stop by often,  Do like their lemon pound cake... usually have that when i"m in a hotel or other place other when I'm not home for breakfast.  I do like a hot Carmel Macchatio (drink it, can't spell it).  Let's face it, they make a good cup of coffee and their iconic everywhere..  Sure, I'll go when I need or want a good cup of coffee.  May stop by some of these days to check out their lunch.  Go Starbucks!

No one is looking to Yelp to figure out if they like Starbucks or not.  They already know. Starbucks does Starbucks well.    Along those lines, this Starbucks seems to be about average.  It has all of your standard Starbucks products, decent service, large windows and a small area to socialize, work or just relax.  I am a fan of Starbucks corporate culture, I believe they treat their employees really well and through their efforts they are improving working culture around the USA.  But, because I've been in tens of dozens of Starbucks around the country and globe I don't tend to rate them 5 stars.  A Great Starbucks gets four, and an average Starbucks gets 3.  This one is pretty decent- but hasn't yet struck me as amazing.   This was my second visit to this Starbucks- of which I anticipate there being many.  It is the closest to my Spring Internship.  If they impress me further I will update this review.

When I walked into Starbucks yesterday 3/17/2017, it seemed really nice inside but when I got to the counter, the lady who took my order didn't say hi just what are you having (Not snobbish) but wasn't personal like I've experience with other Starbucks employees. She took my order and said "we'll call you when it's ready". Not once either did she ask my name. Well she went off to do something and two young guys were talking and I guess making my coffee but not once did they call me to let me know my coffee was ready I just happened to walk over there to see it sitting on the counter. The young man that was working was busy talking to his "friends" or whoever the other people were so didn't acknowledge I walked up to get my coffee and didn't tell me to have a nice day. I've never been treated this way in any Starbucks that I've been too and may not go back.

15+ minutes for tea and a bagel that was no longer warm because it sat for 15+ minutes. If you want to get anything from this Starbucks make sure to order online because they dont seem to give a hoot if the in store customers get their drinks in a timely manner.
Ask for Kip at the drive thru.  He makes a fabulous cup of coffee!!!  I would know.  I go way too often.
Love the crew at this location! The only thing that makes me not go as often is the long drive thru line that's out into the street on some mornings.
this is one of my favorite drive through startbucks locations around Tucson.  Staff is always super friendly and efficient getting you through the drive through.  They also always get my drink right!

Overall the staff is great and efficient. The downside is PARKING! there is none! Please Starbucks build a stand alone store in green hills. I've resorted to the starbucks in the Krogers to avoid the parking situation at this one.

So.... I waited to visit this location a couple of times before writing this review. I visited Saturday 9.14.19, Sunday 9.15.19 and today 9.16.19. I read the reviews  about how slow the store runs, how long the lines were, etc.   I love Starbucks and sometimes I feel that Yelpers give negative or bad reviews just because they have nothing better to do. (Most Yelpers who give bad reviews have lots of negative reviews on their page)  My first visit to this location was Saturday September 14, 2019. It was around 4pm and the store was crowded. There were at least SIX baristas behind the counter, but it just didn't seem like they were moving "fast enough" to accommodate the crowd of people wanting their Starbucks fix.   This visit, I purchased: a Treinta Iced Passion Tango Tea Lemonade, a Tall Salted Caramel Mocha Frappuccino and two Protein Boxes (PB&J on Wheat|Smoked Turkey). The barista prepared my tea as a Tall as opposed to a Treinta. It was horrible and I waited for such a long time, I thought I was going to miss my bus to work.   My second visit was Sunday September 15, 2019. After Saturday's visit, I was in and out simply because I didn't purchase a drink. There was still a crowd of people to wade through.  My third visit today was in the morning before the mall even opened. I was one of two customers in the store. The barista looked as if he was tired and really didn't want to be there. I purchased two Cheese Danishes and a Tall Iced Passion Tango Tea Lemonade. It took FOREVER!!! I actually missed my bus to go home.  It seems like there is no set time of day that will get in|out quickly (even if your purchase isn't complicated). You may want to visit this location if you are strolling around the mall and have the time and patience.    Also apparently this location is in the hiring process for a new store manager. Maybe they need someone to whip the employees into shape, elevate morale and create better customer service.

I have been going to this Starbucks for years and I have never been let down. From customer service to quality of product, this Starbucks always delivers!! Highly recommend this Starbucks, best barista in the area by far.

If we had a zero rating I would give it here. The way you treat all patrons is crucial. The disrespect for our local law enforcement 4 days ago was uncalled for.
