Why can't businesses stick to their specialty? Why must everything be political? From this day forward I will not visit a Starbucks. I will not buy gift cards etc as I often do for my staff. Instead I will go elsewhere. This community is in support of our local police. You might consider that the next time your staff behaves rudely to them. Oh, and maybe take a class in service recovery because your manager just failed!

I go into this Starbucks when stopping into Macy's.  I agree with other reviewers that the baristas are friendly, the lines are typically short and move quickly, however my main complaint with this Starbucks is that the music is usually so loud, much louder than other coffee shops, it makes it hard to relax.  They usually play something so upbeat, it would be nicer if it was something more laidback.    There is plenty of seating available, however it gets crowded quickly, I think from the people coming from Macy's and the shoppers coming from other Chestnut St stores.  For me, this Starbucks is good in a pinch, but it's not my usual hangout...

One of the best Starbucks locations I've ever been to. The staff is on point with the service and the atmosphere is quite homey. What I also like are the barista favorites board. Each staff member states their favorite Starbucks drink on the board, whether it's original or customized. I would recommend this location for study sessions; however if you're studying on your own, bring headphones. It may be a bit too loud for your concentration.

Went to this location a few years ago and it was bad news all around. Coffee might as well have been dirt water. Then i came back last week and they have since gotten it together. All thanks to this man Porker. Made my drink super fast and it tasted great. Really knows how to operate a 'bucks. I will be coming back as long as that exceptional employee is on the roster, someone give the man a raise. Strange name though.

Ordered 2 iced London Fogs. Took 10 min longer than the app said the max time would be. Hardly had any ice so it was neither warm nor cold, but lukewarm. Also couldn't even taste the tea. Tasted like I was drinking milk that had been sitting out for a couple hours. I've ordered this drink in at least a dozen locations over the years and this is the first time I've not been able to drink it. Awful

Worst Starbucks store ever! We waited over ten minutes for 2 drinks and 2 sandwiches that we had to cancel. The one that came out was in a broken bag and ended up on the floor, the other one got cancelled by us because we had to go. First and last time that we visit this store.
So happy to see a Starbucks open up in my area !! Prior to this one the closest one was 16 minutes away !! The staff is very nice and friendly !! It is perfect in the morning on my way to school

Being the only Starbucks within walking distance of the Williams Center, these folks stink at getting customers through the line and out the door in a timely fashion!  I just spent 12 of my 15 minute break waiting in a line that didn't move an inch! This is not the first time, they need more staff.

I ordered via mobile this morning and I usually do for this location but today was exceptionally pleasant.  I saw a new barista behind the counter and she was so nice!  Since the sugar packets are not out anymore due to Covid, I asked for a handful and how generous she was with her handful.  She even asked if I'd like them in a cup, I said no thanks.  What a great Wednesday pick me up.
This Starbucks is a gem. Inside the Bellevue. Beautiful decorative arts carved into the high ceilings. At a great location. If you happen to be in the theater area, recommend to stop in.

Perhaps the SLOWEST Starbucks in the country. Regardless of the hour of the day or the amount of people in the store, I've never left without a wait. Regardless of the specific barista. It must be something about the management and the workflow. Avid unless you have time to kill

This location has been my go-to everyday the past year.  No doubt about the wonderful quality of the coffee or other products they offer.  The customer service here is beyond professional and friendly.  Everyone is super nice and kind.  Ray is my favorite person.  He always goes above and beyond to take good care of your drink and wellbeing. Today in particular, my clumsy self managed to drop my car's key fob into the trash can and Ray came to rescue.  Not only he fished my key out of bin, he sanitized it and made sure it worked.  I am not sure what Ray 's position at the company. I hope someone from corporate office read this and give Ray an opportunity to move up to the management position. I own a small business in Wyomissing and I always appreciate an employee like Ray.
We love this location and the friendly staff.  We are regulars and go about 3-4 times a week.  My daughter loves it too (decaf of course!)

This starbs is 2 mins away from my home & I go there almost everyday and order the same drink but it's sad because they never fill it to the top?! I'm sorry if I'm paying for the size I expect it to be full... like you could splash a little more milk or ice in it because I feel ripped off when I order and my drink is never full! This Starbucks is the only one that does this to me and it makes me want to go to any other Starbucks so this doesn't happen. I just wanted to point it out so maybe, next time it's full. I've also said something very nicely to the barista one time and she filled it up but rolled her eyes. Anyway I would love to keep coming here because of how convenient the location is for me, so I hope this changes.

Love the location but Dominique is absolutely awful. A terrible representation of the company. She has a bad attitude every time I have been helped by her. Not only is she rude but she gets the order wrong 8/10 times. Almost seems like she's doing it out of spite.

Hey,  Went in at night to get an espresso. I order. Three employees were behind the counter, 1 "customer" in front was standing talking to an employee, obviously his friend for about 7 minutes. No one else was waiting for a drink. I finally asked for a refund. The cashier gave me a refund for the amount of the drink but NOT the sales tax. When I asked why I was not given a FULL refund, she stated that they don't refund the sales tax. I've never had this occur before, I don't ask for a lot of refunds in life, hardly ever really, but when I do, they refund the sales tax. You might want to retrain your employees on simple cashier principles. The median age of your employees was about 18. No manager was in sight. I tried explaining that I was due my tax and they just raised their voices.
I came here last week and all but one of my six very basic drink orders were made wrong. So today I just got a straight drip and water. What did they even hand me?

This is a Starbucks so the coffee and food is good. The service at this store though is a different story. They're friendly and nice but how can you forget to ring up and give me my muffin when I'm IN PERSON at the counter talking to your face? This kid was busy on his phone when I finished adding my cream and sugar to my coffee to come back for my warm muffin. He had to ask again which one I wanted and I got it cold of course. Just frustrating.
Always, always out of cold brew. Even at 6 in the morning... please, for the love of God. Have the Cold Brew ready.
Used to be the best (always consist) tasting & fastest on the North side.  Last couple months has turned into one of the slowest & has had to remake our drinks several times.
It's Starbucks! Of course it's consistently good. Service here is friendly and their drive through is quick. They have a good selection of food and will do whatever you want with your drinks.

Last time I stayed in this area for work (October 2011) this Starbucks wasn't here, so I was pretty damn happy to see it when I arrived yesterday.  The drive-thru was full this morning at 9, but they moved the line quickly and the barista working the window was super friendly. I'm a big fan of friendly baristas.
Super quick store!  Definitely worth a mobile order to get in and out. Not super busy.

OH MY GOSH!!! I just left this location and got in the car with my husband and said, "if I wasn't married I would marry that Starbucks employee!" I have literally NEVER had such a great experience at Starbucks in my entire life and I've even worked at Starbucks. Whoever the guy running bar on a Tuesday 10/29 was deserves a huge pay bump! Wow. Ok so I'm a super snob about filling up a Grande amount into a Venti cup. Literally every single time I order it that way they always ask what I want with all the extra room. Honestly there's NOT A WHOLE LOTTA EXTRA ROOM. 10 ounces to be exact - so he filled up the grande cup and poured it into the Venti cup and it goes well over the highest top black line. Like a lot. And it's the perfect amount for the pumpkin cream on top and this is the best day of my life. THANK YOU  THANK YOU  THANK YOU!!!!

Most inconvenient Starbucks known to man. I barely come here because I never have a good experience...but when I do the service sucks, their time change sucks. I understand it could be due to "short staffing" but this time, I just needed a quick water in the drive thru for my drive home from work and they close early at 5. It was 4:52 and they refused my service still. I will never give this location a chance.

This location has to be one of the sloppiest of them all.   This morning, the bathroom was not only filthy, the toilet was backed up and almost overflowing!  The floors were dirty, and the half & half dispenser was totally out, and no one seemed to care or designated to look after these "housekeeping" issues - I guess it's because they were too busy making coffee drinks, but appearances and cleanliness is important, no matter how busy you are!  There is always a wait, and it was prime time (between 9 and 10 a.m.).  PJ's is looking better and better - I won't buy anything here, as a matter of principal - I walk over with a friend, but don't patronize the place.

Consistently I was getting a coffee cup that was filled up only three quarters of the way. Mind you, I never asked for any room for cream. Today I finally sucked it up and asked her to fill it up all the way. I was given the silent treatment. Baristas face said it all. Sorry, if I'm paying 2$ for a coffee I would like a full cup.

This Starbucks is exactly what you'd expect from a Starbucks. The drinks are how they always are, and they have mobile ordering.  The parking lot, entrance, and exit are all a nightmare. There aren't enough spots and it's difficult to get INTO and OUT OF.  I am writing this review only to remind people to BE NICE TO YOUR DAMN BARISTA!!! I watched some psycho get angry because the barista called out the name "Eva" instead of "Ella" (because the cashier "connected" the L's and made it look like Eva). The barista even had to offer her a new cup. Instead, the patron took the barista's Sharpie and re-wrote her own damn name on her own damn drink.  People are fucking stupid. It's coffee. Please be patient and kind.

I am disappointed to find such a terrible Starbucks in such a beautiful place! I worked for Starbucks for a couple years in college and I am a very frequent customer. The employees working here were extremely unaccommodating as well as fairly incompetent. In addition, there was a strong smell of sewage inside which was a little unsettling. There are plenty of other options in the area so I would suggest you avoid this particular one. Rude, unfriendly baristas are not acceptable!
They're always quick with orders, friendly, and make drinks correctly. The Starbucks itself is always clean and well-kept. They also keep free water with cups out at all times

I agree with others. This is the location closest to my work, which is why I have stopped here on multiple occasions regardless of the poor service but today was definitely the last straw.  I have started to give myself a lot of extra time whenever I go to this Starbucks because they are SO SLOW! Today, however they took 30 minutes. 30 minutes in a drive thru. There were only four cars ahead of me, I don't understand why it takes SO long.  Additionally after waiting 30 minutes I got the wrong drink. I could not believe it! I was already late to work so I just left and tossed the drink. Money wasted, I will NEVER go back to this Starbucks again, they are lacking good employees!

I've been to this Starbucks multiple times, they have great service! The only thing that bothered me is that I don't think the barista knew what topping is... I ordered an iced chestnut praline latte with extra topping. I didn't taste the crunchy topping, so I went to ask if she put any and to put more of so. When she went to put the topping, she added 2 more pumps to my drink. I didn't complain because I can really taste the chestnut praline in my drink. However, the only thing missing from my drink is the Chestnut Praline crunchy topping that I love :'( Other than that, great service!

This is actually one of the better Starbucks in Indianapolis to be honest (which is unfortunate). Immediately, I was greeted by very pleasant barista's that gave good Customer Service. Not only was my drink recommended good but it was actually made well, this is what stands out to me when ordering at Starbucks. Went with a friend she too enjoyed the experience at this Starbucks. Normally, when going to Starbucks I'll go with what is closest, however I'll try to get to this one more frequently after this most recent visit.
New management has worked wonders for this Starbucks since my last review.... Some of my favorite baristas work here. :) it's a great place to study, if you can find a seat!
Everything was wonderful! But I do think they should have more people working. I sometimes come before work and it takes forever for me to get out of there!

This Starbucks is by far my favorite in University City. It's not usually over crowded like the one on 34th & Walnut, and the baristas are sweet and haven't messed up an order yet. If there is a deal or promotion, they make sure to let you know about it to get the most on what you are spending for your coffee.
went there to get some work done Poor AT&T wireless connection Should say "NO WIFI" know problem, not fixed.
Very clean, well-run Starbucks.  Parking lot around back. No issues.
Friendly service but the slowest Starbucks Drive thru I have experienced and it's slow every time I go.
I've been a few times with no issues so I didn't really believe the reviews. So I went this morning at 5:30 am and guess what?  The blender was broke.

The drive through at this location looked really long today so I decided to just go inside. That later proved to be a mistake when the woman taking my order took probably 5 whole minutes to take my order. 5 minutes in the grand scheme of things is not much but when the man next to her is serving 3-4 people before she was even done with my order, it starts to feel long. She was very nice and obviously just new but maybe start your newer employees at a less busy hour than 10am.   With that being said, the barista making my iced vanilla coffee did a great job. The best one I have had in a while.

This is an awkward Starbucks.  The parking really sucks.  And the layout is just kind of weird.  I'm not a fan of this location, but I would come here if I'm out shopping at the mall and need a refreshment afterwards.

(4) ~ Very good food & service...They have a Clover Brewing System, which makes a GREAT cup of coffee!;)  And a very good selection of cold beverages & Baked Good items.  My favorite is the Flourless Chewy Chocolate Cookie which I recommend heated up;)

Don't do a mobile order here you still have to wait in the long drive through line to get your order what is the point of this?? Took me 15 minutes and I should have been in and out. Never coming back!
Rude server. We are large group of people And the server was rude. Didn't appreciate her service. Never coming back at this place.
Omg worst Starbucks ever!!! Hire more staff!! It shouldn't take 30 minutes every day to get a coffee! Absolutely ridiculous.

This is the worst Starbucks I have ever been to! Unfortunately, it is the only Starbucks close to my house, so I have to suffer through the pain of visiting the store. I have had many negative experiences here, ranging from the parking situation (which is beyond idiotic) to an under stocked food selection to incompetent workers. Today, I went in around 9:20 AM, I was at the first person to order at the register and place my order for a Venti white chocolate mocha add one pump peppermint and a ham and Swiss sandwich. To be expected, they were out of the ham and Swiss sandwich. Well fast forward 23, yes 23 minutes later, and they finally get my order correct after messing up my drink, adding only one pump of white mocha, no peppermint. My drinks never get messed up, only at the store. Something needs to be fixed.

What am I invisible???  This Starbucks is so UN-organized!  I placed a mobile order - allowing 20 minutes so it would be ready when I arrived.  No such luck - stood there for 15 minutes while the Barista called up everyone but me to make sure they got their order.  Totally ignored me - even though I was standing 10 feet from the counter.  I finally went forward and asked if my order was ready - since no mobile orders were visible and she was now chit chatting with co-workers.  Her comment was oh I'm sorry I guess I didn't announce it when it was ready.  Really!  Thanks a lot!  Great customer service here.  I frequent Starbucks pretty much every day - this is the worse service I've ever received - maybe they were just having an off day, I hope so.
Great service and its always fast moving. A clean, friendly and professional environment. The staff is very polite and extremely personable!

Filthy dirty. We were there at 9 am and it was disgustingly dirty! Then I had to go to the bathroom, BIG mistake!! As I walked in something green dropped from the ceiling and stained my shirt. At first I thought it was bird poop but then I realized it was the air freshener. Then I couldn't turn on the light. I told the lady at the counter that the lights didn't work and she rudely said, "I know"! You should surely take the time to do to the other Starbucks on state st.

I give 3 stars to this Starbucks. (Weep) sadly, the few times I've been to this SB location I've been kind of bummed out by the inconsistent service. There was 3 people working this afternoon, but it appears only 1 barista preparing drinks. My latte tasted funny and not in a ha-ha way, more like, for $5.13 this latte sucks.  I said to the only barista working that I think they used the wrong milk and perhaps that sugar-free syrup and she looked at me like I was crazy. Ummm, I spend probably about as much time in Starbucks as a barista does (hang head in shame here), so, uhh, I kinda know my latte stuff.  O'well.

Wow. Worst Starbucks experience I have ever had. Flew out on a Saturday morning and 3 people were ahead of me in line. We all waited 8-10 minutes for our drinks. When asked what was taking so long we were given extremely rude responses as it was clear all of the ladies here hated their jobs. Shame on Starbucks for not correcting this. Look at the all of the other reviews.
Even in Europe coffee shops are generally not this large and beautiful.  Characteristically friendly staff working hard to serve you great coffee and pastries.

Have been coming here a few times a week for the past two years. It's Starbucks, it's the only one close to the hospitals and such here on S Grand so it's a popular spot. They do get busy but it's always service with a smile, they have been hiring some new great baristas recently as well!  Always enjoy time spent here!   *update* still coming here, still great staff - Kori, Erika, Ella, Miranda, the whole team is just fantastic!   Also for anyone complaining about wait times, this place gets slammed - it's on S Grand one of the busiest streets! Perhaps try taking a seat and people watching while you wait, or do a mobile order as you leave the house (make your drink extra hot.)
I love coming here.  Parking is in the back.  Plenty of places to sit.  I like that it is a reserve store.  They have reserve coffee that are not available at regular starbucks.

The approach to this location is a little inconvenient. Additionally servers like Zack today made the experience even worse. He was preparing the coffee orders, whispering names and expecting customers To wade through a sea of drinks picking up each to determine if you properly identified the right. Ultimately, I took someone else's by accidental luckily it was the same coffee.  Ugh.

Ok, not a whole lot to say about Starbucks other than it's Starbucks. I go here about three times a week for coffee and I do that because it's consistent.  No, I don't order the mocha skinny latte with a shot of anything. I order Pikes Peak because that's all I want. Coffee. And because I only order coffee I find that their price is consistent with other coffee shops.  If you do go to Starbucks on a regular basis I would highly recommend doing the star points system. After 12 stars (1 star for each purchase) you can choose a free anything. Sandwich, custom coffee, dessert. Anything. That's kinda neat and makes it a better value.

05:00 am, the Barristas seemed harassed and unwelcoming. I'll never ever go to this place ever!! I waited 17 minutes until the opening time, no smile, no thank you no nothing. Gimme your money, get your coffee and just go
Very slow. Went to the front of the line about 20 minutes ago and still haven't gotten my drink. Baristas making 1 drink every 3 minutes.
We love this Starbucks... they are quick, friendly, and do a great job.  Other than the hoodrats hanging out on the front step, it is great.  Also. I would clean more thoroughly the front porch!!

So I come here quite often and today I noticed on the menu that there was a new "Caramel Flan Latte" so I figured I'd try it. I ordered it and I overheard the barista telling the guy who rung me up that she doesn't know how to make it. The guy then asks another employee if she knew whether or not they had the recipe...Meanwhile I'm asking myself, "Am I at Starbucks or the local 7-11?!" To make a long story short, I settled for my usual order and they seemed rather relieved. I was tempted to give them the suggestion of taking it off of their menu since no one knew how to make it. I'm shocked that Starbucks would allow this amateur move for themselves and their reputation.
The sorriest drive through wait times!!  22 minutes folks with no way to get out, it was the Starbucks Coffee black hole preventing on-time arrival to work.
Google says they are open til 10:30pm... we go through the drive thru at 10:20... they say "Oh, sorry - we changed our hours an hour and a half ago and we actually closed at 10." Uuuhhh.....WT?!

Yes, I am indeed addicted to Starbucks. I haven't had the pleasure to drink much coffee lately cause of my stress levels, but I do stop in sometimes for just their scones!!! The blueberry one is always the best!!! Highly recommend that one!  I really like the staff here, they do their job instead of messing around or chatting to other employees like you aren't even their. Great place all around.

I just went in this Starbucks and got a delicious coffee. I ordered the hazelnut latte with an extra shot of espresso. It was made perfect! The staff was friendly, and also busy. I had to leave a compliment for all of them. The shop is clean and things seem to run smoothly. I also want to add that during my visit, a woman came in, and proceeded to blame Starbucks/Employees for spilling her coffee. The manager handled it gracefully. I have to say I'm not sure how a barista can make sure you don't spill your coffee, after you have pulled away and you are at a red light ( these are her words). I have been in the service industry, and I know these people are everywhere. It just feels a bit more obscene when they do it around the holidays. Shoutout to all Service Industry this holiday season!

We have experienced this Starbucks go down hill. All the ladies that work here are angry at their customers, Kim used to be fantastic greeter in the morning, now she won't even make eye contact...the service attitude destroys good mornings..so we don't go in this location any more.  Also, the average amount of time is ridiculous.. Overall it's a horrible customer experience.  Come on get with it..
Shoutout to barista James!! He made me his favorite drink called "The Pink Drink" and it was amazing. I will definitely be back more often.

Outside of the 15 minute wait for an iced vanilla latte and growing, the ambiance of this Starbucks provides serenity when patience is necessary.  The staff that is here has changed and in between them explaining how bad their schedules were made; found it possible to make a couple of drinks.   However, aside from the collegial disgruntlement of a staff the patrons go about their work with absolute mindfulness. The cozy fireplace and modern decor invite you to sit down, plug into a book and forget about the bustle of the day.  Meanwhile....I'm still waiting on my latte.

I disagree with Kara.  This is not the worst Starbucks in the area and actually has one of the nicest coffee lounge spaces in town.  Tall lofted ceilings look out to Mt Rose and the surrounding Summit Sierra Mall.  It's actually a very scenic location.  The coffee is like every other Starbucks, good but not great.
They randomly decided to close early today in the middle of the afternoon. Don't waste your time.

Living a convient block away, I don't even want to know how much money I spend here per week, but I am a happy customer :) The service is always on point. The manager (her name is slipping my mind) is always incredibly sweet and positive with high energy while the rest of the baristas follow suit. It's nice that they already know what I want and we can chat about our lives. The more personable the experience the better.   The only reason I didn't give this location 5 stars is because they don't have a doggie leash holder which becomes clutch in nicer weather. And I do prefer to work from their 19th and Spruce location instead because the vibe there is more relaxed. Sorry guys! But I still keep coming back here.

When you are from Seattle, it's hard to have patience for the baristas in other areas of the country... I was the only person in line and my drink still took forever. I asked for blonde espresso but received regular.  And there was barely any caramel in my caramel macchiato. Not impressed.

The service is competent and the food average for a Starbucks.  However what ruins my experience is this horrible lady in military fatigues who seems to live in the establishment and mumbles something at everyone while giving them dirty looks.  Let's call her The Mumblebum.  I was doing work in there, and as I had my laptop out she said somethin insultin.  I said excuse me as if I didn't hear her, then went back to work.  On top of that she hogs tables.  She will put her belongings on a table to save it while she goes outside and sits at another table for a few hours.  I think if you ban her for good, your business will dramatically improve.

This has to be the worst Starbucks EVER and my family and are Starbucks people.  The line at the drive thru is always soooo long.  They can't even make the simplest of drinks, a Grande Pike with 3 raw sugars and almond milk.  What we get is a tall the one day.  We tell them that is not a Grande and they argue with us.  We just left because the line was so long we couldn't  keep others waiting.   Another day the coffee comes to us black.  It's a simple drink!!!!! We even mobile  ordered one day and STILL waited.   They seem under staffed and right now staffing seems to be very important because our other local Starbucks are closed due to Covid.   Once they are open I am done with this Starbucks!  A shame because it used to be a nice place to go.

Be prepared to be underwhelmed and even slightly dissatisfied.    I am without a doubt a very loyal Starbucks customer and have been for years. To the point that I definitely have my local to me locations but I stop by on a daily basis. This does not make me an expert, but it does make me a consistent consumer who is knowledgeable about Starbucks products and services.   This location is located inside of the GSR hotel in Reno, and is frequented by predominantly hotel guests and staff. I can't imagine someone stopping by the hotel just to go to this specific Starbucks.    The seating area is ridiculous. While it is open seating, it's located near the check in/out counters, as well as, gambling sections. It's just not a great spot/location overall.    The service is close to horrendous, and the staff is just the same.   I ordered two different drinks, both fairly easy to make, and I was the second person in line, with no one else behind me. My drinks took close to 25 minutes to be made. Yes, you read that correctly, 25 minutes for two drinks and no one else was waiting.  Once the drinks were ready, despite the fact that I was standing fairly close to the counter waiting, the barista put them on the counter and walked away. Did not call out my name, let me know that the drinks were mine versus maybe a mobile order, etc. then to top it off, both drinks were made incorrectly. Unfortunately, I did not discover this until I had already left the counter and was on my way outside of the hotel. No one wants to trek back into a hotel that is currently under construction, and frankly has become a hot mess to get in and out of. And let's face it, who wants to deal with unpleasant people when they are handling our food and or beverages?!    Would  I stop by this location again? More than likely only under desperation.  My two stars are because if nothing else, the seating area and counters are clean.
The theme song from cheers comes to mind. They know me. This will be my Starbucks no matter where I move. If I ever really kick the habit...... Who are we kidding.
I like the staff they're quick they're friendly they're efficient and they're very helpful with the ordering menu I think all of them should get a bonus or a raise
Let us congregate at St. Arbucks.

Too bad it's a chain, but it least it's in the Davlan (historic building) and has some outdoor seating. I'd rather patronize a non-chain, but no other coffee shop options in the immediate vicinity that fit the bill... Plus, you always run into someone you know there.

Starbucks at Cheshire is one of the best in town. The location is right off the highway, and is very spacious inside. Not to mention it is like the inside of someone's living room, which makes it super cozy. The service is always fast and pleasant, and I never have trouble finding seating. Many people do come here to work and study. Will keep suggesting this as my networking coffee shop before and after work!

It was 7:38 am on a Friday, so of course there was a line. I expected as much. But the baristas were telling everyone that the only size they had was grande, yet when I got to the register, I had the option of getting a tall coffee. (??)  Then I was told the giftcard system was down, and the ONLY reason I went to Starbucks that morning (which is an extremely rare occurrence) was to earn a star bonus. I inquired about if I could still get a star for the transaction, and the cashier told me not to worry about it, just enter the receipt info online.  A bit of searching later that night and NOPE. Had to swipe the giftcard, which I was hiding at the time of the transaction. She could have just swiped the card, I could have still paid by some other means and gotten my stars. Instead I get to feel like this *$'s employee lied to my face.  To add insult to injury, my coffee cup had WAY too much room for cream. I ordered a 12 oz and barely got 9.  Never coming back. There's another location a few blocks away on Olive.

This Starbucks has plenty of space and the staff is very friendly.  The décor is awesome and it's a great place to meet with other people.  The only challenging aspect of this coffee house is their parking lot. It is limited and getting in and out it's complicated. If you make it in, you will be in a great Starbucks!
I ordered cappuccino, extra shot ... Tasted like bland coffee with too much milk. No good morning, smile, or thank you from barista or cashier. Glad its not my local store ... Big disappointment.

At 10:30 the drive thru was packed and slow. The employee working the drive thru was slow and I had to ask for stir sticks/stoppers for the 2 drinks. That was odd, but not too bad.   What was bad you ask? Having to ask for my change. I handed her my cash and was expecting almost an entire dollar in change. She took my money and said "Thank you. Have a nice day."  My response, after starting at her for a few seconds... "May I have my change?" Confused look from her.... "Uhm... yeah. Uhm... how much was it again?"   I don't know if you expected the change as a tip or just had a brain fart, but seriously... GIVE ME MY CHANGE. You didn't deserve a tip after making me wait as you screwed up my order, took your time on my order and then neglected to give me change.  I'll skip a return to this location... at any time of the day.

Starbucks is Starbucks.  We have a love-hate relationship.  They provide me with the coffee I need when I need it, yet their drinks are really streaky.  There are some days when the drinks are absolutely amazing, but there are other days when you can't even taste any vanilla in their vanilla lattes.  It's almost guaranteed that a Cafe Vanilla frappuccino will be made as a Caramel frappuccino, so make sure you check this before you leave!  Their specialty drinks are by far the best (i.e. Pumpkin Spice Lattes and the Eggnog Lattes), but nothing is necessarily bad on the menu.  While the patio here is nice, on busy mornings/afternoons, it can be quite loud as it's right on Sparks Blvd.  However, overall, there's nothing bad about this Starbucks, but there's nothing great either.

Worst Starbucks ever for many reasons. Staff had a very "I couldn't care less, I don't want to be here" attitude. They were out of what I wanted (passion tea), and when I asked what else they had that was similar, they didn't really have any recommendations. When they finally thought of something and I accepted, the lady who made it did a terrible job. It tasted like lightly flavored water. I refuse to pay 4$ for lightly flavored water. What a joke! I asked to have it remade (I have had the very berry hibiscus at other locations and it's delicious so I know what it SHOULD taste like) and got a pissed off look from the woman who made it. So much for any customer service. It was remade by the guy and it was a little better but still not what it should be. Don't worry Manager (whoever you are), I will not be stepping foot back through your doors ever again and I will make sure no one I know does either. Find another Starbucks that is worthy of your hard earned money and makes delicious drinks, this one does not.

This location has closed for good. Today is 6.10.20 and they're out of business.   I asked the cashier at the service area. She said they closed when the covid-19 pandemic started.  She said it was too difficult to maintain the social distancing. She said she's been here 8 years and never thought it would happen. But unfortunately it has.
Basic Starbucks experience. Nothing fancy. Had the regular items. Fairly small seating area. Had an outdoor seating but it was too cold to sit outside today. Friendly service.
Service was slow with no one inside. Bathroom was gross I don't have to much to say about it. The two stars is because my coffee was done ok. Had to have them fix it once. Happy Drinking.

Experienced the most delightful customer service here! I was torn between the new drinks and Cristal was more than eager to assist me! Definitely will be coming back because of how pleasant Cristal was!

I'm not going to write a review based on the Starbucks products, because I don't know anyone who hasn't tried them for themselves. So I will just review this location.   If it were just Starbucks coffee: Fast, Fresh, In my mouth immediately I would give it 5 stars because I love that stuff.   The mark down of 1 star is due to the speed: I go through the drive thru on a regular basis. When I go I see maybe a car or two ahead of me and have never waited less than ten minutes. I even got to the front a couple of times and saw through the window they had one person taking orders from both the drive thru and the in store guests.... seriously?   The other star is for the weird experiences I have had inside. I have been inside only three times and every time I have heard the manager/supervisor whatever she is, yelling at the employees. She could just be a loud person, but it's a small place so really no excuses for this. I feel like I saw all of the patrons kind of pause in their coffee sipping and just look around when she started yelling.   I live in the North Valleys and prefer to go to the Lemmon Valley Starbucks any time I can.

Love Starbucks on Rob Drive in Sharlands Terr. it's always clean inside drive-through is always clean coffees are of course is always good and their sandwiches are very good they have a nice mozzarella tomato and basil sandwich for those of us who don't eat meat

I have only had two drinks from here, and both of the drinks tasted horrible. I was so disappointed because I get the same drinks from every Starbucks. There is nothing like sipping bad coffee especially when the coffee was NEEDED.

Pumpkin spice season! I love Starbucks and when I relocated to South Jersey I knew it was probable that one would not be as close as I had out in San Diego (a drive thru one 3 blocks away). Well the closest one is about 3 miles and so when I'm in the area and feel like treating myself I stop in. This location is usually busy but there is ample parking and the baristas are usually quick getting the orders out. I think that the service kind of depends with who you get and if theyre having a good day or not. I have had the friendliest service to at times indifference and even a little snobby. So, customer service is inconsistent, which is disheartening because like I said I am a fan of Starbucks. This location does have a decent offering of baked goods and food items as well and the usual Starbucks swag available for purchase.

My home Starbucks. The parking isn't great and the dining area is small but the people are extra friendly. All of the starbucks staples and the bonus is the staff who know me by name and my drink when I walk in the door. One of my favorite spots for a small meet up for coffee.

This review is based solely on the drive through ... which I must say - don't make the same mistake I did and go during 11:30 AM on a Saturday. The drive thru window itself is right next to the shopping center exit ramp so if there is any sort of traffic on the cross road, the line gets held up. It look me a while to get my drink and I think it would have honestly been quicker if I parked at Dorgniac's and walked over. Parking is also limited as they share the spots with a couple other businesses (a jamba and a burrito place if I remember correctly)

If you want to be treated as an important customer at the drive up, go to another Starbucks. This morning the person who gave me my order (2 coffees) didn't offer a carrier even though it was clear that I needed one. And when I gave her extra for a tip she didn't even acknowledge it, nor did she thank me. Then, she put both cups on the same side, and when I turned, the tray fell over and one cup spilled out completely, soaking the carpet in my new car. This doesn't even come close to service. I felt insulted, and won't be back.   Someone should let these people know that there are many other choices--in fact the next time I'm in the neighborhood I'll go to Human Bean for my coffee. They seem to care how they treat customers. This store clearly doesn't.

One of the best locations in the area, fast speedy and friendly service makes this my favorite on the way to work stop! Only downfall and I don't think it's lose a star worthy is frozen drinks don't come out frozen if they have espresso like some locations. I think it's just a temperature being a little hotter melting the ice than other stores. Love the staff and will continue to return often.

Look, I know it's Starbucks but I admit that I love Starbucks. Don't hate. Plus it's conveniently located in the gulch and I'm really happy that it is. I came in when they were open for 2 days, and the morning line wasn't bad at all. I'm excited that this will be foot traffic primarily and hope that it won't have the same outrageous lines that West End, and other drive thru locations typically have.  The layout is very conducive for quick service and catering to the coffee goers who are getting take out. This location has some tables but you can tell the intent is to serve people quickly and then move on. I'm glad!  Only one issue I have is that they wouldn't redeem my free birthday coffee and it is about to expire!
Super fast service and friendly people. No line at 7:30 on a Saturday morning. Got three drinks and our food in less than 5 minutes

On our way to Squaw Valley, this was the Starbucks that we had to stop at on our way to the powdery goodness.   The parking lot is a mess, first off.  People taking two spots, tourists sideways into a spot, trucks making 10 point turns to get out.  Absolute madness on pavement.   Inside the store is much more organized and customer friendly than most other Starbucks.  The staff was stuck in "frozen" which is one speed slower than a dead stop.    I thought maybe it was the early hour we were there, but 3 other trips to this location confirmed that there is something in the water there that makes the staff super slow.    The drinks were very good, so I have to give them credit for that and surprisingly still hot after how long we waited.   Overall, a nice Starbucks experience, but well off the service I receive in the Bay Area.
Soooooo incredibly slooooowwwww!!!!! All the time, crowd or not.so so so so so slow. So so soooooooooo s[ooooooopo so so slow

Love this Starbucks for the following reasons:  (don't worry Chestnut Hill Coffee Co- I still love you too). 1. Super Cozy 2. Usually enough seating (but seriously girl on the laptop- it's time you hit the road and give me that awesome chair). 3. Clover Machine (for any of you coffee snobs- you must try this.  You will have another fabulous reason to be a coffee snob) 4. Always a dog chillin' outside with the peeps 5. Friendly service 6. Have always had what I wanted (ie what is on their menu- reference my center city Starbuck's review...) 7. Parking out back/on street 8. Great places to walk with your Starbuck's cup up & down Germantown 9. Cool artwork is pretty much always on display  Thing to Work on...  10. Fireplace- duh!  :)

It took TWENTY minutes for them to make me my egg bites. They kept saying "sorry she's in training." I don't understand how that makes it take twenty minutes when there is NO LINE. When I got the egg bites they threw it in a bag and smushed it, no small flat tray like they usually come in. I will NEVER come back to this location, holy shit I am so annoyed.

This is one of the most inconsistent Starbucks there is. Actually, all the Starbucks in Santa Barbara are horrible! Get it together people. This is a huge chain and the one thing that should be counted on is consistency!

Standard Starbucks. Good amount of parking. They have a decent amount of outdoor seating and smoking is no longer allowed outside. Seating inside is also good. The staff is very friendly and there is an open mic night every Thursday if you're into that.

Haven't been to this Starbucks in a very long time. Not because I don't like it here but just because I havent had time. Also I am not a big fan of the parking lot lol this is why I park where the Snack Shack (shhhh)   I ordered the CARAMEL BRULEE with extra BRULEE (of course)  The customer service here is great!! I am pretty sure the Starbucks employee's name was Bret? He was energetic considering I was half asleep and needed my coffee lol but he made me want to come back.. Which I will!   There is also a side door you can walk out of, which is just a short walk back to Snack Shack where I parked my car. Also near the side door is where all the goodies are such as the vanilla, cup sleeves, stirrers and napkins. If they are low on anything let an employee know they are very helpful and always smiling.Ill be back soon.

This is a great Starbucks! The staff is very friendly and the location is great for getting work done. My old high school was very close to this location and I would often times come over here to get work done. I've recently discovered the java chip frap with 4 pumps of peppermint syrup! So good! The crunchiness of the chocolate chips and ice with the cooling peppermint is awesome! My new favorite thing to order. I've given up coffee at Starbucks all together because I don't like the aftertaste from it, this is great alternative.
I LOVE this location! Not only is it super convenient for me, but the staff is amazing! Always friendly and patient! Especially Megan! She is amazing!

I liked this location but for some reason it was more expensive then the Starbucks in my city. They also have the breakfast sandwiches which was cool because my city's Starbuck's doesn't have them yet.

Stopped into this Starbucks for Octane Blast to get me through the morning. I've visited many of these coffee shops and this is the only one that seems to lack a certain level of organization for ordering.   I walked in and was totally confused people standing everywhere...literally a line out the door for placing orders and receiving. I ordered a Solo Expresso Macchiato and a Blueberry Scone. They must have earned it in the microwave, because when I received it it was a blueberry brick.   However, the overall experience was a pleasant one. The Staff was extremely accommodating...Scone was replaced and the coffee got me thru the day.
Pleasant Experience Clean bathrooms, excellent service, friendly conversation  Bethany was lovely.
This is the slowest drive thru of my life .... I know Starbucks are slow but this is horrible.  I was going to give them a 1 but I am being generous

So this Starbucks is as good as any other SB as far as quality is concerned.My coffee was good, the environment was nice and the whole place was clean. The big problem with this place is parking. It was awful. I had to drive around a few times before there was a spot open and even then I felt like I got lucky to get it. Would have gotten 4 stars, but parking sucks.

This is an above average Starbucks mostly for its location and its indoor and outdoor seating area. Parking can be tricky around here especially at night and weekends. This is a popular area.  This coffee shop can get crowded but I have never had to wait very long in a line here nor have I waited long for my drinks.   Enjoying Starbucks outside at this location usually provides some great people watching.

It's 3pm in the afternoon and the young moron working the counter tells me, they have ran out of freshly brewed coffee.   There is seven people working and they are all dimwits!   Just think this must the only Starbucks in the world that doesn't have any freshly brewed coffee in the middle of the day!  Unbelievable !  Big change!  Ok so the place looks great, the new design looks amazing and more functional and the staff seems to be nicer and orders are correct and several times I have stoped for a coffee in the afternoon and they have hot fresh coffee.  Total improvement!

great staff!!! Sean, Lindsey, Zeb, Stacy, Sara, Aaron, Molly, Beth & Alicia, Daniel, Greg, & of course The Ever Refreshing, Entertaining, FABULOUS, Friendly, Bubbly, Cheerful personality RODNEY great place to hang out  or stop by, while shopping on mass. ave bring back ashtrays!!!  While sitting outside tonight with our Fishmonger, Mary & her daughter B , Daniel did an AMAZING JOB @ cleaning outside & Feng Shui ing the tables, while Lindsey & Greg worked equally as hard inside

This location is the smallest Starbucks I've ever been to. About five 2 seater tables and very cramped order/pickup area. The parking area is small and shared with other businesses with several spots marked "employees only". My latte is excellent!
Large, center of town, clover brewing system, windows/counter for view. Can get crowded and not the cleanest store.

I was so excited when Starbucks opened in my neighborhood. I literally go there every day. In addition to the drinks I purchase, the staff always offers a puppuccino to my dog. My day, and that of my puppy, would not be complete without our morning visit.

This is one of those locations that you can see yourself sitting there for hours on end, studying or reading. I like the glass windows and the ambience. It's got just enough busy-ness from Jasper Ave, while also retaining the quaintness of the residential street behind.  Staff is friendly and food/coffee comes out quickly. It's a Starbucks so you know what to expect. The best part about this location is being able to people watch! They have a steady stream of people coming in and out throughout the day.   I should come to this location more often... it is lovely.

Brand new drive thru location, only third in the district with a Clover machine, Starbucks Reserve coffees.   This intersection is really growing quickly business wise. It is great to finally have a quality coffee shop that fills the gap on Gulf to Bay in between Downtown and the Bridge.

This Starbucks, is just like any other, except-the staff.  The staff here is excellent.  That is why they get three stars.  The location is small, the seating is mostly for one or two people and there are just one or two places that will accommodate a small group.  The location is not easy to get in and out of.  People driving down Wetmore are evil and never let you out and you are in a parking lot filled with Walmart, Taco Bell and Best buy customers--so it is always busy.  At night this area is slightly sketchy, especially bc of the Wally world customers. It attracts an interesting breed, especially at this location.  Don't get me wrong, I frequent this Walmart, but the peeps are seriously, interesting to say the least.  So beware. Oh and it gets busy in the am, slightly less than River and Stone, but there is no drive through here to speed things up.

This message is to management, if you even care. For god's sake, please please please hire more staff! It is unacceptable to have three people working in the store at 7:30am when the drive through line is 15+ cars and the line in the store is 10+ people long. It is true that I used to live in the city and am used to a 2 minute visit. Spending 10+ minutes in this Starbucks just tops the list. I thought maybe it is just a one time thing, but it is consistent every single day and that is why I go out of my way to avoid you, Horizon Blvd Starbucks. I thought a mega giant like you would have some standards...

This place probably has the worst customer service you will ever see. I ordered 2 drinks, expecting to get them together. Instead I waited 15 minutes for my first drink, and never got my second one. I complained twice, and was not listening too, the employees repeatedly ignored my several requests. Is was not until I raised my voice and made a scene, then I got my second drink. They have crappy service, and I am certian that I will never order there again.

Very hard setting. Not comfy at all. Beverages are okay; food passes for refrigerated "food".  Although I thought that all Starbucks are non-smoking, inside and out, they don't enforce the outside rule in the least. Might as well put ashtrays on the table. Maybe No Smoking signs would be a step in the right direction?

The drive-thru lines can be long during the weekday mornings, but if you aren't in a hurry, you can just be patient and wait it out in your private, cushioned, climate-controlled chamber. Most times, the line moves faster than you'd think.  The staff are typically friendly, conversational during slow times, and I've received countless "'I'm sorry" free drink coupons when I didn't even feel inconvenienced.  Some Fridays, you may be behind the mysteriously generous local who pays for your drink, and then you hopefully decide to continue that chain of good will (or not).  I have had a few drink mix-ups, mostly them adding shots of flavor (ugh) to my plain nonfat lattes.  Overall ok Starbucks.

Ashley was really rude to my family. We were trying to be polite and we asked for a reward and she immediately snapped back at us. We were given the wrong drinks- they were not even prepared well. They were chunky and not mixed at all. I usually love Starbucks and I love the service but this was mind blowing and totally ruined Starbucks for me. I am appalled by the service and the lack of any kindness that I found at any previous Starbucks.  We also asked for no straws and they were thrown into our car. We felt unwelcome and felt like we had to leave immediately.

Quick and clean Starbucks to stop at when needing a break from I-24. They have a drive thru which seems to be quite popular but i would go inside to check out the man candy working there (3 hot baristas in one location? Unheard of!) Oh yeah i also went in to get coffee also and it was delightful.

Great coffee Jose, Carlos, and Ruben are the best. Though it is located in a BAD area I recommend. It is located inside of a Fry's store at 555 east grant next door to dollar tree. Beware upon arrival you will be greeted by pan-handlers and hobos. Luckily they have security on certain days of the week to keep the rift raft out.
I'm sure it will get better eventually, but for now I'll continue to go on Terry Parkway.
Just waited 17 minutes in a line of 5 people, they where out of everything, and then yelled at me about it.

Closest one to where I live. I need one closer. Worth the 10-15 minute drive. $5 is a bit steep for a venti frapp. The baristas got my name right, I wanted it wrong. In and out in 5 minutes. Next time I am ordering a doubleshot because I need caffeine.

Most of my visits here have been positive however I wanted to mention that last time I got a refresher it was not completely filled to the top. I feel like if I am going to pay $4+ for a drink that it should be filled up all the way. I have had this issues at other locations as well. Hopefully this is something that can be fixed :)

My favorite Starbucks in the city for people watching, especially in the summer.  On a First Friday, there can be a bit of a wait but the staff is always friendly and the decor has more character than its suburban counterparts.

I was in a hurry, I got in line, placed my order. Then waited. The girls served lattes and everything else to the people behind me before even making my drink! She said it was because they're drinks did not require espresso. If I'm not mistaken lattes most certainly do require espresso!! Other Starbucks won't even serve a premade tea before the next drink!! So basically I stood in line for my turn and got cut by five people!!!!

I stop here multiple times a week and have for 18 months or so. MJ has been an amazing barista and host nearly every stop. First name basis, always welcoming and really brings the joy to our interactions. Staff is always kind, coffee is always hot (or cold, seasonality you know) and I really appreciate the break in the day this stop provides. Keep up the good vibes MJ and crew

This is my favorite Starbucks on this side of town! The Barristas are friendly. The place is always packed with friendly people who are ready to offer you a smile and a seat. The outlets are easily accessible.  SOUTHSIDE ALL HEART!!!
Went there and 1 oven was broke so it took awhile to get my sandwich. Then they forgot to give me the cake pops that I ordered. Was a little disappointed

Looking for a place to sit down, do business, and have a cup of coffee? DON'T COME HERE!  This starbucks is always FILLED with people who don't have a home and USF students who don't want to do their schoolwork in their dorms. I've never been able to meet with a client at this starbucks because there is NEVER anywhere to sit, and being that starbucks doesn't tell anyone to leave, they never leave. Students stay here and complete their whole assignments, so coming here would be a waste unless your coming and going. I think this starbucks should be a caffeteria on campus

If you want one of the umpteen different sugary drinks Starbucks sells you can order one here and get it as quick as you can at any Starbucks but if you want a black coffee pour over I suggest you drop your order off at breakfast and pick it up at lunch because it's gonna take a while; a long long while...By the time you pick it up it will be a cold brew

Not really happy with this location. My friend and i got to starbucks at least twice a week and everytime we have come to this store they never get our drinks right and the lady/ladies who are running the drive thru are rude most of the time. I dont recommend this location.

Our 10 year old son got in line behind the only customer waiting.  Others lined up and were served before him - despite the fact that they arrived later.  We asked what was going on.  Apparently he should have queued in a different direction.  The "Barista" never said a word.   Terrible service

Starbucks is Starbucks. Love them or hate them. Either way I'm only going to provide my view on the specific location rather than their menu selection because, well let's face it... it's Starbucks.   The baristas do seen to have a high turnover here. Can't exactly speak to what that means here but, if you're lucky enough to get a senior one, they'll hook you up. One time I forgot to ask for my extra shot in my grande skinny and I told them to hold off on brewing so I could get back in line. He said that was blasphemy and dumped a free one in there for me.   So the parking lot, yeah... Usually filled to the max (and then some) with SUVs of the luxury brands. One time, I literally saw two Bentleys parked next to two Tesslas in between a few brand new LandRovers.  It's funny that the rich ones always get the spots, eh? My Honda and I parked across the street and defaced Lancaster Ave-road kill-death to get in there.  I also literally saw a fist fight between a BMW business guy and an angry Irish Mercedes guy over that damn parking lot. Thankfully, buff Infinity guy acted like the bouncer and broke it up. NO JOKE. I can't make this stuff up.   At the end of the day this is an in-and-out (of you ever get it) Starbucks. Not the kind for your laptop or leisure.

This location has super fast service.  Drive through service is a plus for me as I was in a hurry.  As usual Customer service was great and my order was spot on. I will def. visit this location more often.

Nice but not so efficient baristas. I waited 14 minutes this morning to get a cold brew. This place is never an optimal morning stop. Even the order-ahead coffee isn't generally ready in a timely manner.

This is a Starbucks like many others, but I'm giving it a high rating because of the friendly service, which really stood out. It's not a bad study location in terms of being open later and having wifi and several tables, although plug access could be improved and I could see it filling up at busy times.
Very rude to me and my friends when asked for water. Told us we suck and then ignored our order when people who ordered after us were getting their coffees. Will not go back

Just like any other Starbucks out there, friendly staff, enough room for everybody and most of all it's so close to my house! I normally have Vanilla Latte, Hot Choc or Mocha, but today I had Iced Shaken and sweetened passion tea lemonade and I love it. I guess I will sticking to it for some time till I'm ready for something new.
coffee wasn't horrible but the employee working behind the counter with black hair & nice nails was very sweet & helpful. Will definitely be back if i'm in the area.
Best Starbucks in Reno. Worst parking lot. Friendly, efficient baristas. Always clean lobby and restrooms. I'm a regular and  love Valerie, Ayla and Cody.

1798044 was really rude, no personality at all. Yea coffee is coffee at any Starbucks but Baristas are what make you or break you! Your job may not define you but you never know what opportunities can come from one interaction with a customer.

I've been coming to this starbucks almost religiously since the start of the year at the same time everyday, 10 o'clock. Everything has been going smoothly until recently. There is a barista with glasses, male, short, dark hair who makes the drinks. He's been filling my grande coffee only halfway for the past 4 times I've been there, today being the absolute lowest amount I have ever had. I do not pay $5 for a grande cup of coffee only to get it half way filled. Idk who he is trying to play but he is so lucky that I was rushing to get to school this morning or he would have had an ear full.   I will make sure to check every single cup I get from this starbucks to make sure they are giving me my moneys worth in coffee. I will also be speaking to the manager to make sure that all the barista understand what a grande cup of coffee looks like.
Monday morning crowd appeared to be fairly well handled by this crew... it was crowded I skipped all but the coffee as it is fast and efficient....as always they are consistent!

I have been driving all around greenwood and every location is closed early. This one, STILL OPEN AT 5:40pm and packed but successfully getting people in and out at a fast pace still with a friendly environment. I am 5 1/2 months pregnant and the second i got greeted, I instantly felt calmer and just excited to get a drink. This pandemic has affected a lot of places I understand that, just helps when the staff is still welcoming and not pressured by the overwhelming amount of customers due to staff shortage at other locations.

Pretty much your typical Starbucks. Ordered a couple of lattes and enjoyed our drinks before heading over to see a show at Walnut Street Theater. I like the fact that if you are drinking your coffee there, you can request a cup vs. drinking out of a to-go cup. Place was clean and service was friendly.

I want to like this Starbucks because it's so convenient for me, but there is never a place to sit to enjoy my coffee. There are 3 tables, which are always occupied, and a small bar where each person, because we are Americans, takes up 3 chairs. So disappointing that they could not find a way to make more room for tables and get creative on how they lay out the merchandise.

Thank you GSR for having a Starbucks! Yeah!  Pretty quick location considering there's always a line. Employees are friendly but fast and they have a ton of options available.  They didn't have their pastry/food showcase stocked because of understaffing so if you want something that you don't see just ask!  Plenty of seating and located right off of the casino floor

the worker asked me what food I wanted and then another worker took my drink order and forgot to charge me for the food and then came up to me and told me I had to pay because it was my fault I didn't tell her about the food. bad customer service and didn't have anything in stock.

There are definitely reasons why this Starbucks has lower ratings than others in Reno.  I usually go a little out of my way to go to the Wedge Parkway location instead of this one.   One of the baristas comes into my work and I always address him by name, however he never remembers me.  Today a coworker of mine went over and ordered drinks for all of us, I also got a cherry oat bar... they forgot it.  I had to go back myself to get it, and they didn't even offer me one of the service cards afterward, so I asked for one.  They said "Oh, we don't have any." I said, "Well, this is an inconvenience for me so I would like something," Turns out they did have them. Why lie? If you get early, like 7:30/8, sometimes it's not busy, which is the only perk.  The mall opens at 10, that's why. There's one really tall skinny dark-haired barista who is always super nice. If you don't see him, head up Mt. Rose Hwy for 2 minutes and go to the Wedge location.

Nice people and doesn't get too busy around 8:30-9am.  Drinks come out on time and there is plenty of parking.  My only complaint and this goes to a few locations now: FILL THE CUP!  I pretty much get the same drink every am: Venti Latte (or venti mocchiatto).  The cup is always about 1.5-2 inches empty from the top.  That would make the drink a grande, so don't charge me for a venti and serve a grande.

No no no no I was thrilled to see this place open near my home.  My first trip 7am Sunday morning and the bathrooms have lock codes on them. I asked the guy behind the counter why and was told so they can monitor who is using the bathroom. I hate this kind of snobbery so I won't be back.   I'm guessing this is an anti-homeless measure but there are no lock codes at the Grand and Lindell location or the S Kingshighway location and homeless people frequent those areas without over running the coffee shops. Locking the doors helps no one but it reveals something sad about the owners heart.  This is a brand new store so the owner isn't reacting to a historical situation. Perhaps they are acting on faulty assumptions about this part of town.  Maybe they are worried about losing business Whatever, doesnt matter. As said earlier, I won't go back. There are plenty of other Bucks in STL and there's a perfectly wonderful privately owned coffee place closer to my house anyway -Restituto (without locked bathrooms). There are two other local coffee shops in a 3 block radius and they don't have locked bathrooms or hordes of "undesirables" hanging around.  I love you Starbucks but this anti-human snobbery breaks my heart. What if a random homeless person happens by and really needs a bathroom break? I know what that is like because I'm one of the millions of Americans who has been in that situation.  I'm not going to spend my money in a place that makes me feel like I'm contributing to more human misery. If I patronize you when your employees are kind to me it will only remind me of your unkindness to others and it will ruin my coffee.

I've come here a number of times in need of a coffee an me nearly every time come away disappointed. While the service is usually prompt, the drink mare way to sweet every single time. It is conveniently located but inconveniently unenjoyable

I like their spinach feta wraps! Im not a sugar person nor a coffee drinker sooooo i stay away from their 1000 calories drinks. And it's overpriced for horrible coffee (Ive taken a sip or two.) Starbucks have become a place for Tweens to hangout and feel like an adult drinking coffee. It's annoying. Lol. If you want good coffee/drinks minus the calories and get yourself some real Colombian coffee or go to a real cafe. I actually don't mind drinking good coffee, but caffeine just gets me even more hyper and I am already hyperactive as is, so that's another reason I stay away lol.
This Starbucks used to be great but the last 5 times I've gone there they've messed up my drink in one way or another and the employees are always rude.

Was dying from major lack of sleep and stomach was angry from all the alcohol the night before. So I stumbled upon this Starbucks, and it was really cute inside with photos of Reno in sepia all over the wall. Although the barista forgot my sugar free vanilla, after adding it, it was one of the best made iced coffees I've had in awhile from Starbucks. It was just what I needed to stay awake for more gambling!

Conveniently located across the street from the enormous New Orleans Convention Center (NOCC) this efficient and welcoming Starbucks was a godsend during my recent NOCC-based convention. I had forgotten to pack my reusable mug, so I was delighted to see that in addition to perfectly crafted drinks, I also was able to pick up a new travel mug that was in the style that I wanted. Thanks, Starbucks for making my Convention experience so much more enjoyable.
This is one of the worst Starbucks in the area. They are consistently slow and regularly screw up your order. Avoid.

Convenient Starbucks to walk into. It's average sized but might be harder to get seats on the weekend. The baristas were friendly and I received my order quick. You can walk from the Starbucks right into the Macy's without going back out to the street.

Starbucks in the Cherry Hill mall is very hit or miss. They're either empty or the line is so long it extends out of the entrance to the Macy's it's located in. Due to the stand's small size, there are probably 3-4 employees working at the maximum. But even with 2 on the register and 2 behind the counter it's not enough to accommodate the business of this location. Also, the space where customers wait to order and wait to pick up their order is cramped. It's not uncommon for people to resort to standing outside of the Macy's and try to listen carefully if their name is called, rather than elbowing strangers in the ribcages just to wait for a frappuccino. The drink taste like any other Starbucks. The baked goods are usually fully stacked and the employees are very helpful and nice. I give them props for being able to serve their coffee with a smile given the factors that make this location inefficient. I believe the mall would benefit greatly if they simply opened up a large independent Starbucks store within the mall.
PANINI - Won't do THAT again. See advertisement photo compared to what I actually received.  Could they put any less attention to detail in that Panini? Service was fast and coffee was good.

This is a great Starbucks --always clean-always organized and a great staff--mainly Kristina who is speedy and makes my drinks perfectly always!  I like that they always have community newspapers available for everyone to read but I don't like the cop who comes in regularly and always take the whole freaking pile of COMMUNITY newspaper and takes them home !!! Hello! The papers are meant to be read there and recycled for others-- not taken with you!  This place is better than the coffee shop up the hill where the staff talks way too loud!

I would have given them a four but the gal that made our coffee wasn't the friendliest which was a bummer because we came a couple days later and the guy working the drive thru was very happy and polite. I guess some people just have there off days, I get it.

Not up to the high standards of service that most Starbucks are know for. I ordered a triple grande caramel mach and when I got it, it felt like only two thirds full.  I was in a hurry so I didn't complain as I should have.     As I was leaving I stopped to go to the restroom and a pregnant young woman coming out warned me there was no toilet paper.  I went into the other restroom and it was also out of paper and dirty.  Very disconcerting.

After landing in RNO, I had mapped this location as my first stop before heading eastbound on I-80 to Winnemucca.  It's a friendly place.  This is one of those Starbucks that asks your name so they can call it out when your drink is up.  Never give your real name, always make one up like "Godzilla" or something.

Went here with my fiance and I have regrets. I was trying to buy a gift card that came prepackaged with a greeting card and it wouldn't ring up. The packaging said the greeting card came with the gift card, yet the girl refused to sell it to me because she insisted it was extra. This guy behind me decided to shout his order because I guess he's just too important to wait in line AND THEY TOOK CARE OF HIM. I was still ordering stuff! Really?!  I got my drink and left. I went to another Starbucks and was able to get that gift card no problem, the greeting card wasn't an extra charge.

Everytime I go to cherry hill mall , I always stopped by the Starbucks that's inside of Macy's . Today being the few times I went , I've always seem to have bad experience with the cashier . The cashier always seem to have an attitude problem and always giving me weird looks . They must be racist towards Asian people . I gave it a two stars because the person that were making the coffee is always nice and very polite . Don't ever want to go to this starbuck again .

This is one of my most frequented Starbucks, I usually like it because there is never anyone in there at the time I go.  It is a 'hit or miss" location.  What I mean by that is that while the service (in terms of friendliness) is always spot on... they can be slooooooow, as in slow motion.   Now that said, they have ALWAYS provided me with a kick ass drink - I always order a triple grande something and who ever is behind the machine has always taken great care in making my drink delicious and hot.  Avoid this Starbucks though during the week around 7 because ALL the high schoolers from next door are in there.

Staff is friendly and great, but this is the second time I have gone though the drive thru, ordered a reduced fat turkey breakfast sandwich and it was cold.   The first time I thought, maybe they messed up or their oven was not working or their freezer works too well.   Now, this is the second time. Disappointing. I guess I will have to check my food before driving off in the future.   Not a good way to start the day.
Horrible service, waited 20 minutes, did not call coffee half glass and cold, had to remake the mocha because the ice was melt, expensive and not quality, shoud be review the prices and services.
I enjoy the 24-hours (drive-thru) and late hours (indoor). Free WiFi... Outside and indoor seating is available.

Definitely not a good example of Starbucks =/ Even though there was no line to order my drink, it took forever for it to be prepared (a tall Americano!! simple drink!)...approx 10 minutes. I was quite turned off by the staff chatting amongst themselves when I was standing around waiting for my drink -- they did not seem very focused on their tasks at hand. Seating is extremely limited and overall not very clean. Pretty disappointed in this Starbucks location...perhaps it's due to being in IV?? I've been to other college town Starbucks and they were by far much better, both in service and amenities.

Traveling to see my husband who started a new position locally, I found myself at this Starbucks.  I currently have a special mug that allows for free coffee for the month and upon presenting this mug, the barista at the register offered a new type of brewing called Clover.  I agreed to try it.  She was very informative and helpful.  A true asset.  Then while seated at the bar awaiting my new drink, another Barista, Royce, gave me some interesting insight into Starbucks,blends and home brew.  He was funny and very helpful.  Even during an obviously busy time, he made my visit quite enjoyable.  The icing on the cake, the coffee was amazing!  Well done!

5 min from me but ill drive 15 min further for better staff. Last week a male employee asked if I wanted my COLD EGGS REHEATED. I SAID SURE IF YOU LIKE TO. Normal reaponse being from the South. He walked away. I asked of he was going to heat it up. He shrug his shoulders & said. You said if I liked. So he walked away helping another customer. Dark big haird mini beard over weight. Sweating & passively rude. So yes Cold Eggs I threw away. Today not a busy Sunday. Having problems with my phone/starbucks App. 2 ppl Didnt bother to help me when i asked for them to help me real quick. On looked on the go said are you at the right store etc.. I had a scan thing pop up said head to the register. That Dark skin thin man walked away. They were working but 3 cars in drive thru & 3 ppl in store, I was one of those 3 inside. Asked a lady who wasnt able to walk away as fast as the other 6 employees who saw needing help but wouldn't make eye contact or answer me or acknowledged that I was at the order counter fumbling with my phone. I could have paid there but no one came to the counter. They were all avoiding the counter and a customer that was FACE TO FACE. After the one lady said something short and brief I had to yell out " can I plz get service!!" After being there for a hot minute (20 min) the girl told me I didnt order yet and had know clue about the scan bar that had prompted me to the check out counter. That same girl said. I Don t know what to tell you and walked away. A very light skinned thin long blonde hair girl. Just TAKE MY ORDER AND LET ME PAY WITH A CREDIT CARD! SHE TOLD ME I CANT HELP YOU. FIRE THESE STUPID-MEANING HOW DO YOU NOT HAVE CUSTER SERVICE SKILLS & WORK AT A STARBUCKS STORE? WALMART? DAIRY QUEEN? DR OFFICE? LAW FIRM? QUALITY PASSIONATE PPL NEEDED AT DR. MARTIN LUTHRT KING BLVD ACCROSS FROM ST JOSEPHES HOSPTIAL. BOSTON MARKET. HIMES & MLK. FIND REAL PPL THAT WILL SMILE. BE HELPFUL. THIS IS RIDICULOUS TO HATE A COFFEE SHOP SO MUCH. EVERYNODY AT MY OFFICE FEELS THE SAME WAY. ITS SUNDAY FUNDAY. NOBODY WAS SMILING. IT LOOKED LIKE A FUNERAL HOME BUT WITH ATTITUDE OF A**ES  GET THE STORE MORE HELP SO THEY CAN BE NICE TO CUSTOMER'S. This store is always busy. Staff are rude. Seems like they ALWAYS NEED HELP. Today hit the spot for a review. Been ordering from MLK for 1 year. 5 min from work too.

Extremely rude older lady with blonde hair running the drive thru. Argued with me over making my drink wrong after she made it wrong twice, and I WATCHED her fill my pink drink HALF with WATER. Won't be coming back here again. Management you need to take a look a your employee.

I use the drive thru at least once every two weeks and the service here is amazing!   One time the barista, I think his name is Michael, gave me a compliment on my outfit. I'm a stay at home mom and I know I didn't really look quite that good with my yoga pants outfit but I put a little bit more effort on my top and I put some blush on. But anyway, he said he liked my outfit so it made my day. He also made sure he gave me enough straws (you can never have too many especially if you have a toddler).  Another time, I ordered a grande drink and I told the barista that I had a birthday reward to use. She asked if I wanted to upgrade to a venti! Umm hello?!! Yes please! I kicked myself for not ordering a venti to begin with but I didn't wanna be greedy so I'm so glad she offered!  They're the best at this location! Keep it up guys!   Thank you!
Slowest Starbucks ever. Seriously. 10 minutes to get a large black coffee. And it's like that every single time. Why do I keep coming back?!

Terrible experience at this location.  They gave away my sandwich to another customer and after informing them that I had not received my sandwich 10 minutes after getting my coffee, they showed no urgency and took another 15 minutes to put my sandwich into the heat....

This is without a doubt the best local Starbucks in Lutz. The baristas are extremely friendly and thorough. They make sure to get your drink just the way you like it - which is nothing new for Starbucks, but each barista makes it a point to get to know you as a customer and as a member of the community.
