The management and the baristas are amazing at this location. They make it a point to get to know you and almost befriend you ( but not in a smarmy way).       The work is quality in any of their drinks and it is always served in a friendly manner. In short they are the best.

There are some nice baristas here, but I must say overall my experiences have left a lot to be desired. A substantial number of my drive through drinks are made with regular milk, even though I always order nonfat. We're talking maybe 1 in 3 is wrong. And by the time I realize it, I'm out of the drive through window and don't have time to come back around for a remake. Sometimes I've even seen the drink has a different color, been assured it was nonfat, and then realized it wasn't after I left. Again, many baristas are pleasant, but I've switched locations because I hate to keep paying 5.00 for a drink made wrong. By contrast, the Harrison Starbucks has never messed up an order. Never.

Two things I love about this Starbucks. First, it doesn't have a drive thru, which is a huge drawing point for me. I loathe drive thrus because it basically just caters to laziness. Second, it's literally across the street from my job at Trader Joe's, so my awesome boss tends to bring us lattes and marshmallow treats more often than not.  There's lots of room out on the patio when the weather allows it. The parking tends to be a nightmare on occasion, but, like I said, I work at Trader Joe's. I'm used to it.

Rudest customer service ever. As a frequent customer of all starbucks, I have never been so insulted. A polite query into asking when they open is retorted with a snappy "we open when we open.". Real classy guy.   Wow. Just the customer service I expected at 6am.

Convenient location, but easy to miss when driving. Parking is very inadequate and entrance driveway too narrow. Otherwise, the building is attractive, seating is adequate, quite clean, and service is good. Nice outdoor seating, more could be added.

I came to this Starbucks while on a trip to the area for a wedding. It took us a while to navigate through the mall (since we'd never been there before we parked on the opposite side of the mall from it!) but when I got there my first reaction was, "Wow, this Starbucks looks so cool!"  I've never seen a Starbucks with this layout or decor before. Definitely a refreshing change from the traditional green and white.  My drink was made quickly and we were on our way. No complaints!
Worst baristas in town. Not only did they not follow the simple instructions for a latte on the side of the cup but they short-changed me by a half a cup.

I know...high price.but it's worth it hehe..coffee at a top eatery is far more costly and rarely more savory...and really....what is money...when you get free friendly atmosphere, manager, workers, etc.  Yes it's nice espresso/whatever/or mocha and sometimes a nice healthy snack..or a serious brownie hehe lucious for me, and I go to the outside seating and do art work, and share with surrounding peeps and workers for free...its, cool and refreshing for me...
The new skinny mocha tastes like warm chocolate milk!! Don't waste your money!! Splurge on the regular.
Great service. Always on top of orders and friendly. Clean facilities. They make the drinks right at this location.

This Starbucks location is my favorite Starbucks in Santa Barbara! They always have great customer service, friendly people, and make my order very quickly. I have come custom to doing the mobile orders when I'm in a hurry and this place always has my order ready on time!  Their wifi is great and there always is space to sit in here. They also have tons of plugs which makes it easy if you decide to stay to get some work down for a few hours. They even have a small outdoor patio with tons of chairs if you feel like sitting outside. The have a large selection of coffee, tea, and food here. They fridge is also always fully stocked so if you need to grab a bite they always have so many things here to choose from! Their bakery pastries and bagel selection is big as well.  My favorite things to get here are the breakfast sandwiches, matcha latte, green tea, iced coffee, and their pesto panini! If you are ever on the go the mobile ordering service is great! They even have a parking space saved out front for mobile orders only.  Overall if you ever need an afternoon pick me up, or a place to study and or get work done come to this Starbucks!
Place seems grimy for s Sunday afternoon. Not sure if it's the place or people. Lots of young girls using internet but not buying anything.

The staff here doesn't have ANY sense of urgency. It's so frustrating to watch 7 people waiting for coffee, 10 still needing to order, and the employees talking amongst themselves distracted and uninterested in customer service. Can't stand this Starbucks.

This is one of my favourite Starbucks! Every time I go they have really pleasant people working, even when I awkwardly lurk around trying to decide what drink to get they are very friendly.   One time I ordered a Red Velvet Frappe but had no idea what was even in it and they baristas were so friendly! They guessed at what would be in it, made it for me, then told me if it tasted gross they would make me something else. (It tasted pretty good to me.) It's really nice having the service being so friendly and helpful.   The location is very convenient and they've got a huge parking space. Unfortunately there's no drive thru, so you'll have to go on a jaunt into the store to get your coffee on.

Are there any Sbux locales out there that truly stink? This place is close to the office I'm temporarily working at and it is always packed! My only gripe (and why it received 4 stars instead of 5), is the parking. During peak hours, its very difficult to find a space and there is no drive-thru.
Yelp standing up for the cop hating management and employees at this store by hiding negative reviews.  I'm sure this comment will be hidden too.
Beware the crazy Russian lady who insists all hot lattes come with at least one pump of vanilla syrup whether you ask for it or not...

Worst Starbucks experience. The woman taking orders fussed a young girl because she scanned her phone for the rewards when the barista wasn't ready. The girl profusely apologized and the barista kept commenting obviously making the girl feel terrible. The woman in front of me had a card not functioning right and the barista made a snide comment about it. And when I ordered I was critiqued for it. To wait in a 45 min long line just to receive a critique at the end... terrible.

Watch out for the  Barista's if  it not their good day!  We found them to be rude and we totally rushed when ordering.  Plus on a  Saturday night around 9:30pm 2 weeks ago the Barista was rushing people out  due to clsoing at 10pm.     On a Sunday an "older" Barista nearly chewed us out for not ordering faster and  almost called me al iar saying my card  "didnt have a free drink on it"  or hastily took my coupon.  SO RUDE

Since I work in Arnold, I visit this Starbucks nearly 4 times a week. Unfortunately, this is by far the most crowded and backed up Starbucks in the St. Louis area. The employees usually do the best they can, but the shear volume of drive thru, online, and in store orders is very overwhelming due to there not being another Starbucks in the area. Regardless of the day and time, it takes no less than 15 minutes to wait for my order and sometimes approaches a half hour.  Starbucks needs to either increase the number of employees on the clock here, or better yet, build another drive thru within a 5 mile radius!
I mean who doesnt love coffee, any and all give it to me! Its so expensive though and sometimes the employees are too cheerful at 7 in the morning. haha

Asked for a $15 reload on my card. $50 was put on. went back to have it corrected. Alexis was very kind however, her manager Lori was not. Not only was her attire not of the Starbucks representative but her attitude was far from matching as well. Before she would even refund the $50 I had to pay the $9 and change for the coffee ( you think she would have comped that)... The incompetent manager did not refund the $50 in fact she charged me $50 more. After all said and done I'm sure corporate will be having a firm chat with her.

This store has hit a new stride in service. The staff is focused and friendly. The things I would change are issues that can't be easily resolved (limited parking for example.) Everything else is running on all cylinders.

This location is horribly slow and has extremely poor customer service. I waited for 20 minutes for my food order. They had plenty of staff working but they sure were inefficient. They gave me half my order and then looked surprised when I asked where the rest of it was. No reason that this place can't be more more organized and customer centric. They were also out of basic items like napkins and Splenda. Not a great experience to say the least!

This is the Starbucks inside Target. I enjoy stopping by to grab a Frappuccino before I shop. If you get the Cartwheel app, there are occasionally coupons that can be used. The other day I had a coupon for 25% off a Frappuccino.

Horrible customer service. Asked a question and the barista about expiring stars and she didn't know the answer but gave her opinion. I reiterated that I need the answer. Stated her opinion again. I said never mind I don't want your opinion and told her she can ask for help. She told me she can't ask for help, WHAT?!? Anyhow... I place an order and she asks if I want the venti size since it's free. I said NO I'm paying for this NOT with stars. After we left figure out she processed the order with STARS!!! So I call the store to complain and see what they can do. The "shift supervisor," Ryan, who answered the phone was just as I knowledgeable as the barista that caused the problem. Kept talking in circles and in the end said to call corporate. This store needs to retrain their employees. Horrible management and customer service.

I have only been to this Starbucks location once or twice when I happened to be in the shopping center.  It's your typical Starbucks, except I don't love the set up.  When you walk in the door there is a table right in front of you, and it seems that the entrance area could get very crowded and confusing during busy time.  While I didn't sit down with my drink, I noticed the few tables around, none appearing to be particularly comfortable or private.  Also, the area where the barista will hand you your finished drink is kind of in the middle of the store, leaving you to stand awkwardly in someone's way.  Regardless, it's Starbucks, and I won't hesitate to go to this location if I'm in the area.
Nicest Starbucks I've been to...and probably the only one I've seen a black guy working at. If I actually drank coffee regularly, I'd probably be here every Monday-Thursday I had class.

This Starbucks is super slow, especially in the afternoon. I just waited 20 minutes to get a single frappachino, and several other times I've waited over 10 minutes. They often only have one barista working, which is not enough considering the number of customers they get. Their drinks are also expensive, like most Starbucks. Overall, I find that walking to Au Bon Pain and getting coffee there is quicker, despite the walk, and much cheaper than going to this Starbucks.

Consistently the worst Starbucks in the area.    Have had the order messed up 5 times in a row now    I know they are busy but they need better guidance and help here.     Not using this location and taking the ride to Cinnaminson .    Going to cinnaminson store where they always get it right and are %100 more pleasant.  Would have been 1 star but grading on a curve for Covid

This Starbucks is conveniently located on my work to work. I prefer placing a mobile order and picking it up in the store, which save time during the morning rush. My drink is always ready on the counter. Great location and friendly service!

I had the longest Starbucks wait I've ever had in something - and that's saying something since all Starbucks are slow.  It took 20 minutes to get a Frappaccino.  For me, paying $5 for a coffee drink is a special treat, but waiting that long is just painful.  I think I'll stick to another location.  At least they give me a coupon for a free drink if they take that long!

I only like coming here because of the drive thru, however there are always so many mistakes with my drink order and today was the last draw. This pregnant african american lady that was taking my order, handle with every customers money. Decided to open a straw and put it in my drink. How disgusting is that. I never asked her to do that to my drink. This is the first time in years that ive been to starbucks that someone did this.

Love this Starbucks!! Ample amounted of seating, even though it is always busy it seems. Everyone is super friend, but I have to say Sean is my favorite barista at this location. I never thought you would build a relationship with your coffee people-- but Sean is so friendly and attentive that you can't help but look forward to seeing him when you get your coffee! If you are looking for a regular Starbucks this is the one!
Very unpredictable drive thru wait time. Sometimes it is normal but other times it is a parking lot. Right now it is a parking lot and it looks like about 20 cars. No movement for about 5 minutes

I've never felt so compelled to write a review about a Starbucks.  I come to this location often because it's in between where I live and where I work. I'm probably here 2-3 times a week, at least.  With that said, every time I'm here my experience gets better and better. First off, the inside is spacious, clean and there's also an outdoor area with chairs and tables.   Second, the baristas are the friendliest I've ever encountered. Whether it's cracking a joke with a customer to make them laugh, telling a customer about a latest deal, or just going the extra mile in general, the service has always been phenomenal. To be honest, it's probably the friendliest Starbucks I've ever been to.  Lastly, they're super efficient. Even when there's a long line, I never feel like I'm waiting too long, and my order has never been messed up. And to be honest, even if they did ever mess it up, I wouldn't even be mad because I know they'd handle it professionally. Best Starbucks ever!

Fast, Friendly, Drive-thru, New & NITRO COFFEE!    I was very ecstatic about this new Starbucks popping up close to home. I had high expectations & was not disappointed. This location has an easy in & out access drive-thru & they're one of the few stores in all of Tucson for that damn delicious nitro coffee-For now anyway, until other stores rebuild & make room for the machine. The inside of the store is nice & clean with friendly staff. I really love their patio because it has booth-type seating with electric outlets! Perfect coffee escape for a student & anyone who truly appreciates the Starbucks experience!

The only reason for two stars is that they are the only Drive-Thru Starbucks in the area. The drive-thru is extremely dangerous. The parking lot is very tight and has two entrances from different roads into the drive-thru. The staff is extremely incompetent. People leave the drive-thru line constantly because it's nearly impossible to get around. My food wasn't ready so they asked me to "pull around and wait for them to bring it out" as if that was easy. I waited in the most inconvenient spot, which pissed off other drivers. I couldn't even get by the door and tried to call to no answer. Then I called and no answer, so I finally left without my food. I called again to ask for a refund, and she told me I would have to go in to get it. Absolutely absurd for how much money I spend at Starbucks. This also completely goes against the POINT of using a drive-thru. If I wanted to go in somewhere, I would have gone to the much BETTER Starbucks down the road. Also my coffee came out barely luke-warm this morning and I had to wait to drink it to microwave it. Terrible terrible customer service, and I will be reporting this location to Starbucks corporate.

You can't ask for better service- the staff is dedicated to making sure your drink is perfectly made and given with a kind smile to boot! I love anytime I go into a business and the staff is happy and playful- and this Starbucks obviously has a great team and good leadership because they were enjoying their work- something rare today! Then there was the order- Very Berry Hibiscus Tea for me, my daughter opted for a coffee creation only she could come up with and they obliged her writing her own order on her cup- yes she is that type and Connor got his 4 pump only latte that every other Starbucks makes with 6 even when he specifies 4. Happy customers for sure!
I had to disconnect their Wi-Fi connection to even make this review. Waste of time if you need the Internet. Maybe at other times it works but it was .3 MB a second.

I don't know why, but I usually have trouble getting my drink made correctly at most Starbucks. I was out with a friend and we decided to stop for coffee here on a whim, and I was slightly worried. But, my drink actually came out perfectly here. The staff were all very friendly, and it wasn't too busy which was nice. If I was ever in the area again, I would definitely consider stopping in here again.

Coffee is usual Starbucks quality and wifi is ok. But I give it 2 stars because it is, by far, THE coldest Starbucks ( or for that matter, any coffee shop) I've ever been too. Don't know why senior management has dropped the ball here since its been like this for years.  Perhaps a marketing ploy...buy more coffee or leave because its too cold to stay! Note: Don't bring a sweater, bring a winter jacket!

This is my neighborhood Starbucks and it's super consistent with:  unfriendly staff, giving me a poorly made $7 mocha, slow service, and negative vibes.  I go out of my way to stop at other Starbucks to avoid a miserable start to my morning.     ....... However I was short on time,  ordered my drink via the app,  walked in and was greeted by Anthony.    A ray of sunshine,  i heard a happy hello, my drink was perfect and left hearing have a great day.    Thank you Anthony for delivering the Starbucks code of conduct to a much needed store!
There is no reason it should take a staff of 5 more than 5 minutes to pour an iced coffee. This location is consistently the worst in Philly.

Great Starbucks. Thank you to the staff for always being nice and hardworking.   Parking is plentiful. Pro-tip - Get your bagel breakfast sandwich next door.  Another Pro-tip - Chinese "I'm ready for a nap" lunch next door.

This is now one of the worst Starbucks in the area. I don't know what happened during the last year, but the service and the quality of drinks has gone downhill. I have been in Starbucks in other states and even other countries, and the consistency always amazes me. Unfortunately, this 82nd/Craig location has been consistently terrible lately. I gave it another try today, however, and I guess I shouldn't be surprised: in addition to not being able to get a simple drink order entered in correctly, once "fixed," the drink itself was so bad I poured it out before I got in my car. I'm done with this location.

Great place to sit or take-it-to-go coffee house in the area.  Staffed friendly and will go out of their way to remember your name and order.  Although it is small compared to some other Starbucks it is always clean and the service is fast and accurate.  Free wifi and they always have soy milk in stock which is important to my wife and me

While the workers at this Starbucks provide exceptional customer service, I was extremely disheartened to learn that this Starbucks severely lacks in its ethics! They have "recycling bins" and trash bins inside, with a recycling symbol indicating the recycling section. While an employee was changing the recycling bag, I asked them if I should wait until they were done so that I could recycle my cup. Their response: "we don't actually recycle. There's a recycling dumpster outside, but we actually just combine our recycling bin and trash bin and throw it all in the trash dumpster". I'm thankful for this honest employee, as part of the reason I choose Starbucks is for their "support of the environment" and developing counties. This Starbucks purposely misleads it's customers and exploits patrons like myself. I will not be back.

The Starbucks, on the surface, is pretty solid. Its big enough, always clean, has a nice outdoor seating area, and the staff is generally pretty friendly.  However, unfortunately, they are chronically understaffed. Maybe its because the coffee served at the offices across the street is horrific (it is, I worked there), but the lines here are just not acceptable. As I write this review, I am 14th in line, with several behind me, in the middle of the afternoon. I'm here several times a week; this isn't an isolated incident.   Hire more people, Starbucks. There is obviously enough business here to support it.
I really try to stay away from big businesses but no where in Tucson can I get a good up of coffee and good service at the same time, not like I can at a SBUX. Starbucks, I'm glad I can count on you.
Great service, so friendly, accommodating for my weird orders!!! The order taker and Anna, the cashier were awesome!

By far, the best Starbucks that I have visited.  I came to this location, since recruiters like coming here for informal interviews. "Let's have a coffee and discuss your experience." The staff gave me a free beverage since I asked for a military discount. Most locations don't even do this.  However, this location hardly had any clean tables.  However, it is a location that I would come back to, if ever I needed a good dose of Starbucks coffee.
A unique, one-of-a-kind coffee experience.  Nothing else like it.  Also a great meeting spot.
Slow but I know they are trying! Between mobile orders and the drive thru be prepared to wait there 20+ minutes unfortunately

Horrible! Ordered ahead on the app, then sat in the drive thru for 20 full minutes behind 4 cars at 2pm on a weekday. When we finally got to the window, we sat another *8 minutes* while the employee tried to figure out our order (very complex: an eggnog latte and a pastry). There were many employees inside, seemingly quite busy, but doing what? Finally got my latte, and it was tasteless and scorched--ended up having to throw it out. What is going on in this place??
Friendly end unique staff which you don't get at a lot of other Starbucks. Really has the broadripple charm. My drinks have always been made just right and never had anything I didn't like

Consistently the best Starbucks I visit...and I'm all over the area. They always remember my name and preferences and despite being busy, I never wait long. Whenever I can, I route myself to this Starbucks for my morning (and sometimes afternoon) jolt.
Two stars for willingness to try. But honestly, how hard is it to make a drink? 3 days in a row, still not done right. Might as well be Dunkin, seems to be a daily hire and fire going on here too.

The reviews are mediocre, why?  So this location isn't bad. It's convenient and the baristas have always been good, no rudeness, but yet not overly friendly. Parking is usually available.  Drive thru doesn't usually take too long. I went to this location almost daily when I was studying for my Real Estate exam. I'd sit there for a couple of hours. Oh, and Dennis, a barista who was at the Big Bend location is now at the 301 location. He's awesome, friendly. My drinks have always been on point except once and they promptly made me a new one. All in all, its not a bad location.

This is not a Starbucks you really want to drive to. Though if it is a weekend you can park in the Tyndall Garage right behind it for free.  This spot can be super busy, but they're also pretty efficient. For a Starbucks this location is pretty charming and cozy. They even have the fireplace going in the winter. When it's sunny outside sitting in the courtyard is seriously lovely.
Typical Starbucks you wait in line you pay it's mediocre.. Think I'm just going to get a cup at the gas station lol

Amazing experience here with the staff! My lattes were made perfectly! The baristas made me feel a personal connection, almost like I've known them my entire life! 10/10 recommend. My favorite Starbucks in town!
I love it here! The staff is all super sweet- especially Martha! Great atmosphere and plenty of space to sit. My only gripe is that parking can be a pain. Still- this is my favorite Starbucks by far

Staff is very nice. Wait time is usually not too bad unless, of course, you go during peak times. Drinks are always made to order. Never had issues at this location. Parking is sometimes limited; seems like this place really needs a parking garage for the amount of traffic flow!

The folks behind the counter at this Starbucks do the company proud. They are friendly and efficient. If you're getting your order "to go" you'll likely be very happy with this location.  On the other hand, the decor & layout of this Starbucks is disorganized and looks worn out. The furniture seems randomly strewn about the room and there is little here that would make you want to hang out very long. And isn't that what Starbucks wants us to do? Time for a makeover here.

Imani's actions this morning really deserve a 5 star review. I ordered to the wrong store and she quickly corrected the issue! Had my drinks in less than 5 minutes. Thank you so much for making the customer experience so wonderful.

Slowest drive thru I've ever been in. I've been to this drive thru multiple times and I'm waiting 10-15 minutes every time. The more annoying part is by the time you get to the window they're just starting to get your order ready, and most of the time there are no customers inside. So how can't you keep up with just a drive thru. I could just go inside but it's not as convenient.   Another problem with the drive thru is that the menu is so far away and on such an angle from the screen it's impossible to read. I don't go to Starbucks that often so I like to look at the menu and I can't see it at all.

Free wifi. Nice seating. Pretty decent baristas. The two black girls had MAJOR attitude but I love my Starbucks so I'll be back. It's definitely wayyyyyy more expensive than my Starbucks at home. I can get a venti iced chai tea latte for 4.23 at home. I got a grande here for 5.81. Crazy!!!

Have no idea how multibillion dollar company can't keep their shelves stocked properly to service their clients. You sit in line for 10 minutes for them to tell you they don't have something. Not just on one occasion multiple occasions

Used to frequent this place a lot in the past. But, recently the drink choices have been limited, to items being out. My favorite drink flavor is Cinnamon dolce, and it has been out. Hurry up Starbucks and get it in, or I can't buy your coffee.
So far I've had nothing but fast and accurate service no matter the time of day. Very happy with the service.

Just a Starbucks. And TIIIIIINY. Seriously. While waiting for my coffee, I was moving this way and that in order to get out of people's way! I saw a few people studying/working - I can't imagine how they do it! So small and crowded! I'd never get anything done.

They're always scrambling around and I wish I could help them! I always think that hitting the order button from home will help and my order will be ready by the time I arrive but it's not. Not sure what can be done to make getting coffee at this location better.   Sincerely, an Apple crisp macchiato lover.

The only difference between this Starbucks and any other one is the staff and the atmosphere, which mostly consists of college students. The staff does a decent job of making coffee. I have not had a bad order at this place so far. Cheers.

I stopped at the drive through for a cappuccino.   Service was very polite and very friendly but a bit slow.  The cappuccino was the hottest I have gotten in a long, long time.  Definitely worth the slight detour I took to get there.
The staff is great here - fast, pleasant and always in a good mood considering the fast pace in the mornings of caffeine deprived customers

This location is extremely slow and rude. I'm not actually sure how Starbucks still allows their staff to be blatantly rude and disrespectful. I've called to complain about 3 times now and nothing has been done. I live literally about this place, yet I get in my car and drive 4 miles to a different location. It's a shame.
I have been here twice over the last 3 years. Got my latte and got out.  No issues - but not a HG (Holy Grail) either.
They always get my order right but I wish they had more tables because this place is always poppin' and there's not enough room to study. The baristas are so kind and always welcoming!

I have to agree that this appears to be a slow Starbucks.  Then again, maybe it was just that they served the wrong drink without a name and then moved on to the next last time I was here.  When this was corrected and the right drink served, it was accompanied by a "Next Drink is on the House" voucher.  That was classy, and saved the place from a two-star rating.  Free wifi as well for what it is worth.  That is unfortunately outweighed in my opinion by the store's location--the largest, crappiest parking lot sprawl in all of Tucson.

So tired of their uncooked bacon in their sandwiches! I ask them to cook it separately, they over cooked the entire sandwich and somehow the bacon is still uncooked and so rubbery it looks raw. Bread was so heard I chipped a tooth! And the bacon was still not cooked! Common sense! Take the top bread off the sandwich and put it through the cooker. It will not cook with the bread on top! Chipped a tooth because they thought if they over cooked the sandwich it would cook the bacon. I am pissed!
friendly but slooowwww service.  The normal Starbucks menu - over priced as always.  Still enjoy the coffees in spite of the cost.

Their ice machine was broken so I couldn't order an iced coffee. Service was quick, though, after I decided on a hot beverage. Condiments/extras were well-stocked, although counter space was limited. The hot coffee I settled on was very good. No consolation for the unavailable iced coffee.
The guys here are fantastic. I don't think I could've survived college without them. Coffee sucks, but I keep coming back for the baristas and atmosphere.

I go to all of the locations, because I am all over the city some days. This is a great location.  The employees are friendly, fast, and the order is never wrong. I only use the drive-up, but it is always fast. There are Starbuck's in the area that could learn a lot from them-some are really bad.
Employees are pleasant, my order was completed quickly and correctly, and the store is very clean.

I went to this Starbucks and ordered a Passion tea and lemonade. The barista informed me they were out of sweetner, and I definitely like it sweet!  I was a bit deterred at first, but she offered an alternative to try it with vanilla. I was a little wary, but gave it a shot. She asked me to wait and try it before taking off to make sure that I was happy, and not stuck with an unpleasant drink. That meant a lot to me because it's such a small thing, but she cared. It must be hard dealing with coffee snobs all day, but she still showed outstanding customer service. I appreciate her, and I'll be back because the drink was also perfection. Their skills and service far surpassed my expectations. I thank her and them very much!

I have been coming to this Starbs for 4+ years. I have been here in the early morning, midday and evening and the staff is always very friendly and efficient. For a few months I went in for my coffee around the same time, always using my app and found that the staff began to recognize me. Nonetheless, the staff is what makes this place work. Starbucks coffee is the same wherever you go, but it's the people that make me come back.

Starbucks is Starbucks, you can get the same drink at any location. I come to this Starbucks because I enjoy the staff. I especially enjoy the positive energy of a young lady named Hailey. She always has a smile when she hands me my drink and seems genuinely interested when she asks me how my day is.

I only do drive thru with this location. Pros: Specialty drinks are usually on point. That is huge. Cons: Convenient yet horrible location. Its right next to 2-75, next to the off ramp. When pulling in off of Hillsborough, you have to go all the way around the building, next to the car line. Some people do not realize this so they sit at the entrance to the store. Its typically always busy. The line can be long and slow moving. There's almost no parking, and that is why I just do drive thru. If you do park, face Hillsborough otherwise you'll be blocked in by the drive thru line (parking directly in front of the store). You have to make a right out of Starbucks from the drive thru side, u-turn, to get back up to the light on Hillsborough. But hey, with all these cons, the fact that they can make a drink right makes it worth the stop-so that's why I won't rate this location lower than 4 stars :)

The worst Starbucks I've ever been too. The bathrooms were super dirty and had no toilet paper. Plus it looked like someone had peed all over the floor. The staff couldn't have been bothered by it. Ordered our coffee and had to guess when it was up because they don't call anyone's names. Super slow service and it's hardly busy. Never going back to this one.

I'm not familiar with this location, but I stopped in on break from a short road trip, and was greeted by three staff members, who were all very pleasant! They joked with me, and even shared some Starbucks knowledge with me.   Shout out to these superstars: Andorra, Kristen, and Trina. Wonderful spirits. Great customer service.  I *will* be back the next time I'm in Philly!

Conveniently located right across from City Hall, nestled in the middle of the expanse of the Courtyard by Marriott's lobby.   It's really just a counter with some high-top tables along the windows of Filbert Street.  Baristas make a decent cappuccino but add too much milk unless you ask for it extra dry.
Renovated to slow down service.  I understand long lines but 3 cars and still over 15 mins?  Will skip this one from now on!

The usual Starbucks... the trend of the service is definitely downwards.  To be sure, the beverages are fine. The food is worth skipping.  The staff, though, continues to be lackluster and underwhelming. It is like they don't even try anymore...   On occasion though, we do get surprised by competent and energetic service... occasionally.

This is a cute little stand alone starbucks (not tucked into a target store like so many)  nice decor staff is friendly....I do not drink starbucks coffee but I like that I can get a hot or iced black sweetened tea.  Found myself chinchillin here with my laptop for awhile one day and it was a nice place to be......

Knowing how hard it is to find the the You Are Here City Edition mugs i was glad to stop by this starbucks store when we were passing TN and thus adding Nashville to my collection! The service was good!
The employees set this Starbucks apart. They are always very friendly and keep the establishment in top notch condition, which says a lot given all the traffic that passes through here.

This review is for the Starbucks located near the food court between Terminal B & C. It is not a review of the new location within Terminal C.  Please see my attached photo of the pure filth that is ALWAYS present at this location. I'm not sure who the manager is of this DUMP, but HE/SHE needs to be replaced. Someone with some backbone needs to FIX this location, otherwise I'm hoping that the airport authorities pull the plug on this location for health concerns as well as super nasty service. I'm come thru this area way way too often and I'm always expecting that change for the better, but tonight... it was the worst ever. The employees here don't know the first thing about customer service and keeping a clean food, coffee experience. They all need to go, and that starts at the top. Otherwise, please PHL Airport Authorities, it's time to shut this place DOWN!   AVOID AT ALL COST.   Better options are located as you walk towards Terminal C.   If I could rate Zero to Negative stars, I would....

I can honestly say I do not review many Starbucks because they seem to be all the same however, this location is a must see. It is more of a work of art in such a large space. They also have open make music with plenty a room to sit every Saturday
The people here really seem nice, but 9/10 they get my drink wrong. I've gotten cold sandwiches, iced drinks with no ice, lattes with no milk...it's just bizarre
This is inside the hotel, so it's convenient if you are staying there. The place is mobbed in the morning, but like most Starbucks, they work quickly and always have a smile.

Always friendly staff. Always make my latte right. Love the protein snack packs and different foid items. The parfaits are low fat and low calorie which is a good alternative to DD that doesnt have such items. Outside is clean too which is nice.

This is one of the worst Starbucks I've been to in awhile.  Staff is super rude. The coffee that was brought home this morning from this location was so disgusting. There was about an inch and a half of vanilla syrup at the bottle of the cup(literally could see out the other side. Just like water) then the expired shots sat above the small pond of vanilla syrup then milk and ice and then 3 more inches of air into cup hit lid. So as one would who spent 11 + dollars on a fancy drink would u take it back. So I took them back. They couldn't have cared less didn't want them back didn't even open the window to speak to me until they finished my new drink to replace whatever it was they made the first time. I don't think I would be so upset about it if it was the first time but this happens often at this location. So I definitely won't be returning to this location at all.

Great location. The shop is not too busy even on a weekend afternoon. There's a lot of comfortable seating and the inside has a spacious feel with many leather armchairs and several small tables scattered about, plus a larger table for students to spread out their books. Food selection is good. The staff are friendly and fast.
Although I am not a big fan of coffee, their hot chocolate & sandwiches are definitely worthwhile. The inside of the business is very well decorated, and a nice, quiet place to get work done.

This place had a really bad costumer service! I always ordered a coffee frap in the morning/everyday so that means im pretty sure to myself i know exactly what it taste like. So i tried this starbucks location inside the safeway and ordered my favorite coffee frap! So heres the first one i took a sip not once but 4-5 times and it taste nothing like a blended ice so i told the girl which her name is (BRIE)  if she can fix it for me because it doesnt taste like a coffee frap to me and then she said "im just doing exactly what it says on our measurements" which i get it so i ask her nicely if she can redo it because it just taste like a blended ice but then when she said after! that if i wanted my coffee sweet like caramel or put a sweetener in it then might as well pay extra for the syrup to add onto it!!! I was like wait if i wanted a caramel frap then i couldve just ordered it but she was just keeps telling me over and over that shes only following the measurements but i was like im not satisfied because i know exactly what coffee frap taste like so i had enough because she wont do anything about it so i was like you know what go ahead just charge me to some syrup or sweetener to it! So i waited and after 3 mins she handed me the caramel frap and took a sip again and it taste exactly the first one she gave me!! I dont know if she just put the caramel syrup on top so im so very disappointed at this point. I told her again that the caramel frap she gave me taste exactly the first one she gave which it taste like nothing but a blended ice like no flavor at all and she goes back at it again and  tellin me that shes just following the measurements. So im getting pissed to be honest. I told her to go ahead and try it so she knows exactly what im talking about cause i saw a leftover on the blender so i ask her to try it and then she did! Ater that she goes "if you want me to add more extra pump of the caramel or sweetener then i can do that but you have to work with me"!and i was like im not trying to be mean or anything i dont even wanna give you a hard time making another one thats why ive been asking her nicely to try it first before she'll give it to me but she keep giving me a hard time telling me a bunch of stuff that she cant fix it because shes just following what it says on the measurements!! What a costumer service you got! She wont even try to fix it and charging me for extra sweetener !! Why!!?? because it taste like a blended ice??!! Never going back to this place again esp when i know that this girl name (brie) is working there. Very bad costumer service i dont even wanna put even a single star for this one!!
Clean and modern. Staff is very friendly. Thought we'd try this new location and we were not disappointed.

Convenient location, but Starbucks need to install some sound absorbing tiles in here.  Can hear the counter staff laughing and talking clearly, not what I want for a rest stop.  Sorry for the bad rating.  I am usually an Easy 5 Star.  The rest room had TP scraps all over the floor, needs policing.

The Barista Kathleen was super helpful in helping me decide on a drink. I didn't know exactly what I wanted and she suggested the frozen strawberry lemonade becasue I said I wanted something without coffee. It was blended perfectly and definitely refreshing on a hot day. I don't think it is on their normal menu, but I will definitely be ordering that from now on. I will say their prices are a little outrageous, but people are willing to pay it. The clay terrace location is my favorite to go to because they have so much outdoor seating.

This store is wonderful! I come here almost every day and the staff is great. It gets very busy during the morning rush so take that into account. But the new ordering from the Starbucks App is perfect to avoid waiting in line. The manager, Merry, has a great staff of baristas right now- probably the best in recent years. The drinks are always great.

This is my favorite Starbucks in Tucson. The staff are always friendly, my orders have always been correct, and they accept Starbucks gift cards (Not all locations accept gift cards).  I like the outside patio, since I always find the drive-thru is packed. It's a great place to meet up with friends and they have couches and many tables for groups. I would recommend this location.
Always messes mobile orders. Employees don't even look like they know what they're doing. Worst Starbucks ever

Came in around 4:30PM on a Sunday. Steven took my order and he did very well for a new employee. I ordered a Twix Frapp (since I have a free drink) which is a lot of syrups and stuff in it.   My drink came in about 3 minutes and it's blended nicely. They forgot the java chips but I didn't mind it.

Super slow drive-thru service. I know I need to give myself plenty of time to go to this location and the only reason I return is because I work nearby. They also seem to run out of things more than other locations I go to frequently. Staff is usually friendly, though they often seem flustered or stressed. Not sure what's going on at this Starbucks location...
Pretty fast considering where this is located..staff is always friendly :)

I ordered a Tall Maple Pecan Latte', with no ice. The barista called me up to the counter to clarify that I didn't want ice. I've ordered "no ice" many times. So I didn't quite understand her confusion. I figured she must be new but she acted as if I was the confused one. I simply restated what I ordered. And she said "ok" with a glib smirk.   What she gave me was half of a tall latte. She Evidently she figured out that I was trying to defraud Starbucks and she was going to protect the company's bottom line.   Again, I've ordered Tall lattes with no ice many times. Never before has a barista stiffed me half a cup of a latte. I paid $4.86 for a half of a tall Latte. Nice.
I really like this Starbucks and Its employees. They were really helpful being as though I did not know what I wanted to order.

This Starbucks location inside the Greenwood Target had some very friendly Baristas working yesterday. Through the Target app; Target has been offering 20% off drinks. I took advantage of that discount several times before the coupon expires. They were unfortunately out of my favorite breakfast sandwiches; however that is something I encounter frequently at many Starbucks locations. The Barista made a delicious iced Americano Cold Foam Irish Creme for me and I got one of my favorite treats - a brownie.

We tried Mobile order for this location as the drive thru was super busy and it was quick, easy and super friendly! Due to social distancing, they are not allowing people inside so you wait outside six feet apart, tell the barista your name when you get to the door and the grab your order! Super simple and no contact as they put it on a table for you and then you take it from there. I recommend this option if you know you are going in advance. The food and drink is wonderful as always. The app lets you customize your order as you would through the drive thru or in person so don't be scared to try that out as I was originally. Thanks for my morning pick me up!

Remarkable customer service!! The staff here are incredible. They took their time helping us find the right coffee for my girlfriend and I. They were a joy to deal with. Will definitely be going here anytime I need some Starbucks!

Sucked. Coffee was awful. I told the barista she made me the wrong drink and she threw attitude at me and said "yo dat' be yo drink!"  After my brain translated what she said I asked her to remake it and she refused. I finally got the manager and she made the drink correct. No apology offered though.

Starbucks service varies and usually always up on standards, but this place is just so awful that I'm leaving a review on yelp for the first time.  I was polite in ordering a pastry, but the lady not only screamed at me because my debit card didn't went through (like, is that even my fault?), and I got yelled at again because apparently she called out to me "five times" (according to her) while I was on an important phone call I suddenly got from the bank and that I didn't respond her right away. I would feel sorry if I was in her way during a busy time, but there wasn't even a line. The lady screamed rudely at me and did not even get my order right.  Simply put, there are many Starbucks near the area that are hundred times better, so just don't come here if you don't want your good mood ruined.
Friendly staff.  But extremely slow. There wasn't a line and if there was it was 4 people. Took forever to get my coffee. Guess drive thru is better at this location.

Starbucks espresso is awful, in fact its not even real espresso.  BUT... this location has a Clover Machine (one of only 2 in the state of Florida) which is one of the more perfect methods for brewing coffee.  So if you like brewed coffee this SB actually has terrific offerings with their select beans, just be sure to ask for the Clover.  And its only a $.30 premium over their regular crap brewed sludge.  But if you want quality espresso you're still out of luck.
Warm coffee and did not warm banana nut bread  Need better service  Cold snowy day and served chilled coffee
Fast, friendly service. Decent number of tables and power outlets. No complaints here.

This Starbucks is absolutely horrible. I work at another but this is the only one on my way into Doylestown. I have never received a drink here that is made right and it's extremely frustrating. My cold brew is watery, my frappuccinos are completely ice and no flavor and my lattes have dead bitter shots. They take forever when there's absolutely no reason. Never go here. Ever.

I got here at like 8am. I ordered my drink at 8:13am and it wasn't even ready until 8:36! Lmao like that how packed it was in there and I was sick! Like I couldn't believe it took so long to make my drink. I told the lovely lady at the cashier register my name is Cyn and she wrote Sam. So when my drink was ready I didn't even know bc she wrote the wrong name. On the bright side the inside was very nice. Cute like your average coffee shop.

This has probably been the worst experience I've ever had at a Bucks. I ordered my drinks and they apparently ran out of the seasoning but didn't tell the person taking the drinks. They then said they sent someone upstairs to get it. It then took an HOUR to get back. Then I still had to ask for my drink two more times before I got it. Ridiculous!!

This starbucks is insane - it takes about three minutes to take one person's order (even though the orders are rarely complicated), and there's usually one line open while the dude behind the counter moves nonchalantly and is also in charge of "microwaving" sandwiches and dealing with every kind of pastry. Right now, on Sunday at 10:20, there's a line out the door and one guy at the register and one person is making drinks. Kind of a general trend in Starkbucks, it seems: I almost wonder if there's some executive order to tell a) managers to understaff their stores and b) employees to take their time. I understand if they're overworked, but  there seems to be no interest in moving the line along. It's too bad, because it's a nice, big space, but this place needs new management.

So I'm slightly concerned about my health after my visit today. As I walked up to the register I noticed a display container of string cheese sticks sitting there by the register. Picked one up and it was completely room temperature. I asked the Starbucks partner if these needed to be refrigerated to which she blankly stared at me, then realized yes yes dairy does need to be refrigerated. She said her associate (the little redheaded man standing nearby) apparently had forgotten to put them in the cooler. She then proceeded to place the box of cheese sticks in the cooler even though they had clearly been sitting out for at least an hour. It's been a while since I've done SafeServe, but pretty sure that was a violation of food safety....who knows what else isn't being kept at the proper temperature. I'll know if I get food sick tonight who to blame it on.
They had 3 reserves (1 from Cameroon, 2 from Ethiopia). Didn't see reserves at any other Starbucks in area. Also spacious and friendly employees (thanks Joseph).

The workers are friendly and go as fast as they can. But really Starbucks needs another store in the Nashville airport. I know there's 2. Both stores have lines that are crazy long!! So I'm not sure why Starbucks doesn't invest in another store. Even if it's a small one it'd help. Lines at this one at 4:30AM we're wrapped around. Not worth the wait.  But again the workers are friendly and go as fast as they can. Too bad you can't use the app to to an order pickup like the real stores. That might help the long lines.

This is the closest Starbucks to my house, I go every morning almost. There is one girl there that has messed my drink up three times in a row now, whenever anyone else makes it its PERFECT. When I went in to get it changed the first time she was super rude about it, so another barista fixed it and gave me an on the house card for my next drink. Since then my drink has been wrong AGAIN 2 times, and anytime she sees me she gives me the dirtiest look. I am not sure how to handle this. But here is a review. Service is quick, friendly, other than this situation.
Always the best service during the week in the mornings. The staff is fantastic and friendly. I go mostly to see them and they make great drinks.

So, this review will be in the perspective of somebody else. There was this elderly man clearly with a vocal problem. So the customer pointed to the point that he wanted. But the barista insisted in him telling her what "the sign says". He was clearly pointing at the item he wanted. He did eventually get the lemon pastry he had asked for.  But this was extremely rude.

So me and  my son were at this morning and as we were picking up his order this young lady was slightly rude and threw his straw on the counter and we left so I will be contacting the store manager to let them know what happened .

This is the closest Starbucks to my home, but I travel to Riverview because I don't care for this one. I mostly do not like the staff, who are always nice but are very loud and "jokey" with another, which -in a small SBux- drastically diminishes professionalism and the "coffee-shop" atmosphere that many SBux customers enjoy (and the reason behind going to a coffee shop).
When I am in town, this is my place for espresso. Curtis even knows what I want without asking, triple shot on ice. My go to coffee shop when I am here in Nola
Fast friendly service, great drinks and prices right. Good is alright since they changed suppliers. Blueberry scones need more blueberries.

Great location and quiet enough to have coffee with friends and hear one another. Love the leather seats!! There's a lot of people who come in to study or work and it's a really good place for that.   The blonde espresso is awesome, if you don't like Starbucks coffee cause it tastes burnt to you give it a shot you won't be disappointed!!!

Horrible latte - I ordered a tall latte and it was scorching hot; I actually burnt my tongue! And the overheated milk rendered it so bitter :( I asked them to remake it 'warm' and it was still super hot...I couldn't drink it. The girls seemed to take it personally and weren't very open to my feedback but I think the machine needs to be recalibrated or something.
When my order is actually correct, it's great. 3/5 times it is right. Staff is friendly just not very attentive to orders.

I like that this SB is situated right beside Save On Foods. There's always an abundance of parking. It does get pretty busy in there but their staff seems to make it up with their pleasant greetings and smiles. I like doing my leisurely reading in the corner of where their couches are.

Delicious sandwich but now i am left food poisoned, dizzy, breathless and breathing hard after eating it. The green tea is good too but it didnt help. I am angry!!!! I just want to be ableto eat regular food n not feel bad!!!

I like Starbucks for the most part. It just doesn't make sense to pay $6 for a drink and they can't even fill the cup up all the way... there's no way they look at it and know it looks right. Wish they had employees that knew what they were doing. I haven't even taken a sip and this is what it looks like! Hate to complain and I normally don't, but paying $6 for this is kind of frustrating.

I went in today, ordered my coffee, took a seat and began studying. Everything was good except it was freezing in there. I look around and everyone has there winter coats on. I had to sit with my winter coat on so I wasn't freezing. For the price of those drinks I don't understand why there being cheap with there heat.

I do not go to Starbucks often, but I do know the level of standards I've seen and accept as a Starbucks customer.  This Starbucks could use an update. The staff is great athough, I did feel a bit rushed even though it wasn't crazy busy, they were very friendly and happy. I was so confused about where the line started so that sucked because a few people got in line who would have been after me before I realized it, but that's on me I guess.  I had a Passion Tango Tea with a little bit of sweetener. Not bad, but I probably won't be going back anytime soon.

Used to be a great location to come anytime to study - service was great and atmosphere was enjoyable. Not sure what happened because on what you think would be an easy Friday morning at the coffee shop, there is no music in the store, the employees' conversation from behind the counter fills the whole place instead, and they're mopping at lunchtime! Time for a change. #makestarbucksgreatagain

Like other reviews state there is usually a decent wait for this location. I have come here once and gotten an order that tasted completely off and the last time I ordered prior to arriving. The app said it was in stock, they verified that it was ready for pickup, and then at the window they said they were out. No reason I should have waited the whole time

Just went through the drive through and the staff was over the top friendly. The girl who took my order offered suggestions and the guy at the window greeted me with a huge smile. Refreshing to see such a friendly staff!
Starbucks are generally great, this one is no different. Consistent great service and good Chai Lattes!  Their bacon gouda is the bomb also.

I HATE Starbucks.  After trying to find a reason to overpay for coffee, still can't find a reason. This place is just like every other I've been to. Pretentious yuppies that try to be something they are not. They want to save the world but drink their coffee in this horrible joint. The employees will always treat you like a idiot because you know nothing about the menu.  If you love Starbucks and want to act trendy, you'll love this place. I'll stick to my truck stop coffee that someone made 3 days prior.

Just what PHL needed. It's conveniently located between B and C terminal in the PHL marketplace, and close to the rocking chairs where you can watch the planes come and go. The baristas all seem happy, but being that this Starbucks has only been open for a week, there is plenty of time to be disgruntled like many other airport employees. One thing that surprised me though was that they didn't have their own wifi. I know the airport has free wifi, but it's incredibly slow, so I was looking forward to an alternative. Oh well, it won't keep me from coming back I suppose.

This Starbucks is always hopping! Love stopping here for a drink or treat while walking around the outlet mall. Don't come here expecting to grab a quick drink and get out...there is always a long line. However, even with the long line I always seem to get my beverage in a timely manner. They have lots of seating outside and also so inside. I do think they need to rethink the layout of the store because when people are waiting for their drinks they are blocking the entrance. All in all good service and great drinks.

One of the worst Starbucks in the area. We went through the drive through(which is a nice feature of this location), and he staff seemed friendly. I was impressed UNTIL we drove up to the pick-up window. I came to realize the baristas were extremely disorganized. The barista who took our order was also delivering us the drinks we delivered. She took frequent stops to deal with other customers orders while still processing ours. Upon paying the barista walked away for a good 2-3 minutes without saying a word. We started wondering where are tea was. After about a few minutes she handed us our drinks, and did not say anything again!!!! I was extremely disappointed with the deserved. I'll go to another Starbucks next time!!!

-- COVID-19 review --  I've visited this Starbucks several, many, beaucoup times before and I'm not sure how I didn't write a review, but here goes nothing...  Just to save time, I ordered ahead, using the app, I love this order ahead feature... I love, love, love this feature. I was using it before Covid-19. I love it, I  really, really do; do you think I love it? You're right, I do.   I ordered a Venti green tea Matcha Frappuccino with coconut milk please, because your girl is lactose intolerant, but I still wanna get my matcha on, so there you have it coconut milk it is. I don't like that they up charge you $.60 for coconut milk, I mean don't charge me because I have a genetic in-deficiency, hello. At any rate...  I entered the facility and per City of NOLA Covid regulations, I had my mask on. I walked to the counter to pick up my drink, they verified me, I picked up my drink & I proceeded to leave.   The barista's hold the drinks behind the counter because I believe there's been some issues with folks doing a 'snatch & grab' of drinks they didn't order or pay for. It's all good for me, no hassle that just reassures me that my drink is super fresh and hasn't been tampered with; DON'T SLIP ME NO MICKIE'S.   I tipped them on the app on my way out the door. I was happy, I received super fast service, the baristas were polite, they greeted me as I entered & asked if I wanted a straw: CLASSIC STARBUCKS BEHAVIOR.   Annnnnndddddd my drink tasted exactly as it should, coconut milk and all... I was very pleased about this because I ordered a Venti so there was lots of DRINK to be DRUNK.   Clean facilities, inside & outside. I was able to do my normal stick and move caper... I was in & I was outta there in a jiffy.   VERY limited street parking.

They have the usual stock of Starbucks drinks, the service is friendly and the line moves quickly enough.  I was disappointed that they did not have the usual lids for iced beverages, instead depending on the open-holed dome style lids.  These are cumbersome and difficult and not exactly the go-to lid for people about to board an airplane.  They need to do a better job of stocking in that area.  They also need to widen the queue area, as most clientele in this location are traveling with cumbersome baggage of some sort making things a little tight.  While they have two registers open, it can be difficult to get to the 2nd register due to to tight quarters.

Every single time I order a blond pour over these people look at like I'm retarded because I prefer it actually poured over instead of on tap. I don't want my coffee on tap, I want it freshly poured over. If your going to make faces every time I order it then don't offer it. I can easily go to another coffee shop that I'm sure would have no problems serving me what I want with a smile. Get it together and hire people that are happy to do there jobs!

This Starbucks is my 2nd least favorite (the least is on Hillsborough at I-275) because it's smaller than most and it's located in a small plaza at a very busy intersection. Most times I'm driving west on East Bay so I'd have to do a U-turn at the lights, then squeeze into one of the tight parking spaces, or drive through the small and narrow parking area to get to the back for the Drive Thru.  Inside is smaller than most, too. There are a few tables and seats, but I don't recall a community table. I believe there's just 1 restroom for male and female and the door is right across from the registers. I generally don't like to sit near the restrooms and would like to see it out of sight.  There's a small patio area in front that I rarely sit in because 1) it's next to the drive thru so there's always cars driving by, 2) the busy intersection of East Bay and Starkey isn't much to look at and 3) most times it's too hot and humid in Florida!  Most times the baristas were just okay, not super friendly or talkative. One time I asked for no ice with my iced drink but I was given ice, but most times my drinks were made perfectly.  I really only stop by this Starbucks because it's convenient; definitely not one where I'd want to hang out, simply because the parking is just too small. Sorry.

I stop by here almost everyday before work and I have never had a bad experience. The staff is SO friendly. They are quick and efficient while still being chatty and kind. Even if it's a little out of the way, I drive to this Starbucks because they are amazing. You NEED to try their iced matcha latte with soy milk. It is AMAZING! Thanks for all the smiles this summer Starbucks!
The barista, McKenzie did such a wonderful job making my drink and it was done super fast! Super helpful and kind! Love seeing her there!
They just shove that pike down your  Throat.  Pour over request greeted with A smirk and wait 15 minutes

Love this Starbucks! One of my fave locations... Partially cause I can walk there from my house.. Lol.  Staff is super friendly and always happy to accomadate any request. Love sitting out on the patio in the cooler air and watch traffic or chill inside on the couch with friends.  Cake pops are my new fave thing!  Def worth you checking them out!

I had a terrible experience at this location from the moment I walked in. Nobody greeted me upon arrival which, if you are busy is understandable, but there was only one person ahead of me and all other customers had been served. I waited patiently till the customer was finished, then was left there while the employees joked and spoke to each other. Again, I typically don't fuss about things. I myself have worked in customer service and understand that often, what a customer perceives runs deeper. Finally someone steps up to take my order with yet again no greeting. I receive my order and proceeded to add my fixings. The station was extremely low on almost every item. The cream was empty, the trash was filled to the top, there were zero napkins, and the sugar had lumps in it to a point where you could not get any sugar out at all. I had to open the sugar and remove hardened clogs for the next poor customer. This is unacceptable! Considering there were at minimum three employees standing around, you would think at least one would take pride in their work. It is safe to say I will not be returning to this location.
What are you going to expect from Starbucks? Their coffee is overpriced shit. You may as well go to Wawa or Dunkin donuts to get better coffee

Made a stop here before heading back from a weekend get-a-way. There's no drive-thru which I didn't like. There is a parking lot and the Starbucks​ is located at​ the end of the first little strip mall surrounded by other little shops. This location was nice and clean. The service was nothing less than very friendly. Also,​ this location is located up the street from USF which I'm sure can become pretty packed with college students meeting and studying. The bathroom was also nice and clean. If I'm ever in the area again I'd definitely make a stop for my favorite cup of brew.

Though I'm not usually a fan of the corporate coffee, I will give this place props for being one of the more cozier places to study at with a group of uni classmates on a weeknight.  They've got a lot of table space that's prime for a few hours of nose-to-the-book cramming, plus they keep this place amazingly clean.  The vibe is down-to-earth enough that you won't have to worry about gossipy Main Line socialites complaining about their latest Brazilian blowout fiasco.  Parking in the lot can be a pain, but be sure to check behind the building and on the street if it looks too full.

Usually they're very good; but last Time they got my order wrong (was supposed to be iced instead). Normally that would be okay, I don't mind waiting and people make mistakes, but then they used dairy milk instead of oat milk in the replacement. I am lactose intolerant, so now I will be in pain for a couple days.  Just need to pay more attention to the label and what people say over the intercom.

Overall, this is a nice Starbucks. It does get a little busy, but what Starbucks am doesn't? The staff is friendly and the drinks are good. The only challenge is location. This one is on the corner of. Amino shopping center and it is a little cramped. Overall, pretty good experience.

This Starbucks is inside of the Target in Town and Country. I was pleased with the service that I received, but I was disappointed that this location does not have breakfast sandwiches. That was the whole point of why I came here before work last week. I was devastated, but my mocha Frappuccino was still delightful.
Ugh. This one is always busy and staff is just not friendly. The place where you get your cream and sugar is always a mess and it's crowded and tight.

It would be amazing if this location was actually operating at 4:30am like advertised. I don't know how many mornings I sit at the menu to end up driving through without coffee, and to see everyone inside not working.

I travel a lot for work. I pride myself on trying all the Starbucks. Today I was disappointed . I made my way to this Location. It is the Dirtiest Starbucks. By far the worst one I have ever come across.

The customer service from the girls here is great!  Taylor, Grayson, Michelle,  and Gabby all work so hard to make sure they get it right.  I had a real "karen" in front of me complaining the other day and Taylor dealt with it with grace and class,  which really,  they don't get paid enough to put up with a bunch of crap but she was nice about it anyways.   Great work girls!
Busiest Starbucks in town but they always have fast and courteous service. And the lines usually move quickly so it is worth it coming here.

The boy working at the drive through this morning was extremely rude and just stared at me without telling me my total and even though he was talking to his coworkers and obviously wasn't talking on the headset he was cold with me and hardly spoke a word and when I said have a nice day he turned around and shot a thumbs up at me. He was extremely rude and disrespectful.

Check under your lid before leaving, as the drinks are consistently underfilled here. This is not a complaint based on just one poor experience. I work nearby, so I've been here tons of times. I am sick and tired of paying for a grossly underfilled drink. The only reason I'm giving 2 stars instead of 1 is that the staff are friendly, and they maintain reasonable wait times despite frequent long lines. However, at the end of the day it's the product that counts, and it's insulting to not get sufficient bang for your buck.
Worst service ever, got my order completely wrong and it tasted like trash!!  Don't come to this Starbucks, you'll be wasting your money
One of the most efficient Starbucks I've ever experienced. Friendly staff and clean environment. I can see why it's so popular. Downside, it is NOT conducive for learning. It is very busy and loud.

I'm a lifelong starbucks drinker, love the place and the happy culture and the service. I went in this morning and the two baristas were major grumpsters. I know it was 6am but man I do NOT go to Starbucks for that sort of attitude. Total turn-off and not a great way to start my day, having to put up with extreme attitude to get my coffee.

Drive thru is the worst ever. I waited in line via drive thru because I had my infant twins with me and just wanted a little coffee. I waited for over 20 minutes for 4 guys to order, I never made it to even place my order cause my babies started crying. How slow can those employees be to cause that kind of backup. Absolutely ridiculous. Never visited this Starbucks again.

How can you let your coffee get cold?  I guess getting here late didn't help but at least a waring the coffee is going suck and your not in the mood for customers would have helped.  The good was you were right next to the REI.

I love Starbucks bold coffee.  Today, like many other days, I stood a long slow line at the Cool Springs Mall Starbucks only to find they did not brew a bold coffee.  The other Starbucks in the Franklin area always have a bold coffee brewed and ready.  The Cool Springs Mall Starbucks is slow and maybe understaffed.  Other area Starbucks are great.
